title: Colosseum
text: A must see for any visitor to Rome, but go to the visitor centres and buy a front of the queue pass/ticket or book a guided tour or you will be queuing for hours. Construction on the lower levels on going but dosent detract from the visit. Once you are in you can wander round at your leisure.If you book the tour you get access to Palatine hill and the forum included, again straight in no queuing. They are near to the colosseum and you have a guide. Very good

title: Amazing Structure and Architecture from The Past
text:
It is amazing structure built in Roman empire, imagining how grande it was during the time. It is better to take a guide tour or reading beforehand so you will understand better otherwise you only see a huge old theater without understanding a history behind it. The area is huge as it is not only about the theater but also the area where became the center of day to day life in Rome. Expect to spend at least half day to explore all the area unless you only interest in taking picture. We visited Rome during Easter holiday so it was super crowded everywhere, you could see queue at Colosseum, Vatican museum and St.Peter Basilica so if your main interests are all the tourist spots is better to consider the time when will you visit Rome. 

One thing that you need to take a note, as we expected it was gonna be packed everywhere, we booked the Colosseum ticket online eventho' u need to pay an extra money but it is worth it rather than you have to queue for hours and might have chance to get fraud by sort called unauthorized travel agent people who you can see at every corner colosseum try to get you and charge more money compare if you buy it online through the authorized website. So what we experienced was, we arrived 20mins before the booking time and didn't expect the crazy queue. The authorized website mentions that we have to exchange our ticket in the ticket booth but due to the crowds we didn't even know where was the exact location to exchange. So this people who wear blue or red or yellow vest tried to lure us to one queue and told us that day was ridiculously crazy. We were worry whether cud make in time or not because seemed that the queue can last for more than 1-2hours. I told my husband to check at the front and asked someone over there just to make sure we queue in the right line. One group in front of me apparently had the same problem but they asked to the wrong person so this people tried to convince them to take their tour and asked to refund the ticket that they have bought without assuring that is actually can be refunded.

A few minutes later my husband came and told me we cud directly go to the line in front of the theater by showing the ticket to the authorized guys who wear the white shirt. Luckily, we were still on time to go inside otherwise we would be devastated and I wasn't even sure you can actually refund the ticket. You can exchange your printed ticket inside after the security check and then go to the post if you take guided tour otherwise you are free to go around the theater. So, please be careful not to ask the wrong person because they are really trying to sell their tour package which can cost double from the original price.

title: C
text: Wonderful experience. We were blessed with a perfect weather, not hot or cold.Very educational. Make sure have great walking shoes.

title: SPECTACULAR
text: Ok the Colosseum is an amazing place, iconic and spectacular from almost all angles. Get your tickets online to save yourselves hours in the queues. Be prepared for changes to pre-booked tours, ours was cancelled on the day but we still had access and wandered around ourselves. It was very busy when we visited in April, summer would likely be extremely busy and a lot hotter so beware! So many staff/helpers outside it’s difficult to know who is legit and who is looking to sell you a tour. My advice is go closer to the queues and speak to someone there. Once inside it’s quieter and you can take as much time as you like to walk around. It is an awe inspiring place you wonder what it must have been like at the peak of the Roman Empire. Who doesn’t go to Rome and visit the Colosseum 🤔👌

title: More Amazing in Real Life!
text: The first sight of the Colosseum is overwhelmingly amazing.  You realize that pictures just don't do it justice.  It was so worth going in and seeing the inside.  We listened to the Rick Steves audio guide while inside, which explained everything nicely without having to listen to a boring tour guide.  We used the Roma Pass to get in.

title: Not to be missed!
text: The Colosseum is a must do in Rome. For heaven sake though, buy skip the line tickets online before you go! Yes it means you are locked into a specific time but that's a small sacrifice as you walk by the hundreds of people lined up to buy tickets!! There is so much to see around the Forum that arriving early and wandering is a great way to spend time. Staff surrounding the Colosseum are very helpful in getting you to where you need to be (middle line). We downloaded a self-guided audio tour (Rick Steves is great, but there are also others) and that allowed us to move against the crowd and travel at our own pace. Our tickets allowed us to go back the next day to visit the Forum and Palantine Hill. That was a great idea too since there is so much to see that breaking it up was a little more relaxing.

title: Gladiators
text: When you think about the size and complexity of this structure, it is absolutely hard to believe how this place was constructed and how is looked in the height of its history it must have been heart stopping.

title: Small Group Colosseum & Forum through Viator
text: Met at designated point without a problem to begin our walking tour.  Our guide was an encyclopedia of information on Ancient Rome and brought the ruins of the Forum to life.  There was some repetition and lengthy explanations, but on the whole a good tour.  Next we walked to the Colosseum and our Roman education continued, with amazing sights and time on our own to retrace our steps and find our own way out.  Two must sees, while in Rome!

title: Amazing Visit
text: Our guide, Claudia, led us in a great tour.  She was knowledgable and personable.  Conveyed the history and events with clarity and relevance.  It was obvious her knowledge of the Coliseum / Forum / Palatine Hill area was deep and a personal interest.  We had a most enjoyable tour, and would recommend.  Ian & Annie

title: Never Fails to Amaze!!! A MUST SEE!!!
text:
We visited the Colosseum on our recent visit to Rome and couldn't recommend it more to anyone. We originally visited for the first time 4 years ago on our first ever visit to Rome and were utterly mesmerised by the size, complexity and overwhelming feeling you get when inside it. 

4 years on the same feelings came rushing back, the initial shock by the size of it when walking close up, the chill down the back of the neck when stepping into the arena it was truly amazing. To think of the contests that would have taken place over the years, the emotions that would have been laid bare on the sand and the atrocities that would have been witnessed it really does make you think.

When we first visited we used the Roma pass and just strolled up to the queue however they have since introduced a new system where you have to book a specific entry time. This proved slightly more problematic...we were advised to walk upto the Roman forum and queue there for a time slot as the queue was shorter.

After spending an hour queuing, we got to the front and asked what time slots were available. We were instantly advised there was a time slot in 30 minutes and that is the only one they are allowed to offer!!! So having queued for an hour to get to the front of the queue for the Roman forum we then had to walk back to the Colosseum and then wait around for the entry time.

Once inside the experience was again amazing and wholly worth the effort but I would definitely be a little more clued up than I was and book online to ensure you can get the time slot you want as opposed to the first available!

title: Throngs of people, long lines
text: It was very interesting, but dealing with the lines, heat, all the people as a little much.  Thankfully met a couple from Manchester England that made it much more bearable.

title: All you could ask and more.
text: Let me focus on handicapped accessibility. First, you will need to join a tour or get an admission time. That means someone needs to stand in line for an hour. The entrances involve long queues, so come prepared to walk aways. There is a good elevator to the 4th floor for outstanding views and museum quality static displays. Nice staff to help. Restrooms are difficult or impossible for mobility impaired visitors. Exiting could involve a walk around half the Colosseum from the elevator. But there is a quick exit available... Ask the guard. All in all a difficult day for a mobility impaired visitor. But it is still worth it. What a building!

title: Our experience in using the Roma Pass
text:
The Colosseum was one of the things we definitely wanted to see when we got to Rome.  After we arrived we purchased two 48 hour Roma passes to get the discounts and to ge bookings for the Colosseum.   We were told that we had to book the entry time for the Colosseum online.  When we tried, there was no availability.  So we thought we were going to miss it.

When we used the passes the first time, at the Palatine Hill/Forum entrance, the lady in the ticket booth looked at our passes, gave us two tickets for the PH/F, and then gave us two tickets to the Colosseum, with assigned times for entry.  These times were a couple of hours out, so we had time to walk the hill and the forum.  And that was when we paid the two euro entry fee for the Colosseum tickets.  Before going through this process, I had not seen it described in any of the stuff I had read about the passes.
Having the ticket and an assigned entry time is great.  When we entered we walked past the hundreds of people in line to buy tickets and went right in.

title: Watch out for ticket touts

title: Best in the evening- review ticketing carefully!
text:
The Colosseum is an outstanding historical landmark, and well worth seeing, and to be patient.  It is important to note that ticketing on the official website is somewhat confusing.  Read the instructions carefully.
     If you have a ROMA pass, sold at TI's, and at most tourist venues, this gets you into your first two venues, from a predetermined list, for free, and others at a discount.  We chose the Colosseum as one of our first venue. What was not communicated is that you must sign up on the Colosseum website, for an additional 2 euros/ pp, for an entry time. If you do not know this, and just show up at the Colosseum, you will likely not gain entry, as the tickets for entry times sell out days in advance during the busy season.
     We paid a little bit more for a timed audio tour, which got us in directly, ahead of what seemed like thousands waiting in lines for entry, or to purchase tickets. The audio tour was about an hour in length, was just OK, but if you wanted access to the floor of the arena, an additional fee is needed. This is why I say read the ticketing page carefully.
I used Rick Steves guidebook, his website for Europe travel, and his audio tour for the Colosseum ( in addition to the purchased audio tour), and Romewise.com as additional sources for information on sites, and tips in Italy and found them very informative.

title: Cool but Crowded
text: There is nothing like this place in the world, both visually and historical. The true story about what occured here is by far the most amazing element of the story. To get the most from the visit get a guided tour or at least an audio tour. Considered downloading Rick Steves audio tour if you can get it. The only knock is crowd control. Lines are long for tickets and to get inside. Make sure you reserve in advance and the on site wait for tickets will be minimized. You will also get the time you aant.

title: Historic

title: Be prepared for long wait
text: The Colosseum is a must not miss but be prepared for long wait lines unless you are in a prebooked tour. There are scammers selling cheap tourist trinkets and pickpockets all over so be on alert. But...once inside the historical significance of this place is overwhelming.

title: Unmissable!
text:
The Colloseum is quite simply unmissable when visiting Rome. It is unique and iconic. Whether you are standing on the floor where the gladiators fought for honour, freedom and their lives, or up where the gods look down on those unfortunate enough to find themselves on the arena floor, the views are breathtaking.
The Colloseum is a wonder of architecture. It is a connection with a pagan past when people thought there was nothing wrong with spectacles featuring mortal combat. It is a place of religious veneration to those who perished for their faith. It is a film set for marvellous films such as Roman Holiday and the Scarlet and the Black. It is a wonder of engineering, they could actually flood this place and hold mock sea battles here.
Most of all it is Rome in one image and is simply unmissable!

title: Book online, and skip the line is our new motto

title: Well worth it
text:
Booked tickets online a month before. Be aware that the big tour guide companies buy up all the English tickets very quickly. As a result all English underground tours were sold out for 2 months in advance and we had to get an underground and panoramic tour in Italian. No big deal as we got to see the places, just not understand what they were saying.
It was a complete minefield finding out where to go to get our tickets! We thought the kiosk for reserved tickets, but no, for guided underground tours you go to the small entrance on the opposite side of the colossuem from the main entrance. You go through security, show them your email and they print out your tickets. Then you go back out and wait for your tour to start. If in doubt, there's loads of staff to ask for help.
Great attraction though and worth the hassle!

title: An absolute must see

title: Obviously great attraction but book tickets in advance
text: My friend & I booked tickets online, and in the process discovered that the Colosseum only takes timed tickets as of 2019. This made entry very smooth, but if you want to guarantee admission on a particular date and/or at a particular time, you really should book tickets in advance. We booked less than 24 hours in advance, and while there were tickets from about 14:30 onwards, there couldn't have been more than 200 left total, which would have likely sold out by the next day after considering in-person ticket sales and the volume of crowds. If you buy a ticket in person & Colosseum tickets for that day are sold out, they will give you a time for the next day since tickets last 2 days. This is why you should save yourself a headache & just book a timed ticket online for when you want it. There are lots of guided tours advertising outside the Colosseum that guarantee you tickets but they are far more expensive - you can easily save money by just booking tickets in advance. Also, you can run a bit late on your timed ticket and still get entry - my friend and I were about 10 minutes late but they let us in despite a staff member at the Roman Forum scaring us into thinking they wouldn't.

title: A visit you will never forget

title: A Colossal Monument
text: The Colosseum is an awe-inspiring testament to Roman building. The Colosseum is more than just an ancient amphitheatre built in the 1st century—it's a symbol of Rome. Naturally, it's one of the most popular monuments in town. To get the most out of a trip inside, book your visit with a guide or purchase skip the queue tickets which come with an offer to follow on with a guided tour of the Forum. Alternatively, purchase tickets to the Colosseum while at the Forum or Palatine nearby, then use those to bypass the impossibly long ticket lines at the Colosseum. This all took the best part of a day.

title: Night tour at the Colosseum
text: I would recommend going at night for better pictures. It’s also cooler and you get a private tour of the grounds. It’s truly a surreal tour once you walk into the colosseum. Be prepared to walk and make sure you wear comfortable shoes. There’s an elevator if it’s needed. The tour guides are kind of serious so a glass of wine before your tour may help :) . Enjoy !!!!

title: Monument of time
text: No visit to Rome can skip the Colosseum. Standing as a testament to the lasting shadow the Roman Empire cast over the whole of Europe, the openness of it all invites an intimate reflection. As you approach, do your best to avoid the street cons and Skip-the-line tours they will try to sell you (hard currency only) and buy your tickets online yourself. Enjoy this and Palatine hill on your own time or get a guide from TripAdvisor rather than the ones on the street. Be advised that the dungeons are currently under excavation. Linger in the exhibitions on the second floor!

title: Spectacular!
text:
Just seeing this amazing monument from the outside is a WOW feeling! After seeing several tv-serial and movies I am finally here at this spectacular building. 

First! By a ticket in advance so you dont have to stand in a line to buy a ticket and then in another line to show the ticket and after that screen your stuff.  

Colosseum has a high Security for comming inside the building, its like when you are flying except for that you get to keep your water. When buying a ticket you receive a ticket to Forum Roman and Palantin as well, the other two you can visit the next day if you dont want to take it the same day. We payed 18€/person for a ticket like that and probably overpaid a few euros but still it was a good price.

There is a lot of salesment outside, dont get scammed. If you have a ticket, you go in the faster line and there are staff helping you find your way inside by the lines cause they keep track of the time when they can let you in. Ofcourse you have to show them that you are there, otherweiss they cant help you but if you have a time at for example 14:00, you cant go in at 13:00, if youre lucky you can go in 13:30. The time is very important. Dont buy ticket on place unless you dont want to wait 1 hour in low season.  There is another line for groups but this is for your guide to keep track on.

When entering you either start on the ground floor or you start from the top and work your way down. On the second floor there is also a museum with interesting story of Colosseum with history regarding the fighters, the animals, the hierarchy of the seatings in the arena, the trade and so on. 

There are best possibilities to find a good photo at the second floor cause there are more space here. There are also some signs around the arena with information regarding Colosseum. 

There is a little boutique at the first floor with tourist objects as well as water if you need for a warm day, the prices are a bit more expensive that outside the Colosseum.

title: WOW
text:
There isn’t much to say other than this is a must see when in Rome. 

If you are a first timer I would 100% book on to a tour. You can do this beforehand or on the day you will find many people walking round who work for the Colosseum waiting to sign people up for tours or just give some advice. 

It is very well organised, the tour guides are great and full of knowledge.

title: Try to see it in this life not the next!
text:
Quite jaw dropping when you think how old this place is.
Being able to walk around, see, touch etc was highlight of our trip to Rome.

title: WOW
text:
It's incredible, we took a guided tour of thr colliseum and roman forum and discovered a lot about this marvellous Attraction.
Wonderful experience

title: Cancelled by Tour Company
text: It rained the night before we were to go on this tour so it was cancelled.  I understand that's standard as the Colosseum can flood.  We were disappointed that there were no more daytime tours available that fit our schedule (first week of May 2019). However, we were able to book an night tour directly with CityWonders that was spectacular.

title: ICONIC
text: Must see if in Rome. Number 1 attraction . Be sure to go with a guide!!!.  The history is incredible.  It is undergoing Renovation at the moment...great idea.

title: Worth a visit during the night.
text:
This time we sent there diring the night, specifically for night photos. the construction is outstanding and the illumination excellent.
During the day the visitor lines are always long, taking hours so you can enter the site.

title: Good but not brilliant
text:
Whilst an iconic place to visit and we really enjoyed the history there are two things I feel compelled to mention 
1. The toilets were a disgrace- they could do much better
2. Self guided audio was on a phone. Personally we would have preferred headphones as it was difficult to see the screen and signage was very poor too

A must see attraction though.

title: A must do for visitors to Rome
text:
I may get crowded, it may get hot in summer - but this is a must do for your visit to Rome. 

Also don't forget to do walk by at night - great photo opportuities.

title: Beautiful
text: Visited 3 times while in Rome. Morning afternoon and night. Loved it! Really enjoyed watching the sunset on the colesseum. Beautiful! We booked a guided tour of the arena floor and it was definitely worth it. We used the Walks of Italy Gladiator Gate Tour.

title: Wow ! Wow !
text: This is a magnificent ancient Arena that is still surviving today. This is a must see in Rome. I am glad that i went for the guided tour, it helps a lot in the understanding of the history of this Amphitheatre. As usual, the Arena was packed with Tourist 7 days a week. My guided tour was only on the exterior of the Colosseum and did not include tour into the dungeon which i think is shame but what to do (We went on Easter Friday) so opening hour to all place of interests for that day is short thus we have to rush through all the places. But not too bad as i think walking around the Arena is already an exhilarating experience for me.

title: Just wow!
text:
So much better in person. We didn’t let the cold and rain stop the adventure. 

Went with a tour. Wish we could have done the lower levels where the slaves and animals were kept along with the rest of it. We did see the arena floor which was pretty cool. Didn’t care for the “English stay on this side” and the other side was for French. Didn’t understand that when we were the only ones there. 

Bathroom lines were absurd. Not even ridiculous, but absurd. Port-a-pots would have been super helpful here. I didn’t even try to go. 

Steps were HUGE and slanted downward, so be super careful!

Now...the rest of it.... beautiful, incredible ruins. 

So glad we went!

title: Paid Tour is Worth It!
text: Booked a tour and it was definitely worth it. History, access and additional details worth learning.

title: Amazed experience
text: We visited the colosseum and the Forum with Enza Brocoli from Tour in the city. Enza is an amazing tour guide with a lot of knowledge of the colosseum history.

title: City Break
text:
Busy but beautiful, best to see in evening when illuminated.
Only need a few min so best to do as you walk around key sites.

title: An impressive building
text: Really a must see place in Rome. This building is impressive, definitelly worth a visit, really a huge one.

title: Wonderful tour!
text: It was a wonderful tour that I recommend highly.  The renovations are ongoing but do not detract from the history and the awe of this historic site.  The best tour guides I have found come from CityWonders.  Very knowledgeable!

title: Colosseum
text: Not with the €30 entry ticket.  Best to take a picture from the outside and move on.  Lots of people and the skip the line tickets sold takes just as long to get in

title: 1 hour? Not quite
text: This tour seemed like a scam. We got there at 2:45. The tour was to begin at 3. At 3:00 we were taken to  another man who said we were waiting for two other people. Then we were handed off to a woman who we paid for our entrance tickets. She then handed us off to a man who combined us with two other people. They were booked at 3:30. He then passed us on to a woman who was the actual guide. At that point they merged us with more people scheduled for a later time until almost an hour passed and were were now a group of 20, it seemed they booked every 10 minutes and added people until we all got angry and tired of waiting! At 4 we began the tour! At 4 it should have ended!!! The tour itself was disorganized. This was a mess. We could not have been more disappointed and felt taken advantage of. Do not take this tour!

title: Very well organised tours - book in advance from official site
text:
We booked on the official site the 45 min guided tour.  They do not let you in until 20 mins before, and once you have passed security you wait in a designed bay for your tour guide.  Only about 20 max in our group.  Given headphones so we could hear clearly the guide speaking.  Excellent tour, no queuing, booked tickets at home online, collected tickets at box office opposite the Colosseum (did not take long) - as I had booked 2 tickets under 25 I thought they would want to see ID of some sort but they did not ask.
Fantastic attraction and good informative guide.  Glad we booked the tour as we would be wandering aimlessly not knowing what we were looking at.  Last tour was 5pm.

title: Fascinating
text:
Such a fascinating building but to get the best out of it do it with a tour of some sort. I prefer an audio guide as I can wind on if necessary.
No matter how many times I’ve visited I always learn something new.
Queues can be very long and it’s well worth paying  to skip the line. Look out for ‘free days’, it will be very crowded.

title: Must see location in Rome!
text:
Make sure to buy a ticket up front.
You can pay extra 8 euro and buy a combined ticket with entrance to a small museum next to the roman forum.
Not much to see in this museum, but it will save you time, since the other desks are always full.

 You can also order on-line, but be aware that these tickets are running out fast.

title: Visiting Colosseum
text:
We took guided tour bought from Get your guide. Com. Tour operated by Enjoy Rome operator.
The tour includes 
Official live guide
• Admission fees
• Fast-track entrance (Gladiator Door)
• 3-hour tour
• Headsets
• Restricted area access (Colosseum Arena

title: No one comes to Rome without seeing the Colosseum
text: I did the underground tour, was fun to see the area but it’s only a small part of the tour and access to the Colosseum.

title: Great place
text: We got a private tour guide for this and she was just amazing with the kids full of information on the colosseum. Very interesting place

title: Must do in Rome,  but make sure you book a slip the line ticket
text: A Rome must do.  There are several tour companies that provide skip the line with a guided tour.  If your like me,  you'll get much more out of the experience with someone walking you through it.

title: Stepping back in history
text: Worth it to pay for skip the line and a guide. This is for most a once in a lifetime experience so get the history lesson.

title: Great place to make Babs
text: Awfully crowded, but filled with hidden nooks and crannies. Great place for making memories and appreciating old ones.

title: Must visit!!
text: Its a fantabulous building and such rich history.. We took a private guided tour and it was totally worth it!!!

title: Dropped my IPhone in the Colosseum
text: We took a tour of the 'underground' colosseum.  My IPhone slipped from my hands and fell into the lion's den, literally.  The staff were helpful in retrieving my wayward device and very goodnatured with my clumsiness.  This place is definitely worth the crowds.  I would recommend a tour. The tour was very helpful.

title: Really need to buy “Fast track” tickets
text: We got to the Colosseum on the Hop on hop off bus where we also bought our fast track entry tickets. No we weren’t ripped off either! They cost us €22 each which included a guide to take us to the entrance. Others were queuing for hours. As for the actual Colosseum, there was not a great deal of information there. The views were ruined by lots of scaffolding, but not a workman/woman in sight! Interesting to visit as world known

title: Impressive structure
text: No doubt about it the historic structure is impressive.  Constant attention by the army of workers continue to maintain the structure and that is to be applauded.  But be prepared to be jostled pushed ordered about as you try to navigate through.  Selfie stick weilding modern day gladiators everywhere.  Ride staff monitoring the admission line.  I wouldn't bother paying to skip the line with a group as by the time U wait for the tour to start .... Listen to 30min waffle outside ... Still wait further at the "skip the line" gate ... Despite the tour operators scare tactics of a 2 hour line without them ... You will be in and enjoying the inside just as quickly.  Download info on the place before hand because most tours are 25 persons plus listening in a group surrounding a quietly spoken "guide" reciting what they googled, whilst surrounded by 100s of similar sheep/tourists.  Don't fall for the .... Only 14 or 15 in yr group routine either .... Thats an outright lie.  My advise ... Go .... Research .... Take water and stand in line 😀

title: Colosseum Tour was great
text: Did this tour of the Colosseum.Tour guide was very good. It was very interesting. I would recommend.

title: A must see
text: I would highly recommend getting a guide and do the underground tour! There is a guide near this that has great English so it is easy to understand.

title: Loved our guided Colosseum tour
text: We had a a fabulous tour with our wonderful Context Tour guide. We were able to start out on ground level, before going up to the area where the public has general access. This was very interesting because we saw the where gladiators and animals were kept before the games began. We learned about how the Romans recycled so many materials from the Colosseum and how the appearance of the arena has evolved over time.

title: Colosseum
text:
Amazing. Interesting history. Can’t believe the detail of the building and how long it was used for.

I would advise a jump the que guide

title: Surreal
text:
This is a must when visiting Rome. I would recommend taking a tour during the day in order to see the colosseum inside. Make sure this a with a group as you can then skip the queue. Be mindful when you enter you will be subject to a search so make sure you don’t have too much on your person as containers aren’t quickly returned back to place belongings in. 
Highly recommend returning in the night as the ancient relic is even more beautiful by night.

title: Skip the line guided tour
text: Shop around but I definitely recommend buying a guided skip the line ticket. Speak up so you don't join the wrong line ending up in queues to get in. The ushers are very helpful.

title: get online tickets and save 3-4 hours
text: The best advice I can give is: get tickets online and change your online reservation voucher to the original ticket in the office there. The line is always long. The bathrooms have a long line so make sure you go there prepared. If you get the city pass the ticket for Colosseum is included too so it is a good deal

title: Awesome sight to see!
text: Go and see- the must do in Rome! Guided tour not necessary for it is still a sight in itself. Loved it!

title: The Roman Guy Colosseum Arena Floor and Roman Forum Tour
text: An excellent, informative tour by Lucina. Although we booked a group tour it was just the two of us so no head phones required which made it even better. Lucina was friendly, fun and very knowledgeable. Would highly recommend The Roman Guy Tours. Very good instructions on meeting point, good signage also.

title: Simply breathtaking
text: This is my second time visiting and I got chills walking in again for the second time.  The history of this place, it just blows you away every time.

title: Magnificent and much larger than I thought it was
text: I'd only seen pictures of the colosseum so to see it in person was another thing entirely. It's absolutely stunning. Lines can be long but worth a wait.

title: Tour of Colosseum
text: We had a tour of the Colosseum with Gio.  She was so passionate about her job and her knowledge of everything was remarkable.  I highly recommend this tour!

title: Time Was Not On My Side, Didn't Go! So Amazed By The Outside!
text: Time was not on my side! I didn't have enough time to go in, but it was a photographers dream to take night photos of a fantastic sight! Now I have a reason to return to Rome!

title: Tip for outside the Colosseum
text: There are employees (information staff) of the colosseum every few feet in the area surrounding the colosseum that will approach you, they are just there to help you and make your visit as easy as possible however it is best to avoid eye contact or engagement of any kind with any other people that approach you with questions (i.e. where are you from) or try to put bracelets on your wrist... it’s best to simply shake your head, look away or walk straight past them.

title: Definitely worth a look
text: Myself and my wife recently visited the colosseum, we booked with a guided tour, which in my opinion is the only way to do it. The queues were 2/3 hours long if you didn’t book, we got in at our scheduled time and after the tour is done you can free roam all you like. It’s a stunning sight to see and the palatine gardens afterwards are also amazing, so make sure you get that included in your ticket.

title: Smaller than imagined
text: we had purchased an OMNIA Card to see this and it allowed us in but to see the pit and dungeons where the slaves lived and fought you had to pay extra which was a pit of a disappointment. A bit of a walk from the metro at Vittorio Emanuele, would recommend TERMINI for this and the Forum

title: Unbelievable!
text: Do yourselves a favour, and watch Gladiator before coming here. This place is amazing! We did a nighttime tour, and were able to go under the stadium, and up to the higher levels as well. Beautiful structure, fascinating history!

title: Gladiators, Lions & Christians
text: What an amazing architectural feat. We were blown away by the complexity of this 2000 year old magnificent building. the things that went on there may have been reprehensible, but as some people say, "That's entertainment!"

title: Colosseum fast track
text: Was worried about all the bad reviews. I never do tours, bought there fast track ticket unguided. Was the first person in the colosseum, had her all to myself for a minute it was pure magic. Grazie Mille Co-op culture! Book there opening tour and show up 45 minutes early. It’s worth the wait to have a wonder of the world to yourself.

title: Incredible
text: So this was the first tourist attraction that we went and saw and it was very overwhelming to be in. The structure looks large in movies, looks small in photos, but is very large in person. Navigating it was easy given that you can basically circle almost the entire thing from the inside (which shielded us from the rain), and it also has stairs/elevators available. The inside was very beautiful. Since we did not do a tour I'm sure we missed a lot of things inside of it, but just walking it worked great for us. Its right near a subway station which makes it very convenient to get to/from.

title: A magnificent piece of ancient history with scaffolds and signs of no entry all over the place.
text:
We purchased our tickets online and that was a `great time saver. 
First off there is nothing but confusion when trying to get on the correct lines to get in and very few people seem to know what they are doing. 
Once you finally get in there is so much work being done it is annoying. For instance the floor of the arena was ripped up in the mid to late 1800s and their still working on it!

title: Must see
text: This is an amazing feat of engineering at any time of human civilization. Take the tour as it gets you in to the Colosseum without having to wait in line for hours.

title: mind-blowing
text:
Photos just don’t do this building any justice, nor indeed any of the amazing Roman ruins. 
First impression is that they are huge and you can’t believe there was the technology to build them so big so long ago.

It is huge. I am almost 2 metres tall, and the ground floor arches tower way way over my head. Then there are multiple storeys above.

There are thousands of touts trying to persuade you to pay them to get you in without queuing. There are also dozens of immigrants trying to sell you things. They quickly guess your language, act incredibly friendly, and want to high five, and grasp your arm, and compare skin colour, or watches, or anything to get you close. I am imposing enough to simply tell them no thanks “No, grazie,” and keep walking. They persist for a few steps but they recognise that I ain’t to be intimidated or conned. 

There are simply thousands of tourists walking around the area, but it's still easy enough to get around, outside anyway. And to be honest, I didn't feel the need to go inside.

I’m very glad we didn’t join a tour – I saw so many harassed people trying to keep up with groups,

You can see the Colosseum from many parts of Rome - it must have been a staggeringly powerful symbol of Roman superiority in its day when there were a lot fewer big modern buildings around.

title: Awesome
text: Another “must see” when in Rome, obviously. Awesome sight to see, inside and out. Again, the lines to enter can be daunting, but worth the wait

title: Colossus visit
text: What can one say about this awesome  site? The reconstruction continues. It was great when our guide pointed out the differences between the Greek and Roman marble, the different tiers of the arena, the entrances where the public, gladiators and animals would enter from different gates, how the Roman aqueducts would flood the arena during the summer months, lots of ancient history to take absorb.

title: Beware
text: We book a Rome tour/transfer.  The tour guide did not show up.  We called and Viator never contacted him to book the tour.  I am very unhappy.  I gues one needs to quadruple check everything viator commits to.   They are refunding the tour but nothing for our angst and aggravation

title: Get tickets in advance
text:
I’ve been here a few times during the last 30 years and it thrills me every visit. It’s an engineering master piece. To imagine how beautiful, brutal and decadent it was , is actually fun.
This time I came with my 4 and a half year old son. He loved the idea of having gladiators fighting with animals although he didn’t know how violent it would be.
The place was so crowded. The X-ray queue was long. Do yourself a favor, buy ticket in advance. Even with ticket, the queue was long. Try to get there early to save your time and to avoid being push too often.

title: Such history
text: Our tour guide through Get Your Guide gave us headphones to listen to the rich history/stories of the times as we walked around. She was very knowledgeable and friendly. Once inside we could almost image back in time. 2 things we didn’t know: 1) there used to be female gladiators as well. 2) they used to fill the bottom of the Colosseum with billions of liters of water, bring in ships and have war games!! Worth the $56.73 pp

title: An obvious must see!
text: Honestly, there is a reason why this is one of the man made wonders of the world! Walking through the colosseum is so surreal when you realize exactly how old it is. It really blew my mind that something that gigantic was created before any kind of modern technology. Tickets can be pretty pricey to enter, especially depending on the type of tour you want to do, but there are some tickets that combine a tour of the colosseum and the Roman forum. That’s what we did and I think it was worth it! Just be prepared for crowds pretty much all of the time.

title: A must in Rome
text:
Well kept Colosseum, one pillar of Rome.  

 I recommend to visit and hire the guide as he/she will also explain the Roman history.  Try to visit early in the morning to have less sun.  

Enjoy; it’s marvelous.

title: Monument to Roman engineering
text:
The most famous attraction in Rome dates back to the first century and was the site of gladiatorial contests, events, plays, executions and ceremonies. The oval amphitheater had a hollow floor with a hypogeum underneath. This was used to provide easy access to gladiators and animals. The floor of the arena was wooden and covered with sand to give the impression of a solid floor.

The Colosseum is a grand statement of ancient Roman engineering and is a must visit attraction in Rome.

title: Built in only 8 years!!
text: Visited this as part of a day long tour and we arrived in the afternoon. Our tour guide Ankler was perfect, easy to listen to and understand coupled with her knowledge and enthusiasm for the history of the place. Its just so huge!!

title: Absolutely awesome
text: Amazing place to visit . The scale of this is immense . Book your tickets on line before you go . Ignore the ticket touts , only talk to people with STAFF on their vests who are very helpful. Go straight to the line 10 mins before your ticket entrance time . Very well organised . Get an audio guide for 5 euro .

title: Amazing experience
text: This was the one place my husband wanted to see.  We made it to the top.  What an experience, what history!  So glad we traveled to Rome.  Seeing this was an amazing experience.

title: Amazing Historic building
text: This was one of our stops on a tour with My Best Tour with Roberto Occhiuzzi. Roberto shared all the information about the Colosseum. This was one of our favorites on our tour.

title: Cancelled Tour
text: I booked 2 tickets for an upper tour to take place on May 3, 2019 at 1:35.  When we arrived to take the tour we were told the tour had been cancelled.  This was a strange event as we had booked the tour through what we thought was the official site for Colosseum tours, Coop Culture.  We were told we would receive a credit refund the following week.  Of course the credit refund has not been processed.

title: A must-see in Rome - epic building!
text:
Came here on our 2nd day in Rome, had a small group tour with audio connection to the guide, who was really helpful and knowledgeable. Very enthusiastic! The building up close is just magnificent and the photos won't do it justice. Takes a bit of time to get in even with skip-the-line through the tour, but worth it. Also they only let a certain amount of people in at any one time which is actually a really good idea so you're not like sardines when you get in there. 
The inside is great and you get a real feel for how things were back when there were gladiator shows etc. Lots of relics to see too. Next time I come I'd love to have a virtual reality option as it would be good to live in the moment of how it was - not sure if they already do this but would be great. 
Only negative is the sheer amount of people using the attraction to sell water (one guy coming round to us about 4 times going "agua agua agua" despite us all having bottles on us!) and the con-men being super friendly and then giving you some crappy bracelet then demanding money for it to help his family - it's a personal gripe of mine as it does dampen the experience a bit. Maybe Rome authorities should be policing the area a bit better as some people could get ripped off by this if they're not aware of what's going on.

title: Even with skip the line a long cue
text: So busy, and bad signing. We’ve made a reservation and have to pick it up at the other side. After picking up the tickets, we had to go in line, but it was soooo long. Waiting time 40 min, before we were in. A lot of restaurations and work inside.

title: Very epic jojo meeting
text: I’m waiting for Giorno and his crew here at the coliseum in Rome to give him the requiem arrow and it is a very epic wait

title: Holiday
text: Watch out for the guys selling tours. Go to the Forum and Plalaton hill entrance and get you ticket this will give you a time to get in the Colosseum which will save you queuing

title: Magical place
text:
The colosseum has an atmosphere of its own.  What an amazing place.  It's so much bigger than you imagine and wonderful to see in person.  
We visited in April 2019 which proved to be ideal as it wasn't crowded and we could take time to really study the colosseum. 
It really is a must see when you go to Rome.

title: Breathtaking!
text: Touring to Colosseum was once in a lifetime experience. From seeing where the slaves, criminals, and animals were kept, to seeing the area floor. It was a highlight of our time in Rome!

title: Awed
text: Took a city tour this time and managed to see things we missed on our own a few years ago. The ancient history we learn is nothing like seeing it in person, you simply cannot believe your eyes. Take a guided tour, they are very knowledgeable and you'll learn things you never did.

title: Bus trip to Colosseum
text: So historical, we just caught a bus from outside our hotel and jumped off at the Colosseum.  After viewing, caught the next bus back to hotel.

title: Colosseum
text: Booked tickets for a guided tour which included skip the line and access to the arena floor. Well worth the extra money. The guide was an archeologist and so very passionate and knowledgeable. We were told they only let 400 people a day have access to the arena floor so make sure your tour included this as it certainly is a once in a life time opportunity. Go early as it gets packed. Our tour was at 9am and it seemed packed even then. This is an amazing piece of history, a must see!!

title: Just breathtaking
text: This structure stands proud but perhaps not of its history. You need to book yourself on a tour to appreciate this place. Tip. Go see the Vatican first you will appreciate the colloseum more.

title: Touristy and packed, but must see.
text: I went inside via a Cosmos Tour, but if you want to go on your own, I think it's like 50 Euros. IMO it is worth it. I am big into history and have a degree in linguistics so I really enjoyed it. It's crazy to think how old this building is. I wish I were more into photography when I went to see it and could show you guys some cool shots. But it's cool.

title: Must see!
text: Number one must see in Rome! Better to get your ticket online and reserve a time (available in 60 days ahead) to skip the line otherwise you have to wait in line for a long time.

title: Such a neat tour!
text: We LOVED our tour of the colosseum, there was so much to learn! Our guide was amazing and we really learned a lot. Unfortunately I don’t recall her name, but we did the 1:00 tour on Tuesday, May 14th. Would highly recommend if you are visiting Rome.

title: Night time, best time!
text: Went to the night tour which was absolutely beautiful.  Was a cold, windy and rainy night but even that didn't deter from the wonderful tour.  We took 5 tours through Roman guy and each got better and better.  Wonderful company and informative, respectful guides and the company goes out of their way to support your needs.  Highly recommend.

title: Great for the history just crowded.
text:
Pay serious attention to how the get a reservation in advance and then where the line is at the site to get the actual ticket. Once this is done you just walk in after security. they were serious about sticking to letting you in at your reservation time.
We had downloaded the Rick Steve's free tour which worked very well. 
The crowds were pretty big and we were off season in early April so I can imagine what it would be like in peak season and hot weather. That said worth the tour for the history. Brutal but that was the time.

title: Amazing Historical Site
text: It is amazing to visit such a massive site built so long ago with such limited resources. A must see when visiting Rome.

title: Nice
text: The individual queue is huge. It can even take up to 2 hours just to wait to enter on the most busy days. However, there are people with a tour info badge which can sell you group guide tickets for a little extra money. The prices usually start from the base price + 5 Euro extra than the normal tax and can go up. It's nice to have a guide that explains everything to you.

title: A wonder of history
text: What a marvel to finally see in person. Amazing what wonderful condition its in-nothing else like it in the world. Rome has so much to offer the visitor.

title: Rome Colosseum Scam / Robbery Alert
text:
Hi all,

This is my first time visiting Italy. Solo traveler. On my first day (yesterday) I visited the Colosseum. After getting my online-reserved tickets from the office, I was approached by a black man wearing blue (similar to staff at colosseum) offering a “free” wrist bracelet and handshake. He was full of smiles and persistent. While I refused the bracelet I stupidly fell for the handshake, thinking that’ll get rid of him. The handshake became a forceful drag to the side fence and cornered off by three other black men demanding money. I was punched in the left arm and chest. They took all my cash, walked away and continued on approaching other tourists.

This all happened at 2pm in bright daylight right in front of the colosseum. I went and found a police who was nearby and pointed them out to her immediately after it happened. She apologised that this happened, she simply showed me how to find the closest police station and said I should report this, then walked on.

It seems to be an organised scheme. As I walked around the colosseum, I saw more black men approaching people with the same bracelets. This was also happening in front of the Castel Sant’Angelo where I went to later that afternoon.

Luckily I was able to walk away with just a bruised arm and lost only money. So I thought I’d share this with anyone visiting the Colosseum and Castel Sant’Angelo area. If approached by men of the above description, AVOID at all cost, don’t even bother giving them your eye contact or speak to them. Walk away.

PLEASE BE CAREFUL everyone.

Stay safe~

title: Expected more
text:
Don't bother with "skip-the-line" or the ticket touts. We got there around 08:30 and gained our tickets at the entrance at 09:20. Don't bother with the audio guide it costs extra and the amount of information provided is pitiful. You also have to join another queue to pick up your audioguide. If you do chose to take the audioguide too then make sure that you book and pay for it at the main ticket point. Otherwise your request to purchase the audioguide at the audioguide booth will be rejected.

Palatine Hill and the Forum are included in the Colosseum ticket price.

Signage again is diabolical.

And you will have to pay extra for a ticket to get onto the floor of the Colosseum.

Entry price €12 per person plus €5.50 per person for the audioguide. The audioguide only works for the Colosseum

title: Beautiful & interesting
text: I would recommend going on an assisted tour, they can explain whatever thing is,lots to see and it’s very interesting. Toilets are available inside. Security is also very good.

title: Awesome monument
text:
Tried the special tour with visit to arena
Worth visitng inside beautiful and one of the great big structure with marvellous enigneering and design 

though nearly destroyed but if imagining the whle structure and history related to it felt awesome

title: The Coliseum
text: Throughly recommend visiting The Coliseum, it is wonderful place to visit, the history about the place just wonderful.

title: Fabulouso
text: We were on an explore Rome yourself  excursion from a cruise ship and only had 5 hours to spare. The Coliseum was always a place we wanted to visit, so was number one on our hit list for the day. We originally were going to book a shore excursion from our ship, but reading the small print, it said if the waiting lines were too long, the visit to the inside would be cancelled, so we went ahead and booked our own on the official Coliseum web site, two months before our visit. It's a good job we did as the lines on the day of our visit were massive, and not only did we book entry, we also booked the very hard to come by and much elusive tour of the underground of the Coliseum and the magnificent panoramic view points right at the top, which people couldnt access. Well to say it was worth it is an understatement. The tour lasted one and a half hours, narrated by an extremely informative guide, and gave us an insight as to what went on under this magnificent place, with info on where the animals were kept, how the lifts which took them and Gladiators worked, and everything in between. We had goosebumps entering the arena  and came out intrigued and full of knowledge about one of the world's top 10 places to visit. A tip is if you want a tour of this caliber, book with online official web site,  and not the skip the line rip off merchants that deal with these tours online or around the city. Instead of the horrifying prices agents were asking all this tour cost was 48 euros for both of us  and you are guaranteed entrance.

title: Lovely!
text:
The ticket to the Rome Colosseum is relatively cheap, 12€ if you buy it inside the Coliseu, and you can visit also the Forum Romano and the Palatino Hill with the same ticket! Lines weren't as huge as we expected them, which was great!
The Colosseum itself is Amazing and you basically time travel once you're there! It has also a very well explained story about what it used to be! :)
One tip, don't buy the tickets online or on the guides selling them outside, you pay more and it's not necessary! :)
Definitely worth visiting! :)

title: Beware of the hucksters
text: We had only a day in Rome & wanted to go tour colosseum.  The minute you get close there are ‘your touts/skip the line’ hucksters salivating at opportunity to relieve you of your hard earned money.  My friend & I were totally turned off & as we’d try to disengage & walk away they’d follow. Fortunately we are a couple of ornery old birds.  We got caught in a downpour whilst in line & hucksters with umbrellas & plastic ponchos emerged. It takes away from enjoyment & I wish colosseum or Rome would regulate or crack down on this nonsense.  We chose to stand in line for 12 euro tix.

title: Cool
text:
The history surrounding this place is amazing. One of the forgotten treasures of Ancient Rome.

I can only recommend a tour. We went with More of Rome and had  Dimitri run our tour. We had information coming out of our ears. We were engaged and it was engaging.

You cannot help but get caught up in the history of the place.

Top notch!

title: Take the Tour!
text: We used Walks of Italy for a small group tour of the colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palantine Hill. Sev was the best tour guide I've ever had (my history-major brother agreed). Fascinating, informative, engaging--it's a Do Not Miss.

title: Awesome!
text: This is something not to be missed! Be aware that if you do not have a guided tour you do not have access to the top tier in the Colleseum.Also, do skip the queue as otherwise you could be waiting hours to get your ticket and to get in!  Its worth paying the extra. When you get your ticket for Colleseum, Palate Hill & Roman Forum (it's all included on one ticket) be aware that you can do it over 2 Consecutive days only! I was told that to see it in the evening all lit up was pretty special by several people but sadly only got to see it in the day.

title: amazing
text:
well what can I say about ?? it's only one of the most famous buildings on the planet. great historical and architectural wonder.
We took the great tour from Romabout.com and skipped the lines like we were bosses!

title: Great monument sadly in ruins now!
text: A guided tour for this monument is not technically required, they are overpriced and not worth the money paid. One can easily spend 1.5 hours in the Colosseum alone. This historic monument is remarkable and a wonder indeed which is sadly in ruins now. People going to visit need to be mindful of steep stairs, diving the monument into two accessible levels. There is a lift available for wheelchair access too. Expect a big queue for security check before getting into the building. Metro is an easy way to access this historic site. Make sure you wear comfortable shoes and clothing as this day trip will involve plenty of walking.

title: Night Tour gives so much more- Worth the $$
text:
A night tour costs more, but it is well worth the price. We only had 1 tour group in front and two behind us the entire tour. The night tour takes you into the basement. Overall, the entire area is much more serene.
We started with a group tour meeting off-site, that pre-arranged for our night tour. We saw much of the general area then ended up at the Colosseum for the tour finish. So we saw more than the Colosseum for the tour, besides more of the Colosseum.
If you are lucky, Nero, the Colosseum's black cat will come up and personally welcome you.

title: Express Pass, Gladiator Entrance to the Colosseum is far and above what we expected! Absolute must when in Rome!
text: Fee was the most incredible guide! So enthusiastic, and, throughly enjoy every second! We highly recommend this tour and more importantly this guide. She was so knowledgeable and truly enjoyed sharing her Love of history with us. Thank you Fee for making history come alive before our eyes!

title: Wonderful Sight
text:
We couldn’t get tickets on line from Colosseum website so when we arrived we bought tickets from one the many ticket sellers outside.  I must say I was a bit dubious when our daughter told us that’s what she did but it was absolutely fine.  We booked an underground tour of this amazing place and the guide was great.  He gave us a detailed explanation of the history of the roman emperors and amazed our group with facts and info.  The tour lasted 90 mins and we stayed in afterwards to have another look around.
When I stood on the small part of the arena floor that has been rebuilt and looked up, I felt overwhelmed.  How they managed to build such a magnificent structure all those years ago is truly amazing.

A definite must see for anyone visiting Rome.

title: Romearoundtours.com great way to see the Colosseum
text: Had the best experience doing a night tour of the Colosseum.  Our guide was an archaeologist and it was spectacular!  I had even been to the Colosseum before, so wasn't expecting to be wowed, but I was.  The tour was booked through Romearoundtours.com.

title: Visiting the Colosseum
text: Very interesting history regarding the Colosseum. I learned some things i did not now. We bought some "skip the lines" tickets and was so very glad we did.

title: Colosseum
text: Excellent tour guide.  Very knowledgeable, clear, and engaging.  Spoke excellent English.  was interesting for 3 hours.  Not easy to do. Great experience.

title: Great place
text:
Awesome attraction. A must see if in Rome. My suggestion would be to pre book a guided tour that skips the large line ups. Do this via a suggestion on the TripAdvisor app, not via one of the hundreds of ppl standing out the front trying to sell you a ticket. 
Take as little as possible inside and when walking to the attraction/s. Too many dodgy ppl around Rome. Once inside the colosseum you are fine though.

title: Incredible outside
text: This construction is really beautiful from outside day and night. It doesn’t have too much amazing things inside. Museum isn’t something special.

title: Must see site
text:
We booked through one of the trip advisor vendors which was a great experience 
Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and accommodating 
Probably better to book through a tour guide before coming here

title: Night illumination
text:
In our last night in Rome we decided to head out to the colosseum and see it illuminated, it was a bit of a walk
From our hotel but we enjoyed other sites and dinner along the way. If you get a chance to visit at night I highly recommend it! It’s beautiful. We tried to get an inside underground tour but they were sold out, I hear they are amazing and would try next visit booking in advance.

title: Doesn’t matter how many times you have been here, it still amaz you.
text: Amazing structure/ building. Being here, you feel you are part of the history. A place must visit when you are in Roma.

title: Visit ... even if just for a photo
text:
It's one of the most well-known landmarks worldwide and absolutely worth a visit to admire the overall structure from a distance and up-close. 

If you're looking for a great photo opportunity and away from the crowds in the background it's best to view the Colosseum from "Parco del Cole Oppio". It's right across the road from the Colosseum but slightly elevated giving a great panoramic short. 

If you're thinking about taking an inside tour it may be best to do this with a tour operator (which also guarantees you can skip the lines) may be the best bet. Lines are never-ending so be prepared. 

Colosseum access:
An easy 20-25 min walk from the Roma Termini station or there's also the subway with a stop right in front of the Colosseum.

title: Badly organised.
text: The people that run the security here are a nightmare. Long long queues and though at one time I would have to take off my wedding ring! Full of unwanted street sellers who do not leave you alone and there seems to be no control over them.

title: WOW.
text: Despite it raining on the day we visited the Colosseum. All I could say was 'Wow'. It is such an impressive structure and the fact it's still standing all these years later is just amazing. We booked a private tour that took in the underground, arena floor and all upper floors with guide as we didn't want to miss anything. Our guide was very informative and got us through the maze and the crowds with ease, although I suspect in high-season it would not be as easy! I could go on for ages, but I think my only advice would be to pay that extra to get a full tour, see all of it and be educated as you do - it's worth the money honestly! Just go and 'Wow' yourself!

title: Unmissable
text: What can be said....coming to Rome and not seeing the Colosseum shouldn't happen! I'd recommend a guided tour to get as much information and skip the queue tickets

title: Absolutely Gorgeous
text: It is a rare thing to see a ruin which has lasted for thousands of years.  Considering the scale and design of this arena; it cannot be comprehended how insane this place is.  what took place here and how they accomplished it.  All of the mechanisms and infrastructure and moving parts to bring gladiatorial games together.  This is a must-see in Rome

title: Bucket List
text: It's iconic, inspired modern day sports stadiums, and one of the most recognized throughout the entire world. Having said that, like many other "must sees", go early to avoid massive crowds. Must book date/time slot to go inside, etc. in advance. Will still have to line up and wait a bit.  If you don't want to go inside, plenty of places for great photos, which are free :-)

title: Fantastic
text: We loved our tour around the colloseum. Well worth paying for a guide. It is like living in the land of the giants. Genuinely unimaginable that something so big could have been constructed at the time it was.

title: Nice historic place
text: Nice historic place but crowded and long eating lines. Also I have been twice and is always under construction and repairs .

title: Brilliant
text:
Had a great tour guide skipped the que 
Very informative cost was ok only about 20 
In the group 
Better than than I expected

title: Great view
text: My first time in Rome,this is incredible amazing. The only thing is very priced to have tours guia and to avoid the long queue. Anyway the visited was great.

title: Honeymoon
text: Amazing piece of art I just felt as if I was born in the wrong era! I will be back again soon one day

title: Beware the scammers
text: Recently visited Rome and it lived up to all expectations, however there are numerous people around the city that try to force things on you insisting that they are free,  they then try to demand money as though you have purchased the item, usually African bracelets,  ignore these people at all costs.

title: Nighttime Dungeons Tour
text: We were scheduled for a morning tour and tour group changed it to the evening which kind of disrupted our plans for the day but turned out to be a great time swap.  Much less crowded and dungeons portion of the tour felt like you were there back in the days of the gladiators.  Highly recommend going at night!

title: To bussy with con artists everywhere
text: Waste of time trying to get. The thousands of tourism traps have made it a hell hole to visit. One of the worst places for tourist con men I have visited. Every 10 seconds someone rammed something in my face to buy and I have visited Marrakech which was a walk in the park compared to the Colosseum.

title: Make sure you book advance reservations
text: Make sure you book advance reservations or you will not get in.  It is very crowded.  Go as early as possible.

title: A transporter back to the peak of the empire
text: Just the fact that a lot of this construction still stands today is a reminder of the glory of this great empire.

title: You can’t miss it while visiting Rome
text: It a must while visiting Rome; lots of history, great tours available but keep in mind that the lines are extremely long!

title: Beautiful
text: If we're honest, the Colosseum is not QUITE as breathtaking as the Pantheon. We saw it on our second day in Rome and it's a gorgeous backdrop for pictures, for sure. However, the sheer beauty of the Pantheon, in our opinion, surpassed it. Still, the Colosseum is a must-see and attracts lots of smiling tourists, and the atmosphere is friendly with plenty of places to sit and marvel at the beautiful structure. We would recommend!

title: Amazing
text: A sight not to be missed. The Colosseum and Forum were unbelievable and the Roman Guys underground with top tier level was the best tour, albeit most expensive but worth it. We enjoyed this sight as much as the Vatican. The history will blow you away.

title: Booked expensive tour and loved it
text:
Paid 79 Euros each to go to all levels of the Colosseum (basement...only 24 allowed at a time...arena floor, main floor, top floor...only 24 allowed at a time). 

This place is a zoo. There are like 6 different lines that all seem to be going to the same place (main floor and arena floor). The price itself is worth it just to skip all the lines and have a bit of personal space. We went in May and they told us that June and July are far more crowded...hard to even imagine. 

Great pictures from the top of the arena and cool views and tour of where the animals and slaves were kept.

title: Magnificent
text:
Must visit! Magnificent structure, especially amazing when it was built so long ago. 

Take a walk around the colosseum for some good photography spots.

title: Colosseum and Palatine Hill
text: The Colosseum tour was good and I then paid €20 to do the Palatine Hill as an add on. Simona is an archeologist and shared her enthusiasm for the history of the hill. Finished looking down at the Roman Forum. Great experience! Excellent guide!

title: Majestic
text: A majestic structure and a must see. A bit crowded due to the metro station construction. Plenty of tours being sold on the spot be careful to check prices and content

title: Colosseum
text: Really beautiful and definitely a must see if you've never been to Italy. The only set back is the people  kind of bombard you to try to sell you various things

title: The Primus of Rome!
text: There is plenty of things to do and see in Rome but none can compare to the colosseum. Its plentiful history and fantastic construction is truly a wonder!

title: Sightseeing
text: Brilliant but disappointed with the amount of rubbish on the streets around, will be back to do full tour of the area of the colloseum

title: Lovely visit
text: We went during the celebrations so we got in free but recommend buying your joint ticket elsewhere as queues were long here so best to avoid if possible!

title: History on a massive yet intimate scale
text: While I've heard about and seen pictures of the Colosseum my entire life, there is nothing quite like actually being inside it. The scale is hard to appreciate with a 2-dimensional representation. Our guide also explained fascinating aspects of the Colosseum's construction, renovation, use, etc. that would seldom be part of passing references. It and the forum next door are a great starting place to understand Roman,  Italian, and even Western history. (Would definitely recommend the skip-the-line option when arranging a tour.)

title: Another Must See in Rome
text: I would recommend paying the extra and taking the guided tour.You get so much more information then the self audio tour.You also get to go more places.

title: Admire the outside - forget about the inside
text: The Colosseum is one of the - if not the - landmark of Rome. The outside is gorgeous - admire it from a distance to see it in its full glory. Many people also go inside, but I would recommend not to. I have done it once and was not impressed. Entry prices are high, it is very crowded and as much has gone over the centuries, it lacks inside the charm the outside has. Save yourself the money and the trouble. Touring the Forum Romanum and the Paladium are much more interesting and gives you a much better picture about the history of Rome in the days of the Roman Empire 2000 years ago.

title: Rainy but made the best of it
text: We went to the Colosseum and Palatine hill with Gianluca and although we trudged through some rain and cold, he was very upbeat, full of special information about the sights and very accommodating to our group. Would definitely book again!

title: Speechless!
text: Can you imagine standing where Noble Romans nice stood in a building built BC? Unbelievable! Once in a lifetime experience.

title: Amazing
text: Had a skip the line guided tour with roman forum 40euro deffo worth it.  Amazing history and you just can't believe this was built over 2000 years ago.

title: A must do!!
text:
Once you get over how crowded it can get with tourists, tour operators, and scammers, you really get a sense of the history and awe of the Colleseum. Beware of anyone who comes up to you and is over friendly, chances are they are trying to scam you by selling you a “good luck” bracelet, first telling you it’s free and then asking for money. I saw this multiple times and it kinda irked me to be honest. 

We booked the Colleseum express tour with gladiator entrance and arena access with City Wonders, it was 10/10!! I highly recommend it. You could spend hours scouring tour operators and find yourself at a lost not knowing which one to book because they all have positive and negative reviews.

title: I Love the Colosseum
text: We visited the Colosseum in April 2019. I absolutely love it, I could sit there and take it in all day. Defiantly worth going down at night to take in the glow and beauty. We also did a tour and went inside, I did not really find that worth it as it is mostly just rubble inside, but still neat to say we were inside.

title: You gotta go!!!
text:
First thing you should do is book the tickets on the OFFICIAL colusseum website as soon as possible as they sell out quickly. We regrettably didn't get to see the underground and arena areas of the colusseum which I believe are amazing. General entry covers the colosseum the roman forum and palatine Hill and costs approx €14.00 the exhibition area is of particular interest. I'm amazed that I could physically touch a piece of history dating back to hundreds of years ago. 

Secondly wear comfortable shoes, as the colosseum roman forum and palatine hill areas are large areas on uneven ground and there is lots to see and explore. Be sure to wear sunscreen and a hat if going in the warmer months and bring bottled water. There are drinking water taps available around the colosseum area to refill bottles.

Be advised that whilst there are public toilets available at both colosseum and roman forum be prepared for a long queue. 

As beautiful as this area is unfortunately there are a number of street vendors constantly harassing you to buy their goods which is EXTREMELY irritating.

title: Interesting Place Odd Dynamic
text: It was really cool to see such a historic place, but there were a lot of people there. If you wanted to see anything you would have to wait a very long time. It was cool how there were tour guides but it was very hard to hear and keep up. Overall it was really interesting and there is a gift shop. If you have a few hours, make sure to stop by.

title: Quintessential Ancient Rome - Buy tickets in advance.
text:
Buy one ticket well in advance on-line to get access to the Colosseum + nearby "Forum."
Basic advance tickets include a specific reservation date and time for Colosseum entry, which allows you to skip the extremely long ticket line and basically go right in - no "guided tour" necessary. Worth seeing inside and out.
The separate included Forum area is even more impressive, but can be accessed any time before or after the Colosseum.

title: Awesome
text: Truly amazing site. We used the metro to get there and the sight coming out of the station is awesome. Just be aware of all the people trying to sell you things,

title: Do the Private tours.
text:
Well happy with the tour. They took us in, easy lines, plenty of information. Great facts. 
Plenty of free time. Left us to own devices after with info for next meeting point. 

Line in is long ish ? Moves fairly well. Metal detectors. Don’t take a bag. Easier.

title: What a sense of history
text:
The Colosseum was a wonder too see. The sense of antiquity was palpable. It is an amazing structure given that it was built 1950 years ago. Heavens knows how the Romans at an average height of 1.65cm managed to climb the stairs inside as they are steep with a high step!
It was not hard to imagine gladiators fighting or wild beasts being killed whilst standing on the viewing terraces. Definitely a must see when in Rome - but I would add that even in May there were long queues trying to get in and it was quite crowded - I would hate to do this at the height of the tourist season.

title: Great tour.
text: The history and story of this building are incredible!  Going under excavation and repair and still very accessible.

title: Lives up to hype
text: An astounding ruin of enormous scale. The spectacles put on here were only a part of Roman culture, but what an insight it provides. The engineering and construction are spectacular, and the historical information is well presented. We only visited the regular areas, yet my wife (not as historically inclined as I am) was blown away.

title: Incredible
text:
Breath taking and really worth a visit.
We did a tour with “Gladiator Tours” our guide was really knowledgable and friendly which we booked coming outside the metro. 

It was busy and the only complaint were the street sellers with selling water and souvenirs were a bit intrusive and became quite annoying...

title: Beautiful and colossal
text: The colosseum is beautiful and is a must visit if ur in Rome. The queues are really long. We had taken the fast track tickets but they dint seem to be too useful since we were anyways waiting in the queue for security chk. If u want to avoid the long queue it is advisable to visit in the morning would be better.

title: Brilliant on the outside
text: The outside of the Colosseum is stunning however the inside for me wasn't worth the payment or queues! Information is freely available and worth reading. BUT our tickets were for 2 days including the Palatine hill and Roman Forum...that was excellent... could have walked round there for much longer, made up for the original disappointment .The audio was difficult to follow so we gave up and read the information boards which were excellent.

title: Tour in the morning
text:
Best day.  Fantastic guide.
Weather in April great, guide very knowledgeable.  Tour went under ground.  Apart from a delay getting in due to a time slot mix up, this was the best trip of the lot

title: Very crowded and difficult to get in
text: The Colosseum is unique and well wort a visit, but be aware that it is not simple to get in.  If you book online you will be pushed to buy a very expensive 'skip-the-line' guided tour.  You will bve shepherded around te sites at the group's pace not yours. The basic ticket includes the Colosseum, the Forum and the Palatine Hill, valid for one entry each but over two days, so you need to plan.  Best advice is to get to a ticket booth early as queues can be long. The complexity and variety of ticket options means that people spend ages choosing and buying tickets.  You will get a timed entry to te Colosseum, so more queues unless you buy the ticket there.  Despite the timed slots, there are large crowds in the venue - best bet is to go early or late, but time slots sell out early on the day.

title: Guided tour
text: We took a guided tour of the colosseum which included entrance to the arena level. The colosseum is spectacular and our tour guide added additional historic colour to our visit. The colosseum is a must see and a guided tour would be a must for me.

title: Great Historical Site
text: Went on a guided tour so got in fairly quickly and found the place to be incredible. To think of its history and all that went on there and who walked the floors that you now stand on! Very cool, only wish the Italian Government hadn't waited so long to preserve the site.

title: The real place where gladiators fought and Christians were martyred.
text: This is a must.  Pro tip: This is the same ticket as Palatine Hill/Forum, so line up early for palatine hill (used Roma pass), then get a time for the Colosseum, and reserve an audio guide for it.  The do the hill and forum, and come out and hit the Colosseum.  You can take many pics without ever entering it, but worth going in. Not far from Peter in Chains too.

title: Great guide - Amelie -
text: Ignorance is very damaging. After finishing the tour of Coliseum, Palatino and Roman Forum with this fantastic guide, Amelie, I re-encountered myself with the Ancient Romans. Thanks Amelie

title: Great Arena tour with Arena Tours - we skipped the lines, too
text: 50 Euros well spent to combine the Colosseum with Palantine Hill and the Forum. Marco was a superb organiser, of the two guides, Maria in particular brought the Colosseum history to life, with some ancient politics thrown in! Our advice is - go to the open area right in front of the Colosseum, look for someone wearing a jacket with "STAFF" on it, they will find you someone like Marco to buy your tour from. Only a limited number go off at a time, so if you don't get there early to book, you may have to wait an hour or even longer. Also, bring food and drink as your next segment after the Colosseum may not depart till later in the day and you might not want to go search for a cafe.
