The Forum ruins are very impressive but the best views of them are from the top of the Palantine Hill, another reason to do the package.

title: Worth to take the tour
text: Take the tour and go inside the Colosseum It is definitely worth it to have a guide as you can learn many interesting facts about this historical place. Take some comfortable shoes and enjoy!

title: Coliseum
text: Spectacular!  I started with a night tour but would not recommend it as the lighting it too dark and you don’t get the history or feel of it’s grandeur’s.  The day tour is well worth it though.

title: Beautiful!
text: Absolutely breathtaking, I suggest getting a tour to go inside, but even if you would like for tomorrow.

title: Nice from the outside
text: Visited the colosseum on our trip to Rome as this is a must do. We did not purchase tickets to go inside and we are glad we didn’t! It was very very busy and lots of scaffolding and building work going on inside which didn’t make it very attractive to look at. The outside was very pretty. Only downside is the men outside contstantly hassling you to buy things but guess you get that everywhere

title: worth a look, but more impressed with the outside than the inside
text:
I was most looking forward to seeing the Colosseum, we got the Omnia Roma card.
When we bought this we were told that we could get into the Colosseum as part of it but we had to pre book ...something we couldn't do until we had the card in our hands that we had collected from the collection place.When we got to the colosseum we couldnt que with the reservation people because we hadn't made a reservation. When we had looked online the day prior after talking to the lady we collected our Omnia card from there were no places avalible until 3 months later!
We were directed to a ticket office at the entrance to the Roman Forum where we had to que for a bit to get our reservation time, just an hour later.
After waiting until 10:30 we joined the middle line que where we qued for maybe 30 minutes for so. the que to our left were going quicker than us and the que to our right was waaaaay slower (that was the none reservation none que jumper que)
ALWAYS ALWAYS PRE BOOK, PRE PLAN! once inside there was extensive security with metal scanners, something we have found at most sites. The inside was a good experience but a little anti climatic, a museum about the colosseum history and the outside was the arena, the floor of which has been lifted. 
If you want to go to the very top then that's another que and a different payment.
I enjoyed being outside and walking through the palatine hill and roman forum looking at the colosseum from the outside more than i enjoyed the inside.

title: Worth the little bit of a wait
text:
Purchased last minute tickets through “get your guide” 
Left our hotel and arrived at the colosseum around 10:50 am and got scheduled for 12:45pm. 
Took the small break to grab lunch before heading in. 
Back in scheduled line for 12:45pm. Only took about 25 minutes to get inside. Not long at all. 
Highly recommend booking some sort of tickets in advance. Either scheduled time or skip the line. 
It was scorching hot and we were glad we didn’t have to wait outside long. Some people waiting longer than an hour. 

Inside was as one would expect. Huge remains. Lots being reconstructed, and lots of marble pieces yet to be pieced together.

title: Must see
text: If in Rome it's a must see. So much bigger than you could possibly imagine. Ignore the ticket touts outside queue and buy them from the ticket office a fraction of the price. Same goes for all other attractions.

title: Visited Twice due to poor weather! Tips for tickets
text:
On the first day, we were caught in the pouring rain, and therefore tour guides were offering tickets usually priced at €90 for €35, including skip the line tour guide with the arena and basement access. However, the conditions were so poor that the basement and arena were closed. We then received a refund. The following day, we returned and saw again in better conditions. We used the Rick Steves audioguide which is a free app and explains things as you walk around, which I highly recommend. We watched Gladiator the day before and had fun acting out one of the famous speeches!
HOT TIP: Would recommend buying the ticket away from the Colosseum line itself and instead of buying the same shared ticket from one of the other sites like the Roman Forum/Palatine Hill which is a mere 5-minute walk.

title: Grand
text: Find a spot and admire this. Lovely walk from accommodation about 25 minutes (San Giovanni). Beware of those selling to tourists as they get quite close and watched many tourists allow them to touch and assist with their money. Be street smart.

title: Nice day out
text: When areas are closed. You can go around in half an hour. Unless you pay for a tour guide which we did not.

title: Wonderful Histrory
text: The beauty of this place is in its wonderful History. We were lucky to get Sev as a our who explained it to us wonderfully. The grand Roman past can be understood by visiting this place although only the structural part of it is now left and not the grandeur.

title: Amazing Building
text: On exiting the metro we were thrilled to see the Colosseum straight in front of us. It looked spectacular. We had booked jump the queue tickets and a guided tour which we would highly recommend. Our guide was very informative and introduced some humour to his tour which made it most interesting. The structure and the history of the Colosseum are amazing and a visit is a must. We then went onto the Roman Forum and again our guide was helpful and gave us the opportunity to explore on our own.

title: When In Rome
text: We were staying for a long weekend 18.05.19. We booked tickets through Trip Advisor in advance, which I highly recommend. You miss most of the queuing hoards and you get a guide in your native tongue. The monument is spectacular and I learned things I didn't know before. However, the tour took over 3 hours and we were dead on our feet afterwards, but fantastic value for money. The guide was super knowledgeable.

title: Sightseeing trip
text:
Visit to the colosseum is a must when visiting Rome.
Stayed at the fragrance St. Peter hotel and this was about 40 minutes walk although buses do go that way every 10 minutes - but the walk was beautiful - a chance to take in other sights on the way - make sure you get up and out early as queues tend to form for all the attractions - ideally get there before 10am. Wonderful visit - easy to take photos from all around the ruins x

title: a must in Rome
text: Amazing experience, but it is better to read the hisotry before  visitng the Colosseum, for better appreciate what you see. We were indide  around 2 hours. We booked in advance the ticket to avoid the long queue.

title: Stunning place full of history
text: This was byfar one of my dream places to go to, I had a great time and enjoyed seeing the inside of this colosseum. Its amazing how people in the old days used to fill it with water and have boat fights in it, that just amazed me.

title: Truly amazing!
text: Seeing the Colosseum in person is just incredible. Seeing it in pics does it no justice! I highly recommend getting the 12€ two day pass ticket for the Colosseum, Roman Forum, & the Palantine. Seeing the exterior of the colosseum is free but seeing the inside just is that much more incredible! A must do in Rome! I was pleasantly surprised by the amount of artifacts presented in the museum within the Colosseum. That was a bonus! There’s a lot of placards explaining various aspects and areas of the Colosseum.

title: Book in advance to avoid long queue
text:
With the intensive security measurement, now you basically need to go through two long queues before you can go into the majestic ancient architecture wonder: first queue to buy the ticket and second queue for the security check. 
One way to overcome this is to book the time slot of Colosseum in advance (the booking is managed by CoopCulture). With the reservation, you can skip the first queue and enjoy a fast lane of security check.

The ticket you reserve online also includes Roman forum and Palatino Hill (where the same "two queue" theory apply as well). While the ticket can help you to skip the first queue, it doesn't have the reservation of time slot for Roman Forum and Palatino hill (or I should say Roman Forum and Palatino Hill doesn't practice time slot reservation). This means you have to queue for the security check.

title: Have to see it to believe it
text: Just amazing, the history and how old it is. Definitely would recommend a guide . We did a three hour walking tour which included the forum and palatine hill. It’s definitely a must  when in Rome. You can Also just visit outside for free if you just want a pic

title: Highly recommend. It’s a must do!
text: Book a tour with city tours. They’re on the ball, they are knowledgeable and make it very interesting

title: We Who Are About To Tour
text:
A lifetime of reading about the Colosseum does not prepare you for the impact of the real thing! As with most other main sites in Rome, plan to get there early and get a tour guide if you want to avoid very long lines.

TIP: Don't forget to the the Arch of Constantine next door!

title: Amazing!
text: This is a “must see” when in Rome. It’s pretty crowded—like most attractions in Rome—but the crowds are well-managed and flow very well. Be sure, however, to book ahead and get VIP access that will allow you to skip the very long lines or you will wait hours just to get in.  And check out the book shop on the 2nd floor; it’s very nice.

title: Must see attraction
text: This one you must see when you are in Rome! Have a guided tour explaining the history of this impressive amphitheatre.

title: Fascinating and steeped in history
text: Firstly make sure you pre-book your ticket and if possible pre-book a tour. The waiting time to purchase a ticket at the venue is horrendous and it is unlikely you will get a guided tour or a audio hand set but if you have not pre-booked but it still worth the wait. The venue is well signposted and there are boards at all the advantage points explaining all about the venue. You should also take advantage of a combined ticked which includes the Forum and Palatine Hill. You cannot be unimpressed by the architecture of the buildings and how the buildings were constructed, it is truly amazing. But remember ! Pre-book your ticket, you do not want to spend half a day queueing for a entrance ticket.

title: When in Rome.....
text:
Go see the Colosseum.  And make your reservations ahead of time, or it gets sold out. If you are told online that it is sold out, go to the Roman Forum and ask for a reservation.  It worked for us.
Rome does a phenomenal job of hiring and training great people to work at all of the venues. Just ask, and they will help.

title: One Of The Must Sees
text: Just has to be seen. The size, the scale, the construction so long ago, the history - it has to be seen. Worth the entry fee. Audioguide could be a smidge better.

title: Definitely a must see
text:
We didn’t go into the Colosseum, but even just walking around it you will get a lot of great pictures and be in awe of its beauty and might. Would highly recommend just for those reasons alone.

If you do want to go into the Colosseum, buy your tickets in advance online. It will save you a lot of time as the ticket lines were very long.

title: got to be seen
text: If you only see one of Rome's many attractions this is an absolute must. The Colosseum is huge, breathtaking and very interesting. We went up to the top tier which gave us a fantastic view.

title: Use a Tour Guide to Bypass the Lines
text: We spent ~$550 for the 2 of us to hire a private tour guide.  It included our tickets to the Colosseum/Forum and the Vatican.  It did not include our taxi or lunch.  However, the knowledge and history shared by our guide was money well spent.

title: My trip to Rome
text:
My parents and I were in Rome last December and I have to say that It was a wonderful experience.
We stayed in hotel called "Hotel Cherubini" in Trastevere neighborhood for three days. Because of our lack of time we decided to visit the most emblematic places around there. The first day we visited the Roman coliseum which I recommend to visit because of its an arquitectonic delight, we visited the Roman foro and the temple aswell. That night we had dinner in "Grazia and Graziella" where you can taste the best pasta I've ever ate.
At our second they in Rome we decided to visit the Basílica di San Pietro in Vincole, which has a free entrance, where we could admire the Moises by Michelangelo one of his more known masterpieces. Then we walk around the Fontana di Trevi known as one of the most beautiful fountain in the world. We had a panini at the well known "Pane e Salame" before we visited the Vaticano (I recommend arrive early or book by the web page because the queues are endless).
On our last day we got up early and went to the Galleria Borghese and Capitoline Museums. And that concludes our trip because our flight back to Fuerteventura left at noon.

title: Wandered past but didn’t go in due to long queues
text: This is a highlight. Even if you don’t do any of the tours inside it’s definitely worth walking around it to see it from all sides. A definite must.

title: A visit you can't miss in Rome!
text: The Colosseum was one of the reasons I visit Rome, and it fulfills my expectations! History lives there!

title: Has to be seen!
text:
The Colosseum... what can i say that hasnt already been said? Nothing! It's incredible and must be seen at least once (much like the Pyramids of Giza Or the Taj Mahal). Its probably the thing that represents Rome the most.

We had "Skip the lines tickets"...a must at a small additional cost. We saved hours with those tickets. It was raining when we got there and i think we would have probably died. However the rain resulted in the parts of the Colosseum being close. Despite that, it was an fantastic 4 hours.

There are small exhibitions throughout the Colosseum: videos, sculptures, relics etc.

Its a lot of walking around if you have troubles with that kind of thing, but they have places where you can rest.

title: Awesome Artifact
text:
I've always wanted to  go see this amazing structure - I've seen it countless times in movies and pictures, but nothing prepares one for actually seeing it and walking through it.
If you have a  'bucket list' this  is  one item which should be on it.
The awesomeness, the splendor it once was and  the  splendor in which it now is  is fantastic.
The  only downside to the visit is the huge amount of people with the same idea as mine - to tour  the building.
I spent hours going through  the  place, but I could have  had spent a couple of days in  looking at all  the exhibits and just taking in the historic ambience this place projects.

title: Learned a lot on our visit
text: The printed information provided during the visit was thorough and interesting. Loved being able to ramble through the different levels of the Colosseum. We were one of the first to enter, but it filled up quickly. Made it hard to get a good shot without tourists in the picture.

title: Awe!
text: The history, structure, and architecture are simply amazing.  This is one of the 7 wonders of the world and a must see if you are in Rome.  The structure is so large that once inside; plenty of room to roam without feeling crowded.

title: Awesome
text: We took a guided tour of the Colosseum which I highly recommend that everyone do. One of the crowning jewels of the Roman Empire and no visit to Rome can be complete and satisfying without touring the Colosseum.

title: A must see!
text: We booked the VIP Colosseum Underground Tour with Walks of Italy, mostly because I wanted to see the underground area of the Colosseum where they released the animals and gladiators and Walks comes highly recommended. This tour also included the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. My husband and I enjoyed the entire tour immensely and our guide was very knowledgeable with all the sites. Although they are doing a lot of excavating underground at the Colosseum, you can still walk around a bit and see how they moved animals and people back in the day. There is so much history here. We were not disappointed.

title: Incredible
text: Definitely one of the must see attraction while visiting Rome, we went to see it in our first night in the town. It was the second time for me but stii I was impressed, trully incredible work. Not to be missed while visiting the city. Plenty of other attractions near by. Be prepared for long queues.

title: Use Price Comparison website to book!
text: Be smart and use a price Comparison website to track down the best price for whatever deal you want to see this and other sites in Rome. We booked a skip the line self guided tour. Avoid just turning up and wasting precious time during your holiday queuing. The Colosseum ticket includes entrance to the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill. I highly recommend doing this during a visit to the Eternal City.

title: Be ready for long lines!
text: The lines and the number of people were incredible. So many tour group options and some are probably more reputable than others. Be prepared to be approached by individuals selling “stuff”.  Our tour guide spoke very broken English—difficult to understand, and we spent a good portion of the tour standing in an archway listening to her presentation.  The place is worth seeing but if you are pressed for time, I would skip the tour and just look from the outside.

title: Walked the circumference
text: The four of us have all visited before so we just walked the outside and enjoyed the grandeur of the the Colosseum.  Went to Wikipedia and read about it at lunch at Propaganda a very nice restaurant a block away.

title: Must see
text: One of the most spectacular sights of Rome - worth the visit - truly an ancient wonder would also suggest a joint ticket to the Palatinate

title: Always a wonderful picture spot.
text:
Im lucky to visit it in a rush rainy business day when tourist are just locked in the hotels, so I enjoyed more the view and the particulars.
The colosseum is absolutely an icon for Rome capital and one of the most famous beauty around Italy. 

-don’t go in a sunny Monday morning around 10 o’clock or otherwise you ll wait 5 hours to get in. 
-wake up early to take a cool picture without people in the background.

title: One of the most beautiful sights in Rome
text:
We visited at around 1 PM , we did not book the ticket before so had to wait more than an hour to get the tickets. there is no line system as soon as you cross the security in particular but ya one can get tickets with less hustle. Its gigantic and amazing. One can climb the stairs to get the best views. Heavily crowded but worth the wait.
Even better pictures during night when the lights are ON.

title: recommend. Coliseum
text:
Unbelievable. Its bigger than I imagined.
And there is so much more to see.
The amount of history surrounding Rome
Is amazing. We took a tour but that isn't enough to see it all. Been here 2 days a d have taken 250 pictures.

title: Our tour Guide was excellent
text: Our tour guide was fun and informative despite the fact it was pouring rain!  She was accommodating for bathroom stops and baby carriages!

title: Colosseum
text:
Easy tip - book online and avoid the queues for tickets that stretch for ever!

Second: Go early, opens 8:30.

title: Was so so interesting, but definitely need a tour guide !
text: Wow... what an amazing experience to visit the colosseum. We had an amazing tour guide who made the experience 10 times better. Would definitely recommend having a tour guide or else you won’t have the detail or the passion from the guide if you went in by yourself.

title: Underground and Upper Level tour
text: What can I say- if you visit Rome you simply need to come here- we got a combined ticket for the 'extra' levels- brilliant guide (reviewed elsewhere) truly atmospheric but BUUUSSSSYYY!!!

title: Shocking
text: DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY Totally unorganized unprofessional run on incompetence. No one knows what they are doing

title: Did not Pre-book
text:
Visited site in May and did not have a pre-booked ticket as we were unsure of our agenda in Rome. However, all was not lost. We read somewhere that sometimes you can get in without pre-booking if arrive early. Colosseum opened at 0830hrs so we arrived at 0810hrs. There were tour advisors in bibs helping people join the right queue. We joined the need to buy a ticket queue, which had about 20 to 30 people in it. The line did get longer while we stood there, as did the pre-booked ticket line. At 0830hrs the gates opened and both lines went through security together. We then went to ticket office and got a ticket. We were in the colosseum looking around by 0840hrs.
As with all tickets for Colosseum the ticket also included entry to PAlatine Hill/Roman Forum, all one site, which can be used once over 2 days.
I do not know how this would work during the peak tourist season, but worked very well for us.

title: Fun
text: It was cool! We had a great and very knowledgeable tour guide, and that is how I would recommend seeing it!

title: Guide lead tour here is recommended
text: Guide lead tour here is recommended and this is not for the elderly. The stairs are sloped forward and extremely steep and hard to climb or descend.

title: Must see in Rome
text: Be ready to arrive really early otherwise will be full of people. Outside is as impressive as inside.

title: Three days are  not enough in Rome
text: If you could spend a day in Rome, the first thing to do is visit the Colosseum. The architecture, the atmosphere, hidden cells in different corners of the garden, even a reconstituted arch hidden from chaotic history make this visit an unforgettable moment.

title: Do not miss during day and night.
text:
Booked 8:30 tour with Italy walks. Great guide who was archeologist who studied there. 3 hour tour w good pace included Gladiators entrance, 3 levels, Roman Forum and Palantine Hill.
Do this first thing in am before crowds and heat!  Make sure to go back at night and eat at restaurants across the street to watch sunset behind Colosseum.

title: Do the full tour!
text: We opted to get tickets for the full tour, including the underground chambers of the Colosseum, where the gladiators were held before the games, along with the wild animals used in the attractions. The Colosseum is huge and impressive; the authorities have recently re-created the 'lifts' used to move the big cats into the area from the basement, and these are worth seeing, along with the tiny tunnels and stone cells which held the combatants. The most impressive part of the tour however, is the view from the highest tier of the seating, which is accessible only to the tours which include the basement. The views over the form are simply breathtaking and worth the fee in themselves. The tour is not cheap at 90 Euros per person, but are the best way to see this extravagant amphitheatre. Brilliant

title: Gigantic, Wonder of the world, Eternal City,
text:
Before I put my review for this place, I would like to share a page from its history.

Introduction: One of the finest and preserved monuments in the world, Colosseum is the world’s largest ever built amphitheater around 70–80 AD. It is located near Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. It is said that it could hold 50,000 spectators and was used for gladiatorial contests and indeed, it is one of the Seven Wonders of the World.

Personal Experience: I had the chance to visit this place in May 2019. It was breathtakingly amazing. The reason, it would be great, to know the history before, is to understand and feel this place better. You have the chance to take a tour inside this place as well, but I highly recommend you to book tickets online in advance, to avoid long queues. This place, at first glance, you will find crowded because there are so many visitors visiting this place, clicking pictures and long queues. Though, it is worth spending some euros and going inside. Near to this place, you have Palatine Hill and Roman Forum, another tourist attractions, you get the ticket of these 3 places together (you can opt though). This structure has seen a lot, and it was in the early 1800s that various stabilization and restoration projects were started, and you see the result of these projects. Another tourist attraction is the Arch of Constantine, placed nearby. 

Travel: I opted for Hop-on and Hop-off bus which I find pretty convenient, as they share the city map along with the audio tour in the bus (double-decker). A sunny day is a perfect day to travel to Rome. There are many options to select the buses, I opted for Big Bus Rome and I find the service worth the money, I would though recommend you to explore. You will find the staff nearby all the attraction places. I got it at Roma Termini. 

Time: It will take 1-2 hours to cover this place and add 1-2 hours for Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. I am excluding the ticket and queue line.

title: 3 hours line up
text:
This monument is very interesting and as an icon of Rome must be seen. line up are huge (in May!) and there are people trying to sell you stuff every 5 feet. some of them quite aggressively...
Not sure if it's worth the hassle of standing in line for hours (skip the line has another line up still very long!). best seen for free from outside.

title: Nice
text: If you take a guided tour, this is far more tha a tourist-must-visit. The temporary expositions are very interesting if you take your time

title: You have to do it if in Rome, still massively overrated.
text: Sure, it's thousands of years old, has a story and its place as a historical attraction, but it's nothing impressive, easily the least liked of our Rome visit. I wont say it's boring but u wouldnt feel like u never want to leave the place, u just keep wondering when will it end. Nothing displayed inside really impressed me so much, something that i havent seen elsewhere. Still i said i was at the Colosseum, meh.

title: How can you go to Rome and not visit the Colosseum?
text:
We've been to Rome more than once.  The last time we took an interior tour of this ancient ruin that symbolizes the greatness of the Roman Empire.  This time, we simply viewed the ruins from outside, as Rome has become so crowded, even in the pre-season period of mid-May, that to visit any of the main attractions that require an admission is now an investment of many hours, even with skip the line admissions. 

The area of the Colosseum is adjacent to the Forum, so both should be viewed on the same day, assuming you have the time to invest in taking the guided tours of each.  Otherwise, just viewing these ancient ruins from their exteriors is an option that may provide sufficient enjoyment.

Whatever you do, you should not miss the Colosseum or the nearby Forum.  I hope you enjoy the few accompanying photos.

title: Great peice of history
text: One of the most iconic and historic landmarks in the world. You must see it at least once in your lifetime. It is a beautiful structure chic fill of history it is a bit rough around the edges on the inside but there is a small museum that explains it's history and shows what it looked like in it's prime.

title: Fabulous.
text:
Just go! You won’t regret it!

Make sure you pre-book tickets and get the skip the line pass.

The only real annoyance is the amount of hawkers who harangue you at every turn to buy their wares.

All in all...fabulous place to visit!

title: The Best Part of Rome
text: This was the highlight of my time in Rome. Being inside and looking around felt amazing and knowing all it's history was brilliant. I would recommend to book this in advance as the queue's thoughout the day never seems to go down. If you can try and get a guide or some sort to help educate you about it's history.

title: One Ticked Off The Bucket List .....
text:
We had the most amazing time here. Firstly, ensure you purchase your tickets in advance. We done this the day before, from outside the Colosseum - plenty of sellers dotted about (common sense will prevail when choosing the right person).

We arrived on the day and were given a sticker (to identify you personally within the group), a set of headphones and an enhanced hearing device. Our guide directed us through the entrance after a short queue at security (this didn’t take long).

Our guide was good - she did repeat herself a couple of times however. Her knowledge was decent and the ‘Rome Guide’ she was informing us from was a very clever source (a lot of before and after illustrations). The tour lasted about an hour and then we were ‘let loose’.

The stadium was fantastic. It is the spine-tingling sight that for most people, defines Rome and is very much a symbol of their history. I was proud to be amongst the six million annual visitors. From the dungeons to the hypogeum to the top tier, the whole construction was immense - both in size and stature.

I recommend the following - take your time on each level to look around. There are various artefacts and models to see as well as historical timelines of the construction. Also, use the first level to get your main photographs - plenty of opportunity for panoramic pictures as well.

I’m still in awe at what I witnessed. That together with the remarkable back story makes the Colosseum an absolute must see. At all costs try and avoid the massive queues!

title: Amazing
text: When in Rome, no one should miss the Colosseum.  What an amazing feeling to look out over the central field and realize the huge crowds that gathered for the excitement of the gladiators and the other entertainment provided for the Roman citizens.  I was surprised to learn this visit that more of the local population was able to view the activities at the Colosseum than I had originally thought.  My wife and I benefited from Rick Steve's audio tour of the Colosseum.  It was a rainy day (take your umbrella) which probably held the crowds in check a bit, but it was still very crowded.

title: Yes, You Should Go
text:
Yes, it's another crowded tourist attraction, but you can't visit Rome and not go to the Colosseum. Even when it rains (like it did today), it's still worth it. I myself wasn't expecting to be wowed -- most everyone I talked to beforehand said we'd be in and out in an hour. But I was actually rather enthralled by the whole setting. And I'm not a history buff. 

When you get here, what will immediately catch you off guard is the overwhelming number of people who are outside the Colosseum helping visitors. Every 10 yards, we were met by a person in a blue vest who would ask us if we already had tickets. We thought they were just the typical sales vendors vying for our business like we see everywhere here. But, no, they actually work for the Colosseum, and the only reason they are talking to you is to make sure you get in the correct line/queue. Impressive.

My advice, some which others have given repeatedly, I'm sure:

1. Buy tickets in advance. Go to the Colosseum's official site (the one with "coopculture" in its web address), not any of these third-party sellers (TripAdvisor/Viator, Ciao Italy, etc.) to avoid unnecessary upcharges or service fees. I figured everyone these days buys tix in advance. You won't believe the large number of people who apparently wait til they get there to buy tickets. The line to purchase tix was long. And those people STILL have to get into the next long line to get in.

2. Skip the live tours (unless you absolutely have to in order to get a ticket). Most of the people on them that we saw didn't look that excited and those groups move slower than you would at your own pace. Today, when it rained, we just stayed under the covered parts of the Colosseum until the weather cleared up. Those tour groups, however, have to keep moving and, as a result, slogged it through the rain. Plus, if you're not an archaeologist or a history fanatic, you probably won't enjoy the hours-long history lesson anyway. (And heck, even if you are, save yourself the money and just tag along discretely with a tour group haha).

3. Opt for a self-guided tour -- And if you have the choice (I forget if you do), only get the audio tour, not the audio & video tour. It is all on an iPhone that they let you borrow, and if you find yourself in inclement weather like we did, you have to pull out the phone in the rain just to see what it's showing. Not convenient. Plus, the video tour is mainly clips of the 1950s

title: An amazing place
text: Its worth setting your eyes on the colosseum and other sites in the vicinity, I did not enter because of the long lines, be prepared to be bombarded with peddlers

title: Visit in May!
text: May is a wonderful time to see the impressive Colosseum!  We booked an 8:45am with Tour in Rome by Tour in the City (just over the pedestrian bridge-Via della Polveriera 11) and the crowds were very limited.  I felt we could walk through the Colosseum with ease and the temperature was not too warm.

title: As beautiful as seen in the movies
text:
This is a must see in Rome. Without a doubt. 
The bad thing is that there are a lot of works around it so it is rather difficult to get the perfect picture. But this should not be a reason to not go and visit this impressive building. 
There are many tours you can choose from and you will definetly enjoy the seeing history with your own eyes.

title: Fantastic experience
text: We paid €40 each for a tour of the arena with a company called Arena Tours. The lady who took the tour was fantastic and so knowledgeable. We skipped the huge queues and got right down on to the floor of the colosseum where we could see up close the 15 tunnels that the gladiators came down into the arena. It was followed by a tour of the Roman forum which was included in the price but I wasn’t impressed with the male guide who took it. Too full of his own importance.

title: Amazing
text: Second time seeing the colosseum and it still amazes me how this was constructed and stood the test of time.

title: A must visit
text: visit with a guide, the best part was the colosseum itself and the museum. Take your time and read the posters. Book a ticket on the web.

title: The Colosseum from the eyes of a 10 year old.
text: I had been looking forward to seeing the Colosseum for ages and it did not disappoint . It was easy to imagine the days when gladiators fought to the death and I think my parents were wowed and could imagine life there as easily as myself. Upstairs there are exhibits from the gladiatorial days. Downstairs there was the arena itself and you could see the two floors underneath the arenas stage. There weren’t any downsides I can think of. If you want to go there I suggest get yourself a guide to explain everything in much more detail.

title: The Flavin Amphitheatre - Colosseum is huge
text:
If you are in Rome, you have to visit the Colosseum.

It is amazing to see, but the history behind is very interesting. Take the tour with a guide and learn about the ancient Rome.

I took a night tour where the Colosseum was empty. We were a group of 25 people in the Colosseum and only two groups were in at the same time.

title: Amazing place
text: Is one of the most impactful and unique places to visit in Rome. We did the tour by ourselves and was good. A lot of history to learn visiting this place

title: Early morning walk thru.
text: It was a rainy day in Rome to day so the crowds within the walls of the Colosseum were minimal and very enjoyable despite to the rain. A must see when in Rome

title: Book via the official website
text: The Colosseo and Foro are monuments you simply must see once in your lifetime and worth every (modest amount of) EUROs charged by the official entity responsible for these - CoopCulture - although be aware of the lengthy queues you will have to endure if you have not pre-booked. You have been warned.

title: fantastic tour for the day
text: a must visit to see in rome. recommend a guided tour to get the full history and points of interest ( including the resident cat ). please be aware if you have mobility problems it can be challenging but dont let it detract you from a wonderful experience where you can soak up the history.

title: Colossal Colosseum
text: Goosebump stuff wandering around this amazing structure. The history it exudes is simply incredible. The Romans were phenomenal designers and architects. .

title: Rome with love
text: In the heart of Rome was very nice : many things to do : visit museums like Coloseo and Foro Romano, basilica like boca de la verita and St Petro.

title: Wow - amazing
text: Overwhelming visit and can't believe this existed so many years ago!  Really well done in the preservation.

title: amazing and breathtaking
text: If you are in Rome this is something you have to visit. It looks amazing and you can learn a lot about history. Definitelly recomended!!

title: Larger than life
text: Go early as tours start here midday it's incredibly large and you'll need to take your time as it's very popular right at the metro station can't miss it.

title: Not my style
text:
The headline does not fit the marvelous collosseum. I just saw it from outside and I can only guess how big and supercool it looks like naturally from the inside.

My complaint goes more to: first, all the annoying people asking you for tickets to not stay in line. They are absolutely not listening if you say no. Doing everything they can to get you into a deal you don’t want. 
Second, the line of waiting people is almost endless. I know a lot people wanna watch and see. But is there no way to limit the visiting time? Or is it already limited? 
I guess I will never see it from the inside in real as I never gonna go there again. I really enjoyed Rome, just couldn’t enjoy that mass of people and I am a bit disappointed that the waiting time to entrance would top everything I ever seen. 100% not recommended for people that don’t want to wait 3 hours in line for entry. Maybe I choosen a rushing time... however.

title: Symbol of Rome and Italy
text: It is the hot spot in Rome. All roads and touristic sites starts from and ends to colloseum. Book before you go to avoid lines of waiting in entrance.

title: Speechless
text: One cannot stand outside or inside and not be overwhelmed by this ironic symbol of the Roman empire. The sheer size and grandeur of this structure takes your breath away, especially when you remember how long ago it was constructed. Then, as you stand gazing down into the arena, the horror of what happen there slams into your brain. 500,000 people and over a million animals died for the entertainment of others. Still, the history and magnificence of such architectural genius cannot be overstated. A must see.

title: If you love history, you’re gunna love this
text:
Once more with all of my European tourist spot reviews, buy your tickets online in advance. You might get lucky on some days and wait in a small line but why take the chance! 

I love history so I really enjoyed this spot. Another action I recommend is read up on your history of the coliseum because you will appreciate it more. This place is will be 2000 years old in a little over 50 years and has seen so much. It has been reconstructed in certain parts to keep it from crumbling down over the years but the feel is still there. Just imagine how many fierce battles were fought at the site you are looking at! Emperor Commodus once fought in this very spot! 

Many cafes and eateries right around it. There are a handful of local Romans dressed up as gladiators ready to take pictures with you for a price but if you partake, make sure you come to an agreed price before hand or they will try and take you for 20 Euros! 

We purchased a local tour guide. The coliseum had a lift for our child in a stroller but after the tour I had to carry the stroller down steps. I asked why and they said because the lift was on the opposite side and we couldn’t go back. I’m young and agile so didn’t have an issue but this is more for people who can’t do that. Make sure you ask about that in advance.

title: THE COLOSSEUM - AN ICON OF ROME - A MUST MUST SEE
text:
Everyone knows about the Colosseum and how big and amazing it is but when you actually see it it is just breath taking. I couldn't believe how big it actually is, ITS GIGANTIC 
I would highly recommend to take a tour. We knew about the Colosseum and its history but going on a tour gave us more information and the right details. 
When you get inside there is so much to see.

Definitely recommend whilst in Rome

title: Majestic relic
text: Beautiful place, get there early and make sure to avoid crowds. There are few public toilets so make sure you have used it. Also it's a good walk to piazza venezia which I recommend

title: Spectacular site
text: This is a spectacular site to see. You cannot appreciate how big it is initially although we didn’t go inside it is still great to say you have seen it

title: Ancient Rome
text: I definitely recommend getting tickets ahead of time, the lines can get long. Also know they can change things at any time, bought tickets to the Belvedere but it was closed that day.  We were a little rushed as we were only able to get tickets for the afternoon but wanted to see the Forum and Palatine Hill as well which is all part of the ticket. It is breathtaking to see all the history. The only bad thing is there are a lot of people around that area trying to sell you something and they can get pretty aggressive. Just don’t acknowledge and say no it will be fine.

title: Highlight of our trip!! Not to be missed!! Get there early crowds are fierce
text: Not to be missed, high point of our trip, worth the money to see this history... we highly hope you go

title: Every Sporting Venue has tried to Replicate!
text: As you would imagine this is on the top 5 list if you are in Rome. Walking in and around the Colloseum is just amazing and you realize that every sports arena is based on the Colloseum.  It is truly remarkable this is still standing.  The lines are very long, so it is a must to buy your tickets in advance and if you can swing it, get a guide.  Hugely helpful in working through the crowds and not wasting time on what you should and should not see.

title: Night walk at the Colosseum
text: Went there after 11pm which was a perfect time to see the stunning silhouette of maybe the most iconic landmark in Rome. Avoid at day time, when the place is awefully crowded.

title: Can't miss it
text: An amazing historical icon that you absolutely can't miss on a trip to Rome. We got a tour (I'd recommend at least finding an audio tour online or something) which is helpful to know what you're looking at.

title: Night tour if you can
text: Try to book the night tour as it’s much quieter than day time and it’s super unique. It fills up quickly and so book in advance .

title: Fantastic!
text: We went with City Walks.  Our tour guide, Gigi,  was very knowledgable and told us facts we probably would not have learned in a smaller group.  Great tour!

title: Not to be missed
text: An amazing piece of history thats a must see when in Rome. We caught the hop on, hop off bus that stops right opposite the Colosseum. Upon entering the area a heap of people will come up trying to sell tickets for tours. We gave in and spent 30 euro each for a skip the line  tour of the Colosseum and surrounding areas. It was worth every cent, our guide was informative and was from America. She explained everything in detail and was very knowledgeable. What an amazing place, even bigger when seeing it in person and we found it to be one of our favourite pieces of history to explore. Would definitely recommend.

title: Way cool!
text: We were in awe!  I strongly suggest you purchase a tour with a guide ahead of time or you will not get the history and information that make this such an amazing part of history and the culture.  Walking out on the Colosseum floor was amazing.  So much history and what a feeling to stand there, but knowing and hearing about all of the death was rough.  We also did an underground tour which was nice but not as good as I had hoped for.  Be informed that regardless of what tour company you choose, the Colosseum guides take over the tour in larger groups once you enter the Colosseum.

title: Just like I imagined
text: Even with all of the renovation going on -- it was something to see and allowed for great photo opportunities,.

title: Wait times ridiculous
text:
I would not recommend buying tickets to this event....I purchased advance tickets through a company called Tiqets....I had to wait over 1 hour to redeem the tickets and was then given a time slot of over 3.5 hours later to come back and wait in line with only 2 hours left in the day.
I ended up not going in the Colosseum and Tiqets refused to refund me my money....don’t bother it is not worth it

title: Can’t be missed.. must.
text: Ofcourse it’s mesmerizing and beautiful. Must visit, couple of quick tips. Carry your water bottle, which can be filled from free drinkable water taps on each floor of colosseum. Pay for skip the line tickets and avoid long lines. It’s worth it. Take the stairs to go on higher floors of colosseum (avoid elevators, it’s no fun). Roman forum is just opposite. Do ensure to visit that as well.

title: Visit at night
text: Outside looked fine; the inside nothing like I'd thought it would be.  Lots of repairs being done and changes made.  Looks great at night with the lights on inside. Plus, you avoid all the people trying to sell you junk.  Did a tour which was included with the Pantheon one.

title: Colosseum
text: Absolutely full of history. Do a guided tour if you can (we went with Artours). This will keep you on track as there is so much history here through the ages.

title: Marvel
text: Definitely busy all times of year, great areas around to sit and marvel at the beauty. Personally I didn’t have time to go inside but I’ve heard great things

title: Worth visiting
text: It's really nice and big, with lots of expositions and tablets of history in it. Yet you need to cope with the crouds.

title: TICKETBAR: Time not available, working on refund
text:
This is a review of Ticketbar, not the Colosseum itself. 

So Ticketbar lets me see all times, then select your time for skip the line Colosseum tickets. 

After booking my time (early morning) I received an e-mail (maybe 8 days latter) that the morning times are not available, and I have to select a different time. I don't want a different time, so I am currently working to get a refund. This has been very difficult so far and I am not confident. 

Also, after I ordered, a message was included that my daughter (10 years old) would need to have her age checked before going in. Does this mean that we would have to wait in line anyway? If so forget it.

title: Booked with a tour
text:
A good tour of Colosseum, we went with a tour which was €35 PP much more expensive than online which you can do by yourself and listen to on your phone and go at your own pace. 

The tours are every 15minutes but it is VERY busy, so decide well in advance of what you want to do you must purchase your tickets at least 2-3 weeks in advance online if you want to go in solo. 

A good aspect is that the tour guide gives you lots of facts which takes and hour. HOWEVER, we had to wait one hour and a half for the next tour guide to take us to the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. They don’t just give you the tickets so you can go by yourself so you’re kind of locked in if you want to go by yourself after. 

Colosseum is good to see from inside but it is run down, there’s not much description so tour guide can be a good idea if you’re not on a budget. The combined ticket is worth the 3 tourist attractions.

title: Tourist Must see
text: Yes, there are some things that are where the tourists mingle but the reason that they are there is because the site is historic, memorial or just beautiful. The colosseum is all 3. The granduer, the stories the walls tell make you feel like you are actually reliving history.

title: Not just a pile of rubble thanks to Barbara
text:
Despite the chaotic queues and despite the ticketing system, we managed to get on the 45min Guided tour at the end of the day (5pm)
I wanted the longer tour so was not sure what to expect but we were not let down by the fantastic tour guided by Barbara. She was the highlight for me. She packed in so many interesting, gruesome and funny facts whilst at the same time guiding a large group through the vast structure. A guide is a must to be able to appreciate the history and Barbara was brilliant. I loved her enthusiasm and knowledge. I wish she could have toured me around the rest of Rome!

title: A must see
text: This is the quintessential definition of ancient Rome. It is a must see. Walking in the arena you can almost feel the souls of the gladiators that fought and died here.

title: Beautiful at Sunset!
text:
We had spent the day at the Vatican. Arrived here around sunset. The area was not crowded. There appears to be renovation going on. Was met by several street vendors trying to get you to purchase their wares. Even after telling them no, they persisted in following you to get you to buy.
As the sun begins to set, the Coliseum comes ago with a radiance that is movie setting surreal. What a wonderful experience!

title: Entertaining
text: Great cultural site. The ticketing process was confusing, we had roma pass but had to go to the fountains to get our tickets activated. Long cues. Worth the wait though

title: Well worth a visit
text: We booked a guided tour with Co-op Culture unfortunately when we got there we were advised that the guided part was cancelled, which we did get a refund for several weeks later, but we could still do the rest of it by ourselves which actually was fine, it just meant we couldn't go to the top floor but saw everything else at our own pace. We thoroughly enjoyed our visit but I definitely advise anyone to get there as early as possible because as the day goes on it gets busier than ever! We bought the combined ticket for the Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill and we spent 5 hours covering it all. As we came away it was very crowded.

title: Ongoing Renovations
text: Impressive scale of architecture used down through the ages, while it's undergoing painstaking ongoing renovations, they do a good job of balancing what you can see and visit among the works.

title: Colosseum capers
text: Went with a your guide and entered at the ‘death door’ to the arena before going up to the public area and around. Really pleased we had a guide though as not a lot of information around. A must see when in Rome.

title: Fascinating.
text:
This is a must, but if you want to see the underground, you have to book a ticket 3 months in advance - we didn’t. We didn’t even have an entrance ticket (they were sold out 3 weeks before we travelled). 
We got up early and queued for only 20 minutes and this included the security check. We ignored the ticket touts and were pleased we did. When ticket touts say you can skip the queue they only mean to buy a ticket, you still have to queue for the security check.
We also paid extra for the video guide. The audio guide would have been enough because the only video shown is of very old films. The audio is ok but nothing special - I would think a private tour guide would be better and more informative.

title: Breathtaking
text:
Had a guided tour and the history is just amazing . The stories this could tell.
The guided tour was purchased direct from the official website which saved us a lot of queuing and being harrassed outside by the endless ticket touts.
When in Rome its a must.
It was so interesting and simply amazing 

Just a side note , pre purchasing your tickets is definitely the way forward 

Outside there are seemingly endless tour touts and more annoyingly lots of guys constantly haapproaching ppl to rubbish and they are very persistant and a firm but friendly no thank you is needed

title: Iconic but also Heavy negative energy
text: Of course the colosseum is iconic place to visit and learn about!  We’ve been there before and this time with a cool guide Marco from Velvety Europe travel. When we truly realize what took place in these walls, it’s horrific that the slaughter of humans & animals is celebrate.  these games were sooo sick.  It’s got a very dark energy for those who are aware.  In the future, I’d rather admire it from afar, at night, lit up - and hope we don’t repeat such past pain & mistakes as a human race.

title: Spectacular. Obvious must see in Rome
text: You can’t come to Rome and not see the Colosseum. If you can, get there early in the morning while most people as still sleeping.

title: Rome Pass Experience May 2019
text:
I had purchased the Rome Pass prior to going to Rome and planned to use it to see the Colosseum while there. I read in several posts that even Rome Pass holders need a reservation to get in, but could not find the website to make reservations. By the time I found the website everything was booked for the days I could go. 

I read a post that said to do the Forum/Palantine Hill tour first with the Rome Pass and the Colosseum was included... this is absolutely TRUE!! However make sure when you get the entry to Forum that you receive timed tickets to the Colosseum at that time. If they don't give you one, ask for it.

They did not give us timed tickets to the Colosseum and I thought we may have arrived to late (it was about 3). After seeing the Forum and Palatine Hill, I asked the staff if this included the Colosseum and they said yes and directed me out the exit. I then exited and was promptly asked for my timed tickets for the Colosseum, which I did not have. I explained to a staff member that we went to Forum, but did not get any tickets. After a bit of a run around, another staffer directed me to a shorter line “to buy tickets” and he said to show the Rome Pass.

I did, and again explained that we had entered the Forum, but did not get tickets to the Colosseum. The ticket office lady took my Rome passes, did some checking on the computer and then charged the 2€ each and we got in! However this whole last 15 minutes of explaining to different staffers would have not been necessary if I would have gotten the timed tickets to begin with.

If you find yourself in this situation, make sure to deal with the Colosseum staff, as everyone else is just trying to sell you a tour (or an umbrella!!).

title: Don't just see it from the outside
text: BOOK IN ADVANCE during tourist season (and even off-season) so you can go right to security and get in.  Outside does not do it justice- you really have to go inside and just experience the massive structure that this is and see the cool museum and history panels/displays they have inside- it's a historical and amazing experience!

title: A lot of scaffolding but really good
text: The Colosseum was very cool to go and see but I was surprised by the amount of scaffolding there.  I had been told that it needed all of the work because ages ago people had taken marble from the Colosseum to build buildings in the Vatican City however now around half of the Colosseum is covered in scaffolding and there are people working on keeping it together all throughout the day.  However I think it is still worth it to go and see.  There is a museum at the beginning and only costs 8€ for people over 18, and is free for those under to enter into the Colosseum as well as the Palatine Hill.  It is very interesting to see and imagine what it would have been like in the days of the Romans and you can see under where the floor would have been into the rooms underneath to where they kept the animals and things (although you can’t go there).  The queues were only about 45 minutes for free and I think that you are only told that they are hours long to get you to pay a LOT of money extra for only around 20 minutes to be cut off your waiting time, so I would recommend choosing to wait rather than pay extra.

title: Good tour.
text: We saw the Colosseum guided by our knowledgeable guide Ursas. He was very helpful and kept things moving through the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. Very good.

title: Awesome - self guided
text: We went with twin 7year olds. Didn’t pay for anything extra and just kind of walked around taking it all in. The biggest hassle was navigation the globe of tours, but it was worth the trip. This was my favorite stop in Rome. We stayed inside for 90 minutes or so before we felt saturated.

title: When in Rome
text: A great sight and well worth a visit, well recommended. Spend lots of time imagining what it would be like. Queue for the audio was massively long as was the urge to get i.

title: The Gladiators outside are fun for little kids
text: Little kids won't make much sense of the place, but they will like the colorful Gladiators outside (who "work" for tips).  Keep in mind the place was built with slave labor and the loot of Jerusalem -- but then, that's the Roman Empire for you!

title: Self-directed tour
text: We had entry tickets for 5:20pm, but not an actual tour.  We listened to Rick Steves audio tour as we walked throughout the Colosseum and he does a great job of explaining its history.  Saw the lights come on at dusk and it's beautiful to see.

title: A walk back through time
text:
Surely this world of wonder doesn’t need a review, but I would definitely recommend to get there as early as possible as it gets crazy busy by even 10:00am! 
Get there early and join a tour, there and guides everywhere and you can skip all the queues.

title: Great tour
text: We booked a tour with Gladiator tours and had a fantastic tour guide who made the history of the colosseum come to life. A must see when in Rome

title: How do you summarise the Colosseum?  It was great.
text: Fantastic and well worth the entry money.  We got tickets online and avoided those 10,000 people outside offering "guided" entry.

title: More history staring you in the face.
text: By learning the history of the Colosseum (takes its name from the colossus which once stood outside) and knowing where most of the treasures and statues went to (The Vatican removed almost everything all those eons ago) and imaging the atmosphere when it was originally built is breathtaking.  You can feel the tension what must have gripped everyone, mostly those that were about to die.  Well worth a visit and guided tour.

title: Really interesting
text: Fantastic place to visit, definitely worth the wait. We had to queue for just over an hour but it was definitely worth it!

title: Great building with great history but the amount of  hagglers in the area is a big-downside.
text: Great building with great history but the amount of  hagglers in the area is a big-downside. Bridge across from the colosseum is good for getting a fuller photograph of the building.

title: Your under own steam
text:
There are many ways to visit as you can see.
We decided to go in under own guidance. We got there early..8:00. Joined short queue which quickly lengthened. Got through security just a few minutes after gates opened at 8:30. A few minutes later, we'd paid for our tickets and were on our way up to level 2 at 8:45. We read most of the information boards and consultd our own guide sheet. We spent a good two hours looking at everything and taking our time.  Toilets in Colosseum were fine so don't worry about that. Take your own bottle of water. Overall, we were happy with the way we did it.
Forum and Palatine included in price 12 euros each.

title: Colloseum, Forum and Palztine Hill
text: Had a 3 hour tour led by joyful Vasco Sintra, a knowledgeable, funny, and engaging tour guide. The 3 hour tour was thoroughly enjoyable, and passed by much too quickly. If you have a chance to use this guide on one of your Roman tours, DO IT! He's fabulous!

title: Wow
text:
A must see in Rome 
We went with a tour upper and lower levels . Definitely the best way skip all the ques and you get to learn a lot more

title: great experience
text:
There is a very informative info wall as you enter. Otherwise, you might want to get a tour or app with info. 
Very cool place; great job maintaining the different layers.
Show up about 5-10 minutes before your scheduled time (and get a reserved ticket w/time beforehand to save hassle). Watch for signs telling you where to go. Be prepared to be in some long lines before you can actually get in.

title: Awe inspiring!
text: A visit to the Colosseum is a must see attraction when visiting Rome!  To still be able to walk around this amazing structure and take in the history of it is truly an incredible experience.  To think that the ancient Romans built this massive structure without modern machinery to aid them is awe inspiring!

title: Colosseum is wonderful beware the men giving bracelets.
text: The colosseum is beautiful and the information given inside is amazing along with all of the very helpful staff that directs you to the entrance you need to be at. However, beware the guys giving out bracelets, they are not as friendly as they pretend to be. They all have the same script word for word and one was slightly aggressive when I tried ignoring him after hearing the same thing from the first two guys. Your best bet is to avoid looking in their direction and try to just blend in. I had a Canada hat on which drew them in like moths to a light in the dark. Pretend they don’t exist and enjoy the beautiful sights around you!

title: Awesome!
text: Frankly the highlight of our visit to Rome, don’t miss it! We went for a guided tour and I can definitely say this made our visit.

title: Amazing
text: Definitely book a tour for an hour, as you beat the extremely long queue. Even if you’re not keen on having a tour guide it’s an hour and you will 100% learn a thing or two and it’s much better to be inside taking it all in, than to be stuck in the line for over an hour. We loved this place a must for any visitor to Rome, and stunning in the dark too!

title: Iconic venue that lives up to its expectations
text: Its a definite one to visit despite the queue and crowds. visit the upper floors and a walk along one of the rings for a good perspective and appreciation of the place.

title: Must see
text:
As soon as we arrived there, we saw many guides offering a tour inside, and we decided to go for it. It definitely is worth it, cause we learned many things we didn't know.
Prepare your self to walk a lot inside the Colosseum and outside.
We went there around noon time, the crowd of people was acceptable.

title: History worth visiting
text: We paid for the skip the line guided tour for 50 euro which also includes a tour of the ancient Roman forum. What to expect is all over the internet and there is no point in talking about here. Check the weather and bring your own umbrella or poncho. If not you can buy one from the many touts around the area. They ask 5euro for a small umbrella but I managed to get the price down to 3euro. Ponchos are 3euro and I suspect that they can be had for 2 or less.

title: book online
text:
We did not book online and felt we were ripped off. When you arrive, you are greeted by people in blue vests who offer the skip the line tour for the Colosseum, the forum and some other place for 40 euro. It took about 20 minutes from the time we agreed to actually go into the Colosseum.  Then the tour lasted only 20 minutes.  Now it is 6:15PM and everything closes at 7. So no Forum, but the guide did say we could come back and explain and they would offer us the Forum in the morning.
Buyer beware book your tour online.
Colosseum was amazing!!

title: Great price
text: Bring a photo of your passport if you are an EU citizen between 18-25 years and entrance is only €2 each! Didn’t need to book ahead and just joined the queue for tickets. Much cheaper than going with any of the tour guides outside.

title: Awesome experience
text: Buy you tickets in advance with skip the line access, my tickets were included with the Roma pass and please do not forget to make your reservations for the time slot for the day you are visiting.  It can get very confusing when you reach the Colosseum and get wrong instructions like we did.  I did my time reservations with COOPCULTURE.IT and asked Colosseum staff where I can enter and they had me on a goose chase telling me I had to change my tickets.  Not true, just look for the coop culture personnel, they have the black lanyards and are right by the entrance to the collesium if you book your time slots  with them this is how they can be found.  Even with skip the line access I had to wait a little, however the regular lines were much longer.

title: Colosseum - a must-do experience
text: Imagine 50,000-80,000 spectators all cheering for the gladiator to defeat his opponent.  It's mind-blowing!  You must buy a skip-the-line ticket and an early morning timeframe to get in.  After a certain number of tourists enter the Colosseum, the park service shuts down entrance and even if you have a ticket, you will not be admitted.  We learned so much on our three-hour tour of the Colosseum and Forum.  Also recommended is getting a small group tour so there are not more than 20 people in the group.

title: Really Enjoyed this tour
text: I learned so much about Roman history on this tour.  I’ve read about and seen photos of the Colosseum but being there and seeing the structure was amazing.   It’s mind boggling to me that the Roman’s were able to build this with no electricity or modern tools.  We took a “skip the line” tour and it was totally worth it.  The guide walked us through and gave us tons of info about the people, the time period and the building itself.  Please don’t go to Rome without seeing this landmark.

title: Must Go!!!
text: Reserve online in advance, so you’ll skip the line, witch is long. The guided tour is unmissable, very welldone and will give you informations that you couldnt get by yourself.

title: Massive structure
text:
This auditorium or arena is supposed to be more than 2000 years old.
Inside is in ruins as people have removed marbles and other stones.
The colosseum initially used for fighting amongst gladiators and later to show off fights with wild animals.

title: Wonderful tour and guide!
text: Azzurra was amazing!  Despite the rain we had a great time learning all about the colliseum.  She was friendly, professional, and just a delight to spend the afternoon with.  I definitely recommend Eyes of Rome.  The best guides hands down!

title: Hot Hot Hot
text: The most important thing you should know when visiting the Colosseum is it’s extremely busy and very very hot ,  not to mention there are large amount of people selling items on the street, mostly cheap souvenirs and sorry but you will have to watch your pockets because there are small children sent to do pickpocketing.  Yeah on the bright side it is a amazing piece of history if you have a chance to go inside I recommended the tour is about an hour long .

title: Awesome, spectacular
text:
The whole place is breathtaking... queue moved quite fast and a lot of history to digest at one go. 

Very impressive and a definite must see when visiting Rome

title: Guided tour was a scam
text: The tour guide was unhelpful and for the cost we paid for the tour, it’s just not worth it. You can walk around by yourself without a guide and the experience might be more enjoyable.

title: a pity
text: this historic sight is awesome but disappointing as having been before I was sorry at the now restricted areas. I paid for fast track only to find there was little or no benefit. The guided tour whisked us round in three hours and in a manner that meant we had little or no time to view whatever took our particular interest.

title: A must see
text: The Colosseum is a must see attraction. You need a audio guide or a guided tour to get the best out of the visit. The entry lines can be long so definitely pre-buy ticket at a specific time and get in without the long wait.

title: Visit to colosseum and Palatine hill
text: If you plan to visit both then go to ticket office Palatine hill and buy a ticket for €12 which will get you into Palatine hill, Roman forum and colosseum. Well worth the money. There are many beggars in the area and people selling jump queues tickets.

title: Spoilt by street hawkers
text: An inspiring momument and naturally crowded , but the experience was spoilt by the number of street hawkers selling cheap souvenirs , bus tours or 'beat the line' tickets ( despite being sold out online ). Every few feet we were met with someone trying to sell us something and if this is one of the wonders of the world , they should control the number of street hawkers.

title: A Masterpiece
text: This site was undergoing through renovations. However, I really enjoyed it. It was my second time visiting Rome.

title: A must see in Rome
text: We were on a guided tour of Rome and time was limited, so we only saw the colosseum from the outside but you cannot fail to be impressed by this magnificent building .... yes it is busy and yes it has lots of street hawkers but you are not going to get a private viewing at such a popular attraction at any time soon so just take it all in and enjoy 😊

title: Awesome place
text: Must visit!! Specially underground tour during the late evening! It is beautiful!! Has lot os history.

title: Great day out
text:
Really enjoyed the day at the Colosseum. The exhibitions on the second level are very interesting. The video audio guide was informative but not amazing if you know a lot already. 
There are information signs up in some areas, but it would help if there were more signs up in specific areas pointing out more points of interest. 
Don't be scammed by the people who are trying to sell you tours while you are in line! They are often just the middle man and do not always give what they "sold" in the line - no matter how nice they are!!!! 
But once inside - just enjoy the surroundings - definitely loved it.

title: Go inside
text: We were lucky enough to get a private tour inside the colosseum which made it a much better experience.  Tickets were prepurchased etc so no waiting in line at all.

title: Awesome
text: Hard to wrap your head around the splendor of the Roman Colosseum.  Restoration work is slow, but the site is amazing.

title: Prepare in advance
text: So much to see and so little time. Best to study a bit ahead of time, so you can digest what you’re looking at through the massive crowds.

title: Amazing Awe-inspiring Amphitheater
text: Our hotel was a walking distance for this attraction. On the day of arrival, we had opted for an illuminated toure of the city of Rome & therefore Colosseum was the first stop. The Colosseum was surely love at the first sight. The fading light od he day added to the beauty & colour of the monument. The tour of the edifice from inside is as enthralling. Our guide was enthusiastic and showed us the nuances of the place. Right now a lot of restoration work is in progress. The fact that this structure is supposed to have come in only eight years is itself astounding. The third encounter with this monument was on the eve of our departure from Rome, very late in the night. This time there were no maddening crowds, and the lights had illuminated the Colosseum and the Arch. This surely added to the majestic aura  & grandeur of the Colosseum.

title: Colosseum
text: MUST SEE!!  Be prepared with comfortable walking shoes.  recommend doing a guided tour paid for your ticket in advance for advanced entry otherwise there will be very long lines

title: Beauty is from afar
text: Many might not agree with me but I feel the beauty of this is from afar or in pictures. When you are right there I feel it is not in awe as I expected it to be. But nevertheless it is a must see and experience it yourself.

title: Colosseum was great beware of the company selling tours
text:
We arrived to see  colosseum. Second visit but my niece her firist time. She did not want to wait in queue. The company offered a ship the wait and visit to forrum and palatine hill. As their was three of use 120 euros. After we paid the money informed trip not till 90 minutes. 
We waited we went on tour the guide was great. She informed us that the forum tour next day everyone else appeared to have been given different times. We had not she also informed us that she did not show us the full tour we paid for. When we went out tour sellers we booked with where gone. Please we careful l normally arrange the tours in advance but was caught out

title: Amazing sight! I’m glad we went!
text: Long queue. But who cares? That’s not actually the colloseum. Once you go in, your mouth drops. Beautiful sight. Over 2000 years old. Wow.

title: Amazing even the 2nd time
text: 2 years ago we did a very quick trip during a cruise today we spent 4 hours through the Colosseum what an amazing trip, with the added displays within the building itself definitely worth the second visit.

title: Great to visit the Colosseum but don't use the tours in front when you walk up!
text: The Colosseum is a sight not to be missed! I didn't plan ahead and when we got to Rome all the tours were booked. So, we went thinking we would wait in line...but the ling is LONG if you don't have tickets. We went with one of the companies that waits for people like us saying you don't have to wait etc...Well, you do...and we bought tickets for The Colosseum and Palentine Hill. It was running so far behind and we had set up a tour of Vatican City that we had to leave and only saw the Colosseum.

title: A wonderful piece of history
text: How can you not be wowed every time you look at the colosseum?  I could study every nook and cranny with every visit.

title: An incredible travel in time
text: No need to say what does Roman Coliseum has meant all over man history. Evefybody have seen countless pictures and heard many histories about it, but nothing to do with been there. Me and my wife we arrived by train from Venice and unfortunetiley we lost a lot of time in the terminal because of a taxi sindicat manifestation. We spent the rest of the day going shopping and getting dinner. When we arrived at the hotel (Hotel Monte Cenci) we asked in the front desk about a guided visit to the main places, like the Roman Coliseum, the San Peter Basilica, Trevi´s Fountain, etc. They recommended us (I would say the perfect choice) a personal guided tour to two places (time couldn´t allow for more for us because we stayed only three days): Roman Coliseum and Vatican. We went first to the Roman Coliseum and from outside to inside it were just shaking in all respects. The whole vista, the hughe mármol columns, the extrange holes that are in al the columns structure oputside the coliseum (all things explained by the guide), the arena (where the gladiators fighted), the place where the Ceasars seated, the big Holly Cross installed by the Pope´s in turn instruction. A great empire in many aspects but I can not explain my feelings. A sense of pain and sadness for all the happenings there. Rome has one of the greatest histoty of all times and everyone should know that history. All the explanations of the guide were very helpful to undestand what we were seeing. It would be imposible to be there without help.

title: Fascinating place
text: We pre-booked our time and took an audio guide, wandering around ourselves.  Well worth it.  Very impressed that they restrict the numbers to 3,000 making it never too crowded, allowing you to walk around without feeling too pushed!  Wonderful place and awe-inspiring.

title: amazing place
text: Simona Mariotti helped us understand all the small details about how this place looked in the past. She showed us the good place for photos and she explained why so many things exist and still exist. She was amazing!

title: Queued a while with skip the line tickets!
text: Of course the colosseum is worth a visit but I’d skip on the guided tour as there isn’t a great amount to see and our tour guide wasn’t the best if I’m honest. He lacked any amount of motivation and he was difficult to understand. The parts we could understand were a bit boring to be honest. He wasn’t helped by the torrential rain but he could have had a bit more energy. We ended up queuing in the rain for a while and took ages to get through security so the skip the line tickets aren’t all that great. Apparently it is an hour and a half or more wait without them though while it took around 40 mins for us to actually get in. The colosseum  itself is incredible and so worth a visit, however there was so much construction / scaffolding work going on that it was slightly underwhelming.

title: Colosseum
text: We had a wonderful tour guide.   So knowledgeable and passionate about teaching us about the Colosseum.  This tour is a must !   Grazie !

title: Fascinating piece of history
text: Did not pre book tickets, turned up on the day and queued for about forty minuets to get in. purchased the audio guide that was very informative. loved learning the history of the colosseum and the games. This is a must see for anyone visiting Rome.

title: Visit
text: Return visit for me , if your coming to Rome and are definitely coming to the Colosseum , I ve found best to book online in advance online , we didn t wait in any line walked straight in st the appointed time no problem, well worth a visit

title: Rome highlight!
text: We did a skip the line tour that was pre-booked, and I would definitely recommend that.  Our guide was knowledgeable and we got to go on the ground floor and upstairs. So cook to walk through history!

title: Must See!
text: The Colosseum was amazing! I was worried that this was being oversold and would be a bit of let down. I was wrong. If you have any interest in history at all this is the place to be. If you have no interest in history you still have to visit just to get a picture. I booked a guide and I would highly recommend that you do too unless you already have a vast knowledge of the Colosseum. There are a lot of stairs and they are large steps so be prepared to climb. All you really need are good shoes and a camera. Be careful on the approach to the Colosseum there are some questionable people outside harassing tourists.

title: Great place but . . .
text:
Along with other places in Rome the Coliseum or Colosseum is plagued by the ubiquitous 'lucky lucky' men. Quite simply, their prevalence ruins any enjoyment that one might get from such a world heritage site.

Stand still for a second and someone has either put a bracelet on you and is demanding cash, or they are trying to sell you tat and junk that you don't want or they are insisting that you buy a tour from them even though all official advice is not to. Meantime some of Rome's finest (ie some of the various police forces) stand around doing nothing - or to put it another way, pestering tourists is condoned. 

Queue's to get in to any of the sites are horrendous, I saw people turned away because their 'tickets' were forgeries or fakes and quite simply I will not be going back until the Roman authorities decide that they will take some action to remove the pestilence that plagues their city.
