Subsequent to my visit I was advised that the only way to deal with the people is top avoid eye contact and not speak. Personally I don't fancy a day of constant vigilance and rancour.

title: Gotta see it
text: Fantastic  tour with Ektour - full of extra information definitely  worth skip the line you voukd be waiting  for hours otherwise . So much history  - the Pantheon was closed the afternoon  of our tour, so we went to Palantine Hill as well, really  interesting to understand  the full history. Be prepared to walk ALOT.

title: Three words- Rich in History
text: The Colosseum was a great monument built during BC period. This was built by the emperor of Rome, mainly for entertainment.This monument is a must see for anyone visiting Rome. My experience was really great and overall I was very happy. Things I would suggest is, don't bring bulky bags otherwise you'll have trouble taking things out and putting it back in. Also, if you want to skip the line I would suggest getting a tour guide since the normal line would take ages to move. My tour was held by Gray line (a tour guide company), our tour guide name was Sara, we went for the English tour. Overall a very informative and descriptive tour guide, she was well versed with every little detail on the Colosseum and answered our questions nicely. This was a very nice experience and highly recommend!

title: Colosseum do’s and don’ts
text: When in Rome, of course, you must see the Colosseum. But,  to avoid extremely long lines, get a tour guide. Otherwise, you stand in line for an hour or more just to get a time to enter the Colosseum, then another long wait to enter. I bought a Roma Pass when I arrived in Rome, but it didn’t help avoid lines.

title: Colosseum
text: What can I say it was breath taking bigger than you think was brilliant to see the actual steps were still just about intact all in all it was a great experience the tour guide was great they tell you all the history of the Colosseum n youd be surprised we loved it

title: Better late than never
text: While planning our trip we followed the Rick Steives guidebook. His recommendation was the Roma pass which gives you head of the line without a reservation. As of March 2019 this is no longer true. You must have a reservation. Therefore we opted to not get the Roma pass and waited in line for tickets. It took about 45 minutes in line at about 2 pm. Wasn’t bad as the weather was beautiful. On a hot day you would not want to wait in line, get the reservation.

title: Great from the outside, PRE BOOK
text:
VERY busy, we arrived at 11 am and it was a THREE hour queue just to buy tickets. Ended up paying 35€ each to join a guided tour, which last 2 hours in both the Colasseum and Palentine hill. The colasseum is much more impressive from the outside, and i actually preferred places such as Pompei and Herculaneum in comparison. Now I have done it, I wouldn't do again. The roman forum and palentine hill was much more enjoyable and scenic, and had far fewer sketchy Italian men trying to shove selfie sticks in my face.

* PREBOOK

title: Highly recommend!
text:
A brilliantly well organised tour. 

The colloseum tour is brilliant as you are shown round by an architect. This tour allows you to visit the lower and upper floors which aren’t open to the public meaning you can have lots of space and get some fantastic photos! The tour guide is really knowledge and has brilliant English. She answered any questions any had and allowed us all time for photos. 

We then had a tour around the Roman Forum by another tour guide. Ours was a chap from Lanchashire, I think it was Tom but I’m not sure. Same again, he was brilliant, really knowledgeable. He gave you time to take photos and would always stop and position you somewhere you could sit or be out of the shade. Although it wasn’t particularly hot I thought this was a good idea. Especially if people are booking in the summer.

The whole tour lasts around 3.5 hours which sounds like a long time but flies by! Would highly recommend.

title: Would be great without the touts/ con artists and millions of skip the line/ tour sellers
text:
If you could view the Colosseum alone or when there are no tourists it would be amazing and magical. We visited in May so not quite peak season and I cannot imagine visiting in the summer months given how many tourists we encountered. 
Aside from the tourists there are touts and pan handlers everywhere selling tickets/ raincoats/ tacky souvenirs. It can feel threatening and you get fed up with saying no thank you. Although some may be genuine we came across a few who tried to engage in conversations in a friendly manner asking where we were from etc we found it hard to walk away or get rid of them and when we did they shock our hand and put some bracelets in which they then wanted €1 each for. Once we had been conned the first time we spent the time looking out for and avoiding which was not the most relaxing tourist activity and took away from the beauty of Rome. 
We did not pay to enter because we only had a day in Rome and the queues were large. It is certainly impressive from the outside and is undergoing restoration so some of it is obstructed by cranes however worth walking to given that it has such heritage in Rome. 
My advice would be to get up and go early am to get some good pictures.

title: Ancient History
text:
We spent quite a few hours just wandering around this iconic landmark. We purchased tickets on the day and had no problem entering the site. 
It can be a bit heavy going but well worth the effort.

title: Amazing
text: We took a gladiator gate, arena floor and Roman Forum tour with ‘walks - Italy, Spain & Greece tours’ and it was the best day we had during our stay in Rome.  This is definitely down to our tour guide (Frederica) who’s enthusiasm for the history of Rome and the Colosseum and her lovely friendly personality was absolutely infectious.  She was happy to talk through every detail with us and answered every question we had.  We were absolutely stunned by the facts about how the animals came to the colosseum, how they were treated, how they got on to the area floor, what the people of these times were like and what they did during these times.  Hearing about all of the emperor’s, the decisions they made and how this all shaped the Rome of today.  I can not recommend this tour and visiting the colosseum enough.

title: An outstanding tour
text: We booked with EU Tours and their communication with us both prior to and during the tour was outstanding.  The guides were superb both the driver who was safe, positive, knowledgable, and thoughtful and the guide at the Vatican who was excellent.

title: Colosseum
text: Ask for Mercedes, she was a super enthusiastic guide! The tour cost €40 for each person and went on for 1h 45min and included the tombs. ❤️

title: Just like in postcards
text: A must see for tourists.  Although there are so many people going to the colosseum, you'd have to wait in line.

title: Stunning
text:
When we walked into the Colosseum we were impressed by the scale of the building.  75 separate entrances.  3 levels.  Dozens of gladiator rooms.  Places for rudimentary elevators.  Huge arena floor.  It was easy to imagine the constant spectacles that occurred here. Overall a very impressive sight.

HINT #1: Get the skip the line tickets.  You'll be happy you did.

HINT #2: Get the tickets that allow you to enter on the arena floor.  You'll be amazed when you walk in.

HINT #3: This is across the street from the Metro Colosseo stop, very easy.

HINT #4: Look for Nero, the black cat that lives in the Colosseum.

title: Great historical site
text: Unbelievable site. I highly recommend to purchase your ticket online ahead of time to skip the line. We saved about one hour or more on the day we visited. The local attendants who work at the site were not very friendly or helpful. The restrooms were atrocious. They were very unclean and crowded with no soap. We had someone in wheelchair and were not accessible.

title: What to avoid at the Colloseum
text: We loved the tour of this extraordinary piece of ancient architecture. It is what happened afterwards that was disappointing and left a bad taste in our mouths. When we came out of the Colloseum we noticed 3 blokes dressed up, 2 as gladiators and 1 as Caesar. They were posing with the tourists for photos so the girls encouraged me to go have my photo taken with them. I knew it would probably cost a few euros but what the heck. So I asked the bloke how much, he said "Not much" so I guessed about €30, since there were 3 of them. They posed and then 2 moved on and I was left with one of the older gladiators, he said €40 So I got my wallet out and pulled out 2 €20's he was standing right over me, he said to give him €40 more for each of them. All up I handed over €130 I was too intimidated to stand up to him. Yes I know you will all say I was an idiot, but unless you were there you cannot know how menacing someone like that can be. I went back to my friends who were horrified and we immediately decided to go find a policeman and try and get some of my money back. 2 of my friends chased the blokes and cornered them saying unless they gave back €100 We would be going to the police, they eventually coughed up the money after my friends said we would put it all over social media.  I am writing this, although very self humiliating for being so stupid, to let others know about these charlatans at the tourist sites. I will try to attach the photos

title: Beautiful even while being renovated
text: All I can say is that it’s beautiful,  be aware of the difficult large steps to get up to the highest level.  It’s all about the history, but it needs to be in the context of all of the surrounding.  The coliseum is beautiful but the surrounding gives it context and purpose.  I spent approx 2 hrs, I think that is enough.  Be careful of the scammers.  Just don’t speak to strange people I’d say.

title: A must to view but not necessarily to come in
text: In this visit we settled for a pic from outside and walked around it, the area is in renovations they are fixing the metro or something else and they were building the road for some sort of competition so it was very crowded and not comfortable to walk around.

title: Incredible
text: The inside of the colosseum is every bit as interesting as the outside.  It is a must see - Just make sure you get your tickets ahead of time!

title: Fantastic piece of history!
text:
Word of advice. 
DO NOT BOOK VIA ROMA PASS! 
I purchased a Roma pass online before going to Rome. I was told to pre book a time slot to the colosseum online before getting there due to the 3000 people limit at the colosseum. However every single time slot was already booked for a month and I couldn’t book online. So I still had to join the que to pre book my time slot 4 hours later.  In that sense the Roma pass wasn’t worth the money because even though I was able to que jump at my time slot. I still had to spend 45 minutes queuing to get a time slot.  

Highly recommended! When you que for tickets purchase them at the Roman forum. The tickets are for collosseum, the forum and the palatine hill. The que at that desk is so much smaller than the collosseum que. You will save a lot of time and sanity. 

Right. Back to the collosseum. If history is your thing. This place is brilliant. Currently there are building works in the bottom of the collosseum which dampens the affect and what you’ve seen in the history books. But you can still appreciate it’s splender. Definitely not something to miss when your in Rome. 10/10

title: Slightly Disappointed
text: Something I have wanted to see for a long time but felt it was a bit of a let down in all honesty and looks far better and more impressive in pictures.  Although visited in May it was still crazy busy and would recommend booking well in advance to avoid long lines. The only tickets I could get were with an audio guide which suited me as I wanted some more information. However, the audio guide actually provided very little useful information. The main irritation I had were with all the touts trying to sell selfie-sticks/umbrellas etc and the guys offering skip the line tickets every step we took which drove me mad! Overall glad I went but probably wouldn’t return.

title: Must see, but...missing a trick
text:
Absolutely amazing. I just felt there was a lack of simple explanation. Running through which emperor or pope was in power and when does not depict the scene when in full splendour. 
We want to see how it looked and what happened. 
The funniest was seeing an American guy read to his 3yr old in a pray the dates that maximise whocares but whatever to whoever. 10 minutes the kid endured.

title: Impressive , but 2hr wait on average
text: We really had lucky star with us coz we saw the parade , saw the jets flew at bare minimum level and also were first in the que of more the. 1200mtr line ... worth everything

title: Rome Ancient Gigantic Marvel!
text: The Colloseum is a marvel accomplishment of Roman Empire.  It is a must-see when one is in Rome.  Visiting the site seemed like you could go back in time as a spectator gathering around the stadium watching the show happening then.  It is an amazing feeling.  I took a Viator upgraded tour of Skip the Line w access to all 3 sites the Colloseum, the Roman Forum, and the Palatine Hills.  The guided tour took over 3 hours with special access entry going through security entrance checkpoint.  Very organized tour with 2 different tour guides both named Sarah.  One tour guide was specialized for the underground tour of the Colloseum.  The main Sarah was for all others.  The tour included the underground the arena the upper ground the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hills. After the tour ended, we could remain at the attractions to spend our own time exploring.  The Colloseum is a crowed place with 40000 tourists per day so the line is always long. Skip the Line tour helps facilitate and mitigate this precious time of waiting.   I guess all of the tour companies partner with the official Colloseum site to provide tourists guided tour and Skip the Line Entrance.  It is a good tour covering everything stated on ticket.  The tour is on time and very organized.  They met at very easy to find location whether you came by bus, metro, walk or taxi, etc.  Highly recommend this tour especially if you have limited time in Rome.

title: Majestic architecture
text: Amazing architecture that you have to see to believe. Breathtaking. But again, the security counters have increased the time needed to enter into the colosseum.

title: Worth a visit to take in sheer scale.
text: Definitely worth a trip / tour inside just to take in the impressive scale of the place but would say just as (if not more so) picturesque from the outside.

title: Another breathtaking sight!!!
text:
The colosseum is a sight you never grow tired of.   I can’t put into words what I felt upon first setting eyes on the colosseum   When the hop-on hop-off bus took the turn and I caught sight of the colosseum.  It was like.... basically took my breath away.

The colosseum during the day or night Exuberantes beauty and history.  

Take a chance a go see for yourself!!!

title: Must see, but not to enter
text: The Colosseum is a must see, but a lot of people want that and therefore it is very very crowded. We didn’t enter it, because it was also under construction.

title: Disappointing, but worth a visit
text:
The Colosseum looks far better from a distance than close up. In fact the closer you get the more disappointing it is. A part from its sheer size and the knowledge that the Romans basically emptied out Jerusalem to help fund its building, there isnt a lot that can be characterised as aesthetically pleasing to the eye. Even the marble was ripped out of it by a Rennaisance Pope to make steps in the Vatican. It looks like a derelict football stadium in some respects. So if you are contemplating queing up for hours to go inside I would think twice about it.
To add to the picture of a sense of disappointment I must mention the huge number of hawkers trying to sell you selfie sticks, power for your mobile, or even scarves. I lost count of the number of times someone tried to press a selfie stick into my hand. They are unlicensed but the police have no control over them. Then there are those trying to sell you a 'get into the Colosseum quicker' tour. Be warned, you do get an alloted slot but it is not an instant queue jump, you still have to queue!
Not surprising there are masses and masses of tourists which further detracts from the experience as you can be pushed and jostled by tours of people. Amongst the most annnoying are those holding up their flags so their entourages can follow them like sheep. These flags can sometimes impede you from taking that one picture of the Colosseum youve been patiently waiting to take. If you climb up the stone stairs near the Colosseum, you get a better overall view of it and the best place for taking pictures, if you can avoid the mainly east european female posers standing precariously on the wall taking selfies of themselves with the Colosseum in the background.

title: Private tour did not disappoint!
text: Learned so many interesting facts about the Romans through our amazing guide Francesco. Booked through Rome City Tours with Maria Claudia & Company. No waiting in line and he easily maneuvered us into areas when other tourists had moved on. Totally worth it he money.

title: No Grazie!
text: Be prepared to be harassed by your peddlers every 10 seconds and reply “No Grazie” endlessly. Online tickets still take half a day to see inside. We didn’t bother and spent our day visiting many other places rather than load a full day just queuing to get inside.

title: Better form the outside
text: Of course you have to see the Colosseum when in Rome. Would be rude not too. However, I have to say the best and most impressive view is from the outside. Especially at night when it is illuminated. To avoid all those queues to see inside. We found the shortest queue was to enter the Roman Forum as they are on same ticket. We also went later in the afternoon which was quieter, oh an we were there in May. I know it is a continual work n progress and they are still excavating. But, there is ample area to maybe reconstruct a small section of the seating. To give you a better idea of how it looked originally. I feel awful when I say I was a little disappointed when it is such an iconic place.

title: Fantastic Historical Monument - BUT BOOK ATTICKET IN ADVANCE
text:
We visited with a pre booked timed ticket from the official website. 

Resist the multiple  touts releers all offering sppedy entracnce, but stick to to official assitans outside to colosseum who have well labelled jackets and official passes around their neck and direct you to the correct entrance for timed pre booked tickets. Wit h ou r tickets we were quickly inside, passing the long lines of people who had not got pre booked tickets. 

This monument is awesome. The scale of the construction has to be seen to be believed. Despite the large amont of renovation works going on , we could appreciate the complexity of the colosseum with the underground systems that prior to my visit I had not fully appreciated.  There is an excellent exhibition on the upper levels which helps you understand the whole structure.  Despite visiting on a showery day in May there is plenty of covered space to keep out of the rain.

title: HAve to see it
text: The colosseum is THE site to see during your visit in Rome. Rich in history and architecture. Get yourself the audioguides don't go without anything as you will enjoy not even 50% of it

title: Good but so busy
text: This was our second visit to Rome (the last one was 18 years ago). Didn’t do the Colosseum back then so this time was a must. But wow what a difference. So so busy! We got our tickets on the day by one of the many sellers outside, we didn’t want to pre book as wasn’t sure what the weather would be like (first two days in Rome it rained). Decided to pay the price of having a guide and getting in quicker and was so glad we did. Learnt a lot and also went straight in. Definitely worth doing.

title: Great landmark
text:
Great landmark, if you wish to purchase regular admission ticket at the monument the line ups could be lengthy. To purchase audio guide or video-audio tour it’s at a separate counter and only Credit card is accepted. The washroom is a mobile washroom and not very clean as the amount of people is crazy. Not enough for the amount of tourist. 
Recommend to buy tickets online as much as you can but if you were unable to do so like me, the best time would be after 5 pm less crowded. 
Tour guides and ticket are sold outside as well by separate companies and they are being offer from the moment you step foot outside the metro or around the Colosseum.

title: Book tickets in advance
text: This was my favourite in Rome! We booked a tour ahead of time.  Our tour guide was very informative. Didn't like the crowds outside of the place but not much you can do about that.

title: Rome
text: Booked with Nicam tours was easy to find the tour guides. Our guide was excellent very knowledgable loved every minute of this tour

title: Must see
text: Well worth pre booking the tickets before getting there as the queues were huge.  Plenty to see here with a small museum there as well.  Access is good or all abilities and guides are availible.  Can be reached if you use he hop on hop off bus service.

title: Must see
text: Went in May, was corwded but not too bad. You have to go if in Rome, probably the only  must see spot. Went with a tour group, the wait was minimal, could get pictures and walk around pretty easily. Whle it was crowded for me our tour guides said it was nothing compared to summer crowds. Check it off the bucket list.

title: Lovely... queues are really long.
text:
After advice from our hotel owner it is more worth while queuing for the Roman Forum first to get the ticket which will let you in there and the Colosseum and then heading back to the colosseum after. Usually the queue for ticket holders which you will be after that is smaller than others, but even if it isn’t, it goes down quicker. 

Amazing views to see enough to look at and to see and read without a guided tour, which we didn’t have, solely because I get bored too quickly and want to move on. Others seemed to really enjoy it but it does take a longer time to look around so it depends how long you are staying in Rome for. 

Reasonably priced for both forum and colosseum. Always people outside trying to sell tickets. Normally once they were told you had tickets they leave you alone. Lots of steep steps inside but access to a lift if needed.

title: Awe inspiring!
text: The Colosseum is an amazing feat of architecture! However, getting in can be a challenge.Ask someone with an official badge where to go to get in (there are lots of different lines), but beware that there are people who try to look "official" only to sell you a private tour.  Buy tickets online ahead of time. However, beware that the top Belevedere level is under construction (May 2019). It didn't look like it would be finished anytime soon. You can really see most everything from the regular level. No need to go down to the lower level- the roof/floor is long gone, so you can see right in. The tour of the lower levels sells out months in advance, and didn't really seem worth it. Overall, an amazing sight!

title: History that we learnt since young from our school/Cinema/Books
text: Sunny day and walk around 3 hours with no shade cover. It’s a signature place in Rome and must come to learn the history from the past of the Emperor and the Gladiator

title: Another example of “over tourism”
text: Sooooo many people! Such long queues even with prepurchase! Travel has become too cheap, so that you can’t see anything for the crowds, and it is killing the planet. I’m probably not travelling too much after this.

title: Step back into time
text:
Really enjoyed the experience,  it's amazing to see how small the old arena looks ,when say compared with what you see in film or TV.. plenty to see  interesting to see how the animals and combatants came up through the floor  . Highly Recommended 

Suggestion buy skip the line tickets before you go 

Look out for the street sellers ,probably one every 10 feet , selling brollys in the rain and selfie sticks in the sunshine 

And the look look men selling Jewlery

title: Has to be seen to be believed
text:
Fantastic. We went in June with one disabled guest. We were led around the queue to a priority entrance. Lift to upstairs. Pictures do not do this place justice. 
Staff great, most were multi lingual. All helpful.

title: Highly recommend the Colosseum Underground Tour
text:
An amazing place to see, but I would strongly recommend a tour.  I did a skip-the-line Underground Colosseum (meaning you also get a tour of the bottom-most level below the arena floor) and it was worth every penny... or Euro...

Also be aware that "large" bags and packs aren't allowed, and aerosols and sprays (perfume, sunscreen, etc) may be problematic and cause delays in the screening process.

title: Worth the stop
text: If you love history and/or architecture you must visit the Coliseum. The sheer size of this structure and how it had to have been constructed are amazing.

title: Beware the Skip the Queue Touts
text: We loved this place but hated the touts, trying to persuade you to spend masses of money to do a private tour with them. One such chap told us we would have to wait one and a half hours in the queue for the colosseum, then the same for both the Palatine and then the Forum. This was so untrue. At 8.30 when the ticket office opened, we queued for only half an hour and then we were in, having saved ourselves 36 Euros between the 2 of us. My advice is to arrive as early as you can. We were there by 8.15 am and then with any luck, you should get in okay, without having to pay this premium to skip the line.

title: Beyond Expectations
text: This is a engineering marvel.  We (two couples) toured it with a private tour guide -- perfect visit.

title: What a Wonder!!
text: Colosseum is an extreme venue that is hard to comprehend what it was like back in the days.  Definitely get skip the line tickets because it is packed.   Also, they limit the amount of people entering so having tickets ahead of time will guarantee entry.  It will take approximately 1 hour to tour.   Then get Forum included in your ticket so you can tour also which is next to Colosseum.   That can take you as long as you want.  A lot of walking  1-3 hours.

title: Amazing
text:
Truly amazing experience. It was very busy but it didn't detract from how breathtaking it is. 
The only downside is the people outside hassling you to buy tat and the rubbish people leave behind on the grounds around the building. 
TIP - make sure you take water with you especially when its hot

title: Colosseum, history, culture and a lot of design
text: The queue to take a tour inside are something to see, so many people. I walked around the perimeter to gain some knowledge of structure and scale. Clearly the ancient, culture were not held back by detail, or made a problem out of logistics, they just knew how to build big.

title: Worth the tour!
text: We took a tour of the Colosseum and getting to hear about the different areas within it, who got to sit where during the actual events, etc., was well worth the $34 euros. We had headphones so we could hear the guide.

title: Real History
text: We did a self guided tour of the Colosseum, actually listening to Rick Steve's audio walking tour.  The lines were really long in the morning and we were getting conflicting information about the use of the Roma Pass.  Everyone was trying to get us to pay $45 Euro's for a guided tour.  We went on to the Roman Forum and came back about 3:30.  Lines were short and we were able to get right in with our Roma Pass.  The Colosseum is a must see attraction when in Rome because of the historical significance.   Not sure I would go back a second time, now that I've been here.

title: Interesting Tour
text: We took the local tour provided by the local groups,  It was very interesting.  They provided great insight into Rome centuries ago. As well as what it looks like and how much it has deteriorated.  The groups were rather large and were distracting to me.  It was hard to ask questions,  I would have preferred a smaller group.  Our guide definitely knew a lot about the history and the Coliseum.

title: Colosseum
text:
World heritage listing property with thousands visiting in 28 degrees of summer weather. 
The sight was worth visiting and a must for anyone visiting Rome.

title: Amazing Architecture
text: Really enjoyed exploring the overall architecture of this historical piece.  The size, capacity and sophistication of this facility for the time in which it was built is amazing.

title: Just amazing!
text: The colloseum is breathtaking an such an amazing structure . I would recommend sparing at least 2 hours to walk around and enjoy the history . At one point, I felt very emotional when realizing that I was standing where the gladiators fought thousands of years ago. We most enjoyed using the hop on hop off tours to take pictures and get around the city . In my opinion, the Rome pass isn’t worth much because you can only use it for one major attraction ( such as the Colloseum) and the hop on hop off bus, and metro. For the price, I would have paid for the entrance fees to the different sights out of pocket.

title: Worth a visit for sure
text: It was so fun seeing this in person! I downloaded Steve Rick's audio guide to learn more about the colosseum and it was super helpful. We went during Rome's birthday celebration in April and the traffic around was crazy. We just took an Uber and had no problem getting around.

title: Must see
text: It was great to see this iconic site in Rome. I highly recommend getting online tickets - save you so much time waiting in line. our pre purchased ticket was for 8:45am and we could see there was already a huge line for people who hadn't purhcased a ticket. we originally wanted to get the tour of the underground and the belvedere areas but they were closed off - would definitely do it next time.

title: Amazing
text: This is an amazing place. The only issue the construction going on to install a new metro line a stones throw from the colosseum. What are they thinking!!!

title: Great tour
text: We took a tour through Get Your Guide. This was a wonderful experience to see the arena, underground and upper level. Highly recommend seeing the Colosseum this way to avoid the crowds.

title: VERY INTERESTING TOUR!!!!
text: We booked our tour through Viator with a live English speaking guide and it was worth every penny. The Roman Forum and Palatine Hill were also included and it was all very interesting. Be prepared to walk and be aware that the ground over at the Forum and Palatine Hill is rough and uneven in some places. It was a little hard on the feet. It was so much better to have a live guide for this one. It makes the places come alive because of the descriptions and information you just can't get doing it yourself. This is a Rome must see.

title: A must do...
text: You can’t visit Rome and not visit the Colosseum. It’s a must do! Go for the skip the lines tickets and avoid summer midday hours... it gets really hot. It would be perfect if you could also see the arena, sine with all the works happening in there you don’t really see anything inside.

title: Colosseum
text: This was a wonderful experience We were very lucky to have pre booked our tickets to go in we bypassed the massive queues and also got to go underground   We would recommend pre booking all trips

title: Citysightseeing guided tour of Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
text: Going on a guided tour you skip the queues and learn so much more. Fantastic guides who knew their stuff. A must if visiting Rome.

title: City break holiday
text: This is a must, get a guided tour as the guide we had was brilliant, his knowledge knew no bounds and was happy to take any questions, well done George our guide

title: Amazing
text: Great place for a brief history lesson. Probably good to have a guided tour, we didn't, because we had small children.

title: World fantastic pace
text: Lovely that historical place.i never regret to visit this part of the Roman life.it was wonderful to be there,I visited with my family and my wife I really love that museum.

title: The colloseum is amazing
text: I had a wonderful visit to the colloseum in Rome with my family and would definitely recommend to everyone. It was the highlight of our trip and appealed to everyone.

title: First class Roman attraction!
text:
What a place! If you visit one sight in Rome then this is the one! 

We paid for tickets to skip the line and it’s definitely worth every penny. We also booked later in the day 4:30. It was still busy but a little more bearable. You must book your time slots to visit otherwise you don’t get in. 

Every first Sunday of the month it’s free entry but the queue is incredibly long and in the heat it’s probably not worth it. 

The audio guide is definitely worth it, adds to the atmosphere. 

I visited twenty years ago and can definitely see the improvements been made. 

Absolutely worth a visit!

title: Night Tour
text: My wife and myself took the colosseum tour at night and it was not a disappointment. The arena looked amazing and walking through the halls at night give an extra chill.

title: Worth seeing
text: Worth seeing in person. Hard to imagine 50,000 Romans cheering in the Colosseum 2,000 years ago. Many people trying to sell cheesy stuff to tourists, be aware of scams & pickpockets. Lines looked long, I didn’t go inside but walked completely around it. I wouldn’t go back to see it a second time but totally worth seeing it once.

title: Amazing
text: It is must in Rome. Amazing building. If you don't want to stand in the line,  you can book a fast pass as I did, but it's a bit expensive. Otherwise you need to arrive early there.

title: Underground Tour
text: What an amazing look back in time. We took an underground tour and it was amazing. I didn't realize what a brutal history this place had.

title: Amazing sight
text: Breathtaking view . Very busy queuing for tickets , so we decided not to go inside . As everywhere in Rome you have to be prepared to be hassled by people trying to sell you skip the line tickets / selfie sticks etc . It can become very overwhelming as you don't know who to trust as they all seem so friendly. Saw what we set out to see and don't feel we missed out by not going inside .

title: Stunning!
text:
The colosseum was stunning and the history blew my mind!
Stunning from the outside and our tour guide took us inside and I’m sure the skip-the-line access saved us hours of queuing, so strongly suggest this option. We took a ‘walks’ tour which I highly recommend. 
The Colosseum wasn’t as busy as other locations in Rome, although this was ~9am on a Monday morning.

title: When in Rome, do as the Roman's do.
text:
My partner and I did the walking tour through the Colosseum and the Forum on our recent trip to Rome and we could not of had a better guide! Mateo's passion for history, his genuine affection for the Colosseum and people skills made the day a pleasure.

We left the tour feeling full of facts and loving ancient Rome as much as Mateo does. Despite the pouring rain we had a great time and would highly recommend this tour to anyone visiting Rome.

Thank you Mateo!

title: Long line but cheap entrance price
text: We came 9.10am and waited  for 2 hours in the line and we spend 1,5 hour in there. The ticket price was only 12€ which made the waiting more valuable. If you arrive later than 10, I think the line would be even longer! Remember this is one of the 7 wonders from the ancient world, so there will always be many visitors :-)

title: Front view really impressive
text: The Colosseum from outside is really beautiful and impressive. I didn't check the pictures online before I entered, so I was a bit disappointed when I was inside. Inside it is basically a ruin. But nevertheless 5 circles.

title: A MUST SEE WHEN IN ROME
text:
We arrived here just before 9.30am in the morning and it was already starting to get busy, we waited in the normal queue which took 50 minutes’ to go through security and get tickets to enter the colosseum and was 12e for an adult ticket, you can also use the same ticket to enter the forum opposite the same day or the next day.
There is a limit to the number of people that can enter the colosseum so if you do not buy a guided tour or skip the line ticket then I would recommend arriving even before 9.30am in the morning or you may end up waiting even longer in the normal queue.
The steps inside the colosseum are quite steep but there is a lift that can be used for people with mobility issues or over 65's only. There are toilets although not the cleanest.
This is a must see when in Rome even if you just go and look at the outside the only thing is you will be hassled by lots and I mean lots of lookie lookie men and people selling the guided tours.

title: Afternoon
text:
Can  you visit the Colosseum in 5 hours from Rome airport?
 
I recently had a 6 hour layover in Rome airport & I was determined not to spend it in the airport. I love Italy & the Colosseum & I hadn’t seen it for a number of years.   Was it possible?  In a word, yes.  To make it possible for you here a few tips:
 
1 – When you check in a your first port of call (Madrid was mine) get them to check you into you second port of call (London for me).  IMPORTANT NOTE - I only had hand luggage so do not know & will not advise passengers with luggage in the hold.
 
2- Control what you can don’t sweat the other stuff. If your flight is delayed this will ruin everything, mine thankfully took off on time and landed on time making everything possible.
3 – As soon as you leave the exit in Rome airport look to your right and 15 yards in front of you is a ticket office for the Leonardo Express (It has Neon signage above it) . What was a major help to me was that there was tiny quick moving queue & wonderful desk clerk). Here you can buy a return ticket on the Leonardo Express (Buy return 28 euros and also by your Metro ticket (1.50 euros return).
4 – Head out the airport turn right, a 5 minute walk takes you to the Train station.  Because you have bought your tickets already, you avoid a larger queue at the station, where you can board the train (MAKE SURE YOU VALIDATE YOUR TICKET BEFORE BOARDING – 200 euro fine if you don’t)
5 – A train leave about every 30 minutes. I lucked in and only waited 8 minutes for the next train. The journey is about 25 minutes.
6 – Once in Rome Terminal (The main train station) follow the signs to metro (well sign posted)  Take the A Line (Blue) 2 stops to the Colosseum.  Again I was lucky and waited four minutes for the metro.  Journey time 8 minutes.
7- The metro stops right outside the Colosseum & is a wonderful sight to walk out to.
8 – I spent a couple of hours wandering round the colosseum and then I went to a place for lunch. * There is a really nice gay quarter by the Colosseum and I found a lovely home run restaurant that served a stunning Ragu pasta. Support small business’s and swerve the bigger name brands.
9 – After lunch have another quick little stroll around the colosseum & head to the Mero. Just follow your tracks back to the main train station. In truth, I did all this in a leisurely 4 and a half hours.
TIPS:
What you shouldn’t do is get carried away and think I’ll just pop over and see the Vatican (or some other wonder) as well. Stay focused and just enjoy a one stop, stress free trip.
Don’t take a car/cab – Rome traffic can be horrid and could easily derail your timings.
Don’t dilly dally.  I was riding solo, so I had no one holding me back or no one to hurry along and distract me from my plan.  There is no elbow room for curve balls or last minute changes. Stick to your plan.
Once at the airport because you have already checked in (clever you!)  go straight to security. Again, I lucked in and the queue wasn’t too long.  Once through I still had a good 2 hours, to chill, grab a beer reflect on a lovely few hours in Rome.

title: do a tour!
text: this site is huge and so incredible with its history. if you have a tour guide you will not miss out on anything

title: Amazing engineering
text: Worth seeing once. To survive 2000 years of earthquakes, wars, and ransacking is astounding. Waited in line 1hr even with ‘admission time’ ticket that took 30min of waiting to obtain (used Roma Pass). Frustrating but worth the visit - to both Forum (2hr) & Colosseum (1hr).

title: Tour
text: Walks of Italy small group tour -great!  Skip the line access. Easy meeting point and great Wi-Fi headsets. Lucked out on that!

title: So Much History..Guided Tour Recommended
text: So much history that a guided tour is recommended. Viator guides are exceptionally knowledgeable and informative about the history of the Colosseum and local customs at the time of the Roman Empire.

title: Make sure to see displays.
text: We went for a daytime and nighttime visit. Daytime was better for viewing the top levels even though they were crowded. Night time tours are done by archaeologists so information provided is superior and if you can do the underground one so you can see the pulley system and sewer as well as gladiator gate. Do your research before you go so you can recognise the gladiator school they are excavating at the moment across the road.

title: What a place
text: To be honest this is one of the best historic place I have  traveled.I would request to definitely take a tour guide for this as unless that you wont be able to feel the place. Its  a must go place.Also there are many un authenticated  person there will try to help you,be ware of them.

title: Overwhelming
text: The shear size was more than I could have imagined before going. Be sure to get advance tickets to avoid the line and bring water. Hire a driver to drop you off and pick you up so you want have to find parking.

title: Coliseum tour
text: You see pictures of the Coliseum but they don't do it justice..  it's amazing to see it in real life. Your guide said it's the most visited attraction in Rome.  The crowds were large but got through security pretty fast. Definitely worth the visit.

title: Truly incredible
text: Due to an event on June2 and building of the metro and the massive amounts of tourists it is quite congested area.  We just walked around the outside having visited the inside 8 years ago.    The atmosphere has changed a lot they have put up screens so you can no longer see inside and the amount of people very aggressively trying to get you to buy bracelets and other junk is a bit off putting.   But the structure itself and the history are incredible.

title: The One and Only
text: Who can come to Rome and not see the Colosseum? It is only is the top 10 sights to see in all the world. Yes, it is over crowded and yes I wish it was in better condition. But it is what it is! One of my favorite buildings in the world!

title: Must See
text: An amaxing structure to have survived this length of time.  Not sure i like the work they are now doing on it to collect even more tourist dollars.

title: Great art
text: Very nice architecture but lot of work going on inside on the main arena and outside. The best option to go in is to book a guide prior visit with reasonable price. This will allow you to avoid massive queues. The visit itself it takes around and hour. We went to see Forum first and then the last thing at 17:00 Colosseum. It worked well.

title: Good tour
text: AJ did a good job of answering all my off the wall questions and provided a lot of good information! Loved the underground tour portion!

title: Walking in history
text: The visit was excellent and the story is told in clear way. We visited it on a damp day but it still worked. Top tip book well in advance and aim for the earliest time you can. It is such and awe inspiring place that it is worth spending the time there.

title: Really interesting piece of history but very congested
text: The best thing about the colosseum is the amazingly different history and multiple uses it has had as the centre point of Roman society over centuries from being central market areas to the place to be for gladiators battles. The queues as with everything in Rome are long so best to do one of the "skip the queue" tours

title: Colosseum and Ancient Rome
text: We visited the Colosseum on a sunny day in June. We had a reservation for a skip the line entry at 8:30 am but our entry was delayed by the ticket office which did not open until 8:30 to issue our actual entry ticket. Once inside we were already surrounded by tour groups, but were able to get away from them enough to enjoy the Colosseum. We the spent another 4 hours touring the rest of the complex and still did not see it all. Be aware that there is no food or water sold at the site except in vending machines. Water spouts are available to refill your bottle if you brought one. Some parts of the site are only open if you purchase a “SUPER” pass. Ours got us into the museum and the newly reopened Domus Transitoria (which requires a separate reservation to visit). The garden on Palatine Hill offered much needed shade and a place to sit and rest. Well worth the visit but plan ahead by purchasing the right ticket and bringing your own food and water.

title: Must see
text: There are some things that you just can't miss if you go to Rome.  The Colosseum is one of them.  I am in awe of the Romans.  They were really brilliant when it came to building and infrastructure.  They could also be brutal and bloodthirsty as you will see when you visit.

title: Great visit
text: Extremely beautiful piece of history. Very good security however informed unable to take rucksacks in to there but many people did. Slightly annoyed as left behind due to thinking we couldn’t take it and should be more clear sprays of any kind including suncream are prohibited. Would visit again.

title: Incredible place
text: The lines are so long, but visiting Colosseum is worth it! Absolutely incredible place, where every brick has s history behind it.

title: Great views from outside
text: We didn’t pay the extra to go inside, but got great pictures outside and could even see spots on the inside. I have no regrets about not going in - walking around it was great for me.

title: Spellbinding
text: Book a tour... so you can hear all the history and understand what your eye are taking in...and... jump the lines. It was both beautiful and sad...so know the majesty it once was but is not just a skeleton.

title: What a site!
text: What a great historical part of western civilization!  We bought the skip the line tickets and got in early.  So much history and tradition.   You can choose a guided tour but we didn't.  You can youtube the tour for free then walk it.  It is amazing and tremendous!  The museum is upstairs,  make sure you walk it and learn a lot of Roman history.  For us, one and done visit.  Beautiful,  historical, breathtaking and now I see the on line stories as it interests me even more, how the heck did they build this?

title: Must See
text: The iconic colloseum is a must see when visiting Rome.  When we visited the were doing a lot of preservation, so much scaffolding  up.  If you do any tours, the underground portion was disappointing, not as much as we thought we would see.  If you want to see the upper levels there is always the chance that they will be closed for preservation, we oly went to the 3rd tier. Make sure you have your good walking shoes on because between the Hill, Forum, and Collosuem you'll have a full day.

title: Goosebump Visit
text: Went on the late night tour which places this magnificent building in a totally different perspective, no crowds but more atmosphere.  You certainly fell the history and energy this place has soaked up over the centuries and you can't help but marvel at the scale of the place.  It is must see and good that every effort is being made to keep it upright..  Would I pay the extra for the guided tour, yes!  You get more information and explanation as to what went on.  Better to go after the crowds

title: Rome Vacation
text: This is one of the best places in Europe that you should see in your life time. Before I was just dreaming how I could go there and the real encounter happened last year during our vacation in Europe. very memorable experience. Hope I could visit this place again.

title: A guided tour is a must
text: We arranged a guided tour with “Mea Tours”. There are many others. Our guide was knowledgable, spoke great English and explained the detailed history of the Colosseum as well as intricate details of what we were looking at. Hilary has a great knowledge of Roman History. The Colosseum itself is breathtaking. A must do when in Rome.

title: Great guide and tour
text: We visited on a 3:15 tour with 10 others - small group tour with forum with Walks of Italy.  My new favorite.  It was super hot at this time, should have done the morning.  But we learned a lot and appreciate even more

title: Amazing
text: This is a must do in Rome, the line was 2 hour wait though there are tourism companies outside offering skip the line tour for 35 euro each I took it as I didn’t want to wait that long not worth it, so much history so amazing to visit, a must do

title: Engineering Marvel
text: This is a true engineering and technological marvel of 2000 years old Roman Empire. The structure is magnanimous and well preserved. I would rate this as number one place in Rome

title: Wow
text: When in Rome visit the Colosseum but I would recommend a guided tour.  Skip the line and lots of information.  Our guide was an archeologist and was very enthusiastic.  It is a crazy busy place but worth the visit.

title: Amazing
text:
The "terrible" rated reviews do make me laugh

This is quite simply such an iconic site that you can would be daft not to go in

Here's a tip, get your combined ticket at the forum where there's hardly a queue

 Also worth getting there early

Then you can just bask in one of the world's best sites

title: amazing place, highly recommended to take a guided tour, you will find a lot of interesting fact about the Romans
text:
amazing place, highly recommended to take a guided tour, you will find a lot of interesting fact about the Romans  
however dis-recommendation (not recommend at all) the TOURISTATION agent.
after paying 50 euro each for guided tour, we left by ourselves in the Colosseum without our guide since they booked the entry time 5 hours after the tour started and the guide needed to left...

title: Loved it!
text: We bought the Roma Pass at the Forum first-  that definitely helped with the line-  we waited about 5 minutes and the regular line was 2+ hours!  Loved the entire visit at the Colosseum!  Take the time to check out the exhibits!  Must Do!!

title: Wellworth visiting ....
text:
A great site to visit, so amazing and the history behind it just superb! Definately worth going on a guided tour as you are told all of the history and the facts, unbelievable after all this time and it is still standing!!
This is a MUST SEE sight .... when in Rome ....

title: 2 hours at the colosseum
text: Great views of the city from inside the Colosseum. Lots of crowd and your best bet is to skip the line for additional fee. Plenty of history and character. The location says a lot about the enduring nature of the Roman Empire. Highly educational!

title: Part of the Rome experience
text: It would be a shame to visit Rome and not visit the Colosseum. We chose to do a Walks of Italy tour of the Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill.  15 people in our group.   I could not imagine visiting without a guide who provides essential information which enhances the experience. Even with skip the line, we waited about 20 minutes to enter because they only allow a certain number of people in at a time. Our guide explained this happens, especially in the afternoon. We visited late May. It was not too crowded once inside, but it was busy. Next time we plan to do an underground tour.

title: Way too crowded!
text: I‘ve been here a couple of times, but I think I‘ll skip it next time. Even though you have a ticket with a reservation time, you have to wait for more than one hour in the sun because they let too many people in. If it‘s your first time in Rome, you probably need to accept that, but it‘s not worth doing it for a second or third or .... time.

title: So much history
text: Our guide, Neils, was fantastic.  Neils was informative, provocative, exciting, and painted a picture of life in room and experiences at the Colosseum.  He was so good that I purchased gladiator merchandise (books/t-shirts) for my family because I was excited about all that I had learned.  The remnants of the Colosseum, come to life with the right guide so schedule a tour and enjoy the view and experience.  Crowded but so enjoyable.

title: The most!
text: One of the most beautiful places in the world. Huge with a lot of history and feels like you are part of history when you are inside.

title: A world wonder worth visiting
text: What is there more to say? Amazing. It really leaves a great impression behind after visiting this. I had skip the line tickets that i bought trough the official website. Trust me, DO IT. in less than 5 minutes we were inside, security check included. It was a very busy afternoon as well. The line was huge so make sure u buy the tickets ! And most of all, enjoy !

title: Staggering!
text: You cannot miss this! Buy your tickets not at the collosseum but about 150 yards away at the entrance to the gardens/Capitol. No queues! Get there ready for first thing opening and you will almost have the place to yourself! I had an elderly, smiling Mum with me, which also accelerated us to the front of any queues on holiday with cries of  'Nonna, Nonna!'

title: Very impressive from the outside
text: It is very busy even when you have a card for skip the line, you have to make an appointment for a special time and then you will find another line.

title: Must do
text:
Tickets should be booked in advance as they have entry times which are strictly enforced . Once you show up at least 30min prior to scheduled time , entry is well organized and efficient .
Colosseum is a Great areas and monument which can be explorers with Guide or self guided

title: Definitely worth bookimh
text:
Book before you go queues are long!
Amazing place really loved it but the guide was worth every penny. The place is surrounded by people scouting for business. 
Great experience for the children

title: Pre book in advance....
text:
Bought skip the line tickets for early in the morning which was a great idea as the queues when we came out for who didn't buy tickets was horrendous. A lot of restoration going on when we visited. Its a very historic place with an interesting but gory past.
 Also visit in the evening when lit up and less busy but watch out for fake good sellers and photo oppotunists, Hundreds about!!

title: Time Travelling at the Colosseum with the Roman Guy
text:
By far the best tour of the Colosseum is the VIP Colosseum Night Tour with Underground & Arena Floor. This tour said we would be alone in the Colosseum and they delivered in a big way.
The meet up area was easy to find and Isabella, our tour guide is a Art Historian with lots of enthusiasm and knowledge to share. She was considerate with the pace (for us older folks) and she stopped often to allow for pictures (and she really knew the knock your socks off viewpoints).  I was thrilled with the photos I took.
The route walking to the Colosseum was manageable with many spectacular views of the Roman Forum.
Of course the highlight of the tour was the Colosseum (which we did have to ourselves - just imagine)!  There were other tour groups but they were carefully timed so that each group had alone time.  We had an excellent tour of the below ground areas, and viewed a replica of the elevators used to bring animals to the arena floor.  The evening was capped off with an opportunity to sit on the area floor and view a video taking us back to the time where gladiators fought (right where we were sitting).
The pre tour information was helpful, if I was to add anything I would suggest a small flashlight/torch as some of the corridors we walked inside the Colosseum were dark with uneven pathways.
If the Colosseum is your destination this is the tour for you!

title: Get skip the line passes!
text: The Colosseum was absolutely incredible! No wonder it’s one of the Seven Wonders of the World! If you go in high season (May-August), get skip the line passes or even better, try to find a tour that includes skip the line passes! It’s so worth it to not wait for hours in the hot sun! I did a guided tour with Gladiator entrance, which is a special entrance not everyone gets to experience! I’d highly recommend investing a little more money, it’s well worth it! Go back at night if you can, it’s beautiful!

title: Amazing
text: A must see in Rome, totally breathtaking and a must see when in the area, currently its €13 to go in but the queues are long.

title: OMG
text: What can you say about it - awesome, magnificent, amazing, wow!!  Couldn't get all around it, very busy, long queues but worth it.

title: Not to be missed
text: The Colosseum is everything you have ever read about it!!.The atmosphere is incredible(as are the crowds, try to visit early morning or later in the afternoon) We booked a guided tour, which was spoilt by the guide not knowing when to shut up! he droned on incessantly. If we went again we would wander around on our own, & hire the headsets available to visitors.

title: Incredible; visit with a guide
text:
My teenage niece and I visited the colosseum on a rather hot day in June.  We booked a tour with A Friend In Rome, and thank goodness we did—not only because our guide was amazing, but also by doing so, we were able to breeze right in to the colosseum right when it opened before the massive crowds.

The history of the building and the innovations of the ancient Romans is impressive and incredible.  I can’t recommend our guide highly enough.  Without a guide, I don’t know that We would’ve had the same wonderful and informative experience.

title: A dream attraction
text:
The Colosseum is a must see attraction in Rome. There is so much history and interesting facts behind what went on during its time. 

Highly recommend to do a tour to get the most of out it, particularly one that does the arena floor. You will really get your own personal space if you opt for that option.

title: Mesmerising trip to Colosseum
text: Just completed my tour to Roman forum, palatine hill and Colosseum. Took a fast track skip the line tour which is preferred unless you want to stand in a long queue. It was the perfect sunny day on 1st June and we had a great time listening to history and stories about Colosseum. Nearest metro is colosseo from termini on line B

title: Interesting
text: Ofcourse you need to see the colosseum while in Rome but i mean i wouldn't take a ticket to see it from the inside unless i am very interested about the history of the place or architecture, otherwise it would be a bunch of huge stones, stairways and crowds.

title: Contrast of Italy Trafalgar Tours
text: This was and excellent sight to see in Rome.  They are renovating the Colosseum to resemble what it was back in Roman times.  We also seen and walked Circus Maximus which now is used for out door concerts. My only compliant is that with Trafalgar we only had 30 minutes and you need a lot more time to truly appreciate this sight.  If I did this tour again I use Trafalgar for Hotels and transportation, but would book my own tours, which you can do in advance over the internet.

title: Do a night tour to appreciate this Place
text:
I don’t need to tell you about this building because  It’s so famous. What I will say is, spend the extra and get a night tour to appreciate it all. Daytime its packed and surrounded by guides and folks trying to sell you their wares. Selfie stick anyone??? 

Go see it, it has an amazing story and an amazing feel. Beware of Nero, he bites. (Nero is the Cat:-))

title: Restoration of Colosseo diminished it's ancient appeal
text: I commend the efforts of the Roman government  in restoring the ancient buildings to provide more stability for the safety of the tourist entering these historical sites. Aesthetically speaking, it appears to be modern and losses its sense of history. It could have been better, if the restored portion exactly replicated the original section. Just a thought.

title: Not a great one
text: Colosseum is a world wonder. But I didn't feel it so  good a visit. Firstly it is very crowded. Secondly not much to see inside.But when in Rome its a must visit due to its historical importance

title: Amazing Historical Place
text: Took a skip-the-line tour to visit it on May 31, 2019. Huge structure surprises all of us, leaned a lot about the history from our Guide. Must visit for all.

title: The jewel of Rome
text: Absolutely stunning. Such a vibe of history from within. Beware all the "info guides" outside trying to direct you to tour guides. They are scams even though they wear official looking vests and lanyards. They nearly got me and it was only when I said I'll be back in time for the next tour so I can take in the external that the info guy got really pushy. At that point I knew something was off. When I finally broke away I was immediately harassed by several more. Just go to the entrance me wait in the queue which took me 15 minutes whereas the guys 9uyside told me the line was 3 hours long.

title: A must see while in Roma!
text: Absolutely spectacular place to visit while in Rome. We were lucky enough to visit with our tour and literally waited 35 minutes in the waiting line.  The wait this day was 5 hours. It’s just mind blowing to see this spectacular building still standing today. You can’t come to Rome and not see and visit this spectacular site. Definitely a must do.

title: Amazing historical destination
text:
You think you know about the Colosseum, and then you visit it live and realize you didn't know anything.  

We were very surprised by the size of it, and the history speaks for itself.  You can do a self-guided tour (and probably wait a long time to get in) but we opted to take a no-wait organized tour and learned so much about the history.  This should be a bucket list destination for everyone, and we're glad we went.

title: It was so busy
text: So many people, a really long line and a lot of "staff members" trying to sell tours. the Colosseum it very impressive.

title: Tour with City Tours Colosseum
text: We booked through City Tours and Anna was our tour guide. She is AMAZING cannot say enough good things about her. She imparted so much information and made the tour very interesting. Every tour that booked through City Tours was very organized and the guides were all great.

title: Beat the queue...haha
text:
We went around Rome on the hop on hop off bus on Monday (separate review on that) and decided that Wednesday we’d visit the colosseum. We arrived early to beat the queue
and at 7:30/8am the queue wasn’t that big. 
It’s normally €12 to get in but the beat the queue touts charge another €20 to ‘beat the queue’ and a guided tour.

We didn’t really benefit too much with the beat the queue, IMHO it wasn’t any quicker and we still had to queue in a different queue for our ticket and then another queue as you go through security scanners. 
Didn’t rate the guided tour and certainly wasn’t worth €20. I recommend getting there early to beat the queue and download Rick Steve’s Italy Audio Tours podcasts. 
8 times a year it’s free to get in any of the attractions and we visited on 5th June which was one of those FREE days. Just wish we hadn’t bothered with the guided tour/beat the queue.

title: Small Group Tour - Livitaly.com with Chiara
text: Book a small group tour with livitaly.com (ask for Chiara).  Chiara is a professional Archaeologist.  Our tour was our family plus one other, amazing!!

title: Unbelievable structure , try counting the bricks...you'll ge busy !
text: Stunning structure. 17 euros to get in (includes entry to the Forum) , go later in the afternoon if you want to avoid queuing more than an hour to get in. No shade when queuing.

title: Skip the line underground colosseum
text: Great tour. Underground and tier 1 and tier 2 of the colosseum. Then you are allowed to go to the Palatine Hill for the rest of the day to explore on your own. Guide was knowledgeable and gave enough facts and time for photos. He did not overwhelm with excessive facts but gave enough to satisfy curiosity.

title: Wonderful Monument
text: We had a great time booking a skip the line ticket to see the Colosseum.  Such a well managed place - there were a lot of people there, but not so many that it was too crammed and unenjoyable.  Loved looking at the exhibition they had happening at the time and see the Colosseum for itself!

title: So so tour guide
text: Overall we enjoyed this tour the sites are amazing and it’s nice to have an explanation about what we were seeing. However our tour guide did not speak English very well so we couldn’t understand much of what they were saying. As we approach the Colosseum the lines were very long. Our guide reacted in a way that made us think that we would be waiting a long time, which we did, but we wouldn’t have noticed if the guide had been calm about it.

title: WOW
text: A truly incredible structure given it only took 8 years to build. We did the three hour tour that included the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. This is really the only way to fully appreciate what took place so many centuries ago. It got us down right to the floor of the arena looking down into the dungeons and cage areas below used to house the warriors, slaves and wild animals used in the arena games. Hard to imagine the horror that took place to the pleasure of the 70,000 who attended free of charge. A guided tour that is a must see.

title: Colosseum
text: It is quite overwhelming! Incredible to see what the Romans were capable of building and the cruelty of its purpose. I recommend using a guided tour. You can go to the front of the line and you have someone knowledgeable about the history. It is a must see in Rome.

title: Second Visit
text: When in Rome, you must visit the Colosseum. Take a walk around or book a tour in advance to beat the huge lines.

title: Impressive, truly worth it
text: Awesome.  Take a tour, avoid the lines.  Bring water, sunblock, hat.  Super hot and moderate walking.  Hubby could not get this, was the highlight of the whole trip for him.

title: Impressive
text: The histories of Coliseum is so interesting that it is necessary more than one day to see and really appreciate every detail of Rome.

title: Must visit
text: Go around 9 am if later then it will take you around 45 mins to get the tickets don’t do skip the line doesn’t worth

title: Colosseum is Amazing
text: Amazing site and worth the visit but be sure to get skip the line passes - completely worth the extra cost! Also spend some time in the newly opened museum area on the second floor!

title: Beware, Beware of Headout
text: Booked and paid for colosseum guided tour but didn’t receive tickets, emailed them and they eventually replied that due to a glitch we had missed out and they couldn’t provide alternate tickets before we flew out. Still waiting for a refund

title: Organised Tour of the Colosseum
text: ny daughter booked this in advance and obviously can't be  missed - without the tour you wouldn't understand the history and where everything was when it was being used - even int he rain it was beautiful

title: The one and only Colosseum
text: A must while in Rome. A glimpse on Ancient Rome Grandeur. You would only realize the monument splendor Only when standing inside the colosseum

title: Wonderful
text: Superb experience. Historical place. A good guide is necessary to explore it. A very good example of Roman architecture.

title: Roman Guy Tours
text:
Our tour guide Manola from the Roman Guy Tours, was such an informative entertaining history expert that you couldn’t help but want to listen to all the facts in a clear and logical order. My mate had told me, “The Colosseum, just drive pass and wave at it” 
I’m so glad I didn’t listen to him.

Thank you Manola for sharing your knowledge in such a delightful way with us, we would recommend the 3 hour tour as I think it’s a minimum to take in the wealth of information that Manola shares with you.

title: Bucket list
text: Pricing is confusing and the queues long!! We found our way there around 8.30am and booked with a official tour guide.  They walk around outside.  54 euros (they tried 67 at first).  The price included the Palatine - Forum and a tour guide. We thought it good value and no hassle.  Tour guide inside the colosseum was v good and brought it to life.

title: Can’t go to Rome and not go!
text: It is one of the most iconic places in the world. It is truly impressive.t Try to get tickets in advance and wear comfortable shoes. You can just walk around the outside and it is still impressive.

title: Multiple visits
text:
This is a true relic. This year was the first time I saw a part of the construction in a part being remodeled. Initially, I thought it was for the stability of the relic. I was corrected..Part of the Colosseum is to be replaced with what was thought to be the original Colosseum. This will allow tourists to see the before & after. I have very mixed feelings about this. My preference is not changing the original ruins & purchasing the book that allows one to see what most popular sites with an overlay
of what was thought to be the original views

title: Amazing
text: Came out of the metro and there it was in all its glory. Be aware of people wanting to take your photo, they want paying.

title: Downes Family
text: Fabulous site to visit. Make sure you have water with you or a bottle to fill at the water stations as you walk around. There is not a lot of shade once you get into the Forum area so sunscreen a must. As with others we found it very confusing trying to find the tour guide as we were constantly asked by people if we had tickets and trying to direct us into wrong queues (unsure who were touts and who were legitimate people trying to help). There was also the usual array of annoying street sellers trying to befriend you and sell you tat. Once inside though away from all these annoyances it is a must do when visiting Rome. Our guide was excellent and knowledgeable, and happy to answer any questions asked.

title: Pre-book your tickets!!
text: We didnt pre book tickets for The Colosseum before we came and we definately should have done! We ended up queing for almost 2 hours in the blazing sun waiting to get in where as if we had pre booked we wouldnt have had such a wait which is to be expected at such a place. It is worth the wait but it can get draining being in the sun without shade for so long. Just be careful of the street sellers trying to sell you bottled water/hats/ umberellas and 'skip the line tickets'. If the products are something you want then go for it but if not then be firm and tell them no thank you. They seem to understand itallian AND english so dont be fooled by them as they are VERY persistant! Be careful with who you are buying skip the line tickets from aswell as they may not be offical! Other than that Enjoy the vast beauty of The Colosseum and also ask for Combo tickets. P.s 18-25year olds only cost €2

title: Stupendous
text:
It's  name says it all: COLLOSAL!
All the might of Roman Empire is on display there.

Just one word of advice: if you do not speak Italian, make sure that your tour guide speaks English. Our guide did not. It was not only her heavy accent. It was also her vocabulary. We booked a tour at our cruise ship, and were unpleasantly surprised.

title: Awesome place, book a guide
text:
We booked a guided tour that took us out on the arena floor and down into the underground area beneath the arena floor. You're only allowed to visit the first and second levels without an authorised guide and with a proper guide you have an entry time slot so you won't have to stand in any kind of line - you'll even go past those who have bought their tickets online.

There's a lot to see here and you'll either need to read up very well beforehand or have a guide to grasp it all.

If you're interested in the Roman empire the Colosseum is a must see!

title: Very good but be careful
text:
The monument is unique and really worth to visit, but beware of:
1. The crowd if it is high season 
2. The hot weather because there aren't places with shadow there. All the time you are under the hot sun. If you are going with small children prefer to go in the afternoon.

title: When in Rome
text:
We only taking picture outside the colosseum and not going inside the colosseum .
So I don’t know is it worth paying or not to see inside the colosseum

title: Ancient Treasure
text: Wasn't too bus late afternoon in first week of June. We just walked around and took pictures. You will be asked many times if you want to buy tickets to go inside. Do not pay extra for skip the line becuase it isn't true. Their is no fast line  because security checks have no priority

title: Must do if you are in Roma
text: The history of this place it’s just amazing. Take the tour with the audio guide, it’s the best option. As you walk around, you get to know the interesting facts about this beautiful piece of art.

title: Worth money to skip the long queues
text: Took this tour with GIUSEPPE what a knowledgeable guy. We had a really fun afternoon with him on this tour. The queues were massive and we passed by them all worth the money alone but the tour guide made the tour with his wealth of knowledge and humour! Well worth the money!

title: Iconic
text: Who would come to Rome and not visit here. Some tips echoed by other travellers.  Always pre-book your tickets before you travel. We booked the earliest time to avoid the crowds but it was still very busy nonetheless. Once you can find the right queue, it is fairly straightforward and unless you are on a tour, you can pretty much bimble round at your own leisure and in what order you like. Beware that there are literally dozens of ticket touts outside and all round the area offering tickets or to flog you tours which can slow you down and become a little repetitive. There are also sellers of bracelets and cheap goods which you can't fail to notice in this location - as rude as it sounds, it is best just to blank and move on to your destination. Anyway, we bought a ticket which included the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill which was great and saved us queuing again once we got there (albeit skip the line queues). In fairness we spent about an hour or so here which was enough for us before moving on to Palatine Hill.

title: Wow Factor
text: If you feel history, this is the place to go and marvel that it is still standing and to imagine what went on here 2000 years ago.  It was thundering when we went which all added to the atmosphere.  Our tour guide was exceptional, unhurried tour of the Colosseum followed by a tour of the Roman Forum - very good value for money, I could have gone round it all again!

title: A little confusing as to where
text:
We had pre booked tour tickets from the Coop culture site.
It was a little confusing as to where to go when we got to Colosseum. It's a huge area with so many people milling around with tour guides and vast lines.
We did find where we had to go once inside-there are meeting points where you wait for your guide. 
Coming out into the open area of the Colosseum is cool! You get a real sense of the scale of things. There is a lot of restoration going on so areas are covered and scaffolding is erected which is unfortunate. With our tickets we got access to Pallatine Hill and the Forum. I'd say leave a lot of time to get around these areas but the Colosseum you can do it in around 1-2 hours depending on your access areas. 
Get there early so you can take all the areas in.

title: Visit to colosseum
text:
We started out early around 9am for our visit it was already busy ,there were numerous ticket touts trying to sell guided tours at higher prices than you would have to pay if you waited and bought the tickets yourself ,
We probably got stopped ten times on our way in and some of the sellers are quite hard to get away from just be firm and say no thanks , they promise you they will get you straight in and you won’t have to wait long we waited in the line for around an hour ,we saw some of the touts taking there groups in and they still had to wait around Half hour ,
The sights were fascinating you wonder how this building was built in the days when there were no proper tools ,
Amazing achievements probably a lot of people died in the making of this magnificent building ,
We paid 12 Euro each to go in and this covered entrance to the adjoining emperors gardens

title: Guided tour
text:
We used the guided tour where you buy the ticket from the guys with the blue bibs, our gide was hilarious (Itallian man, fair hair).
Honestly wished i remembered his name because he made the tour engaging for everyone between 10 & 80yrs. Definatly worth the £35euro

title: An unbelievable experience
text:
We signed up for a guided tour of the colosseum, Forum and Palatine hill with Skip the line tour guides. The lady who guided us through the colosseum was extremely knowledgeable and interesting to listen to.
We were then guided around the Forum by David Battaglino.  This guy provided the most interesting and entertaining tour I’ve ever experienced. His mix of irony and comedy as well as his obvious knowledge of the area, provided us with a most excellent tour. He managed to get the whole of the group to participate in his humour and banter. Not to be missed!!

title: Lovely at night
text: Worth to see it at night, no tourist round it and its lighten up fantasticly. We didnt went inside as we were ther when entry was free and therefore ques were miles long

title: Wow
text: This was absolutely breathtaking! Me and my husband did the tour of the colosseum and it was just amazing! So much history there and with the audio guide it tells you as you go around. The building itself is so beautiful to look at in daylight and night. There are also plenty of little bars and restaurants around it so you are able to have a meal or drink with great views

title: Absolutely Packed...but worth it!
text:
It was a “free day” at the Colosseum so we did not fancy our chances to get in but we were fortunate enough to get in on an informal tour that was very informative and well worth €20 each. The guide spoke very good English and we used head sets so as not to miss anything.
Great tour.....sadly I’m not sure of the name of the company as we just stumbled across the guides and there happened to be 2  places available....lucky us!

title: Spectacular
