text: We had a fantastic experience visiting inside the Colosseum, it takes 2 hours and i suggest to do it with a guide. We did it with a guide and it was very interesting discovering all what  the Roman  3000 years ago were able to do . incredible

title: Colosseum visit
text: A must see in Rome. It is a huge help paying tour guides, as they do have a separate queue. It can take quite long to get in from the normal queue. They are found in abundance there.

title: Amazing
text: It is always nice to see and visit historic places. The Colisée is a must go in Rome. You can almost imagine what was happening here century’s ago.

title: Best of guides
text: Our guide was so knowledgeable. She keep our interest from start to finish, for over three hours. She has researched the history of the Colosseum and was able to tell us about the history. Also explaining what the building were in the Roman Ruins.

title: Do theunderground tour. Most enlightening.
text: Meet at the Colosseum station in a group of 11 and get below in the Colosseum for some new areas unseen before.

title: Incredible place!
text: You will be speechless to see this magnificent place. Go there either early in the morning or at sunset, otherwise you will find very long lines. Wear comfortable shoes for the long walks!

title: Guided Tour
text: Extra money well spend - booked a guided tour through Viator (but what I saw and heard, I think most of the tours are fine. The Colosseum is breathtaking, but to get full experience and the grimm details of gladiator’s life and death, get a tour guide!

title: Great Tour
text: Spent 3 hours in a tour of the Colosseum and the surrounding area.  Great tour, well worth the money.

title: Guided tour with Context Travel
text: Be sure to get there early, once they reach 3,000 people they close entry and then slowly funnel people in as people exit.  Always under restoration but lots to see!  So happy Dimo an archeologist that does walking tours with Context Travel brought us through the Colosseum.  So many hidden stories that aren't told unless you're with someone knowledgable, but even without it's a marvel to view.

title: Must go
text: It's the landmark of Rome. The crowd numbers in the thousands. You should be prepared to buy a combo ticket to enter Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. But we didn't bother to get in as the Roman Forum is more time worthy. View from the outside is enough.

title: Awe inspiring to say the least
text: I've visited the Colosseum twice now and I have to say, this is one of the most awe inspiring places I've ever been. It has a rich history as a part of Rome but it always amazes me to learn that this structure was abandoned and forgotten and only resurrected after hundreds of years.  TAKE A TOUR.  I cannot stress that enough.  Tours get you in the door faster, give you the history and some allow access that you can't get another way. Great place, don't miss it if you're in Rome!

title: Impressive but crowded.
text: Long queues for security and payment. The cashier ignored us for two or three minutes while he counted and made little piles of coins on his desk. Not our money, he was just ignorant!

title: Even in ruins it is majestic
text: We had a great view of the colosseum which was used an auditorium in ancient times... gladiators fighting, animal hunting are some of the cruel acts staged here it seems. Now the marble seats are all stolen but the structure is standing majestic.

title: Iconic
text: Incredibly crowded, but as expected. Did not buy tickets to wait in line and walk around inside. Architecture is amazing.

title: Awesome history
text:
A fantastic piece of history that has to be seen to be believed and appreciated. It will pay to do research before visiting, as the thousands of years since it’s glory leaves a lot to the imagination.  

A tour is a must, with the lines (add the heat) looked like forever. We booked through our hotel desk, and options filled up quickly as we were checking options.  

Our tour was a chaotic experience until we settled in with our guide, being shuffled from point to point, offered different options because the tour was overbooked.  It seems like this is the nature of the business, is a necessary evil of the “industry”.  

As with most places in Rome, beware of pickpockets and the Nygerian “gypsies” who slap bracelets on you for luck, then demand money, as they all have just had a hungry baby.

title: A must go!
text: Definitely do not buy tickets online for this, we booked a tour which got cancelled the same day. We discovered that a tour is not needed, and went there on the day and paid 17 euros each!

title: 4hr guided tour
text:
Couldn’t get the English underground tour and didn’t fancy paying extortionate private prices. Opted for the co-op culture £30 tour of colosseum and forum.
Much depends on the guide you get as I found out at the Vatican.
But here at the colosseum our guide was fantastic. It came to life for me. Wonderful memories.

title: Book Online Beforehand
text:
We booked a package “Best of Rome” ticket through tiqets.com and we couldn’t fault it. Skip the line access included as queues were huge otherwise! 
The colosseum itself is amazing! Such a collosal structure, impressive to see. I’d definitely recommend to see it once in your lifetime

title: Incredible!
text: You can easily spend a whole day here.  The queues are to be expected but this is a truly amazing place to walk around.  Again do not het daughter by the ticket tits outside and their scare mongering tactics,  they claim that they are offering you a free service with tales of queues for 3 hours which yo can beat of yo;pay their over inflated prices.  Do your homework and buy your tickets in line before - we saw a number of people in tour groups who had to join and queue in exactly the same line as we were - there is no golden ticket that gives you instant access.  You all have to go through the same security checks etc - plan you day and enjoy

title: Amazing
text: The Colosseum is a sight to behold. We had skip the line tickets which was great. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable about the Colosseum, There is a lift (elevator) for those who can't climb stairs.

title: Nice trip
text: The colosseum was nice but the street vendors were relentlessly trying to sell us items.  They would place them in your hand without permission and then ask for payment. My 10 year daughter ended up very scared.

title: Clarification for Roma Pass holders
text:
The information on official websites will tell you that, if you want to visit the Colosseum using your Roma Pass, you HAVE to pre-book a time-slot to visit - you cannot simply turn up and get in.

This is false.  We arrived just before the opening time (0830) 2 days ago, and after only 20 mins or so queuing got in for free using our Roma Passes.

No need to pre-book online, no need to pay extra to 'skip the line' touts, just wake up early and get in line before the doors open.

title: Take the Gladiator Tour!!!!!
text: Our tour was the No wait in Line Gladiator tour and a girl named Maria was our tour guide. She was so passionate about this tour I actually felt like I could see the Gladiator games. So much good information and fun!!!

title: Rome Colosseum Tours
text: If you are interested on taking a tour I would highly suggest a tour guide. You can find these tours online in various languages. We personally enjoyed the Roman Guy tours which they also have a tour for the Vatican. The tours allow you to skip the lines which trust me is well worth it as many folks come out to see these wonders. Now definetely bring some water and do not buy them from the street vendors as they find many empty plastic bottles and fill them with Tap water and seal them with some glue to make it appear as though its a new bottle. Additionally do not talk to anyone trying to sell you something as they will not leave you alone until you buy something and then if you do they want more monies. Another thing you will see are many folks asking for money be careful as many are con artists and do not accept any jewelry or flowers. Lastly watch out for pick-pockets. Avoiding all these things you will enjoy your tour.

title: Walking through history
text: Marcello was so great.  He was informative and answered all our questions so patiently - even though I am sure he's done so thousands of times.  He guided us through the ups and downs of the Colloseum realising that some people are older and not quite as agile as others.  He showed us how things would have looked in Roman times and made the remains of the Forum come alive.  The views he showed to us of the Palatine Hill were well worth the climb.  He was an amzing guide.

title: Nighttime pictures
text: Don’t be too shy to find the coliseum at night.  It is beautifully lit and will have a few around it.

title: Too Much HISTORY....
text:
For Historian buffs & children. We did a walking tour of 3hrs which included the Forum & the Colosseum. Without a guide you will be lost & will not understand the importance & significance of this majestic place.

We entered through the gladiator entrance, this allowed us to be more close & understand how the colosseum worked. 

The Forum definitely needs a guided tour, we found it interesting. However, were too tired after 3hrs of constant standing & walking.

This tour is recommended.

title: Be prepared for crowds, security and lines
text: Once you get through the entry lines and security, indeed the magnitude of size is amazing. It’s a bucket list thing you just must do in Rome.

title: Nice place
text: Very beautiful place, but many people around of it. I haven't been inside of it yet, only outside. I come back. ♥️

title: Amazing - good to see with your own eyes
text: This was our one “must-do” attraction to see during our Rome stopover and we weren’t disappointed.  Absolutely astounding construction and it was very interesting to take a 3 hour tour (booked via TripAdvisor) and to hear all about the history. Very well organized tour with a great guide.

title: Amazing
text: A sight to behold even from the outside. Just be wary or all the vendors around trying to sell you tours or other goods and the "roman" soldiers offering to take pictures with you then afterwards telling you exactly how much they expect you to pay!! Metro station is also right across so very convenient

title: Massive
text:
We daily passed from Colosseum while in Rome for a cooking class and we often stopped to admire this wonderful and huge landmark, which Rome is known all over the world for.
We visited the interior few months ago, but I thought the hours waiting in line were not worth it, as you do not see anything special inside and there could be more descriptions.
Despite that, this remains a wonderful attraction, whether you want to wait in line is up to you though.

title: A bit confusing
text: The information given in the Colosseum Tour was great, BUT...the entire area is very confusing to follow. title: Spectacular Site!
text: This is a must if you are in Rome, that should go without saying of course but never know. title: Massively Underwhelming...but you go to check if off the list, I guess
text:
The most impressive view of the Colosseum is from outside, and from other view points around the city. 

We chose to be part of a larger tour group with Skip the Line access (which you get anytime you take a tour and don't just show up). The structure itself was ok to walk through but completely underwhelming considering the tourism hype that surrounds it. It's old. It's massive. It's a circle with a lot of restoration work being done and lots of other tourists just following along with the massive hoard of people inside. 

The experience might be better during the off-season, if there are less crowds, but for me, it was not worth paying for the tour to see and definitely would not have been worth standing in line for 2 hours to get a ticket on the spot to walk around by ourselves without a tour. I highly recommend only seeing it from the outside and if scheduling a refundable tour later during your trip. If you feel after a few days that you still really want to see the inside, then take the tour. If not, cancel the tour.

title: AMAZING!!
text: The Colosseum is an AMAZING structure, built in eight years from 72-80 A.D. The architectual genius of the Romans is hard to comprehend but at the same time tempered by the use of this structure for gladiatorial fights, slautering animals and murdering Christians. We tried to enter the Colosseum around 1pm but the line was too long so we walked around the area and had a late lunch.. the nearby Crab restaurant was excellent. Around 4pm we went back to the Colosseum and we purchased a ticket and walked right in (thanks Rick Steves). There is an elevator at the far end of the Colosseum to allow elderly/disabled individuals to reach the upper level. You can also sign up for a guided tour where a guide will provide you detailed history of the Colosseum and the Forum. You can have your picture taken with a Roman Centurion (for 10 Euros) and there are many touristy gift carts with all kinds of souvenirs. Get a comfortable pair of walking shoes and be careful of pick pockets...

title: No lions in sight
text: While the rough and tumble of the Colosseum's purpose has changed, it is very easy to visualise just how the place functioned. A lot of refurbishment and reconstruction has taken place in recent years and, while to purists this may be somewhat vulgar, the need to undertake such work is clearly evidenced by the state of the stonework yet to be stabilised. This is a very popular visitor site and the walk-up queues were hours long. So a word of warning - book a tour that can be arranged through the several bus lines offering "hop-on-hop-off" services around Rome. Many of these start at the Roma Termini Station. And wear sensible shoes: the gladiators didn't wear high heels!

text:

They will all ask for proof of your tickets as if they work there but then proceed to try and sell you a ‘better’ tour.

title: How we bought tickets for the Colosseum
text:
We recently went to the Colosseum in May 2019 and would like to share how we bought tickets, and hope it helps others! We wanted to be flexible with our schedule so we didn't purchase the tickets online, and instead bought them on site.

8:25 AM – From the Colosseo metro, walked south past the Arch of Constantine and arrived at the Palatine Hill ticket office. It opens from 8:30 AM to 1 PM, and is a brick building on Via di San Gregorio, 30, 00186 Roma RM (use the coordinates 41.887859,12.489696 on Google map, and you'll see it). There were around 15 people in front of us waiting in line. 

8:40 AM – Passed security, we went through the entrance door and on the left about 20 feet, there's the ticket office. For 12 Euro, we got the COMBO TICKET that gives you a timed entry to the Colosseum (our TIMED entry was 9:20 AM on the SAME DAY; you don't get to choose the time, they just give you the time), and an entry to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill which is good for 48 hours. So we went the Colosseum first, and then afterwards, went the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill all in 1 day. 

8:45 AM – Walked from the Palatine Hill Ticket office to the Colosseum. 

8:55 AM – Arrived at the Colosseum. There are a bunch of different lines. We showed our ticket and was told to wait until 10 MINUTES BEFORE ENTRY time to get into the line. So we waited a few feet away from the line. 

9:10 AM –  Entered the line (a line specific for ticket holders). There was another line for people who didn't have tickets and wanted to buy tickets at the Colosseum which was very long. 

10:30 AM – Entered the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill (entrance is across from the Colosseum, only a few minutes walk). 

1:30 PM – Done!

title: Go with a tour group
text:
If you are going there just to take some photos then you don’t need a tour. If you actually want to learn something there are some audio tours you can take but you’ll have a much better experience with one of the tour groups. 

Go in the morning if you are there during June through August.

title: 50th celebrations
text:
We bought the tickets before we arrived, through an organised tour company, this was a great idea. 
Not only did we have an agreed meeting point, time slot but also a personnel guide, for the four of us. 
The guide also had the contacts to be able to get to the front of the queue and zip through all the checks. The guide was extremely knowledgable and engineered a visit below the colosseum to the location where the gladiators and animals where held before the events. At times it’s rather a strange feeling to be walking in the footsteps of ancient Romes, which gives you shivers in the darkness of the underground. 
The history of the colosseum and the reasons why it was build was fascinating, also the reason why it is peppered with holes is amazing, and something  that you’ll be able to ask your guide when you visit. 
Some much to see and read a truly worth while tour, a must for anyone visiting Rome.

title: spend time here just walking around
text:
I did the tour but then stayed back to look around the place. You can get in easily with a tour group, but they will only show you about 20%. I got a audio guide and then explored the place myself.. Lots of stuff to see on the 2nd floor where there is a sort of museum being set up. worth spending about 2-3 hours here if you can. 

I recommend doing the gladiators entrance and the moonlight tours. have to book early. 

The bookshop was quite good too.. reasonable prices given the touristy nature.

Lots of peddlers and beggars outside. Pickpockets too.. usual precautions important. 

DO not accept roses/ free gifts etc from the vendors.. they will demand money as soon as you take them.

Our guide claimed the water bottles are resealed with tap water and are stored in the drains at night. I saw one of these water bottles and it looked quite original and clean, but the price is marked up compared to the supermarkets. I think its probably quite safe to buy the water if you need to.

title: Worth a visit
text: Lots to see, guided tour is best as they highlight all the points of interest and elaborate on the history of the building.  It's fantastic that it has stood for so long.  Wear comfy shoes!

title: Incredible
text: If you appreciate history, you need to see/experience the Colosseum.  Understanding what took place during the 750 year reign of the ROMAN civilization and the shorter period of the Coloseum gives interesting perspective to current times.  The NFL is our modern day Colosseum and gladiators in the U.S.  Enterntainment for the masses, just like the Romans.

title: Fabulous history
text: We bought our tickets online, son sorted them as we were sick of all the tours bothering us outside. This place is not to be missed

title: Brilliant
text: Excellent and a must visit on a trip to Rome. Inside there is lots that has been preserved and it’s great to look around. The queue can be long outside and waiting times can take a while. Would highly recommend getting a tour, which includes a skip the line pass. This cost 20 euro on top of your 12 euro ticket each from tour guides outside. However, you save so much time and the tours are very informative and improve the visit. This also includes your ticket and tour for Palantine Hill, next to the Colosseum, which is also an excellent place to visit and full of history. Both tours last around an hour, but you have around 45 minutes extra after in the Colosseum before meeting for Palantine Hill. You can stay in there afterwards for as long as you like.

title: Eternal Rome in the Colosseum
text:
I returned to the iconic symbol of Imperial Rome after a decade with a deeper understanding of the amphitheater this time round.

Reading bedtime story books of the Gladiators on the “thumbs up, thumbs down” and “we are about to die salute you” details sure helped. And of course the masterpiece Gladiator film made him wanted to see it even more. 

I happened to stand right by Russell Crowe at the Mandarin Oriental in HK while waiting for our cars. No one had recognised him while he stood there smoking with his agent. Just another flabby Westerner. There was no trace of our hero in the film.

It was exciting for my boy to walk around and see the ancient site right in front of his eyes. Together we visualised how it played out with the animals and gladiators who fought in the arena while the Romans cheered on. And we paid attention to the underground trap doors where the hungry beasts and the slaves were once kept. I also pointed out the metal cross above the arena set up there for commemorating the Christians who suffered and died in the Colosseum.

We visited in the early morning before the crowd arrived. It is advisable to buy tickets online ahead of time.

Next time if we come again we must do the reserved Colosseum Underground and Palatine Hill tour for sure. 

Ave, Imperator, morituri te salutant!

title: Fantastic!
text: Awe inspiring building that has endured for thousands of years. A must visit attraction when visiting Rome.

title: Night Tour
text: We did the night tour. Great way to see this. Less crowds and cool weather and our tour guide was great.

title: Excellent
text: Must-see place for sure. It impresses a lot. Not so many people at once. Also, it’s better to buy tickets online with for the specific time, otherwise the queues are huuuuge.

title: Mmmmmm!
text: Interesting - not sure it's worth the money having a guide - go early to avoid the crowds - it's a tick in the box but I honestly didn't rate it that much!

title: Are you not Entertained
text: How can you not be when you're imagining what went on here. I can't describe the feelings I was experiencing as I walked through this amazing cultural Icon. All the times I have seen this place on TV, in movies and in pictures, never thinking I'd ever get the opportunity to be there in person. I would advise having your visit prearranged with a tour guide, in order to avoid the impossibly long lie ups and of course for the commentary. Take an umbrella and rain coat just in case. The day I visited was pouring, but it didn't dampen the enthusiasm or significance. If you forget, don't worry you can purchase off any of the multitude of street vendors that are everywhere in Italy. It's actually fun bargaining with them.

title: Truly a sight you need to see
text: I never imagined how massive the structure really is! Then to learn that only part of the original building still remains, truly a masterpiece!

title: Colosseum tour
text:
Vasco was our tour guide, he was the BEST, not only because his knowledge but he made sure everyone participate, he made it fun. 
I wish he was our tour guide everywhere we go. Thank you Vasco, it was a tour we will always remember

title: More Crowds than a Black Friday Sale
text:
Second time here and it is just too crowded and rushed to enjoy.  I was with a tour, ‘Walks of Italy’ and even they were being rushed by other tour guides.  Everyone is trying to push their way through for the next photo or attraction.

To be honest they should limit the number of tourists at any one time or charge more for a VIP experience with sectioned off areas only for VIP. 

The inside is good if you haven’t seen it before, but to be honest the outside is where all the best photos are so you could save yourself the money and just walk around it for free and take hundreds of amazing photos day or night.

title: So much amazing history
text: Visited the Rome Colosseum last week. What an amazing structure and the history that goes with it. We decided to take a tour to get more information then just trying to walk around by ourselves. Our tour guide was knowledgeable and kind and truly knew the history of the Colosseum and shared that with us. Our tour lasted about three hours but also included the Roman forum. Be prepared  for hot weather and be ready to climb a lot of stairs while you were touring the Colosseum. A Camera is a must because there are many photo ops available.

title: Very cool to see!
text: Really nice to visit this! You wil feel very small while visiting this. I would try to go either when it is not super hot, or morning/end day.

title: Beyond expectations!!
text: Not sure a world famous attraction needs a review but it is worth mentioning the guided tour. We opted for this and chose the one with access to the stage, underground and upper level. It was reasonably priced and money well spent. Worth every penny if you choose to visit. Cheers

title: Crazy busy but a must see
text: Ok the Colosseum is one of those places you gotta go, we had skip the line tickets which was €11 more for the two of us that to buy the normal ticket and hire the video guide, worthwhile in my book as we otherwise would have to wait probably over 1.5 hours in the normal line. Tons of people try to sell you guided tours which cost about €30-35 depending which company you go with. Probably also not a bad idea as you also don't have to wait. Standard entry is €12 and the video guide €6.

title: interesting
text: very interesting place. lots of  ongoing restoration.. the history is great. crowds are huge but the overall experience is great.

title: A must see
text: Don’t bother buying beat the queue or tours , they will rip you off. We queued for about 20 mins on a very busy day and the price at the desk was only about €12 each and teenage daughter was free. One tip is to take some ID for teenagers to prove age as they are free. we didn’t know and so I offered to pay an adult ticket for her , but the guy on the desk said no and honoured the free ticket for her.  The place is swarming with people trying to sell you  fast track tickets and bits of jewellery etc, it gets annoying after a while because it is constant.

title: Awesome historical venue
text: Save yourselves the aggravation of the very long lines to buy ticket and then to get in, pre-purchase a skip the line tour online. That said be mindful that many tour companies claim to get you in at a certain time but that is only if they have enough people in their group. for instance we bought skip the line tour for 11:00am, when we arrived at the meeting point we were told that our group was pushed back to 12:00PM because of delays...lies.. we only had 6 people in our group and it supposed to be 12, so the delay was just an excuse in order for the tour operator to find people onsite who were interested to get into the Coloseum. we did not leave until they had their 12 people. while waiting we witnessed the same from other tour operators that were there. so be prepared to wait awhile if your group is not all together. once in the venue it was awesome & beautiful.

title: The Colosseum is great but pick your guided tour wiht care. Promise of fast track queue is a swizz
text: The monument is staggering. There are lots of people but nce you get inside not too bad. Some interesting exbits showing how the place looked and was used all that tie ago. HOWEVER, there are plenty of people touting for Jump the Queue Guided Tours but beware. It is not fifteen mins it was more like 30 mins and remember all the while the people who run the budiling itself restrict entry when visitor numbers are deemed unsafe at which point the guides just shrug thier hsoulders and say its beyond thier control. And I have to say our particular guide was crap. We handed in our headphoned guides and went in by ourselves to see around the place. Overall, worth a visit but remember whatever way you pay there will be delays.

title: Impressive
text: Paid for a skip the line guided tour (including palatine hill and roman forum), found both the inside and outside of the Colosseum stunning and impressive. You have to go through security to get in so it's worth checking what you can/can't take inside. Recommend booking tickets in advance or getting your ticket at palatine hill as there's shorter queues. Also re-visited and viewed from the outside at night for some stunning photos of it lit up.

title: Disappointed visit
text: We were disappointed with our visit to the colosseum, luckily we didn't have to wait in line since we pre booked our tickets because it wasn't worth the wait. The building is impressive from the outside but the inside was in renovation and crowded with tourist. All unavoidable but made our visit disappointing.

title: Refreshing your Gladiator Instinct
text: Most thrilling of the city's ancient sights. The structure is magnificent. Its under renovation (i.e Ground Level)  when I visited the place in June 2019.  You can buy a combined ticket for Colosseum , Roman Forum and Palatine Hill which is valid for 2 days.

title: Be careful when you book with Headouts Eome
text: wel. In Rome if you want to skip lies at Colyseum or Musei vaticani you have to be the prey of the zillions of agencies that sell sip the line  tickets.you just have to look for the cheapes. all of them oofer thes ame services.that is a metting point and a transfer to the entries fo the 2 monuments.We acrually had a big problem with Headout Rome as the meeting point they advertise in internet is not correct.Luckily enough there was a telephone number numer if their booking confirmation,So I could call and learn where the real meeting point was,after that, we got the usual service.Skip the line is necessary in Rome.. Many times the access to Colyseum and Vatican will be denied when there are too many people inside.pls note that while access to Sr.Peter 's church is free,soemtimes acess is denied for religious ceremonies.. and in any case lines are long as there are security checking point s,.

title: A Must see in Rome AMAZING !!!!
text:
Just spent 4 days in Rome and visited the Colliseum. One word was BREATHTAKING ...from seeing this in photos and depcited in films , nothing could prepare you for the vastness and intricacy of the building which was built amazingy 1000's of years ago.

You will need to book a time slot to have access to the Colliseum , the office is near by where you can do this , we bought an omina card initially before we went , which gave us queue jump tickets to a couple of attractions , but for Colliseum you had to go to the ticket office if there were no on line slots available.

It was very hot at 90 degrees , so although you have a pre booked time slot , yiu still gave to queue due to volume of tourists . We had our slot for 12.25. Started queuing at 12ish and eventually gained access at 1.15.Make sure you have plenty of water and take an umbrella for the shade . You can but cute little Japanese ones there for about €5 .

There are 2 floors to the Colliseum ...its very thought provoking to imaging how many events the Romans had there.There is also a lift which takes you to the top of the Colliseum if your are less able , it's also very wheelchair friendly. 

Stay away from the toilets if you can , they need improving ALOT. There are only 1 set of toilets considering the 1000's of visitors .

They offer guided tours , audio tours or yiu can just do your own thing and wander at yiu leisure .There's lots of facts up by the artifacts which informs you of all the history.

A great place to go , a must see.

title: Amazing
text: We went IN with a tour group but then went on our own to look for about an hour and a half. We had the option to join back up with our group at a meeting place and time for a tour of another place, but we opted out. I would highly recommend entering the Colosseum this way because we skipped a MASSIVE line in the sun and heat.

title: Great tour
text: Used one of the outside tour guides, they are cheaper and do get you in quicker, we used gladiator tours, our guide was very knowledgeable and had good english, we had loads of time for photos he gave us a loads of breaks, well worth seeing

title: It is a ‘must do’
text: We pre-booked a tour which took in the arena, underground and 3rd level - for me defiantly worth the money as you got to see so many different areas - with smaller crowds. Going to the underground area certainly gave a sense of the hell it must have been to have worked (or been forced to work) in that area. Truly an astonishing building. Our guide was great, allowing plenty of time for pictures before we moved on to the next area.

title: Try a night tour
text: Best to try a night tour, less crowded, less hot, nice views. And you can you hear your tour guide if you have one.

title: interesting visit
text:
it is not enough to simply view the Colosseum from the outside. you need to be inside to enjoy and wow at the place. 
however, you do not need to book a tour that takes you to levels that are otherwise not accessible to the public unless you love history and want to find out more. There are already enough exhibits and display on the 2nd level to drown you with information.
audio guides are also available if you are keen. please book in advance.
For a great photo shot, hop on to the opposite side, where the roman forum is. it gives you a good view of the whole Colosseum without the tourists at the background.

title: Wow!
text: Well worth the wait, but be advised that if you are a woman and need the rest room, you may wait over half an hour to get there. Other than that, it is fascinating and evocative of Ancient Rome.

title: A bit underwhelming and disappointing
text:
We bought Skip the Line tickets on Head out for €17 each. This didn’t include a tour, perhaps this is where we went wrong..
We arrived at 10:15 and entered the colosseum at 10:55. The wait was for security only.
We spent around an hour here as there really wasn’t much to occupy us.
I can only recall about 3 or 4 information sections on the history of the colosseum. 
There was no public museum of any kind either which is unusual for a point of interest within Europe. It was very crowded and very hot too making it not  pleasurable to walk around .  
The shop was a size of post stamp and really didn’t have many souvenirs .. 
The theatre was also undergoing restoration on the ground floor.. 

I think €17 per person is expensive , at least an Audio guide should be included in the price . 

As for outside the colosseum..the lack of police presence is appalling . 
We were constantly being harassed by ticket street sellers who proceeded to follow and continue to shout at us despite saying “no thank you, we have tickets” 
We were trying to cross the traffic light controlled crossing to be met with about 5 ticket sellers blocking our pathway so we had to dodge and dive to carry onto the entrance . 
I felt sorry for the genuine workers at the colosseum who were trying to organise the crowds but people were just ignoring them because they thought they were street sellers .
The amount of times I heard “ I am not trying to sell you a ticket , I work here” was just ridiculous . 
We were in wrong queue twice as it was pretty chaotic  ! 

I’m bittersweet about the colosseum, I think I’d rather visit in the winter time and take a tour guide .

title: Evening tour with underground access
text:
Difficulty obtaining tickets due to limited availability was the only downside to this tour.
Met by tour operator taken to entrance of Colosseum and guided through security. Then met by a member of staff who conducted the tour. Limited to small groups and given access to areas of Colosseum that you don't get during the day. Would highly recommend this tour. The guide was very knowledgeable and interesting. The evening tour meant the temperatures were much more pleasant to walk about than doing the tour in the higher temperatures of the afternoon.

title: Ancient wonder, bucket list item
text: Went in late afternoon with a skip the line and underground tour add on. No line at all for skip the line this time of day. Underground tour was interesting don't expect to be walking all over, the access is fairly limited due to conditions.

title: Wonderful Building
text: Wonderful Building, one of the reason to visit Rome during your visit Italy. To skip the line, better pay the guide (imho)

title: Another place to see
text: At least see the colosseum when you go to Italy. There are so much history to it. its one of the structures where the ancient roman games are held. the built of the structure compared to size is amazing. mind you, you have to track long lines to see the inside.

title: A piece of history
text:
Although not exactly the way it looks from the glory days. The colosseum still is a must see for Rome. Can u image building this way back in the day? I think the guide said it took like 80 years. 

Join a tour if u ask me, the guide will give u a background lesson about the colosseum

title: Gotta see it!
text:
We (mom and 2 college age daughters) visited the Colosseum on a Thursday morning in late May. We had arranged for a private guide as this was the start of our Italy trip and wanted to have an professional show us this magnificent historical sight.
I am so glad we spent the extra $$ for the guide. Our guide, Luigi, was extremely informative and told us details and history that we never could have discovered on our own. It was very interesting and awe-inspiring.

title: Great trip
text: Went here 30 years ago and can not believe how much it has changed from a recovery point.   The crowds as huge but credit to the venue they are very good at moving people through safely. Best to pre book a tour I suspect these days

title: Very interesting and beautiful
text: The experience was very interesting. It is good to go to a guide (there are organized groups) in order to find more about its history.

title: One of most popular place of city tour in Rome
text: The Coosseum is closed at 6pm for visitor but opened until 9pm for group, but you must buy group ticket and pay for guide. Actually, I not so understand why they offer like this. But it is nice with a romantic picture at sunset.

title: Remarkable
text: Absolutely amazing structure. We saw it during the day when it was busy, but it is beautiful at night when it is lit up and quieter.

title: Very Impressive
text: Booked a guided tour with Headout. Definitely recommend booking a tour as there is a lot to learn, and you get to skip the queue which is very important, as you could be queuing for hours. By booking a tour you get to go on a private platform only available to people on tours. This is perfect for an uninterrupted view of the colosseum. Did not do the underground tour, however, was told by a walking tour guide that this is a waste of money and time anyway!

title: You have to visit but dont expect much
text:
It was part of our rome tour to visit the Colosseum. I recommend going to it with a tour guide because:

1- you will enter fast
2- without the history of the place you will be lost

In summary: let it be a part of other tour but don't expect much.

title: GREAT DAY , GREAT GUIDE
text:
We booked the Colesseum and Roman Forum in advance through Viator.
Meeting point easy to find and good explanation on how the next 3/4 hours would be spent.
Headphones supplied were perfect, which ensured we didn't miss any of the ample comments the guide provided.
We were more impressed with the Roman Forum than the Colosseum but both are fantastic.
Trip was madee by our guide. We had Fabrizio Bianco. I hope this review gets back to him as he was fantastic for the full 4 hours.

title: Awesome piece of history
text: Probably not much to say that hasn't already been said...it's huge, awe-inspiring and a must-see, but probably not worth spending too much time or money on. Very busy, and lots of construction work going on which spoils the view - but the renovation work will hopefully preserve the sight (and site) for many years to come.

title: Beautiful
text: We went to the colosseum on a walking tour.  I think the teenagers would have enjoyed discovering the area without being attached to someone.  The actual site a truly a historical treasure.

title: The new updates are amazing
text: Bring water bottles and comfortable shoes. We enjoyed the tour with the 7 Hills tour company. We had lunch at the Aroma restaurant which we highly recommend. It has views of the Colosseum.

title: Awe inspiring
text: We did the Gladiator Gate tour with Viator which was really good. Walking out on to the arena floor was awe inspiring, the guide was very knowledgeable and the wireless headsets made it easy to hear and keep up while being able to pause when we wanted to

title: An absolute must!
text: We went in January in the morning and bought the queue jump ticket after reading the reviews before arriving. The queues looked about 20 minutes long so I can imagine how crucial the queue jump tickets are at peak times. The colosseum is as magnificent as you’d expect, with museums and history everywhere you look!  Of course there will be lots of people with cameras taking photos, and be wary of the people selling trinkets and bracelets outside, but aside from that an amazing experience

title: Majestic
text: Majestic..Memorable..mind-blowing. It was our best experience in Rome. Just to think about all the history we walked through in those 45 minutes of conducted tour (take the official tour from Coop..its cheaper and good)..every minute and cent was worth. My daughter was just sucked into the history of that place. I do not have to say..MUST VISIT :D;)

title: Amazing history
text:
Amazing piece of history 
Gives a snapshot of the times in the Roman era and the spectacular construction 
The kids really enjoyed walking around and learning the stories of its construction and the gladiators

title: Scam!!!!
text: We payed 120 Euros for a two part tour. After the first portion of our tour we were instructed to make our way back to the starting point which was about half a mile away to meet with a new guide what we've never meet before. After going on a scavenger hunt looking for this company for over an hour we finally gave up. All they gave us for proof of purchase was a green sticker to put on your shirt that eventually flew off. Good thing i took a picture of it as i was afraid that would happen. This is an absolute scam. Such a beautiful place tarnished by money hungry skum. I will never come back here again. Beware!!!

title: Great Rome experience - must do !!
text: As per reviews, please ensure you buy a skip-the-line pass or enter with a reputable tour provider. Some people were waiting up to 3hrs in hot sun for tickets.  Skip the line tours usually result in entry within or around about an hour,  Please also keep in mind there are a lot of steps and heaps of people. Especially during the day, this may affect your experience.  If going please keep in mind this is one of the most visited tourist destinations on earth. Overall a great experience and once inside you can really imagine Gladiator battles and how life was like in Ancient Rome. I would recommend going with a tour group as the knowledge and narration adds to the experience.  There are security gates with scanning machines upon entry so ensure everything is stowed in a small backpack prior to entry. This is a worthwhile experience. Rome gets hot in summer, so ensure you are adequately hydrated to take on the line rides and rude pushy tourists !! Enjoy

title: Rome in summer
text:
Very nice to see such place ,  war , history and  gladiators .
Problem was too difficult to obtain an access even with fast track ticket .
Dont trust the offices outside .

title: CsabAti Hungray
text: A monumental building that recalls the old world. The area around the building is provided with helpful, dear colleagues.

title: Wow! Wow! Wow!
text:
The Colosseum far exceeded our expectations! We booked a private guided tour which included Palatine Hill, The Forum, an underground tour, the arena and all levels of the Colosseum. We were able to enjoy stunning private views at the top as well as the insightful history shared by the tour guide.

We visited in June, so it was insanely hot! I would recommend wearing cool clothing, a cap and keep hydrated throughout the tour

title: One of the world's best features
text: We booked this trip with I love Rome bus company and found it excellent. It was very well organized and the guide and driver were the best. We have been touring Italy and Malta for 5 weeks and this trip ranked at the top. Make sure you pre-book with I ❤ Rome

title: 80AD
text:
No trip to Rome is complete without a visit to the colosseum. The truly remarkable age of the building coupled with the history is something most of us cannot really comprehend. A structure that has stood for 1,939 years.....

It has stood the test of time due to its amazing engineering. They used
metal rods to keep the structure together, when at some time (think 14 or 15 century) they removed the rods on one side. An earthquake occurred and where the rods were removed it collapsed.  

We did a guided tour but in truth other that getting us away from the annoying street sellers told me no more that a guide book. Get skip the trip with audio or read a guide book. Don’t waste your money. (Vatican definitely needs a guide!)

Toilets are awful so go before if possible. Take water with you and expect the staff to be rude and unfriendly. 

Book tickets in advance....

title: Tour was worth it!
text: We walked to the colloseum with no tickets bought and simply talked to the numerous informative staff (the ones with STAFF on their shirts, these are informative guides who do not ask you for money!) we took a tour for €40 which brought us through the gladiators entrance (no que at all) and throughthe ground and first floors of the stadium. Our guide information was very interesting. This tour also got us a guide around the roman forum and the pallentine Hill. The sun from 11-2 in June was extremely hot especially for two pasty Irish people!

title: AMAZING HISTORICAL SITE - HAWKERS PARADISE
text:
The Colosseum is a magnificent structure.  Quite awe inspiring in fact.
We viewed on a day in early June.
Surrounded by dozens and dozens probably hundreds of hawkers trying to flog all manner of things like roses, bottled water, tickets, hats, fridge magnets, toys, key rings, umbrellas, you name it they had it.  Serial pests....they just would not stop harassing people from everywhere surrounding the Colosseum.  They quite ruined the experienced.  
The crowds of visitors everywhere around the Colosseum were horrendous.  Lines 5 deep stretching hundreds of metres curling around every section.  People (determined to stop others from viewing and moving along), taking selfies everywhere.  Did I mention the longggggggg queues?
Best time to view early morning and late evening.  Forget anytime after 9 and before 7pm during the warmer months.

title: Good place with Audio guide
text:
We visited here with kids. A bit rude staff and Long queue for security checking. But easy to navigate once you are inside. 
Will recommend to get Audio guide which is €5.5 per person. It takes about an hour for the whole tour. Kids were impressed with the place and history which lasted 2000 years. Recommended to must visit in Rome.

title: Really Cool
text: So amazing to visit this place that was built so long ago.  We loved learning the history behind it and what happened to it over the years.  Our suggestion is to go first thing in the morning and avoid it on the weekend.

title: History and loads of it
text:
Amazing. I think best enjoyed by having a tour where you learn more than wandering  will acheive. Goes without saying..
Its high on the bucket list of things to be seen

title: Walk about
text: Beautiful park-like setting just away from the colosseum.  There are crowds,of course, but the ruins are large and you are not shoulder to shoulder.  Plan for at least a full half day on the Colosseum alone

title: Rome tour will be incomplete without the visit of Colosseum
text:
As you enter the Colosseum, you will be spellbound to see the huge structure, its architecture and at the same time you will be horrified to know the purpose of the Colosseum from the guide, the colossal loss of lives during its construction and thereafter.
I will suggest go for skip the line tickets to save time. Otherwise, whole day may be spent to visit this one place.

title: Viator tour with Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
text: The Colosseum is not to be missed and given the crowds, you definitely want to book ahead with a skip-the-line tour. We had a great experience with Viator and our guide, Paolo. The tour was informative, and he was terrific (especially given the blistering heat). While we enjoyed all of the history, the Colosseum is definitely the standout here. If you don’t have a history buff, you might just want to do the Colosseum and skip the rest.

title: Elevator
text: As a Wheelchairuser I was suprised and happy that Colosseum had a lift. We had a very nice tour there!

title: Amazing!
text: Spent a few hours here. Bought tickets online the day before - all a bit confusing as there are so many different options. We bought a ticket for the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine. The we warned about the queues - even if you buy a Skip the line ticket. Outside you have to fend off people trying to sell you stuff you don’t want/need which gets a bit wearing but press on. One of our party has a disability and due to that we could skip the queue and the staff there were very helpful. The place was heaving with people, which isn’t surprising! Loads of people having guided tours clogging up the place - but it is the Colosseum so you have to put up with it. ANYWAY the Colosseum is amazing - worth giddy by through the crowds for. Try to go on a cooler day as it was boiling when we went - phew!

title: Majestic and epic but beware
text: There will be pushy street sellers and scammers, just say a firm NO, and you will be ok. Also make sure you are always keeping a hand in your wallet  so that a pick-pocket doesn't catch you off guard

title: Mercedes and tour were fantastic!!
text: What a great tour!  Mercedes knowledge was amazing!  We packed so much in throughout the tour and were able to see everything.  Thank you for a great time!!

title: One of those places
text: It's so well known and so historic that this was an essential part of my visit to Rome. I loved it. To stand in a space where so much has taken place was quite a privilege. The story of the colosseum tells you a lot about how ancient Roman society was organised. Prepare yourself for a barrage of people who want to sell tours, tickets, water, umbrellas and other bits and pieces.

title: Wonderful but ruined
text: This is my second trip to the Colosseum and I can't help but be wowed by it. The thing that ruins it though, along with every tourist spot in Rome are the people trying to sell you fast track tickets, water, hats and the worst of them all.. the guys who trick you into putting your hand out for them to slap a bracket on it. I had already bought my tickets online and was hoping for a stress free day but found myself getting really frustrated and angry with the constant questions and offerings whilst we waited 45minutes for our allocated time slot. So much so I almost walked away from the place being so put off by the people around me. Even though i decided to stay and went in, it was only a quick walk around it and then I left feeling annoyed from the constant badgering. After we left more badgering came our way from a guy trying to sell fast track tickets and then we had a bunch of African men jump from a wall trying to ask us where we were from and as we carried on walking 2 of them followed us with one man grabbing my partner's arm. This sort of thing massively ruins Rome. It's such a shame because it's such a beautiful place with such amazing history and it is just being ruined by this activity that goes on around these tourist hotspots.

title: This is a circus.
text:
I bought tickets online ahead of time for my family of 12 adults and 3 infants.  I paid for tickets for the infants at the 18-25 rate so that I wouldn't have to stand in line to show the infants to the ticket counter for free infant tickets.
It didn't help that it was 34C / 94F that day, but when I stood in line in the sun to collect my tickets, the agent wouldn't release the 3 infant tickets without seeing the infants.  So I had to leave the line and go get the babies.  Took an extra 45 minutes.
The exterior area of the Colosseum is a zoo.  Hustlers, street vendors, hordes of tourists and long lines...combine that with virtually no shade and high temperatures, and you have a destination with little enjoyment.  
While I appreciated the historic value of the Colosseum, I did not enjoy the visit and will likely never return unless it is during winter.
I enjoyed the walk through the Forum much more.   
The Colosseum is not a destination to which you want to take infants during summer.

title: A treat to see
text: This was great too see one of those historic sites you hear about, see in movies and learn about and all of a sudden there it is for you to take in!

title: A treat!
text: Went here three days ago for the second time still a treat! Go with a guide tour to get in  couple hours before the rest of the visitors!!

title: it is a must
text: a full immersion in the history, it was an exiting experience. I suggest to read the history of Rome before visiting the Colosseum. it takes minimum 2 hours

title: Truly Spectacular - especially at night when lit
text: There were quite extensive renovations and shoring-up works when we were there ... some just normal maintenance, and some due to the vibrations from the digging of the new Metro line.  We decided not to go inside, but rather admire the splendor from outside ... and it is amazing, both for the design and the size of it.  even better was when we came back at night to see it lit, especially since a full moon was rising over it at the time. It is, of course, a must see in Rome - for us, we wanted to experience the grandeur (that we took in from the exterior)... I guess going inside is a personal choice, cost versus queues versus the amount of time you have, and also the interest in the history.

title: Small country
text:
We walk to the Vatican City, join our pre booked tour. Very detailed tour by a passionate tour guide. So many tourists make walking very slow.
Impressive and a must to see. Comply re dress code.

title: Mixed Feelings
text: When you travel half way around the world, you feel like you need to make sure you see the Colosseum.  We took the underground tour thinking it would be a great small group tour.  Honestly, our tour guide, Amid< was great but when we got to the Colosseum, their tour guide had to take over.  It was 100 degrees (38c) and it was extremely crowded.  The Colosseum guide was so slow and repeated herself so many times, we almost left the tour.  She could have finished her tour in half the time.  That would have given us more time in the other stops.  If you really want to see the Colosseum, I suggest downloading a walking tour and doing it yourself.  You can decide how much time and you can direct yourself away from the crowds.  Also, the people outside the attraction need to be tamed.  They harass everyone who is trying to get into the complex.

title: Interesting history
text: We did an evening tour of the Colosseum.  Our tour guide was fair.  We learned alot about the history.  Don't miss the museum upstairs.  It's a must see.

title: Ugh...no one told us
text: They have a new system that even if you have the Roma passes you have to make a reservation.  So we had to stand in that line for 1.5-2 hrs in the hot sun.  So it turned into a very long hot day.  Once we were inside it was okay, but we were so unprepared for the extra time without water, food, bathrooms that we all felt yucky and weren't able to really appreciate what we were looking at.

title: Very very busy
text: I toured the colosseum over tenYears ago.  You are required to go through a metal detector and it was very crowded. We had our own tour guide for thirty of us.  Bring your own water..they sell it for two euros a bottle but it was icy cold.

title: Colosseum
text: 100% suggest a private/semi-private tour company, booked ahead with "I love Rome " pink shirts and trip was seamless.  Tour guide was very knowledgeable, skipped the line.  It involves significant walking and steps so older persons beware.  Bring sunscreen and water.

title: Ancient "Zoo"
text: Being in Rome for the first time I had to see the Colosseum, but it was a zoo. A bazillion people and blistering temperatures made the experience much less enjoyable than I would have hoped. Next time, if there is, I'll come in the off season.

title: Hot and disappointing
text: EVERYONE had skip the line tickets,  so that's excited in the very hot sun... no shade to be had . Ok, it's dramatic, huge, impressive etc.,  but the audio tour is not worth the money. The large tour groups make it difficult for those not in groups to move around. Limited access to the structure was frustrating. Of course you have to go, but be prepared for limited pleasure.

title: Great Historic Place
text: We paid for skip the line queues, and boy were we glad we did. We marched straight though the queues and to the front of a side entrance. We queued for 5 mins then we were inside. Very interesting tour and a lot to see.

title: A bucket list tick
text:
If you go in high season it’s going to be busy and most likely very hot.
We got here shortly after opening (8:30am) and spent around 90 minutes in the line to get in , including going through the security.

It cost 12euro each & that included free entry to the Roman forum opposite, valid for 2 days which is better than the colosseum in my opinion.
OR
You could get a guided tour from one of the many hawkers out the front & skip the Queue at a cost of 50/70 euro each depending on your negotiation skills and then get herded around with a group.

Your choice...
But I think the line gets longer as the day goes on and early morning it’s in the shade!
They only let a maximum 3000 people in & then it’s one in one out!!
So that slows it down too.
My advice is to get there early and
take water/hat 😊

title: Beautiful piece of history
text: I love that no matter where you go in Rome or Italy for that matter, you see historical buildings and sites. This was an amazing piece of history preserved.

title: Historical value
text: I am admire of Roman culture. I love to see colloseum infront of my eyes. Taking pictures and tounching the bricks make me feel i was there. I unable to speak in italian but gladfully they know how to speak english.This is what i do. Enjoying the moment

title: Stunning tour  of the  wonderful colosseum
text: Expert friendly your guide, shining tour of colosseum, Palestine hill and Roman forum. Walking at a gentle pace , where radio ear pieces are given to hear the tour guide. Skip the line passes ease the journey. A must see tour if in rome

title: Incredible but far too many tour groups
text:
First of all this is a wonder to be seen, breathtaking and epic in scale. We did a tour via City Wonders and we had a really nice tour guide called Sylvia who got us in without queuing for a long time. There is a security section and be warned you are only allowed to take in bottled water so you may lose sun cream/sun spray and lipsticks all because some fool once used a lipstick to write on the walls of the Colosseum. 
Also be warned that on a hot day you might not have much shade to hide in as you queue to get inside and through security. 
I only encountered one staff member inside the attraction and unfortunately she was quite rude. 
The reason why I can't give this attraction 5 out of 5 is because they let in far too many tour groups at the same time with tour guides and groups blocking views and access to some of the Colosseum. One of the tour guides was quite rude to my father just because he was looking at the same artifacts as his tour group. I would personally recommend tour groups be restricted to a few an hour or maybe have the tour groups in early in the morning before self guided visitors get inside. 
An amazing experience but could have been better.

title: Incredible Piece of History
text: Rather than pay the skip the line prices, we waited in the regular line and were inside in 30 minutes.  Highly recommend a guide or some sort of recording to help you catch the highlights.  We only needed 90 minutes inside and had already walked around the entire perimeter.  Your coliseum ticket also gets you in to the forum.  They cut off entrance at 6:15 and start shutting off parts of the ruins at 6:45 so get you out by 7pm so make sure you make time to see this other great Roman attraction.

title: Amazing
text: The colosseum is awe inspiring to visit. I listened to the audio from Rick Steves to wander around and learn about this great building and highly recommend it. You don’t need to pay for a guided tour. The only negative is the long wait to get it. There is only shade when you get to the front of the line. Be prepared to stand in the sun/heat for a few hours.

title: Get a guided tour
text: With the tour we got fast pass. I booked a tour through “get your guide” and was so pleased with the experience. We booked the tour where you enter through the area floor, then you see the rest of the colosseum but also palatine hill and the forum. Your guide, Barbara, was the best and made the tour fun and interesting throughout. Definitely a tour worth it.

title: Wow!
text: It is unbelievable that this Colosseum has lasted over thousands of years. The architecture is magnificent. There is plenty of history to look at while you visit the Colosseum. Make sure you get a very knowledgeable tour guide to explain the different artifacts.

title: Must see
text: The Colosseum is a must see when visiting Rome. It has so much history and culture. Would strongly suggest getting there about 30 minutes before opening to buy a ticket. I would also highly recommend the audio tour. They provide you with a mobile phone with neck strap, you walk to a certain point and they explain what you are looking at.

title: Amazing piece of history still standing!!!
text: We did a self-guided tour through the coliseum, it was honestly such an amazing experience...however...lots of people...We had to wait our turn many times for photograph spots or overlooks, but that is an issue with any major tourist destination. Do not skip this if you are ever in Rome!

title: Better than I thought
text: Our trip to the colosseum was better than I thought it would be.  I give credit to our tour guide from Italian Splendour/Massimo. We bypassed the long lines and he took us through the Colosseum and brought it to life with his depictions of how it was in the past.  There are some museum pieces on the upper floor which was nice,, near the shops.  It was very hot about 90 F,  so hydrate before and bring a water bottle.  We did a lot of stairs but I heard there was elevator access.  We did a half day tour of the Colosseum and Roman Forum which I felt was plenty of time.

title: Overrun
text: The area around the Colosseum is completely overrun with people selling you tours, souvenirs and God knows what else, and they are in your face constantly. Construction in the area has only made it worse. Plan on walking by, take a few pictures, and keep moving.

title: Just go
text: So much history to explore here, just go! If you can get tickets from the official site, that is the way to go but you have to book early, as soon as you get your flight!

title: Breath taking !
text: This structure is really breath taking. All the history that goes with it is really amazing. I recommend booking a VIP tour in order to skip the queue and go straight in. Because the queue can easily take up to 2 hours.

title: A must visit... But too crowded!
text:
Colosseum was in my bucket list since childhood, and I was thrilled to have finally visited this architectural wonder! But as I reached the location, I found out that it's been the same for everyone else in the World :) 
Of course it a historical wonder, but the place is horribly crowded! To get inside, you have to stand in a queue that can take up to an hour or more depending on the high season or low season. Tourist season or not, Colosseum is always cluttered and a overhyped destination. I am giving a 5 star for the marvellous architecture and history behind it! Except the overcrowded look of it, I loved the architecture both from outside and inside. However, I do not suggest to go for guided tours. Better to visit without a group or a guide and buy a skip-the-line ticket, so that way you can still take your time to walk around, touch the historic walls and take as many photographs as you want without a rush.

title: Great experience
text: Do the "Gladiator entrance" tour... it's worth the money.  You get to enter through the Gladiator door, spend some time on the Colosseum floor, then your guide takes you up, around and through the whole Colosseum.

title: Stunning to see in person
text: The Colosseum was one of my big bucket-list items, so seeing it for the first time was huge for me. We were able to touch the walls and see where the gladiators fought and spectators cheered. I don’t think you have to be a history buff to appreciate that experience, so I’d recommend it for everyone.

title: History
text: Great trip. We did a guided tour but you can buy tickets and get on your own. History you can read easily. Take lots of pictures.

title: A majestically stunning artifact.
text: The colosseum is one of the most iconic structures in Rome for a reason, it is an excellent example of Roman lifestyle throughout the ages.  I purchased tickets in advance to avoid waiting in a queue.

title: Historic Icon
text:
We booked tickets in advance for entry only. We turned up early on the chance of getting in before our scheduled time but had to wait. Be warned there are loads of people trying to sell guided tours outside which is a bit of a pain. It’s also not clear which entrance you are supposed to go in through as there are no signs anywhere nor where the ticket office is.

Once inside, the arena is staggering and everything you want it to be. There’s also some great information and artefacts once inside. 

As it can get very hot there is a free water dispenser just by the metro entrance if you just take an empty bottle.

Brilliant time.

title: One ticked off the bucket list
text: This is my first visit to Rome and so going to the Colosseum was a must.  It's wonderful to see and the scale of it is amazing,  however visiting it in June takes a lot of patience and ensure you have a large bottle of water because it is hot.

title: A dream come true
text: Amazing place to visit and see. Some sections show what it must have looked like back in its day of use. Still undergoing rehabilitation, we could not get to the ground floor.   This in my opinion represents Rome. just use your imagination as to the scenes that played out within this structure. Use a tour guide for the extra information you would normally not get on your own. Get the "first in line" pass.   The line to get tickets and get in are long.

title: Massive structure,
text: We could only go to the first level as higher levels are undergoing rennovation. The place is massive. There is some climbing involved so watch out for the steep steps. There are restrooms, souvenir shops and vending machines on site.

title: Best tourist thing we did in Rome
text: We did not do the Vatican or Trevi Fountain (heaving with people!) but this one we did.  It was amazing.  We did not have tickets - so I know we overpaid but the tourguide was terrific and enthusiastic and really new his stuff.  Highly recommend this one!

title: A bucket list item
text: I guess it was as expected, very cool to see and would have been disappointed not to go. You've seen it so many times on tv and books and movies that nothing there was really surprising. It was just amazing to actually stand there in person. Since I can't tell you anything more than you probably already know I want go on any further.

title: Take a tour
text: The Colosseum is worth a few hours.  We took a private tour with Ferdinando from Tours By Locals.  He was an excellent and knowledgeable tour guide.  A private tour allows you into the limited access areas and allows you to avoid the lines.

title: A true wonder
text: The Colosseum is one of my absolute favorites in Rome!  It is truly amazing and it has mostly withstood the test of time.  The day we visited it was crowded but so worth the wait.

title: Very nice but not that extra...
text:
The colosseum is very nice from the outside. We did not go into it because the queue was very loooong.. 
One of the most irritating thing was the people who wanted to give us advices and water etc. even though we said no, thank you...
And also, the colosseum was being reconstructed so we had to go to the other side in order to take a pic..

title: Colosseum tour
text: We did a full day Vatican in the first half day and Colosseum in second half of the day. mateyj was a very passionate and informative guide of the Colosseum.

title: Tour totally worth it!
text: We bought a tour so we could walk on the arena floor and hear all the history. It was totally worth it! Amazing history and well worth it to get away from the crowds. Bring a refillable water bottle as there are water fountains throughout. Go early or late, but avoid during the heat of the day if possible. There's not much shade or wind inside.

title: Historic Stadium
text: Colosseum of Rome is Historic Stadium which is point of interest for history seekers and tourists of Italy. It reminds about the old time brutal games. Place of Bloody Fighting among worriers and gladiators to amuse ancient humans. It was under renovation now-a-days. Mostly visitors take guided walking tours of Colosseum but we did it by own because we have already seen such landmarks in Turkey. It was struggled walk, so we did enough photography and move on.

title: Really spectacular and informative
text: Unmissable however expect to run the gauntlet trying to get through lots of people trying to sell you tours

title: When in Rome... you gotta see the Colosseum
text: Must go to place, easy to find and reach with its own metro station. Go during the week and book tickets online to avoid the long lines.

title: 2nd time to this amazing place
text:
Please if you go in the summer- go in the morning.  It gets really hot.  There are fountains to refill your water bottles.  Outside there are many aggressive vendors- ignore them or they will not let up.  
Take your time and get your photos.  It is such an impressive place.

title: Rome’s insatiable need for entertainment!
text: The magnificent Roman Colosseum was completed in 80 AD by Caesar Titus. Up until The Middle Ages, it was actually called: “The Flavian Amphitheater “.   It measures 610 by 500 feet. Archaeologists believe it held 50,000, even though records show it could hold 100,000 spectators.  The Coliseum has 80 entrances.  Our tour guide stressed The Doors of Life and Death.  76 entrances were for “The Mob” and the remainder (2) were for The Caesar and The Vestal Virgins. Our tour guide pointed out the elaborate sewage system to wash away all the blood and gore, after Gladiator combat.  It had many smaller sewers connected to one huge sewer drain that circled the whole structure.  Our tour guide said that historians still debate two ideas: that there were wooden catwalks across the top and the Coliseum could be flooded for naval battles.  I couldn’t see in the present condition the seats of the Caesar and Vestal Virgins supposedly very close to the action. (just at the top of a 15 foot smooth and slick marble wall). This is a must see !

title: Spectacular !
text: The most visited monument in the world and i see why. Amazing history attached to it. Tip ; Get a pass and skip the lines if you want to tour inside. Trust me, the lines are longggg. Easily accessible by the metro. I recommemd going up the opposite side of colloseum to get a non touristy pic of the colosseum

title: YOU CAN HEAR THEM ROAR!
text: Always fun to see places with so much history and to imagine what these might've been like in the days of glory! Truly amazing landmark, and EVEN better at night!

title: Excellent tour with Walks of Italy
text: Amazing guide, Flavia was wonderful.  It was hot, she found shade and water through out the 3 hour tour.  Full of information with a delightful personality.

title: Awe inspiring
text: You are standing in a place that was used 2000 years ago. You are inspired by the scale of what was created so many years ago

title: Was great at night for photos.
text: Got past at night for some photos which turned out great. Went back the next day in the heat and it was worth the tour but was a very warm day walking around.

title: Awesome
text: I was very impressed by the size of the Colosseum and the amount of it that was left. Have a guide when you do it.

title: A Must See, but Crowded
text: You can’t go to Rome without visiting the Colosseum.  Conveniently located at a Metro stop, it was literally colossal.  The only thing taking away from a 5-star rating was the crowds and the ongoing construction.  Neither could be avoided, but the distractions lessened the overall experience.

title: Amazing1
text: We had a guided tour and truly enjoyed it. The guide spoke good English and was extremely knowledgeable. You have to buy the tickets online early enough (months to at least several weeks before). With our oline tickets, we went right throught the security and bypassed huge lines at the ticket office.

title: Skip the lines at the Coliseum / Palatine Hill / Roman Forum
text:
This review is as of July 2019. We did our research as to the best ways to make our visit to the Coliseum as efficient as possible but found that some details were left out of some posts or were outdated. Here are some helpful tips:

. Buy the ROMA PASS. This will allow you to skip the lines. See more below. You can buy this online using Tiqets. Be sure to download the app and login into the same account and your receipt will pop up. You MUST pick up the actual physical ROMA PASS card from a tourist info booth. There is one right across from the Roman Forum: Via dei Fori Imperiali, 1. YOU MUST BRING AN ID FOR EACH PERSON USING A CARD. 

. Next you need a time stamped receipt for access to the Coliseum. This is not an entrance ticket. This is your appointed time to enter into the Coliseum. You can be a little late if need be but not too much. Our time was in the afternoon which was nice as the heat was calming down and the crowds were far less than it was earlier. With the ROMA CARD in hand you can get your receipt from the ticket booth at the Roman Forum across the street from the info center or at the Coliseum. Because you have the ROMA PASS you can go to the line that says “reserved tickets” or any short line for that matter. Some old posts show signs with the ROMA PASS logo but those are not up anymore. Your ROMA PASS allows two free entries into approved local museums/sites and the “Coliseum / Palatine Hill / Roman Forum” is considered one ticket which means you’re allowed another free entry to another eligible site/museum on the list. I suggest the Castel San Angelo as your other one. 

. If your timed appointment is later on then spend time around the Roman Forum and up Palatine Hill if there’s time. This place is HUGE so don’t underestimate the time it will take. For these sites you just go to the entry gate and they will scan your ROMA PASS. No ticket needed. FYI: You can’t go back in once you’ve exited. Once your card is first scanned that’s when the expiration starts counting down. 

. You can go get your time stamp at the Coliseum as well but it can be a bit hectic. The hard part is finding the ticket booth that hands them out and NO ONE will help you or tell you the truth. The ticket booth is on the northwest side of the Coliseum a short distance opposite from the group tours entry (which is a nightmare to get through and based on what I saw I would not do a group tour. You’ll have easier access as an individual). There’s a bit of construction so it’s hard to see and find the small booth. 

. When you arrive at the Coliseum you will be bombarded with people trying to sell guide services. Same is true for most other sites around Rome as well. They wear official looking shirts and badges and they say FREE INFORMATION and other misleading messages but be mindful that they are not “official” but they are licensed to be a tour guide, etc if you do choose to use their services but legit ones are mixed in with scammers so be warned. We had people lie to us about information (“ROMA PASS is no good here”) or people not interested in helping us at all. 

. With your time stamp in hand go to the general entrance at your appointed time which is on the west side of the Coliseum. Again, don’t get in line with the group tours. They will check your time stamp and then let you in. Be sure to show them the ROMA CARD. Our guard put us in the wrong line towards the ticket booth for ticket purchase. You can go straight to the gate and scan right into the venue. Again, go straight to the gate and scan right into the venue with the ROMA PASS card. Yes, you read that right. If you find yourself in the ticket booth line just skip over to the other lanes to enter. 

We did not wait on any lines at all! If we had better information like the above we would of even been more efficient. Go in the afternoon to avoid the crowds! This place is truly amazing a better enjoyed  and appreciated without the crowds. 

Hope this helps and enjoy!

title: Okay
text: It was okay. Just a bunch of rocks and people. Not very much to see unless you really like history i guess

title: Amazing structure, Use the Gladiator Entrance
text: The few extra euros to walk in through the gladiator entrance and standing on the arena floor is worth it. While you only get 10 minutes on the floor, it is quite small and not much to do besides gawk which after 10 minutes is more than enough. Strongly recommend doing a skip the line tour and pairing this with the Forum and HiIll!

title: Changes for the better
text: Back after 20 year absence and loved the changes /modernization that has taken place . The entry is much smoother with the fats-pass style timed entry system and the inside has been updated with museums and video displays.

title: Very crowded
text: Amazing architecture, with much ancient history to learn about. However, it was so crowded that we hardly had a place to stand and take good look & photo taking.

title: Loved it! But be careful, too many scammers!
text:
We booked our ticket online. Went to exchange it at the ticket place in front of the colosseum. But on our way so many people tried to stop us asking if we speak English or if we have a ticket. When we showed to one person our booking he said there is no tickets and we have to stand in a line... we did not believe it and just went to exchange our online ticket to the one to get in inside colosseum. I guess if we stood in the line they would try to sell us a ticket to get without a line... we were very unhappy, they almost grab you by the hand! Anyway, watch out for people wearing blue outfit with the information written on it!!! And better ask people who works for colosseum. 
1) THERE IS NO SUCH THING AS SKIP THE LINE!!! You get a ticket and go through security as everyone else. We exchanged our online ticket and went inside within 5 mins. That’s all.
