2) ITS A TWO DAY TICKET, WE WERE ADVISES BY COLOSSEUM STUFF TO SEE IT FIRST AND THEN TO TO SEE ROMAN FORUM. If you decide to see forum first, next day colosseum ticket won’t be valid!!!

title: Night tour
text: Going at night is worth the extra money for the peace and calm. I didn't get much out of the videos that were shown, and the long walk we did before getting to the Colloseum was interesting at times - highlight was drinking from the water fountain probably! But the atmosphere in the Colloseum was calm and gave a chance for your imagination to work ...

title: Not that fantastic
text:
Must see? Not really. Better invest your time in having good food in a nice restaurant.
15 Minutes waiting if you have online tickets.

title: Colosseum
text: The Colosseum is under repair and renovation which made it difficult to see some of the features.  It was also very crowded.

title: Worth a visit!
text: It really is a colossus, finding it huge in this day and age, one can just imagine what it must have been for the people of that age! We did not go into this monument as from the many accounts we had read and what we were told by one of our guides, there is not that much to see from the inside.  Even a walk around the outside is worth it and as we were given a fairly good explanation with our segway tour (see segway review), we did not feel the need to go in.  If you do decide to go in, get a guided tour, which will help in getting in quicker as the lines are really long, especially during the holiday season.  We would have like to do the underground tour but did not have the time to spend, even with the skip the line tickets.

title: Great place
text: Visited on a hot summers day. Loads of free water fountains. Amazing place and we'll organised. Was just a pity there was loads of scaffolding on the arena floor.

title: Go with guided tour
text: Very cool atraction. I don't regret one bit having paid extra to go with a guided tour (available online or at the entrance, there are guides everywhere offering services). It adds to the experience, knowing all the history and having pictures to compare to the actual site. I didn't pay to go to the arena, the underground or the third floor, but I didn't feel it was necessary.

title: Incredible but HOT!
text: This place is incredible. Even the kids aged 5,6 & 8 were mesmerised but beware it’s seriously hot even in the late afternoon. Whilst expensive, it’s worth booking a family friendly tour as the kids were completely engaged and happy!

title: Excellent experience
text: Best attraction there is in Italy! Beautiful architecture, and amazing story. This ancient amphitheater really brings the best out of Rome. I recommend a guided tour; it was really good to fully understand it’s history. Totally recommend it

title: Fantastic
text: It is really fantastic place to visit. But there was a loooong ticket line, even we were in line around 10 oclock in the morning, we enter inside around 11.30 am. It is better to buy ticket online and make the reservations. But it is totally worth to see it.

title: It's a magnificent place to see, feel and experience ancient history!
text: We visited the Colosseum last week, during a brief 4-night visit to Rome. This was my third visit, but my partner's first, and we both went to the location as part of a group, rather than as individuals. We pre-booked a guided tour of the Colosseum , which also included entry to the nearby Forum and Palatine Hill. We ended up exploring the Colosseum as part of a 'Loving Rome' group, which assembled outside the Forum Hotel, and was guided by a Roman graduate archaeologist called Chiarra, who proved to be both knowledgeable and personable.She had a quite a hard time with a few members of the group - a party of Americans went their own way once they'd gained access to the Colosseum, and one other member of the group complained about the hour or so that it took to gain access to the iconic location. However, this was far less than the waiting time for individuals wanting to visit the site. The queues for entry were, in my opinion, far larger than they were just a few years ago. As a result, reasonably speedy access, via a pre-booked group entry procedure, is an absolute essential for most people wanting to see the Colosseum (but who don't want to spend half a day in a queue waiting for entry). The Colosseum itself is, predictably, a great sight - one of the world's great historical locations.The area around the Colosseum is now pedestrianised , and the outer walls have been restored and cleaned via some power wash mechanism. Inside, the archaeological research continues, and the restored marble seats for rich senators are a new eye catching feature of the Colosseum. Look around you, inside, and it's quite easy to envisage the huge baying crowds of antiquity, applauding or booing the gladiators, and watching the more grisly goings-on at the Flavian Amphitheatre. Even with the heat of the day, and the huge numbers of visitors, we'd still give the site (appropriately enough) a huge "thumbs up" as a magnificent place to see, feel and experience ancient history.

title: Grand piece of history!
text: A definite site not to be missed if you get the chance to visit! I would recommend taking a guided tour as there’s lots of the surrounding area and grounds that has lots of history too! There’s a queue to get inside as with many main site seeing places but once inside there’s plenty of space to explore and imagine what went on in Roman times. My only criticism was the queue for the toilets which took about 50 minutes to get to the front! As I was pregnant at the time they were able to allow me to use another toilet and also offered me the use of a lift up to the top part of the colosseum which was very throughtful.

title: More than expected
text: Expected it to be impressive and it was.  We did a tour thru easitalytours.com.  We met right at the metro station and walked over.  No matter who you book thru - get a tour do you dont have to stand in long lines.  Loved the history, imagining what happened hear 2000 years ago.  Check out the different exhibits.

title: Colosseum Tour
text: The Colosseum is pretty amazing.  The history and age of the structure is unreal.  We took a 3rd party tour which was really good.  We were there on a unseasonably hot early June day.  The place is SUPER busy.  People EVERYWHERE.  Everywhere you walk outside there are people try to sell you stuff and they don’t take no for an answer.  I would think with security issues and crowd control the group that runs the Colosseum would be more thorough and organized for the mass of people.

title: Go late in the day
text: Would have liked to have had an audioguide included in the ticket not asked to download an app-otherwise excellent

title: Fantastic Historical Structure but beware of the Tours!!
text:
We visited the Collesium during our Recent trip to Rome.When you are wt the venue you will be approached by rogue tour companies many of whom have staff on their badges or Tabbards. (These have nothing to do with the venue) They reel you in by offering fast Track entry and a guided tour of the venue.
My partner and I sadly fell for this and ended up paying 90 Euros for a Tour which really wasn't worth it..later on we came across Roman Forum or forum Romano which is nearby.They offered a Ticket for this venue and the collesium for 12 Euro each! 
So I recommend you go here first and Ignore anybody offering fast Track into the venues
It was the same situation at the Vatican with more Rogue Tour companies offering fast Track tours.
The queue at Midday was around an hour but there is a cafe opposite the queue line so one of you can sit there and have Coffee and then swap back over whilst waiting.
The Vatican is so vast that you wouldn't need a Tour Imo as without a Tour for us it was well over 2 hours to look at most of it and a Tour would make it overly long
It is also very busy inside.Tickets are 17 Euro each..
.Whilst you are queuing you will be approached by Asian Street peddlers trying to sell you scarves on the pretence that they don't allow women with Bare shoulders into the Sistine Chapel
When you are about to enter the Chapel they will hand you a free cover to use whilst you are there so there is no need to by anything outside.There are also several cafeterias inside for refreshments

title: Excellent Tour
text:
I would definitely recommend this tour to anyone. Marianna is very friendly and very professional she has a lot of knowledge.
Thank you Ms Marianna!!

title: Absolutly loved it
text: I always wanted to see the Colosseum and it genuinely lived up to all my expectations, its an absolutely beautiful place. It is very busy however and if you cue up it will take roughly 2 hours to get in but I never felt the need to go in anyways. there is a skip the queue thing however you still need to queue to skip the queue. People walking around selling water was useful as well. If you follow it round there's quite a nice picnic arena on a small grass banking. In conclusion, if you go to Rome and chose not to see the Colosseum, you'd be a fool.

title: Long line but worth it
text: Incredible experience!! Went in late May, and since being at other landmarks that time of year ,  had decided to skip the ‘skip the line’ promos. Maybe would next time and probably would recommend the audio or group tour

title: Overwhelming
text: Really worth a visit inside with a tour guide whos final stop is here as you can then stay behind and see the exhibition and go around again more slowly on your own. Better taking pictures towards the end of day when its getting quieter

title: Lots to see
text: Need to go inside and try to see the exibition after your finish your guided tour too. Need at least 2hrs

title: Beautiful at night.
text: So fabulous and gorgeous at night. We took an evening tour less crowded and not so hot. The Roman Guy has a wonderful tour in the evening worth every penny.

title: The Colosseum by night.
text: We can definitely recommend the tours that are led through the Colosseum at night. One gets a totally different perspective. And the night shows are added value.

title: Awe inspiring
text: It's worth paying a little extra to get the backstage tour where you get to see underground of the colosseum and get a close up look at one of the lifts used to bring animals to the arena

title: Santos @ Explore Italy Tours is THE BEST!!!!
text: Santos brought everything together on the tour for my family.  As a young adult, Santos made me feel very comfortable and welcoming.  I highly recommend Santos at Explore Italy Tours!

title: Extraordinaire
text: We didn’t find tickets by internet, so we came like that. There was a waiting line for at least 1 or 2 hours. So we bought tickets from the blue jackets men (35€ for coliseum and Foro Romano). They created a group, gave us a guide and we entered in the coliseum in 15 minutes! The guide was really good.

title: Impressively Colossal!
text:
A must see visit 
We took a guided tour and this is the best way to do this as there is so much behind the scenes information. It is a busy place with many tourists so you need time to appreciate the colossal nature of this stadium built 2000 years ago

title: Holiday
text: Wonderful to visit and looks even more lovely at night. Suggest you organise a small group tour to skip the line and for information. 👍

title: So real - the Colosseum !
text:
See it to believe it ! This almost comes alive. A tour guide would be worth it here. 

Avoid the tour guide provided by the Colosseum on their website. These tour guides are not interested at all - probably at a fixed pay with no incentive to make it interesting for tourists. Get one from the outside tour operators.

title: A pillaged monument...
text: I bought the guided tour for the coliseum, the roman forum and the Palantino. The guided tour was not worth it, in my opinion the skip the line ticket is enough. The coliseum is a big building but in ruins, its great to be inside and imagine how it was on its glory days but other than that i dont see any other reason to visit. I prefer the roman forum and the palantino visit, all ruins but with much more to see. I was on the coliseum 45 minutes and it was too much. If you are wondering why i used pillaged is because after the earth quake that destroyed a big part of the coliseum the marble was all stollen  to other buildings like the saint peters basilica, thats the reason of all those holes on the coliseum..

title: A must see
text: You can't visit Rome without a trip to the Colosseum. The history behind it is very interesting. It is a busy site. We did a semi private tour to avoid waiting in line.

title: You have to see it to believe it
text:
Book your ticket in advance. We did and the queue was frightening (though not as frightening as the one into Vatican City)

Our guided tour is cancelled so we ended up exploring the space. U have to see it just because it is a historic icon of this city.

title: Late spring break
text: Absolutely stunning. It is amazing that this was built in the era that it was,  it’s workmanship is amazing. It is extremely crowded at all times and the lines are long to get in. It is a phenomenal piece of architecture

title: Amazing
text: I’ve wanted to see this in person forever and it didn’t disappoint!  Incredible!  This is a must do when visiting Rome.

title: expect crowds in the mornings!
text:
i traveled with a student tour group so our tickets and entry were done all together rather than how someone would purchase entry for a family, but my review includes some information that's necessary for the general visitor. we arrived prior to the coloseum opening so had about an hour to walk around the exterior and to get in line for entry.  what was most surprising is how often i was approached by individuals asking if i needed tickets who were wearing neon colored vests that were labeled as "staff".  it made them seem official but they were all trying to sell entry tickets so don't heed them any sort of acknowledgement or attention.  like other tourist sites, there are also many relentless vendors trying to sell hats (even if you're already wearing one), fans (as you are fanning yourself with one), bottles of water (as you have one in your hand), and other souvenir trinkets.  but one type of seller is very clever to the first-time traveler as one of our students found out... they were approached with "where are you from?" and then "i have a gift for you..." STOP THERE.  before my student could walk away, she had a bracelet tied to her wrist.  they do this in Paris and once it's on and tied on you, you end up paying monies for it whether you want the bracelet or not. best to know about it beforehand and to avoid any stranger, even if they have the sweetest smile and  kindest intentions.

as for the coloseum, since it's an archaeological site, students under 18 years of age receive FREE ENTRY by showing their passport or proof of age.  the ticket includes visit to the roman forum as well... and both sites require the need for bags/purses to be passed through a xray scanner machine and a walk through a metal detector as you would at the airport.

climbing up and coming down the staircases are tricky as the height of the steps are unusually large, so take your time and don't rush.

title: Need to do when in Rome.
text: The Roman empire is great history, so we had a guided tour at night which was great! The guide gave us a lot of knowledge about the place. It’s all mindblowing going «back in time.»

title: Huge
text: Very good and huge .., There was a metro stop and many shops . Next to the Roman foroum . Must go!!!

title: Colosseum
text: A must see when in Rome.  Very chaotic but hire a tour through a company to get a reserved time so you don;t have to wait.  Our tour was average.  A bit too short and our leader was a bit distracted.  But you can stay inside after your tour.  Highly recommend going up to the museum level and seeing alot of the artifacts that survived.

title: Dont book through VIATOR
text:
Viator does not liaise with the actual tour operators until 50 days prior to your trip and do not follow up with you If they can’t confirm your place. You will get an email stating confirmed booking and then wait until you arrive for the tour before telling you there are no spaces for you. We were one of nine people that booked through Viator for 10am tour and were shafted to 3..... when you are on a ship you cannot do a late tour, or as in our case we had booked plans for the afternoon. Please double and triple check before attending and book with the actual tour operators ... “walks-Italy Spain and Greece” as they give preference to their own bookings first..... Even  though they advertise Viator Expedia and a host of other associates. 
Not worth the angst. 
The colosseum tour was amazing.... don’t be mislead by the gladiator arena floor tour ...... you don’t go down into the gladiator floor.... you simply get a close us view of the chambers from a deck. It is a mammoth structure that has to been seen with your own eyes, just don’t book through Viator.

title: Amazing but absolutely mobbed
text: Visited colosseum yesterday with skip the line entry tickets.  Be aware that ‘skip the line’ means you don’t have to wait in line to buy your tickets but you do still have to go through the security line which when we visited took about 1 hour. You do seem to get some security screening preference if you are with a group. I would guess the wait if you just go on your own and buy tickets that day to be at least 2-3 hours. Good informational exhibits once you get inside.

title: Wow!  So Much History!
text:
The colosseum is an amazing site to see.  It is huge!  The grounds around the colosseum are full of people who want to get a glimpse of this amazing historic site!  We did a night tour and the area was quite.  We returned during the day, and even though it was raining, the place was crowded.  So much history!    

Note . . .
You can easily get to the colosseum on Metro line A (subway).  

Be cautious of the men (sorry didn’t see women) selling bracelets.  They approach you with a discussion often about your shoes.  Then they toss you a bracelet and tell you it is a “gift”.  Ya right....  next thing you know you are hearing about their new born boy/girl (complete with a picture) and they are guilting you into giving them money!  Only good thing about falling for the scam is....  all the other scammers will leave you alone once they see your bracelet.  Lol

title: Gorgeous monument
text: Loved the Colloseum with a tour guide, however it was very busy and surrounded by street vendors. There is a sign at the entrance of the museum to not purchase from street vendors.

title: Don’t miss it! But there are some downsides
text: Definitely go to the Colosseum! However, be forewarned that the lines are long, it is really hot, and the amount of peddlers/hawkers on the street is quite frustrating as they do not leave you alone. Bring water! And possibly book a tour in advance! It is an amazing piece of history and worth seeing.

title: Must see in Rome!
text: Colosseum is undoubtedly the must see when in Rome, absolutely phenomenal and exceptional piece of architecture and history, but I want to warn some of you there are a lot of scammers outside Colosseum offering skip the line with guided tour for £30 do not recommend it unless really want a guided tour but not worth for the money, line usually isn't that big and less costly,

title: Begs the questions how did the collosseum survive when so much of rome didn't
text:
If you plan far enough ahead, can get skip the line tickets directly and save lots of money.  However we didn't went on a guided tour with Livitaly. It was June, crazy hot, tons of people.  Went in the morning, bring water.
Both a monument and a memorial to the past.   
One of best know historical landmarks.

title: What’s not to like about the colosseum
text: Another big tourist attraction,  waited about half an hour to get in.  But I imagine the queue would be lengthier as the day progressed.  Again a return visit for me so not quite so overawed as my son.  Love the feel of history here.

title: Fun historical place
text: It was exciting to see the massive historical monument. There was lots of people and it was hard keeping the tour guides at bay but we found a great place to take some amazing pics. When walking around the exterior we found a rock where people were posing for pics and they were amazing.  We didn't tour the inside but my aunt uncle did and they said it was about an hour too long.

title: Coliseum
text: Magnificent structure. Get there early for tickets, the queue is better at Palatine Hill. We got there early and bought the basic ticket for €12 that covers the coliseum, forum and palatine. Forget the tour guides and use your imagination. Only downside was the toilets and the amount of litter outside, shame they don’t provide bins!

title: Mindblowing
text: Ancient history at its best. We had a guided tour so got lots of information. Very hot on the day we went but i purchased a parasol. Most of tour is in the shade.

title: Awesome
text: Our guide Juan Carlos was the best not to mention the historical places we visited. We enjoyed this tour very much

title: Years in the making well worth the effort
text: What a monumental feet of architecture a colossal site that was built for everything associated with blood sports of every kind & degree which would have amazed & shocked all at the same time . But being finally able to visit such a place was a highlight I will never forget . Thanks to the tour guide who made it interesting & knowledgeable all at once .

title: Beautiful
text: Wow what a beautiful place to visit. It is expensive and long ques to get in but well worth it. It is breath taking!

title: Amazing experience
text: We took a guided tour which was a good decision. Though it was a hot day and they changed our tour timing, it was worth it. Lot of amazing history. Only thing I wished is that the staff changed the trash bags more frequently as they were overflowing

title: Regular entry late in the day
text: My husband and I didn’t spend the money on the skip the line tour and we’re a little worried about having to wait. We went late in the day and only waited in line for about 25 minutes or so. Overall, the Colosseum is a must see but I wouldn’t spend the money on skip the line tickets.

title: Antiquities of Rome
text: It is awesome to see the Colosseum in Rome. Since my youth, I have been fascinated by ancient Greece and Rome. To be walking through the halls of this structure and to imagine the lives of those who were spectators and  participants in the games is humbling.

title: You need to see it if in Rome
text: We did the Skip the line tour and enjoyed the history of it all. It was super hot so please stay hydrated. There are public fountains all over that you can use to refill your water bottles - even inside the Colosseum.

title: Amazing colosseum
text: My teenage daughter and I were in awe when visiting the colosseum .  We didn’t go with a group but downloaded an app that gave us information about the colosseum.

title: Amazing!
text: Obviously a “must see” even when the place is crowded and you are bombarded with people offering ways to skip the line etc. We had a Roma Pass but had not been told in advance that we would have to find their ticket office and be allocated a time slot for entry. Still, it allowed us time to visit the Forum and Palatine Hill before the Colosseum.

title: Buy online ticket
text: It is super impressive to arrive and see the line that there is to enter the Coliseum ..... but if you bought your ticket online, you will only do the line to have your personal things checked ... I can advise you to go with good tennis  , since you have to walk a lot and wait to enter ... if you can pay for a self-guide, make it worth it.

title: Cool place
text:
Very interesting experience.

Make sure you are going when it's good weather. We went when it was raining, we could not enjoy that much because it was all open area. 

Worth the visit of course. Its history.

title: Fantastic place
text: Stunning, pay €40 euros for the VIP tour including palatine hill and the forum with one of the people selling, I get the impression that they all work for pretty much the same company

title: Heart of Rome
text: Don't even think to visit this place with out reservation in summer. I visited on May 3th and saw around 10 to 15 people in reservation line and next to me was non reservation line in which like 100's are standing. Don't waste your holiday time for few extra Euros.

title: Book online
text: If you want to skip the queue and stay out of the sun when waiting to buy tickets, buy your tickets online. We stood in the queue (or lack thereof) for a minute before going through security. The colosseum is under renovation, so seeing the pit was not at its full pleasure. A great visit nevertheless.

title: Amazing Site, Be Careful Where You Walk Nearby
text: The Colosseum is a 10/10 but prior to our tour my wife and I were interrupted in the neighbouring streets multiple times by people forcing bracelets onto our wrists and then demanding money. Believe me, we both were prepared for this sort of thing but they literally threw them in the air at us and then by the time I caught it from hitting my face they were beside me forcing it in to my wrist. Avoid contact with anyone not in your group and stick to the main streets, not any connecting alleys (even if they look safe).

title: Back in Time
text: My husband and I took the underground tour of the Colosseum.  The tour takes you down to where the animals were kept before they entered the Colosseum and takes you on the floor of this might structure.  I recommend this tour as it is a small group tour and things are explained to you without all the hustle and bustle of crowds of people.

title: Historical place with lots of visitors
text: It's one of the must see places in Rome. Loads of visitors so it's better to purchase tickets online before coming. We took official tour of Colosseum, Palatine hills and Forum.You need to walk a lot so not ideal for people with limited mobility. You need to carry water with you in summer and preferably an umbrella too.

title: Bring The O2
text: This place was breath taking.  Not only in the views but in climbing the stairs.  Was not prepared for the amount of stairs to climb but was totally worth it.  You just have to take your time.

title: Essential but hectic
text:
As you would expect with one of the main attractions in Rome, it’s extremely hectic.  Definitely book in advance, we used our Roma passes and had skip the line, which saved lots of time and queueing in the sun.  There are lots of officials around to direct you and they are really helpful. Beware of pickpockets, we had 2 near misses and were being vigilant. Lock your bags. 
There is water available to fill up your drink bottles from public taps.

title: Good experience
text: Definatly food for thought, all those lives lost, although the structre is rather impressive, Glad we went!

title: Unforgettable!
text:
Seeing the Trevi Fountain is like stepping inside a history, museum or art book.  The fountain itself is in pristine condition, its white marble almost glowing.  Added to this, the day we visited there wasn’t a cloud in the perfectly blue sky, there was a light breeze, and the air practically crackled with anticipation.  In fact, we witnessed a young man propose to his fiancée right in front of us💕She said yes; blessings continue to surround the Fountain it seems.
It is said that if one throws a coin into the fountain and hopes to one day return the wish will be granted, so I dutifully three about 10 coins into the crystal clear water at the base of the huge fountain.  It’s hard to overstate the majesty and grandeur of the Trevi Fountain sculpture.  It is at once accessible and overwhelming.  I felt I could study it for days and not grasp the detail not even begin to understand the story it told. 
I will warn you that this is a verrry crowded attraction in a small space. Yes, we were there on a beautiful Sunday evening in June when one might expect crowds, but we could barely make our way down to the fountain itself. 
Bottom line?  When you visit Rome you must see the Trevi Fountain!!

title: Colosseum with a 9 year old
text: Booked " Rome Tours with Kids". We had Marie for our private skip the line tour. It was not expensive and if you have children you will be so so pleased. My 9 year old Grandson was mesmerized. We have been to the Colosseum before, but only now do I understand it. Highly recommend it with the tour!

title: underground/belvedere tour a must
text:
Book in advance online to access this guided-three level tour that will take you to the underground level and also the third level with spectacular views of the Roman Forum. 

You will access the tour on the opposite of the colosseo away from
The long long long lines for people w the individual tickets. 

A must while in Rome!!

title: Stunning
text: It's the Colosseum! What else is there to say? We had a brilliant tour guide who reallu taught us a lot, so make sure to do a guided tour if you can. Also make sure you see the special exhibitions & look at the arena from all angles.

title: great tour guide
text: Took a skip the line tour with Tour in the City tours,Via della Polveriera 11, with Neils as our guide. Very informative and enjoyable from the youngest 9 to the grandparents. would highly recommend

title: Amazing ancient monument
text: I visited the Colosseum for the 3.time, I still love it and wonder about the amazing creature. Must see for everyone, who visit Rome. I strongly recommand to make a time reservation for the entering 1-2 months in advance, as without it you will not be able to enter in a day, very long lines are all day with thousands of people want to get in there and you might not reach the gates.

title: Brilliant
text: This was the first thing I visited when on my trip in Rome and it was amazing. I would advice booking the skip the line ticket as the q is really long. Was great to see such an iconic building.

title: Majestic
text:
I say this to everyone who wants to visit; buy the fast track passes. It saves you queuing for exceptionally long periods of time, and you will be able to waltz in and get a good look at the interior. What a building this is, and I don't think it matters how many times I see it in person, I will always be floored by its majesty. 

Picking the morning visiting hours is best, as you will find it is a lot quieter during this period and you are far less likely to be mobbed by the crowd. We spent quite some time here just admiring the construction and the interior. The gift shop inside is also great for a few memorable souvenirs!

title: Italy trip
text:
Must visit place to understand Roman history.
Amazing facts about Gladiators.. sad stories about the way slaves were treated

title: Busy but worth it
text: We had skip the line tickets booked with city wonders before we arrived in Rome. Thank goodness! The queue was long, and it was boiling hot. We were able to go straight in to the arena entrance which was incredible. Then we had a tour around the rest of the Colosseum. Although very busy, it is a must see when in Rome. We went at 1pm and could still see all we wanted. Note, you cant take sun cream or any sprays into the Colosseum.

title: 3000 maximum tourist limit so hard to skip the line in the peak season
text: Our family booked the skip the line tickets but were shocked of how many people were there who were supposed to "skip the line" as well. The Colosseum was packed of tourists from all over the world, in the hot June summer afternoon, we were holding the skip line tickets but still waited for more than one hour, then our kids collapsed we can't join the tour anymore. The tour guide just told us to follow him because it's out of his control of queue. Later when we try to join the 2nd part of the tour, we lost of the group when transferred to the 2nd venue, as there were just too many people and too many groups so we wasted half year and wasted money but get nothing, just sun burnt and sickness. I hope the tour operator could let people know there are 3000 people limit so you can't always skip the line as we been told.

title: Book a walking tour
text: Booked a walking tour with Viator including the paladin hill. It was so much better than doing on your own as you get knowledgable history through the professional guides plus you don't have to wait in long queues

title: 40th birthday in Rome
text:
Had to visit the coliseum it was a really hot day in June and the metro stops directly by the coliseum. 
Beautiful and peaceful even at 3pm. 
Lots of guides around to purchase tickets should you need a guided tour

title: Fabulous.
text: So amazing when you come across it. Easily to see even when crowded. Such a monument. Amazing it is still standing & looking so good.

title: Amazing
text:
Highly recommend 
You learn do much about Rome and the Romans.Gladiators Emperors etc it’s brilliant 
Rome is amazing do much to see and learn with amazing restaurants

title: Essential to have a guide (audio or real-life) and a Skip-the-line ticket
text:
Carry water and a hat and ensure you have a guide (audio or  real) to understand the design of the Colosseum.

Make sure you walk up to the chapel on the hill. If you're lucky you can see mass.

But the BEST time to see the Colosseum is early morning - when it's empty of visitors and you can admire the beauty of the building.

title: family girl trip to Italy
text: Even with skip the line tickets there was security, water and hat are good. There is an elevator to use if needed.  A guide helps explain, there is so much to learn and see.  They were so smart and inventive.  A must see if in Rome

title: Great history lesson
text: My husband and I went here and enjoyed reading the history. We also went to the roman forum. There is so much to see, eat first and be ready to walk a lot!

title: A piece of History
text: My Husband and I took a tour of the Colosseum just before noon on a Monday in early March. It was such a spectacular site! This is a must for anyone, especially history-interested people. But going as a part of a group tour is an absolute must for several reasons. First, as part of a tour you are ushered almost right into the Colosseum, instead of waiting in a very, very long line for those going in individually. Second, as part of a tour group your guide will walk you through the historical significance of each part of the Colosseum, as well as paint a mental picture of what is not still there today, such as the Marble, wooden floor in the central arena area, etc. If you choose to 'go it alone', you will miss out on an excellent opportunity to ask questions and understand what you are seeing around you, as there are almost no signs identifying anything or even the different areas. Visiting here as part of a tour is a must for anyone who wants to experience historical Rome! They are easy to arrange and you can find cheap and knowledgeable guides at the door before entry.but make sure you are going when it's good weather. this will definitely be one for the books. took me back in time and i would not hesitate to visit here again.

title: New seven Wonders of the World
text: Colosseum is the New seven Wonders of the World. Behind this majestic historical architecture, there is a rich history where it was a place that the slaves was released to the wild beast fighting for survival. Then, it was followed by the combat between gladiators who aimed to fight for the fame and fortune through the winning in the fight. Both activities were called games with audience cheering during the fight. There is a museum in the site which provides the detailed explanation of the architecture of the building structure. We were glad to have bought our online tickets in advance to avoid the long queue as advised by previous tourists’ comments and reviews in TripAdvisor. Security check is still required even advanced booking of ticket is purchased. We suggest to go early in the morning before it opens at 8.30am in the morning in order to beat the long queue and crowds even advanced online tickets were purchased, in order to enjoy better at this amazing man-made wonder.

title: Colloseum - skip the lien
text: We had a skip the line ticket and a guided tour and it was a very good idea, because even with it we probably waited in the line for the 10-15 minutes. In any a case, Colloseum is must see in Rome and you'll be amazed by historic facts about it like battles in the water.

title: Holiday
text: Although it was very hot, and has to stay long time in queue but it was worthed, to visit.prepare hat, and water

title: Unbelievable experience
text: This is definitely a must see landmark in Rome. Buy your tickets ahead of time and get the "skip-the-line" so you're in before everyone and the line gets longer.

title: Worth the Time
text: Hard to top the colosseum. It is crowded in certain areas but people move along. If you are planning to use your Roma pass you must still reserve a time. Be warned it is very hard to get in making a reservation yourself so go to the Roma pass website, but be sure to do it long before you come to Rome! We waited and bought a Roma pass in Rome then tried to reserve a time - couldn't get one for the entire week so we joined a tour offered by Airbnb - which was great but much more than we wanted to spend. Bring water as it get hot inside - security will check all of your items and you pass through a metal detector.

title: Wonder if the world
text: Very impressive to see up close. So much history it is a must do “when in Rome :)” I recommend the fast track pass tickets to cut down on the wait if touring inside. There are also tours to the hidden caverns but we did y do that part.

title: Must see
text: I would highly recommend going to see the Colosseum, how can you not? We pre-booked our tickets online, which did not provide us with the correct information. Basically you have to pick your tickets up from another destination which is a 15 minute walk and this is not noted on the ticket. You can also buy tickets there for £12 each which is cheaper. There is a lot of scaffolding which is not what you would expect. But overall you must visit as the building is very historic.

title: amazing
text: you have to book to see inside the colisseum  but beware of the touts they are everywhere even though we did not see inside it is a truly amazing sight

title: Skipping the line is key
text: Definitely skip the line if you can! Especially in a massive heat wave. We arrived at 11 and the wait was four hours. It was worth it to pay for the tour. Our tour guide was fabulous and did a great job explaining then and now.

title: Avoid Skip the Line vendors, Make your reservation at Palatine Hill
text:
It is easy to get swept up in the chaos of the Colosseum- it is overwhelming.  Here is my advice-
We purchased Roma Pass at the train station.  We arrived at Colosseum at about 8:15.  We walked about 8 minutes to Palatine Hill and stood in a very short line.  We made reservations to enter the Colosseum at 9:55.  We sat in the shade outside the Colosseum until 9:45, watching the other frantic tourists and ‘skip the line’ vendors.  We easily walked to the Colosseum entrance, showed our reservation and went in- no line at all.  We listened to the Rick Steves audio guide.  We and our teenage sons enjoyed the experience.  
After, we used the same ticket to enter the Forum and Palatine Hill.  We listened to the Rick Steves audio guide there as well.  
Overall, low stress, enjoyable, informative day.

title: Coliseum and Roman forum tour
text: Wonderful experience with a great guide named Rosanna. She was very patient and happy to answer questions. Her historical knowledge was very helpful. It was an experience of a lifetime. Thank you

title: REMARKABLE MUST SEE
text: This is a must see that is able to transport you back to Roman times as 50,000 spectators pack the stands for that days blood sports. A very well preserved but always undergoing reconstruction monument to life in Roman Rome. On line tickets a must to avoid long ticket lines. Highly recommend. Hope this helps.

title: Crowds
text:
Beautiful piece of history.
We traveled without a guide, and found about everything we were looking for.

They're reconstructing the main floor of the arena, so it doesn't look so exciting while they do so.

The expositions are great and have very good information. 

All the guided tours should have their own route or time, and there should be another time for everyone else. They do take the whole place and run around like crazy chickens, making quite uncomfortable for everyone else.

The guards should also fine everyone who leave plastic bottles on the floor. We went there around 16:00 hoping that the crowds wouldn't be there anymore and it took us about 20 min to come in (without a previously bought ticket). But there was garbage everywhere, specially plastic bottles...

The price is very acceptable, since you can use the same ticket for the other sites.

title: Wow must see
text: We pre arranged a private tour for just 4 people through what a life tours and are sure glad we did. Did the underground tour and our guide Vasco was fantastic. We quickly were able to move around and see everything. Just incredible.

title: Worth the visit
text: Very interesting to see the skilled early engineering done by the early Romans. Too bad it has been somewhat vandalized over the years. Beware on hot days that there is plenty of line-standing and climbing stairs involved with no water available there.

title: Viator Skip the Line Tour Colosseum
text: Definitely worth the extra planning and cost to book a guided tour through Viator.  Our five year old was able to do the four hour tour (although it was long for her) and we all learned a tremendous amount.  The guides are incredibly knowledgeable and well educated on their subjects.  Don’t spend all the time and money to come to Rome and then wait hours in the heat to then not learn much about the history here.  Bucket list for sure.  Simply amazing.

title: An experience
text:
From the outside looking in,it was amazing to stand in a place that is so relic. Its like feeling the vibes of ancient times and feeling the history behind it.
The police on the other-hand were not accommodating or friendly,especially if you dont speak the language or of a different race. And they were women!

title: A real wonder!!!!
text: Had seen so many pictures of this beautiful place, but when you actually see it, you realize its one of the best structures in the world and the amazing, enthralling stories around it make it even more interesting. It just blows your mind

title: Absolutely fantastic
text: We were somewhat collared by people as soon as we arrived, trying to sell us 'skip the line' tickets, but we're really glad we didn't. We personally really like taking our time around historical sites and reading the information at our own pace. A particular highlight for me here was the original graffiti, which is now behind glass - so keep an eye out for that!

title: As marvellous as they say!
text: We loved our visit to the Colosseum! Our first glimpse took my breath away. It is pretty magnificent, and larger than I expected. We did a tour of the Colosseum through ‘The Roman Guy’ tour company, and the guide was very informative and wonderful with our young children. We did the tour with arena access (where gladiators fought) and entered through the ‘gate of death’. Lots of ancient collections on display within the colosseum, such as graffiti drawn by spectators, and other objects left in the stadium by ancient romans. There is an elevator available for strollers, which we used. Overall a great experience.

title: Nice
text: Colosseum is wolrds beautiful structure it was lovely to be see with my love ones I m enjoy this and lovely

title: Breathtaking
text: This was my second visit to Rome - I enjoyed it so much. The Colosseum is one of those stunning wonders of the world that you just have to see in person!

title: Amazing
text: Amazing historical place. Really crowded during the day. In case you are interested to get inside I would recommend to get some tickets - skip the line. Even I went soon in the morning it was already crowded, even around, nearly impossible to walk around. In the evening is also really magical, but there is nearly no people, but not possible to go inside.

title: Coliseum
text:
We had made “skip the line” reservations before we went. 
I was so disappointed! The crowds were still immense and everyone had “skip the line” tickets. It was HOT, crowded and a waste of time. Poorly organized. Lots of poor people hawking cheap souvenirs, water, promising they could be your personal tour guide. It was enough to see it from outside and listen to my “Rick Steves” audio tape on the Coluseum.

title: Must see
text: This place is full of history. Amazing, you can’t visit Rome without see it. It was little disappointing to see so much scaffolding around it. It looks beautiful during the day but I prefer night view.

title: Unbelievable!!!
text: Amazing to see this great ancient structure still standing. Rich amazing history. Strongly suggest you go on a guided tour. We had an amazing guide did the colosseum and the Roman forum. Incredible to see how far ahead of its time ancient rome was. Highly reecommend this visit!

title: Magnifico.
text:
Be absolutely sure you book a tour. You save time in the line up + learn more.
We went twice (because we didn't book a tour the first time, and it was a waste).

title: Always an iconic place to visit BUT....
text:
I’ve been to the Colosseum many times over the years- as a teen, in College, with my husband and with kids. Over the years I have seen crowds increase drastically to a point where the experience become less memorable- or at least less memorable for the right reasons. Crowds are crazy large, especially in season, and lines are long. Many tour groups come to visit the Colosseum and crowd the plaza in front. 
Having said that, the Colosseum is still fascinating. The views in the early morning hours before the crowds arrive or late in the evening when tourists leave are spectacular. 
Entering the Colosseum is a bucket list item, but if you can, try to go off season to minimize crowds.

title: The Roman Colosseum
text: Wow, just wow! So worth it! We went while it was still daylight & stayed 'til dark which I recommend.  It is lit at night & so pretty. It doesn't cost anything if you just stay on the outside.

title: Incredible!!
text: Amazing to see a place with so much history, and to learn what used to take place here is wild! Every night every week that place used to be packed with people coming to see shows or executions. We did it with a guide and it was really worth it. Go early before the heat picks up!

title: Centre stage!
text:
Spectular.
We booked a skip the line pass  ... these tickets are time critical and you must arrive on time or you can be refused entry ( as I witness !)
Waiting time is really long , queues are simply unorganized..which is a little disappointing as many  people try to push in front of you.
Tale plenty of water and try to stay in the shade.

Once inside, the view is just Wow!!

title: Not worth the 2 hour queue
text: My girlfriend and I arrived at around 12 midday and were immediately hounded by tour guides who persistently asked if we had tickets and wanted to “skip the line” for €30 or so euros each. If it’s your first time you’d think they work for the Colosseum because they all have “staff” written on their shirts and vests but do not be fooled. They only want your money. It’s actually a Guided Tour and not a fast track, there is no such thing as “skip the line”. They claim you can join the tour and then once you are in you can leave the tour but in actual fact you cannot. We decided to do our own thing and wait in the line in the blistering 37 degree heat for 2 hours. Yes it was exactly 2 hours. While in the queue the same guides will ask you if you still want to skip the line because they clearly have amnesia and forget they’ve spoken to you. We spent just over an hour in the Colosseum before leaving as we saw everything. There isn’t much to see other than the inside of the colosseum (only 2 floors) and some of the sculptures and models. If you are considering it this summer please bring lots of water and sun cream as there is no escaping the heat. People will try and sell you water but it doesn’t last and there is a fountain inside but there was a long queue to fill up. All in all it was a good experience for a few pictures and to learn something new but not at this time of year in the heat.

title: Steeped with history and breathtaking
text: Amazing. We bought the tickets whilst there and qued for about an hour and 40 minutes which was quicker than the pre-booked tickets que and we got 2 days. One day for the coleseum the next day for the forum and plantium hill.

title: Obviously a must visit.
text: In june wies extensive and only wsy to best them, pay the premium....  the viewsare worth the wait...

title: A must do.
text: Provided you go early in the morning whilst its still quiet it is simply amazing and a must do in your time in Rome. I would MASSIVELY suggest you book your tickets in advance, although the official website is very unclear.

title: Colosseum
text: Nothing to say...it is Colosseum. The best hour to visit is very early in the morning, less people and very fast entrance, good to take pictures and to go to Roman Forums and Monte Palatino early too. The same ticket you buy for Colosseum works to Roman Forums

title: Super!
text: Super experience beautiful place. There is also an exposition that is interesting. Very hot this time of year

title: Must see
text: It's one of the icons of the modern world and no trip to Rome would be complete without a visit. Unfortunately, due to the tourist hordes (of which I was a part) getting into the Colosseum proper not easy and you need to prebook tickets

title: Go at the crack of dawn
text: Or winter.  The heat in June is bad.  The crowds worse. And go w a tour guide.  You’ll learn some much more.

title: Valentina was a supreme guide to all parts of the Colosseum
text: For about 15 months I have meant to write this review of a tour my partner and I had of the Colosseum in March 2018. It was so good we have remembered the name of the tour guide, Valentina, and kept promising we would let the world know how good a tour can be.  We paid for one that included the restricted areas of the dungeons all the way to the top tier. We learned about life as a slave and labourer in the dark and stinking pit and sewers of the building and saw where the masses viewed the spectacle from the highest tiers.  It was brought it to life, not just by concentrating on how most people experienced the building centuries ago but even how her parents were able to access it for romantic evenings only a few years ago. Not just educational but enjoyable and enlightening.  Getting to the restricted areas is definitely recommended even though the official Colosseum website is not user friendly.  It really is worth it.

title: Well worth a visit
text: A walk through history, wonderful architecture. Although there are long queues, when you get inside it does not feel crowded. I bought skip the line tickets online and we went round in our own time and pace. I hope to go back and do a guided tour.

title: Don't go it alone!
text:
The Colosseum is massive you do need more help than a self guided tour if you are to appreciate and experience the history within these walls.  We were on a small group tour of 6 people with Mea Tours. I would not recommend any more than that.  A private tour if you are not with family and friends would be even better.  Most people only come once or twice in a lifetime, so it is important that you have the time necessary to appreciate the entire structure.  With bigger groups the guide spends more time making sure they have everyone when moving along than explaining what you are seeing. 
Also, with a private tour you can stop at the areas that interest you and not be rushed along to meet a deadline.  
The Colosseum is in the middle of the city.  It truly reflects the "all roads lead to Rome" mantra.  Be sure to look for the chariot ruts in the road.  Going back a second day is highly recommended.  There is so much to see and absorb it definitely requires more than one trip.  I found the elevator system very fascinating.  Moving the people and animals up to the stages must have been a monumental task.  I can't imagine going to Rome without spending time at this magnificent site.

title: Unnecessarily Expensive
text: The title says it all. 34-70 Euros to go inside? That is absurd. If they could charge to take pics, I'm sure they would.

title: Took tour to skip line
text: We got in the long line and one of the tour guides grabbed us and put us in a tour that left within 20min.

title: Guided tour to skip the lines recommended or you will wait forever!!
text:
It’s just amazing and worth the trip there. While it’s been patched and bricked back together in some places, it’s still an amazing structure and rich in Roman history. 

We signed up for the small group tour which was really great and included a walk over the hill to the Parthenon and the Forum. You get a lot about the history of the roman empire and the surrounding buildings on the tour.

title: World Class History At Your Fingertips
text: Every young person should experience this world class attraction to develop a great worldview. I took my children!

title: Stunning and Amazing
text: Must see when in Roma. It is Magnificent.  You feel like you are back in time. Such beautiful architecture and history.

title: Magnificent!
text:
The Colosseum, how amazing!  We had a guided tour and obtained so much information about this structure.  To think, sea battles inside the arena!  
It was our fourth tour, and we always learn something new.

title: The Colosseum
text: The Roman Colosseum is a must. It is really something to see, and amazing ancient structure.  Be sure to get the skip the line pass, you'll be glad you did.

title: Stunning old architecture
text: If you come to Rome “Colosseum” is place to visit and spending time walking around. So many beautiful architecture with history. Better to come early because of a lot of tourists. In summer it is also very...very hot.

title: Better in the expectation then in the reality
text:
Disabled beware, huge crowds, lots of information staff giving you differing places to go ( all but 1 wrong btw) 
Queues for the lift and when up you have issues seeing anything

title: Fantastic
text: A must place to visit worth every penny buy your own ticket do not be conned into a skip the que ticket all you need is a 12 euro timed ticket no waiting

title: Very Crowded yet Astonishing!
text: Despite seeing it on so many films, I was astounded by the sheer size of this building. I advise people to buy tickets before going as the lines are huge. It was very crowded, even though I went very early in the morning. I tried to go again to get better views of the place the following day in the evening, but I was unable to go inside and even then it seemed the number of people outside hadn't decreased.

title: 3 days in Rome
text: We did a guided private tour and it was very informative. Rome was in a heat wave so it was crazy hot and dusty but interesting to see. Recommend doing an early morning tour before the heat starts.

title: A must see ancient building
text:
A very busy place so be prepared to queue even if you buy “skip the line tickets”. 

It’s just an amazing place when you consider how old it is and how’s its survived time.  When I visited 20 years ago I think I went into the underground part which was laid out with lots of exhibits showing how the arena working.  This was not accessible this time but then there must be an element of refurbishment and improvement of the Collusium where not everything can be open all the time.  

They restrict the number of visitors to 3000 inside the Coliseum at any one time this means that it doesn’t feel too crowded (unlike the Vatican)

We were lucky enough to be staying within 100 m of the Coliseum so it made the whole experience for us even better that we didn’t have far to walk to get to the Coliseum.   

As with all tourist attractions in Rome Food & Drink right next to the Coliseum is very expensive I would suggest going a few streets away and looking for places where locals are eating.

title: Amazing wonder in the world
text: This is for sure a must see. What can be said ? It’s an amazing ancient wonder of the world! It’s quite hot but there are parts where you can find shade. Very busy. Go early or late if you want to avoid the crowds.

title: favourite place in Rome
text: When in Rome, I always return to visit it. Amzing building, such old, but it feels perfect attraction for modern people..

title: Fun times
text: Great time, here’s a tip, ignore everyone on the internet and everyone outside of the gate. Yes it’s really busy and we went on a busy day but if you go after 3pm the lines are almost gone. With a empty line the “guy that works for the coliseum but not” will tell you 2 hour wait. The entire city of Rome is this way!

title: You have to go!
text: We didn't have a overbooked ticket and got that at 0800 on the day and were able to get straight in and purchase an audiotour. There are so many guides offering tours outside of the Colosseum for €30. They are the real thing but they do very. We just got the audiotour and that was pretty great. You can also download free audioguides on various apps and listen to them. Also getting there early meant that we missed the huge crowds that show up around 10am.

title: History....hell yeah!
text: Lives up to everything I expected.  Truly lived up to being one of the 7 wonders of the world.  So much bigger than I could imagine. I would avoid guided tours, and also people trying to sell you these tours.  They will lie about the queuing times, just by your own ticket and wander around at your own pace.

title: A must visit in Rome
text: This is a must visit when in Rome. The metro stop is conveniently located right across the street. Unless you go first thing in the AM I'd suggest going later afternoon to bypass the huge lines. We got an audio guide which we found useful.

title: amazing - can't be missed - but pay for an after-hours tour!
text: this is an unmissable Rome landmark. Have a walk around outside it, but also pay for an after-hours guided tour for a good look without needing to walk around thousands of people.

title: Awesome
text: Had a great tour guide and learned a lot. We also learned it is cheaper just to go there and have the staff help find a tour to join.

title: Day or Night
text: We went in the day and it was SUPERB.  The Colosseum repairs are amazing. The rest of our group did a night tour which was spectacular but not good for folks with bad feet or bad knees. Lots of walking and uneven pavement.

title: Roman Guy Tiour, Recommended!
text: our family of 5 (3 kids ages 13-7) booked a tour with The Roman Guy for skip the line tour of the Colosseum June 14.  Our guide Lucina was fantastic.  She had so much knowledge about not just construction of Colosseum, but culture and so many other tidbits it is hard to recall them all!  After the Colosseum she walked us over to the Forum and Lucina showed us several highlights there as well.  For our younger kids the tour was a little long (total about 3 hours) but they all hung in there and listened to everything Lucina said.  She was very interesting and we all enjoyed it.  If you go in the afternoon take a hat and water.  It gets hot in the sun and there is no shade anywhere!

title: a very worthwhile tour as you find out so much more than you could on your own.
text:
Excellent tour and tour guide and would highly recomend you book a tour.

So many interesting things you find out about the place.

title: Skip the line Colosseum Tour
text: Was a good tour the Guide we had was very informative and lovely. Definitely would recommend as the lines to get in were incredibly long.And we went at 8.30am in the morning to miss the heat of the day. Felt a little rushed at times and wished we could have stopped more to look at the exhibits, but in saying that there is so much too look at and take in in such a short time. The other tour groups around made it worth having a guide to get the pertinent facts and information as without it you really don't get the history of the Colosseum a d the city of Rome.

title: Very hot day, boring to see.
text: The colosseum was boring for me. My tour guide was monotone and it was so hot I started falling asleep.

title: A true wonder
text:
You see the photos online or hear the stories from this. But once you're there, it is truly amazing and fascinating how they we're able to build this in that time.
Once you're inside they will provide you enough information how it was during that time etc.. Please be advised, to get fastlane tickets/pre-booked, it took us less than 5 mins to get in.

title: Worth visiting
text: Worth the hour of falling in line. We had the Omnia pass, but we still had to fall in line to get in. Remember to reserve months before if you hate waiting and standing at cues. We were there after 4 in the afternoon so the line was not that long. We had to wait maybe 30 mins to an hour to pass the security.

title: Took a Gamble with On-site Guided Tour
text: Ack! When traveling the last thing you want is to be scammed but when the high is 99 degrees and full sun and a potential 90 minute wait for a ticket to the Coliseum at 8:30 a.m....you make take a gamble. We did. 5 of us bought into a $20 tour of the Colisseum and we lucked out because that particular Saturday tickets into the site were FREE! So $20 got us into the Colisseum with a guided tour that expedited our getting in and then we got a partially guided tour of the Forum. We all agreed that without the guided tour we would have gone in and back out without any more of an education than when we arrived so it was totally worth it for us. We were given headsets at the Colliseum which was really helpful in the big crowds of a few 1,000 people.

title: beautiful piece of history
text: This ancient site is a really beautiful piece of historical architecture.  It really is the centerpiece monument around a visit to Rome.  It is always worth a visit and alot of photos.  beautiful.

title: Incredible history
text: We had a great tour guide, Ahmed.  He lived in Scotland, England, and Italy, and really knew his history.  It was interesting, but not dry like some tour guides.  He walked us thru the Colosseum, past the Arch of Titus, and the Roman Forum.

title: Magnificent as Ever
text: We visited with an experienced guide who is an art historian. She had an iPad with images of the Colleseum in its glory days. It made the visit super especially in 100 degree heat.

title: A Must See
text: We loved our visit to the Colosseum. They are doing a lot of renovation which gets in the way of good photography.

title: Rome best places
text:
Loved it and love our tour guide too. A lot of people but don't buy the water bottle from the people on the streets. Near the kisko there is free water and opposite from there, you will find a fountain as well.
Drink a lot of water for free!

title: “Colossal Collasseum of Rome, Italy!”
text:
Never in even my wildest dreams would I have even thought about being in this place!
It’s larger than imaginable... awesome and unbelievably wonderful to view up at or down on & even from inside this magnificent structure!!! 
Amazingly beautiful... 
Enjoy 👏🏽😎🤔

title: Maximus...we were entertained!
text: Now, this is what I went to Rome for. This one miraculous, engaging, historical piece of human history. This makes up for the litter scattered pavements, the harassment of street scammers and the swarms of crowds so tight and so hot you can barely breath. This one thing was enough for me to forget all of that in an instant. It is beautiful, and only something you can experience if you see it with your own eyes. I didn't go inside it as the queues/money I felt weren't worth it as the outside is much more beautiful anyway and there's currently construction happening as well. Please note that there will be people trying to once again scam you into buying things, especially skip the line, which I believe from reading other reviews is a waste of money anyway so try to avoid contact with them. If you are looking for a place to eat thats a 5 minute walk, check out the Divin Ostilia Wine Bar, it's perfect for some authentic Italian wine and pasta!

title: Amazing
text: Pictures don’t do it justice. An amazing fear of engineering from Ancient Rome. Inside and outside are gorgeous

title: Seeing ancient history...
text: We had a tour guide. Insight is always helpful. It was 93 that day, always plan ahead. The bathroom ran out of toilet paper, have some in your purse for emergencies. Just seeing the colosseum in person is amazing.

title: Historical
text: It was nice to visit and see. I didn’t know that almost a 3rd of it is rebuilding. Also be aware of the other thousands of tourist. Our tour guide was great but there were parts that I could fall asleep on. It was still amazing to see though. Oh and it’s nothing like the movies

title: Don’t go in
text: Find a quiet spot to look back and appreciate what this place is. It’s very important to remember how old and on the other side how modern Rome is. This is the best example of Rome’s beauty.

title: breath-taking.
text: It's a marvelous building, must see place. An engineering marvel. Just loved it. Took a nice tour, knowledgeable guides.

title: Walking Tour.
text: Took a walking tour with the most passionate enthusiast of Roman art and history. His name started with a Ti or Ty. He passed us on to a woman who gave us the night tour of this amazing relic! Mind blowing experience to say the least!!!

title: Visit to Rome
text: The Colosseum is an awe inspiring structure. Within walking distance of other historic sites with in Rome, and is located across the street from the train station.

title: Must-See
text: If you have never seen it in person, it's a must-see. Truly incredible to see in person. There was some serious scaffoliding and reconstruction/restoration efforts happening while we were there. Did a guided tour (thru TripAdvisor) that was well worth the price that also included the Forum and Palatine Hill.

title: Amazing place
text: Apart from the crowds this is a must visit place. Attempt to get tickets before arriving as the queues are very long.  It's a particular problem when it's hot as there is little shade.

title: Wandered around this site
text: We wandered past yesterday afternoon and then again after dinner when it was dark and lit up.  A great wonder to see.

title: It’s historical
text: This place is super full because it’s the main attraction in Rome but it’s an awesome place to learn Roman history, the structure is huge.

title: Amazing
text: Beautiful and amazing lots of other things to see as well must see for everyone visiting Rome we couldn’t get into the coliseum even though we had a ticket as it was still a 4 hour wait in very high temperatures

title: Amazing
text: The sheer scale and size of the structure is huge but really hits you is that this was made 2000 years ago, without all this technology. we had booked a tour with Walks of Italy and the skip the line entry is really convenient. we went in June and the place was extremely crowded.

title: Pay for express entrance
text: Find a ticket office all over Rome and get express entry. You could pay for express entry at the colleagues however it will be more expensive. There are also a lot of sellers trying to push merchandise on you. Try bring your own water as the water that is sold to you there by the street sellers are reused and refilled.

title: Bucket list
text: We went at 3pm in June with no skip the line or guide & only had to queue for 20 minutes in the shade. However, I would recommend getting a guide or audio guide as there aren’t many signs up. I felt we missed out by not having one. It was only €12 each which included Palantine Hill, so don’t be fooled by any expensive tickets. There was a lot of scaffolding in the ‘arena’(see photo) but that didn’t detract from the scale & magnitude of the place. It is awesome. You’ve got to do it!

title: Must see, but do it on your own.
text: We did a tour and it was so long and boring. The Colosseum itself is breathtaking and I would have enjoyed it much more at my own pace and not with a heard of people.

title: Impressing
text: A must-see if you visit Rome. Get the 'skip the line' tickets to save a couple of houres in line. Well worth the extra money. You get a knowlegable guide.

title: Boiling hot!
text: We booked an English guided 45 minute tour and the woman who showed us around (I have forgotten her name sadly) was incredibly knowledgeable and made sure to explain the main parts to us while keeping us in the shade out of the heat where possible. Although it was boiling hot we still had a lovely time and enjoyed learning about the colosseum :)

title: Too crowded
text: It is beautiful monument but the lines are enormous definitely buy tickets online before visit. Should be better organized.

title: More crowded than 25 years ago
text: There are more tourist these days and the lines are getting longer but still worth the wait to see it.

title: Night tour with The Roman Guy!
text: We signed up for the custom night tour for our visit. No crowds! Not very hot. In our opinion this was the best way to see this amazing complex. Great site and great history

title: A lot of ruin and a lot of sun
text: You will not see the colosseum from the movies. There is not much left. We did a tour with tour guide. Make sure to check out the reviews on your tour company. Ours had broken English and was hard to understand. It probably would have been better in our own but then you can’t bypass the long line.

title: Amazing History
text: We booked tickets to avoid the queues (which I would recommend). We had an audio tour....which I would NOT recommend! There are no clear markers to establish where you are in the audio....the video is impossible to see in the sunlight....and the audio is hard to hear. Just being there is awesome but, if you want to know a lot of the 'history' you should probably take a guided tour.

title: Great visit
text: This is a great stop in Rome. Don’t take a guided tour and a skip the line. Watch out for people selling this tour. The wait isn’t long and a self tour is the way to go and the best value.

title: Amazing historical site
text: We have spent around a half day in the colosseum. We strongly recommend you to reserve the tickets beforehand and you can skip the line. If not, it’s pretty sure you will regret. Also, it will be good to have either the audio guide or a guided tour to be more familiar with the history and the information about the colosseum. Some may pay more to get entry to the second level or the stage on the ground level, but it’s not really necessary.

title: Fantastic
text: I don't know what to say except get our tickets early and book a tour. By doing this you will certainly get in and not have to wait long. I was not impressed with our tour but the cost was minimal and it got us in with hardly any wait. The wait we did have was in the shade.  She did fill us in on the history of the Colosseum and then we had all the time we wanted to wonder around. If you can I would use the the 2nd day free on the 2nd day.  Unfortunately we did not have that kind of time so we did the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill all on the same day.

title: Historical
text: This place is great, powerful and beautiful there is a place to you see how the great Roman Empire had some fun.

title: Navigating the chaos!
text:
My two teenage girls and i went to the colosseum and forum today.  We had pre-booked guided tours online through the official coop culture website.  

My best advice?  On site, there are people wearing tourist info/help shirts - make sure to ask your question to more than one (some are simply representatives of tour companies, and it's hard to tell which ones are official site staff members).  

We arrived and asked where to pick up our pre-booked tickets.  One staff member directed us in the right direction for ticket pick up, but told us that despite our pre-booked 1:30 entry time, the lines were so long that we would have to wait 1-2 hours past that time to get in.  He offered us an immediate entry for 35 euros each.  We turned that down and instead headed to the spot for ticket pick up.

There, we were advised that there would in fact be no wait.  We were told to head to the very long line up but to advise a staff member that we were pre-booked for 1:30.  We did not line up but instead immediately looked for a staff member, who told us  that we were in fact due to be in another line up on the other side of the colosseum!  Once there, things went smoothly and we walked in without any wait.

The tour was informative but I think we would have done just as well with the audio guides, so don't feel that you're missing out if that's what you decide to do.

title: Must see!
text: The Colosseum can be a hassle.  There are a lot of people trying to sell you tours when you get there, large crowds and tour groups.  BUT, I just don't know how you leave Rome without going in this amazing landmark.  So, some tips: 1. get a skip the line ticket before you get there.  2. don't do a tour.  We had a tour of the Colosseum in the underground and a few levels up that you can't see on a regular ticket.  While it was a great view, it wasn't worth the extra money.  And, let's face it, every picture begins to look the same.  I'd just find a great (maybe Rick Steves) audio tour and buy a skip the line ticket.  The underground was interesting, but really not worth the extra money or the added time the tour guide took to explain everything.  (That got a bit dry and boring, honestly.)  3.  don't buy water from the hawkers nearby.  They often fill the water and resell it.  4.  DON'T get pics with ANYONE dressed up.  They will fleece you.  All in all, the Colosseum was amazing and definitely WORTH the trip.  You will love it.

title: Glad I’m not a Gladiator
text: Did a night journey under the coliseum, a must. But couldn’t understand the guide well enough. It was a city wonders tour , the first guide Sev brilliant , the coliseum part, wonderful but understand guide was a worry , lovely girl though. This tour is fantastic we started at 930pm so makes a late night, but worth it

title: Soooo Good!
text:
Our tour guide Fabio was fantastic. Straight away, we knew we were going to have a great time because he was funny and friendly and clearly an Ancient Rome nut! 

Be mindful if you can’t be on foot for long. This is a three hour (on foot) tour. There are times when you are climbing steep stairs. It is all worth it but worth considering if you have young children or are unable to be on your feet for long.

Due to the extreme heat, our tour guide was very mindful to give lots of breaks in the shade and would use this time to educate us on the Colosseum and the Ancient Romans. He also gave lots of opportunity for people in our group to ask questions. 

I can’t rate this high enough. When in Rome, book a tour with Walks of Italy.    Plenty of photo opportunities and a chance to be educated.

title: We booked but still queued
text: Booked online, but still a queue to get in. It was only about 20 minutes though, but there is no shelter from the sun and is 35degrees for us. Once in the staff were very helpful as my wife is disabled. There is a lot of walking, but it was fine as we hired a wheelchair. You have to leave photo id when hiring. There are different kinds of tour, and they sell out quickly, like going to the stage but, or going down to the underground bit. Good exhibition on the first floor. Obviously the colosseum was not built with the disabled in mind, but e could access enough to soak up the experience.

title: Ancient History at its Best!
text: Again the history behind these walls is amazing!  It makes you want to grab a history book & start reading as much as you can regarding the Colosseum & all that transpired there.  Make sure you have an excellent tour guide who knows the history.

title: Livitaly tour - outstandingly good - just do it!!
text:
We met our Livitaly guide at 2pm and finished at approximately 5.30pm.  Livitaly were as good as their word and there were 6 of us in our group. 
What a great day.  It was hot but Deborah our guide kept us informed and interested in the fantastic history we were traversing.
The first part of our tour was the forum.  I had been there before but was blown away by the way our guide connected all the dots and provided a wholistic picture of the sight.
Next the colosseum.  It was Sunday and to say there were unbelievable queues of people would be an understatement.  However, Deborah guided us around to a quiet entrance and we were in!!!   We really did skip the line in no uncertain terms.  
The colosseum is magnificent and to stand where the gladiators fought was awesome.
Deborah took us underground for another amazing experience.
It is simply worth taking this tour as entry to the forum and colosseum was seamless and the commentary excellent.  It’s a long tour especially in the heat but a hat and water solves that issue.
We went on June 23 (back in Australia at time of writing)and this tour has to go down as a  one of the best - so just do it!!
Thanks Livitaly and Deborah

title: Site seeing
text:
Excellent historical place to see
Be prepared to walk a lot and climb steps. Worth visiting and learning the history of Rome
We had a guided tour but the group was big so had to keep up but not difficult. Very hot so sun hat and sun cream and comfortable shoes also are recommended 
Free water tap inside to refill bottles 
The restoration works are in progress but interesting to see how the site is coming back in parts to what it looked like then. It’s huge and breathtaking even after looking for half an hour 
One can be carried back in time rand imagine how it must be in the olden times. Guided tours are helpful in giving a better understanding of the layout 
Must visit place in Rome
Loved it

title: Who is not impressed
text:
As a fan of all things Roman, there really isn't much to say about the colosseum that people don't already know...
Dont buy the tickets at the colosseum, get them at the forum romanum or other places to save time.
Even with a ticked the line to get in was well over an hour so bring water and sun protection!
The audio guide was okay but could have provided more in detailed information for neards like me!
All this doesn't really matter, it's still a must see!

title: Buy your tickets online!!
text: Must do activity of Rome and it was very impressive, all of the information is presented well and the audio guide is accurate which is good.
