My recommendation is buy online prior to arriving, we waited in line for an hour and that was getting there at 8.35am. the organisation inside was a bit hap hazard with the security, ticketing and then separate audio guide handout. But still an interesting place to visit

title: Wow!
text: The Colosseum is one of the most iconic sites in the world and when you see it up close and personal you understand why. It's incredible both inside and out. We booked an early morning access tour through the Gladiator entrance through Viator. This tour also took in the Forum and the Palatine Hill. It was well worth it to by pass the massive queues already forming at 8am! Our guide was informative and engaging so well worth the money. The Colosseum will leave you speechless and our guide brought it to life and we were taken back thousands of years. You can do non-guided tours but a guide will just give you that little bit of extra knowledge and little know facts.

title: fantastic piece of Architecture completed in 8 years flat
text: Pls do use a guided tour, else you would not understand the historic importance and legacy of the Colosseum.

title: Didn’t take too long to get in
text:
We lined up to purchase a ticket in the morning (arriving around 845am/9am). Many tour guides tried to tell us the line was 2 hours long and we would skip the tour by going with their group for €30 euro. We chose to wait and were in buying tickets (€12) and entering within the hour. Once you buy your ticket if you wish you can join an official Colosseum tour for €5 - overall much cheaper than the tour offered outside. We didn’t do the tour just look around ourselves however I recommend this if you would like a tour and have time to wait in line.

As for the Colosseum itself I was blown away. Well worth the visit. Soak it in!

title: Awesome
text: It's the Colosseum!!! It's amazing!!! Summer is sweltering so be prepared to sweat....but suck it up lol. Not their fault it's hot.  Tour guides are awesome, views are awesome....I can't say enough about how amazing it was.  You can even recognize some places they took shots from the movie Gladiator. Historic and visual wonder. Don't miss it!

title: WOW!!
text: Such a beautiful place. Definitely worth the wait to get inside . We were able to see the Palatine Hill and Roman forum as well. Took us a while to see all 3 but it was all amazing

title: Book tickets before your trip
text:
We missed out on this tour b/c we did not plan far enough ahead. Get your tickets before you leave. Last week we were there and could not get tix night before we wanted to go. Also, if you are Canadian know that people there for the most part do no appreciate tourists and let you know it.  We encountered many people who were out right rude from train staff to air B and B to vendors and people on trains as well. My teen daughter was pick pocketed, shoved and told to F off more than once  and good luck finding anyone at train italia to give you service they brush you off and the tickets have no info about platforms or where to get the trains and there are next to no staff to provide service. We regularly encountered people trying to rip us off from restaurants putting tap water into a bottle that said mineral water to taxi to train staff looking to line their own wallets...it was constant. 
As Canadians we are accustomed to people being courteous and respectful and honest. I've never been anywhere else in the world where I've encountered so many people who are rude and inconsiderate. We might just have had bad luck but I would not go back to Italy and we toured it top to bottom.

title: Night time tour
text: Loved it. Far less crowds and more intimate experience and also not hot if you do the evening tour. We ate dinner nearby and then did the tour. Would recommend it over the day tour and I have done both!

title: A Must See
text: Our first stop in Rome was to visit the Colosseum.  We purchased our tickets with a vendor just outside.  While we were amazed with the ruins the guide was a bit dissappointing.  She did not bring us over to the Roman Forum and other sites.  She told us to just go on our own that day or come back on another day.  This was a skip the line tour but I felt the wait to get inside was too long. Worth a visit but

title: Blessed to walk the ground where history was made
text: Amazing location, very well organized and very touristy but my suggestion is to be the first in, be on the early morning group, you will feel like you are alone there, huge difference if you try to go in at noon the place will feel like a Sunday market and all you'll see are selfie sticks. there are tour packages that are cheap and yes, you can tell the difference and there are those that are worth every cent and even more, go for the latter.

title: Favorite place ever in Rome
text:
I love the colosseum and everyone should see it once in their lifetime
That’s all I have to say!! Right out the metro entrance, in front of the colosseum, there is a filtered water station perfect for refilling your water bottles..Roman summers ARE INTENSE, try to stay hydrated and repair from the heat

title: Walk in and amongst history
text: Go see the colosseum by day, but take a tour at night.  I did that and it was a nice departure from all of the crowds.  Give it a shot, you’ll enjoy it.

title: Inspiring Visit
text: Construction on the arena floor didn't detract too much from an inspiring visit, particularly for a history fiend. Less pleasant were the crowds and the chaos to get in despite being part of a group with advance reservations and a set tour time.

title: Just around corner
text: Was too,5 min. walk from hotel! Hundreds if not thousands of people.magnificent sight,so much history,loved it,and see at night lit up!

title: Splendid
text: The Colosseum  was a splendid  building  but it was the crowd in high season  that amazed me. We brought the guided tour with skip the line tickets and we managed to go in after 1 hour waiting under the sun at 37 Celsius.  You could  buy several bottles of iced water at 2 euros per bottle and this give you some relief. The site was really  splendid   and it was really a once in your life experience. My wife always  wanted to come before death  and my experience told me that you should  come before  aging. It was really  a challenge  for old people  like me to walk for 2 hours under  the sun of Italy. We took  a lot of pictures  so my wife could go to her next checkpoint  in her life. Please brought the guided tour including skip the line ticket because they entered at a different entrance. You might take 2 to 3 hours waiting even with skip the line ticket in this high season. Anyway  the view was amazing  and  you  should come to have a look.

title: Impressive
text: We went here at night and walked around the structure. It's incredible to realize the history that this building represents. The building is easy to get to and there are lots of great restaurants nearby with direct views of the colosseum.

title: Lots of people but still magnificent
text: Magnificent as advertised !! Too many people/tourists. Impossible to get in if you did not already have the tickets. June personally is a torture of 31 degrees and thousand of  people bumping into you.

title: Visit
text: This is unbelievable, historically mind blowing, to walk around this amazing place, if you have any issues with mobility this is not a place to visit. A lot of walking, up & down stairs, long distances to cover but a must.

title: Colosseum is amazing but Roma pass not worthy
text: Staff say different things. You go to the infopoint asking info. They tell you to reserve. You go to reserve. They tell you come back tomorrow and no need to reserve. You come back the next day and they tell you, you need to reserve. Not so organised system! We were passed from one station to another, and one queue to another. 🤦🏽‍♀️ Time’s wasted even after we organised everything well.

title: A long queue, but a good visit
text:
We missed out on booking skip the line tickets and after reading the horror stories of 2-3 hr queues we got up early with the intention of getting there for when they open. As it goes, that didn't quite work out, but we weren't far off! 

After fighting off the myriad of people touting for our business to take us on all sorts of tours, we joined the queue and after arriving at around 9am we got in around an hour and a bit, which was a result as seeing the queue as came out we were pleased we made the effort to get up. 

We had no guide, but that was fine for us and we spent a good amount of time wandering around the Colosseum. There are several info boards etc so you do get an idea of how things worked etc. 

Overall, a good visit to what us a very popular place.  Numbers inside are controlled, which is a good thing.

title: "Wow the history"
text: ok this was an eye opener so much history and the changes to the buildings over the many years and we are so lucky to have seen what is still standing, truly incredible we did the upgrade tour of the Colosseum and really recommend this. Very enjoyable

title: Impressive building
text: Impressive to see this in real live. But very, very busy. Also there was a lot of construction going on, which took away part of the view. Still impressed by this building, knowing there were no big cranes when this was built some 2000 years ago

title: Great tour by Walks
text: Took a tour with "Walks of Italy" with Ettaro.  An excellent guide who was knowledgeable and made the visit really interesting

title: Trip of a lifetime
text:
We booked a trip to the colosseum with The Roman Guy, which were worth all the money.
Our guide Papa Franko really knew how to keep us entertained through Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palantine hill...

title: Loved it!
text: The Colosseum was a trip highlight. We did not think to buy tickets far enough in advance (they were sold out a week plus online) and just showed up 15 min before opening time to buy onsite. The line was long but moved quickly and was shaded for most of the time. The tour guide hawkers were there in mass and very aggressive. I had one flat out lie to me about the cost of a regular ticket vs. his “great guided tour” rate. He quoted me a price three times higher than what the Colosseum actually charges. The audio guide was interesting and well done for the Colosseum but poorly done for Rome Forums. The guided tours I passed by didn’t seem to have any different info than the audio guide. It was very crowded but really cool to see!

title: Very interesting
text: It's very interesting to visit inside and walk the different levels of the Colosseum.  It can get really hot in summer months so make sure to bring water bottles!

title: Beautiful
text: Wow! What an amazing place! Fantastic place to visit.  Very busy. Easier & quicker to get tickets from kiosks outside of the Colosseum to save queuing. There are a lot of steps to contend with.  There are displays & gift shop on 1st floor. You can pay for a 3hr tour or just pay for entry & look at your own leisure.  There is a fresh water tap on the ground floor for free water if you need to refill a bottle.

title: Think twice about a tour
text: It's hard for me to imagine going to Rome for the first time and not seeing the Colosseum. Its easy to get to and central to the city. Be sure to get a ticket in advance, particularly if you're there May-October. I'm of two minds about a tour-- good because you can get in easily, but unless you really want a lot of detail I think you're better off doing it yourself with a good guide book.

title: One of the Wonders of the World
text:
I’ve been able to check off my bucket list, the Colosseum, one of the new 7 Wonders of the World. Pictures depict it as a massive structure but it was not. Still very big and historical!  I was expecting more. 

We were very fortunate to skip the long main entrance lines (twice as long than Disneyland lines) and enter with the tour group entrance. It does take time to get in due to security screening.  If you have a chance, book tickets in advance and skip the lines. There is no shade in line and that summer heat is hot!

Once inside, you can explore the lower and upper levels. Be careful when climbing the steps because they are not normal steps, more like 1.5 steps. 

Warning: another packed attraction regardless of time so be aware of pick pockets. Also be aware outside, lots of sellers trying to make conversation with you. Lots of construction in the area too due to the new train station they are building. 

Don’t forget to walk around it. Since we were in the area on another day, we came back around sunset and there were less people. Great time to take photos of the outside of the Colosseum during golden hour.

title: An obligated visit
text: The colosseum itself while impressive in size, perhaps has an inflated reputation. The inside of the site has lots of scaffolding that disrupts the immersion into history. The lines for individuals was extremely long and barely moving, so visiting in a tour as I did is highly recommended if not essential to avoid wasting time. There were nice exhibits and good photo opportunities, but without the commentry of a local such as my guide or the audio commentry it would likely be a boring experience. Still it is worthwhile experience first time round.

title: Awesome experience
text: We attended a Colosseum tour with a skip the line tour company. It was great being able to bypass everyone to get into the Colosseum. The Colosseum itself is awe inspiring. Unfortunately there was some refurbishment going on and we were not able to get to see all the areas of the Colosseum. It is definitely a must-do when visiting Rome.

title: Big stadium for ancient massacre.
text: Well, when you visit Rome you can't miss Colosseum. It is hard to imagine how beautiful it was but realizing that it was a most beautiful place for torturing and killing of animals and humans. This is a huge butchery. Of course, building itself probably was super amazing, but the fact of what was the purpose of that building makes me sad. Now it is hard to imagine how it originally looked like since it is just many stones now.

title: A very interesting place to visit
text: Of, fair enough, not like the Acropolis of Athens but still an amazing place to visit. Its worth to pay for the Skip The Line service.

title: Nice day out
text: Unfortunately it was so hot on the day we went we queued for one and a half hours which wasn’t pleasant buying bottles of water for €2 each in the hot sun just to let you know they don’t allow any glass to be taken in at all. It’s the security that holds everyone up.  The gift shop is a bit pants. You will take some good pictures tho.

title: Small Group Tour Rome
text: Used Viator Small Group tour from cruiseport tp Rome.  Very nice and efficient.  Had a private car, an excellent tour guide who was also a civil engineer and took the tour with one other couple.  Very insightful and interesting.  Our second visit to the coliseum.  We learned a lot more about the construction and history on this tour.  Tour also included the Roman Forum and the Pantheon.  Excellent tour and well worth the money.  Very relaxing drive back to the ship.

title: Colosseum 3 Hour Guided Tour
text:
This tour was by far the best experience I have ever endured! Susana, our tour guide was beyond incredible; she was friendly, extremely informative and really knew what she was talking about!
Can not recommend this tour enough!!

title: A "must see" when in Rome and a SMALL GROUP tour is the best way!!!
text: The Colosseum is one of the landmarks of Rome.  You just can not go to Rome without seeing it.  That said, the skip-the-line tickets are the way to go, and if you can afford it, the SMALL GROUP private tour.  I went in a group of 7 people and a tour guide.  She had an audio system so that when the mega loud and obnoxious crowds of 30+ people in a tour group went by we could still hear her.  She was able to adjust the tour a bit as it was one of the sweltering HOT June 2019 days.  She would stand in a shaded area, point out what we were going to see and then we were able to see it without spending too much time in the sun.  Also, we avoided the large group crowds which would have made us stand around waiting for them to move to get a glimpse of some of the details.  Lots of the history and details could easily be missed if you went without a guide or if you went with one of those large groups.  If you are entering the Colosseum without the skip-the-line or group guide, she suggested going to The Forum and buying a ticket there for both places since the ticket lines are significantly shorter.

title: A must see
text: Everyone should visit the Colosseum and learn more about its history. It's so interesting for many reasons, but I wish Rome had recognized its treasures from ancient history and not let so much destruction occur.

title: Amazing
text: The size of it alone is breath taking.  I have seen it many times and it still has the same affect on me.  Most recent visit was with my teenage grandchildren.   Even without a guided tour they were in awe.   A must see in Rome

title: Gladiator fans...a must
text:
Great place!!! 
Good to see the archeological work being done however having scaffolding interiorly and exteriorly does detract from the impressiveness of the building.
Very busy with lots of people stopping to take selfies so you don’t get a real chance to properly look round at your leisure.
Well worth doing a tour and skipping the line (queues can be hours and hours) however this does tend to come at a cost.
Very informative and a must if you are visiting Rome.

title: Go when there crowds are low
text: Try to visit in May before the crowds get so big that they're scary. Also, go in early morning or late afternoon. Lots of guides hang around outside. We actually hooked up with a great art student who really knew is stuff. He had passes and credentials that made getting inside easier. Colosseum restoration work is very interesting, if you can get a tour that focuses on the techniques they are using it's really interesting.

title: Amazing experience
text: We prebooked entry to Colosseum in February 2019. Well worth the visit. Amazing experience to stand where people enjoyed 'entertainment' here thousands of years ago.

title: So beautiful!!
text: Definitely a must see if you're traveling to Italy! There are many different options on this trip so pick something that fits your budget, time and exertion limit. Beware of panhandlers!!

title: Unique
text: It’s the second time that I visit Rome and this fountain. Always with the same pleasure. Don’t miss it.

title: Awesome!! As History says!!!
text:
buy your tickets months before you go because otherwise you'll be waiting more than 3 hours to enter. or you can buy a tour so they can guide you and explain each of the places inside.
 but Colosseum is the place that you need to see by you own eyes.

title: Great Experience!
text: Enjoyable experience!  We did the VIP Caesar’s Palace Tour with Colosseum & Roman Forum.  The ruins are amazing! I wish the Colosseum was not under construction cause we did not see much of it, but seeing it in person is still awesome!

title: MUST SEE
text:
Have been to the Colloseum before but this time we decided to do a tour inside & it was fantastic, we booked with ‘Show me Italy’ skip the line (which is a must) because if you just turn up the line is a mile long & you may wait for anything up to 
2-3 hours, the tour guide was so knowledgeable about the History of the Colloseum, we thoroughly enjoyed it.

title: Very interesting - but way too many people - takes a long time.
text:
We arrived at 0900 and stood in line 2 hours and 10 minutes by the time we got our tickets and audioguides.  Many "agents" were selling "skip the line" packages and tours for double and triple the price, but if you didn't get in those lines early you had about the same experience we did.  
The audioguides were good and very understandable.
Be careful of the temperatures and sun.  There is a handicapped elevator but everything else requires a lot of stair climbing.  Long lines for the womens' toilets.

title: consider watch a document or read a good book - avoid visiting it in summer
text: The ticket is pricey and the crowd management is poor. When arriving, like all others we could not figure out where to start. Even with a purchased online ticket, you still to figure out where to line up to exchange the ticket. It is hot and chaotic, and need much time to ask. Just not a pleasant experience to figure out how to get in even with a ticket. It is just a ruin and a glory Rome lost. It was a master architecture at that time and testifies how great the Roman was at that time. Now municipal service, train and public transit in Rome and south Italy is too much to expect. Better to visit it other than in the summer to avoid heat stroke.

title: Roman Coliseum
text: The Roman Coliseum is a grand testament to the vibrancy of the Roman civilization.  Our guide expertly guided us through this beautiful arena.  Walking through the places where gladiators sparred and lost their lives and where Senators and common folk alike mingled to witness spectacles of a grand scale was indeed breathtaking.  We took more than half a day walking and enjoying this place.  The lines are long, but it will really help if you go extra early in the morning with a guide as you will truly enjoy this Roman masterpiece.

title: Visit it Early!
text:
We made the amazing choice of getting to the Colosseum at 9am. We booked a skip-the-line tour. Thanks to this, we got in there by 9:30. 

Rome in June, can get very hot. Our tour guide knew this and kept us under the shade while explaining amazing facts about the building. 

This place is a must, but it’s even better to experience it with a guide and get there early in the morning to skip the lines and withstand the tough weather.

title: Impressive
text:
The colosseum is as expected an impressive structure
1. Reserve tickets online in advance
2. If you don't have the tickets better to Buy them at Palatine hill or Forum where lines will be shorter. Its a combined ticket
3. Lines are very long so get in early
4. There are water fountains on premises
5. The outside pics are best at sunset

title: Cool but buy your tickets in advance
text:
Great and a must view
However don't get your tickets on the door get them from getyourguide with skip the line there a saviour

title: What and amazing architecture!!!
text: Gorgeous place- so happy to see it. Decided to visit it during the sun set, so the reddish sun rays would reflect on the structure and make it look even more beautiful- we were right- got some nice shots and happy to share them. No need to say or state of recommending it-- if you may do it- it absolutely worth it and memorable

title: Underground Tour in Italian for English Speakers
text:
My husband and I have both been to the Colosseum before, but never with our kids and had never viewed the underground as it wasn’t an option in past travels.  To make this visit different, we decided to book an underground tour to see areas not seen before.  Not wanting to pay the significant upcharge the tour companies charge, I researched (on TripAdvisor) the best way to purchase official underground tour tickets directly from coopculture.  On the morning tickets were released, my son and I both jumped on computers and attempted to secure tickets along with thousands of other people.  The website stalled and crashed under pressure.  A few times I actually had tickets in the cart and then they disappeared.  After three hours of trying, the English tour was long sold out but there were still tickets available for the Italian tour.  Having read that sometimes the Italian tours filled up with all English speakers, I took a chance and booked the tickets.  It took many follow-ups with coopculture to receive my confirmation after my credit card being charged.

The tickets were for 2:05 pm and we were advised to arrive 30 minutes to an hour early.  We weren’t allowed to enter the Colosseum until 2:20 pm.  I was told by the official that this was on purpose to make sure people weren’t late.  As the group entered we discovered that it was indeed all English speakers.  However, our family was the only family that did not come on the tour with a private tour guide separate from the official Colosseum tour.  (Which confirms the reason these tickets are so hard to get, the private tour companies are jumping on them and reselling to other travelers).  The official Colosseum tour guide, who also spoke English, was nice enough and told us she could try to answer any questions we had but was not going to conduct a tour as everyone else had their own tour guide.  Basically, she just acted as an escort for us and the other private tour groups to enter the underground. The “tour” was over in 30 minutes, tops.

The Colosseum itself is impressive and a must see.  But overall, the process of securing and using the underground tour tickets was unorganized and confusing from start to finish.  We got “skip the line” tickets (though we still waited outside in the hot sun for an hour) and entrance to the underground, which was our objective.  Was it worth the time and effort involved?  For us, probably not.  If we were banking on an included tour (which clearly, we weren’t since I booked it in Italian), it would have been disappointing.  If I were to do it again, I’d probably just book regular timed entrance tickets.

title: Colosseum - Crowded but Must Visit
text: My second visit to Colosseum. First one was in 1992. Place is too crowded and Skip the line tour has limitations. Despite crowds - Must visit.

title: Roma. Ban plastic water bottles!
text: We have been looking forward to this trip for years only to find Roma don't pick up rubbish around their most visited sites. So disappointing to see so many staff standing around doing nothing when there were mountains of rubbish around them. Seriously what is wrong with this world. Do you hate it that much you can't pick up a bottle or two. Not ever coming back to filthy Roma!

title: Very impressive
text: What a place to visit, such history to see, very busy on our visit and hot as well but well worth it. Plenty of camera shots to be had. A lot of hawkers outside a bit annoying but enough to ruin day it was within distance from our hotel our hotel that we stayed at hotel glam so very handy for us and had numerous eating places along way to refresh whilst doing the walk.

title: Colosseum .. Amazing place
text: We were shown around by a tour guide who's knowledge was fantastic and very informative. Passed all the queues as we booked our tour before coming which is a must as the queues to get in are massive! If you have mobility issues this may not be the trip for you as there is a lot of walking up and down stairs and a long distance to walk. The Colosseum is a MUST to see just amazing.

title: Collosal :)
text: There are some restoration works taking place right now and that makes it look less spectacular but it is a MUST anyway. We booked a tour and we enjoyed it so so much.

title: Great tour
text:
We booked the tour through Italy rail pass.
It was a great tour.
The tour leader explained it very well.
She has the picture of the Colosseum before earth quake , therefore we can imagine the original building , compared to now.
Fully recommended.

title: Masterpiece
text: Try to have time for visiting the inside of Colosseum. It's well preserved. And also try to visit in the morning time.

title: Summer 2019
text: Historical place and very close to a metro station, security not problem and government trying to improve area.

title: Fascinating
text: Rich of history and definitely a must see while in Rome. We did a tour with audio guide and it was very informative. It’s very hot in summer and towards the end we were tired and very hot. Wear a hat and I’d go a far to even suggest an umbrella if doing Palestine Hill and the Roman Forum as well. We had originally booked the underground tour as well but there was a mix up with our booking so we missed out. If you go, I’d suggest you do that underground tour, but book way in advance and confirm that your booking hasn’t been ‘rejected’ because they’re booked out. We turned up expecting to go down there and were disappointed to miss out.

title: Seen it once
text:
Rip off Rome at its best . Massive queues to get in ,€12 not to bad but we thought we would like to experience it at night . Boy were we surprised €99 for a night tour which is robbery. 
I would recommend going over a public holiday where entrance is free to most places . 
We got there early in the morning and was shocked to find people hounding customers in the queue for €15 to skip the line to a free event . It was good to see but i will never go back to dirty Italy again !

title: Great day out
text: I recommend doing with a guide to get the full experience and skip the lines which are normally massive.

title: Worth seeing even with all the crowds
text: Our guide was so informative.  He was one of the few that had structural engineering background which gave us insight about the colosseum.

title: a must seen
text:
Colosseum, a must seen in Rome
i highly recommend get ticket before heading to Colosseum.
There are all kinds of tour you can join locally or on website and some would have tour guide to explain while you are in Colosseum. but the best part of joining a tour is to skip the lines!!!
Otherwise you would need to wait under the sun for a long long time..............
A bit disappointed as inside a lot of areas are fixing. so the original face is hard to see now

title: Really great
text: It’s everything you have seen and heard about it. We didn’t do a guided tour although that is probably worthwhile. Some great views from each level.

title: Definitely buy a skip the queue ticket
text: We've been before and it is an amazing location. Bought skip the queue tickets but still waited in line for 20 minutes in 37 degree heat with no shelter. Bring a hat or an umbrella if you visit in the peak of summer. Buy cold water or fill your water bottle before you get in line. Watch out for people who spend ages at the viewpoints to take 50 selfies.

title: HAVE TO SEE IT
text: The Colosseum is a must-do for Rome.  The damn Huns may have damaged it in the middle ages but it is still amazing.  We were here 10 years ago and they have excavated even more.  The Romans were amazing builders..

title: Historic site
text:
Go early or You’ll have a long wait to get in at the Colosseum
Take one of the guided tours, 
It has elevator for those who have problems walking

title: Amazing!
text: Just imagine someone of Ancient Rome walking around there to watch a gladiator competition. If you come in the summer time, have sunscreen, wear a hat, and plenty of water because it gets hot there and there’s not a lot of shade.

title: Simply spectacular
text: A truly amazing place. It's hard to imagine the scale of it until you have been there. Well worth an online booking and a queue jump though as the line to get in looked enormous.

title: Rome’s Colosseum - History on top of History!
text: The visit with a guide is almost a must if you want to catch the subtle history all around the Colosseum like the map above an archway!

title: Amazing!
text: What an amazing opportunity! I can’t express how worth it this tour is. Opportunity of a lifetime! It was extremely hot in June so be prepared for that, there is no where to rest. However it is more than worth the visit.

title: VIP Colosseum
text: Have to do the underground skip the line- we had the best tour guide - tons of history and information

title: Still standing
text: Very crowded, skip the line an make on line reservations, using Roma pass the entry is free for the it and The palatine hill. Otherwise perfectly conservatied still a working in progress, you would be amazed what romans were able to build just for having fun. Evening visits or early morning  ones are preferable due to the almost unbearable heat during the day.

title: Unbelievable engineering
text: Worth the visit.  I would recommend early morning in the summer. Very hot to stand on line. Worth it to pay for skip the line.

title: Worth the visit
text: Seeing the Colosseum does give a better feel of how the ancient Romans spent their free time.  They are clearly working to excavate the site so that it will be even more complete in the future.  We came in as part of a group and they did a good job of keeping us moving along without feeling cramped.  The stairs up were very high so it wouldn't be good for those with mobility issues.

title: Should not be missed
text:
This is a great sightseeing landmark ...no visitor of Rome should miss it.
This is where the gladiators used to fight.
The problem is with the long lines of people wanting to get tickets to enter.Online reservation is encouraged.
Patience is needed here.

title: Exceeded expectations.
text: Highly recommend the guided tour, Which includes the underground. You must reserve this in advance as the group is capped at 25. I recommend going back in the evening to get night photos.

title: Wow
text: Absolutely stunning and getting a guided tour was really worth the money the guide was very knowledgeable and playful the only negative I have is it was extremely hot and was nowhere to hide from the sun

title: Thanks Rome
text: It was very hot and my back is weak so it was very hard but still very nice old place! would recommend for the stronger Rome visitors

title: Intimidating
text: When me and my sister (both aged 20) visited Rome, the historical sites were beautiful and fantastic, but we found that the men were rather intimidating and forward with their sexual harassment. I rather disliked the wolf whistling and sinister whispering of “beautiful” or “ciao Bella” as we walked past, and I felt quite unsafe at times. Particularly the men flogging African bracelets would approach us and put the bracelets on our wrists without asking, saying they were free, then proceeding to ask us for money for the things we had not asked for, even holding onto my sister’s arm until we handed over €15. I would recommend travelling with a male companion if you can, but if that is not possible, always say that you’re married or in a relationship, even if this isn’t true, to try and avoid any dangerous situations.

title: Worst tour guide ever
text: The colosseum was awesome.  Tour guide the worst.  Bruno.Think of your worst social studies teacher in Junior High school.

title: Incredible!!!!
text: Seeing this ancient Colosseum was truly incredible.  We booked skip the line tours which were very worthwhile as it fills up quickly.

title: Fantastic!
text: Seeing the Coliseum was phenomenal! I decided not to go inside because the lines were too long. Seeing the outside was enough for me to take in.

title: "Roman Guy" tour very good
text: Oh well what can I say the tour lived up to my expectations of this remarkable place, and was made more enjoyable by the extremly knowledgable tour guide Marco Rossi from the Roman Guy Company - who added humour to his comentary.

title: So historic!
text: If you go to Rome everyone will tell you that this is a most and of course it is, but if you have the chance to visit by day and then go for a walk in the area at night you’ll find the truly gem, is stunning to sea all the structure illuminated, you will fill the greatness!

title: Wow
text: How did they build such a magnificent building. It was mind boggling and learnt so much from the tour operator. Make sure you book a tour operator and not go straight there to buy tickets at the Colosseum. I want to go back again and try the underground tour. When we went it was closed. I could have stayed all day there and just imagining all the games. Although they were horrible all about killing, but they also did bot fights. yes they flood the colosseum with water and have boats doing battle

title: The size and history
text: We had a great tour of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Forum.  Awesome and would do again. The size and history we learn so much.

title: When in Rome you have to visit
text: Amazing to see this in real life and imagine what it must have been like in use. The audio tour is very good. I would highly recommend purchasing the tickets online, although we had to queue to collect the tickets and then wait a further hour, this seemed like nothing compared to those who had to queue to buy tickets.

title: Wonderful!!!
text: This is definitely a must when in Rome! It is gorgeous and full of history! The staff is not the friendliest but they were helpful. TIP: There are people outside the colosseum offering cut-the-line tickets, they will say that you will save over an hour and a half to get into the place and will also charge you for translation... DO NOT BELIEVE IT! It will actually cost less to make the regular line (seriously not even close to an hour and a half, it took us like 15-20 minutes and the place was crowded, so it’s fine!) and you can get the audio-tour or even a guided tour cheaper if you buy it directly at the Colosseum ticket office!

title: # Iloverome
text:
Great tour, excellent tour guide Matt! Very knowledgeable friendly and funny.
We would highly recommended him for tours!
Thank you

title: When in Rome...
text:
We do not normally go to museums, but we all enjoyed the construction and preservation of the Colosseum.
Must haves/dos before entering the Colosseum (if visiting in the summer months, we visited in July):
-BUY ‘SKIP THE LINE’ PASSES! Saved us 2 hours of waiting in line, just to buy a ticket to enter. We purchased passes online, 2 weeks in advance from a 3rd party vendor (Head Out).
-Have a hat
-Have water bottles 
-Have umbrella for shade (a must for all of Italy)
-Have comfy shoes
-Have a fan (electric €5 or manual €1)
-Bring snacks (crackers, nuts, chips, fruit, etc)
Your bag does get scanned, it goes thru the X-ray machine, but snacks are not prohibited.
Do a little research, before you go & see what the Colosseum is all about, especially if you have an inquisitive child.

title: Good site to visit
text: Must see when going to Rome. Probably the most recognisable symbol of the city. I was lucky enough that on the day I went if was free entry which was a nice surprise. You can get some amazing pictures while you soak up the history the colosseum has. Also very nice in the evening when it’s lit up. It was busy but we waited hardly any time at all to get it. It’s a large building so plenty space to move around and enjoy yourself even in the peak of summer

title: The scale was impressive and the heat was oppressive! We were there during a heat wave and I think it was close to 100 d
text: It was hot and crowded the day we went. I recommend a private guided tour. You will want to purchase tickets in advance and bypass the lines. Take time to read about the history of the place before going so you can really soak in and contemplate the scene. The police do not want you buying from unlicensed street vendors and they run when the police show up. Don’t buy from them. Bring your own hat or umbrella for sun protection. If you do carry an umbrella, please be aware if your space and try not to poke people in the face and eyes. If you are not carrying an umbrella, consider defense techniques in advance. It’s the modern gladiator- umbrellas can be weapons. Plan accordingly to protect yourself. I recommend sunscreen.

title: Worth the travails and cost
text: The exhibits as well as the site are quite amazing, and along with a visit to Roman Forum and Paletine Hill, one walks away with a much better understanding of Roman history and life.

title: July visit
text:
We have been here before but wanted to return as it's the Colosseum!! The place as awe inspiring however two moans.
1. You must book online for a ticket and time to get in otherwise there's a massive queue but even with the booked ticket you still have to queue just in a shorter queue and you don't necessarily get in on your posted time! 
Moan 2. Whilst queueing in 37° heat with no cloud cover you are made to queue without any shade or respite from the sun. So bring plenty of water and a hat.. even buy a small paper brolly from a local shop and NOT from the annoying street sellers.
Apart from that well worth a visit.

title: Super busy And packed
text: A must view I guess but the hustle & bustle drives you crazy... skip the queue tickets still take ages due to security. Go early am or risk long delays.

title: A must see!
text:
When you come to Rome you have to see the Colosseum.We had a guided tour which was great as we were given a very informative tour.
There is'nt that much shade in the outer areas so if its hot like it was when we were there (thanks to a heatwave over Europe)..take a hat,sunscreen and water!

title: Amazing but be aware of sellers
text: The Colosseum is amazing, its exactly how you think it will be. However, be aware of the ticket sellers outside. There are so many of them and they keep trying to stop you and even follow you around.

title: What can you say, see with awe
text: A very short walk from our hotel, 10/15 minutes and as you walk, please don’t get the tourist buses you miss so much, the building rises into the skyline and you wonder at its majesty. I can tell you as I child you only ever saw it on tv and in films, but to be stood near it, goose pimples will envelop you. It’s magnificent, not what happened inside, but the marvellous way the structure was completed within the era, it’s an absolute must and we are so glad we went. Only caution, watch out for the lucky,lucky men. They may appear friendly but they don’t spend their time with you for nothing. Be polite tell them no and they will walk away. But if you engage expect to buy something

title: Ticket touts!!!
text:
The colosseum is literally right outside of the metro station and it took my breath away! 
What a wonderful site.
However you cannot move more than 5 steps without having a ticket tout in your face, or someone trying to sell you water, an umbrella, a hat etc etc.
This is stressful when you are also trying to navigate the huge crowds in 37 degree heat, while trying desperately to hold onto small children.

title: Beat the queues, amazing visit, well worth it!
text:
We got here for around 9.20am, we were actually looking for the queue but there wasn't one! Straight to the front to buy tickets and in we went, very quick, and so worth it! 
We visited in March so the weather was good, it wasn't too busy at 9.20am, we didn't book ahead, we walked straight to the front and brought tickets there. Easier to not book ahead and just get there early I'd say!

title: I Love Rome Tour
text: Absolutely awesome. The guide was great very informative and kept us together with large crowds and challenging heat.

title: Amazing
text: Visiting the Colosseum was so much more than I expected.  You can almost see the gladiators entering & preparing to fight.  Definitely a must see while in Rome

title: Gray line tour of Colloseum/Palatine wiith Claudi
text: The Gray Line Colloseum and Palatune tour- A wonderful tour full of history and fun-filled facts!  Knowledgeable yet light-hearted and very thoughtful given the extremely hot weather. Claudi  found shade whenever she could. She is German but her English is excellent and clearly understood. We loved the tour and our guide. We highly recommend this tour, the tour company and especially our guide, Claudi!

title: New 7 Wonders of the World
text: The Colosseo has made the list.  Very, very busy sight.  Sooo many people trying to get in. Buy your tickets on line and if you have a Roma pass need to make an entrance reservation. It will still take you over an hour to get to the entrance.  There are qualified tour guides inside for considerable less euros or do your research and buy a self guide.  But it is a must see.

title: Skip the line and have a wonderful time learning
text: Nina walked us through the arena for and the top deck with a slow and measured pace. It felt wonderful to be in the 72AD monument and highlights the accomplishments that was Rome

title: Really awesome
text: In rome for a week. The Colosseo is a must seen and I don’t regret it at all!! It is wonderful! The architecture is mind blowing! One of the 7 new wonders of the world!!

title: Great Experience.  Learned alot.
text: Our guide was very knowledgable and interacted well with the group.  It only gets 4 stars because it was extremely hot.  Save yourselve some grief and do not go in July.

title: Must go
text: This is an amazing experience. Must go and see . Bring water and snacks because there's no bending machines or people selling food near the Colosseum grounds . Buy your tickets online before arriving is going to save you a lot of time .

title: Remarkable
text: Phenomenal site. HIGHLY recommend going with a tour guide (we LOVED Context Travel)— they can get you past the lines, plus help contextualize and focus what would otherwise be an overwhelming experience. You must go!

title: Fantastic and informative
text: Absolutely fantastic day with private tour guide full of information and made the hot weather a fun day in the sun.

title: Great tour of Colosseum
text: Booked with Arena tours when I got to the colosseum, by a gentleman in blue jacket, the tour was 18 of us, really knowledgeable on history of colosseo, gave us time inside not enough due to long lines, then the palentine hill and forum tours were awesome worth it.

title: Must see when in Rome
text: When in Rome, you must stop here.  Make sure to get a tour or tickets ahead of time.  We did the arena floor tour which was great and highly recommended.  The only downside is all of the people trying to sell you things as you go in.

title: 4th visit, but just as awe-inspiring as the 1st!
text:
It’s currently 40 degrees in Rome, so thank goodness we’d booked for early entry at 09.30 this morning - as we were going in, there were very long queues of people who hadn’t booked in advance, all waiting in the blistering heat.

The audio guide is definitely worth it, as it gives you a real insight into the different areas and what they were used for.

I’m sure if we return to Rome, we’ll be making a 5th visit!

title: Hive of misinformation and disorganisation
text:
*Warning, I’m still jaded from this experience so take this with a grain of salt*

I’ve been to the Colosseum before. This was nothing like my experience with a guided tour. We booked the day before (you will need to book a time which you can do online or next to the Roman Forum). 

We arrived 10 minutes before our allotted time slot and were shown by someone wearing an ‘information’ singlet where the line to wait to get in was for people who had booked their time before hand. The line was in the sun, not moving and we were told would take up to 2 hours to get through. We waited 10 minutes before giving up. In that 10 minutes we were harassed and people tried to make us buy tours, water and junk. I would recommend going with a tour and looking up the tour company. If you do go independently, be prepared for a long wait and bring plenty of water, shade and sunscreen. Arrive at least an hour before your time or try and get in early!

title: Collaseam
text: Went on an organised trip. Fantastic place to visit and lots to see. Sadly not in very good condition but it is in huge grounds. Spent about 3 hours there

title: Soak in the atmosphere
text: It’s what you would expect from such an iconic venue, go with a tour guide though to get the most from your visit

title: A must visit place
text: Very impressive. We visited in June and it was very crowded.  Took a guided tour there, and it was worth it. Make sure to take some water with you, can refill bottles from water taps

title: Lovely relaxing visit
text: I'm disabled and the staff couldn't do enough to help without actually being asked. Lift access was great and we were fast tracked aswell.

title: An amazing building

title: Keep Cool at The Colloseum
text:
Make sure to bring a bottle of water with you as there's an outdoor tap for refills and you can also drench yourself in said water. Avoid any and all ticket touts/merchants because it's just not worth it. 

If you're 18 to 25 and an EU citizen you can get bargain tickets for this, we only paid €2 each, which is an absolute steal for the fantastic views you get both inside and out of the colloseum. 

This also comes with a ticket for the Palatino Hill across the road, which we didn't get to see, but looked good from a distance.

title: You have to go!
text:
There are many ways to see this and you will have many ways suggested to you by so many people wanting your business, some good and some scammers so beware. I suggest pre booking beat the queue especially if it’s hot. We went in 38 degrees.
This is a must see site if you are in Rome.

  It was a hot day so finding the water fountain (top floor above restroom) and refilling our bottles was great.  Only had 45 minutes for the tour stop, but was enough for a quick look and a feel for the size of the Colosseum.

title: Breathtaking!! (Beware of Photo Touts)
text:

Before you know it, they demanded 40 Euros for 4 pictures using our own camera!! After some hackling, we quickly settled for 20 Euros and walked away for the safety of the family. A building which was made in just 8 years and still stand talls today after 2000 years.

Even after years and years of plunder!

This place is just magnificent.

If not the line is super long and gets slowed down once 3000 people are inside and you have to wait for people to exit.  Additionally if you book ahead of time you can get a tour which is awesome!

title: Colosseum and Forum tour
text: Colosseum and Forum tour with AJ with #livitaly tours. Amazing last tour of our trip. We going to really miss Rome. So rich with history, architecture, arts and much mire.

title: A ’Must-See’?
text:
Everyone has heard of the majestic Colosseum. Tourists visiting Rome often visit this building and my friend and I were no exception. We even booked a 3h tour for Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine during our four day trip to Rome, but more on that later.

When we arrived to the Colosseum we couldn’t be anything but amazed at the sight of the world-famous building. We took pictures outside and enjoyed the beautiful view. If you are planning a trip to Rome, I would strongly recommend to visit this almost 2000-year old building and take pictures on it alternatively on you in front of it. 

If you are interested in history, just like I am, you might also go on a guided tour inside the old building. While this absolutely is a fun experience and something that is on most people’s bucket lists, there are a few things that might be beneficial to know beforehand, so you know what to expect and can prepare yourself accordingly.

Firstly, the inside is not near as beautiful as the outside (in my opinion at least). Considering this building has survived hundreds of years, it is no surprise that it looks like a ruin. Being so hyped up these days, I thought Colosseum had somehow managed to survive better than all other equally as old buildings in the world (which might have been a stupid preconception on my behalf). If you enter with another mindset than mine, i.e. of it to be in a not-so-great condition, it will probably be easier to enjoy its historical value and beauty.

Another thing to prepare yourself for, when visiting Colosseum, is the long lines outside. We came with a guide, but we still had to wait in line more than 30 min. When you get to the end of the line you will also have to go through a security check, just like that on airports (with metal detectors etc.). Try therefore to bring as little as possible.

To make sure that you really appreciate the history of the building, I would recommend to read a bit about it on your own before the trip, since you can’t blindly count on your guide to tell you everything about it. Our guided tour for example only lasted 20 minutes inside of the Colosseum before heading out to the next place. 

Colosseum is probably the biggest tourist attraction in Rome and where tourists are pickpockets thrive. When I was in Rome I didn’t witness nor experience any pickpocketing, but you should be aware of its existence. Always try to have one hand on your bag and if possible have the zippers close to your body. Don’t make yourself an easier target by carrying things in your pockets either. What I did experience during my stay, though, was the people who flocked outside of tourist attractions like Colosseum. They sold everything from water bottles and sun hats to umbrellas and wristbands. Do not under any circumstances buy these, since the people who are selling them do it illegaly on the black market. If you are thirsty, try finding a supermarket or café and buy water there instead. These people can be pretty stubborn and if you sound hesistant they will notice and keep talking to you. The best way to avoid feeling harrased and/or stressed is to politely, but firmly, say ”No, no grazie”, which translates to ”No, no thank you”. If you do this as you shake your head and move away from them, they will quickly notice you aren’t an easy target and move on instead. Don’t be afraid of sounding a bit rude, it is important to be firm with them. 

If you can see past the downsides of the Colosseum, it is a fun experience and if you only have these facts in your mind when visiting I believe you will have a great time!

title: Rome!
text: The Colosseum is a must see for Rome. Go as early as you can in the morning. Go with a guide as there is so much history around and to the Colosseum.  It is really amazing to see what the Romans built!

title: Incredible
text: A must see, this experience changed my perspective on a lot of things. Make sure you pay for the guide you can skip the line, should be around $40.

title: Wonderful historic site but beware the queues and rip-offs
text:
The Colosseum is a must see site in Rome. The site itself is awe-inspiring and well wirth several visits.

The organisation of visitors is, however, a shambles.

Firstly, you can expect to queue and queue.  You can book on tne Web. If you don't, even if you have tickets, you must queue at a ticket office, a couple of hundred metres from the Colosseum entry, to be issued with an entry time. You then have to queue to get in. Be warned, the issued entry time is pointless. It is not honoured. If you try to get to the gate on time, you are simply told to go back on the end of tne queue. Queing time on the day we visited was estimated to be 90 minutes to 2 hours. The queue has no shade or shelter. 

It is, however, a target for people offering guided tours. Their major sales pitch is that these tours enable you to "skip the queue". The first prices offered to us was 35 euros pp. Soon after, another seller wearing the same livery as the first offered tours for 30 euro pp. We accepted that offer. 

Did we "skip the queue"? Hardly!! We waited almost 20 minutes for tbe guided tour to commence then found ourselves in another queue, this one for guided tours. We were told breathlessly by our guided that we had "skipped the queue". The new queue we joined finally resulted in admission to the Colosseum after a further 35 minute wait. 

To be fair, our guided tour, once we got in, was very good. Our guide was personable and knowledgeable. She made it worth the 30 euro fee. 

The "skip the queue" sales pitch is, however,  almost fraudulent. 

If you decide to endure the normal queue in hot weather - it was 32C on the day we visited - then take good hats, sun screen and plenty of water.

title: Arena floor gladiators gate
text: Def worth a visit, did a tour with explore Italy very knowledgeable. I did the 1700 Tour lot cooler and not so crowded.

title: Awesome
text: What an amazing building. Would recommend skip the queues as they are much easier and well organised. Very interesting and awe inspiring.

title: One of the marvel of mankind
text: Must see when you are in Rome. Could be very hot during summer. Take it with you water bottle and hat.

title: Scammers
text: It is most see in rome. It is free to get in there is a que, you get told by the scummers that it will be 2 to 5 hours to get in so have a €15 for a tour but your be in with in 20 minute

title: Viewing the Colosseum from underground
text: We visited the Colosseum on our first trip to Rome, it was a great experience as our guide took us underground to see where the gladiators and animals were housed before battle and how they entered the arena, it gave a different perspective of the whole Colosseum. The museum at the Colosseum was really quite great as well.

title: Small Group Tour
text: It is amazing to realize how long this has existed. A small group tour with Walks of Italy was the perfect way to see this site. Our guide said that those in power realized that food and entertainment were the way to keep people happy and the colosseum was one place that kept people entertained.

title: On Everyone's Bucket List
text: When traveling to Rome, Italy the Colosseum is probably by far the #1 on everyone's bucket It's truly a marvel of ancient times and everything you probably expect to see. Be prepared to handle the large crowds and lines . We entered with a group and a guide who seemed to know the tricks to and get us in a bit quicker. Am I glad I visited. Yes. Would I go again probably not. On to other destinations

title: Much Safer To Visit
text: A Lot Has Changed Since I Last Visited The Colosseum In 2005. Much More Security And The Gypsies Are All But Gone. They Have Cleaned Up The Area Around The Colosseum.  Felt More Relaxed But Still Have To Keep Your Guard Up Because There Are Still Pickpockets Around. The Cost To See The Colosseum From The Outside Is Free But If You Wish To Go Inside You Need To Buy A Ticket From A Tour Company Who Will Provide A Guide To Take You Inside. I Did Not Go Inside. If You Wish To Purchase A Ticket There Are Many Places Around Rome That Sell Them. I Would Recommend Buying From City Wonders On Line Before Going To Rome.  I Had Some Friends Do That And They Said It Was Worth It,The Ticket They Bought Also Included The Roman Forum And Palatine Hill.

title: Family holiday
text: A most impressive remnant of a bygone era. The Romans certainly had a different way of entertaining the masses.

title: Amazing
text: To finally see this historic structure was very moving.  To walk where Ancient Romans did, to touch the walls, columns, and pathways was awesome. Spend time just soaking it all In!   Hiring a tour guide and skip the line passes was worth it!

title: Amazing to see in person. Essential to Book Tickets in ADVANCE!
text:
On my second visit and I still agree the Colosseum is definitely worth going in to see - even if you only go for 20 minutes and don't spend hours in there. The history and sheer scale of the sight is incredible. 

The whole ticket sales and queueing situation is absolutely shocking and soul destroying, so yes - I think it is absolutely worth paying extra to skip queues. Also, the earlier the better - because of the crowds and the heat.

title: Colosseum visit
text: It was really good to see the monument, was very crowded, better to go with prior booking in skip the line tickets, was very hot though, better to go before sun during summer

title: Sweltering!!
text: Was amazing to see in person but the heat of the day ruined the experience. Come prepared and I’d recommend pre buying a ticket x

title: Don't book with VIATOUR!
text: Paid over $300 CDN for a premium 'floor arena' access with a guide only to be told before we went in that due to high volume, we would NOT be able to have the tour we purchased.  I could have purchased the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Forum tickets for a total of 24 Euros for my whole family (and done the free downloadable Rick Steves audio guide).  Guides in Italy told us to take up the matter with Viatour at home. My first email complaint came back with 'sorry no refunds, sucks to be you', and then when pressed again, they offered $39 CDN.  Brutal. We'll never be able to get back to Italy as a family again so this was a real bummer.  If I had to do it again, I wouldn't bother with a tour guide, she even skimped on details of 'what the Gladiators experienced' fearing it was too gross for people to handle. THIS IS WHY I AM HERE...to learn this...

title: Skip the line tour with Headout - VIT Group - Chaotic - not recommendable
text:
After our first desaster with Headout for a Vatican Tour which they confirmed and later told us that it was not available, this was the second tour we booked with them and their operator VIT Group in this case. The start was a bit chaotic and the skip the line tour did not get off at 10:45 am as booked. We had to stand in queue for over an hour in the skip the line queue in 33 degrees to get in and had 3 different "guides" during this time. Then we were in they told us due to the delay the tour to the Roman Forum was delayed by 30 minutes which meant for us that we could not participate anymore due to other engagements although we had paid. We went to the meeting point to let them know that they should not wait for us as we would not be able to make it, but at 1 pm the meeting time we could not find anyone from Headout at the meeting point.
Headout never again

title: Beautiful
text: It is an amazing monument. It worth to be seen by someone. I suggest to someone to go inside  once to see the place but if you don’t have time don’t worry.

title: Disappointing
text: Waste of 78 euro for 2 with skip the queue and tour guide just save your money and walk round it I am sure their will be a point where you can look inside

title: Don’t waste your money by “Skip the tour”
text: After taking the “ skip the line” tour and waiting in line for 2 hours I would recommend taking the cab to colosseum, waiting in regular line and renting the translator thingy at the kiosk and call it a day..... save money and wait in the line as long as the people wait in the skip the line.... this skip the line is the biggest scam ever ... it’s not a fast pass not even close ....

title: Colosseum Visit
text: great experience and good place for tourists to visit if they want to do some sightseeing.  Besides the construction work it looked great

title: Beautiful but Busy
text:
Don’t spend all day there.
It will be busy but it’s big enough for some good pictures,
There are some nice food places and a water tap behind so you can get what you need.

title: Gotta go at night
text: Going at night was the best because there are literally no crowds and you can get a better feel of how eerie the Colosseo really is. Good walking shoes and be prepared to climb the most ridiculous stairs you've ever encountered.

title: long queues! alas, still a must see
text:
now.... I suppose you dont ever feel like you can go to visit Rome and not go to the colleseum and I would have to agree. 

however, apparently they have just installed new metal detectors and i kid you not there are two! teo for 3000 people at a time inside the colleseum.... why cant they invest in more????!

we pre purchased tickets and this 'queue' was approx 1 hour to get in. the queue without tickets was 2hours. most of it was unshaded and particularly heavy going in the Italian summer heat especially with continuous Indian men attempting to sell u ice water (likely to have filled up from a tap rather than mineral....but who knows!), hats, fans and sun umbrellas.... it gets very annoying after a while.

there is no visable shops near by or inside for any snscks/other drinks.... nearest place is the metro station.

when in our queue we were offered a guided tour which was promised to skip the line for a discounted price of 35€. which was 20€ less than advertised price and included guided tour of Roman forum/Palentine hill too. 

so we paid.... then waited another HOUR in the groups line to get in. I suppose the only slight advantage was this was generally more shaded and included lots of interesting information by our tour guide whilst waiting in the queue. 

when you get inside obviously it's very interesting and guided tour was good to offer some interesting facts. 

I would recommend a very early visit or a very late one to beat the crowds and consider a guided tour.... 

colleseum need to sort their security lines out,surely they are raking the cash in!!!!!

:(

title: Must see, book ahead
text: Awesome place to spend a few hours.  We went around 11am. Should have been there when it opened. It gets hot and long lines develop quickly. They are doing some construction in the arena so part of the lower level view is obstructed.  It gets quite hot and shade is hit or miss. If you do not have tickets ahead of time you can find plenty of onsite ‘skip the line’ vendors selling tickets. We did this.  They helped to shorten the wait, but did NOT eliminate it.  We still had to wait with a group to get inside.  Our ‘skip the line’ tickets included a nice and informative tour of both Colosseum and Palatine Hill.  We purchased from the guys in red jackets.  Beware of single individuals selling skip the line.  Scammers are in the mix.

title: Great Historical Place
text: I was so happy to know more about this museum and really recommended to visit Rome, I have really enjoyed my trip

title: Impressive & Iconic
text: A beautiful building which immediately strikes an impression; definitely worth visiting during a trip to Rome. Book ahead to reduce the time you spend queuing but do still expect to queue for a reduced length of time as there are robust security checks in place and a huge volume of visitors at any given time.

title: Buy the dang tour package
text:
We  chose to purchase the audio tour, and I really wish that we would have spent the extra money and gone with a Viatour or something because you could barely hear anything and it wasn't clearly marked what way you needed to walk. 

It is beautiful, but it felt more like being in a cattle chute with all of the other tourists than being in an ancient and awe inspiring structure. I suppose I could just pretend that I was there on the day of a popular spectacle, and chalk it up to part of the experience.

I would like to go back, because I feel like it should be better than it was.

title: We loved the skip the line!
text: We took a guided tour with Explore Italy Tours. They were wonderful. Provided ear pieces for my husband who wears hearing aids. We arrived 2 hours early and they volunteered to let us take an earlier tour. They allowed us to use the elevator inside (we are both over 65). The extensive facts they shared made a huge difference. Due to the heat we did not take the extended part of the tour. Well worth our time.

title: Very little is original
text: We had a brilliant guide who we paid extra for from an excellent agency to get in early and bat the crowds. The structure is impressive and there are great photo opportunities. However, our guide explained that almost none of the structure is original, with much of what you see clearly being quite modern and recently put together. Apparently the underground section is being hastily built up with no reference to the past as quickly as possible so as to earn more tourist dollars, which is pretty depressing. With hindsight get your photos with the iconic outside and move on.

title: Dont skip the line !
text: We paid for skip the line tickets for various places in Rome and we were robbed !  We paid on average 3 times the normal ticket price, and the only line we skipped was the very short one to buy tickets.  We still had to stand in the much longer line of tickets holders waiting to get in.  Never again !!

title: Must see- astounding!
text: Pictures don’t fo it justice. Couldn’t see under staging area- being renovated. Our guide was extremely knowledgeable and filled in many historical facts for our group.

title: Loved it
text: Highly recommend! best visit! Kids didn’t want to go and then when they went in they lived it! You should deff go and see rhis

title: AWESOME PLACE
text: This place is a must seen. The history of it it's simply amazing. I will recommend to do a private tour, we did and we didn't have to wait at all to go inside. The tour guide was excellent. I will tell you it is very, very, very hot in the summer time, but well worth it.

title: Amazing views everywhere!
text: My friends got engaged here!!! Will forever be special! There’s some cute drink spot near at night. The whole area is so walkable with so many things to see

title: Make sure have reservations-even better a private tour
text: We were able to book a private tour through European Odyssey and it was fabulous. We did not wait in the enormous line to get in. Then while we were inside they kept shutting down the Colosseum because it reached capacity. You don’t want to miss this amazing place so please precook.

title: Hot and sticky
text: We went on a tour in the afternoon and it was extremely hot.  We paid to skip the line but still waited outside for over an hour to get in.  If you’re going to go do the tour in morning.

title: Amazing building ruined by too many people.
text: Beautiful building, horrible queue times, go very early. Even with the safety restriction on population inside the beauty is ruined in my honest opinion. Worth it if you can skip the longest queues.

title: Ancient History
text: I went here with a Viator booking to see the Vatican and Colosseum. You check in for your tour across the street above the metro station and get assigned a tour guide and get headsets so you can hear them.Ours was Gio from Venezuela and she was excellent.  She navigated us through security right in.  The tour is a lot of walking up and down steps.  Thee is a rich history here and so much to see.  From here we went to Palestine hill. It was hot and Goi built in water and bathroom breaks.  More walking up hills and uneven surfaces.  If you can do it, the views are amazing.  To see the city, the ruins, and gardens are amazing.  Make sure you book the skip the line for this must see in Rome.

title: Could be skipped
text: It is certainly a historic place and could seem part of the top things to do in Rome but if you had the chance to see other equivalent and well conserved theatres, especially in Verona or even Nimes in France, this is certainly going to disapoint you. Too many people there and most of it is gone in side. Needs a lot of imagination to figure how things were in the past...

title: Great place to visit.
text:
We visited this amazing place as part of an organised tour via a local company, it didn’t disappoint.
The day was boiling, 96 degrees and fairly humid. Fortunately near to the colosseum are free cold water drink fountains to fill up your bottles.
The queuing was kept to a minimum by the tour guide.
Once within the structure we looked around the few levels but not on or under the main floor of the arena.
To think it’s survived earthquake, war, invasion, etc is in itself a wonder of the world in my view quite apart from the sheer size of both the structure and how it was built.
I’d certainly recommend a guide as the context , historically, that this supplies is essential to appreciate this building and others around it still standing.
I’m sure I’d enjoy it more in more manageable weather conditions and I’d spend more time there including a visit on and beneath the arena itself.
It’s a very busy place, so look after your belongings on the way there and back including in the arena itself.

title: Magic
text: Rome is magical .. Full of history. I am in love with this city and the Colosseum is a wonder in the world

title: Amazing
text: And amazing piece of history that can not be missed! Definitely spring for the skip the line entry. Regular lines can que for hours.

title: Very enjoyable
text: Great site and on the day we went it was Wednesday it was free admission not sure why but happy days it was a bonus

title: Excellent history!
text: You can view the Colosseum from area Road, the forum, and other sightseeing locations. Buy your tickets early and enter at the appropriate location. Be prepared to walk through a gauntlet of vendors selling water, hats, and other things. Security is tight, and only part of the Colosseum is open to the public. It is an excellent tour and review of this ancient structure!

title: Fantastic
text: Finally got to visit this magnificent building after only ever seeing it on film or in print. Not far off 2000 years old & it can still pull the crowds. It is a testament to the ancient craftsmen that the bulk of the Colosseum survives today although remedial work is ongoing.

title: Skip the line scam from a Coloseum entrance Guide Employee
text: Not sure how this happened to us but it did. We asked for some help from one of the employees with a staff shirt on. He took us over to another employee because a small tour was about to start in 15 minutes for 35 euros per person. Well... were informed that we just missed that one but there was another starting up in 40 minutes with a small group of 12. We decided to wait. She was 1/2 an hour late. When it finally did start everyone seemed confused as to who should be on the tour. There ended up being 38 people. Again... let me reiterate that the man who made the sale had on a STAFF shirt. Our guide proceeded to start the information. We couldn't hear a thing. We stood in the hot sun for another 45 minutes trying to hear what she was saying. She seemed totally bored. She was not very good at her job. At this time we watched literally hundreds of people walk in ahead of us. When it was finally our turn to go through security I was screamed at by the security guard IN ITALIAN for doing something wrong. What is was... I am not sure. Once inside this horrible tour got hijacked several times because we couldn't follow such a large group. We along with another bunch of people bailed. What a huge disappointment. Buy your tickets at the ticket office on site. Wait for the lines to go down and get your own self guided audio tour.

title: Amazing , must do , skip the line recommend
text:
This is a must do when in Rome.

It is amazing to see this structure and although and 1/3 left it is simply stunning.

I would recommend a skip the line tour as when it is hot standing outside for hours is not nice.

We took some great pictures and it is somewhere I will always remember going.

Due to the heat remember to take a bottle of water for everyone in your group.

title: GO WITH A TOUR
text: I went in the summer it was extremely hot but I recommend you go with italy wonders who I went with they were great the tour guide new a lot the colosseum was an awesome thing to see. The have gift shops there I recommend that you go with a tour have them leave you on the colosseum so you can explore it on your own.

title: Colosseum
text:
Breathtaking! I wasn't alone in feeling whisked back through time into ancient history upon entering the Colosseum - a must visit for anyone travelling to Rome.
Ignore the guided tours (€62) when entry is only €12 and you can meander throughout at your own pace and "eariewig" for free the commentaries of guides.

title: Entertaining walk to Collosseum
text: We walked 1km from Pallazzo Vecchio to Collosseum  and enjoyed the one-man bands on the way. Viewed Roman Forum at sunset on the way there. Take a bottle of water and snacks because expensive tourist trap area.

title: A MUST in ROME!!
text: Visiting the Colosseum at Rome is always a Unique experience and surprises me everytime, finding new details and apreciating even more our history and how this structure has impacted our lives in many ways by inspiring today´s architects and engineers. You certainly might have to wait  in line to get your  tickets but it´s worth it ALWAYS!!

title: A MUST VISIT
text: Despite the long wait its worthed. Even though the marble surrounding the whole building haw been totally removed the building is extremely beautiful. Just standing in the stairs and closing your eyes you can be one of the spectators in the Roman times. Its impressive. Somewhere was an secret exit where some gladiators use to use to vistit some of the beautiful ladies of Rome. Does is stand true? The guide said yes.

title: Colosseum
text: WE went on a Tour by Walks of Italy.  Amazing place. Learned so much. WE had a fabulous tour guide who was an art historian.

title: Magnificent
text: After many years of reading or hearing about the Coliseum I have finally seen it for myself. It is most impressive. The history associated with the Coliseum is very interesting, difficult to picture in our present day lives.

title: Paid a lot, and every penny was worth it!
text:
In late June we paid for a guided tour of the Colosseum, including the underground, and the Forum. Yes, it was expensive ($120 CDN per ticket) and it was worth it. First Mido met us just outside the Metro where he gave us a 15 minute tour of the outside of the Colosseum, and brought us around to the back where there was a separate entrance that allowed us to skip skip the line (more on skip the line later).

He then handed us off to Esmeralda who did a brilliant job bringing our group of 12-15 first to the floor, then underground, and then visiting the upper floors. All told we had about 2 hours on site.

We then met back up with Mido who brought us for a tour of the Forum where he did an amazing job of bringing us back millenia. 

Following our tours of the Colosseum and the Forum we went up onto Palatine Hill. Armed with the free cellphone service from our hotel we explored and read on our own. 

About Skip the Line: while we were in the Colosseum there was an announcement that they had reached capacity on site, and until people exited no other guests would be permitted in. As we were leaving we heard people in the skip the line line waiting for over 90 minutes. People in the regular line were waiting over 3 hours. Keep in mind this was pretty much the height of high season, the area under the Colosseum was crazy.

title: Loved the veiws
text: It was beautiful to see, the architecture took away our breathe, and we had a decent audioguide. Would advice making reservations before hand online, on places like the ticket bar.

title: Average
text: Colosseum was brilliant to see, but the queue to get in, and all the scaffolding all over the place let the visit down for us.

title: Tour with Guide Matej
text:
A great tour. Matej was very knowledgeable and did not rush us. Kept the group together.
He was very considerate about finding shade as often as possible on a very hot & humid day.
A very personable young man.

title: Not to be missed - a bucket list experience
text: I have had the privilege of traveling a lot but there was nothing like approaching the Colosseum from afar and then seeing it on the inside. Such a art of ancient and even modern culture. An amazing experience.

title: Huge and very impressive
text: A wonderful sight, a must see when you are in Rome. I'd recommend booking in advance as there is a big queue to get tickets on the day and this time of year it's very hot. Such an imposing sight!

title: Coloseum, skip the que with Mercedes.
text:
This visit to the colosseum being the dream of a lifetime I really wanted it to be rewarding, I can say that I wasn't disappointed.
