My guide a beautiful young women that really knew her history and detail, which enhanced the experience. Knowledge and her anticipating of the needs of her client was appreciated .

I could with her assistance I could imagine Ben Hur and Spartacus on their chariots racing around the colosseum. Really good value doing the "Skip the Que Tour"

title: Brought me back to the time of gladiators!
text: This is a very imposing and stunning structure in the heart of the city. It's very good that some parts are being restored - I am surprised that it is still standing after hundreds of years! Best time to visit is in the morning - and bring a lot of water!

title: 2 weeks European vacation
text:
The Colosseum was one of my favorite activities completed on our vacation. The history is unreal. 
BEWARE of pick-pockets in this area!! We saw 2 people get purses stolen!!  They work in teams!!

We bought our tickets from tour guides outside of the building! It was one of the last tours of the day so the crowd was minimal!

title: Gorgeous
text: Behind the wall their was a lot of history of Rome. If you are visiting during the summer it’s best to visit during the morning, and remember to take water because their is none there. Important reminder, buy ticket from safe websites, because we bought it online and we had a hard to get our money back.

title: Summer Vacation 2019
text: Awesome experience. We took a guided tour of the Colosseum and Palatine Hills.  Our guide was amazing! We learnt so much.

title: Breathtaking
text: You can not go to Rome without a visit to the Colosseum. It’s magnificent with breathtaking views. Definitely recommend a guided tour because there is so much history to this place. The only drawback to our trip was the heat - going in the summer months can be a little challenging because it is all outdoors so there is no air conditioning.

title: Must see but use a guide and go early
text: Must see but spend $50 and jump the queue, get straight in and learn about what you’re seeing. The area around the site is filthy, covered in rubbish and with dozens of Africans illegally offering tourist junk and water, all being constantly chased by the Polizia. Thousands of people decided to go it alone and waited 2-3 hours just to get in.

title: Highly recommended
text: This is a "must visit" tourist attraction. This was the second time I visited the Colosseum. First time we went with no kids so didn't mind waiting in line to get the cheap entrance fee. This time we traveled with 3 kids and it was 34 degr. Cel. so we decided to pay the fee to get a guided tour. If on a tour you basically skip the long line and you also have a good chance to learn something you might otherwise not have. During the tour I learned a lot about the Colosseum and the impact in history and it was worth the money. I strongly recommend to spend the money on a guide even it might cost you around EUR 35 for an adult and EUR 25 for kids 10 and above. I understand the prices can vary a bit.... Enjoy.

title: Highlight of Our Visit to Rome
text:
Visited on one of the hottest days of this year, 2019, but this was the highlight of our trip to Rome. 
Free access on the day of our visit which made the tickets we had pre purchased a bit of a waste of money. 
Longish queues but nowhere near as bad as had been predicted. 
Amazing structure which has to be seen to be believed. 
Opportunities to fill up our water bottles, which was greatly appreciated. 
A must, must do on a visit to Rome.

title: Crowded and hot
text: If you are planning on visiting the Colosseum or the Forum in the summer, i would highly recommend going in the early morning. It is very very hot after 10am, and the crowds and lines are like nothing you will ever see before. The sites are absolutely beautiful, just once again, get there as early as possible to minimize heat exhaustion and the crazy lines.

title: Tour with The Roman Guy
text: The Roman Guy tour had a very knowledgeable and fun guide.  She was inclusive to all of the mobility levels in our group.  Pick this company for your tour.

title: Transport yourself to the Roman era and let your imagination take over
text: So I, personally, preferred Palatine Hill, but this does not take anything away from the beauty and magnificence of the Colosseum. Just walking around the outside is quite a mindblowing experience. Once you are in there, you could be forgiven for being slightly underwhelmed at first sight. It does look magnificent but there is, as with any ruins, quite a bit of renovation work going on. If you just stand and take in the enormity of it, and let your imagination run away with you, you can see just how it all worked. There are a lot of information boards and objects of interest, so you can see how the heirarchy worked in Roman times. There is a lift at the back, but I'm not sure how disabled-friendly this would be. You absolutely couldn't go to Rome without seeing the Colosseum, my only regret was not seeing it at night.

title: Amazing history in the city!
text: Amazing to see something that had survived for so long! Especially surrounded by a bustling city! Unfortunately,  we were here during a heat wave, so keep in mind,  no shade inside,  and lots of steps (not great for my mom with bad knees!)

title: Wow factor
text: Recommend paying the extra for the tour, access to the underground and the arena floor is worth the experience. Plenty of water and time as to enjoy it fully will take 2-4 hours.

title: Gray Line Tours Colosseum and Vatican City tour
text: Was booked for the Vatican City and Colosseum tour in one day. Had hotel pick up with lunch included. Would recommend this to anyone. It was less burden to have them pick us up and since we were doing both in one day we elected to have them provide lunch. Lunch was delicious at the Sarticus (might have spelled this wrong) including lasagne, chicken, potatoes, salad and tiramisu. The Vatican tour was breathtaking. Paola did a good job with our guided tour and was knowledgeable but her English was a little broken and harder to understand. The Colosseum tour was awesome!  Jan did a fabulous job with that guided tour. He must stay in great shape to take this on. He was very easy to understand and took time when he was talking for longer periods to stand in the shade on a very hot day. Also made sure to have people refill their water from the cool water available.  This tour was worth it!

title: Great experience but long lines.
text: Beautiful architecture and history here. We went on a Wednesday morning and got there at 9:00. Rather than wait in the line we went to the side entrance for the Roman Forum and bought a ticket for a 10:20 time slot. We waited around then for an hour but we weren’t restricted to the long queue. All in all a good experience.

title: Spectacular tour!
text: We loved this tour! Being able to skip the line and go on the floor was a great experience. We would highly recommend this tour.

title: Really good
text: So, my family and I didn’t go into the colosseum, but rather looked at it from the outside. It’s beautiful, and a piece of history. You can also do tours, but we didn’t. It’s truly beautiful

title: What can you say
text: Superbly preserved piece of history that is accessible to visitors in the extreme. Large queues only downside.

title: Colloseum
text:
I guess Colloseum it self needs an explanation, but we har a really good experience.
We had prebooked tickets online, with the official site, and they included a guided tour and skip the line entrance.

On our visitday it was really crowded and they actually closed the site for more visitors when we where done.
Skip the line tickets is a must unless you like to que for hours in hot sunlight. They really workded, we didnt spend a minute in lines and the entire site is extremly well organised.
Our guide was wery nice and explained the history and buidling in a really good way.

Based on our experince I would say-Dont miss a visit when in Rome

title: Good trip
text: I had a guided trip to colosseum with underground and arena. We also visited Roman forum. I found underground most interesting however the wasn’t many areas reconstructed

title: Do not miss!
text: We were lucky enough to be two of only 75 people allowed in the Colosseum during a nighttime underground tour.  DEFINATELY worth the effort to book WAY in advance!  We booked with Walks of Italy for a combined Roman Forum/Colosseum tour.  Both of our guides were outstanding!

title: Awesome
text: This is a must for any history lover we booked a skip the line access all areas tour and it was worth all it's money and more would not have missed seeing this,

title: Love Ancient Roman sites
text: Had a bad experience with Roman Empire tours but other than that I had an amazing time there. I would advise anyone going there to purchase tickets ahead of time. Or at the entrance to the palatine hill.

title: Gladiators
text: The Colosseum is a magnificent structure that must be seen at least once in a lifetime. Differently book a tour that beat the lines and goes on below the arena viewing.

title: In the steps of Gladiators!
text: Must do the underground and arena floor with a private tour group to fully appreciate this ancient wonder. We went with What a Life Tours and they were fabulous. We got into places very few people were able to go and we felt privileged. The  Colosseum should be the number one thing on every person’s list who visits Rome.

title: Great tour of the Colosseum!!
text: This was my third Colosseum tour and once again it was a remarkable experience where I learned a few new details previously unknown. This was the second time I took the tour that also included the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum and was disappointed by how little you explore or learn about these areas while on the tour. Overall it is a great tour but if you want to see and learn as much about the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum as you do about the Colosseum I suggest taking a tour for just those areas as my experience has tought me this tour focuses mainly on the Colosseum. I'm hoping I have time left to take a focused tour of the hill and forum so I can get an in depth history lesson on these areas of Rome. Rosa was a great guide who engaged our group and she had a great sense of humor. If your desire is more Colosseum focused I highly recommend this tour.

title: Massive ruins
text: Went on a tour "Through Eternity Tours", and got to see and hear about the history of the Colosseum-definitely worth seeing!

title: Amazing
text: You have to visit it to believe the magnitude of this place. Pictures and online descriptions can’t do the justice. Must see

title: Great Place to Visit
text:
If you ever watch the gladiator or Spartacus you have to go to the Colosseum while you’re in Rome. This place is usually crowded so it’s best to book a tour on trip advisor. If you do that you will bypass the long lines out in front of the Colosseum in this will speed your entry.
The trip I booked was with City Wonders who does a pretty good job getting you around in a group tour.
When you’re inside all I can say is it’s spectacular. Enjoy

title: A must when in Rome.
text: It is a very busy attraction so definitely buy tickets in advance if not be prepared to wait for 3 to 6 hours in line.

title: Get an early start and know how the lines work
text:
This is definitely something you must see when in Rome. Once you get inside you can take your time, learn and enjoy the experience.  The history there is great and I felt they do a great job explaining all the history of it! There is also plenty of water faucets inside to fill your bottles.  For the overall experience it is 5 stars.....The one thing I thought was frustrating is there is really nothing that explains what u have to do when u get there and need to buy tickets...so if this helps one person I am happy :-).  when u get there I would suggest getting there early to get in line for tickets. when we were there on July 12th...there were two ways to get tickets on site. There are some ticket boxes about 50 yards from the entrance or there is a line that forms by the entrance gate (on the right side) which is a line for those who need to buy tickets. Make sure if u choose the one by the entrance, that u r in the correct line as it is not clearly marked (because one line is for those with tickets already and some people accidentally stood in the incorrect line ). We got our tickets at the ticket boxes about 50 yards from the entrance and we got a 9:40 entrance time. The ticket booths do not open until 8:30 (same time as The Coliseum opens) and we got in line at about 7:45am and we were about 25th in line. When we were done touring this site, the lines were crazy long...so I suggest get there early or get your tickets ahead of time if possible. If U HAVE A ROMA PASS you still need to wait in line and get a ticket with an entrance time on it!   
We also bought tickets (same ticket as the one for the Coliseum) that gets you into the Forum which is about a 3 minute walk from there and there was a minimal line of about 15 people when we did this after the Coliseum. Hope this helps someone and enjoy!!  
Also as a side note we did have the Roma Pass and got our moneys worth....but the bus system in Rome can be confusing and some buses take forever it seems. Sometimes a 7 euro cab ride is worth the time and sweat u will save.  :-). ENJOY!!

title: Take the catacombs tour
text: The underground tour takes to to places not readily accessible to regular tours. The views were amazing. Make sure you see it at night as well.

title: AMAZING
text:
A must visit sight
Very enjoyable 
Our guide was fantastic and certainly her knowledge was essential 
Such history to this amazing building

title: Only from the outside!
text: The colosseum is truly a remarkable site, with a budget in mind we had to pick and choose which destinations we took tours of, skipped the lines or entered.  This one we simply walked around and it was amazing just this way!!

title: Just seeing it was amazing
text:
We did not pay to go inside. It was super crowded and was expensive too. We were satisfied in just walking around & taking photos. 
Still amazed that it's a real place. The 'dumb American' thinking🙃

title: European vacation
text: A most see.  Disappointed that it was in bad shape, but glad they are trying to restore it.  It is old and amazing that it is still standing.

title: Must visit when in Rome
text: Your Rome visit is incomplete without visit to colosseum. We took skip the line guided tour of Colosseo and palatine hill. We bought tickets outside the building. Our tour guide was not knowledgeable- our bad luck. Keep on repeating same thing no matter what question we asked. But the grand view /architecture will amaze you no matter what.

title: A must see, get quite a lot with the ticket
text:
There is not much to say, it’s one of those things you just must see. 

We pre booked our tickets and got there early to avoid the queues, I definitely would not advise just turning up on the day.

A lot of it is under restoration which is disappointing but can’t be helped. You also get the forum included with the ticket which is good. Definitely allow 3/4 hours to see everything.

The one thing that does annoy me is when they then charge for an audiobook on top! The tickets are already expensive so too buy two of them does add to the cost, and without them because a lot of it is under restoration you get less from the experience.

title: Loved it!
text: I was very happy to see the history and what the romans built, this is a piece of art! I cant wait to get back!

title: Bucketlist Item Check!
text: This place is unbelievable!! The shear size is amazing doe the time it was built. Of course if you are going to Rome you must see this and it would be hard not to. You can walk around it without paying for any tour but I recommend getting one to go inside. The colosseum does its own tours and are amazing.

title: Be careful around here.
text:
We visited the site while doing a tour of Rome. To be honest we did not go into the building as we were told the queue were more than an hour long. It was a very hot day and there was no way we were standing in a queue in that heat for that length of time with small children. Outside the place was filled with people trying to sell you anything for a bottle of water to sun hats and I'd say if you were to ask the right person probably drugs as well. They were to put it mildly shady characters. They often didn't take no for an answer and I could see them being intimidating to more vulnerable people. 
All in all disappointing but it was good to see the outside if not nothing else.

title: This is a monument to Architecture and Construction Excellence of Ancient Rome
text: Built from 72 to 82AD this is an oval amphitheater in  the center of Rome. Built of travertine, tuff, and brick-faced concrete it is the largest amphitheater ever built. It could hold 80,000 spectators and regularly held games with 65,000 spectators. Even after being mined for marble and stone it still stands and is a testament to the skill of Roman builders.  Truly impressive and a must see place to visit. The line to get in is long.

title: Amazing
text: Purchase tickets beforehand if you can as lines are extremely long and weather is very hot.  We used a guided tour for this which was great as we saw many details we would have missed on our own

title: AMERICANS READ THIS REVIEW
text: Buy your tickets for a tour at the Palatine Hill. No line. Then walk to the Colosseum. You need a booked tour to save a lot of time and hear all the great information from an expert. It is an emotional experience. Check out the Irish Pub in the heart of Rome. The food is the best in Rome.

title: Summer trip & tips
text: * ARRIVE EARLY * I cannot stress this enough! They started slowing down letting people in around 11am. If you buy tickets ahead of time good for you! However I did not and there were no signs out that told you where the ticket booth would be.** the place to buy tickets is at the main gate to get into the colosseum so don’t stress it you’ll go through security first then buy tickets!! The colosseum opens at 830 we arrived about 810 and waited in line for about 45 minutes to an hour. Only paid €12 a ticket. We didn’t do a tour and honestly for this I don’t think you need one unless you want to go underground (that is the only way to see it) we bought a €5 audio guide in English and learned enough! They do have a lift for older people & disabilities. ** Beware of the people trying to sell you on guided tours that are not real guides that is a scam and i don’t want you to waste money! The colosseum is beautiful and can be done in less than 2 hours!! Bring water and enjoy!!

title: Amazing
text:
As expected! One of the most incredible places I’ve ever been. We had a great tour around the colosseum skipping all the very long queues which was worth every penny. 
And then on to the Roman Forum and palatine hill which was just as spectacular.

title: Worth the wait.... and there is a wait.
text:
I should learn my lesson but once again I didn’t pre book and spent 2 hours in the queue for tickets. (There was also a queue for ticket holders but they were going through in less than half the time).

I persevered because I’d had a glimpse inside and I couldn’t wait! Once inside... WOW. It’s just incredible.

Don't be taken in by the web sites charging €40-€50 a ticket. It’s €12 for the Colosseum and the Roman Forum combined. EU kids go free (also awesome, thank you Roma). You can book advance tickets on the official web site.

Inside and out ....this building has to be the highlight of my trip. Just the scale of it takes your breath away and it’s actually incredible to walk around. I didn’t do the audio tour (I heard it wasn’t working) but I will next time. 
Highly recommended.

title: The Best of the Big Attractions
text: MAKE SURE YOU BOOK ONLINE. That is one piece of unavoidable advice as you'll be able to easily get past an hour or two of queuing. The Colosseum is one of the must-see places in Rome and, in my opinion, it's the best of the bunch. While the outside is, like everywhere in Rome, haunted by hawkers; it's still a great area to sit and appreciate the monument that it is. Like all the tourist attractions in Rome it is packed with people, but, it's not as bad as all of the other major attractions and that allows you to truly take in the spectacle of the Colosseum. Make sure you book online and make sure you go

title: A repeat visit just as impressive as the first!
text: The history of the Roman Empire emanates from these ruins. You can almost hear the yells of the crowds. The presentations on the second floor give interesting insights. Plan on some extra time for reviewing them.

title: Impressive to see
text: Make sure book tickets ahead to avoid waiting in burning sun for long time. Great information and view you get there about the ancient history of Rome. Seeing sunset is really nice!

title: Interesting
text:
We took a small group tour of the basement with LivItaly (look for my review).  The LivItaly tour guide provided much background and brought an interesting historical place to life.  In my opinion, a guided tour is the way to visit this landmark.  Otherwise, it is too overwhelming.

Our visit was well-coordinated by the colosseum staff.  The facility was well marked and easy to navigate.

If you go in July, bring LOTS of water...it is HOT!

title: Fabulous
text: Spent a morning here on a trip aimed at children. Very informative. Weather extremely hot so glad we went first thing.

title: worth visiting
text:
so much queue but worth the visit
its a great building, from the inside you see the scale of it. 
you have to go to see it if you are in Rome

title: Book a tour and ditch the guide!
text:
So as much as I love Italy...its not the most organised place in the world....  Whilst you can book online, its hard to know which site is official and unlike most other world heritage site, the colosseum is littered with agents who buy tickets in bulk, which means the official sales office sells out their maximum for the day and you end up haggling with agents for the best price for skip the line tickets.  On a 36 degree day, this is the last thing you want to be doing.  Skip the line tickets with a guide are still the best way to get in, but prepare to walk to the sales office, wait to make a payment and wait another hour till they put you in a group and another hour for the security line...still this is better than 4 hrs in general admission, but there are hundreds of groups and it is absolute chaos. 

That said, once you do get into the colosseum, its pretty phenomenal if, like me, its an architectural marvel you’ve always wanted to see, but my advice would be to ditch the guide at some point and take your time wondering through as they guides will try to get you in and out as quickly as possible.

title: Truly magnificent
text: Great experience on a sunny day. Waited around an hour even with fast track tickets.  Not easy to get a taxi after the visit.

title: Amazing Feat
text:
Here you will come face to face with the potential of man’s intelligence. Colossal
building and remnants of the Rome of old.

title: Can’t be missed
text: What a place of history and culture in Roma. You can’t miss to stop by the Colosseo as one of the 7 wonders. Lot of works in and around but the view if very emotional.

title: Impressive!
text: We booked a tour to beat the queues - so glad we did. Incredibly impressive but as with all the sites in Rome there were so many other visitors it is harder to get that real sense of scale and atmosphere. I have also visited the Arena in Verona and there you get a real sense of how it was, whereas the Colosseum is less complete in many ways. It is a must see if you are there, just be prepared for the numbers of people.

title: Amazing trip
text: Must visit when in Rome. It’s truly a trip in to history.  Go mid morning after the crowds die down . Excellent experience

title: Where was the tour I paid for??!
text: The colosseum was amazing to look at but the daily operations was highly chaotic. No one could direct me to my tour and I had to do a self-guided trip. Where comfortable shoes

title: Not so impressive as expected
text: The Colosseum was in restauration and not all visiting points were opened. The most interesting places arena and the rooms below were closed.

title: Visit to Colosseum
text:
You have to look for a tour with skip the line unless you want to wait more than one hour in a very hot weather.

Great views but very crowded and you don't have enough time to take photos.

title: Expensive and stressful
text: Visited on our first  day, as it was our top priority. Could not locate the  guide . Meeting point was the Arch of Constantine, which was too vague as it was a sea of people. Eventually after phoning for more clarification, we spotted the guides .By this time we had been standing around in the heat  as we were told to get there early.  Just before we went through security my sons girlfriend collapsed and had to be taken to the ambulance crew, so we had to abandon our tour. We waited for them to come out and then joined them for access to the Palatine and the Forum. No refund forthcoming. We tried again two days later with a different operator. All went well at first .Quick entry this time,and the guide was informative. However,we were supposed to have time to explore after the talk,but she took us through the turnstiles, then realised her mistake. She spoke to the guard on the gate we had just come through, but he would not let us back in, so no photo opportunities inside. She said she would take us on to the Palatine and the Forum which we had also paid for, or we could come back in an hour to join the next tour. We had already seen this on the previous visit, and we were not keen to stand in the heat for an hour, so we left. Unsatisfactory and disappointing.

title: Recommend the underground, arena and first two floors tours but skip the extras offered by some tour companies.
text: Great opportunity to see all the Colosseum was by attending this tour. FYI: The tour company we booked wasted 1 1/2 hours of our time outside the Colosseum and actually duplicated most of the professional tour by Colosseum staff. Tour was promoted as two hours from 7-9 om, yet we didn't get to enter the Colosseum until 8:20 pm. Don't get tricked into thinking you have a two hour tour inside the Colosseum and don't pay extra for almost the same information provided by the tour company as the guides inside give you. Our Colosseum staff  guide was totally surprised that many of us knew the answers to her questions until we explained what had happened with the tour company. Many of us were disappointed at paying much more than we needed to pay to get the same inside tour as others and at having wasted the extra hour. The prosecco and snacks are a come-on and with most other tours you are the tour company guide does NOT give the actual inside the Colosseum tour. Buyer beware!

title: Take a guided tour, you will get much more out of you visit
text: How can you go to Rome without visiting the most iconic symbol of Rome's history. We did a combined tour of the Colosseum and Palantine Hill with Explore Italy Tours. It wsa excellent.

title: Still sensational but get a guide
text: We enjoyed our visit with our two boys. Just be prepared that it’s hot in June and crowded. We hired a private guide along with the skip the line access: well worth the expense! We were able to hit the Pantheon, Colosseum and Forum in one day without too much damage!  A lot of walking but so much to see.

title: Hire a guide which will let you go inside
text: Our colosseum trip was with Cosmos tour.It was in June,extremely hot,bus parked like 15-20 min walk away and when we reached they give it to the local guide which they told us was giving us “Around” the collosium tour.What! Why? She said tourist are not allowed inside,which was a lie because we can see tourist inside. From going around the collasium is not worth it if you cannot go inside and understand whats all about. Cosmos ABig No,they cheated us.

title: Loved it ...... but.....
text:
This is such an awesome place! So much history. But, needs more infrastructure to cope with so many people. We had a timed ticket and there was nowhere to tell us where to go. 

Definitely, need more signs directing people and more shade.

title: Wow - --  just Wow!
text: It lived up to everything I read about as a grade school kid - - massive place with so much history.   Get a skip the line ticket though - - there was already a 2 hour wait at general public opening time.   So glad we had skip the line pass!

title: It's THE Colosseum
text: July is hot. Real hot... but even in the extreme heat this place is amazing. There are water springs to fill up water bottles and to cool off. I recommend getting a private guided tour worth the extra money.

title: Coliseum, Palatine Tour with VIT
text: Pietro Paulo was our guide for this amazing tour experience.  He was approachable, knowledgable and very sensitive to participants needs on this very HOT day.  I would recommend him and this tour :)

title: Tour with Discover Rome Tours
text:
The Colosseum itself is beautiful and amazing with rich history. But our experience was ruined by some unprofessionals. 
We bought the Colosseum tour on the spot from the guys selling the service. We were able to get the tour for 30€, which normally is 45-55€ on the internet. We thought we were lucky to save some money, but we were wrong because the tour was very disappointing for many reasons.
Our tour was on 27.06.2019 at about 11:55. We were told that the TOUR will be for 1 hour each INSIDE Colosseum and the Forums, but it was less than an hour in each. The tour started at 11:55 outside the Colosseum, he was just briefing about the Colosseum. We were still in line by 12:30, and it finished by 13:06. Utter disappointment!! We were then told to roam around ourselves, and meet outside the Colosseum for the next tour at 14:00. Our guide did gave some interesting facts in this short duration. Lucky for our guide, that I forgot his name. He was an old man. He passed a very racist remark against an Indian guy in the group, very very sad!!
The Forums tour started at 14:20 and finished at 14:47, again, under an hour.
In summary, these guys seemed scams and not professionals. Better to buy an expensive one, for a better experience.

title: Nice visit but under construction
text:
We could not see much of the floor area because it was under construction. I suggest getting the headphones and going to each station to get a good explanation of the Colosseum.
Buy tickets ahead of time at Palatine Hill to avoid LONG lines.

title: Colosseum Tour
text: Great guide, highly informative and friendly. Our guide was quite knowledgeable on all things related to the Colosseum and Rome. Would highly advise this tour.

title: Get a guide
text: We turned up in July. Had not pre booked a guide. There are plenty of them. 15 euro each for a guide in a party of about 20. The guy was very knowledgeable and it really helped understand he history. I don’t think it would anything like as good an experience without a guide

title: Great hot Family experience
text: The tour guide is not so effective. I must say they need more studies because I have search more the colosseum than her.

title: Awesome
text:
Awesome. The tour including the basement and top level was well worth the price.  

There is a water fountain inside, but only one or two. No food, so plan accordingly.

title: Walk with the gladiators
text: What to say except WOW, if your a history fan, this will be a treat, a guided tour will be better, as you will get to understand more, a must if in Rome

title: As impressive as you think
text: This really is one of the most iconic buildings in the world. It really does live up to it's image. You have seen it in books and photo's a thousand time but to stand next to it is something else. Inside the history just seeps from the walls. All those gladiator battles etc and the fact it's been standing for over 2000 years. So impressive.

title: Book a tour!
text: Colloseum is definitely a must-see in Rome. I would highly recommend getting a ticket for a tour though as the line seemed huge and not ideal to be standing in when it's 30°. The tour we booked allowed us to skip the line and when we got in we got to do our own thing. Also got entry to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.

title: Reality check
text: Impressive to visit.  We took the Underground tour and learned about the abhorrent environment of Gladiators and animals and how they were injected to the amphitheater floor (as it was known at that time). The realization that the sole purpose of the Colosseum was for its entertainers to experience horrible deaths while Roman citizens watched for entertainment was definitely are reality check that made a stark, dark impression.

title: Absolutely Stunning and Moving
text: The Colosseum is a MUST see on any trip to Rome. Definitely buy tickets for a tour ahead of time directly through the Colosseum website. You will save tons of money and avoid the tons of people trying to hit you up for tours when you get there. The Colosseum website is not easy to navigate and can be hard to buy tickets, but put in the effort, and you will be rewarded. The tour is a must. It gives you context to what you are looking at and teaches you so much about the history and how it looked originally before being pilfered.

title: Night tour
text:
Night tour was amazing.   Virtually empty and we were able to take in the atmosphere.  No waiting in long lines in the hot sun.  More importantly, all the aggressive hustlers and hawkers outside are gone at night.  We only saw a few other tour groups there.  The underground is included in the night tour but the forums are not.  

We didn’t see any children.  I imagine that they would have been tremendously bored.

title: Very Nice tour
text: Beautiful tour with Roberto.  He was very funny and we learned a lot.  Very nice to not wait in the line .  Well satisfied.

title: Interesting with guide, brutally hot in July
text: If I had to skip one thing in Rome.... Actually don’t go to Italy in July! Amazing no one fainted or got sick. Interesting of course.

title: Must See iconic world site that Everyone in our family will remember! See a few great tips we learned!
text:
First off - it is worth the hassle, crowds and cost to see the inside of this important and iconic site in Rome. All
Of our family LOVED seeing it. But there’s a few things that made our visit to the Colosseum even better: 1) we bought reservations on line direct from the Colosseum NOT from a 3rd party site which saved us lots of $ and time. Basically we showed up 20 mins before our reservation time and asked the officials/guards working where the line was for reservations and they showed us & told us to come back 15mins before our reservations. We skipped the huge line waiting to go thru security to buy tickets (our security wait was 8mins) and we walked right up. Also the ticket was a combo & allows entrance to the roman forum & patheon which are adjacent to the colosseum for 48 hours after your colosseum visit. 2) we chose the last reservation of the day which allowed much more shade & wasn’t so hot. If my family were early risers I would hav e bought the 1st appointment of the day but teens like to sleep in. You can do something else earlier (like San Clemente 3 blocks away) or just pop out of the metro station which is right there from another part of town. 3) we downloaded a
Quality audio guide on our smartphones/iPods (Rick steves has a great free one) which made the site come alive. 4) bring lots of water if you visit in summer like we did (I brought a small reusable ice pack in my backpack that I could wrap in a small towel & was great at keeping me cooler.

title: Crowded
text: Go early to all Roma tourist sites. Know exactly where to meet your tour group if you book ahead. Can be confusing because of the number or variety of tour companies. Make sure you take water or a water bottle to refill. There is an elevator for those who need it.

title: A Spoiled Weekend
text:
What a disappointing weekend this turned out to be. 
Problems with the booking site led to us having to pay an extra £30 for our 3 day stay. 
The establishment was generally shabby. 
The Receptionist doubled as the chef on 2 of our 3 mornings and told us she couldn’t do poached eggs only ‘ordinary’ eggs. Ordinary being fried!! She also  told me to ‘cook’ my own toast. 
On our third morning she informed us she had no mushrooms because ‘chef had forgotten to buy them the previous day
She asked us if we wanted hot food in a tone that indicated she hoped we were going to say No. 
I would reluctantly give this establishment 1 star.

title: Must see
text: You cant go to Rome and not see the Colosseum, its truly unbelievable how its structure is still standing after all this time. Inside is pretty amazing with how it was designed to house so many people during events. Wear comfortable shoes especially if you plan to climb up the stairs to the upper floor.

title: A must see place when in Rome.
text: Wow. This place is one of the most impressive structures on the planet. Need to see this structure as a guided tour to get the most out of the visit. Being part of a tour also helps in streamlining you through the structure. Long lines. Showing up without a guided tour will consume many hours of your day.

title: Group tour
text:
Easily found spot for pickup, friendly staff, ANNOYING soliciting, tour guid was very helpful in explaining and compressing the history without needing too, overall experience was fantastic 
The ruins are well kept and amazing for being 2000+ years, an experience you can’t miss

title: Breath taking.
text: Great time looking round. Although the queue is long if you are keen to save money and from Europe bring ID proving it and you only have to pay €2 rather than the €12. If getting an audio guide get 1 between 2 cos playing out loud you can both hear it. Be sure to bring plenty of water, there is a fountain halfway down the queue so you can fill up rather than buying from the guys selling cold water.

title: Wow
text:
Fantastic trip.  
Awestruck by the magnitude of this place.
Booked a “skip the queue” tour and thank god we did. Queues for tickets were 2-3 hours in 32 degree heat, we strolled straight in. 
Lots of steep steps, not a place for mobility impaired.

title: My advice this summer - see the Colosseum at night!
text: We booked our night time Colosseum tour via Viator, and it was awesome. It was approximately 1.5 hours outside the Colosseum, then 1 hour inside. We met at  Trajan's Column, walked around to the Parliament buildings,  along the Forum, the to the Colosseum.  Our tour guide was animated, knowledgeable, and humorous.  The only downside was the silly 10 minute movie they showed at the end of the Colosseum tour.  Other than that, the Colosseum is a must see, and after reading so many reviews about it 'being so hot' I would take my advice and tour at night! (8:00 - 10:30)

title: Must see in Rome, but book in advance!
text: One of the most popular sites to visit in Rome make sure not to miss it. The history embedded in this building is worth reading about it and doing some research before going. You must book an audio guide or enroll in a guided tour to fully understand what you will see.  Lines are very long in the Summer (which is when we went), so buy your tickets in advance.

title: Colosseum guided tour with arena floor special access, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
text: My husband and I were part of a small group tour on 30 June and we had so much fun! Our tour guide Latitia was very knowledgeable about the history and culture as well as the architecture.  There was a lot to cover in three hours so the pace was pretty quick but we learned many significant details that brought the colosseum and forum back to life for us. I am so glad we were able to experience this with a guided tour, skip the line and floor access are worth it!

title: Worth having a tour guide
text: Mind blowingly huge - make sure you have your walking shoes and plenty of water. We got a guide and skipped the queue which was great. Can’t imagine what it would be like in the blazing sun. Incredible history from the Greatest ? Empire !

title: A site to see
text: Nobody should visit Rome without seeing the Colosseum.  We did a guided walking tour with a family of 5.  We loved the little speakers in 1 ear so you don’t need to strain or keep up exactly with your guide.  The centuries of history that are preserved here are incredible

title: Epic in a tourist trap kinda sense
text: The colosseum was cool to see.  Huge ancient building.  Fairly crowded and not much you learn by going that you can't learn about beforehand.

title: Simply stunning!
text: This will take your breath away!  Absolutely incredible and worth every minute.  Be prepared to wait in long lines unless you are with a tour group, then you go more quickly.  So many nooks and crannies to it and the stone up close is incredible.  They have a really amazing museum on the second level and gift shop, well thought out.  Loved everything about it!

title: Go late!
text: We were literally the last ones admitted into the colosseum (at 6:30pm). It was uncrowded. We have been there 2 other times, and this was our best visit!!

title: Do it with a guide
text:
There are so many people and we did a guided tour with jump the queue. 
We still had to wait a bit but nothing compared to those who weren’t in a guided tour. 
Get there as early as you can because the crowds just keep growing as the day goes on. We met st 8.30 and when we left the lines were insane. 
Take some change as there are lots of (annoying) street vendors selling water which is frozen and fabulous in the heat. 
It’s quite amazing the structure the romans built. I would love to transport myself back to walk around the city when it was in the days of the Roman Empire.

title: Definitely worth a visit
text:
We arrived at 0830 on a hot July morning and had to queue for over an hour before we got a ticket and were allowed in.
However it was only €22 for access to this, the Roman forum and Palestine hill for 2 adults! Excellent value for money.

The audio tour was not very exciting and not very informative. I think this cost about an extra €5 per person.

If I were to return I would book a guided tour. 

Beautiful site. Very busy. Information not great. But worth a visit

title: Fantastic
text: Make sure you book online before you go or you will be stuck in queues, we had a 45 min tour which was well worth it and then could walk around at our leisure. Our ticket included entrance to the forum etc and was valid for two days

title: Beautiful and must book tickets inadvance
text: Truly a piece of history. I can say now I have been to the Colosseo. Beautiful. It was truly worth every euro.

title: Amazing History
text: Absolutely amazing sites inside and around. So much history!  We had the full tour of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and the Roman Forum. This made for a very long and HOT tour...but it was all incredible to see. The line for those without skip the line tour was about a three hour wait the day we visited. The line was so long and brutal because of the high temperatures. Not many places to get shade. They let so many people in so be prepared for huge crowds. Overall it is a must see in Rome,

title: The best part of our Rome visit
text: We had a guided skip the line tour, and it was fantastic.  This was so much better than we even expected.  It was so cool to walk where the gladiators fought years ago.  MUST SEE!!!!

title: Colosseum with a tour
text:
This was a fascinating visit and I cannot stress enough the importance of booking a guided tour. I booked my tour with takewalks.com
Our guide was an archaeologist and was thoroughly knowledgeable on the colosseum. I personally would not have appreciated the relic without the tour. I think going solo does not let you appreciate the knowledge provided by an expert tour guide. I took my tour on May 28th a Tuesday at around 9:00 am by the time we finished the colosseum (this was a 3hr tour) and walked over to the Roman Forum the place was a mad house. Long lines, vendors and tourist everywhere, heat needs to be put into consideration so it’s best to arrive early. Make sure you take sun block so you can reapply because it’s lots of walking and take sunglasses and maybe a hat.

title: Can’t Miss
text: You really shouldn’t go to Rome without seeing the Colosseum. I would highly recommend purchasing a tour. They get you through the lines and security and bring the place alive.

title: Amazing
text: It felt astonishing just to be standing there. I also felt a sadness when thinking back on it's history but I'm so glad to have been there

title: Lovely from the outside
text: I thought this was far more impressive from the outside than it was from the inside. I wouldn't pay over €50 again to go inside & look at masses of scaffolding. No point in buying 'skip the line' tickets before you go as you still have to queue up to get through security (about 2 hours when we went in July). The only way to get in quick is to buy a guided tour. There are only one lot of toilets outside which cost €1 (not sure about the toilets inside, didn't need to go!). Be prepared to be hassled by people selling bottles of water & Africans selling bracelets.

title: A must
text: A must while visiting Rome ,a huge queue to enter both for security check and ticketing while you can skip the queue by purchasing tickets on line with additional cost , inside you will go back 2000 years .

title: Come on, what else can you say?
text: Awesome! This is a highlight of anywhere. Even if you don't go in. But if you do, it's great to have a guide tell you some interesting facts about it. What an amazing experience!

title: Must see
text: If you visit Rome you must see the Colosseum.  Amazing history in one landmark.  Best to see with a guide that can tell you more about this place

title: Few days away
text: Great place we booked with red bus tours so u get a time to get in and it's easy way of skipping the line it has a great  view  of the colosium  has lifts toilets  and lots of steps very nice you can book a guide which we didn't  try and go late if going  in July it's very hot

title: Good experience but overly crowded
text: Overly crowded , one time watch , best is to take hop on off bus and get off to see the surroundings looks good even in night

title: Gladiator gates
text: We took the 8:30 tour with Davide as our tour guide. He was full of information and he provided humor at the right times. He was very considerate of everyone’s needs and reminded us all to fill up our water at each fountain. It was warm out but there was a pleasant breeze. Entering from the Gladiator gates was quite nice to be on the same level as they would have been. I did expect a little more than just the entry at that level but there was t anything more. There was a lot of construction and excavation going on during our tour but it did not impede or progress. Thanks for a great tour Davide!

title: When in Rome
text: Why else do you go to Rome if you're not going to visit the famous Colosseum? I booked through a company online called GetYourGuide and had absolutely no issues. The guide that took us round was lovely and informative in the history of the building. Definitely recommend paying extra for the guided tour, not just for the guide but also the skip the hours of queuing! Check out hk_traveladventures on Insta for more pics of our Rome visit

title: Worth the wait
text: We waited in line for tickets for almost an hour. Stepping into the colosseum was a breathtaking sight. Even though there was lots if scaffolding work going on, the beauty was still there. Standing in this massive structure, you just could imagine the history that had taken place so long ago. We walked the entire structure, but other than that there isn’t much to do.

title: Must see when in Rome but view from the inside is more enjoyable
text: The crowds of people especially in Summer are crazy but it’s worth it to see this historical beauty. We went at sunrise and took photos before the crowds gathered. We also went on a tour of the inside and this was amazing. Far less people and greatly more enjoyable. Worth the visit. Enjoy.

title: Great place and not too crowded either!
text: We went there on a day that it was rainy and our driver told us that it rarely rained in Mid-July. This may have been the reason for the lack of tourists at the Colosseum as it was relatively empty. It was a great place and very historic. It was under construction when we went likely to restore it to its former glory. We did not do an audio or walking tour but there were informative boards everywhere, people to ask questions, as well as a nice view of the city.  Personally, it was not crowded when we went so this may have contributed to the fact that we were able to give ourselves our own tour. I highly HIGHLY reckoned buying tickets prior to going as the line for buying tickets was longer than for getting in to the Colosseum- and this was on a not crowded day. Over all, it was an incredible experience and goes without saying- it is a must see in Rome.

title: Incredibe
text:
We went on an Expedia excursion: "Your Rome in 1 Day: Skip-the-Line Vatican, Colosseum & Ancient Rome Guided Tour: 8:30 AM, Skip-the-Line Tour" so we visited the Colosseum last on our tour. It was so awesome!!!! It was incredible to be able to look with my own eyes at all of the ancient history the Colosseum holds. 

We luckily had the "skip-the-line" tickets, but we still had a 10 minute line. I would HIGHLY recommend skipping the line with a bit pricier tickets because I'd imagine the line for regular tickets is probably an hour or 2. 

I would recommend a tour as well if you're truly wanting to learn the history of the areas in the Colosseum. There aren't many signs around to explain the actual stone of the Colosseum or what went on inside.

title: Busy But Spectacular
text: We didn't get a tour so we had to walk round and see it ourselves, the entire place is jammed with tourists and people trying to get you to come on tours. When you get inside is really amazing and inspiring, the architecture is beyond beautiful. Fantastic experience and definitely worth going.

title: Road the train to the Colosseum
text: We stayed in the Flaminio District which was beautiful and road the tram and the metro to the Colsseum. We walked around the beautiful structure but did not go in for the tour since we had other sites to see this day.

title: As you would expect
text: Must see on your agenda if visiting rome. Beautiful and easily accessed. However crowd numbers are high, plenty of hawkers around trying to scam you and not alot of places to sit. Recommend a good tour guide to skip the massive queues.

title: Beautifully clean and very populated
text: So many people in the square taking photos.  the fountain is perfectly maintained and well worth seeing

title: Good visit, terrible service
text: Pre-booked our visit, and it was a good time saver. The visit itself was disappointing. First of all, only certain tour groups can visit most of the locked areas. The worst experience was the audio guide rental. It costs €5.50, but you won’t get your money’s worth. It will give you only 7 narratives. We rented two audio guides and one stopped working a few minutes into our visit. I tried to return it and asked for my money back, and was quite rudely told no. Very typical Italian “customer service.”

title: Colosseum
text: Wonderful tour of Colosseum.  Skip the line is the best thing ever.  With a guide, you learn so much.  Our guide, Alex, for Palatine Hill was awesome.  So knowledgeable and personable.  He made the tour a great experience.

title: Skip the line and video guide
text: We loved this visit. We pre-bought our tickets with the skip the line feature. It was worth it as the line at 13h30 was quite long. We just breezed through. We took video guides and we all enjoyed them, even the teens.the visit took about 2 hours and even in the scorching sun, it was pleaseant since therr is a lot of shaded parts.

title: Must see
text: You cannot miss out on the colosseum when visiting Rome ! Absolutely incredible just so interesting.

title: Better to visit at own pace
text: Tours explains the story and history but they have a schedule to follow. I find visiting at my own leisurely pace much more rewarding. Worthwhile to buy skip the line tickets

title: Vacation 2019
text: Visited the Colosseum and was a beautiful place to see we really did enjoy the tour it was a bit crowded however we didnt mind

title: Colosseum
text:
A must see if you go to Rome!

This is not expensive, make sure you get a tour guide, they give you great details and privileges to different areas. Make sure you take euros and not cards. Also bring a lot of water, your glasses, and hats because the sun beams hot here.

title: imagine walking onto the arena where gladiators once fought
text: skip the queue and entry through the east entrance onto the arena gave us the opportunity to look up into the audience, and down into the backstage area. Truly educational

title: Great history, very busy
text: The colosseum is very cool and unique. Definitely worth a stop for anyone visiting Rome and is pretty centrally located. Unfortunately it is crazy busy and pretty hard to get into unless you wait a long time in line. Also tons of people trying to sell you skip the line, etc. Overall worth a visit but keep an eye on your belongings going through those crowds!

title: When in Rome ...
text: An experience that simply can't be missed.   A private guide, if affordable, makes all of the difference by explaining the history behind the building and the event which occurred there.

title: Another fantastic visit
text:
This place does not disappoint and this visit we could see it without the scaffolding and additional screens.
Either book tickets in advance in peak season or look for a skip the line guide as standing in the long queue in the heat to be told that the tickets are sold out isn't fun. Certainly one of the wonders of the world!

title: Gladiator sized experience
text: Must see! Amazing history, even before this incredible structure existed. Definitely take a tour that takes you in the lower level to get a true feel for the planning, architecture, construction and experiences that the slaves/animals/etc. went through.

title: Simply amazing
text: The queue for this site is long but it’s worth it.  The Colosseum can not be described adequately.   You have to get close to it to feel it’s majesty.   You have to get inside to sense the grandeur.  And you have to look from one aide to another to appreciate the scale and to begin to imagine what this huge building might have been like filled with tens of thousands of Romans.  There are also good exhibits tucked in the sides.

title: WHEN IN ROME
text: How can one visit Rome and not see this???? Our tour had reservations so we got in pretty quick. The lines were already long at 8:30 this June morning. Walking into this building gives me a real glimpse into the size of this amazing arena. Imagine 70 to 80 thousand fans watching as the gladiators, slaves and animals battled below. This is a real must see attraction!

title: Great to finally see it
text: Gladiator is one of my favourite films and to actually have been to the colosseum and listen about the history and see it with my own eyes was brilliant. The queues can get quite long, so I’d advise you to purchase a skip the line ticket or get there just before opening at 8:30 as we got there at around 8:45 and still had to queue for just over an hour to get in and the queue was much longer when we finished our tour.

title: DON'T BOOK WITH TRAVELSPHERE!
text: We booked a £4500 holiday "Grand tour of Italy" with Travelsphere 6 months in advance. They waited until THE DAY OF THE TOUR to inform us via the tourguide that there was no possible chance of getting inside as to do that, you should book it back home in the UK. Once they took our money, they lost interest in us as customers. No one could pay us enough money to use Travelsphere again. Zero customer service skills. Also, for anyone who is disabled, forget Italy as they only want healthy visitors. no public toilets and no disabled toilets available in the whole country. BIG DISSAPOINTMENT. The colloseum would have been great to visit, had we had decent holiday company.

title: It's a must see
text: But it's very obviously a major tourist site so it jam packed. But you can't come to Rome without visiting.

title: Fascinating
text: It is one of the symbol place of Rome. You should definetely visit collesium when you go to Rome. You will be witness of history and amazing rome architecture. Unfortunately rome pass does not give fast track anymore, so my suggestion before you go to Rome, take your collesium tickets from internet. Otherwise you will wait so long in the queue.

title: You can’t go wrong visiting the Colosseum.
text: I enjoy the Colosseum when I have the chance to come to Rome. The museum part is great. The views are great. The history is great. Always remember to get you reservations in advance.

title: Colosseum even better than imagined!
text: What a great place to visit! They have the entry process streamlined. Clean restrooms. It was crowded but that was expected. The Colosseum is large enough for crowds to spread out and everyone get their selfie. They are restoring parts of it and it's wonderful to see their effort to preserve history. Definitely an awesome stop on our vacation!! There are lots of steps, but an elevator for those who need it. No food, drinks, vending on site though several people were walking around selling fans and cold water bottles. It is beautiful!

title: Wow
text: I have learnt so much more about the colosseum on this tour. Our tour guide knows everything there is to know about the Colosseum and it’s surrounding ruins.

title: Viator a TripAdvisor Company Tour
text: As beautiful, breath taking, exquisite, and grand the Piazza del Colosseo is ... unfortunately my family & I were unable to tour it as PAID FOR because of the tour guides at Viator.  My family of 22 people planned years for a trip of a life time to Europe!  We prepaid months in advance for the Skip-the-Line Tickets: Colosseum with Access to the Restricted Arena Floor.  We opted for a 1pm tour since we knew we were on a cruise ship & needed ample time to travel to Colosseum.  Our tour was MOVED from 1pm to 9am for no specific reason (over booked maybe?). As soon as we were re-schedule for 9am, We contacted this tour company to let them know of our predicament... we are cruising, cannot control the arrival time of the ship & we may be 5 minutes late but will be there .. they heard our concerns.   As we promised we rushed to arrive at the Colosseum, all together, no later than 5 minutes past 9am and no tour guide, no tour.  We arrived to other tour guides letting us know they have left.  We were not accommodated in any way. We didn't get split up on other tours, we disputed & didn't get our money back.  I don't want this happening to anyone else!  If you choose to book through Viator, please arrive 20 minutes early to not be left behind!  all 22 of us should have cancelled after they moved our time.  Very disappointed & I'm posting to help others avoid a very big let down & hugh disappointment.  We walked the city of Rome, alone, on foot - no tour at all.

title: It's the Colosseum, what else needs to be said.
text: Rome is a place you could spend month exploring and not see everything that is worth seeing.. The Colosseum is at the top of that list. Awe inspiring doesn't say it strongly enough. You have to see it to understand. We were there on a day with a few rain showers, which thinned the crowd a little. Utterly fantastic.

title: Much confusion
text: My brother decided to use the Roma Card to pay for entry. However, you still have to wait in a line to schedule a visiting time, which was never mentioned. Combine this with poor labeling or instruction at the Colisseum. Get yor ticket online along with a scheduled time. When your scheduled time comes be at the east end entrence to go through security. Ignore the long lines of people purchasing tickets.

title: Must see!!
text: A visit to the Colosseum when visiting Rome is an absolute must!! Amazing place, although it does get super busy with long lines. Try and book a group tour which will prevent waiting to get in.

title: Excellent Monument
text: It deserves to be one among the top wonders of world, an excellent piece of ancient architecture and very massive.

title: Colosseum in Rome
text:
The security at colosseum in Rome stole our watch on July 18, 2019 Very bad service
All guest check every. The management know that their staff not senserly

title: Disappointed
text: I came here expecting to see pugilistic bloodsport but instead I am baking in the sun with thousands of other tourists while some guy with a Greek accent or whatever talks about the Romans as if they were still relevant today. Also they didn't serve alcohol.

title: Iconic
text: The Colosseum is all about the history. We did an independent tour with audio ear sets. Being inside the Colosseum you can almost hear the roars of the crowds. Seeing where the people sat, where the animals  were let lose, the sub floors and architecture was amazing. I learned a lot that I did not know. Was very happy we went.

title: Dont go inside
text:
Enjoy this massive building from around and near! 
There is no need to go inside ! Waste of time and long waiting under the sun. 
Walking around  its something that makes you understand why this is one of the best wonder of the world

title: Great visit by golf carts
text: How do you make your visit to the Colosseum new and fresh? Arrive by golf cart! Our group ditched the bus and rented these awesome carts to drive through town, at rush hour, and stopped at a little park across the street. We took pictures as our tour guide went through her spiel. Awesome vantage point, then back on our carts to our next destination.

title: Classic Experience.
text:
I am not going to say a lot about this amazing archeological wonder.
Make sure you go see the Colosseum with a group. You will not wait in lines and you will learn so much more form the guide.
They started restoration procedures they basically remove the dirt that sits on it and you can see the difference in some places and its amazing. Because of the pollution, the colosseum has this grey color now but when is finished it will be so white and nice.

title: Did not disappoint
text: The Colosseum is impressive. A must see stop in Rome. We visited a couple times during our stay and enjoyed a guided tour. My only complaint is that I wished the city of Rome would do a better job at cleaning up and marinating the streets around the site. There were water bottles and garbage everywhere.

title: Pretty spectacular
text:
Such history. 
Backstory provided was a discussion of the book of Romans in the Christian Greek scriptures.
Helped us appreciate what life was like during the first century...
Sobering lessons.

title: What a experience!
text: Not so easy to go in.. be prepared for the possible lines.. but a wonderful experience for life. Better if you have a guide ( audio, person, book, whatever explains details)

title: A sight
text: We booked months before and when we arrived we waltzed past the queues and right into the heart of the Colosseum. I'd only recommend doing it this way in the summer as it became punishingly hot standing in the sun and the queues weren't covered.

title: Book in adnvace, take a personal guide
text:
The waiting lines are a headache even with skip-line tickets. Hire a personal guide from the internet while booking the skip-line tickets. Without a good guide the place is just a huge sand stone ruin. With the help of guide one can get a lot of information about the palce and it´s history. 

When they get ready the tunnels below the arena that will be interesting to visit as there used to be trapdoors and hidden entrances for animals and gladiators to enter the arena.

title: Amazing
text: This is definitely a sight to be seen. Stunning. Recommendation is to book your tickets before you go and pay a little more. The alternative is either queuing in the sun for hours or being hounded by touts for your business.  Do not buy from them, they are just doing what you can do on line and charging  you a premium to do it.

title: Amazing!!!
text: So much to learn. So beautiful.  What you think you know about this place, you will quickly discover more.

title: Really Amazing Structure!
text: Our first visit to the Colosseum did not disappoint.  We toured it through the Gladiator's Gate with Walks of Italy.  You really must see it from all levels, so I recommend an all access tour, you will be glad you did.

title: No need to go in. Believe me!
text: I spent hours and days researching what tickets are best to get into the Colosseum. From rip-off agencies that charge three times the official prices to the official website and all its many many ticket types. I went through hell getting the reservations because all my cards were being rejected. Then of course the queue and the dreadful so-called video guide. All in all we did the experience mainly to tick it off the to do list. But there is nothing that you can't experience from just standing outside and walking around this fabulous building. I know if you're looking for tickets, there is no way you believe me. I wouldn't have when I was desperate to get in! But the truth is that you can spend that money get in Gallery Borghese for instance which has fabulous treasures. Whatever you do avoid the video guide. It is a complete rip off.

title: hop on, hop off bus
text: magnificence of ancient Rome, you can see this on a hop on hop off open top bus but they do get packed, well worth the sight

title: Very busy and too hot
text: Of course it is a must to see but go during night,  it is so crowded and it is too hot during summer, it is still lots of people at night time as well but the lighting makes it more beautiful

title: Unmissable
text: It's hard to review one of the wonders of the world. A must-see! Seems well preserved and maintained. Very long lines without a reservation.

title: City Wonders Colosseum Tour
text: If you're booked on this tour, you're in for a treat! Coloseum takes your breath away when you first see it and its amazing to explore this ancient wonder! Tour guide was so knowledgable and was brilliant at catering to the needs of the group. She gave us small fans to use as the first weekend in July was a scorcher! After exploring the Colosseum we then moved to Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. We had a bathroom break as we entered Palatine hill/Roman forum and the guide was brilliant at making sure we found the shaded areas, and taking us to places we could fill up our bottles! The whole place is just incredible! I would recommend booking a tour with city wonders as this way you can go straight to skip the line.... Line was absolutely massive and people we standing in direct sunlight waiting to get in otherwise. I imagine because there's quite a bit of walking to do on the tour, they would be tired before they even began!

title: Top 5 attractions is Rome
text: One cannot visit Rome and miss this one memorable site. Lots of history in it. A must visit in Rome.

title: 2nd visit after almost 15 years and still in awe
text:
Visited for the first time in 2003 and was amazed. This year it was the same, the entry seems to be more organized and some additional signposts to better explain have been added. But the sights are still amazing to see. 

They have a special tour to the top levels but they need to be booked in advance I think or only available on specific days so suggest you check the websites for up to date details.

title: Magnificent
text: The Colosseum is one of the most famous tourist attractions in the world. Should you still go despite the crowds? Absolutely. Definitely do a guided tour, and pick one where you can walk out onto the Colosseum floor to get the full effect of size of it. There's nothing like it on earth. If you only do one thing in Rome, make it this.

title: Surreal
text: One of those once in a lifetime places you have to visit. Having heard so much about it and having seen pictures, the feeling of standing in it is still awe-inspiring.

title: Walking tour with group
text: We went with a group on this tour. Colin was our guide. He was very knowledgeable and experienced. You could have a chat with him if you do not understand something. I would definitely recommend him. Well happy with him. We also booked similar tour of Vatican City with him and we were not disappointed. It turned out better than expected

title: Nice but crowded
text: Nice but crowded. If you want to enter quickly you must book the tickets from a site, an example coopculture. Best time to visit is it in the morning because you cannot enter if the Colosseum have reached 3000 people inside.

title: Don't miss it!
text: This is one of the world wonders that you simply must see in Rome. Our group tour went early in the morning and had one of the first admission times, which was great because it was not yet crowded. Definitely go early and take your time to enjoy!

title: Really interesting place
text: We did opt for the guided tour since our lake of planning resulted in standing in line for tickets. Not much different in price.

title: Colleseum
text: Well worth it. Especially with colin is hungry.  Very informative educated and worth ever cent . Prepare to walk as its huge

title: An amazing Ancient Roman attraction
text: It is best to go with a tour guide as the history is so rich.  Standing there you can envision what it would have felt like filled with people, and the gladiators, the prisoners and the animals who were all tortured.

title: Gladiators, here I come
text:
If only to see what this would have been like back in the day.  Highly recommend booking a tour (official tours were available to book on the first day of the prior month).  I joined the underground tour and was so fortunate to see beneath the 'playing surface' as well as up high as we climbed up.

Does require a lot of standing and some stairs, so you need to have reasonable mobility.

title: Iconic building
text: Used a fast track option to gain entry with a group and think this was helpful as the Q’s were very long, got the standard entry which allowed access to the ground and first floor level, there’s an exhibition on the first level to view and the views from around the colosseum are amazing, there is options to go to the lower level of the colosseum which I think would take if visiting again

title: Great tour
text: We booked a guided tour ( Kolosseum and Forum Romanum) with skip the line tickets. This was a great experience and the three hours of the tour were packed with fascinating details and great sights pointed out to us by our tour guide (from the Agency Enjoy Rome).

title: Magnificent as always, but Avoid summer if you can!
text: It has been 2 years since I have returned again to Rome.  Last time, I was able to to walk up to the Collisseo at about 5 pm and buy tickets easily.  Now, the lines stretch for a very long way, at least an hour plus long in hot Rome heat.  Sad to say I would buy an advance ticket for the next visit. Even trying to get a "skip the line" ticke which are always far more expensive than a normal one,  from Viator or other travel site was impossible while we were there.

title: Well worth a visit
text: We got there at 8am before it opens at 8.30 and the Q was not too bad at all. Once in it is well worth seeing, I really enjoyed just being in there and walking around although there are audio tours and other tours if you want them. Lots of people trying to get you to buy tours while you are Q’ing so be careful of them, otherwise all great.

title: Guided tour
text:
There is no real way to do this tour without a guide to provide you the history you need. 

It is amazing but you must do a guided Pantheon tour first. This will provide you with the history and understanding of the Colosseum. 

I was a bit disappointed as it was under repairs but do the Gladiator entrance to walk through we here the Gladiators entered. Then walk through the ruins to experience the whole area and history. 

Must do with a guide.

title: Enjoyable
text: Enjoyed our time here. Make sure you book in advance ASAP!!!! It books up even a month before! We arrived for the 8:30 slot when opening and it was the best time to visit. When we came out at about 11am the queue to get in was nearly half way around the colosseum, also a reason to get the earliest spot. If you have bought young people’s (18-25 EU) tickets online you need to go to the main queue by the colosseum to verify your age with ID. Would recommend an audio guide just so you know of the history and different areas of the colosseum.

title: Must-to-see and visit
text: You can't miss visiting the Colosseum in your life: it's simply and extraordinarly amazing! Rome greatness exam0le

title: Colosseum never disappoints
text: This was the second time we'd visited using Walks of Italy and were truly equally as fascinated this time.  Learned new stuff and loved it all.

title: A must visit
text: We visited in July, so we were dreading the crowds and the lines but we were pleasantly surprised. We had purchased a ticket online the day before, with an entry at 8.30am. We arrived at 8.15, collected out tickets, moved to the security check line, picked up our headsets and we were walking around by 8.40 - seamless. When we booked online there were no open tickets left but there were tickets with videoguide, so helpful to open up your search. I would recommend the videoguide, as it provided a Good general introduction to the colosseum.

title: Impressive structure, confusing entrance
text:
A must-see in Rome! An enormous and extremely impressive structure that is dripping with history. Make sure you book tickets in advance, as the queues for those without tickets were enormous at midday - not a fun place to be in 35 degree heat with no shade! 

Quick tip - if you can’t find an entrance ticket for your desired time on the website, try looking for ticket with your guide on a different part of the menu. It’s a few euro extra but that’s how I got entrance in advance - all of the slots without guides were taken up a week in advance for all five days I was in Rome! The guide itself was average, but the main benefit was that I actually got the ticket in advance, which was well worth the few euros extra. A quick tip about the guide - it actually starts 15 minutes AFTER the time on your ticket!

One minor gripe - the entrances are not at all well signposted for those with advance tickets There seems to be multiple entrances with different requirements, and scrums at each of them due to the confusion. Clearer signage and prior instruction on the website/via email would be helpful. Make sure you turn up well before your allocated time, just in case!

title: Rome trip must visit
text: This is a definite must visit!! If you do no pre-purchase your tickets on line for this you are a fool!! The lines for ticket buying were crazy long!! We chose to not have a tour guide for this and I think we did fine. Also you have choices as to what areas you want to be able to go into (upper areas for panoramic view or down below where they kept gladiators and animals prior to fighting). We did the regular entrance. Lots of excavating going on in the middle of the colosseum. They only let 3000 people in at a time so you must pick the time you would like to go. Enjoyed. Stayed about 45 minutes. We went at night too to take pics from outside.

title: Impressive!
text:
Visiting the colosseum is one thing you must do in your life, the history of the Romans is amazing.  The colosseum is very impressive!  We didn’t go inside as there were more people there than at the San Siro for a football match!  We spent an hour walking around it with a Rick Steves audio tour.  
When we return we will definitely arrange a full guided tour of the inside when the crowds are low maybe in the cooler months.

title: Very Pleasant and Learned SO Much!
text:
We recently (July 2019) took two small group tours with Europe Odyssey. We could not have been more pleased!!! The first tour was the Coliseum in Rome. This included the Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. We toured the Vatican another day with the same company. Our tour guide, for both, was Rahul. He was magnificent. We sent up a cheer when he showed up again, a few days later, as the guide for our Vatican tour. Rahul is very informed, kind, and he knows how to find shade, water, bathrooms, and avoid the crowds when possible. We went right up to the front of the line for both tours and loved, loved every minute of both tours. We learned so much and Rahul made it pleasant, personable, fun, and do-able. My head is now full of new things I learned. We will cheer Europe Odyssey Tours and our tour guide on and on. Rahul knew just how to make it perfect for us. His knowledge and the way he presented the information was very helpful. We continue to comment to one another about how pleased we are that we took these two tours with Europe Odyssey and with Rahul. It was SO worth it!!! 

Hooray  for Europe Odyssey!

title: History
text:
We visited early in the morning with pre-booked tickets. Picking up our tickets was easy and entry into the Colosseum was swift. Truly impressive sight. Our tickets also included entry into the Roman Forum and Palantine Hil, which you can spread out over 2 days but we did it all in one. 
I am very glad we went early because by the time we left the crowds were becoming very overwhelming!

title: Amazing historical landmark
text:
A visit to this iconic historical structure is a must.

We booked skip the line tickets (worth it) and included an audio guide.

The information was interesting, easy to follow and plenty of detail.

The monument itself was amazing.

title: Amazing place!
text: This was truly an amazing place to see both from the outside and inside. A lot of people there but it was not that bad when we visit the place, maybe we were lucky. To walk inside of the colosseum was a really amazing and cool feeling. It was just as we expected it to be. The only negative thing I can say was the long que to get in there, but it did pay off waiting. A must to see!

title: Amazing but avoid the queues
text: An amazing site. Try to come early to avoid the crowds. I think you can also appreciate most without having to queue and enter.

title: A Must To See !!
text: To think you were in a place where Romans were thousands of years ago - just unbelievable. If you have any level of interest in history, the Colosseum is a must!  Hopefully you'll have an incredible tour guide like my husband and I did (Alaria).

title: Awesome monument
