text: The monument is astonishing. They have a limited number of people that can enter the monument at the time. The place is safe and they have free water and bathrooms. The stairs may be a challenge to older people. Some parts of the monument are vandalized.

title: Another unmissable sight
text: It is probably the iconic image of Rome, a bit like Big Ben for London, the Eiffel Tower for Paris, or the Opera House for Sydney. Whether you enjoy the Colloseum from the outside, or take a tour, or stand in a long line to get in, it’s magnificent!  No ifs or buts.....it’s magnificent and, well......colossal!!  You can’t not go here! Fabulous!

title: Amazing
text: Stunning , must visit. the scale of the Colosseum is incredible our tour was a night one, so quiet and, you got a real sense of the place.

title: Day trip to Rome
text: Fascinating place - a must do. Try to pay for a tour through the Gladiator’s Gate. Very, very hot in July but fantastic.

title: Family Vacay
text: While it is a must see because of the fountains beauty, historical significance, and to make the famous wish.  I rated this a 3 for several reasons: way too crowded and zero crowd control.  People are rude pushing and shoving to get to their picture and while there is security it is only to tell people not to sit on the fountain.  Plaza Navona was a much better experience.

title: Awesome
text:
It was what it was the queue was hellish way to long and even with the hop on hop off bus tickets that are ment to let you skip the line no you wait in the same queue with everyone else 
Best tip hunt out the tour guides and pay them they will get you through the waiting which depending on the day can be very long when inside it gives you an idea of what it was like but they have alot of areas fenced off so you cant go to everywhere 
But still enjoyable

title: You have to see it in person!
text: The remains of the Colosseum are breathtaking if you are in the right mood and are interested in ancient history. This was a highlight of our stay in Rome. Try to go with a guide or find a tour group to join, as you will learn much more about it that way rather than just wandering around. Incredible to see and feel and experience in person!

title: Guided tour
text: I did a guided tour with a friend through coup culture. The Colosseo was worth visiting but the tour was not.

title: A must see, but overrated
text: Although we had to wait quite long to buy tickets and after that to enter the Colosseum it's worth a visit. It's recommended to book tickets in advance!

title: Obvious must see but get help
text: Most people walk thru the Colosseum, check it off their list, then move on, but there is so much more to see & experience.  Get a good guide book or audio guide, dig into the history of how and why it was built, and it's impact on Western culture and thinking.  It's truly one of the wonders of the Roman era, but impacted our thinking for centuries after.

title: Must see!
text: The Colosseum is the symbol of antique Rome. Its a must see. Oh, btw always take a guided tour - it will save you the long waiting on lines and will actually help you understand the building and the historical context.

title: The Epitome of Savagery
text:
In going to the Coliseum on a guided tour, i realized how barbaric the Romans and now there decedents are (that is most of the western world.) their pass time was witnessing the gruesome slaughter and dismemberment of people by people, people by animals and animals by people. For fun! It isn't far from the movies, sports and video games we as westerns consume in alarming quantities now a days. Mortal Kombat, Resident Evil and House of Velez as well as movie titles like Evil Dead, Cannibal Holocaust and others. How have we evolved? 

The site historically is very cool snd you should find a good tour operator and skip the ticket line. I believe the Palatine hill and valley below are as amazing a site and tour as the coliseum.  Check out the video and photos.

title: Great guided tour
text:
Informative
Interesting 
Not too long 
Well priced
Book on coopculture the official colosseum website 
Skip the line aswell 
Family of 5 was about 70e

title: Colosseum with 5 kids
text: Went with my family: our party had 5 adults and 5 kids (ages 4-10). All managed to enjoy. The adults definitely more than the kids but the kids got through it. Stopped for mid-morning snack (second breakfast) at Cafe Cafe which helped a lot. Also bought kids books at gift shop which helped keep spirits up through the forum.

title: Well, you gotta see it of course
text: We took a gladiator floor tour. We enjoyed it and it started early enough in the day that we weren't miserably hot. It's alot to take in.

title: FREE ON CERTAIN DAYS!
text: Really cool, makes you marvel at what they were able to build 2000 years ago considering it stills stands today. PRO TIP: on certain days throughout the year, this and the Roman Forum/Palatine Hill are FREE. We coincidentally were planning to go on the feast day of Rome's patron saints and so the whole collection of ruins was free. We showed up at 8AM and even though the line wrapped around the Colosseum, we were inside within 20 minutes. **PLAN A TRIP ON ONE OF THE FREE DAYS AND GET THERE EARLY** and if you do, you won't spend a dime.

title: Long queues if tickets not purchased before
text: Nice place to take pictures and an important place not to forget in Rome. The inside is almost similar to what it looks on the outside.

title: Very nice
text: Instead of waiting in the sun for 2 and 3 hours I suggest you take a tour ticket for Colosseo and roma palatino. You get the full experience of having a tour guide and learning many things for a good price!

title: Breathtaking remains of the Roman civilization
text: We visited the Colosseum in July 2019. Not the best time to visit because of the crowds and heat. However, the visit was worth it. We avoided the long lines by getting skip the line tickets. The process worked well. In all, worth a visit any time of year.

title: Do not go without skip the line
text: While it's possible to visit without a guided tour, you will learn so much more and have a much easier time. Its worth every penny. Also make sure to go early in the day while the crowds are lighter and the day isnt as hot.

title: A great tour!
text: Lots of walking, so be prepared. Very informative and personable guide. Unfortunately the audio cut out several times because the crowds were so thick that we couldn’t always keep close enough to the guide to be in range.

title: Pititful experience but interesting visit.
text:
My family and I had pre-booked tickets online for a tour on the top floor of the Colosseum - 30 minutes before our tour began, we tried to search for the place we could collect our tickets. After a long time walking around, we found the place, where they told us our tour was canceled due to renovations on the top level. They provided us with a €20 refund, which was nice, and gave us tickets to a non-guided visit to the rest of the monument which was due an hour later.

We waited an hour in the shade outside when suddenly around 20 Indian (I presume) street-sellers started causing havoc. They carried bags of water bottles, selfie-sticks, hats, portable chargers (complete tat) etc...

They would constantly approach us and everyone else saying "water-water". And when you ignored them, they got angry and started getting rather shouty before walking away. 

For around 40 minutes around the area, awaiting our tour, these people bothered us. 

For those who would consider buying the water off them, they should be wary as they only charge €1 and the bottles they sell have their seal broken - presumably filling up used bottles at the nearby water fountains. 

These people usually hang around the Colosseum and the park nearby. They leave litter everywhere. Police don't care and honestly, after being approached every 5 minutes, it gave us a bad start for our first visit.

At one point, an old woman, roughly 60 years old; toothless, barefoot, hobbling with a stick, dressed in ragged clothes approached us with her hand out. She stood by us for roughly 3 minutes, begging for money - my family and I ignored her. She went to all the people sitting on the benches around the Colosseum before giving up with her huge purse (presumably full of money) on her shoulder and walking back past us saying "tutti merde" (for those who don't know what that means, it means "all s***". She then ran up the long stairs away from the Colosseum with great speed on her "injured" leg, walking stick in hand. We were glad that we didn't give her money after that.

Soon after, we went through security very quickly and avoided the 2 hours non-ticket queue. We looked around and it was very interesting but very busy.

All in all, it wasn't very enjoyable due to the beggars and streetsellers.

title: Lovely Landmark - Recommend Using Roma Pass
text:
The Colosseum is a must-see in Rome, however, I highly recommend purchasing a Roma Pass to gain entry. This Pass, available as 48/72hr passes, allows free entry into the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum - but you MUST book online to avoid the hours of queues. It is very easy (and not more expensive) to do this, and it also allows free public transport and free entry into another museum.

The Colosseum itself is very beautiful from the outside. The inside is interesting and contains informative exhibitions, but the outside is far more impressive.

title: Amazing story
text:
I don't recommend going in July or over the summer time. It was too warm and we did not finish our tour. 

We ordered a tour through some guys that were working on the street and we had to wait until the day after for the tour. We recommend booking in advance. We had an amazing tour guide in the Colosseum itself. I can't remember his name but he was kind and knew a lot about the Colosseum. He had to leave us with another guide for part two of our tour and the guide that greeted us there did not talk at all and just went running though the people and we kindof lost her. We decided then that it was too warm and also because we lost the guide, to turn around. If you go, go during winter time.

title: Worth the visit
text:
Fantastic well worth the visit 
Be warned there lots steps to climb as no lifts 
We went with tour guide which is well worth the money as not only u get to see n hear all about the colosseum but you skip huge I mean huge queues

title: Don’t wait in queues.
text: We bought tickets to jump the queues which was better than standing for at least 2 hours , personally I think this was far better from the outside than inside , as it was all scaffolding inside which spoiled it for me .

title: Great start - From the outside it looks wow!
text: Great start for our short Euro-trip, first time for the kids. It was very warm in Rome, but the best thing they have here is called 'Gelato' , so we ended up eating cheap, delicious ice cream the whole day long. No objection from the kids. The Colosseum is impresive from the outside. After one hour of waiting we managed to get inside, but we were a little disappointed, because there is nothing much to see. I guess you need to use your own imagination to make it work. We stayed for ten minutes and left. There are many ancient Romans walking around to have a picture taking in traditional costumes, so we had our pictures taken with one of them, which I'll post a bit later. This was a great way to start our trip and the Colosseum is definitely not something to be missed.

title: Gap year sights
text: Very interesting to see the colloseum something still there after so many years. Although it was very crowded and very hot. Don’t recommend going in July month

title: Dont waste your time waiting in the line
text:
Ticket queues were quite long as you would expect on this time of year and as it is partly under reconstruction on the inside it was not worth the few hour wait. If you can and truly want to go inside, buy a ticket beforehand. If you dont want to plan ahead that much then i weuld recommend enjoying the building from the parks around it.
Ps. There is trash everywhere around the monument and I dont understand why is it so hard to keep it clean considering the ticket price and amount of people visiting each day.

title: Walk the perimeter.
text: Much of the inside of the Colosseum is covered with scaffolding as it is undergoing major work.  Until this work is completed it is not worth going inside when there are so many other things to see in Rome.  Do walk around the outside perimetre, which has had a 3 year facelift.  You can see what it looked like before the work began, as some has been left original, and also the reconstructed parts, which return it to it’s former state.

title: Great history and definitely a must visit place in Rime
text:
We had a private guide and saw several videos and went over the history and visited both floors.
Bypassed lines due to the guide which I would recommend for all places Italy in summer

title: Awe inspiring!
text: We stayed not far from the Colosseum  and it was amazing to turn the corner and just see this world famous structure there in the street! As we had purchased tickets online, we turned up and just had to queue in the security line. Some if the steps are quite steep to climb (especially if you're only 5'1") but it's almost beyond comprehension that you're in this amazing building where so much happened centuries ago. It's worth taking a drinking bottle (especially the collapsible type) to fill up at the drinking fountain within the colosseum.

title: Incredible to see
text:
Our family loved seeing it. We went in
On the gladiator floor level. It was incredible. We would recommend it.

title: Awesome experience
text: We had a guided tour of this historical place- so no standing in line!! Our Tour Guide was very good and quite knowledgeable about the history of the Colosseum.  Although it was crowded, our guide would find little nooks in the Colosseum, in order for her to give us the history.

title: Fascinating and massive!
text: Lots of walking in the sun during a daytime tour, but so cool to see this ancient marvel! I recommend a night tour during the summer months.

title: Early Morning Skip-the-Line Tour
text: We booked an early morning tour (Walks of Italy) of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum. We are so glad we did! The tour started at 8:30, when it was still cool and not yet crowded. (The tours are allowed express entry into the Colosseum before the gates for everyone open.) I got so much more out of visiting these sites with the tour guide than I did when I visited the first time 17 years ago. Plus, being able to skip the lines, avoid much of the crowds, and beat most of the heat was well worth it.

title: Went to an after hour tour with roman guy
text: Do the after hour tour with Roman Guy...So quiet and no lines. Very interesting. Have a lot of beautiful picture of the Colosseum and not just other travelers lol

title: Skip The Line
text: Used viator.com to book a skip the line colosseum tour.  It was great and the lines were sooo long, that I was thankful we were able to skip the line.  It started raining as we were leaving the Colosseum, so we didn't do the Palatine Hill.  Our guide was great and spoke good english.  Be careful around the colosseum.  People drive really fast and there are several vendors trying to sell tourists everything from bracelets to phone chargers.  Watch you bags.

title: Italian Holiday
text: It was awe inspiring to see the colosseum in person. It was under construction so that muted  the experience but it’s still a must see. You should plan ahead buy your tickets ahead of time. It saves you from waiting in the long lines

title: Tour
text: This was one of the reasons we wanted to visit Rome and this skip the line tour was AMAZING! We learned so much and also saw the Forum.

title: Must Visit
text: Easily accessible by bus captivating and overwhelming with it’s grandeur and historic impact. Lots of crowds, long waiting to enter the site. There are lots of tour guides that provide on site ,an approx 2 hours skip the line tours for about 20 euro per person. Definitely worth the visit buy be prepared with water and hats during the summer months :) enjoy

title: Iconic attraction
text: Possibly the most famous attraction in Rome, this was my 2nd visit and it was still an awe inspiring experience. we'd booked line skipping tickets before we left the UK and it was a good idea.  It was incredibly busy there with people queuing in the intense heat for hours. We had a guided tour which was very informative. The Colosseum is a must visit but booking line skipping tickets is strongly advised.

title: Great to see but sooooo many people
text: Went here today and lucky we had a private guide. Would not go otherwise as may have to wait in line for up to 2 hours in the sweltering heat. Felt safe with visible security, and they limit number of people entering for security also. Make sure you are the early admission. Interesting facts about ancient Rome, and magnificent structure. Also great information museum on second level.

title: Amazing place - dream come true.
text:
One of the places I've wanted to come and visit for many years. I guess the passion to do so came via assasins creed game play. 😊
The visit was great, I used a app recommended by a friend - Tiqets which made the process easier.
The Colosseum was magnificent to see, and the preservation, restoration effort is good.
Lots to see and take in. I would definitely visit again, maybe onetime if the lower level is open.

title: impressive but not exciting
text: did not live up to the hype. it is hugely impressive for what it is and for surving through the ages but much of this tour was based on your imagining what it would have been like. a guide is crtical to explain what all the different thing were and their significance as what you are looking at is RUINS.

title: 40th Birthday trip
text: It’s a must see no matter the lines. I hate lines and crowds; but this was worth it. The history of it and that it was built in it time is amazing.

title: Amazing
text: Crazy to see where all the history took place. How this place was built by hand. The area is fascinating to walk around.

title: A MUST, WHEN IN ROME
text: I and family had been to the Colosseum before, but never on a guided tour. If I were to give  an advice here it goes: take a guided tour including the Palatine. You will have the chance to know amazing details regarding the architecture, the games, and the real activities that according to late discoveries took place at that arena. Take your camera (or cell phone camera)and, from time to time, take a sport to register all the magnificent beauty of roman engineering. At the Palatine, you will see ancient roman quarters and apartments, streets, and if the guide is experienced, will let you know about the roman way of life back at the pinnacle of the roman empire.

title: Fabulous tour
text: We did the three and a half hour tour of the underground and other floors of the Colosseum. Our guide was Magnola and she was great. She presented  the history and facts in a light hearted and fun way so it was interesting and entertaining. It was wonderful.

title: A must-see
text: I went down to the colosseum and just bought a ticket from one of the vendors on the sidewalk.  The bigger companies charged about 50 euros but I found one for 35 euros.  It was a little disorganized and delayed but eventually we had a good guide with a microphone and earphones so we can hear and follow.  The place is PACKED so you'll be fighting crowds but we made it through the tour guide line fairly quickly.  This tour included the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum but a second guide was to meet us in half an hour.  In summer, it's very hot walking around.  I was hot and tired and didn't feel like waiting so I skipped the second part of the tour.

title: Colosseum
text: We spent a great day out at the Colosseum and The Forum. We were worried as we didn't manage to buy tickets before we went. We were given the tip of buying our tickets for the Colosseum at the Roman Forum instead of queuing at the Colosseum itself and it worked out perfectly, no queue at the Forum at all! All in all the Colosseum was a must to do whilst staying in Rome.

title: amazing, but overrated!
text: this was the place my boyfriend and I were looking forward to the most, and it was incredible! the outside of the colosseum was one of the most extrodinary view ever! however, we purchased a tour with 'tiqets' and the ladies who ran it were lovely, but the inside was underwhelming in terms of the view ( a lot of construction currently taking place) but we really enjoyed learning about the history! it is a must see when visiting Rome, but if history isn't your thing don't waste your money on paying to go into the different areas!

title: We went with walksofitaly VIP
text: We visited with Walks of Italy tour and it was amazing, we had skip the line tickets of which we highly recommend, because it was hot and the lines are huge.

title: Exciting views
text: Our first trip to Rome we took a guided tour of the Colosseum. we are very pleased with our tour. we definitely recommend guided tour which skips the lines..and tour guides are experts in the history.

title: Coluseum
text: Amacing place to visit. Where you can learn alot of history. We had an amazing guide, who told the history in a exciting way. He had a good sense of humor.

title: History
text: This place is unbelievable! The history behind it is fascinating! We walked around about 6pm and it was pretty quiet, we paid 12€ to enter and 5.50€ for an audio guide which is all you really need! The crowds were smaller at this time making it easy to see everything and get some great pictures without having to wait long at all.

title: Awe inspiring
text:
This is my third time in Rome, once in my early 20’s and late 20’s and now in my
Mid 40’s. The Colosseum never ceases to amaze me, took my breath away when I saw it. I think because I’m much older now I appreciate the history and I’m so grateful that I got to see it again.

title: All in a days walk
text: Too hot when visited this place. However a great historical architecture and a must visit. Make sure you have water available with you when taking a round of this place , else will be drained out

title: Everything you expected
text: We went towards the end of the season in October and it was still very busy. We had not paid in advance or paid for fast track, but because we got there so early we got straight through with only a handful of people in the queue in front, as opposed to the fast track queue which was extremely long. The ‘colosseo’ itself is everything one imagines it to be. However, if you do not join a tour group you are limited to where you can go, for example only your groups go on the stage and at the highest point of the building. Although it was an amazing experience, it was just as you would picture it. You have to go when in Rome because it is a staple, but not somewhere I would go again. Fabulous overall!

title: In the center of history
text: This is  a place with lots of history. It is the most visited monument in the world, and it is mandatory to visit when in Rome.

title: Dream Visit Spoiled
text: Paid for an excursion from our cruise to visit one of the most famous historical sites in the world. However when we arrived we were constantly bombarded by street peddlers trying to con you in to buying bracelets , selfe sticks and other tacky items.allegedly tro help their poor family back in Kenya. This was a really sad day for us as we had really looked forward to the whole experience for years but instead eventually left the site due to the constant pestering every 30 seconds or so . Surely the local government and police can deal with this constant pestering , instead of looking on with their arms folded watching. Please be aware of this when you are planning any trip to this iconic site.

title: Just wow!
text:
We booked a tour with Via Tour.  The tour starts near The Forum and our guide Renata expertly took us on a 40 minute walk past the piazza Venezia, Compidoglio and Forum.  During the walk, about half a mile, we were given headsets so we could hear our guide clearly above the traffic noise. 
The Colosseo is impressive, especially at night and again, the tour here is fascinating particularly the under arena areas.  A couple of things to bear in mind if you want to see the Colosseum.  The ground is uneven in places.  Some of the steps are steep, although an elevator is available.  Our tour lasted 2 hours in total.  Booking in advance highly recommended, also consider going on a night visit - less crowded and not as hot

title: A real wonder of the world
text: Beautiful place and a must see in Rome. Extremely busy and worthy of an proper arranged tour otherwise you can only see the outside. Inexpensive though for entry compared to some other sites in Rome. The building is beautiful and the view from the open top bus tour is fantastic.

title: Wonderful
text: Went on a pre organised tour with a guide. Definitely best way to go as you skip the line to get in. Queue was over an hour the day we went and really really hot. People were standing in the hot sun. No shade so an umbrella helps. Water fountains inside to refill your bottles. Huge crowds. A guide makes it interesting.

title: Amazing must-see attraction but crawling with scammers and shady people outside
text:
What is there left to say about the Colosseum? It's amazing, a must-see attraction on any visit to Rome. I had pre-purchased a reservation on their website for their own guided tour at 4PM. Since one of the tickets was free (for a child), unfortunately I couldn't print the tickets at home and had to line up anyway at the ticket pickup windows, however I'm sure this line was less busy than if you had no reservation at all. The tour was scheduled for 4PM but the guide didn't actually show up until 4:15PM which left lots of people wondering where she was. Apparently it wasn't made clear that the guide meets you 15 minutes after the scheduled time. Once she was there though everything was fine and she provided an interesting introductory tour. 

Once you're inside the Colosseum everything is fine however be warned that it's a definite circus outside full of scammers and pickpockets and shady types. It was the worst attraction in all of Rome for this that I experienced. There are groups of African bracelet sellers that are going around trying to scam tourists. If you don't know about this, just Google "African bracelet scam" and you'll see what I mean. These guys (and some women too) aggressively will go up to tourists and pretend to be their friend, asking where they are from, asking for the time, etc. and then try to sell them or even put on their wrists cheap little bracelets they are selling for a premium (10-20 euros for a dollar store item) and they get really mad if you don't buy it. If they manage to put it on your wrist by force they expect you to pay for it! Luckily that didn't happen to me but it was almost amusing seeing them approach and try to scam unsuspecting tourists, it was relentless and really took away from the experience because you always had to be on your guard. 

The one negative that did happen to us while we were sitting around waiting to get in to the Colosseum is this man came up to us to ask us the time. I already knew this was a scam so I said "No thank you" but unfortunately my husband had a watch on and he couldn't hide it. The guy asked to see his watch and I was afraid he was going to snatch it off his wrist so I kept saying "No thank you!" and he got angry and defensive, alluding that I was suspicious and rude to him because he was black. He kept going on and on until I finally pretended to wave to someone I knew in the distance and ignored him and then he walked away in a huff. No, sir, it wasn't because you are black but because you are an obvious scammer. I saw him later re-join his group of buddies trying to scam tourists. It's too bad the police here don't do anything to keep these guys away from tourists, it really affected my experience of the Colosseum and Rome in general, as I was constantly on guard.

title: A piece of ancient history worth seeing
text: Definitely a “must see” for anyone visiting Rome!  A busy sight seeing place, but well worth your time to see first hand.

title: Worth the wait!
text: Even though we got to wait 2 hours in the sun at lunch time, this place was definitely worth it! It’s beautiful and well-kept and you can truly feel and imagine its history while inside. The entrance for all of the 3 locations were extremely price worthy as well.

title: Coliseum
text: Too hot but very nice tour with Muora from Roman Guy tour service. Lots of walking so pack water bottles and maybe sunblock during summer months

title: Colosseum
text: Opted for skip the line tour with arena floor. It was great. Very interesting commentary from Barbara and just the right length. Amazing that even 30% of the original survived until today.

title: Family friendly
text: My family (2 adults and 2 kids, 3 and 1) enjoyed our time at the Colosseum, forum, and Hills. The colosseum was stroller friendly and had an elevator to take us to the second floor.  The forum and hills were also stroller friendly but in some parts the walkway was made of cobblestone and we did take it up stairs. We had the skip the line tickets and it took us less than 10 minutes to get through security and the ticket checkpoint. The line to buy tickets was long So I’d definitely recommend getting them in advance.

title: Hot in July but worth it!
text:
This place is extremely hot in The summer months - highly recommend buying prepaid tickets before you make your trip - we were able to skip all the line and they just moved the barrier aside for us when they saw our skip the line tickets. But from coopculture website the official website not the tour guided ones!
The colossium was under some construction when we visited and they told us they would refund part of the ticket price since we could not use the panoramic part - very good of them to offer the part refund but obviously would have preferred to have seen the whole place instead but also understandable that sometimes they have to make restorations for the place to stay open in the future.

title: Interesting place but the staff could not organise a Mass at the Vatican.
text: We had purchased online two tickets for the Vatican. With a time slot of 10:40 On arriving , at 10:00, we saw huge queues of people. We asked somewhere where we should queue , showing them the email we had. They told us to join the long queue ( over 2 hrs ) so we did as told. We we got close to the ticket desk we saw there was two separate queues , one of us went off into the one lapelled reservation desk to ask.  we where told that this was the queue where’s where supposed to join and we had now missed our time slot and wouldn’t be allowed in. The woman at the desk said we can take the risk of the 10:40 ticket and see if the guards let us in round the corner or we could buy a new ticket for 1pm ( it was now 11:45 ) so another hour and a half waiting in the sun. We too the risk of the 10:40 ticket and where allowed in after explain the situation. Once inside everything was as expected but the organisation outside the place is atrocious.

title: Beauty of art
text: It was really amazing tour, very nice experience, spectacular place to visit. I would suggest getting tickets online.

title: amazing part of history
text:
This is a must do place if you visit Rome. It is very busy and you have to wait in a long line. We were told you should not allow the random people around trying to give tours to give you a tour...however we did that and we were fine. We may have overpaid but our guide was very knowledgeable and we got to wait in a much shorter line. It's worth just walking around the exterior prior to entering to get an idea of the size and grandeur of the place.

This place is huge and for how old it is seems pretty well maintained. There are areas you are not allowed to go, but plenty of places you can. You can walk around the seating area. You can do a special tour to see some of the underground holding areas. There are also areas outside of the seating area with displays with info about the history. A gift store is also in the area with some very cool books (among other things).

It was cool to see how the elevators to bring up people and animals worked. There was an area under renovation and it was cool to see how the seats were made. It was interesting to learn how the Colosseum was built. 

It is an outdoor venue, so when it's hot it is hot, just be prepared.

title: When in Rome
text: Amazing tour . Tour guide was very informative and imaginative. Well worth seeing with a guide to better understand the history of the Collosium.

title: Giovanna the best guide
text: Nice experience with an excellent guide  Giovanna from gladiator tours! She made our visit worth it! Thank u Giovanna

title: Got a skip the line got right in.
text: Buy your tickets ahead they work great.  Tour guide was really good and provided so much information we didn't know.  Worth every penny!

title: Avoid July if you can
text:
Hot. 
Crowded. 
Amazing, if you can get past those things. 
Careful if you book a tour. The company that we used - City Sightseeing, the hop-on-of bus company - were well below par. 
Read some reviews.

title: Very crowded and hot
text: Of course the Colosseum is an essential site to visit and it is truest amazing. But it is very crowded and busy and hot.

title: Amazing history- able to walk back in time
text: Would always recommend taking the tours instead of trying to fight the thousands of people in the summer.  The tour provided us with a stress free way to get into the facility and take in all the most important points- and guided by an actual Archeologist!  Very good experience.

title: Absolutely beautiful
text:
We visited the colosseum in July. 

It’s a must do! 
But be careful of the sales men outside, they give you free bracelets, chat to you and then insist on a tip after but the one we spoke too would only insist on a 20 note! 

Take lots of fluids as it gets super hot. 
Also would recommend booking a tour guide.

title: A must see piece of history
text:
We visited the Colosseum as part of a skip the line tour alongside the Palatine Hills and Roman Forum. I would recommend doing the seem to ensure you avoid the queues for the main feature.
Let’s be honest this is probably more impressive from the outside than it is inside. This isn’t to belittle this historic gem it’s just that this is an ancient relic that Rome is doing its best to maintain. As such there is a lot of continual work and parts that you need to use your imagination. I would recommend you use a guide to get the most out of your visit. 
You probably don’t need much more than an hour to enjoy

title: Jaw dropping
text: 100% recommend doing the evening tour to gain full access to dungeons, arena and top floor of colosseum. A guided tour really gives so much information which my family and I really enjoyed. Seeing the colosseum lit up at night was amazing.

title: Excellent organized
text:
If you book tickets on advance it works very well. If you buy discounted tickets (e.g. children) then you have to pick them up in ticket office but that did not take long. Do not go without pre-booked ticket. In general everything is well organized and handled well.
You find information and book tickets on prenotazioni@coopculture.it

title: Impressive but a bit less so inside
text: My daughter’s ‘bucket list’ included the Colosseum so we went whilst visiting Rome.  It was such a sight to behold - although I had clocked it from the plane on my flight in to Rome. The building is huge and very impressive from the outside.  I wasn’t as impressed from the inside and there’s a lot of construction work going on. There were no queues really as they keep numbers limited to 3000.  I had a reservation and got in within seconds. There are toilets and water inside.

title: A superstructure to marvel at but be prepared for big crowds
text:
Its a moot point to say that this is a must see in Rome. In fact this place is absolutely synonymous with Rome and would be first on everyones itinerary. Hence be prepared for massive crowds as you prepare to be stunned by this superstructure that defined the glory of Rome.
ProTip: Get the Roma pass to skip all queues and target going at 9 am during opening hours when the crowd is less. 
Tickets: Preferably get the Roma pass and ensure this is one of the places which is in the free to enter zone. Essentially just ensure that you enter this place while the free entrances remain on the pass. Roma pass is available at all major train stations and airports in Italy and at all the attraction sights in Rome but I would recommend getting it from the stations since the attractions will be crowded
Entrance: Be sure to ask the guards for the Roma pass queue. There is a special line for this which has literally no wait time and you just walk all the way up to security. They will check all your bags so best to carry minimal stuff to ensure the process goes smoothly. When you come to the audioguide section you encounter all the crowd as there is no queue here. Be prepared to wait here a bit. The tour will start from the second level and you need to climb some flights of stairs to get there. 
The first view of the colosseum from the top section will be the most riveting as it reveals the whole panoramic view and the enormity of the structure. You then take a full turn of the second floor and wind your way down to the ground level where you get a closer view of the dungeons under the colosseum. Don't miss the look out point into Nero's palace located on the second floor. An audioguide is highly recommended if you are doing this tour on your own. Otherwise you will be lost in the crowd. 
There are some alternate paid tours like the dungeons tour or the colosseum by night tours. I haven't been to any one of them but they attract lesser crowds partly because the tickets are limited and its more expensive. If you want to avoid the crowds you may pay to buy these tours. But whatever you do, when in Rome this is an obvious one not to be missed. Pair this up with a visit to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill right beside it.

title: couldn't believe my eyes.
text: Place was massive.  It is best to get ticket from a tour place.  We used I love Rome Tours.  You get to skip the lines.  Not waiting for ours to get in. Quite a lot of walking.  Stairs very steep to get to each level.  Bring a sun hat.

title: Amazing, but underground not worth the money
text: I wish we had only gone to the main portion of the Colosseum. We paid for the tour of the underground and was disappointed to find that the one hour tour only went to a very small section of the underground. Didn't really think it worth it.

title: we loved to visit colosseum
text: the day after before we did the colosseum and its awosome to see panaromic view from the inside and standing view from the outside , nice place to visit .

title: Feel history under your foot
text: Amazing! Only 8 years to build it! Best project, which I know :) coliseum shows you real story of people world.

title: A MUST in Rome!
text: A must while visiting Rome!  An overwhelming experience to see this incredible piece of history.  Go early to avoid the heat!

title: Great tour!
text: Book a skip the line tour or you will be waiting in line for 3-5 hours.  We booked through Viator and had a great experience.  Our guide was knowledgeable and funny.

title: Beautiful and Impressive
text:
The Colosseum was even more impressive than I had anticipated it being and it was quite overwhelming from the outside. 

We arrived a little earlier than the time stated on our pre-booked ticket but enjoyed the time admiring the building from the outside. A lot of restoration work is going on inside and it is being preserved very well. There were some interesting muesem artefacts to see which provided some shelter from the open heat.

We had tickets which allowed us to go into the Roman Forum and Paletine Hill which I would recommend doing too - avoid standing in the queue in the height of Summer!

title: Impressive
text: Loved visiting Colosseum, preferred doing it with a friend than with a guide. It was very impressive, need extra time to take it all in, and not rush around it, and preferably not in July when it’s very hot. Plus wear comfortable shoes and clothing, and make sure you have lots of film in your camera.  There is usually hundreds of people there as well, so be prepared and patient.

title: Great!
text: A great place, the proof of glory of the Roman Empire. Although someone may disagree with the brutality of this type of spectacles, the place is fantastic. It’s located in the heart of the ancient city, Roman Forum etc and it is amazing to imagine how this place could look like in the days of its glory. You definitely must see it.

title: Amazing
text: We booked guided tour tickets directly through Coopculture.it for a fraction of the price of a third party tour.  We lined up outside for a total of less than 10mins before we were inside, although we had to wait inside for our tour which actually routinely starts 15mins after the advertised time.  The lady guide brought to life the happenings of years before with a very in depth tour of the Colosseum and the Roman Forum.  It was a very hot day and she arranged the tour to include as much shade as possible.

title: Ah, we wasted our time and money.
text: FULL to the brim with tour parties; we did not get to see what we paid for, and I could see more on the History Chanel than we saw here. Dangerous climbing up and down those stairways with so many people pushing and shoving. We went afternoon. Avoid that timeslot!

title: Colosseum and Roman Forum Tour
text:
We had a very pleasant experience using the Trip Advisor/Viator tour for seeing the Roman Forum. We went on this tour the morning of July 9th and it lasted 3.5 hours. Our very knowledgeable tour guide, Chiara met us at their office which was easy to find. Chiara was so friendly, knowledgeable and approachable. She went out of her way to meet our needs. There were several young children in our group and I loved the way she included them and made them feel so special!  I would add one tip for anyone going on this tour: wear comfortable shoes as you walk on the cobble stones around the Roman Forum.  I give this tour 5 stars as I give Chiara 5 stars too!
Karen Nutsch

title: Beautiful
text: I loved the colosseum and think it was my favourite part of Rome. Even just sitting outside was beautiful

title: Mr. U. Gouws
text:
The Romans were amazing. They were so up front of their time.
Guided Tour also very good. The humidity was very high in Rome when we visit and had to take a lot of fluides in order to stay hydrated.

title: Great!
text: Of course it is a must in Rome! You can feel and see history before your eyes. Just avoid going during summertime, it is too hot and there are too many people!

title: Cancelled Reservation With No Explanation
text: I booked our tour months in advance to have the perfect tour and time, due to the heat in the middle of summer in Rome and they cancelled it, with no explanation, forcing me to book a different tour in the hottest part of the day, in direct sun, which was a 3 hour walking tour and almost caused heat stroke. I would never book with them again!

title: Yes, you have to visit!
text: Of course this will be most people’s highlight when they visit Rome. Next time I visit I would be tempted not to do the guided tour because we were only inside for an hour. Iconic! Lots of places around to refill your water bottle on a hot day.

title: Great cultural monument
text: Great part of the history that is a must if you like history and are interested in culture. Buy the tickets at forum romanum to save time.

title: Viator .... Your way tours
text:
We booked a guided tour to the colisseo to skip the línea and save some time we arrived to the meeting spot on time..... on our arrival our “tour guide” told us that our tour was cancelled and we were going to start 1:30 later just like that with no notice and temperaturas over 30 Celsius and no shade..... never boom a tour with these people

Nos dejaron plantados sin avisar! No contraten tour con ellos

title: A must visit
text: No visit to Rome would be complete without a visit to the colosseum. If you can, it is worth visiting after about 4pm as the long lines have died down and you can get in quickly. The lines earlier in the day are massive. It’s about 12 euros for entry to the colosseum and the Forum. Amazing experience.

title: Must see
text: The experience to walk on ground where men have been fighting for their lives is something one can hardly understand. The cruel world in those days was made romantic in movies but in real life must have been dramatic. Men and women treated as slaves for the benefit of the wealthy. Incredible!!

title: Stunning everytime
text: My 4th visit to Rome and 4th visit to the Colosseum. A must-do in Rome. Strongly recommend booking in advance for the first time slot of the day. The audioguide is also well done.

title: Surprisingly Handicap friendly
text: My wife and I visited because it’s the thing to do when in Rome. However, having been a frequent visitor to the Arena in Nîmes, France, which was built at the same time and kept in continuing use (even today), this was a bit anti-climactic. The good news is that it is handicap friendly, replete with handicap bathrooms, elevator, and wide pathways. The staff was also very helpful.

title: Just Colloseo!
text: I don’t have to write anything about this place. Everybody hear about gladiators, bloody olympics and chistians mertyrdom

title: One of the World Wonders! Must see!
text: The experience visiting the colosseum happened last minute, but it was so worth it. We enjoyed our trip to visit the colosseum. No matter what time of the year you visit there are always lots of people and crowds at this attraction, so plan ahead, don’t stress over the lines and enjoy the history and culture.

title: Beautiful and very Prestigious!
text: Loved the Colloseum,  the museum on the second level. Note is currently under reconstruction and maintenance..Long cue if you don't buy...skip the line ticket in advance!

title: Allot oh history
text: Our time when more in the forum then in the coliseum. Was very nice. You cam stay after the tour as long as you want

title: Not good to visit, renovations
text: All exteriors are covered with scaffolding, due to (at least) external renovations, not good to visit.  Cannot take pictures.

title: Historical visit, but be prepared
text: The Colloseum is a spectacular monument to visit in Rome, with opportunities to explore the upper and lower levels of the structure. Please buy tickets far in advance online! We booked months in advance with only an early evening slot available. Once purchased, when you visit, you must collect your tickets using the confirmation email you received. You cannot automatically join the queue with your email for purchase. Also, there is little shade and no where to purchase drinks. The fire brigade were handing out fizzy water but it was limited. Local bars charge extortionate amounts for soft drinks - 5€ a go!! Bring plenty of cold water and snacks, you will regret it otherwise. The Forum and Palantine Hill are included in your entry fee, and they're great to do in the morning when it is cooler and less busy. Lots of shadey spots to rest and plenty of water fountains and toilets. The Colloseum does not have any of these facilities as far as I could tell. Be wary of men selling waters, umbrellas etc, they are selling illegally and we spectated frequent chases between them and the police! Definitely recommend a visit, just be prepared.

title: Amazing for the time
text: To visit the Colussium was a highlite of Roman visit. To think this could have been built way back then and learn if the events held in it was very interesting   A must see for all

title: Pretty surreal
text: The Colosseum is pretty spectacular. Highly recommended even if you’re not a history buff. I enjoyed it.

title: The night tour was FANTASTIC
text: HIGHLY recommend the night tour. It was one of the best tours I have ever been on. I had knee surgery scheduled and was very doubtful if I could do this but they accommodated me by letting me ride the elevator.

title: Must See
text: We booked fastpass tickets with guided tour through City Sightseeing and it was definately worth it . The guide was very interesting and got through the lines quickly . It's a must see for anyone coming to Rome

title: It’s a must
text:
You can’t not visit this iconic landmark it’s just wow the history the grandeur the story behind it’s just wow 
By night it’s lit up and it’s whole story changes 
Never tired of looking at this building it’s breathtaking

title: The History!
text: Another must see when visiting Rome. I highly recommend paying for a tour guide and reserving in advance. Get the ticket that allows you to go below and above the stage. You get extra information and don't have to deal with all the lines going in the normal way... I believe we did the 2-hour tour for the coliseum part.

title: AMAZING!
text: Mariella was Amazing.   Her description of each detail for the colosseum was on point.  It was extremely hot, and she made sure that we could find shade and water whenever possible.  She always checked if everyone was in agreement if we needed to climb up stairs.  Her love and enthusiasm of history was inviting.  We will recommend this tour to all our friends.

title: Get a Tour Guide
text: It is absolutely fascinating, but I recommend you use a guided tour.  Too much history to better appreciate with a knoweldgeable guide.

title: A real experience. Fantastic history
text: Apart from the long queues in blazing sun and awful toilet facilities. This place is amazing. Well worth a visit!

title: A step back into histoey
text: We stepped back into history today as we followed in the footsteps of emperors, gladiators and slaves. What an incredible sight and opportunity to experience the brutality of Ancient Rome. A well informed audio tour meant we got the most out of our visit, and the skip the line tickets meant we had a hassle free entrance into this majestic wonder of the world. Highly recommended - take lots of water (though water fountains are available inside) and sun cream as there is not much shade in the summer months. Stunning!

title: Get the fast pass (queue jump)
text: Bought the tickets online which was really straight forward. Booked for the 8.30 tour and there were 2 lines which were not well signposted. Once in this should be the top of your to do list in Rome. Get the video guide to help navigate your way around. Be wary of all the 000's of other tourists!

title: Just go...
text: Amazing experience. The history. The grander. It's amazing. Go experience it. and if you do a tour, request to stop at the gift shop otherwise, you'll be like me, without so much as even a postcard and regretting that.

title: Night time tour was disappointing
text:
We booked a tour through a third part, and their portion was awesome.  But when we arrived at the Colosseum for the nighttime arena floor and underground portion, we were required to use the Colosseum guide.

Unfortunately, our particular guide was not very good.  He was very dry in his presentation, he was repetitive, and the information he provided just wasn't interesting.  One example was when we reached a particular corridor, he spent five minutes telling us that people used to walk down this corridor, but sometimes animals might also use the corridor, and that the corridor could be used for people to both enter and exit, and that the animals could also be brought in or out of this corridor, but that it was mostly used by people, except when it was used by animals.  Also gladiators probably used the corridor to go from one place or the other.

After forty five minutes of that, and two short films that used "thusly" and "hither" WAY too many times, we decided to leave the tour before seeing the two main things we came to see - the arena floor and the underground.  We found a staff member and told her we had to leave.  One other couple asked the guide,  it he told them there wasn't a way to exit at that point of the tour.  Another family asked if they could join another group, and several people had taken off their headphones and were no longer listening to the guide.

Another disappointing part of the nighttime tour is the lack of lighting.  Of course it will be dark at night, but there is so little lighting inside that you can't really see anything at all.  Of course there shouldn't be much or it would ruin the ambience and historical feel of the arena, but they didn't apply that standard when they erected a large movie screen on the arena floor.   Just a tiny bit of lighting would have helped, but as it is you just don't get a feel for the size of the Colosseum.  

I'm sure there are great tours and some great guides.  We just didn't get one this time.

title: Best Experience / Recommend Combined Tickets
text:
We bought our tickets on the day at Palentine Hill / the Roman Forum and it was cheaper for us to buy a ticket which gave us entry to all 3 attractions vs. buying a ticket for 1 entry just to the collesuem which didn't make any sense but was a massive bonus for us! Think we only paid around €12-€16 euros each which was an absolute win! We visited Palentine Hill and the Roman Ruins first (these are next door to each other and near to the collesuem) we then had lunch and walked to the collesuem at the allocated time on our ticket. The queues were unreal and we saw many people with umbrellas using them to hide from the heat. We told ourselves we'd leave if the queue carried on for more than 30 minutes (as we overheard some people say it was 2 hours long!) However, for some crazy reason the guy looked at our ticket and we managed to avoid the queues and got in within less than 3 minutes! I don't know if it was a special ticket but it was much cheaper than what they charge online in advance and we even got to avoid the queue so it was worth it! (Maybe we're just lucky!)

Once inside the place is stunning but you don't need to be in there long as it's easily done in 20 - 30 mins max. Worth a visit but if you're going in high summer in the day time  make sure you buy water from a local supermarket beforehand (a litre or more) or fill up your bottle at a fountain, as otherwise you will get hounded by people trying to sell you water for crazy prices and will try to rip you off as we saw many tourists falling for it due to the overwhelming heat. Day or night this place is worth a visit anyway!

title: Impressive remains
text: Every time when we visited Rome, we would spare some time to visit Piazza del Colosseo.  Although the Colosseum was still under redecoration, it still looked impressive.  Recommended to purchase the "fast queue" combined ticket if you have time to see the surrounding spots as well.

title: As great as they say
text: We loved the Coliseum... just walking around it at night,  during the day,  and going inside were all wonderful experiences, and my only recommendation is to learn as much as you can about it before you go and take something that you can get wet and put around your neck to keep you cool if you go in the summer.

title: A,USF to visit
text: Excellent but pay to jump the queue & wear flat shoes as steps very steep. Not good for disabled persons.

title: do not easily tricked into "skip the line" marketing!
text:
okay for the first timer to colosseum i shocked that i have too queue very long to get a ticket. there are a lot of people offering the "skip the line", they really persuasive and good with words but with a high price. they sell wilth a guide. if you are not into the detailed history about the building, this is very boring and wawsting time.
so dont waste money and buy online ticket day(s) before

title: It is in renovation, but still very beautiful
text: We were surprised to see that the Colosseum was under renovation and that they are also planning to build a Metro under it! It is still a very beautiful place. The renovation is more for the consolidation rather than for changing its original style. Be aware of pickpockets.

title: Impressive, iconic building.
text: We booked a tour that gave us early admission.  Definitely recommend this as it gets super busy and super hot.  Guide helped us get the most out of our visit.

title: Still in awe
text: The Colosseum was one of my fave things about Rome. We did a tour that included the arena floor. Walking thru the main procession doors to the arena was simply breathtaking. The sheer magnitude of the design and construction of this place is nothing short of amazing. Learning the history and what life was like in Ancient Rome was also very interesting. I’d highly recommend visiting!

title: Nice but mind the crowds
text: Arguably the most famous of all sights in italy . But on a hot sweltering July summer , anything over half an hour minus the queues outside is really energy sapping. I am sure in pleasant weather one can easily spend over an hour or two. But if its summer , I wouldnt suggest you spend more than half an hour .

title: A must see!
text: Beautiful as always. Even on a terribly hot summer day. Our second visit, we walked the grounds, but did not do an organized tour.

title: Beware
text: Obviously this is on the bucket list of many people - as it was on mine. But time your visit - don’t go in June through August - you will wait hours or be exploited by tour guides who don’t truly have the history right. Very aggressive and unsavory grifters and pickpockets. Such a terrible shame.

title: Incredible
text: This visit is a must when in Rome. The Structure and the accompanying history is incredible to look at. However beware of the people wanting you to buy flowers and other stuff in the area.

title: Amazing!
text:
We went twice, once on a night time tour through a third party.  I thought you would see more of the underground area, but we didn't see too much.  The arena floor we really couldn't see at night either, they just showed us a film on a screen.  It was interesting but I preferred the day visit.
Got tickets online in advance, so skipped the line, line for security wasn't long at all.  It was a rainy day so it wasn't super crowded.  There is scaffolding, but you can still take your pictures without it.

title: Unbelievable place
text: This is an outstanding piece of architecture- a must see when in Rome. We booked skip the line tickets with VIP arena floor access and I can’t recommend highly enough!

title: Breath taking
text:
2nd time I have been here and was bowled over as much as the first 
A must for any visitor to Rome 
Worth a tour of the gladiator fighting floor for more information

title: Colosseum
text: It was smaller than I expected tbh. But absolutely amazing. The store only accepted euros no cards so take cash. My tour guide was a Russian man and thought he was a comedian it was entertaining. It was an amazing sight that I will always remember. It’s a must do but my one warning is watch out for all the guys trying to sell you stuff they are so aggressive and will follow you.

title: One of a kind experience
text: A must see experience. I would recommend off season and early on a week day to minimize crowds. Book skip-the-line tickets in advance. Everything else aside, don’t miss this one of a kind experience.

title: Do NOT USE HEADOUT TOURS FOR THIS ATTRACTION
text:
They were not at the meeting place at the appointed time and have refused to refund.  The meeting point was very crowded and we couldn't find the Tour, it must have been very poorly signposted, if it was there at all!!

Don't use this provider as it is a sham.

We wound up just using another at the front of the Colosseum and it was fine.

title: Good to see
text: The building itself is beautiful but the large crowds and queues is treadful - I would recommend a guided tour to gain the full experience of this attraction. Try and go at night time when crowds have subsided.

title: A must see
text: This is a must see in Rome, however when we went it was extremely crouded and we recommend getting a skip the line ticket in advance

title: Last-minute buying tickets for the Colosseum
text:
Three days ago we woke up with the plan to visit the Colosseum that day. We had not booked any tickets. The official website said tickets were sold out but a previous poster had mentioned buying tickets on the day from the Palatine ticket office so we gave it a whirl. We got there at 9:20 with two people in front of us. We bought two adult tickets for 12 euro each and four free children’s tickets and our timed Colosseum entry was for 10:15am that day. 
The point is that tickets were still available even if the online ones were sold out so don’t think you have to pay for the outrageously expensive skip the line ones. And buy them from the Palatine ticket office just two mins walk away and avoid the massive queue in the hot sun at the Colosseum. They are the same tickets for all three nearby sites (Colosseum, Palatine and Forum). 
We downloaded the free Rick Steves app and the Colossem and Forum podcasts that work without WiFi. Much better than trailing behind a tour guide in the blazing sun, particularly with kids. 
Yes there was some scaffolding up but it didn’t detract from the experience for us. Yes there are touts selling iced water and hats. Just say no if you’re not interested. They will move on quickly and usually politely. We did buy a bottle as it was only one euro and we thought that was pretty reasonable. 
The ones you need to watch out for are the guys who try to wrap a string bracelet on you as they will try to stand over you to buy the bracelet you didn’t want in the first place. Don’t pay them. They will cut it off and move on. They are hoping for a tourist to feel intimidated enough to just pay rather than stand up and say no.

title: Arena tour
text: Having been here 8 years ago,this time  going out on stage was amazing, glad they have got rid of all the tacky tourist stalls and a staged photos.

title: Rome By Night Walking Tour with Irene Amadeo of Enjoy Rome
text: Rome by Night walking tour  of Colosseum to Trevi Fountain with Irene Amadeo of Enjoy Rome! Just a cursory glimpse of the Colosseum but an invaluable walking tour on our first night in Rome!

title: Day trip!
text: Stunning place! Unmissable, the views are amazing! Try go early or late as there is very little shade.

title: Colosseum rome
text: I would definitely recommend booking a time slot to go into the colosseum as the queues are huge and you are standing in the sun for hours if you haven’t booked. You can book online before you go.  We really enjoyed seeing the colosseum and learning about what it would have been like. Beware of the “vendors” outside, most are ok if you just say no but a few put bracelets etc on children as a “gift” and when you won’t pay they snatch them back.  Just be firm anal walk away if you don’t want to. You will be in awe of how spectacular the colosseum is, it’s amazing. An absolute must to visit if you are in Rome.

title: A Colsseum !
text: If you have seen any other, it is just the same. Have a look, but if short of time, just seeing it from the outside is a great experience.

title: Beautiful and a must see 🙈
text: Everyone must get to see the Colosseum on a trip to Rome. It is busy and a skip the line ticket or a tour is recommended.

title: stunning
text: The Colosseo is a stunning piece of Roman architecture. Having already visited similar arenas in Southern France, we decided not to visit the inside of it, but took some really nice pictures at night. Each arch is illuminated, making the whole structure look stunning.

title: Second time was less impressive ...
text:
I recently had the opportunity to visit the Colosseum after my previous initial visit about 6 years ago. I was a little underwhelmed this time..

Outside the building there are hundreds of "guides" who try and stop you to buy tickets. They start touting in an area a good 800 meters or so before you are anywhere near. None of them looked trustworthy or very official. Anyway we were not looking for a guided tour.

The Colosseum itself was spoiled from the subway side by repair works that are ongoing. I clearly remember statues that could be seen in front of the Arch of Constantine from the street, but a whole area has been boarded up and completely spoils the view. You used to be able to walk through the arch, but this has now been closed off (I guess to protect it).

It sufficed for us to walk around the perimeter of the Colosseum to see enough without having to queue to go inside. There was a museum before, not sure if it was still there, but this was worth the visit.

If you are lucky enough to be in Rome on 1st May, then entrance is free (or was before) as it's a holiday.

title: Excellent
text:
We did a tour with Walks of Italy. Great experience.
If you can get a tour early in the morning and skip the line tickets. Best way to go.

title: Amazing!
text: Top tip - book in advance! Otherwise you’ll join the massive queues. Didn’t have a tour or audio guide which was fine as long as you are happy to read all the information on display and/or read up before you go.

title: Amazing History!

We were there for a couple hours and could easily have stayed longer to see absolutely everything.  Loved this experience!  We did not do a tour, but we are glad we bought skip the line tickets ahead of time.

title: See along with Roman Forum and Palantine Hill.
text: Do this as part of the Forum and Palantine Hill and make a day or half-day of it. NOTE- there is a lot of restoration going on at the moment. Gets in the way of your pictures but let’s trust this will preserve the site for many more years and visitors.

title: Stunning!
text: Stunning from the outside. Stunning from the inside. Strongly recommend the view to the Colosseum from the Forum Romanum, which I think some people miss.

title: A must see when visiting Rome
text: Our group booked a small group tour for the Colosseum it was a perfect way to tour the site and learn the history.

title: Amazing..but hot!
text: We visited here in early July and our teenagers were blown away by it. It really is fab. We had booked tickets online before we went and I would certainly highly recommend this! The queues were huge and it was 38 degrees the day we were there. We booked the English guide, 60 minutes I think and this was a nice insight to what we were looking at. Our guide seemed very knowledgeable and was happy to answer any questions we had too. Definitely bring a hat and water, you can fill your bottles inside too. We went across to the Forum and Palatine hill after but to be honest it was too much and I nearly got heatstroke myself and felt very weak! We could’ve used the same ticket the following day but as we were already there we went and it wasn’t the best idea as it was about 1-3pm and just too hot. My advice is to book as early a slot as you can and don’t do too much in one day. It’s not to be missed in Rome though.

text: We choose a tour for the Colosseum, Palantine and Forum Romanum. The Colosseum is a must. What a place! A must in Rome. I strongly recommend having a tour and skip the lines

text:

title: Seriously... GO LATE IN THE DAY!
text: Of course you want to visit the Colosseum if you are in Rome, but as many reviewers have noted it can be a zoo trying to get in with long waits in the hot sun.  So if you are not set on taking a tour and just want to take a look inside (and save money), go near the end of the day.  We arrived within two hours of closing, purchased our tickets in about 10 minutes, spent 20 minutes inside and then the rest of the time in the forum.  It would have been nice to spend a little more time at the latter but worth it to us for the time we didn't spend waiting on line and battling crowds.

text:

You have to use your imagination (or watch Gladiator) to get the full idea of what is used to look like.

That said you must go and check it off your list but I wouldn’t spend a lot of time there.  Get a tour guid to help out.

title: Very Informative

Her enthusiasm really encouraged the group to learn and understand. The Colosseum and Palatine Hill are a must see when visiting Rome, and this tour is an excellent way to experience them.

title: A lot of "Hollywood" myths were dispelled on this tour.
text: As crowded and hot as it was during the height of tourist season, the Colosseum is a bucket list place not to be missed. I had no idea how large it was. Absolutely fascinating. This is the fore runner for modern day arenas.

title: Don't miss this must see attraction!
text:
Beforehand, please note that we did not enter the Colosseum. First of all we didn't have the time, in addition, we didn't want to stand in line for ages. Even from the outside this is a spectacular (and most likely the most famous) attraction of Rome. We bow humbly to the ingenuity of those times!!! Even from the outside this giant structure is impressive, to say the least.
Standing on one of the open areas in front of this place, or walking around the 
structure will make you a certain target for the inofficial merchants that try to sell their umbrellas, hats, selfie sticks, etc. Make sure you are NOT rude in any way, because that might get you in trouble. A polite "No thank you" or "no, grazie" will get you out of the situation.
If you're interested to go inside the attractions of Rome, get yourself one of the Rome Cards or Passes. There are various kinds and it seems each year a new one is distributed. They differ in price and value/eligibilities. What most of them have in common: With a valid card/pass you can use the public transport (Metro, bus, streetcar) for free.

title: Must see in Rome
text:
Buy your tickets in advance and save the aggravation of standing in line. We bought English guided tour from heir official website - Well worth it to know about the history and the use of the collesium. The guide was super knowledgeable and pleasant but was hard to follow sometimes.  

We did this combined with Roman forum. Was super hot in July - Bring comfortable clothing, hats and sunglasses. 
Plenty of water fountains to regill your water bottles all over - remember you can’t take glass and metal inside though. 

Stairs are steep but elevators are available.

title: Fantastic!! A Must See!!
text: We purchased a skip the queue guided tour for the Colosseum, Roman Fort and Palatine Hill. It was intensely hot (even at 4.00pm) so it may have been more comfortable having an earlier tour but the tour guide was fantastic and it was well worth the money. The Colosseum is just awesome and you get a real senses of the way it must have been back in the day. A must see when in Rome.

title: Very cool experience.
text: Very cool experience to go inside such an ancient structure.  I would suggest getting a guide if you can but it is definitely doable without one.  Just make sure you see everything you can including the nearby Roman Forum!

title: A piece of history

title: Cannot miss this tourist attraction in Rome!
text: Amazing historical tourist attraction! We lined up so long under the sun to get into the Colosseum. Got the sun burn. Although some parts were under constructions and we couldn’t see the whole perfect view there, it was a place which we couldn’t miss in Rome. Valuable!

title: excellent service for the disabled.
text: Access to almost all areas is possible by wheelchair. Everyone  goes out of their way to accommodate the disabled. Rome is the most disabled friendly city I have ever visited from the airports to the attractions the only problem is cobbled streets but visiting any historic site this must be expected.

title: Beautiful structure with so much history.
text:
The colosseum was at the top of my list for Rome. From the outside, it did not disappoint. The structure comes out of nowhere and you are just amazed at how incredible it is. 
The lines are absolutely crazy, so make sure you invest in the fast pass! We purchased the fast pass tickets from Get Your Guide and we weren’t disappointed. It is a little tough to find where to go in because Get Your Guide says there is a lane labeled “fast-pass” but there isn’t. Just go right to the front where the line forms and ask a worker, they’ll point you in the right direction. 
The reason for the four stars is the construction or restoration, or whatever is going on inside... it was truly an eyesore. I’m sure they have a good reason for it, but there was so much construction equipment that it really took away from the structure itself and made it hard to focus on the history. 
Overall though, it was absolutely amazing and something you have to see while you are in Rome.

title: Amazing
text: Having seen so many shows on the colosseum it was just amazing to actually see in person. Hard to put into words with the awe we had. Can’t imagine people of the time seeing this for the first time.  It must be on every ones bucket list at least once in their lifetime

title: Long lines
text: There is chaos all around the Coliseum. Might be worth to get your pic outside and skip the sweltering heat. Inside in lineups and crowds. The areas around the Coliseum such as the forum and Palestine Hill are a better walk.

title: Amazing Historical Monument
text:
This should be near the top of your Rome list.  Very interesting history, phenomenal sight.  Here's a few tips from my first visit:
1. Go in the early morning (crowds, heat).
2. Book a skip-the-line tour in advance.  Official sight tour (coopculture.it) is a much better deal than third party tours.
3. Book the basic tour.  Underground, Belvedere (upper tiers) and Arena tours are overrated.  With the basic tour you'll see everything you need to see.
4. Combine with Roman Forum tour - also well worth it and right next door.
5. Bring a full bottle of water.
6. If you book a tour, bring your own earbuds / headphones.  Those provided are lame.
7. Know that if there is unexpected construction or repair or bad weather - you don't get a refund on third party tours.
8. Do at least a minimal amount of reading about the Colosseum to get the most out of your tour.
9. Before or after the tour, walk all the way around the Colosseum for great photos.
10. Plan on half a day.

title: Great piece of history
text: Great to visit both during the day and in the evening (even with the restoration work going on). Guided tours are available and there are men dressed as gladiators wandering around.

title: Two time visit
text:
We’ve visit it first from
The outside, then paid 10 euros for internal tour. Although very interesting, there is nothing to see as it is all under construction

title: Book a night tour
text:
The Coliseum averages 20,000 visitors a day.

We were there with maybe 200 people present because we booked a night tour.   The only drawback was that the shop was closed, but the fact that we weren’t melting in 100 degree heat with 19000 of our closest friends ore than made up for that.

title: Awesome
text: History in front of your eyes. The place is magnificent. We booked a private tour and this was one of many stops in Rome. You enter the lower level and then climb a lot of steps to acces the view from the middle level. They have an elevator for people with mobility issues but was out of service the day we went. As we had a profesional guide with us she gave all the information. You can get an audio guide also.

title: Incredible history and reconstruction
text: This iconic structure blew us away!  What an awesome sight. We engaged a personal tour guide through a recommendation from friends and Maria was awesome. She got our tickets ahead of time for us, knew (of course) exactly which line to get into and we were just so efficient and got in quickly, and she guided us exactly to what we needed to see. We are normally extremely self sufficient but in the case it was for sure worth it. The sub floor was closed the day we went due to rain.

title: Historic Travel
text: The colosseum is amazing. The history about the slaves, gladiators, the battles, the animals and also about the glorious construction is superb!!!

title: Worth it but here's some prep tips
text:
We went early afternoon (despite our better judgement) and decided to buy tickets there (also a mistake).

The line to buy tickets moves relatively quick through the security part (which is outside in direct sun for the most part). It took maybe 15 minutes (but was hot). No large bags, etc. (Check the list of can'ts before you go). The next section was inside and PACKED! Not claustrophobia friendly. You kinda have to weave yourself through or you'll get pushed out of the way. There are no bathrooms anywhere near the Colosseum or inside until you get past the ticket point
 It took maybe 45 minutes total but could have been skipped of we bought a reserved ticket online for 14 euros (it was 12 euros to wait and buy in person). Skip the lines with tour groups are about 50+ euros. I don't think the skip the line is necessary. You can rent audio guides for approximately 10 euros if you want info without the tour. But tours provide access to the platform area closest to the bottom.   
Colosseum is a must see, just he prepared!

title: Definitely buy skip the line ticket
text:
Amazing place to visit, buy skip the line otherwise, 
you will be queuing in scorching heat. Be careful for people asking you about water, hats, power banks and selfie sticks!! Be confident and say no thank you. The police are trying to clamp down on these street sellers, it can be a little overwhelming at times.

title: Pop on pop of Bus
text: When in Rome a must do, the sense of history is over whelming it's everywhere. Where here in July so a little challenging with the crowds. But you just gave to make the atractions work for you. Very hot during the day

title: Must be done: awesome to see
text:
The views outside and inside are awesome.

Well worth a visit and has to be done.

We had not bought tickets on advance but ended up having to pay to join a group.  The cost of this was €35 Euros each rather than €12  but the queue without group was over an hour plus security.

Really interesting views and an interesting museum there.

title: I left a review on the guided tour
text: If you go to Rome, you must visit this attraction. If you are able to do a guided tour, do it Just so much history here, it is incredible.

title: Not the best
text: Getting to the Colosseum is difficult. The streets feel a little unsafe and there are a lot of people trying to sell you tickets and selfie sticks. We found that it was much more ruined than we though, and less impressive than we though. Other than saying we've been there, we unfortunately didn't take much else away from it.

title: on a guided tour
text: exactly how you expect it to be , like bring on a film set very interesting guided tour tells you the history of the building

title: Amazing views obviously
