title: A Rome Must
text: Anyone coming to Rome has to visit the Colosseum. It is so impressive, especially when you think how old it is.

title: Amazing architecture feat!
text: We did a tour that skipped the line, would recommend that! Just the size of this building and the time frame in history make this building such an amazing feat!

title: Nothing but 5 stars

Worth every penny although I did wander off and we lost our guide and subsequently the rest of the tour as a result! However, the Colosseum is awesome. I can only imagine how impressive it looked when it was first built. Incredible building and place to visit.

title: Amazing historic structure!
text: Words just can’t express the awe you feel at the Colessium.   Just amazing! Our children were also awe struck by this beautiful structure and the history it represents.  Just WONDERFUL and AWESOME!!!

title: Beautiful history, poorly managed
text: It is a shame such a beautiful piece of history is incompetently managed.  That being said it is a must see and do go early as it only gets worse as the day goes along.

title: Must see
text: The Colosseum is a must see when visiting Rome. Three tips: book in advance, go early, and book a tour. After studying and reading about this for years, it was no less awe inspiring in person.

title: A must see in Rome
text: The coliseum is a must see, the structure is incredible as is the history and culture associated with it. However, I would definitely recommend booking online in advance as the queues were huge. There is very little shade from the sun as well. We booked a time slot and arrived 15 minutes before and we were allowed straight in. We hadn’t booked an audio guide but decided to purchase a 6€ audio visual guide which is an iPod and some headphones. Possibly next time I wouldn’t bother as there is great information on the signs around the coliseum. Again take loads of water or a bottle which you can fill inside the coliseum for free. The coliseum is incredible inside and it gives you a feel for Roman life. On a different note the toilets were in a disgusting state, I recommend finding alternate before going in. In total we spent around 1.30 to 2hrs in the coliseum. Our ticket also include the palatine hill and roman forum which we visited in the afternoon (several hours after the coliseum). You can use this part of the ticket the next day.

title: Fantastic
text:
We had a guided tour done with the local tourist station and it was fantastic. The guides are fabulous. Lots of knowledge. 
Skip the queue is a must here especially in the hot weather.

title: Book on-line to see this super site!
text: You must book ahead otherwise the lines are huge! Super interesting and great to see. Take a guided tour as there is a lot to learn and the guides are really informed.

title: Lovely view but wish we’d bought the dungeon ticket
text: The coliseum is an amazing visit but if you’re going to go inside I’d recommend you purchase a ticket which includes the dungeons otherwise just walk around the outside as you’re getting a good experience.

title: Awesome Place
text: This place is awesome!! The history in this walls are so impressive! This is one in the bucket list for everyone.

title: Rome

title: Amazing history

text: A tour of the colosseum is a must see.   Buy tickets early  and show up on time.   That way you avoid the crowds.   Walking and using stairs from 75 AD makes one understand the grandure and wonder of the colosseum.

title: Great Place to be.
text:
I had been to Colsseum tour today with a travel guide. It's a must see attraction in rome.Its truly amazing the construction been done in this olden age of Roman.

Its recommend to have  a stroller if u have a 3-5 Year old child, since it might make the child so tried if we take them by foot.There few places where it has bumpy pathways.

title: Excellent
text: We travelled colosseum in may 2019. It was sunny and wam day. We took tourist guide to know about colosseum and it was fruitful. Building colosseum looks impressvie but after knowing its history, it gives feeling of its greatness and compell you to think how it has been standing as the symbol of history of ROME and ROMANS

Inside the Colosseum is fantastic and we could happily have spent all our days here exploring this place. Please visit if you are in Rome with our without a guide...Amazing

title: rome
text: a mind-blowing experience, do not miss this. Watch out for the hawkers, damn nuisance, and crowds, huge crowds, worth it.

title: A huge and impressive structure
text: Pre-booking is essential to help avoid the queues. Need to be there about 15 mins before your allocated time slot and once in there are several displays to read more of the history.

title: An absolute must
text: Yeah, there are lots of tourists. But how can you visit Rome and not visit the Colosseum? Be smart--book in advance. Be aware of scams that promise ridiculous things. Take your time, and walk through one of the greatest structures ever built.

title: Amazing but not essential
text: The Colosseum is breathtaking, from the outside the shear size of it and the fact it has stood as a pillar of Rome for over 2,000 years leaves you Wowed! Sadly, this is when your experience peaks. We asked staff to help us with our ticket meeting point, it was from a third party but the attitude to assist with any sort of entrance was rude and consisted of a lot of pointing and brushing us off. Once we got inside the wow factor stayed but was quickly given the thumb down by any staff member that perceived wonder for loitering, this resulted in a pretty rushed visit and a disappointing experience. I would advise anyone to visit the colosseum from the outside and get some great photos to show off the scale of this amazing ancient arena to everyone you know (there is a great bridge over the street to the left of the colosseum where you can get the whole arena in view). The inside is not essential but still a worthwhile experience if you don’t mind the above points.

title: Must see
text: As beautiful as I've imagined. Lot's of people and very long lineups but so worth it. Definitely a must when in Rome.

title: Beautiful.
text: So beautiful! Definitely an experience. Got to do the underground and arena floor tour! Loved it. Skipped the lines and enjoyed a great tour guide that spoke English. After we saw the museum part in the Colosseum itself!

title: A tour with a guide makes a big difference
text: Last time, I just went on my own, figuring I could read signs and get the idea of the way it used to be. This time, I had a guide, and it was way better. She spoke quietly into a clip-on microphone, and we all had receivers and earphones, so she could present a lot of history, context, and alerts about what to look at while we all walked.

title: Disappointing
text: We had a group of 15 - teens & chaperones.  The general feeling of the kids was one of disappointment.  Currently the floor is all scaffolding & there is also a lot of scaffolding along the sides.  The group decided they wish they had only seen it from the outside & not wasted their time going inside.

title: Would’ve loved to see the original!
text: Seeing the colosseum and forum is a great site to do as a tour. It’s an amazing structure. A must see.

title: Greatttt!!!!
text:
History and more history 
So much to see and learn
Buy online skip the line tickets 
Bring a hat and a bottle of water with you...you can fill the bottle with water in the coloseum 
There are solutions for people with disabilities (elevator)

title: It was as good as always but...
text: Everytime I go to the Colosseum I have chills, the majesty and the stories and secrets held by that enchanting place are incredible. This time I must say there were too many people but Rome is always very crowded so it's hard (actually impossible) to see the Colosseum only you and your family.

title: Better at night!
text: Too many people during the daytime, to make nice pictures, go at night! Also beware of pickpockets, it's really easy to lose something between that kind of mass.

title: Beautiful
text: Very beautiful incredible view from up historical place if you visit Rome you have to go to colosseum

title: still stands
text: I can't believe after all these years it stands tall and beautiful. We didn't had the chance to go inside, because of long lines, but the exterior says it all: beauty, history, longevity, and imagination.

title: Loved it
text:
Booked this guided 1 and half hour tour with a guide with GetYourGuide tours. We also had the Skip the Line pass which was well worthed. There must have been an hour line up just to purchase a ticket and another huge line up to pass security and to get into the colosseum. So there goes about 3 hours of your time. I had booked a 10 am English guided tour but for one reason or another, 2 days before, the guided tour contacted me by email to switch my time. All was done smoothly. The meeting location in front of the metro in front of the Colosseum was also easy to find. From there we met our guide, got our earpiece and off to the Colosseum we went. A guided tour is knowledgable. The place is a must if you go to Rome. It is so impressive. The way part of the building is still standing. We did 1.5 hr with the guide and then ended up hanging around for another hour just taking pictures and visiting what we wanted to see. Once the tour is over you don't have to leave the building. you are free to stay as long as you want. I was there with my 13-year child and my wife. 
A must-see.

title: Skip the line tickets a must!
text: This is an incredible place to visit, but if you visit in the summer like we did, skip the long me tickets is a must!  Tickets are timed!

title: I'm in awe of this amazing place!
text:
The Colosseum is a must when you travel to Rome.  We bought the skip the line tickets with a hop on/ hop off bus for the day, and it was worth it! Skipped right by all the long lines.  There is a lot of walking and steep stairs, but there is wheelchair ramps and lifts.  I really enjoyed walking through all the levels and seeing the exhibits.  

Cons: only 1 toilet facility in the whole place, and the line for the lady's room was entirely too long.  So go beforehand if you can.  Men's wasn't that bad, but my husband said it smelled terrible.

title: Wonderful building, highly recommend paying for an exclusive tour
text: We met a ticket seller one evening when out for a walk to see the Colosseum lit up. We were dubious at first, but the ticket seller showed us his ID, gave us his business card and let us book and told us he would take no money and we should pay in the morning. So we booked a morning tour. We had a tour guide called George. He was fantastic! he really brought the Colosseum alive with his stories and pointing out interesting features. With him it would have been very easy for us to have walked through the Colosseum and obviously recognise that it's an amazing building, but not have appreciated the layers of history associated with the site. George really made it come alive with his passion for the history of Rome. Also, being part of the tour we got to go to parts that are only open to tour groups and we missed the cues.

title: Wow (Not Wow!)
text: Yes, it is iconic.  Yes, it is historic.  Yes, it is cool.  Yes, the tour was great.  Yes, it deserves 5 stars.  However… it didn’t knock my socks off.  Different strokes, I guess...

title: Why you need a guide ... and tip on place for the best pictures!
text: I'm a big believer in using a guide not only to skip what can be horrendous lines but also give you insight into a place you could never get on your own - the Colosseum is no different.  Another advantage for the Colosseum is you can only access the 'arena' - the stage on the floor of the building located at one end - with a tour guide.  We waited only three minutes to enter and you come into the Colosseum through one of the massive archways right onto the floor - an incredible experience.  Certainly take time to explore other parts of the building including the many displays located one level up.  If you want a great photo of the Colosseum do so from the Roman Forum.  There's an overlook that few people go to that gives you a stunning view and a great place for pictures - in the afternoon the sun is at your back.  It's located behind the ruins of the Temple of Venus and Rome up to the left from the Arch of Titus.

title: Rome visit
text: It is amaizing uniqe whole coloseum. It is great from inside, and outside. Must visit in Rome! Near the piazza Venecia, by walk, and metro station.

title: A sight to behold
text:
My daughter wanted to know all about the history and luckily as a teacher I was able to tell her 
It is a sight you must see whilst in Rome and had it not been in the middle of a heatwave I would have spent much longer there

title: Lots of tourists
text:
Unfortunately we couldn't manage to come inside of Colosseum. I haven't seen so many tourists at one spot!
If you are planning your trip to Rome, book your tickets online in advance

title: Wow
text: Wow... Just wow, there are no words to describe this, it's amazing to walk through the stone of so long ago

title: A must see when in Rome!
text:
Took a guided tour of the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill (Walks -VIP Colosseum Underground Tour with Roman Forum & Palatine Hill)- it was fantastic! There are many places in Rome you can visit on your own without a tour, however I highly suggest a guided tour of this site to add context and information to this historical venue. We all learned a lot about the architecture, the purposes of the Colosseum, what visitors would have experienced and the life of the gladiator and animals fighting there. 
If you go in the afternoon, remember it will most likely be very warm, so be smart and do what you need to get the most out of your experience. For example, wear light clothing and comfortable shoes, bring an umbrella to block the sunlight, a hand fan and a water bottle to ensure you (and others in your group) are able to really enjoy the tour.

title: Fantastic
text: We booked a tour with CityWonders through Tripadvisor and it was probably one of the best things we done whilst in Rome. Serefina who was our guide was a brilliant host and very knowledgeable about the whole place. We also visited the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill... I highly recommend both places are visited during anyones stay... Loads of historical ruins and interesting facts. We’ll definitely be booking again.

title: 2 hour walk back in time
text: There is so much more left of the colosseum and it’s ruins than I even imagined. Our guide spoke English very well and had not only interesting facts but wonderful stories entertain us.

title: Guides Tour was our highlight of Rome
text: We visited the Colosseum as part of a guided tour and it was the highlight of Rome. We had VIP access and were rushed through the queues, including access to the stage area on the bottom level. Our guide helped us to really appreciate the history of the Colosseum and would highly recommend. The company we used was Crown Tours, booker through get your guide

title: Fantastic
text: We a group of 4, did the Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill Tour with Brandon. What a magical tour it was, with Brandon knowing his subject so very well. He made the ruins come to life for us, & we thank you so much Brandon.

title: Historic site and must visit
text: I visited with my wife and 10 year old son and stood in awe while watching the colosseum.The site is breathtaking and you feel like you are part of history.However visit on a weekday as the lines are long and keep water with you to keep hydrated

title: Amazing!
text: Enjoyed our tour here in the colosseum! Amazing how the structure has been kept all these years!! Love to see historical places like this.

title: Do the underground and skip the line with archeoligists
text: Use viator or citywonders with a archeologist guide. Fantastic place. Wouldnt miss it! My second time here!

title: Sylvia is excellent with Getyourguide.com for Coloseum small group tour!
text: We had a 2 hour tour of Colosseum which includes a tour of Forum and Palatine Hll.  She is very knowledgeable, very good english, and would highly recommend her and this tour!

title: Gladiator Tours
text: Would recommend taking a tour of the Colosseum and Palatine Hills with Gladiator Tours our guides were very knowlageble with the added bonus of skipping the ques.

title: Private tour
text: Excellent tour guide led us around the Colosseum and Forum sites. We were picked up from our accommodation which was perfect. Amazing tour for teenagers, packed with facts about the history of the sites.

title: Colosseum tour
text: What a fantastic tour we took with Francesca from walks tours. She was so captivating with all the information she spoke about bringing to life the details of the Colosseum and the surrounding areas.

title: A must for any visit to Rome!
text: A tour of the Colosseum is a must for anyones first visit to Rome. I would also highly recommend the Roman Forums right next to the Colosseum. To make the most of your visit, I would recommend a tour company offering skip-the-line access for small groups. We went with CityWonders and they did not disappoint.

title: Iconic
text:
A trip to the Colosseum is an absolute must when visiting Rome.   Make sure you book tickets in line prior to your arrival.  If not it can be a very long wait - in the sun. 

Get an audio guide, or we downloaded the Rick Steves audio guide on yo our phone.  40 mins long.  Very informative.  And free!  

The going is uneven and the stairs are steep.  Not conducive for any who are not fit. No elevators so if not able to walk on uneven ground for about a mile, stay away.  

Takes about an hour, minimum.  Expect little shade and lots of people, the latter making themselves as annoying as possible.  Still worth it, despite the annoyances.

title: Buy your tickets online
text:
Visiting Rome and not seeing the Coliseum is like not visiting Rome at all. Buy your tickets online ahead of time skip the insanely long lines. You’ll be able to schedule a specific time and stroll right in. We joined one of the guided tours and then separated from the group to take photos. If the weather had been nice we could have stayed longer, but at almost 100 degrees and in the bright sunlight, we only spend around 30-40 mins and left.

If you want to get good photos, wake up early and come here first thing in the morning before everyone else does.

title: Worth a visit
text: Highly suggest you grab a tour and skip the lines. Don’t go to Rome and skip this, well worth it. Easy to get to as well.

title: OMG! Stunning
text: A dream come true! Bring euros to use the wash closet and an empty water bottle to fill from the fountain. We did not have time to go inside, but seeing this exquisite piece of history was mind blowing. The ruins we saw on the walk to it were incredible as well!

title: Amazing!
text: One of the seven wonder of the world, do I need to say more? My whole trip was around the fact that i need to visit the colosseum and it didn't disappoint. However, even for those who like history I would suggest to get a guided tour. I got a great deal right there at the door just asking around each company to see what they had to offer. Be aware the tickets price vary but also where you go inside. I git a guided tour of no more than 10 people and we even get to go inside the pitch where the gladiators used to fight. The guide was very knowledgeable and explained facts and details of the building  that I had no idea. Also, we skipped the line ! so it was a huge bonus. The amount of tourist queuing to go inside is crazy! so, yeah..get a guided tour.

title: Fascinating
text: Has to be seen to be believed, fascinating history and breathtaking architecture all in one. Definitely worth a visit with a tour guide.

title: A must-see but not immersive
text: A visit to Rome without the Colosseo is like a painting without a canvas. However, it is difficult to immerse oneself in what the place would have looked like almost 2000 years ago, so much it has surrounded itself by the modern life. The load is limited to 3000 tourists at a time but this is still a large number, especially as people are more interested in selfying their own faces in the historic landscape.

title: Just nice !
text:
If you visit the Coliseum you need to go through the special door that allows you to visit the arena 
We were before on another trip just visiting the upper area and it isn’t the same 
Once you are down there you even feel the thrill of been very close to where those fighters or gladiators were 
The coliseum looks humongous from down there 
Obviously you need a tour guide

title: Explore
text:
Visiting Rome Colosseum is main attraction. Is great place wgich you have to see. Is different when you see in tv or pics than real. You have to feel how big and old is place. 

Around Colosseum is many restaurants offer traditional Italian food.

title: Amazing
text:
Just amazing the scale is awesome 
For something so big it is hidden away down a back street 
The history is wonderful and so close to palatine hill

title: Great to visit at night
text: The Colosseum is a beautiful Roman artifact that remain rising high. Its beauty is most enjoyed at night so I highly advise a night visit for outside pictures.

title: Skip the line
text:
Skip the line but you still may have to wait. Highly recommend a tour which gives you the best understanding in a limited time not just walking around in circles 
What’s not to love?

title: Very Impressive
text:
There's not much to say about the Colosseum that hasn't already been said. First of all I was struck by its sheer size upon exiting the metro, it's truly massive. We queued fairly early for the 8:30 open time which is well worth doing as it's mostly empty before the hoards of people have entered within the hour. The fee was reasonable for a worldwide landmark. There's a few exhibits in the internal gallery behind where the seats would've been which are quite good. There are plenty of spots for pictures form all angles and lots to explore and see without a premium ticket. 

You absolutely must see this.

title: Rome - Bad experience with F&B, retailers in Rome
text:
Rome is indeed a place of culture but my personal experience with their F&B, Taxi drivers (not all of course) & retailers are met with unpleasant services during my 3 days in Rome. I have traveled to Milan, Bologna, Florence, Venice & lastly Rome which is why i understand why most people wont leave a tip when in Rome. The moment we touch down in Rome rail-station, we went to a restaurant within the station & was treated with bad service, (attitude, tonality, etc) After lunch, we tried to hail a Taxi twice which quoted us EUR 20 & EUR 23 where the 3rd Taxi gave us a metered rate which is EUR 8.50 when it reaches our hotel with additional EUR 3 for 3 luggage where we gladly gave EUR 12. Even during departure at the Airport restaurant, we are met with the same attitude from the server whom was like 'semi-throwing' our utensils on our table before serving our food. It is definitely not about the communication portion but the service attitude which is a universal language that everyone can feel. 

To end my story short, happy with Milan, Bologna, Florence, Venice but BAD experiences in Rome

title: Epic visit for a walk down history lane
text: This was epic and phenomenal visit. This piece of Roman architecture is epic and worth a visit. The fact that it was built many centuries ago is amazing. It is recommended to hire the voice guide to fully appreciate the whole experience.

title: Colosseo
text:
What the hell is there to say? It's the Colosseum for heaven's sake! No place like it on earth!
LOVE IT!

title: must see
text: its one of the wonders of the world you have to see it beautiful history and great scenery .book whit cityofwonders for the best tour guide

title: On the Arena Floor
text: I had been to the Colosseum a few times before but this time, we opted to join a tour that would bring us down to the arena floor, where the gladiators entered and where they fought.  Truly amazing and gave a whole new perspective to seeing this amazing place.  The arena floor is open only for those who join guided tours, at least it was at this time.  Those with regular colosseum tickets are not allowed access to this area. I would recommend you join a tour to see the Colosseum from this level -- well worth it!

title: Beautiful
text: Yiu see this place in pictures and on TV  but nothing compares to seeing it in person. It’s amazing.  A memory you would keep for the rest of your life.

title: 5 stars of course
text: Asking for a ranking for the Colosseum is rather redundant.  It is a "must see" while in Rome!  Schedule a tour, bring water and be amazed.

title: Amazing it is!!
text: After the Roman Forum we headed toward the Colosseum, the ticket was Skip the line so we got the direct entry without having to get in a queue. This massive oval shaped amphitheatre is worth a visit. It is the largest amphitheatre ever built and definitely an iconic symbol of Imperial Rome. It is believed to hold about 80,000 spectators and used for gladiatorial contests and other combats. Next to the Colosseum is the Arch of Constantine, the arch celebrates Emperor Constantine’s victory and the battle that made Christianity the Religion of Rome.

title: iconic building
text: Glad we've been there - but visit would have been a bit more comfortable if there had been somewhere to get a drink once we were inside.

title: A must do in Rome
text:
A skip the line or if you can work it into your budget go for a guided tour. Well worth it. 
I personally would not do this site without the above.
Tour guides add real value to the whole experience.

title: Have to visit
text: But the queue is really long... all the time Colloseum is in renovation. Best way to visit is buying a ticket to Colloseum, Forum Romanum and Palatine Hill.

title: What an amazing piece of history.
text:
After a white knuckle taxi ride to the Colliseum and questioning whether taking a taxi was a good idea, once there we were glad we had. The heat was immense and crowds were huge. Fortunately we had Fast Track tickets but due to a lack of information from the tour company we were waiting unnecessarily at the online reservations when we did not need to. Having established our meeting point with the help of staff at the Colliseum we were taken to the entrance place for groups and soon appreciated how much quicker this process was.

Once in the scale of this building was soon apparent. Allowed access to most areas giving you the opportunity to appreciate the history contained within this immense building. Lots of photo opportunities.
Drinking water available too which was a welcome relief on such a hot day. Definitely worth a visit and pleased to say that I have witnessed this remarkable piece of history.

title: Get a private tour-you will thank me
text: We set up a private tour for my family of 5. $150 for 3 hours for the Coliseum and Forum. Best thing we ever did. Got to skip the line, avoid the sweltering heat, learned WAY more than we ever would have learned if we went on our own. Best $150 ever spent. If you are going to do the Coliseum in the height of tourist season—it’s a must.

title: First time
text:
It was amazing!!! Just a great experience.. so much history to learn. 
Id u dont loke big crowds and long lines Avoid summer time trip here it's just so hot and crowded, but well worth it for me.

title: I was expecting more
text: Colosseum is so impressive. But it is for sure that (at least for me) it is more attractive from outside.

title: Tremendous!
text: Fantastic! WoW! I can’t imagine that I was walking were gladiators and even Jules César were before. It still standing up it’s incredible.

title: This place is huge
text: When you go the stories the guide tells you makes you realize the Colosseum is a place where allot of people and animals actually died all for sport and entertainment, but still to see human culture grow and become more civilized and what it used to be like was really cool.  Gotta know your history or history will repeat itself

title: history
text: amazing structure to be explored by walking the district, take a guided tour to learn more and enjoy the surrounding area

title: Definitely an icon of Rome - but too much of a wait for us
text: It really is a must see - you can't come to Rome & not see the Colosseum.  However, if we'd wanted to see inside - the line was over 2 hours long.  We just didn't have the time to wait that long & had just been overwhelmed by crowds at the Vatican, so we chose to make the most of it from the outside - which was a great experience anyway.  Even from the outside it's pretty incredible.  The scale is hard to comprehend until you're actually there. There was a tree stump in the foreground which made for some good photo opportunities!

title: Must see in Rome!
text: The Colosseum is great inside and outside. Enjoyed reading about its significance and purpose throughout its history. Visiting the Colosseum is a must if you are in Rome.

title: Out of this world
text: What an amazing place always sensational been three times.  Beautiful building recently been cleaned the surroundings are also beautiful.  Rate no 3 in world have seen all..

title: Had 3 days in rome at the start of a 5 wk adventure around italy ,Slovenia, Croatia, n Bosnia.
text:
Had a great day wandering around Rome.collasium was magnificent. Vatican city.cistern chapel.Trevino fountain, Spanish steps just to mention a few.
Only thing was the expense.

title: Glory of ancient Romans Architecture
text: When you enter this place you will enter another century. the century of gladiators, Romans and you will be excited about the architecture of this place. the power of ancient Romans is obvious in this place and you will enjoy your tour all over this place. high walls, beautiful gardens around and the main building of colosseum which is the place of great fights of ancient gladiators. you could buy some souvenirs from the shop of this place and don't forget to take some photos here.

title: Roman Forum better than the Colosseum
text: Long hot queues await for the Colosseum. The enormity is amazing as is the history but I felt the Roman Amphitheater in Pula, Croatia was as if not more impressive. What is great is you get the Roman Forum (or old Roman Town) thrown in with the price of the ticket. This visit deserves a whole day. We used Rick Steves Audio guide to walk and compliment a great visit. Finish of with an ice cream and just enjoy the delights of Rome.

title: Guided tour booked
text: Group guided tour booked with timing, but we are not able to reach on time, luckily to get for entry by trying with security staffs. the staffs are good for help. But we lost the guide. The audio is expensive which is not included in the tour. Renovation is undergoing in the Colosseum.We lost to see Palentine hill due to time constrain, it will close 7.00 pm. Try to book for 2.00 pm with guided tour so entire Colosseum and paletine hill can be covered.The Guide can explained the history of Colosseum which interesting about the gladiators.Can take the tram from opposite of the road towards to  turmani station.

title: Higlight of our visit to Rome
text: We went to to Colosseum without buying ticket in advance and it turned out great. Outside Colosseum we were appoached by a lady from "Arena tours" if I understood correctly and got guided tours to both Colosseum and the Palatine at a better price then offered on the Internet. The Colosseum guide was very good and spoke fine english. This tour was the highlight of our four day visit to Rome.

title: High Season Crowds
text: Unsure of what to expect, very crowded, very hot and tour guide did the best she could to keep the group together, moving forward and with enough explanation to feel informed.

title: Tick off your bucket list.
text:
A need to see destination. Very different to how you imagine as you will learn of the changes the colosseum experienced over the centuries. You will be able to walk in different areas of the construction, admiring the enormity of its construction. Future plans for the colosseum include a massive glass like floor that will allow you see what was  under the massive arena. Don't miss it. 

** Do not accept free gifts from local street dwellers outside the attraction. . It is a con. Safely secure your belongings to prevent pick pockets. These issues are not related to just the colosseum.

title: Amazing
text: I have seen the Colosseum many times in photos but nothing can prepare you for the scale of it. Fantastic, so much history. We had an audio guide inside which wasn't very good, there is boards around to read. a must see.

title: Colosseum Day
text: This was a very interesting tour. So much history to be told. We did the underground tour along with going to the top. If you have a problem climbing a lot of steps, not advisable. Would definitely recommend this tour

title: impressive
text: found it so difficult to get tickets so we just viewed from the outside, got annoyed by street sellers but loved this piece of Ancient Rome regardless, went at night to view it too.

title: Don't Pay for the Tour
text: My husband and I arrived at the Colosseum around 7:30am, an hour before opening. Upon arriving, there was already a line forming. We were very fortunate to be in the front and were able to get through security and purchase our tickets very quickly. The inside of the Colosseum was quietest at this time and started to get  packed around 10am. We did not purchase any extra tours and just walked through ourselves, which we were very thankful to have done. You save so much time and money. There are lots of notes placed around the building and you are able to read about the history for yourself. Prepare to spend hours in here, I believe we stayed for 2-3 hours, just exploring and ready the different facts. Definitely a must see.

title: Worth seeing it from the inside
text:
Many people pay to fly to Rome and then are tight when they arrive. Don't be a scrooge. We paid 24 euros for 2 adults and one child aged 12 to go inside the colloseum and pantheon, on 2 consecutive days. We bought tickets on the day at the pantheon where the queues Were really short in the morning for a time slot of 12.15 entry.
The amphitheatre is spectacular and well worth the price. My son enjoyed the day too, as he got to see what he's learned and will be learning at school.
Check it out if you like history and want to learn about ancient Rome. Many people go to the colloseum and say its rubbish but they clearly don't have an interest in historical information

title: Amazing Place!
text: The Colosseum is an amazing place! To think of its antiquity and history is an incredible look into the past. In July it is extremely crowded, but that doesn't lessen its impact. I highly recommend getting a guide to point out various things that will enhance your understanding of the building.

title: Fascinating place
text: Had an amazing time at the Colloseum, such a fascinating place and our tour guide made history come alive

title: Avoid the main ticket line
text: Go to the much shorter line at the Roman Forum where you can get the exact same tickets.  Also the video/audio tour is a waste of money unless you know absolutely nothing about the colosseum.

title: 2X - Do I Know Everything Now?
text: I've been to the Colosseum twice, with two different tour companies, and both times I've seen different parts of the Colosseum and the Roman Forum. I wonder if I were to go a third time would I learn something new/different? Most valuable advice would be to be comfortable and wear sneakers because there are lots of rocks and unstable ground. Also, you can bring your empty water bottle and get it refilled in the Forum's many water fountains.

title: Too Notch
text: Much better experience than the Vatican. Better regulated numbers. Better price. More interesting too.

title: Colosseum - Beautiful!! Tips to buy tickets!
text:
This has to be one of my favourite places in Rome, what a beautiful and 
jaw-dropping place. The ruins and the gigantic view is one of a kind. I stayed here for a long time, brought a basic ticket by standing in a line with audio guide and spent enough time here walking at my own pace, reading and researching more in the place. 

Tickets: if you are wondering how to buy tickets, I would suggest if for a Basic (1&2nd floor visit with audio guide) stand in the line and buy the tickets. There are two lines, which move very fast and it’s just 12€. Don’t over spend by paying the agents. 

But if you want to try the upper ruins and underground, you need to buy a month or more in advance on the official website of the colosseum, otherwise the group tours by various websites are the best.

title: Tour
text: We did the tour with early access and skip the line but didn’t realize how long the tour was(3+ hours). The sites were amazing and the tour guide was good just a little long for my liking and other agreed as well. Bathrooms are scarce. And it gets crowded later on the in the day. Had a great experience seeing the old ruins though.

title: Beautiful - you just have to put up with the cues.
text: This is one of the most famous and important ancient Roman sites so it is well worth visiting but just beware the cues. It is definitely a good idea to book in advance.

title: The place to visit
text: A must see place in Rome. We visited the Colosseum in late June and had to buy a tour ticket to avoid staying in line for 3 hrs. Best to visit Rome and he historical place in off season!

title: Great tour, long day
text:
This was a very long, hot tour due to the weather that day. It definitely affected my enjoyment somewhat, but this was not the tour guide's fault, of course!
Monica was an incredible tour guide from I Love Rome skip-the-line tours. She was so kind and gave such great information. We covered a lot of sights and I learned so much! 
My advice would be to try not to go in the summer or during the heat of the day, if possible. Of course, as was the case for me, that cannot always be avoided.
There are also not many bathrooms around, so that is a bit challenging as well.

title: Cool history
text: Super cool tour guides everywhere.   Easy to find or you can book one ahead of time.  Buy tickets in advance or you will wait forever

title: History Alive
text: We book the guided tour which did skip the line. They closed entry for a couple of hours so the extra to skip the line was worth it.  History comes alive in The Colosseum you feel connected to the past. Take plenty of fluids, you can top your water bottle up.

title: Excellent Tour with LivItaly
text: We had an excellent tour guide Valentina who was very knowledgeable and made it interesting for my 12 year old son.

title: A must see
text: We stood in awe of this spectacular piece of history. While we did not purchase tickets to go in, we spend a couple of hours just walking around the area, soaking in the atmosphere. A family trip, with 6 teenage/young adult children, and everyone enjoyed our self guided tour. Lots of construction equipment around, but it wasn't too bad. We visited both in the evening, and the morning and wasn't too crowded. If you are in Rome, you must go!

title: The best preserved of Rome's ancient ruins
text: This is probably one of ancient Rome's best preserved and most famous buildings. This latest visit was my second visit and I only took time to appreciate it from outside since my time in Rome was limited and on my first visit I had already toured its' interior. Definitely worth a visit especially for first time visitors to the Eternal City.

title: A Must See In Rome
text:
The Colosseum is a must see.  We arrived via the city Hop-on, Hop-off bus. We only walked around the Colosseum on this trip, but we toured the inside previously.  We found it very interesting. There were a lot of people in the area, but it was not overly crowded.

Recommend an advance tour booking if you want to go inside.

title: Book Ahead!
text: We enjoyed touring the Colosseum, and found that the Roma Pass helped to get in a bit faster.  However, if you take the time to book your entry time at least a day in advance, it will make your entry considerably quicker.  We had to stand in a fairly long line to get our entry time once we got there, and watched a few people bypass our line to go straight to the ticket counter.  After that, we got in another line to enter.  Our line was faster because of the Roma Pass, but people who had booked ahead went straight in.

title: Must Visit
text: This place is a must visit. And there’s also a metro stop here. The guide was poor took us inside and didn’t show us anything.

title: Awesome
text: Went at night to avoid crowds, awesome experience. Had guide and film show at end. Visited all levels with detailed explanations of what we were seeing. Also much cooler to visit at night during August when very hot. High point of my trip to Rome especially when compared to the extremely hot, crowded and rushed visit to the Sistine chapel at the Vatican.

title: Amazing!
text: I don’t think you can imagine the scale of it until you’ve walked through it. It was absolutely amazing to see a place that I had always seen in movies. Pro Tip: Get there early and get your tickets at the Palentine Hill entrance, you can buy tickets to both Palentine Hill and the colosseum. The line stretched all the way around the coliseum to get tickets, but we had ours in a matter of 10 minutes.

title: The coliseum
text: The coliseum is a must see! Bring lots of cold water if you visit in summer. If you go with children, make sure they are well fed and visit the store!! They have great books!!

title: A Must See in Rome
text: My son and I took a skip the line tour of the colosseum.  Not sure which company because people just kind of grab you and shuffle you off to pay and hook up with a guide to take you through.  We did enjoy our tour this way, though, and I would give credit if I knew which company we used.  Our guide was very knowledgeable and gave us a lot of neat history.

title: History, history, history
text: This is a once in a lifetime must do. You must take the full tour go underneath to look at everything. I loved this place!

title: Colosseum at night
text: Colosseum at night is lit up and less busy, you can't buy tickets to go in at night - people were inside though, so you may be able to purchase tickets during the day to go at night. We bought a beautiful bottle of wine, sat on a stoop and drank wine and looking at the colosseum, really great!

title: Buy your tickets in advance
text:
I knew we needed to buy our tickets in advance to beat the queues but somehow, we never got around to it, thinking we could still do it on the morning of our visit.  You can't! You have to book at least one day in advance.  This meant we had to suffer the heat for an hour and a half to wait in lines for tickets and then the subsequent entry.  
My advice - book in advance and go early.  Once the Colosseum is full (3000 people), no one is allowed in until others leave.
Another word of advice, you will see tickets for the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Way for €12.  There is also a price of €16 advertised with not much in the way of explanation.  This gives you a 'Super 7' ticket which enables you to visit the museum inside the Forum, the lower ground floor in the Palatine Way and floor of the Colosseum as well as the use of lifts.  If we'd realised, we would probably have paid the extra for the full experience.
As far as visiting historical places of interest goes, these are classic, monumental, truly awesome sites and should be on everyone's list of things to do.

title: Incredibly preserved. Busy but worth it.
text: If you can, book a walking tour in advance - to beat the huge entry queues, but also because it will really help to have a guide help you to understand the incredible stories and timeframes of what deserves to be one of the world's most iconic and emotion-filled places.

title: The Colosseum without a guide/tour group
text: Rome was our last stop in Europe and the Colosseum was a must do for us.  We started to worry that we wouldn’t get inside reading past reviews about how you need to book in advance with a guide/tour. The official online site had no tickets available for the foreseeable future! The story has a happy ending - you can line up and buy tickets and it doesn’t take hours if you get there early.  We joined the queue at about 8am which is half an hour before the Colosseum opens. You have to go through security first and then get tickets - we had our tickets by 8.50am. Hired a couple of audio guides too (which was easy no need for passports/id as I’d read elsewhere) so in total came to 35 euros - for 2 adults and 2 kids (that ticket covers Palantine Hill and the forum too). It was busy inside but that’s what you get in peak season - and it’s totally worth it.  The museum/exhibitions inside are really interesting too.  We heard from local officials that it’s a lot easier to get tickets at the Palantine entrance - so you do that and the forum first then go to the Colosseum after and join the line for people with tickets - so a quick way in.

title: The Colosseum
text: The Colosseum is Rome's greatest edifice and witness to history and legend, symbolizing one of the most powerful empires in history.

title: we book a tour thru Viator
text: we  visited the colosseum in July it was hot   we booked a nice tour on Viator which took thru the arena floor  down to the underground and even to the restricted area above in the belvedere area with a guide to  share its history.  It was fabulous there is some shaded areas around the colosseum  and there is also and elevator  for those who need assistance.  not sure if goes to all levels.  there is a gift shop and it does sell water but  its best to just bring and empty bottle and  fill up at one of the many water water fountains in Rome that provide cold drinking water.

title: Bit of history
text: Had a great time walking around, recommend getting a head set to walk round with. We were very lucky as we tried to get tickets on the site and the day we wanted was greyed out and we unable to but it was actually free entry into the colosseum on the day, we found the queue was very quick but this might be down to the fact that people didn’t no it was free that day. You can’t come to Rome and not go in the colosseum.

title: Pre-Booking a tour always pays !
text:
Flying past long queues is a nice sensation !
The place itself is a historical and architectural marvel. History oozes out of every nook and cranny.
Very knowledgable guide who spoke perfect English.

title: Historic!
text: When the Colosseum opened, the first 100 days saw over 2,000 deaths of animals and humans; the smell of blood was so strong that perfume was sprayed throughout the arena to mask the smell and keep the spectators attentive to the games. This is one thing I learned on my self-guided tour of the Colosseum using Rick Steve's walking tour. If you are interested in saving a little money on a tour and don't mind a little longer wait in line, try out his walking tour and prepare to be amazed!

title: An Italian must-see !!
text:
Visited Italy with my better half - have always wanted to go to Rome especially, and absolutely loved it here! We were only here for a short time, but crammed in so much. In hindsight, we needed another day or two, but we saw some of the best attractions, enjoyed amazing food and loved every second of the trip.
The Colosseum was stunning; an absolute for any trip to Rome. We walked around the sight enjoyed the splendour and size of it, it was truly magnificent! It was fairly busy with other tourists but I guess you should come to expect that of such a huge iconic place. If we hadn't seen it we would've been disappointing-the crowds and busyness were worth it. We didn't stay too long, it isn't really that sort of place; we walked around, took photos and just took it all in - then we decided to enjoy lunch nearby, so still enjoyed the view of the area. We both highly recommend a visit - a must see if visiting Rome.

title: Breathtaking place
text: It’s an amazing place to see, but I recommended to take a tour to skip the line and also to get all the details and history behind this monument.

title: To enter or not to enter thats the question
text: First of all decide to enter before you go there. Entrance fee is 12 Euros(combined ticket for Colesseum, Forum, Palatino). It is free under 18 years of age. After you decide to enter buy your tickets from internet or you get Roma pass. Otherwise you will wait 2 to 3 hours in ticket line. After 3 hour the only thing you feel will be pain not the pleasure to see Colosseum. You can buy tickets from Roma Forum and Palatino ticket office opposite the Colosseum but the line is same busy in the morning. If you arrive in the afternoon buy tickets from Palatino ticket office. Tickets are valid for 3. After you entered that good. You like it. If you decide not to enter it is not the end of the world. Colesseum is still magnificent from outside.

title: Poor weather but still incredible
text: We happened to have our tour scheduled on a day when it was rainy and cold (almost winter cold for Rome) - the tour guide said it was 100 degrees F this time last year though so go figure. Even in the rain, the grandeur of this place blows you away. Definitely a must see highlight in Rome. There are significant lines if you don’t book in advance and even still some lines if you do.

title: Must see - take the walking tour
text: Everyone who goes to Rome looks forward to see Colosseum. If you are seeing it for first time, it defies all imagination. We did the walking tour that covered Colosseum and Forum. It was an amazing experience. Wear your comfortable shoes and clothes. We bought stroller for our little one but it didn't help on the old paths and stairs.

title: A must visit
text: It’s a must visit for any tourist in Rome. Take a guided tour to enjoy and learn the history. Skip the line tickets are recommended. Walks of Italy is a great tour  company.

title: Must do but have a plan for this site
text:
You obviously must do this. A couple tips: (1) prebook tickets from coop culture and get a specific time booked. This will save a lot of time (2) even then, be prepared for a decent wait to get in during peak hours - we waited probably 20 minutes in line at noon (3) the site does not require 3 hours like TripAdvisor suggests. You can certainly spend that long but you can get through the main attractions in an hour

Take the tourists in stride - youre one of them - but plan ahead for this one.

title: Iconic Symbol of Rome
text:
Great experience to walk and see a fantastic piece of architecture and history. Truly breathtaking. 
* Recommended to book fast track tickets in advance to save you waiting in line for hours.
* The Colosseum can be done with a tour guide but I recommend doing it alone, it gives you more time to enjoy the view. 
* Make sure to get yourself an audio guide when you enter.

title: Family vacation
text: A must see if you’re in Rome. Pictures cannot do the colosseum any justice. This was truly Amazing to actually walk we’re gladiators once fought

title: Back to the past!
text: This incredible structure takes you back to the ancient roman times and allows your imagination to reconstruct the missing pieces of the puzzle of how it all worked way back when gladiators and emperors where alive.

title: Visit of the colosseum via touristation
text: Had a brilliant day. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and kind; made the history both interesting and fun. I believe he’s called Ricardo, if not it was the gentlemen who did the 3pm colosseum tour with the three British ladies on 08/08/19. 10/10 would defiantly recommend both the experience and tour guide as he defiantly made the trip worth it.

title: A place to visit to get flavour of our past history
text: An amazing place with lot of history. A must in Roma. Think about the upfront booking and be prepared to spend 1 hour in this place

title: Must see
text: This is a must see. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable. Visit when it is not so hot or bring water and an umbrella. Sooo much history to see. You don’t want to miss this.

title: Colosseum skip the line
text:
Dario tour guide - Inquiries
Walks - Italy, Spain & Greece
+18886838670
Excellent wonderful experience.
Skip the line tour recommend
Thank you Dario for a fantastic experience

title: Superb
text:
Amazing, incredible to be in front of a 2k year old building. I see that it's being fixed to prevent extra damage but it takes some of the charm all those new sections.
Make sure to take plenty of water so you don't need to buy overpriced water from the street vendors.

title: Pay for the VIP tour!
text: We very much enjoyed our VIP tour of the Colosseum, even though it was incredible hot.   The tour guide was knowledgeable and we got to see the lower levels.  The only downside is that we lost our guide when we got stuck behind a couple of girls who were having difficulty with the stairs.   A word of advise would be to be sure you ask your guests if they can manage stairs before you take them on a tour that has a million of them.

title: Amazing
text:
We walking into the bottom of the arena. It's a must!

Wasnt too busy and it was August we went. I'd advice to have a guide rather than going alone. We had a guide

title: The Coliseum will require about two or three hours for a few photos and a preliminary visit. It goes well with a visit t
text: The Historical center of Rome is something one never forgets. A Christian visitor will understand how harsh was being a Christian in those times. Visit the plave Peter and Paul were jailed and the Catacombs near. Just a visit to the Coliseum is something incomplete without the Roman Forum, the Pantheon, Piazza Navona, etc. For Brazilians, if one can arrange a visit to the Brazilian Embassy, one of the nicest palaces of downtown Rome.

title: Simply stunning
text: A must visit site! We had 2 teens with us and they loved the visit too. We did book a guided tour ahead of time due to some confusion with the Roma Pass. Kids don't need a Roma Pass, but they DO need "free" tickets to the Colosseum. We didn't realize that and so 1 day before we left, we tried getting tickets online for the kids and couldn't. So we did pay for a guided tour online and were pleasantly surprised. Great information and details we wouldn't have gotten on our own. The tickets to the Colosseum also include admission to the Forum/Palatine Hill (also amazing!). Probably the best part was not waiting in the giant line at the Colosseum. There are also water fountains to fill up water bottles in the Colosseum - we used these all over Rome.

title: How amazing is this structure
text: Even though it was very hot, I really enjoyed and was awestruck by this amazing structure. So much history.  Our Guide had excellent English and very knowledgeable.  I would like to go back when it is not so hot

title: Are you not entertained?!
text:
What’s there not to like about the place

Should you book a local tour? Nah.  You can just DIY it.  Plenty of things to learn online. As far as taking photos,if you can find a spot w barely any people around- take it already, as it gets pretty crowded so many potential photobombers

title: Crowds Everywhere
text: We paid for a tour to "skip the lines" but found ourselves waiting repeatedly anyway.  Enjoyed the perspective on how old it really is, history of the area, and understanding that they really don't know for sure what it originally looked like.

title: Overhyped
text: One of the main reason we visited Rome was to visit the Colosseum. I was a little disappointed with our visit, we’ve been to a few collosseums in Europe which were fantastic museums/buildings and expected “the colosseum” to be fantastic but it wasn’t more of a overhype then anything else.I do think it’s more impressive from the outside then in. The man in the gift shop didn’t give me any change which was annoying so if this happens to you please make a fuss!On the upside there are plenty of water fountains in the colosseum for you to use. A few exhibitions but they weren’t very interesting. Could have got a audio guide and thinking back I would have preferred using that then just walking around as it would have been nice to known more information then what was given.

title: Incredible! Exceeded my expectations. Night visit is special.
text:
One of the most incredible ancient structures. It was great to experience this wonderful venue first hand. The Colosseum, originally know as the Flavian Amphitheater, exceeded my expectations. 

I was reminded of the first time I saw the Grand Canyon (USA) in person. I had flown over it and seen hundreds of pictures and films depicting it over the years before I visited,  and yet I was still blown away by it. It was the same for me with the Colosseum. It is one of the most recognizable ancient Roman structures, if not the most, and yet I was still in awe when I was finally able to see it in person. And to think that this ancient wonder was built almost 2,000 years ago - truly amazing!

We chose a nighttime tour (City Wonders) and spent over an hour exploring the site with our tour guide, Daniella. The timing of our tour was perfect. We were able to see it at sunset and also after dark, giving us some interesting views and some great photo ops. 

We viewed the entry level, the second level and then went down to the sub-level. There were so many interesting aspects of the place, some of which I knew from historical books/shows and some that I had not heard until that day. 

It is much calmer and quieter for the nighttime tours because they only let a few tour groups in on a staggered schedule. There is no access at night to the general public. They also have a short film that they play for the tour groups, projected on screens in a section of the main floor of the amphitheater. There are two semicircle seating areas to watch from. It provides a dramatic narrative of the life of a gladiator.

title: Awesome piece of massive structure!
text: Still overwhelmed with the size of this structure. Knowing that some pieces of the colosseum were used to build the vatican, it is still standing until today. When we first went here two years ago, we only took the skip the line then bought an audio tour. Now we joined a tour group, which is worth it as he explained interesting facts.

title: Pre book your tour
text: This is one of Rome’s most popular tourist attractions and is well worth a visit. However I’d advise booking a tour online in advance to miss the queues. We went back to see it lit up at night, which was worth the second trip.

title: Flavian Amphitheatre
text:
Awe inspiring structure. Booked a guided tour which was very informative.

Only advice that I would offer is to book tickets beforehand to skip the queues and to book early morning or late afternoon if you are going in the summer due to the intense heat.

title: Must Experience
text:
This is a definite must see on a trip to Rome. Join a tour to skip the lines. I took a tour with Roman Guys and there are allotted times to enter so no long waits.
If you plan to tour on your own, come early to avoid the crowds. Once there are a certain number of tourist inside, they shut the doors and there will be an even longer wait. If you came on your own, you will have more time exploring inside as a tour is rather rushed.

title: Amazing historical place.
text: Visited in August and I didn't think the crowds were too bad. Found it hard to get my head around how old the Colosseum was and all the history within it. Was amazing to walk around and there was some information at different parts of the tour. A highlight of my visit to Rome!

title: Mixed reviews
text: So a mixed review we had a bad start as our booked tour guide there was issues so we had to abandon our visit and go the next day , the colosseum is a must when in Rome as it’s an icon but outside its chaos there seems to be no direction where your supposed to go and any signs , staff to guide but once in the site it is a must the ongoing construction work inside is overlooked as it’s a need but you do need to visit the colosseum but can’t see is going back to soon as once it’s seen it’s seen

title: Lots and lots of stairs
text: The colosseum is one of the must sees but we booked and it was a bit to late in the day to go . So when we had been walking around Rome for the day the last thing you want to do is queue and walk up and down loads of stairs. We had had enough by time we left.

title: It´s not only a grandeur...
text: Today, it´s a grandeur that impresses the eyes; in the past, an unfortunate site to sse, where numberless lives were cruelly wasted. It´s important to remember, and essential to inspire actions towards peace.

title: Awesome
text: A definite trip while in Rome but get there early or book a VIP trip as once there are 3000 people inside, they will not admit anyone until people leave. A decent guide also tells you lots of fantastic stories - we had Andre from Airbnb. Incredible stories!!

title: Not in August
text: I would say to Everybody. Come here in spring.... too much people and cola from kiosk (350ml) 3,5e. Not a good thing.

title: Crowded! Get a private tour!
text: You can't possibly visit Rome without seeing the Colosseum! However, the lines are so long and it's so crowded! Opt to pay the extra money and get a tour. We got a private tour (a bit more pricey) and were very happy with our choice. Either way, group tour or private tour will be beneficial as there is much more history than our limited Hollywood knowledge!

title: Wow
text: Amazing to see what was built and achieved and still standing, loved visiting Roman forum and palintine hill following day however this area was not wheel chair friendly at all, lifts not working large cobbles which is a shame as got the wheel chair user got stuck at the entrance and if she could of got passed that paths improved alot

title: There is an elevator!
text: You'll definitely need a "skip the line" ticket on a tour of the Colosseum. In summer, like this year, it's incredibly crowded and hot. Our guide, Eddie, was aware that his group was sweltering and found us shade whenever he could. He also pointed out an elevator for those 65 or disabled that was available to get to the upper level. We had to walk down but that's easier even in a crowd!

title: Best Tour!
text: This was my 2nd time to the Colosseum, the first time with a tour guide. Vincent with What a life yours was incredible. He truly brought us back and time and was a wonderful story teller.

title: Il Colosseo!
text: In one word... Magnificent! Words can't adequately express or describe the wonder and complete awesomeness of this ancient building. You are genuinely taken aback upon entering and it takes a few moments to take it all in. Then you spend the next few minutes trying to imagine the amphitheatre being chockful of spectators, gladiators, lions, senators and finally the emperor himself! From the moment this great building comes into view and every step you take closer to it, you immerse yourself into its magnificence and you just don't notice the huge crowds at first, as all you do is look up and marvel at this historic building's gigantic proportions. We booked the Skip the Line self guided Colloseum Arena Floor, palatine Hill and Forum tour with TIQUETS (download their app) meeting the tour guide by the Colloseo Metro B entrance.  The checking in process was a tad confusing as we joined one queue only to be pointed in the direction of another Tiquets guide, joined that queue only to be pointed in the direction of another Tiquets guide who then gave us a colour identifying sticker and then sent back to the previous guide to put our names on her list for the security guards at the entrance of the Colloseum. So, lesson learned: before joining the queue, politely ask for the correct guide to go to first for your sticker, before joining any queue... It'll save you a lot of time! We were then taken to a priority first entrance to the Colloseum and walked straight in! Boom! No hassle, no bother! I started videoing from this entrance just so that I could play back our reaction upon walking into the arena... Photos and videos just don't do it justice... Incredible! It was very hot, but eben

title: Must see in Rome
text: This is an obvious must see in Rome. The history of the Colosseum is amazing and the tour inside is a lot of fun (but a little crowded)

title: Amazing time with Vivicos International
text: Get yourself a fantastic tour guide so that you know exactly what you're looking at! We had such an informative one with a lot of in-depth knowledge, charisma, and kindness. Giorgio from Vivicos International Travel is a true gem as he was able to tell us not only about the Colosseum but ancient Rome as a whole. We couldn't thank him enough! It was over a 90 degree day and he made sure we were able to stop to refill water bottles and were situated in the shade as he spoke when possible.

title: Awesome
text: Phenomenal. Try to do a tour because you learn so much history and even get the chance to walk on the levels that restricted to public with regular tickets. All I can say is you gotta see it!!

title: Spectacular venue
text: An absolute must place to visit whilst in Rome. Although it has a very blood thirsty history due to the activities that took place here it really does show off the practical mind of the Romans. You would not believe they were so ahead of their times in practical terms. Don’t miss this place on any visit here. Take care in mid summer as it can be over bearable with the heat and limited shade.

title: Awesome
text: Awesome experience!! Visited with my 9 year old son and he loved it too. So glad I booked the skip the line tickets as looking at the queues in the sweltering August heat... well it didn’t look like fun. The site is mind blowing and our guide was great, very interesting and entertaining. The only downside was being harassed constantly to buy water, sun hats and selfie sticks etc on the way in but a fork no thanks got us through, When visiting Rome you have to go here 💗

title: Need a tour guide
text: Fascinating visit, but would have been more interesting with a knowledgeable tour guide.  We were under the impression we had booked a guided tour, but were instead just walked over to the entrance where we had priority “skip the line” privileges.

title: Yummo!!
text: We had a beautiful night here a fantastic guy named katut served us and his service was amazing he made our night wonderful highly recommend this place very clean and great pizza!!Thankyou team colosseum!

title: Its all in ruin
text: It sucks, the queve is long af even tough i had fast pass i had to stand in the sun with a group even tough i specificly ordered no guided tour. We spent 40 min in the scorching sun just to see something complety in ruins or covered with metalbars. Dont go to the Colosseum its really  a dissapointment

title: Breath taking
text: Taken away by the views and the ruins. Could have stayed all day just walking around and soaking in the history. Absolutely stunning.

title: Bombarded by vendors along the way
text: Wear a hat, carry a water and hold a selfie stick on your way to this historical site. Otherwise you will be bombarded by Indian vendors following you trying to sell their goods. Didn’t make for a very pleasant walk on our first day in Rome.

title: Worth the visit!
text:
The colosseum looks awesome from the outside but it’s well worth going into to get a feel of the size / scale of the place.  There’s also tonnes I didn’t know about it and how it used to work and what it’s been used for over the years.

You can get entry to Palatine Hill (just over the road) and the Colosseum for €12 at the Palatine Hill entrance.  They will give you a set time for going into the Colosseum so if you want to do them both in the same day I’d recommend going early (or booking in advance).  The ticket is normally valid for 2 days so if you can’t get to the Hill because of your Colosseum time you can go back the next day.

I wouldn’t bother getting a skip the line ticket, we went mid afternoon to get the tickets in August and didn’t really have to queue.  Because we went a bit later our Colosseum entry time was 5pm, so we spent about 1.5hours walking around Palatine Hill, grabbed some food and then went over.  There are several restaurants / cafes immediately around Palatine Hill and the Colosseum although not much in the venue itself.  

Most of the hop on hop off buses go past here so again would recommend investing in a ticket.

title: 10 out of 10
text:
Where to even start, this was the main reason for the visit to Rome.

I fell in love with fighting at a young age from the Age of Empires franchise and later through kick boxing and K1, continued by my late grandfather's knowledge of all things roman. He would often read me passages of The Bello Gallico translated by himself.

If I'm being totally honest, there's not much of the original colosseum left as time has taken its toll but just to be inside this amazing building that I have dreamed about since a young child was enough to nearly bring a tear to the eye.

If you're going to build your holiday around one thing, make it this!!!

title: Historical gem
text: Wow wow wow. Best money ever spent. It was such an amazing experience to walk the ruins and imagine how things were in ancient times.  Only criticism was the Peddlers outside who kinda tricked us to giving them money for bracelets. Said bracelets are a gift and then as I walked away they asked for money.  Won’t take the bracelets back and pressured to give money.  But that did not take away from out day

title: Totally impressive
text: Don't miss the Colosseum but plan your visit according to the crowds. Avoid the peak times. Just an awesome place to visit.

title: Must see in Rome
text:
Need at least a day to walk and read the history.
Take lots of water to drink and change in euros for bathrooms. Expect crowds.

title: If not for the heat
text: Unfortunately, we visited this site in July.  It was miserably hot!  Be sure to wear hats and drink plenty of water if July is your only choice.  The tour was well worth the time!  There is so much interesting history.  Both from years ago and what happened since the empire fell.  There’s definitely a lot of walking to do around the ruins.  There’s some information inside which is like a museum.

title: Worth a trip
text: Buy the tickets in advance otherwise you could be waiting for hours to get in. An amazing place, lifts available for those who struggle with stairs. Right next to the Roman Forum and Palentine Hill.

title: Roman deception
text: Guided tour helps. Guide took us arnd with all background story. Monument is preserved well. One can feel the history

title: Worth a visit
text:
Booked a tour via Viatour... provided head sets a short walk away on arrival handed over to our groups English speaking tour guide.

Tour was informative and interesting, standing in the area area looking around gives the best perspective of the scale of the Colosseum.

title: Must see!
text: An amazing must see attraction in Rome. I spent an hour in the colosseum as part of my tour and then another few hours in the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. This was by far the highlight of my holiday as the sight was incredible!

title: A marvellous architecture
text:
We brought last minute tickets on Tiqets after reading to make sure to have the skip the line ticket purchased in advance to avoid wasting half a day in tickets line. 
And trust me I’m glad we did. We raeched colosseum at 9:30 in the morning and the line was already long. We met our Tiqets help just as they had informed and the process was smooth. Around 10-10:15 we were already inside seeing this marvellous architecture and the largest amphitheater ever built at the time of 70-80 AD and held over 50,000-80,000 spectators almost 2000 years ago. Morden day football stadium. It had underground animal pens from which animals and gladiators were lifted to the arena floor and get this the arena was flooded with water to have mock navel ship battles... !!!! Say whattt

The gladiators might be prisoners of war,slaves,criminals condemned to the death sentence. text:

I’d recon to avoid all other sites and tour guides etc. get the audio guide from the site and you are on your own to indulge and immerse in history at your own pace. Drifting away in your imagination in that way I believe is so much better than going with a big group in someone else’s terms. 

Don’t buy water bottles from street sellers as there’s plenty of water in fountains. Wear comfy loose cloths and walking shoes if you play to walk in the forum and the palatine hill.

The reason we did this is because the prices were stupidly high and we didn’t want a tour, so we waited. The queue was actually pretty good fun and the universal language of laughter worked very well. 

The street sellers and ticket touts are very intrusive in the area, when you have young children, they are very scary, getting in your face and trying to sell you various things. I understand that they need to make a living but really? I could have easily punched a couple of them. 

Anyway, with that aside, the actual coliseum was gorgeous and well worth the wait of an hour. The toilets were easy to find and we wandered through the different floors and areas. It was just magical.

title: Wow
text:
A great morning spent wandering around this impressive sight. 

Lots of research about whether to pre-book tickets or not. We did in the end for 8.30am as we wanted to avoid the midday sun. We booked the day before no problem through the official website and arrive just before 8am. 

The queues had started forming but not massively. Two lines. One for no tickets (can buy on arrival) at the gate and one for e-tickets/print outs. 

Our tickets were €24 to include the colosseum, forum and hill. Plus a video guide. The guide wasn’t that great and we didn’t use it but they were the only tickets we could get for 8.30am. 

The tickets are €12 at the door for the 3 sites but without any guide. 

If you don’t mind getting up and there early you don’t need to pre-book tickets. The doors open at 8.30am and by 8.40am we were inside, and the couple we were standing next to who didn’t have tickets were in 10mins later. 

Go see it.

First, yes you should see it, it is worth it.  There are an abundance of guided tours available.  We opted for a skip-the-lines tour which was great.  We were able to explore freely but couldn't go onto the grounds where the gladiators actually fought (specific tours are allowed there). The experience is definitely funny and memorable, but they are extremely demanding and will snatch your personal property like your phone for instance until you pay them.  They actually tried to demand we pay them $100 for the 5 minutes they spent with us.  Just beware.

title: Roma pass(ed barely)
text:
We got into the colosseum with the roma pass 48h.

Since the rule to have a timed reservation the explanations are unclear, to state it friendly.

How we got told to get in (websites included) :
-roma pass
-book reserved time slot
-get receipt of booking at 'a reserved counter at the roman forum ticket office' (romapass.it says so in the FAQ, also coopculture says '.. A receipt to be collected at the ticket desk' 
- 'it is strictly necessary to arrive at 30 before booked time' (also on coopculture.it)
-security check where no bottles are allowed (coopculture.it)
-scan pass
-inside

How we actually got in:
-bought roma pass
-reserved a slot on coopculture.it
-searched where the ticket desk was online: turns out to be a 12min hike. 
-once there it was closed (my bad, its closed on a Tuesday) 
-got in line 'reserved ticket' half an hour before booked time slot with roma pass, ID & email of comfirmation of booked time slot
-got told to come back later.. Twice.. 
-got in @10 minutes until booked time. 
-security check (with bottles they probably mean glass bottles because alot of ppl had their bottles with them) 
-they scanned pass
-done

I hope this will help someone who was as confused as me.

title: Must see while in Rome
text:
Obviously, this is one of the must-see stops in Rome.  You can walk around the structure and take pictures for free.  Tip - if you want photos of the exterior, go after it closes and you won't have 100+ people in your photo.  It's nicely lit at night.
If you want to go inside you will need timed tickets or a tour.  We booked a night tour that was great and included the underground area which you do not get to see on any of the day tours.  But because we knew that we were not going to be able walk down into the Forum or Palatine Hill on the night tour, we went at 9:00 am the same day to see those assuming we would not be able to get tickets to the Colosseum (tickets are generally for all three with the Colosseum ones timed, but they run out of these for the day sometime in the morning).  We went to the Palatine Hill entrance (this is important) where there was NO line, and easily got tickets which included 9:40 am entry to the Colosseum.  Whatever you do, DO NOT stand in the ticket line in front of the Colosseum! It gets crazy long (an hour or more) and there are two other ticket booths very close by - one at the Forum (2 minute walk), and one at Palatine Hill (3-5 minute walk).  
During the day the Colosseum was very crowded and clogged up with tour groups, despite the capacity limits imposed by the timed tickets.  Don't expect to get too much out of the museum area as you can barely move with all the groups stopped in front of the displays.  However, once you get into the interior of the structure it is magnificent and worth the wait.  
When we went back that night it was quiet and obviously more peaceful.  However, you couldn't really appreciate the size and architecture of the interior as well, and the photo opportunities were much more limited than during the day.  With the night tour though came the underground areas, which are not accessible during the day,  We found this area fascinating, particularly the elevator system rigged up to get the animals and gladiators on the stage.  So if you are interested in that consider one of the night tours.

title: Love the history and walking the site
text: The history here is something else along with the views. We enjoyed our visit. Prepare to walk and the walking sometimes is not on a flat surface. The stairs are steep but the views are worth the climb. Get the go to the front of line passes as that makes getting in less of a hassle. There is not parking close so getting dropped off and picked up is a better way to get close enough so you don't have to walk a long distance. The area in front of the colosseum is an area where you have to watch your belonging. Numerous people got up next to us trying to sell us stuff and to help us get in the site. We finally got rude and told told them to get out of our space. They just went over to someone else and started all over again.
