title: Wonder-ful
text:
As far as local attractions go, it's hard to top a Wonder of the World, and ever amongst the minority of destinations that is assigned to I've got to make a case for the Colosseum in Rome as one of the most necessary to see at some stage.
 
Built during the Roman Empire in a frankly audacious feat of engineering for the time, something you'll learn more about should you visit, and the arena that played host to gladiators still manages to maintain a sense of grandeur few alternatives can come close to.
 
Just walking around the structure is mighty impressive, but when there its absolutely essential to go inside at least once. Once you do you have access to ground-floor platforms at each side and you can go to the upper tiers to look down. The floor has long gone, however this is a good thing as it lets you marvel at the series of subterranean tunnels gladiators, slaves and animals were led to the various parts of the arena through.
 
In addition, in the upstairs section of the building there is a small museum dedicated both to the history of the building, and sometimes with guest exhibits, this is fairly fascinating in itself, and is followed by the obligatory gift shop.
 
If there is but one criticism, it's the unavoidable fact that the place is always absolutely HOACHING with tourists, which can mean depending on when you get there you may need to sit in an abhorrent line for a ticket. It's worth noting your tickets also grant access to the nearby Forum, so its actually often quicker buying one there, visiting it first and skipping the que at the Colosseum.
 
Either way, you've got to make a point of visiting this wonderful historical marvel, and with admission a fairly inclusive price there is little reason not to if in the Eternal City.

title: Fascinating
text: To stand a stretch your neck up to this unbelievable colossal construction dating back 80AD, takes a while to digest and get your head around, apart from dominating theatre itself, the stone gate with roman figures / pillars carved high is a sight in itself.  The original stone foundations to the right are sectioned off but clearly visible. There’s scaffolding and building structure work going on, noticeable change in stone. The queue to enter was far too long to stand in, couples, families and groups of tourists, so book prior to visiting.

There is ongoing preservation.  If you can't do a guided tour, definitely read up on its history.  It is beautiful and awe inspiring when you consider how this was built so many years ago.

text:
A must see place...get a guided tour to learn all tt

title: This place is beautiful but the company is unorganized
text:
If you get tickets online from the official website, be prepared to be tossed into a huge line up anyway. If your going in high season. If people bud in front of you don’t be afraid to speak up and ask the staff to do something about it. They will. But you have to speak up or you will never get in. Even if you have pre bought tickets with a time stamp. We bought tickets three months ago online through the official website and still got tossed into an hour and a half line up, just to have people budding in front of us including “walking tours” because coop culture is understaffed and cannot handle these kinds of crowds. Thankfully we were loud and made sure people DID NOT bud in front of us. And one staff member Alessandro was amazing and helped us keep the cheaters “line budders” from getting away with it once we got sick of the nonsense and summoned some help. Once you are inside it is worth the wait, it is beautiful, do not sit on any of the artifacts at ground level as they are trying to preserve it. There was a very rude unidentifiable staff member walking tour guide who was yelling like a jerk. He was not security or staff as far as I can tell. He was the one yelling out “family” and barking at the public like he owned the place. So watch out for that fool. If you are a local like him and that passionate to yell at people for what they thought was righteous maybe go clean the streets and pick garbage and do your part like a proper citizen rather than being a tool and yelling at small children and moms.

My advice make sure you do buy tickets online way ahead of time. Make sure you show up with lots of time to stand inline anyway even if you think you have “skip the line” and be prepared for very rude tourists everywhere. This place is amazing but give yourself allot of time. The online tickets sell out and you cannot get them the day you show up, that is an even bigger line. Good luck :)

text: Get a tour we used Roman Guide because you are able to go to the 3rd level which is locked. Many great views to take pictures. Go as early as possible.

title: Magnificent
text: This amazing build encapulates Rome's architecture huge and so impressive. Just being there is a pleasure, this like the city is a must see. There are ques for entry and when it's very hot it can be uncomfortable, take water. We loved it A MUST.

title: Must See
text: The Colosseum is a must see when visiting Rome. Get your tickets at the site. They are good for both the Colosseum and the Palatine Hill and you can visit each one on consecutive days. It's nice to break it up over two days especially if you have kids (or adults) whose attention span can't handle two historic archeological sites at one. Marvel that the structure has survived almost 2000 years and was almost demolished.

title: A Must See
text: A wonderful part of Roman history that should not be missed.  Recommend taking a morning tour so you're not just looking at things with no explanation.

title: Visit to the Colosseum
text: We took a guided tour to the Colosseum (real name Flavian Amphitheatre). This meant that we skipped The queues and learnt information that would probably not have been clear to us if we’d not had a guide.

title: The Colosseum is a must see.
text:
Full disclosure - I have been here before.
The tour is good.  They give you lots of history and the tour we went on took us down to the floor of the Colosseum.  As impressive as it was; it is not as impressive as the outside.  There is a park where the tour met that was perfect for pictures, a loaf of bread and a jug of wine, and relaxing.
The tour is good, the crowds are ridiculous, and the hike is tiring: of course this was our third day in Rome and we just saw the Forum.
There is quite a bit of walking so pace yourself.

title: Stunning Site
text: Stunning building - would have been more stunning if hundreds of wagons of stone hadn't been "stolen" to build St Peters! Very complicated, intricate building which in its day must have been state-of-the-art.  You may not be in agreement with what it was built for but it was the way the world was then - and, that aside,  you have to admire the ingenuity of the designers/builders.  Magnificent!  Stunning displays inside on the second tier too.

title: Book direct before you go
text: We booked online direct from Coop Culture. We walked there as we’d discovered most areas in Rome are walkable and the hop on and off buses were very expensive. When we arrived we were about 2 hours early for our entry time so decided to go to Palatine Hill first. I’m glad we did as it was much quieter then. As we left the queues were huge! As long as you have tickets the only queue is for security check. From what I could gather there was no such thing as a queue jump ticket for either Palatine or the Colosseum. We then went over to the Colosseum. Again, we only had to queue for security. People without tickets must have been queuing in the boiling heat for hours. It was incredibly busy but just amazing. A must see.

title: Amazing!
text:
Obviously a must see when in Rome, this place is well preserved and it is great to see a glimpse of how it would have been back in the day. Be prepared for it not to be clean but other than that a must see landmark

Tip - Beware of pickpocket and beggers. They are part of a syndicate

title: Archetectural Wonder
text: After seeing pictures of the Colosseum, the real thing is totally different. The place is incredibly huge for the time period it was built, and after all these years , most of it is still there.

title: Take the tour skip the queue
text: Firstly go on a guided tour to ensure you get the most from your visit, guided tours vary but ours has headphones and an excellent guide so you always heard the guide and could afford to  drag your heels a little whilst taking photos and still not miss a word. Secondly be aware that security is high, you will be asked to put all electronic devices through an x-ray machine, and walk through a detector. That said the experience has to be experienced to be appreciated, I had already done a humanities course on the Colosseum and I still learned a lot. Equally my young son's had learnt about the Colosseum at school and were still Impressed by the tour. If you aren't doing a guided tour, you can see most of the site from outside and it is just a ruin with a few information panels and static displays. If you are on a tight budget, you could walk around and then do the nearby sights for free. If you do go in, be aware there are free water fountains inside where you can fill your bottles, so don't waste your money bartering with the vendors outside for bottles of water and do partake of the local water. All in all it was an excellent experience and one I'm happy to recommend to anyone visiting Rome.

title: A Must See in Rome
text: We took a guided tour which allowed us to skip the lines (highly recommend this in the summer months). It is just really neat to see the architecture and explore the structure. If it’s a hot day definitely bring some sun shade (hat or umbrella) and water. They have a free fountain to re-fill water bottles inside. Certainly worth seeing once in your life.

title: Trip
text: Cool place. I recommend but price instead wow. A lot of people and more organization is needed Tourists behave me animals in the jungle but the place is magic.

title: Hot and crowded
text: We went in late July.  It was hot, crowded and expensive but what an amazing place.  My sense is that the historical Rome thing is probably best done out of high season .  However it is the most incredible place.  Takes your breath away

title: Visit just before it closes and beat the queue
text: By visiting 1-2 hrs before it closes you'll beat a lot of the queues but still have enough time to visit this magnificent treasure.

title: The best part of our time trip!
text: An absolute must see for Rome. We went on a guided tour and it was well worth the extra money - fascinating.

title: Amazing experience
text: Would fully recommend having a guided tour to experience the magnificence and history of this monumental creation. Amazing experience and a must see when in Rome.

title: Do the tour with Arena floor access
text: This was our Rome highlight.  We all loved it.  Our guide was good and by booking a tour we got arena floor access which is worth Doing to get a real sense of the place.   History at its finest!

title: Take the tour.
text: Buy your tickets in line, the line is about 30 minutes. Then it’s fairly quick to get inside. Very well organized walkways that take you through everything. Make the effort to go to second level. In addition, make sure you bring water.

title: Amazing building but getting inside was not worth it
text:
One of the must sees in Rome. Still more beautiful from the outside that inside. Also the lines with or without prebooked tour were quite long and inside there were a lot of people. 

Got the best pictures from Colosseum from the outside.

title: Breathless with wonder!
text: The Colosseum is exactly that - colossal! It inspires awe - at how huge it is, and how it has survived as a representative of Roman culture for ages. Beware - the even the Skip-the-line lines are long. But there is no way I would have skipped seeing the interiors - or the stories the guide had to say. It has a bloody history, with it witnessing thousands of gladiators and animals being slaughtered. But it's goosebumps inducing to walk down the same aisle where spectators once sat watching gladiators fight tigers or each other. I didn't have a chance to visit the underground chambers; planning to do that on my next visit.

title: A must visit
text: We purchased tickets online ahead of our trip which I would highly recommend doing, as the queue to purchase tickets on the day looked horrendous. We still had to queue to get in but it moved fairly quickly, was pretty packed inside and toilet queue was also lengthy, but all very worth the waits! Breathtaking.

title: Fast track tickets are a must!
text: I don't think you can really understand the enormity of this until you see it in real life. I would definitely recommend booking fast track tickets because the lines were insane but I'm glad we persevered because it was well worth it.

title: Historical Symbol of Rome
text: Exploring the area while listening to the tour guide about the history of the Colosseum is so amazing

title: Superb ancient monument
text: We did a night tour of the Colosseum which meant we avoided the crowds and got to see it in a very different light - thoroughly recommend it as you get a real feel for the place with such a small group

title: Amazing!
text: As you can expect - it's packed with tourists but it's a must see. Truly amazing, try to take the underground tour if u can.

title: A must see.
text:
Having paid for fast track before we left the UK, we joined the queue to get in, but we found we did not have the correct entrance tickets. Had to go to another booth for the entrance tickets. Because our granddaughter was under 14 we had to take her to the booth to verify she was under 14. There was no-one to help us before we queued up, we found it confusing.
Once through security it is an amazing place. There are toilets on the ground floor, also a lift is available for the disabled to the next level.
Plenty of photograph opportunities while wondering around.

title: Spectacular!
text: Bought last minutes tickets night before online with coopculture.it smooth transaction and able to skip queues. Colosseum well worth visit - even in 37* heat!

title: Historic Wonder
text: Much history and fine photo ops. We took a City Tour. 3 hours was a bit long for me personally, but our tour guide was very knowledgeable and interesting. I would recommend this tour for folks who are really interested in a detailed history of all aspects of this site. For people like myself who prefer a brief overview and like to take photos, I would skip the tour.

title: Evening tour
text: We went on an evening tour with Walks of Italy it included the Cosseum, underground, and access to arena floor. It was a great tour and viewing in the evening provided for some great pictures.

title: Exhausting and boring
text: I waited in line for almost an hour and it was extremely hot. When you get inside you have to wait in a line again if you haven’t purchased a ticket beforehand. The price is okay but only if you visit the Roman forum too. The price just for the Colosseum is too much. It is very underwhelming inside and a little bit overcrowded. There are too many tours happening at the same time and it is not easy to enjoy when you’re alone or with friends.

title: Awe inspiring!
text: 2000+ years of history absolutely awe inspiring! We chose to go it alone and to be able to read and learn about the history was remarkable archeologists were working at the time so we couldn't get down to the bottom, but walking round the side was just as good. Lots of photo opportunities.

title: LivItaly small group tour of Underground Colosseum, Forum, and Palatine Hill
text: We had a small group tour with Giulia on an extremely hot day in Rome. It was one of our best tours on the entire trip. We visited the Forum and Palatine Hill first and Giulia really made them come alive. She had drawings of what the places might once have looked like to help us envision everything. The highlight was the Colosseum tour, especially the underground portions. We saw where gladiators and wild beasts waited to make their entrance, and the reconstruction of the elevators used to make them appear through trap doors in the Colosseum floor. Giulia was excellent and made sure we stopped at every available fountain to stay hydrated and only gave long descriptions when we were comfortably seated in the shade. Highly recommend.

title: A must see.
text: It’s very simple. It’s a must see if you go to Rome. You’ve made it all the way there. So, visit it. It’s that simple.  It’s a gem and a beauty.  It’s a landmark so there will be lots’a people.  But go.

title: Colosseum, WOW!
text: We went on a paid tour and what a great idea that was. The waiting line was about 300 meters long (4 people wide...). With the tou4, we skipped the line and went straight in. The colosseum is the most impressive place I have ever visited. Having a tour guide also made it super interesting. Our guide was so knowledgeable of the place and told us amazing stories and very accurate facts about the place. Great visit, one I will never forget!

title: Must see
text: Amazing place. Definitely worth going to but would advise buying your ticket online as queues were very long and to bring drinks and hats as it was very hot there.

title: Get your guide tout
text:
Fabio our tour guide was amazing, he has great knowledge and kept us entertained throughout the tour. 

It was incredibly hot so it was uncomfortable waiting around in the sun as it was very crowded. 

Too many sellers standing outside trying to sell water , fans etc. which wad quiet annoying. 

The colosseum is incredible and because of Fabio’s explanations and stories it was entertaining. 

Bring lots of water if you’re going in summer, wear comfortable shoes and hold your bags and phones toghtly

title: Shocking
text: It's all after falling down and the chariot race never happened. Julius Cesar was rumoured to turn up the day we were there but never showed.

title: Rome Visit
text: We wasn't able to enter the Colosseum because we did not book in advance and the lines was tooooo long (if you plan to enter purchase skip the line tickets) the outside eara was fine and we enjoyed the place and took many photos... the place is highly crowded

title: Holiday in Rome
text: The queues are very long in the hot sun.  They could improve service by online services or open more cashier points to speed process.  Visitors will end up abused by people offering exhobitant fees to jump the queue which is more than three times the normal fees.  They generate enough revenue to justify employing more cashiers to collect the revenue   . History site of where war games were staged from.  Something of this kind was too cruel and have no room in modern society.  This should never allowed to happen in the first place.

title: Queue.......
text: Fast track entry for us was an hours wait in over 30 degrees heat.....which wasn’t what we paid for, however the Colosseum is a must see sight in Rome and steeped in history

title: Brilliant and informative tour by Rosa!
text:
We visited the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum on Thursday 15 Aug after booking through Trip Advisor.

The tour office was easy to find and was just along the road from the Colosseum. Rosa took us smoothly through the entrance and directly onto the Arena floor - imagining the place full of people shouting for your blood as you walk in was awe inspiring. 

We spent an hour exploring the Colosseum before heading to Palatine Hill. I’m a big girl and was a bit concerned about this part as other reviews mentioned a ‘trek’ , although that’s a bit misleading. There were a few sets of stairs on a slope to climb and while I was a bit breathless at the top, it wasn’t anywhere near as bad as I thought. And it’s worth going up there for the stunning views. 

From there we head down into the Forum, and the ruins are spectacular. You can feel the history of the place.

The tour lasted 3.5 hours, and was very informative. My boyfriend is a big Roman history fan, and enjoyed it immensely. Rosa was a funny and knowledgeable guide and we would recommend her without question.

title: BOOK A TOUR BEFORE YOU GO
text:
WOW!  What can I say that others haven’t already...  this is truly amazing.  

My only advice to visitors is book a tour with skip the queue and book it early in the morning.  We had a tour at 9am and even then the queues to get in were ridiculously at least 2hours.  

Please don’t just turn up.  If you do, bring a hat and water as you’ll be waiting a long time. 

A must see!

title: Must see
text: This is a must see place in rome. But we didnt go inside because there were to much line and its expensive. But the view is beatuful from the outside as well.

title: Outstanding Architecture. Very Crowded. Take Water
text:
We booked a half day tour to the Colosseum. We walked around the Roman Ruins, Gardens and Palatine Hills. The guide explained about aqueducts and the Roman way of life. We saw Caesar’s burial place and other places of interest. This took about 1 and half hour and it was very hot. Do take water with you. 
Then we walked to the Colosseum and heard about the gladiators while walking around the forum. It was very crowded but still very interesting.
Our guide told us that all work had been stopped for restoration. Instead the ruins were just being minimally preserved. This seems such a pity as the restoration work already done looked so complete.

title: Nearly 2000 years old
text:
You really need to book with TripAdvisor!
Otherwise you will stay in the line and wait for your entrance. We had a small group and have a lot of information for more than a half a day it was a really nice tour.

title: Bad experience. Feel like I've been lied to.
text:
I couldn't find the meeting location by email, and by the time I was answered by the office on the phone, I was a 10-15 minutues late. They said it's "too late" to join and had to pay a ridiculous amount of money to reschedule.
But on the next day, I realized I was lied to. Because in reality, We have not started the actual tour until 30+ minutes in abd I could easily join the other day, which made me feel tricked.

And was the tour itself worth the wait? No. It was "meh"and the tour guude was ok at best. Not to mention the "exclusive" arena floor where we spent 5 minutes and was not worth it at all.

title: a trip down history lane
text: ignoring the long lines and smelly bathrooms.... Amazing! you can literally feel and see in your imagination how the Romans of 100 BCE entertained themselves. and the genius of the architecture simply leaves you speechless

title: Historical Place
text: It was in my number 1 for my visit to Rome. It's a great historical place,And the place is similar to what we have read on history.I recommend taking online tickets to avoid long que.At least 4-5 hours time should be allocated if you are visiting in busy times.This iconic landmark of Rome is a must visit place for your travel to Rome.You can imagine how strong the engineering of this building is to have been Constructed such long time ago and existing till today.We can spot renovation works going on.

title: Tour with The Roman Guy
text: Great tour with The Roman Guy.  Knowledgeable guide who had lots of great details and also was an excellent manager of time and kept all stops in the shade.

title: A must visit - but DON'T queue here!!!
text:
If you come to Rome, definitely don't miss the Colosseum, as it is one of the main attractions. However, I would advise that you do NOT queue here!!! Instead, buy your ticket at the entrance to the Roman Forum (the ticket gets you into the Forum and Colosseum for €12 total, or €2 if you are an EU citizen between the ages of 18-25). I would HIGHLY recommend queueing here, as the line is much shorter than at the Colosseum itself: I waited for 30 minutes in August. 
Also, if you don't want to spend a fortune on a walking tour, just use the FREE Rick Stevens audio guide (find it online). He covers all of the information in about 40 minutes and guides you around the site which is lovely.

title: Allways good view!! Go there!!
text:
Is a must!
Even you dont enter, if you are first time in rome you must go!! The walking street going to colosseum because is pietonal, is very very good!!
So please go there!!!

title: Be prepared to queue
text:
Visited the Colosseum in July and although we arrived early morning we had to queue for 3 hours to get to ticket office.  My advice would be to pre book.
Be prepared as the toilets are a disgrace. 
Lift available for disabled or those not able to manage stairs.

title: Massive
text: Make sure you have a guided tour! Make sure you get skip the line tickets! All I've got to say is Wow! Massive!!!

title: Good visit
text: Did a tour here and got to take amazing pics with the view from both inside and outside of the colosseum.

title: Justified as one of the wonders of the World.
text: There are so many superlatives about this unique site. It’s history is studded with many tales of heroics tempered with the appalling loss of life to so many Humans and Animals and horrific injuries for those that survived. All in the name of ‘Entertainment’. We enjoyed our visit but felt that our wonder of it was tinged with sadness for what had occurred there so long ago! As you would expect, Visitors today are dealt with expeditiously and with a great deal of professionalism. The only drawback are the numerous traders outside who cannot take no for an answer!

title: Good to visit with a tour guide
text: We used a tour guide and it was great. She could give additional info about certain areas that were not readily available elsewhere. Since my husband uses a cane and our daughter sprained her ankle the 1st day of our trip, she made sure they were able to take elevators while I did the stairs.

title: VERY busy, book your tickets a few weeks in advance to beat que!
text: as above. Book your ticket before and you skip the huge ques. The place is heaving with tourists. We went inside around 9am and it was a good time but around dinner time it gets busy!

title: It’s Hot & Packed
text: It’s nice but during the month of August it is soo hot and it’s filled with Tourist . I would want to come when it’s cooler and not soo packed

title: Cheap tickets for europe adults between 18 - 25
text:
Big line but it was worth it.
Ticket for europe adults between 18 - 25 years old is only 2 euros per person. Dont pay for skip the line traps that scammers outside offers you. With that ticket you can visit colosseum, roman forum and palatin.

title: Amazing History and Beaty
text: I was in Rome in April and it was amazing, the weather was phenomenal and the people were fantastic. We landed in the early morning and had our driver waiting for us. A local who was amazing and gave us a little tour and history of this beautiful city. The Colosseum was amazing and you could feel the history. If you want to see it even more beautiful, visit at night when they have it illuminated. If you get a chance take the tour and get all the history and information that you may or may not know about and some very interesting facts. Needless to say, I will be coming back again.

title: I wouldn't take the tour. Thats me. Read about it before you go.
text: No doubt its one of the modern wonders. I am a runner. Every city I go, I run. Even if you dont run, get out there early in the morning around 6 am - 8 am. You can walk around peacefully and take many pictures as possible.Crowds and the heat is insane during the summer months. You dont want to be stuck with a our guide for 2-3 hrs with hot blazing son.

title: Impressive in real life
text:
First tip is to go to Palatine Hill for your ticket (unless you’ve booked online through the official website) as that cuts down the time you wait in a queue. If you’re going in summer make sure you have on suntan lotion! 
Didn’t bother with the audio tour, shame about the scaffolding obscuring your view of parts of the audio tour.

title: Fascinating and a must see!
text:
The Colosseum is a true wonder and what you learn on the visit is amazing! We bought fast pass tickets online so that we didn’t have to Queue for so long and it was definitely worth it! We were in a small Queue that took us 15 minutes to get through compared to the main queue that had an estimated wait for 1-1.5 hours 😳 

My only criticism of the Colosseum is that of it’s organisers lack of structure! Unlike with the other attractions that we visited, the Colosseum didn’t have a structured path around, so everyone was walking all over the place, which for someone like me that is a chronic organiser was a bit of a nightmare! I like a route to follow so that I know I’m not missing something!

title: Beautiful
text: Very crowded, with lots of group tours. There is some renovation work going on for the moment. For the best pictures, it is recommended to climb the stairs, which is on the same side as the train station. From there, you can get amazing photos.

title: Good
text: Verey nice place ,we recomend it with a guided tour , and we enjoy it , skip the line also, but avoid it in August it burn out our mind .

title: Fantastic site/ Unorganised
text: The site of the colosseum is fantastic with plenty to see and if you include the ticket to the Roman forum etc then even better, my only gripe is that when entering the ticket area to get audio guides it is confusing and confusing where to use the audio guide

title: Fabulous
text: What can you say, one of the great wonders of the world, although very busy it has to be done, fantastic experience

title: Beautiful, especially at night
text: I did not go in the Colosseum for a tour, just went around the perimeter, which was nice enough.  The views in the evening from the adjacent park are breathtaking.

title: Go early!
text: We went to the Colessem at 9am with a company called Tiqets who do a package deal to skip the lines and go to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill (£27 I think?). Go as early as you can (especially in August as the heat can be brutal in the middle of the day!). Bring a water bottle as there are plenty of fountains to drink from. There are also free exhibitions at the Colosseum too. Enjoy!

title: A must !
text: Walk around the outside then go inside, walk to the upper seating and admire for a while. Also can climb underneath. Getting a feel of life 2000 years ago !

title: Great!
text: Well organized! The coliseum is a must see in Rome. MAKE SURE TO PURCHASE SKIP THE LINE TICKETS! I found the forum more intersting.

title: VIATOR SELLS TICKETS BUT DO NOT PROVIDE THE SERVICE... VIATOR NOT CARE OF CHILDREN HEALTH NO ASSISTANCE ON SITE...
text:
Réservation Viator BR-728428396 

we bought expensive service to VIATOR (no queue service+tickt+guide) 165 € instead of 24 € when buying directly.

When we arrives, no ticket, no client service, no assistance,.... : result we had to wait about 2 hours without assistance of the agency before taking decision to queue and buy our own ticket with two children !!!!

Result : we could not see other location we were planning too and our child got ill of insulation.

Asking assistance to VIATOR, there anwer after 1 day is we shall re imburse tickets and that's all. Ill child : this is not important for VIATOR. Not being in position to appreciate the trip we did to visit Roma : this is not important for VIATOR !

I recommend not to use as very expensive and in our case, this was not honnest that VIATOR collected money but not provided the service

title: Bucket list
text: When you think of Rome, the first thing that comes to mind for most is the Colosseum. This iconic structure leaves you speechless upon first seeing it in person. Wait times in the lines can be quite long, but it’s more than worth the wait. The amount of history associated with this amphitheater is unfathomable. Hiring a tour guide can be very beneficial during your visit. It can be quite crowded so being part of a group can make it easier navigating throughout the Colosseum. If you were only able to see one thing in Rome, the Colosseum would be it.

title: Always beautiful!
text: We visited with our baby and were afraid of how hard it would be with the stroller, etc but it is really baby friendly: elevators to go up and down and even though it is rocky you can do the visit with it. I would still recommend against taking it if not needed but for example the day we went there was heavy rain so it was of great help.

title: Who wouldn't go here?
text: OK, I have been here twice:  once in February and once in August.  Both visits were awesome, though it was crowded and hot in August.  I think it is better to go as part of a group when you come here, for several reasons.  First, you will automatically have skip-the-line tickets and this will lesson your wait getting into the complex.  You enter via a group entrance and then go through security.  Second, a guide will point out things you would not notice if you walked around on your own.  Finally, you will have someone to ask questions if you have any.  The visit here takes at least two hours but you could spend three or four.

title: An astonish historical landmark that need to be on your list
text:
The Colosseum is one of the main landmark in Rome and a must see.
The location is quite Hugh and allow your imagination to reconstruct the activities during the days on the roman empire.
Pro:
1. A must see landmark with the extension of the museum and the different levels.
2. Overall the price is justified and well spent.

Cons:
1. Too many Tourist "Traps" around the site.
2. Taxis charge way too much for the return trip

Notes:
1. The place closed  very early - align your timetable.
2. Due to the amount of rock and stone it gets really hot in there during mid summer - Make sure you have plenty of water - there is a complementary drinking water tab on location to refill your bottles.

title: Magnificent!
text: The Colosseum, while over 2,000 years old, never gets old!  We look forward to seeing this every time we travel to Rome!  If you have the opportunity to tour it at night, please do so, you won't be disappointed!

title: Great tour!
text: The tour guides are so knowledgeable and speak more than one language, very well.  I was surprised to find out that one of the tour guides was an actual archaeologist. This made the tour that much more valuable to me.  She knew what she was talking about and not just from the tour guide perspective.

title: Magnificent Colosseum without the queues
text:
My top tip is to prebook tickets to skip the long lines and an early entrance ticket as it gets busy inside the later you go - we booked 9.30 am tickets.   

We booked on the official Colosseum website and could only get tickets for the Colosseum, Roman Forum, Palatine Ordinary  Entrance + Audio Guide but we found these to be really great value tickets.

You take your ticket receipt and walk straight up to the entrance where there are security staff checking your receipt - they will not let you through until 15 minutes before your ticket time.  Do not join the long lines on your way to the entrance - go straight to the entrance for those with tickets.  All the staff are extremely helpful so just ask someone if you are not sure. 

When you are let in and go through security - which is pretty quick - ask staff where to go for those with reserved tickets with  audio guide and they will tell you where to meet.  We didn't realise that we had an actual guide and you wore headsets provided so you can hear the tour guide on your way around - fantastic.  Our tour guide was Valentina who was wonderful and informative - the tour around the Colosseum lasted 45 minutes.  The ticket did not include a guide around the Forum and Palantine and we would definitely go for that next time - but as our tickets were only 19.50 euros each it was excellent value.  

You can easily access the Forum and Palantine from within the Colosseum without having to join the long line for that too.

title: Wonderful relic from the times of the roman empire
text:
Visited with a 'skip the line' tour (arguable whether it was worth the extra expense) although it was somewhat problematic after a systems failure meant we had to wait far longer than expected. It was also boiling hot but as you go around the colosseum there are a few opportunities to grab a bit of shade - I would not recommended going in the height of the day as my wife and I visited in early morning and it was sweltering then! The experience was, of course, amazing and you can only image what the arena looked like two millennia ago. - a must do in Rome

Make sure you take lots of water or buy from the plethora of water sellers, who charge €1 or €2 a bottle although it's a lot cheaper in the shops. Also watch for 'free days' when the attraction is free to enter - we complained to the tour company who duly refunded part of the ticket price.

title: Be prepared for a LONG, HOT, walk
text: This IS history. It is an amazing place to be. There is not need to book expensive tours to see it, arrive early, and get tickets at the entrance. The surrounding grounds are still an active archaeology dig, so you may not have pristine views.  Easy to get to via the Metro.

title: Must do when in Rome!
text: There is a reason that this is one of the most popular tourist destinations in the World! Do yourself a favour and make sure you get a guided tour, adds so much value and enjoyment with a great guide!

title: Fascinating site
text:
Lots of places to stop and take pictures lots of information and art 
Amazing to be standing on stones the Romans did so many years ago

title: Amazing
text: It was for sure better that we had imagine. Amazing view from everywhere and if you close your eyes you can travel years ago and imagine how it was

title: A stunning must see
text:
An incredible place to see. The history oozes from every stone. It's incredible to imagine it was there all those centuries ago.
Good footwear needed and take plenty of water with you. Street sellars around with water for a euro but it is frozen. 
Nowhere to purchase any food around so maybe visit after a big breakfast
Had a skip the queue pass which is a must. In the heat you would not want to wait hours to get in.

title: For anyone who didn’t buy ticket in advance
text:
I arrived Colosseum at 12:30pm and it’s extremely hot. I started the queue and expected to wait for around 2 hours. However my parents couldn’t stand the heat so we decided to give up. Then when I passed by the ticket office to go to Palatine Hill, THE LINE IS EXTREMELY SHORT WITH LESS THAN 10 PEOPLE AND YOU DEFINITELY CAN BUY TICKET TO GO INSIDE COLOSSEUM HERE. So I queued for 5 mins and easily bought a ticket to access all Palatine Hill, Roman forum and Colosseum. The ticket officer said that my slot to go in Colosseum is at 3:25pm (2 hours after I purchased the ticket). So now I’m bringing my parents to Palatine hill and Roman forum before coming back to Colosseum.
So anyone who is reading this while queuing for the Colosseum, cross the road and buy ticket in the Palatine Hill ticket office!!!!!!

title: Was not worth the buzz
text:
The Colosseum was a pretty disappointing visit and not worth the queue and all the buzz about it. There were a lot of scaffolding at the time of my visit, probably to facilitate restoration works. There were 2 queues - 1 for bag checks/security and the other for tickets which included entry to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.
I highly DO NOT recommend getting the skip the queue line because the queue was not that long. I was willing to wait in the queue because the price for the skip-the-line tickets was way too high. I noticed a lot of tour guides leading the tour groups which had bought the skip-the-line tickets and the tour leaders made it a point to show their groups that they had skipped the line because they paid higher- prized tickets. I don't see how the shorter wait time can be justified by the exorbitant skip-the-line ticket prices.

Bring water, you will get thirsty, especially in summer. Bring food to eat too in case you get hungry. There is no eating places within the vicinity of the Colloseum. We had to walk really far out of the Colloseum in order to grab lunch and the much- needed water, before continuing on to the Roman Forum. 

There will be people selling ice-cold water and hats there but I say, come prepared.

title: Night time tours
text: We went to the Colosseum at night when it was cooler, it gave the whole tour an ethereal feeling, not to mention the tour guide had the most sensual voice on the planet. Highly recommend it for history buffs, lots of good information.

title: PreBuy ticket online, exchange at the Forum desk
text:
Lots of adverse comments on queues. We arrive 11am and queued for 10 minutes at the desk on the outside edge towards the forum entry. Showed my reservation number and received a ticket. 

We then walked past a huge queue who were alongside the colleseum itself and walked straight in. We bought the tickets only the day before and there was no availability on some options but the video tour guide version had availabilities so do check the different options. It was all of 50cents extra each.

title: Ancient Rome Comes Alive
text: Definitely need a guide to learn about the history and actual use of this monument. Extremely busy, try to skip the queue!

title: Crowded but must do
text: When in Italy, when in Rome colosseum is simply a must. It’s super crowded but Nothing else can be expected when visiting world wonder. Get your tickets online, skip the line and enjoy the History

title: A must see to all!
text: I marvel and ponder how they could have constructed such a magnificent structure as this without machinery!

title: Must See
text: Who doesn't look in aww at this magnificent structure. The stories and history behind it amazing. Crowded, bustling, tourist hot spot.

title: Avoid if at all possible!
text: This place is so overrated! Even in March, it was swamped with tourists that have no idea which way to turn and overrun by every possible "guide" that offer "skip-the-line" tickets. What nonsense! No skipping any lines and no "cheap tickets specially discounted for you"! Watch out for migrants trying to befriend you with a very friendly "English chat-up"...you will pay for it one way or another!

title: When in Rome...
text: Come close to see the history, culture, architecture of the old days. It was too busy so I didn’t queue up and go in as i only have one day in Rome. It is already mind blowing just from the outside. So glad I went!

title: Do it
text: So much history here, we paid for the skip the line tour and it was well worth it on a hot day like today. I could stay for hours.

title: I ❤️Rome slip the line tour
text: Is a must. Learn about Ancient Rome with the most knowledgeable tour guide, Francesca. She was an amazing tour guide. Not for the faint of heart. This tour is outdoors in the sun and heat, but worth every minute

title: History at its best
text: We booked through get your guide, Gladiator entrance, fast pass. We got in right away, our guide Fabio was just that, fabulous and informative with a little humor and a lot of care, ensuring we were hydrated through our 2 hour tour,

title: One Word: Wow
text: I highly recommend a tour to explore this breath-taking work of architecture. The Colosseum is one of the most amazing places I have ever been and if you are in Rome this is a must-visit place to go to.

title: Just Wow
text: From the start, I was amazed by the architecture and how big it was. Going in I was slightly disappointed about how little there was in the Colosseum since the original seats were gone and most of the decoration had too but that changed when I saw the floors underneath the arena where they were doing renovations. There was also a museum that was part of it and there was a lot of information about the place that I enjoyed learning. I suggest getting a tour guide since our guide explained everything so well and made the visit one of the best.

title: My husband’s reactions!
text: I had been several years ago and was returning with my current spouse.  Seeing the look on his face when he entered this fabulous giant piece of history was priceless.  It certainly brings home the raging cruelty of the ancient Romans, but also their ingenuity and decadence.  A true wonder.

title: Glorious
text:
The colosseum is absplutely breathtaking. A phenomenal architectural marvel that is a must see attraction at Rome.
Remmember to buy skip the line tickets and print your ticket beforehnd to save a lot of time. 
With this ticket you could also visit the palntine hills and roman forum

title: Night tour of the Colosseum
text: Used "Walks of Italy" to do a walking night tour, started off at the Teatro de Marcello walked to the Piazza del Campidoglio, then walked along the Via del Fori Imperiali to the Colossuem. Once at the Colosseum we had about a 1 hour night tour of the Upper and Lower areas of the Colosseum. We highly recommend "Walks of Italy" The Night tour of the Colosseum was amazing! Best part was no crowds to deal with and our tour group was maybe 15 people.

title: One moment in time...
text: What can I say, it is simply overwhelming. We bought "jump the queue" tickets online before we came to Rome and that proved to be well worth it. Also, on a hot August day it was not half as full as I expected, which was an added bonus. I loved it.

title: Totally liked it.
text: Tour guide could bring history in front of our eyes. I could imagine gladiators walking and fighting in front of us. Really awesome!!

title: Guided tour with Lumi from The Roman Guy
text: Amazing experience. We were very fortunate to be guided by Lumi. She is fun, experienced and knowledgeable! The tour was amazing!

title: A must for a new visitors to Rome
text: Great tour and historical education. Get a tour. Long line ups otherwise. Go in the morning. Bring water and wear comfy shoes.

title: Breathtaking experience
text: Awe inspiring structure, there is nothing much that can be mentioned about this wonder. One has to be there and see it to really feel and get intrigued. We had booked the faster than skip the line tour and it was really very useful, highly recommended.

title: Engineering feat ahead of its time
text: Highly recommend a visit to this amazing building. Pre-purchase tickets to avoid long queues. Allow 2-3 hrs. We then took a break for lunch before exploring Roman Forum and Palatine Hill

title: Some spot
text: What an impressive site. A visit here is a must, one of the most iconic sites in Europe. Tell them I sent ya.

title: A must visit place in Rome
text: A must visit when you in Rome. We didn’t book a tour, but we got tickets in 10. Minutes. Highly recommended!

title: Long queue
text: Amazing place to visit, but it took us a while to queue up to get tickets. I’d recommend trying to join a private tour of some kind which will get you in straight away.

title: More spectacular than I imagined
text: I visited  the colosseum a few weeks ago and I have to say ‘ it was more impressive than I thought it would be. It is enormous and so interesting. If I went again it would be in April or from late September as it was so hot. It didn’t spoil the experience but it made it really tiring.

title: Size matters but so do crowds
text: Only saw it from the outside as even pre-booked tickets had a line that went almost halfway around the Colosseum. Another get there early experience or book a private tour.

title: Colosseum tour
text: We went on a colosseum tour today with Carrani Tours our guide was a beautiful lady by the name Anna this was without the best guided tour I have been on she took her time explaining the hole suite, waited for some slower members of the group to catch up and gave us her undivided attention throughout the hole 3.5 hours

title: Go with a tour guide from Airbnb
text:
We made the right choice by booking a tour via Airbnb. It was a small group with 6 people. The guide got us in straight away and had organised all tickets. 

Recommend to go in the morning. It’s way to hot and crowded come lunch time.

It’s definitely a must-see sight in Rome.

title: Impressive
text: A must visit place. Both my fourteen years and nine years old children were impressed. The weather was hot though in the last week of July.

title: Historical
text: Visited colosseum today amazing place so much history visually beautiful but you can see the cruelties that would of happened here. A lot of restoration still happening here so a bit of a mess lots of people but we entered with minimal fuss.

title: A must see when in Rome
text: When visiting Rome I would say this is the most important thing to ensure you visit, seeing photos of it is just not enough! When visiting ensure to book a tour ahead of time as the queues are huge and they have a limit on how many people can enter the attraction at once, meaning the queue can take a long time to go down.

title: Tiring
text: As a disabled person I found the walking underfoot  broken and difficult. Nevertheless a remarkable place and would recommend it to anyone with an interest in history.

title: Brilliant
text: Upgraded and Went underground as well . Excellent guide.  good value for money  highly recommended. Best to go in later afternoon evening to avoid crowds

title: Colosseum
text: The cokosseum was very intresting for me. It is a wordwide famuos building. There were lot of tuorists on this place but it was wanderful.

title: Long line and pick pockets
text: Make sure to arrive early enough to avoid the Sun and heat, as well as illegal shoppers -unless you support them. Amazing views of Ancient Rome.

title: Obviously a must visit.
text: A beautiful piece of Roman history. Long queue for tickets. Take one from the ticket counter situated in the back entrance.

title: Very nice
text: Can get some nice views of the colosseum and interesting to enter. Can be lots of queueing (during midday) if no pre-booked. Would recommend.

title: Incredible
text: Would 100% recommend visiting. We arranged a guided tour which was well worth it. Unbelievable history

title: Incredible!!
text: We bought the tickets with the people of the hotel and it was the best ideia to skip the lines. Incredible place, full of awsome history. A super must go in Rome!

title: Visit
text: A lot of walking, but worth seeing, we went with a tour group which helped as you skipped all the line

title: Incredible
text:
I'm going to keep this short since I'm gonna write this for six hours if I'm not gonna hold myself back.

The line was INSANE. We arrived quite early to miss the burning afternoon sun. Well we got it anyway. For security reasons they can only have 3000 people in. So the line moves very slowly, both for ticket holders and ticket buyers. For someone who doesn't appreciate the magnificent feat of ancient Roman architecture, it's just going to be a big rubble. But those who appreciate history, it's an incredible view to behold!

title: Great visit
text: We visited during a short stay in Rome. It is a magnificent building from the outside and we took plenty of photos from outside the arena but inside it is spectacular! I would definitely recommend visiting and paying the fee to go inside. There are plenty of people/companies offering tickets but honestly just go to the official counter - we paid 12 euros each for entrance and that also included entrance to two other sites. There was a ticket at 14 euros which allowed you to go on the colosseum floor but it was sold out so perhaps book online in advance if you want to do that. When you purchase a ticket, you’re given a time for entrance. We arrived back at the colosseum ten minutes prior to our entrance time and was ushered straight through to security. It took around ten minutes to get inside.

title: Amazing History
text:
Absolutely amazing experience.   Arrive early and expect to queue to get through security and tickets.

Once inside wallow in the vastness of one of Rome's amazing sites.

Your entry ticket also gets you access to the Forum area to make a 1/2 day experience

title: Amazing
text:
This place is awesome. The museum area is so informative. Even my young kids enjoyed it. Even though it was busy it didn't really affect enjoyment of the visit. Make sure you buy skip the line tickets as we did. Queues look brutal.
A must visit for any history lover.

title: Book a time slot on the official website
text:
The colosseum is a must see. There are lots of companies trying to sell overpriced skip-the-line tickets. You don’t need any of them. Just go to the official colosseum website and book a time slot on the day of your choice. You won’t pay anything more than what the people in the queue pay. And the queue is long! 
We arrived at the booked time, and we were inside in under 10 minutes, including the security check.

title: Can be more visitor friendly
text: Undoubtedly this iconic ancient roman gladiatorial arena is breathtaking. This is a historian's delight and so is for an architecture lover or sports lover or a photographer. This stands amidst busy roads on 3 sides which makes it a bit crowded and also prone to pollution. As an attraction, there is an audio guide and a tour guide. You can buy a combo ticket for colosseum and palatine hill which is right beside it. If you want to see more of colosseum, then be ready to pay more for special tickets. General ticket only allows you access to 2 levels and for rest another ticket is needed - they can improve this though.

title: When in Rome... got to be done
text: When in Rome... got to be done, we went at 3pm and got in quite quick, audio guide wasn’t great but great experience no the less

title: Super ticket with Ticketbar rip off. Not valid for colosseum.
text: I have bought a SUPER  ticket with a company called ticketbar. Only to pay €18 each plus €4 handling charge to be later told it's not valid for the colosseum. Only for forum and palatine. That ticket cost €6 on the official site which sends out  the tickets. It's really an add on to your normal colosseum ticket. Then I find the 2 places I've bought it to see, are not really valid with the super ticket as you have to go on a guided tour to see the house of Augustus and house of Livia. So the ticket is a rip off. There are only 4 things I can go and see AND  I've been ripped off by ticketbar. What a waste of money!

title: Amazing Underground tour
text: Absolutely loved every minute, main tour guide and a second underground guide too, both were very knowledgeable and one of my favourites in my Italy experience.  Well worth it.

title: Stunning
text: Blown away! Did a guided tour. Very knowledgeable and beat the long queue's. On a hot day remember your sunnies and a hat, a bit of time spent outside. The scale of this place is massive

title: Amazing views and history
text: Must get skip the line tickets ! Otherwise it will be hours wasted in line. I took an afternoon tour and it wasn’t as hot and crowded as I heard was in the morning but judging by the line outside a lot of people waited for a very long time.

title: Amazing place !!
text:
We both the ticket advanced by website and schedule the visit for 17:45, was a good time because didn’t have queue for entrance, wasn’t too hot and inside wasn’t crowded.
The place is amazing, but when we think that more than 10 thousand people died there is very said.

title: Iconic
text: Oval amphitheatre located in central Rome. Major tourist attraction. Museum dedicated to Eros is there

title: Hot but must see if, preferably with a guide
text: Loved every minute, even though it was a 99 F day.   Go with a guide to skip the long, long lines and to learn facts that will fascinate and nauseate at the same time.

title: A fast visit
text: Colosseo is very known from everybody, my visit was fast as already booked other tour but I can say beautiful ancient place full of many persons and if not a group pre-booked prepare for a long line...

title: Tourism exploitation
text:
We couldn’t wait to see the Colosseum and to be honest it’s a must whilst in Rome. However, we really felt that it ended up being a ‘ cattle herding experience ‘. We did the ‘ skip the queue’ as we thought it would reduce the pain of lining up in blistering heat ( almost 40 degrees that day). We were provided with headphone sets that were quite temperamental but dutifully followed our guide who I suppose did his best with the huge number of tourists he had. 
The history of the place is captivating but the experience of the tour was less than pleasant. I certainly felt vulnerable as we were packed in like sardines, if an emergency had occurred - it would have been disastrous. 
For anyone planning to visit- look at your options. There are loads and some are limited to levels that you can see. Also- visit at cooler times of day if possible or out of season. 
I know it was our choice to visit in height of season but surly public safety should be a concern at any time of year.

title: Needing no introduction...
text:
The Colosseum is one of the most legendary things made by man on the face of the earth: we all know this. I’ve been here twice, and even coming out of the Metro station that serves it is an experience in and of itself. 

A couple of recommendations: it’s worth booking an organised tour in advance, (with the essential Queue-Jump to skip the droves of those not organised) one that enables one to visit the Arena floor (arēna in Latin means ‘sand’, to soak up the blood of fallen gladiators; yet another indication of the influence that Rome has had on the world’s lingual development), the underground areas and the Third Level; these can only be accessed using an organised tour. 

If tours aren’t your thing (and they are not mine), one can detach immediately after the restricted areas have been visited and wander independently. 

The sheer scale cannot be overstated, even for those who have seen the Pyramids, Teotihucan or Angkor. 

There is free, clean, cold water available from fountains all over the Colosseum, so no need to purchase water from the opportunistic (albeit understandably-so) gentlemen who hawk their wares outside) and ample places to sit on fractured marble in the shade on hot days. 

The interior of the amphitheatre has extensive 
museum exhibits to enjoy and educate oneself about the Empire in whom’s heart you stand. 

It’s an essential, salient, seminal place to visit for anyone. Stunning. Ancient. Wonderful.

title: Amazing History
text: Loved visiting the Colosseum in Rome. It was well worth my time. We went on a tour with city wonders. Would highly recommend them. So knowledgeable! The only bad thing was the crowds. August might not be the best time to go.

title: Absolutely blown away!
text: Oh my goodness this place is absolutely amazing and a dream come true to be here! Such a hot day but our tour was great!

title: A must see!
text: You can’t come to Rome and not see the colosseum! Easy transport via bus/tram from anywhere in Rome. We got the skip the line pass which you still have to wait in line to collect the tickets but it wasn’t too bad. Seeing inside the colosseum is like a step back in time! In August it’s super hot so bringing water bottles to fill in the taps nearby is a good idea to prevent you from wasting money on numerous drinks if you’re on a budget. The water is safe to drink and ice cold.

title: A tourist must see spot
text: We visited in August and used a company that offered skip the line access. Highly recommend using that perk as it is incredibly busy there.  It is a beautiful site to see, but so incredibly HOT and crowded with people!  There are guys walking area selling ice cold water, but they are not supposed to be selling in there & were getting chased by security.  You should definitely bring in your own water but if you forget, don’t pay more than 1 Euro for a bottle from these guys.  The best location for getting the iconic picture is in the Roman Forum.

title: Pick your time!
text:
Amazing place and absolutely fantastic with Children. They will either love the architectural functionality of the place or the gory stories about what was considered entertainment by the Romans.

Having read lots of horror stories about queues we pre-booked, paying €4 per person to book a time. We booked 5pm, by that time the queue to enter for booked slots was longer than the general queue! 

The earlier queues were huge so my advice, if you want to visit earlier book a slot, if you want to visit towards the end of the day I wouldn’t bother. Also it’s was lovely and cool in there by 5pm

title: Colosseum keeps getting better
text: This was our second visit to the Colosseum and this time we decided to take the Colosseum underground tour.  I would say that the Underground Tour is a must do.  However be cautious on how you book it.  We made the mistake of booking it througt a third party online vendor who billed it as a VIP small tour.  But all they did was take our group around to the back, buy tickets and turn our small group to a larger group that numbered more than twenty-five.  The tour itself was great however we could have booked it ourselves and saved a whole lot of money.  Live and learn.  The Colosseum has upgraded the "Colosseum Experience" with new exhibits and displays.

title: Colosseum
text: Colosseum is a truly amazing place to visit. Not really suitable for young children for the reason of extended walking and many stairs, especially on hot summer months. We rented a guided group tour with Gray Line, and it was amazing not to wait in line to get in. Highly recommended.

title: Huge lines definitely buy a queue skip tour
text: The colosseum was definitely worth a visit it was really interesting and the tour we got with get your guide was brilliant. However if we hadn’t spent the extra money on the skip the line tour it would have been a terrible experience. The lines were all the way down the street.

title: my best place in rome
text:
The gladiator films of old and the Bruce Lee film (Way of the Dragon) made the Colosseum a place I had to visit. Despite its barbaric use in the past, it glorifies the best of Roman architecture and use of materials.
A great place. Queues about an hour in August - no better if you buy the Roma pass (as I did) - you still queue!
Great museum inside.
The vast site makes stadiums like wembley look basic.

title: Pre book to enjoy and avoid loooong queues
text:
Great tour ( underground tunnels and gladiator entrance plus upper levels) Checked in on time by the metro station. Easy and no issues. Allocated guide and off to Colosseo. So time to kill as you have a slot to enter so we waited 15 mins with the guide giving info. You use a special entrance with its own security so there is no queue at all. Info says you need passport etc but no one checked anything however you are asked date of birth and name but no one check id, odd. 

Once inside a special Colosseo guide takes over and the other guide just goes with you. She was an archaeologist and very knowledgeable. You go in via the gladiator gate on to the floor of the arena and into areas behind locked doors that most people don’t get to so you feel you get to see more and no crowds. Tours are timed and separated so you don’t end up part of another group. 

Once outside after about an hour and 15 mins you have time to visit shop and toilets before going to the forum. 

You use the same entrance as everyone but there is a group entrance. Only queued for few mins. 

The forum is huge and we spent much longer than advertised walking around. If you want to drop out or wait back you can as there were some older people who were struggling in the heat. The guide spoke a lot about the site and knew everything in fact she spoke maybe too much!  

You finish off on Palentine hill and then back to the exit. We were on tour for 4 hours 30 mins but loved it all. It was very hot and there is little shade in the forum but there are water fountains to refill bottles. 

There is no restriction on taking water etc into the Colosseo or palentine hill

A great tour worth the money and the queues without a guide were huge and in full sun even at 1000am.

title: Book in Advance
text: It is definitely an amazing place to visit. The 12 Euro ticket gets you to Colosseum, Palatine hill and Roman forum. All are fantastic. You will save a lot of time if you book your visit in advance. Also, be aware of so many scams around the sights. There are lots of people selling China printed paintings, pretending they are their own works.

title: Amazing sight
text: The colosseum lives up to the hype and is well worth the visit. We had 10 and 12 year old boys with us and they loved it. It never seemed too crowded. Only negative was there was construction building a new stage floor, which is too bad, but hopefully it will be ok in the end.

title: very impressive with access to multiple levels
text: So glad we bought our tickets for the colisseum in advance as the queue was about 2-3 hours long.  Very crowded, but very impressive

title: Amazing
text:
Amazing monument.
It's definitely worth visiting, especially since the ticket price also includes visiting the Roman Forum - wonderful! The ticket is valid for 2 days! :)

title: Very busy
text:
We ended up not going inside due to the large queues so walked around the outside- booking before is highly recommended & you can skip the larger queues then.

Walk with purpose or you will be pestered by people trying to sell you things.

title: Colloseum
text: A city with a great history but the coloseum is in reNovation and It was very crowded there ..You need to wait there more than two hours for enter in the building

title: Colosseum at night
text: Would highly recommend the Colosseum at Night tour. Very informative and just the right amount of walking. Rome and the Colosseum at night is truly breathtaking.

title: GREAT HISTORICAL PLACE
text: Definitely jaw dropping experience.  Italy is a friendly country!!! The colosso is stepping back in time💪👍

title: Impressive but worn out
text: The building looks the best from the outside! Not worth paying the entrance.. the inside isn’t worth the money

title: Impressive from the outside, not worth it on the inside!
text:
Beautiful place, steeped in so much history, it was very impressive from the outside and didn't gain from the inside. It must be said they are doing a good job of preserving it, but i'm surprised they are building a metro so close to it!

One other thing - the ticket touts are just annoying!

title: A must see when in Rome: the Colosseum and Forum
text: We took "The Roman Guy" tour of the Colosseum thaty goes underground to show the entrances to the arena floor and lifts for the animals.  It was worth the extra cost to see the underground works.  Our guide, Amanda, was great.  Very knowledgable and admitted that the Colosseum was her favourite building ... you could tell!  She was animated and great with the children on our tour, respectfully answering every question posed to her by adult or child.  Amanda also adapted to the very hot conditions on our day by extending our tour by a bit in the limited shade of the Colosseum and reducing our time in the "Pit of Sun" Forum by a half an hour or so.  Appreciated on a 35 C degree day!

title: Holiday
text: Seeing the colosseum in pictures does not prepare you for the size of it. It’s very busy but that is to be expected worth the visit. A tour is worth doing if you don’t know much about the history and it gets you through the fast lane

title: Colosseum under the moon
text: Fantastic wonder of the world. A must see. I would recommend you go on on a guided tour to fully appreciate the history of this amazing place.

title: Truly Amazing Piece of History
text: The Colosseum is so rich in history, that it is a must see while visiting Rome.  Be sure to do a tour that includes all 3 levels so that you can experience all that it has to offer.  It gets crowded quickly, so I would recommend a tour early in the day.

title: Guided-beat the queue tour
text:
A must see when in Rome. Our guide Eva was awesome very caring, aware of other groups, patient and every so knowledgable on her topic. She brought the tour alive giving us insights into the past.
Was part of a walking tour with the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill from Vivicos International travel.

title: Wonderful
text:
Great visit but literally and absolutely full during summer . Do not recommend . Better should be I’m may or September .
Any locals selling every kind of staff and coming on you just in abuse way.

title: Win the crowd.........
text:
We put ourselves in some form of trance whilst walking towards the awesome arena of blood sports built on the site of a man made lake for Nero.
I wondered if there was a mili-second of a flashback to Leicester square in 1985 seeing two young sun tanned models being walked towards us smiling but also aware of the moment when eyes would meet followed by a sense of awareness. Lake Garda is certainly not man made but a boat that exploded during a thunder storm certainly could have been. 
Gladiators we salute you!

title: Skip the Line
text: Skip the line is a must. You will be harassed nonstop on your way into the colosseum, but once inside, you will be transported to a different time. An hour visit is enough to see it all.

title: Astonishing!
text: I'd definitly go for a more in-depth, more time inside the Colosseum next time.  It's a constant growth in excavations and new information about what really went on there.

title: Unbelievable history.
text:
The colosseum has the most unbelievable history. I recommend getting an audio tour, but be aware these are extra and you need to wait in another line for these. I used Headout tour operators and bought a skip the line pass. The colosseum is small and fits I think about 3000 people at a time. The line to get in was over 2 hours. 

An absolute must if you visit Rome

title: Sunny day at Roman’s Capital
text: First glance at this huge masterpiece of architectural was immense, I saw it when I was traveling to the city center from my hotels near Manzoni station; just can’t believe my eyes , the size was the way more than I expected!  Visiting the site took about 2 hours and it has three floors.

title: Check out 2nd floor museum
text:
Must have advance tickets! The line to buy was hours long, so go on the web to order. So very interesting. Bathrooms gross, but seems common in Italy. Take your own paper and perhaps wipes. 

Meanwhile, the second floor houses a museum with many artifacts and dioramas of the way it was.

title: Private Tour
text:
We had an amazing private tour at 5:30pm after the heat with @imagoartistravel ask for Alessio!
The crowds are too much during the day

title: Worth seeing...
text:
...but only if you are also planning to see the Roman Forum, because 12€ for either of them would be a bit much, but both together is justifiable.
Possibly one of the most imposing structures I have ever seen. Strangely enough, almost more impressive from the outside.

title: World Wonder
text: Must include if visiting Rome. Get a City Walkers Tour (skip-the-line) and try to get Lorenzo as your tour guide. Knows his history & sprinkles it with humour. Great experience 👍

title: Great attraction.
text: A marvel of engineering built so long ago.  We took a guided tour - the only way to do it and highly recommended to do so.  Tour guides know exactly how to navigate you through the Colosseum and of course to provide the history of the place.  A very busy tourist attraction swarming with many people.  I highly recommend a skip the line tour to avoid waiting.

title: Stunning!
text: The colosseum is amazing! I have seen lots of pictures but when you see it in person it’s just amazing, so much history so much to see, it’s truly stunning, we took a tour and it was great I truly recommend going with a tour guide, they explain and tell you all the history of the colosseum. Very interesting and informative!

title: Wowzers
text: It's huge. The story line doesn't change, so if you do a little research ahead of time, you really don't need a tour guide. Use the restrooms ahead of time because the line can get fairly long and the smell can be a little overbearing. Be prepared to walk and climb stairs, although there is an elevator reserved for anyone with a handicap. The view is incredible. The size is massive and although it is being restored at the moment, most of it's entirety is original and you can even see the upper levels that were for lover class citizens. It's absolutely incredible. There is a gift shop in the Colosseum but also vendors outside too. They can be pushy, so watch your wallet but all in all, they sell great gifts and will bargain with you.

title: It’s a must see in Rome
text:
We booked our tickets onlines but we were too late for the underground tour so we had the general admission tickets. We picked the earliest time which was 8:30 and it was perfect. 
It wasn’t too hot or crowded. If youre going , book at least 2 weeks in advance :)

title: Great ancient wonders
text:
There are so many world famous attractions in Rome and Colosseum is always the number one tourist spot to visit in Rome. One thing to keep in mind while visiting Collosseum in summer is to buy skip the line tickets in advance from Coop culture site. When we visited Colosseum, the no skip the line tickets is very long and waiting time can be more than two hours. Since we had bought the ticket in advance, we just went straight to skip line ticket and within 15 minutes we were already inside the Collosseum. 

The Rome Colosseo is the best Roman stadium in the world located in the capital of Roman Empire. While we are inside the coliseum and walking in the podium, we can feel the grandeur of this place. This place is full of history of greatness of Roman empire. No wonder Rome Colosseo is always on permanent member of seven wonders of the world and no 1 attraction in Rome.

title: Colosseum recommendations
text:
As one of the seven modern wonders of the world, of course it's a must see. However, accessing it can be confusing and there are a few things I recommend to ease the visiting experience.

1) Avoid all of the touts trying to sell you tickets. They aren't official. Don't waste your time with them. You'll get ripped off. The only official people are those guarding/standing at the entrance to the queuing area and those working in the sales booth.

2) You must reserve a time-slot. Even with the Roma Pass - which grants free entry - you still MUST go to the sales booth and get tickets for a reserved time-slot. I think this was 2 Euro a ticket.

3) There are numerous types of tickets you can get, each granting various access to the several potential levels to visit, e.g. the highest point of the Colosseum, the gladiator tunnels, etc. I recommend doing extensive research online to determine what exactly you want to see.

4) Avoid the lines and buy your tickets online.

5) Take your time and read all of the signs and other tidbits spread around the Colosseum! Excellent historical background is given.

6) There are toilets inside the Colosseum.

7) Beat the heat and go very early or as late as possible. Additionally, the later you go the less people inside.

Have fun!

title: Not the best organised
text:
It is a must see place if going to Rome but a bit chaotic getting in. My wife had ordered and paid for tickets in the UK but failed to receive any response in terms of e tickets in any form let alone pdf. She had emailed Italy the same day querying the fact. The email went unanswered and so she called Italy and was assured it would be ok. On arrival we had the receipt showing your money had gone and so had to queue up, exactly what we had tried to avoid. Following a back and forth with the ticket office outside it was only the fact we could find the ignored email sent on the same day that got us in. It worthy of mention here, the online booking after jumping through numerous hoops charges you a booking fee and a printing fee, although we could not print. 
Colosseum, well worth a visit but save yourself some money and download “Rick Steve’s” app and download the specificities guide. Invaluable and free.

title: Colosseum
text: For €12 you can check out as much or as little of the colosseum as you like. It is of course one of the most amazing places we have constructed as humans, to walk through a structure that is millennia old is quite something. There are many places to grab refreshments near by just by walking across the street.

title: A Rome Staple
text:
How can you not give 5* 
Don’t go inside not worth either the queue or entrance fee.
For next few years they’re restoring top balconies.
I’d approach from the circus maximus side to see it behind the arch of Titus, and you can slip left up onto the palatine which gives you good views over the forum.
As with most of Rome, research before going to give you the insight and beware pickpockets.

title: When in Rome...
text: The colosseum is an outstanding piece of ancient architecture. We did the night time tour and then came back again the next day to see it in daylight. We didn’t have tickets prebooked when we went back during the day but only queues for 40min, despite ppl trying to sell their “skip the line” tickets to us and telling us we’d be queuing for 90min minimum!

title: Rome pass
text: Very easy to spot on the corner of a busy road, right next to the Roman Forum. I recommend getting the Rome pass so you can skip the lines. Very crowded. Tour groups go out often and are segregated by language.

title: Lots of ancient history
text: Our guide good but it was terribly hot day and he should have directed people who wanted to leave to do so. Never had time in gift shop during tour, told there was one at end but there wasn’t. Only a bookstore.

title: Beautiful fountain
text: A must see in Rome even if it’s crowded! The architecture of the fountain is gorgeous and the water turquoise.

title: Mind-blowing structure - but not enough access all areas
text:
Don't get me wrong - you can't visit Rome as a tourist & not go to the Colosseum. It's a mind-blowing building.
However, considering how long some have to queue to get in the accessible areas are disappointing - essentially half the ground floor & a quick lap of the 1st floor is all you are getting. 

Everything else is seemingly out of bounds or for special guided tours - with the associated uplift in cost. 

It's a shame as we felt it detracted from the splendor.

Get a ticket online which allows you to slip the main ticket queue & slide down the reservation line. We did it whilst standing in front of the Colosseum when the horror of the long line of people waiting in the blazing midday sun to buy a ticket became apparent! Took all of 2 mins, cost 28 Euro, kids u-18 free, the ticket lasts 2 days & gives access to the Colosseum, palatine & forum. Job jobbed.

Do go - you have to on your first visit to Rome!! But if your anything like us you'll be left with a slightly unfulfilled feeling, as if you'd only just got to see the surface, not the really good stuff that lays beneath.

title: Really worth visiting!
text: We’ve had a self guided tour though Colosseum but we were impressed about the state of it, even it was very hot that day we spent 2 hours looking around.

title: Exceptionnal place to visit absolutely in city trip to Roma.
text:
