Exceptionnal place to visit absolutely in city trip to Roma.
Thé site is extraordinary well préserved and you can imagine easily what it was 2000 ago.

title: ITS THE COLLOSEUM!! 💕
text:
What’s not to love? The building is breathtaking... 
when we drove past it going to our accommodation I teared up. 
Wish we had bought skip the queue tickets because the line was almost to the street, no shade and was so hot. 
I was just in awe of the structure. 
Definitely recommend buying tickets in advance.

title: Just amazing
text: Have wanted to visit here since i learnt about the Romans at School years ago and it is so worth the visit .It impresses home on you how cruel yet clever the ancient Romans were. Incredible building and knowing you are walking where Romans sat and watched the games there is something very thought provoking about the whole experience knowing how many humans and animals perished there .Not to be missed if you are in Rome .

title: Long line at the Colosseum
text:
It was hot in a 45 minute wait line.  There were incidents where it appeared two Americans were singled out for confrontation with muslims.  I was one of those people...I have little tolerance for that kind of activity

Inside it went well there were exhibits and well as inscriptions and sculpture ruins on the  second floor.  It was a little confusing because it was so big and parts of it do not exist any longer.  

on the lower level you got a close up view of the infrastructure that went into staging events there.  We had a good time.

title: Go in the evening so you can see it lit up.
text: Ride the hop on hop off bus here so that you can see all the sites on the way. Rome involves a lot of walking so whenever you can get a ride do so. I recommend seeing much of Rome during and after sunset since the sites are beautifully lit up and it’s not so hot.

title: History
text: We worth a visit. Recommend ordering tickets online to avoid long lines to get in. Near the metro so easy to get to.

title: Colosseum
text: Immaculate in the experience and the history behind the making. Highly recommend getting a tour guide and purchasing the extra steps to get the view you see in the picture!

title: Trevi Fountain
text: So many tourists in one spot. Probably best to visit at a less busy time of the year. Please be courteous and let everyone have their turn at photos.

title: Under renovation, but breathtaking from outside
text: Sad so much renovation was going on when we went to visit this beautiful place.. I think seeing it from outside was more breathtaking from outside..

title: Impressive Structure
text:
This is the defining landmark of Rome and deservedly so.  In its own right, the Colosseum is a magnificent structure.  The fact that it was built 2000 years ago and still standing is even more amazing.  We got in with a tour and was glad to have done so, especially given the line we saw when exiting.  Without a tour guide, I don't think we would have gotten as much out of the visit.  I went here without a guide previously 20+ years ago and while there were no lines (I don't even remember paying an entrance fee), I did not get as much out of it as this time around.

This is a must visit sight.

title: Incredible!
text: This was one thing I was really looking forward to when visiting Rome and I am now so glad I have seen it for real. I had booked a tour so had access to the ground level and learnt quite a bit more about the Colosseum that I didn't actually know. The only downside I would say is be careful around this area because I noticed that seemed to be where the pick pocketers would hang around. They were very easy to locate and would try to complement you and then shake your hand to distract you from being pick pocketed so do watch out for this!! Over than that It didn't ruin my experience personally as I was fully aware of them in the first place.

title: Fab couple of hours
text: We booked for an English tour of the Colosseum- £19 instead of the standard £12 entrance but well worth it, the tour guide Lauren was very informative and explained things very well. It is worth going inside rather than looking just at the outside. Ticket also included entrance to  Palentine Hill and The Forum both of which are well worth visiting...need a full day to do all three but ticket lasts 48 hours but be aware that Palentine and The Forum must be done on the same day.

title: Amazing as always!!
text: This was my third trip to the colosseum and it is as amazing as it was the first time. We did have to queue for 3 hours in the boiling heat but men selling water were always near and a water fountain was in the queue! Definitely a place which cannot be missed is when visiting Rome. I would consider buying skip the line tickets in peak season.

title: Fantastic thing to see
text: The Colosseum is so well worth the visit. The structure is absolutely amazing and you totally understand the values it holds once you are inside. Would recommend the skip the line tour as they only allow 3000 people in at a time and standing in a queue in hot temperatures is not the best idea

title: Wonderful old ruin
text: The Colosseum is a wonderful place to visit, you can really imagine what it was like when first built. We queued to get our tickets for the ticket desk inside the Colosseum it wasnt to long to wait, got a bit harrassed with people trying to sell us tickets outside but just said no, the only problem was when we were coming back we hadn,t purchased a return ticket on the metro and the queue was really long, longer than getting a ticket for the Colosseum.   You can spend a few hours here it is so interesting.

title: Colosseum
text:
Excellent experience. Personal preference, but I would not recommend a guided tour. Pay for the audio headset and go at your own pace and see what you want to see, not what the tour guide wants to show you. Amazing history on display.

I also found a ticket booth a quarter mile away from the Colosseum that gave me a dedicated time to arrive, so I was able to skip all the lines and just show up at my designated time (which was 45 minutes from the time I purchased my ticket). No need to pay more at the "skip the line" vendors, just buy a ticket at one of the area ticket counters, away from the actual Colosseum, where there are no lines, then head to the site at your designated time, no waiting.

title: great experience when booking online
text: Once you book online, choose your schedule and plan on a solo tour if you like, is easy and you can do it standing on your head. It is a horrible place due to the suffering and what took place, but shows how much can happen when the wrong person gets power. Is history but it was awful and sad.

title: Culture through Toursim
text: Having the chance to see this superb architecture is just like immersing myself in the culture and living in the roman empire.

title: Must see!
text: Must see attraction for anyone in Rome! Prebook tickets before you get there because the queues are huge and you don’t wanna be standing too long in the sun!

title: Lifetime experience
text: The history books in school don't do the coliseum justice in person. We did the tour with a guide via the headout website (Coliseum, Roman Forum & Palatine) and the guide was impressively knowledgable with all aspects of the tour.

title: Do a tour
text: Amazing place but not much signage and you could go around in circles if you don’t know where you’re going or get lost in the crowds

title: We only queued 10 min. Here's how.
text:
Firstly buy your ticket online from the Coop website. This is the official website. We paid €19 each for the adults and €2 for the kids. You chose a time. We chose 11am. That gave us a 45 min tour from an archaeologist at 11am and as much time as we wanted just wandering around inside. It also includes entry to the Palatine and the forum.

If you don't have kids you simply take your ticket (either printed or on your phone) to the front of the entrance queue approx 30 mins before the entrance time. The massive queue is for people who have either just bought non guided tickets or haven't bought a ticket at all yet.

With the tour ticket you get your own line for both entry and security screening. We literally walked in. The other line was at least 50 metres long. Plus the capacity is 3000 people but because you have a tour to get to they give you prority.

Now i said we queued for 10 mins. That is because if you buy kids tickets at €2 each you have to physically collect them to prove they are kids...fair enough. We got there at 10am. To get to the ticket collection point just go to the entrance to the coloseum and then turn around 180 degrees. You'll see a small row of ticket offices and a bit of a queue.  It moves fast as everyone is simply collecting tickets. We waited just 10 min.

I did a ton of research and very nearly bought expensive tour tickets with private companies. So glad i didn't as our guide was great, we all had earphones so no shouting.

Have fun.

title: Pretty Amazing
text: Pretty amazing place with incredible history.  Get a ticket prior to arrival to avoid a long line that never ends.  Do a tour or self tour if you like.  I think with a tour you might get more inside info rather then read a lot of signs   Take water, and they do have refill stations

title: Womderful
text: Just wonderful . One of the most amazing places I have ever seen . It’s beautiful and you must see it . It’s a much better experience than the Vatican . So wonderful . You must go .

title: Must see!
text: This of course is a must. I actually think it looks more stunning from the outside and especially at night. Please make sure you get a 'jump the queue' ticket, we bought ours before leaving home. So glad we did as no way would I have stood in the queues especially in the August heat! There is a lot of renovation going on but I suppose there is always going to be. If in Rome this has to be visited!

title: Bucket list
text: You've  just got to experience this along with everything else Rome has to offer. A fantastic experience, even if you only do it once.

title: Pretty Great, But Crowded!
text: So many people outside and inside; not to mention the hecklers outside who are unbearably forceful. Get the skip line tours in order to enjoy this experience! The coliseum itself is beautiful (moreso on the outside). Of course it is a ruin so the inside is worn down but it’s a cool experience nonetheless. It’s difficult to get a decent picture though. The lore and history of the coliseum is what makes this attraction great so read up before coming here!

title: Ancient Rome tour
text: Excellent guided tour of the Colosseum, Forum & Palatine Hill. Our guide gave us invaluable insights into the history of each area & what events took place in ancient Rome. The tour was easily walkable even in the hot Roman summer. Much recommended.

title: Crowded!
text:
The Colosseum is a historic and beautiful building in Rome, however it is unfortunately ruined by the number of tourists who swarm the premises, all ejecting their selfie-sticks into the air! The queue to get in is also unbearable, as people were waiting 2-3 hours to enter. Luckily, we booked a tour as part of Tiquets, which allowed us to jump the queue and gave us priority upon entry. If you are visiting the Colosseum, ensure you pay extra as part of a tour, otherwise you'll have no option other than standing queuing! (The tour itself is not regimented in nature, which is good).

The views from the Colosseum are very nice and this attraction is definitely worth visiting. However, the sheer volume of visitors there makes enjoying the overall experience somewhat difficult.

Finally, I would like to remark that the gent's toilets were very unappealing and needed a good clean to get rid of the disgusting smell wafting out the door.

title: A great look into rome’s history
text: While it’s a great place to visit, it is a shame that so much of its splender has been lost. I recommend taking a guide to cut lines and to hear more about its amazing history.

title: Mind Boggling
text:
Although we've watched several shows on TV about the Colosseum and it's history and I've also read several books about it, nothing can really prepare you for experiencing it in person. 

It's upsetting to know that it was used for bloodthirsty sports and Christian prosecution but it remains an architectural marvel even after many centuries.

title: Stunning
text: OMG a bucket list experience ticked off.  It was so good having the prepaid ticket.  Went to the evening slot and just strolled in no waiting in the longs lines.  Pre-booking from home is a must.  The place was nearly empty as we chose the evening experience. We did it with no tour guide and since there are pillars all over the place explaining the different areas we definitely saved a few pennies. The evening time slot for us was the best as it was 38 degrees and there were no crowds.  Try and go midweek it seems the most quietest time.

title: Wow
text: Great experience well worth a visit, we didnt book tickets in advance so waited in q but tbh it was quick cost €12 each but that allowed u next day entry to visit ruins great value...

title: Coliseum -
text: It was a ruin more than a structure which was disappointing. Since we have been so fascinated my it because of all the movies that we have seen, the expectations were much more than what the real thing had to offer. Plus the heat made it all the more difficult to enjoy!

title: Amazing
text: Amazing place, but I will recommend to travel in September, less hot & humidity, building with 1700 years old 🤗

title: Private tour guide
text: I would highly recommend a private tour guide for the Colosseum. it is well worth the money for the amount of history they provide.

title: Great experience
text: This was everything we expected! Ensure to buy tickets in advance to avoid baking in the heat though!! Otherwise, great value and an informative venue.

title: Buy your tickets online
text: This place is a live history, we showed the girls part of the film the gladiator(avoiding the violent parts) , and suddenly they were very impressed and wanted more details.

title: Visit at night!
text: Have been to Colosseum several times, including underground, but this was first night-time visit. Loved it! So much cooler, so much quieter.

title: Ticket touts swarm in and sell you everything
text:
This was our first thing to visit in day one, it’s boiling as height of summer. It’s busy. I was not happy about being approached about 20 times with my kids, the men selling everything, kept high fiving my son and then not letting go of his hand. The scam is they give you a bracelet and say it’s free because they love your county, then he places one on my daughters hand and then asks for money to feed his kids....it’s awful. Where are the security measures? You see police with machine guns stood about, but this is like a swarm of bees, it’s just really overwhelming and intrusive. Although I was safe with my husband who is not about to allow any nonsense it’s just not good. All the way down the steps they are there and get used to it they are all the landmarks and sights. 
The queue was massive it’s hot and there no shade at all, you think there maybe enough money from the billions of people to place some shade/parasols/canopy’s about. 
We headed to the Palestine Hill entrance and for our ticket there for the sights- TIP this was worth the walk as then you walk back to colosseum and can walk right in, no queues! 
So the place was amazing, and to think what happened there. We got the audio guides provided by them (didn’t bother with guided tours) but these had no map, no structure, no signs about which way to walk. We found all the sights poorly signposted, and in user friendly. Having said that was good for the kids to hear a bit more history. The big group guided tours take up all the room everywhere. They stop in all the shaded parts. Half the people looked hot and bored. I think we got away with a bit of a grind there! 
I think in all amazing to see, considering the age and history there is there I found it all a bit taken for granted? The road surrounding are terrible and not kept nice. The dodgy sellers and pick pockets are not a nice attraction- get rid of them. We saw undercover police corner a seller in a car and seize his phone and bag, but this was a car in the pedestrian area where there were hundreds and hundreds of people walking. I just think if they could make the perimeter larger and somehow stop this so close to the attraction it would be a better experience. 
Again staff very unhelpful and rude much like at all the attractions. What a shame. 
Would have been nice to see some ‘Romans’ and ‘gladiators’ even if they stayed in the shade or walked about where the massive queues were. Even a simulation of noises at the games and lions roaring at times. 
Maximus would be disappointed he died in vein and this is what his history is about!

title: Uhhhh duhhhh it’s the Colosseum!
text: Take a private tour by a qualified, educated, legitimate guide not one of the guys handing out flyers. And don’t cheap out get the skip the line guide. You do NOT want to line up with the masses in that heat.

title: Night time visit is a great way to see it!
text: Long lines under the sun not your thing? Wait until nightfall for a dramatic and leas crowded visit to this most famous of landmarks in Rome. Wether emerging from the metro or one of the many roads leading to it, the colosseum, never fails to impress. I thought the beauty of this monument was best appreciated at night, when it is skillfully lit to highlight its arches and height. The crowds are thinner at night and if inclined to do so, one can book a night tour as well. I was happy to walk around its perimeter and admire architectural beauty and catch a glimpse of the monuments around it.

title: Tourist visit
text: Great experience, so historical I did the audio tour. I brought my tickets in advance so I didn’t queue

title: Impressive ruin well presented
text: The Roman Forum ticket includes the Coliseum, so although we'd been a few years before we decided to go again. They have improved the displays to make it easier to understand what you are looking at, and the structure is in any case very impressive. Well worth a visit.

title: Amazing - Must Do !!
text: Say no more - if only the walls could talk ! Not sure about express lines, all companies offer it! Tour guide is critical and 90mins should be max.

title: See it twice, because when in Rome
text:
We did a tour with a company (see my reviews for the tour!) And we ended up also coming back later that night to see the Colosseum at night (since we weren't too far away). The difference of night and day views is incredible. 

I would HIGHLY suggest booking a tour to go and explore the Colosseum, as well as going back at night or another time just to admire it one last time.

Photos are from both times visiting (same day)

title: Colosium
text: colosium is amazing but i'm so sad because i didn't get in because buying a ticket is too long and the colosium entry is too long and i can't wait for that but take a picture outside of colosium is cool too

title: I am in love with this place
text: I would recommend you to visit this place exact time of sunset , if you are photographer you will get all perfect shots

title: A must see
text: We got to the Coliseum about 9.30am and already the ticket queue was snaking around the exterior of the Coliseum, it was estimated about an hour’s wait. We simply walked further along to the Forum entrance, where there were only three in front of us and got tickets for €12 which gave us entry to both The Forum and The Coliseum. We immediately walked back (about 5 mins) and went straight into the Coliseum, no queuing. The Coliseum itself is amazing and quite honestly it’s splendour is difficult to put in words. Simply, when in Rome it’s a must see.

title: Overhyped
text: Colosseum is known worldwide, but when you go inside you can see nothing special. Better view is from Forum Romanum.

title: Great
text: Impressive archeological site with good explanations inside. In the afternoon we did not wait at all in ques for entering and we really enjoyed it.

title: Awe inspiring
text: An awe inspiring structure which is well worth the visit BUT crowds of other visitors tend to take the gloss off it a little. Our guide with Monograms gave a very full description of all aspects of it. The usual throng of selfie takers in front of awesome views were the worst part of the visit!

title: Amazing
text:
Been a dream of mine for a long time to see the different ruins in Rome, the colosseum didn’t disappoint at all! Me and my partner booked online which helped us to skip most of the queues so I definitely recommend booking ahead. Once inside it was really busy but we took our time and really enjoyed seeing this amazing structure from the inside! 

Before you go in be really careful of people trying to sell you various things, umbrellas, cold water, tickets for the colosseum and hats. There are water fountains all over Rome and inside the colosseum so don’t fall for the iced water being sold everywhere. Take a water bottle! (Especially in summer months)

title: When in Rome
text:
As the saying goes when in Rome … The Colosseum is a must and a sight to marvel, the spectacular structure with its history and stories surrounding it. After this tour our group headed towards the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum, a picturesque stroll whilst listening to stories from our knowledgeable guide. 
Pre-booking for fast track entry is recommended to avoid the long queues a must see tour.

title: Wonderful look at history
text:
We pre-booked tickets and were extremely glad we did as the queues were very large. We got an audio tour (note they only accept card as payment for these). 
A wonderful look into history and architecturally beautiful. A must visit when in Rome.

title: Exterior better, inside not that much
text: The truth is that the general view of the colosseum is much better than stone walls from the inside... But the exhibitions (also inside) concerning the history of the colloseum are informative and presented in an interesting way so it is totally worth seeing! One thing to remember: while buying the ticket, pay attention to the entry time, you'll get a precise hour when you can visit the colosseum.

title: Maffia
text: Normally the visit is free, as mentioned on their website the first sunday of every month. So I got in line today, Sunday, September 1st. Waited for a while and then they decided it was not free today... So they do whatever they want. It’s just a scam and I didn’t pay the ticket off course. Stay away from it.

title: Spectacular
text:
This historical site was quite a beauty. We were lucky to reserve tickets as there were very long lines for non reserved tickets.

Would recommend allocating an hour and half here. There are steep steps to consider if you are travelling with older people.

The best photo point are on the very top floor of the colleseum.

title: A visit to the Colosseum
text: We booked our visit through Viator and we're glad we did, skip the line entry and great commentary throughout. As part of the tour we also went to the Romun Forum and Palatine Hill. It gets very crowded so keep on eye on your belongings, wear comfortable shoes and take a water bottle and hat. Also helpful to pack tissues and wet ones for visits to the toilet.

title: Beyond compare
text: Really enjoyed the Colosseum.  Highly recommend.  Absolutely amazing!  Would definitely come back again.

title: European Vacation.
text: Staying at the Hotel Forum, the colosseum was a short stroll up the road and very impressive. Unforgettable in fact! make sure you buy a ticket to beat the queue if you wish to view internally. Helps to get there early too. Some nice restaurants down the road after your visit which serve nice cold drinks/wine and great pizzas.

title: A great tour!
text: Giorgio was a fantastic guide, very engaging and entertaining. It was a very hot day and after 3.5 hours his passion and knowledge didn’t falter. Would def recommend. Thanks Giorgio

title: Great tour
text: Had a great day here. Got a audio guide which only cost €5. Spent about an hour here and learnt a lot. We got skip the line tickets which was worth it.

title: Go at night to avoid aggravation
text: I went to the Coliseum our first night in Rome and was amazed how beautiful if was. Of course it's closed so you can only see the outside at night, but it's wonderful all lit up. We went back another day so we could get inside. We got tickets and had to wait on line for an hour in the blazing heat with the throngs of tourists, only to get in and realize it's much more impressive on the outside. I wish I had gotten the tour tickets where you skip the lines and learn something. Maybe that would have made the inside more interesting. The way we did it, I regret spending the money to go in and just see broken stone everywhere. I found it much more impressing (and free) viewing it from the outside, both in the day and night. Watch out for aggressive peddlers and pickpockets. Four stars just because it is the Coliseum and it's amazing, not five because getting inside was a nightmare where we lost a couple of hours of our precious time in Rome.

title: Always worth a visit
text:
Still a brilliant experience. Been there twice now and loved it again. 

When in Rome, you gotta see it.

title: No matter what if in Rome you must see the Coliseum
text: The Coliseum is a must see when in Rome. We were waiting on a line for over 2 hours in the sun and heat. Still we felt the experience was worth it.  Skip the line people still had to wait though for a shorter time.  It is a wonder of the Ancient World.  Loved it!

title: Amazing and Must-see Tour
text:
These places hold so much history, that (in my opinion) you would be a fool not to do a guided tour. You will miss out on so much.

I had booked a guided tour from home, via GetYourGuide. The tour called Colosseum: Underground Skip-The-Line Group Tour. The tour involved the colosseum incl. underground, and a restricted area, the Palatine Hill and Forum Romanum. 

It was a small group of 7 people. Really nice size. Our guide came from Roma Experience Tours. The group was assembled at the Metro station next to the Colosseum, and from there we walked to the colosseum, and through Security. Inside we met up with another small group and our colosseum guide. Please note that the colosseum has their own guides.

I did a few tours during my stay, and the common thing was, that tours in English, are Italian guides speaking English. So, you have to expect an accent, and the colosseum guide had a lot of that, making her somewhat difficult to understand. But she was very knowledgeable. You could tell, that she was really passionate about it, but you just had to listen carefully and pay attention to her.

We started underground. There wasn’t that much to see there, but the guide had a lot of stories. From here we went up, and walked at the top of the spectator seats. From here, the tour also offered a private walk to the very top of the colosseum. A restricted area, that required access. So, there weren’t anyone else up there, and you had the most amazing view over palatine Hill and the City. A perfect picture spot!

After the colosseum we parted, and our small group went towards the palatine Hill and forum romanum. A beautiful walk, where our guide had so many stories. She was really sweet, and a good and knowledgeable guide. Easy to understand. I really loved this tour, and if you are in to history, stories, ruins and beautiful nature, then this is a must-see tour!

title: Great piece of history
text:
Visited the Colliseum on a guided tour. Really great place and lots of info on various levels.
Did the arena floor access which wasn't really worth it to be honest as you didn't see much more.
Really impressive and glad I went!

title: Well worth the entry
text:
Buy your tickets before to avoid the lines. 
This is such good value, you get two venues, we spent a couple of hours there and had lunch and continued to the next which is equally as impressive, and very large, so much so we didn’t manage to see all of it because they were shutting up. 
Well worth it!!

title: Colosseum
text: Would recommend pre-booking and booking the fast track entry. This avoid the queues for the bag search/x-ray and entrance tickets. Very interesting visit and a 'must see' while in Rome

title: Tour
text:
Took a tour and it was great as they were able to answer questions as well as going through the history. Went to the stage area first then to the what would be have been the seating areas. The tunnels are being fixed and will be a great addition to walk were the gladiators walked. 
They had toilet facilities and water fountains to refill your bottles.

title: Interesting landmark, service not too good!
text: The colloseum is quite impressive and doesn’t really need further explanation. The service though, not too good. It was a very hot summer day and there was only one toilet building to be found. That’s not nearly enough for the amount of people visiting the colloseum during summer. Also there’s only two water fountains at the colloseum to fill your water bottles. Service could be a lot better, cherish your tourists a little bit more!

title: A must see in Rome
text: We had a great private tour here! Of course it’s a must see while in Rome but the private tour is definitely the way to go. Such a amazing piece of history I felt you learn so much more having a tour guide to ourselves vs a big group with head phones. Do yourself a favor and splurge for the private tours while in Rome absolutely worth the money.

title: Built in 8 years!
text: The size of amphitheater is just wow. Most of it gone now. Marbel for S.Peter Basilica, limestone washed away... but you can still imagine death and life of gladiators and animals... also Holywood has it all wrong!

title: Bus site
text:
My wife was clear that seeing the outside of the Colosseum was enough as it was an old ruin.
She isn't wrong but it's amazing to think of the history behind it.
There are lots of street sellers who are very pushy. We found these the most pushy out of the whole of Rome.
The queues looked worse than the Vaticans which is saying something.
Would recommend.

title: Wow
text: The photos of this place don’t do it justice! Even from the outside its amazing although i do recommend a guided tour, we got to see inside with no queues and had a knowledgeable person to tell us what we were looking at. It’s such a shame its fallen into such a state, it would’ve been even more impressive in its day.

title: Wonder of Wonder
text:
Such rich history. Go for the conducted tour but be sure to select inclusive of the Pallatine Hills only if you are up to it. A 3 hour walking jaunt in the hot can be quite energy sapping. We even watch the Gladiator show that night and it was worthwhile IMHO. 5 days 4 nights in Rome is just nice. Booked the 3 days ticket for 18 euros. Can practically go everywhere with it by bus, trams, metro. Taxi can be hailed by the side of the road and within the perimeter of the city are affordable but hard to book as they do not speak english 
Rome is a city of tourist as evertwhere there is history. If there is one place ti visit, it is the Colosseum.

title: Impressive and requisite visit in Rome
text: The Colosseum is one of those destinations that you really should visit while in Rome, especially if you're interested in history.  It's stunning and overwhelming and gorgeous, and well worth your time.  I would recommend getting a tour directly from the ticket office instead of one of those annoying private tour guides, as the Colosseum's tour guides will know all the nooks and crannies.  Beware of hawkers trying to sell you random things outside the Colosseum, they can be really pushy.

title: Go with a tour guide - lots of background info!
text: It lives up to the hype, truly impressive history. Middle of June with heat and crowds might not be the best time of year. Its also unfortunate that so much of it seems to be in "renovation mode." There are objects of 21st century construction everywhere so its hard to just soak in the grandeur of what's "real."  Wouldn't have missed it though.

title: Colosseum
text: I would like to thank the wonderful people at MEA tours Rome. Specifically ALEX, his manager (sorry I don’t know his name) and last but definitely not least our Tour guide ERTURK! He managed to make the Colosseum even more spectacular then it already is on its own! ERTURK was pleasant and extremely knowledgeable and managed to keep everyone engaged throughout the tour despite the heat!! That’s not easy to do and he did effortlessly. If you plan on taking this tour when your in Rome, please look them up! I promise you won’t be disappointed :)  VWright Chicago, IL

title: Book in advance!
text: Book in advance. We used tiqets for skip the queue tickets. They messed up our times but we were able to go later on in the day. Luckily our hotel was only 5 mins away! An enjoyable visit.

title: Must see - hot day but no regrets
text:
My wife and I took a tour of the Colosseum during our stay in Rome. We were in a small group of 6 people which helps to follow and enjoy the tour.

The tour start point was easy to find and started on time. The guide was wise and made the whole experience very pleasant. 

I would recommend this to any person planning to see the Colosseum and ancient Rome.

title: Spændende at opleve historien
text:
Nice place to see, if you want to here more about the story, go with a guided tour. Long queue without 'skip the line tickets'
There is a couple of water tabs with drinking water, inside to fill up a bottle. Which is a good thing in the heat.
Outside there is a lot of foreigners, trying to sell a lot of things, like water, hats, umbrellas etc. Some of them are very provoking and aggressive. This was really unpleasant (these are outside every big attraktion)

title: Colosseum
text: I went many years ago without a guide. This time I went with a guide and it was well worth it. A knowledgeable guide really helped me to understand the whole reason behind the colosseum. Wonderful structure to see and understand.

title: NO LONGER FREE 1st SUNDAYS OF THE MONTH
text:
Queued for an hour this morning having read on “il-colosseum.it” that museums in Rome, including the colosseum/forum, are free on the first Sunday of every month. 

Got to the ticket office and asked to pay €12pp. Apparently the above website is not the official website. MIBAC is the official one but good luck navigating that. 

According to staff the free visits ended in January (yet reviews on here in March suggest free sundays?!).

Now there are just selected dates that are offered free which celebrate national holidays etc.

title: Absolutely breaktaking
text: Definite must see! Even better than pictures, absolutely incredible. Our kids ages 3 and 5 loved it too along with the stories about the gladiators

title: Remarkable
text: We saw the Colosseum as part of a tour and I highly recommend taking a tour. You get to go in a back entrance and have access to points regular visitors do not. There are water stations throughout as well as various spots with busts of Roman emperors, original art, and lookout points. It’s beautiful from the outside but the inside is an amazing work of architecture. There is scaffolding set up on the floor of the Colosseum but still worth the tour.

title: Duh, a must see!
text: How can one go to Rome for the first time and not visit the Colosseum?  Yes, Rome in August is hot.  Yes, the crowds are thick. But everyone is in it together and if you are patient and not rushed, you can really enjoy your time.  We took great photos and took moments to sit in the shade and we enjoyed the displays on the 2nd tier.  We did not book a guided tour nor did we book our entrance time online.  We followed Rick Steves' advice and purchased our tickets at the Palantine Hill entrance (zero line), got our entrance time to the Colosseum for the same day (you have to visit the Colosseum before the Forum and Palantine Hill).  Bring water bottles to fill (as is true everywhere in Rome there are deliciously cold water fountains in the Colosseum--never buy water in Rome!).  We spent 2 hours here.

title: Iconic place!
text: We took a guided tour and it was great decision.    Highly recommend! You should be their to experience the history and dive into the atmosphere of ancient times!

title: Needs more drinking water points.
text: Absolutely amazing structure and the audio guide is a must. Excellent value and a must see in Rome. You could literally spend all day here but with very few places to buy COLD water and a lack of drinking water fountains you do leave here parched.

title: very pretty
text: definetly a place to go and visit however was too hot and the ques were enormous so we didn't go inside worth a look from the outside though got some lovely photos

title: Fantastically beautiful Roman oval amphitheatre
text: The World’s most famous ancient Roman amphitheatre, with a capacity to hold up to 50,000 attendees. It was built using travertine limestone. We used a free online audio guide. Buy your tickets in advance. Queue was long.

title: What an amazing place!
text:
This is an amazing building with a very interesting history. We loved the tour and how easy it is to imagine how it would have been in the days when it was being used. 
A very popular attraction and I would seriously recommend booking a skip the line walking tour to avoid the long queues in the heat of the day

title: Fabulous tour
text: We had a private tour to this site.    Very nice.  Lots of walking but great history. Gotta see this if in Rome

title: Ancient Rome
text: Thoroughly recommend visiting the Colosseum  whilst visiting Rome. We read up before we travelled that booking in advance was recommended. I totally agree with that statement! The Colosseum can only hold a maximum of 3000 people at any one time and during our visit the gates were closed twice! We arrived 10 minutes before our booked slot of 10am and the queues for the "book on the day" were already 40 minutes long! Also checkout Palentine Hill and the Roman forum which was part of the Colosseum ticket

title: Tourist visit
text:
Great visit long tour but impressive building 
Lot of walking bring good shoes
We had hot weather about 90
Very humid

title: Sight seeing
text: Visited here yesterday it was very busy as first Sunday in the month museums are free be prepared to wait we booked in advance on line b4 hand

title: Must go when in Rome
text: Book tickets before you go or you'll queue for ages! A must see whilst in Rome. Very busy when we visited end of July

title: Who visits Rome without seeing the Colosseum?
text: It was August, tourists from every corner of the earth came. The sun was blazing but that only allowed for better lighting for my pics. We got our tickets online from a third party and it costed more than getting it from the colosseo web site. We didn't have to wait in line more than 10-15 mins though. This ticket did not include dungeon. I actually thought it was a taller structure. When you get there you may think the same as I. Many persons were running from the sun and its heat, huddling up in shaded corners. Be sure to wear layers if you want added protection from the sun. There are water fountains at the colosseo, and if you rather, there are guys outside who sell frozen bottles of water. Trust me you will need it haha. We had a great Great self guided tour. Ooh Italy so much to see, so little time...

title: Do not visit without a skip the line ticket
text:
A must see in Rome, but be sure to have a pre-booked, skip the line ticket. We breezed right through the admission gate. Those without tickets were stuck on a queue that looked to be at least a 3 hour wait.

We used Get Your Guide and couldn’t be happier!

title: Thomas our guide from Livitaly Tours
text:
We had a fantastic afternoon touring the Coliseum and the Roman Forum
It was educational without being boring 
We stopped occasionally in shaded areas to catch our breath and get cold water We definitely learned a lot and the 3 hours flew by
Can’t say enough how good this tour was
The only rough part was at the beginning when we couldn’t find our group
The directions on the paper were not specific enough
They did keep calling us on the phone to find us which was nice

title: Colosseum
text: We arrived in the afternoon and walked all around the Colosseum.  While we did not do an “official” tour I would recommend at least spending the time to walk around this amazing historical monument.

title: It's the Skydome
text: Or any number of contemporary football fields in any large city of the globe. It's a ruin in a state of semi-perpetual repair. If you arrive at early or later hours just line up for tickets. It takes a couple hours to tour maximum. We did an exterior "skip the line" type tour and ended up walking 2 blocks away just to pay and start the tour. Waste of time! Did I mention 150 Euros for 3 adults and 2 (free entry age) kids. 100 Euros too much for this stadium and no game was playing the day we were here. It's warn-out Roman concrete. Nothing spectacular what so ever. Palintine area is way more significant but it has been looted by the Vatican. Coluseum is a tourist trap oven. About 30th on my list of top sites - of which about 15 others are in Rome.

title: Ancient ruins
text:
Although the Colosseum is one of the most visited monuments in the world, it is really not worth the hype (according to me). Mostly ruins - lot of preservation and restoration work underway. Hence, it looks work in progress. We did an exterior "skip the line" type tour that saved us some of the queuing time. 
Note - we visited in June and it was hot - really really hot. Thankfully we were carrying hats and umbrellas.

title: Amazing
text: We took a private tour to see the underground, arena floor and top levels. Thisbis one amazing place and can not be missed.

title: Worth a visit
text: Only 12 Euros per person to get in. We only qued for around half hour although option to buy skip the line prior to visit. Worth seeing for history aspect. Be careful of people around the colloseum trying to sell you items. Some try to distract you and then pick your pocket so be on guard.

title: Breathtaking, guided tour recommended
text: We pre booked the English guided tour which enabled us to skip the line which was quite long.  Fors for the tour were you got to know the history of the Coliseum and hear stories, against's would be that you don't get any free time to wonder about and explore which I would have liked. Book direct for the cheapest option

title: Worth to visit in early morning.
text: The Colosseum was one of the main reason why i went to visit Rome. I was little bit disappointed about little trash around and not enough rubbish but anyway its a wonderful and impressive place. Highly recommend you to wake up earlier and come around 6-7am so you can enjoy the Colosseum for yourself !

title: Roma - Colosseum
text: The line wasn’t too long, but very hot in August.  A lot of ticket scalpers...just say no!  I recommend going either really early or a different month, maybe late September.  Avoid October, since we’re told a lot of tourist come to see the Pope in October. Bring water for everyone in your group and eat before you go. To see this with your own eyes is remarkable. If you have bad knees, be careful...the steps are very steep!

title: Must See In Rome
text: Simply an amazing place to be able to visit.  You can really get a feel of what the Colosseum was like 2,000 years ago.  I highly recommend you "skip-the-line" and take a tour so you can see both the above the group perspective and below the ground perspective.  We took our 3 and 5 year old and had a great time.

title: Roman empire
text: this is impressive and an iconic place to visit.  The do it yourself walk around is probably enough -just to soak in atmosphere and imagine the gladiatorial escapades.  Late in the day  seemed best to avoid some of the busyness

title: Great tour guide
text:
We have just been on the “skip the line colosseum and Roman forum tour with Walk Talks tours. 
The whole booking process with the company was great, even when we made a mistake with the dates they kindly changed it for us. 
The meet and greet on the day was seamless too. 
Our tour guide, Maddalaina was very informative in a great way that us engaged during the whole tour. 
The tour starts at the  gladiators entrance and straight on to the arena floor before moving up the colosseum. 
Maddalaina ensured our well-being throughout the tour giving time for comfort breaks and keeping hydrated. 
Her knowledge was exceptional and made a huge difference to visiting such a place on our own. 
Brilliant day, thank you!

title: Amazing
text: Just incredible to see a stadium this old still standing.  Just a site that has to be seen.  One quick item for the people of Rome - please clean up the garbage around this truly astounding site.  It looks like there is trash still on the ground from the last gladiator battles.

title: Colosseum
text: Fantastic visit. Accessible for disabled visitors.  Loads of information and audio guide good vfm. The 2-day ticket covering Palatine Hill, the Roman Fora and Colosseum was 16 euros. Try to go to the colosseum early or later in the day (was open till 7.15pm when we visited) as inside the colosseum the was very hot in the middle of the day. Took us  between two and three hours to complete the visit. Another tip - get your combined ticket from the Palatine Hill ticket booth as the booth for the Colosseum has very long queues.

title: Holiday
text: Really good enjoyed it alot. Worth the visit. Would go again. Interesting artifacts dotted about. Very hot. Two day tickets are worth the money. Kids will like it as well

title: Everything You Read Here Is True! Incredibly Awesome!
text:
The Colosseum tends to be THE site people visiting Rome want to see and it meets every expectation! Everything you read here is true: It's massively impressive, it's incredibly hot (we went in July), definitely get tickets in advance, ignore the people selling you things outside and it's something you need to see. 

We had booked a tour of the panoramic level-- which took forever to book logging into the website-- only to arrive and have that top level be under construction. We not only lost that exclusive access, but the tour guide as well. It was still incredible. Having the ticket in advance, got us in right away. There are enough signs to give you some information if you want to forgo a guide, but as with most things in Rome, having someone to explain the details and history make the experience more meaningful. Still, with the signs and the help of google to answer our questions, it was an incredible visit.

title: Great,  but too hot
text:
We entered the Colosseum.
This is what we can say because we saw the inside, but probably, you don't need to get in if you don't have time.
It's not so different from what you would expect from outside.
I found the Palatine hills more interesting.

title: Absolute beast of a monument
text:
Definitely worth seeing. Didn't do the tour but walking around the perimeter provides some views of the inside. 

The detail and grand scale of this site is breathtaking.

title: Things to do before you die - visit the Colosseum: check!
text: I chose to buy the entrance tickets with the guided tour before traveling and through Tripadvisor. It was a very good option. The experience included not only the Colosseum, but also the Palatine Hill and the Forum. The information and the sights are overwhelming. Definitely, “something to do before you die.”

title: A must see for everyone
text:
It is hard to describe the grandeur of this place in words. No one should miss out on the Colosseuum. 

Pls keep in mind the weather while visiting given the open areas in the structure inside.

title: A must see (of course!)
text: Even though there is some restoration going in progress, this was still on the list to go see.  We did not buy tickets beforehand; so we had to wait on line for only 45 minutes!

title: Iconic Relic
text: When in Rome one has to see this human achievement. However, don’t cheat yourself and go on your own. Get a guided tour so you can get context. It was helpful to learn, for example, that the wild animals were kept in small compartments in the dark unfed for days. No wonder they came into the arena so violent. The Colosseum is well preserved. It’s a bit challenging to navigate for those with mobility issues.

title: The famous Colise
text: You have to know that there is no place to sit while you visit. In august , this is very hot. You need to bring water and good shoes.Good to go with a guide and have your tichet ahead if not you can wait for 2 hours or more under the sun in lign , no place to sit!

title: Family vacation
text:
Amazing to see the history, not happy to see all the garbage laying around on the ground.
Line ups to get in are very long and on a hot day makes it even longer.

title: Colosseum and and surrounding area
text:
Found a recommended tour group and bought the 3 hour skip the line tour.  Overall experience was amazing and our tour guide did a great job.  Some areas were closed after rain the day before, so be prepared for that, disappointing but it is what it is. 
I had a normal sized north face backpack.  It certainly wasn't small and not like a camping backpack.  The guards didn't even bat an eye to any backpacks.  I was super worried but asked my tour company and they seemed completely not worried.  My back started hurting from lugging around stuff so it was not the best idea.  If you bring a water bottle you can refill at various fountains along the route. Otherwise there is a point with a dispensary where you can get filtered still or sparkling water.  We brought our own.  Don't be afraid of the water, my wife was hesitant, but I drank it all day and it was cold and delicious.

title: 2 Days in Rome
text: Beautiful tour 2hrs across the colosseum forum and gardens. Ruins right in front of you. The. Walk was a combo of stairs and curving hillsides. Set up a guide to explain the rich history.

title: Value for money
text: Well worth booking tickets in advance and visiting. I spent 6 hrs visiting the whole site. The majority of the time at Palitine Hill with 2hrs at the Collesseum. If hot take a water container and fill at the free fountains on the way round. You can use your ticket to visit on the next day if you dont manage to see it all in a day.

title: Book a tour to save queueing
text: We had heard about the long queues to get into the colosseum so we booked a tour through the official website.  We were appointed a time to arrive and went to a different entrance than non bookings, we had a short queue for the security check then met our guide who was very knowledgeable and pointed out things we would have missed on our own

title: Amazing
text: We took the tour at night and it was amazing! The tour guide gave a lot of information! We had an Airbnb that was facing the Colosseum and when you open the window you can see it it was pretty amazing

title: Just the tip of the Iceberg in Roma!
text: Of course, you must visit the Colosseum, but do spend time at the Forum and surrounding neighborhood!

title: Friends visit in rome
text: Nice tourist place to visit.So many ancien things to see and learn how people leave centuries ago. loved it

title: Astonishing
text: It's huge, it's old and it is still looking good. And thanks to the whole pre-booking of tickets dispersed the crowds quite a lot. I was truly impressed

title: Great Night Tour of Colosseum
text: Great tour to start our vacation!  Very informative tour as we learned about more than just the Colosseum.  Getting to see it all after hours gave a whole different perspective.

title: Must see!
text:
I have seen the Colosseum from outside a few times, but this time we decided to go inside. Was the best idea! We have book our time slot few days before with Roma Pass. So easy to skin the line. 
Colosseum is a must see, very important part of the history of Roman.

title: One for the books!
text: We got a skip the line package. The only way to do it, in my view. Great guide: Ferdinando! Nice fellow with good english and ability to answer questions. Very knowledgeable. Make sure you go to the washroom before viewing as the toilets are not ideal... We got stuck in a thunderstorm before seeing the rest of the tour and right after the colosseum.Hoping to get reimbursed. We'll see. I'd recommend for going once. Bring an umbrella just in case!!!

title: Lots of history but know what you’re doing
text: The Colosseum has a lot of history, and in the pictures you see online and in movies, it looks like it was built centuries ago (which it was), but when you visit it in person, you learn that many Popes throughout history have tried renovating it with modern-looking bricks (which I didn’t take picture of). It’s a bit off-putting at first, but once you get used to it, the history is great. My advice is to 1. just know that you’re not going to get the movie-perfect sights, 2. keep a stoic, jerk-like face when passing people trying to sell water or take pictures with you and they won’t bother you, and 3. try to find a tour that also covers the Forum, which is right next door. Happy travels!

title: Beautiful from outside
text: The only let down from this magnificent architecture is the queue to buy the ticket, its chaos..other than that its amazing , when you get out of the station its tthe first thing you see

title: Rome vacation
text: We made a terrible mistake to visit Rome in!! It was unbareably hot... that was the only minus... other than that, its fantastic!!

title: Incredible
text: Our hotel was very close to the coliseum. Being a runner my first look at the majestic coliseum was during my early morning run.. it was such an experience viewing it with no crowds around. We later took a tour of the entire structure. Such a huge part of the Roman History. Truly a must see while visiting Rome.

title: A true wonder of the world
text: What an experience to see the Colosseum.  We did a 'skip the line' tour, and received a very comprehensive guide - I don't think it would be anywhere near as good unless you go with a tour.  The building itself is in many states of repair and it's breath taking to stand and visualise exactly what happened years ago.  I wouldn't recommend going in June as it was way too hot.  Spring/Autumn must be a better time to visit.

title: Beautiful but too many tourists
text: My second visit to the Colosseum, the last 45 years ago.  So many tourists, I highly recommend buying your ticket to see the Colosseum online well in advance to avoid waiting in line.  The ruins are magnificent of course and if you spring for the tour which you must book in advance you are able to see more of the complex than a general ticket.  The tours also come with an audio guide.

title: It’s a must see but...
text: Unless you have a guide there’s little to understand about the building’s past. It can be crowded and different tickets provide access only to particular areas, so what you see may be limited. However, you have to go, so...

title: A must when in Rome.
text: It’s currently have some restoration work etc done. It’s magnificent. I would recommend getting a guided tour as there are little things you will miss of you go at it alone. Beautiful yet sad with the history and reasons as to why it’s here. However a remarkable point of historical culture

title: Wonderful
text:
We ore booked our tickets which was a hassle from the beginning. We arrived very early and picked up our tickets.
Once inside we opted for the sound system not with video it was informative but there was a lack of signs to tell you where to go.
Leave your sets on the way out not back at the start as we did, again it was confusing.
I

title: Italy Tour
text: We are on a tour and visited the Coliseum.  You have to stop to see it.  It is the most frequented site in Rome.  It is amazing that all this was built over 2000 years ago and is still standing.  The sun was hot so bring water for you journey.  There is an elevator for those who can’t manage steps.  The steps are high and slanted so be careful on the way down.

title: Group tour
text:
Organised by IBPSA conference held in Rome sep02-04
(IBPSA= international building performance stimulation association) - a real treat for a group of us, accompany persons. The tour guide was excellent -walking commentary in a very crowded environment. The pictures posted speak volumes of the Colosseum. Pictures don’t do justice -You have to come and experience see it for yourself! 5-star tour...Skip the line and buy your tickets online from BIG BUS company/Colosseum Tour at Trip Advisor. 
The Tour is exhausting but very engaging  experience with a good guide, taking us back to a sporting arena of ancient days of -busy hive of gladiator activities. 
Well worth your day jostling with spent the massive crowd gathered here!!

title: Worth a visit
text:
Great amazing sight a must see tour when in Rome 
You get hassled by people who want to sell you tours and skip  the queue , which is annoying, starting prices are €35 and upwards which seems tempting when u see the lines of people waiting to get in , luckily we decided to skip that and go and eat and have a walk around the shops , by the time we did that and came back to the colosseum the queues had gone and we paid €12 euros and walked around ourselves and saw inside this amazing building, also the same ticket gets you into the Roman forum which is also spectacular

title: Breathtaking history & architecture
text:
We were left surprised & knowledgable after an hour & a half of walking round the amphitheatre. Incredible historic value & we learnt a lot. Very enjoyable day out. Easy queuing/ticket system & good security. 
Definitely worth going out of your way to see & paying to go inside, a once in a lifetime sight. 
The only thing that spoilt the atmosphere for us was the scaffolding and works done on the floor/stage. We couldn’t get a feel for the history whilst floorboards were in place for construction. However depending on when you go, a spectacular visit.

title: The Colosseum is must stop for all trips to ROME.
text: Make sure you pre-buy your tickets online well in advance. This helps you walk through the crowds to the front of the line to ensure you get to enjoy this historical site without wasting time in line.

title: Franchesca our guide was the best
text:
We had a great time being guided around the Colosseum and Palatine hill with Franchesca from Get your guide. The romans were amazingly sophisticated in their architecture and even managed to engineer a partially closing roof for the stadium, something we still struggle to get right even today !
Well worth paying for a guide we skipped the lines

title: Fantastic history
text:
Second visit to this wonderful amphitheater and it is being well maintained for future generations - we visited the locality and did not enter the venue for a second time partly because of the long queues and a scorching hot day!

The only negative was the large number of migrants selling tat and having difficulty taking no for an answer - police trying to control but not very successfully 

Well worth a visit

title: A Must See
text: It was our first visit inside the Colosseum.  It is amazing to see this well maintained structure.  Be sure you visit both floors. We bought skip-the-line tickets and we were able to bypass the long line up for the Colosseum.  However, there is no skip-the-line for Roman Forum/Palatine Hill.

title: Must see attraction!
text: Unfortunately our tour in Rome only included the outside of the Colosseum. This was disappointing but there were huge queues so always prebook and if affordable, get skip the line tickets. For us, it was still an amazing place to see. It was massive but then it did hold 50,000 Roman citizens watching the gladiators! Our guide explained that each ground floor archway was numbered so that seats could be easily accessed- like stadia today. It’s extremely well preserved and we will definitely return to tour the inside.

title: Breathtaking!
text: Before you even travel to Rome it really is essential to pre book your tickets. We bought omni and Roma passes which made it so much easier and we skipped all the huge queues. This place is a must see but make sure you bring water not just because of the heat but because of the amount of walking and climbing steps😊. To be walking in the steps of Romans and to look down into where the gladiators sat and waited to enter the arena is hard to describe. I found it quite emotional looking at all that history but would not have missed it for anything. It is a place I will never forget - it definitely has the wow factor.

title: A must see
text: You should book a tour in advance to avoid the long queues to get into the colosseum. Beautiful views of the colosseum forum and gardens.

title: Colosseum Visit
text:
Very amazing place to visit...
Even it is a hot weather but worth it..
People are friendly, specially our Tour Guide Renata.

title: Coliseum
text:
My girls were so excited to see the coliseum 
We booked tickets in advance through GetYourGuide and paid 12.80 euro for adult over 18
Under 18’s are free
The queues are long so best to book and skip the queue especially in the heat!
The ticket includes the Roman Forum and Palentine hill
Amazing to see
Need about 2 hours here

title: Wow
text: I think the colosseum can’t be described with words. We went at 17.00 and had almost no ques and waiting for tickets. The stuff was really friendly and helpful and the site itself was breath taking!!

title: The Home of Gladiators!
text: Get yourself a tour guide to truly enjoy this site!  Just staring at it is not enough!  Our guide told us of the sequence of the various battles and took us down into the floors to see what it was like and the “ holding pens for the animal to animal battles!  Wonderful history!

title: Get there early and you’ll be fine
text:
My friend and I got ourselves worked up for the supposed 2 hour queue in the sun. We got up early doors and arrived at 8:45am. We tried to get the tickets from Palatine Hill as suggested by Rick Steve but found the signage poor and got sent all over the place by the staff. After walking around for 30 minutes in the moderate 9am sun, we decided to just get in the queue and were in within 20 minutes! We paid the extra €5 for a guided tour which was ok but we’d recommend saving your money and downloading the free Rick Steve audio tour which was way better! 

Definitely go to Roman Forum and Palatine Hill too which is included in the price (there are also Rick Steve’s audiotours for these).

title: Worthwhile
text: Very interesting place, imagine what life was like as a Roman citizen gladiator or prisoner slave. The tour guides give you interesting facts and lots of information very worthwhile tour

title: Great sight - must see
text: Was a little sceptical of seeing another ruin but my wife wanted to go and I’m glad we made the effort! It is something to behold when you think how this was every constructed without modern tools.

title: Amazing
text:
Bought our tickets online from Coop culture, recommend doing this. Our tickets were entry admission with guided tour , this was enough for us to experience, 45min with guide then can continue to wander at your leisure. €23 ea adult and our son who is 17 was free entry just paid for guide.  I printed confirmation and you pick tickets up at a booth near colosseum. 
Was extremely hot as summer, take a hat or umbrella as there is no shade in surrounding area.

title: Amazing 💕💕💕💕
text:
48 hours is simply not enough time to get around Rome, there is so much to see, better to buy tickets in advance as the waiting time, heat and lines is a challenge itself 

You are spoiled for choice in Rome 💕

title: History came alive.
text:
This is a must when you visit Rome .Our guide was very knowledgeable.The views are amazing.
Lines are long use skip the line tour and also wear comfortable shoes.
Go back in history to the gladiators and the Roman Empire.

title: Love! Love! Love!
text:
Amazing place with so much history.
Only negative factor was half way during the tour it rained heavily which cancelled the rest of the tour.
But we came the next and completed the tour.
Certainly a place to visit with a guide.
Thank you

title: Spectacular
text: Absolutely worth a visit, we went about 4.30pm and got in within 15 minutes. There is plenty of information on the 2nd floor, enough that I don’t think it’s worth the audioguides. It took us about 2 hours taking our time.

title: Unlike Big Ben..
text: ..Colosseum is larger than expected! It is almost impossible to find a perfect position to take a photo of this gigantic amphitheater!

title: Wow
text: The colosseum is iconic and didn't disappoint. Get tickets beforehand as the queues are very long. Also get an audio guide if your Roman history is sketchy. If you want to go down to the lower levels, this is extra and tickets are limited, so again, book ahead. We missed out, but will go back . Amazing atmosphere and a must visit place!!

title: Great!
text:
We took the evening tour and it was awesome. Less crowds, less heat! Plus in the evenings you can see the dungeons which are not open during the day since they’re being worked on. (Or so I was told) 
The only draw back to the evening tour  would be- it’s somewhat difficult to get good pictures as it gets dark. I’d still do it again though to avoid the heat and crowds. We took a group tour. Highly recommend!

title: Impressive!
text: A must see if you’re in Rome! The history here is incredible and the work they’re doing to recreate what was is amazing.

title: An amazing site to behold
text: We visited about 10 minutes before they stopped selling tickets and it was quiet with no queues. I recommend the time of day. The sun was setting as we walked around. It has an awesome set up where you can dive into the history and beauty of the place or you can just try get that perfect 100 insta pictures and annoy everyone else 😂

title: A Tad Over Rated
text: The Colosseum is a marvellous engineering achievement for it’s time & the stadium must have been sensational in it’s day, but we got there on a very crowded day & along with the heat, it turned us off. To get a better outcome, I suggest a group early access.

title: Incredible
text: Simply awesome to see this unique monument to Roman history up close and personal. There is a real sense of history here and you can appreciate the genius of Roman engineering in all its scale and spectacle.

title: You must go back!
text: I’ve been to the colosseum several times before, but they have recently discovered so many new findings in their archeological digs. A new museum has been erected on one of the levels that displays the elevators and pulls systems the Romans’ used to transport the animals onto the arena. As well as confirming that they did fill it with water for naval battles! The new discoveries are incredible! They continue to dig underground as I hope to visit again in a couple years to see the new findings. Skip the line- pay the extra it is worth it. We easily walked in approx 11:30 am and when we left the line was wrapped around the colosseum. 🤷‍♀️

title: Skip the line and guide
text: We did our tour with overome, the group consisted of 8 people excluding the guide. I believe a guide is a must when visiting a historical monument as they can give you perspective of the colosseum , forum and palatine hill that you won’t attain if you’re not a historian. It’s very expensive but worth every penny.

title: Must visit
text: One of the most iconic places in Rome, this is a mandatory stop for any traveler. Buying regular tickets, as most people here will tell you, takes quite some time so it is recommended to either buy skip-the-line tickets online or book a tour. I went for the second option - although you can visit the Colosseum by yourself, having a guide will greatly enhance the experience as there are some details which you would miss out on otherwise.

title: Unbelievable
text: The Colosseum was in your face right there in the middle of Rome.  It was huge and so beautiful for a building over 2000 years old.  It was the highlight of my trip.  Take a tour!

title: Tiqets is a must!
text:
We booked last minute with a company called Tiqets, they are the only ones I saw that were really put together with uniforms and umbrellas/flags that didn’t look like some random doing it. One of the cheapest around and my god was it worth the £25 to go straight into the Colosseum without waiting in that line.

I used their mobile app which was so easy to use and reminded me when it was close to meeting time with very clear instructions.

After that straight into the Roman forum, by the way DO NOT WORRY about the whole back thing they were no bother with it and brought a big backpack with a camera bag. Only thing is they want it opened to look inside which was not a problem.

title: Small group tour of Pantheon and Colosseum
text: Azzurra gave us a great tour of the Palladium, Pantheon and Colosseum. She was upbeat and energetic and very knowledgeable about her subject. We covered a good amount of territory in the three hours and we saw and heard so much. She is proud of her City so we heard lots of interesting stories of the past. Highly recommend this tour.

title: Wow! Bigger than I thought!
text: Everyone said get your tickets early , take the fast tract etc bit we choose to buy tickets on the day. Under 18s are free ( which lots of early purchase tickets won't tell u). Just take id and under 25s have reduced price. We did queue for 20 min. But queue kept moving. Option to add guided tour given but we choose to walk ourselves. Worth getting the combined ticket with Palatine hill and forum. Amazingly preserved . Lots of photo opportunities. Start at upper level and work your way down.

title: Collosseum
text: We took a guided tour round the Colosseum and it was worth every penny. To see and hear about its history and it usage over the years gave a visual display of how life was in times gone by. Well worth a tour. We booked through trip advisor and avoided the que a good move.

title: Amazing!!
text: I had no idea of the details and history there!  Well worth the time, and great to get a professional tour!!

title: Trip to Rome
text: The best trip our family had in Rome, guide was very communicated and ready to answer any questions that the kids have. Excellent experience.

title: Spend the money on a good tour to skip the line
text: We booked a tour with Through Eternity tours that was a VIP experience to see the Colosseum and Palatine Hill. This tour was given by an archeologist. The Colosseum is truly a masterpiece. This tour brought such insight to the building as well as Roman life. This was the highlight of our trip.

title: Tiqets is the way to go for colluseum visit
text: When tech works to help you navigate a far away place it's so satisfying. That's how we felt using tiqets.com to run our collusuem tour. Smart phone reservation, skip the line, visit the ground floor not normally available to even the regular visitors, and access to palantine hill. Valentina, Vilma, and Deborah were great hosts and even helped us get in a group after we missed our time. This was an amazing and seamless experience.

title: Amazing!
text: We took a night tour of the Colosseum and were also able to explore the underground area with our a guide - an archaeologist. We went in August and were glad to have this night tour - only two groups and we had the place to ourselves - well worth it as you beat the crowds and the heat during the day.

title: A few days in Rome
text: A visit to Rome is not complete unless you go to see the Colosseum we had a skip the line ticket which was well worth it when you see everyone queuing in the hot sun.

title: Remarkable historical site
text:
We took a tour with a private company, but, either way, I would definitely recommend going.  It's amazing to stand in something that old and just imagining being there over 2000 years ago.

Much is closed off for renovations, but that is to be expected with something that old.  There is still a tremendous amount to see.

title: Last minute tour
text:
We signed up for a skip the line tour at the last minute for 30 Euro per person.
Had headset and small group of about 15 people which made it easier to see and get good context.

title: A part of history you should not miss! **With a guide you skip the Line**
text:
Although it can be full of tourists groups, don't pass this one up!
The best tip we got several years ago is to use the guides who wait just outside the Colosseum.  They have badges and a certification they wear around their neck.  Most of them speak English & are students in university, or older. 
GET THE GUIDE!!! 
I think it's around 40 euros (maybe more now) but they walk you around the Colosseum and what could seem boring looking at ruins, jumps to life when you hear the history and the stories that happened in this place.  Also, the guide will take you through a separate entrance so you don't need to wait in line. You pay the guide & the ticket price and they take care of the entrance. It's so easy!

Go in the morning (before lunch at 1:00) and there are also guides who will take you to see Nero's place and a few other landmarks besides the Colosseum.  
**Also don't take the Metro to get here - the Subway in Rome is quite a mess closing weeks for renovation  or because they discovered another historical artifact (since the city was built on top of itself) it's much faster with a taxi or Uber.

title: Worth the queues - best of Rome
text:
I always judge a place on the ratio of time to get in versus time spent in. And on that basis - even though the queues are large - the Colosseum scores top marks. Lots to see - loads of history - size and scale for impact - friendly for families (even a 2yr old in a buggy) - well organised - and of course the amazing Roman forum gardens and buildings next door!

Let's put it this way - here you will spend hours - at other famous sites in Rome you will spend minutes

title: A classic
text: Do the audiovideo tour. Takes about an hour and takes you all round the Coliseum . Book your slot as can get v busy

title: Amazing!
text: Lose yourself in the history! An amazing sight to behold! A definite must see, despite the crowds and heat of August! We didn’t book online or buy tickets beforehand, 5 minutes in queue to pay by  credit card and then another few minutes for bag check and we were in! If you are 18-25, take your passport and you can see for 2 Euros! That includes Colesseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill 😀

title: A great insight
text: This was, of course, an awesome monument, all the information you can learn about why this gruesome arena was built, its original purpose and who sat where, was fascinating: A great visual history lesson!

title: Lots of illegal sellers
text: Colosseum is great. No need to get inside. There is only ruins and cross there unless with a guide. The disadvantage is illegal sellers of little things that will “attack” you to spend at least one euro for there not useful things.... If you manage to find spot without people (park behind) you can make beautiful pictures:)

title: Not as dreamy as you think but a must see
text:
Much of the structure has either been historically pillaged, left to rot, damaged by natural disasters and the general elements. Hodge-podge restoration and support is in place to stop it from falling down. It's nothing like you think - just a skeleton from what you imagine, the center of it is just a mess of digging and scaffolding. Still - it's a genuine piece of history with a lot to it - although I'm not fond of tours, I think seeing it without a tour by someone knowledgable is a waste of time. There's a lot of context and detail you will miss out on otherwise.

Make sure to visit it at night too.

title: This will leave you in awe!
text:
The Colosseum did not disappoint!

As huge and incredible as you would expect.

We took an early morning tour (through City Wide Tours) which was great - it was less crowded and helped us beat the heat!

HIGHLY recommend either doing a tour or purchasing entry ticket in advance....as lineups for the poor souls that didn’t were waiting up to 4 hours!

Incredible history and glad to see the gov’t has made an effort to restore and maintain this space.

Roman Forum and Palantine are worth seeing as well.  Enjoy!

title: All we expected, and more...
text: As advised we booked online with CoopCulture on the Colosseum website before we flew to Rome. Many of the tours were already sold out but we managed to book a 45 minute tour covering two levels of the Colosseum which turned out to be more than enough (the tickets included entry to the Forum and Palatine Hill as well). By booking we avoided the large queues. The tour start point was easy to find and we set off 15 minutes after the scheduled start time of the tour (as is the case with all the tours). As well as the Colosseum itself our guide took us to see a number of the artefacts on display, and explained the history of the Colosseum in such a way that it brought it to life. You could imagine being part of the crowd attending an event. After the tour finished we were left to explore ourselves. An amazing experience...

title: Wow
text: Amazing seeing it first hand. The architecture on every building is priceless. Pity they are not looked after better

title: Worth getting skip the line tickets!
text:
Purchased skip the line tickets through Tiqets. So glad we did!! We didn’t have to wait and was able to get VIP access to the ground floor which was really cool. We had Valentina, Velma and Deborah. We were super late for our timed reservation and thought they wouldn’t let us in but thankfully they accommodated.
They were all so nice. Thank you again!

title: Absolutely mesmerising, loved it.
text: We had a guided tour through  'get your guide' he was very passionate about his subject and made it come alive. This also included skip the queue which is appreciated in the heat. If you love your history this trip is a must!

title: Awesome
text: Very  well organised venue geared to dealing with vast numbers of tourists, that may be thanks to the wonderful architecture and planning of their ancient forebears. It is of course the Colosseum a breathtaking awe inspiring monument to what mankind can achieve no one going to Rome should miss it.

title: Guided tour of Colosseum
text:
Spent a morning viewing this amazing site
Guided tour gave us a quick entry but other than that the guide was boring. Rambled on, usually off topic. We went to see the Colosseum not to hear the whole history of the Roman Empire. Would have preferred to look around ourselves. Not really worth the extra. He was so bad we didn't continue with the tour to Palatine Hill and Roman Forum.
We bailed and went for a cup of tea

title: Amazing
text: I went to the Colosseum last month with my daughter. Luckily I didn't have to queue because I bought the fast track ticket which let me go to Roman forum and the hill.
