If I had to queue maybe I had to wait at an hour maybe more. When I went in it was amazing. It was like a dream come true. My daughter really enjoyed it. Our first target in Rome was this place and it didn't disappoint at all. We felt thrilled.

title: Awesome ...
text: Colosseum is one of the greatest iconic place must to visit in Rome. There were huge line for getting in , it is suggested to have pre booking if wanted to visit from inside . Also it’s huge area and require 2-3 hour for visit witch is quite difficult with young kids. On the other hand it’s nice also to visit from outside for having some memorable clicks.

title: Historical awe
text: You don't realise how far we have come as humans until you walk through our history. The Colosseum was huge and I would suggest getting a guide to explain all the little facts about this great place. Summer was sweltering when we went through but walking through the passageways were cool. Stairs slope down so hold onto the railing! Make sure you wear comfortable shoes as there is a lot of walking and the floors can be uneven around the colosseum. I thoroughly enjoyed our tour and the place.

title: Huge
text:
An amazing experience 
Definitely worth a visit. 
There is quite a lot of beggars and people trying to give you ‘free’ bracelets then want you to pay after so be ware 

Also get here early to avoid waiting

title: Excellent
text: We chose the self tour that included entrance via the gladiators gate. Amazing experience and definitely worth the money.

title: Spectacular
text: THe bus tour gives great exterior views however we returned the next day to venture in, we reciebed a tip to go early or late to avoid hideous queues, at 5pm we we in swiftly and managed the Roman forum and Palatine hill all were amazing.

title: Awesome.
text:
Pictures do not do this place justice. 
As a Roman history lover I was amazed by this place. You cannot appreciate the size of the Flavian amphitheatre until you are in it. 
We had a tour with a guy called Donald. He was also superb. A must see. Will be back.

title: A must-view!
text: One of the best place we've visited during our trip. I recommend Walks of Italy for the Colosseum visit. We learned a lot and we came in by the gladiator's gate :)

title: Stunning - definitely worth a visit in Rome!
text: Steeped in history - I spent a good couple of hours here wandering around with an audio tour learning about it....would definitely recommend as an essential on the Rome sightseeing list!

title: When In Rome
text:
Easy to find ... and very impressive... quest were long so get there early .
There a quite a few people selling dodgy stuff but just say no and keep walking and they leave you alone.

title: Nice but...
text:
Even with pre-booked tickets skip the queue guide it's not so quick, you still have to queue to go through security, which took more than 20min. Once you inside it's not so much to see because the Colosseum is only 30% off what it was (Verona arena is better preserved then this). It's impressive for the size it was 2000 years ago, but now it's just a shell. Lovely 
gardens and archeological site next too the Colosseum. Recommend to buy "skip the queue" tickets unless you want to spend hours in the queue (they are huge in the summer).

title: Take a tour guide for this trip
text: We were lucky with the tour guide were allocated. Renata booked via Headout. 3 hr tour. Very informative. Avoided the queues. As well as the Colesseum the tour also takes in the Forum Roma. Again another informed visit with good views from the Palatine Hill.

title: Very cool place
text:
My husband and I didn't pay for a group tour for this - we just bough the audio tours (5.5 Euros each) and that was enough - it was very crowded (we went at 10 AM on a week day) so try to get there early.

It's an awesome part of history and the audio guides were worth it - without them you would just do a 10 minute walk through. If you are really interested I would suggest a small group tour.

The tunnels were all under construction when we were there so I didn't feel like there was much to see, but was still worth going.

Also - the bathrooms are gross so go before you get there.

title: Interesting Roman History
text: We took a guided tour of this venue while in Rome.  The history was quite interesting and you can imagine the deadly games and various persecutions.  The Colosseum is undergoing some extensive renovations which, in my view, takes away something from the visit.  I was glad to at least see some of the original steps.  As much of the area is cobblestone, walking can be difficult.  The stairs can also be a challenge for those with mobility issues.  There is an elevator for those that cannot walk the steps, but that is limited to one level.  Because of the ongoing renovations, access to the floor and lower areas is off limits.

title: Great price and very very interesting
text:
Really well preserved. Lots of good info inside to read. Good value for €14 per person including Roman forums entrance. 
Advise booking in advance online, as k think you now need to choose an entrance time. We did that and had no problems and a very short queue getting in.

title: Breath taking
text: Amazing how the Colosseum still can be in such good conditions. I never expected it. Definitely worth a visit, especially in the early morning.

title: Historical place
text: This place is so old . It was the place where the Roman emperor held gladiator fights and live entertainment for the people and it was all free . The place was  built around the first century . The structure of the building still very well reserved with the original travertine marble . I mean the history behind it is just amazing . I suggest you buy tickets with a private tour . I suggest my best tour and use Andrea kaminski as the tour guide since he was so knowledgeable of the history of it plus you don’t want to wait in line for two hours . My husband and I we went right in and it was the perfect tour .I’m so amazed how beautiful this place was . A must see in Rome ..

title: Go with a tour guide and be amazed!
text:
This is the kind of place that you really need to go with a tour guide, unless you know a whole lot about the Colosseum.

Even with the guide, it took quite a while to get through security, but that is one of those things that I do not complain about. The safety of the people and the Colosseum is paramount.

We went during the hottest week in July (the same day that it was 108 in Paris.) While it was slightly cooler in Rome, it was still really hot. If you are going in a hot day, take and umbrella for shade. There are shady places, particularly if you go up the stairs, but downstairs on the main level where you enter, there are not many places to get out of the sun. 

It would also be a good idea ot do a little research before you go so you know what you are going to see. That will make it more interesting. And be prepared to walk up VERY steep steps. I mean really high risers that are quite a bit taller than steps you would find in a building nowadays.

All in all, we (2 teenagers and 2 adults) were amazed and thrilled to see the Colosseum. The amount of preservation is quite remarkable given the age of the building. It is a tiring tour, particularly if you climb up to the upper levels (or that could be me being old and not very much in shape.)

title: A little disappointed by the inside
text: Personally I was disappointed by the inside of the colloseum. Having gone through all the fuss inside, even with a guided tour ticket that got you in faster, I was unimpressed with how little you can see. You're only really allowed to walk around it and I feel like I could have guessed what it looked like from the outside. There aren't any exhibits or anything like that to help you get a feel for what it used to be like. You just get the guides explanation and I feel like they really downplayed some of the antics the would have gone on in there!

title: A Must in Rome
text:
This is an great place that you should visit when you are in Rome. 
It could be by day but in the night it looks amazing. 
You can check any tour to have a guide visit inside.

title: Imposing symbol of Ancient Rome
text:
Say "Rome" and an image of the mighty Colosseum is bound to flash in your mind. This ancient amphi-theater which has been witness to several historical gladiators battling against wild beasts and humans alike, to several upheavals and executions has stood the test of time and been one of the strongest symbols of ancient Rome.

A ticket to the Colosseum costs only 12 Euros and there are lift facilties available for those who need it. However, for visitors with wheelchairs or any such challenges, the trip may be a tad cumbersome.

Several levels above and below the ground reveal how these bloody spectacles were enjoyed by Roman emperors and audience. The labyrinth of tunnels, passages and rooms below where the gladiators and animals were kept give you the goosebumps as do the magnificent arches where monarchy sat to witness the bloody theatrical displays.Some say that naval battles were also recreated here by filling the underground tunnels with water. 

Just being in the same space that was once walked upon by the legends of Roman era is overwhelming ! Do visit when in Rome.

title: Awesome
text:
booked this tour through viator, sub contractor was Italy wonders.
Our Guide Renata (Rene) was brilliant she was not only very knowledgeable she was interesting in what we needed to talking about & had one to one conversations at any time.

the tour was just brilliant  we leant about the colosseum its self who fought in the arena, who went to the games what it was all about, one word fantastic thank you Renata brilliant tour

title: The Feats of Engineering are remarkable
text: Many people go just to see the Colosseum. But if you pay attention to the construction and the sheer size, it is truly a man made wonder. Book tickets ahead online and get there early. Admission is timed and restricted.

title: Breathtaking destination!
text: There are so many historical buildings in Rome, but Colosseum is absolutely breathtaking. You will need a guidebook, you can pick up bits of info from the many guided tours that are going around, but it is worth taking yourself off to a corner and imagining what it was built 2000 years ago.

title: Always breathtaking
text: It is always breathtaking to see this magnificent piece of history and architecture with plenty of room to ‘rome’ around. Too may people trying to sell you stuff though !

title: When in Rome...
text: Colosseum is a must do in Rome and doing with hordes of other tourists are just part of the experience. Easy to do if you able bodied and don't mind walking. Skip the queue tickets are worth the while.

title: Colosseum
text: Totally and utterly awe inspiring.This is one place everyone should visit once in their lifetime. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable but chilled as well. A truly memorable experience.

title: History comes alive.
text: The Colosseum tour was stunning. It caused great empathy for the people and animals who suffered here for hundreds of years. A vast propaganda machine that kept many Roman citizens on the side of government. The tour guide did an admirable job navigating the vast crowds.

title: Cattle Train
text: Though it was great experience for me personally they let to many in, you lost sight of what it was at times there were that many people inside at once and felt it was like a cattle train. There is continous restoration but you lose that old to new as more and more is getting repaired, maybe it was just me but came to Rome for this yet enjoyed the Vatican more.

title: Nice place to look at for 1 hour. Much constructions so hard to imagine anything
text: Much constructions so hard to imagine anything. But beside that it was an impressive building. One of things to see on my bucket list

title: Interesting!
text: Visit the coliseum it’s worth the tour price and learning what happened there is amazing. It’s so much more than just where “circus” was held. It was also a church and more.

title: OMG
text:
Amazing !!!  
You have to go !!!!! Rome Is the best city ! 
Colosseum ... omg ! The best place in Roma !! You can find bear restaurant ... aroma ... vero good!!!! 
Dinner with a view! 
Magic !

title: A must see when in Rome
text: You can view this from the outside but I would recommend a tour so you can view the internals which were extremely interesting.  Tours should be done with a guide as you only get a set time inside and a guide will explain the history.  Lots of opportunities to take some great pictures.

title: Impressive
text: With a little imagination, you can project yourself in the past. Majestuous, free entertainment and food, and covered fron the elements. Yes theColosseum was covered!

title: Get in early
text:
We arrived at 8.15am, had our tickets by 8.45 and was inside the Colesseum by 9am. Ticket sales were at the Palatine hill (it was a bit confusing, but plenty of helpful staff to point you in the right direction).
€12.50 per ticket, we didn’t bother with a guide (mostly we heard what other guides were saying and watched a few documentaries to fill in the gaps).
Pretty amazing place

title: Must see
text: We opted for a 1 hour express tour by Viatour. I was worried it wouldn't be very good but it was very informative and didn't need to be longer if you don't have much time. We were taken round a lot of the Colliseum by a knowledgeable guide who made it very interesting. He opted for shade where possible to explain things. The only problem was finding the tour office. Thank goodness we arrived 40 minutes early or we would have missed the whole thing

title: First time in Rome
text: After seeing Gladiator, I felt a connection to this place. Lots of history here and the structure was amazing

title: A staggering construction by the Romans
text: There are lots of way you can purchase your tickets to avoid the queue. We decided to just turn up, waited 20minutes and were in. Entry €12 avoid the line price €20! The scale of the areana built more than 2000 years ago without computers to help is just amazing, the height, wow!

title: Colosseum
text:
this is a can't miss attraction.  Even our 10 year old grandson was in awe of this 2000 year old manmade structure, filled with history, even impressive without a tour guide.
It was great, and a great deal of reconstruction since our last visit, is also a great addition.

title: Walking tour of the Palantine Hill and Colosseum
text:
We took the trek with our guide Sylvia from Grays On Line. Sylvia was a fantastic guide, really knew her stuff and kept the group on the march over a very lengthy tour.

Highly recommend a tour with her if you get the chance.

title: Traditional visit in Rome
text:
A “must visit” in Rome. 
We got the $12 € ticket to Colosseum and Palatine Palace. Great history. Great value for the ticket.

title: Amazing!!
text: This was an amazing work of art.  A must see in ones lifetime! It is incredibly intricate and the engineering that went into it is beyond comprehension.

title: Poor guide
text: The guide was very clinical in her presentations period we overheard other guys bringing the history the life period unfortunately ours was more of a archaeologist teaching a class

title: Rome - August 2019
text: I think my hopes were too high.  But the smaller version in Pula, Croatia provided a better tour and seemed to provide guests with more information about the lives of the people during that era versus the recorded walk through that was provided for the Colosseum.

title: Built in 8 years, unbelievable
text: This is a must see while in Rome and do yourself a favour, spend the extra money and buy yourself a "skip the line private tour" ticket because the lines at the Colosseum are the longest I have ever seen in the world, even longer then the line for the Statue of Liberty New York in summer? and the tour is fantastic.

title: When in Rome.......you must see the colosseum. You will be able to “been there” a lot later.
text: A must see in one or another of the many forms of tours you can do here. While we were in Rome it was. Stinking hot so we decided to do the night tour where the temp was more like 23C rather than 36C during the day. While you might not see the detail of everything like the daytime tours, you get to go down into the working part of the arena where all the animals and gladiators would have waited. There is also small audio visual shows as you walk around with your guide. We really enjoyed it.

title: Fantastic
text: We had an extended tour including the underground areas. The tour guide was excellent but was an officail from the Colosseum itself. I wish we ha booked directly with them rather than from an agency that were than happy waste our valuable time. We were an hour late starting due the agency.

title: A must see.
text: I highly recommend taking a tour when visiting the colosseum.  It is so worth hearing about what you are looking at, and having someone point out the often overlooked.

title: Toured underneath and all around
text: Took tour via Crown Tours. The were simply the “link” to the ticket and the tour with certified tour guides. Great, informative and beautiful visit!

title: Wedding Anniversary Vacation
text: Incredibly big. Lots of steps. Definitely get the 1.) audio tour and the 2.) skip the line pass. Also, make sure you visit all of the structures on the grounds, not just the Colosseum.

title: What a masterpiece
text: It was too hot to visit in August and make sure to carry water(there is a water pipe inside though). Skip lines may not be needed and do negotiate with the vendors i you are travelling independently.

title: A must see
text:
You can not visit Rome without visiting the Colosseum.

I would strongly recommend booking the queue jumper tickets online, this saves us well over an hour. The only problem you may have is that the tickets will be booked for a certain time, you will have to factor in travelling through Rome from your hotel. 

The Colosseum itself was fantastic, you cant comprehend the size until you are there in person. 

I would say a guided tour or the audio guide (€5) is a must just to get the information across. 

There are no food or drink stands so take some with you (you will be there for around 2 hours).

Overall it was a fantastic sight and well worth the money.

title: Overpriced and Over Rated!
text: As usual Security Guards at the entry gate are rude and thoughtless. It isn't  a language or cultural difference. They simply don't understand the meaning of manners or what a boost tourism is for their economy. I saw what I wanted to see without putting up with their behavior.

title: You can't miss it.
text: This was my second visit to Rome, and there has been a heavy increase in the number of tourists that go to the Colosseum, so I highly recommend buying your tickets in advance to avoid the long lines. I suggest you buy them through the official Italian culture office to avoid paying a surcharge to an agency, and with these tickets you can go directly to the Colosseum entrance rather than having to first go to the agency to pick them up. We did not get a guide nor an audioguide but getting either may be a good idea. We went at 5 pm; the weather was cooler and the place wasn't nearly as crowded as in the morning.

title: Colosseum tour with guide
text: We visited the Colosseum with a tour guide and we highly recommend it. The group was around 20 persons and we where provided with relevant any interesting information re this magnificent monument.

title: Guided tour_highly recommend
text: Wonderful visit! Very crowded, but an impressive piece of history! Highly recommend booking a guided tour, as there are so many information that a guide can share, that you couldn't find out otherwise! Will come back!

title: Definitely worth the time
text: Great place with so much history but I would suggest taking a guided tour as there is so much that would be easy to miss without. Lots of renovations taking place but it doesn’t take away from the sights inside and out

title: Must visit
text:
The Colosseum is a colossal MUST visit.
Skip the line ticket in advance will save time. We took the option of a guided tour which was excellent. Bear in mind the heat as this tour is out on the open and takes 3 hours. By the end you are mentally and physically tired.
But the size of the Colosseum and the history are breathtaking.
The Roman Fort and Palatine Hill are also good.

title: Truly Breathtaking
text:
We toured the Colosseum last Wednesday and booked a guided tour with Show Me Italy. Their guides really brought the history to life and we had access to the ground level and then all other levels.

Combined with the nearby ruins this is a must do.

title: The Colosseum, the Pryamids, the Parthenon - Iconic Ancient Structure
text:
It is amazing to see the Colosseum on your first visit to Rome.  There it is, in the middle of the city, as it has stood for nearly 2000 years.   I took two tours,  a self guided audio tour during the day, which allows you to see the Colosseum from the perspective of a Roman citizen who entered through one of the main gates and got to sit in the stands.  

The second was a night underground tour which took us through the gate that the gladiators used and into the underground where the gladiators and the wild animals waited until they were brought up to the arena floor by elevators.  Then you get to see the arena floor itself where the gladiators stood and did battle.  Amazing tour.

title: Must visit, but get your ticket on the way
text:
When in Rome - you must see the Colosseum. It is an incredible building and just so impressive that it was built in 70-80 AD.
Our only issue was finding a place to buy the entrance tickets and the signage at the venue was useless! You are also constantly hassled  by ticket touts. 

Buying options at/near the venue:

As you walk up the hill towards the Colosseum and just before the tourist office - on the right hand side of the road is a small ticket office. The queue here was small and it took about 10 minutes to get the tickets. If you are EU citizens remember to take ID for your under 25's as they get in for 2 Euros. You can then use the queue for "ticket holders" to enter the Colosseum and this moves quickly.
The ticket is valid for 2 days and covers the Colosseum, the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.

You can also buy tickets at the Colosseum, but you have to get into a long queue, that passes through security before you enter the main building where you can buy your tickets - a lot of slow queuing!

Despite the ticket hassles, it is definitely a "must see".

title: Historical Experience
text:
First, if you are in Rome, you must come here.  Second, if you come here, I highly recommend you purchase a skip the lines tour unless you happen to be going off season.  Fortunately we did, and it saved us us a ton of time.   Even if the tour wasn't good (it was fine), skipping the line was worth it and it likely saved us 90 minutes as the lines were incredibly long.   
As far at the Colosseum it was a special experience to see something with so much history.   The tour explored all around the exterior and interior or the colosseum and the surrounding area of forum and churches.  Warning to inexperienced travelers if you go in July/August.  Bring water and a hat or cap.   Much of the area is in open sun and it gets very hot.   Also have cash as not all area vendors take credit though the gift shop inside did.  There were areas where some street vendors were pretty aggressive and will profit off of your thirst or heat exhaustion if you are not prepared.
All said, there are only so many places where you can see this kind of history and culture and this is one of them.  Bring your camera and enjoy!

title: Don’t waste your time or money with Tiqets!
text:
Let me start off by saying that the Colosseum is definitely worth visiting at least once in your life. My two-star rating isn’t a reflection of the site but, rather, of the company Tiqets, which was the company we used to skip the line at the Colosseum.

Tiqets promised that we would skip the line and gain entry into the Colosseum and then the Roman Forum at Palatine Hill afterwards. Since we were in Rome only for several hours because it was a stop on a cruise, we were running on a very tight schedule in order to get back to the boat on time. Tiqets did help us to get into the Colosseum relatively quickly, which is why I rated them with any stars at all. (However, they did also say that you couldn’t bring backpacks into the Colosseum and plenty of visitors had backpacks.)

Our reservation was for 1:15. The Tiqets agent said that we would have time to see the Colosseum and then we would meet up outside the Colosseum at 2:45 to head over to the Roman Forum together. We told the agent about our tight schedule and said we would need to head over sooner since we had a train to catch to go back to the port. She said that we could probably meet up with the earlier group to go in. We ended up getting to the meeting point at about 2:25, with no other group to be found. Then 2:45 came and went. Still no Tiqets agent. (And we had four people in our group, so we scouted out the whole area for any Tiqets representative; there was no one.) At 2:50, we called the company to ask where the representative was; the person who answered the phone said that the person must be on their way so just to wait a little longer. 3:00 came — and nothing. Finally, after waiting even longer, we gave up. We tried to enter the Roman Forum on our own with our Tiqets ticket and were told that we needed to be escorted by a Tiqets representative, as the “ticket” was actually a voucher and not a ticket at all. (Go figure — “Tiqets” only offers vouchers.) So, we never made it into the Roman Forum after all.

After getting back from our trip, we contacted Tiqets and explained the situation, asking for a refund since we didn’t get what we paid for and it was because of Tiqets’ error. The reply was that the agent was where they were supposed to be and we weren’t in the right spot. When I assured the “customer service” representative that we were in the correct meeting spot and that there were other people in our group who were also looking for the representative around 2:45, first I was told that we were in the wrong spot (we weren’t) and then I was told that we would not receive any refund because they didn’t hear a complaint from anyone else. Now, I can’t speak about anyone else’s experience or timetable — maybe people took more time in the Colosseum than we did — but what I do know is that we did not get what we paid for, and when I informed the company, they made no attempt to rectify the situation. “You should have called us,” they said. When I told them I had and to check their call logs, they simply ignored that and said they understand this situation is frustrating but cannot offer a refund. While I am not able to get my money back, I can at least let other people know about the situation so you don’t waste your time (and money) with this company. Either look for another company to skip the line at the Colosseum or just allow extra time to wait in the regular line. This frustration just isn’t worth it.

title: View from the past
text: What a great experience! Buy tickets online to avoid the line and bring sunglasses and you are ready to go! Guided tour is highly recommended but not essential.

title: Stunning!
text: Excellent landmark to visit. Absorb all the history while you're there. I highly recommend listening to some relevant audio whilst there to get a real feel for the place. If booking through a third party for a tour, be careful as the quality can differ hugely.

title: Thriling
text: The famous Colosseum where gladiators fought and died with honor. We took the metro and got off at Colosseo metro station and as you get out of the station, its right there. Must see when you are in Rome. There is Roman Forum, Vittoriano within walking distance.

title: Expect long queues or book your ticket in advance including the underground chambers
text:
It’s a must visit and there is a combined ticket with the Roman Forum. 
Fascinating history. There is a very good and well stock shop with lovely items for sale. Expect st Leary 2 hours there to visit it properly.

title: A must see....
text: Spend the extra money and take a skip the line, dungeon tour, well worth it from the bottom to the very top....spectacular sight given the age and history.

title: Wow
text: You really have to see this place to believe it. We had a guided tour and he was very informative. 100% recommend

title: Gladiator day!
text: This colosseum was absolutely amazing. The history behind it and what really went on inside. I recommend the pass the line tour with a guide. Well worth it. No waiting in line. She was a historian for Rome. Had great lessons and stories. Claudia was her name. There was a HUGE storm that rolled in which actually was kinda neat. The lightning and thunder almost sounded like you were there back in the day. I recommend good walking shoes and sunscreen as it is hot and not a lot of shade. Bring bottled water too. You can refill it inside at the fountains.

title: A "must do" when in rome - skip the line is the best
text: ALWAYS choose a "skip the line" tour. Lots to see here in not too long of a tour (perhaps an hour?).  You just HAVE to go inside and see as much as you can!

title: One incredible site to see
text: Before you go to the Colosseum, read about it’s construction, original uses, and history through time. Well presented and maintained. Second level has several historical displays and artifacts. Tickets are sold with start times to prevent overcrowding.

title: Amazing
text:
We were there on the summer. You must get your tickets in advance to skip the lines, it’s worth it. I would suggest I guided tour because you have so much information and so much to see there that you can become easily distracted. 
And have comfortable shoes, appropriate sun protection and water bottles. It’s really, really hot!

title: Average
text: I came here after me & my husband booked a long weekend away to Rome for my birthday & it’s always somewhere I wanted to visit. It is such a beautiful building & would definitely recommend to go see it at least once but wouldn’t return as prices are ridiculous & the people certainly put me off. I first got told when asked about prices that it would be 12 euros but would have to wait in a que for 2hours so I could go to vip & pay 25 euros instead & wait just half hour so decided on that but when I went to purchase my ticket they told me the next cheapest option would be 75 euros not 25 as vip u would have a tour guide with u so paid that, too then be told u only get to visit the two floors & if u wanted the full tour it would be over 100 euros so left it at the 75 euros. The guy who did the tour was ok but u didn’t get too see much at all & it was over pretty quickly so wouldn’t ever go again. Plus the Italian ppl are just so rude & ignorant, barging in to u & no apologies whatsoever!

title: Colosseum effort
text:
This place is a monument in ruins..... if you are interested in history then it is a site to be visited. Only a part of the original structure is intact and rest in ruins.... it is high time that the government rebuild the same to its original glory so that the visitors can get a better picture. 
During hot days you will be dog tired going around the open spaces.
Visit the underground if you manage to get the tickets as you can visualize the brutality of the past.

title: Haven't a clue about queues
text: The Colosseum is worth a visit, and it's good to go early to avoid queues, or maybe get your ticket beforehand. We had a long wait in a queue to buy tickets, and the Italians are not were organised when it comes to doing queues. Be wary of officially dressed men looking to sell you all kinds of queue jumping tickets. It's 12 euro for the Colosseum and the Forum, which is well worth it. Maybe go to the  Forum first for tickets as it tends to be quieter.

title: Family Holiday to Rome,
text:
Well this is all about ancient history. :-)
Just be careful about the 'water sellers' and African scammers selling rubbish.
Be strong and tell them to **** off.
Then enjoy the place.

title: Heart of Rome
text: Must see this place, get a skip the line tour and you won't regret it. Was so fascinating learning about the colleseum, and imagining what it was like once upon a time. It's a shame the Catholics stripped it all for their churches. What a marvel it would of been.

title: Magnificent
text: What can you say, it's an amazing structure. Lots of work and scaffolding going on that impinges on the experience a little but our guide had good stories to tell and made it a good visit. The steps were a bit tiring for my older companion; she was able to use the lift to the upper level thankfully. It was very hot - forgot my hat and regretted that! Lots of people yet things seemed to flow well. With the Forum and Palatine Hill it was a long but interesting 3hr visit to the area.

title: Must book online and enjoy!
text:
Ok. I did Rome twice in a year.
This is a must see place.
book The Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill in the same trip online.
But get a 24 hour ticket so you can visit the Colosseum on one morning or afternoon to spend the time listening to your Audio guide which comes with your ticket and enjoy the history.
The next day you can come back with the same ticket and do the 2-3 hour walk ( we wanted to enjoy this visit by walking, viewing and resting whilst we walked. There are areas you can stop to sit, rest, fountains with free drinking water so take a plastic bottle with you. You can perfectly enter with it full or empty ) around the Roman Forum and Palatine hill in ( must do...) comfortable walking footwear. It is hilly so be prepared to walk up and down the hilly areas.
This is an excursion for fit people. Not really for people pushing wheelchairs or strollers or the in-firmed due to the different surfaces of the walk. If you fit the category that I mentioned before, then you will have areas pf the forum and hill which may be complicated to climb.

title: Visit with family
text: It is a very nice experience and kids are free to explore this iconic symbol of Imperial Rome. So grateful to God the we have the chance to see one of the best tourist attractions.

title: Not to be missed!
text: This was my second trip to the Colosseum. It holds so much history and beauty, I don’t see how you can go to Rome without seeing it. I think that going with a tour is the best bet as It helps to have some explanations of what you are seeing and you get to go in without the rest of the crowds.

title: A must see!
text: Fantastic to visit.  Paid a little extra for a fast track ticket to avoid the queues which was brilliant. Audioguides are available in the Colosseum. Toilets are a bit small and big queues go before you visit to avoid a twenty minute plus wait.

title: Incredible and a Must See
text:
The Colosseum in Rome is a must-see. It's truly incredible to walk through the halls of where the Roman gladiators fought thousands of years ago, seeing the magnificent architecture and learning more about the history of the structure, as well as the people. It's just too amazing not to see. Make sure to bring good running shoes, as the stone surface will hurt your feet after walking on it for a long while.

I did the express skip the line tour from City Wonders led by Valerio. That saved me a good 1-2 hours of waiting in line. I do recommend taking this route.

title: Cheap parking available 4 min walk from colosseum
text: If you want cheap parking go to ‘Piazza Celimontana, 50 Parking’. It’s only a 4 minute walk. You must park in the blue parking bay if you want a cheap parking space. Sunday is free. For Monday to Saturday it’s €4 for 8 hours.

title: I was entertained!
text: My excitement at simply seeing the Colosseum when driving past was palpable but my experience visiting it topped that feeling. The sheer size and enormity of the structure and the knowledge that it has withstood centuries fills you with wonder. There are plenty of placards around explaining various elements of the structure and its history and i certainly learnt a lot in the concourse museum style areas as well. The fact that admission is limited means that it never really feels crowded but my recommendation would be to buy the skip the queue tickets online before you travel as we walked pretty much straight in whilst others melted in the heat whilst waiting. There is a lot of work taking place at the moment so some areas are not open to the public and i feel a visit in the future will be necessary so i can see the underneath areas too.

title: Mr
text: Okay for history lovers. Must book 2 weeks in advance to go into the floor. Very busy and alot of people. Lots of foreigners selling you stuff outside and conmen. You have to get a skip the line ticket

title: Incredible take
text: Especially if youre a history buff. I would not recommend the tour company we used they were not good.  Lots of restoration work going on and it was fascinating to see how things were originally built

title: Short visit
text:
This is a must on visiting Rome. Do not be put off by number of people. Book a tour I used Getmyguide and they were excellent.

This is the heart Rome

title: GO
text: Just go and see it for yourself.  You have to.  Its amazing.  Buy tix online to skip the queue.  Thieves hang around so keep your stuff near you.

title: Astonishing
text: I had a great time and I loved the tour so much. Better to take a tour than going on your own you will enjoy it more and you will learn things you didn't even know.Better to pre-book your tickets online to avoid queuing for too long though.

title: Amazing
text:
This is a must see! Take your time for the visit, don't rush it as you really want to se it all: outside, inside and below... Get a guided tour as you will have a lot of anecdotes and historical info that helps getting a complete idea of the place.
Better to prebook your tickets online to avoid queuing for too long though-

title: History of the colosseum
text:
The Colosseum is awesome because it makes you imagine what life was like and the seating for 50,000 spectators watching the gladiators. 

We joined the queue at 8:30 am but there was still a long queue which was about a one hour wait  to buy the tickets from the ticket office. We could only buy tickets for the colosseum as the tickets to the forum had sold out. Tickets were only €12 per person. We have been here before so it didn’t bother us but I suggest you buy tickets online to avoid disappointment. 

The Colosseum staff seem to be under resourced because there was very little assistance to manage the crowds and queues. It might be a good idea if management added ticket machines at the site as there are only two ticket offices. 

Also beware of scammers and scalpers that wander among the queues to sell tickets. 

If in Rome it’s worth a visit.

title: The Colosseum tour was great but the Forum tour, as part of this tour was absolutely amazing
text: We were lucky enough to have the same tour guide as for our Vatican tour - Fede. She is wonderful and so knowledgeable and her passion sweeps you up as she informs you of the history of the Colosseum, The Forum and Palatine. We learnt so much and the pace of the tour meant we had time to take everything in and ask questions. I cannot recommend this tour highly enough and we will definitely book tours this way again.

title: Fantastic
text: We visited the Colosseum last week. We avoided the skip the line and guided tour tickets and did it ourselves. We didn’t queue for very long but it was about 3pm. We then rented the audio tour which meant we could spend as long as we liked in places that were of real interest. Quite a lot of excavation work is still being carried out on the floor and it was fascinating to watch. It wasn’t crowded at this time at all so we thought it an ideal visit. No matter how many times we walked past it after our visit it was still an awesome building. It’s beautifully lit at night and surely no one could visit Rome and not visit the Colosseum.

title: Private Tour with Barbara De Rosa
text: Awesome experience and Barbara was great! We enjoyed this tour and highly recommend this tour! Truly amazing addition to our trip.

title: Go early if you can
text:
Very hot as we got into the Colosseo today. We are so glad we got the Roma pass & made a reservation for our entry time. It only took us a few minutes to get inside because of our preparation ahead of time. The lineup for the tickets was over an hour long. So we wandered around the Colosseo without a tour guide & it was fine. We had an afternoon entry which is why I think it was busy. If you can book an early entry on coopculture. 

We went up to the second floor where they were exhibiting some artifacts for the time of building. Going up the stairs will be hard for those with mobility problems. The steps on the stairs are quite big. There is a lift past the ticket office so be sure to use that if you're mobility challenged or just too tired to go up all those stairs. My thighs were burning after those stairs! LOL

To go to the lower levels of the colosseo you have to pay for a special ticket. It costs more than the regular admission fee. With your admission you can also go back another day to see the Palatine Hill & Roman Forum. The map of the grounds shows how big the place is so pace yourselves. I bought myself a T-shirt from the Colosseo gift shop. Cheesy but it's a nice shirt.

title: Amazing site
text: The one of the very few places that’s worth the trouble to standing line for hours. It’s a must see.

title: Take the Tour
text: It's worth the time to take a tour of this centerpiece jewel of Rome.  Marvel at the evolutions this landmark has made over the centuries.  The whole area around the Colosseum will keep you busy for hours if not days.  We stayed at a hotel blocks from it- The Lancelot.  I highly recommend this lovely hotel, too.  We also stayed at The Capo D'Africa Hotel.  Another highly recommended hotel.

title: History
text: We had a tour of the Colosseum by City Wonders tours.  Louise was very knowledgeable.  Beware of the men selling bottled waters outside, they use sewer water and keep freezing the bottles . . .

title: Colloseum tour
text:
Historical and not suitable for elderly with waliking or leg injuries 
Lots of construction and lots of people

title: Great historical site
text:
PLEASE buy your tickets before getting to Rome, since the line you'll have to wait in will waste most of your day. I purchased our tickets months before we went and were so glad we did.  They were "skip the line" tickets and so worth the little extra money and all the time we saved to explore more.  Sure there are hundreds of people who buy these tickets but you have to pick a time slot to get in and they only allow so many for each time slot.  Our wait was only about 1/2 hour by the time we were allowed in and went through the security.
The colosseum is very interesting but we didn't linger long as once you see it that's all there is to do, as well as take some pictures of course.  I probably would not go back again but it was definitely worth the time and money.  I have read some about what went on there and would like to learn more since I visited.

title: Incredible!!!!!
text: It’s just amazing, recommend buying tickets online, the line to buy tickets there looked like 2 hours long.

title: Impressive structure with huge tour groups
text: The structure and history are fascinating.  With a good tour guide it is well worth the visit.  Expect lines and crowds, but luckily it's big, so it holds a lot of tourists.  Make sure you get a good guide.

title: obvious but necessary
text: if you went to Rome and didn't go to the Colosseum... well go back to Rome and go to the Colosseum. is impressive even if you don't like history the way I do. the atmosphere and the architecture are unlike any other place.

title: Be sure to get tickets in advance or you will sunburn!
text: This is a must see attraction, but be sure to get tickets in advance and then get in the correct line to get in.  Otherwise you will wait forever and burn in the Italian sun!

title: History
text: Although huge part of the Coloseum is under reconstruction, still a must see once in Rome. Its the place where Gladiator was filmed. You cannot but have the chills...

title: Must See
text: If you live history you must visit. Take an audio tour or even better book a guide. It’s worth getting an insight and understanding of the history that took place there!

title: A sight to see
text: The colosseum is definitely up on my list of favorites. It’s a special place to see. I recommend getting there early because there is less people in the morning. You can go with or without a tour guide.

title: Colosseum
text: Amazing but so hot definitely need a big hat our guide was mindful to keep us in the shade but spoke too long then had to rush around to see everything before our next tour.

title: A Must See Classic
text: My family visited the Coliseum on a very warm late summer day. It was amazing to see and learn about this violent time in history. You will need to bring your own water and snacks as there are no vendors in or around the Coliseum. It is much larger on the inside than what it appears on the outside.

title: Will not recommend
text: The sales people were overly active and simply ruined much of the experience. They did not take no for an answer and became physical after a while. The very experience inside the colloseum was ok. Would not recommend this

title: Magic
text: The Colosseo is the most beautiful thing I’ve ever seen. It takes my breath away every time I see it. I highly recommend the tour inside, I went in the late afternoon and there was a perfect light for photos and not too crowded.

title: Good, very turistic of course
text: It was good. Extremely turistic  but what to expect I guess. Is a nice visit and I would recommend it to don’t with a guide otherwise you won’t understand anything

title: A must see because of the history.
text: A must see because of the history.  There is much walking on this tour.  We bought the ticket that included the underground.

title: Must see!
text: Absolutely beautiful piece of history, definitely recommend. It is crowded and there are people trying to sell you water and random crap but so worth it! Purchase a ticket in advance to skip the queue otherwise you could be standing there for hours!!

title: Impressive to look at
text: We took a night tour and while the colosseum was cool to look at, it was not a favorite of ours in Rome.  Think it would be fun to see with a strong storyteller who could make the inside “come alive” again.  Note:  Our tour guide told us about some controversy with the ownership of the group running the Colosseum.  She warned us about the employees not being very friendly- she was right.

title: a wonder in my mind
text:
a surreal experience, an excellent guide can make the experience of these ruins into a walk into ancient history. well preserved well projected the Colosseum is an amazing piece of history. 

its a walking tour and the only disadvantage is that physically challenged people cannot go up to its various levels. 

ensure you have a good camera and are equipped with sturdy shoes. the stairs can be killing on your knees but its well worth it all. carry water for sure.sunscreen and an umbrella is handy if you are visiting in the summer months.

mostly the visit takes about two to two and a half hours. if you wish to stay longer and possibly go to the center of the pit that has the trap doors for people to come up it may take you some more time. we spent half a day.

its a place to lose yourself and imagine the history of the bygone era.

title: Must go inside/upstairs
text: Definitely worth going upstairs as you will see and touch stones that are more than a thousand years old. It’s a great part of history. Will recommend going inside for sure

title: Amazing but the rubbish outside!!!!
text: You can't say any other but this is fantastic, but totally ruined with all the rubbish and plastic bottles on the grass outside, I mean there must be hundreds of bottles totally ruined the area.

title: Amazing antique amphitheatre
text:
The must visit place in rome.

Better you get your tickets online, so you can avoid the long queue. 

Get an audio-/videoguide (5€) for more info about it's history or better take a personal guide.

Very nice picture place at day times (but hot) and even more in the nighttimes.

title: A Must Do in Rome
text: We absolutely had to do this when in Rome and no regrets. What an amazing edifice to have been constructed in such early times - unbelievable - and to now be preserved and constantly maintained as it is. As we did our guided tour of the underground and heard the accounts of early activities in this magnificent venue we could almost feel the atmosphere of centuries ago and get a glimpse of what it must have been like to be part of the audience in this magnificent arena. Sadly, however, we missed the first 30 minutes of our prepaid tour, along with nine others, due to a Colosseum employee giving incorrect directions as we entered about where we were to wait. Hence the four star rating.

title: Beautiful
text: Nice ancient heritage. Must see when in Rome. Make a reservation if you can. Saves you time. Very beautiful and rich historic site. Not so busy.

title: When in Rome...
text: Of course when in Rome, you need to visit the Colosseum.  While it was an amazing experience to walk where the Romans once walked, it was crowded and you can't walk within the "floor" unless you have special reservations.  Glad I got to see it, but the Forum was much better.

title: Worth going
text: We did a private group tour for this and really glad we did. It’s monuments like this that you visit Rome for. I recommend going inside but at the very least visit the outside.

title: Amazing
text: Skip the line tickets is the way to go. Fantastic attraction, and a good gift shop. We visited on a hot sunny day, not much shade, but there is water fountains to fill empty bottles up. Definitely a must see.. was blown away but the architecture and size. Expect bag checks before entering. This attraction gets really busy so skip the line tickets is a must.

title: So Much History
text: Don't miss the Colosseum.  Skip the line if at all possible and ignore the street salesmen.  If you can, see this place with a knowledgeable guide to make sure you don't miss anything and don't rush your visit.

title: The first super stadium
text:
Did a skip the line tour, which was a little more, but the amount of people there and at the Vatican the next day they seem worth it.

Plus 700 TERRIBLE reviews out of over 140,000
About 1/2 of 1.

People wait till the last minute then get over charged. My favorite bad comment " it's a tourist Trap"

We are tourists, that is why we are here. To see something that is not in my home city. 
If you do not like "tourist traps", like the Vatican, Colloseum, Pantheon, Villa Borghese, then why travel or go to a place where no one is travelling to like Alaska in winter, or Siberia in, well anytime.

The forum and the surrounding areas are probably more interesting than the Colloseum.

An amazing structure built 1000 years ago, it is being refurbished now in sections.

title: Fascinating
text: Did a tour with Romaround Tours. Our guide was excellent, knowledgeable and entertaining. So old and such an engineering feat. Allow 1.5-2 hours for this

title: Busy !!
text: really busy.  the audio was €5.50 and to be honest - not sure it was worth it - get a guided tour instead.  The forum and palentine hill are pretty much in the same place - get a ticket that encompasses both.  take a water bottle - the water is free from drinking fountains round the city

title: Colosseum, Panatine Hill and Roman Forum.
text: The ticket giving access to the above is well worth the money. Be warned that although worth getting the fast pass, it only fast passes you for the Colosseum. You have to queue with the rest of the ticket holders to get into Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. It was dusty and wear good walking shoes.

title: Colloseum visit
text: This is an amazing place and well worth a visit. Be prepared to spend a good couple of hours and ladies go to the loo before your visit as the toilets are disgusting and you have to queue as there are only 4 working. This is the only thing that let it down for me and they certainly could improve it.

title: Better than art history class
text: Went on a tour w/ Francesca from ‘Together in Rome’ who majored in Renaissance art history @ University. Not only does she love the subject, she knows how to make it interesting so that you love it, too. She ties every thing together from Ancient Rome to more current art in such a manner that you walk away absolutely amazed.  Wish I had found her and ‘Together in Rome’ on the 1st day of my trip rather than the last, but now I have an excuse to come back.

title: Decent
text:
Great sites. Must do but it can ruin the wonder when the scaffolding is blocking most of the views. This is a given as restoration of the stonework and reinforcing parts is a constant thing.

Worth going regardless its truly a wonder.

title: Fantastic
text:
Really worthwhile visit being one of the best sites in Rome. The downside is the awful tours that crowd the place, making it impossible to see parts whilst there. 

Also, the long queues can be a nightmare, so I advise you definitely purchase a skip the line ticket before going. 

The Audioguide is also really insightful, and worth the price paid. It is available in multiple languages too, which is very useful.

title: The best €12 of the trip
text:
Just as amazing outside as inside it’s well worth the €12 entry. 

The day we went was incredibly hot and although there is water onsite from a few taps it’s worth taking some on with you just in case 

Really enjoyed the experience

title: Awesome! Go with a tour!
text:
We booked through Trip Advisor and used Tour in the City, skip the line. Well worth it.
Guide was excellent and attractive and knowledgeable. 
She made the the tour even better. 
The office was easy to find. We took an uber so no issues. We showed our online tickets...easy peasy. 
We used head sets and was pretty good clarity.
Will recommend this to all, especially the tour company! Well done!

title: Do a tour
text: Don’t just wonder around, take a guide or much will be lost on you. I went with “I love Rome”, the girl was very informative and had visual aids to show what it would have been like in its day.

title: HOLY WOW!
text:
I mean how can you not be blow away when you step inside. Rich in history, architecture, spirituality and so much more....a must see! 

Pics of it do NOT do justice you have to see and tour it yourself

title: Ancient
text: It was good that entrance tickets were already part of our tour otherwise entry would have been long. Security was tight. The collesium has a lot of history, is an interesting place to spend a couple of hours - take an audio tour to help with the story of the place.

title: Easy access and well organized
text: Have bought the tickets online is specially gratifying for this one. You get in easily and free to get to know each part of it.

title: Great But Lacking Imagination
text: It’s ok. I feel they could do more to give you a sense of what the it was like. The security guards staring at us like we were going to do something wrong is a little off putting. Bit intrusive.

title: Historical
text: A great place to visit and feel how it was when the gladiators ruled the building.  Even though there are renovations ongoing you still get a feel of history. When you climb up the stairs or climb down, the staircase is steep and worn out from the many years of people climbing up or down.  It is well maintained and with the skip the line options we got in rather quickly

title: Crikey!
text:
Extraordinary place, it history you feel here is staggering but a very different life and culture.
Take the audio tour - it definitely gives more life and history to the place

title: Just amazing
text:
We visited with a tour and thoroughly enjoyed the experience on the gladiators arena. The queues are long so taking a skip the line tour is the way to get in with no hassle. The history is mind blowing and just being in the colosseum is special.
Be aware of the street sellers outside especially the ones who give you a “free” bracelet and tell you that because of the colour of their skin the Italians do not speak to them. They then tell you about their children and show you a picture of them , they will then ask for a bit of money for the kids and try to give you more bracelets. Do not be fooled, they are all over Rome telling the same story . Just keep your wits about you and enjoy the history

title: One of my favorite sites in Rome
text:
I have been to the Colosseum twice. Once I did a smalll group tour. Most recently I did the audio tour.  I found the audio tour very comprehensive and easy to use. I prefer the audio tour as I could take my time, repeat sections, and not be in the herd. I could step off into an open space and wait for the crowd to go by. The pace was more to my liking. 

Tickets:  purchase then in advance or buy them from the Forum ticket office which doesn’t have the long Colosseum ticket lines. The best time to get to the Forum ticket office is 8:30, they open at 9.  Or at 2pm, but it is typically warm in the Colosseum at this time. 

There are bathrooms in the Colosseum.  After you go past the 12 and 13 windows for the audio guides, there are restrooms (WC), on your right.  These are the only ones in the Colosseum for the public. 

There is so much to see in Rome but the Colosseum continues to be one of my favorite places. Plan to spend 1.5 to 2.5 hours ones you are in the Colosseum. Bring water, as it can be warm.  

I don’t think this is a great activity for children under 12 unless they find Roman ruins interesting.

title: Old giant in Rome.
text: It is really worth while to buy tickets in advance or reserve a time slot with the Roma pass. We could skip a huge line by doing that.

title: Impeessive
text: We loved our visit, and it was definitely something special to see. One heads up, the queue was insane, and we went in off peak season. Queue jump is advised, or get there early!

title: Collesium
text: A must see ! An ancient stadium that still maintains the aura of a present day live event. One of only a few places I have been that gave me the chills !

title: A Must See
text: This is a must see sight. If you're able to, view this site with a tour to get more details and skip the line.

title: Great tour
text: If you’re in the area definitely visit, it does get crowded so I recommend getting the tickets with a tour group the lines get very very long if you go on your own.

title: Wow
text: The place is huge and it only took 8 years to build, using slave labor. It is easy to imagine it filled with screaming people, watching the spectacle and horror below in the arena. Under renovation now, but still spectacular. A must see when in Rome. Went as part of a tour, so no waiting in line.

title: Beautiful and scary
text:
We visit Colosseum with a guide and heard a lot of stories about the old days in Rome.
Colosseum is a beautiful building and the visit was the climax of our two days in Rome. It is a must see and I think it is great to come there with a guide.

title: History saved by shear size
text: The Coliseum is a must see on any visit to Rome.  About half of it is still relatively intact.  The remainder was quarried and used throughout Rome to build the newer attractions, but it is well worth the visit.

title: A must stop
text: A must stop when in Rome. I find the best way to appreciate it is to simply take it in from a far as you walk along the route.

title: Historical Ruins
text: This is a must visit site when in Rome. Unbelievable how this old structure is still a magnificent place to see. So many visitors it was worth paying the fee to skip the line. Got some really great pictures and had time to get a thorough view of what was once a place of Rulers and Gladiators.

title: Incredible piece of history
text: The Colosseum is an attraction you simply can’t miss when visiting Rome. It may seem out of the way but it was once the city centre of Ancient Rome. I recommend you buy tickets online as they often close to people without tickets as it can get incredibly busy with people without tickets queuing for 2-3 hours. The Headout tour with the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum is the best one I have found, I have been on it a few times and the guides are always very knowledgable and entertaining.

title: Wow
text:
In the heart of Rome, huge Roman building which gives you a feeling of the Roman Empire strength!
Very impressive. Located next to the Colosseo metro station and next to the Roman forum.

title: Amazing .
text: If your in Rome the Colosseum is a must.  Amazing history and facts. Book a group tour early in the am to beat the queue and crowds. Gobsmacked

title: Speechless
text: One of the most ancient places on earth to visit, lots of history absorb while there, even though it was very hot the day we were there, it was well worth it. Do not stand in line, buy your ticket on line and skip the long line ups

title: Incredible!
text: It was incredible to walk on stones that people have walked for thousands of years.  Long history, many different uses over the years.

title: Coliseum arena tour
text: Booked this tour and really pleased we did, you get taken through the back entrance thus skipping the queues, you then get taken into the arena, so experience standing where the Gladiators did all those years ago. We also had a tour guide who was very knowledgable, so we learnt a lot about history. Definitely recommend.

title: Colloseum
text: Absolutely amazing and well worth the trip though for first timers, like us, a good tour guide is a must.. Ours was very knowledgeable and informative (Walks Of Italy) and made the day visit much more enjoyable as you learn about the greater area and history.. Our experience was worth every cent!!

title: A must see in Rome.
text: The Colosseum is one of the many must see sights in Rome. Like most of them though, it is extremely crowded so be patient.

title: Great place to visit, just don’t use Tiqets
text: A great albeit one of most touristy place to visit in Rome. Because of the latter and huge line to go in/out, do book/buy angry tickets in advance, including skip the line (which will save you loads of time!). We found out that a lot of operators offer combo & skip the line tickets, so do take the opportunity! Only thing to mention - DO NOT buy tickets from TIQETS! We had awful experience with them when bought combo Colosseum skip the line & Roman forum/Palatine hill. For 32 euro per person, we expected everything to go nicely... but little did we know! While we got in & out of Colosseum ok, the Tiqets guide didn’t show up to get us into Roman forum at stated time & place! With a few other group members, we tried calling the guide multiple times (didn’t pick up!) and waited for almost an hour for her to show up! Tiqets Customer Service couldn’t solve the problem at spot (we spent additional money on all those calls while were standing outside of Colosseum!) and  frustrated, we had to buy additional tickets on the spot. :( The result - ruined mood, double paid money for tickets... :(

title: VIP Colossium Tour
text: Our host David (with City Walk) was very good, very knowledge, answered our questions and was great for the 3.5 hour tour.  Highly recommend.

title: Must see
text: Great attraction as buy ticket online as only need to buy the colosseum ticket as the other site across the road was not that great. Loved the colosseum as it is amazing and not to be missed when visiting.

title: Outside
text: The colosseum is beautiful outside. It looks exactly as i thought it would look - big, old and intimidating. Inside was a big let down - it’s just ruins, and not very nice looking ones. Not worth the money to go inside.

title: Very busy, get the fast pass and book early.
text: Was a great visit, just make sure you pre purchase tickets to avoid waiting in the sun for hours. We pre booked our tickets and were inside within 10 mins, was very busy inside, you have to fight for a photo opportunity so if you're tall this help.

title: We did both the night time tour and day time
text: This is so interesting!! Doesn’t feel like a tour. At the day we had a German girl that had moved to Italy a few years ago , we wish we remembered her name? she spoke so charismatically that we we’re transfixed!! We could have listed to her all day! She was brilliant and so interesting.

title: The iconic symbol of Rome
text: Ruins of ancient colosseums can be found in many parts of Europe and even in North Africa - an important legacy of Imperial Rome. This one in Rome, I believe, must be the largest of them all - capable of seating over 50 000 people when full. Considering that it was built in the first century, you cannot help being amazed and awed by the expertise of the ancient Romans. Though substantially ruined in many parts, the structure is still very much an architectural beauty, which deserves to be visited and appreciated. It is history in visible form.

title: Worth It
text: A very busy attraction but worth visiting  worth a guided tour to get a real feel for its history. Easy to get too via Metro

title: So So So impressive
text: I took the three hour guided tour and every minute of it was interesting, I would definately pay for guide or you wouldn't relise just how great a building it is

title: Impressive
text:
The Colosseum is just such an impressive landmark. We visited 5 years ago and purchased an audio guide which I would thoroughly recommend. The history of this place just gives you such an insight into the past and sends shivers down your spine with the events that occurred there. We loved it so much then, we went back again.
Make sure to prebook to skip the queue and also go early to miss 5he crowds that accumulate during the day. There is a scanner security check so don’t take backpacks or any other metal items as you won’t get through.

title: Not to be missed
text: A sight to behold.  With online tickets, it doesn’t take long to get through lines/security.  We went on a Saturday afternoon at 1.30pm and had no problems.  Very busy  but still plenty of room to move about.  A remarkable piece of history.

title: Amazing spot
text: Get there early but get there!  A must see particularly if you can avoid the crowds. The head phones are helpful.

title: Guided tour
text: Excellent tour with an excellent Canadian tour guide. Unfortunately I forget her name but she was excellent. The tour was great very interesting. Would highly recommend

title: More impressive outside but still you have to go....
text: I also found the spectacle of the Colosseum both from a distance and up close to be amazing, but once inside it felt "as expected." Maybe I have seen too many pictures of the inside. Still, one has to go! The regular entrance tickets were sold out so we ipted for the relatively inexpensive Colosseum run tour. It was short and fine. Perhaps if we had done the night-time tour or the underground tour, it would have been more amazing, but that was just too much money for me. What I DID enjoy unexpectedly were the exhibits around the edges so take time for those. About 1 hour is good.

title: Awesome
text: Anyone visiting Rome must surely visit The Colosseum right? We used an organised tour guide and it was definitely worth it as it skips all the queues. It’s amazing to think Gladiators trod the earth here!

title: History
text: Lines are long as expected. Buy your ticket in advance. It’s a historic spot and everyone wants to be there.

title: Beautiful
text: Beautiful by day.....Exquisite by night! Worth going to see it twice! Duding the day and lighted up at night!

title: Typical
text: It is totally what you would expect. Did a night tour and was forced to watch a lame video. Leave before the video.

title: First Sports Arena of its size
text: It's amazing to think of the scope and grandeur of this arena in its time.  Still remarkably intact - notwithstanding the pilfering of its marble slabs after the plaque and an earthquake.  Grim but fascinating.

title: Go early
text: The lines are long but more importantly no one seems to get moving early even when this s the coolest part of the day. Take advantage of the cool morning as the colosseum opens at 830am and the lines are not that bad early. By the time it is 2pm the lines are crazy long. Well worth a visit and get the audio guide as the info is incredible. Info I have never heard before.

title: Collosseum
text: We took a golf cart tour immediately upon arriving to Rome. It was a great way to see all the significant monuments throughout Rome and get a lay of what surrounded us. Our tour guide, Sylvia, was very personable and along with pointed out the history surrounding us, she also pointed out great bakeries, pizza and places to shop along the way along with trivia and interesting facts about the people and contrasts amongst different areas of Italy.

title: Reserve a time!
text:
Do yourself a favor and get a Rome pass and then reserve a time online.  You will bypass a very long line to get in for only 2 euros extra (beyond the price of the Rome pass).  We opted for an audio tour for 5.5 euros apiece.  Much cheaper than a guided tour.  
The site itself is an engineering marvel.  However, it is short on displays and information.  For example a reconstructed model would have been nice.  But maybe not possible with several 1000 visitors a day.  
Use the bathrooms ahead of time.  Very long line.

title: Amazing sight!
text: Do not miss out on visiting the colosseum! Get fast track tickets so you don’t have to wait in a long queue. It’s an amazing sight inside and out and make sure you see it at night too! This was our 3rd trip to Rome and we never tire of seeing this iconic building!

title: Trip to Italy
text: The Colosseum is enormous and so much history.  Lots of walking and people around.  We even saw the last cat that lives there.

title: Walks of Italy
text: Skip the line using walks of Italy was the best ! People waited in line for hours to get in on their own, you get what you pay for , we went with a group of 15 , had time to take pictures and Luigi our guide was very knowledgeable! Awesome !

title: Go during the day
text: Just finished the night tour with underground and now have to go back during the day to see the bloody thing. Yes the underground was cool but not an extra $200 cool. The movie you watch is.....I’m not even sure what was going on with it.

title: The best!
text: We always enjoy visiting the Colosseum. They are working on the outside area around it so had limited view in areas.

title: Imposing and beautiful. Well worth s visit.
text: You must visit The Colosseum.  An organised tour will explain the history of this imposing structure.  The vast structure is fascinating in how it was built and why and for whom.  It’s different uses over the years etc; a very rich history.   We were recommended to do the tour that included the underground, but except for the mechanics of an ancient lift and the drainage system  this part was a bit boring.

title: Colliseum at night
text: We opted for the night tour when its closed as we heard how busy the place gets. Thank goodness we did as it was soo hot...Its incredibly beautiful at night...

title: Does what it says on the tin
text:
We got a skip the cue ticket with out a guide. 
The company met us at a meeting point and took us into the colosseum via the bottom level. We got about 10 minutes to view the colosseum from the bottom
Level and then went up to the first and second floors. Lots of water fountains to fill up water bottles. 
The size is impressive and it is well organised Inside, on level 2 they currently have an exhibition which told you interesting facts. Getting up and back down to level 2 had steep steps. 
We had to rush abit as we had to get back to the meeting point for roman forum, so had to leave the colosseum.

title: It's absolutely awe inspiring.
text: its a must to visit, obviously. Absolutely book your tickets in advance. We didn't, and we thought the queue was moving quickly. After 40 minutes in the queue we thought we were nearly there, but it was only the queue for security, there was a whole new queue after that - in all, we queued for longer than we were in.  BOOK!

title: Spectacular
text: You're not expecting it to be so big.  It has to be seen in person as pictures don't do it justice. When your guide gives you the history of the place it's all the more impressive

title: Amazing landmark!!
text:
We booked the guided tour with skip-the-line since we were told how long the wait could be, and we are so glad we did. No waiting at the entrance, and the guide knew exactly where to go and give us insight on the rich history of the massive Colosseum. She even knew the spots for the best picture! 
Colosseum is no doubt one of the must-see in Rome, preferably with the guide of some sort. You cannot possibly know all the meanings of the different markers, holes, and etc.

title: Spectacular
text:
A definite must-see on any visit to Rome. As with most attractions in this city, it is super busy, but still worth it. The scale and architecture of this arena is just impressive

A few tips:
-Take a guided tour if possible and estimate around 4 hours for this at least
-Tour the Coliseum, Palatine Hill and Roman forum together. It will provide a good historical context (e.g. Julius Caesar's tomb, original marble tiles and the spot of land where Rome actually originated)
-Wear comfortable shoes and keep hydrated. The Palatine hill is a bit of a trek, especially if its a hot summer day.
-The underground caverns of the Coliseum were disappointing. Most of it is being restored and we did not find the extra cost worth it.

title: The Colosseum
text: Unbelievable to see something so old that has endured the test of time. Truly a wonder of the world.

title: Vacation
text: Wow so impressive and worth the trip. But plan ahead. Go early and get your tickets ahead of time. The crowds are huge!

title: Worth taking the guided tour
text: We tried booking an unguided tour and then one with an audio guide - there was no availability, so we went for the guided tour and we were lucky to do so! Our guide was knowledgeable and make this experience so much more better. Also, make sure you pre-book your tickets, as the queues are superbly long - whereas the ones for pre-booked visitors feel like fast-track VIP ones as you just pass right through all of the unnecessary queueing

title: Outstanding! A must see!
text: Out of Rome’s many must-sees, this is definitely in the top three. Recommendations: book a tour, preferably small-group (we did through Airbnb), go as early as possible, specially if it’s going to be a hot and sunny day and use comfortable shoes. Do NOT go on Sunday. Visit includes Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill. Need a minimum of 3 hours.

title: Amazing place-tour booking essential
text: We booked a tour some time in advance (party of 12 friends) what can I say- amazing place....so much history and a thought provoking tour guide really made some of the viewing points come alive. I’d definitely recommend a tour-we bypassed a huge line of people who had over an hour wait. Also, I don’t think you could possibly understand the workings of the coliseum without someone explaining things all the way round. The tour took over three hours in 30 degree heat-it wasn’t for the feint hearted but I feel we’d have missed so many things had we looked around without our amazing tour guide. One of the most spectacular places I’ve been to-if you come to Rome it’s a must!

title: Pretty cool
text: After visiting the Vatican, which I thought was very boring (no offense), this was pretty awesome. It speaks for itself, really.

title: Colosseum: Great!
text: The Colloseum is a great sight to see, however it was extremely crowded when we arrived. Crowds don’t bother me too much; but the construction nearby, and the amount of people trying to sell you stuff and attempt to put items in your hand is just too much. I’d recommend this place around the evenings instead of the day otherwise be prepared to deal with everyone and their mothers along with 30+ people trying to sell you cheap items for an outrageous price

title: Night Visit Zero Crowds
text: We had the chance to circled the Colosseum at night after dinner and it was a great romantic experience and a great way to avoid the crowds.

title: Wow,  Its more magnificent when you really  see it in person
text: I have seen photos, videos, of this impressive structure but to be actually there seeing its magnificence , size, history and marvel at the construction.I could  manage some of the steep stairs, but because of my age I was able to take an elevator down, which would have been difficult.The  guide was great and very supportive  of our needs, Her knowledge of this structure and the Paliatine Hills really helped to understand all the history.

title: Seeing this is a must.
text: You must see the Colosseum when your in Rome. It is spectacular and definitely a must see when in Rome. If you can take advantage of a skip the line ticket you will save a lot of waiting time.

title: Trip
text:
Nice expirience but, it is a part of history. So thats dont need a rewiew.

But advice for you, get in there early.

title: Good preservation of history
text:
This place is best visited with audio guide until you are too much into history.

We did this by audio guide and enjoyed walking around and listening to that history at our pace.

This place is very busy so recommend prebooking but don’t get there early as they will only let you in only 10 minutes before your time.

Good photo spot too.

Remember to wear flats and carry a hat or umbrella for sure.

title: WOW
text: Pre booked a trip with Viatour to Skip the line to get in. Well worth it as the queues were long. We had a party of circa 20 people and our guide was Donald. Excellent guide and very knowledgeable on all things Colosseum. Once inside your guided tour last about 1 hour then your free to wander the upper areas of this amazing structure. Well worth a visit and definitely a highlight when in Rome

title: Impressive building though much work done & underway
text: This iconic building is certainly impressive to see up close and especially from the interior. As would be expected for such an old building it is in a pretty sorry state of repair and actually on looking closely you will find that much of it is no longer get there, with the bits remaining often heavily amended over time. This is only to say that it may not meet people’s expectations from movies or edited photographs and postcards. It is undoubtably an immense human accomplishment with a significant historical story to tell. The ticket to access this attraction also gains access to the forum and palatine hill across the square.

title: Wow
