text: Seen lots of pictures film clips but to see it in the flesh is amazing we didn’t do it but inside tour would be very good

title: Beautiful Tourist Trap
text: The Colosseum is beautiful, but it’s bogged down with huge crowds and scam artists. Beware anyone trying to talk to you because they at best want to sell you something, they want to make it seem like they’re friendly and want to give you a free bracelet, or they just want to take your wallet. Definitely do a skip the line ticket. The colosseum itself is wonderful, but try to go in the early morning to avoid crowds.

title: An incredible place
text:
Definitely the highlight of our visit to Rome. What an incredible place. Absolutely staggering. I have seen it so often on television but I was not prepared for the sheer size and scale of the Colosseum. I took my breath away. 

Our tour did not include entry to the Colosseum but seeing it from the outside confirmed that it is deservedly a wonder of the world.

title: A must see
text: Lines are long so purchase your tickets in advance from reputable site. You'll have a wide array of 'peddlars' trying to sell you tickets as you walk up. Much easier to be prepared.

title: A classic destination though a bit overrated
text: Everyone knows what Colosseum is, I will say this: by the time you've waited in line for some hours it doesn't seem quite up to snuff. I mean, it is a ruin. It is impressive but each floor is pretty much the same and, honestly, the best parts of the Colosseum are on the outside where they 're free to view.

title: Worth it!
text: Go with a small group with an educated guide. Worth every penny spent. Make sure you spend time there understanding the history and purpose, don’t rush.

title: Must see!
text: With tickets in hand, we found ourselves surrounded in awe. This is an amazing site to see, truly a must see. Words can’t describe it and photos can’t either. Many people dream about seeing this place, but you get the chance, it won’t disappoint. I would recommend a tour with advance tickets. This is a very busy place, however we felt safe the entire time.

title: Very Long Queues
text: We went early and didn't buy pre paid tickets. Chose also not to do a guided tour but it took nearly 2 hours to get through to the start and it was so packed it was hard to see anything. We took a audio set for 5 euros and it had only 6 stations on it for information. Underwhelmed would be my feeling....maybe better in the middle of winter, but it felt badly organised and chaotic and perhaps a bit unsafe with crowd control too.

title: Historic
text: Amazing structure, but best to take a tour to get the full history and also access to places you can't otherwise go. Make sure you take a hat, water, sunscreen and wear walking shoes, as it is on uneven surfaces.

title: Left Behind by Guide
text: Barbara was a good guide but she left us behind in the middle of the tour. I couldn't get down the stairs fast enough to keep up with the group because I'm disabled, and she walked off and left us. We had headsets and recd a call from the office threatening to keep my driver license if we didn't return their headsets. It's a big place and we walked there from their office. We got lost coming back. Tried calling but a rude man wouldn't help us. Use another company. Go early to avoid the crowds.

title: Underground and upper floors private tour
text: Very cool. I recommend getting a private tour of the underground and upper levels (best views) and skip the lines.

title: Brilliant day and wonderful views
text: Five of us went, we had skip the queue tickets, which is a great idea as it was a hot day. Met our guide who took us through security. They say no large bags but standard backpacks are fine. You can walk up as high as you like or legs will carry you. Great pictures to be had

title: Amazing
text:
It is 100% worth doing a tour that does the underground and top floor. If you come here, do the full experience.

If you go in summer it is very hot and prepare to be harassed by people selling things outside the entry.

title: Get a Guide!
text: This place is amazing and you really need a guide to whisk you around the lines and provide the history of the place.  Be prepared for crowds. I mean crowds.  Without a guide you will be wasting a lot of time.

title: Strollers/Prams Dont Do It
text:
Pictures dont do it justice, absolutely spectacular and leaves you breathless thinking of the history that has taken place inside.
We have a two year old child which we brought along with us in a carrier thankfully as we learnt from the previous day at the Palentine Hill/Roman Forum that the ground and stairs around the collosseum aren't suitable for a stroller pram.

title: What a great place
text: The history here is unbelievable!  It is extremely busy but has to be seen to be believed, well worth a visit.

title: Amazing Tour!
text: We truly enjoyed our tour with Musement Tours with our tour guide Alessandro who is very knowledgeable! We highly recommend this tour!

title: Impressive Colosseum
text: Worth pre-booking to a avoid the queue. We took a guided tour which was about an hour. No queue other than the security check. Really enjoyed had good tour guide and learned a lot about the period and the history of the structure!

title: Beautiful!
text: As we expected, the colosseum was amazing! So beautiful and lots of history. I would definitely recommend going with a guide or a tour as there are basically no plaques or signs with information which was annoying. We wondered around and read the Wikipedia page for more info. We went at 845am and waited to get in for probably 1 hour.

title: Beautiful historic place a must seen
text: So many things to see but unfortunately they are doing some refurbishment at the moment. We booked a tour with a guide however they just dropped us there and let us find out thinks for ourselves. Best think would be go with one tour guide company that explains you the whole story of you want to know more about this historical place.

title: Great Tour - Get your Guide Company!!
text: We had the first tour done with John Paul with the Colosseum and he was wonderful.  Then the Roman forum which was amazing was with Brian and he took us all over the place.  Great tours with these guys

title: A little overrated
text: When in Rome- you MUST go to the colosseum. I mean it was OK- and interesting- waiting in line for maybe 1.5 hours was worth it- only because it costed 2euros to get in (EU resident between 18 and 25). There was about a billion people inside- AND construction was being done- so we were a little dissappointed. But I guess its just one of those things! Now I can say been there, done that. Won't go again. But- it was interesting to see!

title: Impressive monument
text: By visiting this monument you can discover the secret of Ancient Rome, reservation of ticket is highly recommended to avoid a long lineup , Do not miss the Roman Forum which is very close to the site.

title: Too many touters
text:
Rome has become the city full of annoying touters. Street sellers are relentless at the Colosseum. I was lucky to have first been here 30 years ago. Way before the long lines, obsessive selfies, and annoying touters. 

It is a great building full of amazing history. Probably best to organise tickets or tour prior.

title: An unguided tour
text: We were straight of the plane and decided to stretch our legs at 8:30am before the crowds built up and it got to hot. Very interesting place. I would recommend getting a guided tour so that your visit is more informative.

title: Second time and still amazed!
text: When we booked we only had italian as an option from the official website. (Underground and top level)  Word is scalpers by up all the English tours. The group was not overly large. More than half were non Italian speaking customers on the tour. Our guide could speak english but choose not to speak anything other that Italian accept when she wanted our attention to move forward or to follow. I understand we booked a different language but  they need to review the process better. Because we did not speak Italian were could not even be provided with the head set.(just incase we understood a little) we have been before so it was not bad without a guided commentary. We new this coming into the tour so we would not have to wait in line and skip the very long lines ... no wasting valuable  time! The Colosseum it self will not disappoint you.

title: Unmissable
text:
We had sighted the Colosseum from a distance the day before we visited it, and at the time I thought that would be enough, but it isn't. Don't be fooled into thinking that you don't need to go in and have a look around. It is so much bigger than you think when you are standing beside it.
Our son had paid for tickets on line to enter at 5.30 pm - this was quite good as the heat of the day (around 33), had begun to cool down. It also meant that we did not have to stand in a long queue waiting - we collected the tickets and walked right past the waiting crowds. It is spectacular - but when one considers the gruesome nature of some of what was acted out there, it kind of brings a sober note to the visit.  Personally I think that a trip to Rome without visiting the Colosseum is unthinkable, but be warned that a 9 year old boy, who was with us, found it boring and let us know - maybe a bit young to appreciate it.

title: Book the underground tour
text: Definitely worth a visit but do the underground tour with dungeon access. We booked this tour and it was absolutely brilliant enter through the gladiators gate out onto the arena floor, down to the underground dungeon area and then finish off with all the upper tiers access and then roman forum entrance after that.

title: When In Rome!
text: What a spectacular monument! This is definitely a must! There is so much to learn about Italian culture, and this is a highlight. I highly suggest getting there early if you can. The line to get in is long. If you’re able to, purchase the fast track. It will provide you with a quicker access into the colosseum.

title: Colosseum tickets and lines
text: Didn’t pre book until day before and realised all the skip the line tickets either were sold out or marked up to ridiculously high prices. Lines were very long at Colosseum but not at the forum or hill so we lined up, got our tickets for the 3 sites(€12) and the lady gave us the time 12.15 and said go to Colosseum then and skip straight to security check! Except when we went there they said sorry everyone that isn’t in a tour has to line up and we waited another hour anyway. By the time we went in and looked out there was no line... Colosseum has free water to fill up so bring a bottle and be ready for some amazing history!

title: Wow
text: Loved it - worth getting a cab to drop you off and run around the whole thing if you are in a rush. But it was fabulous to see

title: The Gladiators
text:
Nice historical place. Too busy, the line the day we visited the Colosseum was long. 
Once inside you go back through history...and you remember the film The gladiator,my favorite one

title: Amazing place, just mind blowing
text: Very good value, kid are free. Massive venue but very busy. Get there early to avoid queues! We bought a ticket that got us into The Roman forum, Palestine hill & the colosseum for €12 per adult and my daughter was free

title: A Must see on anyone's list
text: Skip the line tickets are a must. We were very please to prebook as it saved well in excess of an hour standing in the blazing sun putting up with people pestering you to buy "Tat".. that's the ranting over !!!!  The Colosseum itself was awe inspiring  and absolutely fascinating with a very interesting exhibition inside. .... my advice is .... Go and do it.

title: Wow
text:
Wow, just as expected. It is a must visit place... 
construction around it is the most annoying part. But worth visiting

title: Night tour
text:
I went on a night tour with likely the worst tour guide I've ever had. Weird to think a guide could ruin the Coliseum experience, but he managed. 

Aside from his constant meandering monologue, being at the Coliseum is extraordinary and at night, bellisimo!

I think you can go on your own and have an even better time.I know I wish I had.

title: Somewhat disappointing
text: My first time to Rome and was somewhat disappointed in this and some other attractions in that there was so much scaffolding and construction equipment around that it totally distracted from your reason for wanting to see it, felt more like being in a construction site.  Very unfortunate.

title: Roma🇮🇹
text: The eternal city: Roma, the capital of the Roman Empire. A lot of attractions and monuments, the colosseum is the top.

title: End of day is time to go!
text:
We went on a Sunday in mid-September, around 90 minutes before it closed (last entry is 60 minutes prior). There was a ~10 minute line to buy tickets after you went through security, and then we were in - much to our surprise, it wasn't crowded at all. They claim they allow a max of 3,000 at one time; I would estimate there were fewer than 750.

I got the audio/video tour (the video part was useless - clips of Hollywood movies - I think there was an audio only version which would be just as well) and my husband downloaded the free tour from the free Rick Steves app (highly recommend this) and listened on his phone. For cost and quality, I'd recommend the Rick Steves.

title: Visited with walking tour
text: The Colosseum was as gorgeous as you’d think it be! I wouldn’t wait around to get a ticket in the queues outside as they are rather ludicrous in size when you can get a walking tour for a good price and with fantastic added detail.

title: Booked tour
text:
Day tour was very interesting.lasted about 3 hours.
We went late August 2019.
Weather was still very warm. 
I would recommend going as early as you can in the morning and take an umbrella to shade yourself from the sun as there is no shade.
Our guide Como was fantastic and very knowledgeable.

title: Colosseum by moonlight
text: We went on a colosseum at night tour and was the smartest decision we made. No crowds or heat of day! Thoroughly enjoyed Tour guide Halina before we went into the colosseum as the tour guide changes after you enter colosseum. Full moon was out that night and made for a beautiful evening.

title: Long waiting for average experience
text:
We did not purchase the ticket in advance and we waited for at least one and half hour to buy the ticket. Actually to be honest, there weren't that much to see.
I do believe that you may as well just take the photos from the outside. 
That's just my personal view.

title: 6:30am sunrise was amazing
text:
We were awake and on the go early and decided to see the colosseum at sunrise and it was spectacular! Not a heap of crowds either and a small little cafe up the hill to relax with afterwards.

You can’t go into the colosseum that early but it’s a much better time to get your photos.

title: HIstory Geek's Dream!
text:
We visited the Colosseum on my birthday in June 2019 and it was absolutely fantastic. I love history and I have never been anywhere that has been as incredible as here.  They have done a great job at maintaining the site. What I think they have managed to do really well is incorporate the tourist/learning side of things whilst still keeping it as clear as they can. 

We went for the first session of the day (08:30) and we're glad we did. By booking we didn't have to queue for tickets and when we entered the arena it was nearly empty which made it so much more special. By the time we had finished the first floor it was getting very busy and by the time we left it was packed out. I can imagine the magic fades when you're surrounded by thousands of other people.

title: Mini Trip
text:
Amazing place, but the Colosseum can be very busy in the high season. Depending on how long you want to spend at the Colosseum, although fairly busy, it's best going late afternoon around four o'clock. 

If you intend visiting the Forum too, buy a super ticket when buying your tickets to the Colosseum. This allows entry into the Colosseum, Forum and, the museum on Palatine Hill which are well worth the visit.

Again, the Forum will get busy, so get to the entry gate eary on the morning.

There is a lot of walking involved, so good walking shoes, or sandals would be beneficial. Take a water bottle with you, there are free fill-up points around the site.

title: Be careful which ticket line you wait in!
text:
The experience of being in the Colosseum was amazing however staff could be nicer and more organized.  When getting into the ticket line there are NO VISIBLE signes over the 2 designated lines.  Stood in the left line as both right & left looks equal in size.  Waited over an hour to get to the from of the lone to see a VERY SMALL sign at waist heigt for both lines saying "pre-purchased ticketes" and the right was "needing to purchase tickets"  was then made to get to the back of the right line. Waited over 2 hours.  Once in the covered part of the tix line waiting to purchase the staff was beyond rude to those in the line about the wait times.  
Its best to but the "skip the line" tickets for sure!!bLesson learned the hard way

title: Its the Colosseum! Awesome, amazing
text: one of the best sites- so amazing. Please wear sneakers or low shoes as there is tons of walking, and it is slippery.

title: Colosseum - Short Review
text: What can be said about the Colosseum that hasn't been said? Only advices I can give you are pay the additional cost to get your online tickets because the queue can be very long - and if they reach the limit of people inside brace yourself for a long wait. Also wear comfortable shoes and I suggest pay no attention to the all "army" of people selling stuff at the area.

title: Roma
text:
Impressive city with its monuments and history
Needs more cleaning
Noise is terrible and the amount of tourists
More cleaning

title: A must do
text: The history is just amazing and the engineering beyond belief for the build date. If you are in Roma and don’t visit I would be shocked

title: Rather a lot of scaffolding
text: Sadly it is hard to see beyond the works but still amazing, would love to see it in it's entirety but a must see anyway, and we understand that it needs work.

title: Lovely
text: Had a great tour of the Colosseo. Learned a lot of information about the construction and the period in which it was built.

title: Gladiator's arena
text: Colloseum is one of the must see in Rome. Not only from the outside but also from the inside. Tickets bought by the official site are not expensive, and they are also valid for the palatino hill and forum Romanum. Colloseum itself is filled with history, impressive how People thousands years ago could achieve such a engineering genius. You must see it!

title: A must see!
text: Just an amazing structure.   This is a must see if you go to Rome.  I suggest you take a tour and that you go early, no reason to wait in lines.

title: Queues
text: A sight to behold, so intricate and amazing.  If you don't get a queue busting ticket be prepared to queue for approx one hour +.  We for there just after 9 am and queued but it is monitored.  A fee of €12 to gain entry and you can get onto the upper level and look in.

title: Not sure.
text:
Booked a skip the line tour which was a good idea, this takes you to the arena floor and on a platform.
There is lots of work going on to retain the walls high up, these are covered in scaffolding and on the area floor there is work taking place below ground so the entire area is covered in scaffold again.
I think with hindsight I would have kept the mystery of the arena and stayed outside where it is much more impressive.

title: Visit to the Colosseum
text:
My husband, daughter and myself arrived at 8.30am without tickets. It was unclear exactly where to line up to buy tickets. We lost time trying to get help from someone to explain where to line up. Once we found the line it was relatively quick to get into the Colloseum. It was an awesome experience. 
Our tickets also covered the forum and ancient ruins adjacent to the sites. You need to allow at least 3 to 4 hours to walk around. We did not have a guide but enjoyed it immensely.

title: Amazing landmark
text: You cannot not have your breath taken away when you see the colosseum. Every time I visit Rome I delight in seeing it. The queues are hideous so definitely get a queue jumping ticket but it’s worth it. Even just walking around the outside it amazing (and free!).

title: Breathtaking
text: When I visited Rome in 2016, I unfortunately didn't have a chance to see this place from the inside, but even the outside of it was breathtaking. Definitely worth visiting.

title: Don't miss it!
text: I bought a guided tour with the skip the line, and it was well worth it. Very beautiful place, do not miss something so magnificent.

title: Be sure to book enough time!
text: Amazing views but be sure to book enough time and to bring water as it can get very hot very quick! I also highly recommend getting yourself a snack before you go in at a nearby cafe.

title: Coliseum
text: We visited the coliseum late in the afternoon. At 4.30pm there was almost no queue, walking up to the entrance for a bag check, and then less than a 5min wait to buy tickets. We were able to wander around without the need to rush photo shoots. Definitely worth visiting without huge queues, crowds & when it's cooler too! I think that last entry is an hour before sunset, but worth checking.

title: Magnificent at night  outside!
text: Toured with Rolling Rome on a golf cart...Awesome experience. Then went for 3hr tour next day with a guide from Air BnB and not so awesome !  Spent far too much time in Caesars Palace and Dungeon, ( which was good) but didn't get to Colosseum until just before our 3hrs was up and we had another appointment :(   so only got to see 15 mins before having to leave!

title: Arena floor and Level 1 visit plus Roman Forum
text: Amazing historical sites enhanced by The Roman Guy's tour. There was a meeting in the arena which restricted the visit somewhat.

title: Very busy
text: I last came here some years ago and must admit it was a lot busier today than last. It seems that money has taken over from what the experience is...too long to wait, luckily we got a outside tour guide which meant it was more expensive but at least we got to beat the rest.  Great to see but......

title: Big bold Rome
text:
Number one attraction in Rome.
Brought to life by a bit of imagination and a great tour guide from Walks of Italy. We paid a bit more to visit the underground section where the gladiators and animals were held. Well worth it

title: Great tour!
text: Our guide was very informative and extremely likeable.  The guided tour is the way to go for the Colosseum.  We had a great time!

title: A must see when you visit Rome
text: The place of course is quite busy. You should avoid to visit during weekend. We were there but did not go inside. If you want to go inside you need to go through guided tour and reserve beforehand

title: Taxi
text: We only went around by cab he was brilliant charge 15 euros from our hotelwhich was only a 15 minute walk from Vatican City

title: Absolutely breathtaking
text: My first visit to this iconic attraction and I was not disappointed. Also great that it is on the bus route so no need for taxi.

title: Full of history; worth the visit with a guide
text: Our guide gave us some fascinating history of both the building (and network of hallways and rooms) and the life and times of a gladiator.  Coupling both was a fantastic mixture of information that brought the Colosseum to life.  Now when I look at the building, it brings up many images from how the gladiators fought as well the as political reasons for building such a facility in the first place.  Go in September and make sure to get a "skip the line" ticket.  It took all of 5 min to get in from the time we arrived.  Without such a ticket, we were told it was a 3 hr wait in line to get tickets and enter.

title: Always great and a must do!
text: We did visit the colosseum before, but this remains a must see in Rome. Loved it again and was again impressed by it. Also good to see that further restoration is ongoing and that there will be more and more to see and to discover. We booked a ticket online end of the day and it was a bit less busy than during the day.in about 90 minutes you can see it all and shout some great pics.

title: Amazing
text: Must see without hesitation. So much history, amazing feeling to see in side the Colosseum. To think up 70,000 people watching the games and events taking place.  Would highly recommend.

title: Horrible organisation
text: I made a reservation in time so I will not stay and wait hours at the entrance and when i arrived there no one from staff really knew English or wanted to help me. I was shocked by this, The Colosseum is beautiful but the horrible staff and the workers on the last level really pissed me off.

Booked the walking trip of rome through TripAdvisor and glad we did! The tour guide was amazing and as an archaeologist knew her stuff! Part of the tour was the colosseum. There isn't much I can write that hasn't already been said but I would recommend a tour as there is very limited information at the site.

title: Beautiful. A ‘must see’ location.
text: I don’t think you could come to Rome without visiting the beauty of the Colosseum. It’s easily accessible by public bus or metro. I would highly highly recommend booking tickets beforehand because the waiting line for those that hadn’t looked exceptionally long on a really hot day. It can be crowded and there are many tour groups passing through but this is to be expected. A wonderful experience.

title: Lovely piece of Roman history
text: Weather was hot, the crowds huge but was a wonderful experience. Highly recommend buying skip the line tickets. Glad I didn’t have to wait in the long queue in the heat.

title: Underground and Upper Levels
text: Group tour to the Colosseum. Pay the extra money for the underground and upper levels-much better views for photos. The only downside is all of the construction and restoration. The scaffolding really takes away from the experience.

title: Amazing piece of history
text: Amazing when you realise how old it is, for somewhere nearly 2,000 years. I didn't go inside unfortunately but its amazing to see!

title: It's the Colosseum! It would be amazing if it was built today!
text: Let alone in 70 AD! This was my fourth or fifth trip to see it and every time I see things I missed the time before and I'll probably go back again!

title: Colosseum
text: One the best thing I did was buy a ticket to skip the line. You can be hours just to buy the ticket I could live without the tour guide. I like to do  things on my own. To much talk that make no sense.

title: Shame!
text:
This is an amazing piece of history. It is well worth a visit; it's absolutely stunning. The views across the Forum are spectacular.
However, numbers should be limited further, it is almost impossible to move in the place. Signage is poor and many parts are closed. 
There is a single, small Portakabin housing the only toilets: the stench coming from them is terrible, there are clearly not enough for the thousands of visitors (my guess would be about 5 cubicles) and the queue for the loo was ridiculous. 
It is worth noting that you can't walk all the way round in a circle due to some areas being closed. This makes for an awful long walk to try to get out as the directional signs did not take this into account.
For those who find walking difficult, there are lots and lots of steep stairs and a single lift, so be prepared.
It's a shame, this is an iconic piece of history and I should imagine that virtually everyone who visits Rome comes to see The Colosseum. It should therefore be far better organised to give a much more user friendly experience, particularly, when one takes into consideration the money they must be taking in admission fees!

title: This is a most see, magnificent!
text: We bought a guided tour with skip the line and it was totally worth it, with it we got access to the arena area were others don't.

title: Breathtaking
text: As you round the corner or turn up a street and the Colosseum appears you suck your breath in as it is such an amazing piece of history right before your eyes.  As you get closer and take in the whole structure there are not words to describe it.  We took a tour of it at night as the pictures showed it lit up in the inside but this is not the case.  It is interesting to see it at night but I would imagine a day tour would be better.  We did see the underground which was unreal!!!  Always purchase skip the line tickets ahead of time as the crowds are crazy.

title: Wonderful experience
text: We were surprised when we arrived and were told that entry today was free of charge! Although there were quite a lot of people,  it was an incredible experience and we were able to walk around freely.

title: Rome in a day...Walk away tour
text:
EXCELLENT.  All inclusive... Coliseum, Historic Rome, and Vatican. 

Personalized small group tour.   Includes best Gelato in Rome. 

Tour guide Marcos was excellent and knowledgeable.

title: Again amazing visual history and the guide made the difference.. Giuliano

Totally worth my visit, a space to walk in order to make you an idea of the magnitude that reached the Roman Empire, a mandatory place to visit!! 
I was very excited when I entered the Gladiator’s Gate’, the entrance gladiators themselves used thousands of years ago. If you ever decide to visit the Colosseum, be sure to visit the museum dedicated to Eros (Greek god of love) located on the upper floor of the outer wall of the building.

title: Don’t miss this historic site in Rome, Italy!

title: Iconic symbol of Rome and Gladiators
text: Probably the most iconic symbol of Rome, the Colosseum is a cant miss stop when visiting Rome. Getting tickets is an absolute must; don’t just view this architectural marvel from the outside.

title: Fantastic tour and educational experience
text: We didn't book tickets in advance and when we arrived the Colosseum the line wrapped around the one side of it. We were approached by a man who offered skip the line guided tour, tickets were $30ea. We knew he was probably a scalper, we took a chance anyway. He led us to another area, where we were put in small groups and given a guide. As it turned out, we really enjoyed the tour guide, he was excellent. Yes we paid over retail for the tickets and skip the line, but it was WELL worth it. The history of the Colosseum is very interesting, while the ruins are not a lot to look at, the stories and history is a must. Do take this tour, and definitely pay more for Skip the Line for any tour you go on - you will not regret this. Enjoy!

title: A must see if you go to Rome
text: Take a small group guided tour of the Colosseum underground. You are in the shade and you get the experience of what the gladiators went through. You get to skip the crowds and its worth it. Our guide said that the Colosseum will set a record number of visitors in 2019 at 9 million. Go in the morning you will be glad you did. You next get to see Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. There will be a lot of walking and steps to climb so wear very comfortable shoes. You just have to go to see the Colosseum if you go to Rome...you will be glad you did.

title: Amazing - we did the 3 hour tour, so worth it! Book in advance (even to enter on your own)
text: Amazing to see in person. We did the 3 hour tour of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum. booked through the official Colosseum site. The options were 3 hours or 45 minutes, and we felt 45 minutes would be rushed, and we knew we wanted a guide otherwise we wouldn't know everything we were looking at. The 3 hour option was excellent. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable, and we learned a lot of cool facts we wouldn't have learned without a guide. The three hours flew by. Recommend doing this! Definitely book in advance, no matter what option you do to avoid waiting in line. Tours book up fast, too.

title: Brilliant
text: Do yourself a favour and go. So much history. Get in a tour do both night an day. So much I never knew.

title: Clairebear2323

title: Worth to see
text:

title: Iconic place
text:
Enjoyed our visit to the colloseum, though if you visit the Colloseum after the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill, you are going to be hot, tired and hungry, so if you can break up the trips it would be a better experience.
Amazing place to see, shame they don't have VR like Circo Massimo but very enjoyable and iconic place to visit.

title: please talk about around
text: yes colosseum is a very big and stunning building. the ruins around the colosseum carry much more architectural importance. some funny people wearing roman clothes for taking picture with is unnecessary.

title: amazing
text: Amazing to see in person. We did this on our own but would have been nice to have a guide. Ticket booths were slow.

title: Colossal!
text: Simply amazing, awe-inspiring and BUSY!!! Go early or, as we did, lose the crowds and turn up at 17.00. They close at 19.00 but you have enough time and no shuffling!

title: Crowded and amazing
text: It’s the Colesseum - so it is amazing.  Its incredible to see this history and hear the stories. I recommend getting a private guide and entering through the ‘VIP’ entrance to save time and get a better tour experience.

title: Collosus Colleseum Spectacular
text:
Sensationally good August September weather in Rome means walking around inside the Colleseum gives you the ability to really emerse yourself in the feelings of the goings on many hundreds of years ago. 
From walking around the outside to entry its a sensational piece of structure. I advise strongly purchase tickets on line either in Rome or before you leave home as the ques can be horendously long, I'm talking over 1.5hrs. We were on a tour group and while we did pay more for the entry tour groups get you vip entry to skip the ques, that I would definitely advise you get to maximize your day time seeing the sites as opposed to wasting hours waiting to see them. 

With restoration work well underway rest assured none of this prevents you seeing the architecture. Typical of a stadium, there is little to no wind inside, so when it's 36 outside it's up around 42 inside so a water bottle, sunscreen, a large brimmed hat is a good move. Naturally there is a gift and refreshments shop where we found a great 24page A4 size book Ancient Rome showing images of the current structure alongside beautifully created images in the day, well worth €8 as a coffee table talking point back home.
There is an elevator for those that steps are not an option and once up the structure you can virtually walk all the way around to take in the sights and it's really awe inspiring. 
Even though it wasn't used as a holiday resort in its day, you feel privileged to be able to walk in and around it today. 
I'm summary book on line (tour groups skip the ques) allow 2hrs (once inside), take water, if it's hot sunscreen a hat and of course a good camera that takes HD video and panarama wide screen shots.

title: Visit to Coloseum
text: Very interesting visit. Enjoyed it in spite of rains.Reminds of so much of history and tales from so many movies. It is good that Italy is conserving its historical treasures in exemplary manner.

title: Busy but fantastic
text: There is no getting away from the crowds and one of the worlds most popular tourists sites, buy it ia must do. The entrance is well controlled to ensure as little crowding as possable and that and having an excellant guide who not only gave an excellant insight into the venues but ensured we kept away from other groups if at all possable. The venue is fantastic and i would go again but i felt the tour guide helped to make the tour around the arena and the forum very informative.

title: Amazing
text: This was my second visit to the Colosseum and it was amazing. This is a must-see stop when in Rome. If you can get a guide that can give you the history and background, it is worth it. Try to buy skip the line tickets because lines are long during peak season and while you will still have to wait with a skip ticket, it will be quicker than the regular line.

title: Unpopular opinion
text: It sucks. It really does. Or at least it did for us. Me and my girlfriend waited for hours (after we got a “skip the line” ticket) just to see 10 000 people walking in circle (literally) taking pictures of metal.They were renovating the colosseum so there was nothing to see, it was all covered in metal and there were actual construction workers with their power drills walking around. We literally payed €45 to go in and get out 2 minutes later. Do yourself a favor and go to the Vatican.

title: Magnificent and dont pay for skip the line
text:
The second time we visited the coloseum. First time in 2010 and nog again in 2019. Still one of the top must do's in Rome.

What we did in 2010 and again in 2019 is: DONT BUY your skip the line tikeckts AND DONT WAIT IN LINE at the colloseum to buy your entrance tickets. Buy your coloseum tickets + tickets forum romanum at the forum romanum ticketbooth. The tickets include coloseum and forum romanum. There were 8 peoples in front of us at the ticket boot vs 200+ peoples in front of the coloseum. Tip: buy your tickets + audio guide at the forum romanum ticket boot. The quickest way to skip the big line.

title: Incredible.
text: It's always been high on my list of "must see" one day, and I finally got there.  Considering how long ago this was built, the Romans were very advanced with regards to engineering and being forward thinking, and attention to detail.  Such a pity we couldn't see it as it was when it was completed, it must have been magnificent.   I would recommend (as we were) that you go on a group tour, the crowds are horrendous and the groups skip all the queues, which is essential in that heat, and you get the full running history from your guide. Brilliant.  Groups are very easy to book and worth every cent.  Enjoy.

title: Colloseum
text: We did not pre-book tickets, waired two hours to get in, but so did all the other queus. The staff are not very friendly, but I suppose security is more important. They are busy renovating some of the places, so we could not see everything. I am a bit disappointed with the shop, there is not much to buy except books. The tourists are very helpful and everyone gets a chance to see everything and take pictures.

title: Skip the line Colosseum including palatine Hill and Forum
text: Booked through Viator.com and tickets came through from a company called Dream Tourism. Badly named. Initially good communication re change of meeting point, and emphasising to arrive by 9am for the 9.30am tour. But office was locked and when we rung, told to arrive at 9.15, at which point we were put on a sofa and told to wait. Operator constantly on phone. Out time slot elapsed and eventually we were taken to a general meeting point where we were linked up with a guide. I am glad the other lady was found a Spanish speaking group, everyone else in our group was recruited off the street and when we numbered twelve we were given to a guide and had to queue for approximately 1 1/2 hours in hot sun, being passed by offical tours in the express queue. photo shows the empty queues used by official operators. We eventually cleared security at 11.45 (for a 9.30 tour) and everybody including the guide had lost enthusiasm. We got an abbreviated 15 min tour and our guide (who would not give us his name) was off, leaving us to look for another guide (Jill) at 1pm. She collected a number of waifs and strays from other groups and gave us a good tour of the Palatine hill. All most unprofessional, loved the sights but all I could say is don’t book through Viator. In fairness the trip we booked with them and was assigned to City wonders the day before was fantastic, Christian was a wonderful guide, full of passion for art and we loved it.

title: Rome
text: The Colosseum was amazingly. We bought skip the line tickets and were treated to a wonderful guided tour of an amazing historic iconic place. It’s hard to believe that 2000 years ago mankind could build something like that. Not just the Colosseum but the Forum and many other structures. Be prepared for a lot of walking and stairs but well with it.

title: The most iconic and beauty in the world !

This monument is the most iconic, painful and sad of all time.

A place to go!

title: Well worth a visit
text: Amazing place I would recommend visiting with a tour guide to maximise your visiting experience and allow you to hear all history, this can be done alongside the forum and pallentine hill.

title: You just have to go
text: Maura was our guide and made the trip enjoyable.
