title: Magnificent historical ruin
text:
The Colosseum in Rome, Italy, is one of the Seven Wonders of the World. Having said that you would expect it to be five star! Well the history is there and parts of it looks magnificent but going there, or rather getting into the Colosseum is rather problematic. 
There is a lot of work going on outside the Colosseum, this means finding ticket offices or even knowing where to queue to get into the Colosseum is very confusing. You can prebook a ticket online but you have to do this at least a week before you arrive. You can queue at the ticket office opposite the Colosseum and get a ticket for a specific time either that day or the next, then you join the queue to the left of the gate that enters the Colosseum. Or, you can queue at the gate to the right of the entrance to Colosseum for general entry when you work your way to the front of the eternally long queue. It is all very confusing - especially as you are confronted but scammer after scammer trying to sell fake tickets. 
Inside the Colosseum there are so many renovations and restorations occurring that the colosseum is shrouded in scaffolding. Having said all this, the Colosseum is still a magnificent sight to behold.

title: All hail the mighty Romans!
text: It goes without saying the Colosseum is a spectacular spectacle to behold. The architecture the stories the whole experience is fantastic, we had an Air BnB experience tour which was great and needed as there is a lot to see and you can walk past some key areas if you don’t know what you’re looking at. A must for anyone in Rome!

title: Made our whole vacation!
text: The Colosseum is massive and amazing!  Unreal that this majestic arena is still standing since it was built in 72 AD!  Definitely a must see on everyone's bucket list!

title: Night tour - amazing
text: DO NOT VISIT DURING THE DAY we were a small organized night tour...1 of only 3 tours I booked during our 4 weeks in Italy- and it was amazing. We only saw 2 other small groups on the whole visit...had the colosseum almost to ourselves. Worth every cent

title: Largest Amphitheater
text: Firstly, you’ve got to start your day off bright and early because sleeping in doesn’t exist when you’re traveling. It’s always a good idea to start off with one of two things, wherever it will be hottest (and Rome gets HOT) or wherever it will be most crowded, and the Colosseum meets all of the criteria.

title: Must do attraction in Rome
text:
We spent half a day here with I love Rome tours. They were great and the guide was one of the best we have ever had. She was brilliant with here knowledge and passion for Ancient Roman times was excellent.
We did all three sites here and they were all unmissable  attractions of Rome.  The que for the Coloseum was over an hour long but our group of 25 people gain entry in under 10mins. This was the best attraction I have ever done.

title: Made in Rome Tours
text: The tour itself is excellent and booking a guided tour of this kind is an absolute must to avoid the queues and to cover three sites in one excursion - Colosseum, Palatine Hill and the Forum.  However, our party was much larger than we had anticipated and although our guide spoke English adequately, he was often difficult to follow and  although attentive and inviting questions he was prone to ramble a bit.  Several of our group confessed to switching off for periods of time.  Having to use ear pieces throughout  didn't help - although necessary it meant that greater care was needed to provide clearer explanations and more information than we received.  That said, our guide was really nice, and the overall experience was good - I would rate it 3.5 if I could!

title: Amazingly grandiose
text: This was the attraction I wanted to visit most during my vacation in Rome. We purchased a guided tour that allowed us to skip the line. Definitely worth the price of 19 Euros per person. We had a beautiful sunny, warm day to visit this site. The ticket also included visit of the Forum and Palatine Hills. You can easily spend a day visiting all 3 sites if time permits.

title: A must see in Roma
text: To go to Rome, you need to visit the Colosseum at least once. It’s like the most iconic building in Rome. You really need to do this with a tour guide who can tell you about the history of this structure. To think of the ingenuity used to do some of the modern things we have today is amazing. Our guide mentioned they are doing further work that will make the experience of visiting even better so will probably do it again.

title: Colosseum in Rome
text: Had a great tour and of the Colosseum with the Roma Guy tour company. Would highly recommend using them.

title: Great place to visit
text: Fascinating place to visit.  Amazing architecture and history.  We also visited the forum and Palatine hill as part of our visit which was stunning.

title: Colosseum/Palatine Hill/Forum tour
text: I throughly enjoyed the tours. My tour guides, Maura and a British gentleman, were very knowledgeable and friendly.

title: Really quite amazing
text: We only had one day to walk around and explore this amazing site. Amazing to see but sad it was not more well preserved. A must see for anyone visiting Rome

title: Coliseum and Forum Tour
text: Amil Bello was our guide for the Colosseum and Forum and was outstanding.  She was very attentive to everyone's needs before the tour began.  Making sure radios were working for everyone and whatever questions there were, she answered precisely.  She was very patient throughout the tour and was very very easy to understand.  Her explanations of everything were spot on.  She was very intelligent and knew the details of the sites we were experiencing.  She made everything come to life and we would be happy to have her on any tour, especially an art tour.  She did an amazing job.  Keep doing what your doing Amil.  Your the best.

title: inspiring
text: paid extra for skip the line well worth it as the queues are large. Our guide was superb and the tour really was very interesting a must see & do when in Rome

title: Great History
text: Well worth a visit. Pity about the long queue but ultimately worth the wait to see. Got an audio tour which was a good way of finding out about colosseum

title: Awesome place
text:
It is so unbelievable to be in a place like this. Having seen and heard so much about it did not disappoint.
Recommend a visit and book a skip the line tour to avoid long queue to get in

title: A must see in Roma
text: This was my 2nd visit to Rome and the Colosseum but it had been 30 years since then so I decided to go again. I took a tour because I like hearing the information from the guide and I didn't have to wait in line. It was just as wonderful this time although if I hadn't been on the tour I would have spent more time there. We got to go on the floor level which I don't think was that big of a deal. The main advantage is that it wasn't as crowded. The Colosseum seemed cleaner and in better condition than I remember the first time. It's an iconic landmark and a must see when you're in Rome.

title: Lived up to my expectations, wonderful experience.
text: Would definitely recommend a guided tour; with so much background information given on its construction and the shows put on, it brought the Colosseum to life. Our guide was very knowledgeable, describing the building of the Colosseum, gladiators schools, the work behind the scenes on show days and the hierarchy regarding the audience. A bloody part of Roman history but I felt it paid homage to those people and animals cruelly killed there. Definitely worth a visit.

title: Ticked a box
text: I'm glad I have visited but it didn't blow my mind. The biggest let down with my experience was the guys that constantly bug you for stuff... The staff and police should send them packing. If you go try to ignore them and don't buy or accept anything from them. Buy your tickets from the staff only. It's really busy so give yourself plenty of time.

title: A must see
text: Sadly Rome grew around the Colosseum, so it's a permanent construction site as it gets hurt by the permanent traffic around. Bit anyways: you must have seen it

title: Coopculture Underground & Belvedere Tour
text:
If you can get a ticket, the Coopculture Underground and Belvedere tour is well worth doing. It gives you access to the area under the arena and to part of the third level of the Colosseum.

On the day of your tour, take your ticket that you have printed out and go to the Stern Gate entrance about 10mins before the time listed on your ticket.  Show your ticket to one of the staff manning the entrance.  They will give you a sticker with your entry time and meeting point printed on it (my entry time differed by about 15mins from that listed on my paper ticket).  Put this on and hang around the same meeting point until you are let in.  Pass through security and you will be pointed to the meeting place where you wait for your tour guide.  

There were about 20 people on my tour but you all get headsets so hearing the guide isn’t an issue.

Once the guided tour is over, you are free to explore the general access areas on your own.  Your ticket also gives you access to the Forum/Palatine either on the same day or the next day (which ever you prefer). 

It can get hot in the Colosseum so make sure you bring a water bottle and hat.  There are water taps where you can refill your bottle for free inside.

title: Well worth the trip
text: The lines are long and in tourist season it is packed, but most certainly a must see place to visit. Hard to imagine and wish we had more time to spend.

title: The best experience ever
text: We arranged to join in with a group tour from outside the entrance with romaround tours and had a good  experience. Our tour guides were animated and knowledgeable. It was a Sunday and very busy - and there were too many people in our group (22 rather than the upper limit of 20) which meant we were not allowed through the fast track entry. This annoyed several group members as we ended up waiting for about 45 minutes to enter the Colosseum. In the afternoon we met another guide who took us though the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. This was massively enjoyable due to the guide...a very animated Romanian who has lived in Italy for approximately 17 years and speaks brilliant english. An experience not to miss if you visit Rome.

title: Our visit 26 September 2019
text: Undoubtedly a fabulous and breathtaking experience. We did the Colosseum along with the Roman Forum and certainly not disappointed. There is a lift for less abled which allows for access to the upper level. If you’re going to visit, take plenty of fluids with you as there’s none available at the site and it’s hot. You won’t be disappointed

title: Poor management by colosseum
text: Today we paid for skip the line tours of the colosseum. On arrival with our tour it was announced that there were too many people in there and the gates to the public closed. We had to wait an hour to enter. This is far from skip the line. Even City Wonders tours were shocked that so many tickets could be sold on a day when they know how many people are allowed in at one time. Very disappointed as your was then rushed to finish on time.

title: Loved this place
text: We went on an early morning Gladiator tour. Taking you on the gladiator level including the regular level also. Everything is so amazing. To think how old everything is and to learn the history along the ages. Plenty you don't know about this place. Enjoy!

title: Great place to visit
text:
It was great to see the ancient building where the gladiators used to fight.
There are also lots of information pieces on the walls so you can learn more about the venue.

title: Do not miss if you are in Rome
text: This is a must see.  Absolutely amazing that this was built almost 2,000 years ago and still stands today.  But advance tickets and use a guide if you can.  Improves the experience immensely.

title: Definitely take a skip the Q tour
text: Anyways if you’ve seen Gladiator that’s he finished product. It’s still a massive structure. Like a lot of ruins it’s a bit of a oncer. Most tour bind it up with a walk around Palintine Hill and Forum making it a bit of a walk around in the hot sun.

title: Absolute must visit. The essence of Roman Rome.
text:
A symbolic emblem of Rome across the world this was our number one visit.
The history, architecture and intimate details made our visit fascinating.
Worthwhile arranging skip the line, but not worth a guide.

title: A Must visit
text: By taking an on/off bus you can see most attractions.  Its reasonable and they have headphones which explain everything your viewing in many languages.  Enjoyable ride especially when the weather is perfect.  Ride on the top for a better view & fresh air.

title: Very Historic!
text: The Tour went on for 4 Hours, to avoid and skip the long cue you need to book online, it cost much but it saves your time. Very Historic and lovely place. Our tour guide is perfectly nice, her accent is a bit strong though. We manage to go till the top bottom. It’s very interesting place to go visit.

title: Great excursion
text: The history and stories that go along with tour is a must see.The structure just to think how it was built and designed is amazing and the games that were played there with the animals and gladiators

title: A must
text:
This is a must visit Roman attraction. It’s just incredible. 
Book your tickets in advance to avoid the queues. We did ours on the official website and for €14 we got a designated time skip the line entry to this AND the Palatine/Roman forum next door which is also brilliant. 
I personally don’t think you need the specialist tours - just make your own way round at your own pace. There are great displays to give you extra historical detail and context. 
Be mindful of the many touts outside who ambush you as soon as you arrive!

title: i loved colosseum to viist every time !
text: really i loved to visit Colosseum every time when im around in Rome . its amazing masterpiece around the world. i missed it when i was inside , i feel the gladiators and there life style .

title: Great tour
text: Experienced the best tour package. Good explanation and the guide is extremely considerate. As our family is lucky that the weather is not hot, the cool breeze in the colosseum helps sleeping baby... Pick a day that is not sunny.. not much shade hence could be stuffy and hot which could make all experience bad..

title: Impressive, on my birthday 23 september the visit was for free!
text: Also impressed all those pictures shown with the theme "peace" made by children from all around the world.

title: Coliseum tour
text: Fabulous tour, Matej Polverini(Matt) was extremely knowledgeable and provided great detail and commentary throughout the tour. Well worth your time.

title: An amazing building
text: Book direct with Colosseum it's the cheapest, and tours can miss things so make sure you go to the very top and below ground. Their architect guides are knowledgeable and interesting but beyond the monumental scale, the Colosseum is a bit sad looking, perhaps not the spectacular stadium you might expect.

title: Great
text: Fantastic and stunning inside and out. Reccomend a guided tour to bring the place to life and to skip queues

title: A historical place made glamorous by movies
text: The history of Roman empire ,gladiators, huge space to seat 50000 people has been made glamorous by modern movies. A view from outside and historical perspective is sufficient for a small trip.

title: Mr
text: A must see if you are in Rome.  Impressive even from the outside. Found a nice restraunt hidden on the hill beside the collesium. Great service surprisingly reasonable price

title: Impressive
text:
We visited the Colosseum at 16:00 and bought our tickets on line thus avoiding the queues. Unlike the vatican museums there was plenty of space inside to see the attraction and was well worth the visit. Impressive from the outside and the inside.
Would have liked to see it lit at night but ran out of time, theres always next time.

title: How can you not rate it 5
text: Spend a few minutes imagining the spectacles that took place here and the effort it must have taken to build given the primitive tools available at the time.  So much Roman history is wrapped up in this one place  - you have to visit

title: Awesome
text: If you are into history, you will love this place.  Take the guides tour as they will explain everything.

title: Private Tour
text: We splurged for a private tour and were able to skip the line. It was worth every penny. The lines are extremely long so prepare ahead of time.

title: Amazing
text: Amazing we don’t normally do the tours but was so worth it learnt loads. Make sure you book in advance.

title: Hot day walking tour
text:
Plan accordingly, this tour allows water bottles to be carried, and a few fountain stops.  

Our visit was hot, weather, no breeze.  We arrived near the Arch of Constantine, with easily a thousand other people. 

Downside: tons of peddlers on the streets, selling, water, toys, and they constantly try to sell you something.

Tour was good, guide was knowledgeable, headsets worked.  Placed in the American speaking tour.  Tour size, when booked was to be 30 people max, I believe there was 40-50people in our tour, a little too big.

Palatine, Roman Forum, and finally the Colosseum ( which we had to wait in line, due to that they limit the number of visitors to 1000 at a time. Only when people leave do they allow more people in.  Of and there is a metal detector to go through when entering into the Colosseum.

Bring: water bottle, small bags only and wear comfy shoes, as the trail is rocky.

title: Colosseum tour
text: Great tour with guide Barbara (blue eyes)  she was so knowledgeable and enthusiastic.  It’s a long tour but well worth it.

title: Bucket list place!!!
text: Please pay the extra for the guided tour. Everything I hope for out of the trip. The history was rich and i loved it.

title: Better than expected; prepare for crowd
text: With restoration, this was somewhat more difficult to navigate but it was still a highlight.  Lots of good views of surrounding sites, too.  Prepare for the throngs of tourists, but don't miss this.

title: Wow amazing place
text: Great visit but super crowded on a Sunday and the security to enter made it worse as it slowed down the lines. Lots to see and really impressive very much worth a visit.

title: Touring the Colosseum
text: Visiting the Colosseum is a must. Do your research before your visit and refresh your memory about the history of this marvelous landmark. We’ve all seen the pictures but seeing it in person is truly remarkable.

title: Colloseum Visit at Colloseo
text: As a family, alone, friends, or couple Colosseo allows diving into ancient Roma history , I recommend tale a tour and if it is possible have the fast pass by Omnia Card.. depends on you and your organization and the time

title: Absolutely amazing
text: Hard to believe this was built in only 8 years, given the lack of cranes in 72 AD. Probably worth getting a tour guide, combining with tix next door to the Forum. Allow 3-4 hrs to see Colosseum and Forum.

title: NZ family trip to roma
text: Robert was amazingly informative making a subject which could be challenging for some, fun and memorable. We were able to imagine what it would have been like to be romans and gladiators all that time ago. Thanks for telling us the truth about the Colosseum.

title: Colosseum
text:
The Colosseum was wonderful ! But, DONT buy your tickets here !!!
We paid $148.00 for 4 tickets, we were "7 minutes late" for our tour due to not being able to find the place to pick the tickets up (seriously we were running like we were on the amazing race) and they said our tickets had EXPIRED !!! UGH !!!
So.... not wanting to miss out on the Colosseum, we ended up having to pay for tickets again at the gate anyways. We truly enjoyed the Colosseum. 
Just DONT buy Tickets here !

title: Amazing
text: By far the best part of our trip to Rome! The history of the place is awe inspiring. We had a guide and skipped the lines which looked very long so I would recommend doing this if you have the money. Going into the colosseum at gladiator gate was incredible especially if your a fan of the film.

title: Best attraction in Rome
text: Even when this attraction is busy it’s still great to visit. Hundreds of touts selling water and other tat outside but you just have to ignore them. You need sturdy walking shoes everywhere in Rome but most definitely here as there are lots of steps and they are uneven and steep in places. There is lift access but there is only one and there was a queue. Make sure you visit the loo before visiting here as the facilities are lacking and there was a half an hour wait for the six ladies loos. There is a disabled loo but you have to go all the way to the back of the ladies portaloo and up the slope to find it hidden in the corner. Magnificent building and you will be in awe of the scale of everything.

title: Less than the hye
text: Don't get me wrong - the Colosseum is amazing to see, but buying tickets and going inside was somewhat disappointing, although you should do it once if you have the time (side note - we weren't able to tour the lower areas, and I've heard the night tours are great).  We had a Roma Pass to help avoid the lines, but there are just way too many people.  We were only there for about an hour.  If you have a short stay, I'd avoid going inside the Colosseum and instead spend more time in the Forum

title: The Grand Colosseum
text: Its best to purchase skip the line tickets. I travelled in November and there were no lines and the weather was also great. It was not too cold and I could walk around without getting too hot. Even though there was no line to enter, it was quite busy inside with everyone trying to find a spot to take pictures that will not have photo bombers. All in all it was worth it. I did not have a tour guide, but I could listen in to other tour groups and therefore learnt quite a bit.

title: One of the highlights of our Rome visit
text: Don't miss it and be sure to take a tour. We did one which included the Arena and also the underground parts - totally worth it!

title: Huge
text: Its definitely worth buying a Roma pass as you get Palatine Hill and the Forum in with this. Get there early 8.30ish and with pre booking you can be near the front of the queue. Any later and the queues for tickets are huge.

title: Utter rip off - 2 day ticket
text: Bought a 2 day ticket to the Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palantine Hill - found out that you do not get to choose when you visit. Utter rip off - BE AWARE THAT YOUR VISIT TO THE COLOSSEUM IS TIMED!!!!

title: Beautiful, shame about all the scaffolding!
text: Can’t fault the colosseum itself, scaffolding does take away from it slightly and the crowds but inside it’s just as awe inspiring as you’d expect.

title: Retired
text:
We toured the Coliseum and Ancient Rome with Miguel on Sunday 29/09/2019. We have been on many tours over the years and this tour was the best we have ever experienced. His knowledge was incredible and we learned so much - makes us want to learn even more!
He was easy to understand- which was greatly appreciated by us! He interjected just enough humor to keep our energy levels high through out the tour. 
We are going on a tour of the Vatican when we return to Rome on 9/14 in the afternoon and we so hope that Miguel will be our tour guide then too!!
We hope he is recognized for being a talented, highly educated scholar of Ancient Rome.  He certainly was a highlight of our trip. 
Thank you for providing such excellent professional and personable service and congratulations on such an excellent employee!

title: One of the iconic sites to visit in Rome.Simply the best-but ignore strangers
text: Clearly one of the vital things to do in Rome. If you do this as part of a hop on hop off tour you may take a while to get back on as the crowds here are usually large.The annoying "centurions" who try to pose for photos for a payment are now thankfully largely a thing of the past but there are still 'helpful' strangers who will try to engage you in conversation in order to make a euro-or considerably more if you are not careful. A guided tour may be an option if you are not afraid of information overload but these vary considerably in price and quality and the two are not always correlated. Whatever you decide, get a pull out illustration and wallow in the atmosphere if you can alongside all your fellow tourists.

title: Fancinating construction. Lots of Steps!!!!
text: My friend had a lot of troulble with the steps but managed. Take this into consideration. Views at the top are great.

title: Recommend guided trip
text: Amazing place to visit. Recommend going on guided tour to get the most out of it and skip the queues!

title: Accosted by tour guides
text:
There are massive queues to get into the Colosseum, so many guides hang around Via Del Colosseo to grab passers by interested in visiting, my one charged €35 for having quicker bag checks and a radio earpiece that allowed us to hear the guide better, but I could have saved €15 had I just gone up to get a normal ticket. 
The major issue is timing. If you want to go to the top level of the Colosseum you need to be there by 13:00, as this part closes at 5pm. You could be queuing for at least an hour then to get round all the displays it could be 4pm by the time you get to the top level. One benefit of the guide I had, was taking us to the gardens and house of the emperor, which they now call “Beverly Hills” as that compares to how the Romans used to live...

title: Colosseum is a wonderful place
text: Colosseum a beautiful place with Roman architecture. It is a huge amphitheater. One of the worlds wonder surely is a site to visit.

title: Fascinating, historic spectacle!
text:
Had booked tickets on-line and downloaded the clio-muse app onto phone which gave us so much insight into the history of the Colosseum and Palatine Hills, I  certainly advise pre booking as the crowds were enormous to both venues. 

The Palatine Hills meters from the Colosseum is a vast, beautiful and green spacious area steeped in history too. We bought a simple lunch and drinks at a super market across the road from the Colosseum and enjoyed a wonderful picnic in the grounds.

 Please be aware of multiple street traders "pushing" their goods at both site entrances which is annoying and distracting.

title: Amazing and powerful
text:
Must visit, very interesting and powerful. Ordered directly on site, showed tkts on phone, NP.
Didn't use a guide though can be nice if visiting with kids.
Don't hesitate to go and don't think to skip...really is an amazing piece of history one must visit.

title: An amazing sight
text: We were looking forward to seeing the Colosseum and had tickets in advance but unfortunately the weather was terrible - rain, wind and very cold.  We still looked all around both levels but unfortunately it was too cold to stay long.

title: Hard work
text: Like every part of tourist Rome, this place is crawling with 'lookey-lookey' men pushing phone chargers, selfie-sticks, scarves and hats. They spoiled most of our trip. Fortunately, once you get into an attraction, they disappear.....you then get hundreds of other tourists, nightmare. Rome Council ought to get their act together to clean up the city - literally. I've never seen so much grafitti. And most of the streets feature horrible cobbles. Rome is very tiring. But it's done now. Good luck to future tourists, you'll need it.

title: Stunning ancient monument - well worth a visit
text:
We did a skip the line tour, which to be honest, was dissapointing in that we seemed to be ages getting inside. This could have been becasue they limit the number of poeple inside and have to slow down the entrants, so even those on skip the line have to wait.

Once inside, however, we thorougly enjoyed the sights of the Colloseum. we bought the audio guide, only 5 euros and well worth it, with a lot of really useful background and some colourful details of the gladiators, etc. Absolutely worth the wait even though you don;t really need to spend more than 1 hour inside to see everything. 

I don;t know how long the normal lines are so I cannot say if it is better to do that or try a skip the line tour - ours was shambolic at best - ANCIENT AND RECENT.

title: A FEW HOURS WELL SPENT
text:
This is truly a GEM for all humanity.

I came here with my family for a tour, and it was a couple of hours extremely well spent. It was worth EVERY EURO spent, to have a guide to explain the history of this place, which gives you an excellent picture of what is fantasy… and what is myth... all the way through to reality. I never imagined the "magic" of this place.

Besides being PACKED with history, and filled with stories that will make you laugh… cringe and understand what this place would have been such a long time ago. We have all seen movies about this place, but hearing stories and proof of what history REALLY was, simply changed the game for me. NOW… I can understand much better what went on in this place all those many, MANY years ago.

This is a short report… so simple say:
GO… LISTEN… LEARN… UNDERSTAND.
It will change the way you think about history, since movies are what they are (exaggeration of what was reality) and facts are facts, which help everyone understand life in the times of the Caesar.

GO… with a guide, and take full advantage of this wonderful place.

title: Be sure to skip the line!
text: A paid skip the line tour is totally worth it u less you want to sit in line during some of your visit to Rome!

title: A true spectacle
text: You can stand in aware every without going inside for a tour - yes crowded if you do go for a tour inside - price to tour is OK - toilets and issue though

title: A dream came true
text:
A must see without any doubt.
A guided tour there was the perfect solution to skip that long queue under the merciless sunshine. It took us 3 hours while strolling from Colosseum to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Such an amazing experience in total!

title: Amazing
text: I was working on cruise line. Four of us went there. It was absolutely beautiful. Lot of tourist visiting this heritage. Easy to find food for lunch too

title: Hot but how could you not go?
text: We visited in late September.  The crowds were large.  We had advance "skip the line" tickets.  I would not have waited in the line we saw.  It was very hot (upper 80s).  But there is an amazing amount of history with explanations inside and the construction is awesome.  Be aware of the stairs and few places to sit.

title: Must see!
text: Well worth the time to see the Colosseum. We bought skip the line tickets which worked out very well.

title: Stay away from Headout
text:
I booked a Colosseum tour on 21-Sep-2019 from 2:30 pm. When I reached there , nobody was there.I called the number mentioned in the ticket . They assure me somebody will be there on time and request me to wait. Around 2:35 pm when I called them up, they told "there is no tour for Colosseum at 2:30". You check with Headout and sort this out.
I was in a big trouble, I requested the local tour organiser to send me email mentioning that there is no such tour. They emailed me back.
I returned from tour but still there is no refund and I am struggling.
Please do not book tour from headout. There are n numbers of good tour organiser (like City Wonders, walks italy spain and greece ). Don't use Headout else be prepare for last minute embarrassment.

title: A visit whilst in Rome
text: Another piece of Rome's history this building is a testament to the era it was built and ponder at the moments through it's history,we did not go in as the queue was phenomanel and we did not have the time which was a shame but a lot of the attractions were the same so i recommend booking in avance.

title: A must-see in Rome
text: Beautiful place to visit, breathtaking construction and a real sense of grandeur. No glass bottles aloud inside but it is worth taking a little plastic bottle of water on hot days as the shop inside sells out by lunchtime. Lots of steps and uneven underfoot so not ideal for those less mobile. Still, a must-do when in Rome.

title: Unbelievable!
text: What’s not to like. An engineering marvel that speaks for itself.  Be sure to take a tour to get the full perspective!

title: Lucky day
text:
My wife and I arrived at the Colosseum around 3 PM. It had rained earlier in the day, and we hoped that the crowds would be a bit lighter. We were not disappointed, and were able to get our tickets within about 10 or 15 minutes. Only a few hustlers bothered us as we approached the entrance.

I had tried the day before to get skip the line tickets, but these were unavailable. This would’ve been a complete waste of money. What also made the experience enjoyable was that the crowds were relatively thinner than usual and that we were given audio visual aids which we found very helpful. I would consider these better than being on a guided tour.

A lot of changes have been made to the Colosseum since I last saw it four  decades ago, and they are continuing to work on improving the site. It is a magnificent structure and well worth seeing.

title: Love it
text: Beautiful Colosseum have been before but thoroughly enjoyed again the renovation of the under passages is amazing

title: Surprised
text: Great building and would have been an awsome structure to see in its glory days, was very surprised as to what actually went on here from the guilded tour information and to learn about its historical goings on, all I'll say is be prepared to be surprised by its history.

title: Yes this place is great
text: It was over 90 degrees in August when we toured but it was great anyway.  Standing in the middle of that ancient building and looking all around is definitely something to check off the bucket list.  Whether you use a tour or go on your own, you have to go.

title: Amazing
text: Booked tickets to avoid the queue, well worth it. The colosseum itself was amazing, the history and the shear size of it makes it a must for travellers to Rome.

title: Avoid the tour guides!
text: The Colosseum is such an amazing experience for the fact that it is so beautiful and has so much history behind it! I felt it necessary to write a review on here to warn other tourists not to believe the tour guides that pester you outside of the Colosseum. There are a lot of tour guides that will approach you and offer you a tour of the colosseum for 25euro and they sell this to you by saying you can skip the three hour long queue if you go with them. We decided against going for a tour and queued up for normal entry and we were waiting a maximum of 45 minutes. Don't be put off by the length of the queue, it moves very quickly and is never as long as what the tour guides will tell you!

title: Taste of ancient history
text: Gladiator battles, mock naval battles, feeding Christians to the lions. Astonishingly huge considering what it was and when it was built. It is completely comparable to today's modern sports stadiums. Worth of a skip-the-line ticket.

title: One of the best construction
text: One of the engineering wonders built by people using the technology of that time. You must not miss this goal.

title: Tick box when in rome
text: You can’t come to Rome and not do the colosseum. Unfortunately I left it to late to organise a tour with the official company that sell these with the colosseum but would 100% recommend pre-booking a time slot to attend so that you don’t have to wait in a larger line on the day waiting to buy a ticket. The process was really easy and they start to let people in 10 minutes before the scheduled time on your ticket. Probably only thing that annoyed me (and had nothing to do with the colosseum) is the Instagram culture of people trying to take that great photo for their profiles. Had to fight the crowds and dodge selfies to get decent shots of the colosseum and try to enjoy the history!

title: Impressive! Disabled travellers, take proof of disability. Much smoother visit experience!
text:
The Colosseum itself is an absolute must see for anyone visiting Rome. I was hugely impressed by it and blown away by the amount of it which is left. However, the real purpose of reviewing is to help anyone who has a disability who is visiting. 

Prior to visiting I had read that if you have proof of your disability (an EU recognised parking permit worked fine for me) then if you go to one of the people who work on the queues, they should let you skip the lines. The queues were hours long I'm assuming and this way it allowed me and one companion to enter skipping the queues and also free of charge. We were accompanied through the barriers and security screenings to the ticket booths and given complimentary disabled and carer tickets.

The lower level of the Colosseum is accessible - every set of steps has got an alternative ramp option if you look hard enough for them. They're not always directly next to it, mind. There is also a lift on the external walkway which will take you up to the upper level. This can get busy and we queued to get into it - something to bear in mind.

Excellent care taken to make this historical sight accommodating for visitors who have a disability but it isn't especially clear unless you knew the tricks beforehand :)

title: Worth ticking off the list
text:
I didn’t particularly enjoy my visit because I’m not interested in the history - however if you are, this would be a great visit I’m sure. The steps inside are very steep, but lifts are available too. 
Also *** buy your tickets at the palantine hill entrance (about 5 mins on from the colosseum) and there’s no queue and you get timers entry - we smugly walked past the entire queue to buy tickets and then came back last them all with our ticketed entrance!

title: Two millennia of history
text: Beat the line booking for the first entry of the day will enable viewing with less line-up and less people present. Fortunate to be there on a clear sunny day - great for photos

title: Beautiful and historical place
text: Definitely worth the visit. This place has a lot of history, and it is a must see.Depending on the time of the year, it may be very crowdy and with long lines to get inside. I recommend purchasing anticipated tickets.

title: Impressive
text: The Colosseum is a must-see, of course. During our visit we learned interesting facts about it, such as — it has been built in only 8 years due to the arches (less construction material) and the budget (mainly financed by the plundering of the Jerusalem Temple in 70AD!).

title: Beautiful place to visit!
text:
The Colosseum is a beautiful monument with beyond intricate architecture!!
For a detailed tour one requires more than a day, to absorb the beauty from the outside and also understand the history by taking a tour inside. 
The views also vary depending on the time of the day. You can get splendid pictures at sunrise from one end and on sunset from the other end. I personally found the sunset side more interesting for photography. 
There’s a hillock near the Colosseum which is a good vantage point.

title: Interesting history but hard in the heat.
text:
Buy your ticket at Palantino hill and saves two hours in the Colosseo queue - about 500m from the colosseum . The audio/video guides are old technology and we didn’t get too much from it. Too many tour groups and if your not with a guided tour you can’t go on the Belvedere or arena floor. 

Having said all that if it is your first time you will be amazed. It is a spectacular site even with the crowds and heat. Take plenty of water in non-winter times.

title: A Must See
text: A must see - recommend walking around the exterior on your own and then follow up with a guided tour who can really bring the time period to life.

title: AMAZING!!!!
text: Truly a wonderful experience. It was very busy but absolutely well worth it. You can spend hours there.

title: Rubio & Ivy
text: Nice place. Great to see a piece of the Roman Empire. Recommended in winter time due to the temperature and less crowded. Purchase ticket to skip the line. You will be very fast inside.

title: Colosseum
text: Unfortunately there was a lot of work ongoing when we visited, so we didn't really get the "wow" feeling. It's smaller than I had pictured but when in Rome you have to visit the Colosseum.

title: Conducted tour
text: Did the Viator conducted your . Fantastic guide Renate, very knowledgeable and entertaining . Well worth booking this as without her it would have been pointless. Also avoided queues which were enormous. 3.5 hours flew by before we realised it was over. Highly reccomend this tour, fantastic value for money. A brilliant day.

title: Ancient Rome
text: Prebook your skip the line tickets is a must as queues can be huge. All tickets are timed so make sure you arrive on time. The place is simply old Rome stunning.

title: The History
text:
The History and feel of this place is fantastic. 
I would advise getting there early to beat the crowds opens at 8.30 
Bought our tickets at a official kiosk nearby for 12 euros each which gained us access to the colosseum.  Palatine hill and the Roman forum excellent value and even better way to spend a few hours appreciating the History of Rome .

title: Amazing
text: Don’t want to miss.  Plan out a good 4 hours.  We had booked a tour but was told “it overbooked “ by error. Ended buying tickets at the Palantine hills area with audio guide and paid less than half of what tour charges.  We explored at our pace including the Palantine  hills, the Roman forum and other surrounding sights.  Take your walking shoes!

title: Nightmare
text:
It is disorganised. There are no signs for queues so you could very well stand in the wrong queue just to be turned away.
Different days are free. You are best to go with an organised tour or do plenty of research regarding ticket options before you visit. Be sure to ask if your ticket option really does guarantee fast track. Those who have commented on walking tours that had to wait while the guide got tickets are probably not waiting anywhere near as long as the prepaid tickets which seem to move at the same pace as not tickets. 
If it is going to rain they could stop people entering until enough people leave and therefore the queue will stall.

title: Too many people worth to buy skip the line ticket
text: Buy skip the line ticket!! Worth to have a free audio guide tour! Download (rick steves europe) for amazing free audio tour!

title: A must visit when in Rome
text:
We bought our tickets online for English guided tour of 45 minutes from their official website. On seeing the enormous queues, we felt happy that we saved 2-3 hours by buying the tickets in advance. The arrangements for entrance and tour were very professional. 
It was such a great experience to visit one of the 7 wonders of the world. 
We took some good photos of the colloseum across the street Nicola Salvi.

title: WOW
text: We went on a tour today, it was so awesome, the guide we had was very informative, I was blown away by everything, would also do it again

title: What more can be said - except...
text:
Tip #1. Get a guide.  Do not think you will get the full benefit of the experience of the Colosseum without someone who has the detailed knowledge of how, when, and why it came to be, and the role it played in history as civilizations came and went.  A good guide can really bring the experience to life for you.

Tip #2. Be sure to enter thru the “Gladiators Gate” to the Colosseum Floor”. This is a limited access are and gives you an amazing experience as the gladiators would have know it to be in the 1st century.  Not to be missed.

Tip #3.  Bring a hat, water, and sunscreen.  Oh, and a few extra € or credit card as there’s a nice gift shop on the third level.

Tip #4.  If you’re going June-August make sure it’s either the very first thing you do that day or the very last.  The masses become impossible to maneuver through very quickly as the day progresses.

Enjoy!

title: TAKES YOU BACK IN TIME
text: Seeing the Colosseo from outside , you are awed , being inside , well it simply takes you back in time and makes you imagine how it was in this same place hundreds and thousands of years ago...

title: Nice to see, nicer to stay away
text:
Yes. We get it. It’s a historical landmark, it has a rich story and is definitely worth learning about. That being said, the place is absolutely SWARMING with tourists, buses, cars and with that - scammers, street thieves and other questionable characters.

Unless you truly feel urged to see the colosseum with your very eyes, I would personally strongly recommend to stay away from that place and go read about it in Wikipedia while sitting in a nice café or rooftop bar (of which Rome has many!) somewhere else - far, far away from that place. Good luck!

title: Nice to see the Colleseum but it was very busy.
text: We visited the Colleseum via booking with Tiqets. It was all a bit chaotic, when we got into the Colleseum we were told to wait for 10 minutes, then shown were the rest rooms were and some of the attractions. Then to meet by the gate outside an hour later to visit Palantine Hill and the Forum. We had down loaded the audio guide to our phones. After about 45 minutes of looking around we had seen enough and decided to leave and miss the other parts of the trip. As we left a storm came in and rained really hard, so we found a nice cafe to have lunch and let the storm move away.

title: Colosseum by night
text: We had a booked guided tour through the oldest parts of Rome in the late evening After dinner. Ended at the Colosseum with tour inside close to midnight. Late but very good sinne there were very fem people around and temperature much More convinient Tham during daytime. Very interesting and professional executed.

title: Just amazing!
text: This was wonderful piece of history! Had a tour and learned a lot; fun facts: Inside, the Colosseum had seating for more than 50,000 spectators, who may have been arranged according to social ranking but were most likely packed into the space like sardines in a can (judging by evidence from the seating at other Roman amphitheaters)

title: Go In The Morning
text: Beat the crowds and the heat by going early in the morning.  A tour with the underground, bottom floor was worth it.

title: Beautiful part of Italian History
text: If your in Rome make this one of your stops, purchase a ticket and go inside.  Wonder the grounds and stop at The forum.

title: Worth a visit but not as spectacular as I was expecting
text: I had been looking forward to visiting the Colosseum and it looked imposing as we walked towards it but after queuing for over an hour I must admit that I was a little disappointed. You can get a sense of the history within it but maybe it was just me as it just didn’t tick all of the boxes. I’m glad I went to see it as it’s got so much history around it (including the various ruins along the road as you approach the Colosseum) but if I go back to Rome there would be other places I visit instead

title: Walks Of Italy Gladiator Gate Tour
text: We had a fabulous tour through walks of Italy Davide was our guide. His knowledge was impressive to say the least. He took us all through the forum palatine hill and the colosseum. It was fantastic and a real highlight of our trip to Rome.

title: What a site
text: The tour was great. The view amazing and to see how big it is and how many people could be inside. The history of it and all the games and animals that were used was a surprise to me. You will not be disappointed and you should see it lit up at night. Beautiful.

title: Awesome history
text: Colosseum is a must see attraction if you are interested in Roman building techniques and machinery.

title: Rome Visit
text:
Booked on line for looking for Jump the Queues tickets, take some time doing this as there are so many.
Took the plunge and paid on line for following day, we had to meet our group at Constantine's Arch at 15.00 
Decided to make a day of it and walked from our rented apartment Via de Frezzia about 1hr arrived with 40mins to spare which was lucky because as we approached meeting point it seemed 50% of Romes tourist had purchased Jump the Queue tickets for the same time, after a bit of nervous asking different operators we joint our tour.
Our guide was a young lady called Franchesca who was very knowledgeable and willing to stop and answer any group questions.
Everyone has seen the Colosseum on TV and in magazines but not until you stand next to it and wonder how sometime so big was constructed without huge machines and its age its AWSOME !!! Our tour included Roman Forum and Palatine Hill 3hrs of walking and taking in the sights and information, it was the best history lesson we have received  since leaving school.
Top marks to Franchesca and I Love Rome tours.
PS no wonder its one of the seven wonders of the world.

title: Colosseum, Palatine Hill Tour with The Roman Guy
text: I took the detailed tour that included the lower and upper levels. Excellent tour. Our guide was excellent. At one point the Colosseum guide takes over and I missed our guide who was more personal and interesting. Huge place that is rich in meaning and detail - you really need a guide to make sense of it all. Excellent tour. Excellent guide. Be prepared for heat and crowds. Good job The Roman Guy!

title: Great place for imagination
text:
This amphitheater is fascinating. It is massive on the outside, but you will not feel at all what it is. Only inside. No need to rush. You have to go in and turn on your imagination.
We arrived 10 minutes before 9am, queued for 20 minutes, and walked quietly everywhere. Online order is not obligatory.

title: A Humbling Experience
text:
This was by far one of the best travelling experiences I have ever had. My main agenda for this trip was to view this incredible edifice and I was not disappointed. 

I could not help but pause several times during the tour to reflect on the history of the Colosseum as well as the construction of the structure itself and what must have gone into it. An imposing reminder of past historical events.

The free water fountains (found throughout Rome not just at the Colosseum) are a pleasant surprise and very welcome in the heat.  Being here helps you to understand how the original Roman users of the Colosseum would have experience the entertainment on offer.

title: A must see
text:
Spend the day 
Pre Buy tickets 
Get there early
Take water and shade etc 
Do the tour if you can but the forum and Palestine hill will take up a lot of your time also but again well worth it

title: Be prepared to be amazed
text:
We had a guided tour of the colosseum which was really good was very informative , it was a very small group of 8 which was even better as you didn’t have to wait for everyone to keep up while you went around or wait to hear the history when you stopped .

I would definitely recommend going with a tour guide as you not only get to  beat the queues but you also  learn more about the history then you would if you went without a guide .

We also took a night tour organised by the colloseum by booking that ticket on the spot. Apart from going 'underground', (you don't see very much of it), it didn't really work for me. Give this a miss.

title: Colosseum Roma
text:
We visited the Colosseum as part of a group tour. It is a very busy place with huge crowds and high security. I was a bit daunted with the size of the crowds, which was to be expected.

That aside, this is a must see visit when In Roma. The history is fascinating, the stories amazing and the size and construction beyond comprehension. Add this to your list of must see events when in Roma.

title: Go EARLY!!
text:
My husband and I went to the Colosseum on a free admissions day despite many people telling us to avoid it. We grabbed our free ticket in the Palatine Hill line (which is right near the Colosseum line). The Palatine Hill line had 100-200 people in line and the Colosseum had maybe several thousand at 8/8:15 AM. After grabbing our ticket we were able able to get into the "ticket" line at the Colosseum and go straight through security. Everyone in the Colosseum line had to first get a ticket then go through security. Anyways, we were some of the first people inside the Colosseum at 8:45. It was very quiet and peaceful. 

We then left the Colosseum and went to Palatine Hill afterwards and again very quiet and peaceful with very few tourists around. After leaving Palatine Hill around 11ish the Colosseum vibe changed. Thousands of people everywhere and it was difficult to just  leave. Also all the hawkers came out by this time (very few at 8/830!). We even saw a fight break out! 

My opinion: Go right at opening!! Its too chaotic after 10 or so. Worth visiting if you can go with fewer tourists. Way too busy later in the day to enjoy.

title: Great historical tourist site
text: I have been here on holidays which it was free of charge. There was not enough room to swing a cat, the lines was packed solid and the entrance queue was about one hundred 💯 and fifty meters long and there was not proper signs to find the correct queue. Besides when I asked about buying ticket 🎫 from one of the officer 👮 s, he responded me ironically which was not appropriate, and I had to ask ordinary people to find out about tickets...

title: History unfolds
text: What we read in books about colosseum seems a way too short when you see it. It's actually history unfolds when you see it. The amphitheatre is very unlikely to be forgotten...with closed eyes and feels you really feel...chariot race, gladiators fighting, victorious been hailed and losers down with bloodied bodies....it's all there and not to forget the architecture and refine nests of Roman Empire....they had it all...

title: July heat and humidity
text: No matter the heat and humidity, you cannot miss this archeological site.  There are natural spring water fountains to refill water bottles.  Do NOT buy from the vendors, the water quality cannot be trusted.  Also bring umbrellas!  It makes the heat tolerable.  We hired a tour guide, Marzia.  She was fabulous, there were some issues with our Roma Pass (do not recommend getting a Roma Pass!) due to last minute changes of the policies at the colosseum.  But she got us in with some smooth talking.  Definitely get the audio guide for inside if you do not have a tour guide.  We only did the basic inside tour and that was more than sufficient, especially b/c of the heat.  The basic tour still took us 4 hours.  Our 4 kids range in age from 20-9.  They all enjoyed it very much.

title: A must see !
text:
This place holds a whole history, many tragedies and many stories.
It should be visited on your first time ever to Rome.

title: Stunning
text: The Colosseum, officially the Flavian Amphitheater, is almost 2000 years old. It was built on the drained lake of Emperor Nero’s Domus Aurea, in an attempt to wipe out his legacy. Let’s be honest, this was a place where people made sport out of there suffering of others. People died here for recreation. We took an official guided tour with Coop, lasted 45 min and was full of detail. The ticket entitles you to also enter the Forum and Palatine Hill over 2 days. I recommend coming earlier in the morning when the crowds are more bearable.

title: Bucket list attraction
text: The Colosseum isn't Rome's most perfectly preserved edifice. That's the Pantheon. But the 2000-year-old theatre is still one of the world's great wonders. Bigger than you might think, it's a Roman skyscraper. Building began in 70AD and took only eight years. Then it was in use for the next 500 years for performances and was used for other purposes before becoming derelict and forgotten. But even in the mid-18th century it was still visited. How do we know? In the Galleria Borghese (also recommended) there's a little Canaletto of the Colosseum as it was around 1742-5, covered in vegetation. It shows a hawker selling things to people on their way in. (The Galleria also has authentic Roman mosaics of gladiators sticking spears in wild animals, the entertainment for which the Colosseum is most famous). But unlike other Roman theatres, you can't stand in the arena. Currently everything underneath it - the scenery stores and animal cages - is being excavated. There are crumbling, inaccessible staircases that give some idea of the thousands who used to attend a single performance. But it's hard to gauge where the seating was and what it may have looked like: there's virtually nothing left. Admission is just to two levels, not to the very top, where the women and slaves would have sat. No matter what time of day you go, you'll be in the company of hundreds of other tourists from around the world. Even if you've booked in advance it'll take you a very long time to get to the front of the queue. Bear in mind, incidentally, that you must book. The ticket is for three attractions - also the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill - all in the same locality. The ticket is apparently for "two days". But we were caught out when we tried to visit the Forum on the second day. You can't do it. You have to visit all three attractions on one of two days. This really isn't clear.

title: Pre book
text: Even though we pre booked it took about an hour to get inside . The building is magnificent , not to be missed

title: Very busy
text: I d recommend anyone who s planning to go there to be early or book it online coz I was there on Saturday and the Qs were long and you could wait there for Hrs. We booked a guided tour but it was let down we waited for an hr in the Q and at the end it was worthless.

title: Colosseo under the stars
text: I did celebrate my birthday, well actually the day after my birthday with a few of my close friends last night at the colosseum. This place is magical, I love it with all of my heart.

title: Amazing from outside
text: Die to it being free entry on the day we went we felt the queues too long to join so didn't go inside. Had a good walk around. There were lots of street sellers which got quite annoying.

title: VIP access including on the stage

Our guide was very knowledgeable and we enjoyed it thoroughly. We didn’t have to queue to access the site, and we went in through a VIP door that led us onto the area where the gladiators fought (the stage). Was well worth the money and I’d definitely recommend to everyone.

title: Awesome!
text: Fascinating place steeped in history and our tour guide was full of interesting facts. Awesome place

title: Roma
text: Great place pity guide wasn't great and rude. Didn't speak enough English to take an English speaking tour

title: A real blast from the past
text: Definitely one not to be missed. An amazing view of `Rome's distant past with equally amazing stories to behold. But a tip worth noting; try going there by public transport. It will cost you less than 2 euros while the tour companies charge a small fortune!

title: Amazing!
text: Amazing to see by it would be helpful to have a guide.  Lots of construction but still worth the trip.

title: Worth visiting
text: Example of nice ancient engineering. Though it is mostly damaged now. Impressed by the tunnel system (which used to be underground in past). Ticket is 12 euro (includes Palatine hill and Roman forum). Valid for two day but Colosseum on the same day (palatine hill and Roman forum can be covered same day or next day).

title: It is just simply THE MUST see or thing to do in Rome

I actually had a better time this time with the lower-priced skip the line tour then the first time with a custom guide who charged us over $ 400 but was way too technical with the details. Get an iPhone App like Rick Steves or some sort of guide and that is all you need to take in the sights and history of the Colosseum.

title: A great History
text: One of the great Roman structures that is worth the visit if you are in Rome. Great history and value behind what it is why it was built.

title: Confusion at its greatest

The staff who knew were we have to stand was at the beggining! Of the line. After going to the correct line everything was better. It is a huge place where you can see a part of Roman era.

title: Colosseum
text: Spectacular Roman building with its historical story, although not much to see inside specially for the kids.

title: Wonderful
text: It's absolutely fabulous, I really advise to buy skip the line tickets otherwise your going to wait four hours to enter and you'll be tired when you get in .the way the collseum was built is fascinating .

title: The home of gladiators
text: Amazing (and colossal) piece of history! Almost heard the bloodthirsty crowd cheering in the stands while gladiators fought in the middle of the arena. Always wanted to see this gruesome yet fascinating part of history.

title: A wow moment

The queues can be massive so get fast track prepaid tickets, well worth it.
You cannot believe how this magnificent building was ever put together.
So much to take in.

title: HEADOUT TOURS IS A SCAM
text: Simply writing this review to add to the other horror stories of headout tours scamming people by not having their tour guide show up. I paid for skip the line tickets and showed up 30 minutes early to the meeting point at the arch of Constantine as instructed. After waiting for some time I start circling the arch as other group tours are coalescing, but the one with the supposed “Tours About” sign wasn’t there. Emailed customer service about a refund and they bold faced lied and said the guide was there and I was a no show. Don’t let these morally bereft crooks take your money, learn from mistakes and those of others who have been scammed

title: Breathtaking
text: As we were dropped off to meet our guide ,I was awed by the Colosseum.its massive structure was breathtaking! Fascinating tour, except for a couple of moments when I pictured myself fighting inside the ring with thousands of people screaming my name, with plenty of interesting facts. My only disappointment was not having the time to see the inside. Coming back next year to see the inside!

title: Very impressive
text:

unbelievable architecture and history here!

title: Colosseum at Night

The meeting place was difficult to locate and required a great amount of walking before even getting to the Colosseum. There’s very little light to see anything, and the tour consisted mainly of three boring videos. Very few pictures were possible due to the darkness.  Do the day tour instead.

title: Inspiring but audio guide overrated
text: We loved the Colosseum. It’s great to actually visit somewhere that you’ve seen hundreds of times on tv or newspapers. It’s well signposted and easy to get to, but pretty unclear where you enter if you’ve pre-booked tickets, which I’d definitely recommend. The one downside for me was that the audio guide provided felt a bit useless and lacking in detail. I was happy reading the signs as I went and checking it in the guidebook I’d brought with me, but I felt a little disappointed with the audio guide. I guess it was better than nothing, but I’ve had better in different places. I suppose what I’m trying to say is that it could do with updating! The Colosseum itself is remarkable, however. Make sure you visit.

title: "OK"
text: It is still a big ruin and restauration works are possibly going on. A skip the line ticket works well as the crowds here can be very big. You can then go in fast, walk around take some pictures and get out again. One hour inside is more then enough time to see it.

title: Super cool
text: The place is massive. You walk in and feel the history. It is unmatched. I’m glad we were able to experience the stories history of such a wonderful landmark

title: Worth the stip
text: We only saw it from the outside, but it was well worth it. Our gold cart driver took us to the best place for a view and picture.

title: The Colosseum
text: A phenomenal structure with great history - well worth visiting but I suggest you pre-purchase a ticket either online or in Rome before you get to the site. Go in the morning before 10 before the crowds and before the heat.

title: Colosseum at night Fantastic!
text:
Atmosphere at its best.   Beautiful lighting and with a small group tour guide, it included sound effects and short video and 3 projector characters.  Felt like we were the only ones there
 Highly recommend.

title: We battled the colluseum
text: Crowded, even in low season but enjoyable. Plan on 3 hours to see this great ruins. Make sure you get the video tour to maximize you visit. Pre-purchasing tickets is a MUST. We saw hundreds of people in the purchase ticket line that were ultimately denied access because of the number of people. The video-audio self tour is a bit confusing and difficult to find the spots wherr to play the bext segment, but once you figure it out, it was very informative. We spent 3-4 hours total with waiting outside for our time-slot to enter, waking around and exiting. Palatine Hill, just across the way, is a must see too!

title: Incredible !!
text: It’s a must when you visit Rome put at least a day aside to do it properly - don’t use any of the bars and restaurants that are immediately facing the colosseum as they are very expensive, the food is rubbish and the the toilets resemble the underground of the colosseum in 80ad

title: Lots of history
text: We elected not to go inside due to walking restrictions of one person in our party but still enjoyed walking around.  So much history and lots to see even from the outside.   Go early if you want to avoid the lines

title: Must do - book queue jump tickets!
text:
This is a definite must do when in Rome! It was by far our favourite attraction in the city.

The queue to get in can be insane so make sure you book queue jump tickets (with a specific time slot) before you want to visit as this will save you hours!!

title: Fantastic
text:
A must see when visiting Rome. However, book tickets online to skip lines and preferably it’s better to book with a tour guide. 
The only negative was the lack of toilet facilities considering the volume of people waiting.

title: Colosseum
text: Was amazing to see, incredible this was built so long ago with little tools. Little bit sorry it was dirty around the place and full of weeds around the ground etc.

title: Colosseum
text: We took a private tour which was well worth it. Get your tickets ahead of time, they only let so many people in a day.  Buy the front of the line pass it’s worth it with the crowds.

title: Typical Tourist visit
text:
We booked a Fast track entry to the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill with multimedia experience with Tourist office and were very pleased with what we did. 

Firstly we had to collect the tickets not at the site itself. We then were walked to the entrance to the Roman Fourm and were given approximately 2hrs to walk around which was not really long enough but with our youngest as 11yr old was enough time just.
The tickets we had meant we didn't need to queue to buy them at site or with the illegal sellers. 
The fast track line was very long but did move quickly so was worth the extra cost.
We also got Multimedia earphones and was helpful to know about the history of what we were seeing.

I think I would have liked more time in the forum but with kids what we saw was great. Really happy birthday take your own water and snacks be prepared for 4-5 hrs at the sites.

Beware high pick pocket effort in the area around the Colosseam... AND don't buy anything from the illegal sellers

title: How do you not rate this a 5 out of 5?!?!
text: This is the icon of Rome, folks.  Beautiful structure.  We were a little disappointed that there was so much work being done on it for restoration, but obviously it is needed in order for people in the future to see it!  We took a private tour to avoid waiting in a long line and so we could visit from the top Belvedere level to the underground.  The general public is not allowed to go to these spots without a tour company.  Amazing to think of the effort it took to construct/design this place.  To me, the guide you pick is the most important part here.  Our guide was okay, but missed opportunities to really bring the Colosseum to life and to spark our imaginations while walking out on the floor.  My biggest warning here is that the area is filled with scam artists and hustlers...people trying to sell you selfie sticks, bracelets, water, etc.  We figured out if you simply say "no" firmly and keep moving, you have no issue.  We moved a little off to the side to avoid the biggest accumulation of these folks and didn't make ourselves out to look like we would welcome a conversation with someone.  Some of the scammers try to spark conversations with you by telling you how nice your shoes are and how great the U.S. is, etc.  Don't talk with them!  There is a small gift shop there.  They have tons of books, some small knick knacks...I bought a neat postcard that changes appearance depending on the angle you look at it -- shows the current appearance and how it would have looked years ago.

title: A very historical tour
text: Our tour guide was very energetic and informative. She really seemed to enjoy her job.  The ruins and Colosseum are very impressive, but be forewarned, there are a lot to stairs to climb on this tour!  Highly enjoyable.

title: Just OK
text: This rating would be much lower if it wasn’t a bucket list and must see in Rome. Even with a “Skip the Line” tour it still took about an hour to get thru the line. There really isn’t much to see.

title: Need to see once in your lifetime
text:
Simona was a very knowledgeable guide and a pleasure to spend the morning with and she spoke our language (English) very well. 
Such a historical site and well worth the visit.

title: Awesome
text: An iconic structure that was on our bucket list. Beautiful day and easy check in with our skip the line tour. Why you would bother standing in the sun for hours for an unescorted tours beggars belief.We did the underground passages and Roman Forum tour, great guides, well organised and the site speaks for itself

title: Free day
text: Went first to Palatine hill, and had no waiting time, just went through. Walked around Hill and forum for 4-5 hours. Then we went to Colosseum and for our line we went inside again without that much of a waiting time. Very good experience at no cost( we had water bottle and snacks with us).

title: The attraction everybody wants to see.
text:
Capacity is limited so the 'skip the line' tickets could be seen as an investment.  When we were there the entry was closed, briefly, due to numbers. An unexpected heavy shower soaked the line waiting outside.
A guide or a good guide book is needed to get the most out of this attraction.

title: Amazing Roman architecture
text: Awesome! must see in Rome. Better to take skip the line tickets to avoid the long queues. High rush in the morning but well managed queues and limited number of people allowed in Colosseum at a time. Afternoon will have less footfall.

title: Interesting attraction - definately get skip the line tickets though - it took us 1.30 hours to get in
text: Interesting and well presented attraction, however - ques to get inside were lengthy - it took us 1.30 hours to get in.

title: Colosseum
text: Beautiful place. However, if you plan to visit during the day when we were here, prepare for a lot of tourists.

title: Very long waiting time
text: A very beautiful and historical place, I recommend pre ordering tickets. Dont miss the restaurant " Luzzi " which is near the colosseum.

title: Colosseum
text: Had to stop by and see the Colosseum while in Rome. So huge. Beautifully lit up at night. It was amazing.

title: Colosseum
text: View the colosseum close-up and a far, day and night. The crowds are huge, watch out for pick pockets. Get tickets in advance.

title: Crowded and hot
text: I realize this is an Italian treasure that people around the world want to see.  And, yes, everyone should get a chance to visit the Colosseum.  Our guide did her best to tell us about everything but, the Colosseum personal hurried her along. Not her fault.  Too many people.

title: One of the top things to see  on the planet
text:
There is a bit of long queue to get in and then you need to queue for the audio tour device but it was well worth it.

This place is incredible and I only spent just over an hour inside but I wish I spent all day in it as there was that much to see.

title: I would highly recommend using a tour for this attraction
text: This is a very popular attraction. I highly recommend using a tour with skip the line tickets. If you are only in Rome for a few days don't waste your time standing in line just to get inside and not know what you are looking at. We were in the building within 30 minutes...everyone must go through security. The regular line was winding around the building. I couldn't imagine standing in line it the hot sun in the middle of the summer.

title: Colosseum
text: Colosseum is magnificent from outside 12 euros to Q to get in we went at 1030 took 1hr, no Q ticket was 25 euros it depends how tight you are for time, 5 euros gets you an audio guide which was interesting with 5 stops on it you can book evening tickets to go in dark in cellars which I didn't do

title: It's the freaking Colosseum!
text: How anybody can not give this 5 stars is beyond me, the structure is amazing. The underground tour was great and our tour guide was terrific. The best advice I can give for touring the Colosseum is buy the higher priced tour guide services, their guides are experienced and the groups are smaller.

title: Awesome group from 12:30
text: Italian guide was magnificent. Many thanks to him before he passed us over to David. For Palestine experience.

title: Amazing structure in an amazing city
text: The sheer size of this place is beyond belief. It is being restored over a large area and this is an eyesore at the moment. Hopefully it will look great after all the work is completed.

title: Massive, Impressive but, oh goodness, the lines!
text: The Colosseum is of course a must for any traveler to Rome. Being able to learn about it's history and have some previous assumptions debunked was great. However, the lines were insane and we even had a scheduled entry time on a private tour. We still waited over an hour just to get through security. It was so slow and took up at least 1/3 of our tour time, we had to rush through other part of our tour because of the time drain here at the Colosseum. We love history so it was great to see nonetheless.

title: Worth it
text: Even with the crowds and the limited time in any one place, this is still a magnificent sight.  I recommend booking in advance and getting a 'skip the line' ticket as the waiting time is literally hours.

title: Amazing
text: Book your underground tour yesterday!  We didn’t realize they were booked sooooo far in advance. We did the above ground tour ourselves and enjoyed what we saw but I think guided might give you more info. Maybe next time for us.

title: Historic
text: So, in my opinion I would put the Colosseum, Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill all together as it felt that it was one tour when walking. it. We were on a tour and it started with the Palatine Hill and ended up in the Colosseum. The first part was a 3 hour walking tour and it was hot and exhausting. Our guide was very good, but could have used more benches to sit down. What can a person say about the Colosseum except full of history and it is something you must see for yourself to appreciate it.

title: Incredible sight
text: It’s almost incomprehensible this place was built over 2000years ago, in just 8 years. It’s worth seeing up close.

title: Great to visit the Colosseum but..
text:
I remember visiting the Collosseum several years ago and concluded that it was a valuable and treasured Roman site but turned into a roundabout and very difficult to visit.

Several decades later, the same is true. the tickets to visit are 12 Euro, which is not a problem but the resultant queue is.

Great to view from the outside but too time consuming to visit.

title: Amazing Views; Long Lines
text: The Colosseum is a lot to take in, but a must for your visit to Rome.  Be prepared for long lines and long waits even if you get talked into paying double or triple the price to skip the line. Be sure to check out all the levels possible and also know there's an underground tour.

title: Breathtaking
text: Spent my birthday at the colosseum was well worth it. Beautiful day, sunny good weather in October 👍🏼Very big, lots of waking, interesting. Best is to get one of those tours with skip the queue, well worth it.

title: A must see
text: The Colosseum is a must see! However do not take a tour with a guide. The guide will spend more time talking about the history etc then you do seeing anything. To see the Colosseum and walk through it on your own is a great experience. Unless your a history buff that needs to hear all about all the history etc. you can save yourself a lot of time and see more sights.

title: Holiday visit with friend
text: Although Sunday is free admission the queues are extremely long so we joined a tour when we got there. We had an excellent knowledgable guide and the tour was not too long.  We were able to continue by ourselves until we were ready to leave.

title: History and Beauty
text: Although the Colosseum is not as grand at it once was centuries ago, it's very impressive to see the size and structure. Unfortunately I only saw the outside, and I've heard the tours within are worth the money. However, the biggest tip I can provide from my experience is that you need to see it during the day and night. We liked it so much that we took our wedding pictures in front of it!

title: Colosseum
text: Ok, I was waiting for more emotions when I appear inside but it wasn't like this. from outside you feel more the greatness of the Colosseum.

title: Go there only if u love history
text:
I love history do I enjoyed the place. U can close ur eyes and u can imagine the roar of the crowd. The place is huge u will need an hour or more to see the place. Rick steves audio guide helped a lot. Buy the ticket from the official website for best price and reach at least 20 min before ur scheduled time. 

Now if u r like my son and hate history, then it will be just a pile of stones to u!!!

title: Collesium
text: When you leave the subway stop, you are greeted by a gigantic work ... you are fascinated ... going to the Roman era, you find yourself in the depths of history in the arena area where there is great fights.

title: Amazing
text: Totally worth a visit thoroughly enjoyed our time here - we hired a video tour and went round at our own speed.

title: Must see but book a private guide
text: If you want to get the full benefit of knowing the history and experience you must have a guide.  We booked with a private tour guide, skipped all the lines and learned much more than expected about the history of the area.  Amazing!

title: Wonderful
text: Great view of Colosseum without entering. Nice day yesterday. We took many photos and our hop on bus was there waiting for us.
