title: Best-Preserved Ancient Roman Monument
text: The Colosseum was built between 72 A.D and 81 A.D, started by the Emperor Vespasian (emperor 69-79 A.D.) and completed by his son, Titus (emperor 79-81 A.D.).The site chosen was a flat area on the floor of a low valley between the Caelian, Esquiline and Palatine Hills (three of the famous seven hills of Rome). The intent of these two emperors was to build the greatest amphitheater in the world on the land that their infamous predecessor, Emperor Nero, had stolen from the city to build his own private lake. To undo Nero’s harm, Titus’s father, Emperor Vespasian, drained the lake to build the Colosseum. It was a gift to all Roman citizens and a shrewd and calculated move to gain public favor. The work crew was 60,000 slaves. The colosseum is oval in shape, measuring ~620 feet long, ~510 feet wide and ~165 feet high (about a twelve story building). The interior oval arena area is ~290 feet by 180 feet, with a 15 foot wall around it. This large arena (80 different numbered entrances) for gladiatorial shows and hunts of wild animals became the symbol of the Eternal City. The shows, starting in 80 A.D., were free of charge (as was the food too) and the tiered seats were assigned according to the class of spectators. Roman citizens sat according to their place in the social hierarchy, ranging from slaves and women in the upper bleachers to senators (names of some 5th century senators can still be seen carved into the stonework) and vestal virgins (priestesses of Vesta, goddess of the hearth) around the arena floor. Spectators were given tickets in the form of numbered pottery shards, which directed them to the appropriate section and row. Amazingly, some of the games lasted up to "one hundred days!" This was the case of the inaugural game in 80 A.D. The colosseum is reported to be able to hold 87,000 people. The entrances, corridors and galleries were built so that tens of thousands of people could get to their seats and leave in just minutes. Today, the number of people allowed within the colosseum is limited to ~3,000 - 3,500 to avoid crowding.  This capacity limitation does result in long lines and wait times to get inside, but the wait is worth it to observe this historical wonder. The monumental marble Colosseum was primarily built to entertain the people with brutal and barbaric games.  Gladiators (male & female - gladiatrix) were slaves, criminals or prisoners, often captured in war, that were trained in special schools to fight each other, sometimes to the death. Some people, including women, actually volunteered to be gladiators. The gladiatorial games ended by the year 438 A.D. while the shows with hunts of wild beasts continued until 523 A.D.  During this time more than 400,000 people died inside the Colosseum. Another estimate is that about 1,000,000 animals died in the Colosseum as well. In fact, the brutality was such that up to ten thousand (10,000) animals were killed in a single day! Most often, hungry animals fought other hungry animals, but sometimes hungry animals fought against gladiators in contests called venationes (wild beast hunts). Animals (captured mainly in Africa and Asia) that appeared included lions, panthers, leopards, cattle, bison, elephants, rhinoceros, giraffes, bears, tigers, deer, wild goats, ostriches, dogs, leopards, crocodiles, boars, hippopotamuses, and rabbits. During lunch intervals, executions ad bestias would be staged. Some reports, debated by historians, are that water battles were held in the colosseum. If held the water, would not have been more than three feet deep and the vessels would have been miniature versions of the war ships. Further, these water battles (if they happened) would have ended when Domitian (emperor 81-96 A.D., younger brother of Titus, had the hypogeum (two story subterranean area) built below the colosseum. The shows were conducted rain or shine. The colosseum was constructed so that a huge red canvas could be stretched over the entire structure to keep rain out. This could also be used to keep the sun out. Two hundred and forty mast corbels were positioned to hold the canvas. The covering was operated by thousands of expert sailors. Standing in the colosseum and imaging such a canvas above your head is awe inspiring. Today, visitors can see below the floor of the arena into areas to where a labyrinth of rooms, hallways, cages and underground passages exist in a two-level subterranean network. This is where the animals and gladiators were kept. By the early 19th century, the colosseum's floor lay buried under some 40 feet of earth, and all memory of its function—or even its existence—had been obliterated. In the 1800s, archaeological excavations attempting to reach it were stymied by flooding groundwater. Finally, under Benito Mussolini’s glorification of Classical Rome in the 1930s, workers cleared the earth and revealed the ancient underground for all. In addition to eighty (80) vertical shafts providing instant access to the arena for gladiators and caged animals, thirty six (36) trap doors in the wooden, sand covered arena floor allowed for special effects. The arena, like a modern theater, was flexible enough to adapt to any scenography. Stages were changed in little time with the help of complex machines, ramps and elevators. These devices were operated by slaves hidden in the levels below the Colosseum. During our tour, we walked around the exterior of the colosseum observing the wonderful detailed construction, arches, windows, columns and architecture. We then went inside to the main arena floor and looked upward into the tiered seating areas, reflecting on how the vast size of the colosseum must have impressed and perhaps scared the gladiators entertaining the massive noisy crowds. We also were able to climb the stairs and go to an upper terrace which provided the ability to walk around the interior of the colosseum, looking down on the arena floor and the open areas of the hypogeum below it. Over all the scope of the colosseum is not to be missed when visiting Rome. Seeing the colosseum with the help of a well informed guide can facilitate understanding of just how magnificent this structure and its history are.

title: Amazing Tour of Coliseum and Palatine Hill
text: Wonderful tour of the Coliseum and Palatine Hill. Our tour guide was exceptional!!! So amazing. Would definitely recommend Viator.

title: Amazing history that you can actually feel
text: Having read about the Colosseum for many years, studied during school years and heard accounts from other travellers, to actually be there is beyond words.  Simply amazing place that you must see and history that you should know.

title: A must see in Rome!
text: Pay for an underground and skip the line tour - makes the visit so much more pleasant and you get so much more out of it! the underground and views from the top level are the best part!

title: Historical and amazing!
text:
The colluseum in Rome is the most amazing site not only for history buffs but for sports enthusiasts.. just imagine what may have happened. 50,000 fans in a one of a kind sport facility built in 7 years..with engineering we don't know how to repeat. 
Animal fights by morning, executions at lunch and gladiator fights to the death afternoon.. Glad I wasn't born back then but sure is an incredible building.  And it survived 1,000 years of abandonedment.  A must see.  We did a guided tour with a group.  Bout tickets through viator on line. Had hearing sets we hich were great.  This was a package including the Palatine Hill.

title: A must see
text: This is an amazing sight.  Get the Skip The Line reservation for this site.  Loads to see and learn about the building of the structure as well as its uses throughout its lifetime.

title: The lines are ridiculous
text: Don't go unless you buy a skip the line ticket. It is ridiculous. Line up for 2 hours get inside then line up for another hour to get a ticket. Complete chaos! Colliseum obviously amazing but unless you have literally nothing better to do than queue up while on holiday don't do it!!!!

title: Colosseum, jewel of ancient architecture
text:
No need for tour guide...
Admission line is long. Get there early. Should take 1-2 hours to walk the site and appreciate this ancient architectural jewel.

title: Great Privileged Colosseum tour
text: Wear your sneakers, you’re in for a treat!  Our guide Julia was terrific, knowledgable and had a great sense of humor.  Good headphones, good navigation through the crowds, she really took the time to explain all the history behind everything.  A must see. Highly recommend.

title: As amazing as you expect
text: After seeing pictures of this structure for decades, it was very special to actually be there and climb around it.  Our Monogram tour guide did a great job explaining everything

title: Amazing walk up and tour
text:
First of all, I recommend that you walk to this attraction. You will be impressed by the ongoing historic renovations that seem to spring up everywhere. Then you turn a corner and there it is in the distance.

Even though we have all seen pictures of the Coloseum nothing compares to its grandeur even from afar. Then as you walk up the details are impressive.

Would recommend taking a tour which bypasses the lines. We started at the ground level and it was so much fun just looking up and daydreaming about what has happened in that very spot.

Then we took the underground tour which was mildly interesting. If I did it again would spend more time above ground and on the higher levels. Very interesting exhibits as well. Very fun experience.

title: it was awesome!
text: awesome structure,I did not go on a guided tour.I went in August it was very hot,be sure to have a hat, cold water.The only problem was the long queue to get ticket.

title: Great Guide
text: We had a guide called Peter who was informative, interesting and very pleasant with it. It was a tour which focused on certain parts of the Colosseum and was an ideal tour. When his tour had finished he took us to the Palatine and although he was not then our guide explained about this site and made sure we knew where to go.

title: Awe inspiring!
text: We had a combined tour of the forum and palentine hill, along with the Colosseum and it's difficult to frame in words quite how impressive, it was, simply awesome! Due to a mess up with our booking, we were put on a guided tour and In think I can recommend this option. Our guide was knowledgeable and enthusiastic and certainly added to the experience. You definitely want to skip the line too as the other queues were huge!

title: Colossal Colosseum
text:
The Colosseum stands as a beautiful relic of its former glory. It is still such an awesome structure and I can only imagine how intimidating it would have been in its full glory.

I did not go on a guided tour as the queue was too long and it was an extremely hot day. If visiting here in Summer, be sure to have a hat, cold water with ice in a flask and a parasol.

title: School group with guide
text:
Very busy but very worthwhile historically
Queue long, get prepaid tickets if possible, take time to see around. A guide is excellent for taking in all of the history

title: Amazing experience.
text: Will go back there one day as I didn't have enough time to see everything I wanted to. I was there until it closed because I wanted to stay and see it lit up in the evening. Lovely experience and well worth the visit

title: Not what I expected
text: I think i over-hyped it in my head, it wasn’t as grand as I had imagined it would be. It was worth the experience but not nearly as awe-Inspiring as other things in Rome

title: Magnificient
text: A "Must See" when in Rome. Joined Skip the Line English guided tour and entered through Arena Floor. Worth paying the price for.

title: history
text: Good to visit with parents. I took my mom here who usually don't travel much. she got impressive by the history that Rome holds.

title: If the Romans had played football.....
text: This is where they would have done it. It’s an amazing public ampitheatre. So much happened within its walls. Public gatherings. Politicians. Christians and lions (not so good). Mock naval battles (the place could be flooded). On and on. This is where Romans went for public entertainments. Close your eyes and let your imagination loose. Transport yourself back 2000 years in your mind. The Colosseum has recently been restored so it’s better than ever. Do not miss it.

title: A must see
text: Get up early to avoid the long queues in the sun for tickets to Colosseum. We arrived for 8am and there were less than 50 people in front of us. It opens at 8:30am. The ticket is also for the Forum and cost 12 euros for an adult so very good value. We recommend the audio guide which is additional cost. This does not include the arena tour.

title: Colosseum Forum and Palatine Hill
text: We bought our tickets from tourist information, they cost €25 and covered everything.. beware of private tours costing twice as much as not necessary.. we skipped queues and went straight in ... it really is breathtaking

title: Colosseum
text: Deffinitely a must see, when in Rome. Unfortunately we forgot to buy skip the line tickets so we had to wait. Great thing we visited it in October, so 30 minutes waiting and we were in!

title: Too long waiting line
text: I think everyone knows Colosseum and I'm sure it would be very interesting visit it and see it from inside. But the line for entrance is too long (waiting several hours). It just doesn't worth it.

title: A must for History buffs.
text:
You can't have a trip to Rome without visiting Colosseum.

Recommend booking a tour to learn majority of its history.

Fwiw, for some reason, Colosseum only has 1 bathroom and they allow 3,000 tourist each day.

title: Monumental
text:
You must visit this beautiful piece of history. We purchased a skip the line (totally worth it) guided tour to the Coloseum, Palatine Hills and Roman Forum.  

We did not finished the Roman Forum as we were to tired by then . Our tour was about 4-5 hours which to be honest, is a little to long but it you can break it into a 2 day trip. You will definitily enjoy it more (lots of walking, lots to see) 

Eat before you start, bring water with you. There is nothing close-by to buy a snacks other than a vending machine.

title: Great history
text: We went there Friday and as we got off the hop on hop off I asked where the ticket office was. We walked about a block and got in the line. About 20 minutes later we had our 12 euro tickets and walked about 2 blocks to the colosseum. There was a very long line they said was about 2 hours long or more and a very short line. The short line was for people who had tickets and the very long line was for people to buy tickets. The didn’t know you could go to the ticket office and get them there. We walked straight in without any waiting. This place is huge and had plenty of signs in English to know all about the colosseum. We walked all about the ground floor and then walked up steep stairs to the second level. The view there was great and they also had displays of ancient artifacts and statues with descriptions by them. If you go to Rome, you must see the colosseum.

title: Colosseum Pilgrimage
text: No trip to Rome could possibly miss this iconic and historic building. Don't go without pre-booking a tour with a guide which will help minimise the queuing which can be several hours long otherwise. Alongside , lies the ancient Forum so a joint tour is highly recommended. Comfortable shoes and plenty of water essential as is a good hat if visiting in the summer.

title: Amazing
text: As soon as it comes into view you’ll be overwhelmed by the size of the Colosseum. I’d recommend a visit and allow yourself time to explore

title: Overpriced and overcrowded
text: Constantly hassled by ticket touts and huge queues that dampened enthusiasm. Didn't bother going in in the end! But have been before and didn't over rate it then either. Other Colisseums in Rome that are free access btw.

title: Great arena built long before NHL
text: Amazing construction almost incredible that was built so long ago.  The architecture of this place is extraordinary and the architect definitely a genius.

title: Impressive
text: Not sure how to make a review of an important historic monument.. Made of many stones. Impressive. Recommend the audioguide.

title: Must see
text:
In Rome, you must see the Colosseum.
The history is amazing.
Think of Caesar watching lions and Christians.
Think of all the movies you’ve seen.

title: None Days in Rome
text: Our fourth trip and it gets better every time thanks to Silvia and Serena with Eyes of Rome. These tour guides are experts in everything Rome and so much fun to be with. The tours are spectacular and cover Rome and most of Italy. We will be back and we will use eyes of Rome.

title: Very Busy
text: Went to Rome at end of September thinking the tourist season was over but was sad to find out there were still hoards of people. The tour of the Colosseum was very interesting and Palatine Hill was worth the trip.

title: A Necessary stop
text: Every tour needs a skip the line but even with that we spent more time in line than actually walking aroungd looking at the relics. Even with a really good guide in Daniella , it was to crowded to enjoy.

title: Magnificent ancient relic
text:
What a massive building. So impressive even from a distance. The queues were very long and the wait to go in was over 2 hours so we looked from the outside.
Beware having a beer in the local cafes as they charge up to 50% service charge which you only discover when the bill arrives.

title: Crowded and late entry
text:
I used the Headout Tours booking.
Arrived at Colloseum on time for 0930 start but was told to wait until 1000 which seemed common.
Eventually shown in by the guide. Used the audio guide to self explore. Very interesting site. Gets increasingly busy as expected, it may be worth going late in the day or at night.
The guide met us at the exit point to take us to the Palatine Hill entry.
This was very beautiful with great views and due to the size of the sight more freedom to move and explore.

title: Great structure but average official tour
text: We got mesmerised by the history and gravitas behind this ancient marvel. But our official tour guide was nothing short of horrible! We were rushed through despite less crowds and certain cold figures were thrown at us. It definitely dampened the experience a bit

title: Lines go forever
text: This is a must to see in Rome, but the lines to get in are way too long especially in the heat of the day, I recommend you but ticket on line with a guide to skip the lines, if not you will no be a happy camper

title: The Colosseum
text: What movies depicted didn’t do it justice.  It’s so much more amazing up close.  Go inside if you can.

title: A colossal experience!
text:
Not our first visit to The Colosseum and absolutely not our last. It is and always be a very impressive and massive feeling to enter this historical Roman Arena. A MUST see historical sight. Put it on your bucket list!

Tip: book your tickets online long before your visit. Will save you hours of waiting.

title: A must to visit
text: This is a must to visit when in Rome but would recommend getting a private tour. Roma card queues were approx, 1.5 hours long to gain access. Our tour while a bit more expensive (20 people) skipped the whole queue.

title: Outstanding experience
text: Booked a tour via Trip Advisor and it was ace! Skipped the queue which is a must and visited the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. A fantastic tour all round.

title: Impressive
text: We visit place almost at last time slot and were pleased to find not so crowded. This will allow us peacefully wakled around, enjoy the architecture and fascinated views.

title: Interesting visit
text: We visited with friends - interesting to experience the colosseum close up. Definitely worth booking tickets in advance as it allows you to skip the (huge) non ticket queue.

title: Gotta See It
text: Most of us know what this is, where it is, and some other details.  But if you are in Rome its a must do.  There is no substitute for being there and learning more details about the place.  We would strongly recommend a tour guide.  Not one of those tours where you are 2 of 50+ people.  We had a personal tour with Dony from Context Travel - he was excellent.  Will use him again when we return with the kids in a few years.  Not sure how it worked, but we skipped 2 hour lines and other shorter lines and kept moving through the tour.  Worth the marginal extra cost.

title: A must see in Rome
text:
The Colosseum in Rome doesn't need a review it is a must see.
Recommend to book tikets in advance. At a guided tour is available in many languages.

title: Guided Tour Of Collesseum, including underground tour & Palatine Hill & Forum
text:
My sisters and I did the Walks of Italy tour. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!! Especially instead of the audio tours. I did an audio tour of Versailles, which was informative, but the Palace didn't "come alive" in the way our tour guide, Giamluigi "Gigi",  made the Collesseum come to life. I'm so glad we splurged on this tour!! From Gigi's personal, childhood remembrances of playing futbol in the Collesseum to the history he shared of the "games". It was all so interesting! He gave us details that aren't described in most audio tours.  Enjoyed it very much! Highly recommend going in early as possible. The crowds become stifling very early on. Can't imagine not doing at least a Skip-the-line tour pass, because we saw the waiting lines, and they are crazy!! 35,000 people visit daily. That makes for a very long wait!! 
Gigi was awesome!! Thank you so much for enhancing our trip!

title: Hassle trying to get free child ticket
text:
while the colosseum itself was obviously very impressive, was incredibly difficult trying to get in. i am 15, meaning i am entitled to a free ticket if i provide identification. we bought tix through big bus tours which was great. we skipped line and it was a breeze. asked lady working at big bus who led us there if my student id card (that had my name, d.o.b, photo, etc) would be ok and she said yes it would be fine. 

so we go to the desk we were told to go to in order to get my free ticket. handed over my id card and she basically said uh no this isnt id. my dad was very frustrated and despite my reserved personality, i voiced my frustration too. i said, this is literally called an id card like what more can we give. she said passports only. we were told to bring id, not explicitally passports so we had left them at airbnb to keep them safe since they are much more valuable than an id card which can be easily replaced. she pushed it back and we said again "it has my photo and my date of birth, we cant go back to get the passports now, the tour lady said it was fine.' she walked off in a huff and gave us the ticket but had so much attitude. these attractions really need to get their act together because we had many issues getting in to multiple places in rome and staff were not even respectful about it and had a real uppity attitude.

title: Pre-buy of a tour is a must
text:
Otherwise, you will have to make a line of 2 hours to get in. 
Additionally, depending of the tour you hire, you will have access to parts that you can’t go to if you enter on your own.
I purchased a tour that took us through the different levels.

title: A must do.
text: As you would expect it’s the centre piece of Rome and will be on anyone’s list to do. This was my first time and was a great cultural experience. Firstly avoid the “likey likey” man there are hundreds of them selling you crap “ I like your trainers (sneakers)” is a favourite line to get you chatting. Ignore them they go away but are persistent. If you enter from annibaldi street under the bridge there are official tour guides there who guide you to a official tour person for €35 per person, you are given a coloured sticker while you wait for the next tour this seems to keep other less official guides away from you so wear it in a prominent position . you get in the coliseum with a tour guide bypassing the 3hour que but you have to do the security check regardless. The guide describing all to see for 60 min but we had her for 90 well worth it, this cost also gives you entry into the palatine hill and roman forum,again with a guide who tells all you need to know and more it will all make sense after. This was another hour plus before being left to your own devices for as long as you want to take photos. This I can’t recommend enough. Was a great day in 25 degree heat so take plenty of empty bottles as there are free water fountains around directly outside the metro station is the closest. Hope you Enjoy as much as I did.

title: awesome!
text:
WOW! must do if in Rome! How did they build that??!! 
Look out for the cheeky Centurians touting for photos. can be pricey to let you take their photo!

title: Roman Coliseum
text: Outstanding visit. Had a skip the line tickets and personal guide with about 20 people. I’ll be back

title: No long wait and good price
text:
Bit tricky to identify the actual staff as tour leaders were everywhere. Lines are fairly accessible and clearly marked and staff were happy to answer questions. We were let in right on our booking time and once through security we had no lines at all which was great given the hot weather and busy crowds. 
Definitely a must see but maybe skip the tour inside if you don't have a specific interest. Very cool.

title: Colosseum
text: What an impressive visit at the Colosseum with a guide from the crown (purple sticker) he brought back the history that took place

title: History at it's finest
text: I mean how can you visit Rome and not visit the one and only Colosseum? It's magnificent and stunning. Hard to believe it was conceived so long ago.

title: Outstanding piece of architecture
text: Grand construction, engineering and architecture- all in one. Come around 9 and you will not have the hassle of long queuing. Impressive!

title: Take a guided tour of the Palantine Hill, the Forum & Colosseum - it is really cool!
text:
So the Colosseum is smaller than I thought it might be - but nevertheless it is really cool to walk through - along with the Palantine Hill & the ruins of the forum and temples surrounding it. It is obviously very rich in history and it is impressive how they were able to build such big structures over 2,000 years ago - and some are still standing! 

I highly recommend a guided tour which will enable you to skip REALLY long lines and also will provide a lot more detail and colorful history to your visit!

title: Terrific - buy a guided tour ticket.... well worth the money!
text: Terrific! Buy a guided tour ticket....well worth the money! The lines are ridiculously long if you’re just buying a ticket and see it on your own.

title: Touring Italy
text:
The Coloseum is immense..knowing it has withstood so many centuries of wear
..and still intact enough to make one feel history come alive..the weather was perfect..we elected not to tour inside..very long lines..walking around the perimeter was astounding.One could almost hear the masses of ages gone by cheering and jeering the combatants..

title: Beautiful view of history
text: Amazing look into the history of Rome and the Roman Empire.  Took a private tour and loved it.  Would go again but still with private tour.

title: Spectacular
text: But you don't miss much by going inside after enduring long lines even with a pre purchased ticket.  Do a walk about of the perimeter and be wowed at its immensity and  construction

title: Better than the movie
text: This is really gorgeous. Be there is like to go inside a book and live the history. Go there, walk around, do a break to just see around.

title: Spectacular venue but watch out for the fake gladiators - they ain’t Russell Crowe !
text: Amazing venue and worth the visit. Watch you don’t get ripped off by the fake gladiators and photographs as many tourists still get caught out.

title: Must visit
text: From Termini station its only two stop and get off Colosseo station, walk around few minutes..very easy to find the location..And the experienced was a memorable very interesting place to visit and explore and to go briefly with the Roman history..one of my bucket list ✅ ! Amazing place..Highly recommended...And to everyone who wants to come and visit Colosseum I highly recommend to book ahead your ticket or book online a group tour guide make life easy, the line is absolutely chaos....👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻😍

title: Better from the outside
text: Horribly busy. Timed entry with multi-ticket to Palatine and Forum.  Not made clear that my husband's useful penknife (corkscrew/bottleopener etc) was banned and so we lost it to the security never to be seen again.What is more the so called two day entry only works if you do the Colosseum the first day and the Palatine Hill and Forum the second. Also this too was not made clear so we were reused entry to the Forum on the second day as we had already been to the Palatine Hill in the afternoon of the first. All very confusing in the crowds, noise and heat.

title: You have to see it, but not the most interesting place
text: We did the underground tour with Walks of Italy.  The guide did an amazing job, but I did not find the Coliseum itself all that interesting for the amount of time we spent there. Palatine Hill was of more interest.

title: Antic, full of history and beautiful
text: Colosseum is one of a lifetime structure. Build by slaves in time of the roman Nerone, it represent a park of history of people and life in the capital city of Italy.The amfiteather is located in the heart of Rome, near to Roman Forum.I recommend september and october the best period of year to visit. Also, take a guide with the group.It costs 35 € and you will listen all the history that it represents :)

title: Must visit
text: A great area with the heart of rome . Feels like the history of nations started from there . The desgin and architect of it makes it extremely fascinating . Entry fees around 16 euros p.p .

title: amazing roman monument
text: i believe none has to be introduced to this place. Practical tip- if you stay in Rome few days go there during the day and in the evening!

title: Awesome
text: Exceptional. Went with group and skipped a few lines. Amazing history lesson. Guide did an exceptional job in English.

title: A Must See!
text:
We pre booked tickets and entered with a group. The queue was very long so recommend pre booking. 
No more than an hour needed to walk around it. Definitely a must see when visiting Rome.

title: Get your Roma Pass in advance, then schedule your time slot
text: We bought a Roma Pass online, but found it very confusing on how to get a ticket and time slot to see the Colosseum. Some information found online was incorrect and should be ignored! Best way to use the Roma Pass to see the Colosseum is go first thing when they open in the morning and get your time slot for the day. Make sure you have your pass with you when getting your ticket at the Colosseum window. While waiting for your time to go in, go see the forum and other places in the area. If you want to go see the dungeons and underground areas, you need to reserve tickets far in advance. This would be separate from the Roma Pass ticket.

title: Went on 4th October- Entry was free
text: We went to Colosseum on 4th October and the entry was free that day. We went at around 3pm and we enetered the main area within 10 mins. There were no crazy lines or queues. It was an easy process

title: Too commercial
text: I expected it to be busy but was disappointed with the number of touts and parts kept getting closed off - not my favourite site

title: Excellent monument. Very busy, leave plenty of time
text:
The colosseum is a beautiful place to see. The staff on the day gave us wrong information which meant that we queued a long time for wrong things, so take your time and ask more than one person. 
The area is super busy, careful of pick pocketing. 
If you book a time at the colosseum, do not line up for the archeological site next door near that time. The site is huge and you need a good 2 hours for it.
The colosseum takes around 2 hours too, depending on how busy it is. 
I was told that November-February are quiet and the March-October are super busy.

title: Magnificent
text: We loved exploring the Colosseum! I’d definitely say to book your ticket in advance and still get there for 8:30! We arrived at 9:30 and had to buy our ticket, we didn’t get in until 12:30 😞. On the bright side it was so worth waiting for!

title: Great venue.  We viewed areas others could not access.
text:
Wasn't sure I would like this activity.  All six persons in our group said this was wonderful and a great learning experience.  I would recommend the tour we took to anyone.  

If you have the complete tour, you will find there are steps involved.

We also had a tour of the area of Pantine hill.  Excellent.

title: A must do visit when in Rome
text:
For the best deal, book direct on the Colosseum website. This allows fast entry and a place on a guided tour.

The tour takes about 45 minutes (once it gets going!) and contests for space with other tours and self guided visitors. You certainly gain insight from the tour and once over, you are left to explore at your leisure.

I would advise early morning visits to allow time to then explore the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum adjacent (entry is included in the ticket if you pick the right one.)

title: Must see
text: One of the greatest moment of our trip to the Rome. This impressive art work is a must see for every Rome visitor. To avoid long waiting lines, buy ticket for 12E at the ticket office of Roman Forum. After that you can go directly to Colloseum and then you have another 48hours to visit Roman Forum.

title: Very Busy
text: Beautiful structure and so much history make this a must see, but lots of nearby construction for new metro station and packed with tourists.

title: Colosseum with Arena Access and Ancient Rome Small Group Tour
text: Dennis was one of the best tour guides...he was able to depict Rome in a way that made us feel a part if the once great city.

title: Fascinating
text: Absolutely fascinating! I had booked a guided tour but had trouble finding them at first. Glad I finally did because it was super exciting. My only complaint is that I booked the skip the line tour and turns out the day I went it was free admission and skip the line was not valid. Having said that, lines were way too long and I didn't get a refund. So, just make sure when you book it that it's not free admission day!

title: Amazing building
text: It was better than we expected. We didn't realise there was so much history still being discovered around the colosseum! Palatine hill and the forum are a must.

title: Colosseum
text:
We booked over here for this trip paid to skip the queue, What a great choice, we had a video of how rome would have looked then the tour guide took us to the entrance. We were inside in 10 mins our local guide was fantastic told us many facts about the
 Colosseum Well worth a visit

title: Lots of history
text: Great place to visit. We learnt lots about the history of the colosseum and surround area. It’s very busy so per book tickets

title: family day out
text: try and prebook. lots of hagglers that spoil the experience for everyone. great piece of history that the locals are just ruining. Govt needs to step in and place some rules

title: So not miss!
text: What a wonderful experience to walk through the Colosseum and the Forum. What a spectacular place! My Husband and I purchased tickets online, and instead of using a formal guide service we used Rick Steve’s audio guide. It was terrific! We were able to walk around on our own and at our own pace. Spectacular, and a must see if you are in Rome.

title: Are you not Entertained!
text: Amazing place to visit and a must stop if your in Rome. Like anything else in Rome there is usually long lines to get in. I personally think just looking at it from the outside is good enough. Visit Rome during the Fall to minimize the wait times or you can always book a tour if that’s your thing.

title: Great Experience!
text: If you are in Rome, this definitely a landmark that you don’t want to miss. It is one of the seven/wonders of the world, so be sure to include this location on your agenda. You won’t regret it!

title: Classical Roman experience
text: What can you say?  It had been 38 years between visits but the Colosseum is one of the most special places I have ever witnessed.

title: Trip to Colosseum
text: This place is stunning! I would recommend you get an official skip the queue ticket, even in low season. Do not fall for the "skip the line touts" they charge you to push in!

title: Colloseum
text: Breathtaking. It was wonderful to have your own private tour guide. You can here everything and ask questions. They are so passionate to tell you everything they know.

title: Amazing!
text: It just amazes me, all the history that is here. Walking around trying to imagine what it is was like. Our guide from What a Life Tours, Carlotta, did a wonderful job taking us back in time.

title: Colosseum visit
text: Unbelievable sporting arena......hope it doesn't collapse or fall-down......ideal place for a cricket game or a rugby match. Recommended.

title: Incredible. The blueprint for all arenas.
text: It is stunning. Built all those years ago by people ahead of their time. An amazing building. Yes, we know what happened in the arena both good & bad, but look @ the design, built with hand power, not machines.

title: A MUST!!
text: I was almost going to skip the Colosseum in our Itinerary but Thank God we didnt. We only spent a few minutes here but to see it in person is majestic. What a historic piece!

title: 4 days in Rome
text: Incredible structure, very interesting and informative but arrive early otherwise long ques and gets crowded........................................

title: Sensational, great feeling to be there
text: Such a famous spot and such a long and incredible history. I was sensational to be there and I learnt a lot too. Suggest download the Rick Steves audio guide its free and knowledgable and entertaining.

title: the history is so overwhelming.
text: there is so much to see and learn that you need more time to see it all. we enjoyed every tour and tour guide, learned an exceptional amount of history

title: Amazine
text: So much better in real life - very long queues but you can buy your ticket and come back at an allotted time if you want

title: Beautiful
text: We visited the Colosseum during the day and the lines were massive. Booking a tour to skip the lines is the way to go.

title: Great trip
text: This was an amazing tour with the underground with Viator. Lots of walking and lots of stairs , just so you know. Our guide was very informative. We had a group of 8.

title: amazing to see
text: My wife and I visited here, and, as she said it was just like a sell out pop concert all leaving at once. There were 10's of thousands there, but plenty of space to wander around. Fantastic sight.

title: Great but confusing with tickets
text: We tried the guided tour of 45 min that we bought online the day before. This goves access to colosseum, pallatine hill and forum romanum for the whole day and pallatine hil and forum on the next day. Fine value for the money. Only problem (now forgotten) is all the digfferent suppliers of tickets and how digficult it is to find the right ticket offices/lines. So advice: buy online vis coop-culture.it and go in front of the colloseum / where all the lines are with your proof of purchase and the find look for the official staff wearing white shirts. They are very nice and patient :-). And bring water for when standing in line if its a sunny day.

title: WOW
text: This was an amazing opportunity to see this shell and to learn all about the history of the Colosseum.  Don't pass this up while in Rome!

title: Magic and Fantastic
text: I am really impressed by the structure  and its preservation. Great experience and I could imagine myself back in Roman empire.

title: Amazing
text: This place takes you back in Roman times when the place was lively and operational. Great experience to witness history. Amazing architecture, though it is ruins, it is still gripping and one of the most visited attractions in rome.

title: Great
text: Went  at 5pm sun going down was great to see very busy audio guide was okay too book online hopefully going back next year to take my children

title: Honeymoon in Roma!!
text: Wow! Standing in the middle of history like that! Gorgeous time of year! Great guide!! Lots of info about thousands of years of mankind living here! Incredible place!!

title: Beautiful and Historic
text:
I waited over 30 yrs to see the Colosseum. I love all things Roman and that type of stuff. The Colosseum did not disappoint. It is absolutely beautiful, and impossible to even imagine how it was built so long ago. We did a tour with a separate company, and it was well worth it. We didn’t have to wait in any lines, and we were able to go in the underground sections, the arena floor, and the other upper levels. This is an absolute MUST see if you are in Rome. 

Only warning I would say... be cautious and keep aware of your surroundings around/near the colosseum, because this is a prime area for the hagglers and street vendors trying to get you to buy their junk for absurd prices. It may be rude to say, but just tell them firmly NO and keep moving. They will move on to their next prey.

title: Sad
text: Not like u expect they are slowly repairing it but not as it was but newer design doesn't fit in will old style more like a cyborg of its originality

title: Amazing archetural feat
text: this is truly a masterpiece of archetectural wonder.  When in Rome, one must visit this location.  It is nearly impossible to conceive and points out vividly the superiority of this culture. Ancient recorded history in the americas is only 300 to 400 years while Roman hisory spans an unimaginable multitude of centuries.  Go see this wonder and imagine what took place there.  be sure to arrange for an intimate guide.  Be this I mean a tour with only a handful of other people (4-6).  You will have questions and do not want to have to compete with 20 other tourists.

title: Colossal!
text: Use a local guide to get the most of your visit to Rome. The traffic through the exhibits was heavy but the written explanations were very good.

title: Good
text: Well worth the visit. Amazing that this was built when it was. Bought tickets online to avoid the queues.

title: External of Colusseum
text: Crowds to get inside, having once seen it, we enjoyed walking the perimeter and the nearby Palatine Hill.

title: Rome must see!
text: Iconic and a must see on everyone’s trip to Rome. Not as busy as the Vatican, we took a guided tour and walked palatine hill and the fort ourselves. We’ve all seen the pictures and many films, it’s a great piece of architecture and to see it for real is great, it is busy but that’s what you would expect. Tick it off the list and then go for a walk and sample a leisurely late lunch in one of the many cafe’s nearby...

title: Amazing tour
text: We took a guided tour including the arena floor. The tour was amazing, and the guide very informative. There were a lot of other visitors as to be expected and at times we felt a bit shepherded around. But a visit to the colosseum when in Rome is a must.

title: Amazing place
text: Visited Colosseum with son. Did the 45 Colosseum tour, which was well worth it. Wojld strongly recommend.

title: Fantastic historical site
text: Get a skip the line ticket. Worth going in and walking around the top. See it at night.  We had a guide. She provided useful information.

title: Pre Buy Tickets
text:
The queues to get into the colosseum are huge so i would definitely recommend buying tickets online before you go. Our tickets provided us access to the colosseum via the gladiators entrance and then we were left to tour the colosseum for just over an  hour before meeting up with the guide again. The guide then led us to the Roman Forum and got us in before allowing us to explore the ruins at our own pace. 

In my opinion it is a must do when visiting Rome.

title: Fantastic site but very long lines
text:
One of the magnificent structures of ancient rome.
This is a must visit land mark.
But be advised the lines are very very long.
Could take from 1hr to 2hr.
If you don't want to wait for so long and you can afford it buy skip the line tickets in advance.

title: Amazing
text: Managed to turn up on the Sunday that was the free one of the month so this was a bargain. Unfortunately outside people where still falling for the touts offering skip the line, our line was shorter! Inside is just wow!

title: Colosseum
text: Outstanding!! This was probably my favorite tour of Rome. There was so much to learn about the Colosseum and the surrounding areas. (Romsn Forum and Palatine Hill) Our guide Gigi made this experience incredible. He was informative, funny and made it a personal experience! Would highly recommend it!!

title: Brilliant Tour with Fantastic guide Dennis
text: The tour took about 3 hours but the knowledge Dennis has about the history is phenomenal. His passion about the history shows through his explanations and he gives opportunities for questions. He is an easy voice to listen to! The tour took us through the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and a Roman Forum.

title: Amazing History
text: A magnificent architectural masterpiece. If only the walls could speak about the bloody battles that went on in this amphitheater. The sheer size leaves you speechless.

title: The Wow Factor!
text: It was amazing to be walking down the street and there was the Colosseum!  All that was expected and more.  Just surreal!

title: Coloseum
text: The most powerfull site in rome. Amazing building from 2000 years ago...70000 sests that was fealed in 30 minutes!

title: Awesome
text: Get a small group guided tour as s must. Miss the queues and you get so much extra from a guide. Colloseum is just mind blowing and you really feel yourself walking in the steps of ancient history. Truly fabulous

title: Truly impressive
text: The Colosseum is a must see in Rome truly inspiring. So many of the features an designs used in this 2000 year old building are still in use in football stadiums today.

title: Awesome
text: Learned more correct information about past civilian society and how things really were. So many similarities as today in our country. The politics as well. History really does repeat itself.

title: Beautiful
text: What an amazing place. So much history. Very busy lots of people. We pre booked a tour and got in very quickly. A must when in Rome

title: A must see, one for your bucket list
text: All that can be said about the Colosseum has already been said, all I can add is that it gives you just that feeling of what it must have been like living in ancient Rome.  Just brilliant.

title: Worth the queueing
text:
We stood in a line to gain entry for an hour in pouring rain. Which was good as otherwise it would’ve been a longer wait. Once inside the audio guide didn’t work and it was a hassle to exchange it. 
But all in all, very much worth a visit. Amazing building and a great history lesson and a reminder of how power messes people up and what they can do when not stopped.

title: Wow!!!
text: This was my favorite because the tour took us to the floor of the Colosseum and the view was breathtaking. So many interesting and historic places to see in Rome.

title: To many people.
text:
Colosseum is of course a very impossant building and history. 
I think it has way to many visitors. It is just as interesting to watch the bilding from the outside. If you enter, expect several queues and people everywhere.

title: What you would  expect
text: I'm not sure taking a tour is any great advantage other than quicker entries.  Most of what you see inside you have already seen on tv. It was sad to me to see the destruction from pollution and stupid previous attempts at saving the structure.  I enjoyed viewing the outside more than the inside.  Such a world treasure deserves better care.

title: Oh the history
text:
Amazing visit. The history within this place, you can almost feel it.
Make sure you precook a time online.

title: Worth a visit
text: Piece of advise book skip the queue tickets! Was very interesting & defo worth a visit. A lot of history & information. There is restoration work going on so a lot of scaffolding about

title: Amazing
text: if you are visiting Rome you must visit the Colosseum. It is amazing to think how Romans built that enormous building without the tools that we have now a days. Make sure you pre-book and reserve your visit. There are a lots of different companies which do that. I also think it is worth it to have a guided tour.

title: Live History
text: This was indeed a very interesting place to visit in Rome.  The stones tell a story and if you are a history buff, prep yourself before you go to better appreciate it.

title: Beautiful Rome and a watchful eye for the Pick Pockets
text:
ROME IS BEAUTIFUL ~ WATCH OUT FOR PICK POCKETS
We have just returned from a wonderful holiday in Italy for 2 weeks. We used Trenitalia (country) trains which were punctual and clean and we travelled to Venice & Firenze, then back to Rome. We would suggest buying all your train tickets online through Trenitalia and reserve your seats. We bought some tickets at half price online as opposed to the ticketed price when we arrived at the Station. 
For the last few days of our trip in October 2019, we stayed in the area near the Colosseum which was near the Rome Termini. 
Pick pockets came on the train and targeted us. 
A local girl told us to be aware of the 2 men standing near our cases and to call the Italian Polizie if he confronted us.
We were followed from the 'metro train' we caught from Civitavecchia to Rome. It was very frightening. A girl held my husband's arm and would not let him go, until we gave her information. A stern look from me forced her to look away and she let go.
We were then followed from the station to our taxi, and just as we were getting in the taxi, they wanted to know where we were going and the taxi driver told the man to 'go away'. We were vigilant everywhere but as told by the locals, we stayed where the gypsies usually live, and we were vulnerable in places like Rome Termini metro, buses, airport, train stations and museums. 
Italy is a wonderful place but do not underestimate the pick pockets and their ability to affect your holiday. Stay watchful and be vigilant.
Grazie Mille.
Val & Peter, Knoxfield, Victoria

title: Magnificent!
text:
Absolutely fabulous,the history of the colosseum is rich and fascinating, if you want to go inside book ahead with skip the line otherwise you will be queuing up for at least 3 hours .
Well worth a visit,highly recommend

title: Anciant Rome
text: Great! If you go after 16:30 waiting in the line for buying tickets takes 15 to 20minutes and for 17euros you pay for 1 entrance to Colosseum and with that you can enter Forum Romanum at that hour it is less crowded and waiting in the line is really worth it.

title: Re:Night tour
text: We did the night tour and it was great. There were not the crowds and it was a small group of 20 people. We also went to the underground and it was all amazing. Would do this again in a minute.

title: Whatever you thinking think BIGGER..
text: I knew it would be huge but never did I think it would beyond massive. Be prepared for lots if people but they do have a elevator for those who can’t do those big steps. Totally worth seeing if your there and make sure you have a guide. I can’t image doing Rome without one.

title: Organised chaos
text: A must do when in Rome. Buy your "timed access" tickets from the ticket office at the Forum or Palatine Hill rather than at the Colosseum itself to beat the queue. Ignore the tour touts and just walk to the front of the queue at the Colosseum (just to the left of the long ticket queue) 10 minutes before your allocated time - easy peasy. We purchased tickets at the Forum at about 8.45 which came with a 9.35 entry time. Just long enough to grab a drink, get your bearings, and take some stunning external pictures before turning up at the entry point at 9.25. Enjoy.

title: Take the underground tour
text: We booked the underground tour, skipped the line. Our tour guide was an archeologist, lots of info and when she always stopped us in the shade to talk. Tour included the colosseum, forum and Palentine hills, it was great

title: Nice!
text: I had a day to kill while waiting on a customer. There is lots of construction going on around the Colosseum right now but you can still walk around it and get some good pictures. Evening is a good time of day now, you get the sun at your back for photos. I do highly recommend purchasing tickets in advance to get though. The line to “pay at the door” is longer than most amusement park rides, and if you don’t do the research in advance, you won’t be able to get a ticket online via smartphone if later in the day for same day entry. So needless to say, next time I pass through, hopefully I’ll have time to plan...

title: Roman pleasures
text: An amazing structure, once inside seeing the working of the Collesseum just defies belief. Recommend a guide as the information they pass on is amazing, putting everything into context

title: Lots and lots of people but impressive
text: Of course this place is packed with tourists.  We hired a private guide who got us skip the line passes which were very nice.  It’s an impressive structure but packed with people.  A current restoration has closed the lower level for now but you can see from above. We were able to find a few spots were we could get a good view away from the huge tour groups. Those huge groups should be banned they cause a lot of problems and are a horrible way to see the sights. Avoid them. You can hire your own guide for about the same price if there are four or more in your group.

title: Lots of walking
text: I thought I was prepared for the walking but the area is so huge. Our guide was very knowledgeable but a bit of information overload...I'm sure my photos will prompt my memory. Tour also included Palatine Hill and the Forum.

title: Amazing! Go early or book in advance
text:
This was such a amazing monument to visit, there's no words to describe its magnifency.
Okay so pratical advice: The ticket for the coloseo includes entrance to Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. If you're a citzen of european union with age below 18 it's free and between 19-25 it's only 2 euros! 
We got to the line at 10ish, we stood in line for almost one hour and as soon as we were getting to ticketline they stopped letting people in that hadnt already passed the security check as it had reached its full capacity, we just didn't understand if it was for the whole day or until people started exiting, but we think it might be the first option.
So either get there really early (depends which month youre going, in summer maybe its nearly impossible to do this) or just be sure and book a time slot in advance so you dont have to worry

title: Wonder of the world
text: Nothing like this in the world. A must see. Definitely should be on everyone’s bucket list. You don’t have to go in to be amazed. Be prepared for crowds and pushy vendors

title: Skip the line Tour for Collosium, palantine hill and roman forum
text:
Totally worth it as the lines were really long. Skip the line is a real help. All the three are situated close to each other at walkable distance. We covered Collosium first and then went fir the others. Try to get the morning slot as the three require time to go through and get a feel of the place. 
Seeped in history, the place is a worthy visit at night too with the play of light and shadows. 

We did both as we were staying at walking distance from the Collosium.

title: Amazing
text: To be standing in something that old and observe how complicated the structure is with it's underground tunnels and series of lifts, makes you appreciate how advanced the Roman Empire was.  Well worth a visit to anyone who appreciates history.  Make sure however, that you either book tickets in advance of your visit or spend the small amount of extra money and do the guided tour route.  Otherwise be prepared to spend over 2 1/2 hours waiting in line just to get a ticket.  While I was one of those who stood in line, it was still worth the visit and was one of the highlights of our trip to Rome.

title: Worth a visit!
text: We visited in the early morning on a weekday to try to beat the crowds. We first bought a combo ticket at the Mamertine Prison museum next to the Forem. This combo ticket allowed us to visit the Colosseum, Forum/Paletine Hill ruins, and the Mamertime Prison. You can also just buy a combo ticket at the Forum ticket booths if you don't want to see the Mamertime Prison. But you reserve a Colosseum reservation time at the time you buy your ticket. The lady at Mamertime Prison booked us a 10am time slot to visit the Colosseum. No line there, unlike the Forum/Colosseum ticket booths. We entered 10 minutes before our time, went through security and then spent several hours exploring the inside on our own. No need to pay for a tour, unless you want to visit the area beneath the original floor. They have an excellent museum in the second deck area that gives a lot of great information about the history. After the Colosseum we visited the Forum/Paletine Hill, and finally the prison where the Apostles Peter and Paul were held by the Romans.

title: Incredible!
text: I was brought to tears by the site of this structure.  I would definitely recommend using a guide.  This was the highlight of my trip to Italy.  Definitely a “do not miss”!

title: Amazing!!
text:
Loved the Coliseum. Waited about an hour to get in and was a 
 awesome experience!
Went in October and the weather was lovely, low 20's centigrade.  
Was not worth buying the skip the line queue in my opinion but it would save you 45 minutes if you do.

title: AMAZING ! But -  Guided Tour ⭐⭐
text:
What can I say that hasn't already been said here .? 
  It is amazing , a real part of history and just how advanced the Romans were . I won't go into the history just review our experience. 
  A GUIDED TOUR IS A MUST !!  You will learn so much more and be shown all the best spots for photos etc. Pre book ypur tour as they do sell out. Our tour guide was great , we skipped a rather huge queue as well which was a bonus. 
 Learnt so much and just having an expert I form us really made the day. .
 * Tip- it gets really hot so try early morning , especially if you have kids. .
5 stars

title: Truly amazing
text: An amazing look at history.  Hard to grasp the beauty of this monument.   Feels like you can almost imagine being there during the Roman Empire.  One of my favorite stops on our vacation

title: A must see
text: Visiting Rome means meeting with history!!! Colosseum is a part of Roman and world history /heritage. Don't just go around, get inside. You will be amazed!!!

title: Huge.
text: The sheer size is breath taking. Give yourself plenty of time to visit. There are queues so be aware.

title: Amazing with a Capital A-Go with a Professional Tour Guide to get the full experience.
text: We took a tour of the Colosseum with Roma Experience Tours. The tour was called Gladiators Arena, Colosseum and Underground Tour. Our guide was an archeologist with years of experience, not just with the Colosseum, but was an expert in Egyptian archeology and hieroglyphics as well. She was very interesting. We were able to visit the dungeon area, the arena floor and levels an upper level of the Colosseum. Afterwards we toured Palatine Hill and the Forum. Lots of walking, 4 hours worth, but definitely worth the time and money.

title: The reason to visit Rome
text: The colosseum is definitely one of the greatest wonders of the world. Once inside the arena, you’re transformed to a different world. The size will amaze you and the fact that it is still so well preserved is great. I love the fact that the Italian government is doing more to preserve this site by closing the roads around it to protect it from pollution. If visiting, definitely go with a guide so that you can hear all the wonderful stories & history that accompanies this place.

title: A place to visit
text: One of the most important places in the world, you’ll understand a bit of our civilization, because all started here.

title: Get Your Guide Colosseum, Palatine Hill, Forum tour.
text: Our tour guide Marcus made this a fantastic tour which we will remember forever. He is a historian and his passion for history was infectious. For 3 hours he had us spellbound with the tales and facts of this fascinating place. Highly recommended.

title: A must see location
text: If you are in Rome there are two places you have to see with your own eyes. One of them is the Colosseum. Despite the thousands of tourists a day, the area around it is free accessible and really clean.

title: Stunning but too busy
text: Really enjoyed our visit to the Colosseum however the queues to buy tickets to get inside the arena were horrendous so we didn't bother. Can wander around outside for free and can see some of the Roman Forum from the paths. Lots of ticket touts telling you they can help you skip the queues, ignore them. If you really want to see inside buy a ticket online before you travel. There are packages for Cosseum and the Roman Forum but they are quite expensive.

title: Colsseum
text: Great monument and when you visualise what it was used for it is amazing. To get in there are people everywhere and lines for miles. I pre booked our ticket online for a specific time to attend and there was no line as you go through the reservation line. Felt sorry for all the people lining up while we just walked in with no line. Book a ticket on line is all I can say.

title: Crowd is big but
text: October is start of the off season but still lots of people. Per order tickets with arrival time. Line looked crazy if you didn’t. Also lots of bracket guys and people trying to sell jump the line access. With your pre purchased tickets look for the little sign with a lower case “i” that is were you go in. Kids are free. Once you walk in and are past security stop at ticket office just before having your main ticket scanned to get your free kids ticket.

title: Everything I Expected
text: I was warned the Colosseum was under construction, a bit worn out, etc. But I was still in awe of this structure and its history from the past centuries. We took a group tour which pointed out some things I would have missed on my own. A visit to the Colosseum is well worth it to anyone visiting Rome.

title: Leave it til later the afternoon
text: We arrived around 5.30pm and didn't have to queue to get in, closes at 7pm. However,  we didn't do a guided tour, we just looked around ourselves. Was much cooler in the afternoon, not too many people and great photos as the sun was going down.

title: Dinner
text: Very good food. Friendly service. Excellent Aperol Spritz. Family atmosphere. Even though we came later, there was always a free table for us

title: Breathtaking
text:
We took a tour of the Coliseum and Roman Forum on Wednesday. 
We walked to the Coliseum and booked the beat the queue tour when we got there .
It was well worth it,  as we went for a Peroni then our tour started.
The Coliseum is awesome as is Roman Forum - The history is awesome. 
Well worth a visit.

title: A must see
text: We didn’t have time to book a tour but drove to it and walked around out front. Such a grand thing to see and all lit up at night making it even more spectacular.

title: Amazing!
text: Definitely worth a see, even if you don’t do a tour of the inside.  It is breathtaking both during the day and the evening.

title: As expected it was awesome
text: Without this your visit can’t be completed and its a great place with lots of points for taking good photos. It must be visited during day and night as well.

title: Great to see with arena access
text: Best part is newly allowed arena access, especially for kids. Strongly recommend. Colosseum can be seen with one go - once in a lifetime, but arena access gives it new flare and aspect worth going again.

title: Great place but why is it so poorly organised?
text:
Great place, when you get in. Sadly Italians accept so many "skip the line salesmen" making the visit a small nightmare. Signs are extremely poor outside which causes chaotic situations. Maybe it is on purpose to reduce numbers of visitors. 
Audioguides inside where broken 2 out 4... how poor a system can accept this. And you think they just put them back again despite the tourists complaining. 
When all that is said it is a must to visit.

title: Guided tour
text: Great place to visit but do with a guide or you will miss a lot. You need at least an hour to get the benefit. We enjoyed every minute.

title: Yes do believe the hype
text: Many have mentioned feeling underwhelmed/disappointed when they finally got to the Coliseum. I have heard it mentioned that it was small, that it was all broken, and- and this is my favourite, said by some supposedly well-travelled celebrity who always takes part in travel- themed discussions- quite rundown, dilapidated and dirty. The thing is, when I first visited I was 9 years old. I was not old enough to have developed rose-tinted glasses for the great site. I went and saw and accepted and for all its old, ancient splendour. I fell instantly in love, probably the first time I understood unconditional love as I had absolutely no preconceived ideas about it! And now that I have grown up and will it now look, perhaps underwhelming? Perhaps a lot smaller and dirtier and messier and more broken than those that I saw so many times in my dreams at 9 years old? It was exactly as I remembered in all its splendour. The passing years made not a bit of difference to its beauty, it is I who has grown up and will grow old while it remains the same. And I am able to come to it now with my own history and my own experiences and all my own past ups and downs to bear. And it is more magnificent than ever. So... highly recommend, but if possible, please try and forget any preconceived ideas. And it is over 2,000 years old, perhaps it is allowed to be a bit old and dilapidated.

title: Amazing History and a Must See while in Rome
text:
Stayed in Navona which is right in the city centre and tourist area so everything is around here.

The Colosseum is a must see while in Roma. Sad that so many died here in ancient times. The entire surroundings are so beautiful and so much history here. Easy to get around the area too.

title: Colosseum
text: The Colosseum, even just from the outside, is an absolute wonder. A must see while in Rome! We visited twice, once to walk around the outside and the following day for a guided tour of the inside. If you’re on a budget and cannot do a paid tour I recommend taking the metro system to “Colosseo” and at least walking around the Colosseum from the outside. It’s “wonder of the world” status is much deserved.

title: To visit once in a lifetime
text:
I went this October to Colosseum and will not experience this the second time.
The building itself is exciting to visit if you are to thing if its history.
But the never ending line that took more than 3 hours was exhausting :(

title: Absolute must-see in Rome
text: The history, architecture, and construction of this amazing structure is captivating.  We had a guide, so understanding the back-story made the visit more enjoyable.  Late afternoon visit was not too busy and made for some nice photo opportunities.

title: A must!
text: Been visiting several times since I was a child in 1973 - even though more restrictions apply over time it is still an amazing place you have to visit... get your online reservation early enough and visit as early as possible in the morning.

title: unforgettable
text: visiting this site takes you back in time and you can see the past in front of you.    The size of this facility is incredible and pictures do not provide the real feel of the place.   Do take time to also visit the forum across the street with so much history.

title: Huge historical place
text: We enjoyed a stroll here but it was a bit too sunny so we didnt stay long. The crowd was also a bit overwhelming.

title: Europe anniversary
text: We visited the Colosseum during our stay in Rome, well worth the trip, just to have the opportunity to visit such a historical monument was a value to our anniversary

title: ROMA pass
text: With the ROMA pass, we paid for an reservation fee and a slot time so we weren’t waiting hours in the line which you can order online and just pick up your receipt from an office which is a 10 min walk from the Colosseum. There was thousands of people, even in October. There is staff around which a very helpful, just gotta be careful of the street buyers who will harass you with stuff they want to sell. Would recommend coming here.

title: A must see when in Rome
text: How can you not stop by to visit? We did a short tour that got us quick access in. Tour guide was not well informed, or at least didn't provide me with anything of full interest for the colosseum. She mostly talked about everything outside of the colosseum, like St Mark's cathedral. We explored the forum on our own and found it enjoyable. Both areas are definitely marvels to behold. Summary, suggests using a tour to get in quickly. Then doing your own exploring and read up on facts yourself.

title: Coliseum with kids
text:
We booked with a group so we could skip the line, but made the fatal mistake of going in the afternoon.  Honestly, it was just crackers.  Don't do this.  Unless you go early, it's just not worth doing it later with kids in tow.

Whilst we were queuing outside in the 'skip the line' queue to go through security, they announced that the Coliseum was full (??) so no more people were going in. And once we did eventually get in, it was crazy busy.  Our tour guide valiantly showed us all the highlights but with so many other tours there too, they all use similar frequencies so there's the bonus of hearing the tour in all kinds of unexpected languages!  

It's a wonderful and impressive place, but the kids and grandparents didn't enjoy how busy it was.  Everyone was already wilting from waiting outside in the sun for so long as there's not really any shade.  There are some steep stairs inside which are tough for little legs, but they have a lift if you need it.  We really enjoyed Palentine Hill afterwards and would recommend you spend more time there.

title: A lot of history here.
text: The colosseum is one of the things you need to see if you go to Rome. It is very large and crowded with tourists but well worth seeing.

title: Great Visit
text: What a treat to experience one of the most famous of all Roman artifacts.  A joy to get the insight of an experienced guide as well.

title: Amazing!!
text: Very glad I paid extra to go inside the Colosseum! It was definitely worth the money and highly recommend

title: Larger Than Imagined
text: What history walks and talks here. 50,000 spectators cheering and jeering here centuries ago is so thought provoking. Taught me how much larger the world population was than than I had thought. What a well preserved piece of history and culture and architecture. Just be prepared for. Rowdy. Guides are recommended to help manage the confusion.

title: Amazing!
text: Clearly they have spent millions rebuilding the colosseum so we may feel it’s former glory.  Buying tickets in advance is a must.  At our scheduled time we walked right in with no lines.  There is so much to see.  Free drinking water and bathrooms.  Great museum and displays.  I highly recommend this historical site.

title: An epic ancient arena not to be missed
text: The Colosseum is truly as grand and impressive as all the pictures you have seen and looked at in preparing for your trip. It is epic in size and scale and clearly is the blueprint for our modern day domed arenas. I highly recommend hiring a local guide to go here and the Vatican. These licensed guides bypass hours long lines and get you right in...often through special entrances that avoid all of the crowds, At the Colosseum our guides tickets got us onto the Colosseum floor level where the Gladiators used to battle. General tickets only get access to the upper tiers of the arena. You can easily tour the entire site in an hour or so. Don't miss it. It is amazing!!

title: Amazing
text: Get a guide for the tour. Puts in the front of the line and more importantly the guide can tell you the history of the building and what happened before and after the Roman Empire. The size of the arena and stadium is huge!

title: Reccomend to book in advance
text: We didn't go inside this time as we been there before. Reccomend to book a guide for this attraction, and visit it early in the day, as it gets really crowded by lunchtime. Well worth a visit, an terrifying history...

title: Rome
text:
The most beautiful part of this museum is Colosseum which is still an iconic symbol of Imperial Rome and is listed as one of the “new 7 wonders of the world”. If you’re looking to get an inside tour of Colosseum, make sure that you buy tickets online way ahead of time since it gets sold out really fast and you have to stand in a long line if you don’t buy it online. in case it was sold out online you can get one of the Colosseum tours and you get to skip the line with those too.

Unfortunately, I didn’t know that and didn’t buy the tickets online and also all the tours were sold out that day so I didn’t get to get an inside tour of Colosseum!

title: Book early visiting slots. 3000 people cap at a time
text:
To visit Colosseum it's best to book a time slot online as their is always huge que outside the entry area. they only allow 3000 people at once inside so better to book a early morning slot and reach their 30mins before your time slot. They stop the entry until numbers goes below 3000 people. 

Also they provide guided tours inside. If you wish you can also get 6 Euros audio video guide which is very easy to use and gives you a great idea of the history of Colosseum. I took audio video guide and had a great time inside.

title: Get my guide Tours
text:
Would recommend this tour company. Our tour guide was Fabio and he was brilliant. He put his tour group first in the sense of making sure we could have best possible experience. This is a busy busy tourist attraction with many tour guides and he was “on it” from start to finish.
Thank you Fabio.

title: Pre book get there early AM
text: Again - did not go in as queues massive and we had not booked beforehand. Lovely to see from outside ; but beware the many polite touts pretending to be officials who say they will get you in [as they wont] without queueing

title: Roman History
text: If you are the least bit interested in the ancient Roman world, this place can give you a brief glimpse. I recommend purchasing a Romapass or using a legitimate Italian website, one that ends in .it to purchase a pass that will also allow entrance and reservation time to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Making a reservation time will will save you a great deal of waiting and possible disappointment in the line as they will stop allowing admission if capacity is reached.

title: Mr and Miss
text: Amazing and perfect!  We learnt a lot about the history of the colosseum. We used Crown Tours and the tour guides were amazing.

title: Research ticket options before visiting
text: We liked the colosseum as we found the history element very interesting and description given on the audio guide of good value.  We would recommend that you either book tickets in advance or buy them from the Palantino ticket office as this will save you an awful lot of queueing and waiting.

title: A fascinating monument, but do get a guided tour
text: This is one of the most awesome buildings of the ancient world that most of us are every likely to see. Consequently even in October the crowds are massive and if you don't book in advance you will have a long wait to get in. The best way to see and understand this fascinating building is with a guide. Many of the most interesting parts of the building are only accessible with a guide (the reconstructed arena floor, the area beneath the arena and the newly restored area on the fourth level). These are all worth experiencing and being informed about. As the Colosseum is now only a skeleton of its original self someone to help you make that important leap of imagination is essential.

title: Buy tickets online!
text: This was my second visit to the Colosseum. Still amazing, and absolutely worth the time and money. Full of history. We saw the movie Gladiator the night before to get into the right mood. BUT consider visiting in the winter months! There were crazy many people now in october compared to december when we visited last time! The waiting line to buy tickets were incredibly long, so I’m glad that we bought tickets online (getyourguide) with a fixed entry time, and walked right in. The price is the same and it also gives entry to Forum Romanum and the Palentine hill.

title: Impressive!
text: The Colosseum visit is a must! Get a small group tour to secure best access and information. It’s an amazing piece of work and history.

title: Went on the Free Day
text:
So everything in Rome is about crowds.   We went to the ruins and Coliseum on the Free Sunday (first Sunday of the month).  Yes there were lines but they weren't overwhelming. 

We had contemplated not going in.   Our was worth going in.

title: Horrible wait times
text: Thought we would avoid the crowds in October, plus had the Roma Pass, which used to help you with the lines.  Nope.  One 2 hour line to get a ticket and time, and 2 hours more to get in.  We gave up and left.

title: Amazing experience!
text: Great place you can feel the history in here. Tons of people in here, so buying the skip the line ticket from Big Bus was the best choise of the day!

title: Coliseum
text: The coliseum for me was a little disappointing its worth visiting but you should spend more time at the forum than coliseum because there is not much to do you can cover it in an hour or so..

title: Amazing
text: We felt there was no need to book an overpriced tour and we were right . The Colosseum was walking distance from our hotel and watching the hot and tired tourists following a flag in groups of30 or more I was glad we were independent! It is easy to wander around the Colosseum and soak up the wonder of this incredible structure. I am in awe of how Rome has incorporated ancient buildings into the ambience of this vibrant and modern city.

title: It needs some eork
text: We got some skip the line tickets. Although the regular line seemed to be moving quickly. The colosseum is a major tourist attraction. The operators need to maintain the washrooms and all the little things. We rented the audio tour for 5€ each. It was ok  but hard to find the location points.
