There is an elevator for people with mobility issues.

title: Amazing
text: Recommend the underground inclusive tour - absolutely fascinating. Guides really know their stuff but wear comfortable shoes.

title: Historical
text: It is worth every penny to go into the Colosseum! Walking around here you could feel so much emotions about what it represents.

title: Most recognizable building in Rome
text: Very impressive historic site.  Buy your tickets in advance.  The Colosseum does not disappoint.  Metro stop lets you off right at it.   Lots of small eateries near by to grab a bit when you're done.

title: Colosseum - a must when visiting Rome
text: Somewhere to soak up the history of the city. An incredible place to go and admire the work of the Romans. The sheer size of the Colosseum is quite something, and to think that it is still standing is very impressive. Top tip would be to purchase entry tickets in advance, the queues to buy on the day are incredibly long. Also get there early as security checks can take a while to clear.

title: So totally ⒶⓋⒺⓇⒶⒼⒺ
text:
Hmmm.

I’ve been but wouldn’t bother going again. Ticked it off the list n’all.
My problem with this - and other things in Rome - has been that I really don’t feel like the city is making the most of what it’s got. More on this later...

We did a queue skip ticket and got there at about 8.15 and the queuing to go through the bag check (of sorts) was minimal. If you leave it much later than that though, the queuing looked pretty mega.

The coolest thing about the whole visit was seeing the place fairly deserted at sunrise-ish time. Walking away from the Colosseum at about 11 was so crowded it was pretty gross.

Even very early, it was very busy inside, and we were some of the first people in. I could not go in any direction without seeing a selfie stick, phone screen, walking through someone’s photo or finding myself ensconced in a loud American conversation. 

I had an audio guide but to be honest, watching scenes from the film Gladiator were more useful. It was pretty basic. I learned one fact which was that apparently the whole underground thing was initially filled with water and they had proper ship battles on it - cool. 

I kind of just feel like the whole thing could be done so much better in a more controlled and thoughtful manner. Sure, the ‘let everyone loose (nearly) everywhere’ approach is inclusive and probably keeps entry costs down, but it wasn’t the majestic, awe-inspiring experience one might expect. 

Be brave Romans - close it down, miss out on the millions and squillions of daily revenue for a bit and make it way better!

title: Night tour.
text: Was worth going but a bit over priced. Saw things you wouldn’t see on a normal daytime tour. The underground bit of the coliseum was a highlight.

title: Beautiful history lesson

title: A must do in Rome
text: We enjoyed our visit to Rome and had pre- booked tickets prior to our visit. This is a must do, if you want to avoid the queues. It was very busy and we had to dodge past people trying to sell things outside. The views are amazing from the top. We did not get the audio guide as my son would not have concentrated for long enough. We did our reading before the visit.

title: Stunning Colosseum!
text:
We booked ahead to enter around 09:00 and it worked well - we queued for about 10 mins only.  Once we were inside, we also picked up audio guides which were excellent - they also have clear diagrams of the Colosseum shown on the screen of the audio guides.

There is a fixed route to follow but there's loads of opportunities to get some stunning photographs, and you can also watch the Archaeologists at work which is fascinating.

We bought a combined ticket which also included the Forum and Palantine Hill sites which we could visit over 2 days so that was excellent - they are all quite close to each other.

title: Tour
text: Five stars, great sights and even better tour guide. Really went in to great detail and had a brilliant sense of humour

title: Impressive
text: I am glad we visited this!  We did not do a tour of the inside, but just walked around the grounds outside.  It is impressive to say the least.  Walk a little farther around the grounds and you will see remains of things that must have been associated with it.  We did not get the information, a tour would give you that, but we were there and so were thousands of other people.  Long lines to get in.  Tour hawkers, souvenir hawkers, beggars, water bottle sellers, all took away from the history of this place.  You are hard pressed to visit any attraction without being bombarded by these people.

title: A fascinating history
text: The Colosseum is obviously a must when visiting Rome. We visited at around 3pm and the lines were quite short and there was plenty of room to read about the history and view the entire internal perimeter. Be sure to purchase the multi site ticket which also allows you to visit the Forum and Palantine Hill over two days. It really is a monumental structure, you can barely imagine what it must have been like in its prime.

title: Amazing
text: Visited the Colosseum as part of a guide. I would recommend using a tour guide because you learn so much about the history.

title: Colosseum is a Must See in Rome
text:
We bought the Skip the Line tickets and our tour guide accompanied us through the entrance of the Colosseum & Pantheon Hills.

Our guide was awesome!

title: OMG
text: This place is amazing given that it is 2,000 years old.  You get a real sense of the history of the place.  Signage is good (so you know what you’re looking at).  It was busy but not too bad.  It has to be top of the list for a trip to Rome but pre-book!

title: Awesome

title: Amazing Colosseum

title: Breathtaking heritage

title: Do not miss this!!
text: Another must see...inside and out!  We bought tickets ahead of time which helped a great deal. Expect lines, but try to arrive at opening. The history you witness is undeniable!  Yes, you will want to watch “Gladiator” again when you get home!

title: Nice

title: wonderful

title: Loved this experience...
text:
Can't miss a visit to the Colosseum when you come to Rome.... incredible venue.
We booked a tour which included 'skip the line' tickets, and seriously worth it. Don't wait in line. We were there in October, a quiet season, and still the line was crazy long!

title: Good experience

title: Worth the visit.

Well worth the visit, lots of steps there is a lift to get you up if you have difficulty with the steep steps.

title: Really amazing and worth buying Online Ticket

They need more toilets in there as the queue for the ladies was huge but apart from that we enjoyed the exhibitions upstairs and very glad there was a lift so we could access the upper levels.

title: First visit

title: Very interesting
text: We visited this in October, booked a quick access ticket which did get us in a bit ahead of long queues but you still have to wait a bit. There is access for disabled which we appreciated for my elderly mother. We used the audio guide that you can buy inside, it was ok but I’ve used the guided tour before which I think is better, you get more from them.

title: Amazing and must see place. Tip to skip the long line
text:
We had a great time exploring the colosseum.
We had a lovely time taking photos too.
I just noticed that the line is very loooong.
I was lucky I did some research before I went because there are Rio off tickets online and in the street. I would advise visitors to buy a ticket in the palatine or forum ticket booth which is just near the colosseum ( if you get out from the colloseum metro train station just turn right and at the next traffic light you will see the palantine ticket booth) that’s a very vital tip. Because there’s a short line ( remember that a ticket of the palatine included the coliseum and the forum so it’s 3 in 1) it only cost £12 and kids below 18 are free. The ticket can be used in the colosseum and you can go and skip the long line therefore saves you time and money.
Enjoy every moment coz the place will bring you back in Roman time.

title: Stunning history
text:
Aside from the obvious wondrous history and massive structure, there’s not much to add to everyone’s reviews and experiences. As with most everything in downtown Rome, it’s a tourist attraction you want to and must see, so be prepared for the crowds, the wait, the pushing and shoving. 
We booked our visit for first thing in the morning. Chose to get a RomaPass which allowed us to pretty much walk up to the X-ray/bodyscanner, which was nice. The self guided tour was good and the audio guide helpful. 
Be prepared to wait in long lines at the restrooms and finding no toilet paper or seats on the commodes. 
I’d rate this attraction a 3 for the less attractive amenities but how can I give this structure less than 5 stars?!

title: Walking in history.
text: Amazing! I would advise booking tickets online. If you have no knowledge of history I would book a guided tour. Arrive there early so as to avoid the crowds. We were there in October, early, and although there were lots of people, not many compared to summer.

title: Amazing.
text: The history and sheer enormity of the colosseum make it a must see. It was hot and crowded even in October, but we are very grateful we spent the largest portion of one day in Rome here.

title: Amazing!

title: Magnifico
text: Had to queue for over an hour to get in but worth it once you are in. Lots of information boards and breathtaking to walk round. Got in for €2 euros as well due to being EU citizen between 18-25.

title: Everything you expected and more...
text:
The Colosseum is something that must be experienced. It doesn't matter if you're a history buff, etc. It is just incredible. There is something to see for everyone.

We chose a tour that just got us in through the door and let us tour the facility on our own. title: Dad
text: One of the highlights of the trip we enjoyed every aspect of the tour. The guide spoke very good English so everything he conveyed was understood completely . The history that this grand place can be overwhelmingly awsome so take it slow allow the entire day for this event.

title: Significant Part of Roman History
text: When in Rome a visit to the Colosseum, built during the first century AD, is warranted.  This impressive structure provides a glimpse of how the ancient Romans found entertainment.  During our visit in October 2019 the interior of the Colosseum was undergoing significant restoration, which will likely continue for quite some time.  My wife and I used a private guide to visit the Colosseum, and it was well worth the cost.

The downside was the amount of people selling tat and being quite forceful for a sale, they were less at night though. Taxis readily available day and night nearby! Highly recommend the colosseum to anyone and everyone.

I would in future take a tour as I would of liked to find out more about it and gone underground which looked cool but was under construction. Another one of those pinch me moments when standing in such an iconic amphitheatre. Again wall to wall people. Overall a must do. Would highly recommend.

title: Don’t miss this piece of Roman history
text: What an absolutely amazing historical site. The stories are magical....vestal virgins, Caesar, Nero, Romulus, Remus......gladiators.....let your imagination run wild.   Book a pre book tour though or you could queue for ages.

title: Go early, bring a guide
text: My number one piece of advice for anyone desiring to see the Colosseum is to arrive early! I showed up promptly at opening time and was able to wander through without hordes of other tourists, but by the time I was leaving an hour or two later, it was starting to become very crowded. Do your research ahead of time so that you can better understand what you are seeing and can seek out some of the important features of these marvelous ruins. I booked a private local guide, and I highly recommend this strategy. She knew all the best ways of getting around the Colosseum and other nearby Ancient Roman ruins, and it made the morning go very smoothly. I also learned a lot more and was able to better understand the significance of different features of the ruins. This is a definite do-not-miss site to anyone who is visiting Rome. Even if you don’t use a guide, it’s worth doing a little homework ahead of time to get the most out of the experience.

title: Top 5 to see in Rome
text: Very worth the trip and a guided tour.  Like other venues in Rome, this is worth researching in advance to have a better appreciation of what you see and hear.

title: Buy skip the like passes
text:
This was the first place we visited in Rome. We arrived around 10am to find a massive amount of people standing around. We had no idea on where the line to purchase tickets was. There was 3 different lines and the one we decided to get on was for pre orders. After much confusion we decided to leave and go to the Forum which is right next door. There we were able to purchase a pass that was for 2 days and it allowed you to visit the Forum and the Colosseum. After purchasing the passes for about 11 euros per person. The man told us we had a reservation time for the colosseum in 15 minutes. We rushed back there thinking we could just walk in since our reservation time was right then. Right away the man told us to get in line. There were around 100 people in front of us. It took about an hour to finally get inside. If you don’t want to deal with lines, go online and buy a priority skip the line pass.  It’s worth it.  
The colosseum is huge.  There are tons of steps to climb. They do have an elevator for the handicap. The bathrooms are by the entrance and there was a very long line to use them. I enjoyed visiting minus the time wasted trying to buy tickets.

It was very busy, but there are fleeting moments where you can take photos without anyone photo-bombing you! There are beautiful photo opportunities outside the collosium (if you walk around it). Wear comfortable shoes and take bottled water from supermarket with you (as it gets very hot and touts sell a small bottle for €2!!). text: When in Rome you must visit the Colosseum, & you won't be disappointed. It's a striking view from afar & gets better as you approach. title: A few tips for a must see sight.
text:
The Colosseum is one of the major attractions in Rome. One of the few man made structures ornate and complex that has survived for two millennium. It is worth the visit.
We visited in late October and toured the structure from the underground to the top level (the Belvedere). We booked with Touriks and they were very professional. 
You can decide from the review if you think it’s worth the extra time and expense.
If you visit:
1. Unless you are going from November (minus the Christmas season) to April), think about booking a ticket ahead to avoid the long lines, or get a Roma pass.
2. Do not visit when it’s raining, it really diminishes the experience.
3. The underground and the Belvedere can only be visited with an archeologist. The underground tour is more interesting as you learn about the workings of the Coliseum in its prime—it was THE Imperial arena for one of the largest empires in history. They close the underground when it rains.
The Belvedere is worth the visit if you like taking selfies and photos, it’s much less crowded than the regular level.
They have a elevator for people with walking difficulties to get to the underground (it’s fits only three persons—one has to be the archeologist). But no elevator to the Belvedere and the steps are high and steep—there is a railing to hold on to.
4. Bring a bottle of water if you are visiting, there are no fountains or toilets in the coliseum.
5. There are toilets outside the Coliseum on the east end, you need one Euro to enter. They did not have toilet paper when we went. There is cafe to the right of the exit from the Metro on the upper level. It has toilets (and a line to get in), bottled water there €2.50 each.
6. The website states that you cannot bring large back packs into the Coliseum, two persons in our group had 15 to 17 inch backpacks, the backpacks were inspected, but the guards let them in.
7. If it’s a sunny day, think about bringing a hat. Wear comfortable shoes.
Have a great visit!

title: Fantastic experience
text: A must do. Ensure you spend enough time there. Pre book the tour to ensure a good entrance. Visit all the levels to make the most of it.

title: Amazing
text: Definitely a wonder despite the crowds The scale of the place is overwhelming and hard to believe it is 2000 years okd

title: Must see in Rome
text:
Going to the Colosseum is a “not to miss” activity! We purchased skip the line tickets through the Big Bus. It was a great decision to purchase the skip the line as the regular lines were very long. 

Everyone goes through security scanners so be prepared. It was easier for me to put all of my items in my small backpack prior to going through the security scan. 

Currently part of the colosseum floor is covered, so you won’t see everything. The structure is an amazing place to visit with a lot of history. The steps are fairly steep, so find the elevator if you have an issue with stairs. 

There were a lot of flies and bugs around, so you may want to bring along some type of insect wipes or something. The day we visited, the credit card machine was down in the gift shop, so you were out of luck if you wanted something and did not have enough cash. 

The bathrooms are small and did not have a good way to dry my hands. We had to wait a long time as an attendant began cleaning the stalls and we were down to only 1 out of about 6 stalls. We also did not see anywhere to purchase water.  

I would recommend going to the colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill all in the same time frame for the day as they are basically next to each other.

title: Tremendous building
text: Definitely the number one Roman symbol. So many great stories linked to this monumental building. It is definitely worth your time taking a circle over this 2000 years old stadium.

title: Great experience but lots of people
text: Had skip queue tickets but still a wait through security and then to purchase audio guide. Good audio guide but over too soon. Lots of scaffolding spoilt the overall experience and access limited.

title: Visit with a private guide
text: On 15 Oct I visited Colosseum with a private guide, MariaRosa, mariarosa_pm@libero.it and it was a great experience, Very moderate price, speaks IT FR and perhaps more

title: Those Romans build big
text: Quite a place. Hard to believe all the history that goes with this place. Disturbing audio commentary tells the story of gladiators and horribly violent games for the amusement of the upper classes. If you go, watch your step, they made buildings that last centuries but apparently had not invented levels yet and the stonework can be treacherous for walking. Tons of people!

title: Night tour
text: Remarkable experience with great guides. Very surreal at night time Ang fantastic to get into tunnels underneath and see the way the games were choreographed. The walking tour with Mario beforehand was also very informative and entertaining.

title: Not sure if it’s worth paying to go inside
text: We visited on a rainy day, but the view outside was still great.  Not a huge fan of historical ruins so didn’t bother paying to go in.  There are other sites like the forum and palatine in the same complex.

title: Holiday
text: I thought visiting the colosseum, was one of the most interesting sites in Rome, and it has a very important role in the history of Italy, and for generations of people throughout the world, it has become a must see attraction, I would recommend to anybody who has this site on their visit plan to pay for a fast pass, to avoid huge queues, as they are worth it.

title: Amazing site
text: Wonderful site with phenomenal history. Only problem, street vendors who refuse to let you go and make every Effexor to sell you bracelets (placing them on your wrist as a “gift” and refuse to take them back until you agree to give money).  Beware the line of “nice shoes” as soon as you respond, they are there for a sale, and can be abusive when you continue to say no.

title: Impressive
text: The structure is very impressive. Be aware that there are long line ups, unless you pay to skip the line or go in with a tour. The stairs are steep. But there is an elevator. The ground is uneven. Be prepared to spend some time to take it all in. Highly recommend arranging a tour in order to fully appreciate the Colosseum.

title: Best to get skip the line tickets
text: We went mid afternoon and it was very busy, we had pre-puchased skip the line tickets for a set time and were so glad we had as the queues were very long to buy tickets on the day. There are only  3000 people allowed in at any one time, so those waiting to buy tickets would have been waiting for well over an hour just to buy a ticket alone, we were inside and through the security checks within 15 minutes. The Colosseum itself is wonderful, hard to describe the feeling when you walk in, lots of people, but then it would have been full of people in Roman times.

title: Very good
text: It took about 30 minutes to get inside the Colosseum, it would have taken hours without the skip the queue. Very happy with the service.

title: Part of the “hop on and off bus routes.
text:
It is of course one of the sights of Rome that is a “must see”.

However, on a hot day it may be easier to remain on the open topped bus and rely on the photo opportunities from there. The crowds ,like anywhere in Rome, make a short visit very tiring and a disappointment since we were returning with fonder memories of quieter times in the eternal city. Unless you want to do an in depth tour of previously viewed sights we suggest the open buses are a perfect way to see the many tourist places on offer, then return to those that you feel worthwhile for a less busy run around.

We found the  need to see everything again became less critical as our aching bones increased and our wallets got thinner. We did make sure we went to the Sistine Chapel, this we had missed on our last visit to the Vatican some thirty years ago. That was worthwhile but the walk to the Trevi Fountain was sadly over crowded to the extent that even waiting for hours it is unlikely to allow a good photo opportunity, just as well we had some from our last trip.

Staying at the Bernini Bristol was a great choice as it was so central to the many places we wanted to see. It also was ideally placed for a couple of bistro pubs where a snack and a beer was all we needed to see us through the long day or upon a late night return or a pre dinner “happy hour” beer or wine.

title: Great sight in Rome
text: We had skip the line tickets with a provided guide and it was great. No hassles to meet up, we got a great tour and afterwards could spend free time walking around inside. If you see the long queues outside then you will appreciate your skip the line tickets.

title: Incredible Colosseum
text: We purchased the gladiator floor access and forum tour.  The Colosseum blew my mind. The history behind it and the changes it had over the centuries is unbelievable. Definitely worth while seeing  the inside and having a knowledgeable guide is worth the money

title: Incredible
text: Our tour guide, Stephano did an excellent job and was so knowledgeable and provided us an in depth tour of the Colosseum. The other members of our tour did not show up so we had the privilege of having a private tour. Stephano was extremely animated and brought 2000 years to life . He gifted us with a book depicting these amazing sites then and now! We highly recommend this tour!

title: Fantastic tour
text: So happy we paid for a your guide, our driver helped us, we received lots of information, and got ahead of the long line up for entry.

title: Wonder of the World
text: Definitely a must see, but no need for an expensive tour - just book the basic on-line tickets, go in via the reserved tickets queue and download a free audio-guide (if that's your thing). Cost us 28eur for the pair, and we were inside in about 10 mins for security checks, and spent about 2 hours wandering around the site and seeing all the different bits. The museum upstairs is pretty good, and the main attraction is the overall architecture of the place.

title: Don't Miss The Colosseum !!!
text: I'm not much for history but the Colosseum was amazing. You have to see the size of the structure and then learn that it was built in a very short time. Be sure to buy a tour that has a guide to tell you all about it and that will allow you to enter at a specified time and not stand in line for hours. We bought a tour that entered thru the "gladiator entrance" and allowed access to the stage. Well worth it.

title: I came. I saw. I left.
text: If you see pictures and read all the history hype, you can avoid a long wait line, huge crowds, a port a john bathroom, and a big disappointment.

title: Coliseum ROME 🇮🇹
text:
Absolutely stunning ruins steeped in history. This place is a must see if visiting ROME. 

My advice would be to get there early to avoid the massive queues unless your paying for an advanced entry ticket which saves you a bit of time.

title: Just have to see
text: With a skip the line tour (essential) you may not get to spend as much time as you'd like here but definitely a need to see!

title: Nice to see
text: Really nice to see from outside, but worth the wait in line or skip line though. Inside was plain and nothing special

title: A must when in rome
text: Quite majestic! I would recommend booking a tour online well in advance, otherwise you could spend the whole day waiting in line. Don’t be fooled by the dozens of “guides” outside offering skip the line tours and guiding you to the wrong lines and miss informing tourists.

title: Go early and see Sunrise!
text:
Seeing the Colosseum through the eyes of my guest, a first time visitor to Rome, was special.  It is a "Wow" moment in this age of technology, and bad architecture.  It never ceases to amaze.
Go watch the sunrise and you will never forget it.

title: Don't miss this fasinating piece of Italian history!
text:
Unbelievable building that has to be visited.
You will be amazed to see the fabulous arena where Gladiators competed and lions roamed.
 
Advised to pay extra for skip the line and guided tour to enable you to learn all they is to know about this historic, ancient building.

title: Awesome
text: Pretty cool. Arrive early for easier access. It is amazing to see the location and all the ongoing renovating.

title: Unless you are interested please don't go
text:
Regretfully, the poor old Colosseum is overwhelmed by tourists, many of whom I suspect only have a marginal interest in Ancient Rome.  I can only assume that this is due to a widespread obsession with gladiatorial games, as if these, and only these, defined Ancient Rome forgetting other popular public activities of the time such as visiting the baths, going to chariot races, attending the theatre, shopping at markets etc.

As a result of the Colosseum's status as the second-most visited monument in Europe, strict visitor quotas have had to be applied.  As opposed to the Palatine Hill et al, I agree that this is an unfortunate  necessity.  As a result, there are long lines, even for ticket-holders.  Visitor numbers are measured hourly.   I did not intend to visit this time having been here in 1980 and again in 2010 but booking a guided tour to the Palatine included one to the Colosseum, so what the hell I thought.  The guide turned out to be excellent and my third visit the best; it was the only time I did not concentrate on it being either a) an incredible piece of functional architecture or b) a terrible place of death for about half a millennium.  In conclusion, if you go, I suggest a good guide.  If you are only vaguely interested, spare the Colosseum: there are other sites in Rome.

title: Busy but worth it!
text: Get a guide... they are worth it. Ours was an archeologist and led us round for 3 hours with facinating info and stories.

title: Savage
text: Just breath taking. Literally feel like russell crowe, are you entertained haha. We did skip the queue and audio self tour and it was the way to go!

title: Book tickets online
text: It’s so easy to book tickets before your trip online. It’s a three hour wait on the day if they even have space. The colosseum is a must see in Rome. It is more impressive on the outside than inside I personally thought. Although we just had standard access. I’m sure upper and underground were better but I didn’t book far enough in advance to get these and I booked 8 weeks before we went. We hired an audio guide but it didn’t give much extra information than our guide book and I think ppl are more there to tick it off their list of places. It is spectacular from the outside the inside is over rated a little. Still you should go.

title: Lucky VIP entrance
text:
We didnt book any ticket in advance and were pretty disappointed looking at the queue. But were approached by 2 random indian guy to purchase 35euro per ticket to join a tour group in order to cut queue like a VIP.
We nego till 65 euro for 2 pax and legit went in with a small group and even get some english tour guide. I wonder what's the individual public ticket price.

title: A historical must
text:
The place is a must if you are visiting Rome, we did a semi-private tour and it was fun to picture the idea of the Romans and the Colosseum back then. There was a lot of walking and stairs involved.
I would recommend hats and sunglasses if the weather is sunny. Also get water bottles because you can fill them by water fountains that are found everywhere.

title: İf you have problem with ticket
text: We didnt wanna wait for quee and there was people asking about fast track ticket which is cost 35 Euro per person. And we did pay for it but there is little ticket place in metro staition in Side left Corner you could buy for 25 Euro !! I wanna share this !! Apart from this story collosium is magical place must visit in life time👍

title: Takes your breath away
text:
There are few times in my life where I have felt small and insignificant. This was one of those times. The idea that you are walking on ground that was built in the BC is awe inspiring. I HIGHLY recommend doing a tour group to enter. We did, any of them are good, and it saved us tons of time!

They only allow a maximum amount of people on the coliseum at one time and a max number of entry tickets a day. This means you either have to get a tour group and skip the line ticket first or wait in line for up to 3 hours. Get with a tour group.... it was amazing to learn the history of the gladiator fights and the history of Rome.

title: A week in Rome
text: The Colosseum, was wonderful from the outside, we had been inside on a previous visit. Extremely crowded and entrance queues around 1-2 hrs long

title: Must see!
text: Amazing place full of history. You must go there at least once in your life! Make sure you buy ticket in advance.

title: Really interesting but get a tour guide
text: If you get a tour guide you can skip the huge queue! But otherwise it’s really interesting! Also bring water and don’t buy the water there from strangers!! It isn’t drinking water!

title: Out of this world
text: We took a guided tour of the colosseum along with palatine Hill and Roman forum. The 3 hour tour seemed a bit long for the kids but was definitely worth the time and money. You will be able to appreciate the place a lot more with a guided tour rather than doing it on your own. The place has a character of its own with over 2000 years of history.

title: BABARA
text: Realy interesting tour, a wery skild an funny tourguide with great knowlege about the place, can higly Be recomended, Barbara is the greatest and loves littel babys😉

title: A great night
text: We did a night time tour and it was great.  It was a small group, there is no line to enter.  The night time is a great way to see the colosseum.

title: Manic as expected!
text:
Feel the authorities ought to clamp down on the hordes of unofficial sellers of skip the queue tickets that harry tourists on arrival. We decide to queue-it took an hour and at about 9 to 10am in October it was not that bad! Temperature is very comfortable at this time of year. A cheeky family tried to smuggle a gaggle of friends into a queue jump and an official shot over and sorted them out! Waiting in the queue gave us some lovely pictures of the shrieking blue green parakeets that nest in the colosseum.

The drawback of buying tickets on the day was that they had sold out of tickets for the lower and upper tiers so we just did the ground level. It was still pretty spectacular. We were in about half an hour and by then had enough of crowds and set off further from the city centre.

Be aware that even early the queue halts often-broadcasts announce the limit has been reached and they have to slow the entry down. We had a rucksack and were a bit uneasy on seeing signs they were not allowed but it was scanned and waved through. Most tourists seemed to have them.

title: Worth seeing in Italy
text: I booked the tickets to Colosseum via Tiqets which allows me to skip the line, access to arena floor, Roman forum and Palatine Hill. It was well worth the money and i was able to download the audio tour provided by Tiqets.  It did take us more than half a day to go through all the sites but it's well worth the money, lots of history.

title: Colossal
text: Well you think that what can you write about THE COLOSSEUM..... Start with the name..... Flavian Amphitheatre is what it was originally but named it so probably because of the Colossal statue of Nero nearby....... or by the fact that the stone from here was used in Vatican Churches......Or the fact that provision was made for rapid evacuation of around 75 thousand people in a couple of minutes in case of an emergency....... CAN GO THERE AGAIN AND AGAIN AND YET AGAIN

title: Skipping essential,
text: Excellent experience with a super guide. Booked a group tour which only had 12 people and our guide was full of information and was lovely.

title: 30th Birthday Weekend
text:
A wonderful historical building and a lovely morning to wander around. 

We purchased our tickets from the official website before travelling and we are glad we did as the queue to get tickets was very long and would be hours worth of waiting. 

I believe we were about €28 in total for 2 adults this was excluding audio guides. It did include entry to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. 

You can download audio guides on your phone for free if you want to save some pennies.

The experience outside the Colesseum is slightly ruined by the pestering sellers for tickets, selfie sticks and phone charging banks. Do not give you phone yo anyone like this.

title: Amazing
text:
We booked skip the line tickets before leaving UK. Children were free. You arrive for designated time slot. 
Didn’t take long to go through security.
Was amazing to visit.

title: Worth the trip
text: Much more magnificent in person than in photos. It’s best to reserve tickets online beforehand, so you don’t have to wait in a long line. I used Rick Steves’ audio tour on my phone rather than a guide.

title: Colosseum at night
text:
It was interesting to view at night. I booked the tour through Viator and my husband and I had a good experience. It was costly but worth seeing once in a life time.
I thought I was being prepared with this Italy trip but I  was not. I could have booked this tour months in advance directly through the Colosseum website and I would have saved myself some money.

title: Incredible
text: A must see when in Rome. Highly recommend booking a tour for best info and reduced wait time. The structure is massive and u dergoing repairs, but simply amazing.

title: Great informative guide
text: The colosseum is under renovations I think on going, our guide was an archeologist, and provided great information, I’d say an hour was perfect, it’s above the metro where you meet had to GPS it.

title: Holiday
text: Even though we had tickets the waiting in line killed us. Really spoilt our time. We are talking hours of waiting. The crowds OMG

title: A must see
text: Seeing the coliseum is a absolute must. Pictures do not do this monument justice. I highly recommend getting a English speaking tour guide. You meet them in front of the coliseum then they walk you past the lines right inside and start explaining things to you. Make sure you get a tour guide with arena access because not every gets to go down there. October is the perfect time to see it because the weather is about 60 degrees and the crowds are a lot smaller than the summer. There are still a lot of people so definitely get a guide I used headout for multiple tours in Rome and they were all great experiences.

title: It's so big that it's... Collosal !
text: It's big, it's bad, it's the premier ruin of ancient Europe, it's - the Colosseum !!!  Get a tour guide who speaks a language you know.  That makes a... colossal difference.  They know where to go, in which sequence, and provide history, too. Makes it much better.

title: Awesome
text:
Get a queue jump ticket for early AM....and you’ll be a happy chappy! :) 
Have a look at photos to see what you’ll get!

title: WOW
text: Cant really say much execpt Wow! All I can say is book a skip the line tour - queues are over 3hours long to get in otherwise - we had a fascinating time here.

title: Skip the line is a must
text: We took the big bus and dropped us off right by the entrance of the colosseum. We enjoyed our visit and didn’t spend any time waiting on line, which was great.

title: Great
text: Very interesting place to visit. We did the skip the line and guided tour with Tiqets. It was great. Worth the visit if you are in Rome. I don’t think I would make a special trip to Rome just to see the Colosseum. But it is still worth seeing.

title: Amazing!!
text: Pictures don’t do justice! It is so incredibly huge you don’t realize it until you’re standing right next to it. Even more amazing at night!

title: Big and old AF
text: Kinda like a big 02 arena if it was made of brick and some of it had crumbled to the ground. Would be better if they sold beer

title: Got to be on the "to do" list
text:
Booked a tour before we travelled and glad we did. Group of just seven and an excellent english speaking guide, with a sense of humour. Not a huge amount to see inside but the scale and sense of history make it a worthwhile visit.
Busy enough in October and warm, 25 degrees. Hawkers not as bad as I was expecting and the road up to the monument had a nice variety of buskers and artists.

title: Amazing!
text:
We booked online which is a must and make sure you do this as soon in advance as you can! The public ticket queue is massive! 

Brilliant experience and just amazing.

title: Glimpse of Roman Empire
text:
Two quick tips:
1. Buy skip the line tickets as this place has long queues
2. Take official guide who beautifully explains the history, architecture in 45 minutes

title: october 2019
text: great experience seeing this fantastic piece  of history queues about one hour plus so skipped line 30 euro with guide which included the forum for me worth money.

title: A must-see
text: Whilst very touristy, this is an obvious must-see in Rome. Definitely try and get a tour or a guide when you go in. I personally think walking around without one was not as educational/fun as having someone with you. Get there early to avoid big waits.

title: Colosseum - Can bring tears to your eyes it's so amazing!
text:
The site of the Colosseum is one many see only in the movies or travel magazines, but I count my blessings that I've been able to see it twice.

We didn't get to do a tour within the colosseum this time as we were only in Rome for the day but the shear sight of this structure was worth it all the same.  Reconstruction is always underway to this olde but a goodie.  Well worth a look even if it's just from the outside - but so much more interesting to take a tour - but don't forget to book ahead.

title: A highlight of Rome!
text: We did the Colosseo as part of a tour, and our tour guide was AMAZING. He really painted a picture of what it would be like and got us to imagine how things would have been. Such a huge building, with so much history - it's a must see when in Rome!

title: Historic place
text: The colosseum in Rome is a must visit place on any trip to Rome. The history of the place really makes you wonder. We took advantage of a pre booked trip where we could skip the line which saved us queuing for a very long time as the queue stretched a very long way. If you don’t per book a skip the line group activity (it wasn’t much more than the normal entrance price) then get here very early to avoid a huge queue.

title: Pre-book on the official site
text:
Go to the official colloseum site and book a slot but select the guided tour. You get fast track entry and FAR cheaper than forking out for a tour though another company. If you dont pre-book you have to queue and my gosh those lines are long! Because I'm registered disabled I was able to book online. For my 10 year old and I for entry with tour guide was €7. 

It is an amazing place and exactly as you imagine it to be. Go as early as possible before all the crowds.

title: Great site
text: Colosseum was great but our tour guide (through the Colosseum) was terrible. I would recommend bringing your own tour guide or using an audio guide.

title: Prompt Admission but Little Else
text: It was somewhat difficult to find the vendor for this even.  There was no voucher exchange for a ticket  as advised in the voucher, We   were only given one hour to visit the colosseum and then meet the vendor who led us to the Palentine Hill and Forum (With no ticket we could not visit independently after seeing the colosseum ). All we received from the vendor was a sticker. The vendor did not show up until 15 minutes prior to our admission time while we were cautioned that if we were late we would miss our visit.  We were "dumped" at the Palentine Hill with not even an offer of a map of the site.  Only benefit was prompt admission.  Awesome site.

title: Very Good
text: Stood in a long line outside the Coliseum to get tour tickets but it was worth it . The last time i was at the Coliseum ( 20 years ago) there were no lines at all and few people . This time we chose a tour with guide 19 Euro. The guide was very good with a wealth of information and stories contradicting the myths and movie legends. This is a fast paced one hour tour worth the price...besides your ticket is good for 3 days and includes entrance to the Forum and Paladino Hill .

title: Incredible
text: We've all heard a lot about this construction... but all the words can't describe what you feel when you see the entire "stadium". It may leave you speechless. I reccomend buying your tickets online and pay attention to frauds near the place: some guys will try to sell you "tickets" that aren't valid.

title: Roman Guy Tour Excels Again
text: We loved this tour of the Colosseum, Forum, Emperors Palace and Palatine Hill.  We ran late and the tour guide kept going.  She was excellent and is easy to understand with a real passion for the subject.

title: Beautiful place
text: Here you have ocasion to see why the Roman Empire was so great. When you visit this place, you imagine how much blood was shed here.

title: Great place to check out if you have time!
text: For one, too many renovations have been done to reconstruct it and the Roman Government is in the process of reconstructing the structure some more. You barely get a chance to go near it especially during the So in the long run, not much history will be preserved. If I were you, I would visit it before it becomes too modern.

title: Awe inspiring monument
text:
The crowds were amazing, with long queues on a really hot day. The structure of the building is truly amazing. We stool to contemplate the history of the building and the ability of the Romans to organise their affairs so that class distinctions is such a recognised part of their social structure, no doubt built on fear. 
There is so much we can learn from Roman history.
Spend a few hours here in wonder.

title: Stunning
text: Booked a trip with GetYourGuide company , used them before and again didn’t disappoint. Very well organized company . I recommend getting a organized tour for this attraction as the queues were very long .A must see when in Rome.

title: An amazing site but very busy.
text: Your imagination can run away when you witness this iconic place. How did they do it?  A marvel to this day.

title: History at its best.
text: You cant make a trip to Rome without coming here! I jumped on the tour bus which was fab as i was able to jump on and off at the stops and come and go as i please. I would reccomend going back after sunset as i did for some more pics as there was no1around and it was very tranquil and i felt like i could appreciate it more when it was like that. As you can imagine in the day it was over run with people.

title: What can you say IT'S THE COLOSEUM
text: Wear your best walking and climbing shoes there are many steps. Book your tickets in advance or go in a group to bypass the lines. It's pure history and amazement that a society and people, intelligent enough to build and construct something like this built it to watch fellow human being killed, torn apart by wild beasts and held 50,000 people to watch.

title: If you want the tour - Book WELL IN ADVANCE
text:
I walked to the Colosseum from our hotel.  It was a bit of a hike (nearly 2 miles) but the sights along the way were worth it.  I will say that the scrum of tourists, tour groups, and general Rome traffic around the thing does take away from the ancient atmosphere of the place.  Also, the tour groups are numerous.  I opted to just walk around the exterior (free, and easy to do) versus take the guided tour, as the day I went there were 3 cruise ships docked on the Italian coast and the excursion groups rolled up in dozens upon dozens of buses - all at once - and their exodus onto the street clogged the traffic even more.  

Directly across from the Colosseum is the Parco del Colle Oppio, a lovely city park that was much quieter, and offered great views of the Colosseum.  I'd recommend grabbing a beverage from one of the numerous souvenir shops and going there to sit and view the magnificent building in relative peace.

title: Few tourists
text: I was able to get some awesome photography while there because there were so few tourists. Don’t get me wrong, there still were a lot of tourists... just nothing that was overwhelming!

title: Private Colosseum tour with Marta Marsili was extraordinary
text: We had limited time in Rome and wanted to maximize every minute.  We hired Marta who advised us exactly what ticket to buy two months ahead of time.   She then navigated us past the crowds and other tours so we were able to see the Colosseum with an up close and intimate view.  We’d seen it years ago but her knowledge and expertise made the old stones come alive in a way that made it an out of this world experience.  Marta is exceptionally knowledgeable and used old photographs and drawings to enhance our understanding and experience regarding the history of the site.   We appreciated that she didn’t speculate or guess at answers to our questions.  Having observed other tour groups of varying size, we would never consider touring the Colosseum any other way.  It was not cheap but the personal nature of her tour and the ability to navigate around others to get to see what larger groups could never see made the expense more than worthwhile.  Marta also guided us through the Forum in a way where it felt like we’d traveled back in time.

title: Mixed Experience!
text: Visited October Half Term 2019. The queuing situation was completely ridiculous and unacceptable - two hours from 11am. Not sure why it is so poorly organised and chaotic!!  Recommend you do your homework to find the best way to pre-book and "skip the line".

title: Totally amazing but let down by queues
text:
Completely blown away to visit here and see it for real rather than in photographs. We didn't do a tour and took an hour to go round it all. My whole family enjoyed it.

Negatives that need addressing : queuing very confusing. There was a long queue to pick up our prebooked tickets, then two queues to get in (one for prebooked and one for people to buy) Noone knew what was going on. We were 9am and have to say once the place opened, the queue to actually get in moved quickly but didn't expect to stand 45 minutes in queues from 8.15am!!!!

title: Roma Pass - skip the queue
text:
An amazing experience! We got two 72 hour Roma Passes, from the tourist shop near to the entrance. Then went straight to the ticket booth, not queuing and went in within 10 minutes. Without the passes, the queue was about one hour plus just to get tickets. The Roma passes were 38.50 Euros each, but include two major attractions, free transport for the 72 hours and discounts on other attractions after you’ve used them twice for the two free ones.
Beware of the people outside trying to sell water, ‘skip the line tickets’ and anything that they think they can make money on. They pester you a lot but after being told no many times will eventually move on to the next tourists they see.

title: Awe inspiring
text: We tried to get a Roma pass while in California but were unsuccessful. title: Rome Highlight
text:
This is THE place to go to on your Rome adventure. Avoid the last Sunday of the month - entry is free, however, lines are huge and no official tours or skip-the-line. You need to be there about 6am to get in early. Things to do. Walk around in daylight and do the same at night - different feels and plenty of opportunities for great photos. Avoid the peddlers on the outside and those offering small tours - they aren't as well 'educated' and whilst the numbers in the tours may be smaller, the information may be dubious. Get an official tour (we had an archaeologist who had spent time working in the Forum area) as they are better organised and more accurate. They are constantly working on the site to keep it going so there will be some impediment somewhere. When we were there, they were working on the underground area so there was no tour to go through there.

I can't help but be dismayed that this magnificent place was plundered and not maintained over time. With a little imagination and some help from the guide, I was able to picture this in its glory. MUST DO.

title: Favorite Experience in Rome
text:
Visiting the Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill was my favorite part of our trip to Rome.  We read information about these sites before we visited and after we visited to better understand and appreciate the history.  

I spent a long time trying to understand the best way to get tickets for 2 adults and 2 kids that allow us to enter the Colosseum at a designated time.  In short, I bought 2 adult tickets through coopculture.it for a certain time for 28 Euros total.  The price has gone up since then though.  Because I wanted to get the kids in for free, but there wasn't an option online to get kid tickets for free, I couldn't print our adult tickets.  Instead, we had to go to a line to pick up online tickets that is farther back than the line with the black signage that wraps around the Colosseum and is past trees.  This line is actually 2 lines----1 for those with printed tickets and 1 for those who want to buy tickets.  Once I got to the desk to pick up online tickets, I asked for 2 free kid tickets.  They printed the set, and then we were able to go into the line for those with printed tickets---paying a total of 28 Euros for the 4 of us.

Security is tight- take your passport for ID. 
Watch out for scammers around and ticket touts and people selling selfie sticks. If anyone comes up to you all enthusiastically saying hi where are you from how are you etc. They’re a scammer.

title: History brought to life
text:
Belated, but heartfelt thanks

On the 4th of May, my daughter and I were part of a group tour to the Colosseum in Rome, one of the Seven Wonders of the Modern World. From Francesca's vivid presentation, we could imagine this Flavian amphitheatre with its public spectacles gathering over 65,000 people, among which the ones where emperor Trajan celebrated his victory over brave Decebalus (leader of Dacia, placed on the present day territory of Romania) back in 107 AD - with contests involving 11,000 animals and 10,000 gladiators that lasted no less than 123 days... Thank you so much, dear Francesca, for having brought this imposing construction, with its over 2000 years of history, close to our hearts. 

Maria Burs & Irina Popescu, Romania
4 November 2019

Note: Francesca has a BA in History and Archaeology and works with "romeprivateguides".

title: A must-do at least once
text: The Flavian Amphitheatre as it was originally called (the 'colloseum' actually referred to a giant statue of Nero that stood nearby) is of course one of the greatest buildings of the ancient world and one of the most interesting in terms of both the architecture and its use. It's claimed that more people died here that any other patch of dirt of a comparable size anywhere in the world. Frankly it's a nightmare to visit given the queues and the number of people everywhere - and it's not helped at the moment by the fact that the surrounding area is currently a building site with a metro station under construction. It's many years since I actually went inside but I always walk down the Via Sacra and check it out when in Rome. Interestingly on this occasion I saw two young men (archaeologists?) using scanning equipment in the grass area in front of the building; even now they obviously think there are new things to find. Definitely the most astonishing area and building, so if you're on your first visit to Rome gird your loins and check it out. You won't be disappointed.

title: when you imagine Rome, first thing flash is Colosseum
text:
Well when you imagine Rome, first thing flash is colosseum. Like Tajmahal is to India or Statue of Liberty is to the USA or Christ the Redeemer to Brazil or Eiffel Tower is to Paris. 

Well, my fav part (History) -Its construction was started by Emperor Vespasian of the Flavian dynasty in 72 AD and was finished by his son Titus in 80 AD. For over two thousand years this has been a landmark location in Rome, and it was once home to the infamous gladiatorial battles that were watched by tens of thousands of Roman citizens, not to mention Roman Emperors. The elliptical amphitheater could hold up to 50,000 people who turned out to watch gladiators do battle, people be publicly executed and enjoy other forms of entertainment. This stone and concrete structure, built in the first century, was the largest amphitheater in the Roman Empire. It is considered one of the Romans’ greatest architectural and engineering feats.  The last recorded games in history were celebrated in the 6th century.

The emperor Titus inaugurated the Colosseum with 100 days of games, which took the lives of more than 2,000 gladiators.
The Colosseum had a canvas ceiling to protect people from the sun. The machinery and cages were located beneath the arena.
There are some theories that the Colosseum was filled with water for naval battle recreations, well NGC or other historians proved they are right.

You’ll find lengthy lines almost anytime you visit. To beat the queues, you can purchase a ticket either at their site (which fills very fast). We bought the ticket at 18 euro pp at another site which skipped the line ticket and also allows you admittance to the roman hill and Palatine Hill. Trust me, 2-3 hours was waiting in the line that too under the burning sun. So folks get your tickets online and reserve your entry time to the Colosseum. that's important. Even if you have a pass with skip the line privileges, you will only be able to visit the Colosseum after you reserve a time slot.

The restroom facility was very messy and smelly. Not sure maintenance was there or not. When I came out, there were 100s of Kids drawing hanging on the fence. It was incredible. 

Located to the southwest of the main terminus train station, the Colosseum is easily accessible and has a metro station in close proximity. This mighty structure is one of the most renowned and iconic landmarks in the world and a trip to Rome would not be complete without visiting the Colosseum.

title: A true bucket list item.
text:
One of the most famous Roman buildings still standing and something on my to do list for a long time. This magnificent stadium built to entertain the citizens of Rome looks like a ruin, but the Italians have actually done an amazing job of preserving this important piece of heritage. The most iconic part of the Colosseum is of course the oval shaped Outer Wall. The Hypogeum is still there. Even though the floor has long since disappeared, the labyrinth underneath the floor is clearly exposed and it felt amazing wandering through the labyrinth as the gladiators once would have done. Lastly, the boxes where the emperors would have once sat.

A true bucket list item.

title: Confusion reigns supreme.
text:
We booked our "ticket" on line through the Museum's website for 08:30 hours. The booking is in a form of a voucher. You then  are required to queue to obtain the actual  ticket. We arrived at 08:00. Well, confusion reigns supreme. We attempted to gain clarification on the process, but like so many others were simply waved off with a finger pointing to a queue by an "attendant" fluffing around with paper signs and retractable tape barrier. So we waited, watching all the other anxious tourist trying to understand the system while other large queues were forming at the Colosseum entrance not at the ticket office. Once the ticket office opened another attendant told us we should have joined the queue at the entrance, but no matter now as we're now at the front of the ticket office queue. 
The voucher stated that an ID was required to be left at the audio kiosk to insure the return of the audio unit, the kiosk attendant kept our entrance ticket, I realized this a couple of minutes after, thought that must be instead of an ID as the ticket is required to enter the forum etc. But you guessed it, no, just another stuff up. So after being bounced around four different kiosks receiving four different failed responses to get our tickets back we gave up and left. 
Motto to the story:
Keep asking, keep vigilant and don't give up your ticket. 
PS
Our first experience on the Italian railway system has a familiar feel to it, keep vigilant or you could be left behind

title: Unbelievable lack of anything resembling organisation
text:
Our visit today has left me gobsmacked at the utter uselessness (and bad temper and rudeness) of the Colosseum website / ticket offices / queue guides / security. Ditto the Palatine and Forum entrance, and speechless as a result of an interaction with two ‘guides’ or ‘security staff’ at the Museo Palatine, who did not even know where or how one could buy tickets! 
It is as if everyone connected to this area is conspiring to prevent visitors entering! And please don’t ask questions or expect decent signage as to how to gain access or which queue to join for what - no-one knows anything and you end up with contradictory information! Even the audio guides blether on at times about thing that obviously are not there! And after paying for the audio guides in the Palatine/Forum, you get told they must be returned by a certain time! 
I did make my way around, I did enjoy the vast historical architectural sights, but would I seriously recommend as a ‘must see’? Hmmm.... not unless you do a great deal of research beforehand and probably spend even more on a good quality private guide. I have no problem with paying to see and support the ongoing works of such a historical sight, but this definitely felt like a rip-off - and especially as a two day ticket means nothing! You have to use it on one day only! 
Will I be back? Definitely not!

title: Massive & Magnificent! Be careful if combining with Roman Forum & Palatine Hill tour!
text:
This ancient landmark is so unreal to see in person. Unfortunately, there is ongoing renovation currently, so it broke the mystique of the landmark by seeing workers in hard hats. But, it is the off-season so I guess it needs to be done sometime. We made the mistake of combining Palantine Hill & the Roman Forum, which they toured first, with the Colosseum being last on on the tour. I would have preferred to do the Colosseum, then the Roman forum, then Palantine Hill. So caution if you buy a combined ticket because there are no refunds.
But worse than that, we bought a multi-combined ticket that included the Vatican/Vatican Museum/Sistine Chapel on the same day. So by the time we got to the Colosseum, our group was so tired that it was hard to appreciate the information from the tour guide. So take heed of this suggestion for your itinerary as we walked 6 miles in total on that day of touring.

title: HISTORY IS AMAZING!
text: This is a must see while in Rome! We used "Skip the Line" touring company & were very pleased with them.

title: Busy but amazing
text: We were advised before we came to Rome to not bother going inside the colosseum and I’m glad we didn’t yes it’s beautiful but inside is the same as outside the queues are massive even if you have skip the line tickets and it’s so busy all the time day and night we stayed here for a few hours walking round taking photos and the metro line stops right outside

title: The best of Rome
text: Ci siamo trasferiti da poco a roma, simbolo di Roma, magificenza schiacciante, a must to visit if you come in Rome

title: Guided tout
text: Guided tour with Get Your Guide - it was excellent.  We took the three hour tour of the Colloseum, the Palatine Hill, and the Roman Forum.

title: An energetic tour of the Colosseum
text: We did this walking tour with Giovanna.  She was very knowledgeable and kept the pace fairly steady.  There were a few confusing instructions and we couldn’t always keep up with her since there were so many people present getting between us.  This probably would have been better had she used her flag more.  It was a great tour otherwise.  Lots of details and history.  We really enjoyed it

title: Great to see
text:
Be sure to book before you go avoid the question. And
 Get a tour . Or download Rick Stevens guide it free and very good

title: Touts spoiled the visit
text:
As a family we had a short visit to Rome, so decided to walk around the city to see the architecture instead of queuing for tickets. We found many wonderful churches that we could visit for free and enjoyed seeing the sights at a pace to suit ourselves. In this way, we walked around the outside of the Colosseum and admired the renovations and understood the history of what we could see. 

What spoiled this place for us above all other places we visited were the people hassling us to buy bracelets etc. They make a point of shaking your hand, asking where you're from and trying to give you free stuff whilst telling you a happy story about their lives. They don't listen to 'no thank you' and keep running after you to ask about your family etc. My teenage daughter was physically touched by them too. We didn't see this level of harassment anywhere else, other sellers were polite but these people were unbelievable and spoiled that visit for us.

title: Colosseum
text: Wow... an amazing piece of history, a must see.  We had a guide which resulted in no queuing and detailed facts about the colosseum. We went in October which was busy but not too over crowded.

title: A great structure that is a must see when you are in Rome
text: If you have only 1 day in Rome, then do nothing except visiting the Colosseum (of course in addition to tasting Gelato and the delicious Italian cuisine). If you are not with a history or archaeology background then you need only 2 hours to complete the visit.

title: Totally worth it
text: We went with our young children and it was amazing. I highly suggest taking an official tour as we were able to learn much more than if we just walked around by ourselves. I can’t recommend this enough!

title: Il Colosseo
text: Didn't get a chance to do the tour inside but it's breahtaking just taking it in from the surrounding area - so much history!

title: long lines but moves quickly
text: The Colosseum is one of the must see attractions in Rome. However, long lines can deter you.  The people at the Colosseum know what they are doing.  We went on the free Sunday (1st Sunday of the month all Italian museums are free).  You still have to wait in line to get a ticket and then another line to get in.  There is no "skip the line" option on the free Sunday. The line to get in wrapped 1/2 way around the building but the staff kept it moving.  We figured 1-2 hour wait but we were inside and through security in half an hour.  A definite must-see.

title: Thieves and rubbish
text: Thousands of tourists crowd in so you will find it difficult to get photos at ground level.Beware thieves! Thieves are everywhere here so keep your valuables safe. All this detracts from what you are trying to do. Filth and rubbish everywhere too. Touts buy the score trying to get your money offering bogus tickets to something you don't need or junk you don't want.

title: Pleasure
text: Amil was a terrific tour guide for our walking tour of the Colosseum, forum and palatine hill. She showed great energy, knowledge and also had a sense of humor. This was one of the highlights of our trip!

title: Interesting tour
text: The Colosseum is an imposing structure and very interesting historically.  It is well worth taking time to tour it thoroughly.

title: Colosseum
text: Was very well to see the Colosseum which I saw many times before but was in TV & this was the first time to see in real.

title: It’s history.. take the time
text: We booked thru viator and our guide was Damien .. he is an expert. Best tour and very informative. Take the tour it’s the small details that matter

title: Amazing historical landmark!
text: My partner and I visited the Colosseum last Wednesday 30.10.19. We prebooked from the UK and picked an 8.45 am slot. Booking ahead really helped as the queues can get very long even in October!  This is definitely a must see if you visit Rome! Absolutely fascinating to see when you consider how old it is! Palatine Hill and The Roman Forum are nearby.

title: Amazing breathtaking place
text:
A must see! There is nowhere quite like this place. 
Give you an amazing sense of history and the information available is easily accessible

title: Amazing
text: A fantastic experience!! Excellent weather. Paid for a personal group tour of 14 people. Skip the lines, lower level, Palatine Hill etc, Amazing!!

title: A great monument that is not well kept
text: The colosseum was one of the sites I have always wanted to see. I went there with my wife and we found a crumbling site that has not been restored at all and looks the same in pictures from 1900... that was a disappointment for us as this is one of the interesting sites to see and we literally couldn't see almost anything because most of it is closed and look quite ruined...

title: Brilliant visit
text: Most definitely worth a visit. Top tips which we found out and really helped. Get to the colleseum between 7 and half 7. It opens at half 8 but you’ll be waiting the least amount of time and it’ll be quieter when you get in there. Get your ticket here and go straight over to the forum. Don’t bother with the audio guides. Your in a group of around 30-50 and with that many people your fighting to actually see what the guide is telling you about. Instead opt for Rick steves Europe exploring app and have a free tour on your phone with your headphones. There is also a rick steve’s forum guide which is brilliant. Forums a lot less busy so make this your second stop AFTER the colleseum. Go the first Sunday of the month and you get in free along with the Vittoriano with the amazing views which you can get the lift up to the top.

title: Book in advance - Roma Pass may help
text: A great visit to a quite extraordinary place. The history here is rich but the line is long. So do yourself a favour and book in advance. We got us a Roma Pass - they hold extra tickets for holders of these cards (They still need to be booked in advance at coopculture) and that saved us probably 2 hours waiting in line (IN NOVEMBER). We visited with our own audio guide on a smartphone app and that worked quite well.

title: A stunning piece of history
text: We went to the colosseum on our first day in Rome. We were lucky the walk rather was sunny and warm as I could imagine this not being much fun on a rainy day. From the outside it is just a stunning piece of history. Such a beautiful site. We have pre booked tickets so just skipped the queue. Inside was howeve very very busy and there was a lot of refurbish emend going on. Glad to have seen such a wonderful piece of history but don’t think I’d need to go back. Still a very enjoyable afternoon.

title: Historic rome colloseo
text: A well preserved historic site, thus they have to limit a certain number of visitors at a time. Buy online tickets or come later in the evening to avoid long line for tickets

title: Amazing Roman building
text: We had a guided tour and it was a hot day, best option is pay a little more and take the guided tour which skip the long queues and get to see everything quickly with a history lesson.

title: "Great Icon"

title: Magisterial

Right now there are restoration projects underway and the results are astounding on the arena floor and some seats. I definitely recommend booking your ticket beforehand however because the queue can be large.

title: Colosseum by Night Tour only way to go

You are then handed off to an official Colosseum tour guide. Overall 2 1/2 hours of Roman history. Lot of walking but so worth it.

title: Do a tour!
text: Highly recommend buying a skip the line tour for this. Walked straight in with our guide instead of lining up for hours. This place is amazing to visit and do a tour.

title: Don't miss

The stories that go with the construct, such as all the marble basically being stolen for the Vatican and other churches was insightful.  Combined with the Forum and option to climb to the viewpoint of Palatine Hill was a good combination.  I would not do this without a tour guide.

title: Wonderful experience
text: Once in a lifetime experience.  Make sure you pay for skip the line passes or hire a guide.  The lines are very long even in October.

title: Fantastic - not ot be missed
text: How do you describe a historical monument, active archaeology site and amazing view of the past?  I guess just this way!  You cannot visit Rome without spending at least 3 hours - guided tour recommended

title: Stunning
text: What a place. it's incredible - I cant wait to go back to see it once it's finished!!! It doesnt dissapoint - worth the effort of going to see it.

title: Touriks tour guide for the Colosseum, arena and the Roman Forum and hill tour.
text: Booked on line in the morning for our tour. All details and directions on where to meet were very well laid out. Everything was hassle free, our tour guide Eleanor, was excellent & very passionate on explaining the history of Rome. The tour lasted around 3.5 hours ending in the colosseum where once the tour finished  you could stay longer. Fantastic trip . Thank you again.

title: Colosseum
text:
