The only problem with the visit is that Rome is now so full of visitors, that it is just impossible to fully enjoy any historical site or museum.The only solution I imagine would help, is for the authorities to limit the number of people who enter per hour.

title: go without a tour
text: I recommend buying your own ticket online and go without a tour.  The tour guides will try to rush you through the exhibits.  Everything the guides tell you can be found online or from reading the plaques on site.  Take my advice, buy your own ticket online and go at your own pace.

title: Colosseum
text: Awesome architecture and history. Visit the Forum and Palatine Hill nearby. So many photo opportunities.

title: One of the Best Attractions in Rome
text: A must-see when in Rome! Take a guided tour that accesses the underground area to learn about the history of the colosseum and see where animals and gladiators spent time before battles. We went with LivItaly and had a great tour!

title: History
text: This was my 3rd visit to Rome and I never tire of visiting the Colosseum.It is a wonder to look at.I did not go in this time as the lineup was long but just viewing th outside architecture is worth a visit

title: Amazing piece of history..
text: We got into a tour at the last minute via Get Your Guide and joined an ongoing group.. the colosseum is an absolute spectacle from inside than what you see outside.. it’s a shame that over 70% of it is already destroyed.. it’s a must visit Wonder of the Modern world!

title: Grander than you can imagine
text: Getting in and walking around this amazing historical monument is truly historical. It takes you can in time. I've visited both via guided tour and on my own around the outside of the Colosseum.  Take your time, take lots of photos and buy yourself a mini Colosseum paperweight. It's worth it.

title: Outstanding... #1 in the shortlist
text:
When in Rome...do like the Romans..indeed!
If you have to pick a site/monument to visit in Rome, start with the mighty Colosseum.
Make sure you have proper walking/running shoes (imperative! especially if you include the Forum and Palatine Hill, and depending on the ticket you can do it in 2 days, or same day... carry light and check the weather... dress accordingly). Do the 'paramount' by acquiring the 'skip-the-line' tickets beforehand and you'll skip the ginormous entry lines and breeze to entry. 
Jaw-dropping magnificence experience and if you're an history buff, well then polish up on the info beforehand and you'll make your visit that much more interesting. Likewise, including the arena is a worthwhile addition if one relates to the historical and ongoing archaeological works. 
Like all and any monument in Rome, taking photos can be a daunting exercise that requires some patience...be prepared for tons of people everywhere and waiting your turn!
There are lots of restaurants/esplanades around in the peripheral roads and most offer a partial view of the Colosseum, awesome great if you're having supper and see it all lit. 
This is the #1 monument to visit if you have a shortlist...or any list.
Thank you Rome...

title: The lines!!!!!
text: This is the one place I would recommend a skip-the-line purchase. We didn't do it and had to pass on seeing the inside. People flock to Colosseum. But seeing the outside was also very impressive.

title: The colleseum of ROME!
text:
The colleseum is a monument to the city of Rome that has stood for many decades and is visited by many in their flocks as I found out on this particualr visit.

The visit began with me collecting my joint ticket which a gave me access to the Roman Forum site and the colleseum and I collected it from the pick up point located outside. Upon entering I was searched along whth the many thousands... yes I said thousands... which shocked me a little as I was wondering where was everyone going to stand.

I was earlier informed the best time to visit would be either first thing in the morning or after sunset, I had a busy schedule so to turn up when I did and see the hoards of people all being  searched and waiting to get in, to say yhe leastvI was worried I may not get out without having to queue up. 

Anyway enough digressing, i waundered around the three different levels.. normal ticket will get you access to the two lower levels, and the third level can be accessed with further appointed tickets.. the views as you can see from the pictures were great alespecially with such a well lit day and amazing views whichballowed me to capture the venue.

I felt like I had arrived at the place where battles had been fought, were men had won glouriously and been slayed after a untriumphant attempt... so much history and moment qere captured in one place and witness here and was amazing to attend such a prestigous place that obviously looks like it fallin apart from the outside but thats what makes this place so memorable.

I would advise people to visit thisbsite when in Rome, I would also advise to visit at a good hour that suits you please remember rhat it will be very busy.

title: No need for "skip the queue" in Spring or Winter
text: €16 to get in and you can purchase a head set for €5.50 Euros if you wish for a tour inside. No need for touts outide who are a real pain. We only waited 20 mins and you stay in as long as you want.

title: Chaos but worth it.
text:
I had an epiphany in Rome but it was nothing to do God, I realised the tour guides, whose eager groups of tourists create bottle necks absolutely everywhere in the city, only exist to serve the all important ‘Skip the Line’ tickets. If the major attractions like the Colosseum and the Vatican Museum, simply ran audio tours in 8 languages and all tickets were timed and had to be purchased online beforehand (like the incredibly well organised Alcatraz in San Francisco Bay), it would all be a much better experience. But this is Italy and I guess chaos is part of the charm. 
Once we’d spotted our guide’s tiny black flag in a sea of flags by the Arch of Constantine, waited for the rest of the tour group, waited for the tour guides to decide what they were doing, waited while one guide took all the children away to get the under 18 tickets (child safeguarding? Not on my watch), queued with the other ‘skip the line’ groups and queued at security, it took about an hour to get in (I couldn’t help thinking we could have just queued for a normal ticket). Our guide then told us we had an hour in the Colosseum before the second part of our tour to the Roman Forum.
To be fair once you get in there it’s amazing and some of the exhibitions are really informative. And actually you only need about an hour. Do yourselves a favour and move past the inevitable bottle necks as there really is plenty of space further round to take your time, get great views of the arena and soak up the sense of how enormous the Colosseum would have been in it’s heyday.

title: 2nd visit to Colosseum & Forum and would do it again!
text: Our guide, a charming gentleman, was incredibly insightful & knowledgeable. The tour pace was perfect & we were engaged the entire time. It was my second time (different trip in 2011) visiting the Colosseum & Forum and I was as intrigued as ever. The preserved structures, ruins, and depth of history is mind blowing.

title: It was okay
text: For me... one time for a seeing the Coloseum in my entire life is enough. From the outside is this building for me more impressive than from inside.

title: So interesting
text: Pay the extra for the tour to the parts under the stage. Our guide was so knowledgable and answered all our questions.

title: When in Rome.
text: Heard mixed reviews about visiting the Colosseum but decided to give it a try and was not disappointed. Arrived at the Palatine hill ticket office at 8.50 am with no queues, and walked straight to the kiosk to purchase our tickets, of which we were given a 9.45 am entry time. with the tickets you walk straight through to security, then straight inside the Colosseum. You can take in the amazing structure and sheer size of this amazing structure at your own leisure. We were in awe of the place and what occurred here in the past. The 12 euro price include entry to the Roman Forum and Palatine hill.

title: Beautiful
text: This has to be the most impressive and beautiful building I’ve ever seen! Didn’t know what to expect upon arrival however it went way above all expectations. Did the outside in daylight and night tome and they are both just as beautiful as each other. Wish I had gone inside but I’ll save that for my next visit to Rome. A must see when in Rome

title: Walking around doesn't do it justice
text: You can't see Rome in a day, but you can walk around it while you there.  This was one of the spots we walked around.  It's massive and impressive with destruction.  It deserves another visit when we can take a tour or go inside and appreciate it.

title: Impressive and a must-see attraction
text: Although I’ve seen the Colosseum on a previous visit to Italy many years ago, I was impressed all over again to see it a second time. Best to go on a sunny day (as we did)to really appreciate.

title: My Second Visit with family
text: My second visit but still enjoyed. My sister bought a skip on line tickets and of course we were able to skip the long line. Very impressed with the staff, very helpful and speak good English.

title: Tip for free entry days
text: It so happened we were in Rome on the 3rd of November ( official free first Sunday of the month) and a glorious sunny warm 4th of November,  one of the extra free government days. Sunday was the busiest. To beat the line at Colosseum you go to the gate at Palastine Hill around 8 -8.15 am , not busy at all  , opening at 8.30. Get your free ticket and walk back to Colosseum. ( 5 min walk)  There are 2 lines. One for the people waiting in line ( yes long one) and the other line for ones  already having a ticket, so that's you,  superfast entry ,  Visit RF and PH after Colloseum. No audio guide available so download your own. No Arena access on those days either.

title: Escorted tour
text:
We were approached by an escorted tour seller just outside the Colosseum, which we went for as included other attractions.

Beat the queues, which is a must. The tour guide spoke impressive English and I honestly don’t think we would have got as much of an experience had we gone it alone.

A must do.

title: Honeymoon couple
text: Colosseum was one of the most interesting sights in Rome. Just a shame that almost every sight is under construction in October-November

title: History revisits
text: Buy the express tickets or else will end up wasting more than 2 hours waiting to get in . Take a guide , it will make u go back to history and live

title: Must see in Rome, Italy
text: Recommend purchasing the skip the line tickets ahead of time.  This is one of the world's most visited attractions.  And there is a good reason for that....it is incredible.  Take the self-guided tour (headset with audio) and marvel at the history of this amazing place.

title: History
text: Was a cool experience learned a lot. The place was huge and a lot of walking and climbing of stairs be prepared and get a good guide.

title: 2000 Years of History
text:
Definitely purchase your tickets ahead of time on-line! This shortens your waiting time considerably. We chose to do a self-guided tour around the colosseum (I had several guidebooks to tell us about the history) and we just toured above ground. You can pay extra for a guided tour or to go underground. For our family, simply walking around was amazing. We took tons of photos and were in awe of the whole place. It was truly an amazing experience.
We heard it was crowded in the mornings so we went late afternoon. It was summertime and hot but up at the top a breeze blows and the view is great. Highly recommend this.

title: A beautiful reminder of how ancient and awe inspiring Roman civilisation is.
text: To walk the entire circumference of this building is to gain an understanding of the wonders of the ancient world. Truly a fabulous experience in the day and the evening. Roma Police are on duty throughout the entire time this site is open and play an active role in keeping people safe from the scammers who hang around many of the tourist areas of Rome.

title: Colosseum Rome
text:
What can be said about a place standing over 2000 years an being visited continuously for years?
Be patient if you didn’t buy tickets online but don’t get desperate, the line goes a lot faster than it seems.

title: Amazing 2 C
text:
What an ancient and beautifull place 2 C!
Do buy an roma pass and skip the lines.
Loved to see the amszing colloseum and the special arts.

title: History text book comes alive
text: My family and I enjoyed our Colosseum experience, however it was very crowded and difficult at times to keep up with tour guide.Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and was difficult to keep up for the one hour due to so many visitors.I would suggest a longer tour and perhaps a smaller group if you can get a small group.

title: Early Morning Gem
text: Go early in the morning and listen to the Rick Steves free podcast tour for an economical and hassle free time.

title: Looked past it's best!!
text:
Place was falling down and looked like an olde ruin :)

We visited 21 years ago and they haven't done much since then.

title: Colosseum
text: We had a 3 hour tour which was fantastic and so insightful. You could spend all day at this historic place. If you are visiting Rome ensure you visit the Colosseum.

title: Wonderful day
text: We chose to have a guided tour entry bu the Gladiators gate, the tour guide was brilliant. His knowledge and time spent explaining and answering questions was invaluable, yes you can go in without but I believe the added explanations and negating fact from fiction was worth it. Fabio ur tour guide was funny and knowledgable and instead of the usual "flag" he had a light sabre and was very easy to spot. Well done Fabio for your tour.

title: Night time tour
text:
I’ve done the colosseum tour a few times throughout the years as I’ve been traveling to Rome with people who have never seen the inside. I would recommend it. If you do a guided tour as I have done previously, you get a history of the landmark and get to bypass the lines which can get quite long during the day! 
This time I had the opportunity to do a night time tour. It’s definitely something to see and the underground is quite neat...you’re standing in a piece of history! 
My tour was in Italian. Our tour guide was great! She was very thorough! Unfortunately the tour was all sold out in English so we opted for the Italian tour and my partner does not understand Italian so it was up to me to translate everything. He however was more interested in taking pictures anyway so made my life easier and I just gave him the important bits of info!! The pictures from the inside are beautiful if your camera can capture it!!

title: Awesome!
text: For only €16 each, we got entry into the Coliseum, Forum and Palatine. Excellent value for money. All three sites are just awesome! We spent over five hours there. Wear comfortable walking shoes!! My only complaint would be that there isn't any tea room or coffee shop. It would have been lovely to have a sit down and a drink as it's a long day doing all three sites. Great visit though and I would highly recommend it.

title: Amazing
text: Wow this def a place to see, we did not go in but it was still fab, lots to see around. After your walk to take it all in top of the road across from it go to The Royal Art cafe  wow views and food fantastic, we went twice was so good  😍

title: Wow
text: What a wonderful experience visiting this world famous sight. Equally as magnificent by night, all lit up. Interesting and fun. Worth the 16 euros to enter. A great photo opportunity too! One of my hi lights of my time in Rome!

title: Nice city ROMA
text: We're lucky to spend 3 days in Rome. The weather is good and we enjoy our trip to many place in Rome Colosseum and many nice Piaza's

title: Touristy
text: I love the building from the outside and from the inside, just so-so. It's also very touristy, so maybe this affected my impression

title: Amazing!
text: What more can I say. Wonderful building with so much history. Make sure you book a time slot in advance to avoid a long queue. There are some toilets inside near the ticket office but be prepared to queue. We had read that your not allowed to take backpacks in and there were signs up saying this too but everyone has one. There are water fountains inside as well on both the ground and first floor. We spent almost 3 hours here, it was a wonderful experience.

title: Rome tour
text: Loved the colosseum and forum tour , Stephenie was a very good guide very knowledgeable, glad we booed a tour as you’d had to queue for a long time and wouldn’t have learnt as much .

title: Must do it!
text: Wow so big! You have for sure to book online ticket so you skip the line! Also you must go at the palatino hill and general at the hills around (same tickets with Colosseum)

title: Collesium arena and underground tour
text:
Briiliant tour with our guide AJ very informative guide who made us all feel included in the small tour ( 6 people) the tour itself was fantastic and being able enter the underground section of collesium was brilliant 
Would recomend this tour with livitaly and guide AJ

title: another time

title: A guide will enhance the experience

If it's your first time here, go inside, and even better, hire a guide so that you will better understand what you see. No visit to Rome is complete without seeing this archeological wonder.

title: Skip the line Coliseum, Roman forum and palatine hill.

Stunning.if you want to know more about history you can not go wrong. There is so much to see and hear. You get the chance to go up to look down into the excavations, and hear how ancient Roman civilization was developed. Even the myth of Romulus is explained in a realistic way.

title: Walking in the steps of history!

title: Tour

title: See The Colosseum With LivItaly Tours With AJ Through Viator!
text: AJ was an amazing guide!  She was so personable and so informative!  The five of us enjoyed our small group tour!  AJ's enthusiasm of the history of this unique building captured our attention and she took us to many of the other sites near it!  We will definitely use LivItaly again!!!! Thank you AJ for making our dream come true!

title: Day trip
text: Colosseum is beautiful, definitely prebook as ques very long, as is the que for toilet.lovely day out at the Colosseum

title: impressive
text: use the metro to reach colosseum easily, book your tickets in advance to skip the line, a guided tour is more lively and to get to know the Romans

title: Amazing
text: The visit to the Colosseum and the surrounding areas were absolutely amazing.  My husband and I did a day trip from Naples and truly wish we had allocated more time for Rome.  We went at the end of October and the weather was perfect.  We were approached by a local tour guide and we decided to do the tour at 35 euros per person.  It was a four hour tour which included the Colosseum and the park right next to it.  It was well worth it as we were able to skip the long lines and it was very informative.  We will definitely be back to Rome.

title: If you see nothing else, visit the Colosseum
text: When you visit, imagine the entire Colosseum covered in marble and in each archway a magnificent statue.  BEHOLD the glory of Rome! Envision chariot races--horses pounding the earth, charioteers careening around the entire surface of the arena! I have been here of late (this past October!), but I can honestly say I've visited the Colosseum with friends and family over 100 times! That is no exaggeration! The first time was in 1964 with my brother after I graduated university.

title: Fantastic Morning in Rome
text:
Despite the November rain we thoroughly enjoyed our visit here. 
Beware of the massive crowds it is definitely worth buying a 'skip the line's ticket. It really is a once in a life time experience.

title: Book in advance!
text: Book online and save lots of queuing. It’s a must though. Epic proportions, amazing history. Over 400 years a million people died here for entertainment. Pretty barbaric.

title: History and Grandeur
text: Walking down the street towards the colliseum is awe inspiring.  The first glimpse made us want to walk faster as it was amazing to see.  The area is crowded and if you want to tour inside, get your tickets in advance.  You will NOT get in without making arrangements in advance.  There is a lot of history to see for several blocks around the colliseum.  Remember that the city is old and has grown up all around the old forums and temples.  Many old stones and materials have been used in the newer surrounding buildings and the area deserves at least a day to appreciate the history.  This is a highly photographed area and I'll contribute a few.  Enjoy!

title: Absolutely and interesting place
text: The colosseum absolutely interesting and visit that’s worth taking. The colosseum is a big place so there’s a lot of walking. Please wear your most comfortable shoes or gym shoes.

title: When in Rome...must do
text:
Goes without saying Colloseum is a must do when in Rome! 

Definitely pay the extra amount to book yourself onto a guided tour, and recommend organising this in advance. A tour guide will give you all the history and interesting stories etc, otherwise you’d just be just looking around with no context (as there are no info boards etc to read)

Make sure you also get skip the line access as part of your guided tour!

We did the standard tour, there is also an underground tour, which we’ll save for next time.

title:
BE AWARE !

Black people from Africa Robb people around Colosseum during day time !!!!

Police should stop this !!!!
text:
Just 2 Days ago, this is my story: 

I walked around colosseum to buy tickets, when a black man from Africa approach me. He putet something on my hand without my consent , and then he asked for money. He was agressive verbal and fizicaly. I try to give his thing back but he stop me . I give him back the thing he putet on my hand, I was very nervous and scared in the same time.
