title: 6th trip to Rome and best tour I have been on!
text: We toured yesterday with Silvia and she was fantastic! Even though we have been to Rome several times, we still learned so much new information yesterday! Silvia was funny and incredibly knowledgeable! I highly recommend this tour!

title: Gladiators entrance tour with Roman forum and Palatine hill
text: Enjoyed the tour. Very knowledgeable guide and made seeing the sights knowing the history fun. Booked through Walks of Italy

title: the tuors
text: The tours are by foot and very crowded with people from all over the world .Its a fantastic experience.Puts your life in perspective,with a new energy from this trip,so POSITIVE

title: Interesting vist
text: Worth seeing would go again. Some dodgy areas under foot so take care. You certainly get into the atmosphere of the place and can imagine the gladiators and audiences.

title: Still a great experience
text:
We had been to the Colosseum about 12 years ago, and were blown away by it then. We arrived late in the day, there was hardly any line-up, and we had a great visit. This time, we were traveling with someone who had never been there, and half the fun was watching her reaction to the majesty of it all!
It was interesting to see the ongoing restoration work, and the changes since last visit- for example, being able to access the floor. The exhibits are informative, and it seems like the traffic flow is managed well. We wish we would have had time to book the 'behind the scenes' tour- we'll do that next time.

title: Colosseum
text: Rolling Rome Vincenzo as guide. Amazing knowledgeable and friendly.  Truly proud of his Italian descendants and of this amazing site.  Best guide in Italy

title: Good but expensive
text: Love architecture and history but felt that this was over priced. Yes definitely pre book tickets as queues are very long. We queued the previous day but was unable to enter as sold out. We chose to go round ourselves without a guide. Yes it was amazing but not worth the money

title: Colosseum
text: Mesmirizing architecture,be prepared on really big line for tickets so buy them online and skip the line.

title: Great tour!
text: History comes alive when you enter this place. Tour guides were outstanding. Would have loved to see the upper floors as well.

title: Excellent place to visit
text: We visited this monstrous amphitheater in a sunny day. It is fabulous. There are some damages because of natural disasters but luckily a shoe factory has invested some money to save thus masterpiece. Definitely is recommended.

title: Ok. But trash, long queues and pickpockets detract
text:
It a very interesting building and an important part of Roman history. But we endured long queues, pickpockets and lots of trash on the ground. These all detract from the experience.

Also, why after all these years have they still not finished building it ?

title: Short visit
text: We visited here but found the ques way too long and busy. So we walked around the outside only, which to be fair is still stunning.

title: Amazing
text: Anyone going to Rome this building should be your first to visit on the list so much to see and learn until you have been there you wont know the building is on a massive scale for size

title: Self your, interesting
text: I paid €12 and my ticket gave me entry to both the Colosseum and the Forum. Really interesting. The self tour is at your own pace with a virtual guide via a handset.

title: Stunning
text: You you travel here by metro the view is stunning as you exit the station. Definitely recommend you get guided tour (small group), well worth the money to skip the line and get all the info at a nice pace.

title: Honeymoon
text: Very busy and you must get there early because the line gets really long. The weather was nice while providing an excellent opportunity to take some memorable pictures.

title: Gladiators play pen
text: This was one of my bucket list items. I loved every minute of it. The feeling you get when you step on to the stage was unbelievable. To be in a place that was once the ultimate warriors arena. The history was just unbelievable. This is a must!

title: Amazing!
text: Get an underground and third level tour to really get good views and information. These tours are very affordable and available on the Coop culture website. Only downside is that they release on one day for a few months time and sell out fast! Check out the Rome forum for more info. The views from the third level are amazing!!! You only get these through the official coop culture website. The underground portion was underwhelming though.

title: still a magnificent view
text:
good as expected, come early to queue. long wait after midday. 

metro construction around the outside so some support structures are built to hold the building itself. not nice for pictures.

title: Worth it!
text: Definitely worth the wait, my advice dont "skip the line" its double the cost. Buy it online or at the door on the day

title: Amazing, a must visit
text: Loved our visit to the colosseum, it’s an absolute must when in Rome. Highly recommend the palatine hill tour too

title: Must see
text:
Must see attraction when in rome
We did a 3 hour tour. Was great. 

When visiting I would definitelt recommend a tour.

title: Post cruise
text:
Should have ordered skip the line tickets cause waiting in line was such a waste of precious vacation time and the ticket line is poorly organized. 
Once inside was disappointed by all the scaffolding and construction going on inside and the regular entry ticket doesn’t give you access to all levels. Spent more time in the ticket line than actually touring the location.

title: One of the most beautiful monuments
text:
If you are in Rome you have to visit the biggest romanesque monument.
Home of gladiator
A must see if you are in the city

title: Fantastic Roman amphitheatre
text:
Well worth the very reasonable entry fee. We would have been prepared to pay more because the ongoing restoration and preservation work must cost a fortune. Our combined ticket also included the adjacent Forum and Palatino and was excellent value for money. We did both attractions in one day but could have spent longer in the Forum. It's worth booking ahead to jump the queue for the Colosseum but there was no similar advantage at the Forum which was a bit annoying. 
Toilet facilities for women are inadequate.
It would be good if the museum area included more about the history of the Colosseum rather than about Carthage.

title: Skip-the-line Colosseum (incl. arena floor) / Forum /Palatine
text:
I booked three tickets online in March 2019 though City Wonders for this excursion whilst visiting Rome with my archaeological colleagues. The ticket price was EUR 36.00 each which Viator handled for City Wonders. One of our number had to cancel which he did several days in advance of the scheduled visit. I contacted City Wonders to claim a refund under their +24 hour policy and eventually a refund of just EUR 26.46 was issued via Viator! When queried they said that the ticket price was a special discount price to their handler, Viator and said that they, Viator, should repay the difference. Viator have said that City Wonders should pay......and so back and forth we go! Neither company wanted to, nor has, honoured their customer policy, clearly stated on each of their websites, that they refund in full any cancellation before 24 hours of the scheduled visit. Viator have only, on behalf of City Wonders, refunded EUR 26.46. Despite several emails seeking a full refund, they have not responded in offering the difference.

I would strongly recommend that you book any excursion through a company that does not use a third-party payment company.

Of the Colosseum experience, we were allowed only 20 minutes on the arena floor and just 40 minutes within the monument!!!! Book an individual combined ticket in advance (as recommended above), obtain a 'paper' guide and enjoy the monuments at your leisure not in a tour or group led.

title: Might and Magnificence of Rome
text: Despite being ravaged by barbarians, later civilisations and earthquakes, the Colossum remains one of the greatest of all Roman monuments and an absolutely quintessential attraction to visit when in Rome. It is well worth getting a skip the line ticket, especially if you want to visit around lunch time- but you need to do this at least a couple of months ahead of your visit as there are only limited tickets available at each time slot throughout the day! We took a private walking tour which was fantastic.

title: Wonderful History
text:
Having taken my parents to Rome, this was one of the main sights on their list of things to see. I advise you to get here early, as queues can be quite lengthy (We had a Roma Pass, so booked our time slot & still queued for 20 minutes). 
To walk around it, you realise what a spectacle it would have been & how imposing a structure. Spend an extra €5 on an audioguide to make it really worthwhile & learn the history that surrounds it & the layout. 

Well worth a visit, but if you only want to walk around the outside then go in the evening when there are no tourists & it's beautifully lit up.

title: A mess because of the metro.
text:
The Colosseo is currently the victim, once again, of modern times. It seems like it is one of those perennial martyrs of Rome: In the Middle Ages and Renaissance, the structure was used as a quarry for building materials, and now she suffers the indignity of temporary walls/barricades taller than the average man obscuring her view from the street. The metro will be quite handy for Romans and tourists alike, so the inconvenience of blocked access just has to be dealt with in the interim. One can only hope the metro station is being expedited. 
I am lucky insomuch as I have been to Rome multiple times prior to this trip, and have been to the Colosseo under different circumstances: no walls/barricades and (mostly) unobstructed views. Now one must get closer to get the view and the experience does change. In the end, the visit is still very much worth it. 
I have done the general admission to the Colosseo twice prior to this trip, and did the more costly nighttime tour this time around. I recommend both, as the lighting does make a difference, but the nighttime tour was wonderful as there were only a couple groups of about 40 in the structure rather than the thousands packed in during a normal day. If you are questioning whether you should spend the extra money for the more exclusive tour, I would say that I do not think you will regret it. Like the Vatican Museums, this is one place that the extra money truly makes a great impact on the quality of your experience.

title: Still Standing
text: The famed Colosseum was a magnificent historic site. The lines to get in were very long so we paid a small fee to go straight into the site. While some of the structure has fallen from decay, there is still a lot to see.

title: A must
text: If you're in Rome, the Colosseum is a must! Tickets are 16euro which includes entry to the forum. The history of the colosseum is rich and interesting - even it's name was not meant to be the Colosseum, named  Amphitheatre Flavium originally, but because it was located near a statue of a colossus...the nickname stuck. Beware of rip off merchant's promising you queue jumps and the like. Tour guides also promise a full tour of all 3 sites around the Colosseum, but take your money and only pay for a group pass to the Colosseum,doing a runner at the end, so make sure if you book a tour, to be sure your ticket covers the forum too. There are men walking up and down the queues selling water and souvenirs at extremely inflated prices so beware. As well, scammers thrust bracelets and other bits of rubbish at you and then demand money. Do not stop for these people. Lastly, if you don't want to wait hours and hours in frustration in the entry queues, book your tickets in advance. When numbers in the colosseum reach 3000, the entry of further tourists comes to a virtual stand still. Wear comfy shoes and pack lots to drink, especially if you go in summer.

title: Interesting
text: We managed to get tickets for €2 as we took our passports to show that we were 18-25 and lived in part of the eu. So an absolute bargain. The place itself is interesting, its mad to think what when on there whilst you walk around. We arrived at 10am and managed to get inside within 40minutes and this was when the weather was nice, as we had expected to be in line for at least double the time.

title: Too many people!
text: This is a beautiful place but the crowds ruined our experience.  We had tickets but when we went to get a tour we were told we couldn't get a tour until Christmas.  We did the walking tour but felt like we didn't learn alot.  The site was so pretty in the night, but be careful about what you are getting with your ticket!  And buy them ahead of time.  We were told tickets were sold out for weeks.

title: Amazing place
text: We came here today, this place is amazing it takes your breath away, we highly recommend that you book your tickets before you go or go to the booking office outside the coliseum or the forum and get your tickets there DON’T buy from the people on the street they over charge you buy 25 euros more, we got a 24 hour skip the queue ticket & you get entrance to coliseum, forum and Palatine for 16 euros per adult and 5 euros with an English tour guide we had a lady called Chiara she was fantastic, if your disabled you go in free and also your helper,

title: Must see
text: I booked early with tour agency so It was amazing experience for me.But the tour included also Roman Forum and Palatine hill.

title: Colosseum
text: Glad we booked a guide. Going to such a historic place Without being told about the historic relevance and description of events is pointless. She knew so much and was very passionate. Highly recommended. If I had the chance, I would have booked the underground tour, but satisfied nonetheless.

title: Site to see
text: This is another of Rome’s beautiful sites that is better appreciated if with a guide, they explain so much and you get really understand what happened back in the days when this Colosseum was in it;s glory.

title: A Must
text: The colosseum was top of my list of attractions and it didn’t disappoint. It’s so easy to get wrapped up in the atmosphere. Beware of lucky lucky men the are all over the place.

title: The highlight of our trip!
text: Our tour guide was phenomenal!!  She made us feel like we were actually there when the Gladiators fought!  She made the place "come alive"!  I would say this was the best tour we've ever had!

title: A must
text: If you visit Rome you must do the Colosseum. It wasn’t what I expected but well worth the 16€ each. The queues weren’t too bad and going through security was quite swift.

title: Nice little break
text: Spent 3 days in Rome. We hadn't book a visit to the Colosseum but arrival we were met by a lovely Italian lady who explained the pricing guide for entry and how if we booked to be part of a group tour we would virtually walk straight in to the Colosseum rather than wait in line. We weren't disappointed one tour guide explained and showed us around the Colosseum. Then a lovely chap took us on a further tour of palatine hill. Which was very informative. Including his explanation of Titus which he told in 37 seconds. Really enjoyable tour would definitely recommend.

title: The colosseum
text:
The colosseum its self was amazing  the fact there is still original parts to this building is fascinating and I would make sure you get to go underneath the arena as it gives you a real insight in to the working of how it would have been.

The only downside is it is impossible to get within 100 meters of the colosseum without being stopped every 2 seconds for someone to try and sell you a tour, rain coat, charger bank and a whole host of other rubbish,

title: Excellent
text: Fabulous tourist attraction, worth paying extra couple of euros with a guided tour to pass the big queues which can take up to 2 hours. Once inside we did our own thing,steeped in history. Amazing at night time.

title: Iconic landmark, though a bit overrated
text:
I found the Colosseum itself to be a bit overrated - the Colosseum area in general (if including the Roman Forum & Palatine Hills) are amazing and comparable to other 'World Wonders', though I found the Colosseum by itself to be no more special than many other ancient complexes around the world that did not receive this distinction.

To skip the long ticket que - get a Roma pass (48-72 hours) online, which gives you access to 1-2 museums free of charge - as the Colosseum is one of the pricier (if not priciest?) museum, it's worthwhile using the Roma pass for this one (it also allows you access to the Roman Forum and Palatine hill), which you can access just opposite the Colosseum as soon as you exit it. 

You can pick up your Colosseum with a Roma Pass at the small ticket office opposite the main entrance to the Colosseum and then enjoy the walk past a huge line of people waiting to get tickets in person.

title: Two thousand years as Italy's top tourist attraction
text: The Colosseum is an amazing piece of architectural history. You can walk around the building on the outside to admire it for free, but I suggest paying admission to enter.  General admission costs 12€ and grants access to the Colosseum and the Roman City. For  22€ admission, you gain access to the basement and the 3rd level of the Colosseum, but you must be accompanied by a professional tour guide. These tickets tend to sell out, so you can order tickets in advance online for a 4€ fee. Beware the tickets bought online are for specific times and you may be refused access if you’re late.

title: Breathtaking
text: It is just amazing to visit, but we learned that just having tickets is not enough.  Get a tour long before you go.  Our Airbnb was across the street so we had a beautiful view night and day.

title: Real history of colosseum gory & invention
text: 3 hour tour worth every penny, how Colesseum was built, and financed, emperor's, and surrounding historical buildings hidden within other buildings, very interesting tour

title: Looks better from the outside
text:
Really disappointed in the Colosseum. Looks great from the outside, but little remains on the inside and there is no signage to show what it used to look like. Not worth the €16 to get in, although to be fair the ticket gets you into the Forum too.
Save yourself some money and go to Nimes in France instead where you can see a smaller, but mostly intact Roman amphitheatre.

title: Wow wow wow
text: Did I say Wow!  This place is amazing.  The inside is as amazing as the outside. If you can not have time to see the outside, at least go view the outside. Amazing, and Wow!

title: Mind-blowing
text: It's just so amazing to walk around and be able to touch a structure that is almost 2000 years old.  We did skip-the-line tour that had access to the restricted underground area, and included a tour of the Roman Forum.  It gets crowded very early so buy tickets in advance!!!  We heard the public announcement that it was at full capacity at 9:30 AM. Our guide was great, he gave us so much history and background.  You have to visit the Colosseum if you visit Rome.

title: A great place to learn about Rome’s History and just surreal to witness this structure in real life!
text: I went to visit the Colosseum in July with a family member and it was the time of the heatwaves in Europe and we could really feel the difference in temperatures after returning to Italy after visiting Malta for 2 weeks. We went on this skip the line tour of the colosseum with Gray Line Tours and it was honestly a life saver! The heat was so unbearable and if we had to wait in line then we probably would have fainted! Thankfully, Gray Line Rome offers skip the line tours and that was the best way to explore during Peak Season. The structure itself is marvelous and the guide did an amazing job in telling us all the important parts of its history but I feel that visiting in the Summer was a mistake on my part because it was too hot to stand! Nonetheless, it’s a brilliant attraction and a must visit!

title: Breathtaking structure
text:
I always dreamed on visiting the colosseum. The structure is breathtaking. I was wondering how could people 3000 years ago able to build the colosseum without cranes and lifting equipment.
You really need to be there very early to avoid the very long queue.
On the main road you can go up the stairs to get a better view. The only downside is the African people who try to have conversations with you and ask for money. Some will grab your hand and tie bracelet and again ask for money.

title: A tour is recommended
text:
A must see here in Rome. 
By joining a tour,  we were able to go into the restricted area,  and have interesting sites pointed out to us.  My husband and I both think that if we were alone,  we would not have enjoyed the site as much. 

By getting the ticket to the Colosseum,  you will also gain access to the Palatine hills and Roman Forum. 

There are free days every month too, you can check the offical website for the date.  However, the line to go in on free days can be atrocious.

title: Be prepared for queues
text:
Very very busy. 4 to 5 hour wait to get in so we left the queue in the end. Lots of people pestering to by skip the line tickets but other reviews say they are a rip off so we didn’t do it. 

We had a stroll around the outside which was pleasant

title: Colosseum
text: Wow what a awesome place to visit, didn't get a guide just wandered around my self taking pics, cost €16, well worth it

title: A must-see
text: We did an organized tour of the Colosseum in order to avoid the queue.  I think that's the way to go because the guide was quite knowledgable and put what we were seeing into context.  The outside views are interesting but it's certainly worth a trip inside.

title: One of a kind
text: A monument you have to visit when you arrive in Rome. It’s part of the history of the mankind and pretty unique

title: must do in Rome
text: Impressive from the outside and even more impressive from the inside. Buy the tickets online to awoid crowds. Consider yourself taking a guided tour, it gets so much better than doing it on yourself. Remarkalbe sight. Do not hesitate visiting it while in Rome

title: Short break
text:
We walked through the park and wow it was amazing.
We booked our tickets inside didn't use the guides it was double the price it was absolutely amazing a sence of real history don't get stung by the guys dressed as Roman soldiers taking pictures with them is costly and they get angry anything less than 5 euros.
The price includes the gardens also amazing architectural sights takes a good 4/5 hours to get round. Lots of cobbles comfy shoes a must

title: Very busy tourist attraction
text: Stunning, Fantastic and totally amazing, however..... the amount of people there !!! and we visited in November. We booked a tour guide who was brilliant and I do think for your first visit a must, however when we return we will visit the Colosseum on our own to have time to really see all there is to see.

title: You can almost hear the roar of the Crowd
text: I had heard of the colosseum since I was a youngster and had been in Rome before , but had not made the chance to see this huge marvel.  I should have come years ago, if so I could have used this chance to come again. I will not attempt to describe the monument or the feeling. I will advise that you donot save the pennies here. Get a skip the line guided tour. If  you can find something better get it. I promise that you will not be disappointed.

title: A must see attraction ... and hard to miss!
text: Such an historic place to visit. Definitely prebook tickets. The line was really long at 2:30 in the afternoon when we went. We ordered on line through Coop Culture for a specific day and time and walked right in. With our tickets, we were able to pick up videoguides immediately after passing through the turnstile. The videoguide was very comprehensive and we were able to tour at our own pace, stopping and starting the video for views and photos.

title: very interesting place but not worth the money
text:
I was so thrilled to see it for the first time outside
I took a ticket from of websites online of 30 EUR that includes (skip the line), the ground floor and ancient Rome city access
Was surprised that big part of it is under construction
was surprised that we are not allowed to go inside it where the fighters used to go from
surprised that you do not need more than 10 min inside as there is nothing important to stand for, specially with the construction
the audience stairs are almost totally demolished
you can find free water taps inside :)

title: Audio guides waste of money.
text: Wherever I visit I tend to use the sites audio guides. I don't expect them to be very in depth but just to get an idea. Of course I do research before my visit. These were the worst ever. Poor signage to start the 'tour'. The commentary was banal and boring. Don't waste your money.

title: Impressive
text: Even when u re not an old stone fan or don t love history u get under the impressive building and the charm of the neighbourhood. This is a must for all human on this planet

title: Colosseum at Night - outstanding
text: Highly recommended, took in a lot more than just the Colosseum which was amazing, only a couple of tours running at the same time but we went down to the tunnels as well as the Roman Forum, etc.  well worth it would do it again.

title: Vist to the Colosseum
text:
I recently visited the Colosseum and Rome in late October 2019.
It was one of the main sites I wanted to see. It was well worth the 12 euros cost that also gets you in to the forum. I would highly recommend the Colosseum ruin's and the museum that is inside the Colosseum. This is one of the  Roman worlds best sites to see. I would highly recommend the Colosseum as one of your sites to see on your trip to Rome.

title: A must see
text: Got tickets online from the Colosseum official website and even at 9:30 in the morning skipped a very long queue. What to say that hasn't been said already? It's a must see.

title: Colosseum on a Rainy Day
text: It was cold and rainy, the best day to go since it kept the number of visitors down.No need for queue-jumping tours We didn't need to book in advance and we didn't take a guide. You can always read up about it before or after. Still, it was spectacular. It excites the imagination and as you climb higher you get a better appreciation of the scale of the building.

title: Rome trip
text: This was an amazing place. My husband and I were pleasantly surprised by the wonderful displays of artifacts,  videos and history boards on the upper level inside this historic landmark. Very educational and inspiring look back at this ancient society.

title: WOW, magnificent and awe inspiring
text:
This was truly awe inspiring and at times I had goose bumps.

So much history and our tour guide was so knowledgeable.

Get tickets before as there are large queues and ensure you walk around the gladiator arena.  It is amazing.

This is worth every penny you pay and I can keep going back as it is magnificent.

title: Obviously a must see
text: It's an obvious must see whilst in Rome! You won't be able to get in prior to your designated ticket time, but in a way this was good due to how busy the levels became! Biggest flaw is poor signage around the colosseum area PLUS the horrific numbers of ticket touts and individuals trying to sell (+ PESTER) unnecessary things to tourists. Rome had a surprisingly large military presence, so I was disappointed that this level of disturbance was allowed in tourist heavy areas - it often ruined our experience because of the amount of time we spent saying, 'no thank you', 'no were not interested'.

title: Amazing ancient Roman history
text: This is a must see when in Rome. Be sure to take a guided tour so you hear the history of the era and how significant this was at the time.

title: Awesome
text:
Its huge n awe-inspiring...we had booked the tickets online n took the metro from our airbnb place..jst step out of the metro stop n there it is..it ws a hot sunny day n being sunday roads wer free of vehicles..

Pre-booking helps as te other queue is v long..they let u in by time slot and as we had time in out hands v walked to nearby places-colonna n ..saw the splendid ruins as we walked..

Ther is a separate entry gate fr pre-booked ticket holders.. ask the coop culture volunteers fr help.. Security is thorough..jst read up on wats allowed n u shud b gud to go..v had carried water n sm light dry snacks..We had audio-video self tour n te i-pods are collected frm a separate counter-all at te base level wher u enter..aftr which u take te stairs to go up..do chek te instrument (my husband’s had sm issue n he gt it replaced)..

Once in u can marvel at the grandiose structure..te AV guide is quite helpful..do try te gift shop

title: Guided tour
text: First visit to the Collosseum from the inside. The tour guide we hired off the street was atrociously bad -fatal error - we learnt our mistake the hard way and ya only to blame. Notwithstanding same, going through the interior was not any more special and we could have got a good gist from outside itself.

title: Great
text: Being here was definitely awesome - so much history! The bad: they are currently doing a TON of work on it so there are workers & scaffolding EVERYWHERE- makes it difficult to take it all in. By time we found way to get in to the exhibits we only had 20 minutes left of our tour. Was a bit disappointed - so glad I went, has been a dream of mine BUT the things I mentioned made it hard to fully enjoy

title: Amazing experience
text: Absolutely loved our time exploring the colosseum! The LiveItaly tour we did was brilliant - we were taken underneath and saw and learnt about tethered the animals and gladiators waited before their battles. Highly recommend a tour so you can learn about the history, hear the stories and understand what you are looking at.

title: Interesting place to visit
text: Very good idea to go with a guide. Our guide was Helena Beatriz Talledo, She is very nice and very Profesional.

title: Simply a must when in Italy
text: We had a great time. We had purchsed the skip the line tickets and that made the day very stressfree. My 77 year old mother was with us and the elevator made it easy for her to get to the top. Do not be afraid to ask.

title: Amazing Place
text: What a great experience. This is the second time I visited the Colosseum. You must go but make sure to have a guided.

title: Colosseum
text:
Visited the site  really enjoyed it! 

If you go pre book saves lots of time approx 2 hr wait at some times. 

The site has lots of steep steps to climb so make sure wear comfortable shoes. 

I would receive getting a guided tour it would better than the video or radio guides that you can purchase at the booth upon entering. 

It can also get very crowded so be aware if you take small children.

title: A must see underground
text: Seeing the Colosseum from any viewpoint is worth it. Do the Underground Tour & elevate your experience.

title: Go Sooner Than Later
text: My friend and I really didn’t plan to visit The Colosseum, thinking seeing it from the outside would be good enough. Our Air B&B host offered to reserve us a private tour. This was definitely the best way. We had toured the Uffizi and Vatican, Sistine Chapel, St. Peter’s Basilica with small groups and we much preferred this tour because of it being more one-on-one. Super interesting to see it from the inside and to be able to see the exposed lower floors. Be advised...they are actually rebuilding the Colosseum floor where the lower floors will no longer be exposed. Terrible that they are actually adding to an ancient ruin and not just preserving it. We were told the idea is to require a separate ticket to be able to see the lower floors where the slaves, criminals, animals were held. So, if it want to see it before this work is complete, you better go soon!

title: Amazing to see
text:
Amazing to see up close, arrived early approx 10am and managed to get in within 20 minutes for basic entry of 16€.

Some areas closed off for renovation which is understandable. 

Good to see a small museum also within the walls to show off some old findings

title: Colosseum
text: A must see. It is amazing that this was built so long ago. Very easy process to get in. You can buy tickets online as well.

title: Spectcular but too crowded
text: As we expected a spectacular building - an achievement for the Roman empire that corresponds to it's greatness.

title: City break
text: A must when in Rome! I didnt use the guided tour, I went in solo and had downloaded a app which gave all the information I needed. There was loads of people outside trying to get you to book the guided tour saying beat the 1.5 hour waiting time. I walked straight up and straight in so would have ended up paying so much more for nothing really as it's not somewhere you will spend hours at!

title: Colosseum
text:
Too busy for me people ever where massive queue to get in 
Probably better to book online 
Looks great from outside never been inside as refugees to pay 30 odd € for entry and queue for hours

title: Still a work in progress
text: We had fast track tickets which meant we didn’t have to line up. This made the experience so much better. The interior of the structure is still being restored and it is amazing to see the outlines of the rooms that were under the arena. It is a very imposing building.

title: Amazing Archeological Site
text: The day we visited it poured down. We had good umbrellas each which was a must in November as it is very stormy. The Colosseum was breathtaking and eerie. The guide was a must as much more information is given. Pre book to get your guide. We used Grey Line. A smaller group would have been better so less than 25 people is better as you can get separated easily. Palatine Hill was also visited in the site adjacent. This is the site of the first known Roman civilisation. It was like being in a time capsule.  We were also lucky enough to be on the site as it closed and was wet so we got some fabulous people-free photos.  A must see in Rome.

title: lots of history and a must see in Rome. decided to pay extra to cut the line
text: fist we wanted to stay in line and wait for our turn but then we noticed that we might as well pay extra to go worth tour guide and cut the line. was worth it. lots of history and lots of walking. and photo taking. was worth it.

title: A Bit Crowded But Great
text: Had a tour guide, she was awful, however the Colloseum is remarkable. Being part of a guided tour we were hustled through much too quickly, would like to have spent the good part of the day wandering around inside. Overall this is a must see.

title: Seeing was Believing
text: Our guide explained the history including the construction of this wonderfully preserved structure and what happened to it after the Romans fell out of power.  There is nothing like seeing it for real.  Our guide helped create an image of what it would be like to be a gladiator in those times.  I loved that overlays that he brought along to show us what it would have been like in its glory

title: Take a tour with a good guide service
text: It’s an incredible place, with history, power, grace beyond what I ever imagined... from the exterior, to the basement and all the way to the top; this is Roman history on such a grand scale that it boggles the mind. The football stadiums of the 1980’s seem pale in comparison - and this from 2000 centuries earlier.... insane levels of technology, ageless weight and with all the years that the structure was stripped of ifs nicer and richer parts and still it stands!

title: The place that has my heart
text: The best place ever. Whatever you do when you go there it is amazing so just go and see it. I stood on the arena this time which was amazing.

title: Of Course, A Must See
text: This is of course a "must see" to visit the Colosseum when scheduling a trip to Rome.  What I would add is to go ahead and arrange an official tour to skip the lines and also arrange the tour for seeing the "underground" of the Colosseum as well.  We (family of 3) enjoyed the underground perspective and history of how the underground was used to help run the games / performances.  It was literally like a very complex "stage".  Very interesting and enjoyable.  Note that the underground floods easily and will be closed often if raining...

title: Bucket list ticked off, do not miss going here.
text: Anyone not impressed by the sight of the Colosseum needs to check they still have a pulse. From the moment we first saw the Colosseum from the "wedding cake" we were captivated by it. Our three days in Rome and this was the only tour we both had to do. I'd pre booked the trip and we arrived two hours early to take in the ambience of this ever so slightly famous landmark. Having lapped lapped it once and had a drink we found our tour. The young lady Egle who took us round was fantastic. The tour we booked was the Colosseum, Palantine hill and the Forum. From the moment we passed into the Colosseum you cannot be anything but blown away by the sheer scale of the place. 80 individual arches, 4 floors above ground and what would have been four floors below. Over 2000 years of history and the tour just added to the majesty and magnificence of this iconic venue. We were in the Colosseum for just over an hour but saw everything and the guide was brilliant in her narrative about all the horrors that had been exhibited here over the years. We enjoyed the Colosseum so much we went back later that night and again the following day. We are already planning another trip to Rome and the Colosseum.

title: Rome winter 2019
text:
A place full of history. It took us back to our childhood memory, watching lots of movie with Rome history.
Sincere advice to those wanted to visit, please pre book your tickets online and print it out. The crowd is too huge at the reservation desk. 
You can straight enter the colosseum with your printed tickets which safe lots of time and hassle.

title: Pre Booked for the self viewing Guide group.
text:
we used City Wonders. meet at the small hillock about the metro station and then checked in with our guide. lovely lady Serene xxx . she had nice nice wicked sense of humour and explained why Coluseum is so called.   got that fast access then allowed a period of one hour to view at our own pace.   would do the same self guide again... you then do tour own pace.  Took mega amount 
of photos as you do and then onto Group access via the scanning machines onto the Pallentine Hill for more ancient ruins of room with various temples along the way.

title: Magnificent
text: We went on a disability tour and it was easy to get around and a must do in Rome. Yes, it is crowded but November is an excellent time to go because of the cooler weather.

title: Very cool, a lot of history!  Well internally signposted, objective explanatory signs/
text:
Comment in English and Portuguese:

Very nice tour, you can do the whole tour of the colosseum in an hour and a half calmly.  Everything is very well explained, the environment is free, which makes everyone more comfortable.  I recommend 100%, must pass in Rome.

Passeio muito legal, é possível fazer o passeio todo do colosseum em uma hora e meia com calma. Tudo está muito bem explicado, o ambiente é livre, o que deixa todos mais a vontade. Recomendo 100%, passagem obrigatória em Roma.

title: Amazing!
text:
It was amazing to visit the colosseum yesterday. Be warned that there is a lot of construction work going on outside due to a new metro line being built and also inside. 

Paid to go through the gladiator entrance which was worth every penny! Just check where your meeting points are as there are a few. 

Lines to pay tickets weren’t that long however there was a half hour wait. 

Be warned that the steps inside are very steep so wear comfortable shoes.

title: What we do in life,echoes in eternity
text: Amazing place..but there are too many immigrants trying to sell you something or trying to cheat you with stupid words..stay away from them..You need to wait too long if you want to enter the Arena.

title: Takes lots of imagination
text: If you go, do yourself a favor and use a very knowledgeable guide.  Without her, it would have been very difficult to extract a worthwhile tour.

title: You got to go in and see it all!
text:
We enjoyed our walk inside and outside one of the most recognisable places in the world. It has all the facilities you need and of course can get rather busy in the best spots with all the insta wannabes.

We did get full access inside the venue too and I loved the internal museum part with the artefacts.

title: Monster
text: A real monster of an arena, which in November was shielded by a a lot of construction work, it didn’t look the the same on our visit because of the local works but we saw it and we now appreciate the size of the structure.

title: Must see
text: Of course you have to see the Colosseum when in Rome, we opted for the underground tour which takes you underneath the stage to where the gladiators and animals were kept, you also got to see all levels of Colosseum that were closed off. Definitely worth it.

title: An incredibly historic site
text: Anyone traveling to Rome, Italy has to visit this at least once.  I recommend that anyone who does go to book a tour guide to better appreciate the venue and get a historical background.

title: Pre book online
text:
Fab to visit but lots of hard sell traders. ONLY book your ticket online in advance from certified site.
Book an early slot to avoid long queues. 
Lovely at night.

title: Amazing place
text: It's a must see when you're in Rome. Stunning and breathtaking. I really loved it! You can also visit it by night, which is very romantic :)

title: Amazing
text: Would recommend a tour guide and allow plenty of time. Easy to get to if you are based further out using the metro. Was the best place to visit in Rome.

title: Lovely experience
text: A must see place in Rome! We enjoyed our time at the Colosseum. We didn't take a tour but it was fine.

title: Colloseum by night
text: We did a tour with citywonders Rome and booked the Colloseum by night tour which is an after hrs tour.  The two guides we had were exceptional. One on the walk to it from Trajans column and the other from inside the Colloseum. They had a vast knowledge of the history of the Roman Forum and surrounding area right up to the Colloseum. Well worth missing the crowds during the day, the group's are small and we got to visit an area in Colloseum which is not open during public times in the day.

title: wonderfull
text: what else? best city in the world. many times here and i never change in my mind. hope to see you soon

title: GO AT NIGHT
text:
If you prefer large crowds, and the sun, then go during the day. I must say that the night tour is the only way to do this tour (My opinion).  The night tour: the weather is agreeable, the moonlight stadium with the dim lights gives the tour an intense ambiance that allows even the mediocre tour guide a shining star.  (That wasn't our case).
The tour walks the cobblestones streets, with a history lesson that was outstanding, and ends in the colosseum with no one else but your group (maybe one or two more groups).  This was an amazing tour----AT NIGHT

title: Incredible History
text:
There is nothing like actually visiting the Colosseum in person. The sheer size of the place and the atmosphere. We did the tour WithLocals and got a skip the line pass, which is well worth doing. With so many things to see and do it was good to have a local who spoke English and gave us a great insight into the historical aspects. 
The Colosseum is a must do when in Rome.

title: Who am I to quible..
text: ...awesome historical site. You gotta get a priority entrance as we did with a proper tour but we left (to go to the included Palatine Hill and Forum) feeling as though we would have liked a litttle longer to wander around. So, maybe, need to look at different visit deals or come back another day just to see this site

title: Do the Colosseum right
text: The Colosseum is a great site in the middle of many other sites all within walking distance. With lots of folks visiting you want to do everything you can to avoid the lines and get to places that really give you the total experience. Highly advise that you hire a guide and get the opportunity to visit the top "viewing" floor as well as the bottom layer were you can see how things worked under the Colosseum.

title: a must for anyone with a taste for roman history
text: Yes, it is full of people, but it is a remarkable piece of Classical Roman building and surprisingly intact. You really get the sense of what went on here, and it is well signed and explained. A guide is even better. There are plenty of steps to climb, but there is a lift if you can find it so do go up high to get the full perspective.

title: Overrated Colosseum
text: Very crowded, lots of construction, danger of pick pockets outside. It is a must-see, however do not expect to spend too much time and watch your back.

title: Crowded!
text:
Crowded place and looks good from outside. Did not go in due to the crowd!

If you are really interested in going in, do book your tickets online in advance so you can beat the rush!

title: MUST SEE - when in Rome!!
text:
Awesome structure! Great visit - go rain or shine! Lots of history! We rented 1 audio device that tells the history of it, but while it gave some historical info, 1 audio device will work to share between 2 or even 3 people. Everyone doesn’t really “need” one. Also, your phone may give you more info. 

We did not do the tour group, don’t really need to, you’ll get a map. 

(Avoid taxis, if possible)

title: Bucket list tick ☑️
text:
We spent the afternoon here on our last day in Rome. We opted to join the queue and not fall for any of the ticket touts flogging guided tours for €35.
We queued for an hour (12-1pm)& paid €16 each (€6 for video guide -not really necessary). 
Delighted I did the visit - was content to appreciate the grandeur of the historic building rather than focus on listening to a guide. Definitely worth visiting when in Rome. The whole trip from queue to exiting can take about about 3 hours depending on how long you want to wander around inside the Colosseum.

title: A MUST see when in Rome.
text:
In the heart of ancient Rome this amazing architectural wonder is a must see. We had a private guide and that was well worth the cost--no lines, great parking and he did the narration, including a very descriptive explanation of just what this place was like originally. You can see where the Emperor sat, how live animals were lifted from below for the "shows" and the gate the gladiators used to enter. What a magnificent site.
It is located near several other major sights so you  can walk to the major Arch, Palatine Hill, and the Forum

title: Vacation
text: We visited the Colosseum and walked around the exterior but Google had a different closing time than actual, therefore, we were not able to do a tour.

title: Unbelievable history
text: A fantastic location to visit and learn so much about those that have come before us. Well worth a visit, even for those that have only the smallest interest in history - there is something to interest everyone.

title: History
text: Amazing... Absolutely amazing.. No Rome without Colosseum..a pure history... and shivering by simply imagining the roar Al around..

title: Long Lines
text:
So the Colosseum, yes it is mandatory but is it worth it? I don't think so.
The arena at Pozoullli is better.
The lines were long and the touts were plentiful. The ticketing only had two ticket sellers in the windows which is appalling for the length of the line. They are deliberately trying to push tourists into using the tour companies.
If you go there after about 2:30 the lines are short and you can do this joint in about 2 hours.
We were certainly under impressed, the exterior would have been good enough to be honest.

title: AMAZING!
text: If you're under 26, I would highly recommend not booking this in advance and book the tickets while you're there. As a travel agent, I was looking to book this in advance and I'm glad I didn't. We got to the ticket office, they asked me and my partner our age and our nationality. We told them we were British and gave our I.D and they told us it would be 2 Euros to get in! I had been looking at it online before hand and was expecting it to be £20+ but was pleasently suprised. We also got in straight away and it was amazing would definately recommend.

title: Colosseum
text:
Get your advanced tickets by getting the Roma Pass and Omnia Card. Go to the booth that is three quarters up the via Labicana on the right, set back a bit so easy to miss!

Queues wern't too bad.

title: Only if you've pre booked
text: Hopefully we had booked on line and we skiped the enormous line! The Palatine hill was very beautiful and worth visiting. The Colosseum was ok but under any circumstances I would wait for hours in the line. Definitely buy your tickets online!

title: Italyrometours
text: This was a wonderful part of our tour with Italyrometours and as usual Max made the Colosseum come to life with his knowledge and presentation. Outstanding tour!

title: Glorious
text: Amazing feeling and experience on a beautiful sunny day, definitely recommend a skip the lines tour as the ques can be really long. Had an excellent guide with us and the overall experience was excellent.

title: Astounding
text: It's incredible to actually visit the colosseum in the flesh- pictures don't compare to the wonder of this fantastic structure. We pre-booked so barely had to queue to get in. My only gripe is that you don't actually get to walk the entire circumference of the colosseum but what you do see is fantastic. There are a few signposts inside but we find the audio tour to be useful and informative.

title: Amazing building in Rome
text: Nice place to visit. Get there early to avoid lines and get photos with less people around. No need to pre-book a ticket. We found we got in quicker than people who had brought tickets already.

title: Awesome
text: Seen this magnificent construction on t.v. many times but the sheer size of it took my breath away. The queues to get in were so long that we did not have enough time to go in, but walking round it we saw and learned so much.

title: Best tour with City Tours thru Viator. Funniest tour guide ever.
text: The experience at the Colleseum and Roman Forum will last a life time. Novemeber is a great time to travel. The crowds are so much less than peak season.

title: magnificent place
text: awestruck all around this place. it is magnificent and you can wonder at its size and atmosphere, and wonder how it was built. Incredible

title: Fantastic Roman Experience
text: A must visit for anyone visiting Rome. Thankfully we were part of a guided tour and did not have to endure the very long queues of individuals, so we had priority entrance. Unbelievable to see such a historical building. A lot to see with a nice shop on the upstairs floor. Information boards at every entrance. You could sense the gladiators entering. The toilets however were something from ancient times!

title: Unbelievable
text: I took about a 100 pictures of this one, inside and outside. Just imagine what the slaves have been through to get te stones up. A true landmark of human history

title: Centre of history
text: This is what you come to Rome for! The history. You can feel the grandeur as you walk around this great piece of architecture and image the things it must have seen.

title: Colosseum A Must
text: This was an amazing visit of the most amazing sites in the world. If you're visiting Rome don't miss touring the Colesseum!

title: terrific!
text: colosseum is something that can't be missed when in rome.. i recommend a guided tour   because you can't really understand what you're looking at if you don't have someone  to give you explanations with you. with a guide you can as well visit colosseum areas normally close to the public.. my friends and i did the tour with Fun Tour and it was perfect!the office is very close and the staff very friedly.

title: Must Visit
text: We went to this place via Hip on Hip off bus service and visited the place in July 2019. If visiting in summer please do carry sun glasses and hat

title: Great at day and Night
text: You need to do a tour so make sure you know what you want to see. For instance not all tours go underground (where the gladiators and tigers were). Some might be just colosseum or include other areas such as the forum.   Be sure or you may be disappointed.

title: A Must See
text: I’m a serious amateur photographer and I really wanted an evening shoot here.  I was lucky and the only night I had was a rainy night and due to construction I got some great reflective shots here.    I’ve also been here before and did the inside tour. Definitely worth seeing and experiencing.

title: When in Rome...
text:
...Go back home.    Nah kidding the rain did spoil the experience but we got flogged an unbrella and then able to stay a bit longer.  Its not as big as i thought nor glamourous as they make it appear on the TV but its something you have to see atleast once when in Rome.  

I suppose it would be better if you go inside but we gave that a miss due to the weather.

You know when you watch those Youtube videos and after every 5 mins theres an advert, well its a bit like that, you check out the colloseum for a few mins then get approached by bengali unbrella babu, then walk on and check out more of the colloseum then get approached by bengali ponch babu.

theyre not aggressive, or too pushy but can get annoying

title: Must to see
text: The most interesting place in Rome for me. It is huge and I was impressed. I higly recommand to pay for a guide. It will definitly worth it.

title: When in Rome
text: Being inside the Colloseum brings chills you cannot explain. I felt like I was an observant and at the same time I am one with the gladiators and residents inside the big structure.

title: Our Recent Visit
text: The Coliseum is a must when visiting Rome. The architecture and sheer size of this arena is awe-inspiring. You can easily imagine what happened at this wonderful historical site. I strongly recommend having a tour guide and TripAdvisor has many excellent options to choose from. The tour is informative and helps you to appreciate what you are looking at.

title: Fantastic self guided audio tour!
text: Lots of tour companies selling tours of here, but we opted for much cheaper option of self guided audio tour. Information given was fantastic. Together with lots of information boards around, this was well worth the money saving. I wanted to visit this attraction for years and it was well worth it. Ticket price also includes Palentine and Forum. Would highly recommend and don’t waste your money on tour guides!

title: Wow
text: I visited during the day and during the night too. We did the night tour which was worth every dime of our money. You can take lots of pictures from all around the building. Rainy season is in November so bring your umbrellas and ponchos.

title: Amazing history
text: The Roman Colosseum is an amazing structure. It must have been an intimidating site for visitors upon seeing it for the first time during the Roman Empire. The lines to get in are long but they move fast. The views from inside are amazing and you can just imagine the thousands of gladiators who lived their life in the colosseum. A visit to Rome would not be ccomplete without seeing the colosseum.

title: Mighty
text: The Colosseum is revolting and impressive at once. Humanity has made some progress one would like to think. But perhaps not...

title: Check opening times before going
text: The Colosseum closed 3:30 pm, but as I have seen it before, it is impressive and worth entering.  Tickets are required.

title: Beautiful Site
text: This site is beautiful. It is currently being restored but you can still visit. It's a very popular attraction and can get very busy. Paying with a credit card will get you access inside faster than with cash because there are 2 separate lines and credit card payment gets you into the reservation line. The museum is great and nestled right inside the Colosseum. Be prepared to walk up and down some steep steps though.

title: Always say yes to Colosseum
text: Our third  and fourth time visiting. It was a pleasure to visit again. One visit by night one by day. Both different and special on their own ways. Enjoy

title: Impressive
text: Be prepared to queue! It is worth it though as this is one impressive building. We did it without a tour guide and enjoyed being able to take everything in, in our own time. Definitely a must see when in Rome.

title: 2000 years of history
text: Unreal to see in the flesh and to wonder around def worth seeing   What must  it have been like 2000 years ago

title: Huge.....!
text:
You can see the Coliseum by driving by, walking by or paying for a ticket to go inside.
In any event it is not to be missed on a trip to Rome.
I do believe it’s free to go in on the first Sunday of the month! 
There are other attractions which are free also on this day.

title: Feel the History
text: I am not sure why this place needs a review. Anybody visiting Rome must go there and at least see it from outside.

title: Amazing Colosseum
text: Bought tickets ahead on official website has scheduled time for you to enter. Hard to believe this structure has been here so long.  And excavation continues today.  This is tied with St Peters as my number one sites in Rome.  Give yourself at least two hours to tour even more time if you have it.  Ticket also included entrance to the a Forum a block or so away.  Climbing stairs inside could be a challenge but take your time it’s worth the climb to see from above in the inside

title: Do not miss this!
text: OK, so you know everything about the Colosseum, that’s great. It’s still on the can’t-miss list as it is just so simply impressive. They built this thing 2,000 years ago. To be inside and soak in the atmosphere of what this place was like back then is pretty cool.... we saw it in November and th Elaine’s were not bad - cannot imagine the summer lines...

title: Colosseum by night
text: Loved this tour. Very private with no crowds or line up. So picturesque at night. Our Group was maybe 25 people. We passed another group early in the tour and another group towards the end. Informative and insightful. Worth the bit extra $.

title: Amazing History
text: Went to the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum...so much history packed into one day! Loved it...our guide was extremely knowledgeable, and November was a little less crowded

title: Magnificent
text: Being in Rome and not visiting Colosseum would be a pity. It is magnificent and astonishing. Esspecilay if you think that it was built only in a few years without nowadays equipment and it is still standing. It is fascinating how was everything organized-a few thousand people can enter or exit the Colosseum in a few minutes. I feel sorry that it was partially demolished during the centuries and building bricks were used for other houses.

title: Not to be missed!
text: Obviously the Colosseum is one of the major attractions in Rome and for good reason. It has been very well preserved and the detail remaining is incredible. In order to get the most out of your visit, I suggest hiring a tour guide - ahead of time, through a reputable agency. I recommend Cristina Giannicchi (crisgiannicchi@gmail.com) who explained way more than I would ever have got from a guide book or recorded tour!

title: Totally worth it
text: Ok so we thought we would do the tour independently. When we arrived the most annoying thing was the people trying to flog tours and the lucky lucky men horrendous they don’t leave you alone . We paid €16 for the colosseum & Palatine hill both stunning. Audio is €6 if you want it. Surprisingly we probably waited 20 minutes to pay. The queues to collect tickets were longer !

title: A must see
text: Of course, one must see this site. Hearing how this was built in AD 79-84 with the technology they had then and what it became, seating at least 55,000!, and what still stands today is a testimony to ancient Rome's skills. I would suggest a guided package tour that includes Palentine Hill and the Roman Forum as well

title: Wonderful
text: The Gladiator Tour was well worth the price. A very thorough description of the construction of the colosseum was given as well as lots of interesting detail about the events held there. This was followed by an enjoyable tour of Palatine Hill with great explanations of the ruins viewed. It is a 4 hour tour which requires a fair amount of climbing up and down steep hills and steps so bear this in mind when booking.

title: Put on to do list
text: This is also one of the beautiful and historical places to visit. You can enter into the Collesseum also by buying tickets there instead of internet.

title: Truly Breathtaking!
text: My visit to the Coliseum was breathtaking. I was in awe to be see this amazing piece of history up close and tour the inside. This is a must see if you visit Rome. I highly recommend booking a tour in advance so that you can skip the long lines that are waiting to enter.

title: Great tour
text: We did the guided tour of the Colosseum, Our English guide did a great job of telling us all about the history and interesting things about the place.  We would recommend the guided tour.  They had us meet at a white tent and only let us in a little before it started so you do not have to get there too early.  Enjoy.

title: Essential when in Rome
text:
This is a great thing to do when in Rome that is quite cheap. I suggest getting tickets which included entry to the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum to save money as these are also must do activities.
You should go early if you feel like it will be busy, such as on a weekend, to avoid any queues. Also I would suggest getting an audio tour so you aren't just looking at some organised stones. Having an audio tour really lets you appreciate and understand what you are looking at and informs you on some amazing things you would otherwise miss out on.
Other than that it is a great experience and do not regret going!

title: Amazing!
text: The experience in the coliseum and roman forum was absolutely amazing! You cannot imagine there was a whole city around there. It’s really interesting and the tour guides delight you with all that information and pictures from the past.

title: Great monument, long queues
text:
There are always queues and there are a lot of people who try to give you "skip the line" tickets.  
Also, there are a lot of annoying vendors who try to make you buy selfie sticks or other stuffs that you don't need and they are being agressive at some point.. So annoying.

title: Live up to the hype
text: Not many places as popular as this really live up to the hype, but the Colosseum is awesome. We went with a small tour group and got access to the stage, it really puts the size of the place into perspective.

title: Small group tour with Barbara Mantovani, BIFAIV guided tours.
text: This was a truly magical experience and our tour guide Barbara was brilliant.  She told us of the history of this significant piece of history.  Highly recommended! !!

title: Colosseum Tour
text: I loved the Colosseum Tour and the Roman Forum. I did a skip the line tour. The tour guide was an archeologist who was working on the dig site at the Forum. She was so passionate about the Colosseum and the Forum. She gave us so much history and information about all the things we saw. I highly recommend the skip the line tours anywhere in Europe. It is great to be able to walk in to all these places and not have to stand in a long line with the masses.

title: It's the colosseo
text: Why am I even reviewing the colosseum 😂? It is a must when you visit Rome you haven't been to Rome if you haven't been to the colosseum and that's it.

title: Roman Icon
text:
Some tips - Best to use a tour if possible. Access is easier and group tours have separate entry. The guide gives details and makes it a story. We also tried the night show but it was booked. Be aware of pick pockets - someone in the group had an incident. Have heard many cases, best to be over prepared and avoid being the victim. Visited in November, and guide said August/Summer heat is intense. 

Colosseum is impressive.

title: Very steep stairs
text: I guess the Romans liked enormous rise on the stairs even though it seems going by Roma armour on museums' displays they weren't too tall. Otherwise a lot of construction going on. It gets a 4 just because it is after all the Colosseum and I guess some of ancestors died there. Still is an impressive structure.

title: Classic good
text: Not that many people, which really is great. It’s 18€, it includes a museum inside, really informative.

title: Use a tour guide !
text: Using a tour guide will help you skip the lines & off some insite into the history of the building - ours was very knowledgeable - well worth it ! x

title: Must visit
text: You can’t come to Rome and not visit the colosseum, I would recommend that if you are planning to go I would book on line well in advance especially if you want to see all of it as it gets booked up quite quickly and you do t want to be  disappointed on the day. I would defiantly go on a guided tour or at least hire out one of their headsets as it explains all the history of the place and gives you a better understanding of how it used to be. Depending on what time of year you go, it gets very hot in the summer but there are water fountains around the venue where you can refill your bottle. A must see whilst in Rome and was value for money, would defiantly recommend coming back in the evening to see it lit up outside. Make sure you have comfortable shoes as lots of walking on uneven ground. Also, a selfie decent stick is a must it gives you the chance to take some great pics of your time there.

title: Incredible
text:
If you love your history then this is a must!
It was the main reason for my trip to Rome and I wasn’t disappointed- even in the rain it blew me away!
It was better than I could have imagined and pictures just don’t do it justice!
We were super lucky in that in the day we went it was free entry.
I tried booking online the day before but everything had sold out. There’s a tourist office just minutes away where I suggest you book your tickets it’s much cheaper.
 Being November there were none of the ridiculous queues (2-3 hours) we had been warned about- although I’m sure that’s the case in the Summer.
Lots of touts trying to sell yours, umbrellas and ponchos - even if you already have them so that got tiresome pretty quickly but they’re everywhere unfortunately. If you don’t engage with them you should be fine. They certainly didn’t detract from my enjoyment of such a breathtakingly spectacular piece of ancient history!

title: Really it is a nive place.
text: If you are in Rome then you must visit this place because otherwise, your trip will be missed something from the most famous landmarking of Rome.

title: Majestic
text: We bought online tickets and English guided tour through the official website coopculture.it. It was cheaper than what most third party operators are selling for. The place is not that big and can be easily covered within an hour. There is a small lift for strollers or older visitors who may not be able to climb the steep steps. Impressive historical which must not be missed.

title: Excellent!
text: Surprisingly baby stroller friendly (there were elevators). We did a skip the line tour and it was very worth it. An epic monument and testament to the scale of Roman civilization and architecture.

title: Vacation
text: Our guide was exceptional.  She made it seem like you were the only one in the group.  Extremely knowledgeable.

title: Its a must!
text:
Of course its beautiful, of course its a must!
It was too much crowded with thousands of people around and the weather was so much hot! We didn't even think of entering the monument. Maby another time. But it is really breathtaking!

title: Amazing
text: Wow wow wow amazing what an experience well worth the money  put on your walking shoes bring a drink and a picnic and enjoy

title: Viator tour in Rome by Tour in the city
text: Our tour was going to start at 9.30am we reached the office at at 9.37tour office was just opposite to Colosseumoffice person could have done something to direct us to reach to guide but he never helped we lost our tour and without refund please be careful

title: Amazing
text: Amazing architecture with amazing history behind it. Definitely worth a visit and definitely worth paying extra for skip the line tickets

title: Main attraction
text: You can’t miss this place during your visit to Rome , 16€ entrance ticket , just go and enjoy the history of this place

title: There is only one word...
text: Awesome... definitely worth a visit if not 2, but going round with a guide is a must especially if you get one that is educated and speaks your language well

title: Ticket price was disappointing
text: The only thing I was very disappointed about was that the ticket price for people older than 25 was 16€ while the reduced for 18-25 was 2€!!! I just has my 26th birthday a week ago so i has to pay 8 times the price that my brother paid ( he is 24 almost). It has no point for such a difference!!!!!

title: Ancient and beautiful, but Gladiator's Entrance is pointless.
text: We booked a tour to go in the Gladiator's Entrance due to a lack of availability of other tickets and a lack of a willingness to wait in line. It was merely a different (and quicker) entrance out onto a small stage over brickworks that were all under construction. This entrance was kinda pointless (except for speed, with nothing special really. We then went on a quick tour around, barely getting to walk around because if we left the tour we'd miss the Forum. What was saw was cool and amazing but under construction and very crowded.

title: Great
text: Worth going to see. Unfortunately couldn't get into the arena because it apparently was fully booked till mid December

title: Amazing Place
text: We could have bought the skip the line tickets with a guide online but decided to just go on our own. It cost us €16 each, which included the Forum & the Palatine.  We could have got an audio guide but didn’t bother. We arrived just before 0830hrs when it opens to buy the tickets which were inside the Colosseum. The queue line is quite difficult to figure out with no clear indication of where to go before reaching the entrance. There are some ticket booths across from the Colosseum but they only seemed to sell group tickets & we were directed to the massive queue which included people who had pre-booked. We found the right place eventually but decided to go back early on another day as we didn’t want to queue for 45 minutes then not have enough time to see everything. You also have to pay extra for the arena part but we didn’t bother.

title: Very good
text: Loved our tour there definitely need an audio to walk round with although mine was to quit and could not here right

title: History outdoes crowds

title: Amazing
text: Would definetley recommend using a guide, we used Rolling Rome Gold Cart tours and was fantiastic.  It was great skipping the long lines.  This is our second time in Rome and what a difference it made, having a guide.   The first time, many years ago, we were completely clueless what we were looking at.   This time, it was so much more meaningful to understand the history  and the what you were actually looking at.

title: Great to visit
text: Great to visit. Big part of history. When visit on 30th November no need to book an advance ticket as waiting was less than 30 min (arrive 11AM at the gate). Good that there is a ticked for a Colosseum / Forum Romano / Palantine Hill together.

title: Amazing!
text: Wow! It was hard to believe seeing the Colosseum with my own eyes. It lived up to the hype. We got a Roma Pass that allowed us to skip the lines. And the lines were long. We also purchased an audio tour for about 6 Euros. It’s a must see while in Rome!

We waited no more than 30 minutes (in late November).Summer will be very different and Skip the line tickets may well be worth it.Well worth the visit but check the closing times as they started closing at around 3.30

title: Still impressive scale even by today’s standards
text:
The colosseum is a must-see monument if you are visiting Rome. It’s scale is impressive even by today’s standards. 

From there don’t forget to visit the nearby Imperial Forum

title: Take the official tour!
text: We booked the English tour, and were glad.  You can visit the Colosseum un-guided, but you will miss finding out about many historical facts and locations that can easily be overlooked on our own.  Plus, you'll skip the line, which can get very lengthy.

title: You will never believe the impact of your first entrence into the Colosseum
text: Thanks to our great tour guide Christina Baruffi; we were able to understand the workings and depth of history of this magnificent structure. The amazing work of the Romans to the extinsive recontruction by the past and current Italian governments should be a template for the United States, honor our past not just doze it and rebuild

title: History
text: The Colosseum is incredible there are vast ruins to explore and every where you turn there are incredible pillars busts or other pieces of history. You definitely need to wear comfy shoes as everywhere is cobbled so hard on your feet. There are a lot of people trying to sell you things around the place but if you are firm and say no thank you they don’t tend to hassle you. Tours are available but we were quite happy to explore with a good guide book.

title: Iconic
text: Not much to add as this place speaks for itself, but a major league tip is to go to the Forum first - your ticket covers the forum, colosseum and palatine hill, and the queue is very much shorter ( actually non existent the day we went). Then you can skip the queue at the Colosseum and go straight to the security check, saving yourself hours of waiting in line!

title: ICONIC
text: This is a don't-miss caliber landmark.  I have been here numerous times and I discover something new each time.

title: Viator Tour at Night
text: We toured the Colosseum at night, including the underground tunnels and the Forum with a tour group booked on Viator.  The two tour guides were incredibly knowledgeable with their historic facts!  The night tour and the yellowish lighting set the mood to view the Colosseum from the perspective of Gladiators, wealthy Romans and ordinary citizens.  We much preferred the night tour than going during the day.  I highly recommend this tour!

title: Small group Tour with LIVITALY - excellent!
text: Did a small group tour of Colosseum 8:30am on a Saturday (6people max) with Livitaly tour guide Dario. Saw the dungeon/underground as well as the forum and palestein hill. It Was absolutely fantastic! I cannot say enough great things about livitaly tours. Great information but not overwhelming-answered all the questions very knowledgeably. Not much more expensive or same price as big group tours and definitely got a personalized experience. You cannot go wrong with any of the livitaly tours (we did Vatican as well-also excellent).

title: History under reconstruction
text: It is definitely worth visiting once in a lifetime, but the place seems to be under reconstruction and I could not feel the magic of the ancient walls. I believe a solution for this would be to temporary close it and open it when it’s ready. Also, there are not enough signs or they are not very clear to tell you which queue or path you should follow.

Unfortunately,we had arranged a free walking tour around Colosseo,but the tour guide never appeared.There was a big line to enter ,so we did a tour by ourselves  around the archeological area .Next time we will go inside by tour guide 
Don’t miss it

text: Great tour, very knowgeable tour guide.  Meeting place is for the tour is not right next to the Colosseum, we had first difficulty finding where the tour starts and get the earphones.  Tour is good and well worth the money.  The tour with Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill took approx. 4 hours.  Lots of walking and sometimes pretty hilly locations.
