title: Ruins
text: The whole place is in ruins. It is much prettier outside than inside. So don't bother to go in. If there would be less people, the place could be better.

title: Coliseum, Forum and Palatine Hill, oh my!
text: As part of our goal to visit all Seven Modern Wonders of the World, my husband and I recently found ourselves on a Head Out tour of the Coliseum with Guide Renata. She was extremely knowledgeable and able to convey the significance of the sites in context of the social and political environment at the time of Julius Caesar and the subsequent Roman emperors. She is an excellent storyteller and helped us envision what it was like to live in Rome, regardless of one’s social class, and attend an event at the Coliseum at the end of the first century. Look no farther for a top notch guide for your visit to the trifecta of Ancient Rome!

title: Stunning Place to Visit
text:
We went here on our second day in Rome. We bought tickets from the Colosseum's website - coopculture (dot) it -- You can select English in the upper right, though the translations aren't great. I recommend reserving your tickets in advance through them as it's cheaper than most other tour sites and they offer their own tours, as well as a simple audio tour you can do where they give you a headset. We did a night tour and had to book a few weeks in advance to find an opening, so check early!

The night tour was beautiful! So much fun to see it at night. The normal ticket booth is closed and the one in the building is restricted, so you have to go to the group ticket entrance and show someone your receipt, and they'll let you go inside to grab your official ticket, and then you have to go back out to wait by the door. There wasn't a good explanation for this on the website so I (and everyone who I saw do it) were confused about where to go and what to do, but it was pretty simple once we asked someone working there. Just look for the group entrance and there's usually someone in blue answering questions and letting people in.

The biggest trouble we had were the scammers/street hustlers who hang out here. You have to watch out for them (and your wallet). The gladiators will demand money for pictures if you let them take them with you, so keep moving unless you want to pay up. Others will try to come up and tie things on your wrist as a "gift" and then demand a tip. All sorts of scams - birdseed in your hand so you can take pictures with pigeons and then demand money. Compliment your shoes to get you talking and then try to steal your wallet or beg for money. Basically, unless you see something telling you they work there, the safest bet is to keep on moving and ask them to leave you be as usually anyone approaching you there is going to want money one way or another, and if you're buying souvenirs, it's cheaper elsewhere. The staff was very friendly, though, and we heard them speak at least 5 languages, so that was nice. 

I definitely recommend the night tour if you can get it, and the regular tour (of course) as that's just as much fun. Totally worth a visit to see this place as it's amazing to stand next to. The night tour's biggest benefit was there were so few people, I could get some amazing pictures. Just test out your nighttime settings on your camera or phone first so you know the right ones to do for the best look.

title: Free sunday
text:
How can you come to rome and not see the Colosseum?
1st Sunday in the month free (basic) entry, was more than adequate. Yes it would have been nice to have seen this monument from on high but would we have appreciated it any more? Unlikely!
More than happy to be able to say "We've been there!"

title: All it would seem
text: The Colosseum in its total grand jury was everything I thought it would be. I almost felt like I could feel this crowd screaming as a Gladiators competed. Elia realized that I was surrounded by about twenty thousand people and maybe that's the crowd I heard. Yeah, a large crowd but it's well worth the wait.

title: Must-see, but some advice...
text:
Of course you'll want to visit if you're in Rome, but here are a few things to keep in mind.

Buy your ticket in advance.  If you don't, you can try getting here at 8:00 a.m. (it opens at 8:30) and sometimes the line hasn't built up yet.

Be aware that like many other tourist sites in Rome you WILL be solicited numerous times by hawkers, peddlers and scammers who surround the site.  Some sell trinkets, some claim to offer admission tickets at a discount, some claim to be tour guides.  Ignore them all -- no eye contact and no response.  Just keep walking.

Rant: I can't understand why the city tolerates these scammers harassing people at their most popular sites.  Everywhere you go -- Trevi Fountain, Vatican Museum, Colosseum, Spanish Steps, etc. is rife with them and you feel like you're running a gauntlet to get to your destination.

title: pay for skip the line
text: You are going to go to it; everyone does. Get a skip the line ticket. Security is long enough without having to wait even longer.

title: Simplistic & Breathtaking
text: I loved this tour. My friends and I chose a skip-the-line, self-guided tour, and it was a piece of cake! I recommend going early in the morning because it seemed to be the least crowded time of day. We were able to take our time and really soak in this historically beautiful site. However, if you are looking for an in-depth tour with plenty of information regarding the colosseum, I would recommend choosing a tour with a guide. Overall, I am satisfied with the experience that my friends and I had.

text: We booked our visit ahead of time which included "skip the line access". What this means is that you wait in a different much shorter line than general admission.  It was worth the few extra euros.  The site itself is fascinating.  I don't think a visit to Rome is complete without a Colosseum visit.

title: Stunning
text: This is the identity of Rome. Almost all pictures of Rome show the Colosseum the half crescent which still stands from about 78AD. Among the oldest monuments it was a grand arena where over 50,000 people could assemble for the games and see beast vs beast, man vs beast and gladiators fight. It had a VIP enclosure with sails on top which is not there now but the local travertine blocks of stone have held well.the best photos are from the outside but its inner cellars arena and corridors have their own tale to tell

title: Amazing!

title: Skip the line or get there early... The choice is yours.
text:
I was in Rome for a short weekend with my wife, and had the option to book skip the line tickets online, just as I had done for the Vatican on the Saturday. I couldn't get the times I wanted due to flight times, but when I arrived at the site on Friday, tickets were suddenly available at a premium price - beware! The other option was a 2 hour queue, or so I was told. Left it for Friday and was back in the area Saturday afternoon... Same again.
Decided to get up sharp and arrive well before opening time on the Sunday. I was not disappointed, arriving half hour before the opening time at 08.30. The rain that has beset most of Europe made a return and drove most of the tourists away, but out came all the dodgy umbrella and poncho sellers.
More importantly, I was first in the queue with my wife and was soon on my way inside. Plenty of space to look around Levels One and Two. There are options to go 'underground' and up to Level Three, but with the long journey back home looming, we didn't bother.
So, this is an iconic site worth visiting - the ticket includes the Roman Forum, which is also well worth a look (we didn't because we would have missed our flight home). I can only assume it gets very busy most of the year as it was on Friday and Saturday, so if you don't mind planning ahead, it could be worth getting the skip the line tickets. Otherwise, be prepared to queue. Once inside, the site is well preserved, but then you will know that if you've got this far. Probably best without children, especially any with additional needs such as ours.

title: Impressive

I do recommend the audio guide as it is detailed and comprehensive and there is a lot to learn. title: A Must See
text:
We booked our self guided tour of the Colosseum online through Viator for early in the morning. You need to book online now if you aren’t with a tour group...don’t try to just turn up! I also recommend booking for as early as you can imagine, as the site gets very busy and crowded very quickly!
Our online booking didn’t include the audio headset but we just paid and picked those up on the day for about 5 euro. 
IMO a self guided tour with an audio headset is not only cheaper, but is much less restrictive on your time and agenda as you can spend as much or as little time in each location around the site as you like. 
Colosseum is a must do, and our tour ticket included the Roman Palantine and Forum as well. Very good value, and I highly recommend!

title: Icon of History
text:
Magnificent - but you really need a great guide or have read extensively on the subject to really get the most from your visit. A skip the line ticket is an absolute must as is a firm but polite demeanor to get through all the trinket / guide sellers out the front and on the main roads leading in.

Once inside it is a little sureal and a good opportunity to seperate the facts from the fiction regarding what went on here and who the gladiators actually were and were not.It is a testament to the Romans of the time that this was built and remains standing - it is a magnifent structure and naturally a must see when in Rome.

There is often other exhibits going on inside that can be really interesting - take the time to seek them out and learn a little more.

title: An icon that is a must in Rome
text: What to say about a monument that is one of the most visited in the world????.....Buy a skip line ticket online if you want go inside.

title: Spectacular but very little information provided
text:
We pre-bought Omnia passes which offered "Skip the Queue"  for booking entry times. On arrival we found there were no arrangements for Omnia Pass holders and huge queues. Eventually we were directed to agate with a shorter queue even so it took over an hour to reserve an entry time. Fortunately it was off season and we got an early time slot. 
At the entrance there were large crowds, many waiting for their time slot and again it a was unclear which queue to join.
The imposing structure is well worth seeing but inside there is very little explanation of the features and history.

title: Magnificent
text: The Colosseum is of course magnificent. If you going during busy season don’t forget to get your reservation but for our visit in December we could have skipped a reservation and been just fine.

title: Full of history
text:
A grandiose place full of history ... I do not regret the hour and a half of waiting to enter.
On the other hand, the audioguide is rather succinct and leaves us on our hunger.

title: Historical Site worth visiting
text:
Great historical site to visit to learn about Roman history and history of mankind in the early ages as a whole. 
Like any other great attractions, security is tight and entering a highly sought after attraction would have endless queues and throng of visitors. Travel light to ease past security and buy advanced tickets that provide skip-the-queue option to save on queuing time. Expect lots of steps and best to allocate at least 5 hours to truly experience the history and learn about the rich heritage. 
Ticket also includes 2 other sites so you can expect to spend up to 2 days to immerse in a great historical experience.

title: Amazing Sight
text: We toured this place by ourselves and it was awesome. Its an amazing place to visit and see all the history and artifacts.

title: Breathtaking
text: An amazing experience!! Booked the VIP tour where we started off at the bottom, saw the dungeons, gladiator gates, arena floor and top floor, then the Roman Forums. Was so good, so much to see. Unforgettable experience

title: Great but .....
text: We bought an official tour, not just a skip the line but with a tour guide plus paid extra for a tour of the basement, which whilst the guides knowledge was good, I felt that part was not worth the money, you stand within a very small base beneath the colosseum but you do not get to view very much at all, you are stationary in just a few feet whilst the guide tells the stories which were interesting.   Loved the knowledge and enthusiasm of the guides.

title: Beautiful
text:
We visited the Colosseum as part of our trip to Rome. It's a beautiful grand building.  We did not do any of the tours as there is so much to see. 

There is plenty in offer outside so if you don't want to be bothered best to grab a leaflet and say you have one booked.

title: Disappointing
text: I have to start by admitting that myself and my friends did not have a guided tour and if we had perhaps my review may be higher. However as beautiful as the Colosseum is I found it rather underwhelming. A good point is that if you prove you are from an EU country entry is just €2. However apart from walking around and using the opportunity for photo’s there isn’t much else you can do.

title: Stay outside guys. It's not worth the price nor the weight to get in.
text:
I know it's difficult to hear that the Colosseum isn't great but listen to me! We've all ruined it.

From the outside, the building looks spectacular, leave it at that and move on.

The scaffolding and new arena floor completely ruin any stunning views you'd hope to find inside so stay out.

We spent longer getting through security than we did walking around the amphitheatre and for good reason, the view from all angles is exactly the same; charming ancient rubble covered over with rickety scaffolding and lazy temporary staging.

I don't recommend. It's a real let-down.

title: Amazing!
text:
This is an amazing place for anyone! 

My husband and I bought a general admission ticket (without a guide or tour) and downloaded an audioguide on our phones for free. It was very enjoyable, and we didn't have to wait around on a large group! We chose the Rick Steves audioguides, but there are plenty available on the Apple iTunes store and others, so look around! 

Also, your ticket is timed and they will not allow you entrance to the line until 10 minutes before your admission time! 

If you book online, keep your ticket in a safe place afterward as it is the same ticket to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill!

title: Extraordinary!
text: a monument of great value! very helpful staff despite the crowd. we run into an excibition on the top floor which was really interesting

title: We love the Colosseum
text: I purchased the adult ticket online and printed at home. After passing through the security line, I collected my daughter’s ticket (below 18, free) at the ticket office. The Colosseum, a massive historical structure, is impressive on the inside and outside, despite some ongoing restoration works inside. It’s advisable to either take a tour or do your own research to truly appreciate the history behind the Colosseum. We spent about two hours there. The night view was amazing.

title: Impressive
text:
We visited the place the first sunday of the month and it's usually free entrance on this day, that is why, million of visitors are comming.
We "brought" the ticket on the Roman Forum, and we were lucky because there was not queque line so we entered very fast, also because the ticket was for all the sites (Palatine Hill, Roman Forum and Colosseum), we didn't have to wait to enter to the Colosseum, and as I said, there were million persons on the line...
This is a must to do in Rome, Impressive, we toured by ourselves and reached every corner of the compound...
You breathe History, you can imagine how was in that time (with the help of Gladiator movie I have to confess...) it is so well preserved....
Great place to live the History!

title: No trip to Rome is complete without seeing the Colosseum
text: Drive past it, walk it, see it-- the Colosseum is a must see during the visit to Rome. Virtually all the hop on/off tours stop here as well as other city buses. Whether day or night, feel the magnificence. The only thing that slightly spoils the feel is the modern "necessity" of wire fences outside.

title: Iconic and beautiful
text: I lived 2 blocks away from the Colosseum when I was studying abroad and when I went back to Rome the 7 years later I got a little bit teary eyed when I turned the corner and saw it again. I love the neighborhood immediately south east of it that no one ever really makes it to.

title: Must Visit
text: Once you touch the building you will feel the ancient and get the memories of the gladiators. Coolest information that you will get as gladiatorial bouts were originally part of funeral ceremonies.They didn't always fight to the death. The famous “thumbs down” gesture probably didn't mean death.They were organized into different classes.

title: Love it!
text: I love Roman history and this was a must. If you get the chance take the tour that allows you to go out on the arena floor and basement. Highly recommend that!

title: Sad but interesting
text: Lovely to see, dad hit interesting, we did a tour and glad we did got loads of info and we skipped the queue .

title: Amazing
text: Amazing view.. we waited in the line about half an hour or so was totally fine.. cost about 16euros to enter..

title: Impressive
text: Truly one of the most impressive monuments in the world. It's a place everyone should visit. The ticket is rather expensive (23,5€). It is a good idea to book your ticket online in order to avoid the line for buying the ticket and be sure to find one. You still have to go through the, airport style, security check line. Be aware that you have to book a time along with the date. With the same ticket you can also visit the, nearby, beautiful Palatine hill and the Roman forum in the same day. The crowd in early December is very reasonable.

title: Too many touts
text: We were going to visit the colosseum on a free entry day but it was really busy and there were loads of touts trying to sell skip the queue tickets and trinkets, etc. It all got a bit claustrophobic so we decided to just admire it from outside. To be fair it’s pretty impressive even without going inside!

title: Wedding anniversary visit to Rome
text: Booked skip the line to visit the colosseum with Roman forum & Pallentine Hill. Definitely worth doing this as lines can be long. Guide takes you in past security then let you roam round on your own. The colosseum is one impressive attraction, a must if you're visiting Rome. We spent just over an hour looking round this ancient monument. I enjoyed it much more than I expected. The only downside is the looky looky seller's who constantly pester you as you wait to go in

title: not a lot to it but so historical
text: not a lot to it but so historical so recommend it while in Rome..so much to see and the street shopping is super

title: Spectacular
text: I really liked looking at this magnificent structure, both by day and night. Definitely reccomend. The Metro and hop on buses stop very close. Quite a lot of construction work internally so didn’t go inside.

title: Left us in awe
text: Amazing place to visit, a must do in Rome. Would say that going in December was quite nice as the crowds weren't so bad, but we still ended up buying skip-the-line tickets online to be safe. Security was tight but do be wary of people trying to sell you things/touting/offering to take pictures for you outside the colosseum itself.

title: Amazing
text: We have all seen the photos and know that Rome is steeped in history. But when you see the scale of it and think of everything that went on in that area its sureall. If you get the chance you can visit at 6.00 pm. BRILLIANT

title: Multi-level tour of Colosseum
text: Highly recommend taking the tour that includes the lower and upper levels; tour guide was knowledgable and great photo ops. We purchased a guided tour on the Colosseum website, well worth it!! Early morning tour is best before it's too crowded and get there early to locate your tour group. Buy online ahead of time and print receipt with bar code.

title: Fantastic
text: I love ancient history and this place is amazing.  I's everything you would expect.  It's a must do while in Rome.

title: City break in Roma
text: I deeply recommend the travellers to buy Roma pass in order to avoid huge queuеs for Colosseum and to save some money for transportation and entrance fees for other objects.

title: Great historic place to visit
text: The Colosseum is well worth the visit. While this is a relic it shows the magnitude of what was achieved by early Romans. Much has been restored and there is an exhibition to visit along with the outside areas of the Colosseum too. Buy tickets in advance or at the venue as on-street tourist sellers charge a premium.

title: Lucky lucky men spoil it
text:
We visited the Collessium on our first day in Rome. The ruins actually start across the street where you see partial ruins of what looked like cells where the fighters were kept. 

Part of the building has been rebuilt due to it falling apart over time. 

I don't think a tour is needed as you see it all and plenty from the outside. The tour is expensive at around €35 each. 

Very busy as there is a main road right outside it. The Collessium itself is fenced off and there are lots of lucky lucky sales people in the grounds trying to sell bracelets and other items who don't leave you alone and follow you until you stop. One boy followed us so much my husband had to turn around and ask him to stop or we'll go to the police as we felt uncomfortable to which the boy swore at us and turned rude,so be careful when you go here. Unfortunately these lucky lucky guys puts a downer on the place as you want to leave to get away from them to feel safe. 

If these guys were moved away then this would have been a wonderful site to see and enjoy.

title: Excellent attraction to visit
text: Really good attraction to visit. Easy walking distance from our hotel. I was walking with a stick, the staff immediately took myself, my husband and our friends to a disabled entrance. We skipped the queues and were directed to the easier route to walk around. The exhibits are excellent, lots of interesting information all around without the need to go on a guided tour. Very good value for money, need to be prepared wait for photo opportunities. We found folk to be very accommodating. Easily a half day needed. The free water from the drinking fountains are all very safe to drink.

title: A Must See in Rome
text: As with all of the tourist destinations, the Colosseum is a must! We took a guided tour that took us above and underground and was worth every cent. Unfortunately the Colosseum was under renovation but you were still able to walk where the Romans and Gladiators walked. It is quite amazing and our tour guide was very knowledgeable. These are the type of excursions you want a guide for. They are worth it!

title: Astonishing place
text: This place will really take your breath away. Just be advised that if you go on a really hot sunny day you will burn. Protection and hydration is really necessary for walking around the colosseum

title: Colosseum and Forum
text: The Colosseum and Forum were our children’s favorite area to visit during a recent trip to Rome.  We took a self guided tour and it was perfect.

title: Book a guided tour with the organization running the place for next experience
text:
Travelled with 3 children and we booked a guided tour by co-op, the company/organization that runs the site . We paid €33 per adult for a tour and €17 for children above 12-18 and €2 for children under 12 that includes a guided tour to the colosseum, a visit to the underground section, the ground floor as well as the third level. These tours allows visitors access to these places that are not available for people buying general admission tickets. The tour provided a very good comprehensive overview of the site as well as awesome photo opportunities from sites that are not congested with visitors . These tours take only a small group and are much more pleasant . 

More importantly, it is cheaper than booking for guided tours by companies that bring you to the general area but at twice the price. Other private company are selling the same stuff at €50 but doesn’t bring you to these places such as the underground and third level . Good to purchase these tickets online exactly 2 months before you intent to visit the colosseum to be sure if a place at their official website coopculture.it to get the best deals. 

The tickets also include visit to the Forum and the palatine hills as well . Good to visit Colosseum first followed by the forum .

title: Wow!!!!!
text: As you make your way from the military museum and ruins of the Roman Forum you see the massive Colosseum!!!!  Just think about the intensive labor of building such a gigantic place!  Then think about all the tragic events that took place there....

title: Crowded...
text: Happy to have seen it... Must see, but so many people it is not fun. The personnel are not kind and tired of tourist. So, yes it is worth seing but unpleasant... Make sure to buy tickets online to avoid line up.

title: Great Tour
text: Our tour guide was Maya and she did an excellent job of explaining the history of the Colosseum and imparted a lot of great information.  It was such a great tour.

title: A dream come true.
text: For me was One of the very best Places in Rome. You really can feel history when u arrive to colloseo! I took a tour from a random Company, but i trully recommend you to visit the arena, for me was the best part.

title: BREATHTAKING!!!
text: This will stick to my mind for all my life. I feel I have to come back sometime. You just can get enough of this amazing building and its history. We had en pre-booked tour and passed all lines, directly in to the colusseum.

title: Amazing Colosseum
text: This was my 2nd visit to Rome and the Colosseum is thé attraction to visit when you are in Rome. I avoided the long ticket queues by booking online. There are always a throng of tourists here, but if you go early in the morning you can avoid the throng. We stayed a week in Rome and I started almost every day by taking a walk up the road to the Colosseum. One visit is just not enough.

title: Very nice place to visit
text: Very nice i would go again the best time to go is at nite. But we did both day and nite. Beautiful place

title: amazing place
text:
I would recommend an skip the line ticket, well worth visiting
an audio guide would also be required,

title: Must see
text: Amazing place, it’s a historic place that is must see, you will learn so much, I would recommend a tour guid so will explain the history behind it

title: Take the tour!
text: Fabulous historic site.  We used the Roma Pass for entry then booked an English tour onsite. It was really great, gave us some insight plus took us to all the best spots and explained about how to get to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill which is included in the ticket.

title: Gladiators arena.
text: Very interesting.Have to spend at least for a day here.Try to explore the whole colosseum and the areas surrounding it.Definitely five stars!

title: Interesting and beautiful
text: Don't miss walking around the palatine hill and the Romanian forum.. It is a few hours walk, beautiful and interesting

title: Amazing!
text: I did not really want to go, but my wife convinced me. I am really glad that she did because the Coliseum and the nearby forum were spectacular. Just the engineering that it took was unreal. I believe it is a sight everyone should see if they go to Rome.

title: Must-see once!
text: We came to Rome on a cross country roadtrip specifically to see this monument live. Thankfully we secured an amazing guide and ended having an unforgettable experience.

title: Amazing and iconic
text: This is simply an incredible sight, it is vast and imposing. It was a glory to explore and I recommend the tours. We wanted to do the vaults but there were no English tours left, so we got a basic English tour of the main levels and then booked a tour of the undercrofts in a different langauge so at least we could see, even if we were not compeltely sure what was being said. It must have been an incredible spectacle when in use and full - it is immense! Quite busy but very good value - especially when the ticket includes the forum and Palatine Hill. Brilliant.

title: Beautiful Colosseum
text: Great visit, beautiful surroundings. Lots of tourists( as one could imagine) Great place to walk around and if you are interested in history-great stories and facts to be heard.

title: Wow
text: Absolute fascinating especially the stone which declares that the colloseum was built with the plunder from Jerusalem so it belongs to the Jews!!

title: Old building
text: I wasn't so impresed about Colosseum. Yes, it's an old building with history, but now there are just walls. Inside the building it's nothig, only stones and walls, nothig special and the entrance ticket are expensive.

title: Coloseum
text: Maybe the absolute site to visit in Rome, must get ticket early otherwise you'll have to stand long in the queue waiting as always there is so much people there to see itm totally impressive and a must !!!

title: Great trip
text: Great trip to the Coliseum despite the rain. Booked in advance to avoid all the touts. The exhibition at the top is great.

title: Must do when in Rome
text: The Colloseum is one of these spots you have to visit when in Rome. It’s an impressive building and with the audio an video tour it comes alive.

title: Wau 😍
text: What a grand place. I will never forget this. The history is really present and it makes you realise how small we are. ❤️

title: Brilliant, informative and fun.
text: We booked a tour of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum through Expedia and it exceeded all expectation. Our guide George was friendly, patient and very funny. It was a 2 and a half hour tour and the time flew by.  George painted such a vivid picture of life in Ancient Rome that I could easily imagine how the buildings looked thousands of years ago.  Highly recommended (and an added benefit is that the selfie stick sellers leave you alone when you’re with a tour).

title: Beautiful arena
text: It is a must see when visiting Rome. The view is amazing. Also it's beautiful in evening as it is luminated from inside.

title: Ore inspiring place
text: The size just takes your breath away.  You can’t visit Rome and not come here. Easy to get, we used the metro.

title: Must see
text: This Colosseum is a must see. We booked our tickets in advance, because we were afraid of the cue. Well in December there are none. We could have bought the tickets right there. We travelled wirth a 10 month old, and I was no problem. We just brought our carrier with us.

title: Scammers ruin it for tourists
text:
The actual archeological site of the Colosseum is amazing. It’s a must do while in Rome and it’s so fascinating to hear about the ancient history. 

The poor rating reflects the hoardes of scammers and aggressive “tour guides” (unofficial guides/everyday folk who really really really want you to buy a tour from them and promise that you’ll get to skip the line). 

We attended today (14th Dec) and found it hard to get near the Colosseum to line up because of the sheer amount of people who try to stop you, and sometimes even grab you, trying to sell you cheaper tickets, take you for tours, ask for ‘gifts’ for their family back home, push items into your hands to then ask for money, and who try to ask you if you speak English to them scam money out of you. 

I estimate in 10 minutes that we directly encountered around 30-40 scammers. It’s quite stressful as well with the fear of being pick pocketed and targeted, so it wasn’t the greatest experience overall.

title: Amazing
text:
Not the first time I’ve been 
Queue as usual massive
Lots of works outside going on 
Sort of spoils the atmosphere for me 
I know they are essential works 
Still 
Will come again

title: Breathtaking attraction
text:
The whole experience was amazing starting from exploring the outside of the building ending with existing through palatine hills. 
The experience drove my enthusiasm to read about the history of the Roman empire and the date of the building.  I would definitely visit it again.  And would recommend to everyone. Just one advice,  book online as doing this would save you a lot of time.

title: book skip the Queue tickets online
text: need to book skip the Queue tickets online - even then theres still lots of people, you cant go in though until your allocated time . to busy for me and very very Hot

title: Must visit in Rome
text: Please use a proper guide for your tour. Give it at least 2 hours to soak the feeling.. amazing space enough to wow you.

title: Old ruins visit
text: Interesting visit to the colosseum. Great history and the venue itself is something to see. The building is massive and at 52m at its highest point. Lots to see and read.

title: Standing in the Arena
text: The Colosseum is even larger from inside.  We stood on the floor of the arena and got to see the workings of the Colosseum that produced the spectacles that entertained the Romans of the first century.  Incredibly, it held 50,000 spectators.

title: Must see!
text:
What can I say about the Coliseum? Obviously it is one of the sites that you have to see when you go to Rome. We booked our tickets through Tiqets prior to visiting Rome and this proved to be a good option. We were greeted by the staff at the designated meeting place and had a VIP entrance to the arena floor of the Coliseum.
The Coliseum itself is absolutely magnificent, however the subsequent walk around the Palatine hill, and the Roman forum were equally as amazing. Definitely one to remember!

title: Night Colosseum Tour
text: Great tour through colosseum and guide very knowledgeable. A bit too dark to see each of sights she spoke about, probably better to see early afternoon.

title: Stunning
text: Absolutely beautiful place. We booked tickets with Tiqets (not a guided tour) and they take you inside and make sure you're happy.

title: so old and so close to town
text: I was initially amazed at how close the Colosseum was to central Rome and the size. we had a guided tour provided by the tour which was really good. however we were told that the line to get inside was very long and we went in off peak. There is so much history here it is just so amazing. I also believe that the cost to get inside is expensive but do not know first hand. well worth the visit.

title: Definitely worth the visit with the guided tour
text: We signed up for the guided tour which covers the arena as well as the top level of the colosseum. The site itself is awesome but with the guided tour to cover the arena/underground as well as the top floor, it gave a really different perspectives to how activities were like back in those days. In addition, the top floor gave a wonderful view of the colosseum and its surroundings.

title: Amazing history
text: Very eerie hearing stories of what happened here, the torture for amusement must have been horrific. The view from outside would be sufficient for us but then it totally depends on your interest in this kind of thing

title: Forum & Colosseum
text: Recommend taking the time to do this guided tour. Spring was a good time with comfortable temps and small crowds

title: Symbol of Rome
text: This symbol of Rome is slowly being renovated, but a lot of its original majesty (marble siding and many statues) is missing through neglect and theft.

title: Some Good but Some Very Bad
text: The Colosseum is a "must see" attraction in Rome.  However prepare yourself for some very large crowds.  DO NOT WAIT until you are in Rome to buy your admission tickets.  All of the limited admission tickets may already be sold.  Buy your tickets well in advance of your trip.  Aditionally, go for the Underground Tour.  You will get a good understanding of what this amazing structure was designed to do.

title: Breath taking
text:
3rd visit to the coliseum but this time we took our young son!
If you are visiting Rome then you cannot miss seeing this breathtaking building! 
It is like stepping back in history! Queue times were very quick despite many vendors trying to encourage us to buy “skip the queue” tickets! 
The only negative is that you do get harassed too much by street sellers outside (which seems to have got worse since our last visit a few years ago) which would not normally be an issue but can be intimidating if you have young children.

title: December visit
text: Great architecture, centuries old, you can spend hours in it, walking, enjoying great views, luckily it was a bad weather so minimum of tourists :)

title: Great to view from the outside
text: Not sure if it’s worth the ticket price to see the inside but definitely a must see when visiting Rome!! There are off course touts and ringers outside so pre booking may have its advantages but brave it and queue. The inside details the history and construction of the building and it’s only a leap and a stroll from the Roman forum!!

title: Experience an open air museum
text: Rome is a great place to visit and the colosseum is a definite "must-see", lots of atmosphere and the scale is impressive.  Just keep an eye out for all the hawkers.

title: More stopping by...
text: One of the great sites in Rome. It is worth stopping to view, I have gone inside in the past but with a limited time availableI would  say to view from outside only.  One bad point is the sellers outside, you cannot walk far without being approached with Selfie Sticks etc.

title: Larger than life
text: the construction of it all, the gore that was not only acceptable but applauded there- so much history!

title: Off season best
text: Went in December and literally no one there for the 9am tour we had. A wonder of the world. Seeing in person is breathtaking. Checked it off the list but wow

title: Incredible Historical Site
text: There is so much rich history at the Colosseum.  Our tour guide from Joy of Rome Tours was absolutely amazing.  The site is rich with history and artifacts.  The exhibits are very interesting and helped to tell the full story.  It's just an amazing thing to see in person.

title: Very Enjoyable tour
text: We toured the Colosseum with a guide and was able to access the underground levels plus the third level. It is a sight to behold with interesting history.

title: Great place
text: Visit colosseum early in the morning. Book your tickets online. We reached here at 9 in the morning and waited only 2 minutes in the line for security. The trio colosseum, Palatine hill and Roman forum can take almost all day. Plan your visit accordingly.

title: First visit
text: Wow what an amazing sight, it’s huge... from the outside it’s breathtakingly beautiful and large, we ignored the touts about the ques and it was down... once inside it’s a little tricky to work out your route but go with the flow..... lots of work being carried out to keep it preserved but you can still get a feel for the history and events that took place here....

title: Crowded but awesome!
text: I got the OMNIA w/ROMA Pass and this worked out well, as i was able to visit Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palantine Hill with one single ticket. To help others if one has such a Pass, either you can make a reservation online for $1 Euro or stand in line and go to the reservations desk and make a reservation (which is what i did). Also booked a Tour for additional 4Euros and you can make it there itself. the tour was good which gives you a perspective of the place. I would reserve a whole day for the 3 sites.

title: Great Tour and Tour Guide!
text: Our visit to the Colosseum was made perfect by the enthusiasm and knowledge of our tour guide Gulio. He was so passionate (and funny) about all of the information that he shared with us. I highly recommend Rome Private Guides!!!

title: Enduring fear...
text: This place is so truly awesome,  even now as you enter the arena you can feel a shudder in the spine, thinking about the horrors that were perpetrated here in the name of fun and entertainment....I recommend getting a guide though as a good one like ours can fill out what you already know with a bit more depth. 8 years to build for example..

title: A must-see
text: What a sight. There is so much history here. We took our time and walked around the entire outside parameter....it was gorgeous.

title: Must-not-miss when in Rome
text: A must visit attraction in Rome which needs to be better preserved, would take 2-3 hours to go through in entirety. One might want to enjoy the horse cart too.

title: Love
text: So Beautiful one of the place more beautiful in the world. i love this place i love italy. We return in March 2020

title: It’s hard to imagine that the huge building you are standing in is the INSIDE portion of the original massive structure!
text: History and “stories” are awesome, whether it’s construction itself, how the system of pulleys lifted animals and people into the arena, who sat where and why, uses over hundreds of years....incredible - and right in the middle of traffic and today’s world!

title: Such Magnificent Sight to Behold...
text: Where you once just see it in movies, books, or paintings, being near is totally an awesome experience. Once can just feel what it was like then...

title: It is the Roman Colosseum, do I really need to say more.
text: There is nothing in the world like the Roman Colosseum. Emotion got the best of me when I entered that sacred wonder. You really just have to experience it for yourself. It is amazingly overwhelming.

title: Amazing
text: A must see in Rome. Before visiting better do research on how you prefer you visit (solo, tour), what you want to see,history behind it,when better to visit (day and time) and how to enter (security checks)

title: Amazing Place!!!
text: The Colosseum is a must see if you visit Rome!!!! We spent a couple of hours exploring all the palaces in and around of it. I would definitely recommend joining a group with a guide, otherwise it would be difficult to know what you are looking at!!

title: Great
text:
We didn’t book before and the line to get ticket wasn’t big so it was fine! 
We got one ticket for 3 place 
Colosseum - Palatino - Forum Romana

title: Must visit of course
text: It was not one of our highlights, it it is ofcourse a must-visit attraction when you're in Rome. Recommended to get your tickets online to avoid massive waiting lines.

title: Stunning
text: Just back from here and what an amazing imposing place this is. However all we could think about was how cruel humans were back in the day. The slaughter of thousands of Christians for entertainment is shocking. When the metro was being built several years ago they uncovered a tomb with thousands of bodies and animals. Shocking but a place steeped in history

title: History and glorious ruins.
text: Wonderfully educational opportunity and step back in time. Well worth the effort to get past the ticket sellers and others trying to push things on you. Like being a Christian offering in the circus. A must see.

title: Don't miss the museum
text: I visited Colosseum at the end of a very long day but I had planned in such a way to see only the places mentioned in the audio guide to save time and energy. Whatever else you see or don't see, please do not miss the museum. Most people are too tired after walking around that they skip this gem. Details about life at the Colosseum, rare animals that were used in the fight, shreds of pottery, models of colosseum are all kept here.  Remember that even if you buy a ticket beforehand you still have to stand in a queue and pass security check - so this is not a place to be seen in a hurry. If you have mobility issues, walk to the end of ground floor and take the lift.

title: Colosseum
text:
It is a neccesary visit if visiting Rome.  Strongly advice to visit it.
It give the visitor an idea how ancient civilisation lived.
How prisoners were killed.
Another way of killing is by let ferocious animals attact the culprits.

title: Huge !!
text: Must Visit place in life time ! Book ticket in advance to skip the queue ! Try to visit at night as well to enjoy the view !

title: An amazing monument
text: Don't get inside. You can take amazing photos and enjoy it even from outside. Try going at night. It's extraordinary!

title: Colosseum Night Tour
text: I recently took the nighttime tour of the Colosseum and loved it.  We had access to the gladiator tunnels underground. Fascinating. Excellent archaeologist tour guide (Luigi) from Walks of Italy who also explained the Roman Forum and Capitoline Hill, bringing them to life and putting them into historical perspective, as we walked through those areas enroute to the Colosseum -- a bonus!

title: Visited twice but I'm still amazed!
text:
Make sure you purchase your tix and validate by calling the Customer Service in advance.
Purchase the Roma Pass as it is the good option!!

title: Rome's Symbol of Power and Prestige Lives On
text: The Colosseum stands out as the epic symbol of the history of Rome. The structure rises into the skyline announcing the power, prestige, and creativity that is the history of the city. There was a somber feeling as one senses when visiting a war memorial from lives lost within the walls. One cannot pass by or through the Colosseum and not be moved inside. Definitely an absolute must see on a visit to Rome.

title: Centerpiece of Rome
text: The Colosseum is a magnificent structure in the Center of the old city. It was used in ancient times for gladiator fights. Seating 80,000 gladiators fought each other and animals for their release of slavery. You can see the floor below where there were cages of animals, where the animals and gladiators came out of to fight.

title: Miss Ruth Ashdown
text:
Again a must see!!! 
I’ve been once before in summer and was full of tourists and street sellers. This time raining and lots of building work...
Loads to see around it, on a nice day loads to do!

title: Oldies but goldies!
text: Given its age, the Colosseum is still showing great. I was amazed by the way it was constructed(size,style,etc)

title: Crowded
text: We had family members with us who are first time visitors of Rome so Colosseum is a must-visit sight even though we had been here a few times before. We pre-ordered our tickets online and in order to fit everything on our itinerary, the only time that would work for us was on our day of arrival. We came a little less than an hour early for our 4:30 entrance and had to wait until the exact time to get in. I was really surprised that it was still very crowded that late in the afternoon. Good thing was we just walked right in. This is such an amazing structure with so much history. We came back a couple of days later just for an exterior view and photos after sundown. Absolutely beautiful!

title: The Colosseum
text: One of the best places l have ever been, it walks you back through the history of Roman Empire as depicted from the movies and documentaries we see on TV about the Gladiators and Emperors. Structures still standing, breath taking architecture and findings...

title: Beautiful
text: Beautiful attraction for everyone who wants to know more about history. We payed to go with a group and a tour guide that explained to us the history of the place .

title: Busy Busy!
text: We've been to Rome a couple of times, but only visited the Colosseum once since it was super busy. If you're trying to avoid big crowds and lots of people try coming in the morning and be around when it opens. Probably near closing time would not be a bad idea either. Definitely do not visited around 12-3, because around these times its the busiest ever! It opens at 8:30 am and closes at 7 pm. I'm not sure for the ticket prices, but I think its around 25 €. It also depends on which type of package you get because there are various ones. If your're looking for the busiest crowds; they're in the summer for sure. I think the best time for travelling is in the autumn; especially if you're looking to visit  popular and big cities like Rome.

title: One of the Great Sites in Rome
text: Yes one cannot be in Rome without seeing the great and famous sites of the ancient city such as this the colosseum from such a famous period in history - there are other colosseums in the world such as El Djem in Tunisia but this is the most famous

title: A classic
text: Even if you don’t enter the Colosseum, the view is spectacular. I think it’s particularly beautiful at night. The inside is marvelous but it’s a bit ruined by the fact that they are always repairing something. And yes, I get that it’s an ancient monument that requires constant maintenance, but it ruins both the view and the photos. I think that’s the only thing that I dislike; the rest of the visit is very good.

title: Great wonder of the world - The Colosseum
text: I got a chance to for fill a lifelong dream of visiting the colosseum and Rome. It was really awesome seeing the structure in real life instead of in movies and through pictures on the internet. I didn’t go inside because it was late but also because it’s so expensive. I did a trip around the colosseum and took pictures. Alongside the Vatican and Fontana di Trevi it’s a must do location when in Rome.

title: Incredible to see up close and personal
text: Walking around the colosseum was incredible. After seeing it featured in so many films, it was amazing to see it close up...and to  appreciate the architecture and the atmosphere. We didn’t buy tickets to go inside as we felt that it was enough to walk around the site and take it all in. Well worth a visit.

title: Fascinating but...
text: Like other popular tourist sites in Rome the site sells itself. To be able to walk inside such a building so many years old is a privilege. The museum on site is interesting and well presented. The other good thing about going inside once you have a ticket is that you are not continually confronted by non- locals selling umbrellas, ponchos, automatic phone holders and trashy momentos. There are so many of them and some get aggressive and angry if you ignore them. The other group who are locals parade themselves as assistants who can give you information on fast tracking the queue ... for a price once they have ‘assisted’ you. The enjoyment of Rome is being impacted by these people.

title: Un lugar para volver
text: I have visited this place twice, it must be said that it is one of the most incredible monuments I have ever seen, preparing comfortable shoes and a good camera.

title: Loved it
text: I loved our trip to the Colosseum. We had booked a tour and definitely would recommend as we didn’t have to join the long queues. The colosseum was incredible and very interesting. Lots of photo opportunities.

title: When in Rome, You just have to visit the Colosseum
text: Definitely a must do when you're in Rome, a magnificent structure steeped in history. A glimpse into ancient Rome and its structure of society.

title: Beautiful but please book early
text:
Its beautiful and breathtaking but!
Only if you would love to know more about the history behind it.
Not much blood and gore details which was disappointing but okay. Pg13.
Lots of construction and restoration going on, spoils the photo a little bit. But okay.
Like i said u must really want to see it otherwise dont go! 
5 stars because i really wanted to see it

Wanted to see some blood and guts :(

title: Go early to minimise queuing; you need to see it from inside
text:
Apart from the Vatican, the other must visit in Rome is the Colosseum. The long line in front of the Colosseum coupled with the Bangladeshi men chasing you (as is the case across Rome) would definitely be major deterrents in considering going inside.

However, do consider seeing the place from inside. The ruins, seats, scale of the place is worth absorbing.

Good to spend an hour inside; depending on the hour of the day, queuing could take 15-60 minutes. Advisable to visit in the morning when the crowd hasn’t started building up.

title: Worth a Visit
text: It's one of the places that must be visited when in Rome. The scale and detail of the structure is absolutely stunning. For visual effect, if you are keen to capture memories of the visit, it is ironic that the best views are from the outside rather than inside.

title: Visit to the Colosseum
text:
Very impressive, but also very crowded. That said, one can’t not be enthralled with the history. 
The ticket line was long but moved very quickly. There was a definite lack of signage.

title: Line up ok
text: We did not purchase tickets prior but were aware to get there early to avoid crowds. This worked for a 930am arrival and took probably 30 mins max before we were inside and the same time as those who had online tickets. Beware of people trying to sell you tickets outside- ignore and just go to entrance. Rather disorganised inside as no clear guidelines/path to follow but interesting regardless and of course wonderful preserved historical site. Without guided tour you cannot visit downstairs which is a shame so would recommend going this as seemed fascinating. Great site otherwise but so very busy with tourists and this is the quiet season!!!

title: Amazing, but somehow could be more
text: There are few attractions as impressive as the Colosseum in Rome, and it is an experience well worth making. While there were many exhibitions on the second level which help contextualize the whole experience, and even though we had a wonderfully knowledgable guide (Sergio) I left the Colosseum feeling it could have and should have been more. Maybe because there was little mention of the yet poorly understood and hard to conceive awning mechanism, or the impossibility to access the multiple underground / under-arena areas. I wanted to be blown away, and instead I was just awed. Well worth it all the same. Book a guide, get your tickets in advance if possible, get there early and give yourself ample time to stroll around the different areas and levels.

title: Colosseum is amazing
text: Do this trip in the morning and be prepared to stand in line and walk up/down stairs. It was hot and people were passing out but there were EMTs nearby. Bring water. This place is so amazing and our tourguide was very knowledgeable

title: Astonishing
text: It was my dream to visit the Colloseum and when i exited the metro and saw it i was in awe by this artwork. You need to visit it when in Rome,it actually brings you to the Gladiator movie!

title: Amazing sight, badly organised
text: The colosseum itself is outstanding to see and wander around. However it can be complicated to see where the correct place to buy tickets is online. The best way to do it is to ignore all the tour guides and people who approach you outside trying to sell you tickets and tours and go in yourself to the official ticket office. The audio guide is good but can be a little hard to follow.

title: Interesting but tour got long.
text: Interesting history but our tour got long and a bit boring.  My back was giving out after a vatican tour and then the colosseum.    The history vs. anything in the USA is amazing.  Our world is so recent vs these ancient cultures.

title: A true wonder.
text: The top tourist attraction for any visitor to Rome. Be prepared to find lengthy lines all around. A true amazement of a structure built over Two thousand years ago. Restoration of the Colosseum are in progress. Purchase tickets beforehand as you will have to wait along time to enter.

title: amazing piece of history and our collective culture
text: you should do an tour of the place to fully appreciate the history and magnificence of this place which is part of our popular consciousness.

title: Great history
text: The Colosseum is so great. So much that happened and what a great exhibit that they have in there. I recommend taking a tour so you can learn about the history, but just being there is incredible.

title: Get Skip-the-line tickets
text: Fortunately, our group had pre-booked skip-the-line tickets. Otherwise, we would have been stood waiting in a queue for hours. Very disorganised, with many people trying to sell tickets and no clue of who is official and who is not. However, once inside the views are amazing. There is the option to purchase guided tours or headphones but we didn't do so. There are descriptions of the different things around, like in a museum, which helps to understand things. Worth visiting if you are in Rome.

title: Money-grabbing management
text: The Colosseum itself is great - this 1-star is for the management. The ticket is €16 each as a combined ticket for the Colosseum, the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill (no individual ticket option available), but if you don’t wish to pay extra to pre-book your ticket or join a guided tour that their staff members were trying to sell to you outside the site for €40/person, you are the least welcome visitor at the site. Others are filing in, but your turn never comes. When I tried to book tickets about a month before my visit, the official site showed that no ticket was available for the dates I planned to stay in Rome. But when I checked the websites of travel agencies, there were plenty of tickets apparently, price starting from €25 each. I believe we all know what’s happening here. Profiteers.

title: Family tour
text: Great tour and very informative.   CARRANI VIAGGI tour was very good.  Out tour guide was very informative and kept us energized.  Skipping the long lines was amazing.

title: In awe
text: Colosseum is outstanding. Whether you just enjoy the many different views outside or if you do the tour inside (my opinion it’s worth it). One of the most beautiful and stunning things, with so much history.

title: Great when inside
text:
Hopeless organisation when trying to work out about lining up to get in. No staff around to ask but lots of sellers there trying to upsell their particular tour.
Once inside it is a perfect place to visit

title: Colosseum: take a guided tour.
text: Very nice and interesting. But really take a guide, so you wont miss all the details of its amazing history. We had Laetitia and she did a great job and made it really worth while.

title: Highlight of our trip
text: We booked the the all round tour so we could make the most of this first time trip to Rome. We got to go into the underneath in the dungeons to see where the gladiators came up from to the main floor as well as the animals. There is a mock elevator down there as well as the sewers. We got to step out onto the arena floor and see the underneath before we went down there. The tour guide was very informative and told us all about the different areas of seating that are still just about visible. They are doing a lot of restoration work on the building but you don't overly notice the workmen. After seeing the dungeons etc we went up to the first floor for a better view of the arena. After wandering around this floor, we went up through a locked gate up into the third tier tunnel where we could see the sheer height that people would have been at to watch the performance. And also we could look down to see the box where the roman emperors would have sat. We then went up another smaller flight of stairs to the very top where the views were absolutely stunning. well worth paying the money for. The tour guide was funny and informative. Would definitely recommend.

title: Awesome
text: Impressive place although the entrance is chaotic. Get your tickets beforehand and  e there early because the lines will become endless if you decide to "take it easy". The history there will overwhelm you and it will give you a quick glimpse as to how Roma used to handle itself. It is certainly a must do thing while in Rome.

title: Excellent place to visit
text: Love the natural structure of the Colosseum. It is definitely amazing to see how ancient people built this structure. It is one of the Instagram sights that I would recommend. If you are keen in the historical background then you should pay for the entrance. Or you can just take photos from the outside and I guess it is enough.

title: Fantastic Sights, but be aware of the tourist scam
text: I love Colosseo, and also went in to enjoy the majestic awe-inspiring historical sights. But be aware of the scammers who dressed up in historic outfit and trapped you for pictures even if you were just passing by. They will trap you, ask you to take a photo and charge you for 20 Euros right after.

title: So full of life
text: We were in Rome for Christmas and this city was just bubbling with people everywhere. Visiting the colosseum was fun.it is a magnificent structure. when it is lit it becomes even more gorgeous.so much activity and cafes around made our visit very festive.

title: My favourite historical site in Rome
text: I have visited Rome 3 times and always spent some times in this amazing historical building. This month I attended a five-day event in FAO, only 2 bus stops away from the Coloseum, so that I could enjoy the view of the building every day and night. I always impressed with its structure, its history during the decades of Roman Emperors. I had a chance to enter inside the building in the afternoon on my second visit, but in the evening, with limited lights and its giant shadow, Coloseum has very different appearance.

title: FREE Day for tourists in MAY!
text: Once a year it is open FREE to tourists... BUT you still need to get a ticket from a booth.  We lined up about 8am, tickets opened at 930 am and we went straight in past all the poor people who had lined up since 6am - with no ticket.. the reason?  Because they limit amount of people through the gates at a time..

title: Rome must see site!
text: I suggest you pay extra and sign in for the reserved entrance. Its a bit pricy but you avoid the long line going in.

title: Colloseum
text: They did skip the line at colloseum. Nothing more to say as they do not offer guides. Not much more to say!

title: amazing architecture
text: This was such an amazing trip for our family. It was a bucket list item for my husband. My family enjoyed the nighttime guided tour.

title: Amazing
text: It is definitely a place to say you went to.  The Colosseum floor wasn't what I expected.  It looked as though construction was going on.  But overall, a must see when in Rome.

title: History comes alive at the colosseum
text: Even in the rain, this location was a fascinating look back into the previous century. A nice mix of translated museum explanation along with a friendly tour guide (Patrizia from The Tour Guy) Broughton things to life.

title: Historical experience
text: We had a great historical experience despite the overcrowding and refurbishment of the coliseum. I tour guide from funtours was outstanding.

title: Colosseum
text: Take a guide instead of an audio guide. Don’t fall for trap where the tour operators provide mobile app as guides. The giant structure will consume an hour. Since roman forum and palatine is nearby, take the complete tour in one go. It will take total of 4 hours.

title: Here's a tip.
text: If you rock up and discover you can no longer buy tickets for the Colosseum at the gate don't panic. We headed to the Roman Forum and  bought a combined ticket there. After a couple of hours exploring the Forum we headed to the Colosseum with a timed entry no problems.

title: Must see in Rome
text: There are so many things to do and see in Rome, But visiting the Colosseum is a must!  A small advise, don't visit it during the day time as it's too crowded and will ruin the whole experience.

title: Top attraction
text: The most visited monument of Rome, there is a good reason for this, as it is awesome. Yet, there is also a downside, long waiting lines and overcrowded in and outside.

title: Must See
text: This one place you want a tour guide. You will miss so much without one. Impressive Awesome   Get reservation

title: Gladiator tour
text: Don’t do the Colosseum any other way than this private tour.  Ask for Matteo the best guide ever.  Knowledgeable and engaging.  Learned more history of Rome in 3 hours than a term in college.  But only Matteo.

title: beautiful views.
text: Didn’t go in as long queue. Looks amazing from outside. Watch out for pickpokets. Also, people dressed up as vikings and taking picture and asking for €20 for photos.

title: amazing
text: Amazing architecture. The queue is not long during to the low season. We really enjoined it  Highly recommended.

title: Colosseum Rome
text: To finally see the magnificent architecture of the Roman Empire was impressive to see what could be built in Ancient Rome given the times.

title: Neither that colossal, nor that grand!
text: It's a nice monument but to see from the outside it is its best feature! It looses all that mystery and admiration as soon as you step inside mainly because it offers of nothing to revive that wonder of the time!

title: colosseum guided tour eyes on rome
text: Private Guided tour through eyes on rome, more expensive, but worth the extra cost.  We had the same tour guide as Vatican, Guide new her history, very imformative, Would recommend this company

title: Unbelievable
text: This building and it’s history are without parallel in any visit to Rome. A godd guide provides massive value added to the experience.

title: "Skip the lines"
text: Best time to hit Rome is defiantly in December. I would recommend doing most the events solo. The group tours always had crazy lines and no one seemed to be having fun. ask the receptionist for audio set and walk right on in see the place at your own pace.

title: Only amazing from outside
text:
This building is totally overwhelming! It looks amazing from outside. The composition with modern building is really interesting. It’s nice to walk around and see it from different perspective also at different time of the day. 
Complete not worth to go inside. First too many people, everybody are pushing to the photo spots. And second there is even no info what is what if you don’t have guide. Better to stay outside and safe some money.

title: Christmas kick off
text: So in love with Italy at Christmas. The people the markets, the decorated store fronts. The roasting chestnuts, and the energy of the city was just so magical.

title: be prepared - VERY CROWDED
text: The Colosseum.is obviously a must visit but be prepared to squueze in during the peak season holiday period. It is recommended to pay a little more for the skip-the-line tickets where the local guide will explain the histpry and culture also.

title: Gladiator!
text: Historic building, worth a visit while in Rome. The queue was quite long, but fast enough. A little tip : don't carry big backpack, to minimize time during security check.

title: Excellent
text: Magnificent from the outside. Day and night. We didn’t go in but happy to stroll around it. Very lively at night around the area.

title: Buy the Skip-the-Line tickets
text:
I was there in the winter and thinking the lines would not be long. I was wrong! Thankfully, I joined a walking tour that includes skip-the-line admission tickets. Totally worth it as I see the line snaking around Colosseum. It will be a big pile of ruins to you but my guide called "DC" can explain to details to look out for for a glimpse of its glorious past. 

Fun fact. The colosseum was built in a lake after it was drained. The lake was drained in an attempt to erase any horrific memories of Emperor Nero.

title: Breathtaking
text: I first laid eyes on this monument 2 years ago while visiting Rome with my husband. This year, the feeling of seeing such history as you exit the Metro is a wonderful experience.

title: Ancient treasure
text: The Colosseum in Rome one of the most breathtaking ancient site in the world. It is worth booking tickets online avoid the lines.

title: Must see before you die
text:
Amazing architecture from Roman regime and well preserved. Due to time constraint, we didn’t join any tour, but bought tickets online. Took us a while to find the entrance but we were told to queue at the ticket counter to collect the tickets with our online booking confirmation.  At 8am, the queue was really long with at least 50 pax, though it moved really quickly.

There are few stories, basement is out of our reach as entrance is only allow with tour guides. Still we managed to get around on our own and snapped plenty of photos. 

After trying from all angles, we realised the best place to snap photo for the entire colosseum is from Palatine Hill. It was quiet and less touristy. 

Be early to beat the crowd.

title: Collosseum
text: One of rome s place to go. But the ticket including the Roman forum. Roman forum completes the trip.

title: Memorable Experience
text: What a place , Brilliance of Roman Empire , The architecture, once u enter it , just feel like in time of Gladiators

title: Amazing History but exceptionally chaotic
text: While I highly recommend this breathtaking ancient site, the chaos that surrounds their booking and viewing is abysmal. We were told 4 different stories as we tried to use our Roma Pass. We were routed 3 different times back and forth. It was almost enough to make us leave. Be prepared to be frustrated. However, once inside, it is amazing. Many people have walked those paths and it was amazing to follow them.

title: Colosseum Arrival tip
text: Family of five bought tickets via the Colosseum website for a reserved time entrance at 8:50am.  Small reservation fee for children, free if we would not have reserved a time.  Metro station exit is directly in front of the Colosseum, 2 minute walk away.  If arriving with a voucher, be sure to get in the lines furthest to the left from the Colosseum.  The closest line is for those without a voucher or tickets.  Workers around the venue were nice and helpful with directions.  After moving through the fast line, and having a security guard check the reserved time (do not show up more than 30 minutes early than reservation time), we went to the ticket counter that was marked above "reservations".  She issued the tickets, which were good at all three sites in the area.  Best times to visit are early or late.  Toilets, book stores, and elevators are available.  Need no longer than 1 hour to visit.  Then can walk to the Forum and Hill.

title: Cool
text: Too many people and traidors. I could saw biotoilets for builders from outside. Toilet cost money. Wasn't inside

title: Bucket list
text: I used the audio tour to learn about the structure. Make sure you show your Red and white transportation pass from the Omni a card at the ticket booth  if you pre-book an entry time so you don’t lose 14€. In retrospect, I would have taken a guided tour with a live, human guide.

title: Amazing
text:
The more history you know about this wonder, the more amazing it is to view. Of course scaffolding in restoration efforts taint the magic a bit but this is reality. 

I viewed interior on a trip 5 years prior to 2019 trip and that was the best experience 
(Photo 2)

title: Wow!!
text: We tried to get tickets for the Colosseum and was told the quenue waiting time was 3 hours. We were bombarded by ticket touts from from every directions. We managed to find a tour company that got us into the Colosseum. We paid €105 to get in with a guide. I was worried it was a scam but was pleasantly surprised at how much I enjoyed the tour

title: Finally got in there
text: Last time there it was closed due to flooding.  Got in there this time and had a thorough tour.  It was awesome.

title: Incredible
text: The Colosseum is a MUST if you are in Rome, the site is spectacular and it completely justify why it’s one of the 8 wonders of the world.

title: Amazing
text: I would recommend taking the night tour. The Colosseum at night is breathtaking, not to mention the crowds are not there.A must when in Rome

title: A must visit in Rome!
text: This place is surreal. Unbelievable. I can  never get tired of gazing at this beautiful structure. You have to just stand there and think about all the things that took place here. It really is a lot to take in. You have to see it to believe it. Definitely take a long walk inside

title: Awesome - must see in Rome
text: Not much words are necessary. Thats the number ONE spot for Rome visitors. Make sure you come early and have skip-the-line-tickets. The number of visitors are limited to 3000, ie after that they close down the Colloseo and wait until some people have left. You may wait for a long time if you are still in line. Have a guided tour to understand the history and importance of the monument.

title: Must do
text: Always buy a ticket for the tour I visit Rome, always a great experience albeit a bit crowded, gives you a good perspective of Rome's history.

title: Colosseum visit
text: A must for a visit to this famous site is to pre-book a tour visit . This will enable you to by pass the visitors who just turn up. Even so we still had to join a lengthy , tour queue which took almost an hour to process through.How long ordinary visitors took to get in anyone knows. A good tour guide, like the one we had brought the site and it's history to life. Many of these tours also include the Forum site which is just across the the road so well worth the extra cost. Ignore the bottled water sellers - if there is one thing the the Romans prided themselves in was providing plenty of free water fountains - and they are still there so take a reusable bottle with you and drink away !  To summarise , a fantastice site to visit but be prepared to hang around before you get in. I feel that the site is reaching maximum capacity and something will eventually  have to be done to refine the control of the crowds.

title: Beautiful but beware the queues
text:
There’s a reason why the Colosseum is the most visited museum in the world! 
The queues are horrendous though and inside it is packed with tourists. 
Definitely worth purchasing a skip the line pass. If that’s not in your budget then come as early as possible to try skip the queues. 

Expect there to be lots of people in your way the whole time but embrace it as part of your Colosseum experience. 

The steps to get to the top and to go down to the stadium floor are very steep so watch how you go. 

Otherwise, a wonderful experience.

title: HEADOUT in ROME -----    DO NOT BUY TICKETS HERE!!!
text:
December 29th, 2019.   Unpleasant customer service. We arrived in time, on the dot.  The group had left without us, they were very unkind the way they treated us, the lady at the front desk yelled at us, her supervisor did not blink an eye, listening to all the conversation at a desk behind.  Even when we requested to come and speak to us, she did not move. Tickets bought online and completely lost, they were unable or better still not wanting to reallocate us or even assist us kindly. The lady was rude and very unpleasant.
They do not know how to treat customers at all. The ticket point to get your tickets is extremely far away from subway, around 20 minutes walk.  Once you buy it online they give you all the information on where to pick up your tickets, so you pay first and then you get the surpise about the distance. Awful customer service. Money lost for three party members. We did not see the coliseum, a complete rip-off. Not recomendable at all.

title: Great
text: Fantastic to see the history. There was an Egyptian themed road show at the same time which was great. Definitely recommend this with the ruins opposite!

title: When in Rome..
text: This really is an amazing site. It’s best to go with a guide who can share details about the history, show you the best photo spots, and guide you through the crowds. Walking up the road to the colosseum is part of the experience as you go past ruins, excavations, street performers. Best to combine with Palatine Hill and the Forum.

title: Long queue
text: Extremely long queue. Its wise to pre book the trip to Colosseum which is guided tour. Little expensive to book but worth to save 2 hours of queue.

title: Simply Amazing
text: The Colosseum is the most impressive site in a city full of impressive sites. A must visit when in Rome and it wasn't overly busy when we went. If you only do one thing in Rome, this is it.

title: Colosseum
text: Don’t be afraid of the long lines, they are fast! Lots of people and if you are not taking a guided tour, I recommend their book guide for 5€.

title: Always impressive, but extensive renovations in progress
text: The Colosseum is a sight not to be missed in Rome, even if you don't opt for a tour. There is extensive renovation taking place, so some views and access may be obscured for the next year or so.

title: Fabulous!
text: Having visited the Colosseum in Rome the year before and battling with the crowds this was a real treat.  Hardly anyone there and it's very atmospheric.  A few OH&S issues, but just be careful the higher you get

title: History that Comes Alive - A Bucket List Item Checked
text: As you walk around and take it all in, it's amazing how much the history comes alive. It's a very busy attraction, so be prepared to wait in lines. We were there in November, which I assume is off season and it was still plenty busy, but still well worth the effort. Don't let the crowds scare you off, Rome is probably on everyone's "must-do" bucket list, as it should be, and the Colosseum is a must see in Rome.

title: you will be pick pocked
text: It is sad that pickpocket is prevalent in Rome so much and the city is not doing anything about it. My wife lost the credit card, money and insurance card. Fortunately for us my wallet was not stolen we came back home in one piece. I will really advise against travelling to the dreadful city in the name of history.

title: Colosseum / Rome
text: I visited the colosseum today, only from outside, it was very crowded, so much people, and now they made the metrostation just before this most awe-inspiring ancient monument, this enormous amphitheatre of nearly 2000 years, I can beliefe it, but it is so. Entrance 16 €, years ago it was for free, but this times are ower; but Colosseum is the most impressive monument in the city.

title: Still crowded
text:
This structure is huge. The view from the street at night does not do this structure justice. 

We took a tour to view this and it did not disappoint. Our guide was quite knowledgeable and related the history to our modern times. She pointed out the various materials that are part of the original structure as well as all the “new” bricks that have been placed for the integrity of the remaining original structure. You can view the Colosseum without a guide, but you will miss the history and knowledge as well as descriptions to reconstruct it’s original form.

This attraction is always crowded, but I was able to get a photo from the upper street early on New Year’s Day and there were no people! The best time to get a photo that is family portrait of another family free is at first light of the day!

They also had the NYE fireworks near here, so cool!

title: Christmas in Rome
text:
Very crowded but a great experience.
