Do not expect to get in quickly, even with skip the queue tickets and beware of dodgy ticket touts outside.

title: Bday trip to Rome
text: This was the type of trip that happens once in a lifetime.  Visiting Rome has been on bucket list forever, I am so glad and fortunate that this dream came true.  There is so much to see and do that I will have to get back there one day to finish what I started.  Happy Birthday to me.

title: can't afford to miss this one
text: You can't afford to miss this one, take option to skip the line in advance or on the stop via resellers, do hard bargin because there is a lot of room in price, you can make it around 30-35 euro per person for guided tour from 110 odd euro price, if you can bargain well, there are many bangadeshi, pakistani and indians reselling the tickets to visitors. Avoid baby trolleys because it difficult to manage there.

title: Historic
text: Great place to tour, although very busy when we went, get a tour is the best plan,  so much history to take in.

title: good experience
text: Queue system is very bad. Better avoid the group guide that too with extra payment for skipping queue. See by your own, it is the best thing so that you can go everywhere without hurry.

title: Best days of our Anniversary
text: A must see when you visit rome. Take the skip-the-line tour for more convenient visit and take a lot of memorable photos.

title: A great piece of history, a steep climb to the top
text: We enjoyed visiting the Colosseum - be prepared, though, that to go to the upper levels there are steep steps.

title: Bring ID / Use free audio tour
text:
We bought our tickets online two weeks before, waited in a queue for about 5 minutes to pick them up. Make sure everyone in your party has ID! The tickets were in my name but my boyfriend needed ID to prove he was British for some silly reason. Luckily he had a picture of his driver's licence on his phone and the attendant allowed it. 

Saw mixed reviews on the guided tours available so opted to use the Rick Steve's Audio Guide app for all the attractions I visited in Rome - if you're travelling on a budget, this is the way to go!!! The audio guide is amazing and clear - he tells you exactly where to walk to and you can pause it if you want, unlike many of the tour guides that show up late and rush you through the attraction. There's not much to see in the colosseum as a whole so I'd say of all the historical sites that you may want an actual tour guide for - this isn't one of them. It can be done in under an hour.

title: Why to you go to Rome, if not for the Colosseum?
text: There may come a time I visit Rome and do not visit the Colosseum. It's hard for me to imagine that time. On this visit my family took a nighttime tour of the underground portion of the  Colosseum. On every visit I learn new things, I have new questions and find new images that ignite my imagination. While I hope to never experience the brutality of what went on in this arena in the name of entertainment, I have come to understand why the events may have occurred. Linger for a few minutes, climb the steep steps, look at the seating areas and finally see how the class system was levied on the people. It is all so fascinating. Frankly, in many ways, the stadiums of today are not that much different... Go to the Colosseum.

title: Book ahead!
text:
The official website is clunky and ticket slots do seem to be snatched up ever so quickly. But nevertheless we managed to book our tickets online and ahead of time. When we arrived at the site and saw the horrendous queues, we were glad that we only faced the one queue to get through security. This still took a good 40 minutes, but once inside the place was terrific. The various  displays and temporary exhibitions helped to bring it all to life.

A few days later, we circled the Colosseum as part of the We Run Rome 10K run and it looked magnificent. I would say well done to those Italians of the past who took it upon themselves to transform what had become a ruin into such a fantastic monument.

title: Fantastic
text:
I’ve been to colosseum twice before and wanted to show it to my kids. They loved it! 
The only negative are the trash everywhere around, and an impossibly long line. ( And we came around 9am, and got in only around 1pm.)

title: Exceptional
text: Pre-book or he prepared to queue for hours. Worth it. Book a guided tour for real insight into the history. Would go again.

title: As history goes, this is a must see
text: The most iconic attraction in Rome does not disappoint. Very awe inspiring to walk in the footsteps of ancient Roman rulers and combatants. We purchased our skip the line tickets before hand through the Coloseums web site as this seemed to be the least expensive option. Tickets included entrance to Pallentine Hill and surrounding ruins. As everywhere in Rome, expect crowds.

title: Great place with lots of history
text:
One of those few iconic places that are still alive from the old times.
It is definitely a must see along with Roman Forum, however it is better to book tickets in advance otherwise you will stuck in long hours queue.

title: Exceptional
text: I loved how the building is keeping its greatness even if it's been built many years ago . I went there thr first after I dropping my luggage to the hotel . Although it was raining but I could not wait to see it . Here are some photos that I took myself .

title: Must see
text: A must see when in Rome. It gives a true perspective of what ancient Rome was like. It’s very crowded but when you go early it’s not that bad.

title: Tour guide scamsters.
text: Long queues in cold weather. Tour guides trying to con you into buying tours or skip the line tickets. The lines are exceptionally long and there is a clear lack of organization. The employees have no empathy and clearly lack experience dealing with large crowds. We felt like animals being horded into the arena. Whilst the Colosseum is impressive, the experience is dampened by the over crowding and poor organization.

title: You must visit it
text: You can see it from he outside too, but if you are there, you should go inside. The line is long, but it's fast. The atmosphere is great, just like in a movie.

title: Magnificent plus wheelchair accessible
text: Not only magnificent but also disabled friendly for such an old monument! The lines are indeed horrible, however people with disabilities skip the line and don t pay tickets. There is also an elevator to the upper floor and wheelchair accessible.

title: A Must see
text: Very crowded but a must see when you are in Rome.  Our guide was excellent went on a very wet day but it didn't spoil the visit

title: Iconic
text: We visited the Colosseum on a guided tour that entered through the Gladiator Gate.  The Colosseum is simply spectacular as an iconic building.  The guide pointed out details we would have missed if we had toured on our own, such as the Roman numerals marking the entrances.  I was glad we were able to be on the floor and get the view looking up into the stands.  Not to be missed when in Rome!!

title: Gladiator arena!
text: When you look at this structure and knowing that it was built almost 2000 years ago, you will begin to wonder the technological skills of the Romans. Just stand there and let your mind wander the Colosseum and the blood that has been shed by the many gladiators and Christians who have shed their blood in that arena. Roman blood sport, something the modern world would never want to see. I am happy it is in our history.

title: Stunning - awful queues
text:
Firstly the Colosseum is a stunning place to visit. 
The history eeks out of every piece of masonry and just leaves you breathless. 

There are plenty of artefacts to see as well as the stunning structure itself. 

No wonder it is one of the “7 modern wonders of the world” 

The downside - we booked tickets when we bought the Paletine hills tickets. 
We were given a time slot to enter the Colosseum at 11am, advised you get there 10 minutes before hand. 
Well after 45 minutes we eventually entered. 
Unless you get there an hour earlier than your slot, or 1st thing in the morning (if no pre-booked tickets) I don’t know what the solution is. 

Apart from the queues it is definitely a “Wonder”

title: Of course a must see
text: Despite the crowds this is still an awe inspiring place to visit. We took a "skip the line" tour and were very glad we did otherwise would have likely needed at least another couple of hours to get through all the lines and crowds.

title: Rome=Colosseum
text: Very cool place, we visited in the end of December, to get inside wait line was over 3 hours, so it’s better to get a tour for specific time. Very old and interesting place, a must in Rome!

title: Coloseum...Get into 100 AD to 500 AD
text: Mega amphitheatre... were the ancient Roman's watched the brave games, ruthless attacks by animals... above all... fake floors were used, underground cages for animals and slaves... elevators to bring those carnivorous animals to floor to attack the gladiators. The public arena to exhibit the king's power and also to create a fear what happens if someone go against the emperor.  One of the ancient megastructure... must visit by historical fans...

title: Go on Last Sunday of Month, When Entry Is Free
text: Or go when you can arrange to see special areas, which are closed that day. If you study the construction and siting (by the Forum), you have to be amazed. The gift shop is well worth a stop, especially for presents.

title: Beware Ticket touts
text: Lovely place to go and see but all the ticket touts spoil it. If you don’t pre-book a tour (very expensive) just stand in the queue early in the morning get there before 9 am about an hour and you’ll see the lot for €16

title: Amazingly big !
text: I went on a Sunday my last day in Rome . And I could discover this part of the Antique Rome History with a few surprises . I could admire the landscape of the Colisseum . And come back through the Roman forum to my hotel walking as the part of the city was closed to cars .

title: Beautiful
text: The best attraction of Rome. The ticket is not so expensive. There are a lot of people but I think it’s normal.

title: So much history!
text: Guess what, it's very busy, but well worth a full tour, from the bottom to the very top! Devote several hours to fully appreciate all the Colosseum has to offer.

title: Must visit but average
text:
It is a must visit in Roma. I feel bad that too many maintenance work and it required you to queue up over 2hrs for ticket buying. 

I think the queue up problem needs to be solved

I like this ancient building much

title: One of the must see sites in Rome
text:
We booked the skip the line tour and underground tour with Viator and whilst these are not the cheapest, circa £100 per person, they are good value. 
Firstly slight negative, when book your ticket, you are given a time and meeting point from where your tour will depart from, unfortunately a lot of the other tours depart from the same place so it can be a little confusing finding your tour guide! Also the local hawkers know this and it feels like you are constantly hassled to buy their tat!
Like all tourist attractions in Rome it is busy, even with the “ small group” guided tour, that said you really should book a guided tour to get the most from your visit. Our tour guide was extremely knowledgable and there was a lot of information departed over the three hours plus tour. The tour we chose included the underground part of the Colosseum which was fascinating and for this part of the tour we had a different guide who specialised in this particular part of the Colosseum. There are plenty of opportunities to take photographs and ask questions and both of our guides spoke very good English.

title: Beat the lines and visit for free
text: We visited colosseum on the first Sunday of the month, which is the free visit. IMPORTANT!!! DON'T queue for the tickets at the colosseum as the lines even in the morning were insane - easely 2h+ in the line. We came in the morning around 10 (which we thought already was late-ish) and got our tickets from the entrance at the Roman Forum, where there literally was no queue at the ticket booth. Another option would be getting the tickets at the entrance of the Palatine hill just after the arch of Constantine. The ticket encompasses Colosseum, Palatine hill and Roman forum. Once you get the ticket you go past the huge line at the colosseum, where people queue for the same ticket and go straight to security control. This was the place where we had to wait the longest - around 5-10 minutes... General advice - if you enjoy history then waiting in the queue for around 1h is worth it. If more - probably not, as I think it's most impressive from the outside. After the colosseum visit, we managed to get in palatine hill again past the queues with the same tickets. On regular days just book your tickets online in advance.

title: Awesome
text: My daughter and I had the privilege of visiting the Colosseum 2 weeks ago. Absolutely awesome. We had the guided tour, with the best tour guide. The Colosseum is beautiful during the day and at night it becomes even more spectacular. So thankful to have had this awesome experience.

title: Interesting
text:
Some of the Colosseum is original and the other parts have been recreated. There is also that big chunk that is completely missing. 

There are parts that you can't actually access and some of it is covered so you can see what is down the bottom. However they have opened up sections. 

I really didn't expect the location, when I see photos it looks like it alone somewhere with only a few things around it, but it is actually in the city and it is crowded all around.

title: Incredible architecture and history
text: Yes, the lines are unbelievable, but they are worth it to view this amazing structure. I have nothing to add that others haven't already said, only to encourage you to see it for yourself. Make sure you get tickets ahead of time ('skip the line") or you may not get in when you go.

title: Colosseum - symbol of Rome
text:
Some tips: it is worth to book in advance via Internet. You can book directly on the homepage of Colosseum for 16 EUR (October 2019) or a guided tour for 20 EUR. The guided tour takes 45 minutes and they tell a lot of interesting things about the building and the battles, the life. After the tour you have the opportunity to walk around the building and look everything individually. 
If you do not book in advance there might be a very long queue, it might happen that you need to wait more hours on the sun. So I do not recommend this.

Once you have the booking you need to change it into an entry card. You can do it inside of Colosseum but it is better to do it outside - there is a small ticket office (not directly near the building) where was no line, it took 3 minutes to get my ticket. Inside it would have taken more time.

title: Absolutely stunning day
text: Me and my partner loved our day at the coliseum just gutted about the little information portrayed about Wednesday being free entry so was disheartening to here we could of go in for free

title: You don't have to visit it
text:
Old , and very Iconic place in Rome you don't have to go inside very busy most of the time ,
Very close to the Train station

title: Come here with a guide, it's really helpful and informative!
text:
One of the wonders that you just have to see it for your own eyes. 
It's so grand and the architecture is simple amazing! 
it can get tiring with quite some steps to climb.
We were there with a personal tour guide who did a wonderful job giving us information about the history of the Colosseum, we learn so much from the trip!

title: Historic
text: Beautiful and history at your fingertips...see it at night as well! Day and night are so different. We loved the walk all around the grounds to see the angle of the Colossus

title: Absolutely fell in love with this place. A must see.
text: I loved this place it was beautiful so much history. It a definite must see and I highly recommend doing an organised tour with a guide. You get queue jump. Security is strict so only small bags allowed.

title: Amazing
text:
Get the earliest possible entry ticket for the Colosseum.  Lines were manageable before 0900 hrs.  We passed the Colosseum at about noon and the lines circled the venue.  

The Colosseum itself is amazing.  You can stand there and image the history that passed through.  We took the basic entry and walked around for about 30 minutes.  There is a small gift store in the Colosseum for trinkets.

title: I’ve seen better...
text:
When in Rome you must see the Colosseum, is what I thought. Well... maybe I’m spoilt but I’ve seen much better archeological sites, in Turkey (Epheses), Iran (Persepolis) and even in Morocco. The thing here is: they’ve stripped the whole site from all its features. There are no reliefs, no mosaics left. There’s only stone left.
The scale though is impressive, and so are the views.
If you haven’t seen any Greek or Roman sites you will enjoy it; if you have, reconsider going.
If you go, be sure to buy a ticket online. You will have to spend some time queuing anyhow but far less if you’ve got a reservation.
The audio tour is allright. A visit won’t take longer than an hour.

title: AMAZING
text: Amazing is the only description possible for this global icon. Wanted to see this place all of my life and finally made and not an ounce of disappointment. Stunning!

title: The number 1 thing to see and do in Rome.
text: Best day in Rome was coming here. So much history and so many stories attached to the place. Yes, get ready for the crowds but it's worth it and if you get there early all the better. Loved it!

title: JUST ROME
text: The first thing that comes to mind when someone says Rome is the Colosseum of course without a doubt. Unfortunately, it was undergoing some repairs while we were there but something about Italian architecture that never makes you tired of the sites even if you see them over and over and over again. No need of any marketing for this world wonder.

title: Loved it
text:
I had waited 50 odd years to see this and generally when you wait so long the anticipation builds so high that when you get there you are disappointed . 
Not with amazing structure we booked a skip the line tour which is a must if you want to enjoy the complete experience 
I cannot recommend this highly enough go soak it in and enjoy it is all that you think and more

title: Well worth the visit
text: We went in December, You will always get queues but in December it was very easy to get in. You have a time ticket and they hold you to those times. Security like an airport with bags checked in the x ray machine, belt and any objects in a box. Lift inside to the second floor for the aged and disabled. Very friendly staff and spend as long as you like once in. Lots of steep steps inside, small museum explains the history very interesting. You can walk down to the arena area but we did not pay to go to the lower areas. Guided tours sounded good but expensive. Watch out for dubious characters just hanging around outside. Cafe outside across the road is very expensive, but if you go to the supermarket just round the corner you can buy an extensive range of food at normal prices, drinks, pizza, fruit, milk. Everything that you would expect to get from a supermarket. All these items can be taken into the Colosseum.  Audio headsets can be hired but expect a big queue for these. Toilets inside the Colosseum but the ladies queue is long, gents is fine. The ticket you buy includes palatine hill a must to visit, and allow a good two hours as their is plenty to see and look out for the Parrots on top of the hill. I did not know that there were parrots there but apparently there are two types in Rome. Metro really close to the Colosseum, worth the day ticket price to allow you to get around Rome easily. Six euros if I remember correctly. How could you visit Rome and not go to the coliseum

title: Dont rush in!
text: Ok it is a very large construction with a lot of history but i dont think worth's the money to go inside. the surroundings of the Colosseum are very beautiful. Walk up the road behind it and visit two very small but very very beautiful churches.

title: Scenery
text: A kind of visit not to be missed when you are in Rome, special light effects at dawn and an organized crowd

title: Colosseum by night was a great experience
text: Nice view, forget about crowds, beautiful and peaceful place. I totally recommend you to book this visit they are just a few months of the year. You can book though internet BUT be careful. The real cost is abut 20 euro per person. I was about to pat in another website that is your first option in google for 50 euro per person!!!! Better to check  :)

title: The outside is more interesting than the inside
text: Not much to see inside and you can actually see most of what you get to see fro outside but since it's a combined ticket to the Roman Forum it was worth it. The Roman Forum was more interesting and loads more to see there.

title: Stunning views of Colosseum at night
text: Walking outside the Colosseum and nearby parks and gardens.  Lots of churches to visit within 15 to 30 minutes walk.

title: Amazing
text:
Having walked round the outside we wondered if it would be worthwhile going in. So glad we chose to enter. Thought it was magnificent. To think such a structure was built so long ago and so much of it still standing.
We benefited too from free admission and were only too glad there was an elevator to take us from 1st to 2nd level

title: Great experience
text: I was thrilled we went, but so glad I'd done much due diligence. Getting tickets in advance to avoid lines.

title: Still have to see it...
text:
Regardless of conditions, make sure to fit this into your itinerary!

I've been here when there's no lines and just recently where we waited in que for 2 hours.  Chalked it up to being the day after Christmas.  Either way it's a must see.

Be leery of the middle easterners  selling their wares and "skip the line" promos.

title: A must when in rome
text: My 2nd visit .love history n this is something u can't miss when in Rome. Tickets getting expensive but I suppose it's needed for maintenance of such a amazing structure. Purchase tickets b4 hand if want to avoid the big line. Theres free entry the ist Sunday of every month.ticket includes  entry to palatine hill n Roman forum.

title: Fabulous historical ampithetre
text:
we had a great tour in the Colosseum, the day we visited it turned out to be a free day admission :) 
Lots of fascinating history if you're into that sort of thing.
The only downside to this and the other major attractions are the street hawkers 'giving away' their bracelets - then harass you for euro - take my advice and do what i did - buy one for 50 cents and after  that, every time a 'brother' tells you he loves your shoes or some other crap opening line, i took the bracelet out and said 'hey brother i love your shoes!' ..  off they go to find another mug to buy their crap.

title: Awe inspiring!
text: Being able to actually stand where emperors stood and gladiators fought was a moment for a lifetime.  There was construction going on and you weren't able to go to certain areas.  However, the many displays around the interior of the Colosseum were very informative.  Study up on the history of the Colosseum before visiting and it will have more meaning.

title: Colosseum tour
text: We took a guided tour of the Colosseo which was very good. I don’t think we would have appreciated some of the history without the help of our guide. I highly recommend visiting the Colosseo and take a guided tour to really learn more about the history - well worth it!

title: No guide pre booked tickets
text: It was fine to wander around in our own time without a group or guide. If I came back I might book a guide. I am not sure if I would book the Roman forum again but if you’re only coming once you may as well. Definitely needs a day to explore and enjoy the sights, we left half a day and it was not enough. Well worth a visit. Wear comfortable shoes and bring drinks/snacks.

title: The Colosseum was amazing
text: I’d always wanted to visit this icon and finally got the chance so I grabbed it. I did a tour with an English speaking guide which also included Palatine Hill and the Forum. I’d highly recommend doing these with a guide so you not only miss the que but get the most out of their knowledge.

title: Over crowed
text:
great to see the history.
The government needs to move on the street sellers as they let the area down

title: Best!
text: One of best tourist spots in the world! Im so proud ive been here. You can feel the ancient aura once youre inside the colosseum.

title: Colosseum
text: A definite must. Touch the stone, hear the sounds, feel the excitement! A journey through time. The museum is also very interesting. Enjoy

title: Love it
text: Wish i had more time to stay in this lovely place. It was my first but i plan to go there again in next summer

title: Visit to Colosseum in Rome
text: Great day with family - tried to do all the highlights of Rome in a short time and pre booked the tickets for the Colosseum -  spectacular building

title: Amazing, but also not as you might expect.
text: Don’t get me wrong... the colosseum is an incredibly well maintained sight rich with historic significance and tales of what we call ‘modern civilisation’ but... it’s just a great big area full of tourists that looks better from the outside. Damn, I can’t believe I just said that.. I mean, the reality is that you’ll still go and you might feel the same way, but you have to tick it off your bucket list. Make sure you know a lot before you go - there are info boards, but they really don’t give much insight into a place that is holds 2000 years of history.

title: Family vacation
text:
OMG. No words. Worth the flight from Australia. 
The history is amazing and what’s remaining from earthquakes and time is magnificent

title: A beautiful heritage site
text:
A beautiful heritage site. 
Huge in size, very impressive.
 There is a very long queue to enter, To avoid it, you may want to buy a ticket in advance or stick to a tour guide. 
Notice that outside the building there are crooks who want to sell goods, stay away from them. 

You may want to read before, or while searching for interesting historical information on the web.

title: Incredible
text: This is such an incredible place in world history, and it's on so many people's bucket lists that it gets so crowded so quickly. Getting tickets online will save a lot of time in line. Or, hiring a private tour company to take you through.

title: Great Experience
text: We originally had a pretty expensive prepaid tour, but our flight was delayed a day and we missed it. We were pretty bummed but decided to schedule an “on your own” tour. We used a popular guidebook with audio, and had a great visit to the Colosseum. You really don’t need to pay for a personal guide.

title: Highlights of Ancient Rome in some 100 minutes
text: The route of the tour in Kolosseum, in Forum Romanum, as well as in Capitol Hill was excellent. The guide made extraordinary efforts to accommodate our wishes. A little bit more historic background information would have been helpful.

title: One of the modern wonders of the world
text: The visit to the Coloseseum is a must while in Rome. Purchase the tickets in advance to avoid the lines an take an audio tour to grasp its full wonder but beware of guided tours that offer much and deliver very little in terms of information and knowledge. Combine the visit with the Palatine Hill and get the audio tour to grasp its extend because otherwise you'll see only rubble.

title: The belvedere
text: Buy a tour to the upper level and the undergrounds, it is very worthy. We had a great time and the guides were super good

title: Great tour guide
text: A great visit to the colosseum. We booked in advance a tour which was useful as waiting outside the colosseum you get harassed by venders trying to sell you bracelets and sunglasses. This is a bit of an intimidating experience for tourists. The guide inside the colosseum was really knowledgeable and made the experience

title: Amazing
text: Does what it says on the tin. Queues are long though unless you get a priority ticket. Walk up to it is amazing

title: Cheapest underground and tier 3 tour
text:
We visited the colosseum yesterday and it was fascinating. We wanted to do the underground tour (which can only be done with a tour guide) but didn’t want to break the bank doing it. Most advertisements are from third party operators and easily in the $100/pp range. 

I came across the official site of the colosseum and not sure if it’s recent but they have started doing tours of the underground. You can sign up directly on the site and it’s only 31 euros per person and 38 if you do the 3rd tier. We only got tickets for both and glad we did since you get to see parts of the structure that no one else can. And the views from top are fantastic. 

The entrance for this tour is on the opposite side of the main entrance so have to walk around. Security check and entrance took less than 5 mins. Must show ID to get in so bring your passports. And because of the price I thought it was going to be an audio tour and was pleasantly surprised when an archaeologist greeted us as our tour guide. 

The ticket also includes the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill and is valid for 2 consecutive days so if you run out of time, can visit the next day. We did the 9am tour to the Colosseum so had time to do all. 

After the tour, take time exploring the artifacts in the museum area. There are some very interesting things there.

title: Explore italy tours👍🏻
text: We had a tour with exploreitalytours we had the no waiting(queues) underground all areas if you get a normal ticket you only get to go round the 3rd level I think but ours was everywhere and really was good our 1st guide was amazing but cant remember her name,once we were done in the Colosseum our next guide Alex took us into the palatine hill and forum he was great too really informative we came away feeling like we had really learnt something, so overall really pleased we did the tour as we was a bit reluctant at first theres a lot of people in rome that want to sell you something and we was offered a tour this way it was only when we were taken to the office we could believe we wasnt be scammed .

title: Wheelchair accessible, a great day out.
text: Fantastic wheelchair accessibility. I went with my friend in a wheelchair and she was able to see everything and was treated with respect. She got to have two people go in for free with her and loved seeing the colosseum. Lots of good information and a nice little book shop. Would be good if there were some seats to stop and sit on or somewhere I could have stopped to feed my baby.

title: Amazing piece of history!
text: The colosseum is even more impressive in person than one would imagine from history books.  The structure itself is incredible to see. We were lucky to see it as part of a private tour through Jewish Roma. Our guide Sylvie was outstanding.  Definitely buy tickets in advance and if you have mobility limitations, note that there is an elevator but there’s still a lot of walking.

title: Fantastic Value For Money
text:
Booked on Colosseum ticket website a skip the queue guided tour of colosseum palatine 
 hill and the roman forum

title: Must be visited
text:
Great place, i recommend a skip ticket to avoid waiting 1h or 3h outside.
The visit is 2h-3h, many explanations inside the Colisée.

title: Inspired by history

title: NO WONDER IT IS RECOGNISED THE WORLD OVER
text: Get there early to avoid the crowds.  Better still, get a 'Jump the Line' ticket, but beware who you buy from.  Avoid 'My Roman Tour' business.  They are disorganised and a rip-off.  Be warned.

title: Amazing
text: This was a fantastic place to visit and my first time I would recommend buying tickets from touts outsiders with fast pass as ques can be nearly two hours long just to get through security then onto ticket office part. Lovely piece of history and out tour guide was lovely as well

title: Go back to Roman Entertainment Glory

Imagine the games and battles - executions and shocking treatment of animal and man as you walk the stones and see the areas where these events took place. Recommend hiring a guide to learn insights on this and other Roman venues. I really enjoyed!

title: Midnight photos
text: My husband and I decided to explore around the Colosseum just after midnight in order to avoid the crowds and get some great photos by full moon. The next day, we got our tickets and went inside. The amount of people is intense and personal space is not a thing there. We also chose a day that rained in the hopes that less people would be there, which worked out quite well for the outdoor portion. This is my favorite attraction in the world.

title: Coliseum and it’s golden ages.
text: We took a guided tour to Coliseum. It was great because our guide Nicola from City wonders made us travel centuries ago until the golden ages of the amphitheater!

title: Incredible
text:
My wife and I visited the colosseum in Jan 2020, we didn’t book tickets online and arrived shortly after opening and managed to walk straight in! The colosseum was waaaay bigger than we had anticipated and there was so much information on all the activities that occurred at this historic place. 
We did not pay for a guided tour but instead decided to go at our own pace which was (in my opinion) much better than walking around in a small crowd.

title: Italian beauty
text: This is a must see when visiting room, I recommend a guided tour as the information gives a great insight to the history, also pay for que jump it saves a mountain of time. Look at the beauty with your eyes and not through the camera of a smartphone. Amazing

title: Worth the Visit!
text:
When visiting Rome, the colosseum was one of the things I wanted to see, however initially didn't feel bothered about going inside as it's so nice to see, and the queues were huge.
However, we stopped off for lunch and on the way back came across a little stall which was selling tickets for the whole of ancient rome at a very reasonable price. After a bit of convincing we decided to go, with these tickets we got to skip ALL them queues and it was so worth going in to see!!! I am so glad we came across these people, because the inside is just amazing.
Although it seems busy on the outside the colosseum is so big crowds do soon split up.
You will NOT regret going inside, I highly recommend a visit!!
Ancient Rome was 100% my favourite day in Italy.

title: Oh you should see the colloseum!
text:
If you aren't smart enough to know alot about history or you ain't great at the arts or architecture you probs love the film gladiator though right? Who doesn't?!
If theres one thing to do in rome it's to go and see the 2000 year old colloseum and learn more about it. 
Highly recommend spending the money and doing a 3hour tour of the colloseum palantine hill and roman forum. You can pay for these tours online. We did ours through I ❤rome // get your guide and was well worth the extra money! 

Dont pay extra for the arena floor. Or upper tear you can see it all through the standard  levels!

title: AMAZING!
text: Our recent trip to the Colosseum was fabulous. We had a short wait before we entered and then had plenty of time to look around. There is so much to see and so much history to learn about. We were a party of 6 and we all loved th experience.

title: Amazing!!!!
text: Amazing experience but you need to make sure you use the right tour. Go underground!! If you can get a guided tour the archeologists they provide are great at brining it to life! It is cold down in the underground so bring something warmer.

title: Amazing tour, to see what we've seen in movies for years! Unreal, and the views around the area are fantastic
text: It was surreal to be in this place, don't miss it along with The Roman Forum right nearby. A piece of history no one should miss when in Rome.

title: Wow just wow
text:
Not really one for culture but this has definitely given me food for thought. The vast scale of what was achieved all those years ago is mind blowing. 
Worth a visit for anyone.

title: Private Guided tour
text: Happy we scheduled a tour, we got there early and still had a short line once we got to show tickets we were sent to left and bypassed other lines.  Guide took us to area with No crowd she was able to talk and point things out 10 to 15 minutes before the people started coming.  She than directed us to another area with only a few people.  Lots of walking and steps but great tour.  Numerous areas of Colosseum are under restoration.  Lots of history which the guide was able to share.

title: So stunning, it never bore me.
text:
This ruins, the history of it, it never fail to amaze me.
It is so beautiful, that it was not enough to see it just once - even if it's only for looking at the structure from outside.
Don't forget to buy your ticket in advance to avoid the long queue :)
I think it's also necessary to have lunch at the restaurant or cafe that offer the view of the Colosseum, you wont regret it.

title: History!!
text:
Everything in Rome is history but do t waste the opportunity to take your teens there and tell them about the history. They will engage and the visit will be unforgettable.
Definitely a must to visit in life!!

title: worth visiting
text: Glad I bought my ticket weeks before I visited the ruins and you should too! It was so quick to go inside...all i can say about the ruins is WOW! Its a good idea to join a tour to see more and have a better look..but if you are not planning to stay longer than an hour its okay not to join a tour...Gracie Roma! ❤️🙏🏻

title: Wow
text: Have to say this is an awesome sight, the history it holds is madness. My advise to anyone going to pay the extra 7 euro for skip the queue, if not you could be waiting hours. We had a tour guide called Roberto lovely Italian man with perfect English and is very passionate about his tour. He gives a great in depth story of the happenings at the Colosseum. The Colosseum itself leaves you in awe at its height and structures. I hope people enjoy it as much as I did, please pay the extra to skip the queue it's worth it. Oh and a bit of advice that I wish I was given, when the Africans who are selling stuff come over to you DO NOT take anything off them even if they say it's a present, as they will follow you looking for money, there is security guards who try and stop it but basically they can do what they want.

title: A must see when you are in Rome
text: The colosseum is a must see when you are in Rome. Its so much a part of Roman history and the walk up to the top of the 3rd level has a lot of great information about how the arena was used and other interesting facts from the era. You should always buy your tickets beforehand so you can skip a lot of the lines. Be aware that kids get into a lot of these types of places (museums etc) in Italy but you will still need to get them a ticket on the day, which can slow you down a little when gaining entry. Also, be wary of the hawkers wandering around outside. They were not too aggressive but some took quite a few "no, gracie" before they would leave us alone.

title: An awe inspiring edifice. This is a testament to imaginative we can be.
text:
This is a must see before one passes. It was part of an Italian tour. 

Just to see the structure and the remnants, it’s incredible to behold. The Romans were imaginative and innovative to construct such an edifice. 

The pathways to and from the colosseum are over 3k years old, that’s hard to wrap my head around when I haven’t even lived for a half a decade, let alone a century to chip away at the millennials of time this site has been standing for, it’s fantastic to feel so awe struck. 

It’s such a great site that when I came back with my aunt and cousins, we had to see it again. It’s a site I see every time I am in Rome.

title: Great tour but expensive
text: After booking with a tour company we thought we had nailed it ,we paid €48 for a guided tour and then got asked for another €20 when we got there for the headset and ear piece or we could just follow them into the Colosseum to avoid the queue. The place it’s self is amazing but for €68 we should have at least had some sort of brochures or at least a hand out. Be careful when booking with tours as it was €16 to get in, Big difference in price.

title: Breath taking
text: It was a surreal experience so beautiful and breath taking. The downside was the ticket vendors that constantly harass you non stop to buy a skip the line ticket or a group experience etc. Dont get skip the line because although it may seem like a long line, the queue go down really quickly.

title: Must tour the inside

title: Italy 🇮🇹 Tour

title: Beautiful

We bought our tickets online which were only £23 for the colloseum and the roman forum, i would highly recommend doing this as the queues for people purchasing tickets whilst there was ridiculous.

title: Colosso

title: Rome Fórum& Colosseum

title: Majestic

title: Exceeded expectations

title: Winter visit to Colosseum

Not worth the money in my opinion, best bit is normal entry and so much cheaper (EUR 16?). Tour guide was boring, stern and difficult to understand. Then our meeting point 3 was hidden through a closed off entry!

title: Colosseum

title: IMPRESS

 Of withstanding that it’s a great place to visit.   Despite the damage over the years it’s so majestic, and really feels like your seeing something special. I would recommend

text: It’s a shame there are so many pushy sellers of souvenirs and wanting to be your paid guide, precook a guide.

title: A walk through Roman history
text:
Came here with a local tour guide as part of a small tour group. Just incredible how this structure has endured for almost a couple thousand of years, and the stories of how it was used for staged naval battles, gladitorial fights to the death, executions; an ancient symbol of the perennial "bread and circuses" meant to appease and distract the masses -- something still relevant and ongoing today.

"The idea that people can be pacified by food and entertainment when they should be rallying to their prescribed civic duties isn't a new one. In fact, the concept was first described in ancient times by the satirical Roman poet Juvenal, who penned the Latin term panem et circenses, which means "bread and circuses." Don't miss this vital part of human history.

"Two things only the people anxiously desire -- bread and circuses," he wrote, lamenting the failure of citizens to take action as the democratic Roman Republic fell and the heavy-handed Roman Empire began.

Within a mere 100 years, Rome underwent massive governmental changes. What in 133 B.C.E. was a free republic that relied on a voting populace and an assembly system morphed into an embittered autocracy by the first century C.E. [source: Beard].

Thus, Juvenal's term, "bread and circuses" went viral, used by scores of people -- then and now -- to describe people who voluntarily trade their democratic freedoms in exchange for stable-yet-controlling government."

title: magnificent
text: what a place! what a feeling! amazing place, just think about what is passed by this place. goosebumps.

title: Amazing
text: We booked the Colosseum on their official website in a combined ticket with Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. You have to choose the time you wish to visit the Colosseum. We chose 11am thinking we could do the Forum, then Colosseum and then Palatine Hill. Not so. Forum and Palatine Hill are the ONE entry ticket so we only had about an hour in the Forum before having to leave to go to the Colosseum. Upon leaving I asked if we could get back in later, but the attendant said no. Only allowed to enter once. Big disappointment. The Colosseum itself was truely amazing including the museum inside. You could spend at least 2 hours or more there. After leaving we went back to the Forum ticket office where we first went in and they remembered us. I explained we had to leave early and they let us back in on the same ticket, so we did Palantine Hill after all. I wouldn't count on being able to do that again so I strongly advise that it's best to do Colosseum early in the day then both Forum and Hill after lunch as you need at least 3 if not 4 hours to see just those two. Or do Forum and Hill first and Colosseum at around 3pm. The cost for all 3 was only E18. Very reasonable.

title: if you like history
text: a must do in Rome in if you like history. book a quide and learn so much more. skip the line is necessary

title: A must see

title: Amazing!
text:
There is no doubt the Colosseum is just amazing! We went inside, the queues were large and very disorganised but somehow we got in very quick after an initial 20 min wait. 
It was utterly sweltering heat though and there is no cover in there obviously so between the overcrowding and the heat, my 9 year old just wanted to hurry on out of there but he managed. 
Our guide was knowledgeable alright but his italian accent was so thick that my son hadn't a notion what he was saying🤭
Shout out to the lads selling ice cold bottles of water everywhere we went while in Rome. Would have had heatstroke otherwise!! 
I suppose the Colosseum is worth the price tag to see it once but really the best parts are outside and the surroundings. We visited here as part of our hop on/hop off tour.

title: Amazing experience! Backpack rules for anyone wondering
text:
We booked a tour through Crown Tours, once approached by a man near the colosseum. Was a good option as the que was huge as it was around 12 o’clock. We skipped the que and went straight in! Very knowledgeable tour guides! 

I only took a small handbag as me and my boyfriend were worried as the rule for backpacks was hard to find out what sizes were allowed. The security never scanned or checked my bag as it was small, people with backpacks (mini to standard size backpacks) had their bags scanned. title: Went to see the Carthage exhibition
text: I wanted to visit here to view the Carthage exhibition which is held in both the Colosseum and outdoors where the temples are. It was quite confusing to find the right place as none of the ticket sellers seem to know the best place to go first. The queue was shorter for the colosseum so we went there first. The exhibition is excellent with artefacts from all over the Punic empire. We didn’t manage to see the outdoor installations as the heavens opened and it poured down. That couldn’t be helped of course. The colosseum is a very interesting place to visit and they are reconstructing part of the floor. There is airport style security as at all major attractions these days but once you’re in you are free to roam. Really good gift shop.

title: Colosseum and the Vatican
text: Colosseum is really great and worth seeing. Vatican is a nice place to walk. The paintings of Angelo are absolutely great! You definitely have to see the catagombs in which the Christians escaped from their followers.

title: Well worth seeing - make time for it and get a guide!
text: This was incredible, I got the guided tour of this, the Palatino and the Roman Forum, it cost €31 and lasted a lil over 3 hours. I would say get a guide, you won't be able to make the most of it if you don't, it's well worth paying the extra €15 (it's 16 for just entry). title: Educational Experience
text: Wow! First, if you book your ticket in advance, go early because it can be very confusing getting through the crowds and finding where your tour starts.  We had an amazing guide who was passionate about history, spoke English, and made the history of the place come alive for us. You definitely should bring food/snacks and water. You pay almost double the price if you want to sit down at a restaurant.

title: Great historical spot
text: Great historical structure, picture spot. A must to see during visit to Rome. Should plan ahead during busy season.

title: A gladiatorial experience
text: My wife and I purchased online tickets for the 2.45.pm English speaking tour of the colosseum’s underground, arena and upper levels. Our information told us to be there in good time so that we could clear security. We duly arrived 15 minutes before the advertised tour time and we were given a labelled sticker indicating our tour for 3.00pm and we were told to return to the Stern entrance for 2.50pm. When we returned we were asked to type in our names and d.o.b onto a tablet. At 3.00pm our tour group of approximately 20 people were scanned by security and taken inside the fenced outer area of the colosseum. We were then met by our guide who gave us a set of ear plugs and a mobile microphone which enabled us to clearly hear his commentary. Our tour began by walking through the eastern gate tunnel leading out onto the partly covered arena floor. After this we were led down the stairs into the underground passages, A lift was available for easier accessibility. Our guide explained the backstage story for this incredible amphitheatre and you almost felt the presence of the gladiators and animals who entertained the 50,000 spectators. Following our tour of the underground passages we were led upstairs to the upper levels where you had emperor like view of the oval shaped arena. . The tour was money well spent and it also included free entrance to the Forum and Palestine hill which we took advantage during the morning before our afternoon tour of the Colosseum.

title: Time history
text: Once in a life time walk thru the colosseum. It was amazing! Skip the line is worth every penny. And make sure to walk all around.

title: Colosseum review - prices, experiences and Roman Forum/Palatine Hill
text: Great experience at the Colosseum. It’s right outside the metro station. They are currently building a new metro line outside the station, however this does not detract from the experience. You simply walk around the billboards and head towards the tickets. The price for a visit to the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine hill is €16 for adults (18 and over I believe). This lasts 24 hours and is valid for only 1 entry into each attraction. There is an option to include entry over 2 consecutive days and includes the underground of the Colosseum and ‘SUPER’ site (not too sure what this is) and that is €22. What is not advertised in the price is the 18-25 EU citizen discount. The entrance to the three attractions for this is €2!! This is what I got. It’s not advertised so just ask at the ticket desk and make sure you have some form of ID card. If you haven’t bought a ticket, go directly to the colosseum as you buy them inside, don’t get drawn in by anyone trying to sell you tickets. We went on 23/01/20 and arrived around 9:15am and there was no queue for tickets and there was only a couple of people in front of us at the security check. We left the colosseum just before 11am. There was also a temporary exhibition inside. They are doing some work on the Colosseum so there is some scaffolding, but it was still great. Just be aware of the people outside the Colosseum trying to sell you selfie sticks, water bottles etc. I would say don’t bother pre-booking online and paying an extra fee and also we didn’t feel like a guided tour was needed as there was information inside the Colosseum. It was busier when we left the Colosseum but it didn’t appear to be overcrowded. We then went to the Roman Forum/Palatine Hill entrance, there was a queue which seemed long but we were at the security checkpoint after less than 15 minutes, the main reason for the queue is that you have to go through a security scanner and put your bags through too. In all it was less than a 20 minute wait. This was the line for tickets already bought (we bought ours at the Colosseum desk). The Forum and Palatine Hill is huge and has loads of information as you walk around. They have fountains to fill up water and walking to the top gives a lovely view of Rome. We did the Forum then went to the Hill. It’s a rather big site so give yourself 3-4 hours to cover the Colosseum, Forum and Hill. 100% recommend visiting here!

title: Sad to think of it's original purpose
text: I went with a guide and learned so much including why it is still standing. It was an architectural marvel with it elevators lifting the animals and humans up from the basement. 5000 animals and 2000 humans lost their lives in the "circus" arena in the first 100 days. Too much information.. I haven't even scratched the surface.

title: Trip to Eternal City
text: A must! Get a guided tour for both Colosseum and the Palatin Hill. Guides are charismatic and knowledgable. After the guided tour is complete, you can stay as much as you want exploring the PH on your own.

title: Plan ahead, pre-book your tickets, don't be foolish
text: If you want to get into the Colosseum do yourself a huge favor...plan ahead, buy on-line, get there early!! otherwise you're not getting in for hours/half your day will be gone -waiting in enormous lines or getting ripped off by scalpers offering "cut the lines" for dbl. the price.  And I can assure you most of these people are operating illegally w/no certifications or affiliations to legitimate tour companies.  I've done the big guided group tours during previous trips, wasn't my cup of tea. There's one person trying to educate 30+ people.  It's also chaotic trying to stay together intertwining with other large groups. Consider a private smaller group it may be worth the extra fees.  There's nothing like the Colosseum so if you've got one shot at going in during your trip...be smart...do your homework.

title: Impressive
text:
Great place to see. Lots of information. I understand all the scaffolding but I would of just like to of seen it as it should be.
Saying that it is still impressive.

title: Beutiful Roman Landmark
text: It was amazing to finally see the Colosseum. It was easy to access with a subway stop just nearby. Inside you could see the different seating areas and the maze of passageways underneath the floor, all while getting an in-depth history lesson from an audio guide.

title: Colosseum
text: Got fast entry which helped.  History and museum fascinating.  Sunny day and not too many tourists in January.  Highlight of my trip.

title: The Colosseum
text:
The colosseum 
 We went on Europe trip from Bangalore  during April-May 2019 with Chariot travels its duration being 18 days . What I enjoyed most is Rome, the eternal city. Being an erstwhile history student and an avid reader of Shakespeare ,the Roman ruins evoked a lot of  mixed feelings , be it awe, admiration or excitement!
We stayed in hotel Midas Roma , which is  located in a strategic position with very good services . It took  about half an hour to  visit the Colosseum, an amphitheater of ancient  Rome . We were accompanied by an English speaking guide who explained various aspects  of colosseum .The first thing that struck us  was its  antiquity and enormity . This amphitheater which  served as the major entertainment venue for public ,  evoked  many thoughts about the people of Rome in those days and how they enjoyed the barbaric games like gladiator contests, executions , animal hunts ....
Is it due to the fact that  as the Romans conquered  and expanded , they tried to emulate the culture of the lands like Greece and Etruria  or did they want to create fear in their enemies and criminals ? 
We marvelled at the engineering and technological skills of the Romans when we watched the 
arena with 36 trap doors and hypogeum with two level underground network of corridors...that stood the test of time  despite an earthquake and plundering by people.....a man made marvel.....a must for tourists visiting Europe ....

title: Weekend in Rome
text: We bought tickets online, we skip the line. The tickets are Also for the forum for the same price...

title: Great visit
text: The new présentation of the Colisseum is most rewarding. The explanations allow one to seize a better understanding of the unequalled monument and its historical role and evolution

title: Superb

title: Get a skip the line tour

This should really be called the Vespasian Amphitheatre, for example, and modern research doesn’t show that Christians were ever killed there. Genuinely fascinating and there are tons of interactive exhibitions on the higher floors. There’s a lift (not well publicised) if you have mobility issues. Allow several hours.

The views from the colosseum higher levels are great. We went for the earlier group meeting time around 9am / 9.30am, was glad we did too as by about 10am it was starting to fill with tourists.

title: An Amazing vacation
text: the Colosseum was the 3rd part of are tour,  Barbara C. are marvelous tour guide had many stories about the Colosseum,  from every different vantage point,  she would talk about  the fights between lions, tigers, rhino, and of course the gladiators,  the amount  of blood that was shed here over the years as many as 50,000 spectators watched and cheered,  Barbara tells us the people in the stands were seated by there position in society,  as everything we have seen, there is an overload of information,  and if your lucky enough to get Barbara all the better.

title: Timing is everything!!
text:
We booked a full experience ticket, guided tour in English, for 14.45pm.  The tour was late and we were then ushered out of the attraction by over zealous guards as it was half an hour to closing time. 16.30pm was closing time. These guards would not let my elderly and disabled mother use the lift.  She was forced to exit by the stairs. 

The extra money paid for the tour and to access downstairs was certainly not worth it, albeit not a great amount.  In hindsight we would have booked just normal entrance and walked around ourselves.  Although the underground does need a tour guide but does not really add to the visit.

title: my favorite monument!

On the downside the queues were very long and it took ages to get inside, even as part of a tour (well worth the €15 that we paid) the security seemed excessive and slowed it down a lot.

title: Astounding

We had the fast track and audio guide advertised by Tripadvisor and was poor, but the place itself made up for it. Get the 'beat the line' 16 Euro tickets. These give you access to all 3 sites and can easily fill a whole day. The Roman Forum was eerily quiet, very surreal considering how noisy the city is! Thoroughly recommend this if you go to Rome.

title: Amazing

title: Great landmark

title: Still standing

title: Nice place

Nice place to visit though and you can get some great photos.

title: Nice historic place

title: Very big difference since 2012
text:
End October 2019 - a tour with Musement - no guide, just quick access - booked through app recommended by hotel.

This was a half day tour of the colosseum, self guided, no headphones for a 1.5 hours. We met at the rear of the stadium at 10.40am, ticket cost me E29, but this included entry into the Palatine Hill / ruins too [see separate review]. It was like a skip the line ticket and granted us entry to the floor. The guy who met us had an orange t-shirt on. 
We had just over an hour to check out the Colosseum which for me was just right as the stairs are really hard to climb [steep]. There is a life for those who need help, but they will check this is true before giving you access. I was turned away. 

The floor section is amazing and you can see where they are excavating and restoring the gladiator sections etc. There are small displays with artefacts all around, which is new as I didn't see this in 2012. The gladiator tour was extra and wasn't running on the day I went [a Monday]. 

There are 2 shops on different levels and I advise going to both as they have different items to each other. *No climbing on the walls - they are very strict and like to blow whistles and run after people to tell them this. [Understandable].

There is a bit of a OH&S issue as there are a few entry points but only one exit point that we were shown. 

I recommend coming here at different times of the day to photograph it. ]

I liked that this was a self guided tour and I could read the signs myself - English and Italian. You may want something else and a longer tour including the gladiator section. Personal choice - but it was all I could get on the week I went and I was happy. 

*Be wary of the street buskers and gypsies trying to sell you crap. They are very persistent.

title: Don’t get the audio tour guide
text:
Obviously it’s a magnificent Roman building, so you can’t not visit when in Rome 

But it could be sooo much better organised. 

We paid for the audio tour and 3 of the 6 recordings were in closed off areas ! We searched and couldn’t find them. When we complained at the end the guy just said ‘ oh we didn’t know they were closed’ 

The lady in the ticket booth was lovely, but everybody else we spoke to was rude and unbelievably unhelpful. We felt like a huge inconvenience for them.

title: Visit Roma and Pope John pal the second.
text: It’s really nice place to visit and the city was very clean and tidy. We had a great time there. The weather was hot but the transportation was very nice. Places were clean too.

title: Queues were horrible, but once inside... WOW!
text:
Absolutely stunning! it's really quite something to stand there and imagine how it could have been (no mobile AR). Yeah unfortunately there are a LOT of tourists that are constantly competing for the best selfie spots, but with a little patience and ingenuity you can cherry pick some great picture spots too. The sad thing was, they get their picture but don't really spend the time actually "looking" with their eyes :( and just take it all in. (a lot of pushing near the closest spots to a big viewpoint)

Of course had to take a selfie too (sign of the times), but we spent most of our time trying to soak up everything.

The queuing to get in could have been handled much better as efficiency was lacking, considering the sheer number of people to process. Go EARLY! Even then the queues are huge, but at least the sun isn't quite as hot.

title: Interesting visit after an unpromising start
text:
We pre-booked tickets for this site. As it was our 1st day in Rome and the ticket machines were not working at our local Metro station, we were slightly late arriving for the allocated time. When we finally reached the correct entrance a very abrupt young man asked "Why are you late?" Obviously some customer care training would be useful! The process of obtaining tickets and headphones was laborious and needs speeding up. 
When we finally entered the exhibition and the Colosseum, things improved! The exhibition is a useful starting point. Obviously the Colosseum itself is amazing, an iconic structure. The process of building such a spohisticated structure at the time beggars belief. The history of the early usage is naturally quite brutal but neither of us was aware of the later use of the Colosseum for Christian purposes.
Worthwhile visit and some lovely views out too.

title: Impressive
text: Walking by this historic building, I was amazed by the engineering and craftmanship of ancient Rome. The size and precission considering the available tools is Impressive. This is another must-see in Rome.

title: Great family trip
text: Great family trip to Rome. We squeezed a lot in a week and saw so much. Great restaurants and great views!

title: The History!

title: Watch out for wrong info
text: So the three level tour was great and guide knowledgeable, views were beautiful.i knocked a star off for the staff, we were told to go to meeting point 3. 1 and 2 where easy to find..3 no where to be seen, when you ask staff they tell you to wait by meeting place 2.  Which is wrong.. we missed our tour. I managed to sort it out and got on another tour. But staff just did not seem to care. For those going meeting point three is behind a Corden off area with a rope you have to ask staff to let you through.  Signage is poor.

title: History

title: Must Do
text:
We did a tour that included the Underground, Arena and Upper Levels. Well worth it. The official Colosseum guide was excellent. 
The queues outside to get tickets, and then to enter was massive. We were so glad we booked through a company that organised it for us and we went straight in. Yes we paid extra for it but the guide around the Forum was excellent and overall much less stress for us. 
It was absolutely packed in the ground and general public areas, but it was so nice to go underground, on the arena and the upper levels where we had time and space to truly get to absorb the atmosphere and take in where you are.
Either way you really can’t go to Rome and not go into the Colosseum.

title: Amazing

title: A must see
text: It is a cleeche but a Colosseum is a must. Book tickets online to avoid the crowds. The tourist shop inside is quite nice.

title: A must see
text: Obviously the Colosseum is a top attraction in Rome, it truly is breathtaking. My only recommendation would be to book the ticket from the official site and not through a third party. I paid €22 for arena access (you get to stand on a platform at ground level, rather than up in the higher tiers) and the ticket price includes access to the Roman Forum, or you could pay €16 for general access and Roman Forum entry. I found some operators changing double or even triple the price, so save yourself some money and go through the right channels. Alternatively you can buy your ticket at the Roman Forum where the queue is considerably smaller and go straight to the Colosseum.

title: THE Colosseum
text: The Colosseum was bigger and more impressive than I expected. It was easy enough to get a ticket and no queue to get in. The toilets were disgusting, they need to be cleaned more regularly

title: Holiday Trip;
text: Wait to get into Colosseum is rather long, but well worth it.  It was rather cold as we went first thing in the morning.  A must see is you get to Rome

title: Iconic symbol of Imperial Rome!
text:
It is one of Rome's most popular tourist attractions and a symbol of the city. Construction began under the emperor Vespasian in AD 72 and was completed in AD 80 under his successor and heir, Titus. The Colosseum could hold an estimated 50,000 to 80,000 spectators at various points of its history over the centuries. It was used for entertainment till the early medieval era, 5th or 6th century.

Must see from inside at least once!

title: Spectacular and a must do when in Rome!
text:
Booked priority tickets online, using the GetyourGuide app.....very good app.
Couldn't believe there were queues in January...it must be choc-a-bloc in peak season!!!!!!
We bought a combination Ticket that included the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill.
It was a skip the line ticket....which I would recommend...we saved ourselves at least 20 minutes of a line/queue!!!
Try and get there early as there are good photo opportunities outside the Colosseum.
If doing the package (ie Colosseum, Hill & Forum), I would recommend bring a packed lunch, sandwiches, etc....it can be a long day.
Bring good walking shoes, like runners....no high heels!!!!

Give yourself at least 3 hours to do the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum combination. (Be prepared for a lot of walking!!!) Jan/Feb best time to go!!

title: In Awe of History
text: Our trip was done with a "Skip-the-line with hosted entry" ticket. We were early for our originally booked time at the Tourist Info Station, but the staff were kind and flexible enough to allow us to enter without having to waste time waiting. The activity started with a video to show us briefly the history of ancient Rome and the places we were going to visit. Thereafter, a staff led the way to the Roman Forum / Palatine Hill and gave clear instructions on the time we could visit the Colosseum after exploring the first two places. There was no explanation / talk along the way about anything as this was what we had booked anyway (just hosted entry, not guide). Great to have skip-the-line tickets although it is low season in winter. We were blessed to have great weather that day, but just wondering what would have happened if it was rainy etc - Would the staff leading the way provide us with umbrellas etc? In any case, the places left us in awe - it was just breath-taking to see so much history with your own eyes, even without a guide explaining. Wonderful experience!

title: Must see attraction in Rome

Our guide was enthusiastic and very informative and because we were only part of a small group,it felt more personal and we got to learn a lot more about the history of the place.Definitely book a guided tour as there is so much to learn you would never know anything by just looking around yourself.As we were part of a small group our guide was able to take us to the perfect spot in the Roman forum, to get a fab picture with the colleseum behind us.

Also just beware of all the scammers around the colleseum wanting to sell you random things,or take your photo.If you politely say no and walk away without making eye contact they will leave you alone.Unfortunately they are all over Rome in the tourist areas but just be smart,ignore them and walk away and you won't get scammed.

The lady at the casher desk NEVER told us that we must show up at the time which is on the ticket entrance !!! (11:20 for us) and it is so close to the credit card recipe, so we thought it was both the same time (or a small difference between the too clocks, (credit card machine and tickets printer)).

Rome is a great city to spend your time and visit, but if you are short of time, skip the inside visit of the colosseum. We should spend 32€, we paid 64€.

title: Wonderful Sight
text: Our first sight of the Colosseum was on our journey in by taxi to our apartment.  It is awe inspiring in its sheer size and the fact that it just sits in the middle of converging roads with cars, taxis, scooters and motorcycles skirting around it, beeping their horns on their way to work or just getting to their various destinations.  Outside is impressive but inside it is trully wondrous.  You walk along the various paths that Roman gladiators, soldiers Christians and others would have trod and you eventually arrive to look out over the arena where many fought and lost their lives.  On the lower levels there are screens which display events surrounding the Colosseum.  Here you can sit and rest before taking the steps up to a viewing area which directly overlooks the arena.  Much of the original seating is still in place, although the arena itself has mostly disappeared with only the supporting columns still standing.  As you walk around the upper levels of the building, there are various displays where you will be able to see things such as the pulley system used to lift animals such as lions into the arena where poor unfortunates would have been waiting to fight for their lives.  The stairs that you need to climb are many and are not easy to negotiate.  However, there is a lift which is available for those who are elderly or infirm which I would strongly recommend using.  The Colosseum is both wondrous and hellish in equal measure.  It is truly amazing to stand and look at the vast seating area and to envisage the thousands of people who must have sat there baying for the blood of the poor souls due to fight for their lives and yet it is a magnificent piece of architecture and one I am truly grateful to be able to say I have seen and stood in the footsteps of those who have gone before.

title: Buy tickets beforehand.
text:
I paid €20 per person for admission to the Roman Forum & the Colosseum. Bought the admission ticket online prior to arriving which also allowed a queue jump. 
**Do not buy the ticket from an English/ tourist website. I looked online in English & for €30 inc. VAT per pax, my partner is italian & he found then for €20 inc. VAT**
The ticket office (for the one we bought online) was just opposite the entrance of the colosseum, took less than 10 minutes to get that. 
Bought the audio guide from inside the colosseum for €6. It was basic information it it was still interesting.   
Disappointing we couldn't go downstairs into the underground/ basement level due to maintenance. 
Very busy, lots of people so you need to be patient. 
Honestly, looks better from the outside than on the inside but I found the history & information fascinating.

title: Roman Requirement
text:
I don't think it would be wise to skip the Colosseum when in Rome. Truthfully, you probably won't be blown away by the interior, but it's important to understand the uniqueness of the stadium and the Palentine Hill and Roman Forum are both extremely historic and beautiful. But if you choose not to go inside, the least you can do is walk around the area as it does offer a lot of nice views.

A few tips for other readers, if you go early enough, especially on a weekday, you won't need to book in advance. I actually showed up without a ticket and was able to get in no problem. The queue of people right at open was so small and they actually have two lines - one for prepaid and another for walk-up. The walk-up line had only six people in it haha and is located on the inner circle/right line, you'll understand what I mean when you arrive, so if you do go at open, you have a very good chance of getting in,but this was on a Tuesday in January. Also, do not bring a selfie stick and do not buy one from the people selling outside.

title: Bucket list trip
text: We pre purchased the skip the lines ticket for this visit and are really glad we did.  We visited on a Friday and the line was easily a 1/2 mile long.  The colosseum is beautiful with a lot of history.  The downside to this visit are the vendors walking around constantly trying to sell things.  They will do their best to force a bracelet on you as a “gift” and then demand money.  If you say “no” they will follow you.  One grabbed my sons arm and we had to physically pull my son away from him.  I wish the police could get a better handle on these activities.

title: A must do - self guided tour
text:
BUY TICKETS BEFORE YOU GO! We decided to do a self guided tour & we booked through one of the sites paying £22pp for entrance to colosseum, forum & palatine hill. 

It is manic, it is one of the biggest attractions in Rome however the queue for pre-bought tickets flew. Defo buy tickets and print off at home before otherwise the queue is horrendous!

Not much to read so doing the self guided tour was more to visit and see it for ourselves. We researched and learnt everything ourselves after!

title: Very beautiful and great history
text: We went to Colosseum because it was on our to do list. Is was very beautiful and a great experience. It is very big with a great history. A guide is necessary.

title: I guess you have to do it when you're in Rome...
text:
... of course you do, and you would regret it if you hadn't, but be aware it is really badly organised, so don't do it on yourself, take a guided tour with skip the line. If you don't you will have to queue for an hour minimum due to security checks, then you can buy a ticket for Forum Romanum und Colosseum, which means after you visited the Colosseum you leave it, go to the Forum Romanum directly next door and spend another hour or more for the next security check. No skip the line here without guide though.

TIP: make sure you have no bigger bags with you as they'll just send you away after queuing for hours. There is no place to store your luggage !!!

SKIP THE LINE: you can book skip the line tickets even with Google, but there are certain entry time so make sure you do that on advance.

title: The Sight Of The Colosseum Can Only Be Described As Spectacular and Striking
text:
Construction of the Colosseum began during 7 AD under Emperor Vespasian but it was in 80 AD that the Colosseum was inaugurated by Emperor Titus. It was then a further two years for the Colosseum to finish work that allowed the 50,000 visitors into the largest Roman amphitheatre. What’s more interesting is how the structure of the Colosseum was divided in relation to social class - people who were closest to the arena were those of higher class and had a greater rank in society. At the very bottom of the Colosseum seats were reserved for Emperors, Vestal Virgins and those forming the Roman Government then the next tier found those of noble class or knights to find their seats. The third tier was then reserved for Roman citizens and soldiers, this tier was even split into two sections for those who were wealthy and for those who weren’t. The fourth and final tier were reserved for slaves and women, to make matters even worse, some groups were banned such as actors and gravediggers. Most importantly the best seat in the house belonged to the emperor who sat in The Emperor’s Box, who paid for these games which added to happiness towards them to avoid dislike. Everyone had a ticket for their specific seat and tier which represented their social class and status, allowing 50,000 people to access the Colosseum at one time. 

To get into the Colosseum visitors would’ve used the 80 entrance arches, within a matter of minutes everyone would’ve found their seat and be reminded of their place in society. It’s incredibly surprising to see such an organised system so advanced for its time yet the real organisation comes in the underground complex made where animals were caged and the stage sets prepared. Sand was put over a wooden floor to prevent contestants from slipping and also soak up spilled blood whereas trapdoors allowed for animals to be hoisted into the area by lifts. One of the longest killing sprees was seen under Emperor Trajan who held a 117 day killing spree which involved 9000 gladiators and 10,000 animals. When two gladiators were fighting, it was seen as a privilege to die in the arena in front of such a crowd but this was a rarity. Emperors spared gladiators where most deaths were caused by injury from fighting and blood loss except for occasions where cowardice was seen which both enraged the emperor and the audience. 

Astonishingly, by 40 CE the Colosseum had been abandoned and in replacement shops and houses were built within the structure. It was then used as a fortress against invaders and more stone was pilfered from the Colosseum for numerous projects across Rome. Luckily the Roman Colosseum has been part of an extensive restoration which took place during recent years to both replace the arena’s floor and remove grime caused by traffic pollution. This restoration didn’t come cheap nor easy as it cost €25 million which was funded by Diego Della Valle, who’s a shoe-and-luxury goods maker: Tod’s. The generosity came after the need to help care, protect and preserve Italy’s art and architecture, not to mention the numerous archaeological treasures across Rome.
