title: BETWEEN 18-25 CLARIFIED
text:
Just writing this review to clarify that if you are BETWEEN 18-25, you are also allowed to use the reduced ticket if you are 25.

I called and asked since this was not clear to me and the reviews on here had mixed answers

title: Valentines rugby visit to Rome
text: Great tour, well worth the price.  Very helpful and well informed guide, ensuring all guests needs were met.  Awesome place

title: Enjoyed the rugby and took in a bit of the tourist nonsense but not too much.
text: Seriously I wouldn't bother too much. Place is overrun with ticket touts, pushy sales people, queues, zillions of tourists and piles of litter. Most of Rome is ruins, so just wander round a bit, you can see plenty from the streets for free. Then avoid the tourists hotspots and take in some tucked away squares and back street trattorias and get into some conversations with locals. Generally very friendly. Then that's that done.

title: Morning Visit
text:
Brilliant place to visit.We had lovely weather,take your time to walk around.
Can get very busy try to start early.Lovely in good weather.

title: Great tour of the coliseum
text: Excellent knowledgeable guide which gave you a new perspective of what you were looking at throughout the coliseum

title: Should have been 10 in the group - ended up at 19!
text:
This could and should have got a five star review - the one reason it did not is the original blurb quite clearly stated no more than 10 in the group. We started with 12 of us plus the guide but on entering the Colosseum we were joined by another 7 so 19 in total. 

The guides were fantastic and getting to go into the depths of The Colosseum was an experience - shame that we paid for a small intimate group and ended with a crowd - a bit of a rip off really.

title: Very nice visita
text: We had an excellent tour with tour guide Elisa from “petites promenades” - we all enjoyed the history of such an impressive place. Two parents and 4 teenagers 😊

title: Good,  but would have helped to know how long tickets take to come through before paying
text: Didn't know until booked and paid for that tickets might take 30 minutes to come through. Had a slight panic as entry time was in 10 minutes. Tickets did come through, just, but would have booked a later time if I'd have known. Apart from that, all good

title: Guided tour
text: Great guide in Laura Lye and all well organised and getting access quickly was a real benefit. Covid security good and well managed

title: Culture and history!
text:
To much people around it! however this place is extraordinary, great history and culture.
We took a tour to explore each part of this incredible place and learn about the ancient culture.

title: Wonderful historic experience
text: The Colosseum is a very interesting and inspiring experience.  It's a great place to visit in February.

title: The one and only
text: It is a experience to have. The Colosseum is an amazing place itself but is surrounded by street vendors and travel agents.

title: Iconic
text: We went with a tour company and had access to the sub-floor level and could see all the 'workings' of this vast place.  Then a view from the top.  A must do

title: Spoiled with ticket sellers and tour companies
text: Unbelievable structure, unfortunately the experience is a bit tarnished with all the tours and ticket sellers outside of it trying to coax you in making it very unattractive. I would advise anyone to book a ticket in advance online (if able to find where to go).

title: A must visit on each trip
text: I visit the Colosseum each time I am in Roma. I took the tour of the interior four or five visits ago. It has so much history and was wonderful to tour inside, but in subsequent trips, I've just visited for the beautiful views of the exterior and walk around for a short time. This is definitely my most highly recommended site. History and beauty!

title: It was more than awesome, I'd recommend it to anybody looking for convenience on a low budget. It's the best deal!
text: The fact that I managed to get the tickets the same day in the last minute! I was afraid the Colosseum officials wouldn't recognize the tickets but they did! Thanks to the tickets we managed to cut through the line and headed straight in!

title: Amazing
text:
Vistited on our first trip to Rome. Booked online on the official website. Full experience so included the Roman Forum etc.
Just amazing to be there. Being right there where so much has happened in history. What an incredible structure and lots of information around the place to increase your knowledge.
Really wwll planned out and the Roman forum was unreal too. Some amazing temples (you’ll see…). It’s been said many times before so if you haven’t been…go!

title: My new favourite place.
text: This place is just incredible, I could have spent at least the whole day there. I wish we had used a guide though to get more out of it. Watch out for the rogue traders out the front, buy online or from the official place only.

title: Absolutely Dictatorship
text: Purchased my ticket for 23rd and although it says, valid for 24 hours from first time use, I wasn't allowed to enter at first. That their website doesn't permit date and time changes, based on availability is also quite insane. Truth to be told, I had to do a PCR test and this was confirmed after the ticket has been booked. Member of staff asked me to book it again, and pay again. I moved my PCR test and entered, but this is rather unacceptable for those with unforseen circumstances during COVID-19 pandemics.

title: Magnificent place
text: A must see in Rome, experience the arena floor and get a picture and feel as to what happened years ago

title: visit to colosseum
text:
one of the most remarkable sites of the antic roma
you have to see it to undertand the size of the roman fights between men and animals or slaves vs slaves

title: Quintessential Colosseum Tour!
text: Nicola was an amazing guide! Even as a history teacher, the colosseum and Roman forum would have been pointless to visit without a guide as fun and informative as he was!

title: Underground Roman Coliseum
text: I have been in the coliseum several times but this time I took the underground small tour. It was excellent. The guide had a PhD in archeology so her insight on the history and findings of this site made a difference on my experience. I will definitely recommend this tour.

title: Best of Rome
text: The Colosseum, one of the 7 wonders of the ancient moon, is today a symbol of the eternal city of Rome. To understand the constructive performance of this magnificent monument I suggest an experienced guide.

title: Wonderful great experience worth having a tour guide
text: Fabulous experience loved every second tour guide was amazing very knowledgeable  one of the top 10 things to do in Rome

title: HeadOut made it amazing
text: This was my first time using HeadOut. I was nervous but it worked perfect. Enjoyed history with a lesson! We skipped the lines, got an audiobook with the tickets and learned so much. It really made our experience special.

title: A must visit every visit to Rome!
text: Spend the money for a quality tour and explore this amazing piece of history in Rome! Doesn't matter how many times you have  toured the Colosseum, there is always something new to discover in this magnificent piece of ancient history!

title: Poor organisation Collosseum
text:
Pff what a poor organisation around the collosseum. Hundreds of people asking  ;do you have a ticket?? 
Just get some ticketoffices around the stadium and let the police get rid of all these idiots!

title: Great!!
text:
Great place for the ones that enjoy history... The full experience worth every cent...
Lots of information and beautiful art to contemplate...

title: The colosseum
text:
Overall the colosseum and the forum were my favourite places to visit in Rome, and even if you're not a history buff like me, it is definitely a staple. One cannot go to Rome without visiting the colosseum.

I went with my mom, and we did the full experience, a guided tour of the entire colosseum, underground, arena floor and the exhibition, and it lasted between 1h-2h give or take.
The tour guide explained everything about the history and architecture without making it boring, and overall enjoyed very much the visit.

title: this is ROMA
text:
fantastic place in the heart of the eternal city don't need to write any particular thingh about it
just go and visit the place

title: Excellent experience, complete and fun!
text: The tour guide was really nice and knew a lot of information. She dominated the language and made us feel comfortable and secure, giving us time to admire the view and even take pictures. The price was totally worth it because it was super complete.

title: Each arch has a story to tell
text:
Our guide told us that the Coliseum was built on the dried up private lake of the pagan emperor.
Each gate had a Roman numeral and inside seating was allowed for the privileged and the rest of the spectators would stand up

title: Wonderful
text: Defo worth a visit; there are lots of displays with info.  We paid to go in the arena too, which was lovely.  I do think it's a bit price heavy given we paid €48 for 2 (€22+€2 booking fee), but if the money goes to the upkeep, it's well worth it. This needs preserving it is amazing

title: Coliseum
text: The knowledge of the guide was impressive. He answered all questions and  made sure all members of the group were together.

title: Rome
text:
Italy Rome and the most iconic structure, the Colosseum , filled with History. 
Beautiful experience. 
Totally recommend 😍

title: Great trip. Handy ticket order system
text:
The visit was great with lots to see and information given by the guides 
The website for booking the tickets was really easy , simple to use and fast to release the tickets.  It was followed up with what’s app instructions 
I would definitely recommend

title: Amazing tour experience
text: Me And my brother had an amazing tour of the collisum. Our tour guide Giulia Paglari  who us an archeologist herself gave a wealth of information and  was patient with the group while we asked questions.   The 6 person group was very intimate and provided  great 1 on 1 experience.  Highly recommend them

title: The Grandeur That Was/still IS!
text:
Millions of photos are probably taken everyday in this site— and it is definitely understandable!

Every angle and corners here does not just boast of beauty but also of tremendous history! I myself, probably took more than a hundred pictures but it’s still not enough to capture the majestic image of the colosseum (in and out).

The tourist-guided trip was totally worth it! You pretty much just pay for the entrance fees.

One has to see this place to understand the grandeur that was (and still is )Rome!

title: Cool but a couple drawbacks
text: It’s the Colosseum, so it’s hard to not be impressed by it. Really cool experience to walk around in it. The only draw back was how crowded it was when it was not peak season. Can’t imagine what it’s like during the summer. The directions to the entrance weren’t clear either, which was frustrating. The address sent to me was for a place across the street. Just follow the crowd around the colosseum and you’ll find it. Glad I went.

title: Outstanding
text: Ivana, our guide was amazing!  She never drew breath the whole trip, her history knowledge was incredible, best guide I ever knew.

title: A glimpse into the past at the Colosseum
text: The Colosseum was truly remarkable.  I loved walking the arena paths and imagining life during Roman times.  We visited during February and the crowds weren’t too bad.  Artifacts from the time were on display and the columns were huge.

title: The most amazing Tour Guide in Rome!
text: I Love the guide! She was amazing! Her name is Laura Antonucci, I highly recommend her as a tour guide, she knows everything about Roma and Colsseum!

title: Amazing
text: It's all been said before - a must see place.  Unfortunately the hassle of having to book on-line and the endless checks due to their covid rules made it such a hassle.

title: Stunning
text: Did as part of a tour in order to skip the lines, but I wish we had done this again alone so we could have been less rushed to explore parts of the museum bits that we simply rushed by. We didn't get to go underground and again, I regret not booking that part of the trip - but alas, it's something we will do the next time we revisit!

title: Nothing Like The Colosseum
text: 1 of the 7 wonders of the world, your breath will be taken away as you enter in and see what you have only seen on screens or in books. It's simply amazing. We have been to Rome twice in the last 5 years and both times we carved out space to visit these grounds and were amazed once again.

title: Beautiful and interesting tour to Palatine Hills, Forum Romano and the Colosseum
text: We bought tickets Skip the line  Colosseum, Forum Romanum and Palatine Hills through Get your guide and Enjoy Rome.  It was perfectly arranged. Our guide Federico el arqueólogo was maybe the best part of all. A lot of information, with a humorous touch. He knows a lot about ancient times and knows to translate that to the modern times. The perfect way to learn and not forget. World leaders should listens to and learn from him, the world would look a lot nicer if they would! Thank you Federico for the beautiful tour and the information you shared. Book through Enjoy Rome and ask for the English guide Federico.  Highly recommendable.

title: Amazing experience
text: This was an amazing experience and a must do while travelling to Rome, we were in an organised tour where we had head phones and the tour guide had a microphone and she explained everything to us while walking, I would not of enjoyed this as much If I wasn't in an organised tour and just went around alone. outside the Colosseum is a lovely little coffee/snack restaurant where we had coffee before we went in it was lovely and fantastic service. I would recommend this visit its very interesting and spectacular photos.

title: Great historic experience
text: Our tour guide - Francesco was amazing. Very animated story teller and very informative. He held the attention of our 9 and 11 year olds as well! Highly recommend this tour, its a great window into the history of the area!

title: Not to be missed
text: Must see when in Rome. We went in March temps in the 16-18 degrees which made it alot easier when walking around. The site is huge and I could not recomend enough talk a guided tour to make sure you get a full history , most tours will mix and match a few sites together and also skip the queues which is a huge bonus.

title: 1000 Years & counting
text: An absolute must visit place. History lessons in front of your eyes. Pure delight , thinking it was made 1000 years + before. Just wow.

title: Head Out are rip-offs
text:
This is a warning to AVOID THE COMPANY HEAD OUT when buying tickets to the Colosseo. They come to the top of Google searches - and you pay more than the set price, with a promise to 'Skip' the queue. But there are hardly any queues in Covid times. And the extra charge is exorbitant.

Another thing - the audio tour promised as part of the package never came through, and I ended up using the one provided by the Colosseo. 

The Colosseo is an incredible place, and a must-see, but go in the morning so you have time to see Palatine Hill and the Fori Romani as well because these are also must-sees, and you cannot buy a ticket separately. And do NOT book with Head Out. Tickets should only be €16. Be sure to go directly to the link for Parco Colosseo. 

Head Out also promise a discount on your next trip with them - but this never materialised. Go to The Vatican Museums to see my review of what happened with them there. Truly professional scammers. Very sophisticated - a different website for each monument, in multiple languages.

title: One of the wonders... and you won't wonder why
text:
Really interesting to wander around one of the man made wonders of the world.  Very very helpful staff, not too crowded, sensible numbers allowed in at any one time.  Amazing to see the sunset over the ruined tops of the building.
Highly recommend

title: Bit disappointed
text: I was looking forward to this visit but he sheer volume of people at the site was almost overwhelming which couple with pandemic and security measure made the entry very protracted. The building itself is spectacular - but visit off peak !

title: A MUST BUT....
text: It can't get more accolades but the site is best seen from the outside. Even in the off season and with Covid, the place is utterly mobbed. If you have limited time in Rome, see it just from the outside.

title: Colesseum WOW
text:
What can I say everything I thought it would be and more 

Buy your tickets before you visit so much easier but there are plenty of ticket sites available 

Need a good couple of hours to look around probably then take a walk over the road to the forum and palatine hill

title: Manic!
text: A must see in Rome and last time we visited was great however…..too many tour groups and too many guides sending you this way and that, The Colosseum itself is amazing but the experience is baffling, the guides send you through the bookshop presumably to spend more money? My own personal experience was ruined by a very rude and aggressive tall guy at the bottom of the elevators. Shouting “wait!”, rolling his eyes and then adding “I am talking to you”….he also directed us out of The Colosseum when we in fact had more to see. Avoid the elevator and this guy….

title: Immense structure. So much history
text: The Colosseum is the most immense structure in Rome. The history behind it is so interesting and gives you an insight into life in Rome at those times. I would highly recommend the tour as the info given is so interesting and informative. Well worth the visit.

title: Easy discovery
text:
It was easy to find and pay. With this site I got mine discount for 18-25 years European citizens. Other websites does not show the possibility.
I was able to enjoy my travel easily.

title: Must-do site obviously, but let me longing for more info.
text: It's a really impressive site.  I   was struck at how big it is.  I've heard other people say the opposite.  Covid-19 lockdown rules  impaired the experience for sure, but even so, as with the Roman Forum, I   would think more signs and information would be warranted.  We thought we had a tour, but it was unguided access to various areas, not including the underground parts.  I  recommend having a guide in your own language, and probably a couple of YouTube videos in advance.

title: An Absolute Must-See
text: You cannot come to Rome without enjoying all that the Colosseum has to offer. One of the star attractions and an absolute must see on any tourist's list. Being so popular, as expected it can be busy much of the time but the control of tourists was great, we went midmorning and seemed to be slightly less congested. Despite the large volume of people we were able to enjoy the tour and learn about the history behind the famous structure....as well as get some classic photos. Certainly one not to miss irrespective of it being your first or tenth visit to Rome, a joy to behold, you won't be disappointed at all.

title: Impressive meeting with ancient history
text: One of many "must see" places in Rome. Strongly advise to book tickets on internet. And be ready to spend at least half of day there, and pray for the good weather. Hardly not to be impressed.

title: Fantastic
text: The Colosseum is such an amazing part of Rome, and it lived up to our expectations! We bought our tickets online and got the full experience including arena. We tried for the underground tickets, but they were sold out for a month prior to our visit, and we didn't want to pay the inflated tour operator prices. It turned out that our experience was fantastic even without it. They give you a sticker to wear if you have arena access, so that you can move around freely. We encountered almost no lines, and our visit was timed for mid-afternoon. The place is so big that even though there were a lot of tourists there, they were very well spaced out. The signage was good and so interesting! The metro stop is right across the street, so this was an easy and impressive sight to see. Highly recommend!

title: Colosseum and Roman forum
text: All the process was very simple and we are very happy with the experience! Having the tickets prior to the entry was very helpful.

title: Great tour
text: Our guide was very knowledgeable, took her time to explain any questions, we loved every minute of the tour and her English was excellent much better than our Italian which amounts to grazi and il conto. We were very lucky as the sun was shining.  We also learned some facts such as lasagne meaning layer as in the layers of stone and who months of the year were named after plus much more.  Well worth the money

title: A lot of walking!
text: Well worth going as it is spectacular, but expect a lot of walking - it is a very a big place!  We combined with Roman Forum and Palatine Hill which was 3 hours on our feet.  In hindsight i'd probably have split them.

title: 2-day Colosseum Forum Palatine pass-buyer beware!
text:
The Colosseum, the Forum, and the Palatine Hill are remarkable sites in Rome and well worth your time and money.
That said, my wife and I bought 2-day "Full Experience" tickets including visits to the Colosseum, the Forum, and the Palatine. We visited the Colosseum and the Forum on the first day. 
We returned the second day to visit the Palatine Hill and  were astonished to be denied admission. We learned that our ticket covered ONE entrance to the Colosseum (which we knew) and ONE entrance to the COMBINED Forum and Palatine. The second day of our ticket was useless. Pleading ignorance and seeking accommodation for our misunderstanding with the person attending the gate was useless.
What this means is the only schedule for which a 2-day ticket makes sense is if you intend to visit the Colosseum on one day and the Forum and Palatine together on another.
Even the language on the Coopculture website is confusing. It reads "One entrance to the Colosseum..." (ok) and "An open entrance to the...Roman-Palatine Forum...". 
This is just outrageously stupid packaging by the management of the sites and pointlessly bureaucratic behavior by the gate attendant. Needless to say, we left that second day both disappointed and furious.

title: Awesome
text:
We bought skip the queue tickets from the Headout App online. 12 noon was the first slot available so we thought it was a good idea to do the Forum first.
Painless exercise. 👏👏👏👏
Does what it says on the tin. 👍👍👍

title: Stepping out of a Time Machine
text: I want to give a BIG Thank you, to our tour guide  * Maria Assunta * she gave her heart and soul, taking her time and explaining the History of The Roman Colosseum. A Very professional and knowable guide, you can definitely tell she loves her job. She answered everyone's questions, giving examples so you could digest the information more easily. I'm glad we had  * Maria Assunta * as our guide, what I experience wasn't a tour with a guide. It was a University level history class. Bravissimo ✌️😎

title: A wonderful three hour guided tour of this magnificent place
text:
We boked a guided tour a small group of people with an English speaking guide.
It was a three hour tour,plenty of walking, lots of steps and wonderful views.It was fascinating hearing about the history of the Roman Empire while actually standing in the middle of the Colosseum.

title: Great Day: but be forewarned about Full Experience (2-day) ticket purchases!
text:
My husband and I bought a Full Experience (2 day) pass for the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Day 1 we went to Colosseum in the morning (we spent about 2.5 hours, including a long line and slowdowns for ticket checks, security checks and vaccination checks) and Roman Forum (3 hours in the afternoon). We came back the next day to finish with the Palatine Hill and discovered that all tickets are for ONE entrance to the Colosseum (once you leave you can't return) and ONE entrance to the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill  (which are COMBINED into one visiting area.)  Unclear that the Forum and Palatine Hill are consider all one place, we returned to finish the Palatine Hill the following day. To our chagrin, we were told we had already used our entrance to the Forum the day before and were not permitted to re-enter. Pleading, explaining begging (in whatever broken Italian I could muster) did no good. They refused. Rules are rules. When we tried to purchase a 1-day ticket while we were there, we coudn't -- their website is a disaster for mobile phones. Every time we entered the info to book the entrance, the website took us back to the beginning. After 5 attempts, we gave up, incredibly frustrated. We’d never have bought a 2 day ticket had we understood the rules. If you're thinking of spreading out your visit over two days, much better to buy two 
1-day tickets. We’ll prob go back — but the lack of clarity -- and their refusal to allow for this misunderstanding, esp because we paid additonally for the ticket, was infuriating.  Hope this prevents others from making the same mistake we made.

title: Roman Coliseum Tickets
text: From start to finish our booking and our tour of the Coliseum was amazing. I'd recommend using Headout especially if you're unsure where to purchase your tickets.

title: Amazing!
text: If you go to Rome, you must visit coliseum, definitely! Take a guide, it ll be useful and instructive to better understand the construction, the architecture and the usage of this arena.

title: Humbling Experience
text: I visited the Colosseum during a business trip.  We took a guided walk around the city and to the Colosseum.  It was amazing to see and hear about the history of the area and the Colosseum itself.  It is definitely worth a visit, and the guided tour is highly recommended as we would never have discovered so much about the history and structure if we had not done this.

title: WOW just WOW
text:
We bought an online skip the line self guided tour as couldn't appear to find a self guided tour without the addition of an admission to Palatine Hill and Roman Forum. This is misleading as the skip the line entrance is only valid for the Colloseum itself. 
We walked passed the entrance to the Roman Forum and the queue was stretching all along the street and bearing in mind that this was March.  The grounds to the Roman Forum are immense and so are Palatine Hill so we didn't fit them in in 1 day.

The Colosseem itself was a sight to behold in the flesh. The vastness was truly overwhelming and well worth the visit. It is constantly being preserved so do expect scaffolding and the prescence of construction workers around the building.

We had to show our Covid passes prior to entry and bags were x-rayed so it felt a safe place to visit.

Our ticket didn't include access to the basement layer so again choose your ticket wisely depending on what kind of interest and individual need you have.

It did warn of steep steps and my oh my they were so steep so it would be advisable to take the lift if a member of the party had any knee or back complaint. 

Please be aware that there are personnel around the areas asking if you have tickets with id badges but the cynic in me wonders if some are con artists trying to make money so I would advise ththat tickets are pre-booked.

Like all tourist attractions there are people selling, scarves, selfie sticks and water so do be vigilant with your personal belongings.

Go and enjoy yourself you will not be disappointed.

title: Walking tour with Headout
text: The tour of the Coliseum (including underground) and the forum and Palantine Hill was fantastic, our tour guide Maya was brilliant, very knowledgable and answered all all questions, she even helped with recommendations for restaurants to get the the most authentic Roman pizza & pasta. Would highly recommend this tour to anyone, especially if you're lucky enough to get Maya.

title: HOW CAN YOU POSSIBLY 'RATE' THE COLOSSEUM?
text: But, you should know that you must book your tickets in advance if you want to get inside.  DO NOT wait in the mile long lineup to purchase a ticket.  ALSO, spring for the extra inside tour.

title: Walking through history
text:
Stunning, impressive ....
No words can discribe the feeling being inside an ancient bilding with such a dimension.
A must when you visit Rome

title: Truly Amazing
text: Just so beautiful! Amazing views and so well preserved. Lots of history to learn about inside. Lots of time to take photos and explore at your own pace. Queues went very quickly. Lots of staff about to guide you to the right queue.

title: Worth going
text:
We booked for the Colosseum/Arena and the Roman Forum on the official website. 22 euros for an adult and if your children are students and have their ID they can get in for 4 euros.

It is a fantastic site to see and you can easily spend a few hours there. Watch out for ticket touts and sites advertising skip the line tickets for +£45 per ticket and over as whilst the ques were busy, they went down quickly although not sure how would be in the height of Summer.

title: Rome Colosseum
text: This old colosseum was incredible.  Our tour guide shared such interesting information about the colosseum.  It was very impressive.

title: TOURIKS - FABY IS FABY-ULOUS!
text: Last time I was in Roma, we missed the undergound portion of the Colosseum. So this time we decided to splurge and get the tour plus go in a small group. Of all the things to see, the Colosseum and Forum provide such an exciting and historical context that captures the imagination of young and old who from all of the world to experience and see ancient Roma up close and personal. We lucked out with an incredible guide, Faby who is passionate and expressive about history and the different layers both physical and historical of Rome. Use Touriks and go for the small group if you prefer a more intimate experience. Because of how things are timed, groups are only allowed a certain amoutn of time. Also Faby told us that during COVID the restorers were able to come and clean parts of the underground of the Colosseum so that now you can walk all the way through as opposed to before when this wasn't possible. There is so much to learn and digest about the history of Rome and also how the Colosseum was built and the activities which gives you a window into the gladiator times with emperors and life as an ancient Roman. Check out Faby and TOURIKS!

title: Mankind history
text: It is amazing visiting one of the most important places of the mankind history, great opportunity for some education for the family, be aware of pickpockets.

title: Colloseum
text:
Beautiful place to visit at least once in a lifetime. The vibe there was so fresh and vibrant at the same time. 
And no matter how long is the queue. It usually is only a waiting time of under 9m

title: Arena tour of the Colosseum
text: Amazing experience.  Very knowledgeable tour guide.  Met up outside the Colosseum and walked in.  We didn’t pay for the underground tour but could see those areas very well from the arena level.

title: Koloseum, pýcha a pád.
text: Magnificent monument, must see when visiting Rome. Maybe it is worth it to book a tour guide for more entertaining experience.

title: Smashing bit of history
text: Really enjoyed this trip.We booked a tour with Crown tours and Robbie/Robin? Took us through the palatine hill and was very good,efficient and informative. We then had an underground tour of the coliseum which was informative and enjoyable.It is a busy attraction with all the street sellers etc but the attraction is a must see bucket list type thing so do your research, pick a good guide company and enjoy.

title: Great tour of Roman Colosseum
text: We had a wonderful tour of the Colosseum,  Palatine Hill and Roman Forum led by John Tinto of Through Eternity Tours.  John was very insightful,  entertaining and patient to answer all of our questions.   I highly recommend taking this tour with Through Eternity.

title: Beautifiul Rome
text: We’ve visited Colosseum in April and we’ve choose to buy tickets on Headout. The reservation was very easy and intuitive on the site. The acces at the Colosseum was fast indeed and everything went smooth. I totaly recommend using Headout for ticket reservation and visiting Rome

title: Worth the money!
text: I loved the fact that we started and finished at the same point (Colosseum) and that we got in both tours an overview of everything from above (so literally an overview) and we proceeded to go downstairs to appreciate it in more detail. I can't remember my guide's name but she was awesome too! Very engaged and fun, kept it interesting and it was almost perfectly historically accurate, so great job on that too!

title: Colosseum
text: Colosseum (Italian: Colosseo), also known as the Flavian Amphitheatre (Latin: Amphitheatrum Flavium; Italian: Anfiteatro Flavio), is an oval amphitheatre located in the city centre of Rome, the capital of Italy. Built with bricks covered with mortar and sand, and originally covered with travertine,[1] it is the largest amphitheatre ever built and is located east of the Roman Forum.

title: Wow!!!
text:
Fantastic experience, it’s a must see when in Rome. We organised our trip online before going, so it was easy. The guide was so helpful she had messaged my husband keeping him updated with the tour. We met her and she then told us where to go and what to do once we were in The colosseum. 
We didn’t have a tour so we were free to walk around which we enjoyed. We paid for The Arena which was a great atmosphere. It was inspirational, definitely recommend. Cristina was our guide and she was lovely.

title: When in rome....🇮🇹
text: An absolute must if in Rome. One of the 7 wonders. The name itself   is a review. I was blown away. Roman Forum also brilliant. Loved it!

title: Beautiful Clean Reasonably Priced
text:
Beautiful building, worth a visit, would advise getting a tour guide!
Not much information around, and no signs so struggled to get around.
Buy your tickets online before you go as you cannot get them there and the internet is really poor.
There are also time slots for visiting so booking advance is a good idea.

title: Inspiring History!
text:
As one of the new seven wonders of the world the mighty Colosseum had to be visited! Although I have visited many other Roman amphitheatres and sights such as El Jem and Leptis Magna the Colosseum really is on a different scale! 
A guided tour helps you avoid the queues and gives you a better insight. There is a lift for the less able.

title: Fab trip with family!
text: This trip was the reason we came to Italy, it all began with an 8yr old (having learnt about the Romans at school) saying he wanted to go to the colosseum. So we did. And it did not disappoint. He and we all really enjoyed the visit, having booked tickets through get you guide, we enjoyed a stress free visit, no guide as looked around at our own pace, all signs and information were written in English as well as Italian so very easy to navigate and learn.

title: Best Viewed from Outside
text: I've done the tour inside but I prefer to view it (for free) from the outside because it is so iconic.  There's not a bad picture to be had from outside.  It's just...collossal!

title: Rome Awesome
text: The best experience ever, Rome its a fantastic city to walk with some friends or family. Really its awesome everything there.

title: The Roman Guy's - Colosseum Underground Tour with Roman Forum and Palatine Hill with Serena
text: An outstanding tour of the underground Colosseum, Forum, and Palatine Hill with our guide Serena. Her shared knowledge and insight as an actual Roman archeologist is phenomenal and makes this tour so much more meaningful. Our second time visiting these ancient ruins, this time with our children(12, 16, & 17), and everyone truly enjoyed the experience and picture painted as we roamed about this magical place. The area is awe inspiring on it's own, but having someone as knowledgable as Serena guide you takes it to another level.

title: Fantastic, so happy I went
text: Just brilliant, everything I expected and more. Went on a tour, with Walks of Italy, guide was Gigi. He was great and made the experience even better.

title: An amazing and unforgettable experience
text:
Thank you so much for a wonderful experience. Our fantastic guide, Lia Caso, encouraged us to use our imagination and go back in time to fully appreciate the splendour of The Forum, Palentine Hill and Colesseum. With enthusiasm and good humour our family enjoyed every second of the tour; it was the highlight of our trip to Rome for our 14 year old daughter. Thank you so much for such a memorable afternoon. 
The Birley family, England

title: Great jaunt to Ancient Rome!
text:
Great tour with a great duration! 
Amazing guide in Barbara- so knowledgeable!
The crowds are horrendous but Barbara did her best to accommodate everyone!

title: Fantastic
text: Fantastic. A highlight of our trip to Rome. We visited with a tour company and skip the queue tickets - worth going with a guide who can unlock the history for you.

title: Worth a visit if you don't mind crowds
text: We booked our tickets a week in advance - I'd recommend booking earlier, to get better time options. We bought the "full experience" which included access to the arena floor - but signage wasn't great, so we missed the arena entrance and had to go round again when we finished, after asking staff. Ticket also included access to the Forum, which was good.

title: Excellent
text: Booked directly from Coopculture official site.  Found it easy to get in and little queue at 9.30 am.  It is impressive and well worth visiting inside.  We did the arena floor access and level 1 and 2.  The underground option sold out really quickly so wasn’t an option. I would say I think it worthwhile to do the arena floor if you can.

title: Get your tickets in advance!
text: There's an exhibition inside for you to recall the history of Colosseum if you might have forgotten the things you learned in high school. You can buy your tickets online and I recommend you do it.

title: Cancelled
text: Arrived this morning Colosseum only to be told there was no guide to give us the tour so we missed out on both tours with no guide . hoping now to get a full refund from TripAdvisor and the third party city tours.

title: Inside, out, & around the Colosseum
text: Dario was our amazing guide of the Colosseum and he was AMAZING! There were 6 people in our tour and Dario showed us all the levels of the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and the Roman Forum. He explained all the history of these areas and made our tour fascinating and imparted much knowledge to us. His passion was contagious.  Thank you Dario for a most memorable tour!

title: Great tour!
text: We had a great trip around the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Forum. Our guide, Linda, was informative and gave us good information about Roman Rome. Well worth the time and money.

title: Coloseum
text: No waiting in line. Great views. My family enjoy in Coloseum, Forum and Palatin. Free entrance for children up to 18.

title: Amazing
text: Its difficult to leave anything other than a 5 star review for what is a spectacular site.  We arrived slightly ahead of our arrival time but despite it seeming extremely busy were through security quickly and easily.  Once inside the tour groups can become tedious as they block the paths and make the place seem crowded, however once you break free of them you can wander around at your own pace and take it all in.

title: Amazing experience
text: Vasco the guide tour is fantastic! Is portuguese. We love the tour guide with with.. he told us a many things about Rome.. I recommend absolutly

title: Beautiful monument where video surveillance should be placed
text: We were a group of 10 people.we really had wonderful time in collosseo. It is really magnific. It tells so many different stories.i highly recommend the guided tours

title: Deep dive into the Roman empire!
text: Loved the experience - the tour guide was open to questions and explained in greater details about the history of the Colosseum and the surroundings. Totally recommended with Bibbo Tours.

title: Everything as expected
text:
HEAD OUT ONLINE RESERVATION 
Tickets Colosseum and Roman Forum with Multimedia Video
Everything was good! We went 45 minutes earlier at the touristation office to get our tickets , we watched the multimedia video which was very nice and we headed towards the colosseum where we waited no line . At the roman forum and the palatine hill we waited for 15 minutes which was bearable .
The disadvantage that there was no guide as mentioned in the site where we booked the tickets (they said there were not enough tourists waiting --- but there were approximately 30 tourists at that time) .
The advantage is that the entrance they told us had no queue.
On the whole , everything was as it was expected. ROME is the eternal city :-)

title: May the 4th not be with you (in Rome)
text:
Booked a tour package to see the Vatican and Colosseum in one day. We went to see the Vatican early in the morning and didn’t have any issues getting in. However, when we went to the Colosseum later that afternoon we were turned away at the gate because our tickets read the wrong date….a date that was a week past our departure from Rome. We made sure to book both tickets for the same day, as why would I would to waste 120€ to see the Colosseum AFTER we returned home. My friends had also double checked the date to confirm everything was in order. I emailed them and they refused to cancel, or even amend our tickets for the following day. They were completely apathetic to our situation. It’s a shame that a tour company who represents such a historic part of Roman history refuses to assist in situations such as this. Now these tickets will go to waste, as four less people will be able to see the Colosseum on May 4th at  2:45 PM. Look a little empty? It’s because we’re not there. 

Make sure you triple check everything before booking with this company.

title: Just Amazing
text: Awesome and the top must see sight in Rome, make the time to spend wandering around and just soak up the history.

title: A must see
text: Everything you expect, a must see. My tip is to go early as the crowds are vast and only increase as the day goes on. A guided tour is probably the best way to visit as they give you quicker access and information as you go around.

title: Colosseum - Fantastic trip
text: We went on the tour through Vatican Tours. It was a fantastic experience made better by our tour guide Dennis who was a lovely guy throughout and was incredibly enthusiastic and informative

title: Cannot miss
text: One of my favorite attractions in Rome.  The history is exciting, gory, and intricate.  I definitely recommend going with a guide to the underground portion!

title: Fascinating
text: The Colosseum has always been a must-visit  iconic Roman structure. Its history is fascinating  We also toured the Underground and Arena which I highly recommend.

title: A lot of people but still worth the visit
text: Well, what can you say. This is just a very impressive building! It is incredible that it is almost 2000 years old.

title: Coliseum, Capitol Hill and Roman Forum
text: Great way to see and understand some of the history for these 3 landmarks. David, our tour guide, was excellent, had a great sense of humour and was charismatic. I definitely recommend it!

title: Nice to see from the outside
text: My boyfriend and I came here on a massive 20km walking tour of Rome. We didn't go inside but it was nice seeing this spectacular structure from the outside. To be completely honest, it looked just the same as other collesseums I've seen in my time - this one just so happens to be the most famous of them.

title: A must do in Rome!
text: This facinating building must be seen and visited inside! However, make sure to book your ticket in advance as there a great lines starting a long way before they open in the morning..

title: A day at the Colosseum with Dennis as our guide
text: Dennis was a great guide with tons of information, pictures and stories. He is very passionate on the subject and seems to know his history well (not that I know much about Roman history). He was easy to follow in the crowd and he did a good job keeping and waiting for everyone.

title: Its an amazing place to visit but beware of scams : my advice is to not buy tickets from coopculture!
text:
It's indeed an amazing place to visit, but there are some things that you should be aware before buying tickets, specially if they are from the "official" ticket line Coopculture.... 
In few words, I payed for a lot and only was able to visit the Roman Forum and Palatino... I bought 5 tickets in the beginning of the month (so they knew that a Pope visit was about to happen) for my family with the full experience - including colosseum arena visit,  Roman Forum, Palatino super sites access for 2 days - in the end we only had access to the Forum and Palatino, without super sites because they were all closed! and the Colosseum?! Well the Pope was there in that day so they closed the entire area and the ticket was not valid to be used in other day o.O... I did what the guys at door told us and sent an e-mail to coopculture asking for refund and took more than 2 weeks to see a reply saying that they will not refund the visit to the Colosseum... This is a SCAM they sell tickets to dates that they know that people won’t be able to visit and then they invalid the ticket for the next days saying that you need to buy another one online and this will be refund!  
In the end the place is amazing but please talk to a turist guide and buy a ticket directly to them instead of  this organisation or you will lose money as we 😞

title: Stunning by day or night
text: Hi skipped doing a traditional tour and hired a scooter and scooted around the Colosseum at night. It was absolutely stunning and totally free. My favourite of all the monuments.

title: A brilliant day out
text: Great tour of the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill with Jessica. She was very knowledgeable and engaging about the history of Ancient Rome and pointed out things you wouldn’t have seen by yourself. We’d highly recommend booking!

title: Tour of the Colosseum, Forum, and Palatine Hill
text: Our tour with Jessica was brilliant! She led us through the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Forum, both guiding us on things to spot and recounting interesting facts and stories, as well as giving us ample time to roam freely and take in the surroundings. Highly recommend!

title: Smooth experience
text: Booked a ticket for the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill, everything was smooth and without delay. Weather was great, the site was interesting.

title: Guided walk of Colosseum
text: Great experience. Easy to find the place to meet and the rep was very knowledgeable. The radio and earpiece provided worked great also to allow you to take your own time in.and around the places.visited. Top guided trip and well recomended to try.

title: So much history
text: One of the best experiences from our trip to Rome. A visit to the Colosseum is a must when in Rome. I would definitely recommend a guided tour and booking tickets in advance. There is so much history here. No words or pictures can describe this experience. Our tour guide was knowledgeable and we learned a lot of new things about the place. We went on a Saturday afternoon so it was a bit crowded but it was manageable. We were told that in the mornings it is less crowded.

title: Incredible history
text: We used the Roman Guy Tours and Claudia was our guide.  She was absolutely fabulous and this was the best tour that we had of several during our time in Rome... Spend the extra $ and get the underground tour, Forum and Palatine hill with a great guide to make the most of this lifetime experience

title: Great experience and tour
text: Knowledgeable guide who answered all questions. Good pace, it was not rushed. Beautiful scenery. Paths can be quite rocky so a good pair of shoes is needed.

title: nice
text: nice to see all the thoughts that went into making the colosseum. we had a tour, not sure with wich company. i saw several documentaries before comming... so i was excited to see the lift... or the drainage for when arena was filled with water, or pipe lines for water... that was not included. so i am not sure if its the tourist company issue... or the open areas of the colosseum.

title: Colosseo
text: Truly great, took the underground tour, it's a must. It is amazing how the structure survived all these years, Tour guide was very knowledgeable, and explained all the details expertly. Hope to tour again someday.

title: Must-visit!
text: I have visited Rome three times, but each time it is a novel experience. The Colosseum is an attraction that delights, again and again, an authentic example of history and tradition!

title: Amazing and not to be missed
text: I didn't think I wanted to see the inside due to concerns about the crowds. I am so glad I booked a tour that started first thing in the morning. I learned so much. The tour included the Forum and the Palantine Hill and I highly recommend a combo tour of this nature.  Wear good, comfortable shoes.

title: History comes alive
text: We didn’t take the tour just walked around the outside and the area nearby.Definitely worth visiting and marveling at all the history that took place in this area.

title: Impressive, but do use a tour/ guide
text: Second visit after 6 years and this time we did the underground tour with a guide. Very difficult to get tickets but we got lucky. Getting the history and an explanation of what we were looking at made all the difference  enjoyment.

title: Not as expected
text:
When i arrived they mentioned that I should by my ticket online, the office ticket unfortunately can’t help you by buying the ticket even if you have problem in their website.

I tried to buy the ticket around 5 times and each time i got blank page when i chose pay button. I explained the issue to the guy in the gate and he directed me to the ticket office.
But the ticket officer provide me phone number that I should contact to solve the issue. 

For me it was wasting time, try to book your ticket before you come since there’s no technical support and the place so crowded  by the people.

title: Awesome day
text: Laura was excellent guide, she was very knowlegable and had an interesting way of telling the stories. She was very helpful, I would higlhy recommend her.

title: Early Tour of the Colosseum
text: We toured the colosseum with Walks of Italy, I would recommend touring with a tour guide and go early, the crowds in April are bad I can only imagine what it is like in high tourist season! Lots of walking and lots of steps, not good of you are not used to walking on uneven ground. Our tour guide was great and made sure we had breaks and were hydrated.

title: Just Awesome
text:
We really enjoyed the tour and visit to the great historic monument. It is a must visit.

Mistake - Biggest mistake we did was we did not book a guided tour and only booked "Skip the Queue" ticket. This was a mistake!! We had to buy an audio video device at the office for additional 6 euro and frankly it was not great...PLEASE do book your tour guide online before you visit...we definitely repented.

Go prepared to walk a lot...sunny outdoor...carry umbrella and water. It took us more than 2-3 hours to roam around at our pace and enjoy this place.

You can get the best view for your pics from the Roman Forum temple site towards the Colosseo.

title: Colosseum and Forum
text: Palatine hill and forum visit. Booked very last minute but allowed me to access Colosseum Palatine hill and Forum within a few hours of getting to Rome. Booking easy.

title: Spectacular, interesting but unhelpful staff
text:
I wasn't expected much visiting the Colosseum since I'd already been twice in the past, but frankly the place is still spectacular and the arena area (we had full experience tickets) really gives you a new look on the monument. Plus the exhibition on the first floor was more interesting that expected and provided useful explanations.
Unfortunately I can confirm from other reviews that the staff is rather unhelpful. Just as we came out, we realized that we had left our guidebook of Rome inside, the staff absolutely refused to let one of us back in to fetch it, or even to tell us where to find a "lost items" office. No big deal but it was frustrating.

title: My first European trip: An incredible virtual tour
text:
Today I was able to take a virtual tour of this historic monument, with the help of the ‘Google Arts & Culture’ app.
It was a once in a lifetime experience. The immersive technology of the app helped give me a very surreal experience. The tour started right outside the amphitheater. I was able to walk along the perimeter of the monument and then go inside. Looking at the historical gates and walls of the monument. The cultural significance and history of this monument is undeniable.
The ancient yet beautiful architecture lives up to its reputation, and I felt really privileged to be visiting this monument (even virtually).

title: A good experience
text: The staff was very friendly and helpful. I was with my kid and it was so easy for us to move around.

title: Overpriced but worth the visit if you’re in Rome.
text: Tickets are slightly over priced. I expected to be there longer, was slightly confused at where to start and where to end. Glad I visited as it was beautiful to see.

title: Must see this if in Rome
text: We stopped mainly for photos and did not enter the Colosseum which requires a ticket. This is an amazing ruin. Expect large crowds.

title: Symbol of Rome
text: This is the symbol of the City and one of the symbols of Italy. It's a very beautiful monument, even if it can be take care better, and a visit is necessary if you are in Rome. The entry is expensive but inside is very beautiful and you learn how the ancient spectacles worked. Also by outside is Imposing

title: The main site to see for history fans
text:
Make sure you book tickets ahead of time to get in.

They have done a great job of making this a great attraction, with the route taking you across the current site, and a history of the site displayed throughout (including a family living in the colosseum).

title: Magnificent monument
text: The Colosseum is an magnificent monument. Of course the history of the site is very bad and brutal but nowadays, the preservation is perfect and the employees are very gentle.

title: Great visit
text: Impressive! Take some time to learn the history using an audio guidance or tour guide. Travelled with kids so did not have the time dor detailed history lesson

title: Simply awesome!
text:
This was excellent and awe inspiring. 
We booked a tour of the Colosseum. Roman Forum and Palatine Hill so was a rather challenging (but doable) walk!
A guided tour is the only way to see this. The guide was friendly and knowledgeable and really conveyed the story behind each artifact which brought it to life.

title: Good price and no problems with entry
text: Realy good price, no problems with entry, easy purchase. Also it was possible to skip line and there was no holdups

title: A must see for me in Rome
text:
The colosseum is something that I dont want or need to review. Its an amazing landmark, so full of history, so impressive and in my opinion the must to visit place if you go to Rome.  So go for it ;-).

What you need to consider:
- book your visit online before going, as you cant buy a ticket there
- dont worry too much about entry times and about crowds - the ticket you buy is valid for 24 hours, and from the moment you enter the first attraction, you have plenty of time to see the other ones as well
-- a lot of people are visiting, but it spreads out really well, and we never felt uncomfortable

title: Wonderfull guided tour of Colosseum, Arena floor, Roman forum.
text: At the beginning of the trip the guide(Laura) started from the start, like literally. He gave us the insight of the place and the detailed history behind it. He was funny too sometimes, and had a handy image book to make us understand how the place looked before it was robbed by the romans. it was like story telling and learning at the same time.

title: A must-see
text: Absolutely stunning experience - the Colosseum really is a must-see in Rome. There is also a museum inside the ruins that are wonderful.

title: Visit
text: Magnificent monument easy access with the pre booked tickets. The only minor issue was the works carried out around the site but this is temporary.

title: Fast track Colosseum tickets
text:
Very well organised we had a great day!
Looked around the Colosseum in the morning and then had lunch in a nearby restaurant, waited for the hot sun to cool down and then spent the afternoon in the Forum.
Excellent value

title: Colleseum Tour
text: The experience was simply amazing. Our Tour Guide was Gabriel who made the attraction so much more interesting. Our microphones at the start were buggy but he immediately rectified the issue to make sure our trip was perfect. As we made our way through the Colleseum grounds, his explanation and description of each part really opened our eyes. I'll be thanking Headout for its ease of use as I knew when, where and who to go to. I'll definitely be recommending Headout Tours for making everything easy and comfortable.

title: Beautiful
text: This is one of the best places I've ever visited especially at night just to walk around inthe neighborhood it very nice

title: Unbelievable
text: You just can’t go to Rome and not visit the colosseum! We booked our tickets in advance and all tickets include the forum and the palatine hill. Lots to see.

title: Amazing Of Course
text: Last time in Rome, we did a small group private tour and that was hands down, the best! This trip we were just wandering leisurely and wanted to just stroll  by the Colosseum. To our surprise, we really enjoyed having the time to just stand and marvel. We were able to walk entirely around the outside. We found a railing to sit on and again just discuss and imagine all the stories of the past. So, whether a tour or just a few moments to yourself, its worth the walk to the Colosseum.

title: Worth the visit
text:
You just can’t go to Rome and not visit the Colosseum! You have to book your tickets some weeks in advance and all tickets include the forum and the palatine hill.
It's pretty impressive from the outside, maybe not that much from the inside.

title: Great tour
text: Our tour with Crown tours was easy to book. The tour was cheaper than others and good quality. Our guide was knowledgeable and shared some great info.

title: Great Guide
text: This was our second visit to the Colosseum. During the first the area below the arena level was not available. This time we got below the arena level.

title: A Step Back to Bloody Ancient Times
text: We ordered the least expensive tickets through coopculture.it and I recommend it.  We arrived at the Colosseum fifteen minutes before our designated time (12:20 pm) and went right in.  We had already downloaded Rick Steve's free audio tours of the Colosseum, Forum, Pantheon, and Heart of Rome walking tour and got just the right amount of information.  The Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hill can be covered in 1-3 hours.  You will  need more time for the walking tour and Pantheon.

title: Utterly mesmerising
text: This is a must-see. Stunning and inspiring. Fascinating history too. Queues were not long because we had pre-booked. At times it can feel a bit crowded with school and tourist groups but we just relaxed, let people pass us, took our ease and relaxed into it. Audio tour guide is okay if somewhat basic. All staff were courteous and professional. We adored it here. Look out for hustlers outside the Coliseum - they will try and charm your Euros from you with their patter! Nice and reasonable places for lunch nearby if you turn left on exit.

title: Great experience
text: Stroller friendly, highly organised, place very well kept. Used a tour company to buy priority tickets (luckily as there was a long queue at the entrance)

title: Exciting and sad
text:
Mixed emotions upon finally seeing the coliseum. A place where people fought to the death as free entertainment for the Roman population. Now in ruins due to recycling… the damage is all from the people taking away the marble, granite and bricks to be used elsewhere. That’s why Rome is the Eternal City. We had a guide who explained so much of the construction and destruction of the building, how the fights took place and how people were chosen to become gladiators.

Be careful to wear sturdy shoes. The internal stairs are very steep. Only one bathroom near the entrance.

title: Easy purchase and access
text: Easy access and easy to purchase the tickets.      I one set of questions on receipt by email of my tickets that was quickly resolved on chat.

title: Beyondmagnificent
text: The Colosseum definitely lived up to its reputation — it’s magnificent in its scale and the fact that it is so well-preserved.  While it was crowded, it is worth the wait and the exhibits that they have to display life in Rome at the time of the Colosseum’s active use is fascinating.

title: Colosseum with skip the line to enjoy the experience in Rome
text: If you buy skip the line tickets you save a lot of time. We where there in the mid od May and we literally walked into Colosseum and Forum Romanun. The staff are very nice. Do not take large bags with you,you can use Bounce or any other drop off (storage) lockers/places.

title: Amazing
text: The sheer size of the place and when you visit, you can appreciate just how advanced the Romans were in engineering design etc. And to think that most of it is still standing.

title: So badly organised
text: I walked to the colosseum and tourist guides directed me the completely wrong way to the entrance making me panic I would be late for my Headout guided tour. Luckily I was on time so I asked a member of staff where Headout tours meeting point was. His attitude was horrible basically saying “I don’t know & I don’t care”. To add to this I couldn’t find the Headout tour guide anywhere so they didn’t even check their list of people booked on for the day and just started without me despite me being there on time. The colosseum must not have a maximum capacity because it is heaving and hard to enjoy any spots. Headout is a scam!

title: Great!!
text: Awesome expirence with the guided visit. Just go and look alone isnt the same. I recommend the tour in portuguese with Glória.

title: History brought alive!
text: The guide was excellent and really brought the history of Ancient Rome alive. She had a thorough knowledge of her subject and a vivid imagination.

title: Amazing!
text:
Just amazing! 
I want without a guide and still, you can learn a lot about the place and its history.

title: Spectacular
text: This is a must see if you are in Rome, pay the ticket price and go into this amazing building, so much history, sad history but still an amazing feet for the time, well for any time!!!

title: A must see in Rome
text: This was an excellent tour. Led by Silvia Ferrari from Jewish Roma Walking Tours her knowledge of the history and wonders of the Colosseum and Palantine made the day.

title: Perfect Colosseum trip
text: Great and interesting trip with Headout Tours. Paola the guide was excellent, very enthusiastic and understandable.

title: Truly amazing !
text: Walking back in time, you cannot believe the size of the place it is enormous and absolutely amazing. We walked a round the top with our guide we are fit so were able to make up the stairs however there is a lift for those with mobility issues but it's not easy if you have problems walking. There is so much to take in and we were lucky to have really good guide. Give yourself plenty of time to investigate the area and it is ideal for children to give them an insight into Roman history.

title: Roman Colosseum
text: The Roman colosseum, it is a huge amphitheater that allows us to transport ourselves to a past era of glory and honor, it is very impressive since in it several battles were made to the death such as: gladiators and animals, in the coliseum participated up to 50,000 people as spectators, the construction of the coliseum began under the emperor Vespasian, around 71 AD.C. then the building would suffer four earthquakes and already entering the Middle Ages it became the quarry of Rome, which for me is a very impressive tourist area of Rome, for its stories they have, which make it look very interesting to go to know.

title: Nice to see
text: Definitely you would need a guide to explain it all to you. All is being preserved after many earthquakes have taken their toll

title: Rome
text: An amazing experience with a lovely guide. Far more complex than I imagined and yet so modern as if it had been built yesterday. The Roman’s knew a thing or too about concrete that’s for sure. And it’s still standing even if not complete. A little chilling if you know what went on there though

title: Must see, book ticket in advance on official website
text: Must see, book your ticket online through official website in advance. Don’t recommend to go for the more expensive option to have access to the ground floor as without the ticket you can also see the ground floor on the other side, so nothing special unless you manage to book the underground tour as well. First visit Colloseum as you have a ticket for specific time and then continue to Forum Romanum etc.

title: Must see
text: An historic structure that most people are aware of. I think it deserves its place as one of the must see sites in Rome. (There are many). Even with the crowds it's very atmospheric. You have to book your ticket online and it is worth every penny.

title: Wonderful place to go!
text: This is an amazing place, that one have to visit once in Rome ! Next time i would take a guided tour,  as I think it could provide more information for kids

title: It was great!!
text: The visit in Vatican was awasom!!! I think need the all day for this, another for the Colosseo an another three days for the other shigts.

title: Best site to visit in Italy
text: I highly recommend colosseum to visit if you go to Roma by seeing that you can travel to history and feel how was the Custom of that time.

title: Wooow place
text: One of the most beautiful places not to be missed at any cost while being in Italy. It can be crowded so plan earlier

title: Great tour of the Colosseum and surrounding area
text: Paulo was our guide on Saturday for the 3 hour tour - he was awesome! Very knowledgeable and engaging, bringing history to life and ensuring the whole group was enjoying their time. title: The most spectacular of all the ancient arenas.
text:

Get tickets online in advance and avoid the lines they said and we did, but still there was a darn long snake of a line wriggling itself along for a fair bit past the arc of triumph sitting between the Colosseum and the Palacio so do arrive in time and prepare for a line that should not be. Heaven knows what you do if you don’t have tickets in advance, maybe you have to return some other day, I don’t know.
The Colosseum is a sight to behold and the personell is very helpful. Prepare for steep steps and have a water bottle handy but a must see no doubt.

title: Impressive! Once in a lifetime visit
text: We took a bus to get here because for unknown reasons to us, the subway line was not working on the day we visited.  Our ticket was a self guided tour of Palatine Hill and the Colosseum.  We arrived early in the morning and spent a lot of time walking in Palatine Hill area. We really enjoyed it because it was not crowded yet and the weather was pleasant in the morning.  When we left Palatine Hill to go to the Colosseum, the place was pretty packed. Our entrance to the Colosseum was at 1 PM. We arrived to the Colosseum early and had to go thru different points till we got a person that motioned and pointed out where we needed to go according to our ticket.  I had bought our tickets on line and that worked well. The walk up those big steps is not easy, or at least it was not easy for me. My husband had no trouble.  If you are a person that has trouble going up the stairs, bring a cane and use the handle bars.  We walked at our pace and took time to take pictures.  I liked being on our own as we stopped when and where we wanted. The guided tours are interesting but they spend a lot of time with people just standing in the sun and listening to the stories.  The Colosseum is very impressive because of the size of it and the history behind it.  One thing I would advise is to be sure you have a return ticket because we found out the public buses DO NOT tickets on board and there are no places to buy tickets.   So the options are to walk back to your hotel or to take a taxi.  We did not see a subway station near by.  Only the buses but we could not board because we had no tickets.

title: Don't buy water from the street sellers!!
text: A must see when in Rome, but take your own water bottle. We were tipped of by our guide that the many street sellers(mostly migrants),waving water bottles collect dirty empties from bins, fill at a free water fountain and pretend to break the seal by "opening" the water bottle for you when you buy.

title: Excellent. Go see.
text:
Probably the highlight of most people's trip to Rome, and it is truly spectacular. I can't really add anything to what has been said already, but I would say to be ready for the crowds, and ready for the heat. There are fountains all over Rome to get water from, but there are none inside the Colosseum, so be prepared. The queueing system is pretty good, but I would recommend booking in advance. 
So why not 5 stars? Well, the last time I was there (30 years ago), you could go down into the lower levels, but not any more. Shame.

title: Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill Tour with Arena floor access
text:
Our tour guide Marcello made the trip, the information and knowledge he had was second to none. He was funny and really interacted with the whole group. As we visited on a very hot day he took the time to do several water stops, got us shade as and when possible. He also made sure all of the members of the group stayed together (doing several head counts throughout the trip!).
You need the tour guide to help visualise how it was back then and MARCELLO done that! Worth every single penny and would do it again.
If your going in a hot month be prepared with water, hats, sunscreen etc. Yes it is Hot and yes there is little shade, however there is not a lot you can do other than not go in a hot  month if you don't like the hot weather.

title: Worth vitising!
text:

Early in the morning we were  on the Pallatine Hill, then Forum Romanum and Colosseum in the afternoon).  There weren't eny queue at 3:30pm so I strongly recommend this time period. 

The only minus is an online application which you are buying along with the ticket. It wasn't pricise enough and it  was very difficult to listen to it end find places which are described.

title: Ask for maya - friendly, knowledgeable and articulate! We learned so much.

title: Absolutely amazing place to visit!!
text: The colosseum was easily the most amazing thing I've ever seen in my life. It's honestly hard to put into words! I suggest doing a private tour (Eyes of Rome is a great choice). The larger tours are terrible especially if it's really packed. Make sure to book the tour that allows underground tunnel and arena access. It was incredible!! As well as a tour of the Roman Forum and Paletine Hill. If you are going in the summer months be prepared for HOT HOT HOT weather! Bring water bottles and an umbrella to help block the sun. Some people brought fans and/or spray bottles. I'm not kidding when I say it was scorching hot. There is no food available in the colosseum so eat beforehand. Wear breathable clothing and comfy shoes too.
