text: Walk your way toward this historical site.  And do take an experience tour guide in advance for the Collisseum. But read about its story before hand.

title: Very useful ticket package
text: Useful audio guide. All of the basics were included. You can get the overall picture about the Colosseum and it's history. Of course, priority entry line is also big benefit. Would definitely recommend.

title: Coliseo romano
text: Everything was awesome! The guide knew every single detailed about the places and she was very helpful.

title:
maya= perfect for the job.
good guide and she takes the time to explain & enjoy
text:
Colosseum visit and garden with all the beautiful places and ruïnes 
The tour geven by Maya was nice helpfull and she explained a lot of Rome and the history

title: Worth seeing outside, inside a little less so
text: The outside of the Colosseum is very impressive: it's imposing, it's a symbol of ancient Rome, and you can't quite get a sense of it unless you visit. The inside, however, didn't seem worth the money paid to get in: the interior looks like a large football stadium

title: Great Visit!
text: What more can you say? It’s the Colosseum. Very busy but well controlled by the staff. Take a hat and sun cream as it is very exposed at times

title: Perfect tour of 3hrs and 15min
text: Colosseum and foro romano tour w. MARIA EMANUELA ( I am not 100% sure about the second name, but she has said she was the only woman w. 2 names Maria being thebfirst one) a lady that has said she has been doing this job for the pasr 20 yrs. She was a very welcoming and kind person, perfectly informed about anything we asked, very clearly speaking, enthusiastic about what she was speaking....alltogether a perfect guide, so the price wr'had paid for 2 persons ( daughter & mother) was well worth the tour. Thank you , Maria Emanuela

title: Just do it
text:
This landmark is the most recognizable one in Rome. If you can only visit 1 place in Rome, then this should be it. 

Please book your ticket and time slot beforehand, otherwise you will have to buy then from those ticket touts from India and they charge a hefty premium. 

Made sure you know what type of tickets you are getting, entry into the Arena and underground tour are usually not included.

title: A must see !
text: When in Rome this is one of the must see's, one of the most iconic sites of the world. Used the hop on, hop off bus to get here and walked around the perimeter taking in the history and atmosphere.

title: Confusing official park website, denied Colosseum entry despite park membership purchased.
text:
We purchased a PARCO Membership for the family for Euro 80 that, according to their official park website wwwcoopcultureit , would provide "Unlimited admission for 12 months from the date of activation to the Palatine, Roman Forum and Colosseum area". To our surprise, this wasn't the case. The ticket office denied us entry to the Colosseum. Even with park membership, we still would need a reservation. So no Colosseum for us. We could still visit the forum and Palatine, but the experience remained frustrating.

They need to update their website providing clear information to travelers.

title: For History amateurs
text: A perfect place for History amateurs, if you can visit it with a guide or audio guide don't hesitate (well spent euros).  Otherwise you will feel a bit lost, despite the beautiful views

title: Organized and knowledgeable!
text: The tour check in was organized and efficient. Our guide, Alba, was knowledgeable and a great storyteller. Highly recommend.

title: As Awe-Inspiring as you Expect it to Be
text: Tip: Book tickets ahead, and book for an early slot. We got to enjoy the site with hardly any crowd. There are different tours/accesses allowed with each ticket type, and it can be a bit confusing. Just trust the staff to point you in the direction you're supposed to go.

title: Wonderful experience on our Rome family holiday
text: The tickets exchange went smoothly and we wasted no time on queues. The guide on the app was useful but the maps were a bit unclear. Not particularly appetising explanations but we appreciated them nonetheless.

title: Colloseum Tour
text: The best tour guide ever! She was so knowledgeable and amicable. So fun and learned so much! I would recommend her to anyone!

title: Amazing and enormous
text: The Colosseum is huge inside, and even though just a small part of it is open for tourists, be prepared for a big walk and a huge amazement!

title: Roma
text: Great experience and touch of history . Very hot weather- about 40 degrees but if you drink well you will be fine. Rome is nice👍

title: Hollywood or the Roman Empire
text: Once you know the true history of coliseum you will understand it was the Hollywood of the Roman Empire.  The terrible time was the killing of the Christians.  But overall it was entertainment for the middle and upper classes of society.  What is amazing is all they could do back in that time.  The underground is two to three stories deep.  Truly amazing.  Read up before you go.  Try to book a small group or private tour.   Have fun.

title: Mist see 🙈
text: I bought my tickets at previous day that I planned to access Coliseum. We arrived even sooner than time on the ticket. But guard allow to access with no questions. So we get our audio video device which was quite useless, because contained very basic and some time incorrect data about Coliseum. So, we turn off devices and enjoyed trip. Thank you very much. We also booked tickets to Vatican museum via Hedout service.

title: Crowded but nice
text: The place was really crowded, so we took a tour on or own outside and around the buildings. This was really ok, and we had a good look at it all.

title: Colosseum in person visit left me in awe of ancient Rome
text:
We used the skip the line and that saved us a good bit of time as always lots of people in this popular atraction.

That a building this large with so many alternatives for different atractions including live animals and even naval battles is incredible.     Also the acoustics was something I did not know that worked so well inside it.

After visiting it I am in awe of roman architecture.

title: Guided tour of the Colosseum
text:
It is an amazing architectural feat. When you see the the stage ,seating, and areas for movement it is impressive. With a knowledgeable guide it became a larger reflection on Roman society. The brutal heat when we visited limited our time at the location. 
To fully understand and enjoy the colosseum a guided tour is a must.

title: Amazing place
text:
We absolutely loved our trip to the Colosseum and Roman Forum. It’s a magnificent piece of history and it didn’t feel too busy once you’re inside because it’s so big. We got some great pictures and spent about an hour in there. Make sure you take water with you on your trip, it was so hot when we went so we’re glad we took water with us. 
The only down side was that some areas were closed off to most people. There’s a platform bit overlooking the underground floor and the underground floor itself were closed off with gates  with padlocks and rope. The guards or tour guides only opened these areas and let people through if you had a certain ticket it seemed. We had no idea about this so we were a little gutted. Had we known we would have paid more for the access.

title: Truly spectacular
text: We did a tour with City wonders and Mateus our guide was amazing and so knowledgeable about the area. Truly happy we did this! Thank you

title: Underground tour
text:
We took the tour that included the underground area with the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill areas.  So much history!  
They are restoring the colosseum, but when we were there, there wasn't any work going on.  
There were a lot of people in the colosseum, but it didn't feel over-crowded.  Our group had about 15 people.  There were some great photo opportunities inside.  
It was nice learning about the history from our guide.

title: A MUST visit when in Rome.
text: Overwhelming to see the Colosseum for real! You can feel the history when outside and inside the building.

title: Great tour even with younger children!
text: What an awesome tour guide!!  He had all the history of the colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill and made it so interesting. Our 6yo and 9yo loved the entire thing and didn't complain once (which tells me they were having an awesome time). Highly recommend this tour. Best to book early morning to avoid the heat!

title: Unforgettable
text: Impressive. Gigantic. Amazing. Unforgettable experience and place to be, to feel the history and imagine Rome in that time. If you can, come as early as possible especially if it's in the summer.

title: Great Experience of History
text: We were glad to have been able to get tickets at the last minute (day before), as we were on a tight time schedule with our travelling plans and didn't want to miss out. It was super easy and the experience of seeing such amazing history was well worth it. The audio package was great as we could hear the information as we went along at our own pace.

title: Must See
text: incredible structure.  This is an attraction we spent the extra money on for a guided tour and I highly recommend this option.  Very crowded place and without the guide and skip the line passes you would be wasting valuable time navigating people/crowds and wasting time trying to figure out where to go.  So spend the extra money.

title: Great day out - Get the arena floor tour pass
text: Such an impressive site and we highly recommend the area floor ticket as the views from there were incredible.

title: A must see when in Rome but beware if you don’t like crowds!
text:
I booked skip the line tickets, can’t say if it was worth it as the queuing system was all over the place! Not very well signed and manic busy. 
Once inside the crowds are no less and it feels like your being herded around with everyone. It’s a one way system so easy to navigate but if you want to look at any of the artefacts be prepared for a few bashes and bumps.

title: What's Not to Like
text: Marvel in the beauty that is Roman architecture from the outside, inside or just by walking around the area. You cannot go wrong strolling around the area.

title: Cheap and very nice
text: I bought tickets using the European student thing which is why the ticket was 4 euro, so very cheap. I enjoyed it very much.

title: Unforgettable tour
text: We traveled as a family of four, and this guided tour of the Roman Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill was unforgettable. Our guide was Miss Sara Ponzo and she made a big difference, because she is passionate about what she does, she is calm, kind and conciliatory. She made the most of the experience time. Unforgettable. Thank you very much.

title: I liked it
text: The Colosseum was nice to see and worth a visit if you're in Rome.There is also more things to do when your not in the Colosseum itself.

title: Walking where the Romans walked
text: This was breath taking. Just to see it as you walk up was amazing. Walking on the main level was amazing but going up to the 3rd floor had the best views. They also had artifacts that were from the time period. That was neat to see. Walking in and through the colosseum made you think of history

title: A Must See if in Rome!
text:
If you like history and architecture then for sure you have to visit the Colosseum. The waiting line went by very quick! Be prepared that there will be a lot of people and sometimes even difficulty getting around the crowds. 
I would suggest going maybe super early in the morning to avoid the crowds. 
Other than that the experience was amazing!

title: A must do
text:
A must see if you enjoy history and architecture, we only had a short time there so were unable to do a proper tour with headsets etc.  But just to experience the sheer size of the place and to imagine what happened there all those years ago is mind blowing.
We used Headout.com to book tickets in advance, it was really simple to book the tickets to the Colloseum and download them on to my phone.  
We were able to skip the queue to get in and only had to show the confirmation on my phone.
Couldn't have been easier.

title: A must-see part of any visit to Rome
text: We totally enjoyed visiting the colosseum. It is so much more impressive than can be portrayed in pictures. The queues were well managed when entering and moved quickly. Once inside there was plenty of opportunity to get out of the sun and sit down.  Highly recommended as a place to visit.

title: Perspective
text: The historical highlight of Rome, where slaves built this magestic building and died for Roman entertainment. Beautiful when you look at it from one perspective, but when you think about what it was in 64AD … not so much

title: Colloseum Tour
text:
Elena our guide tour was amazing.
We had a lot of extra questions and she took the time to answer all of them, and also helped us with recommendations of what we should visit next and where we should eat traditional italian food.
She made sure we had bathroom and water breaks.
It was a very long tour on a very hot day, but we are so happy that we took the tour, and we will do  it again if we have time.
Make sure you take  the tour, if you wanna get more out of the experience of visitng the colloseum.
Thank you again.
PS. Make sure you get there on time, we almost missed the tour, because we got lost.

title: A must see!
text: We were in awe with the beauty and magnificence of the site. We were happy with the infrastructure, and we had no problems to get around with a baby in her stroller.

title: What can you say, it's the Colosseum
text: Got to go if in Rome.  Hard to rate a one of a kind place like this.  make sure your get a tour with skip the line pass.

title: A must see with a guided tour!
text: We wanted to visit the Coliseum, the forum and the Palatine hill. We had the great idea to use Headout. We got skip-the-line access to the various sites, were able to get on the arena floor. But, more important, we got a great guide that gave us a lot of great information about the Coliseum, the forum and Palatine hilll. Our experience with Headout was easy and super pleasant. It is a must!

title: Everything OK
text: It all worked fine, as promissed. Had no trouble going into Colloseo or Foro Romano. It's a visit you cannot miss.

title: Nice short break
text:
We couldn’t find any tickets available through the official website so we went for Headout!
Great choice! They do not overcharge to be a ticket reseller and they’re very precise with all the info you need to redeem the tickets.
We were expecting a small and brief introduction about the colosseum but it’s actually as I mentioned just a tickets reseller.
Totally worth it!

title: Second visit and still impressive
text: This was our second visit to the Colosseum and it is still impressive. We purchased skip the line tickets and we would highly recommend this as you can be waiting in the busy queues for ages. We originally tried to buy the underground tickets but had no luck so had to settle with the Arena visit. As expected it was very busy and it was a hot day. No social distancing. Toilets are available inside, basic and clean enough. We would advise taking water or drinks with you as there in nothing available to buy in the complex apart from a cafe opposite. Fountains are available on site to refill your bottles. It is an impressive site during the day but we would recommend visiting at night as it’s spectacular all lit up. Please note that there a many street sellers around, they are harmless enough but be prepared to say “no grazie” a lot. For ladies I would recommend wearing trainers or sturdy shoes and not sandals as the the Roman Forum and Palantine Hill is cobbled and you need supportive shoes. Well worth a visit to the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palantine Hill.

title: Amazing
text: I was in awe of this ancient ruin. It is best experienced with a knowledgeable guide who can help you to connect all that you can see with the history of the colosseum in a meaningful way. It made so many movies about gladiators etc... come alive in my mind while standing in the colosseum. We were able to explore most of it but not the two levels below ground. That was slightly disappointing but overall this is a site no to be missed when visiting Rome.

title: Roman Coliseum in all its glory
text: The Coliseum in Rome speaks for itself. A grand structure with long history in the City. A must see artifact, and bookends the area with St.Peter's Basilica for an abundance of Roman History.

title: Very bad experience with Headout Rome!!
text:
We took tickets for the Colosseum and the Roman forum with the audio guide option. When we arrived at the Colosseum, they told us our tickets do not include audioguides and that we should pay them extra.
The only information mentioned in Headout’s website was that the audio guide is offered by Headout and not the Colosseum, and THAT WAS IT. There was utterly no other information where to find this audioguide, not in the website, nor on their app, nothing! 
Even the people who work at the Colosseum had no idea where to find them. 
Eventually we took the tour without the audioguides even though we paid for three! 
We were extremely disappointed by this scam!

title: Better than in photographs
text: The colosseum is amazing to view in person! It was actually smaller than I thought for being so big!? It is a must see!

title: walking through history
text: to see the colosseum is a sight to behold! going with  a tour guide and listening to the stories at the colossum was a wonderful leasson in history!

title: Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
text:
Our small group tour was excellent. Definitely book the early morning in summer because it gets very hot, as the day goes on. Though our guide made sure to stop at shaded areas for explanations along the way.
Our guide, Sylvia is an archaeologist and made the tour very interesting.

title: Amazing
text: The Colosseum is a must-see of Rome. History unfolds around you. Great monument. You definitely need to prebook a skipo-the-line ticket because the line for the tickets is very very long and you could wait very long to get inside.

title: Great guide, awful audio equipment
text: Our guide was great, but the quality of the equipment was awful. Changed our headphones 2 times and we still were not able to get a good audio during the tour. We struggled to follow many of the stories from the guide. We won't be recommending it to anyone

title: Words can't describe it
text: You do not have to be a history buff to appreciate it. The building is 2000 years old and STILL standing. Definitely recommend the underground tour to see how the "backstage" activity worked.

title: Do not buy Viator tickets or guide
text:
We reserved Viator guide for colosseum and pantheon. The guide did not show up. It was our last day in Rome, we were lucky to get tickets without guide. We had to run around Rome to get these tickets, ruined our day. 
DO NOT BUY THINGS FROM VIATOR, UNRELIABLE!!

title: You learn so many extra things with a super friendly, knowledge guide
text: You learn so many extra things that you didn't know yet and get access to places you are normally not allowed. Our guide was very knowledgeable and knew when to take breaks in the shade and where the less busy water fountains were to cool down

title: Book via Headout
text: Must visit when in Rome! Booked my ticket through Headout, they emailed the ticket near the visit date.

title: Book tickets well before your visit date
text: We booked tickets for the colosseum and Roman Forum via the official website. I booked a week before, so missed out on tickets that would have allowed us underground, but we could go on the arena floor (which not all can).  To me, this is Rome.

title: A must do visit in Rome
text:
The Colosseum is a must do visit when coming to Rome, and going inside shows you so much more than you may have understood previously.
We booked a (Crown Tours) tour, which we were lucky to have a really enthusiastic guide with a wealth of knowledge about the history (what we know so far!), also being able to skip the queues enabled us to see more of Rome rather than spending hours in queues, booking is definitely recommended.
Plan ahead, bring water, and enjoy the history!

title: Once in your life
text: In this wonderful city You must visit the Colosseum and I Fori Imperiali. In the city center. Sfidando il caldo, ho goduto nuovamente di questa unica vista

title: Awesome experience
text: Tour guide was great, informative and really showed us some great stuff even thought it was 100 degrees out he had a great attitude and we had a great time. Would recommend for sure!

title: Kind of boring
text: It's not necessary to see this from the inside -- if you've seen a roman arena before, you've seen this one...

title: Thoughts of Gladiator!
text: We had a wonderful visit to this spectacular venue. The history and splendour of past days really draws you in. Tickets can be bought online and our ticket also included the forum and were good value at around £15 each. A must see for anyone visiting Rome

title: Not mad but disappointed
text: Not sure what all the hype was about bc we waited in line for 30 minutes and were patted down twice just to see a bunch of broken rocks. There was no AC and not a single person would share their water after we found out there were no fountains. Although, the tour wasn’t a complete flop, we were able to see a young child fight his mother as she took away his iPad. It wasn’t the gladiator vs lion action we were looking for but was the most exciting part of the trip. Overall, the place was filthy, crusty, and dusty. Could use maintenance and a good deep clean. Would give 1/5 but the child was entertaining. 2/5 final review.

title: A must see place
text: Pure excitement indeed! It is simply unbelievable to see the arena finally in its glory. You could feel and hear the roar of the spectators during the horrible games.

title: July visit
text: We found this was badly organised inside it but it is worth seeing.  Also looks great from the outside !

title: WOWWWWWWW
text:
WOW WOW WOW 

This place was utterly amazing!!
I would highly recommend visiting here when in Rome. We did a tour that involved this, the Palentine hill and the Roman forum. It would fantastic. I would 100% book to go on the flighting floor. I had been advised by a friend before not to get the video guide as it just shows modern film references, but we went on a guided tour. I would recommend this. 

You got to see all aspects. make sure you are aware of what each door is used for and how the theatre was used int he roman times. It is utterly fantastic.

I think you should also go and see it in the dark also. It is utterly beautiful. Look at the photos!

title: Guided Tour of Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill
text: Our guide Letitia (#73) was perfect! She was very enthusiastic, a very good English speaker, full of knowledge and interesting in the lecture. She made sure we had enough water to drink.

title: Best tour! Definitely worth the time and $!!!
text:
Having Alessandra as our tour guide was an absolute delight! She was knowledgeable, clear,
detailed and incredibly funny as
well as likeable.  A natural teacher and her passion and excitement for her work is more than evident.  First class!

title: Try to get a tour that includes the floor, great perspective
text: The colosseum is amazing.  We took a tour with the Roman guy that allowed us on the colosseum floor.   The floor is a must do, the perspective you get is incredible, it is where the animals and "entertainment" entered the floor.   Lot's of iffy stairs, so be aware, but a must do!

title: Never tire of this amazing icon
text:
First time taking a tour - have been prior - but thought good for daughter!
Amazing to go there again after 18 years and a must see if you visit Roma

title: Coliseum Recommended
text: One of my favorite site in Rome despite this insane July heat. A truly amazing experience. The sun setting over the Colosseum was amazing.

title: Still impressive (after visited many times)
text: This sight needs no further explanation. Did not go inside, since we have been there a couple of times before. Just impressive in it self, walking around it. And thats free as well!

title: Must-see in Rome
text: Amazing experience, wish we would have had a guided tour but we were a bit late with our booking. Entrance signs were not sufficient when arriving to the Colosseum.

title: Timrless must-see in rome
text: Visiting the Colosseo and seeing it from the topmost accessible floor. Awesome experience. Incredible that this building is 2000 years old and packed 87000 people!

title: Must see
text: We really enjoyed visiting Colosseum, this place was highlight of our trip. We went with guided tour which also helped us to understand the history behind this magnificent place.

title: Lovely place, BOOK ONLINE.. steer clear of ticket touts..
text: 2nd time here. Love the history of this place. Didn’t realise you had to book (as didn’t before pre covid). YOU MUST BOOK ONLINE!!! It’s very simple but and theres big BUT.. BEWARE OF TICKET TOUTS at the front .. I’m too savvy for their bull. One tried to tell me it’s all sold out for today. Showed me on his phone and it said nothing available all in red. He said I could join a group, €40 for me and €20 for my daughter! No chance.  I looked myself and found many slots available.  I said show me your phone again! As I showed him mine he walked off! Clearly a screen shot and not actual time! SO BEWARE… don’t get sucked in and pay double!! If you have a few hours to spare before your time slot, go to Palatine hill as you can use the same ticket for this. Enjoy 😊

title: Legendary Roman Landmark
text:
One of the most famous landmarks in all of the world and a must visit in Rome. The Colosseum is basically everything it's reputed to be and more.

Tickets cannot be purchased at the Colosseum as there are no ticket offices there, you must buy them online. There will be people that will sell tickets at the front, but they will have various ways of getting you so better to be safe and purchase online.

Getting tickets will allow access to the inside of the Colosseum (though the outside is quite nice in its own right). There's some pretty good viewing spots across the board and there's even a nice exhibition on I believe the second floor available to view that explains the history behind the Colosseum. 

There is a ticket that allows you to access a certain portion of the "viewing decks" as well as the Emperor's Box and Arena Floor, though it is a fair bit more expensive than the regular ticket.

title: Out of this world
text:
Thankful that we had a knowledgeable guide with us who could share the history of the place.
Being a popular tourist spot, one has to jostle and even 'wait' for that photo moment. 
Just wished we had done some reading up and visited the place instead of joining a tour, just so that one can spend more time just admiring the history and views of the place.

title: World destination to visit
text: Iconic and timeless, this place is a portal to the past.  Yes, there are many tourists enjoying it along with you.  This can be minimized by joining one of the walking tours that give access to other parts beyond the main access areas.  Do some research ahead of time and make it a bit of a scavenger hunt to see things while on the tour.  Don't trust anyone outside, they are almost all out to extract something from you.  Take plenty of water as well, it can be hot as mid-day approaches.

title: Immortal amphitheater
text:
The highlight of the visit to Rome. Perhaps it is one of the iconic landmarks of Rome and even Italy.
The Colosseum is next to a metro station, "Colosseo", very convenient. There is a free water provider, remember to bring your own bottle. 
Recommend buying a skip-the-line ticket in advance. 
Restoration works were being done in part.

title: Beautiful
text: This is an experience everyone should have.  It is amazing either at night or during the day.  This should be on everyone's bucketlst

title: Monumental 3-tiered Roman amphitheater once used for gladiatorial games, with guided tour option.
text: The Colosseum is an oval amphitheatre in the centre of the city of Rome, Italy, just east of the Roman Forum. It is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, and is still the largest standing amphitheatre in the world today, despite its age.

title: Stunning
text: It was amazing to stand and imagine what this place would have been like at the height of Ancient Rome.  It is a must see.

title: The greatest landmark in Europe
text: The Eiffel Tower and the Colosseum are perhaps the two most recognizable landmarks in the world. But what the Colosseum has, is a weight of history that truly captures the imagination. I'm not sure why, but we went the day it was free to enter, which was a bonus!

title: Do the tour
text: Worth doing the underground tour - great experience and avoid the crowds more - knowledgeable guide.

title: Great Tour, especially with Radu!
text: Really great, the tour guide (Radu) was incredibly personable and knowledgable, and really brought the history alive in an enjoyable way. I certainly would not have appreciated the forum half as much without the tour guide. Tour company was well organised and instructions clear throughout. The tour gave us lots of time to enjoy all the different sights.

title: great and a must see
text: No brainer - must see; going on a tour allows you to skip lines; also it may be worth it to see the underground (at the very least the arena floor through the Gladiator Gate).

title: Not to be missed
text:
I didn't do a guided tour for this & kind of wish I had. It's an amazing experience overall but I think it would better hearing some of the history about the site. I would definitely recommend going later in the day (I went around 5:30pm) when it's cooler and there is shade in the colosseum. There are some step stairs to get up to the 2nd floor but there is an available life. Very cool & definitely worth a visit. With a ticket it was very seamless to get in with no waiting. 

Something to note - taxis will wait at the top of the stairs by the entrance/exit and try to rip you off by charging 35 euros instead of using their meter. If you walk to the left across the street there is a taxi stand and we had luck getting a regular fare taxi back to our hotel.

title: ''A monument with bad memories from the city's past''
text: The visiting was easy. But it seems that the profit of the tickets let the people (huge numbers) to wait in queue. To many tickets in the same hour!!!

title: An unavoidable and definite must-see!
text: One of the most recognized and respected sightseeing places of Rome and Italy is a definite must-see when visiting this mesmerizing city. Although it's a bit confusing when it comes to the philosophy of entering the Colosseum itself, make sure to avoid guided tours which are really expensive and not that much useful. Make sure to book your visit online, it's the best option if you want to avoid scam. Other than that, you won't regret visiting the wonder of human architecture!

title: Unmissable and breathtaking
text:
We pre-booked our tickets in order to avoid the inevitably huge crowds. We were pleasantly surprised that tue queues moved very smoothly and we were able to pass into the attraction without any delay. We visited on a searingly hot day and the fan misters along the queuing system were most welcome. Our tickets included access to the arena floor and this is an addition which we would thoroughly recommend. It really gives a sense of the scale of the place that is not as striking elsewhere. Despite the popularity of the attraction, we never felt crowded or rushed, which is not true of other attractions in Rome. 

The climb to the upper levels is via a pretty steep set of stairs - if you’re feeling the heat you’d be well advised to take your time or make use of the elevator (even if there’s a queue for it).  The upper levels provide an amazing view of the whole arena and there are many opportunities for photos. There is also an excellent exhibition on the upper floors. 

This is an attraction not to be missed if you are in Rome. The ticket price (€20 on the door or €22 to pre-book) is cheap at the price considering how iconic this place is. Make it your business to enjoy a truly breathtaking experience on your visit to Rome.

title: Definitely must
text: We book a tour in advance to Vatican and Colosseum tour, while tour of Colosseum was three hour with “City wonders”, it was great to skip the line but, you can do this by yourself and see more instead of listening to story over 30 minutes in each corner, but it can be our guide as well who over explaining . Place itself was definitely must see, there was a higher area that you need to book in advance to get up there, which I did not know about and you need to book to get in the very low area where Gladiators were in advance as well. To be honest I did not fee like we missed anything because I did not book for those special areas.

title: A site we will never forget
text: History shows and documentaries are no comparison in seeing the  Colosseum in person. The size of this historical site is amazing!!!!

title: Great day!!!
text: Very nice place, but is good to have ticket booked before, children are for free so don't buy tickets for them. You can come to the office and show your tickets for adults and then you get free tickets for children. It is good to come 0.5h before you tickets are booked. Headout had the best price and hours to us so I can recommend them.

title: This visit it's a Must Go at least once in your Life!!!!
text: Excellent experience. It took us at least 3 hours to visit the whole Colosseum with quality. To see everything, To enter the Arena. To read all the details of the things that were found there. The 48h to visit the Colosseum, Forum and Palatino Hill it's essencial for a quality visit on these 3 places. I would suggest a good sun protector and a hat. Drink lots of water in the founts there to keep yourself hydrated. Try to do little pauses sometimes on the shadows that you find. I would say that this visit is a MUST GO AT LEAT ONCE IN YOUR LIFE.

title: How to get the basic cheap ticket
text:
So, the coleseum. Just there in July 2022. Ticket prices are 18 Euro to get in, cost is actually 16 but you MUST buy online tickets and there is a 2 Euro surcharge. I couldent find where to buy direct tickets online, because all the shark spots keep popping up. What you do, is go to the coleseum and right in front are huge posters about how this is a cultural site etc. On this huge wall is a QR code, and you just scan it in your phone and it takes you directly to buy the 18Euro ticket. However your ticket is linked to a timeslot, so you may need to wait for an hour or so until you can purchase a ticket for an open timeslot. Was no big deal, we just spent the hour waitimg to stay in the shade and read all about the coleseum and gladiators and stuff on wikipedia. Didnt need to pay for a guide. You pay double for a guide, and there are 30 people in your group so you cant hear them anyhow, and they tell you the same thing. 

With this ticket gets you onto the first 2 floors to walk around. There are some interprative signs on the one floor, good views, and a fountain of water to fill your bottles right inside the coleseum as soon as you walk in. Oh, and bathrooms also. We did not buy extra to go down into the basement. You can see it all from the top anyhow.  

Was good, you have to do it once in your life.

title: History lover's dream
text:
Absolutely amazing tour serviced by Crown Tours.  It was an extremely hot day and our tour guide took an easy pace with plenty of stops in the shade. 
The colosseum is am amazing site and as a a lover of history it was amazing to be able to tour this site and see it up close.

title: Easy pass into the colosseum!
text: Great experience, I was with my family and we bypassed the long lines easily, this was the institution I heard about but didn't know I needed.

title: Gladiatorsssss!
text: Another must-do when in Rome. Book everything in advance on Headout and head there slightly earlier as the area is super crowded. It’s stunning, especially with the guided tours.

title: Amazing to see
text: Apart from the tour guide, this was a great monument to see while in Rome. I wish I had skip the pallatine gardens and just saw the colosseum, it was very hot the day we went.

title: Remarkable and left me looking at it in Awe
text: Being able to walk through a piece of history and to actually take in everything about this remarkable site where so much has happened - both good & bad.  The sheer size of the building, the steps inside,the underground tunnels, etc. displays the level of intelligence, foresight, & determination the Roman civilization once had. Unfortunately, it also stands as a testament to the lack of human life the Romans were known for.  I doubt anyone visiting Rome would miss out on this site!

title: Crowded but still lovely
text: Went on a hot summer afternoon and there were many people. The exterior is impressive and awe inspiring, the interior contains exhibitions on the history of the place and offers views into the arena. Overall informative  experience

title: Colosseum , Arena and the Gladiators
text:
Great experience and worth watching inside it. Looking at the arena from top and imagining what has passed in this area in history is both exciting and regretful. Although the monument is a symbol of civilization, what has happened inside is not!
I do recommend to take a tour guide and visit the area.

title: Well worth the effort
text:
Really worth the effort even in 37 degree heat at 1400 this was a truly enjoyable attraction. 
Only complaint was the gift shop was very narrow and so crowded we couldn’t see anything…but that is hardly a complaint.

title: Wow

I do wish we did a tour as there would be so many interesting facts about the colosseum that we don’t know. I also wanted to do the underground access but it was sold out. I really couldn’t believe what I was seeing and couldn’t wait to read books and watch documentaries about it at home. title: Perfect day on colloseum

title: Pictures don't do it justice
text: Still big by the standards of today, you have to visit to truly appreciate the scale of the Colosseum. Buy a ticket before you travel - you can get them from coopculture.it. We purchased a combined ticket which included access to the Roman Forum and Palatine too, you get a specific time to visit the Colosseum but you can choose to access the Forum and Palatine at a time that suits you provided that you do both within 2 days. Allow 1-2 hours to get round. The route is one-way starting on ground level and then going along a first floor gallery which is a museum too. You finish back at ground level. If you can, try and book a ticket that includes access to the arena floor (this costs a little extra) so you can experience the view that gladiators would have had.

title: Colossal experience

title: Colosseum tour with Rome Private Guides

title: Basic tour but worth it.

title: The doormen at the entrance were rude, unhelpful racist and misogynistic.
text: Once upon a time this place was filled with brave Gladiators.  As a visitor, today you will find incompetence racist, misogynist, undertrained, aggressors targeting British visitors.  One particular doorman threatened  a brown woman with his martial arts!! He should be sacked. Unfortunately when I raised this with his supervisor he was just as inclined as his subordinate. We won’t be back. But will anybody care enough to investigate? No, nothing has changed.  Many fear change and some fear more than most.

title: Head out Colosseum tour

title: Fun times in old Rome.

title: Traveling with Primary Aged Children
text:
I had missed the boat in getting entry tickets to the Colosseum from the official website, and just as I was worried I'd have to pay a 200% markup to some tour agency to get in, I was pleasantly surprised to find an official tour in English from the Colosseum directly - that not only included the entry (on an otherwise sold out day) but also a tour of the Underground and onto the Floor.

The tour itself went to some fascinating and well preserved ruins in the Underground - showing the early elevators and cutting edge technology of the time.

However, she tended to lose her audience the more time went on.  Not only did our kids (ages 5 and 8) eventually tune her out completely (and play quietly on the floor with their cars) but many of the adults did as well.  

At first several were enthusiastically asking questions but she kept deferring the questions because she would explain it later, but then she never did.  She went into great detail about various types of stone and the different time frames of construction - and I dont think it was the group for it.  They wanted stories of real life, not about the geology of the stone.  I still don't know how they accommodated so many people with regards to toileting as she deferred the interesting question and never answered it!

title: Colloseum Rome
text: A definite bucket list tick you need to see. Our visit was the day the closed the colloseum due to extreme rains but we managed to get in, just in time. Rome is a must see city.

title: evening visit
text: we opted for an evening visit to ensure cooler weather and we were glad we did. Easy queue and entrance and quick, easy tour to see what is on offer. very busy but very efficient stewards ushering people to where they need to go. Great views of time gone by and great historical importance.

title: Wonderful site
text: A must see when in Rome; be sure to buy skip the line tickets.  The site can be very crowded.  Don't miss the Forum while there.

title: Must go spot!!
text:

Whatever you do, BOOK IN ADVANCE! There are many people scattered trying to 'help' you get tickets, and we saw some people fall for it. They want to help you by taking you in the opposite direction of the Colosseum, DON'T DO IT!

Also take your own drink or buy from the shop inside, do not take water from people selling them from bags outside, we saw a couple of people take the water, change their mind when they heard the price and then get hounded until they paid. 

We had to wear masks when we went, so it was really hot but I would definitely go again. Loved it.

title: Official tickets from Coopculture. No queues, well organised, great experience
text:
We booked line-skip tickets (with arena access) via the official website coopculture.it.    This is much cheaper than reseller websites. 
 The site provides specific entry timeslots - you have to complete the transaction quickly though or it times out.  I accidentally selected the wrong date; they advised me to rebook and they refunded me for the incorrect tickets in in full. 

We printed the ticket in advance, gave it to staff member at the start of the queue at the Colosseum and were given orange stickers, showed past the queue, through bag check and into the arena.  Fantastic experience.

Forum and Palatine were included in our tickets but the instructions weren’t clear on how this worked; did we have to go to the Colosseum first?    To be on the safe side that’s what we did, and they told us that we could enter the forum via any of the entrances by giving the same printout with QR-code, to scan. So presumably we could also have done the Forum and Palatine first.

All in all, well organised, good value and great experience.

However, I would do a tour next time. I think you get the little treasures of info, and these can be booked online too.

title: How Can You Review One of the Wonders of the Ancient World?  Let's Try...
text:
I had only seen the outside of this place in 2019 as no tickets were available, and even then it was awe inspiring.
 This time though, a trip inside was even more unbelievable. 
Things you need to know: tickets ahead of time are necessary, you cannot walk up and get tickets, and you need to do it online and pick a time. Once inside, you might want to get a headset to narrate some of the more known and little known facts of the site. A Roman History student may know much of that information already but there will still be some pieces of information you might not know. 
The architecture inside is remarkably well preserved, and much of the evidence of rubble and wear is due to this being considered the 'quarry of Rome' for centuries, providing building materials for much of the city. Despite this, the condition is impressive. 
The recent availability of below floor level where acts and staging was lifted to the floor level of the the Colosseum back in the day yields another level of wonder, available for a separate admission. 
Your ticket also lets you into the remains of the Forum just outside, which contains both ruins and fairly intact buildings, temples and roadway. Watch out for the heat here in the summer. 
History buff or not, everyone visiting Rome should make this one of their first stops. There is nothing like this anywhere in the world.

title: Beautiful!
text:
***It is free to enter on the first Sunday of every month.
 
We had a lovely couple of days at the Colosseum.  The only downside was the amount of people trying to sell us tour guides.
 
We went to see it, one day, but were told the tickets had sold out for the day (apart from ones with a tour guide), so we just explored the outside & came back another day, to see the inside.
 
We initially visited late in the afternoon.  There are a LOT of people trying to sell you thinks in & around this area, it gets extremely annoying.  A random man, dressed in jeans & a Tshirt, told us all Colosseum tickets were sold out for the day, apart from one he could sell us that would get us a tour guide.  The Colosseum was only a 10 minute walk from our hotel so we decided we’d enjoy the view from the outside & come back another day.  Whether or not he was telling us the truth about the tickets being sold out, I do not know.  He definitely didn’t work there & had just prebought tickets to sell, with himself being the tour guide.
 
When we came back, we were pleasantly surprised to find out our entry was free.  We still had to queue for a free ticket, though.
Please do not be put off by this queue, it looks huge but really isn’t (about 20 minutes).  We still had people trying to sell us tour guides – one man actually got annoyed at us when we said we didn’t want one.  He said “the queue is really long for the free ticket.  I can get you a tour guide for €25 each & you only have to queue for 15 minutes”.  We politely declined this offer & he became visibly annoyed & said “€25 is cheap!”.  We do not care how much it costs, we prefer to find the history out for ourselves, we did not want a tour guide.  I would suggest you do not ‘just get a tour guide’ to skip the queue because it doesn’t work.  The tour guide just queues with you.  We actually had a group with a guide behind us in the queue so we got the whole thing for free anyway!
 
If it’s hot, take lots of water with you.  There are no water fountains nearby & I think the closest toilets were in the Roman Forum, next door  (you get free Roman Forum entry with your Colosseum ticket).
 
There are lots of restaurants & convenient stores, nearby, but they get very busy.  We queued for some water in one shop for about 15 minutes.

Getting to the Colosseum was very easy with the Metro in downtown Rome - that was accessible from the train directly from FCO airport.

There are plenty of water filling stations, but no food options.

There was a small room with some vending machines that include chips and chocolate bars. BRING SNACKS and an empty water bottle there is a LOT of walking.

title: Hot and busy but so worth it.
text:
Absolutely stunning, still can’t believe we walked in the footsteps of ancient Romans! The scale of the monuments is breathtaking. 

We had combined Colosseum and Forum tickets. There was nowhere to refill water bottles in the Colosseum and the only toilet facilities were at the start so plan for that, particularly if you have children with you. We went round the colosseum  at our own pace. It was very busy but we were still able to see all the displays and soak up the atmosphere. 

Our ticket sellers (Get Your Guide) advised us to use the Via Di San Gregorio entrance for the Forum & Palantine Hill - this was excellent advice because you have a gentle slope at the start but then it is amazing views across Rome and it’s a steep downhill to the Forum. We felt very sorry for the people doing it in the opposite direction slogging up steep flights of steps in the August heat. Palantine Hill and the forum have water refill stations, vending machines, and toilet facilities, all of which is very welcome. 

Allow lots of time for these attractions as there is so much to see.

I was very glad that I booked entry to the Arena floor - that was well worth the visit.  We had a great experience and had plenty of time to get back to the Airport for our flight home.  The only confusion was where I misread the time - my email stated the booking was for 1:30 but the actual ticket stated 1:45.  I thought we had to be in the queue at 1:15 for the 1:30 entry, but that time was actually our time to line up - the ticket was for 1:45.  My mistake!  Other than that, no problems at all.  Will definitely recommend.

title: My Top Ten Tips
text:
What can I add to an incredible place that has over 148,332 reviews?  Here’s my starter for 10….

1.  Book a Tour.  Trying to gain entry on the day can lead to disappointment.  Or be very expensive.

2.  Book your tour early to get what you want.  There is a mind-blowing choice of options, so take the time to research what’s on offer.

3.  If you can afford it, book onto a tour with small numbers.  We booked a guaranteed ‘Six Places’ through Livtours (highly recommended, and reviewed separately).  Less time in queues, access to the more restricted areas of the Colosseum, and much more personal than following the herd of a larger tour.

4.  And if you really can afford it, book a tour that permits access into the lower layers of the Colosseum.  These tickets are restricted as there are limits on visitors permitted into these areas per day, but it really is fascinating down there.

5.  Don’t forget your photo ID.  That can lead to serious disappointment as it was mandatory for our tour entry.

6.  And try to pack light for your visit, lots of airport style security on entry, plus it’s no fun resembling a Himalayan Sherpa as there is quite a bit of walking / climbing / descending involved!

7.  In summer, it can get really hot.  It even hit 34 degrees Celsius on the day of our visit, early September.  Sun cream and a hat.

8.  And a water bottle.  You can refill at several recharging points inside.

9.  Try to do a bit of reading on the Colosseum and gladiators before visiting, I think that by doing this you will appreciate so much more from your visit.

10.  And outside, be mindful of pickpockets.  They don’t always look like the stereotypical street urchins either.  Be wary of the beautiful people, working in pairs, designer clothes and immaculate make-up.  With labelled shopping bags, empty until they become useful cover for newly acquired wallets, phones and sun glasses!

text:

We chose the full guided tour with the underground, arena and upper areas. Our guide was an archeologist and was able to explain not just the architecture, the significance of features, but also give us a feeling of what to was like n the dark underground with the smell of animals and roaring of the crowd.
You also get a better feel for the size of the people in ancient Rome when you are constantly ducking to make it through the arches underground.
My 10 yo son enjoyed it very much and was able to repeat some of what he learned, but he was mostly in awe.
