title: Like a College History Course in Just Three Hours
text: During our trip to Rome we booked a Tour of the Coliseum through Viatours. This three hours small group (16 in group) tour included the Roman Forum, Palatine Hill, and the Colosseum. We covered a lot of ground (both physically and historically) in this three hour tour. Our guide Guido provided subject expertise regarding the history, the architecture, and the politics that lead to creation and later decline of these amazing structures.

title: It is what it is
text: It’s been a lot of years since we have been here. But not much has changed except for the scaffolding up on the inside. You can spend a whole day in here. Get your tickets from reputable places don’t get stung by the guys on the street who take more money from you and claim to help you to skip the queues . This is an amazing place to be. You may get goose bumps when you are there. It’s also a beautiful sight in the evening as they have it all lit up. Looks eerily amazing

title: See it from the outside?
text: Colosseum is great, but seeing the inside involves long lines, and there is not much to see IMHO. I am with Rick Steves, who famously suggests skipping getting inside the Colosseum, as the most interesting views are from the outside -- and free.

title: Marvel at the genius of the Romans
text: This was my second visit. It’s still awe inspiring second time around. Got in early to avoid the crowds. The Romans were such inventive architects. Obviously, this was a place where some grim events took place as well and it’s important to be mindful of that.

title: Fabulous to actually see
text: We booked small guided tour with crown yours.  Found them easy to find to join group and guide was very knowledgable and humorous.  Her name was Christa.

title: Colosseum visit - highly recommended
text: One of the most popular tourist destinations in Rome and obvious to see why. Very imposing and impressive. Spent a great afternoon exploring the Colosseum and Forum. Not too crowded and lots to see. Highly recommended to anyone visiting Rome. Good value for money. You need a good few hours to see everything and take it all in. Make sure you pre book.

title: Book Book Book
text: A wee trip in Rome and off to Colosseum. SOLD OUT ONLINE FOR THREE DAYS. Only available tickets were Tours selling outside. So an 18E ticket is now 40E each and all sold out by 11am for that day.  Having said that the tour guide was excellent and allowed entry to both Forum and Palatine Hill. Allow 3-5hrs

title: The Colosseum
text: The place, the guide, everything was great and impressive, a place full of history and grandeur.I love it❤

title: Spectacular Place
text: Approaching the Colloseum you get to appreciate the size of the place and seeing it in person beats any photo. Yes it's busy and you have to be patient as you tour but it's well worth it. Take time to reflect on the size and stature and put yourself in history and imagine it when it was full, when it was heaving and  full of noise to fully appreciate the magnitude and importance of this site.

title: Great historical value. The rest is awful
text: This is history! The big one! Hence 3 points out of 5. Would have given all marks, but there is only one and need to include all aspects. So 5 for great historical interest. I mean, you need to see it! After you've read about it. All the rest is bad. The long lines of tourists, inside and outside, police, no parking. Actually the building itself is somewhat of a disappointment, as some restauration would really do it good. Look at St Maria in Firenze...

title: Very good Collosseum tour
text: We paid for a small group English language tour if the Collosseum, Palatine Hill and Firum- money well spent!

title: Fantastic
text: Colosseum is the centre of this ancient town and highly recommended togethet with a visite to the Palatine Hill.

title: Awesome!
text: The History that involve the colosseum is unique, you will feel that you are traveling in time and you will able to explore all the features and scenes that this place have for you, don't forget your sunscreen if you go in summer time.

title: i miss him
text: I am extremely disheveled. Went to Rome, and the colosseum was broken. it’s been this way for years, and they still haven’t fixed it. i don’t understand why, and it makes me very upset. secondly, they killed my great great great great great great great great great great great great great great grandfather, julius caesar, here. i have decided to haunt cassius’ ghost, because they behavior was simply unacceptable. on top of that, they killed my other greta great great great great great great great grandfather here, pompeii here as well. on top of that, there was no roof, and the ghost of napoleon bone apart mugged me here. no one did anything. the people here throw mustard at you, and try to jill you with javelins. very disappointed.

title: The best historical building in Rome.
text: Magnificent. Amazing. Superior. This building was my favorite. Stadium of all stadiums. Much walking and much seeing. Elevator can be used. 2 flights up and 3 down. Steep. Long queues and many people. Lifetime event. All roads led to Rome.

title: Do the underground tour
text: The underground tour was totally amazing and so informative.  I am lost for words at the barbarism of a so called advanced society slaughtering people and animals for pleasure.  The construction of this building and the history of the architecture is worth the visit.

title: Amazing
text: the Colosseum is a wonderful building. I love the way Italy preserves their history. The guide was excellent and very informative. We took the tour the day we arrived. It was very hot and we did not have water. We had to leave the tour early.

title: No prebooked tickets vell in advance , no Colosseum
text: WE only learned too late that you have to book tickets on line and tickets sell out days before you visit. We only had one Saturday afternoon, probably the busiest moment to visit and the tickets were long gone. WE visited from outside. Whart a pity !

title: Gigi was a superstar
text:
Excellent experience , I studied Roman history and Gigi brought it to life , so attentive and knowledgeable 
Made the experience so so special

title: Utilize LivTours!
text: Great Experience! Highly recommend using LivTours...very knowledgeable guides! Tours are conducted in a professional manner, on-time, and you get to skip much of the long lines. Well worth it!

title: It is ok to purchase tickets and negate getting tour guide unless you know someone trusted and well informative
text:
Buy for yourself tickets or get a trusted and only trusted tour guide. 
You can get inside the colosseum with your family (if you have kids or yourself is under 25, perhaps you would be requested to show your identity at the hut for the security), you would enjoy the scenery. There are projectors and other signs and boards which can give you a comprehensive overview about what you you are looking for in informative way. Of course, if you like the Roman history, then better off getting for yourself a trusted tour guide or Google or read some books.
If you don't feel that you got robbed off, then I wouldn't recommend to ask for any tour guide waiting at the gate, sometimes they are available online as well.
First, these people are sure that you wouldn't be able to review their performance which is terrible.
Second, they offer you the same price as any verified tour guide, which is unfair.
Third, these people wouldn't really care about you, only they mind the money, once you reach the gates of the garden, they will disappear.
Eventually, I wasn't happy with the attitude of the tour guide, he was showing off a lot, not well presented,  no manners at all.
Apologies, I didn't want to say it but the Egyptian tour guide was not the best choice today, I think his name is Amir, with somw help from indians ans romanians plus italian manager.
I don't know their names so I mentioned their nationalities hopefully you would find better.

title: Super Friendly!
text: Friendly, informative and patient staff. Did exactly as described - skipped the long queues and got taken straight into the Vatican with no problems - great experience!

title: Roma on tour
text: Extraordinary places, incredible impressions with a true feeling of our world's history. That was great experience and I'm very pleased that I had an opportunity to see all of them!

title: Early Morning Visit
text:
I booked a Skip the Line: Colosseum Small Group Tour with Roman Forum & Palatine Hill, Semi Private with Arena Access on Viator. As from reading many travel blogs, it was recommended to book tours before going. 

The tour guide, Paula, was fantastic. She gave a great insight into the history of the Colosseum.

I went to the Colosseum early in the morning, there wasn't as many people about, queues didn't seem too long for entering.

title: Our night time tour was excellent!
text: This review is for the Luna sul Colosseo (Colosseum under the Moon) tour available through Coopculture. They offered both English and Italian language tours. We took the Italian tour (English were fully booked more than 3 weeks in advance of our late Sept visit), but since one of us speaks Italian, that was fine. I highly recommend the tour after walking by the Colosseum earlier in the day and seeing the very very long lines and hordes of fellow tourists. Our tour group was about 15 people and only one other tour group overlapped with ours time wise, meaning we had the entire Colosseum to ourselves. And we waited only 15 minutes to get in (no line, just security!) The tour was fascinating and focused principally on the underground sections of the structure. Our guide was an archaeologist and very knowledgeable. Note that you have to reserve tickets in the name of each participant and provide photo identification at the gate. If the name doesn’t match the id, you are not allowed to enter.

title: Great experience
text:
Great experience. Took the french tour "Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill" with "Show me Italy" and it was super.
The guide was very knowledgeable and provided a fantastic and entertaining tour.

title: Great experience and a must if you are in Rome
text: Everything was great, from the reservation to the end of the tour. Learning about Roman history is awesome and getting to appreciate it irl is even more awesome.

title: Breathtaking
text: Lack of English signage and help to advise on what que you were meant to stand in a bit disappointing. Had skip the line and to be honest was not worth the extra money. The actual colosseum was amazing

title: Awesome Tour
text: Really great tour guide, with interesting stories and lots of facts. Help get some awesome photos too. Thank you.

title: History at your fingertips
text: Learning the history was great and enjoyed seeing the uncovered ruins. Lots of people, but this is expected.

title: Great time
text: Had a private tour through Tours by Locals, Simona was awesome, extremely knowledgeable and very detailed in her history

title: Coliseum love it.
text: It’s all you think it will be and then some more. It was An amazing experience to be there. I can’t recommend it enough. History lovers will be overwhelmed by the presence of the structure

title: Great experience, thankful that this piece of history has been preserved and not completely demolished to rebuilding rom
text: Amazing experience, not too busy inside which was great, would say the 15minute slots are strict but once you get in its clear the number of people inside felt right.

title: It worked just fine
text:
It worked just fine. As simple as that. The audio guide we received by email was a great idea!
Thanks

title: The best.
text:
What an amazing full day it’s massive and the colosum is more than we expected…
I highly recommend you go.

title: Amazing
text: The Colosseum didn't disappoint and was a fascinating experience. I would advise to book tickets very far in advance. We couldn't get any directly as large blocks of tickets are sold to tour companies who charge for a guide as well as the entry fee. As it happens we did enjoy the tour (usually we're not interested in this sort of thing) but it's just something you need to be aware if if you're travelling there in peak tourist season. Take in a bottle of water if it's hot.

title: Colosseum Guided tour
text:
Very interesting.
A guided tour is a must!
We had our booking cancelled last minute.com…nightmare!
Walked up to the Colosseum & stopped by one of the guys asking if we had tickets? Blessing in disguise!
He walked us to there office & we booked a full tour cheaper than we had booked online!
We found out some of these companies are booking or so we thought tickets that aren’t even there!
You can do it when your there! Make sure you go with the person to their office!
Great experience! A guide is a must for this!

title: Awesome guided tour
text: Well organised. Great guide, Emanuele was funny, interesting and smart! Please book first time 9am! After that it gets too busy. The whole experience was Awesome! Highly recommend!

title: OUTSTANDING GUIDE DENNIS
text:
I have traveled extensively, and I found Dennis to be one of the best guides I have ever learned from. He is very pleasant and extremely knowledgable about Roman history. He brought passion and enthusiasm to his
guiding and shared a great deal of interesting information that I found extremely interesting. I recommend Dennis most highly.

title: Exceeded expectations
text: We did a tour of the Colosseum and ancient Rome with ViaTor - City Walker tours. Our guide was Elisabeth, who could not have been more informative and interesting. The tour well exceeded our expectations, particularly inside the Colosseum. The tour of ancient Rome was ironically slightly spoilt by the constant presence of overhead helicopters - there for security for a present- day hustings and election rally. Otherwise, everything was very well done with one minor exception; the initial meeting place was hardly as described; and the area was full of other tours which made joining the tour challenging. But it was well worth it.

title: Adventure at the Coliseum
text: Great experience with Susana. Interesting and easy to follow history of the Coliseum, Roman Forum and Palestian hill.

title: AMAZING COLESSEUM
text: This is a must see when in Rome. Ques are long so be there early. but oh my the beauty of this is well worth the wait

title: Great Food
text: Probably the best meal we had in Rome - and all of them were good!  Piero, our waiter, was super attentive,

title: Tour Guide to Colosseum
text: This is my first time to the Colosseum and Roman Forum. Initially I was a bit hesitant to pay extra for a tour guide because I thought it wasn't necessary. However I bought the tickets anyway and it was the best decision! Our tour guide from Headout (I'm so sorry I can't remember his name, but he's an archaeologist) was amazing at explaining everything and I really felt like I could imagine how Rome was like back then. I spent 6 days in Italy but I must say that this tour guide was definitely the highlight of my trip. Worth every penny spent!

title: so much history
text: It was a great experience, but i would advice visiting the hill with a live guide, as it is an amazing place

title: Great
text: Great place to go to but over crowded and uncomfortable. Found it hard to pay attention to the history and keep up with my tour guide.

title: Amazing. Highly recommend
text: What an extraordinary place. The history behind it is amazing. Me and my partner had a tour guide and we’re glad we did. Definitely worth the feet ache

title: Just like you expected (but a bit smaller)
text: Basically it’ll be exactly as you expected but surrounded by looky looky guys and maybe a bit smaller. The entrance is super hard to find, you randomly have to go into the metro, but entrance itself is refreshingly efficient. All completely fine once you get in, but nothing special either really. It’s essentially derelict but I’m sure it was lovely.

title: Each time, amazed
text:
This visit, we toured the bowels of the Colosseum. This is a must! There is only so many tickets per day so be sure to go early.
history is incredible.

title: The Amazing Colosseum
text: Did a guided tour with the Roman Forum included and it was excellent. Our guide was an archaeologist so very knowledgable about the history behind this amazing structure. Would highly recommend as this is an absolute must if you visits Rome

title: Book a Tour!
text: Such a cool part of Romes history. We did a walking tour through the colosseum and Roman forum. Wasn’t to much walking but the tour was worth it. We learned so much more than we would have if we had just walked it ourself. We also got to skip the line with our guide.

title: A must to see, preferably with the guide
text: It was a part of the bigger tour. A must see, having a guide their who was able to tell us interesting facts and made us experience those ancient times was a plus.

title: Nice and easy
text: Entrance for Colosseum is quite easy. Just follow the sign, and get into it. However, it's a little bit difficult to find the entrance of Palatine hill. Do not follow Google map directions. You need to take a walk for about 5~10 minutes from Colosseum Metro Station. The entrance e Palatine hill is shown in the figure.

title: An interesting visit to the Colosseum
text: Our guide Maria was very knowledgeable with many stories of the gladiators and the battles which took place in the Colosseum. She told us about why the ladies we made to sit high above the Colosseum floor (in case they had been involved with one of the Gladiators blah blah lol). Maria told us how they would flood the Colosseum floor so there would be a sea type battle. She explained everything there was to see and understand at the Forum and Palatine Hill. She encouraged us to ask questions and made sure we all were together in the group. Highly recommend this tour.

title: Thank you
text:
I want start of by saying thank you!! It was great the instructor was very knowledgeable. 
Right on time and gives all the details of iternary.

title: A must visit in Rome
text: You can’t visit Rome without going to the Colosseum. I didn’t go inside or do any of the tours. But to stand outside and see the enormity of it and to think about back in the day when it will have been full of gladiators, is mind blowing. I thought it was an amazing structure and I’m glad to have seen it

title: Lovely ancient ruin
text: Great but need to keep area cleaner and also the whole of Rome.  What do we pay huge tourist tax for. Every where we went litter and graffiti

title: Bucket list item that didn’t disappoint
text: Had a guided tour arranged by our cruise ship around this amazing place. Incredible to be visiting such an iconic site but spoilt by how many people are allowed in at any one time despite ourselves being pre booked.

title: Get a tour guide!
text:
I really love this part of Rome, we were on a bigger tour , including the Roman forum and also Palatine Hill.
I really loved how knowledgeable was the tour guide, she was from I love Rome agency , her name was Leticia. 
I recommend to see all the big attractions with an agency/tour/guide , because , in that way you could really experience Rome, you are getting into this ancient and exciting vibe during the tour and if you are history nerd you would need more and more of those interesting little and big facts! :D

title: Fantastic day out! Extremely interesting
text: The Colosseum is a very powerful and interesting attraction not to be missed. I enjoyed visiting the Colosseum at my own leisure and i'm glad I didn't join a tour group so we could take our time wandering around. I would advise snacks and drinks while wandering around, you need a lot of energy to get round the Roman Forum following the Colosseum. We purchased little video guides for around 6 euros each but we ended up switching them off, it was too bright and sunny to watch the little videos on screen. Not many toilets or no cafes on site, there is however a few vending machines beneath the gardens before you enter the roman forum but a cafe or 2 would have been really useful.

title: Amazing!
text:
We all loved the Colosseum. We did our own tour and collected our ear phones at the check in desk. 
It's so fabulous to think gladiators battled here. We didn't go into the arena but you could clearly see down to it. Worth a visit

title: Everything was good and fast
text: Everything was perfect, I love each part of clloseum  I enjoy my time. I recommend to every visitor to buy fast truck tickets.

title: Excellent guide brings Coliseum ruins to life
text: Had a great tour of the Coliseum underground, Forum and Palatine Hill  with LivTours. A great deal  of historical information  was shared by our guide in an interesting and sometimes comical fashion.  We learned a lot and have a much clearer picture of life during that period of time we would have missed without our guide.

title: Excellent
text:
It was a very nice experience. Very easy to find the entryway. 
Would recommend it to everyone who is interested in some little history.

title: Colosseum
text: Great Tour with Ivana! Many exciting und interesting  informations. Everthing was perfect. Thanks you so much, Ivana!

title: Brilliant tour of a wonder of the world
text: A must see for any visitor to Rome. We were lucky enough to have Lumi as our tour guide from Crown Tours. Well worth the money we paid to show us all the bits of history that you may just walk past. 3 hrs sounds a long time but it passes very quickly as Lumi has the knowledge to keep you interested and a great delivery style. During the 5 days we were in Rome this was the best experience we had. The tour included Colosseum and floor, Palatine Hill and Forum. Brilliant.

title: When in Rome do as the Romans!
text: When Rome you have to go and see this. We booked the Underground and the Arena guided tour on coopculture a week before we visited, we found limited availability, so would recommend maybe booking earlier to get more time options, though our 12.30pm wasn’t bad. The ticket includes 3 sites the Colosseum Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum and is for 24 hours from first entry, all 3 sites are next to each other. The booking process was straight forward, be sure to print off your tickets and have your ID to hand, booking in advance and tour meant we didn’t have to wait around. Definitely recommend a guided tour as gave a real in depth insight. Wear good walking shoes or trainers as it's a long day and floor surfaces are are cobbled and uneven .Take a hat as theres not much shade and empty water bottle there are plenty or water fountains. Don’t bring bulky bags as there is security and it's busy. Bring your own food/snacks as there aren't any cafes and finally we did all 3 sties in one day - in high insight we would do them over 2 days as we were exhausted!

title: Don't miss this.  I wanted to do it at sunset but arrived at 5pm and I toured The Vatican that morning my fitbit said I did 26,0
text:
A Thursday afternoon at the colliseum. Although I kept thinking of the Monty Python movie "The life of Brian" as I walked thru the levels of the colliseum. 
We're the Popular people's front... no the People's front of Judea.   Spiltters...
But really the colliseum is amazing and definitely worth a visit.

title: Must see in Rome
text: This was my first tour in Rome and it totally met my expectations and beyond. Our tour guide was awesome, she was attentive, kind and shared amazing facts about the romans back then. I would recommend this tour to all my friends and family.

title: Disappointed no tickets
text: WE did not book in advance and there were no tickets when we wanted to visit. Very disappointing ! They sayy tickets sell out more than 30 days before your visit !!!

title: Good deal
text: It was good. But actually it would be better to have more instructions on the redemption of tickets. I took me some time to search the redemption point according to the instructions . But I found no one at the redemption point given by the instructions. Eventually, I could get in directly with the ticket I bought on Headout.

title: Recommend to visit during night time
text: We visit there twice during the night and day time. In summer, night time is the best time to visit colosseum. The night views was also amazing.

title: Very well organized tour
text: We booked the tickets "Skip the Line to Colosseum" via VIATOR.  While making the booking, I did not know how VIATOR Agency would work.  They had made the arrangements with the local tour company - CROWN TOURS.  Clear directions were sent to us via email.  We met the guide, Roberto at their office.  This was a 3-hour walking tour - fantastic.  Our guide Roberto was very knowledgeable and very friendly.  It was a pleasant experience.

title: excellent- kept the kids happy
text: must do with a guide and the floor access to gain the vantage point and perspective of a patron in that area is amazing.

title: Just WOW!
text: I'm not one to take guided tours normally but you definitely should for this place to learn so much more about the real history (not what is portrayed in films)

title: Colosseum
text: Excellent tour and guide. Our guide was so informative. Totally awesome. So much to see and take in. We totally enjoyed this tour.

title: Amazing
text:
Easy, quickly, and without complicated. Excellent experience with my family. 
We are there whit children, adults and seniors and was perfect

title: Great and Insightful!
text: It was a beautiful and insightful  experience.  Our guide Roberto was great, he had so much knowledge  that I was left with no questions.

title: Enthusiastic tour guide for unforgettable trip
text: Our tour guide Giuseppi was very knowledgable about all aspects of the tour, was happy to answer questions and really brought the experience to life with his enthusiasm and humour

title: My favorite!
text: Because of its storied history, this is a must see!  Definitely recommend getting a tour of it.  Could have stayed longer, but crowds were large on our visiting day.

title: Colosseum Private Tour
text: Today, I had a private tour, through Travel Curious, of the Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Trajan’s Forum.  My guide was Andrea Morganti. He is a second generation Rome Tour Guide and extremely knowledgeable.  He speaks excellent English and has an impressive knowledge of Roman History.  The details he conveyed and the connections to the different historical eras of the city and monuments was astounding.  Thank you for a wonderful morning I won’t forget!😁

title: Outstanding presentation by our guide Paola!
text: You have got to do this and let Livitaly Tours get you there! The historical features of this ancient arena are amazing and brought out in a knowledgeable and lively guide...Paola!

title: Highly recommended
text: The guide was lovely and knowledgeable, perfect group size and we saw so much. The guide allowed time to stop for pictures and for us to ask questions

title: Exceptional experience
text: Incredible tour which covers all remnants of Roman empire within distance from Collosseum. The Arena tour was exceptional too

title: Ground floor Colosseum and Forum tour
text: We had an excellent 3hr tour with Marta! She was so knowledgeable and communicated with her group  in a way that was easy to understand and included a side of humour. Our tour included the ground floor and the forum. The 3 hours flew by! We could have stayed and explored a lot longer but unfortunately had a cruise to catch. Finding the meeting place was easy, as the directions were detailed. From there, we had headphones given to us so we could all hear the information easily. We highly recommend this tour and Marta, as a guide!

title: Preservation of astonishing history.
text:
The Colosseum in a must visit if you're visiting Rome. The architecture and preservation is impressive and the history that is bound with the place is astounding. It's crazy to know that around 400,000 people were killed in the space of 400 years within these walls. 

The quing wasn't too bad and you have to pace yourself otherwise the experience won't last that long.

title: Colosseum
text: Interesting historical area. Must visiting in Rome. Take 🎟 tickets from Colosseum Official site. Flat shoes 2hours tour

title: Great venue, Dreadful ticket process.
text: While staying in Rome, I booked and paid online for two tickets to the Colosseum. Basic entry tickets - no guide. I received a text directing me to a website and this then presented me with a voucher. Staff at the Colosseum entrance told me that the voucher was not valid for entry and I had to find a GetYourGuide representative to convert this into the actual ticket. I spent an hour looking for this non-existent person. The directions from your website were completely useless. Eventually a ticket including a valid barcode appeared on my phone and I was able to visit the Colosseum over an hour after our allotted time slot (15:30). The whole process was extremely stressful and nearly ruined our day. How can you treat customers like this? An explanation would be welcome.

title: Gladiator Delight
text:
Informative tour with lots of humour, The group were encouraged to take part play acting , which made for a very enjoyable few hours , Also our guide is a professional archeologist, so was more than able to make the most of this fantastic experience. A big thank you to Alissandra , 
    Fabulous time .

title: Crazy busy and chaotic
text:
Getting tickets is a nightmare and basically you cannot get them on the same day unless you pay twice as much and get scammed by GetYourGuide  - 79Euro instead of 32…well…
At least we got in - beautiful historic place, no question but I would not come during a weekend again…

title: Worth a visit
text: The colosseum is well worth a visit.  It is steeped in history and magnificent to look at and imagine how it would have been. We had a guide Barbara who was very knowledgeable and pointed out lots of facts as we toured the different tiers.  We enjoyed the trip.

title: Massive structure
text:
The structure of the Colosseum is really huge and impressive.  There are many interesting things to see.

I joined a tour group and everything was well organised.  It is highly recommended to sign up for a tour in advance as they will take care of the ticketing and entrance nightmare.  Plus you have a guide who will explain to you about the history and information of the place

title: Great tour of Ancient Rome
text: Very good tour! So much information… to really understand this place a tour is highly recommended! Our guide was very good!

title: Wonderful experience with Adelayda!
text: Loved to visit the Colosseo! The tour guide, Adelayda, was excellent! The weather was great. there were a lot of tourists.

title: Perfect!
text: Amazing experience! I bought my tickets on Headout and I had no issues. Everything happened perfectly. Just showed up away the scheduled time and showed my ticket. Don’t have to say that colosseum worths the visit, right?

title: Colloseo
text: It was nice because we did not have to exchange our ticket. There is a queue where you go faster without waiting for the others and they just scan your QR code. You can Only go inside 15 minutes before the beginning hour.

title: Perfecto
text: Our guide BARBARA the best!!!!! She gave us a lot and interesting information, and she was very funny as well

title: Guided by archaeologist!
text: Amazing guide by an archaeologist!!! Excellent narration of the history and amazing facts , awesome tour and highly recommend

title: We Stopped for Photos
text: In our taxi we stopped and got out to photo this monument. The angle was pretty good and the shots came out nice. Our driver knew the best place to stop.

title: Absolutely Awesome
text: This was my best trip on my trip to Rome, awe inspiring building. Guides fantastic knowledge of the architecture added to the experience.

title: Underwhelming despite it being the #1 thing I wanted to see in Rome.
text: Worth visiting, but meh. Underground is not worth it. Nothing much to really see that you can't see from above.

title: It was interactive and kids (ages around 10 years old) really enjoyed it, and the structure itself is magnificent
text: We had a great experience !!! We visited the colosseum with a guided tour with our kids and really enjoyed.  Kids really got into it and we adults felt we did our part ;)

title: Great tour
text: The best place to visit in Rome - the forum right next door too. You can visit here alone with about an hour; in a tour it’s more like 2 hours. It’s an amazing building and well worth it. We bought our tickets with  Tiqets - slip the line self tour. Was perfect.

title: An experience to behold
text:
Why would you go to Rome and not visit the Colosseum??

We decided to take a guided tour which included the palatine Hill, Roman Forum and the Colosseum itself.

Overall it was a good choice, but be warned it took a while, and during days when it's hot and humid, the 3 hour pre-Colosseum walk was a work-out - even to the point of some of the party didn't have the energy to see the main event.

These guides are good, as especially with ancient history, you learn so much more than you would just wandering around looking.

The Colosseum itself is busy, and will always be busy, so be prepared. It is worth it, seeing what was, what is, and the notes behind it all.

title: Amazing Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum tour with Alessandra
text:
Alessandra was amazing, she is super passionate, you can easily tell. She has such a positive vibe, and even if we got a little tired, her enthusiasm kept us going.
Great amount of information in a short time, I really enjoyed finding out so much about the history of Rome - Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine hill.
Thank you for a great and inspiring experience!

title: Worth to visit Colosseum
text: If possible recommend to book entry time as early as possible. Later and afternoon crawdy sightseeing.

title: Colosseum
text: A great experience. A guided tour is preferred. There is so much to see in Colosseum and Forum Romanum.

title: A MUST see for a trip to Rome
text:
One of the most famous land marks on the planet, with history dripping out of every archway, this has to be the MUST visit when in Rome.
Tickets need to be purchased in advance and you will need to check the collection point as these are spread around the local area (as we found out). We opted for a non guided tour, which was much easier (and cheaper), plus the fact that there are many signs and information points around the Colloseum, the need for a guide is lost a little.
Be prepared for the touts and other low life’s around trying to cheat you out of your money, with “free” gifts, etc.

title: Crowded but worth it
text: No one can deny it is magnificent. Make sure you pre-book your entry and I would recommend a ticket combined with Pallatine Hill. Be ready for lots of people, but be patient. Make your way trough the exhibition and the arena and breath in history.

title: Colosseum and Forum Tour
text: It’s worth seeing, along with the Forum but the crowds, even in mid-October, were horrendous. In fact, all of Rome was overrun with tourists.

title: Once in a lifetime
text:
A mind shattering unbelievable experience.
So hard at times to understand and absorb the 2000 years of history at your feet. Sit down for a while  and appreciate the historic surroundings , the build quality , the architects of design and of course the poor souls ( and animals )  who died in the name of  " Entertainment " .
Strongly suggest buying the quick entry passes and  go for the tour with guide and audio.
Couldn't believe how busy and hot it was in October , so take plenty of water . The only annoyance was the amount of salesmen trying to sell water , selfie sticks ,hats and phone chargers.  Do not miss and also ensure to visit the Vatican... Could write for hours about our visit there .. Oh boy !!!!

title: Guided Tour helped skip queue and got to cover Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
text: Bought tour from Get Your Guide and covered the 3 historical places in good time.  Was a comprehensive tour though long 3 hours with lots walking.  Colosseum tour, including to upper levels and arena helped appreciate the historical site from various fronts.  Wished there was more historical information on the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum.

title: Fantastic Tour
text: Everything was great. The tour guide Marcello V was fantastic.   I would highly recommend this tour to anybody who is going to visit Roma.

title: Totally worth
text: It was very good experience. I didn’t have the guide, but I could explore by myself and I got in 20min earlier. Overall, great experience!

title: Very crowded, but overall a nice experience
text: We went into the colosseum with a guide. He was very good at waiting for my 84-year-old grandmother, and was happy to explain again if she didn’t get it the first time (we’re danish, so english isn’t her first language). The place was very overwhelming and impressive, compared to the other monuments we’ve visiter here in rome. Can recommend.

title: A must to see in Rome!
text:
A must to see in Rome. Here is what I learned and I am sharing:

-Check the weather if it is too hot avoid if you can. There is 1 washroom and not lots of water fountains

-Book a tour, a private if you can, as the tour groups usually are too large.

-There aren't lots of signs in English explaining the site, and that is why a guide tour is the best way to visit

-This place is huge you need a day to visit everything. Do not book anything else, do part in the morning and the other part in the afternoon. 

-The day we went it as +38oC we gave up around id day was it was too hot. I imagine that if it is rainy is won't be fun as well.

title: Do it on your own
text: Tour organisation was catastrophique. Bazar, different prices for the same service. Chaos. Don‘t  buy your ticket on the exit of the Metro station, it is more expensive.

title: Coliseum Roma
text: It's part of the beginning of how our world has been shaped. Some knowledge of how and why the Colosseum was created definitely hightens the experience .

title: Don’t Trust Your Roma Pass
text:
Rome, as always and as you would expect; is awesome!
The Roma Pass is however a total waste of money! Even though it promises free admission to attractions, it doesn’t tell you that you need to prebook for everything (& with very limited capacity at all attractions).
Colosseum didn’t have a ticket available for at least a week (unless you paid an extra €95 for a guided tour!).
Roma Pass what a waste!!

title: Colosseum, Forum and Palentine Hill  Tour
text: Excellent tour - skipping the queues and brilliant tour guide Eddy kept us informed and interested throughout the tour. Well worth doing for the insights into Ancient Rome. Great for adults  and teenagers ~ interesting and fun so everyone  enjoyed

title: Nice
text: Easy and fast entrance. You should be there 10-15 min before your scheduled hour. We arrived 10 min before and had some time for pictures. Fast entrance and them security check. All was fast and the audio guide worked perfectly.

title: Amazing but very crowded
text: Breathtaking when inside, the audio/video guide was worth the extra money. However, the crowds both outside and inside are huge. The entrance is very crowded with dubious looking men trying to sell tickets while wearing ‘tourist information’ badges. Had to book my self-guided ticket through an online agency as the official website were sold out. Couldn’t get the arena floor, but I don’t feel I really missed out. The large tour groups inside are a nightmare, with tour guides stopping everywhere and blocking the way.  Lots of disorganised queueing to get in, bag check, ticket scanned and to collect the audio guide.

title: Excellent site. Terrible website and organization.
text:
We were really excited with the actual site, words are not enough.
But...
We got our tickets from the website and it was obvious kids do not have to pay. So we bought 2 tickets for adults priced for 36 euros, and 1 ticket for our daughter priced for 0 euros. We received the ticket on line, but there was no mention about the ticket of our daughter.
We went to the actual site and we were refused entrance because our daughter had no ticket.
We had to buoy line via a smart phone a new ticket, again for 0 euros, just to get a fresh bar code for our daughter, and then we were allowed to entry.
Indeed silly.

title: Amazing Colsseum
text: Just an amazing site.  The scale of it. Hire Valentina S. from Tours By Locals.  She is an archeologist.  She gives you a great one on one experience. She is a Roman.

title: Highlight of our trip
text: It was our biggest highlight of rome trip. We really enjoyed all parts of antic city. Really worthy for that price.

title: One of the best tours ever!
text: Great tour.  Make sure you can walk.  The guide was very informative and kept every flowing smoothly.  I would recommend this tour to everyone as long as you can walk a good bit and climb some fairly high steps.  I would not have missed this for anything!

title: Rome visit
text: On my must list for a Rome visit.  It was beyond amazing.  Went with a local guide that was amazing and offered so much information and detail to the site.  This is a must when in town.

title: Always book ahead.
text: I bought a 3 day Roma Pass. I was told that included entry into the Colosseum. When I got to the colosseum, I was denied entry because I had not booked. At no time was I informed that I had to book. I carefully checked for any conditions to entry. All time slots were taken up for that day and I was leaving Rome the next day.  As a result, I never got inside the Colosseum. Very disappointing.

title: Colosseum the only one
text:
Nice customer service affordable prices, it was a fantastic experience! 
The guided tour was interesting and not so long or boring like others

title: Great experience
text: We booked the Arena Floor Special Access Tour of Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill. Had a great guide, Maya, who explained us a lot of the history and art of those places. Loved it!

title: Coliseum tour
text: Amazing organisation, fast passing, low prices, best customer service, I highly recommend, perfect audio guide

title: Hard to put into words
text: This place is hard to describe.  It is almost unfathomable to comprehend.  It was built so long ago and it is still standing (to a degree).  Very impressive to think that man built this structure without earthmovers and cranes.  There is a "lift" for those who are over 65 or people with disabilities.  So impressive!

title: Rome- Colosseum and Roman Forum Tour
text: The Colosseum and Palatine Hill tour is a must when you are in Rome! This tour gave such great insight into the history of ancient Rome and how it has influenced our world today. Elizabeth from the Headout Tour was an amazing tour guide. As an archeologist, she was so knowledgeable about both the contruction of the Colosseum and the history as well as the past and current excavation sights. Definitely worth experiencing!

title: So much history to learn/experience
text: Book ahead with a tour guide so that you can skip the lengthy lines waiting to get in, and also so you can be assured of entry on the date/time that you want. There is so much history to learn about the Colosseum that it would be difficult to really appreciate it without a guide, unless you do some homework and study up on it before you go.  We learned a lot, and the only criticism that I had about our guide is that she was far too "cheerful" when discussing the gory public deaths that occurred as "entertainment" for the Romans.

title: A must see but beware of touts
text:
A must see but very crowded. Allow plenty of time for security and to find your guide if having a tour  - and be careful of touts. We booked tickets for entry and a guided tour for 23 euros each from the official site CoopCulture.  The tickets said the meeting point would be at 'Sperone Valdier'. We never found any sign saying this. We asked two people with seemngly official badges and both misleadingly claimed that our tickets were only for entry and did not include a guided tour, which  would cost an extra 20 euros. But when we just went to the entry point that most people seemed to be using, we were at once told that our tickets did indeed include the tour and directed us to a meeting point inside the Colosseum. The tour was good though the crowds hampered it to some extent. 

If I we were booking a guided tour again, I would in preference book one for the Forum, where a guide would have been more vaulable, given the wide range of monuments from different periods there.  An audio guide would suffice for the Colosseum for those who need it.

title: Beautiful
text: What a beautiful place to visit. We were in Rome for a trip and this is a must see in Rome. Just walk around the Colosseum and take beautiful pictures

title: Amazing History for you and all
text: We took a tour of the Colosseum and we were amazed at every turn of this ancient place of sports and entertainment.  We enjoyed every detail our guide gave us and we enjoyed some great eating in the city which made our day in Rome a great one.

title: There is a reason that the Colosseum is on every list
text: This surviving example of Roman life is a marvel, with a rice and varied history.  Having tickets and being with a guide is a must.  Be sure to visit the surrounding archeological area as well.  Note that the crowds can be quite large.

title: Overall lovely experience, very informative, detailed & covered virtually everything👍👍👍👍👍👍 👏👏👏👏👏👏👏👏👏🍾🍾🍾🍾🍾🍾?
Great visit!
text: We visited the colosseum having purchased tickets online prior. Entering was quick and easy. We decided to rent an audio guide for €6, which had information and videos to watch, explaining the different aspects of the colosseum. The audio guide taught us a lot about the day to day runnings of the colosseum and what the different parts of the colosseum were used for. We enjoyed walking around the at our own pace and playing the videos when we wanted to. We very much enjoyed our visit.

title: Exploring the antiquities of Rome
text: Sondra was our guide for the day, and she was exceptional! Very well versed in the history of Rome, and she provided remarkable insights. I can't emphasize to you enough what a fantastic job she did.

title: When in Rome you have to experience the colosseum!
text: Great tour… Maria our guide was very knowledgeable and more importantly you can tell she loved her job and history.

title: Get tickets at kiosk and not touts
text: We were greeted by several ticket touts with official tourism badges. They kept saying kiosk is closed, It wasn’t! You can’t buy ticket at the kiosk but they do direct you to official website and we booked tickets that way at 72 euros. Be prepared to spend the whole day there and bring food. No cafe inside but plenty of free water. Don’t book a tour guide, most information they were saying is all on Wikipedia and in the info guides.

title: Crowded beauty
text: It's a good idea to take a guided tour of this beautiful building to be able to appreciate its history as well as to skip the lines. Very photogenic place but extremely crowded even in late October.

title: Tour of coliseum
text: We had a guided tour with Marco the 1st in the colosseum, it was a rally educational and well explained, we learned a lot about Roman history, the historical

title: Recommend
text: Super fast and safe way to get tickets. Recommend  for 100%. Great experience and no long lane to enter.

title: Icon of Rome
text: This is a must see when you are in Rome. You can see it from the outside for free, but worth taking a tour to get the full history. Apparently no Christians were ever killed here, which is interesting.

title: Excellent guide, amazing place
text: Booked through Crown Tours, guide was Guiseppe. Absolutely excellent, worth every penny. So well informed he made every aspect interesting. It was a hot day (27 degrees) and the group had lots of questions so the tour ran over a bit, but he stayed cool and calm throughout. The Colosseum was just mind blowing. The Forum and Palatine Hill could have been overwhelming but actually the guide made it all clear. We took other tours during our visit to Rome, but this was without doubt the best.

title: A must!
text: Awesome places you must to visit (Colosseum, Forum, Palatino). Love the audioguide: walking around by myself and spending the time that I want and that I prefer on each monument, was the best decision!

title: Eye opening experience
text: Had an excellent guide who was also an archaeologist!  The tour of what would have been “under the stage” incredible.  Well worth seeing!  Crowds were big, but we did get to see everything we wanted!

title: Don’t miss this,
text: Still can’t believe we were here. No words can explain the magnificence of the creation of this structure in those times.

title: A piece of history which makes your hair stand on end
text: The Colosseum is easily reached via Metro -  Colosseo - just a few minutes away. Advise to buy tickets online in advance, you can pay a bit extra which will get you admission to the ground floor. Most tickets will include Roman Forum and Palatine Hill as part of the admission you will need an entire day to spend at these sites. There is security at the entrance you will get your bags checked. The basic ticket admission will get you to a couple of levels and this is enough to get a feel of the arena and to get great pictures. An amazing place to visit - did not dissapoint

title: Wonderful experience
text: Wonderful excited experience. No waiting in the queues, I've bought slip the line ticket online and I strongly recommend that.

title: Incredible Tour
text: It was incredible getting to tour the Colosseum. We got a guide through Get Your Guide to do the Roman Forum, Palantine Hill and the Colosseum. This was so absolutely worth the money and the guide was fantastic. Plan on touring these areas for at least half a day.

title: Colosseum at night
text: The colosseum at night is 100% the way to do it!! The hour spent with the guide before outside the colosseum wasn’t needed and although she was good it was information overload. However it was definitely worth it to see the colosseum at night all lit up and quiet as it’s 1 group at a time. Meeting point is Ultimate Italy which was confusing.

title: Classic
text: Well, if in Rome you cant really miss out on the place where the gladiators fought with just about everything that was thrown at them! Again do get a guide or an audio guide to make the experience even better

title: Absolutely fascinating
text: Had a brilliant time going underneath the arena floor to the underground to have a look and learn about everything to do with the colosseum and it's history. Had a tour guide for this and would highly recommend.

title: All was great! Definitely recommend it!!
text:
The tour was great, as was our tour guide, Scott!
We definitely recommend it!!

All the places were amazing and we learned a lot!!

title: Wow, wow, wow
text: This is a magnificent monument. Definitely worth a visit. Allow yourself a couple of hours as you will not want to rush this. Just fabulous

title: Fabulous
text: It’s immense! So much bigger than you imagine and so much history. The audio guide was great, just could’ve done with bit more guidance as to where to start

title: Great experience at the ancient Rome
text:
It was amazing.
We arrived just in time to the check-in location. All very organized in different groups. Our host, Barbara, did a great job explaining in details each place, specially the Colosseum.

title: Appericate the history, rude tourists, not so much
text:
The Colosseum is beautiful, it really is and has amazing history, you can not take that away from there.

However, tours guides and people on tours do not care for you, they will block entrances/exits, they will blank you, shove you, my son got hit in the face with someone's backpack, no sorry, nothing, it was crazy in there and seemed more of a fight to find a space to appericate the history!

I am unsure why tours could not have a board up with images like the Vatican to explain parts, why there isn't staff around making sure that crowds more along or least not block places and why people are allowed massive backpacks in there and use them as a way to shove adults and children out their way! 

The queues were also confusing with people randomly lining up outside and not actually queuing for anything it turned out (lol), everyone seemed to join them.

We went during the evening in the end but not inside and it was stunning! Lite up, quiet, just lovely so I would recommend a night tour or be prepared even when you book in the late afternoon, its still very busy! 

I would definitely come back but not for a tour or to go inside, the outside around there is beautiful!

title: look at my review
text: An unforgettable experience of being able to experience and see ancient Rome for yourself. Special thanks to our guide Alba from Grand Tours SRL She offered us an unforgettable tour of the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill with her descriptions and personal storytelling method. Thank you Alba and all included Organisations to realize this.

title: WOW
text: This is AWESOME.  It is unbelievable what this was like.  Would have held up to 50k people.  Having a guide was important to get the full effect

title: Unmissable but book early and beware the signage
text:
Booking tickets was a bit of an ordeal with so many providers/offers/combinations etc. but got there in the end. Try the coopcultute.it site first but don't leave it too late though, only a handful of tickets available the week before we travelled. Under 18's are free and the fact they had a different time slot to the rest of us wasn't an issue for the person on our gate. 
There's a lot of building work nearby which meant the signage to Forum/Palatine was all over the place too with some pointing in the opposite direction to the actual entrance. 
Once in, the arena upgrade is absolutely worth it, only €6 more and allowing access through the tunnel out onto a large platform with bench seating and giving a sense of the 'all around' experience. Did a self your with a guidebook to give the highlights so was able to go at our own pace and soak it all up. Unmissable!

title: Fantastic
text: Amazing experience, amazing tour guide who was absolutely fantastic I could have listened and walk around all day long. Would recommend this to anyone who has a trip planned to Rome

title: Knowledgeable guide
text: Yes, good support, the Colosseum experience flowed smoothly , guide was very knowledgeable , she took the time to explain thoroughly

title: Rome through headout
text: It was one of a kind experience and the staff members have been very helpful all throughout the process

title: Colosseum is a must-see in Rome
text:
Very good tour. Just a must have in Rome. 
We were visiting Colosseum with arranged group, with a guide. I usually visit such places on my own, but I was  happy with guided tour, as I could visist the arena level and learn a lot more than just about the Colosseum.

title: Beware of Covid
text:
Just tested positive for covid and tried to amend dates then received this

Dear Sir/Madam,
we are sorry to inform you that it is not possible to amend a reservation beyond 24 hours after the payment.  As stated in the conditions of purchase, services are not changeable or refundable except for reasons directly attributable to Coopculture.
Best regards.

Money wasted, surely they should change their t and c’s and not encourage people with covid to attend and spread the infection.

title: Amazing
text: Amazing to walk up on this ancient ruin in the middle of a busy city. Get there right when they open as the crowds are overwhelming later in the day. Beautiful location along with the Forum and the Palatine Hill. Not much to see so you have to use your imagination. They have tours and audio guides for sale. We used the free Rick Steves app. and that was enough for us. If you are really interested in the Colosseum the Rick Steves app will not be enough for you.  Obviously a must see in Rome.

title: Must see
text: An epic experience exploring the Colloseum morning when the weather was lovely. Lots of history and iconic visuals combined with your imagination and an audio guide, making for a really interesting time.

title: A 'must see'
text: If you don't see this wonder of the ancient Roman times when you're in Rome, you may as well not go. Take the tour with a guide - you won't regret it.

title: Colosseum Forum Tour
text:
Our guide Lea was fantastic. She was very knowledgeable and shared many stories about life in antique Rome and not only limited to Forum/Colosseum. She even managed to keep the younger participants engaged with her energy and storytelling.
We felt as if we were part of Roman society 2000 years back.
Many thanks,

title: A great tour.
text: The guide was great .😊 And the tour wonderfull. A lot to see. A lot to visit. A great experience...

title: Great tour and entertaining
text: Barbara was very professional, knowledgeable and entertaining. Thank you for the great tour of the colloseum.

title: Saved the day
text: Paolo was an awesome guide he was like having a history teacher you wanted to learn From. We almost missed out on this sight because of all the changes after Covid and pre purchasing tickets. but found carpe diem Rome and they saved the trip we were able to find last minute tour for a great price. I highly recommend loved the small groups too.

title: Awesome trip with head out in rome
text:
3 hour tour with head out in rome
Awesome tour wonderful guide
Great knowledge 
Very informative and enjoyable

title: Excellent
text: The guide was very good, interesting explanations, convinued venue, easy to byu tickets and find the place

title: Lots of fun and great experience for all!
text: I’m not sure how we actually got in: we didn’t book tickets and met some random tour guides outside who we paid €35 each and had a 2 hour tour around the colosseum. It was the most fun and the tour guide was hilarious. I would in the future book in advance as I think we got lucky and lots of others are just trying to take your money. It was a fantastic day and we even got to go into the Roman forum which was also included, we were very lucky. We waited for about 2 minutes in the queue before getting in. The day after, the queues  looked miles long so we were grateful we got to go when we did.

title: when to visit
text: By far it is better to see the Colosseum at night when it is flood lit and there are fewer people. if you travel between June and September i suggest a night visit only. There are night/ evening visits as tour guides. we did not do the tour guide.

title: Great way to visit!
text: Really a great way to discover this emblematic place, to have all the information ans skip the lines

title: must see!
text:
on of the most wonderful places on earth! 
it is one thing to watch it on tv, or see pictures of it, but walking towads it, it becomes bigger and bigger, and it is so full of history and beauty!
rome is an incredible city and the colosseum is the most interesting place in it!
I recommend it 100%. go, you´ll see i´m right

title: Felt disingenuous but tour was good
text: We bought these tickets last minute after following signs for the ticket booth. We paid €40 each for a guided tour, other reviews have stated different prices for the same tour so be careful for that. Before the tour started we were told some story about how the police had contacted them the previous day saying they found some of the tour radios in the bin. So, for us to get the head sets we had to give a form of ID for the tour guide to keep as “precaution” so we wouldn’t steal the radios. This felt really disingenuous as we overheard someone say they needed to take ID from one of the other language groups with a different story. We should have been told this before paying the money for the tour as one couple didn’t have ID and were refused a headset. Inside the colosseum is obviously very busy and the tour guide had nothing to hold up so we couldn’t identify her, so many of us were lacking behind from the group struggling to find the guide. The tour itself was good and the tour guide was very knowledgeable. All in all 3* is generous as from the get go the whole thing felt a bit disingenuous but as we were last minute buying tickets this was our only way inside. We did get our ID back but be aware they will ask for this.

title: Civitatis Provided an excellent tour from start to finish.
text:
Went on the gladiators gate tour with Civitatis and can thoroughly recommend it.

The whole tour was excellent. Very knowledgeable and friendly guide who took us through all aspects of the tour at a manageable pace. 

Stops for explanation were well spaced and time was plenty to fully enjoy all aspects of each part of the attraction.

title: A great experience
text: Great day visiting colosseum, forum and Palestine hill. Lots to see- all very impressive. A fair number of stairs involved ( there is an elevator at colosseum). Highly recommend. Good to read up on history first.

title: Such a masterpice
text:
Such a lovely day and informative one !
With an a beautifull and great guide !
The colosseum its a must do thing in life , and a most visit when in Rome !

title: Amazing building but good to understand the history
text: We visited with a tour and I think we got more from the visit than if we had visited on our own even with an audio tour.  Amazing to see the different levels and understand the reason behind all of these.  Well worth a visit.

title: Great tour
text: The guide was very knowledgeable and approachable. Gave us enough time to soak up the view and take pictures too. Rome is an unbelievable city.

title: A 'must see' if you are in Rome.
text: Timed tickets for entry can be hard to come by, and as of September 2022 were not even made available until exactly 30 days prior to our planned visit day.  We followed our professional guide's advice to purchase tickets that came with an audio guide (about 5 Euros more than the base ticket), which were available when the base tickets were sold out in the first 10 minutes after going on sale (9AM Rome time, 30 days ahead).  With our guide, we didn't need the audio tour.  We also didn't spend extra money for the "arena" access - mostly a waste of money.  Tickets include one-time access to the Palatine Hill and the Forum (good same day or next, but only once).  Truly a  marvel of ancient Rome, and much larger and more complete than expected.  If  you are in the area after dark, it's worth a look when lit at night (exterior view only).

title: Part of a tour booked through Viator.
text: Highly recommend using Viator to book a walking tour of Rome. Fantastic service and request Paola! She was so knowledgeable and passionate about the city and its history

title: You can’t go to Rome without visiting the Colosseum
text: You really can’t go to Rome without visiting the Colosseum, it really is a must see. Packed with so much history it’s so very interesting. As it’s name suggests the structure is colossus, seeing it in pictures and on tv can’t prepare you for just how huge it is in real life. Queues for entry can be long especially around mid-morning, we found it best to pre-book our tickets and managed to drop on tickets that included the arena floor, plus a 2 day pass to the Roman forum, booked through the Colosseum website.

title: OMG! The COLOSSEUM!
text: It feels surreal to be in the presence of this iconic remnant of ancient Rome. We happened upon it one evening at sunset, and it seemed to glow as if aflame - stunning.

title: Excellent
text: Our guide Eddy was excellent. Really informative and made the tour even more interesting! We loved the Colosseum tour and highly recommend booking with head out. You can do the tour yourself you but you won't get the full experience. We recommend going early in the morning to beat a lot of the crowds then have the entire rest of the day to explore and enjoy. Definetly go for a guided tour. Having the earphones so you can hear the guide as well was also a brilliant addition!

title: Go at night
text: Toured the floor of the colosseum at night, fewer tourists and fun to see it all lit up. The guide talked too fast for me to catch much of what he was saying, but still enjoyed the experience.

title: Beautiful
text: Beautiful and worth traveling to see this spectacular structure! Loved it and definitely get a guide to explain everything.

title: Beware guided tour scammers
text:
Beware scammers!
As we approached the entry a very insistent guy conned us into a guided tour, said it was about to leave that tickets were sold out online and we paid 40 euro each and were given an ear peice and off we went for our tour, it lasted about 1 hour and then we were rushed out and dumped at the gate of the forum, because we did not hold a physical ticket we were unable to spend more time in the colosseum. Had we had a ticket we could have checked out the forum on another day, as doing both in one day is a lot.
Basically have nothing to do with any insistent person who approaches you.
Do a self guided tour and take your time.
Of course the sites themselves are incredible.

title: Last minute tickets from Headout
text: Best part was we got last minute tickets for the day tour at the Colosseum and others, and able to do self-guided tour as expected! Very much legit!

title: Amazing
text: Really a fantastic ancient sight to see.  We took a guided tour, and appreciated skipping the line, and having an English speaking tour guide sharing stories of ancient life.  Very interesting.

title: Awesome experience
text: The experience was perfect, my friends and I were impressed by the history of the place. We will definitely be back again.

title: Fantastic experience
text:
Fantastic trip and our guide was so knowledgeable, helped he was an archaeologist.
We had a great 3 hour tour of Palatine Hill and Roman Forum.

title: One of those bucket list sights
text: Tourists (that is to say OTHER tourists :o) by the gajillion and, once you've done the admiring and taken the photos, it's a great place to sit on a wall and people-watch. Loved it.

title: Absolute chaos even in the low season
text:
Considering this is one of Italy’s main attractions, the organisation is non-existent. There is no central official place to buy tickets online, there are a million third parties all selling different things. 

After reading horror stories of people being denied entry even after pre booking tickets, we decided not to book online and to try to get official tickets at the ticket office.

When we arrived it was swarming even in mid-November. Hundreds of scalpers trying to sell tickets and they all wear homemade “Tourist Information” badges. There is clearly zero regulation by the Italian authorities or official owners of the site.

There is one very small row of (what appear to be) official ticket booths which were all closed when we arrived.

I’ve come to the conclusion there’s no right way to do this - pay your money and hope for the best. If you choose a guided tour, it’s completely up to chance if you have a good or bad time.

We paid the scalpers for a guided tour which was so-so. The guide was a real Italian but he was a terrible tour guide. And he tried to leave us at the Colosseum even though all entry tickets include the Forum and Palatine Hill too.

title: Tour of Colosseum and forum gardens.

title: Walking through History
text:
Amazing experience. We ordered our tickets online in advance. I recommend doing this because we did not have to wait in line. We were directed right to the front with no problems. 

Walking though the Colosseum is definitely a must for anyone visiting Rome. It took us a total of 2 hours to walk through the entire structure. Inside it is very crowded. There are many people admiring the scenery along with taking pictures. Please make sure you dress appropriately. You will be walking around a lot so bring comfortable shoes and loose comfortable clothes. 

When we entered, we opted to purchase one of the self-guides to help us learn the history as we walked through. The guide is a little iPhone gadget with earplugs. As we walk around the Colosseum, there are numbers posted on the walls. When you reach one of the numbers, you turn on your guide and click on that exact number. Each video is around 5-7 minutes and gives you a brief history lesson of the Colosseum. 

You are able to walk along the bottom, but I believe you need a tour guide for that which we did not do. We walked along the outer banks the entire time which was fine with us. We were able to see everything. 

The Colosseum is located right in town so when we finished, we walked across the street and had some food and drinks.

title: A must see when visiting Rome

It is worth buying the ticket online, as you can skip going to the ticket office (even though you can't really skip the numerous tourists). With any entrance to the Colosseum, you'll be able to access the Foro Romano within 24 hours of your entrance. I personally think that the supplement "full experience" is worth the shot, as it allows you to briefly escape the crowd to side exhibitions.

title: A walk through history, but be prepared.
text:
The Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Palatine Hill are the highlights of any visit to Rome. There are many ticket options and much to see. Arriving with a plan will help. 

TIP #1 - Buy your tickets in advance online at the official Colosseum website, where passes to all 3 sites start at 16 Euros. If you are unable to plan ahead and must buy your tickets on-site, buy them from the ticket office that sits between the Colosseum and Foro Romano. There are many private tour guides wandering around offering guided tours, often approaching people already in line for tickets. Their prices are much higher and provide the same access as official tickets from the ticket office.  

TIP #2 - Arrive early. Although you qcan’t completely avoid the crowds, the crowds are lighter early in the day. Tickets may sell out, leaving you with the choice to either come back the next day or resort to one of the private tour guides mentioned above. We arrived around 2pm with no tickets, because we weren’t sure when our schedule would allow us to visit. Tickets were sold out, and we were leaving town the next day. Our only option was to hire one of the private tour guides wandering around, who was able to get us in immediately, but it cost us 40 Euros per person, cash on

TIP #3 - There are security lines to enter each site, with nag checks and metal detectors. The lines are quite long at peak times. Allow time for security lines, even if you arrive with tickets purchased in advance. 

TIP #4 - The Colosseum closes early - at 3:30pm when we visited in November. Security swiftly clears everyone out shortly after. If you arrive in the afternoon, allow yourself enough time to see what you want before they kick you out. 
 
TIP #5 - Unlike the U.S, where public restrooms are seemingly everywhere (and free), they are very scarce in Italy and almost always cost money. Never pass up a restroom, as you may not find one when you need it, and always carry some 1 Euro coins with you, which is what most restrooms cost.

title: Incredible construction
text: The colosseum is impressive. The story behind it and the way it functioned is amazing. I love to imagine that place full thousands of years ago. I suggest taking a guided tour to learn all about it.

title: Guided underground and arena tour
text: The minute we arrived at the Colosseum you see how truly  remarkable it is.  We had pre-booked and went to the entrance for 10.15 am  I was told we were too early and to come back in 15 minutes.  I thought the time on our paperwork was the tour time not the actual entrance time.    Better to be early than late tho so we had a wander took many photos (can you ever have enough!)  and returned at 10.30 am.  The lady just told us to walk between the barriers to the entrance area it was chaotic so many people walking around not knowing where to go next.  We spotted a man looking at paperwork and he scanned our barcodes.  We asked him where to find our guide and he told us  Stand 3.  There was no one to greet us but eventually the tour guide came, gave of us earphones and the receiver unit so we could hear her.  Finally we were off and I have to say we absolutely loved the underground experience fantastic to go and look at it all upclose as we followed our guide while she told us all the history and described not only the use of the underground and arena but with her description you could imagine the  atmosphere in this vast place.  Definitely worth paying 34€ each adult for the English archeological didactic tour.  There was a 2€ admin on top of the 32€  tour for booking online.  Our time at the Colosseum was amazing.

title: Colosseo Roma
text:
I Highly recommend to do these venues with a guide.it makes the tour so much more interesting and gives you much more context to what you are looking at. I do not recommend visiting a venue without a guide. And you can not buy tickets at the door they must be bought online. 
I found it difficult to locate our guide and tour because every tour had the same meeting point. As well on the ticket it was not clear what company the guide was from. It should be written in bold so they are easier to locate.

But overall great time. I loved the palatine hill tour and excavation site the best.

title: Unmissable

title: Must see!
text: We did the open tour included with the ticket, guide was very friendly and we learnt a lot! What a place!!!

title: A million time more iconic than the Eiffel Tower!
text: A visit to this iconic building is a must.  I have been going to Rome on and off for almost half a century and this magnificent building never ceases to fascinate.  Our recent visit was with our grandchildren (and their parents!) for whom my daughter had arranged a personal guided tour.  While it didn't come cheap it was worth every penny.  Our guide kept the kids' attention brilliantly.  Even if you don't go to the expense of a personal tour (which by-passes all the queues), a guided audio tour is recommended, but book in advance.  Our tour then went on to the Forum and the Palatine which were also impressive.

title: Do not miss it and go with a guide!

We took a tour via Travel About and had Laura Antonucci as a guide.  She was beyond phenomenal and made our trip to Rome, which was fabulous already.

title: Roman Collesium
text: Fire the guide! On November 10, we met at the assigned place. We were given listening devices the the signal was fuzzy, at best. I wish I knew the name of our guide, but he was too far ahead to read his name tag. With a group of at least 20, he never took a headcount. At the first photo op inside the Colessium, we stopped for a photo and when we looked up, our guide was nowhere to be found.  Even other guides from your company couldn't find him.  We went back to the meeting place without seeing any of our tour.  I got no satisfaction from your person on site. Calls to your office went   unanswered and no apology was given. Gerald Holmes Please check your lists from that day, and indicate any information you can.

title: Immediately took me back to the movie Gladiator
text:
I’ve wanted to see the colosseum for as long as I can remember and to put eyes on it in person definitely made me feel good checking this wonder off of my list. I immediately started thinking of the movie Gladiator with Russell Crow and how things used to be before the colosseum became a ruin. 

I recommend booking a tour guide to get the most of the experience and to better learn the history of the Roman Empire. As with most places, there’s people trying to sell you entry tickets and all types of other gizmos and gadgets.  Seeing and being able to walk around the colosseum was awesome, the sheer engineering and design alone is before it’s time. I recommend going to check this out.

title: Thank you, Marzia!
text:
Mr E and I booked an English language tour through CoopCulture (we're wary of 3rd party, but this was linked by the official site and recommended by LonelyPlanet). Through this, we got to explore the underground of this fantastic landmark with the tour guide Marzia. Marzia was knowledgeable, professional and very organised - our fellow tourists were excellent company too. You could just imagine the history here.

The Colosseum is undergoing some restoration works currently - in the words of Marzia, undergoing a shower and a shampoo - but this didn't impact our trip. Buying through CoopCulture this way also got us access to the Palatina and the Roman Forum.

If you come to Rome this is a definite must-see. The only downside I can think of is locating the entrance, it's not signposted very clearly, but all the staff were very helpful and once we were in, there were no issues. Bring some decent walking boots!

title: Amazing Colosseum!
text: Rosy’s underground colosseum and arena tour was simply the best. She is an awesome guide who took extra time to explain the marvels of the colosseum. Her passion and knowledge about the colosseum was evident. We learnt so much from her!

title: Another Must See in Rome - The Colosseum
text:
Get your tickets well in advance. We did a self-tour, accompanied by Rick Steves' great audio guide, which we recommend downloading to your phone (bring ear pods or headphones). We didn't get to go into the tunnels (now exposed) where the beasts and gladiators prepped for battle. That requires a paid tour, which we kind of wished we had purchased. Take you time here. It is crowded, and there are many, many viewpoints, all worth a visit. Also be prepared for a LOT of steps. Our time recommendation (3 hours+) is because the Forum and Palatine Hill are right next to the Colosseum, and included in the ticket.-- both very much worth visiting at a leisurely pace.

Pro Tip: Get the RomaPass before arriving in Rome.
