title: Thoughts from my visit to the Colosseum
text: Ever since I saw the movie Gladiator, I have been dreaming of visiting the Colosseum in Rome. I opened the Google Arts App, and there I was, standing on the street overlooking the Colosseum. At first sight, its huge immensity took my breath away. It was once an arena that housed bloodthirsty Romans as they watched gladiators tear each other apart. I walked through the trenches built for the Gladiators. Row after row of humble houses constructed out of grey rock were overshadowed by the huge seating stands above them. The stands themselves were stacked on top of each other. I imagined crowds of Romans screaming and cheering for their champions. As I entered the amphitheatre, the first thing that I noticed was the renovation. I was distracted by the shiny steel beams that contrasted with the old rock. In the heart of the arena lies the sand pit where the bloodsport took place. I climbed on top of the high overwatch from where the royalty watched the carnage. Watching from above, I noticed the pits inside the arena, from which caged wild animals must have been unleashed. Then I climbed below and entered the cells from which the Gladiators must have made their entry. Standing in this entryway made me realise that I cannot judge the Romans for enjoying the bloodsport. We modern humans today are drawn to the same violence in action films. We still flock together to watch MMA and boxing matches. This revelation burned deep within my heart as I ended my virtual tour.

title: Truly eternal, the icon of Rome
text:
The first thing that comes to mind when thinking about Rome is the Colosseum and that deserves to be the one. It feels like a spiritual experience being there. Happy that this piece of ancient architecture still stands for the future generations to learn about the past. 

As for the layout, there are exhibits along the side of the colosseum detailing some architectural details of the colosseum including carvings. The gift shop is also in this area and while it was pretty busy, they hold high-quality souvenirs to take home.

Totally worth the admission fee, and don't forget to visit the neighbouring Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.

title: Great experience
text:
The Colosseum
is an oval amphitheatre in the centre of the city of Rome, Italy, just east of the Roman Forum. It is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, and is still the largest standing amphitheatre in the world, despite its age.
The Colosseum is built of travertine limestone, tuff (volcanic rock), and brick-faced concrete. It could hold an estimated 50,000 to 80,000 spectators.
Definitely you have to visit this place , it's a great experience. Don't forget to get  your tickets online so you don't have to wait. Also bring together some water especially if you visit in summer.

title: Magnificent Monument - a Must See!
text: Great experience to explore such a historic monument. We booked tickets in advance, about 3 weeks before our trip. Be aware of street seller scammers trying to get you to buy tickets or merchandise which are not official! Try to get an early slot if possible as the Colosseum seems to get busier as the day goes on. People and bags have to go through security scanners upon entry and you get given a coloured sticker depending on which ticket experience you purchased. I would recommend paying the extra €6 for an audio guide to provide you with information when going round the site. The crowds were vast and made some of the exploring frustrating due to the volume of people. Nice gift shop where we bought a couple of souvenirs at reasonable prices. Enjoyed looking at some of the exhibitions (written English translation was provided) but again, it was very busy so a struggle to read the information around other visitors. Limited toilet facilities - you need to go at the start and then don’t get any more opportunities as you leave through a different route than entering. Overall, a great and unique experience that we would do again if we could!

title: Breathtaking example
text:
Spectacular setting.  Even though I have visited smaller arenas still in use for bullfighting, this one dwarfs the others.  It is amazing to get to the top, and the underground section where animals and gladiators emerged from.  I still have not figured out how they reenacted naval combats... 

Book your tickets in advance.  Crowds are limited, rightfully so.

title: LOVE THIS PLACE
text: you have to see this place. But do yourself a favor and buy a skip the line pass and forget about the tour. They move too fast and you don't see what you want to if you do a tour. buy the skip the line pass and enjoy

title: One of the "must see" sights
text:
We booked the full experience, so got to see all three levels guided by a very knowledgeable archaeologist. (This is not a place to rock up and hope you can get in, even in "quiet" times. DO book ahead or you will be standing in line for a long time and not see so much). We started in the basement level under the arena, then went to the arena floor level and finally up to the "seating" areas.  The removal of stones to use in buildings around Rome meant this area was less well preserved than the amphitheatre we went to in Croatia (sorry, I can't remember the name) which is still used for concerts, but the rest of it was considerably more complex and the input from the guide made it a very interesting visit. After the tour on the way out there is an interesting permanent exhibition, and a couple of rather cramped souvenir shops - they were very crowded, so we didn't buy anything. (If they had a more spacious shop I might have been tempted.) Please note the toilets are on the way in ONLY - you can't access them on the way out! Our booking was 11.30, we were admitted 20 mins before and we emerged back onto the street at about 1.45.
You definitely need to see this once, I'm not sure I need a repeat visit.

title: Amazing
text: Absolutely amazing to visit, overwhelming, you can't appreciate it until you have been. Don't go any earlier than your allocated time slot as you will be turned away. Be prepared to climb lots of stairs, relax and admire the history

title: Brilliant
text: Amazing history brought to life, getting a guide was the best thing to do so nothing was missed. Our tour guide Jessica was brilliant full of info and lots of laughs along the way

title: Great experience BUT book your tickets in advance!!
text: We visited Colosseum during the first day of our visit in Rome and we were surprised to hear that all tickets were sold out for the whole week ahead!! The only option to enter Colosseum was to book a guided tour which we finally did. The experience was indeed breathtaking however there were thousands of people around (we should not have seen such an overcrowded attraction before) while the guided tour was moving very slow and there was not much available time to enjoy Colosseum at your own peace. Considering that we would not have the option to enter Colosseum again during our stay in Rome, we decided to (unfortunately) drop the guided tour to Palatine and Roman forum and stay more time inside the Colosseum for a walk and some photos. To summarise, highly recommended but in order to avoid surprises book your tickets well in advance of your visit in Rome. Early morning time is recommended for your visit.

title: Great experience in the Colosseum!
text: The underground tour of the colosseum was so cool. Getting to go to the underground area was extra neat. My local guide Giuliano was super knowledgeable about the colosseum, roman forum, and all things Rome. He was very helpful with us as well, helping people with the steep stairs and making sure people were safe on the uneven cobblestones. He made the experience extra special.

title: Stunning and not to be missed if you love history
text:
You can't come to Rome and not visit this piece of history.

Do book your tickets in advance and do book an underground tour. These are limited and primarily offered by the Colosseo Parc and some specialist guides. Check the 'didactic' tickets on the official website. Don't be fooled into booking an underground tour with a general tour company: many don't have the authority to offer them and will tell you on the day there's an issue, give you a partial refund and take you in for a standard tour.

Our guide was Eleanor, an Italian archaeologist, who was knowledgeable and bought everything to life - and had a brilliant sense of humour. The 2pm tour started at 230pm and lasted about 60-90 mins, after which we were free to explore the upper levels.

We then went on to The Forum and Palatine Hill - all part of the same ticket.

title: Classic Rome
text: That was an amazing experience. You must buy your tickets online in advance and download them onto your phone.

title: Fantastic I was ENTERTAINED.

It was very busy so be prepared.

title: Amazing!

title: Fabulous from the outside
text:
This is one of those places that is fantastic to walk around and see but once you go inside it just feels a bit of a let down.  Don't get me wrong, it is an awesome building and well preserved/restored with some superb exhibits inside.  The history of the structure is very well told and illustrated and then it's fall from grace and eventual rebirth as a tourist destination. It lacks information boards while walking around but you're probably expected to take a guide.

It goes without saying to book tickets ahead and get there early.  The views of the Arch of Constantine make the visit worthwhile and it would probably have been sensible to either book a night tour or the option to visit the lower levels, which we didn't do. 

 My notes from our visit in Feb 2023 are 'We walked inside and past all the exhibits to the first viewing platform to take some pictures while it was empty before going back to the exhibits.  The Colosseum was built by the Flavian emperors, successors to Nero, over what used to be his artificial lake.  Loot from the temple of Jerusalem funded the building work.

These told the story of the structure, from it's construction with socially designated seating, a sun shade and the immense logistics required to house the animals, victims and competitors as well as cater for all the spectators.  After the empire collapsed the building became a cattle pen, workshops and housing.  The decline continued into the medieval period when the marble was largely stripped and burned to make quicklime and the iron bindings between the stones looted and melted down.  Just re-reading that sentence makes me seethe! It wasn't until the nineteenth century that the value of the building as a historical legacy was recognised and restoration started.

We continued our tour around the second level and then back to the ground floor level.  The size was vast (it seated around 80,000 people) but the arena area didn't appear that big although we couldn't actually see the much of floor as it had been excavated to show the tunnels and rooms beneath.  It's easy to see why it is such an iconic and influential building though.'

My favourite view was from Via Nicola Salvi, watching the sunset and the Colosseum lit up.

title: Collosseo is a must-see
text:
The Collosseo in Rome is simply impressive. We bought the combined ticket for the Collosseo and the Foro Romano-Palatino (valid for two days) and the price was definitely ok. During the main season, a lot of people are visiting these two places, therefore it makes sense to buy the ticket online and quite some time in advance (at least one week). My wife, little sons (5 and 2 years old) went to Rome in August. As it's usually very hot this time of the year, we recommend to visit the Collosseo either in the morning (right after the opening) or in the evening (two to three hours before the closing). Even though it's the main season and the Collosseo was relatively crowded, everything was well organized and we enjoyed the atmosphere there.
Definitely a must-see from our point of view.

title: This is Roma!!!
text: Marcus was an amazing guide! A true story teller and made the Colosseum and the Roman Forum come alive for us. This is Roma!!!

title: A colossus by any measure
text: You cannot appreciate the power of ancient Rome fully unless you see (and preferably tour) the Colosseum.  Recommend you book a tour that includes arena floor access (newly opened after recently completed archeological renovation).  See it in daylight, drive by it at night.  Unforgettable.

title: Colosseum

title: Must see!

title: Very hyped - poorly run, expected more ?!
text:
Yes, the colosseum is spectacular BUT on site chaos and people milling everywhere rather ruins ( excuse the pun) first impressions. Very poor signage - many nationalities visit from all over the world - not always clear information given in English for where to 1) buy tickets , 2) enter 3) order an audio tour on site ? 4) buy an illustrated map of the site or indeed a pamphlet or booklet 5) exits not clear - same ‘map boards ‘ shown all over this site. VERY VERY LITTLE SIGNAGE TO EXPLAIN AREAS & WHAT YOU’RE LOOKING AT! Coming from UK most historical site’s & information boards to read. 

- Toilets - very few - not well sign posted 
- Cafe - drinks, food etc hidden in a few vending machines not sign posted 
- Shop / information centre saw one hidden away - INSIDE the colosseum but not sign posted - shown on any literature 
- TICKETS ( we paid 40€) each - absolutely nothing to show for them - not even a plan of the whole site with basic information 

By the time you’ve made it to the colosseum- travelled from your country / hotel / bought a ticket - YOU SHOULD EXPECT MORE. 

Shambolic - we visited N Cyprus & Salamis was more professionally run - nice map / free leaflet with tickets. Plenty of nice cafes & toilets available and clean. Can’t say the same as this for Rome’s centre stage WORLD UNESCO SITE.

Very sad & disappointing. Took the shine off so much excited anticipation. 

Go to the amphitheatre in Verona !

Beware- steps up to higher levels VERY DEEP & VERY STEEP - again no warnings from outside / before your visit or on the tickets’

title: An architectural masterpiece. Its grand staircases, ancient history and impressive design are unparalleled
text: It's a must-see for anyone looking to experience the grandeur of the Roman Empire. From the outside, the Colosseum is an impressive sight, with its giant arches and columns, and the interior is even more stunning. The sheer scale of the structure is awe-inspiring, and its ancient history adds to its grandeur. Its impressive design and grand staircases make for a truly unforgettable experience. Colosseum Italy is a must-see for anyone looking to experience the grandeur of the Roman Empire.

title: City's landmark, business as usual
text:
Easy to recognize and arrive, always full.  Prebooking is highly recommendable and well organized by hour slots to enter the amphitheater depending what is chosen: the seating area (cavea) only or both cavea and arena.  The Roman forum area is included in the ticket after finishing the colosseum.

The Flavian amphitheater, the largest in the world was inaugurated by Emperor Titus in 80AD in perfect timing after Pompeii disaster to cheer up people's morale.  The name of colosseum derived from a colossus statue of Nero (emulating Rhodes colossus, ancient wonder) which was nearby and it was built over the drained artificial lake of Nero's imperial palace because on those days, people were busy erasing his works and persona (damnatio memoriae).

Sadly years and centuries later, this amphitheater became source of construction material for other Roman buildings and palaces including St. Peter's basilica, looted, spoiled and other natural causes like abandonment and earthquakes, reaching the current looks.

title: A highlight of our trip!
text: Myself, my mother and my two young adult daughters did the Women of Rome Colosseum tour. The tour was amazing! Our guide Michael (a brilliant lovely woman named for her grandfather) was patient, funny and very informative. She was extremely knowledgeable and was clearly passionate about the subject. We learned so much from her: my daughters were especially moved by the story of the vestal virgins! She really brought the lives of these ancient women to life! It was a highlight of our trip!

title: Booking Tickets
text:
The two tips that we found most helpful for the Colosseum were firstly, book well ahead and secondly, book through the official site (CoopCulture). 
There is an absolute mire of sites selling various combo tickets and tours online but it’s best to head directly to the official site where it will be cheaper than booking through other tour companies and middle-man sites. 
The basic admission should cost you €18 (€16 +€2 booking fee). If it doesn’t, you may have accidentally gone onto another site.
Be aware that €18 is only the very basic entry and doesn’t include the arena, underground, level III Level or the ‘super’ sites. 
Even the official site is confusing at first. It has its own long list of options with various exclusions and inclusions. However there is now an excellent guide posted on the Tripadvisor forum which clearly explains each of the different tickets. It’s headed ‘Colosseum underground, upper levels, full experience’ (it’s the first entry on the Rome forum). 
NOTE: If you’re looking for somewhere to eat and drink after the Colosseum, Monti is a great area. We headed here and found a number little bars and restaurants around Piazza della Madonna dei Monti. It’s an interesting area to explore. We browsed  vintage shops and artisan boutiques, stopped for gelato and climbed the Borgia Stairs to see the Michelangelo masterpiece of Moses at the church of St Peter in Chains.

title: Must-see
text:
If seeing the Colosseum from the outside already leaves you amazed, you have no idea of how it is from the inside. Definitely is a must-see when in Rome. Try to visit it as early as possible to avoid the crowd and hot weather (if visiting during summer). 
You will need to book the ticket in advance through the website. 
It is free every first Sunday of the month. (in this case you cannot book it, just collect the ticket directly at the ticket office on the day of the visit). The ticket allows you to visit also the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Allow at least half a day to visit both, a guide or audio guide is recommended. Wear comfortable shoes.

title: THE GLORY THAT WAS ROME
text: The archetype for many amphitheatres built during Roman times, the Colosseum is a compelling attraction. Its exterior has bettered stood the test of time than the interior. You can see the three floors with differing columns: Doric, Ionic and Corinthian. No seating whatsoever has survived. There is sufficient descriptive material in several languages. You need some patience a) dealing with the large tour groups and b) getting a rail view for photographs. Some tours now include the sub-floor area where the animals were kept before appearing in the arena [via ramps]. The combination ticket with the nearby Forum/Palatine Hill is 16 euros, good value as you will spend 3 to 5 hours visiting all three areas.

title: Amazing

title: Fantastic but avoid the "Tourist Sharks" selling "skip the line" tickets
text:
The Colosseum is an impressive structure and well presented as a museum.  Fascinating to see it for real and appreciate the magnitude of the whole building.  This is a must see when in Rome.   

The visit was spoiled however by the 'tourist sharks' prowling around outside offering 'skip the queue' tickets.  We were totally taken in by them as they wear very authentic looking "official guide" lanyards, and flash a screen of the official booking website up showing no available slots for that day.  After we'd paid more than DOUBLE for their 'guided tour', I went online and found out there were still plenty of tickets available within the next hour or two and for less than half the price we'd just paid.    Bottom line is - don't get drawn in by their spiel, go to the official website and book your tickets well in advance (even the day before is possible).  If you're travelling during peak times I'd suggest booking your tickets before you leave for Italy.   

We continued on the tour anyway.  The tour guide was using a childs book of pictures and pointing out the key features on the outside of the Colosseum, and went on for about half an hour.  we realised he was having to use up time until the entrance time on the tickets.  When we did finally get to the gate there was a problem as there were too many in our group and they had to ask some people to go a different entrance. 

Once inside we chose to go our own way rather than follow the tour guide who was really not giving any more information than was already available on the museum boards and information points.  We wanted to go at our own pace.  

Well worth visiting, just go the office website route to pay for your entrance.

title: Highly reccomend the Colloseum Crown Tour with Lumi
text: We went to the Colloseum with Lumi with Crown Tours and it was just excellent. She explained everything in such an interesting way and the tour was packed full of information. She did not waste a minute of our time and it was soooo worthwhile. We did not pay more to go down to the lowest level and I did not feel that we missed anything.

title: Sign up for the underground tour!
text: A few years ago the underground tour became available.  We learned so much and loved seeing the inner workings of the Colosseum.  We highly recommend getting the full experience.  Buy your tickets a couple months in advance!

title: Are you not entertained?  Well, yes I was….  but it took a lot of time and effort.
text:
Is there any structure in the world more iconic than the Flavian Amphitheater?  The Eiffel Tower or one of the Egyptian Pyramids?  Probably.  The Taj Mahal?  Maybe.  Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta?  Ahh, no.  In any case, the metrics for the Colosseum are well known to historians and avid tourists alike: eight years to build with 100,000 workers, spectacles with over 50,000 onlookers, almost 2,000 years old, etc.  Although it is best known for holding gladiatorial spectacles it also showcased hunts, public executions, and even reenactments of naval battles.  After finishing use as an arena for combat in the 5th Century AD, it subsequently served as a cemetery, place of worship, commercial center, and, well, one of the world’s busiest tourist attractions.  

I visited twice during my week in Rome.  The first visit was on a Monday via a “full experience” ticket through the official website.  My plan was to escape the crowds by visiting early in the week, but this was thwarted by the completely chaotic scene I experienced at 2PM.  Organization was poor, tourists were many, and my hour on site almost worthless.  It was almost impossible to navigate around the building; I was able to see most of the ground floor but just a glimpse of the first, and although it seems some people had audio guides I was unable to find one and left very frustrated.  My second visit was later in the week on a Thursday with a small group via Liv Tours; this was the polar opposite to the first.  We started off with a brief visit to the Forum (see separate review), and after pausing to see the stadium in profile from the Temple of Venus and Rome, toured the inside starting from the underground passages and moving upward to the ground and first floors.  My guide provided expert commentary and showed us the best places for pictures, releasing us at the top to buy souvenirs and take it all in at sunset. 

Is the Colosseum worth the time and effort for a visit?  Of course.  It is simply too iconic/influential/pick-your-superlative to not put forth the energy to make a visit, regardless of your interest.  With that as an introduction to the conclusion, if you don’t choose your plan of attack (pun intended) carefully it can be a frustrating experience especially for those who make the effort to fly from the US and don’t plan ahead.  One has to take matters into your own hands and make up for the lack of organization with your own.  I would suggest some reading about the attraction ahead of time and more importantly a small-group tour (Liv Tours is highly recommended) to make the most of your experience.

Enjoy.

title: Amazing structure
text: This was our 2nd visit after a 10 year gap and it still never ceases to impress. It is just awe inspiring the work involved and the complexity of build techniques that must have been used to complete such a monumental construction.  Added to this the history and gruesome purpose of the structure make it a must do attraction.

title: Terrific view from the point of view of a Gladiator!
text: This was such an incredibly interesting tour! Gigi did a great job of bringing the Colosseum to life for us. The marvels of an arena that could almost be comparable to one of current times! Absolutely astounding! Elevators and wild animals and concessions! Who knew? And an egress system better than most arenas today, we should learn from them. Just wonderful. I would highly recommend this tour. I toured the colosseum years ago without a guide and missed out on so much great info!

title: Arena Entrance was Highlight
text: Never been fond of the Colosseum and its "depraved history"--but this Tour was well done. He was informative and energetic and we enjoyed some of the perspective he put forth. And you can't change History--so you try to look for the good in all things--which was our plan from the get-go.

title: Great place - just too many people and long queues
text: Booked a tour through the internet with ‘I Love Rome’ . We’re to meet at 1330 for a 1400 start but it was just chaotic. We eventually set off at 1410 whilst standing around in the rain. It took a further 45 mins to get to the security post and a further 10 to get through the ticket section. The guide was super knowledgable but used a iPad as a visual aid which was difficult to see with a group of 30. The Colosseum is awesome. They have done a great job in preserving this work class, one of a kind monument; they just need to deal with the queues more efficiently (maybe more than a single entry point).

title: Fascinating visit
text: Absolutely fascinating to visit.  We had a guided tour which was well worth it.   It is very busy and booking ahead is essential.  We had an extra person join our party and our guide tried to get a ticket.  He was passed from one person to another and when the price went to 65 euros decided it wasn’t worth it.

title: A must seen
text: Consider hiring a private guide, it is extremely busy and you will get your money's worth. The history is palpable.

title: Do not visit it on holidays - it may be a closed area. Be careful not to visit this site on holidays.
text:
We took a lot of photos. It was a magical place. You can take a lot of photos but be careful when there are a lot of people. 
Be careful not to visit this site on holidays. It may be closed around this area and the transportation might be canceled.

title: A huge disappointment
text:
I'm quite a huge fan of ancient history, museums and exhibitions. I was looking forward to get onto the Palantkne Hill, Forum Romanum or the colosseum. Neither one was accessible due to a pointless booking system where sometimes times lots are available and then some 10 seconds later aren't anymore. No one at the the gates where helpful or trying to assist.
The next ticket available is in 14 days? Do one really has to book that much in advance. Pardon my french, but get your **** together. Rome, you can do so much better.

title: Worth a visit
text:
We had read reviews regarding how busy this place can be so we booked via the Roman Guy tours. Their price includes the option to skip the line. It was indeed super busy when we arrived and the queues were unbelievable. 

The history provided by the guide helped to get an understanding of the history of the Colosseum. The guide allowed plenty of time to take pictures and to just enjoy the place. It’s not one of those places where i would wish to visit again, but now that I have seen it, the knowledge will stay with me forever. 

This place is definitely worth a visit, it gives a lot of insight regarding the  history of the Roman people.

title: Book well in advance and book everything
text: This is one of the most popular places in the world and even in March it’s full. So if you don’t book don’t expect much sympathy. I would also advise booking skip the queue tickets and definitely the underground tour. The whole thing is amazing and we booked for the colosseum access all areas, skip the queue and also the forum and palatine hill. The underground tour is well worth doing as it’s timed and numbers are limited and you get a guide. Our guide was fantastic and she was a trained archaeologist so you get the real story with extras. Also the underground (not really underground) tour is not crowded and is out of the mad heat of the day and with it you also emerge on the terraces which is where the crowds are more numerous. Done right this is a great place to visit, but take my word for it be a Boy Scout and be prepared.

title: Its a Scam be aware
text: Its a scam. Don't waste your Money booking this very experience guided tour. All other tours start outside the Colosseum Metro. Not Tiqets, they meet up over 2km from the venue. Our taxi was stuck in traffic. Tried to cancel as couldn't reach start point on time. We ended up paying again, but much cheaper rate for much better tour from outside the venue. Tigets will not refund. Scaming tourists everyday. Be aware. Its so much cheaper to arrange outside with a tour that actually exists.

title: Rude and inexperienced staff at Colosseum
text: On crutches after a recent knee operation but did not wish family to miss out on the full Rome experience. Purchased relatively expensive Skip The Line tickets 4 days in advance of visit. Got separated on tour when staff refused my family access to lift (despite having 1 other person only in large lift). Unfortunately stairs do not come near where lift is. Left in labyrinth trying to find my way to exit to find my family. Kids were very stressed as I took a very long time to reach them given my mobility issues (crutches). Staff were super unhelpful. Would not permit me to take a short cut to the exit despite my explanation. Apparently I had not paid the extra fee to access this part of the Colisseum.  Could not care less about the tour at this stage as was in real pain and needed to exit. Added 40 mins to time needed to reach the exit point. Spoke to 4 staff members in total. All but 1 were super rude.  Unskilled in customer service and bereft of any compassion for disabled person. AVOID THIS VENUE AT ALL COSTS if you have small children or have any mobility constraints. Second time visiting and definitely my last

title: 100% must visit
text:
It is very impressive and the feeling to be in there cannot be described in any words or seen in any pictures. It’s everything in one: emotion, architecture, views, aura, history… 
The only thing which could be improved is clarity when purchasing tickets, to make clear what you get and which areas you are allowed to visit. However - its an improvement to people, not to the object itself. 100% must see in Rome!

title: A must see
text: I visited Rome with my mum, daughter and niece. It is an absolutely beautiful place and visiting the colosseum was a must. It was very busy and on this occasion we decided not to do a tour. Having photos taken outside and around the area was good enough for us. There was street performers around and the ambiance of the place was such a happy vibe. A must see in Rome. Perhaps we will do a tour when we go back.

title: A perfect trip
text: We paid for the whole colosseum experience - skip the queues, underground tour and arena access. So glad we chose this as it was well worth the money. We also had a fantastic tour guide, Francesco, who shared his wealth of knowledge and made the whole experience more enjoyable.

title: Must - Visit Attraction
text: The Colosseum in Rome is a must-visit attraction that's steeped in history and grandeur. It's the largest amphitheater ever built and incredibly well-preserved, giving visitors a glimpse into ancient Rome's gladiatorial combat and epic spectacles. The underground chambers and passages are a highlight, where gladiators and animals were kept before their fights. The stunning views of Rome from the Colosseum are an added bonus. The Colosseum is an unforgettable experience and a testament to Rome's architectural and engineering prowess, making it a must-visit attraction for anyone traveling to Rome.

title: Interesting and great pictures
text: Our tour guide was a friendly, kind and knowledgeable.  Joining the tour made getting into the Colosseum so much faster and easier, as it is a bit chaotic.  Also, having the earpiece so you can hear what the guide is saying really helps for a great experience.  I highly suggest booking this tour as well as bringing an umbrella and raincoat as it was raining our entire trip.

title: Full Experience
text: Get the full experience ticket and if possible, spend two days or 1.5 days there.dDevote the second day to the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill and on average, one can cover 5 to 6 SUPER sites

title: Marvellous
text:
Fantastic experience!
No ticket lines anymore, you have to get tickets beforehand. Arranging the tickets can be a bit of a hassle but if you want to explore by yourself, get the tickets as early as you can - they can be sold out even a month before. I recommend getting them from the official site! One hint - if the tickets at the time you want to visit are sold out at the online service, don't give up. Sometimes there will be new ones (maybe due to cancellations etc) one week ahead and if you're fast you might just get them. Just wait and hit refresh with patience.
When you get your ticket, arrive at the gate 15-20 minutes earlier to the time of your reservation - we didn't have to wait in a line at all and got in very easily.
Everything worked out very fine.

title: Fascinating walking tour
text: Lots of walking and steps.  The architecture is fascinating.  How it was possible at that time period is hard to believe.  Do take time to view it after dark.  Tours are during the day.

title: A must go to while in Rome
text: Visited with a route guide, which I’ll also write a review on. This is a must go see attraction while in Rome. The views sole view from the outside is breathtaking, plus the history behind it makes it even more grand.

title: Guided tour the way to go!
text: Once again, I’m so glad I chose the guided tour over a guidebook!  Much more personal and interactive.  Asking questions and being shown more details.

title: Once in a lifetime
text: Something you should absolutely do once! A massive piece of history, interesting to learn about. Wouldn’t go out of my way to do it again but don’t regret going!

title: Must visit
text: If you are just interested in seeing the Colosseum and not knowing all the history then just have a walk from the outside and there are a couple elevated area allowing you to see inside.The advantage of taking a tour is that you get a hands on history lesson.It is necessary to book on line for tickets and even then it can be difficult, when you book the tour your ticket is included so it takes the hassle away.This is a once in a life time opportunity.

title: At night with all the light it is beautiful
text:
Marvel at the Colosseum
Rome’s tenacity is best illustrated in its ancient monuments, remarkably intact nearly 2000 years on.

title: Worth the visit
text:
Probably the most famous amphitheatre in the world, the Colosseum looks just as grand in reality as it does in movies. It's massive, ancient and has the aura you'd expect. There are several different angles you can take pictures from, depending on where/what street you're standing on. It's also worth taking a walk round the outside of the place. There's a toilet on the outside you can use for a few Euro cents; the queues to get into the Colosseum can be quite long though so pre-purchasing tickets before getting there is helpful; it can also get quite crowded though this isn't surprising. 

Overall a visit to Rome wouldn't be complete without at least one visit here.

title: Beautiful must visit
text: Beautiful must visit site when in Rome, lots of people, tourists everywhere, take a tour to avoid long waiting time

title: Buy tickets before trip
text: Book tickets before your trip. They will be sold out at the visit day. The only and the best way to get to the tickets without previous booking is to buy a tour from Indian people near the Coliseum. They are a little more expensive, but you will get perfect tour with guide (Italian guide). They are just selling tours and organize groups.

title: Not what you think
text:
I wouldn't bother buying tickets. We had tickets but went on the wrong day, so we walked round and was really disappointed. It's not as it looks online, it's very built up around it and a lot of it has been remade  
My friend booked a tour for herself and her husband. Her tour guide was late and only stayed with the group for 10 minutes.

title: More Insane Crowds
text: This monument is extraordinary but the real experience is that of the throngs of people, the giant crowds, the fences, gates, guards, and the rules.

title: A must do when in Rome
text: This is the choice you should make. The tour guide was excellent, one tour guide called in sick and so we had to double the size of the group, however, you would never have known because our guide went above and beyond to make up for this unforeseen problem. The information we received throughout the tour was so interesting and all with a wonderful sense of humour. We went in April thinking that the  crowds maybe less, they were apparently but still huge. The instruction of the tour of where to meet was easy and thorough. The organisers were friendly and very helpful trying to appease some difficult customers who were really over the top . Beware though, make sure you book your tickets before your trip, there were a few very disappointed people who couldn't purchase tickets on the day. Recommended highly!

title: Impressive Structure
text: You need to go through airport-type security at the entrance including x-raying of any bags.  There are a set of steep stairs to climb up to the upper level.  There is an elevator for those that are mobility-challenged.  There are no public washrooms in the Colosseum.  I had booked an afternoon tour with a tour company and we didn’t have to wait in any line to enter the Colosseum.

title: Excellent City Wonders Colosseum Palatine Hill Roman Forum Guided Tour.
text: The company was City Wonders. Our tour was 3 hours, the three in one being first the Colosseum, then Palatine Hill, then the Roman Forum.  Our tour guide was a young Spanish woman named Amil. She was excellent. She spoke perfect English, very clearly and at a good volume which we heard perfectly well throughout the whole tour on the in ear headset provided. Amil was very knowledgeable and also had a sense of humour so kept it light and interesting and amusing. She allowed time at all the best spots to take photos, plus she also took a photo of each couple who requested her to. This tour was 100% excellent. Thank you Amil and City Wonders.

title: Underestimated
text:
I will be totally honest, like always.
The Coloseum is overrated and too touristy.
I don't think visiting the Colosseum is worthing 16.00€. The price is too high and there are many other things you can to during your visit in Rome. And for free.
For example Piazza Di Spagnia, Fontana Di Trevi,The Vatican (St. Peter's Basilicais free too) and many other things.
But you should 100% visit it at least from outside. The best time is at noght so you can see it illumonated.

title: trip to Rome
text: We had the most wonderful tour guide through the colosseum.  She described the life of the gladiators and  what took place in the Colosseum.

title: Impressive
text: Many many tourists. Book in advance if you can. Even pay a little more with travel agent website, it may worthwhile. Magnificent place

title: Fantastic
text: It's hard to describe what it feels like to stand in a place so steeped in history.  You can almost feel like what it would be like to be there in Roman times

title: Scam, fraud
text: This tour guide is a total scam and fraud. We were two people who booked the tour for Colosseum. We went to the meeting point on exact time, but they were not present and they never arrived. After that we searched whole area around the Colosseum but we never found the tour guide. I suggest you all to stay away and do not book with this company. Otherwise they will take your money and will disappear.

title: When in Rome you have to go.
text:
It has to be done, but bear in mind, even with a guided tour, its likley you have to queue at places. Not anywhere near as dense as the Vatican Museum, but still very busy. 

Great to hear about how its use has changed over the years, as well as demonstrating mans inhumanity to man, nothing changes!

title: Go early!
text:
I highly recommend the small group tour including the underground and the Roman Forum. A pricey tour but you go into the Colosseum before the main crowds. Our guide was very knowledgeable and the underground parts are well worth a visit to give you a different perspective.
A truly remarkable place. I would probably advise if you can to do the tour but go back and spend more time in the forum as you do not see it all and after 3 hours in the hot sun you are ready for a break.

title: Supersite
text:
Fantastic building. Bathroom spaces could be cleaner. A must-go place in Rome. They should provide better access to more areas of the building. Audioguide in multiple languages, including portuguese. 
Family friendly place, except at the entrance, where people are waiting to enter at a given time, but as they wait outside there are many pickpockets and street salesman that are persistent to the extreme.

title: Enormous but the best tour in Rome
text: Guided tour is way better than do it yourself, especially the skip the line type with an experienced guide. The Colusseum oval is 190m by 156m on plan and there are amazing sites to view on several levels. There are some lifts too. Worth combining  your tour with the Roman Forum adjacent to it. Best day ever!

title: Must go for first-timers to Rome
text: The Colosseum is full of history, other than being a wonder of the world. It is best to join a guided tour, which we benefited from the vast knowledge of the guide.

title: Truly a monumental sight
text: It's an ancient monument that stands grandly in the city center and is a must-see for any traveler. It's an impressive structure, with its four tiers and 80 arches, and the sheer size of it is awe-inspiring. But don't let its grandeur fool you, it's also a lot of fun to explore! You can wander around the arena and imagine all the gladiatorial battles that took place here, or you can climb to the top of the Colosseum and take in the amazing views of Rome. It's a great place to take pictures, too! So if you're visiting Rome, don't miss out on the Colosseum – it's a monumental experience!

title: Self-guided walking tour of Rome
text: We ended up doing a self-guided, ad-hoc tour of Rome that included see the outside of the Colosseum, the Palatine Hills and some views of the Roman Forum but from a distance.  Judy isn't big on museums and lectures so this worked out fine plus our hotel (Golden Tulip Piram - Rome) was near the train station so only about half a mile from the Colosseum.  Definitely walk Rome.  Beautiful city and lots of friendly people.

title: Crowded, Crowded.
text:
I would like to say this was an enjoyable visit, but I cannot.
Tour guide did their best but it was too crowded to enjoy.

title: Great guide!
text: The tour guide Martha was fantastic.  She was very knowledgeable and passionate about the history! She took us through the Colosseum and brought us to areas that were less crowded and provided alot of stories to go along with the information!

title: Colosseum group tour
text: booked with arena tours..Lots of waiting around whilst groups were moved from one place to the next.  The skip the queue to get in was ok. Guide was very good and informative.

title: Must See Attraction
text: I have wanted to visit Rome all of my life, I have a huge interest in anything history related and this is one of the jewels in the crown - a must see. Honestly it did not disappoint. Literally right next to the Metro Station and as soon as you step outside the station it is right there in all its beauty. We did a guided tour booked through Tiquets with Crown Tours and it was amazing - so so glad we booked a tour as our guide was amazing and just added to the whole experience. Lots of fountains giving free water (still and sparkling) and lovely cafes and restaurants surrounding the area. Be warned there are loads of people surrounding the Colosseum trying to get money off you by offering bracelets and water - do not fall for these, unfortunately they are everywhere but it did not distract. Would highly recommend.

title: Beware: Local restaurants scam customers and gangs of pickpockets in metro station
text:
BE VERY CAREFUL - THEY HAVE VERY REAL PETTY CRIME PROBLEMS IN AND AROUND COLLOSEUM

Take care of restaurant scam....

My partner and I visited a local restaurant just in front of Colloseum. We ordered 2 glasses of red wine. The waiter was over friendly in pushing us into accepting a bottle of red wine. We said firmly "no, only two glasses". Next, an over friendly waitress approached our table and opened a bottle of red wine saying "you ordered wine". Once opened and sitting at table, surrounded by people, and tired from lots of walking through Rome we just said yes and accepted bottle. When bill arrived it was €100 for the bottle. In that situation we were effectively trapped since we had "accepted" the bottle. When I questioned bill I was told waitress had offered us the most expensive bottle in their restaurant. Also, they know full well if complain on social media then they can afford not to care since every hour there are literally tens of thousands of hapless tourists unknowingly walking past restaurants who will unwittingly stop (and be vulnerable to same scam)

It is a well know scam: the restaurant effectively corners you and because unthinkingly you have accepted the open bottle then if reported to police or complaint then it will not be upheld - it is a subtle rip off.

To make matters worse as we caught metro train at Colloseum metro station my partner had her wallet and credit cards stolen by pickpockets as we boarded train.

title: Walking tour
text: My husband and I had a walking tour of the  Colosseum arranged by our travel agent. The guide was informative and helpful and answered all of our questions about the Colosseum.

title: Must visit
text: It was incredible a must visit if in Rome it is beautiful, we loved our visit would definitely go again, must book early we went on a tour with Tiqets it was very informative, there were lots of 3rd party tours I do recommend going on one there’s so much history to learn and they all know the answers and stories of what’s happened over time

title: Amazing
text: A must visit for anyone visiting Rome. The lines were long even with skip the line tickets. But once inside it was incredible. The sheer hight of the building is amazing!

title: Horrible
text: We booked a tour for coliseum in Rome. The tour guide didn’t show up I called the company twice. They told us to go to the entrance if coliseum. We did The man at gate said we didn’t have tickets. I showed him our voucher. He still wouldn’t let us in. Fortunately another tour guide came to us and asked if our guide didn’t show. She said that happens a lot. She included us in her tour(for extra cost). Trip advisor was contacted by our credit card company and Trip advisor said we didn’t show!  We were there. They didn’t show up!  Trip advisor will not refund the money. Our first tour guide was wonderful. My advice is to book with a company from the place you are going

title: Huge crowds wrecked the experience!
text:
In itself the Colosseum was all that it should have been. Exceptional historical site. 
However the huge crowds and queues largely spoiled the magic. Even with tickets and hardly any queuing once inside the numbers inside were insane. What a turn off. 
Probably no different to any other site of historical interest in this overpopulated over commercialised world. Trevi fountain; Spanish Steps; Vatican same thing

title: A must see in Rome
text: We tend to be do-it-yourself tourists. So we went there, only to find that all tickets for the day had been sold. So we went online and found an affordable tour. We did quite a bit of walking. I had hoped to be able to stand on the floor of the Colosseum only to find out that the 'floor' is actually covered with enumerable wooden walls.

title: Rushed Palatine Hill was a stupid idea.  Don't do combined tour.
text:
We did "Skip the Line: Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill Guided Tour".

Palatine Hill was first.  It was extremely rushed.
  We even lost a family who couldn't keep up..

Guide showed the first 5 people things from his book, and the other 20 saw nothing.
I have no idea what we were looking at, because guide had no time to answer questions and we were too far away to know which building he was pointing at.
Guide kept saying "take pictures and I'll tell you later" but sometimes he did not.
Someone asked a question and he said "I'll tell you later", but he did not.
This was because the coliseum tickets were timed too soon, and we had to run to the coliseum line.
  Forum and Palatine Hill were ruined for us.
Don't do this tour with 25 people. 
 It left 5 minutes early, and there was not adequate time for the Forum and Palatine hill.
Organization running tour put stress on everyone, by ordering Colosseum tickets timed too early..

title: Amazing
text:
We arrived as part of cruise ship tour & couldn’t get tickets within our timescale.  However, the place is amazing just to walk around the circumference, as is the accessible part of the forum.
Crowds are huge - I hate to think what this is like high season.
Pre-booked tickets would be a must.

title: How NOT to visit the Colosseum
text:
Our visit to Rome was rather spontaneous so when we tried to book tickets to the Colosseum on the official website it was all sold out. We did not want to buy the overpriced ticked from resellers which you can find plenty online and decided we would sort it out on the spot. We visited the site two times in aril - once at 11 a.m and once at 17 p.m. First if all it is quite obvious that the Colosseum is a beautiful site from the outside, it is stunning with it’s size and amazing architecture. You just can’t imagine how the ancient people created it. 
Back to our attempts to get in, at 11 a.m it was absolutely crowded and the important thing is - there is no ticket office on the site! Absolutely the only way to get in is to buy tickets online - on the site there are QR codes to the official website that for us was all sold out. At 17 p.m, near the closing time, it was much less crowded, but still we found no option of buying tickets. There were plenty of local resellers on the site suggesting tour tickets, but we didn’t want to visit as a part of tour group.
So, if you want to visit the Colosseum and go inside, you need to buy tickets online way in advance! Or, pay more to buy from resellers either on the Internet or at the site. 
We still enjoyed our visit very much from the outside since the Colosseum is so beautiful, we went all around it and were so impressed, we also came back at night to see the beautiful lighting. 
A must-see in Rome, whether you just come by to look at it or go inside!

title: Underground Tour a must see
text: This was my 3rd trip to see the Colosseum and the best one yet. I booked an ultimate Colosseum underground and ancient Rome tour for 3 PM in English with the company Explore Italy through Viator. Our guide, Gigi was fantastic. He was so knowledgeable and energetic, taking us to the underground and explaining each area as well as through the Roman forum. Will definitely be booking another tour with him soon.

title: Radu and Tour was awesome!
text:
The whole tour would have been not as great or informative if it wasn't for Radu! He was very helpful, humorous, and just overall awesome! 

Radu is the MVP of tours. Would buy tickets again in a heartbeat.

title: Book in advance- tickets online only. Avoid scammers
text:
We booked well in advance via Expedia and found a ticket which also included access to the Roman Forum. 

There are loads of touts operating outside the area which offer ‘last minute spots’ on tours but they charge over inflated price and there’s no guarantee they are operating officially. 

You can only buy tickets online, there is no official ticket office on site. It’s very busy with booked time slots. 

We much preferred being able to enjoy the site on our own at our own pace since the tour groups are often in excess of 20 people. You are packed in and go as fast as the slowest person in the group.

title: Absolutely wonderful place
text: Wonderful piece of history . Pre booked tickets so no long queue . Leisurely walked around and passed a couple of hours . Nice gift shop inside , and lifts available for disabled people

title: Excellent for history lovers
text: A must see on a visit to Rome. Make sure your visit includes a visit to the underground area. We took a guided tour which included the Forum.

title: Amazingly breath taking
text: Well what can I say? It was amazing from start to finish. We chose to have a guided tour which got us through the queues.  The coliseum tour  was very  informative and exceptional. We opted not to pay the extra and go under ground and we were happy with our choice.it was a brilliant day. The amount of history was astounding. To see the height of 48ft and imagine it was built 2000 yrs ago. Fascinating well worth a visit.

title: Cheapest tickets purchaised
text:
Due to cheapest tickets no access to arena floor or under the coliseum 
Big disappointment. 
Defo pay more for the full experience 

Also worth paying for a guided tour as so much not said on the recorded tour 
Not the collisions fault. We didn’t discuss what we wanted from the experience and did get what we payed for

title: Incredible building
text: Very busy but skip the Que.ticket proved extremely good. The building itself is mind boggling and can only be appreciated by going inside.

title: Poor Ticketing Options
text: Very interesting and a must see if you are in Rome. The only downsides are how difficult it is to buy tickets (no tickets available at the attraction) and there is a lot of modern brickwork.

title: very interesting place
text: small group with a well knowledge and friendly  guide, that explain everything very well,  I enjoyed this tour,

title: Great visit but shame about the ticketing process
text:
We enjoyed our trip to the colosseum although found the ticket process challenging and confusing hence the 4 star review. We couldn’t get skip the line tickets in advanced as these were sold out and didn’t want a prolonged guided tour (these moved very slowly in general). 

On our second last day we found the basic admission ticket for the next day (online on their website) and booked this. We didn’t wait in any queues at all and had a nice tour around the ground and second floor, enjoying the views in beautiful sunshine. 

I think they release the tickets the day before to try and put pressure on people to buy guided tours which is a shame. Overall we had an enjoyable experience!

title: Colosseum, free visit.
text: This attraction was free because it was the first Sunday of the month. The line to gain entry was long but moved very quickly 25 mins max. The colosseum was amazing and well worth the visit.

title: Huge highlight of Rome
text:
The best part of Rome was visiting the Colosseum. It’s size and cultural importance to the Roman Empire is a sight to behold. 
Go with a guide, totally worth it.

title: Overrated Tourist Destination
text: Overrated. Too crowded. You can’t even take a decent photo because it’s like being inside a sardines can, could barely move and the lines were too long. I regretted coming here. The photos you see online are 1000x different in reality.

title: awesome Colosseum, a must do on your bucket list
text: Whatever plans you make on your trip to Rome, make sure the Colosseum guided tour is checked and booked in advance. Key to get a guide who is passionate about giving you an amazing experience. Radu was so much fun and brought the tour alive.

title: Amazing Ancient Structure
text: Loved every moment at the Colosseum, such an amazing experience to see this phenomenal structure up close.  Such a rich history surrounding this structure, we did a guided tour which was amazing as our guide was a wealth of knowledge.  It was very busy so be prepared for that when you visit.

title: ONE OF THE MANY MUST SEES IN ROME
text: Well worth getting advance tickets to avoid the queues.   Only downside was the number of very large groups that blocked specific areas for a long time and the young women that were having strike a pose pictures taken and again blocked the viewing areas for a long time.  If you want a guide I would suggest a small party and not a large group.  Be aware there are a lot of steps but oh so worth the effort xxx

title: Stress at the Colosseum
text: The history of the Colosseum blows me away, but the crowds, even at 10.00am, were hard to bear and the tour tours are very frustrating. We chose to look around ourselves, but it wasn’t signed well, so we selected audio tours.  The voice was pompous and boring, so that wasn’t great.  Other people jostle you, even when you’re trying to take photos, which was frankly annoying

title: Just mesmerising
text:
I loved learning about Ancient Rome when I was little. I actually choked up a little as I approached the place on a bus. I dunno why. But the place is huge! Really mesmerising. If you go to Rome, you have to see this place. 
Just beware of the Africans trying to give you things! It will always result in either my wife has just had a baby or my baby is 1 tomorrow. Then asking for money. Unfortunately, they are at all the attractions in Rome. Most will leave u alone if u refuse to talk to them. So best advise I can give is, just say no and don’t engage with them

title: Buy tickets ahead of time
text: On last trip to Rome we didn’t have time to do the Colosseum so it was a must for us this time and it didn’t disappoint. We booked in advance and that saved time getting in. It is very busy with everyone taking pictures so be prepared for that. It is stunning and something in your life that you need to see

title: Colosseo visit
text:
Nice snd unique experience 
Better go early before 12 and book tickets before your visit 
Very helpful staff 
Worth the time to see it

title: Buy tickets in advance
text: You don't know how big the colosseum is until you're actually there. We bought tickets through headout because the colosseum website sold out in a day. Kids get in free, but you have to go to a different location to get them. It's not a big deal. It's a 5-minute walk. We were in the colosseum for about an hour. It does suck to fight through all the tour groups, though.

title: Colosseum Visitor
text: Absolutely amazing place.. A 100% must see if you are going to Rome. We found booking a guided tour was the best decision we could have made. With a guided tour you jump the 3 hour queues..

title: Crazy how well it still is!
text: This is also a must see in Rome! Lot's of people though. We had a private guide for our group of 7 people and she organised tickets with no waiting lines, so we could enter directly. It is very impressive and with the right explanations, one feels as if being present 2000 years back in one of the "shows"!

title: Must visit
text: This s our 3rd time in rome the beautiful city.Colosseum is a must to visit when in rome.we always use martha as our guide,very knowleagable guide.add this in your bucketlist

title: Gladiators ready
text:
Get a tour guide. 3 hours. Fantastic. 
Book the tour online before you arrive. They are well organised and you can here the commentary through head phones. Weekends are busier.

title: Must see
text: You should definitely visit the Colosseum if you find yourself in Rome. We had bought the tickets online and got in quickly without waiting in lines.

title: Amazing history ~ go with a tour guide.
text: The edifice is so iconic is it very cool just to see it but going through the Colosseum with a tour guide give you context and history that you would otherwise not get.  I recommend a tour to this location.

title: A Monumental and Fascinating Look into Historic Rome
text: Built in 70-80 AD, this historic site stands tall as a reminder of the days of the Roman Empire. It was used for gladiator fights, public executions, and other large-scale events. Its iconic, elliptical shape and the four-tiered seating arrangement make it an impressive sight to behold. Its exterior is made of travertine stone, and its interior is made of concrete and brick. Visitors can explore the ruins, which still contain many of the original features, and take in the grandeur of the Colosseum. The nearby archaeological museum provides additional insight into the history of the Colosseum and the Roman Empire. Visiting the Colosseum is an unforgettable experience that will leave you with a greater appreciation for the history of Rome.

title: Fantastic
text: The Colosseum is everything you think it is and much more. We recommend a private small group guide. No more than 15 persons. Pay the extra to "skip the line". Two days would defiantly be better than one. This site is not for someone who is claustrophobic.

title: A must see!
text: This is a must see when in Rome and a bucket list item for me. I managed to get my tickets online a month before the date we wanted to visit. Unfortunately I was not fast enough to get the ones for the floor area as they went too quickly, but I was happy to get tickets at all. Our entry time was 2:15pm and we got in without any wait. The site is very crowded, both around the perimeter and on the inside.  Although it does take away from the experience somewhat, I was thrilled to be there regardless of how many others were around me. It is mind blowing to walk around a structure that is over 1900 years old and in such good shape. Iconic!

title: Fascinating
text:
We paid for guided tour through GetYourGuide. It included skip the line to the Colosseum, Arena, Roman forum and Palatine Hill. Our guide was excellent, giving 2.5 hours of informative history. 
Absolutely excellent!

title: Don't miss the underground
text: Book a private or semi private tour- get skip the line tickets and tickets for underground.  We had the best tour booked with Viator.  The Colosseum is so interesting and cool to see.

title: Know before you go
text:
The staff and security teams don’t seem to have ever dealt with tourists before. They’re belligerence and lack of basic crowd management skills was stunning. Zero stars for that.

We also got stuck a Viator (violator is a better name) ticket scalping scam that didn’t help the situation. Zero stars for that.

The Coliseum itself is a marvel. It’s more than I ever expected and I had to keep reminding myself I was really there. Our evening tour guide was great. That gets 5 stars.

My advice? Book directly through the Coliseum, go in knowing the security and visitor staff is disorganized/not ready to deal with the public, and but that all behind you once you get inside as it is truly a marvel and worth it. 

Just go direct. No Violator or other scalpers.

title: Perfect
text: Most beautiful thing I have ever seen, I done the night time vip tour, so much more beautiful at night

title: Amazing!
text: Sonya was excellent! She is a student archaeologist, who has worked on site excavating the ruins. She was so excited about her topic and engaged the thoughts of her group.

title: All Roads Lead to the Colosseum
text: A must visit!  Go early to avoid the biggest crowds (and in summer the heat.)   Buy ticket in advance to avoid the lines.  Truly surreal to stand in such an ancient place in the middle of a modern city.

title: A credit to the Romans
text: What a credit to the Romans for building but also the government for preserving such a site, magnificent site and unbelievable that they could build such a structural so many years ago, yes there is a fair bit of walking and some stair climbing so do your research for the least crowded time and pick cool weather as not much shade to be had, but terrific.

title: Amazing trip to Rome
text:
It was amazing. I would love to go back again and take my time looking around. 
We did a skip the line tour. I'm pleased we did because the queues were really long.

title: Historical Colosseum
text: AMAZING! You can't go to Rome without a visit to the Colosseum.  We went on a tour which included Palatine Hill and the Forum. Rich in history - some amazing stories! I would recommend a tour- we didn't encounter huge lines (April) - but we were able to go in quicker with a tour.

title: Queues and queues and queues followed by congestion inside.
text: What a great place ruined by queues and hoards. Keep clear of this place. Just awful queues to get in (even when you have bought a skip the queues tour) and when you get in you are beset by hoards. Such a massive disappointment.

title: A must visit
text: A must visit when coming to Rome. Be prepared for crowds and queues, patience is a must. I had pre-booked a VIP tour of the Roman Forum and Colosseum with arena floor access, I highly recommend you try book a tour like this, was a breeze walking past the massive queue and get valuable information of the history of this amazing building.

title: Well worth a visit, but you’ll be grateful for skip the line tickets!
text:
A fascinating visit which clearly demonstrated the advanced capabilities of the Romans with lots to see. Highly recommend. Also recommend doing an audio/guided tour as we learned a lot about things we would have missed otherwise!

Side note: skip the line tickets are almost mandatory due to the crowds, we were so glad we paid a bit extra for them so we didn’t have to queue outside in the hot sun.

title: This should be a memorial site
text:
We visited the Colosseum on a busy Sunday and viewed its magnificence from the outside. We couldn't get tickets as, apparently, they are free once a month and were at capacity.
A week later, we went back and joined another tour to the inside and the forum next to it. Sadly, the guide was a grumpy rude man who didn't really want to be there! 
But we are so glad we saw inside this amazing place. Do not miss it.
Luckily, the guide dumped us after the Colosseum, and we wandered around the forum at our own leisure. There is so much history here. Be prepared to spend a lot of time here. It is definitely worth it. 
Tip... Beware the touts trying to get your money. If you dont have and can't get tickets, find a tour and ask if they have room for you. Dont pay more than 35 to 40 euros for both. 
So many people died in this Colosseum, hence my title.

title: A Must See on Your trip to Rome.
text: Breathtaking. An amazing feeling of stepping back in time. Go early if possible to avoid crowds and long lines.  The Hop On and Hop Off bus stopped near the Colosseum.

title: Colosseum & Roman Tour
text: Combined tour of Colosseum, Roman Forum, & Palatine Hill.  Would highly recommend combined tour.  Provide yourself 3 hours minimum.  Well worth it.  Tour guide very pleasant and very aware or her guests.  Went out of her way.  Very knowledgeable.  Make sure to book your trips ahead of time to ensure you do not miss out.

title: A Must See!
text: The Coloseum is a must see if you are in Rome. I recommend getting a skip the line ticket if not a full tour. Pictures do not do this incredible structure justice.

title: Fantastic loaction
text: Visited with The Tour Guy on a jump-the-line guided tour. Well worth it. Lots to see, despite the crowds.

title: Start with Palatine hill and then Colosseum
text: We started our tour on Palatine hill  on a self - guided tour which gave us an opening for the upcoming view of the Colosseum. Then we went all the way around to ensure we captured its views and perspectives from different angles

title: The Ancient Beauty with a unique architecture style
text:
Built in 70-80 AD, this concrete amphitheater was designed to host gladiatorial contests, public spectacles, and other events of the Roman Empire. Its impressive size and grandeur is a testament to the skill of the Romans in engineering and architecture. 
Its elliptical shape is surrounded by four tiers of arches, and its exterior is made up of travertine stone blocks. Inside, the Colosseum is divided into three levels, with a capacity of up to 50,00 spectators. 
The structure also features a hypogeum, a network of underground tunnels and chambers used to house gladiators and animals before they were released into the arena.

title: Take the VIP tour
text:
Wow. What an astonishing structure in the centre of Rome. This is not to be missed. We paid extra to do the tour with “Italy with Family S.R.L” and went for the VIP small group Colosseum underground and Roman Forum. 
The tour started on time and the guide was very informative indeed. We all had headsets so we could listen to the guide without any issues due to the noise from the crowds. 
The tour lasted the full 3 hours, so do make sure you have eaten before hand and take some water with you. Avoid the sellers outside the colosseum who will rip you off for bottled water in the queue. 

I can’t thank the guide enough for all the interesting information given during the tour. She really was amazing. 

Be prepared to walk up and down many, many stairs. This is definitely not recommended for anyone with mobility issues or poor fitness levels as it is full on. 

The views of the arena and the underground gladiator and animal areas are worth the additional cost. I’m so glad we did it. I’ve seen it from outside the last 2 visits to Rome but very glad I went in this time. 

You can go without a tour but be prepared to book online in advance and the queue is still very long as you need to take PHOTO ID and pass though a customs type entrance screening. 

Avoid taking large rucksack as they may not let you enter.

title: Amazing
text: Amazing tour of a wonder local.tour guide Amazing so knowledge. Incredibly sites .took so many pictures.

title: Strength and Honour SPQR
text: Pre book a guide.  We did this after vatican, forum and palatine hill...which maybe was a mistake as we had digested so much info up the this point our brains, feet and legs were gone.

title: Rome
text: It was a great experience to get to see it, but the crowds were pretty large and it took a while to get in. Did love the history of this place and would recommend visiting.

title: Building magnificent ( from outside
text:
We visited on 2nd June - Republic Day.
Queued for hours to enter at 2pm only to be told ....even though advertised as FREE on a public holiday we still needed to queue at office for a FREE TICKET ! inorder to enter.
Staff unhelpful.
Signage poor.

title: What's not to love!
text: Its amazing to experience with a private guide! Also, if you need to use a lift, there is an elevator for those with mobility issues. The stairs inside are quite the workout!

title: Disappointed in new policy
text: We had this tour booked 11 months in advance for our group of 13 people with Perillo tours for today. We had the skip the line pass too. Last night at 7 pm we got a call from our travel agent that there’s a new policy in place where they no longer accept the skip the line or honor this as of June 1. So for everyone who has this already booked beware. We were unable to go into the colosseum now as we couldn’t wait the 2-3 hours online due to our whole day being booked with other tours. We were able to walk around the outside but honestly we were very disappointed. We really wanted to see the inside and were upset with the way this was handled. As for the colosseum it’s an incredible landmark, even just from the outside. The area around is very crowded and very dirty. It’s a shame this beautiful piece of history has so much trash surrounding it.

title: Are you not entertained
text:
We unfortunately could not get advance tickets so got up early on the Saturday so we could be there for about 7:45. There was already a reasonable queue and the ticket offices open at 9am. The queue does get big incredibly quickly and can stretch right round the colosseum. Once open the lines moved at a reasonable pace.  Just be aware that people will try and skip in line.  What was brilliant was the ticket seller ridiculing someone who was asking for tickets without waiting in line.  Once we had our ticket we were pretty much straight Into the colosseum. We also upgraded inside to view the arena area which I would highly recommend. It gives a whole different perspective. 
It’s a stunning piece of architecture and well worth a visit.  There’s currently work going on so would love to see the difference when it’s complete.  There’s a free water fountain outside as well which is handy given the heat.

title: Bigger than you think
text: Our three hour guided tour was excellent. The size of this place exceeds expectations as does the story of how it was built and how it worked. Go early to avoid the crowds.

title: Disappointed in Italy
text: We set up our skip the line tickets to the colosseum a few months ahead of the trip. On the day of the reservation (3 hours before the reservation) I get a message that we were cancelled. There is nothing we can do now. The place is booked through our trip.

title: Disorganised chaos.
text: Absolutely chaotic. Poor organisation and signage. Too many people allowed in at once and groups just cause even more delays. The bottom tier is extra but they don't make that clear at all when you buy a ticket. Staff unhelpful when you are not sure which was to go. I actually wouldn't recommend the stress.

title: Wow Amphitheatre😊
text:
Tour agencies book “skip the line“ bulk tickets and it’s difficult to get tickets online. They resell it for extra 20 ch 

Great amphitheatre..Take approximately 45 mins to cover all.

title: Stunning
text: Amazing structure. We went on a guided tour through Get Your Guide. They cancelled original tour they day before, but offered us a 20% discount and luckily we managed to get booked on another one at the last minute.

title: I love it
text:
Italy, “Colosseum” I always want to see it since my childhood and finally I was there. It is wonderful and impressive but worse thing is very poor management of tourist and tickets. That’s why only four stars. 
They have to improve selling tickets to tourist and make it little easy way. For example selling tickets online with timing and limit of one hour maximum inside the colosseum.

title: This company is horrible
text: This is a horribly run business and I recommend you look elsewhere. I booked two tours and they screwed up both. They are incompetent and shady. They said they had a technical issue with one tour and we found out it was overbooked and we were bumped last minute. Run from them.

title: Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill
text: This tour organized by Crown Tours, via Get Your Guide, was by far the best we have ever experienced. Our tour guide, Jan, was informative and entertaining, as well as concerned for the welfare of his tour participants. He managed to schedule his pauses and educational asides in shaded areas and made sure that everyone had access to fresh source water during the tour. He answered questions and even provided information related to other tours that we had been on. Wonderful guide!

title: Absolutely stunning and worth the visit!
text: This was our third time in Rome, and although we had visited the colosseum before, this was the first time we hired a tour guide. The guide is worth the price!  They’re able to bring the stories and the colosseum to life through their stories. Great for a family with kids!

title: Awful staff! Disgusted
text: Staff were awful! Tubes were down so we were 10 minutes late for our allotted slot and they wouldn’t let us in. They laughed in our faces and were smoking and blowing it at us. Absolutely disgusting. Contacted them directly to speak to them about it and never received a reply.

title: An amazing experience not to be missed.
text: Fantastic experience, choose your tour carefully as the amount of information provided can be overwhelming depending on your interest in ancient history

title: Must see site!
text: The must-see site for the visitor to Rome.  We had the Skip-the-line ticket for a timed - entrance  and ran into no problems. It was painless. Online reservations are required! I need not describe the marvel of this ancient  monument — crowds were large even in late May but it was worth it. We did not have the floor / basement pass to the coliseum - it was sold out — but it made no difference for our  enjoyment. Please note the coliseum ticket lets you enter the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill within 24 hours.

title: Seen from above to "all the way down" :)
text:
