After entering with my group was first heading to the "big open area" for just kind of "a preview" (with our guide just staying on one spot); then it was heading up to the "level with displays" not too many in number of artifacts & drawings with the "most interesting" in my opinion being these "heads of statues" in a glass case and which yes are the heads of some of the statues once existed "looking out" from the windows of this great structure (and where I split up from my group); then it was stepping out onto the "viewing terrace"  with what I would just describe as an "understated magnificent" view of the "big open area" (yes in all its entirety from up here which is a lot more than what had been previously seen from the ground level upon entry to the site) and where the best part of seeing it in my opinion being the view to all the way down to the "once-underground area" with plenty of stone columns since there's no longer the amphitheater floor which I'm sure once existed (except for like a deck on one end partly covering that underground area, and yes not sure for how long the availability of such view with their plan to install a "new floor" as I heard on the news some time ago), and yes also great was being able to walk along that upper terrace for pretty much the whole circle and take in the view of the "big open area" from many various angles I would say (and actually going back down to the ground level from like the "opposite side" from where I had come up, but also checking out the "pretty good" souvenir shop on that upper level after coming back in from the terrace); and finally it was a quick walk-around the "big open area" where it was great to get a "closer view" of those once-underground columns already seen from the terrace above (as just mentioned) but yes it was a closer nicer view of them and the next best thing I would say to actually seeing them from all the way down (for which likely a "special ticket" is required).

So yes, it was a "nicely worthwhile" skip-the-line group ticket (for "about twice" of the cost of the regular ticket which is only online with no on-site ticket office) which I obtained just upon arrival at the site's "entrance area" (also thanks to the gentleman who approached me to join the group) and which also included the Roman Forum "ruin site" & the top of the Palatine Hill (with a "viewing deck" & some more ruins) which we headed to next after I'd rejoined my group back outside at the end of the visit here.

And yes, with this site being one of the "7 Wonders of the World" (as per the "currently existing" structures and not those of the ancient world of which only one remaining), really all the more special about this site for me was it being my "last of the seven" visited and which was also among the four "easy sites" in terms of on-site strenuousness (with the other three being the Taj Mahal of India, the Christ the Redeemer of Brazil, and Chichen-Itza of Mexico) compared to the three "strenuous sites" but which were also pretty rewarding I would say mainly for like "an immersion" in their amazing nature surroundings (the Great Wall of China with its 2 sections done, Machu Picchu of Peru, and Petra of Jordan).

title: plan way ahead
text: It took a while to figure out the official on-line ticket site, and when I did, I checked multiple times a day for tickets, two months in advance, and never found any. You can no longer buy tickets on the day you want to tour. Our hotel found us a spot in a small tour for $125 each. They said tickets have to be purchased on-line months ahead of time from the official Colosseum site. It was a very good tour, but I hadn't thought we would have to spend that much money so see the Colosseum. Much of the time on the tour was the Forum and Palatine Hill. This was my fourth visit to Rome. I've never seen such crowds. If you can, go in the off season. We went in late May. I can't imagine what it will be like in July and August.

title: A Colossal Time at the Colosseum
text: From the awe-inspiring exterior to the majestic arcades and the grandeur of the Coliseum, it will transport you back to the days of the Roman Empire. You can almost hear the roar of the crowds as you walk around the ancient ruins. And while it's easy to get lost in the history and grandeur of the place, there's still plenty of fun to be had. From the odd-shaped stones to the ancient graffiti, there's something for everyone to enjoy. It's a great way to spend the day and get a unique perspective on the Roman Empire.

title: This place is the stuff of legends....and EVERYBODY comes here when in Rome
text: What do you say here?  This place is the stuff of legends.  We’ve seen it in movies since we were kids and it’s one of the most famous man-made buildings in the world.  To go inside and see it up close and imagine everything that took place here for more than 2,000 years is just amazing.  You really can’t come to Rome and not visit here.  Just expect that everyone else thinks the same thing and there are A LOT of people here.  Buy tickets ahead as the lines are long if you don’t - and when I say ahead of time, try to book at least 2 or 3 weeks ahead.  Even with pre-bought tickets, you’re still going to be waiting a long time.  If you book a tour, though, you will get to skip the lines and then also get a guide to talk you through it to.  Worth it.  Highly suggested with a tour as there are just so many details you cannot even imagine to think about.

title: DO timed entry and skip the line
text: Glad that we did both.  The lines were very long and we walked right in for our 10AM entry.  We did the self-guided.  Our ticket also included the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum.  A LOT of walking with steps and inclines.  I wish that we had also taken the underground Gladiator tour.  Maybe next time.

title: A MUST See!!
text: My family and I enjoyed the tour here. What a remarkable history and beautiful monuments. This is a must see and spend sometime to soak it all In.

title: Good time, Poor service
text: The tour of the Colosseum was great. Really enjoyed the guides knowledge and the underground access. The business that sets you up with the guide gives you very little instruction and was not helpful. Would have been better if an explanation of what we needed to do was clear or if they had just answered the message I sent.

title: Amazing Day Tour
text: I had a day tour to this nice place; our guide was very knowledgeable, and he took us to explore every corner and learn the history. The architecture is incredible, as is its design. 100% recommended.  I will be back definitely!

title: Great piece of architecture and history of Roman Empire
text: The structure is massive and it tells a lot about the Roman Empire, its architecture, history and lifestyle.

title: The Best Sports Venue of All Time!
text:
With free entry for wheelchair users and modest entry fees for all else, the Colosseum is excellent value for being a must-do in Rome. Wheelchair accessibility is taken seriously, which I appreciated. One can admire the stunning views over the ancient gladiator arena that once was host to the world’s most epic sporting events, and you can also get up-close and personal with the underground tunnels and passageways below the arena if you purchase a guided tour (I didn’t on my visit).

Come early to avoid crowds, but during peak seasons, you’ll still need to be patient. The wait is worth it!

title: Extremely over crowded
text: Very over crowded.  Skip the line tickets will still see you queuing for over an hour.  Overly officious Colosseum staff.  Avoid in busy season.  Walk around the outside, take a few photos, then get out of there!

title: Must see in Rome!
text: Amazing history. You must visit the Colosseum when in Rome! It is truly a marvel of architecture and engineering.

title: Rome tours
text: Seeing the colosseum was amazing. To know the history and see what was accomplished back then was jaw dropping. I would recommend doing a tour to hear and see all the details of this special place.

title: I may give the Colosseum a second chance...
text:
We toured the Colosseum only because we wanted the ticket to get into the Forum for a private tour.  To be honest, the idea of the Colosseum as a death venue grossed me out.  We purchased the English audio tour.  The crowds were unbearable so we didn't even try to follow the route of the tour!  Instead, we found a quiet (sort of) place to sit, and played through the entire tour.  It was well done and took about 45 minutes.  Had no interest in going up or down a level and fighting the crowds, group tours, etc.  We just stayed near the entrance the entire time.  

Our guide for the Forum chewed me out and said the Colosseum is an amazing place if only for the architecture.  Next time I'll do the private tour of the Colosseum with Mirabilia Urbis tours.

title: Worth a visit
text: Amazing amphitheatre. Wish we had the chance to go in, but our travel agency ASA Holidays only provided a photostop outside it. Great pics of the place from different angles. Must visit when in Italy Rome.

title: Toilets were filthy and the attendant man was very aggressive and rude, the colosseum itself was nice to see
text: It was nice seeing the colosseum however my little one needed the toilet so off we went, it was €1 to use the facilities (i would assume this was for the upkeep of the toilets) the attendant man on the turn gates we’re extremely rude and aggressive shouting at young girls if they was struggling with putting the coins in and wouldn’t help, I was charged twice even though I just had to go in with my little one. On entry the place were absolutely disgusting it had never been cleaned, there was brown stuff dripping all into the toilet roll people didn’t know if it was poo or rust from the toilet holders. The floor was wet through, door didn’t have lock just piece of string. The man was then shouting at everyone to get out, get out when we’re washing hands to let more people in. Needs a visit of the health n hygiene people and the man needs sacking

title: A must see!
text:
A must see in Rome! And very easy to get to by metro.
It’s tricky to get advance tickets from the official page Coopculture.it because as soon as the tickets are released they are gone within seconds so it takes a lot of patience and page refreshing several weeks prior to the trip but its worth it. We managed to get 2 basic entry tickets for €18 each which also included entrance to the Roman Forum and Palatine hill. There are options to buy tickets on the day but the queue is enormous 2/3 hours long. There were lots of  ‘unofficial tourist info guides’ outside the colosseum offering tickets at majorly inflated prices. 
With the general 24hr ticket you have 24hours to enter the 3 sites. We were told by 3 different members of staff that we had to visit the colosseum first then we would have 24 hrs to visit the other 2 sites which are close by. This is not the case as explained by a 4th staff member, you can actually visit any of the 3 sites first and your 24 hour time limit will start from the first site entrance. You just have to make sure you respect the entry time booked for the colosseum itself. For example we visited the Forum and Palatine hill Friday afternoon at 13.00 and the returned at 09.00 the next day for our colosseum visit. 
Overall really impressive sights that are worth seeing.

title: Spectacular
text: When the bus turned the corner and we saw the Colosseum rising up in front of us for the first time I think my heart stopped for a second. We got a timed ticket in advance which I highly recommend. This is a main attraction so it draws huge crowds. One benefit of the crowds is that it helps you imagine what it might have been like during an event 2,000 years ago. It can be pretty hot so bring some water and a hat.

title: The Wonders of the Colosseum
text: Despite the summer heat, I trekked from my location in Trastevere to the Colosseum. It was all worth it when I arrived because my visit there was absolutely incredible. Admittedly, I felt a bit nervous at first, but my initial worries quickly evaporated as I marveled at the ancient architecture and historical significance of the Colosseum. The experience of exploring the various areas of the landmark was an exhilarating adventure, and the views were breathtaking. Regardless of whether you are traveling alone or with companions, I strongly recommend visiting the Colosseum. It's an unforgettable experience that will undoubtedly leave a lasting impression.

title: Do not miss this
text: All the reviews are right. Nothing prepares you for this grand structure and the stories it inspires. It is very crowded, it is massive, there are parts where the steps are steep and it is completely worth your time. Must take a guide or a guided tour to get the best of the experience.

title: Still has a massive wow factor
text:
Visited 15 years ago and it’s still an awesome structure. Unfortunately there were thousands of folks on the day we went. Long long queues….  2 hours minimum.
So we joined an organized tour which was more expensive but we only waited 35 minutes and had a very good guide.
No doubt a good guide can make these visits special.

All l can say is that you not seen yet you need to put it on your ‘to do’ list.

It’s a. Magical place

title: Interesting but go when the weather is cool
text: Our guide was wonderful, but it was too hot and crowded to really enjoy. We went in early June and it was already packed with tourists.

title: Well worth the money!
text: The underground tour was well worth the money and adds to the story.  Our five year old granddaughter did exceptionally well walking the 1 mile and then staying attentive to the tour….she even asked the guide’s questions!

title: Colosseum Gladiators Access Tour Fantastic Vantage Point!
text: We took the Colosseum VIP Gladiators Access with Arena and Ancient Rome Small Group Tour. To stand at stage level where the gladiators once stood was an awe-inspiring experience. Our guide was very knowledgeable and shared a lot of the history of the building and events that took place there, where VIPs sat, and how various players moved through the building. The tour group size was not too large. The Roman Forum and Palatine Hill were also great stops on the tour and we learned a lot from our guide about the significant buildings and events that took place in these locations as well. It was well worth the time and investment. Skipping the line was also a huge time saver. Our guide also recommended a lunch spot and even helped us find the place. She was a fantastic help.

title: Too crowded
text: It's still an amazing place to visit, especially with the right guide. If you are going on your own without the pricier priority access it's usually a longer wait.  What I can say is this, I would not go again unless I had younger kids and only for them.  It's so crowded that it was impossible to enjoy the magnificence of the place itself and the history.  Way to many people cramped into the available space to enjoy. They should make reservations required and limit the number of people to preserve the experience for all but that won't happen bc it probably generates time of money

title: Great tour with Alessia
text: We had a wonderful tour of the Colosseum with Alessia.  She was super knowledgeable and also very easy to chat with, open to all our questions.  Thank you!

title: A magnificent place
text: The Colosseum in Rome, Italy is an ancient, magnificent monument made of concrete and sand. Its grandeur and size are breathtaking, and it's a must-see for anyone visiting the city. The building has stood for centuries, a testament to the Roman Empire's power and legacy. It's truly an awe-inspiring site that will leave you in awe. Highly recommended!

title: Colosseum and Ancient Rome tour with Lorenzo at Liv Tours
text: Amazing guide Lorenzo at Liv Tours gave exceptional information about the Collosseum and Ancient Rome. I highly recommend 10/10!

title: WATCH OUT FOR PICK POCKETER'S
text: Be very, very ..... careful for your valuables ( cash, credit cards) pick pocketer's are so professional there... with a blink of an eye they steel and can ruin your whole vacation .... this is a spoken so much , yet it can't be stopped !!

title: Awesome
text:
Awesome!!!
No words to describe this place.
You need to buy tichets from online site.
I would recommended.

title: Beautiful remains and a must-see if in Rome!
text:
We weren’t able to book tickets in advance as they had sold out on all the dates we were visiting in Rome. 

I had seen a recommendation that if you arrive early and join the queue, you can pay on the day. We did this and arrived at the Colosseum at 8am. The queue was reasonable at this time and we waited an hour for it to open. Total queue time was about 1 hour and 20 minutes. We got in straight away, and truthfully, doing it this way saved us so much money, the pay on the day price is a fraction of what you will pay for a guided tour. 

Once inside, we purchased the audio guide from the Colosseum for €6 each. The audio guide was average at best, but as we knew nothing about the Colosseum, it did provide us with some additional information which was helpful. 

We took it at our own pace as we weren’t with a guide. I think we spent about 90 minutes in total having a wonder around. It was crowded, however, it’s literally the main attraction in Rome, so I wouldn’t have expected anything else. Be warned, the tour guides that fill the place up will just randomly stop and kindly act like they own the whole place. 

Would recommend heading there early if you are struggling to pre-book, definitely worth the trip! :)

title: Must See
text: The Colosseum is a magical place. We used I Love Rome as our tour company and our guide was amazing. Definitely recommend visiting

title: Ancient Wonder of the World
text: How can one of the Wonders of the Ancient World not get 5 stars? This place was unbelievable! It is a massive fête of construction at a time when there were no machines to move things and cut things into place. It literally took us a bit over two hours to tour and our tour guide cut some sections because it was so crowded and so hot, but what we were able to see was unbelievable. Our tour guide did an excellent job of explaining all the who, what, where, why and how about the Colosseum. The Romans indeed were master builders. No trip to Rome is complete without a tour of the Colosseum.

title: Experience history
text:
Visited Colosseum along with Roman Forum & Palatine Hill in a guided tour booked through GetYourGuide and had a very nice experience. These places have a lot of history & detailing which is important so I feel having a local guide really makes the experience good and meaningful. 
Few other important tips:
1. Try and avoid visiting on weekend or on public holidays, if possible, as the crowds can swell on these days.
2. Buy "skip the line" tickets in advance as the queue to enter can be miles long.
3. Caps, water bottles and comfortable shoes is a must! There are taps to refill water in Palatine Hill.
4. For Colosseum the gladiator arena tickets are helpful for the overall experience. However, going to the basement level in the dens doesn't make much sense, avoidable.

title: Stunning
text: This could be the most incredible location I have ever visited. An absolute must see. So much history, and so much has been preserved in the Colosseum. There is a visit flow which works really well so you can see so much of the site on multiple levels, with many great photo opportunities. We had a tour guide at the beginning and she left us to do the last bit alone at our own pace. Perfect.

title: Go !!!!!
text:
What an experience me and daughter will never forget !!! 

As you walk around the corner, it pops up out of nowhere.....and it's more amazing than you can imagine.

A dream come true, so much history and such a feeling of being part of something immortal.

Every where you turn in rome, there is something to look at,to explore.

Just go .... and book the underground tour ..pay extra to explore the whole of the colossuem...its worth it....more intimate and will be the best part of yiur trip. Skip the line and be educated by a tour guide.... we used getyourguide.com brilliant and best fair price

title: Rome visit
text: Amazing to walk streets where people walked thousands of years ago.  Ate food that was awesome. Enjoyed the Italian culture

title: Colosseum skip the line tour
text: Anyone planning to book a Colosseum, Roman forum and Palatine hill tour- avoid booking it after lunch as it was really hot but it is a must see when in Rome.  The magnificence of the Roman ruins is unarguably stunning. There are lots of skip the line tours available but be prepared to still wait behind all other tour groups before you.

title: Bucket list visit completed
text:
A must-do if you're in Rome! We visited during the monthly Domenica al Museo (first Sunday of the month wherein nearly every heritage site in Italy is free entry) and so got to the site at 7:15, and only had to wait 20 minutes from opening at 9am before getting our free ticket for the Colosseum, Forum and Palatine Hills.

Exploring and learning more about the site's history, particularly away from the well-known ancient history, was really interesting. Being able to walk around the 'top floor' is such a unique and special experience!

title: This skip-the-line, guided tour is amazing. Hits all the key highlights of the complex
text: Our tour guide, David, did an outstanding job in highlighting historical facts and added color with anecdotes of the Colosseeum, Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum during our 3-hour tour. He made sure to stop twice at bathroom facilities and watering points to keep us all comfortable. The pace of the tour is relatively quick as there is so much to cover but my wife who was using a cane at the time was able to stay with the group oftentimes at the tail end but without missing the guide's oral presentations. I rated this tour as Very Good because of an unexpected snafu: the tour operator, City Wonders, who collaborates with Viator, was short on personnel that morning. Our group max was advertised as no more than 24 people but our group was doubled-up to 49 people. That meant that we were deprived of a more intimate, convenient interaction with the tour guide and the awesome Roman complex. It became very difficult at times to stay with the group because of its sheer size and the thousands of people fighting their way through the complex. While City Wonders promised a partial refund, it took 9 email messages and 25 days to get a partial refund from Viator (City Wonders denied allowing their groups to grow beyond 24 which was absolutely not true).  Regardless, we strongly recommend this tour during your next visit to Roma.

title: 5 stars
text:
Absolutely loved this tour, started off at 10am, a good 3 hour session through the Collesium and then the Roman Forum and the hills later

The heat was something else so be prepared for that, but it was so surreal being in that environment. A morning we will never forget

title: Tips for Colleseum
text: DO NOT get sucked into paying a guide who comes around when you are in the queue and tells you they can get you in for $50Euro.  The queue goes quick and you pay circa $27 Euro for a ticket - including onto the ground level.   It's awesome, must do experience.   Other tip, don't go on the first Sunday of the month - free day - insane.  But, a great experience, do it, and the Roman Forum terrific too.   Both, a must do.

title: More incredible than photos!
text: Incredible structure, and HUGE! I would recommend a guided tour to really learn about the history and understand the significance of it.

title: Messy place
text:
The local government really has to clean this place up - too many scammy tour guides and people with bracelets that run up to you to try to make money. And they get mad at you if you try to mess with them!

Could have been a fun place to visit but unfortunately it was ruined by the people there.

title: IMPRESSIVE PLACE!
text: Truly impressive to visit such an amazing and historically rich place. If you're in Rome, you definitely shouldn't miss out on this great experience.

title: Definitely worth it
text: I was really questioning if I wanted to visit a place which was used as an entertainment for bloody sport. The purchasing of tickets was put off until the last minute, and when I did enter the colosseum it was worth it to see the history and the architecture. Walking in the arena and the underground is not something you will forget.

title: Great day out! Lots of history
text: Great day out. Make sure to get a guided tour and go to Palatine Hill and the Roman Forum. Bring a water bottle as you can fill them up at the water fountains. Plenty of walking so wear comfy shoes

title: A Jewel of Ancient Rome
text:
You can see why the Colosseum is the number one attraction in Rome. I was fortunate enough during my stay to have a few high vantage points to photograph this magnificent structure both by day and night.
Early morning and late at night are the best times to take external photographs of the building as there are no people around.
If you go inside opt for your own self guided  skip the line tour as the queue is shorter and you can take as long as you wish.
It was very busy for our 3pm visit mainly because of tour groups who ruin the experience in every venue in Rome.
The interior of the Colosseum is mind blowing and definitely worth the visit when you stay in Rome.

title: Must see. Worth it
text: One of the finest historic site of world, feel and see to believe it's Grandeur. How could they resign & built this ..... Mind-boggling. Need atleast 2hrs for Collassium only. Go for skip the line option

title: Colosseum and Forum - City Wonders tour with Elaria
text: Elaria was very knowledgeable and friendly ( She is an art historian).  3 hour tour with steep steps and it was very sunny and HOT, very crowded at 3:30.  Cult of Vesta was very interesting.  Beautiful gardens on Palatine Hill at end of tour with wonderful views. She stopped to allow for bathroom and water breaks.

title: Collosseum 2023
text: Visit historical site was good .we had booked a excursion which was very vague .The 2nd part of trip takes you into Roman Forum were the host dud not show up si we was left to explore  the ruins on out iwn

title: Worth the visit
text: The history of this place is so sad, but very important to learn so that it does not repeat itself.  The architecture is amazing.

title: Best part of visiting Rome!
text: Amazing; amazing; amazing; if you visit Rome and don’t visit the Colosseum that would be a major shame; the history of this place is so rich that you really need to take a guided tour when visiting;
A beautiful place with an interesting history of ancient gladiators and animals. I loved everything from start to finish. The sightseeing was wonderful and historic. I definitely recommend

title: Premium coliseum forum Palentine hill tour
text: Went on premium coliseum roman forum Palatine Hill.  It's was over 100 degrees but our tour guide was best. Marco was his name.  Even in extreme heat he was funny, informative and kept everyone engaged.  Ask for Marco  the best

title: Astronomical Colosseum, Astronomical Line-ups.
text:
A monumental amphitheater, the Colosseum, is in the center of Rome. A gigantic structure, it's architecture was an innovative, circular, free standing construction of stone and concrete, a marvel even in todays engineering. 

This arena, in it's heyday, was the scene of thousands of hand-to-hand combats between gladiators and contests between men and animals. It was a ancient fight club with up to 50,000 spectators looking on.

What astonished me about the capability of the Colosseum was it's complex relationship with the water of the Aquaduct where the floor of the Colosseum could be filled/drained, on a whim, enabling naval engagements.

This is one of Rome's major tourist attractions (line ups are astronomical)
attesting to the 7 million visitors yearly.

title: Avoid Show Me Italy Tours
text:
Rome: Colosseum Underground, Arena Floor and Ancient Rome: Several days prior to our tour, we were notified that the underground portion of the tour had ben cancelled. We were flying out to Rome the next day and really didn't have the time to dig too deeply into the matter. After several annoying, 'what do you wanna do' emails, we chose to take the alternative tour offered rather than the full refund as we had our entire Rome tour schedule booked with a driver and guides. We were offered the...Rome: Colosseum Underground, Arena Floor and Ancient Rome tour MINUS the underground portion of the tour. The tour began 5 minutes after the originally scheduled tour. The original tour cost Euro 629.40 (@$705USD for 6 people)
     Our tour began with the Ancient Rome portion. This consisted of a 2+ hour walking lecture of the ancient Roman Forum area in 93 degreeF blistering heat and unreal crowds. NOT FUN. We then walked entirely around the outside of the Colosseum only to enter a seriously long queue (30+ minutes) for our "SKIP THE LINE COLOSSEUM TICKETS! Once inside the colosseum, we made our way to the arena floor and were allowed approximately 20 minutes to take pictures. It was at this point that we said 'enough' and left the tour. As we left there were only about 5 people left of the original 20-25 person group. It took us almost another 30 minutes to find the only available exit from the colosseum. 
     I was given a partial refund of @30% of the tour cost for our trouble. After the tour was complete, I noticed that our electronic tickets had been changed to reflect a different tour than what we had originally booked with the ticket price now
showing the original price minus refund. Never we we told this was to be some sort of educational lecture or that the ancient Rome portion of the tour would be conducted first. As with many people, we had planned to limit our time in the Forum area due to its dry subject matter and time constraints.
     I had heard that many of these tour operators offer tours with tickets to venues that they DO NOT HAVE but plan to get later when the tickets are offered to the general public. They have a distinct advantage during ticket sales due to computerized buying programs and the fact that tickets for these tours are offered exactly 30 days prior to the tour....down to the minute. For example, if you want a ticket to the 10:00 am Underground Colosseum tour on 31 July 2023, those tickets would be put on sale on 1 July 2023 at 10:00 am......ROME TIME!!! This means the tickets are available to me in the Midwest US at 03:00 am 30 June 2023 at which time I can attempt to get 6 tix, competing with numerous computer programs to do so. Not very consumer friendly if you ask me.
     All in all, I think my refund was much too low considering, and they never told us what the new tour would actually be as I would assume most people would say no to the offer. Last minute changes like this to a larger group's touring schedule tends to elicit the desired acceptance to new tour offers. I was duped. Too late for me, but not for you. DO NOT USE "Show Me Italy Tours" who is one of the vendors supplying "Get Your Guide Italy." Be forewarned, I'd give it zero stars if I could still write a review!

title: Gateway to hell, unpleasant
text:
Oh dear. What a disappointment.

Be Aware!

- Don't listen to the indian "gentlemens" strolling the square in front of the Colosseum. They will offer you skip the line pass and rip you off without getting access to actual site.

- Buy only from ticket office or in advance from internet! NEVER TRUST fast offers on site!

- Ticket line is 1-2 hours in blazing sun, no shade whatsoever, very inhuman

- If you have multiple persons, ticket office wants to see them all before selling tickets to you, to avoid frauds

- When you finally get your ticket, you have to wait another 1-2 hours, before you are allowed to enter

- If possible, use private tickets, not guided tour. Guided tour waiting time in security check is unbelievably long

- take plenty of water with you, another set of indian "gentlemens" will try to rip you off with water bottles (3-5 eur each). There is only one water faucet to refill your bottle...

- be aware, that you are not allowed in shade of the 2-3 trees surrounding Colosseum, security and police will force you to leave the shade and whoopdidoo back to the blazing hot sun

Now, for the Colosseum itself. It still is a magnificent marvel of engineering and build quality. The idea of cool air under the pillars still works and it feels great.
The site itself is a bit overengineered, too many display stands, bookstores blocking the view of actual walls of this great structure.

All and all a little too small bang for the buck and unimaginable waiting time. In my opinion, you will get a better experience walking around it...

title: Coliseum Visit
text:
Visiting the Coliseum is a must. You need to be part of a guided tour to appreciate more the ruins and to have a deeper understanding of the place. Avoid summer time as it is too hot.
Plan to visit the ruins area after sunset to be able to shoot great photos

title: Must see
text: In my opinion, Colosseum is one of the two must-do activities. Other one being Vatican and its museums. But the only drawback for both of them is getting their tickets on normal prices. The official site never shows tickets available. We also had to buy a guided tour with 3 times the price. But this was one tour which we did not disappoint us. Our guide was very knowledgeable, patient and informative. Colosseum is a huge structure which gives a great impression of the empire it represents - in terms of being 'colossal'.

title: Sunday visit
text:
We were there on the first Sunday of the month, the entrance was free for the Colosseum and the Roman Forum. Still, there was a queue for the free ticket (half the circumfererence of the Colosseum) but it went so fast that in an hour we were inside the monument. 
Enjoy!

title: Bogdan was Fantastic
text:
This was one of the best parts of my Italian trip, which is probably not surprising it being the Colosseum.  The thing that took it over the top was our tour guide, Bogdan.  He was funny, beyond knowledgeable, passionate, and a great speaker. He even recommended the best restaurant of the trip, Popi Popi.
Bogdan's employer's should be happy to have someone who puts so much into their job.
Thank you again Bogdan!

title: Disappointing
text:
Waited over 90 minutes in shadeless scorching heat to purchase tickets. My wife was waiting under a clump of trees to avail of some shade. A policeman asked her to move away from under the trees…..to where? To burn in the shadeless open heatwave? Very stupid policeman!!
I don’t understand which donkey decided to move the ticket purchasing (and queue) to the exposed outside instead of utilizing the existing sales windows within the shaded lower corridors of the Coliseum? He/she needs electric shock treatment to reactivate the brain.
Coliseum visit was very disappointing. The ruins have been repaired without any thought….just by using concrete…,without even a try for maintaining aesthetics.
The only good part of the visit were the various water spouts which provided cold clean water.

title: A wonder especially when you find out how wrong the movies are
text: This is a signature sight of Rome so you have to go.  Earlier or with a group is better.  Do take pictures in the morning and evening to get the full enjoyment of this architectural marvel.  And make sure you have a guide book of some sort if you are solo otherwise you will not really appreciate the history of the place.

title: INCREDIBLE
text: Amazing historical place to visit in Rome.its extremely incredible during the sunset.you can buy ticket on site

title: Pretty unorganised online ticket redemption system
text: The rating is not for the building.. of course this is amazing if not a little unloved ( visit the amphitheatre in Pula Croatia if you want to see one that has been much better looked after).. of course walking in the footsteps of gladiators is too amazing for words.. what was hard was that all the pre booked tickets needed to be collected way too far away from the attraction.. and seriously you must prebook if you are travelling in summer….  we then had to go to another office to redeem a child ticket, then walk to the Colloseum in blazing sun.. I get that Rome is hot but why wouldn’t you have the office at the railway station? Across the road from everything.. or set it up together with the ticket office ? Just spoilt the experience. Maybe book a small group tour to avoid the run around

title: Visit yourself without a guide. You can use the official audio option
text: Queue was a little long so we chose to pay a guide to skip the main queue. Didn't have to wait too long however guide raced around and was not very informative. 50 euro each for ticket. We would have been better waiting in the queue and taken the official audio option. Guides are just eager to do as many groups as they can. But of a conveyor belt. I'd advise to just buy tickets from the main ticket office.

title: Lions and Tigers and Bears, Oh My
text:
We did a tour of the Colosseum, and while the Tour Guide was very good and informative, I believe the Colosseum could be toured just by purchasing a Skip the Line Pass.  When visiting Museums, there is always a story behind the painting and often the Tour Guide is educating you on what you are looking at, however when it comes to the Colosseum, there is only so much they can say.  The tour guide was friendly, informative and knowledgeable, however most of what was discussed, my family and I already knew.  This is no fault of the tour guide, I just think so much is already known about the Colosseum, there are not a lot of secrets to be told.  We visited the Colosseum about 20 years ago, and I will say they have done a nice job restoring some sections and adding new features, which was quite nice.
While we didn't see any Lions and Tigers or Bears, we did meet Dorothy from California, and she was very nice.

title: Interesting but very very hot
text: Went on a tour and our guide was great. However, be prepared to stand in the direct sun and heat for a long time. The crowds were huge and our guide did not stop for 90 minutes straight - no bathrooms and no water.

title: Coliseum in July
text: Opted for the skip the line pass. Which was worth It due to the long lines. Did the audio self guided tour. That’s was also worth it. Due to the fact the guided tours have so many people. it’s hard to hear them and you have to go at there pace. I would only do  a guided tour if it was a private one. Went in July was extremely hot and humid. No dress code. Lots of walking. Instead of getting a cab rode on a lime scooter which was fun.

title: A must when visiting Rome!
text:
We toured the Colosseum, Roman Forum & Palatine Hill first thing in the morning on a very hot July day.  Despite the heat, it was an experience we'll never forget. To be inside the walls and on the floor of such a historic structure was a dream!

The staff working at the site were helpful and kept the throngs of people moving. We never felt rushed.

There are a few 'nasoni' (water spouts) where you can refill your water bottle on the Forum & Hill grounds. 

A must when visiting Rome!

title: The Colosseum is an Iconic Roman Symbol
text:
What more can be said about this iconic symbol of Rome?  The setting is spectacular, the history is barbaric, the design is outstanding and memorable.  This building is a strong representative of the power of the Roman empire.  Yes, it is fun to climb around the Colosseum and see all the perspectives and  decide where the best seats would be.  No, I do not really want to know how many animals and people died each year--this just reveals a very sad truth about violence and humans.  
This is a must-see in Rome for all those who want to know more about Roman history.

title: Magnificent masterpiece of architecture!
text:
The exterior was a part of our walking tour. 
Magnificent wonder of the world sits here as a reminder of how slave labor built so many buildings for the wealthy or those in control. The way it was constructed is an architectural masterpiece!
Worth seeing while in Rome

title: Rome's Timeless Marvel: Unveiling the Spectacle of The Colosseum
text:
The Colosseum is not just an ancient relic; it's a testament to human ingenuity, engineering prowess, and the indomitable spirit of an empire that once ruled the world. So, the next time you find yourself in the Eternal City, don't miss the chance to unravel the enigmatic allure of the Colosseum.

"All roads lead to Rome, and once you're there, the Colosseum will take you on a journey through time."

title: 🏛️🔥 A Sizzling Roman Adventure! 🍦🕶️
text:
Oh boy, visiting the Colosseum on a scorching July day was like stepping into a real-life oven, but let me tell you, it was the most epic adventure of my life! 🌞🔥

As I approached the grand Colosseum, I couldn't help but be awestruck by its sheer magnificence! 🤩 It's colossal indeed! The sun was shining brightly, and I felt like I was about to embark on an Indiana Jones-style quest, minus the snakes (thankfully!). 🐍🎒

I must admit, my first thought was, "Thank goodness I brought a hat and sunscreen!" 😅 But hey, what's a little sweat compared to the chance of exploring one of the most iconic historical wonders on Earth, right? 😎

The line to get in seemed never-ending, but my patience paid off. Once inside, I felt like a gladiator ready to take on whatever challenges the day threw at me! I even imagined myself in the arena, shouting "Are you not entertained?" to the imaginary crowd. 🗣️🏟️

The architecture and history surrounding me were mind-blowing! I couldn't resist trying out my best "Roman emperor" poses for the camera. 📸 Who knew ancient columns could make such great selfie backdrops? #EmperorVibes #AncientAndFabulous 👑

Of course, the experience wouldn't have been complete without imagining myself cheering on the epic battles that once took place here. "Thumbs up" to the gladiators and "thumbs down" to the heat! 😅👍👎

But let's talk about the best part – the much-needed gelato break! 🍦🍧 Rome in summer demands icy treats, and I indulged like there was no tomorrow! It's amazing how much joy a simple scoop of gelato can bring, especially when you're trying to cool down near the ancient marvels. 😋❄️

Overall, my day at the Colosseum was the stuff of legends! 🏛️🌟 It might have been hot enough to roast marshmallows on the pavement, but the memories I made will last a lifetime. I felt like a time traveler, soaking in the history and admiring the architectural genius of the past.

So, if you're thinking of visiting the Colosseum, don't let the summer heat scare you away. Embrace the adventure, bring some sunscreen, and prepare for an unforgettable experience! 🏜️🔥

#ColosseumConqueror #RoastingInRome #GelatoGladiator

title: Looking Great
text: After many visits I found that the best time to view the Colosseo is early evening. All the crowds have departed and the touts have gone home! You can slowly walk around the site and appreciate the structure. If you see someone signing their name give them a rev up!! I never get tired of viewing this relic and it is a highlight of Roma for sure!!

title: Rome can't be done in a day
text:
Having disembarked a cruise for a tour of Rome, our travellers were not disappointed... much. If you plan to do a tour (in a day of Rome, mid June), don't please don't, the queues are long, the wait hot. Take the Colosseum. Lives up to your dreams, large amounts of tourist can ruin the surreal experience. Mainly, as many are so eager for that social media picture they nudge, budge, step on you and into you. The free for all outside is like boarding a plane with no seat booked.
Good Luck. Taking a child in a buggy?
Make sure it's an all terrain as ancient Rome has stone roads (not those little cobble stones) around its glorious colosseum. 
We never got inside 😢 for a look around as our tour bus gave us an hour at this monument. You cannot do Rome in a day.

Don't get us wrong, do a visit but we suggest doing very early or very late and stay in Rome for awhile to make entering the colosseum and getting the feels of history... top priority. Oh, take a refillable water bottle you will need it in June.
Alla prossima ❤️‍🔥

title: Jossipe colosseum tour
text: Jossepie our tour guide was exceptional... very knowledgeable and gave a great and interesting tour. Would highly recommend requesting him.

title: A must-see for history lovers
text: This ancient amphitheater is a marvel of engineering and architecture, built by the Romans in the first century AD. You will be amazed by the colossal size and shape of the structure, which could hold up to 80,000 spectators. You will also be fascinated by the history and stories behind the Colosseum, which was the stage for gladiator fights, animal hunts, executions, and other spectacles. You can explore the different levels of the Colosseum, from the upper seats to the lower arena, where you can see the remains of the wooden floor and the underground passages where the animals and gladiators were kept. You can also visit the nearby Roman Forum and Palatine Hill, where you can see more ruins and monuments from the ancient city. The Colosseum is a must-see for anyone who loves history, culture, and art.

title: Spectacular
text: Very nice experience. Rome is a beautiful city and the Colosseo is spectacular. If you go in Rome is a must have.

title: A must-visit spot that does not disappoint
text: I recommend purchasing an advance visit with a guide to understand a little more about the history and also the construction. It is indeed an impressive monument that carries the weight of its history at the same time. Very interesting!

title: Spectacular
text: We couldn't stay in Rome without visiting the Colosseum. Truly, this place full of history is worth seeing. As we had already done other activities earlier in the day, we found our guided tour of the Colosseum and Palatine Hill a bit long. Duration of 3 hours, you must plan to walk a lot and have water!

title: Very interesting
text: The sheer size of the building is hard to grasp your head around! I would 100% recommend pre booking tickets for the colosseum as we struggled to figure out where we could even buy tickets on the day! It was pretty busy but once we had our tickets we were effectively straight in. The colosseum itself was fascinating yet can feel a bit samey all the way round haha. Maybe on a less hot day it would’ve been more enjoyable.

title: Book a guided tour to get the most out of the visit
text: The area is spectacular and a must to visit when in Rome. We booked a tour to make sure to get a proper introduction, but make sure to reserve some spare time after the guide to dig into all the details.

title: Must see
text: The Colosseum is truly impressive. The only negative point, the sound from our headphones for the guide was really not good.

title: Wow!
text: It was surreal. You see pictures, but to actually be there is another thing. Wow! We had a “no line” tour which is definitely the way to go!

title: Colosseum
text: We booked with (Take walks) Colosseum and palatine hill skip the line. The tour guide Laura Sensi was very knowledgeable and had plenty of time to explain the history to you and answer questions. The Colosseum was breathtaking and would recommend visiting. The queues are very long, but with skip the line you go past and start your sightseeing experience straight away.

title: Colosseo, Rome Italy
text:
The history behind the structure of this Amphitheater is marked by a significant value to the present day in Rome. Seeing it up close is an accomplishment on a traveler's bucket list. 

The weather was so high on the day of my visit (27 JUL 2023) that you couldn't really appreciate the whole place. However, it is remarkable to see it and learn how people appreciate the tourism system, given the amount of tourists around Rome and other nearby Italian cities.

title: Magic Energy
text:
Marvelous ! From the point of view of the tremendous history that the place houses, and how they have managed to maintain it throughout the centuries!
I hope not to visit it in the middle of summer since the heat is overwhelming and the number of tourists I feel doesn't allow you to connect 100% with the energy of the place.
It is very magical to walk through it imagining everything it was, I loved it and I would go again anyway.
The line to enter is long, but everything that happens to get in will always be worth it.

title: An all time wonder
text: I feel lucky to visit this iconic attraction in Rome. All facts about this Colosseum shall be known from Google.  We booked tickets online in advance.  This ticket includes visit to Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palantine hills. The slot for entry to Colosseum is fixed while booking the tickets.  It was an amazing experience  sighting this building.  We went by metro train to reach this spot. The moment I came out of the station..I could see this. Thrilling..The price of the ticket vary depending on the entry to different levels of this elliptical amphitheater. No words to describe the beauty of this. It could hold 50000 spectators at a time. Imagine the time it was built with no electricity and no technology  ...the human brain and physical capability had created such breathtaking building 😇. Wear proper shoes, hat and with water bottle 😆. Take time to see leisurely from all angles. They close it by 7pm. You need 2 to 3 hrs to see it all levels, so plan and book accordingly.  The great Roman Empire.  Before visiting read completely about this and start imagining once you enter. There is lift facility inside to reach different levels only for disabled/ people above 65 yrs .

title: Visit to the Colosseum
text: Unmissable visit to this beautiful monument. I couldn't find tickets online, so we paid for a group tour at Civitatis, it was the best decision since they explained many interesting things to us, the entry was very quick. In summer it is very hot, don't forget your bottle, there are several fountains along the way where you can fill it.

title: Join a guided tour and marvel at this protective monument of the Roman Empire
text: This iconic amphitheatre built between 70 and 80 AD has become one of the most celebrated sites of antiquity. Built over four levels, the Colosseum has a unique and remarkable architecture. Its ground level consists of 80 entrances located in its perimeter. Above this level, the Colosseum has three consecutive galleries, the arcades, which host the rows of seats. Above the arcades, there are four tiers of seating, divided by aisles. Further up, the Colosseum had a central area once used for chariot races. As remarkable as its interiors, the external part of the Colosseum hosts fascinating vaults, from which visitors can appreciate astonishing views of Rome.

title: Recommended of course
text:
We pre-ordered tickets at get your guide which is recommended. The trip is very well organized. My daughter forgot something at the security which was towards the last hour of the trip. I waited for her and therefor lost the guide. As this happened after 2 hours, we missed the Palatine hills. We were not aware that there was a number of the organization we could have called, but also the guide was not waiting for us. The last part is disappointing. 
The guide was very knowledgeable so therefor the rating is high.

title: Essential visit
text: It is an essential visit. Buy tickets at the box office. They are very cheap. Outside there are a lot of people trying to sell you tickets with a guide, and they tried to scam us. So be careful. But the visit is worth it

title: Great!
text: The Colosseum in Rome is like stepping into a big piece of history! It's this giant ancient amphitheater where gladiators once fought and crowds cheered. The walls and arches are impressive, and you can imagine the excitement from way back. It's a bit crowded because everyone wants to see it, but it's totally worth it.

title: very impressive
text:
always worth seeing, and impressive, and our type goes relatively early in the morning, it is not so crowded with tour groups.
and the annoying traders apparently only come at midday

title: A must visit
text:
Despite waiting in a queue for over an hour to buy a ticket this is a must visit on any trip to Rome.

Ticket was reasonably priced and gave you access to other gardens out with the Coliseum

title: Colosseum ♥️
text: It has a wonderful architecture, it is a great feeling to wander among history. Morning hours are calmer than other hours of the day, you can choose morning hours to take more beautiful photos. It is almost impossible to get in, there is a very long queue, if you do not want to wait, you can make a reservation online or come very early and get in the queue. In a central location, it should definitely not be missed.

title: Go Early! Underground/Arena Tour is Amazing
text: We had a guided tour of the unground basements and up on to the arena floor and it was an unforgettable experience. Our guide was able to give us so much information that we wouldn’t have otherwise known and walking in the steps of gladiators and emperors was incredible! Stepping out into the sunlight onto the arena floor and having the tremendous structure of the colosseum come into view on all sides left me awestruck. The colosseum has been a site I’ve wanted to visit since childhood and I was not left disappointed - I would go back again and again! We went first thing and I’m glad as it obviously gets incredibly hot as the day goes on.. we were melting by the time we left early afternoon so I don’t think I’d want to be starting my visit at that time (in late July at least).

title: Worth visiting more than once
text: Second time visiting the Colesseo. There are changes that have been made that added to the understanding and experience of this important site. But be prepared for large crowds.

title: Amazing place. Management not up to par
text:
Incredibly beautiful and charming. The Colosseum is a marvel both outside and inside and I recommend a visit to everyone. Thinking and visualizing what the shows could be like inside is a stimulating work of imagination.
Every time I come back it's an incredible experience!
Having said that, unfortunately the management and staff are not up to such magnificence and I cannot award 5 stars as I would like.
I'll tell you what happened to me yesterday and my considerations on the matter: I returned to visit the inside of the Colosseum with some family members who come from outside Italy and, rightly, as closing time approached, two "gentlemen" of the staff began to urge the people remaining inside to go to the exit.
As a citizen of Rome, however, I find it difficult to describe the feeling of discomfort and at the same time anger that I felt in front of my family and the other tourists surprised by the unspeakable attitude of the Colosseum staff.
Two men who already from their physical appearance presented themselves in a sloppy and vulgar way (one even approached me with a lit cigar in his mouth) with an aggressive and intimidating approach - without any reason - and with their precarious English, they shouted repeatedly: "close, pelase", which doesn't make sense when you read it like that, but rather than a kind invitation to go out it seemed like an insult full of hate. Whoever wants to understand, understand. A couple of tourists then stalled for a photo. So after being kicked out in a verbally violent manner in Italian, one of the two employees continued to vent about these two unfortunates by shouting to himself, but in front of everyone, in (yes) perfect Roman complete with swear words.
In my life I have carried out jobs in contact with the public and I am well aware of how stressful it can be, but how can you justify such an attitude?!
You need to have self-control and a way of acting and not just focus on the magnificence of the place. In short, we are talking about the staff of the Colosseum, not the village shop. The Colosseum: the most important monument in Rome and one of the most important in the entire world. One of the 7 wonders! How do you have staff like that?! I have traveled a lot and have rarely encountered situations like this.
When my family members asked for explanations, I found it impossible to justify the behavior of the Colosseum staff, which also partially ruined our experience.
Rome, a city that lives off the glories of its antiquity, a city that could be the first in the world and which instead is unable to valorise any of its treasures.

title: Being emersed in th history of the Colloseum
text:
The tour was 3 hours long. We were guided by a Tour guide who was very knowledgeable about the history of the Colosseum.

Our visit was in July,   it was extremely hot. Walk with water, and comfortable shoes.

title: Unforgettable guided tour of the Colosseum!
text: Our guided tour of the Roman Colosseum was unforgettable thanks to our wonderful guide, Rossana (number 53), who with her vast knowledge of history and archeology, added to her acting skills, made us experience the secrets of ancient Rome, the Colosseum and its protagonists. Truly unforgettable!! The best guided tour we have had on multiple trips, super grateful!!!!

title: One of the 7 wonders that suffers from its success
text:
A must when coming to Rome. We therefore take the Rome Pass because it is better to reserve your time. 11:50 a.m. for us and a lot of people.
We passed the checks well (3 in total, including one with a security gate).
The age-old and majestic work is incredible. But so many people. Groups who keep passing by with their guides... tourists (like us) who all want the same photo in the best exposed places.
Brief. To see, but very early if possible and knowing what awaits you (not counting the number of hawkers selling hats, umbrellas, water).

title: Colosseum after hours
text: I can't praise this tour enough for its flawless execution. The team at the meeting point greeted us with friendliness and made identification a breeze. The guide's expertise and genuine humility were a winning combination. The after-hours Colosseum visit was an absolute gem, sparing us from the daytime bustle. Palatine Hill, a haven for capturing stunning images, left us awestruck. This tour was a true delight, and we're eagerly looking forward to embarking on it again.

title: Flavian Amphitheater - Tourist
text:
It is one of the main monuments in the city, for me it is in the "top 3" of tourist visits in Rome.
It was built between 72 and 80 and had a maximum capacity of eighty thousand people.
It was used to entertain the population, mainly with gladiator fights and other varied public spectacles.
To see the interior, I recommend arriving well before opening time, because as we visited the Coliseum on the first Sunday in June and entry was free, as it is on the first Sunday of every month, we spent two hours or so in the queue that started nearby. of the Arch of Constantine and went almost completely around the monument to the entrance, next to the Via Sacra. I don't know the entry price for the remaining days.
There were people going to the Roman Forum first, because the queue was shorter, but they had to go back because the ticket had to be obtained at the Colosseum. A good measure, just a single ticket delivery site.
Inside, facing the Metro station area, we can see some pieces and images on display, and I also think that on the top floor is the souvenir shop.

title: Amazing Experience
text:
It was an incredible experience to colosseum.
Beautiful place.our guide was very great.amazing architecture.
Its a must

title: Pure culture
text: It's hard not to imagine the splendor of the times when you visit this monstrosity. Take a guide, you will learn many unexpected things.

title: A temple of history
text:
Magnificent place of memory; where the testaments of the Romans and the damage caused by the popes of the time intersect.
Great guided tour in French by a passionate guide.
Plan to buy your ticket on Coopculture.it at least 15 days in advance.

title: Outside vs. the Tour
text: Five years ago I took a tour of the interior of the colosseum and I think everyone should try to experience it at least once. However if money or time are an issue visiting it after hours from the outside is well worth the experience. Note that there is an overlook for taking great photos if you visit the subway station across the road from the colosseum. Enter the station and take the stairs on the left hand side to find it. There is also a water filling station there that gives you the option of still or carbonated water for free. Just bring a bottle to refill. Another great angle is from Palatine Hill. However, note that the walkway is gated at the very end of the hill at night so you have to return to the coliseum to exit. Keep your belongings hidden and close in this area. Do not expect those who are overly friendly to be genuine or to have good intentions.

title: unavoidable
text: Everyone has heard about it. Impossible to go to Rome without taking a trip there. Your guide will happily complete the information on the splendors of the time.

title: Majestic
text: A very good walk, it is really colossal. Tickets per adult (you have to queue for 1 hour to get them) with access to the arena and the Roman Forum are 21 euros and children are free, inside they offer you an audio guide for an extra 6 euros. In the vicinity they offer you guides who speak your language and charge you 50 euros per adult and between 20 and 30 euros for children without waiting in line.

title: If you don't think the Roman Forum is a version of pizza, you don't need this tour guide. An audio guide would have been better.
text: German-speaking guided tour of the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Palatine for €55. Very superficial, little content, difficult to understand German, tour guide was very small and did not have the usual ability to recognize people, so she often disappeared in the crowd and we wasted some time trying to find the woman again. Rome was supposedly founded in 700 BC.

title: A must do- make sure you prebook and with the official website
text:
Prebook timed tickets well in advance- use official website coopculture.it - kids will be free so when you get into the colloseum you will need proof of age to get children tickets- they happily accepted photocopies of our children’s passports showing photos and date of birth.
The site itself is amazing- as we didn’t book tickets until a couple of weeks before we went we couldn’t get basement tickets (which will do in future)- this did not hinder the enjoyment of the visit- a must do when visiting Rome

title: Great for picture taking ... but risks to be stressful.
text:
As we had already visited the inside of the colosseum many years ago, we went for a nice stroll and picture taking and avoided waiting for a long time, being harassed by scammers/street vendors....

Overcrowded and chaotic if you want to visit the ruins.... 

Police car took regular turns in order to chase the illegal people waiting to take advantage of the tourists ... (tours, skip the line tickets). No fake "gladiators" (expensive picture taking scam) were to be seen ...

title: mandatory in rome
text: location right in the center of the historic center of Rome, always full but with guides the tour is much wiser, as you cut lines, and don't waste time on minor things

title: living history
text: MAGNIFICENT! Full of history and life, it is a must-see if you go to Rome, walking through the same arena as the ancient gladiators is priceless, without a doubt an experience to always remember

title: Amazing structure worth the visit
text: Pretty much required when in Rome! Amazing structure. Can use a tour guide unless you really know they are passionate about it, might be better to do without a guide at your own pace and interest. Obviously August is very hot though! So better in colder months I’d assume.

title: Guided tours probably not worth it
text: Colosseum is amazing, definitely worth the visit. Plenty of signs and information to stop and read. It’s very very busy, crowds everywhere. Guided tour we had was very unorganised and we learnt more reading the signs.

title: Skip the queue!
text: Instructions and punctuality of ticket distributors was spot on. Once seeing the huge queue for people purchasing on the day admission tickets, we were delighted to have bought the skip the line tickets. Picked up the tickets easily and quickly. Got to walk around the site leisurely, taking in the historical sites at our own pace. We were there for just over an hour and there are toilets (free/public) inside the venue

title: Breathtaking and rich in history
text: What can we say about the Colosseum... It's an incredible monument rich in history! The visit is breathtaking. It is the unmissable monument of Rome!

title: The symbol of Rome par excellence
text: Monument of splendor and uniqueness. the true symbol of Rome which embodies the vastness of the Roman power of antiquity and in today's times expresses the immortality of the history of the Roman Empire. absolutely worth seeing.

title: Magnificent history destroyed by ignorant invaders
text: To visit this magnificent structure, see the workmanship of yesteryear and marvel at their skill and foresight  was enlightening.

title: Magnanimous
text: Is awesome. Two-day visit to the Colosseum and Roman Forum. Little time to admire such monuments and assimilate all the information.

title: I only received a receipt but not the ticket when I booked the ticket online. I went to the ticket office and it was done.
text: Be sure to buy your tickets in advance! We booked our tickets online, but only received a receipt, not a ticket. We went to window 5 at the ticket office and got it done in no time, without having to queue up again. The Colosseum is smaller than expected. There weren't that many people, at least not to the point of being crowded, but it was too hot in the summer, and we left in less than an hour.

title: It's a must visit place or else you will regret
text: This is the must visit place is Rome so that you get to know the history of ancient Roma. Make sure to take a tour guide or else you will just be taking nice picture but not knowing the history. Tour guy company we used had a very knowledgeable archeologist and very funny as well.

title: An amazing experience
text:
Great place. Felt it was majestic. A must see when in Rome. The entire Italy is gorgeous, recommend also Milan’s Duomo. The tour we got was very nice,  the guide was patiently explaining, warm and kind.
Felt welcome
Will surely be back
Hard to describe what we felt seeing it from the inside but also from the outside, the history behind, the height of it , everything about maes you feel humble and small having in mind what  great empires were built by humans(not a fan though) but its impressive nevertheless.
Enjoy!

title: A must visit
text: A must visit when you’re in Rome. Almost impossible to get tickets on the base price which is $19 euros.we have to buy guided tour in order to make it there before we leave. It took three hours to finish with the forum and the plantine, and cost 72 euros each person. I recommend guided tours because it gives a lot of information

title: The coliseum
text:
Very nice family visit
I really liked the Palatine Hill too.
It smacks of history.
Impressive

title: Roman Coliseum
text: Majestic!!! An unmissable visit. Congratulations to the municipality of Rome for the level of maintenance and conservation of all these works

title: Coleseum
text: The Coleseum is iconic. Something we all see in movies on TV etc bu to see it "live" is just I describable. It is absolutely stunning. See it with a walking tour guide to get a good I sight and learn about things that are not included in history books. We did our tour with City Walking

title: The greatness of Rome
text:
Visiting the Colosseum is indescribable, only being there can make you understand what you are visiting.
I believe that alone it can serve to demonstrate the greatness of Rome and its empire

title: Absolutely breathtaking site
text: We visited both the Colosseum and the Roman forum. We had a guided tour which was fantastic! I highly recommend having a guide, otherwise the sites might seem pretty meaningless and overwhelming. Make-sure your tickets are pre-booked in advance as the queues for both these archaeological sites are absolutely massive! If you go with a guide you have priority entry. I also highly recommend finding a local ristorante to eat in and escape heat. There are no cafes or shops onsite. Some tips to cope with visiting this attraction in the busy heat of summer is take plenty of water, cooling towels, a hat, fan and sunscreen. Also wear comfortable shoes and light clothing as you will be walking a lot, on often uneven ground or up/down stairs.

title: History and Architecture.
text: There is no way to talk about going to Italy and not visiting the Roman Coliseum. A grandeur of ancient engineering, perfectly maintained to this day. I recommend not only visiting, but also buying a ticket and entering it. The subway has a station right in front, which means there is no need for Uber or Taxi.

title: DO NOT BOOK THROUGH TIQETS
text: Excellent but DO NOT BOOK WITH TIQETS and if you do MAKE SURE YOU READ EVERY BIT OF FINE PRINT. 1) The time they say you are reserving to visit the Colosseum is NOT the time you will be allowed into the Colosseum. 2) You may be given two different sets of instructions as to where you must get physical tickets. This can mean a lot of extra walking around in potentially dangerous heat. To the extent humanly possible, book directly through the Colosseum’s own site. That’s what we’ve always done before and will be doing in future to avoid confusion.

title: Do Not Book with Colosseum Tours!!
text: We booked our tour online through Colosseum Tours. They sent us a confirmation and directed us where to meet our guide for an 8:30 pm tour. We arrived early at the meeting spot but no one from Gemini Tours (who we were supposed to meet) was there. Other tour groups came to the meeting spot and assured us we were in the right place, but still no one came. We tried to call the company and got voicemail several times. We emailed the company but got no response. Finally a guide from another group was able to get in touch with a guide from Gemini Tours on his phone. She said she had no record of our reservation for that day (or any other day). We received an email the next morning with a refund but no apology or explanation of what happened. Very disappointed not to see the Colosseum on what would have been a great final night in Rome.

title: Wonder
text:
Every time you see him you are fascinated.
This year we also visited it inside to satisfy Mattia's (7 year old) wish.
Pleasant experience.

title: Magnificent experience
text: An incredible experience and a sight to see as the sun was going down. To think it was made way back when with no machinery. Something I will remember for the rest of my life. A truly magnificent visit.

title: Gorgeous
text:
Very nice visit to this wonderful monument!! We bought our tickets directly from the official website. No waiting. Perfect.
However, I would have liked to have a museum inside or outside the Colosseum... With period outfits rather than standing to contemplate the stones.

title: The giant
text:
The magnificence of this giant.
Beautiful experience absolutely worth going with the guide who explains the history well

title: Much better with a guide!
text: I visited the Colosseum and the Roman Forum 5 years ago. This time I decided to do a guided tour (Get your Guide) and it was worth it! Firstly because there are incredibly long lines to get in and secondly because you learn so many things that aren't in any guidebook. Because of the extremely high temperatures, I wouldn't do this tour with small children!

title: Go see
text: Went to see the colesseum, definitely buy skip the lone tickets from viatore. We had a great time it was very interesting

title: amazing
text: we booked with City Tours, ~Eddy was amazing made our day fantastic and could'nt of asked for better 5 Star

title: Experience the 3rd and 4th floors
text: In the summer, it is mandatory to visit in the early hours, organized visits. We took the elevator to the 3rd and 4th floors, a new experience and view, but we didn't know that a tour of the other floors was not included. Including the forum, it takes at least 3 hours

title: Hail, Caesar
text: We liked the conservation and the immensity of the building. It is incredible that it has been there for centuries and today we can have a piece of Roman history almost intact. For a few minutes and hours we felt like authentic Romans enjoying a battle between gladiators.

title: Very poor experience and will never use them again
text: Booked, confirmed and paid for a sunset tour of the colosseum.  The guide did not show at the appointment tour time and we were 35 minutes early. After a phone call and on line,chat with ‘Get your guide” I was told the tour was reschedule to 45 minutes earlier.  I was sent supposedly sent an email which I never received nor did they attempt to reach me by phone.  We obviously missed the tour and not only that they would not issued a refund because they said did not cancel in the required time even though they rescheduled the tour, ridiculous. Adding insult to injury it was then too late to buy non guided tour tickets. Do not trust them. Very poor customer service.  I will be stopping payment on my credit card.

title: A stop in Rome on the way to southern Italy
text:
We visited and looked at Rome and the Colosseum, as well as the neighboring districts, from the afternoon until late in the evening. And we were overwhelmed. Not just by the architecture, but also by the old town and all the hustle and bustle. A particular challenge was taking the subway, an experience that is hard to match if you are not used to it. 
We really liked Rome, but we only stopped there on the way to southern Italy. 
But everything we saw, we marveled at and admired... it was just much too hot.

title: Colosseum tour with Spanish guide
text:
We took a tour of the Palatine Hill and Colosseum through Get your guide
Super recommended tour
We had a very kind and friendly guide, you could tell he liked what he did, despite the heat, he tried to find shade
 If I remember correctly, her name is Eva Herrera, the only Spanish guide.

title: Beautiful
text: Boiling but beautiful day. Incredible building and you are allowed right into and up it! Only happy memories of this!!!

title: Great
text: Food was great and delicious, the staff was accomodating and kind and fast in giving what we are asking them.

title: Amazing
text: Just Incredible this place in the middle of Rome it's spectacular impossible to go to Rome and not go to the Coliseum

title: Very interesting to visit
text: Very interesting to visit and make the outside tour and inside tour .no need for a guid just read signs and visit internal museum for history

title: Worst service in the tourist sector
text:
I don't understand what kind of employees who work in the tourism sector not only give wrong directions (my colleague and I had already bought the ticket but they sent us to the queue anyway and when they realized the mistake, they didn't even apologize ) and do not know how to speak English, this being the universal language, nor do they make the slightest effort to try to understand it.

A shame! I don't intend to go back.

title: Coliseum
text:
Absolutely a must-see during your visit to Rome.

For good prices, look for guides in the surrounding area who will offer you the guided tour at a good price.

title: Memorable, awesome place to visit!
text: Visited the Colosseum as part of a tour along with the Roman Forum and Palestine Hill. Strongly advise you to visit earlier the better in the day, myself and my daughter did the 9am tour…perfect to miss the heat and the crowds later on in the day. The Colosseum is such an awesome place to see from the outside but to go inside gives you the full sense of what went on there all those years ago. Standing where those fierce battles were fought is an unbelievable experience and one not to be missed. You can’t go to Rome and NOT visit, we never tired of looking at it❤️.

title: Colossal
text: From the distance it seemed "small", but as soon as we got closer it opened in front of my eyes. It is truly a marvel. Never seen anything so "colossal". It is truly titanic.

title: Exceptional history and a must visit.
text: An exceptional piece of history that should not be missed in your visit to Rome. Top tip is to prebook with a guaranteed time and fast track, otherwise you will be queuing for at least a couple of hours unless you pay to join a tour.

title: Breath taking!
text: This experience was breath taking. We booked a tour of this and the Palantine Hill which was exhausting due to the heat. Definitely wear comfortable shoes as the flooring is original and therefore uneven. Take bottles of water as there are places to fill up your water along the walk. Our tour guide, Diana, was fantastic. She was passionate and engaging throughout the tour.

title: SOOO hot but still well worth the visit
text: Had a wonderful tour guide and it was just a great time. Wish we would have been able to go down below the floor but apparently the Italian gov had a limited number of tours allowed to preserve the site.

title: But what are we talking about?
text: Probably the most important monument in Rome and one of the most beautiful in the world. The symbol of the Roman empire. I recommend booking guided tours well in advance to avoid the endless queues.

title: Essential. Also with a cafe recommendation.
text:
Got to be done. Get the arena floor as an extra, it really makes you appreciate the scale of the place. 
As an aside, and because I had trouble pinpointing this place on TA, go to the ‘Caffe Roma’ out the front of here. (Above the metro, up the steps). There is a fountain nearby to fill water bottles, be careful of the unscrupulous salesmen and the cafe is very reasonable for being so close to the Colosseum. Four of us had a drink and snack for 31 euros. In addition, they have on the menu, gluten free items and lactose free drinks. Plenty else on the comprehensive menu too. We had no trouble getting a seat.

title: Unmissable
text: One of the most famous symbols of Rome, you can't miss it. Beautiful from the outside and even more so from the inside.

title: visit to the Colosseum
text: I had a lot of fun and had an interesting day, the only flaw was the long wait to enter but still recommended

title: A must see!
text:
Didn’t manage to get area pass but not an issue. Just amazing. Fascinating to see all the different aspects/ages. 
Book early to avoid extra costs!!!

title: Ancient History
text: As far as I'm concerned, one of the best places in the world. Easily reachable by all public transport (but also on foot from Termini Station).

title: Had a great tour with Giulia of LivTours at the Colosseum and Forum
