text: Our family of 4 (with pre-teen kids) really enjoyed the tour of the Colosseum and the ancient Roman Forum with Giulia.  Although it was hot, we got a lot out of the experience.  Giulia was understanding of our need to be a bit flexible, had a lot of good information for the kids and adults, and she also had and gave us a special LivTours virtual reality viewer that we are able to use to see how the sites looked originally and we can bring them home and use the app to show friends, too.  It is very clever!  We learned a ton, had fun, and beat the lines because Giulia already had out tickets and knew where to go -- this alone is really, incredibly helpful when traveling with young kids.  Do not underestimate the value of it!  Because we could conserve our energy for touring the sites instead of trying to find them and buy tickets, we all enjoyed the experience more than we would have otherwise.

title: It was worth it.
text: Truly spectacular, I recommend visiting it using a guided tour since you learn much more, despite the heat, the tour was unforgettable, very enjoyable and professional.

title: Good day out
text: This was a great sight seeing spot. It was somewhere I had always wanted to go. The ruins were brilliant to see. To read up on what went on here and to see it in person was great.

title: The must-see in Rome
text:
Without much comment, this is the site par excellence.
If you are in Rome it is obligatory for you to go there.
You may not be able to go inside the coliseum, especially if you are a large family, but you have to go around the site

title: Must to go…
text: Lovely historical building but too much walking. Make sure you wear trainers when you go Rome. Enjoyed our visit back in February

title: ‘The landmark’ of Rome
text:
Probably the 'landmark' of Rome. 
You have to see it, day and night. An impressive insight into Roman culture.

title: The symbol of Rome
text:
We visited the symbol of Rome and the empire, nothing that I can add to the other thousands of reviews, the only thing I recommend you do is with a guide who explains the history to you and, (if you are lucky like we were) will show you experience this spectacular archaeological site.
If you are interested in seeing the interior, pay attention to the tickets, you have to take the Colosseum underground.

title: Great experience, but buy tickets ahead of time if you can!
text:
I visited the Colosseum in May 2023 with my husband and 1 year old son. I highly recommend buying tickets online ahead of time if you can - we did this and we saved ourselves a lot of time! We bought the tickets for the Colosseum, Palatine Hill and Mamertine Prison together in one package online and could collect the tickets from the Mamertine Prison which had about a 5 person queue to collect (rather than the hundreds of people queue you get at the Colosseum), so would highly recommend doing this so that you don't have to wait around in the sun.

The Colosseum was great and was accessible for my child's pushchair as there was a lift that took you up to the next level. Some areas (such as underground) are not accessible by the lift, but this didn't bother us.

There are a lot of people around at the Colosseum and it does get very busy, but it's such a huge impressive building, you can't really go to Rome without visiting it! Overall, a really good visit!

title: Truly colossal, must visit place with so much history behind it
text: We had booked a guided tour from Take Walks for this one combined with the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.  It was good to hear about the history and significance of all the sights.

title: Impressive
text: Ancient building with an impressive view. The Colosseum offers both tours and audio guides. You can easily walk through it without a guide and read the texts at the relevant locations. There is a water dispenser at the top. In summer, please remember to bring a hat/cap as there is hardly any shade. The book store on the first floor has lots of offers (books, postcards, bags) as well as souvenirs. Unfortunately, once you are down on the ground floor, you are not allowed to go back up. Overall, a cool experience that you definitely have to do at least once!

title: Disappointed amazing history
text: Purchased skip the line tickets from Tiqets to the Colosseum and Palatine Hill Mamertine Prison. By the time we worked out from the instructions how to pick up our tickets , it made no difference to skip the line still had to line up for entry and security checks. Was very hot and once inside the colosseum there were no real instructions as to where to go in the crowds of people. So many groups blocked the entrances. When time to exit it was very hard to actually find the way out. Did enjoy it and the history.

title: Training of employees in dealing with disabled people is recommended!
text: Interesting sight. The necessary infrastructure for disabled and chronically ill people to visit is also available. The staff working at these facilities should be trained. When the relevant entrance ticket is shown, which has already been checked at the cash desk for eligibility, it is inappropriate to look up and down and back to see what is wrong with the person. There are also illnesses that are not immediately apparent to outsiders. This annoyed us throughout the entire visit. Instead of taking the elevator, we took the steep stairs.

title: FANTASTIC
text:
THIS MORNING visiting Rome with friends who are seeing the city for the first time.
And here is the Colosseum in front of us, one of the most fantastic attractions in the world.
Even just from the outside, the scent of history emanates from every corner.
A dive into the past when we were able to build such impressive works that still maintain their charm intact today!

title: Magnificent
text: Hundreds of people so there must be patience and a good mood. There is quite a wait if you do not have a ticket and it would be good to have booked tickets in advance. Simply, if you want to visit the arena as well, you want to pay above

title: Must in Rome
text: It is one of the most spectacular buildings in Rome, an amphitheater (although commonly known as a coliseum) that is extremely well preserved and was once the tallest building in the city.

title: A go-to place
text: I've always wanted to walk on the floor of the Colosseum only to find out that it consisted of a series of long gone trap doors. Oh well. To me the Colosseum represents an engineering feat of the Romans, along side of their ruthless conquests. We had a helpful guide, found out that just showing up doesn't equate to getting in if all of the day's tickets have been sold, and appreciated the elevators as an easier way for the disabled to get to upper levels. Alas, some of Europe is actually disabled friendly.

title: A bit of a disappointment
text:
A must-see in Rome, there is no way you can be there without visiting the Colosseum. We were a bit disappointed. Maybe differently - it didn't delight us enough to make us fall to our knees. Maybe we had too high expectations.
In any case, you must buy the ticket in advance. You can buy audiobooks in many languages - I recommend it. Get ready for a lot of stories, a lot of curiosities and a little imagination. As you will find out, a huge part of the Colosseum has been demolished, so you have to find out for yourself what it looked like before.

title: Hail Colosseum!
text: If it is among the seven wonders of the world there must be a reason, imposing, majestic, if you close your eyes you can hear the screams of the people watching the games or imagine the fights. Then at night....

title: Book tickets with coopculture
text:
Obviously a must see when you are in Rome, it can be baffling to know how to book tickets etc..
Easier to book yourself through - coopculture - they are the only official seller of tickets, everyone else is 3rd party seller so you will pay more. 
Tickets are also timed entry so no need to pay for skip the line access. 
Also, children under 18 are free!
We paid for the self guided tour - 8 euros each & it was excellent, brief history & you can walk around at your own pace. 
Your tickets also entitle you to 24 hour access to  Roman Forum & Palatine Hill.
It is very busy & hot & lots of group tours, we booked for 4.30 which was great, slightly cooler.
Metro station right opposite the Colloseum & lots of decent places to get food/drinks.
A completely mesmerising unforgettable experience.

title: Simply great!
text:
We visited Colosseum on a very hot day. Luckily there’s one free water spot outside and one inside as well. Hi highly recommend buying your tickets in advance and only. It seems it is easily sold out on popular days.

There was much interesting to see at Colosseum and you should definitely go inside. We were blown away by the sheer size of it.

title: Family Colosseum
text: Perfect open space to enjoy the antiquities of a city like Rome, be sure to visit it at night. After Yam historical visit we went at night and it is another spectacle

title: Unique experience
text: Second time going, and I will visit it every time I return to Rome. It captivates me. Enter without lines. And although I went during high season, I was able to enjoy it. Yes indeed. Very hot and it's my turn at 12:10. Unbearable.

title: Must visit place. - Rome’s top most attraction
text:
We had booked the underground tour, which has only limited entries in a day. We could see the underground areas, reconstructed Arena Floor, and enjoy the views from the first and second tiers.
The Colosseum Underground and Dungeons - Used for storing slaves and animals, the Hypogeum (or Underground) featured two levels and an entire network of Colosseum tunnels underground. This was where gladiators, wild animals, and the condemned awaited their gruesome fate. Our guide told us stories of the likely uses of the chambers and dungeons that once hid underneath the original Arena Floor.
Arena Floor and Gladiator's Gate - Ascending to floor level, we were showed the Gate of Death (or the gladiator's gate) and out onto the reconstructed Arena Floor, which was the heart of the Colosseum and where the savage games took place.
First and Second Levels of the Colosseum
As part of our guided tour, we visited the first and second levels of the Colosseum. The views from these parts of the arena provided an excellent opportunity for photos, although my wife  and kids were very tired, so they did not come.
The tour had started at 2:30 PM and by the time we finished it, it was 6 PM. 

It’s quite an enriching experience. At night I went again to sunset pictures and Blue hour pictures of Colloseum

title: Well worth a visit
text:
Paid for Tour of Colosseum which also included entry to Palatine Hill and Roman Forum
Queues for tickets extremely long so better to book Skip the Line or a Tour if possible/available as seemed to make entry much more efficient 
Well worth cost of ticket 
Stunning views and structures
Museum pieces within the Colosseum fascinating
Guide  leading Tour gave good information on the History of the Romans and the Colosseum which we would not have discovered if on our own exploring

Must see  landmark when in Rome

Plenty of fountains to refill water bottles 
Extremely hot  in open spaces
Large site so took nearly four hours to see most of what lay within the three sites we visited 

Can view much of the Forum and outer Colosseum without having to purchase tickets to enter as pedestrian walkway alongside these historical areas

title: Coliseum
text:
Mandatory stop!
Second time we came and it was as cool as the first!
This time we bought tickets online and took the day to stay there - it was much easier!

They are super organized and so there are no queues or confusion!

We suggest going early and having a coffee outside, in a café with a view of the coliseum!!!

title: Unavoidable !
text: A must-see in Rome! Magnificent and majestic! Recommended visit with wire cutting guide which includes the forum and Palatine! Everything is magnificent despite the world!

title: Top on a guided tour
text: A must-see for your first visit to Rome. Take the visit with the forum and the Palatine Hill grouped as a guided tour. It's definitely worth the detour like that. Counted 3 hours of visit but great

title: A trip to Rome wouldn’t be complete without seeing the Colosseum
text: It’s difficult to put into words what it’s like to experience the Colosseum in Rome. My family and I did a guided tour that was wonderful. When in Rome, it is an absolute must see.

title: Viator - Avoid
text:
We were booked at 4.30pm due to our train coming into Rome at 3pm. However, our train was late which had a knock on effect to getting to hotel and the meeting point of the tour.

We were on our way and the guide called me at 4.15pm which is the first contact I’d had. I explained we are running about 10 mins past the start time and she said it was ok but send my location so she could track me which I did. Messaged again at just before 4.30pm to advise we’re 4 mins away and then called when we arrived. No-one was there so I called the guide and she said they went and started. At no point did the guide say they needed to start before.

I understand the voucher says arrive 30 mins before start time but it’s not that obvious and I had to look a bit harder for it.

No refund is being issued, not even partial.

title: Amazing ruin but check tickets!
text:
An essential place to visit however pre-book tickets and visit early to avoid queues. Be aware when booking that there are different ticket types that allow access to different parts of the site - the standard skip the line tickets don't give access to the arena floor or underground.

The site itself is big and there is lots to see. Lots of exhibits and nice spots to take photographs. There are also toilet facilities and a gift shop on site, but nowhere to buy drinks or food other than a water fountain.

Site can get incredibly busy, so plan your trip accordingly.

title: Best way to understand its history is to take part in a guided tour. Recommended.
text:
An interesting alternative to visiting the Colosseum is to take part in a guided tour, which illustrates its origins, history and curiosities.
It made us see this architectural wonder from another point of view.
Recommended.

title: The Coliseum
text: The Colosseum is a huge ovoid amphitheater located in the center of Rome. It is the largest built during the Roman Empire. It is 189m long and 156m wide. With its 250,000 tons of stone, the construction of the Colosseum required 100,000m3 of travertine, mounted without mortar but held together by iron staples. It remained in use for 500 years. The last games continued until the 6th century. Many vaulted spaces, under the stands, were used as homes or workshops and there were still tenants in the 12th century.

title: A must-see for tourists
text: It's a massive concrete structure that stands tall and proud, and its history is as captivating as its architecture. Tourists flock to the Colosseum to take in its grandeur and explore its many underground passages and vaults. The Colosseum is a must-see for anyone visiting the Italian capital. It's a magnificent feat of engineering and a testament to the power of the Roman Empire. From the intricate carvings on the walls to the vast underground passages, there is much to explore and discover at the Colosseum.

title: Colosseum Tour Extraordinaire
text: Our tour was through Liv Tours.  Unlike the other tours with 15-20 people following a flag, our tour of 6 was led by a guide, Alex Chiarot, who clearly reveled in the history and art of the Colosseum and who was beyond charming and interactive with his guests.   We all learned so much fascinating lore of how the Colosseum was used and the beginnings of Rome.  Highly recommended by all who were there and who traveled into the history of ancient Rome with Alex Chiarot. (alexchiarot.com)

title: Exemplary roman architecture
text: Spectacular structure with so much history and architecture behind it. One can easily be mesmerized by the inner architectural beauty and the history can absorb you back to the olden Roman times. Must visit place in Rome to be seen together with the Roman forums and Palatine Hill

title: Unimpressed, but a must do
text:
Is it amazing to see its architecture from afar? Yes. Is it cool to follow the steps of history? Definitely. Is it mind blowing to see the ruins of the marvel? Not really. 

I wasn’t expecting to love this place originally bit I still left underwhelmed. There are only a few spaces that are preserved enough to transport you to another time and exhibits are mot the greatest if you are not with a guided tour. 

Be aware of this but go to appreciate how humanity has been capable of amazing and horrible things for a long, long time.

title: Great visit
text: It’s a magnificent monument to visit. You can’t go wrong any way you visit it. Only the expected long waits and crowdedness.

title: A piece of history
text: One of the attractions is the historical monuments of the capital.. Guided tours a little expensive, but with a discount for teenagers and people with disabilities the price has dropped quite a bit.. The only flaw are the various tourist guides, official and fake, that circulate there around...

title: Must visit in Rome
text:
It is the first visit that should be made as soon as you arrive in Rome, either to visit it inside with a guide or just to see it from the outside.

Magnificent construction from the Roman period practically plundered for the construction of St. Peter's Basilica in the Vatican, where most of its marble blocks, floors, statues are located... even so, a visit is a must.

title: Great experience
text: Great attraction but book before you go and get skip the line tickets the ques are enormous. Crowded once inside but the lift is a bonus for those that need it.

title: A Wonder of Ancient Architecture
text: Located in the center of the city, this large oval-shaped amphitheater is constructed of concrete and served as an entertainment venue for gladiators. A visit to the Colosseum is a unique and unforgettable experience.

title: A trip to the past
text: Very interesting, it's a shame that the guide we had was super unfriendly. I recommend doing the visit with the gladiator arena and underground included and with skip-the-line entry. If you don't have tickets, the lines are very long.

title: UNFORGETTABLE, YOU HAVE TO VISIT IT ONE DAY
text: A dream come true...must be on the list of places to visit in life. You will step into history and feel something incredible being there. There is more than just the arena where the gladiators fought, there is an entire museum behind those windows that you always see in the coliseum. There is a collection of things left by the gladiators, the public who attended and even animal remains. Magical place...I hope to return. Arrive on time and I suggest buying online. Online tickets are available one month exactly on the day you want to buy, patience

title: Worth a visit once
text: You feel small in this huge building, we had warm but cloudy weather, which we were grateful for. Buy guided tours through registered companies not from those who go around trying to sell to those standing in long queues... for us it took about 2 hours to get inside the gates which is completely normal if you don't buy guided tours. If there's nothing good to eat and drink when you're there, sort it out beforehand

title: Beware of CoopCulture Official Coliseum Site.
text: I bought the tickets directly from the official ticket seller, called CoopCulture. The purchase went through my credit card and I received an authorization from unicredit but no confirming email. I sent in a request for tickets and help form 4 times. Never received a reply. I checked both email and spam folders constantly. I saw several complaints about this on other sites. We are going directly to the ticket office tomorrow an hour in advance of our entry time and hoping we get in. Buyer beware! (I had better luck with non-official vendors for the Parthenon visit this morning). CoopCulture—where is your phone help?

It’s a must

Photo opportunity

title: 150 Meter Line to Buy Tickets - READ REVIEW SO YOU KNOW HOW TO PLAN AHEAD
text: PLEASE READ SO YOU CAN PLAN AHEAD:   Buy your tickets for the Colosseum ONLINE weeks before you arrive in Rome.  Otherwise, you will stand outside in the rain, sleet, snow, sun, heat, wind and weather for HOURS and HOURS just to get a ticket to get inside.  There is usually only 1 single person at the ticket office selling tickets.  There are no covered areas to stand under and be out of the elements.  We tried standing in line for about 1 hour with horrible heat and sun, until we finally just gave up.   All we got were photos of the outsides.  There are tour groups offering a way to skip the lines.  Some are real and cost more than a regular ticket, but worth it.  But some are scams so be careful.  Keep your valuables in a safe at the hotel or leave at home.  Hold on to everything and zippered pockets are the best for pants as they have more protection.  Lots of bottled water being sold in the area and you will need it.  One (1) Euro is the going price and that is very fair for cold water.

title: Magnificent
text: Colosseum is regarded as a must see in Rome. It is indeed magnificent, by the historical, architectural and artistic point of view.

title: It's an unmissable visit if you're in Rome.
text:
Probably the most famous monument in the world and symbol of the greatness of Rome, the Flavian Amphitheatre, better known by the name of the Colosseum for the colossal bronze statue depicting Nero that was nearby, is located in the archaeological heart of the city, and from almost two thousand years it tells an uninterrupted story of charm and magnificence.
It is is an imposing construction which, with almost 2,000 years of history, will make you go back in time and discover what society was like in Ancient Rome. 
The construction of the Colosseum began in 72 BC. with Emperor Vespasian and ended in 80 with Emperor Titus. Once its construction was completed, the Colosseum became the largest Roman amphitheater, with its 188 meters long, 156 meters wide and 57 meters high.
During the Roman Empire the Colosseum allowed more of 50,000 people to attend their favorite shows. 
The exhibitions of exotic animals, the executions of prisoners, the reproductions of battle scenes and gladiator fights were the entertainment shows of the Romans for years.
The Colosseum was in operation for more than 500 years and its last shows were held in the 6th century. Nowadays the Colosseum is, together with the Vatican, the major tourist attraction in Rome. 
Every year it is visited by many millions of tourists. In 2007 it was chosen as one of the Seven Wonders of the Modern World.
If you are in Rome, you can't leave without visiting it, even from the outside.
It would be better to visit it from the inside, and with the help of specialized guides, discover the secrets and hidden corners of a truly amazing monument.

title: Coliseum tour
text: Excellent tour. My only suggestion is that there is only one toilet block just inside the coliseum. Tour guides should offer the use of this bathroom before continuing. You don't get a second chance.

title: Unmissable in Rome
text:
To visit this place, months in advance we bought tickets on a website that included a visit to the Colosseum (with access to the arena), the Roman Forum and the Palatine with a guide in Spanish. Although the entrance is more expensive (around €53 per person), you avoid the line to buy tickets to access the Coliseum and everything is explained to you in Spanish.
The meeting point is at the Arch of Constantine, to begin the tour of the Roman Forum, then the Palatine and at a certain time, they take you to the Colosseum to visit the first two levels and the arena.
The total visit lasts about 3 hours but it is worth visiting if you go to Rome.

title: Iconic place and definitely worth visiting!!!
text: Definitely worth seeing !!!  Stunning views. Just buy your tickets online in advance and be careful with people who sells them in front of the Collosseum .

title: Incrediblen experience!
text: The Colosseum is an awe-inspiring testament to ancient Roman engineering and entertainment. Its colossal size, intricate architecture, and historical significance left me speechless. Walking through the same corridors where gladiators once roamed sent shivers down my spine. The atmosphere is palpable, and you can almost hear the roars of the crowd. However, the crowds of modern tourists can be overwhelming, so visit early in the day for a more serene experience. Despite the crowds, the Colosseum is a must-see, offering a glimpse into the grandeur and brutality of ancient Rome that's both educational and awe-inspiring.

title: Good guide, overbooked
text: Good tour of colosseum and palatine hill. Unfortunately the group was overbooked and made the experience much less personal. Valentina, our guide, was personable and informative. Difficult to manage so many people.

title: Crazy!
text: The coliseum is simply crazy! Knowing so much history, the architecture and engineering of the time, as well as the customs, is very surreal! However, buy tickets well in advance, there are a lot of people, a lot of tourists and the information is confusing, you have to show up and ask!

title: Colosseum visit
text: Until you see it you won’t understand the accomplishments and awe that went into this. There is a lot of history to the colosseum. My suggestion is to go early and book a tour to get around some of the long lines. I used Viatour and they were very friendly and knowledgeable. All in all it was well worth it.

title: Unreal place
text: Up until I saw the Colosseum upclose and personal, it's always been Petra in Jordan that has topped my list of ancient must-sees. Well, the Colosseum might or might not top that but is now my top tourist attraction in Europe.

title: Book ahead, get a tour
text:
Well we didn’t have time to go inside so I couldn’t check out whether the cats are still there like they were in the 1970s:)
It’s obviously a must do when you come to Rome, I remember thinking it was smaller than I imagined when I first visited in 1976 but this time it was definitely  back up to scale and bigger than I remembered!

You can’t fail to be impressed, even from the outside…..we took time to sit on some bits of Roman masonry absorbing the place.
It’s busy obviously and red hot in high summer so you get a hint of what it must have been like to fight and die here, we carried plenty of water with us to wander around in the heat.

The take aways to remember are… buy tickets in advance as the queues are long and you will be pestered by people trying to sell you tickets at inflated prices.
Get a guide, you will gain so much more from the experience.

title: Book in advance or you might not get in....
text:
I've given this only 2 stars as we actually didn't get to go in....the queue was about 3 hours long and when we got to the front they had sold out of tickets so we couldn't go in. 
Book in advance and you'll be fine!!

title: Beautiful Colosseum
text: I had a package tour to the Colosseum and our guide Kate was very knowledgeable. Kate took us to explore every corner and learn the history. The architecture is incredible, and Kate showed us original designs of the Colosseum with a roof and statues in every archway. The place is amazing.

title: Nice if you can get tickets
text:
Yes of course you should go! It is incredible to be standing in it. 
My problem is with how to get in it.
Tickets come on sale a month in advance and get snatched by bots. The only way to get in is to be a bot or to buy the tickets via a third party tour operator for an exorbitant amount of money. 
The different types of tickets make no sense. Don't count on the Colosseum website to clarify things, or I am too stupid to understand it, but it doesn't matter anyway because you can't buy tickets via the website cause they are getting bought by bots!
There is also one toilet in the whole Colosseum and it's at the beginning so don't miss your chance! 
There is a serious lack of explanation about anything. But why are you complaining? You should have listened in history in elementary school! 
It was very hard to find out what objects were allowed to be brought in, only to have accidentally brought one without issue. Good thing I didn't plan on lighting fires with my deodorant!
So overall yes it is amazing. The search for tickets not so much.

title: Very impressive - definitely a must see
text:
Extremely beautiful and very impressive! Definitely a must-see and well worth a visit! 
The ticket must be exchanged for an entrance ticket beforehand. But it was quick and easy. The ticket shop is within walking distance of the Colosseum.

title: Disappointment
text: Well, I was a little disappointed with the Coliseum. The first time I went I couldn't get in and I was sad so on the last day of the trip I had an empty seat I managed to go so I didn't get a seat, the line was quite long and we went very early in the morning before the ticket office even opened. Okay, we got used to the queues in Rome more or less, but after cutting the ticket I waited another two hours in line because we had to get in at the time the ticket was written. When we managed and entered I was disappointed I expected more imposing more beautiful, I don't know....

title: My number 1 in Rome
text: This is a must see. The Colosseum is one of the 7 modern wonders of the world. In my opinion, it's a bit exaggerated, but let everyone judge for themselves. The amphitheater was built by the emperors of the Flavian dynasty in the 1st century. The condition is average. The first impression from the outside is not impressive, especially the queue for tickets. However, it wasn't that bad, we only stood in line for about 40 minutes. And here's a note - tickets are for specific hours. I waited 1.5 hours to enter. On the ground floor, the coolness and scale of the building are surprising. There are a lot of people inside, but the traffic is directed according to the sightseeing route. We visit the ground floor and two floors. There is a souvenir shop, I recommend the nicer bronze souvenirs. I recommend this facility.

title: Magnificent
text:
What to say when faced with one of the seven wonders of the world? Just to admire.
It's not the first time I've seen the Colosseum, but every time it's always a surprise.

title: Crazy

title: It is its ensemble, its monumentability
text: It is a place where you can learn about the history and culture of imperial Rome. It is essential to visit with a guide. That greatly complements the visit.

title: Gigantic
text: To visit the Coliseo, go with purchased tickets, as it is impossible to buy them for the day, you choose the day and time in advance. Immense queues to see this wonderful Roman construction.

title: Must DO!
text: This is a must do if you are in Rome. Second time there but first with a guided tour and I highly recommend doing so. if not at least buy tickets in advance.

title: Amazing
text: Amazing. The history amd stories about the coliseum are amazing. 2000 years old. It is one of the coolest things I’ve ever seen. Love going down to the arena. The area around the coliseum is awesome.

title: Do not use Tiqets or Omnia if you visit Colosseum
text: Do not book with Tiqets or Omnia. They changed the time multiple times a day before my scheduled time and I never got to see the Colosseum or a refund. Several others in my tour had a similar experience. I'm sure the Colosseum is amazing from the inside, but the ticketing situation is a travesty.

title: guided tour ruined by the behavior of the guards inside the Colosseum
text: the Colosseum is magnificent. shame about the security service inside, rude and unprofessional. closing at 7.00pm and 6.30pm they practically started pushing us out. they escorted us to the exit in barbaric ways, as if we were slaves in the times of the Roman Empire. shame, excellent guided tour ruined by their behavior.

title: No communication so it didn’t happen
text: I booked the tour on my phone the night before. The tour said it would pick up at your hotel. When I entered my hotel, it didn’t show up. I guess it wasn’t on their pick up site list. But they never gave a list. I tried to contact them via the “Contact Tour Operator” messaging on the Trip Advisor website to ask them where to meet a couple times before the tour time. I also called their contact phone number and left a voicemail message with my phone number. I went to the Colosseum in hopes that they would tell me where I could meet the group. They did not contact me at any point. I asked for a refund in the Contact Tour Operator messaging and still haven’t received communication from them. A list of pick up points or ANY communication would have been helpful.

title: Very impressive

However, you can easily enjoy it on your own with a guided tour. Also stopped by in the evening which was a nice photo-op.

title: Speechless!! Highlight of Rome!

title: Our tour guide was cooler than Taylor Swift
text: Gianluigi is the GOAT of tour guides from EXPLORE ITALY. There is 1) Jesus Christ, 2) Andrea Bocello and 3) Gianluigi Bruno. title: GetYourGuide….avoid booking with this company!!!
text:
Avoid booking through this company at all costs, unless you want to be disappointed!! I booked in advance tickets for the Colosseum tour for my dads birthday with my family, we flew to Rome specifically for this trip, only to be told a few hours before the tour that it had been cancelled, due to a “political event” which didn’t happen or interrupt any of the tours going on, despite us seeing groups from the same company going straight into the venue in front of us. 
Customer service were rubbish and unhelpful, and gave us no alternative or option for us to attempt to turn round this already awful disappointing day. All I received was an email to say the tour had been cancelled with no actual reason. Shocking, you put faith into these companies (and pay extra for the service) to guarantee bookings, and to be told it has been cancelled with no actual reason is completely unacceptable, and has ruined our family trip to Rome!!!

After a few cancellations we finally got on our tour last week.
We went to the colosseum in the evening for the underground and arena tour.
It was fabulous - very quiet with few people around so we had time to enjoy it all.

It’s an absolutely fantastic place

Just wonderful.
Purchasing tickets and booking is a bit cumbersome, better to do everything online to avoid long queues

title: History through a striking building
text: An impressive number of people pass through the area and one imagines that something impressive will be waiting to be seen: The Colosseum. Imposing. Fascinating. Its size and characteristics dazzle. If one uses a guide they can access the information necessary to think about history and life in Rome in ancient times. Every stage, every war and power. Much to see and hear. It's all really fascinating.

title: Visiting the Colosseo in Rome was a journey back in time, an experience that etched itself into the core of my travel me
text:
Visiting the Colosseo in Rome was a journey back in time, an experience that etched itself into the core of my travel memories. This ancient amphitheater, a symbol of the grandeur of the Roman Empire, is nothing short of awe-inspiring.

From the moment I approached its colossal arches, I was captivated by the sheer magnitude and architectural brilliance of the Colosseo. Stepping inside felt like stepping into history itself. The remnants of a glorious past surrounded me, each stone whispering tales of gladiatorial combat, roaring crowds, and the cultural richness of ancient Rome.

What struck me most was the palpable sense of history that permeates the Colosseo. Standing in the same arena where legendary battles took place was a humbling experience, a reminder of the ingenuity and ambition of ancient civilizations. As the sun set, casting a golden glow over the ancient stones, the atmosphere became almost surreal, as if the ghosts of the past were watching over the monument.

title: Shorter Wait and Stroller Accessible!
text:
One of the best things to see in Roma! Wife and I went with our one-year-old in a stroller and it was beyond worth it.

Queue/Wait:
Unlike some of the other attractions in Rome (viz. Vatican museum and St Peter’s) that you get to skip the line with a baby stroller, the Colosseum is a good hour-plus long wait. We chose to book a tour and from the start of the tour at the meeting spot we were inside the Colosseum in under 15 minutes. (Yes it was worth paying >$100 total for the guided tour).

Stroller Accessibility:
While we did not go underground, the main floor and top floor are easily doable with a stroller, but going down the stairs can be intimidating while carrying a stroller- lots of forward momentum. There is no good place to park the stroller there if you were to leave it and carry a baby, so I would not recommend banking on that. Worst stroller parts is the road to the Roman Forums since it is large basalt stones and very uneven.

title: Informative tour with a great guide.
text: Absolutely amazing. This is the first trip that we booked any tours BUT if you don’t book one in advance, you may have to wait hours in line. The only thing I think could be improved is the meeting area. The tour guide was exceptionally fun and full of facts that we never had heard before! You can book different tours but we just did the one of the Colosseum with the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. The colosseum gets packed fast so booking early is the best way to go. We loved it!  We booked our tours through Get Your Guide.

title: Impressive building
text: One of the most fascinating buildings I have seen in my life. It is huge, in addition to the large number of tourists who were around it, it could be accessed from many different positions that are great for taking pictures.

title: Must see thing to do in Rome!
text:
If you do one thing in Rome, you need to visit the colosseum! 

We did a tour and saw it at sunset and went out on to the gladiator platform. I would have loved to have seen the underground where the animals and slaves and gladiators were kept but it was sold out. 

Tickets are released a month before hand so make sure you book early if you want full access!! Go early morning or late evenings. 

Photographer? Make sure to get to Palestine hill at sunset to get some lovely shots from the outside. 

Full of history this was great to see it up close. Our tour meant we did it in an hour and a half but could have spent longer here.

Lots of steps but there are lifts in places if you need access.

title: Nearly 2000 years of history and must that you thought you know about this is not correct. Get a private tour
text: Worth talking the private tour. We had a great guild and our tour included the forum and the hill as will. Easy to get too. A must  see

title: It's not worth waiting in line.
text: It is more beautiful on the outside than on the inside, if you are not interested in hiring a guide to explain the history to you, it is not worth waiting in line for 3-4 hours. Because they only have 5 people selling tickets. Chaos really!! And if you have the Roma Pass you have to have booked months in advance otherwise you will have to wait in line 😅. The forum and palatines are a lot to walk around! You can also see it from the street and it is the same! And like everything in Rome full of people!

title: Amazing tour of the colosseum in Rome!
text: We went through the company city wonders.  Our tour guide was Emanuela- she was absolutely amazing!   We booked the tour of the Colosseum and the forum through city wonders and it was absolutely perfect.  We all met at a designated spot, received radios so we could hear the guide well, and she spent the perfect amount of time in each place and gave the perfect tour.  She was so personable and engaging to everyone on the tour-  I wish she had been our guide throughout our whole trip!  The price for this tour was very fair.

title: Quamdiu stabit Colyseus...
text: After almost 2000 years it is still a unique show. Queued for over 2 hours under the sun this October 2023. You can only pay by card (no cash). We purchased the Full Experience ticket to see EVERYTHING underground + arena + Roman forum but the underground can no longer be visited due to places being sold out for the whole month... anyway it was worth it. Only they couldn't find the "gap" to exit onto the Arena floor... asking the staff they sent us here and there... in the end we discovered that we would have to display a green sticker on our shirt which testified that we had the full ticket but they didn't give it to us at the entrance! We managed to find the exit to the arena only thanks to a girl, an authorized guide who was there with her group (who I thank so much and I take this opportunity to point out the difference with the non-authorised ones outside who offer you skip-the-line tickets stratospheric prices) who, just by hearing us speak, directed us towards the person who was guarding the cordon that inhibited the passage to the arena area and the basement (which, however, we could not see, as mentioned). In practice, you exit outside the walls (but remaining inside the gate) to re-enter the arena directly! The show that opens at the end of the portico is wonderful! I had already visited the Colosseum about ten years ago... the entrances have changed and there is this new area... as it is today it is much more evocative... maybe it's okay revised the signage or information to the visitor who has paid to see everything he is allowed. However, it is absolutely worth it if you like feeling completely immersed in history.

title: Unmissable
text:
An unmissable tour of Rome, for sure. The historical grandeur of this construction is incredible.

It's worth going on the tour as you can see all the levels of the Coliseum, including the basement.

I also think it's important to go with a guide to understand the stories and details.

We bought our ticket with guide online and everything went well.

The ideal is to go in the morning when it is a little less crowded.

title: Drifting area. I ask for more cleaning, more checks. For the rest he has a lot to tell. Let's take care of history
text: We have to take care of places like the Colosseum. It's filthy, dirty and they absolutely must take action.

title: Meh.
text: Yes, there it is. So not amazing. One gets more from photographs. Entirely not worth the effort to schedule tickets, wait in line, and tour the thing.

title: a visit that was very worthwhile
text:
This visit was very well organized with the tour group and we were impressed by the Colosseum.
Our guide really did a great job, the entrance tickets were organized in advance through our travel agency, which we were with on site, everything was very well organized.
We personally really enjoyed this tour.

title: Too many people!
text: If traveling in Italy you have to see the coliseum but we certainly don't enjoy the crowds. We purchased a tour to skip the line, but even with that, there were lines everywhere. I think marking the lines better might. Our guide was constantly telling individuals that they were in the wrong line, and this isn't even the busy time of year.

title: Nice to see!
text: The colloseum is definitely a beautiful and awe inspiring site! Even from the outside you get a good view. Inside is very busy and some areas get very clogged up. The most annoying thing we found was the guided groups as they would all stop and completely block up an area so that you couldnt see exhibits or read the signs or even get past them on some occasions.

title: Beautiful!
text: Coliseum is Coliseum and nothing more! It is a breathtaking work! Even after 2 thousand years! It's gigantic even by today's standards! Imagine the times of the empire! It was certainly a source of pride for the Roman people! Although it was used for certain acts that today we consider barbaric! But everything in good time! At that time it was not seen like that! Until... the Catholic church assumed temporal and spiritual power and dictated the rules! No fighting! And then... the Coliseum died!

title: A top ten bucket list attraction
text: Beautiful, awe inspiring, amazing, a must visit. History is so important to be able to improve the future. 2,000 years old and still standing.

title: Wow!
text: In spite of the huge crowds and scammers doing their annoying stuff, this was amazing. Our guide was an archeologist who was knowledgeable and very passionate about the place. We took the underground tour, and it was appropriately creepy. I could almost hear the crowds cheering as they watched the brutal scene unfolding before them. (I wasn't aware that the Colosseum presented spectacles 24 hours a day - the slaughter is inconceivable to my modern mind.) Absolutely overwhelming experience, the best part of the trip.

title: Great place buy your tickets only though the colosseum
text: The colosseum was brillant so much to see and do .we did the full ticket well worth it .BUT DO NOT  BOOK WITH VIATOR .Took our money we had tickets and cancelled day before .we had paid for fast track tickets  .Then had to wait 2hours in a queue to get in .On the day VIATOR HAD THEIR WORKERS OUT TRYING TO SELL FAST TRACK TICKETS .What a joke seeing Trip advisor was who put us on to them in the first place to book tickets .We weren’t alone quiet a few people in the queue to get tickets said the same about VIATOR

title: Beat the queues and enjoy a well guided tour of the colosseum, and entry into the forum.
text: Definitely worth it - the queues were so long and we went straight in - we hadn't appreciated how busy the sites were. Our guide was lovely and interesting, looked after her 'flock' of 20 people.

title: Don’t Use Dream Tourism! A Nightmare!
text: Dream Tourism realty let us down on this. We were to receive our tickets 24 hours in advance which were prepaid. We got nothing. Twelve hours before the tour I called TripAdvisor to tell them I got nothing. After they contacted Dream Tourism they and I were told the tickets were going out at 11:30pm and 12:00am that night. When I woke at 6:50am the next morning I had no tickets. I contacted Dream Tourism on the listed phone number and was told to email them my TripAdvisor voucher through WhatsApp.  When I ask how to contact them through it they hung up. I called TripAdvisor who worked with me 3 hours before our tour was booked to get my tickets. TripAdvisor was patient and stayed on the line to figure out what was the proper contact info for Dream Tourism in WhatsApp. I was able to send in my voucher to which I received tickets 1.5 hours before my tour. When I got to the Colosseum it was not the original tickets I ordered. Dream Tourism sent me a WhatsApp message they will refund me the full amount of the tickets. I’d give them a zero rating if I could. Waiting to see if I get a refund or if I need to contest through my credit card.

title: It's worth it with a good guide
text:
From the outside it is impressive and even more so if you take into account that it was built centuries ago with the tools and logistics of that time.

Inside it is true that it is quite deteriorated, but you can get an idea of how incredible it would be in its golden age.
You can visit both some sections of the stands and the underground where the gladiators and wild animals waited... as well as a reconstruction of the system they used to "elevate" them to the combat arena.

I think that if you visit Rome it is worth the visit, but...if you go with a guide who tells you historical information.

title: Beauty surrounded by squalor
text: We did a skip the line tour with Carpe Diem and this was the best way to see everything. The Colosseum is awe inspiring and when you get inside you get a real feel of how it would have appeared to ancient spectators. The score of 5 stars is based on the Colosseum itself. The surrounding area is dire however, it’s dirty and very crowded - watch your belongings. The metro station opposite has a distinct smell of urine wafting out as you walk past and the area is full of ticket touts. Do not listen to anything they say, book a tour or tickets online instead. We were shocked that such an iconic monument was surrounded by such a mess, shame on Rome for not doing something about this.

title: The colosseum palatine hill and Roman forum
text: Absolutely brilliant! Our guide was knowledgable and friendly. It was very busy at the time we went and she guided us to the ‘best parts’ allowing us time to take photos and soak up all the history.

title: Global icon.
text: There are no words to describe this great historical work that continues to resist and resist over time, which makes historical Rome a power in ancient times.

title: 'the veil of strange verses'" Dante (Inf IX 63)
text:
From a scientific point of view, everything has been said about the Colosseum, or at least it is believed that everything has been said. In reality it is one of those iconic monuments, where the archaeological investigation, the restoration, the statics of the monument are subordinate to the use of mass tourism.
It is today understood with an 'industrial' and speculative approach, where what is contrary to the 'formulas' of the valorisation of current cultural heritage is a 'stumbling block'. Who has ears to hear....

title: A Roman open air museum
text:
An open air museum and I advise a tour which cost £168 for 3 people but well worth it. 
It’s just a stunning place and if you are a fan of that period then it is a must
Spectacular

title: Very bad - not recommended!!

Once for the ticket from Tripadvisor and then for the ticket for our son, which would have been free if we hadn't booked through this platform! In addition, it was no longer possible to visit the Roman Forum that day! But we left the next day! We should have been informed of this! For that price: zero service!

title: Impressive Colosseum
text:
Good tour in an area with incredible history. You only realize it when you are in the middle of it.
Our guide Andrada managed to keep us interested for three hours with great enthusiasm.

title: Very impressive !
text:
Very impressive ! we took a wire-cut ticket on Head Out and at the meeting point (Tourist Office opposite the Emmanuel II monument) we watched a preparatory video for the visit.
We did this visit without a guide, but with a paper guide.
We also visited the interior and it is very impressive
Our ticket also included a visit to the Roman Forum which is 400m from the Colosseum.

title: Visit the coloseum
text: Great visit and still amazed at how big it is. We visited during the day and did the tour, which is definitely worth booking beforehand. The queue was extremely long for tickets on the day. Also great to visit in the evening when it's lit up and looks amazing.

title: Nice memory
text: Ah it's surprising to find it in the middle of a boulevard and in the middle of the roads but a nice monument, a sensational moment and a lot of emotions to see it

title: Book tickets!
text:
We enjoyed our visit and it is a marvellous building.  The explanation displays are clear.  It was very crowded but worth spending time to see it.  
Our standard ticket did not include the basement area, where they kept the animals etc.  This was only accessible with a booked tour which was disappointing.

It is really good that the ticket was a joint one with entrance to the Forum and Palatine hill in a 24 hour period, so we were able to access that area the following day.

title: Great!
text:
Third time here, second time inside and it still amaze me to see this beautiful place! 
The fact that this was possible to build in just around 9 years back then, is for me unbelievable !
It is such a beautiful place both in day time and in night time.

title: City of Wonders Rome Tour Cancelled at the Last Minute
text: Trip planners BEWARE.  I booked a private tour of the Colosseum/Palatine Hill for my family of 7 back in March 2023 for close to $1000 with City of Wonders - 7 months before our trip.  These companies make you pay in full at the time of booking.  Last weekend was our scheduled tour and 10 minutes before it was to start, their customer service line in Ireland called my cell phone to cancel the tour!!!  These companies are getting too large and really do not have contingency plans in place when they make mistakes.  People like us travel from all over the world and make plans.  Cancelling at the last minute ruins the trip and these companies really don't care.  My recommendation is to hire a tour company that will give you your guides contact information from the very beginning so you stay in contact with them as your trip gets closer.  With these large companies, you are just a number and not important.  City of Wonders Promise is "Our team is dedicated to making sure every experience is a great one."  Well that can't be farther from the truth.  Thank you for ruining our Rome weekend.

title: wow
text: Just wow. My family was blown away by the rich history and brilliance of the ancient Romans and their colosseum! Loved it ❤️

title: Not Colossale enough!
text: My experience of il Colosseo was probably a bit better than “average” but clearly a full rung below “very good”. For a stadium that’s nearly 2,100 years-old and has had all the wear and (in some cases deliberate) tear it’s had to endure over those millennia, it’s survival, partially intact at least,  is impressive. But maybe I’ve just got this Hollywood “sword and sandals” image fixed in my head thanks to Spartacus, Gladiator and countless other Colosseumesque flicks, but I was expecting an arena of that stature to be much larger and grander in scope and size.  A field day for archaeologists and historically massicciamente significativo I’m sure, but I was left feeling a little underwhelmed.

title: Stunning
text: Pre booked our trip before travelling to Rome. It is an amazing experience and an absolute must if visiting Rome. Enjoyed it much more than the Sistene Chapel.

title: Journey to the past
text:
Lovers of art and culture get lost in the corners of the Colosseum, even recreating scenes from mythical movies. Fascinating, very well preserved.

Enjoyable tour, you have to dedicate time to it. You can go with a private guide or with an audio guide, which is purchased at the entrance.

title: Colossal
text: Excellent place. It is worth going on a tour with a qualified guide to understand the historical explanation. The property speaks for itself. Nothing to add.

title: Clever and very smart construction far ahead of its time
text: We went on a guided tour of the colosseum, and it was worth every penny. So many stories and information we got that we never got to know otherwise. We were there before the tourist season, yet the queues were insanely long already in the morning. But when we went guided, we were lucky. We chose the first tour in the morning, which was also an advantage as the area gets very hot when the sun was high. Fantastically clever and ingeniously built! The technology was far more complicated than I thought at the time. We shall not talk about the drama! Very beautiful, powerful and absolutely a must have seen if you are in Rome.

title: Total disappointment
text: Disappointing. 3 hours waiting time. All tickets sold out online 1 month in advance. Ticket Mafia buys everything in advance to sell on site for €69 for groups and skip the line (instead of the €18 standard price). I was so excited to visit the Colosseum at first, but after everything I lost all joy. Absolutely not worth it. 0 points

title: Stepping into history
text: We really enjoyed seeing the colosseum and learning about the history. The initial part of our tour which includes palatine hill and the Roman Forum was very interesting but too long for the kids. We booked through get your guide.

title: I thought the Colosseum was colossal
text: My wife and I visited the Colosseum as part of a package tour with the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. The skip-the-line tickets were well worth it.  Our hotel was just three blocks from the Colosseum and across the street from the Forum.  I am still mesmerized by the Colosseum and the thought that it was the state of the art design of its day.  The arenas in Nimes and Arles are impressive but this is the granddaddy of them all.   I think it is colossal.

title: On must see list
text: I would’ve given it five stars but there’s just way too many people definitely worth going inside. We had a skip the line ticket and I’m thinking it’s worthwhile.

title: Amazing
text: Booked full experience tour tickets through coopculture. These are only released in clusters one month before so took a bit of dedication to get the tickets. We wanted to do the underground and this really did not disappoint. Our group was about 25 people but you are the only group in the underground at that time and it really did feel special and we learned so much. By doing this tour we were well away from the crowds until the end of the tour which again made the experience feel really special. As once you are released into the crowds it is so busy in there. The tour was so informative and I would recommend it

title: The Roman Colosseum-An Ancient Wonder of the World
text: The Colosseum in Rome is a must see.  It is an awesome site outside but, to truly experience this you must go inside.  My wife and I were in Rome this month,  the lines to the Colosseum were down the block.  You must purchase a ticket in advance.  Once inside, you are just amazed that this arena was built 2000 years ago.  It that's your breath away to imagine what it was like coming to this place.

title: Coliseum is a sight to behold!
text: Iconic amphitheater in the city of Rome, over 1900 years old, belongs to the history of Rome, very large, which was used for gladiatorial and animal fights, a place of entertainment in ancient Rome! To not lose!

title: A piece of history
text:
Very nice place to visit.
Very visited and crowded. It is imposing and takes us back to the time of gladiators and Roman circuses!

title: Must see but let down by crowds and poor ticketing system
text: The Colosseum is a must see and the audio-video guide is good. The little museum area with various exhibits is also interesting. However there are a number of negatives that must be addressed: booking tickets through the coopculture website for late October a month in advance was near impossible (we ended up opting for a membership instead which at least allowed us to visit even though we were still unable to book the underground tour); signage for the entrance and various queues is poor; there are so many areas that you can’t access (especially without the full experience tickets) that the inside is a bit of let down; it was far, far too busy particularly with large tour groups who walked trampled carelessly over small family groups. For an attraction of this scale the practicalities really need to be seamless for it to be an enjoyable and beneficial experience.

title: Beautiful but....
text:
The Colosseum is a marvel of architecture and history. It’s a must see!
However, be careful, you have to book your tickets many weeks or even months in advance, otherwise you will be forced to do like us, in an endless queue.... Also note that the cash desks only take credit cards , no cash...(We lost our card and after 2 hours in line, fortunately a lovely French tourist was kind enough to advance us the price of the ticket)
It should also be taken into account that to have access to level zero of the arena there is a supplement.
Prepare your visit in advance, that's my advice, but don't be fooled by tour operators who try to resell tickets for twice as much!

title: Far too many people...
text: The Colosseum is always a worthwhile destination. However, the joy was somewhat spoiled by the crowds; I was unable to take a photo without strangers' heads this time...

title: Nice trip to the Colosseum
text: A nice trip with a great guide Marcello V. His explanations were sometimes funny, fascinating and clearly understandable in English. He also spoke Dutch.

title: Waste of my visit to colloseum
text: We never visited the Colosseum as the ticket deliverer never turned up. We phoned the deliverer and never answered the call. So we missed once in a lifetime visit to the Colosseum  took this up with Tripadviser and till today nobody came back to me

title: Unfriendly reception at the entrance
text:
The tour of the Colosseum was 
impressive, but the joy of it was significantly dampened when we entered: an extremely unfriendly guard at the entrance, a mafioso type, refused entry to all visitors and sent them back with harsh words, even though the tickets said that you should arrive 10 minutes early.

title: Brilliant
text: What a piece of history . Imposing and if you think about what actually used to happen in there then quite intimidating as well. Very busy all the time and sadly too many people trying to sell you tat.

title: An unmissable experience for anyone visiting Rome.
text:
My visit to the Colosseum was truly memorable and enriching. This iconic monument is a stunning testament to the grandeur of Roman history.
The beauty of the Colosseum is breathtaking. Its architectural structure, with its impressive arches and passages, is a masterpiece of engineering and aesthetics. Walking through the halls gave us spectacular views of the arena and the city of Rome.

I highly recommend visiting the Colosseum with a tour guide, as this not only saves time but also offers a deeper and enriching understanding of the history and importance of this monument, whilst allowing us to appreciate its stunning beauty and architecture. An unmissable experience for anyone visiting Rome.

title: crumbling and old.
text: Much ado about nothing. not even got a roof? when they finising it. needs a bit of maintenance, seems to be parts missing.

title: Colossuem
text:
Staff Friendly, smooth entry, so glad we booked inadvance and had skip the line tickets. 
Paid extra for an audio guide which was very informative.
Not rushed to move through but better signage / one way system would have been helpful.
Toilets awful, doors broken, missing toilet seats, soap dispensers and water not working - bit of a theme with toilet facilities we found in Rome.

title: Absolutely an experience to do 😍😍😍
text: The experience was nice and nice, we couldn't go in but the view from outside was crazy. I recommend anyone who likes Roman things to absolutely go and see it.

title: Don't pay rip-off prices.
text: You have to see it if you visit Rome but it is a huge tourist rip-off. If you want to get inside you have to either book weeks in advance, join a 3-hour queue - which moves at snails' pace - on the day or pay exorbitant prices to one of dozens of tour guides who have been allowed to buy tickets in advance so that there are few left for sale directly to visitors. The tourist office have clearly become aware of this disgrace and have just decided to make more tickets available on the day, but really need to address the scandal of resale of tickets. You actually get a pretty good idea of how amazing the place is just by walking the whole way round outside. Then spend the hours that you saved not queueing by going to the Baths of Caracalla (just as amazing and almost empty) or the Baths of Diocletian for huge, awe-inspiring  Roman architecture.

title: Ancient
text: A must visit in Rome. Queues. But it's worth it. Getting ready to walk through the Colosseum...

title: Beautiful to see in person!
text: We didn’t pay to buy the tour I side as we arrived late in the day. We read the placards along the fence (there are doing some excavations and restoration). It might have been interesting to see inside but I was contented with being next to its exterior!

title: Like in a movie
text: The Colosseum, located in Rome, is an iconic site that fascinates tourists from all over the world. This majestic monument, built in the 1st century, testifies to the greatness of the Roman Empire. Its spectacular architecture and imposing dimensions capture the imagination. By exploring its ruins, one can imagine the excitement that once reigned, when gladiators and wild animals clashed under the excited gaze of spectators. Today, visitors can stroll through the corridors and stands, admire the remains of this ancient arena and feel the history that permeates the place. The Colosseum is an unmissable testimony to Roman history, a fascinating tour that will transport visitors to a bygone era.

title: An unforgettable experience
text: The impressive limestone structure offers unique entertainment with its gladiators and is a must for every visitor to Rome. It is an amphitheater that offers an incomparable experience.

title: obliged
text: you can breathe a unique air. the atmosphere is as I imagined it. it is one of the places to see in Rome, with a short tour of the interior

title: A spectacular monument of ancient architecture
text: Built in Rome in the 1st century AD, it was the scene of gladiator fights, animal hunts and other spectacles. The Colosseum is a masterpiece of ancient architecture that combines different styles and techniques. The facade consists of four floors, each of which has a different order: Doric, Ionic, Corinthian and Composite. The interior structure is a complex system of vaults, corridors and staircases that could hold up to 80,000 spectators. The Colosseum is a monument that reflects the power and culture of the Roman Empire. It is a must-see for anyone visiting Rome. You can explore the Colosseum with a guided tour or on your own, but don't forget to book your tickets in advance.

title: Circus and spectacle in Ancient Rome
text: It’s a blessing that the Colosseum survives in the form it is—despite the passage of Millennia and its past use as target practice by invading armies—as a visual image for us of what social and recreational life was like for the ancient Romans. Particularly fascinating to see the unearthed, claustrophobic maze of compartments below the arena floor, a bit like an ant farm, which housed the gladiators, the slaves, the wild animals and their handlers.

title: A must-see in Rome
text:
We pre-booked tickets for this, which is highly recommended. We were able to walk here from our hotel.  When we turned onto the Via Serpentini at least half a kilometer away, we could see the Colosseum dead ahead.

They have roped off lines set up for walk-up ticket holders so you can just approach the staff with your printed or phone voucher and they can direct you properly. There was also a bag scan to ensure you were not carrying any weapons or explosives. 

Once inside, there are several kiosks for audio guides, meeting points for tour groups, restrooms, etc.

The basic tour routes you along a set path.  There are a number of placards and exhibits along the way, as well as spots that are good for photos. 

You begin by stepping out to roughly where the emperor's box seats would have been. There is a large cross here. There is apparently a long-running debate about how to represent the Christian martyr aspect here versus just the non-denominational Roman history.  At one point in the past, the Catholic church even planned to build a church inside the arena.  The cross is the current compromise. 

You can proceed a bit to your right but I believe the left path leads to the stairs up to the 2nd level, where the remainder of the tour happens. From this point, you have a better vantage point to see down into the floor of the arena (and the levels beneath). You are also routed through a series of interesting exhibits about the construction and subsequent use of the structure. They even include archeological findings such as bones of snack meats the attendees ate.  Some were quite interesting. 

You can then visit each end of the long axis of the stadium. Each provides good views and photo ops, depending on what time you visit and the lighting conditions. At one end there is a platform with the 'classic' view of the interior; this is quite crowded. At the opposite end is a similar view plus the gift shop and book store. 

You then descend to ground level again and can check out the short axis view opposite where you began. The exit is on this side. When we visited, there was a kiosk where you could refill a water bottle for free here. This is also the closest point from which you can then access the Forum (which is often sold as a combined ticket with the Colosseum). This is a short walk but this entrance to the Forum also seemed to have the biggest line; you may want to walk around to another side for a quicker entry.  YMMV.

It helps to do a bit of pre-visit research about the structure to better appreciate it. I thoroughly enjoyed this visit. If you are visiting Rome, you should definitely make this one of your stops!

title: See it both day and night
text:
Awe inspiring building which captures the history and magnificence of Ancient Rome. Even after two millennia with the effects of time, it remains a stunning amphitheatre which can transport you back to the era it hosted spectacular, and brutal, games.
You can get tickets in advance on line though the choice of time slots is more limited the closer to the date you buy. We visited in November and noted that there were long queues in early and middle morning but much, much shorter queues around lunchtime and early afternoon. This corresponded with the fact that we could not book morning tickets but had a wide choice of lunchtime slots. This worked for us but be warned, we got sent back from the entrance twice as we had arrived ahead of our allotted time. It seems that you should not arrive earlier than 10 minutes before your due time. Take ID as this will be checked.
Once inside you can access the ground and first floors which includes exhibits and some explanatory boards. You can also book the arena floor and underground tour if you select these options. We booked the ‘full experience’ tickets which includes the Coliseum entry at a given time coupled with the  arena floor plus gives you entrance to the forum without need for a time slot.
We returned at night to get pictures of the illuminated Coliseum which is just as breathtaking as when experienced during the day. Night time tours can be arranged if you want this.

title: A riveting encounter with ancient grandeur
text:
Visiting the Colosseum with my husband was truly a journey into the heart of ancient Rome. As we entered the colossal amphitheater, the sheer magnitude of its architecture left us breathless. Imagining the roars of crowds and the gladiatorial contests that once took place within those ancient walls added a profound layer to our experience.

Climbing the tiers, we were treated to panoramic views of the city, each angle revealing a different facet of history. The intricate details of the Colosseum's construction and the innovative engineering of its time sparked our curiosity and admiration.

Walking through the same passageways where spectators once eagerly awaited spectacles, we felt a profound connection to the past. The combination of the Colosseum's historical significance and its enduring architectural splendor made it a highlight of our journey.

In the embrace of the Colosseum's time-worn stones, my husband and I forged memories that will undoubtedly linger in our hearts. For anyone seeking a blend of history and architectural marvel, a visit to the Colosseum is an absolute must.

title: A must-see for anyone interested in the history of the Roman Empire
text:

From the moment you step foot in the Colosseum, you are transported back in time to the days of the gladiators. The sheer size and grandeur of the structure is breathtaking, and it's easy to see why it was once the center of entertainment for the Roman Empire. 

The Colosseum is a great place to explore and learn about the history of the Roman Empire. You can walk around the naves and explore the various levels, or you can take a guided tour to learn more about the history of the Colosseum. There is also a museum at the site, which is filled with artifacts from the time period. 

The history and grandeur of this structure is something that you won't soon forget.

title: The Colosseum is a must-visit when traveling in Italy
text: The Colosseo is located in the center of Rome, Lazio, Italy. It was built in 72 AD and was the largest oval arena during the ancient Roman Empire. It was built by Emperor Vespasian to please the soldiers returning from triumph and to praise the greatness of ancient Rome. It was built under the orders of the Roman Empire, so there is the Triumphal Arch of Constantine next to it. The Roman Arena is composed of a series of three-story circular arcades and the top floor. There are 80 arches on each floor. The stone decorations of the Roman Arena are Corinthian, Ionic and Doric from top to bottom. The arches contrast with the regular beauty of vertical and horizontal interactions. Due to partial damage, the three-story structure can be clearly seen. When traveling to Italy, you must visit the Colosseum.

title: A fun day. I loved the history of the area.
text:
My husband and I visited the Colosseum and Palantine Hill the 2nd week of December. I’ll list a few things I wish I had known before I went.

1:  Purchase tickets in advance. Fortunately we were there off season and we got to the Colosseum just as they were opening. There were no tickets left online but we were able to get tickets at the location. Unfortunately the tickets for the “basement” were sold out but we were still able to see plenty. You can purchase a dual ticket for both the Colosseum & Palantine Hill but just remember it’s all one ticket and they will scan your tickets a minimum of three times at every location. If you have paper tickets don’t loose them. Also, a standard entry ticket will not allow you access to the museum or any special exhibits at Palantine Hill.

2:  I have nerve damage in my leg and sometimes walking uphill is difficult. Pretty much everything here is uphill. There were elevators at the Colosseum so I have to assume some of it is handicap accessible but not all of it. Even with having to sit down every once in awhile between the Colosseum & Palantine Hill I still walked 20,000 steps that day and I still didn’t see everything.

3:  The stairs to climb up to the upper levels are, I believe, original Roman steps. They are irregular, steep and sometimes narrow. Something to keep in mind if stairs are difficult.

4:  There are a lot of spots for photo ops. I noticed everyone wants to congregate right inside the doors to take their selfies. Trust me, the place is huge. You’ll find plenty of railing further along and you can always go back if you feel you missed something.

5:  We took a taxi but there is a train stop right across the street and it looks like they are building a new stop on the same side as the Colosseum. It would be super easy to take the train or the bus.

6:  People tend to dress much nicer in Europe than we do in the States. Even to explore 2000 year old ruins. I had on a Lynyrd Skynyrd tee shirt and my husband had on a hoodie and we might as well had a huge banner over our heads that said Americans. lol. I dress for comfort and don’t care but I did overhear two women in the restroom lamenting the fact that they didn’t know and they wished they had packed nicer clothes. So if that’s something  that might concern you just know that it’s not uncommon to see women in very fashionable clothes and heels wandering around the area. I also noticed that everyone was dressed like it was freezing out, puffer jackets, gloves, scarfs, hats, even though on the day we were there it was about 55° and I was in shirt sleeves.

title: unmissable
text: The Colosseum is one of the most iconic monuments in the world and one of the most visited archaeological sites in Rome. The Colosseum, originally known as the Flavian Amphitheater, was built in 80 AD. under the emperor Vespasian of the Flavian dynasty. It was completed in 70 AD. under the reign of his son, Titus. The Colosseum was used for public performances, including gladiator fights, theatrical performances and animal hunts, it is a masterpiece of Roman architecture and one of the largest amphitheaters ever built. It can seat up to 80,000 spectators and is known for its use of arches and columns. Its facade is characterized by a series of arches and overlapping floors. The amphitheater was designed to host a variety of events. The central part of the arena, with the wooden floor, was covered with sand to absorb the blood during the fights between gladiators and animals. Over the centuries, the Colosseum has suffered damage caused by earthquakes, fires and looting. However, many of its original stones have been reused in other constructions. I advise you to book tickets online, because the queue to enter is really very long.

text:
Aaaah the Italian organization... a world... but a world... what's the point of making time slots if it's to be all on top of each other like sardines in a box that's too narrow.
A happy mess made up of screams and selfies, groups of 50 bewildered plebeians with no autonomy of their own blocking the corridors, stopping at the top of a staircase to take photos even if it meant blocking any exit… a mess… but a mess… we ended up leaving in order to return at another probably calmer period to savor this place which undoubtedly deserves a little more tranquility. When will we recruit intelligent personnel in Italy capable of understanding that making figures is good but making sure not to make each visit a permanent nightmare is better?

title: Awesome

Remarkable
