title: Amazing
text:
Absolutely amazing and quite surreal to stand on the arena floor and look up. The Colosseum is so vast — you really have to visit to fully appreciate its size and the feeling of being surrounding by thousands is years of history — both of grandeur and brutality. We toured the coliseum with a semi-private tour (max 6, so with our family of five, it turned out to be private) from LIV Tours and it was really great. I recommend booking a tour to learn the full history and background for better understanding. I also recommend the earliest arrival possible to avoid the worst crowds. We arrived at opening and within the hour the crowds were thick. Due to changes in CoOp Culture booking, our underground tour was canceled, but we enjoyed the experience and didn’t feel we had really missed out (although we would have gone to the underground if possible). What I somewhat regret is not having taken more time just to explore and stroll around within the coliseum; we had the option to also visit Palatine Hill, so I feel the time at the Colosseum was a bit rushed as our tour guide moved on quickly. We had the option to stay, but didn’t. Stay as long as you can and take it all in. TIPS - wear sturdy shoes, uneven ground throughout; visit the arena floor early to avoid crowds, go with a guide; wear a hat or bring a sun shade, as it is very hot in summer with little protection from the elements; if you have kids, be sure to bring their passports for proof of age for the free-entry tickets.

Visiting the Colosseum was an amazing, almost emotional experience. Our family all agreed that it was a special experience, even if we couldn’t quite understand why — perhaps the sheer size and grandeur, the  rich history of the gladiators and bloodshed. You’re walking in history and you just feel it when you’re there. Don’t miss visiting the Colosseum if you can.

title: Colosseum=Wonder
text: Wonder! The most beautiful historical structure that I have ever visited, there are immense queues of people to enter inside and many tourists to admire it from outside. The place itself is very beautiful (I live nearby) I don't know what to say. I recommend it to you

title: colossal, impressive
text: Brutal, a gem that perhaps should be cared for a little more although of course it is worth a lot. But the lines of tourists there are, I say, will leave a lot of money. Impressive and colossal, to think about the games and spectacles that the Romans and Caesars saw.... you imagine yourself in another era and dream.

title: Truly a Wonder
text: Definitely lives up to the hype and can’t be captured by any pictures. I highly recommend a guided tour, as exploring the ancient ruins while learning about its history was a wonderful experience.

title: Must see
text: The Colosseum is definitely worth a visit. If you get the chance, you should also take the opportunity to see the inside of the Colosseum. Very interesting.

title: Just amazing!
text:
We visited on a weekday in December, but admission was until 3:30 p.m. We went to the ticket booth after 3 p.m., so we were a little late.
The Colosseum was built in 80 AD by Emperors Vespasian and Titus. The interior of the Colosseum, built about 2,000 years ago, was simply breathtaking. Its size, beauty, and architectural technique. You can go up to the second floor as well as the first floor. I hope you will enjoy the view of the Colosseum from above and the ruins that spread out to the west of the Colosseum.
There are many ruins in Rome, but I thought this was No. 1.

title: Always majestic
text: We might not do the review but we had a great time. Every now and then a dive into the culture of the past is good for us. Imagining what could have been an engineering masterpiece in the past.

title: Night Time Colosseum Tour
text: We decided to book a night tour of the Colosseum, not for any other reason that it would give us the rest of the day to do other things. We're so glad we did, what an experience! The darkness and the lighting added to the atmosphere and we were only one of two groups in the whole colosseum. This was definately the highlight of our trip to Rome!

title: Pick up place for tickets shut.
text:
I booked a Skip the line Coleseum Roman Forum and Pallentine Hill entrance ticket. 
We had to collect tickets from an address near the colleseum before our entrance time 10:00. We arrived early- the building was closed. We returned at 20 mins to our entrance time ( the place was about 10-15 mins walk away) still no one at the building. I then had to call the Ancient Roman Tours office at my expense on  a foreign phone.  Once I spoke to someone he texted my tickets through- not sure why they couldn’t have done this to start with and save us a journey. 
Other than this our trip was fantastic. We got audio guides at €6 each and spent over 5 hours wandering around all 3 venues. It’s a pity it started in a stressful way .

title: Do not miss this historical site!
text: An amazing experience in Rome that we’ve read about since history class as children. I recommend not missing this. We did not pay for a guided tour and had to wait in line for over an hour. It may have been nice to have someone guiding us through the history instead of trying to navigate through the museum with so many people.

title: Stunning

With a great guide.. 3 hours of visiting the entire site. Exceptional guide who explained everything to us: history... role and successions of emperors: architecture and history. Only happiness
But: WHAT A CROWD

title: Our visit Dec2023
text: It was a remembering place for me. It has a wonderful view in day and night. but I think the Italian government should stop construction around it.

title: An Absolute Wonder!
text:
This is a combo review for two separate tickets/visits, both purchased from the official Colosseum ticketing site. 

The “Wonders of the Colosseum: Full Experience Plus Didactic Tour in English (Colosseum, Forum/Palentine Hill/Supersites).” An archeologist guide took us through the Colosseum, including the underground and arena. She was extremely knowledgeable & the tour was amazing and interesting. This was only guided part. Afterwards, we entered the Forum and used Rick Steve’s audio guide on our own. The Forum is so fascinating & the audio guide was good. Then, we were really on our own for Palentine Hill which was overwhelming b/c of how large it is — we simply ran out of time (the Super Sites all closed early, at 3, 3:15pm). We entered only 1 Super Site: Domus Tiberiana, as recommended earlier by our Colosseum guide. It was all too much for one day but you only get one admission for the Forum/Palentine area & cannot return the next day once you use it. 

We also took a second guided “Colosseum By Night” tour after hours because we wanted to see the lights in the Colosseum. Meh. We could better appreciate the view in the daylight. But, we were pretty much the only people in the Colosseum at night, which was very cool. The night tour repeats the daytime tour and our daytime tour guide was much better. Also, it was very cold in December. The after hours Colosseum tour would work well if you either cannot schedule daylight visit OR if you simply want to avoid the masses and the summer heat.

title: New years eve
text: What a sight! Lovely during the new years eve. A lot of people as you would expect, but great experience. Would recommed to find a place close to the Colosseum during new years eve in Rome.

title: Accessibility and bathrooms
text:
I had already been to the Coliseum alone once in 2018, I returned in October 2023 with my family. My mother-in-law injured her foot and we need to rent a wheelchair.
Tip: renting a wheelchair was easy and cheap,
Using the Italian emergency was easy, fast and free,
The Coliseum has accessible elevators and bathrooms,
Whoever is in a wheelchair cuts the queue for the attractions and is also a companion,
Forum and Palatino are unfortunately not as accessible with a wheelchair, but there are those who take the risk.

title: Great experience. Amazing place.
text:
We booked our Full experience tickets (€24 each) with Co-op Culture website a few days before (this was classed as shoulder season, still busy but not crazy!) for 12:55pm entry. This ticket was valid for 2 days but you have one entry each to the Colosseum, Palatine Hill & Forum.  We did Palatine Hill & Forum the following day-this isn’t timed, go early before the tour groups. 

Things I wish I’d known: there are a few different queues. (buy on the day ticket queue was 90minutes wait at the time we got there). 
- With a pre-purchased ticket go to the shorter line-ask a member of staff, they will help! It’s very busy outside and gets confusing.
-You need the ticket on you phone to get in-bar code is scanned. 
-You can join the line 10minutes before your time slot. 
- The person booking the tickets needs ID (& maybe those under 18 with reduced price tickets-this didn’t apply to us)
- They will scan your tickets on 2 occasions so keep them handy
- all bags are put through a scanner, so are you!
- Audio/visual tours are available from the booth inside at €6 each. They use iPods with headphones (you could save money by sharing the headphones with someone you’re close to!!)
- Not many toilets (they are near the audio guide booth) long queues.

We really enjoyed our time at the Colosseum, it’s an amazing place. We found the self guided audio good enough for a first visit but would think about a guide if we came again. 

Take water with you! There’s nowhere inside to buy any (that I saw).
We were there for 2.5hrs.

title: Awesome experience!
text: Visited on a cool but very sunny day. The experience, because of the good weather, made all the difference as there were a number of occasions when you are outside and if it had been rainy much of at would have been affected. The guide we had was absolutely incredible and I think that makes all the difference. We think her name was Jeanie but not her real name . She spoke fluent French, German and English. The latter she said was her weakest language. Well, you would never have known. She was so fluent in her presentation and extremely knowledgeable. She held everyone in the palm of her hand. The visit to the Colosseum and the Forum lasted possibly around 2and a half hours but that passed very quickly. It made all the difference having the guide as they point out so many details that would be lost to the untrained eye. The building was awesome and with the presentation it virtually took you back in time to experience what it may have been like. Well worth the visit.

title: It's worth it
text:
Gorgeous gorgeous!
It's worth the visit, especially with a guide. Lots of cool information that adds a lot to the tour.
It is very crowded there, it is important to choose a good time to take your photos.

title: Exclusive - Official website
text:
We had booked in advance through the official coopculture.it website. It is the only official site and therefore guarantees you to pay the fairest price for a suitable visit.
There were no more visits to the site but by calling and discussing, the person indicated that every day, exactly a week before, places opened up on the site. So the Thursday before our visit we booked a guided tour with access to the basement for the following Thursday (only possible with a guide).
This tour was only available in English and Italian.
Although we found many of the explanations on the panels upstairs, the visit was nevertheless very privileged and exclusive, away from the crowd until access to the arena platform.
We were able to immerse ourselves a little better into the world of gladiators.
The ticket also included entry to the Forum and the Palatine, for which we downloaded the audio guide in French for 5 euros on the official app.
Be careful, it's difficult to do everything thoroughly, but the audio guide allows you to make selections.
We also bought a small guide book with superimposed drawings to represent the monuments as they should have been.

title: Important site, very crowded even in January, park surrounding is important too
text: This important archeological site is located in a larger park focused on the central part of Ancient Rome.  The curators have done an amazing job of preservation and restoration and the view from inside is impressive.  Downside is that is very crowded - our tour guide spoke only Italian so we sadly missed a lot of detail. (Yes, I know, I am studying Italian now...). It is also a bit depressing to realize that the basements (with restored elevators, etc) while fascinating were filled with doomed people and animals and that the whole structure was built largely so that people could be murdered for entertainment, not the highest achievement of Roman society and culture. In my view - except for the spectacular interior vista from the second floor -  better to have read a book about it and enjoyed the less crowded park surrounding.  There is also an excellent bookstore.

title: Witness the past.

title: Step into a piece of history.
text:
There have been a few changes to the Colosseum since was last there 13 years ago. There are many displays of artifacts and models that are worth seeing. There is also the option to go onto a platform onto the arena. There have also been many improvements to make it more  accessible to less mobile or disabled people, such as ramps and elevators (Which do not affect the appearance.)

What really lets the Colosseum down is selfish tour guides who take up the vantage points for long periods of time lecturing their groups, meaning others can't get in to take photos. Tour group leaders also need to ask their groups to only take up 1/2 of a path or walkway.

A visit to the Colosseum is a must when visiting Rome. A bit of advice - DO NOT purchase anything from touts or people walking up to you trying to sell you things as they are not licensed sellers or even residents of Italy. Book any tours either directly through the Colosseum, or through a licenced tour operator.

title: Colossally spectacular
text:
The spectacular Colosseum is synonymous with Rome, just like how Eiffel Tower is with Paris and Big Ben is with London. Completed in AD 80, it is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built, capable of holding up to 80,000 spectators and playing host to many gladiatorial games, plays and even public executions over the centuries. Although substantially ruined by earthquakes and stone robbers, the Colosseum is still a renowned symbol of the Roman Empire, even making it to the exclusive New 7 Wonders of the World list. 

This structure which has stood for nearly 2000 years absolutely lived up to its hype. There is no better way to experience the magic of the Colosseum than to explore its ancient ruins while learning about its history via its evocative museum displays and imagining yourself as a gladiator fighting for your life or as the Emperor witnessing the gruesome contests from the box seat. What would make the experience more surreal is to have performers dressed up as gladiators, slaves, Ancient Roman citizens and the Emperor, and entertaining visitors with mock skits and performances. 

It is advisable to reserve your timed tickets online in advance if you want to avoid the long queue at the ticket counter. The interior walkways and museum area closer to the entrance are extremely crowded, with many visitors and tour groups jostling for spaces, but the further you walk on, you'll find more spaces and vantage points for your photo opportunities and obligatory selfies. When in Rome, do what the Romans do, and visiting the Colosseum is a must do! Truly phenomenal!

title: Our favourite place to visit in Rome
text: Our favourite place to visit in Rome.  We actually visited twice during our stay.  Very busy even mid week in January.  Amazing place.  Highly recommended.  We used the hop on hop off bus for travel to and from our hotel.

title: Feeling like a gladiator.
text: The Colosseum, an enduring symbol of Ancient Rome, is a breathtaking marvel that transports visitors back to the era of gladiatorial combat. Its monumental architecture and historical significance make it a must visit destination, offering a vivid glimpse into the grandeur of the Roman Empire. The echoes of the past resonate through its corridors, making the Colosseum an iconic and captivating experience for history enthusiasts and tourists alike.

title: Must see but a few tips…..
text:
Obviously one of the “must sees” when visiting Rome and you won’t be disappointed.  A few tips:
- Bring your headphones and download the app before you visit as there are stands with numbers all around and you can input the number and the app gives you details of that part of the colosseum and the history etc.
- With the above app you don’t need a guided tour, although we booked the underground tour and it was good to see below the colosseum floor and history 
- Buy tickets on line in advance and you won’t need to queue
- Be prepared outside for lots of people trying to sell you tickets and merchandise.  The ‘sellers’ try to give you “free” jewellery and bracelets and then ask for money for their children!  Best not to engage.

title: Remarkable experience
text:
Everyone who is able to should make it a point to visit the Colosseum! 
To say the Colosseum is historically significant would be a ridiculous understatement. We’ve all heard about it, read about it, been lectured on it, maybe watched a documentary about Ancient Rome, but to go and walk in the footsteps of the extraordinary people who built this wonder and created a thriving civilization makes you appreciate it on a different level. 
There is so much to see and learn by visiting; from the social constructs and hierarchies of Roman society to the many innovations and  building techniques that are still being utilized today! It was an incredible experience that I will never forget.

title: Buy online ticket beforehand and bring your ID
text:

As a lot is outside rainy weather might not be the ideal time to visit.

As the ticket includes several fora bring a lot of time and stamina. And your ID! as you need it to enter with your online ticket. Yes, you want to buy that before hand.

title: " The Roman Coliseum"
text:
The Colosseum is magnificent, this was the largest amphitheater of the Roman Empire.
Gladiator combats, animal fights, grandiose festivals and the death of Christians took place there.
It is an impressive place, the entrance costs €20, but if you go on the first Sunday of the month, you enter for free, after standing in a long line, where you have to wait more than two hours.
It's worth it, you will enjoy it!!

title: Amazing

title: Breathtaking place
text: Wonderful trip and place is charming but be attent that it will be difficult to find place to park your car and it's crowded  but the place is breathtaking 👌 you need to visit

title: Full Experience Attico.Pren.Intero
text:
This ticket cost us €24 each 

You have access to
* Levels 2 & 3 - the BEST views of the Colusseum and up to Palatine Hill
* Level 1 - incl. the Full Access only arena floor

Buy your ticket in advance from the official Coop Culture site and you will be able to head straight to the entrance area, massively reducing your wait time. 
We joined the queue at 11:05 and were inside within 10mins. 
Arrive early (our ticket was for an 11:15 entrance)

Head to the brown lift, which will get you up to level 3… This is truly an amazing experience! 
From entering the covered tunnel to the best views in the Colusseum…. You will not be disappointed!

Then get the lift down and head to the Arena floor… only available to Full Access ticket holders. 
Here you’ll experience the enormity of the arena and get great views of the underground maze. 

Head back up to level 2 for the exhibition. 
This gets a bit more crowded because everyone has access, but well worth the visit and it provides great insights. 

Finally, back to level 1 and towards the exit, but take time to stop off at each of the viewing platforms… some great photo opportunities along the way! 

We originally considered an underground tour, but are pleased that we opted for the Full Access Attico ticket instead!

title: Shambolic procedure
text: A fantastic place. Ruined by complete disorganisation. You get there with no signs or information of what to do. People harassing you to join there tour parties for fast track entrance. We joined one at 20 euros per person still took 2 hours to get in. There are no toilets outside except in information centre and only one inside. We went on the first Sunday of the month which is free entry. Might be worth getting ticket on line beforehand. Once in well worth a visit. As usual many people trying to sell you stuff.

title: Waste of time
text: Like being cattle herded to market. Wish we’d just looked at it from the outside to be honest. There was not much to see inside and it was a faff getting in and out again.

title: Journey through the ages

Be careful, there are a lot of people there, which makes walking and enjoying it a little difficult, but it's worth it. Don't forget to visit this incredible place.

title: Epic history and site!
text:
I visited the Colosseum on Jan. 29th as part of a group guided tour I was taking. From the moment we walked up to the Colosseum it was awe inspiring and the history shared by my guide was incredible. We were able to go inside and walk around while taking in the extraordinary history our guide was sharing with us. I learned a lot and much of it surprised me. It was very crowded as it’s one of the top sites in Rome. One big downside was the bookstore, there are two inside and both closed early so we couldn’t get a souvenir. The one downstairs was actually open but when I walked in the attendant yelled at me that they were closed and forced me out. The store wasn’t closed and she was just on her phone talking and didn’t want to serve the customers (I wasn’t the only one she turned away). It was the most rude experience I had in my week long visit all over Italy and shame on her for behaving in such a way. 

Other than that I enjoyed my visit to the Colosseum and would recommend a visit. Just go early morning to avoid the larger crowds.

title: Colessuem
text: Tourist advice before you go: buy Yr tickets online from the official site, £15euros each roughly. This gets you entry to Colessuem and Palentine Hill/roman forum. The Colessuem is huge and worth a walk around to take in all the history. We didn't do a tour, so can't comment on that.

title: The story

title: Mandatory visit, but be patient
text: A must-see historical site, but I found the visit unorganized and tumultuous. Of course the place is incredible, but the queues, disorganized direction of the visit and little access to interesting places made the visit to the Colosseum the least interesting of my stay in Rome. Even though we chose the Full Experience visit, we only had access to a 'mezzanine' above the basement (with a great view, without which the experience would have been even worse). text: Exceptional Roman building: that said, it is essential to take a ticket to visit before leaving (to avoid more expensive street sales on site) and be patient to enter.

title: Becareful you go the right way and not get overwhelmed by the crowds
text: It is interesting to see the architecture but tbh even in off season it was absolutely heaving. If you are not in a tour prepared to be bombarded by the tour guides and the mindless sheep following them and bumping into you. We ended up going the wrong way and missed half the tour and suddenly was at the exit. As we walked past the exhibition we went down the stairs when we should’ve went left. When we told the attendant this at first she said that’s unfortunate but it is one way. I wasn’t having this and spoke to someone on the exit who was shocked to hear this and sent us back saying to look for someone in yellow jacket. They weren’t them but only staff we could get to. I challenged her again as I wasn’t missing half of what we paid for. It was apparent that while we were there at least 2 other groups done the same. we were able to go back round I think only because we said we missed the arena (which you pay extra for). I said to her clearly this happens a lot and she said yes! I imagine this has all happened since they have privatised and got external services to operate it charging a lot to enter and stuff you full of people. There should definitely be better signage/ block off the way we went so it is one way.

title: Nice tour
text: We did the guided tour in order to get access to everything and it was nice and informative. Everything was well organized and it was easy to find your way to the meet up location. Tours were offered in English, Spanish and Italian. Obviously a must do while you’re in Italy.

title: Breathtaking
text: Exceeded my expectations, the sense of history was overwhelming! As it was low season, not too crowded. Hard to sum up in words but you simply cannot go to Rome without visiting here! We found going to the ‘Welcome to Rome’ 3D visual show in the city a day earlier gave us a real sense of how it would have looked when complete and what happened to the structure over the years (also the forum etc). One for everyone’s bucket list.

title: Disrespect for tourists
text: I purchased a guided tour of the underground for myself, my wife and my ten year old son. I arrived at the meeting point on time and the employee who helped me left us waiting. I lost the group and I had to run after them. But the worst part was entering the Roman forum. Total disorganization in the queue, with groups accompanied by guides jumping the queue all the time. And the Coop employees are condescending about it. After at least 30 people passed by, I didn't accept another group and went in. The employee started yelling at me, and a security guard came and took me out, along with my crying son and my wife. They told me to go to the end of the line. I justified it, I apologized for the stress, but they continued to treat me badly. I bought the ticket on the official website and felt completely disrespected. About 4 employees treated me very badly, harshly and shouting, in front of my son and wife. The visit is interesting, but the company should be aware of this disrespect.

title: Wonderful Must Visit Attraction!
text: Must visit attraction in Rome however, if you are planning on visiting we suggest you get there before 9am like we did, it gets extremely busy. We got there at 8.30am and the lines very not very long, plenty of time and space to visit comfortably in February, we got lucky with the weather it was perfect but soon as we were leaving the lines were getting long and it was chaotic at best. We pre-booked our tickets online and the process was pretty quick early in the morning but order seemed to be getting lost from what we could see when leaving. So get there nice and early for a much better visit.

title: Roman Coliseum
text: Colosseum is monumental, beautiful, imposing and incredible but you can breathe a gloomy feeling where you can see the suffering of the people who passed through there and died, in any case a must-see in Rome, it is worth entering even if they tell you not to.

title: Magnificent monument but the organization be careful
text: Magnificent monuments to absolutely see!! On the other hand, the organization on site is a disaster, there is no real line to queue, nor direction of visit and even less for the audio guide, if you see a few signs here and there it is very rare !

title: Worth seeing the Arena
text:
An easy 5 star for this beathtaking piece of history. Yes it's incredibly busy, full of people trying to scam you but get inside and soak it in.

Having been before I wanted to take the family and added the arena element which allows you into a semi circle of the arena floor which gave a wonderful and calm position to appreciate the site. Well worth a couple of extra euros!

title: Rough process to enter independently, waiting in heat for hours
text:
I give the Colosseum site high marks, but the process to get in is poor. We wanted to see the Forum, but could only buy tickets for both Colosseum and Forum. I tried several online websites but tickets are all gone for online sale weeks in advance. Your only option is to stand in a long line and be hounded by scalpers trying to sell you a tour. We arrived about 20 minutes before the ticket booth opened and had to wait in line over 2 hours in the heat and then wait another hour before you could go inside. There must be a better way.
The Colosseum itself is a very interesting site. There are exhibits inside that try to explain the history of this place. There is a gift shop here too. The sheer volume of the crowds though can be overwhelming. We had been here before 10 years ago and did the underground tour which was interesting. However, I must say that I find the games that took place here to be barbaric and I don’t understand how so many could want to attend the gory events.

title: Arrogant and rude personnel
text: Colloseum as such is nice and interesting, but personnel, staff, security etc. are extremely arrogant  and not service minded at all! They are there for the visitors and not the other way round. Many of them should find another job.

title: Awesome experience
text:
Such a fun and knowledgeable place to visit. I wasn't too keen but was not disappointed. Took a walking tour which was great as i wouldn't have known back story behind a lot of things. It's a lot of walking if you want explore everything here which I highly recommend.
There are few security check so go light with small bags etc  and ID is a must. 
Good walking shoes if you are an explorer. Use restrooms when you have the opportunity.
Definitely a must see !

title: A jump into the past
text: the atmosphere of the place and the presence of archaeological finds allow visitors to completely immerse themselves in the past era and imagine what life was like during those times. The feeling of being transported back in time makes the experience unique and evocative, and ensures that the charm of the past is still alive and vibrant today

title: Run down almost like left to ruin
text:
Was a lot more broken than i thought it would be, at £15 a pop you'd think they'd invest in repairing it. Untidy.
Don't recognise it from Gladiator or Enter the Dragon.
Lots of Senegalese outside selling tat.
Harder to get through than an airport.

title: Very pretty, went with a guide and found out a lot of interesting information
text: This landmark is a staple of visiting Rome, the architecture was amazing and there is an amazing yet indescribable feeling when standing on ground that has seen so much history and has such a rich past.

title: Definitely a must see place
text:
In addition to hosting many terrible activities such as gladiator fights, animal hunting, and executions to entertain the Roman people, it was also used for various purposes such as shelters, business shops, religious barracks, and Christian tombs. While its original name was "Arena", it later became known as "Coliseum", which is the name of the statue at the entrance.

Today, it is one of the top tourist attractions in modern Rome. It has also been called one of the New Seven Wonders of the World since 2007.

title: Colossal Arena
text: To see, this ancient monument which is rather very well preserved is truly impressive. You absolutely must book your tickets in advance on the official website (co-opculture) this makes entry much easier. On the other hand, no organized circuit inside, it's a bit of anarchy. It lives up to its name, even if it does not come from its impressive size.

title: Everything is great!!
text: Great guided tour. Very professional. Very well organized and documented. Three hours that felt short. We will repeat with civitatis. Sofia-Madrid

title: Ancient wonder - be organised!
text:
BOOK YOUR TICKETS ONLINE, IN ADVANCE THROUGH THE OFFICIAL COOP CULTURE WEBSITE! Kids go free (though you need to pick up a ticket for them once you’ve got inside) 
This done, we avoided all the crap from the touts outside. Yes, it was busy, but I think that is to be expected… it’s a major tourist destination in a major city. With prepaid tickets, you join the queue 10 mins before your entry time (admittedly, this isn’t enormously clear and a bit of a jumble of people) then be prepared to pass through airport style metal detectors and have your bags x-rayed. 
Once inside, again, yes it’s busy, but what an amazing piece of history. Husband and 9 year old didn’t agree, but for me, the busyness of the place added to its atmosphere - it was very easy to imagine it full of roaring, cheering, booing ancient Roman spectators! 
We bought all access with arena floor tickets, which was well worth it. We spent nearly an hour on the arena floor taking photos and soaking up the atmosphere. 
We spent 3 hours exploring the Colosseum self guided  (recommend the DK travel guide book or the Lonely Planet one!)
Went back in the evening after it was closed, when the whole city had a different fun vibe - and got some great photos. Felt reasonably safe (and there were certainly lots of police around) - just keep your wits about you and don’t make life easy for pickpockets!
Great experience we’ll all remember for a lifetime.

title: SCAM DO NOT USE
text: AWFUL. do not use this website. It is a scam. We were denied entry at the colosseum even though we had paid for the all access ticket. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. I was left very upset. The ticket agents scanned my tickets and then denied my entry so they are claiming that the tickets were used even though we were not allowed in unless another ticket was bought.

title: Brilliant guide
text: We had the most wonderful guide. She is a retired Chemistry teacher from Liverpool, ultra knowledgeable and amusing. Her name is Felicity (Fe), We loved her!

title: We LOVED it.
text: We booked online way ahead of time and we were glad we did as the line to get in was HUGE. We rented the audio guide and slowly meandered through. We LOVED it! There are wild parrots living in the ruins.

title: Gorgeous
text: Visited in October with magnificent weather. For me, an essential visit during a stay in Rome. Dated combined ticket purchased 1 month in advance, no waiting at the entrance.

title: Spectacular
text: I knew it was wonderful but they still pleasantly surprised me. Very well explained, very interesting story, very immersive. It really is a MUST when you go to Rome. No wonder it is one of the seven wonders of the modern world.

title: Great discovery
text:
Pleasant time, even if there are people, pleasant visit.
I recommend, very beautiful monument.
What a great outing in Rome

title: Great tour of the Colosseum and the surrounding sights
text: Anna guided us and gave us excellent information about the Colosseum, the Roman Forum and Palatine. We were all impressed by her expertise and her friendly manner.

title: Too crowded and unorganized
text:
Right now it seems that their business is in the guided tours (€41 pp), if you want the tickets buy them online (€18 pp), if you decide to buy them there it may take more than 2 hours to get it at the ticket office.
On the other hand, the organization is non-existent, everyone is sent to the same place despite there being other alternatives, so the crowds that form inside are shameful, since the recommended capacity is far exceeded.

If you want to enjoy the experience, it is best to go first thing in the morning.

The most beautiful and interesting thing is to see it from the outside.

title: A blurry beauty
text:
A monument seen and reviewed from all possible angles, but which when I go back to see it in person always seems too suffocated.
Undoubtedly a stop not to be missed for anyone in Rome, but its beauty at times seems a little blurred and commodified by tourism

title: Er symbol of the city
text: As per the title, a very interesting visit to the symbol of Rome (come on romaaaaa), with a climb up to the top floor, enjoying a sublime view over the entire amphitheatre, thus being able to understand how my great-great-great grandparents who were senators of Rome felt. urbe during the shows

title: Colosseum
text: History of Italy!!! On the occasion of the marathon we visited the Colosseum!!! Ticket too expensive!! In fact we visited it from outside!!!!

title: Good experience
text: The atmosphere is great but be prepared to book in advance and it solds out really quick. Book online and be prepared to queue. The inside is nice but too many people everywhere.

title: Colosseum VIP Access with Arena and Ancient Rome Tour
text: Brilliant day out and guide first class experience learning all the way round very busy at times but most enjoyable and highly recommended booked with Vaitor

title: Spectacular Colosseum
text: The Colosseum stands as an awe-inspiring testament to ancient engineering and the grandeur of Roman entertainment, offering visitors a glimpse into the heart of history.

title: Awesome imagining a gladiator stepping out into the arena
text:
The Colosseum is amazing to walk round the outside which you can do for free. Also interesting to go at night to see it lit up. 
We decided to pay to go inside and to go to the arena floor. The arena floor ticket means you get to stand on the modern replacement wooden floor partially covering the underground section. That's all there is to it, but I am glad I paid extra for it as it was awesome to walk out onto the floor imagining the many gladiators who had experienced it. There is a large exhibition about the Colosseum, and also about Trajan's column, to go through before you get to the seating areas to view the inside of the Colosseum. We went for 0900 and we were allowed entry 15 mins early (but no earlier). We had minimal waiting time through the ticket entry and security checks. As we left about 2 hours later, we could see the crowds building up and queues were much longer despite people having tickets. 
Worth a visit.

title: Best time… ever!
text:
We had four brilliant days in the historic city. Be prepared to walk, walk and walk some more. We stayed centrally to the city but you can’t be near everything.. walking is the preferred mode of transport as you will pass many adhoc things to do. There are historical church’s open for free & full of interesting frescos, paintings, artefacts and history. Many museums, too many for our trip but something for everyone.

We visited the Vatican which takes your breath away with so much talented art under one roof. The colosseum tour is amazing. We pre booked our tour to include the underground of the colosseum & paletine hill… not a cheap ticket but well worth the expense. If you can stretch to the cost, do it. This is a one off tour for us, we won’t be likely to go again so it’s a must do. Our guide was very knowledgeable and described Ancient Rome perfectly bringing lots of extra info into the whole experience. Your imagination just can’t do it all justice.

Other things that we did that were also fascinating was the Forum in addition to Trevi and the pantheon. Please visit piazza Navona & do the underground tour as this used to be a stadium in Roman times where Olympic type games were held unlike the colosseum which was for Gladiators only.

If you want to see something that isn’t archeological the visit the Capuchin Crypt… bizarre! But what a weird arty sight it is, not to be missed.

All entrance fees are reasonable and not unexpected in a major tourist destination. We did lots more but I’m going to leave that out as it’s your trip and you will want to explore. Have the best time, we did!!

title: Unforgettable
text: If you don't buy the tickets online, you have to wait in a long line, many people will approach you wanting to sell you a much faster guided tour without waiting in line, it's a lie, you're not going to wait as long nor are you going to enter as late as they tell you. They try to trick you into hiring the guided tour, it is much more expensive, since the least would pay the same as an adult, architecture and fine arts students have discounts that they do not.

title: Visit to Roman history
text: The most spectacular historical structure of Rome is built with incredible construction techniques. On the second floor of the magnificent building, there are exhibitions containing detailed explanations of the other monumental structures of the novel and the construction of the Colosseum. It is an activity that will take all day with the Roman Forum. Unless you buy a special ticket, you cannot go down to the arena floor of the Colosseum and cannot use the elevator. It is a very long walk and steep stairs. get ready to go

title: Great experience ticked off.
text: A great experience that if in Rome you have to do. I didn’t book any tours, etc. and felt that walking around the outside was enough to take it all in. The queues are big so book before you come to Rome and chose the skip the line tickets. Overall, a great experience that is ticked off for me.

title: Very beautiful
text: Very admirable to see this. The building is starting to show signs of wear, but still has many charms. Once again we had a very good guide who put a lot of passion into such a tour.

title: History and Trip
text: I've always wanted to visit it and when it's there it's much better, an incredible experience, it's well worth it for those who know the history and like to explore

title: Brilliant but so so busy
text: The Colosseum, is amazing and definitely worth a visit but it is so busy! We had pre purchased tickets and that made access extremely easy, staff were very kind and patient. The building oozes its history, the views are incredible but at all times you are surrounded by thousands of people.

title: Rome
text:
Magical place that smells of history.
Fantastic!!! There are several places to feed or rest. A must-see together with the imperial forums.

title: False Advertised Headout & Show Me Italy Tour - no ARENA FLOOR ACCESS to Colosseum even we paid for it.
text:
Do not book your tour with Headout "Colosseum, Roman Forum, & Palatine Hill Guided Tour with Arena Floor Access." We paid Headout for the event and the touring company is "Show Me Italy".  We paid and booked this tour in advance and we never get to see the Arena Floor.   The only reason we booked this, is because the title stated "ARENA FLOOR ACCESS" It was one of the highlights we look forward to our five-year planned trip to Italy.   When checking in with the tour guide, he said he didn't receive a ticket to the arena access then took us to a view point to look down at the arena and said this is the best spot and took off.  

I demanded an answer and waited over a week still had no answer from Headout.  There are a few people from our group asking as well,  I hope they will see my post and add their complaints/experiences to mine.  Thank you for your time.

title: OK
text:
The truth is a bit disappointing
Very busy with people
There is nothing really different or impressive about the place whether you see it from the outside or go inside.
A mandatory site is true but not something that is wow in my eyes

title: Colosseum - Colossus
text: I would venture to say that of all the attractions in Rome, the Coliseum is the most popular. What does that mean? That you need to buy tickets in advance. In addition to ensuring that you don't end up without a ticket (and leave without visiting the Coliseum – which would be quite a frustration), having your ticket on hand will save you a lot of time, after all the queues there are huge.

title: impressive
text:
had a wonderful time
exploring , i would say the outside is far more impressive than the inside . worth a walk around the full ruins .

title: The Colosseo is a must see in Roma.
text: Even if you have been there once it is worth it. Make sure you get there in the morning because the afternoons are different in Italy. I found finding a tour guide was weird. The could organize things so well but they just can’t get their act together.

title: If you grew up with a Saturday football match ritual the Colosseum will resonate with you
text: We are so lucky to have these windows on the past still standing after 2,000 years - and it remains a delight we can still identify with the bread and circus sporting passions of the masses. Wrong language, but plus ca change, eh? A wonderful building. NB. Will be busy and there are hawkers prowling but they are generally friendly folk and part of the fun.

title: There are no words…
text:
It is thrilling to see the majesty of a monument so important to our history and which has made history...
Beautiful, imposing, impossible to miss if you come to Rome

title: Brilliant place, go early, and in winter.
text: Amazng place. We went at 8am in winter, crowds quite low at that time (maybe 150 people in total there). We passed by again at 11am and it was very crowded (thousands) - even in winter, so early paid off. You'd be crazy to endure summer crowds and heat here.

title: One of the key historical architecture of Roman and European heritage
text: It still remains the largest ampitheatre in the world. It's architectural and profound engineering has enabled this structure to still stand after all this time. This wonder and its beauty still attracts tourists from all over the world. Can take a few hours to cover the entire place.

title: Best tour in Rome
text: Fantastic tour, Barbara was superb she brought the history of Rome and the Coliseum to life. We really enjoyed our day in the thunder & rain.

title: A Wonder of the World
text: An amazing wonder of the world located in the centre of Rome.The collosumm is easy to get to as the area has its transport station and many bus and tram routes that will get you there.As you can imagine it's very busy but well worth it you just have to choose your times accordingly and be patient They lots of places where you can purchase tickets in the area so it's a well organised place to visit You must stay for darkness and sunset as will be amazed at the lighting and the spectacular views from around the many vantage points

title: Unavoidable
text:
To do absolutely despite the crowds.
Guide strongly recommended to enjoy the experience of the Colosseum as well as the museum and forum around it

title: Don't forget photo I.D is you want to go inside the Coloseum.
text:
Amazing building from the outside.
Sadly we weren't able to buy tickets after standing in the queue for 40 minutes to be told that we needed photo I.D.
There were no signs up that we could see saying that you needed I.D and no staff explaining this to everyone.
Just wanted to make sure future visitors don't turn up without I.D.

title: The history of Rome
text: It is the most important Roman amphitheater, as well as the most impressive monument of ancient Rome that has come down to us! It can be visited in a group or privately with a guide!

title: Very busy, lots of big groups and no room to appreciate it
text: I believe we got unlucky in our timing but our trip to the Colosseum was ruined by huge groups of students and other tour groups, who kept blocking the entrances and the display of information and history of the Colosseum. We tried our best to navigate around them but it felt like the place was above capacity and we could barely move. Everyone we encountered was either oblivious or rude. Soured the experience for us and we actually couldn't wait to leave, which is sad (our time slot was 2.30pm on a Monday). One good tip we were given was to do the Forum/Palentine Hill the following day and to use the other gated entrance which is less busy. The tip paid off as the main queue for the Forum was massive and we pretty much walked in at the other smaller entrance.

title: The most unforgettable experience in Rome with the Trevi Fountain... very friendly people and I have never seen so many tourists all the time
text:
To travel is to live !!
Italy...Rome has one of the greatest monuments, the coliseum...many more but that is the greatest...unforgettable

title: Colosseum tour
text: Went on Colosseum tour with David Battaglino as our guide. Very informative with a bit of humour as well which made tour very enjoyable. You will not be disappointed.

title: An Impressive Historical Monument
text: By exploring its ancient arenas and intriguing corridors, we delve into the rich and eventful history of ancient Rome. A must-visit for history and culture lovers.

title: A great visit!
text:
A great pleasure to see such a monument!
Need an audio explanation by sector following our trip to the site!

title: Magnificence
text: The Colosseum is a majestic work that embodies the grandeur of ancient Rome. Its architectural grandeur and its troubled history make it a timeless icon. Walking within its walls is an experience that transports you back in time, making you reflect on the grandeur and complexity of Roman civilization. Its beauty and charm will remain imprinted in the mind of anyone who visits it.

title: Well worth the visit
text: We booked a tour, but the guide didn't take into account anyone with disabilities so we struggled to keep up with him. We ended the tour with him 1 hour before the end and looked round ourselves. He didn't give us chance to look at points of interest  he just talked then carried on walking. Very disappointed but the place itself is amazing

title: This guide needs to take a breath.
text: An iconic place to see. Our guide talked too fast, walked too fast, did not check when the group got separated from her. Some of us could not understand her English. Overwhelmed with the amount of information that was given at such a frantic pace.

title: NOT THAT EXCITING AND NO TOILETS IN THE ENTIRE PLACE
text: Not exactly that exciting. No washrooms in the entire place. How is it possible an international tourist attraction has no toilets.

title: A little overrated
text: Just a bunch of remains, looks nice on the outside, inside loads of people, nothing much to see. You need to spend at least £70 per person to walk around all areas. This is a rip-off to be fair

title: Yes or yes you have to go!
text: Unforgettable experience, as a recommendation, arrive on time if you go on your own, there is not so much line, the longest line is for the tours and they have a different access given by the number of people who take tours. In my case we went on our own and the museum explains the history of the coliseum. In front of the coliseum there are some very good cafes to stop by for a drink and contemplate the monument.

title: An excellent visit
text: One of the world greatest attractions. But do yourself a favour. Buy your tickets in advance. I would also recommend you buy both the level 1 and 2 and the main floor. Get the audio guide. The one you download is not much good.

title: An old world beauty
text: It needs no introduction, just seeing it day or night justifies the trip to Rome. Ancestral traditions and pure Roman culture speak for themselves. Entering and walking through its corridors takes you back to ancient times, a time of gladiators and Roman orgies.

title: Good experience
text:
Steeped in history and a must see for anyone that loves that era (gore and all) 

Just remember to bring your passport of some form of ID as we wasn’t aware of this

Also there are lots of steep steps

Just watch out if your booked and there are a lot of tours about as they just seem to steam ahead without any regards to those who are enjoying at their own leisure 

Also if you want to walk around the underground you need to pay for this so check your tickets include this. If not there are ticket booths indoors 

Other than that it was good to walk around and take in the history

title: magical, fleeting moment mixing stage fright, fear and awe while you imagine entering the arena two thousand years ago
text:
We got the Full experience ticket lasting for two consecutive days, valid for Colloseum and Forum Romanum. Thanks to some computing glitch, the first attempt to buy tickets had money "reserved", no tickets arrived into my email. second attempt was a success. i have yet to check if i was charged twice (i think not, as the ticket provider stated so).

full experience ticket had access to arena. alternatively, you could get tickets to the underground or the spectator space.

You largely pay for the engaging moment when you enter the arena. then, you snake through the crowds towards exit and the colloseum spits you out back into the crowds of tourists and dinghy people trying to sell you a tour of colloseum.

title: A moment of history
text: the Colosseum is worth seeing for its history but once inside a little disappointed very little renovated earlier consolidated by concrete and crowded with people with limited access. On the other hand, Montpalatin and its gardens are a pleasant time to do.

title: Impressive monument! The ticket is expensive!
text: Our visit this time to Rome coincided with the great marathon which reached over 350,000 tourists. Wonderful weather and certainly an imposing monument that is definitely worth visiting when you come to Rome! A particularly steep price if you want to get in.

title: Amazing!
text: The chance of a life time! My daughter booked a night visit and so worth it. Excellent tour guide who took care to inform us of every aspect of  the Colosseum, taking time to answer any questions other visitors had. No words can describe such a visit. So worth it.

title: Grandioses
text: Magnificent but I remain convinced that you need to take a guide. The audio guide is nice but nothing beats a real guide. No need to book at 8 a.m. the crowd is there the same as at 10 a.m. – 11 a.m.…

title: Justine a great French-speaking guide
text: Guided tour obligatory to understand the monuments, we had Justine French-speaking guide it was perfect, she answered all our questions and illustrated her visit with lots of anecdotes. I recommend this guide

title: Fantastic history but get a guide.
text:
We did the full underground tour which is well worth it to get a real feel for the place and understand the history including the hospital and training ground places (ludus Magnus) and how they were used. 

We had plenty of time and were pleased we had our guide re just navigating the queues to get in!  Well worth getting a guide.

title: PHOTO PHOTO :-)
text:
lovely place

It's in a bit of a bad state but we have to think that it's been there for over 2000 years.

visited inside and it's fantastic

title: Toilet Alert
text:
It’s a must see and, as you would expect, it is very busy. 
Very few toilet facilities in the immediate vicinity and the very limited ones inside are always busy and also pretty grim!

title: Book the full experience to the Attico.
text:
As with everywhere in Rome, buy a fast-track ticket! Tip: pay a little more for your ticket to include a visit to the Attico - the very top of the site - which was fascinating and very peaceful away from the crowds, with amazing views. 
Below the Attico, tour guides with groups constantly stopping infront of you but that can’t be helped. Very busy but the wander around the practically deserted Attico was special. The site is, of course, unmissable. 
Loos always a problem in Rome (and Italy in general).

title: So much to visit in Rome
text:
Located in the heart of the Eternal City, the Roman amphitheater is always beautiful to see...
Excellent public transport service to reach the location

title: THE EMBRACE OF THE COLISEUM FROM THE TOWER OF SAN BONAVENTURA
text:
Looking out from the medieval tower overlooking the church of San Bonaventura, located on the Palatine Hill, with the unusual view of the Colosseum provides a unique and enveloping emotion.
Admiring one of the most iconic monuments in the world from a privileged point of view allows the gaze not only to appreciate the imposing grandeur of the Colosseum but to extend its gaze onto the greenery that frames it together with the skyline of Rome and the Celio district.
In this enchanting setting, among the thousand-year-old ruins of the Palatine Hill, you can breathe the very essence of ancient Rome, with its timeless charm that continues to amaze travelers from all over the world.

title: Symbol of Rome
text:
An awe-inspiring monument

The Colosseum is an awe-inspiring monument that is a must-see for any visitor to Rome. It is the largest amphitheater in the world and was used for gladiatorial contests and public spectacles for over 400 years. The Colosseum is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Italy.

History

The Colosseum was built in the first century AD by the Roman emperor Vespasian. It was completed in 80 AD by his son Titus and was inaugurated with a series of games that lasted for 100 days. The Colosseum was used for gladiatorial contests, animal hunts, and public executions. It could hold up to 80,000 spectators and was the largest amphitheater in the world.

The Colosseum today

The Colosseum is still standing today, although it is in ruins. It is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Italy and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Visitors can tour the Colosseum and learn about its history. They can also see the underground chambers where the gladiators and animals were kept.

Tips for visiting the Colosseum

The Colosseum is open from 8:30 am to 7:15 pm, from April to October. It is open from 8:30 am to 4:30 pm, from November to March.
The entrance fee is €16 for adults, €2 for children under 18, and free for children under 6.
There are long lines to get into the Colosseum, so it is best to buy your tickets online in advance.
You can also buy a Roma Pass, which includes entrance to the Colosseum, the Roman Forum, and the Palatine Hill.
The Colosseum is located in the center of Rome, so it is easy to get to by public transportation.
There are several restaurants and cafes near the Colosseum.
Overall

The Colosseum is an awe-inspiring monument that is a must-see for any visitor to Rome. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Italy.

title: Iconic ancient landmark and symbol for the city of Rome
text:
Colosseo is a landmark ancient building and symbol for the city of Rome. It was constructed between 72-80AD by Emperor Vespasian and Emperor Titus and is considered to this day, one of the greatest achievements of engineering and architecture the World has witnessed. It is a jaw-dropping structure and 'wow' experience for many visitors when seeing the Colosseum for the first time. 

Opening hours are 8.30am, closing times vary depending no the season. There are a variety of different ticket options available including combo visits along with the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill and another with the Vatican museums. It is highly recommended to do so online and in advance through booking sites like Tiqets to avoid disappointment of being sold out on the day you try to visit. 

Given the colossal size of the Colosseum, you will see this ancient structure from many different locations around Rome. In addition to seeing the building from up close, you will find excellent viewpoints nearby at Palatine Hill, the street along the Roman Forum and elevated grounds below Campidoglio. Views from elevated grounds just across Via Nicola Savali are really nice as well.

title: Attico is the way to go
text:
We bought Attico tickets through the CoopCulture website.

Despite there only being 8 tickets released per half hour slot, they do not seem to be in as hot demand as the Underground tour tickets. Still, get on the CoopCulture website 30 days beforehand when tickets are released - note that in the days leading up I kept searching availability and did a dummy booking to check I understood the process, and the website blocked my IP address suspecting I was a bot! I had to run around cafes and use their wifi on a different device to get around it.

We turned up expecting a security queue, but they wouldn't even let us past the first check until 10 minutes before our reserved slot. We cleared security etc within 5 minutes.

From there we went to window 12 to ask to add the Arena floor to our ticket - this was free to do, so no additional charge. And they added the Arena floor even though Arena floor tickets themselves were showing as sold out for our date.

So from there we headed up to the Attico via the lift (though note you need to climb a flight of stairs or two in order to get the best views) - its a truly excellent experience as you get the best views in the house AND there's hardly anyone up there so a really peaceful and relaxed experience.

There is no tour element on this ticket, so you go at your own pace which we thought would suit our kids. 

We then headed down to the Arena floor for a different perspective. This is a bit busier, both are worthwhile though. 

Overall, we enjoyed our visit, but it does require some organisation unless you are going to spend a lot of money on tours (but note that tours can only get your tickets 30 days out too, and if they fail to do so they will cancel your tour or offer you a different tour).

Overall, well worth the extra 6 euros per adult ticket on top of the regular entry ticket (childrens tickets were free).

title: Rich History and Beautiful Architecture
text:
A magnificent experience of rich history and intricate architecture wrapped up in only a few short hours of exploring the breathtaking elliptical amphitheater. Throughout the build there were a multitude of signs giving a brief overview of the dense history that brought us to the colosseum we know today: the stage that held gladiator fights, executions, public spectacles and the like beginning around 70/80 AD.

The colosseum’s long history is apparent when wandering the building, not only through the information provided to create the museum that it has become today, but also through the ruined walls and scuffed floors, each part a reminder of what had once occurred there. All the way down to the passages that are opened up in the centre of the amphitheater, allowing us to see below what the audiences of the battles that took place would have been able to see themselves. 

As for the more modern touch you’ll find around, there’s vending machines stocked with water for when the sun is blaring down. And asides from the undeniable allure of the history that the colosseum holds on it’s own, the exploration through it is enhanced by the novelty stores you find that hold a variety of stock - including a bookshop that has a wide range of informative pieces on the colosseum, the history of Rome and some staples such as The Iliad and The Odyssey. 

Truthfully, it was an overall greatly fascinating experience that I would love to have again down the line (an experience which I would recommend to anyone that has the opportunity to take it).

title: Synonymous with Rome
text: The image of this world famous amphitheatre stands synonymous with Rome, just as the Eiffel Tower is for Paris and the Tower Bridge for London. The Colosseum is about 20 Centuries old. The ragged outline of the building was caused because the people of Rome didn't think much of the Colosseum when it wasn't in use anymore and scavenged freely building material from the outer walls. The same happened to the Pantheon in the 16th Century when the current Pope allowed Bernini to scavenge all the bronze from this in disuse fallen church to create the famous Baldacchino in the St. Peter. The people from Rome in earlier times didn't appreciate their historical buildings as much as we do now. The time that you could step into the Colosseum for free is long gone. The entrance fee now is about 16 Euros per person. On line tickets are very popular, so sometimes the line for regular tickets is shorter.

Unless you want to go underneath the arena floor or go up to the 3rd tier, this should be more than enough for the average visitor. title: Bit of a disappointment and be careful with tickets!
text:
Quite disappointing and overall, I regret not going back to Ephesus instead (which to me is a far better experience and has lots more to see, preserved in a better state). 

We entered via the Forum entrance at 9am in the morning (which was great as it was very quiet and we hardly had to waste time queuing). The very nice lady gave us our tickets and pointed to the 10am on it and said that’s when the colosseum would open. I asked if we had to be there for that time or if we could go in any time after that and she relaxedly said the latter. 

We enjoyed strolling through the Forum and pretty gardens until 11 and then went to enter the colosseum itself. We were abruptly told we had missed our time and when I explained why, I was told to speak with someone else…3 unsympathetic people later and I was starting to get a bit deflated and frustrated. I finally got to speak with someone at the ticket office and explained and she stated it was impossible for us to have been told that (which made me cross as I wouldn’t lie) and that we needed to pay again. I suggested perhaps I could feedback that it hadn’t been clear at all and signs around the ticket offices would be helpful to ensure people fully understand, irrespective of what someone verbally tells them (as there may be language misunderstandings etc) and she said that was no good, they’d heard it loads of times before (so it clearly wasn’t impossible to have been told that AND they have chosen not to make it clearer) and I shouldn’t feedback to her and I could email the park (I haven’t been able to find an email since being back). 

We ended up paying again as we had come all the way and it would be stupid to go home without seeing the colosseum (though to be honest, I was quite unimpressed by it compared to other places and very put off by the handling of the staff). 

All in all, really wasn’t worth it for us (though the wisteria were very pretty in the gardens).

title: A Timeless Journey: Exploring the Colosseum with My Family
text:
Our visit to the Colosseum with my family was a captivating journey through time, immersing us in the grandeur and history of ancient Rome. As one of the world's most iconic landmarks, the Colosseum provided a backdrop for unforgettable moments of discovery, wonder, and connection with the past.

Step into Ancient Rome:
From the moment we laid eyes on the majestic Colosseum, we were transported back to the glory days of ancient Rome. The towering structure, with its impressive arches and weathered façade, stood as a testament to the ingenuity and engineering prowess of the ancient Romans.

Walking through the grand amphitheater, we could almost hear the echoes of cheers and the clash of gladiatorial combat. Our imaginations ran wild as we envisioned the spectacles and events that once unfolded within these ancient walls, from epic battles between gladiators to thrilling chariot races.

Educational and Interactive Experience:
Our visit to the Colosseum was not just a sightseeing excursion but also an educational and interactive experience for the whole family. We joined a guided tour led by knowledgeable experts who shared fascinating insights into the history, architecture, and significance of the Colosseum.

Learning about the construction techniques used by ancient Roman engineers, the social dynamics of the games, and the lives of the gladiators added depth and context to our visit. Interactive exhibits and multimedia presentations further enriched our understanding of this iconic monument and its place in history.

Panoramic Views and Photo Opportunities:
As we ascended to the upper levels of the Colosseum, we were treated to panoramic views of Rome's historic cityscape. From this vantage point, we could see the majestic ruins of the Roman Forum, the towering dome of the Pantheon, and the sprawling city beyond.

We snapped countless photos to capture the beauty and majesty of the Colosseum, pausing to soak in the breathtaking views and create lasting memories with our family. Each photo served as a reminder of our shared adventure and the timeless allure of ancient Rome.

Family Bonding and Shared Discovery:
Exploring the Colosseum with my family was more than just a tourist attraction—it was a journey of discovery and shared bonding. We marveled at the ingenuity of ancient Rome, pondered the mysteries of history, and reveled in the joy of exploration together.

As we walked hand in hand through the corridors of the Colosseum, we forged memories that will last a lifetime. Our visit to this ancient marvel served as a reminder of the enduring power of human creativity, resilience, and imagination across the ages.

Conclusion:
Our experience at the Colosseum was a testament to the timeless appeal of ancient Rome and the profound impact of history on our lives. As we bid farewell to this iconic monument, we carried with us not only memories of its grandeur but also a deeper appreciation for the rich tapestry of human civilization.

title: Incredible energy!
text: A beautiful tourist spot in Rome. An incredible energy. It's worth buying tickets in advance and getting to know the inside. It's incredible inside.

title: Fantastic but buy ticket in advance
text: We bought tickets via GetYourGuide. There are many providers and ticket types to choose from. We chose a skip-the-queue ticket including a 25-minute multimedia video, which was really nice. The provider was the tourist office, where the physical ticket had to be collected and where we watched the video. The tourist office is located at the back of the Roman Forum. Afterwards we had 2 hours on our own in the Roman Forum before entering the Colosseum. A good tip is to use the entrance to the Roman Forum at the opposite end of the Colosseum, here there was almost a queue in contrast to the long queue at the entrance closest to the Colosseum.

title: Exceptional site but poor organization and expired audio guide…
text:
The site is exceptional. Nothing to say, we'll be amazed inside.
But a few negative points all the same: no information on having to present your ID, no information on what time to show up. Some sites tell you 30 minutes before and when you arrive, 1 in 2 people are turned away. It's true that it's complicated to put a sign at the entrance to one of the most visited sites in the world... for your information, it's 10 minutes before so there's no point in coming before...
Lousy audio guide. Old iPod from the 2000s, this could use an update. Once again, it must lack resources with 3000 people per hour…
Take a guided tour early in the morning and there you will really live the experience to the fullest!!

title: Rome!
text: History in the open air! Such a spectacular monument is always worth admiring even though it has already been seen over and over again

title: Impressive monument.
text: An impressive place at first glance. I will never forget the first time I saw the Coliseum in all its grandeur, even though it was so weather-beaten. When you enter, your imagination soars, even more so if you have hired a local guide. I highly recommend it.

title: Attico visit procedures, almost like visiting by yourself
text:
We just did the Attico tour, April 2024. 
They allow 8 people per half hour which means you essentially have the top section of the colosseo all to yourself.  Zero crowds and basically zero other people. Thats fantastic. 
When you get inside the stadium, walk all the way as far as you can go around the ground level. You’ll eventually get to a tiny elevator. There will be a sign that says wait here for the attico experience. Wait there. When the elevator comes back down, they’ll get you. It takes you up high and then there are still more steps to go even higher. 
Once you’re back down, walk over to gate 12. It’s by itself, not part of gates 1 to 11. Stand in that line (the audio guide line) and present your attico ticket to them. They’ll give you a sticker to access the arena level. When you have that sticker, you can go there. Without an arena ticket, it’s nearly impossible to really see into the underground. 
Finally, visit the general admission area where most people are going. 
In general, the lines are well maintained. You really do only need to be there 15 minutes early.

title: Yemen, Sanaa

title: Book an early morning slot before it gets too crowded.

title: A great  tour  experience of this historical landmark
text: I took a tour of the Colosseum with the tour agency Towns of Italy. The tour guide(i forgot her name) was friendly courteous and gave us detailed information before we enter and and while inside. My ticket also included visiting the Roman Forum and the Palatine(Adjacent building across the street)Which continues the rich history of the emperors who lived over there and the public buildings(Basilica) of that time. I visited Wednesday April 24th.

title: Must do but allow extra time!
text:
An awesome experience! We loved the Colosseum and it really is a must see.
Getting in can take a while even when you have pre-booked tickets, they are not very efficient or well organized for a major attraction, so make sure you have more than enough time to explore!
There is a museum section on the upper floor which is really interesting and shows a lot of the tools and methods used to build these structures. The signage is good but it gets crowded so it can be tricky to see/read it all.
You need to purchase an all inclusive ticket to access the upper floor which is definitely worth it, it also gives you access to the Forum so hang on to your ticket! 
Overall a great value ticket as both sites are well worth the visit.

title: The wonder of stone
text: The Flavian amphitheatre, built by Emperor Vespasian in 72 AD. and inaugurated by his son Titus in 80 AD, it is a monument built above a dried up lake, which was part of the gardens of Nero's Domus Aurea. Consisting of three arches plus an attic where the flagpoles extended to unfold the velarium, large sails operated by sailors of the imperial navy, to provide shade for the spectators. It owes its name (Colosseum) to the previous presence of a bronze statue of Nero with the features of the god Helios of about 30m, called Colossus. Under 18s do not pay, at the entrance by showing your ticket (previously purchased either directly on the site or from one of the many agencies that sell tours, they tell you to go to counter 10 to get free tickets for minors). I personally booked with Headout, where an audio guide was included in addition to the tickets. Tickets include Colosseum, Forum and Palatine. Depending on your budget you can choose the basic tour or the full experience, with the second you can also access the Colosseum underground and see Augustus' study on the Palatine Hill which it deserves.

title: Very Nice
text: Quiet walk, everything ok I don't have much to say but I would recommend seeing it to believe it very relaxing but at the same time tiring

title: Historic!

title: Beautiful

title: Alllllssoooo got to stand where they burned Caesar's body.
text: I booked on Expedia for about $35/person with free cancelation. It was called "Walks: Skip the Line: Premium Colosseum Tour with Roman Forum & Palatine Hill Confirmation" by City Experiences/Devour Tours and it was a guided our and I believe it was either this tour or substantially similar, and it was a lot like how it sounded. I was with about a dozen other people with a guide I would describe as a woman in her late 20s to mid 30s who was originally from the Boston area. She worked pretty hard and knew a ton. I was pretty impressed with her knowledge of the forum and everything in it. There was a young kid that kept asking her pretty tough questions, and she was up for it. The meeting point was the edge of a park and the wait wasn't too long. There were excellent ruins on the Palatine Hill, which has a good view of the Circus Maximus. The forum and Palatine Hill is a big place. I didn't realize you can stand virtually exactly where they burned Caesar's body, and our guide pointed out the old Senate building. The tour has a lot of hills. There are water fountains. Lots of stone so bring comfortable shoes. In the Colosseum we didn't get to go down to the floor. It was largely in the middle ring. We did get to walk up to the Emperor's box. You can also see from the Colosseum the gladiator school across the street where they were kept. Also the gates of the victors and of the dead were pointed out to us and I think we may have left through one. Also got to get up close to at least two arches. The guide had some visual aids in a booklet. It was long enough and hot enough we were very ready for lunch afterwards. Pretty happy we did this.

title: Plan your visit carefully
text: The Colosseum and the Roman forum are very interesting to visit, absolutely buy your ticket online 1 month before the date of the visit on the official coopCulture website, 18€ for a single visit, or 24€ for the Full experience ticket, it includes arena and with luck the underground passages, we also have access in the Roman forum to 8 great sites in addition with this full experience, but plan for a minimum of 5 hours of visit, we were not able to visit them all because we had not planned enough time for this, we had 3h30 too bad because the site is well maintained and pleasant to explore (if it is not too hot). We did not take an audio guide or guide because very good quality documentaries are available on the internet with 3D images. We regretted the lack of information to enter the Roman forum, 6 entries are possible and indicated if you look carefully on the ticket, almost everyone wants to enter through the entrance in front of the coliseum towards the arch of Titus , there was a queue of more than 1 hour to 2 hours while walking less than 5 minutes further we passed by the Palatine Hill without waiting. No staff from the site went in the queue to give this information . By having the right information and tickets purchased in advance, you have a good experience which can quickly be ruined if you wait in line for hours.

text: Great experience and a must for all visiting Rome for the first time. Expect long queues so would highly recommend booking a skip-the-line tour to get fast tracked and also provide invaluable knowledge about the history of the Colosseum and Roman Forum etc.
