text:
A trip back in time that you absolutely must do. A must-see in Rome.
The entrance to the coliseum gives access to the Palatine Hill. It takes 2 to 3 hours for the visit.

title: Coliseum
text: Had wanted to visit here for as long as I can remember and finally got there. It is everything I had hoped for. Personally the outside is as impressive as the inside for me. The one major tip I would give is definitely pre book a ticket to go inside, the queues for on the day tickets can be long, and if it's hot not something you want to do for an hour or more, other than that enjoy.

title: Worth a visit! Just make sure you pre book your ticket!
text: One of the best things to see in Rome. We purchased our tickets on the official Colosseum website for €18 each about a week prior to going and had no issues at all. We walked straight through the gate at our allotted time, had our tickets and bags scanned and then we were in. I downloaded and used the free MyColosseum app for an audio tour and it was OK (maybe a 6/10). If you are really interested in the history, I would recommend booking an official tour with a guide. We only had to wait a minute or two for people to clear the way for various photo opportunities. Certainly worth a visit. Kept us busy for just over 1 hour.

title: Dissatisfied
text: The tour as well as another tour through trip advisor was canceled at the last minute. When one travels as far as I did for these tours and then can’t get another one can ruin a trip.

title: Colosseum was Classic!
text: Camila was lovely and so knowledgeable. This was a free tour and we got so much out of it. The guides are not paid and rely on tips. It was disappointing to see that some travellers took advantage of this and did not tip especially as our group was quite small. It was an excellent 1h 45 min walking around this amzing historical sight with breathtaking views around every corner.

title: Totally recommended
text: Spectacular, we went with a guide and he reminded us of the times of greatest splendor of the coliseum. A very charming place…..

title: Colosseum, exterior photo
text: If you want a good photo of the Colosseum from outside, we recommend the small wall on "Via Nicola Salvi", in front of the "La Biga" restaurant. Historical and emblematic place that you cannot miss if you go to Rome.

title: The feeling of being cheated
text: We purchased full experience tickets on the official website for 5:15 p.m. We went to the meeting point for the basement tour at 5:10 p.m. and about ten people were waiting with us. Around 5:45 p.m., someone came to us to tell us that we wouldn't be able to go down to the basement because we weren't at the meeting point at 5:15 p.m., which is not true. Despite argument, we were unable to visit the basement. We felt like we were being cheated. The visit was still interesting, but we really wanted the basement. Too bad, because it leaves a bitter taste...

title: Amazing to see
text: You MUST go see the colosseum when in Rome. Even if you don’t go inside it is amazing to walk around. Look at all the ruins nearby

title: Single
text:
I recommend purchasing tickets through the official "Coop Culture" website, which opens for the next 30, this way you will pay cheaper than on other websites.
If possible, book the earliest times, which are better, with fewer people.
Finally, I don't recommend the audio guide sold on the website, it's very messy, it doesn't give the information as simplified and you have to look for the audios to understand, the advantage of having audio in Portuguese (BR).
If you speak English or Italian, I recommend the ParcoColosseo app, which is a free audio guide with lots of information, valid for both the Colosseum and the Roman Forum and Palatine.

The Coliseum itself is incredible, take advantage of every opportunity you have to go see it, day and night, inside and outside because it is incredible.

The visit is interesting and it is important to have an audio guide or tour guide to learn more about the history.

title: A must-have
text:
Architecture and history rub shoulders with every stone, at every turn! Impressive to see and admire.
The only downside: the crowd!!!!

title: It's worth the visit, but going in? No way. Queues to end the day.
text: Imagine the confusion getting into a football stadium during the Classic. It's this madness. There are people from all over the world in a total conurbation. But the grandeur of the Coliseum is worth at least passing by to take photos.

title: Pretty
text: Seeing the historic sites is exciting. There are always a lot of people and the queue is long. The surrounding hygienic conditions are also not very good. There is a lot of room for improvement.

title: History and fun
text: A piece of history and great for family visit. We all learn about the history and story of the colosseum, so a visit there enrich our knowledge even more. No doubt it is a major tourist destination.

title: THE GREEN BALL
text: nice experience, shame about the complaints of the spirits of the Rumasti Roman soldiers imprisoned in the cells screaming "centurion, centurion!" continuously.

title: Always busy
text: The Colosseum is impressive. Getting into it is not. I was there 3 times in April 2024. You stand in line for 1 1/2 to two hours to buy a ticket. The ticket you buy may be for an hour or two later. Then you get in the entry line when it’s your time. If you want to get in with the least hassle, there are usually tour guides who’ll add you to their group for about 55 euros. Where the lines are entering there are obvious groups. Many times a guide will stand apart yet near the group. Some will likely approach you if you are close to the line. 55 euros may seem like an expensive entry, but worth it to me, and you also have a guide. The Metro stops right at the Colosseum. It is very crowded at that station. There are announcements and signs warning of pickpockets. Tour guides told me Rome tourism has no off season. Midsummer weather is hot.

title: An obligatory stop
text:
The Colosseum is the largest surviving monument of Roman antiquity, and is, in my opinion, a site of great historical and archaeological rather than artistic interest.
I visited it with a ticket booked online, and I must say that entry, at the appointed time, was quick and well managed.
Inside I would have liked perhaps a few more information panels.
The route, however, is well thought out, and allows, in about an hour, to observe it in all its essential aspects.
A must-see on every visit to Rome.

title: Burnt and creamy
text: The first time I slowly walked across the burnt, cracked, old white stones, the sun was lighting  fires in my brain, then dad said ‘ice cream?’ Maybe I’ll visit next time.

title: Buy tickets with a guided tour!
text: Unless you have a guided tour, it isn’t worth fighting the crowds to see the inside of the colosseum.  Even with the audio app, it wasn’t worth the effort to fight the crowds.

title: A historical gem
text:
Beautiful views of the Colosseum from all around. 

A few mixed feelings about the ticket process. Couldn’t get the official website to load and tried for weeks ahead of visit, looked online at tours but didn’t want to be restricted with timing and the reviews all were a bit mixed. We couldn’t find anywhere to buy a standard ticket online. You have to que at the ticket booth (on the opposite side of the wall which isn’t sign posted) while you get accosted by touts offering tours for €76 per person (which dubious as you have no idea if these people are legit). Que didn’t take long though and cost €18. They were sold out of ‘full experience’ tickets but that only meant we missed out on the attic and underground - no real issue. You then have to que again for entry, but luckily for us we went first thing so didn’t have to wait long at all! Although seems a bit inefficient having the ticket office on the other side. 

The security staff are amazing, very thorough!

There’s lots to see but not much to read so probably a tour would be beneficial!

title: Magic monument
text: A truly magnificent experience! We came to Italy in April this year and my god it was beautiful... the must-see in Rome..

title: Unforgettable Adventure
text: Visiting the Colosseum with friends was amazing. The atmosphere was electric, and the history was palpable. Despite the crowds, the experience was well-organized, and the views were breathtaking. A must-visit in Rome for history buffs and adventurers alike.

title: UNIQUE COLOSSEUM
text:

A visit to this historic attraction is a must.
Always book tickets online to avoid queues (I booked in advance from the Colosseum's official site at a very good price). The ticket includes entry to the Roman market and the Palatine Hill. Excellent walk with sneakers, hat and mink!!

title: Amazing site to visit
text:
We bought our tickets from the official website before we left UK. They go on sale exactly 1 month before you travel. You book the time slot you want to arrive at. You need to arrive at least 15 mins before your slot. Print tickets at home before you leave UK.  They disappear quickly so book asap.We bought the Full experience Attica at 24 euro each. This gives full access to all levels including the highest level via lift with some steps up to top level which werent bad at all. 
We went 8.45am and just join the line to go in .It opens 8.30 so only waited about 15 mins. You need your passport for ID not photocopies.. Our ticket included entry to the Forum and all the sites across from the colloseum. Spent practically the whole day there. Amazing to see this structure and how well it has stood the test of time. Highlight of Rome.

I decided to visit Rome for the sake of the Colosseum. Even at school, the pictures sank into my soul. The Colosseum is very impressive. I took 100 thousand photos.
Adore!

title: An Unforgettable Journey Through Ancient History
text: Visiting the Coliseum was a truly unforgettable experience. Stepping into that place full of history was like taking a step back in time. Although it's always full of tourists, it's understandable as it's one of Rome's most iconic landmarks. In fact, a trip to the Italian capital would not be complete without a visit to the Colosseum. It is the beating heart of Roman history and culture, and to be there is to witness the grandeur and impressive legacy of an ancient civilization.

text:

title: Fantastic
text:

title: The price of a closed gestalt is 17 euros and 3.5 hours.
text: Having visited Rome many times, I never managed to visit the Colosseum from the inside. The length of the line was scary. And finally, I decided to close the gestalt. I spent 2.5 hours in line; the Colosseum itself took an hour. I did not respond to offers from speculators to sell a fast pass, like most of those who were worth it. The largest surviving ancient Roman structure is impressive even though the zealous Romans did not plunder two-thirds of the amphitheater until Pope Benedict XIV dedicated it in 1749 as the site of the murder of Christian martyrs and saved the remaining third. I was inside amphitheaters in Arles, Verona, Tarragona. From the outside I saw the amphitheater in Nimes. The Colosseum is, of course, bigger. Started by Emperor Vespasian (the one who said Non olet - Money has no smell) in 70 AD. , it was completed a year after his death by his son Titus in 80. The latest improvements were already carried out by Emperor Dominician from the same Flavian dynasty, which is why the Colosseum was called the Flavian Amphitheater. They built it on swampy soil, which was not easy from an engineering point of view. But the Romans were still at the peak of their power in the 1st century and solved the problem. Only lining the building’s perimeter required 100 thousand cubic meters of travertine. Not a trace remains of it, because all the beauty of medieval Rome is based on cannibalism at the expense of its ancient predecessors. Only imagination, and maybe modern video reconstructions will allow you to imagine the velarium - a huge awning over the amphitheater that protected all spectators from the scorching sun. The Colosseum is now visited by about 6 million people a year, who calmly disperse in the passages cleverly made in antiquity. Below, where there was an arena (harena in Latin - "sand"), the hypogeum is visible - the remains of the engineering system below, where there were mechanisms and auxiliary rooms where gladiators and wild animals were kept for cruel performances in the Colosseum. Hydraulic mechanisms made it possible to lift even elephants from below! After walking around the perimeter and photographing everything and everyone inside and even a little outside, you can get ready to leave. It all took 17 euros per ticket and 3.5 hours. Not bad for one of the 7 wonders of the world...

title: Excellent service, beautiful place!
text: Simply speechless! Wonderful, everything perfect! I was in Pisa, Livorno and Rome, the perfect destination for family, outings with friends and to make incredible memories!

Book early. We had two tours cancelled due to changes in their visitation programs, so we didn’t get a tour or the underground. Regardless we had a great visit.

text: The architecture and history about colosseum is a special. But I want to ask from everyone who  visited there to pray for animals and slaves died for ancient Roman’s and their kings celebration.

title: wonderful experience of visit to Colosseum
text: It was a great time visiting Colosseum in Rome ! It's huge and old was really cool to see. the inside of the attractions gives the feeling like the gladiators are really fighting inside. Also, the views from the high points of colosseum was also great and gave a scenic view of Rome.

title: A fascinating expereince
text:
Visiting the Colosseum was an unforgettable experience that transported me back in time to ancient Rome. The sheer size and grandeur of this iconic amphitheater are awe-inspiring. Walking through its ancient corridors and imagining the gladiatorial contests and spectacles that once took place here was truly captivating.

Despite the crowds, the atmosphere inside the Colosseum is electric, with whispers of history echoing in every corner. The architecture is remarkable, showcasing the ingenuity of Roman engineering and construction techniques. Standing in the center of the arena, I couldn't help but feel a sense of reverence for the historical significance of this monument.

title: What a spectacular must see
text:
We lined up at 730 the morning of our visit with about 20 people in front of us, the ticket booth opened at 830 with the line moving very quickly. It was very easy to access tickets on the day.  The ticket cost 18 euro, is valid for 24 hours & includes entry to Foro Romano Palatino also.
A BIG TIP!! Take your ear buds & download the app at the entrance whereby it follows you around the Coliseum with a very detailed commentary as you go.

text: Area congested with construction and the metro inaccessible due to the chaos of people. Worth the view and some souvenir photos but preferable in the evening or early morning

title: Magnificent Structure-Chaos As You Go In
text: It is a must see in Rome  as the building is magnificent and historic despite the many areas that are  barricaded and not accessible. If you could design a dysfunctional entry process , the people who manage the Colosseum. have done a magnificent job as  it was a total mess. We had an e-ticket and were advise to form a line  but close to opening at 8:30 am they assembled the tents and entry barricades and the crowd swarmed and chaos resulted. title: My visit to the Colosseum in Rome
text: My visit to the Colosseum in Rome was nothing short of spectacular. Stepping into this ancient amphitheater, I was instantly transported back in time to the days of gladiator battles and grand spectacles. The sheer size and grandeur of the Colosseum were awe-inspiring, leaving me in awe of the architectural genius of the ancient Romans. Exploring the corridors and stands, I could almost hear the roar of the crowds and feel the excitement of the games. The views of the surrounding city from the upper levels were breathtaking, offering a glimpse into Rome's rich history and vibrant culture. Overall, my experience at the Colosseum was unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone visiting Rome.

title: Historic place . Photographs come out really well inside and outside. Book your tkt on the official website.
text: Place in itself is charming. Ruins inside could be better maintained and history could be better explained. Photos come out really well. Also I suggest no need to book skip the line as lines are always there. I also suggest you go in the evening when lines are lesser. title: Fun & expensive 4 day trip to Rome with my husband
text:
My husband and I went on a little 4 day adventure to Rome, we went site seeing and when we went to the colosseum, we didn't have any tickets (sold out online, so book atleast 6 months in advance if you want to go underground) however, we overheard this tour guide assistant gathering some people to come join the English tour, so we asked and we did it, cost £40pp for the guide plus regular ticket price pp but it was worth it to hear about the history in depth and have a laugh with some strangers.

The views from some of the attractions were amazing, every church we went into was so beautiful, so much detail and history. 

Good transport, I'd recommend the bus from the airport, tax is extortionate. If you're going into Central Rome 30 mins in taxi or 50 mins on bus. Also I recommend buying a metro/bus ticket for X days you're in Rome for as you can use it on both metro and bus whenever. 

The only "bad" more annoying  experience we had in Rome was the con artists trying to make money by throwing African jewellery at you saying it's s free but then ask you for like €20 and it happens everywhere. There's even guys with roses interrupting your meal (if you sit outside) to ask if you want to buy a rose.

There's lots of queues too, so if you're wanting to see attractions like the trevi fountain with less people go at like 5/6am.

Expect to spend around €70 for your evening meals :) 

Was just nice to go on holiday with my husband tbh

title: Nice building

title: A majestic journey through history
text: Stepping foot into this iconic historical landmark left us speechless. The grandeur and magnificence of the Colosseum were truly breathtaking. As we walked through its ancient corridors, we couldn't help but imagine the thrilling gladiator battles and vibrant spectacles that once took place here. The rich history and architectural brilliance of the Colosseum were evident at every turn. Our children were captivated by the stories of emperors and the tales of ancient Rome. This visit not only deepened our understanding of history but also created treasured memories that will last a lifetime.

text: I booked a tour with Get Your Guide but apparently I managed to book a tour without a guide and I also paid for a ticket for a 17 year old which apparently is free I was very disappointed as we had no one to explain the history and we ended up on a self tour the sights were amazing but lack of a tour guide was unforgivable this company must know that the reason you book through them is to get a tour with a  guide. title: Don’t expect the workers to be friendly, nor compassionate.
text: I took my 83 year old mother and my 82 year old father for the first time from the U.S. today 5/26. I had bought our tickets online. We were so excited! We got through the line after waiting 30 minutes or so. The guy checked our tickets and let us through with a sticker. We walked a ways taking pictures. Then we got to another point, where they were checking tickets again. Unfortunately, it was my fault, but we came a day early. Our tickets were for the 27th. I said can we please just go today (since the place is an absolute zoo) plus we almost missed our time,  since taxis aren't as easy to get as they say. The lady was so callous and cold. No come back tomorrow, or purchase new tickets! The line for new tickets would take at least a hour or more to stand in line and mom can’t do that! There were hundreds upon hundreds of people there. Two more elderly people wouldn’t make a difference,  plus we had tickets, bought and paid for, but just for the next day!  I’m not sure what kind of people turn away 2 people in their 80s?We left and saw some other sites. We will return tomorrow, but unfortunately my mother will have to stay behind, since she has health issues and can’t make the journey two days in a row, plus she has to conserve her energy for the Vatican! It just broke my heart that they are more concerned with making money and following the rules, then using their common sense and compassion to allow my mom to experience what she flew all the way from America to see! Shame on you!

title: Amazing place
text:
Incredible place to visit. You can get tickets from the colosseum website for much cheaper than other places listed. These tickets will also get you access to the Roman Forum. It is €16 per person for the self guided basic ticket on their website though you will need to book probably a month in advance at least. Sometimes tickets are available closer to the date but it is not guaranteed since this is a very popular attraction.

Even with a ticket you will need to wait in a line to get in. The skip-the-line tickets are just to skip the ticket purchase line not the line to enter the colosseum. The line to purchase tickets here is around an hour to hour and a half and the line to enter the colosseum is around 30 to 45 minutes. Make sure to arrive earlier than your time to enter since you will be in line for some time. There is a metro station right outside the colosseum.

title: Grandiose monument
text:
What a splendid monument. Part of Roman history. Who hasn't dreamed of one day visiting this place.
It suffers from its reputation with a considerable number of visitors per day.
To be able to access it easily and quickly, we booked a guided French group tour "Colosseum-Palatine Hill-Roman Forum" lasting 3 hours.

title: Amazing Architecture
text:
We booked tickets for a guided tour through an external site.
Even though we had booked the guided tour you still have to wait in a line for groups although it moved quickly. When you get past the first checkpoint you then have to go through airport type scanners and your bags go through an X-ray machine.
The Colosseum is an absolute breathtaking arena now so you can only imagine what it must of been like when full of 65,000 people. There are plenty of exhibits about to explain the history of the colosseum if you are not on a guided tour.
The inbuilt air conditioning system within the colosseum must be one of the first ancient world.
Although the colosseum has been partly destroyed by two earthquakes, much of the original structure remains.
This is a must visit if you are in Rome.

title: Worth paying a little extra for additional experience.
text:
We got our tickets directly from the Colosseum website. To do this, you need to be ready at about 9am exactly 30 days before you want to visit, because that’s when the tickets are released - and they go quickly. We got the “Full Experience” tickets, with timed entry, which include the underground and the arena, but not the attic. It cost 24 euros each for the adults, our 13-year-old son was free. But you need to make sure you order a ticket for children at the same time - there is an option to choose which tickets you need once you’ve put the total number that you are getting. Everyone needs ID as well - and the name on the ticket must match exactly the name on the (e.g.) passport.
When you get to the colosseum, join the security queue at the white tent. With these tickets, once through the bag scanning, you will be taken to a separate line (quicker than the other lines) where you show your tickets and ID. 
Inside the Colosseum, there is a waiting area on the right, past ‘information’ where you wait to be escorted to the underground. (Even if you haven’t booked a tour, you need to be escorted there - they won’t let you in without an escort). We went with a group of about 20 or so. The escort doesn’t give a guided tour, but shows various parts of the underground area, including a short video. 
The underground tour leads directly to the arena part of the tour, where these tickets give a slightly different perspective to everyone else, in a less crowded area. After this, you are free to explore the rest of the Colosseum, including the second floor where there are a number of displays with history, etc.
Definitely worth a visit, and we are glad we paid the little extra for the additional parts of the tour.
One word of warning! If getting the option to do the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill with the same tickets, they are only valid for the day of entry and the following day - it is NOT 48 hours from the time of entry!

title: History
text: A legendary place and you cannot leave Rome without visiting it. Historic place. If you haven't seen the colosseum, you haven't been to Rome

title: Overwhelming and impressive Amphitheater, ancient and dazzling history
text:
It is wonderful and exciting to see this structure with so much history, and shows an incredible past of techniques, activities and customs, which would be very different today.
The structure is very well preserved considering the years that have passed and the changes through which it has survived, including partial demolitions, vandalism, looting, theft and also attempts to erase a past that existed.
Just like us, there are THOUSANDS of people who visit it daily, so it must be done in advance, and in addition, it is possible that it is advisable to do it with a group from an agency that will help move through the lines more quickly and close the complete tour with the Explanations and well guided. It can be from one hour, at least, to several hours depending on the level of depth and route you want to take.

title: Can't not visit the Colosseum when in Rome!
text:
Absolutely wonderful time with family. We visited multiple times, first to visit the in the evening when the crowds have mostly dispersed - personally that was the best time for visiting and experiencing the Colosseum (Amphitheatrum Flavium), during the day the crowds would make the place look like a concert is going on. A sea of heads (so to speak).

On our second visit we took a tour and having someone walk us through the history and significance of everything we saw made the experiance much more worthwhile.

Another ✓

title: Enjoy
text:
We booked online directly through the colosseum, no need to book through tour companies. Unfortunately I was late booking therefore the online time I could book was 1:30. 
We arrived at the colosseum around 12:30 as unsure of the process. 

Children are free, but unable to book these in advance theeefore on arrival go through the advance booking line and once inside after security there is a ticket box and they give you the child’s ticket here…. Really simple process! 

Queues look huge but go down really quickly and we were inside within 15/20 minutes. 

This was our main reason to come to Italy alongside Pompeii however I must be in the minority but I found it quite underwhelming. The amount of tickets they sell your squashed inside, constantly being moved along, everyone pushing to get a photo opportunity we couldn’t enjoy it. 

It was nice to have visited and we got lovely photos but in all honestly the outside is the most impressive and I kind of feel I would be satisfied with visiting the outside rather than actually going in!

title: Worth a visit
text: We did a tour through Viator / highly recommend. Beautiful place to see and lots of history. Queues were long but moved quickly.

title: Stopped for a quick visit
text: We were short on time but this structure truly is massive and incredible. Wish we had more time to enjoy it. Clearly a highlight of Rome

title: Simply historical
text: I love walking in the historic center of Rome with my family and looking at the spectacular monuments it offers, and the Colosseum is certainly one of these

title: Good organization and perfect guide
text: Great experience! Of the hundreds of guides there must be in Rome, we were lucky to have Carmelo. He made the 3.5 hour tour of the Colosseum, the Forum and the Palatine seem like 30 minutes. Carmelo had a great knowledge of the history of Rome, which he embellished with small anecdotes and an entertaining narrative for the little ones too. , he made us immerse ourselves in ancient Rome like no one else and he had excellent care and attention with all the members of the group, you could hardly get careless and get lost with him in charge. Thank you very much for making the visit an unforgettable experience. Thank you!

title: Impressive
text: An impressive building, both inside and out, with a rich history. We took a tour of the underground parts of the Colosseum, which was very informative and professional. The museum was also beautifully set up, with an impressive collection of artefacts and miniature versions showing how certain parts were created or looked like. A visit to the Colosseum should definitely not be missed during a visit to Rome.

title: A must see!
text: So in awe of ancient sites. It's fantastic to see this in person and must definitely see the inside. Don't miss the displays that show the history of the place.

title: Simply marvellous
text: It's a mesmerizing place and definitely one of the wonders of the world. It has history written all over. The architecture is simply amazing. It is always very crowded and thus I suggest you pre-book your visiting time. The arena is one of its kind.

title: Amazing

text:
Very slow crowds, tickets have to be bought online, the lines to get it at the box office are an easy 2 hours.
Apart from this, it is spectacular

text: To see once in your life! Big regret of not going inside because the outside is incredible! It’s something to do while you’re out of town

title: Must see at least once in your life
text: Exceptional life, a place to visit at least once in your life and delve into its history and that of the civilization that built it

title: The Classic Site, Huge Crowds
text: One of those places that you have to see in your life.  The ruins are huge, take up a big area.  And one can imagine the incredible events that were once held here.  Expect to see huge crowds all around you.

title: Amazing building, poorly managed and staffed
text:
We booked in advance for a guided tour. The day before arrival we received an email saying they were no longer doing guided tours on Fridays or Saturdays. And they were going to refund the tour guide price. When we arrived we had nothing but hassle about our tickets saying that they had already been used. At each of the site colosseum and forum we were treated like criminals. Clearly they had changed something on our ticket. One lady who I was asking for help just kept turning her back on me and walking away to deal with somebody else. What made things really bad was that there were clearly many guided tours going on, not just by external providers but also the colosseum site. So why lie to us!? Also why couldn’t we have been given headsets to guide us around the site?
As an aside the site really needs to do something about the size of organised tour groups. They are ridiculous, and ignorant of anybody else visiting the site making it virtually impossible to see any of the exhibits.
Wonderful site, managed by clowns.

title: Terrible service, amazing building
text: Went through the painful process of getting our full experience tickets with the arena and undergound included, which included constantly refreshing the terrible ticket sale website. We arrived at the colosseum 15 minutes before our 9:30 am time slot as recommended. After showing our tickets at the entry we asked where to go and were pointed onwards. We continued to ask the staff we encountered along the way for guidance and were just ushered on to the next area. Eventually when we asked the attendent at the entrance to the arena, she said we needed to be with a group to go underground. We were eventually directed back to the entrance where amongst all the people and other signs there, there was a small sign saying undergound group meeting place. The guide there told us we had missed our group and that there was nothing he could do. When I asked if the two of us could just tag along with the next group, he said it was fully booked and gave us no additional form of recourse. There was no indication during the booking process, recieving the tickets, or upon entry, indicating that we would be joining a group to go to the underground, and there were no instructions given on what to do when we got there. We took every effort during the planning and execution of our visit to gather all the information to make things go smoothly, and tried utilize staff to further this. Overall it is terrible customer service and a terrible experience.  If you are going, just get a guided tour and avoid the terrible service that they have developed.

title: The Colosseum does not consider people with disabilities.
text: 05/22/2024 - 08:45 a.m. The place is impressive, terrible work staff. We were accompanied by a disabled person who uses a cane. A woman with straight black hair (staff) who was in the first filter in the line pretended not to listen, we spoke to her several times when she finally paid attention to us and we commented that the person had a disability, very serious and in a bad way (rude) told us to go stand in the long line. So why do you announce on your website that access for disabled visitors and a companion is a priority? We previously paid for all our tickets online (5 tickets), and we did not validate entry to the Colosseum free of charge for the disabled visitor and her companion as advertised. Finally to leave we used the “special exit” for the disabled and (a bald French-looking man to be a little more specific) equally rudely yelled at us to leave. Just remember that because of tourism you have work and because of  tourists you eat.

title: Fast, efficient, less expensive- do it yourself entry
text: Booked an entry timed ticket at their official site for just 18 euro each. That way we didn’t line up for anything except security. The entry was efficient and swift, Incredibly crowded but Rome seems a popular destination right now so we expected that. More signage would have been helpful to describe what we were viewing but it was an incredible experience nonetheless. Highly recommend our do it yourself approach.

title: A Benchmark
text: Truly a benchmark on which any future stadium is based upon, futuristic even considering when and how it was built. It’ s a must see but I do recommend you go with  a guide, it adds a lot to the experience.

title: Too crowded to be enjoyable
text: So busy with tourists that there's little pleasure in visiting this magnificent monument. Very restricted access with the 'ordinary ticket'. Try to get tickets for the underground and arena areas, but they sell out as soon as available. Consider just a walk around the outside (also heaving with tourists) and a trip to Ostia Antica, where you can enjoy the ruins of a whole Roman city in peace. It's on the outskirts of Rome, but can be reached by metro.

title: Why wouldn't you go see it??
text:
Colosseum
Why wouldn't you go see it?
It's huge, impressive and housed real gladiators!!! 

Do not go on the first Sunday of the month, its a very very big queue. Yes it's free to get it, but it is up to three hours wait. 
Ignore the touts, on the first Sunday, there is no skip the line. No pre bookable tours starting in an hour. If you pay and come back in an hours, he is gone and with your €50 each. Don't get scammed. 

You can walk all the way around the outside, fill up water bottles from any of the fountain areas. Toilets cost €1.20 each. Only one set there and they are dirty and smell. 

There are many tours, we spoke to a couple on a two hour tour. The evening tours are more expensive but allow you into the lower areas. You need to book these online and well in advance. We tried 5 weeks before flying and all booked up. 

Make sure you walk back at Dark to see it all lit up. Loads of police there at night so it is very safe to be there at Dark.

title: must see

title: Prefer official tickets online
text:
If you go to Rome it is mandatory to see the Colosseum and the Forum at least once.
There is always a long queue for tickets but you can easily book on the official website without surcharge. The ticket is also for the Forum but entry to the Colosseum is limited so you must choose a time and show up appropriately. Calculate the times well. 
Go and get the audio guide before entering, otherwise you will miss a lot and don't rely on the app with the audio guide, I wasn't able to use it.
It took me an hour to visit it.

title: Much improved compared to the past
text: A visit to the Colosseum, one of the most representative sites in the history of humanity, is always something not to be missed. title: A must see. But you know that already.
text:
We spent over 4 hours in the vast area that comprises the Colosseum, Palentine Hill and the Roman Forum, with a tour guide. It was expensive, but I definitely think having the guide brought it all to life.

It's hard to comprehend that you are walking on roads and touching walls that were built by slaves thousands of years ago. 

We learnt that almost all of the marble, the statues and the precious stonework that decorated these vast places has been 'stolen' / 'recycled' over hundreds of years, right up until the start of the 1900s when new laws were created. Whilst portable objects left the country in their thousands, and new palaces and mansions were built in Rome itself using some of the materials, the vast majority of the stonework was taken by none other than the Vatican. (From the tone of the guide's voice and the way she described these stories at different stages, we sense definite animosity amongst today's Romans towards the Vatican for this.) 

The amazing thing is that as the city continues to dig and expand (from new archaeological digs in existing sites, to the extension of Metro line C), they continue to find new and incredible things under their feet. Just recently, the black stone (Lapis Niger) that was at the very centre of Ancient Rome, was uncovered right in the Forum!

title: An obvious must see/do if in Rome
text: Just a must do on a visit to Rome. We would definitely recommend a full experience guided tour of the underground - Katia our guide was superb, and the information she provided helped massively in giving context to everything.

title: Full Experience Arena ticket is not worth it
text:
The visit is interesting for those who are visiting Rome for the first time. Just go.
Tip: the Full Experience Arena ticket is not worth it, so buy normal tickets anyway, as entry into the "arena" adds absolutely nothing to the experience of the visit. The best photos will be taken at the level of the normal visit, towards the end of the circuit.
Is the coliseum beautiful? Just on the outside. Inside it may have its historical value, but, truth be told, it's not pretty at all. If you don't have time to visit the interior, you don't need to regret too much, as viewing the outside is already 50% of the experience.

title: In need of renovation
text: Really run down, building in a bad state of repair, air conditioning really poor. Good to see they cleared up the blood after the last show.

title: Colosseum,  Roman Forum & Palatine Hill, Rome /Italy
text:
When in Rome you have to visit the Colosseum for sure but please do not forget to visit ancient Rome areas like the Roman Forum & Palatine Hill. 

It is absolutely worth it.

You can get individual tickets for all three areas and explore on your own or you join a smaller group tour with skip the lines tickets (recommended in scorching Roman summer) to avoid long dysfunctional, chaotic waiting lines. 

Honestly though,  keep in mind that tour groups can be hit and miss even when you book with solid providers (via Viatour etc), at the end it all depends on your tour guide dejour, if (s)he /they are up to the task, work the headphones, speak loud enough in your language, have the right knowledge / answer your questions and even show up at all on time....

title: The Colosseum….Incredible Experience!
text:
This is the second time I have visited the Colosseum and I was once again blown away by the history, cultural significance, and the ingenuity of this amazing structure.

There are so many Colosseum experiences, not to mention individual or group tours, to choose from that it can get overwhelming. If you take one thing away from this review is to reserve a ticket for at least the arena floor and the underground experience. Both of these areas are not to be missed.

Stepping onto the arena floor knowing that is where gladiators stood, with all eyes on them, waiting for the combat to begin is an incredible feeling. From that vantage point you can look up into the spectator’s seats and into the emperor’s box. The arena floor is not the original but that does not diminish the feeling.

The ultimate experience is exploring the underground levels of the Colosseum. It is a maze filled with passages, small rooms, and tiny spaces used to hold animals. There is a recreated “elevator” that uses ropes and leavers to lift the animals to the arena floor. Image the smell, the dank, the heat, and the fear from the gladiators, animals, and the prisoners who would become fodder for the animals.

The Colosseum MUST be on your list of places to visit, go inside, and experience!

Earthquakes, stone robbers, and a modern-day graffitist cannot dim one of the most amazing “New (modern) Wonders of the World”.

Now the practical….
-Purchase time-entry tickets from the official website, Parco archeologico del Colosseo. They offer inexpensive tickets for all of the different experiences and different timeframes (24 hrs, 2 days, etc)
-A bonus the Colosseo ticket also gets you into the Roman Forum and the Palatine Hill so it is great value for the price.
-If you want to visit the Colosseo purchase your timed-entry tickets online do not wait in line there. It is long, you may not get the time or date you want. Speaking of getting the date you want, purchase your tickets well in advance. I tried to purchase my tickets for a Saturday two weeks in advance and none were available.
-Do not fall prey to unofficial channels when buying your tickets. You may end up paying too much or the tickets may be fake.
-If you rather not do the go on your own thing you can book a guided tour.
-If you are on your own download the official Colosseum app. It will give you information about what you are seeing and where to go. 
-There are no information panels so that app will come in handy.
-Have a government ID with you. Not a photo copy but the original.
-Do not be late for your entry time. If they are sold out and you come late you may not be allowed entry.
-Your backpack, purses, etc will be searched before entering. 
-There are stairs (some steep) and a lot of walking so wear comfortable and sturdy shoes.
-Bring a bottle of water.

title: Interesting to us again and again
text:
We wanted to show our kids this amazing historical landmark and it was just as great as our visit 10 years ago. The reserved ticket system worked well (few lines, they made people wait until their time slot, quick security check), and we easily found the window where we could get the kids' free tickets. 

We listened to Rick Steves Audio while we toured, and everyone was fascinated by the "imagine this..." scenarios. You really can picture what it used to be like if you know what to look for in your mind. 

Some sections are really busy, especially at the beginning of each level (you walk mostly in one direction; they control the flow) and around the museum part. You can find some less busy spots to peek down into the lower levels and look around if you are patient. You can also find shade when needed on a hot day. Definitely see this once in your life!

title: Very good
text:
The Colosseum is a breathtaking site that every traveler to Rome has to see. The largest amphitheater ever constructed, this magnificent structure is a striking example of the Roman Empire's mastery of architecture and engineering.
You can't help but be amazed by the Colosseum's immense size and scope as soon as you walk inside. Picture the clamor of the spectators and the gladiatorial fights that formerly occurred inside these walls.

The Colosseum is a fascinating historical excursion. Here, history comes to life, and you almost feel as though the gladiators, emperors, and onlookers who once called this famous building home are still there. The Colosseum is a unique experience for anyone interested in ancient Rome, history buffs or not.

It's definitely worth it when you are visiting rome. Also, the Roma forum and Palatine Hill are within walkable distances. So you can plan everything in one day. 
  The night views are highly recommended. The restrooms are kept and maintained very well. Beautiful place don't miss it.

title: Respect the engineering. This is not your everyday amphitheater.
text: For or against bloodsport, you can feel Roman history with every step. What remains is so unbelievably solid that it's difficult to believe that such a place could ever fall. Schedule in advance for a guided tour. Gift shops and elevator available. Photos don't do it justice. There are busts of Nero and Julius Caesar. Additional history right on the same plaza. Heart breaking to see that some people defaced it by scratching the travertine.

This ticket also gives access to the 1st and 2nd floor of the Coliseum and the forum.

title: Amazing place, Terrible experience
text:
My wife, child and myself purchased our tickets for this attraction weeks ago and we arrived around 7:45am for our 8:45am entrance time. We asked several different people about the queuing system but could not get a straight answer. we finally found the correct place to queue around 8:00am  but when 8:15 came the place was an absolute circus with no organisation at all, when the staff finally arrived they decided that the thousands of people needed to move back 4 metres which then caused more chaos. This is hands down the worst managed mayhem to a beautiful attraction I have ever witnessed. 
What made the day worse for me was when my wife. Who is physically disabled and cannot climb stairs without assistance asked to use the elevator, she was greeted with “sorry but this is only for disabled people” I am not sure if this is something which is seen across Rome but we have never been put in the position to explain ourselves before. Hidden disabilities are actually a thing. This has left a very sour taste in our mouths and I believe needs addressed and staff members better trained to handle situations like this.

title: History Alive
text: The Colosseum is, of course, iconic and well worth your time. We chose to do an underground tour, which was a great way to "skip the line" and experience another part of the Colosseum.  There are elevators and ramps, but I question how truly accessible it is for wheelchair users. We happened to be there on first Sunday and considered going to the Collossuem for free, but by 8 am the line was wrapped around the Colosseum, so we didn't bother.  If you do a tour, there are people wearing badges with tourist  info center (or something to that extent) in blue that will help you try to find your tour groups. Finding our group was the hardest part of the process.  The underground can only be accessed with a guide so it wasn't crowded at all I believe there was only our group of 19 and a tour that was running 20 minutes ahead of us. The rest of the Collossuem is crowded, the line to the toilets looks horrendous.

title: 31st July at 9:30 AM
text:
31st July at 9:30 AM

The Colosseum remains a breathtaking historical marvel, offering a glimpse into ancient Rome's grandeur. Visiting on the 31st of July at 9:30 AM, we encountered extremely hot weather, with temperatures reaching 38°C. While the monument itself was spectacular, the experience was slightly marred by a few factors.

Firstly, the area outside the Colosseum was filled with aggressive vendors trying to sell various items, which detracted from the overall experience. Inside, despite arriving early, the site was already very crowded. We've visited before in March and found the experience more pleasant, as the crowds were smaller and the temperatures cooler. For future visits, especially with children, we recommend avoiding school holidays if possible.

The Colosseum offers minimal shade, and seating is sparse, making it challenging to escape the heat. Despite these challenges, the history and ongoing efforts to enhance the educational experience at the site are commendable. The addition of new learning opportunities and exhibits enriches each visit.

However, it should be noted that many parts of the experience now come with additional costs. From accessing the upper levels and the wooden platform to guided tours, there are many extras that weren't always in place, making the visit more expensive.

Overall, while we highly recommend visiting the Colosseum, we advise doing so outside of peak tourist seasons to avoid the crowds and heat. We rate the historical experience 4 stars, but due to the busyness and heat, the overall enjoyment was somewhat diminished. Nonetheless, it's a site worth visiting for its rich history and significance.

title: Amazing History!
text:
Having never been to Europe or Rome before, excitement levels were high and the Colosseum didn’t disappoint. We were part of a private tour group of only 6 and we were toured around the venue by Serafina. Sera was very experienced and extremely knowledge. Her attention to detail and clear communication is a credit to her. 

The Colosseum itself is quite amazing. Incredible history is felt when you walk through the outside of the venue, through the stand & out onto the arena via the Gladiators Gate. 

It was a very hot day when we visited recently, so I do recommend covering up best you can to avoid the sun and take plenty of water as there’s no real opportunity to buy whilst inside on the tour. 

A definite must when in Rome.

title: An Unmissable Historical Wonder
text:
Visiting the Colosseum in Rome is an experience you cannot miss. This imposing structure, symbol of ancient Rome, transports you back in time, giving you a taste of the grandeur of the Roman Empire. The majesty of the amphitheater is impressive both on the outside, with its mighty arches, and inside, where you can imagine the crowds that once watched gladiator shows and games.

The visit is well organized, with clear routes and detailed explanations available in several languages. I highly recommend booking a guided tour or audio guide to fully understand the history and curiosities related to the Colosseum. The view from above is breathtaking, with a breathtaking view of Rome.

The Colosseum is not only a place to visit, but an exciting experience that leaves an indelible mark. Although crowded with tourists, the charm of the place remains intact. I suggest arriving early in the morning to avoid the longer queues and enjoy the visit in a quieter way. Ultimately, the Colosseum is a must for anyone visiting Rome.

title: A Visit to The Colosseum Rome
text:
We were very excited to be able to visit this amazing place.
We booked our time slot but it was still extremely busy and crowded. 

The Colosseum is seeped in history and we downloaded an app which we could listen to as we went round but I'm think if we ever revisited we would book a tour guide.

There is no where to buy drinks or grab a snack in the Colosseum but there were water fountains outside so it's a good idea fill up water bottles before you go in. (We went in August in high temperatures, so this is something to be aware of if visiting during the hot Summer months.
Likewise there are no toilet facilities inside either . They are located outside. Unfortunately if you leave the Colosseum to go to the toilet you won't be allowed back in.

We enjoyed our visit despite the heat and crowds (We expected it to be busy as we went during the school holidays)

There is lots to read and displays to look at.

title: Must see in Rome 🇮🇹

title: Amazing but be prepared to wait
text: We booked a guided tour with “skip the line” tickets. Only the line for Skip the Line tickets went halfway around the complex and took about 35 min to get in. Once inside it was again crowded, but it’s still an amazing experience. After our tour was done, we stayed longer and got to find some more quiet areas that allowed us some breathing room.

title: Top 1 places in Rome
text: Ancient and so much history in one place. The architecture is stunning and the vibe is empowering. It’s a must if you are in Rome. Get ticket for the Arena.

title: A must see!
text:
Yes it’s extremely busy some might say even overwhelmingly busy and yes it’s boiling hot during August! However, what a site it is!! Just be prepared and you will be fine.

Only book online through the official site as they will send you email tickets instantly and they will be half the cost of unofficial/second/third party web site bookings. 

Also take a hand held fan, wear plenty of factor 50 suncream, freeze a water bottle and take it with you and have an empty bottle so you can fill up the bottle in the free water fountain, down load the app or use google maps to show you the free water fountains available all over Rome. 

Don’t walk here in the heat as you will need your energy to walk around the site, so take a taxi it only cost us 9 Euro from the Trevi fountain area to get to the colosseum or take a bus. This way you arrive with energy. 

We booked an official tour from the official web site. It was nice to have a tour, however the guide didn’t tell us anything we didn’t already know, so the knowledge given isn’t detailed or in much depth. If you don’t like heights then it might be best to not book any tour guide as they all take you to a high level. In that case you would be better off booking only the entry ticket.

Also ensure you bring your passport as ID or they won’t let you in, they will not accept a credit card or email with the tickets in its place. You must have photographic ID, so take your passports. My husband ignored this information, which is even printed in small writing on the tickets they emailed us. He thought it would be “fine” and we would get in. However, they really do ask for ID and we were stopped from entering. 

Lucky for us we had a lovely member of staff who after much begging from us ushered us through without our passports but that was pure luck! So definitely bring passports!

title: A guided tour is the way to go
text:
We did a small group tour that included the underground. We bypassed all the lines and walked right in. It’s worth the extra money. 
The place is beautiful and a must visit in Rome.

title: Emotionally Historical

title: Spectacular Colosseum visit with Underground Access
text:
Always wanted to visit Rome to see the Colosseum. Booked my holiday about 2 months beforehand and thought I had plenty of time to book my tickets on the official website.  I feel it is very confusing to buy tickets on the website.  I missed the release date for tickets for the day I wanted and tickets were sold out. I was not happy especially when third party tickets were so expensive as they included a tour guide.  I noticed that more tickets become available closer to the date when you need it for so I bided my time. I wanted to try and get tickets for the full experience with access to the arena and underground but they were like gold dust. I checked for underground tickets everyday, multiple times a day and was lucky one ticket was available to buy even though there were 2 adults and 2 children to buy tickets for.  I wasn't able to buy anymore underground tickets and bought a full experience ticket with access to the arena floor for the same day for my husband.  If you are travelling with children, you need to collect tickets for them inside the Colosseum and they are free.

On the day of our visit, the number of people visiting was unbelievable and it was a gorgeous sunny day. They will not allow you in until 15 mins of your booked time slot. The queue for the underground tour is different to the arena floor tickets, so you need to check where you need to enter. We booked for 2pm therefore the queues were not too bad. I went to the underground tour while my husband and children had arena floor access. I was in the second group to go at 2pm and there were only 7 of us while the first group was packed. The tour guide doesn't really talk, its more or less a guide giving us access to the underground. We watched a quick video of the colosseum back in the day with the gladiators and were then left to see all parts of the underground. I loved it, it was amazing to see the area where the gladiators and animals are waiting before they go on the arena floor. We were down for about 45 mins and then our tour guide took us to the arena floor where I met up with the rest of my family. 

We were able to go to every level except the attic level and every level was amazing.  The architecture and scenery was breathtaking.  We really enjoyed visiting the colosseum and it really is a must see in Rome, absolutely amazing!!  Well worth the money and if you are lucky enough to get the underground tickets, do it, even if you have to go alone like I did!! So worth it. You must remember to bring your passport as they will check.  The ticket includes entry to the Palatine Hill and Roman Forum which isn't another amazing place. You can get fantastic photos of the Colosseum in Palatine Hill/Roman Forum as it is right beside the Colosseum. Simply beautiful place.

title: Too Crowded
text: Visited the Colosseum on August 6, 2024, and it was really hot on this day. Locating our representative to get our tickets was no problem and we were actually able to get them early but could only enter 15 minutes before the scheduled time. Once inside, this place is a mad house. They should definitely limit the amount of tickets sold each day cause people were literally shoulder to shoulder trying to move around. The experience was not pleasant because trying to look around while trying to move through the crowd caused so many issues. There was way too many tour guided groups of 20-30 people filing through. I would suggest going during the off season if you want to enjoy it and actually be able to walk around. We spent about 25-30 minutes inside before we had enough of the crowd and exited. Remember to bring an umbrella or else you will bake in the sun.

title: Imperversible
text: Obviously it is Rome's number 1 Attraction along with the Roman Forum. If for your own sake, prepare for big queues. Reserve a good time for both attractions.

title: Nice outside self tour
text:
We just arrived at Rome's cruise port. We took a shared 100 euro per person cash ride from the cruise port to our hotel. It cost us 200 euro cash for our shared ride. We safely arrived at our Radisson Blu GHR hotel in Rome. We previously booked the Viator Colosseum tour. We met up with our friends at the hotel prior to our check in. In the afternoon we took Uber from our Radisson Blu GHR hotel to the Viator Colosseum Tour meeting area. We went to where the other tour guides were located. We could not find any Viator members in this meeting area based on their colored shirts. We eventually learned from other tour members that the Viator office was located up the street. We went up the street to the office waiting for our tour to start. We started our tour, but our younger tour guide announced that this was an aggressive working tour. The tour was for the more aggressive expert touring so we gave up after about our first 15 minutes of walking Rome’s historical streets, since we were not as fit as our aggressive Viator working tour guide. Our friends said they would complain to Viator about the misrepresentation of the tour on their website. Nevertheless we had the opportunity to tour the outside of the colosseum free of charge, but not the inside of the colosseum. 

The Colosseum (Colosseo) is an elliptical amphitheater in the center of the city of Rome, Italy, just east of the Roman Forum. It is the largest ancient amphitheater ever built, and is still the largest standing amphitheater in the world, despite its age. Construction began under the Emperor Vespasian 69–79 AD. It was completed in AD 80 under his successor and heir, Titus. We were not happy with this Viator tour, but we were impressed by the Colosseum from the outside, therefore I rated this place as 5 stars. See my several pics.

We eventually stopped at a nearby restaurant for some local draft beer and snacks before we returned to our hotel via a taxi from the Colosseum. We had to walk up to a major street to get the attention of an empty taxi. Tomorrow is our last full day in Rome, therefore we already booked Viator’s tour of the Vatican, which I am looking forward to. More to come regarding our tour of the Vatican.

title: A visit to history
text:

title: Well worth a visit

title: Colosseum

title: Basic ticket no longer allows a meaningful view of the site
text:
Unbelievably awful relative to how it used to be.

The basic ticket is now restricted to a route that actively prevents you from actually seeing the Colosseum!!!!

You used to be able to access the Colosseum floor level and appreciate the sheer scale of the stadium above you.

Now... you're bottled into a route that winds you up and around the exterior of the building, in narrow confines, being pushed and shoved through a route that has been designed to feel claustrophobic throughout and where you can't see any of the "exhibits" due to the over-crowding (most of them are filler anyway, much less impressive than the building you're being prevented from properly seeing).

At the very end of your route, you'll have a chance to fight a massive scrum of people for a chance to see the Colosseum how you used to be able to see it.

The "pay extra" tickets allow you to see what you used to get for standard price.

Essentially, elitists have taken over the building and created an experience that is far more pleasant for the rich and actively harmful to the poor.  The whole thing is now money-grubbing and plutocratic by design, but I guess the conservators have captured some of the truth of what a visit to the Colosseum would have been like for the Roman plebs - crowded, uncomfortable, and you can't see Jack Sh1t.

title: "Echoes of Glory: Discovering the Majesty of the Colum"
text:
The Colosseum in Rome is a breathtaking testament to ancient architecture history, and stepping inside is like stepping in time to the era of gladiators and grand spectacles. Its iconic exterior, with its majestic arches and weathered stone, evokes a sense of awe, while the interior design showcases remarkable engineering that has withstood the test of time. 

The remote yet vibrant atmosphere surrounding the Colosseum enhances its historical significance, transporting visitors to a place where thrilling battles once captivated crowds. Wandering through the remnants of this once-grand arena, you can almost hear the echoes of the past, making it an unforgettable experience for history enthusiasts and casual visitors alike. The blend of perfect design and rich storytelling solidifies the Colosseum as a must-visit landmark steeped in ancient glory.

title: An incredible experience…if not for the nasty rude staff
text:
The Colosseum is something that needs to be seen in person, in order to fully appreciate.

Pretty incredible to witness this structure from the inside and outside. There is also plenty of information made available inside the experience for customers to fully appreciate the significance of this structure. A must-see in Rome.

===

While the Colosseum was an impressive feat (once you do get the tickets and come at the right time), the staff managing it was the exact opposite.

First of all,  when we first arrived, it was raining heavily, and it was physically difficult to see where the ticket office was in the heavily pouring rain. Amongst the chaos, one of the staff members at the entrance shoved us into the pre-security screening area. Assuming we needed to get our belongings screened before proceeding to buy a ticket, we completed that step. Once in, we tried to buy tickets. To our surprise, we somehow passed the point where we could buy tickets, but before the point where they scanned tickets. When we asked nearby staff how to buy tickets, instead of helping direct us, they told us we should have already had tickets, and they suddenly became hostile towards us.

Once realising we may be in the wrong place (and not being able to buy the tickets online right away), we asked if we could exit so we could find the booth to buy the tickets. One staff let us out while simultaneously accusing us of forcing our way in and cheating them. When we tried to explain what happened, they just immediately cut us off, told us to get out and ignored us. I’ve never witnessed such incredibly rude and nasty staff in Europe. Especially given the fact that it was THEM who got us in that area in the first place, and we maintained being polite to them. The staff treat the customers like scum. They could not care less. These people give Italians the bad attitude reputation that they unfortunately carry. Shame on them.

title: Genuinely breathtaking,  honestly awe inspiring place.
text:
Our first glimpse of this amazing place was on an open top bus as we toured the city the day before our booking and all it did was add to our sense of excitement about our planned tour. 

Booking in advance is the only real way to get in without significant queues, but if you do this it is important to stick to your entry time (you have 15 minutes have either side of the stated time), we saw people turned away for being significantly late.  Airport style security for all too but once inside it's well worth it. 

We got a ticket which covered the Colloseum, Roman Forum and Palatine Hill.  Allow most of a day to do the whole lot.  We spent half a day and there was still so much more to see. 

The Colloseum itself is, frankly,  incredible. You just don't see something like this very often in your life.  2000 years old, so much still standing,  and with the opportunity to actually touch it and experience it first hand without glass walls and from a distance.  

There are options for those with mobility issues and a lift is available which will get you to most of the site. 

Toilets are also available but always worth long queues. There is a bookshop and giftshop too

title: Guided tour in the basement
text:
It was a really interesting visit, with many historical, architectural and engineering details. We saw for example the shafts of the elevators that led to the arena (there were 80), we found that in reality the gladiators until the third century were the slaves that no master wanted to buy because they were ugly and sick (the hunters were the stars of the arena), that among the condemned to death who were mauled by fairs tax evaders were well represented (in ancient Rome not paying taxes was considered one of the worst crimes), and a lot of other information. 
Note that out of 22 people who made up our group, only 4 were Italian plus one who had studied it at school, the other 17 did not understand a word, they had booked this tour only because those in English were sold out. The Guide told us that it often happens that there is no one who speaks Italian.
After the guided tour in the basement (which takes about 70 minutes) you can visit the rest of the Colosseum on your own (apart from the penthouse which requires another guided tour).
Really interesting and recommended if you can book.

title: Free access to Colosseum on 1st Sunday of the month
text:
Do not be alarmed by the long snaking queue of people to obtain the free ticket for entry into the Colosseum. We reached the Colosseum at 3 pm. We joined the queue at the front of the Colosseum just after we came out of Metro Colosseo station and the queue kept moving. After about 35 minutes, we were given an entry ticket. We followed the queue to reach the entrance. We tapped the ticket on the gantry and there was a quick security check. There was another ticket tap at another device and we are finally in the Colosseum. 

The free ticket allowed us access to the top of the Colosseum too. 

The road just outside the Metro is closed so no vehicle was allowed to access it. Initially, we wanted to take a bus in front of the Metro to go to Pantheon.

title: Unmissable
text: The Colosseum is a marvel that transports you to ancient Rome with its impressive structure and history. If you want to make the most of your visit, I recommend booking tickets in advance and choosing the first shift, very early, to avoid the crowds. Also, passing through the area again at sunset or at night is a must: seeing the Colosseum illuminated and taking photos at that moment is magical. This Roman icon is an incredible experience, day and night. Unmissable!

title: Avoid purchasing tickets from scammers around the Colosseum.
text: You can buy a skip-the-line ticket on the official Colosseum website. If these tickets are no longer available, definitely do not purchase “tickets” from scalpers around the Colosseum; instead, wait in line yourself. Scalpers are not allowed to sell any type of ticket, and they certainly do not have access to skip-the-line tickets. For a fee of 30 Euros and with misleading or false information, they will only offer a service to accompany you to the ticket office, where you still have to purchase a ticket yourself (you will need to provide an ID, passport, driver’s license, etc.). A combined ticket for the Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum costs 18 Euros. The service offered by these scammers is misleading—they are not allowed to guide tours. They might tell you something about the monument outside for 30 Euros, but they won’t accompany you inside. You won’t learn anything from them that you couldn’t find on Wikipedia. The Colosseum, Palatine Hill, and Roman Forum are definitely worth visiting, but avoid these scammers who only damage the reputation of Rome’s landmarks.

title: Amazing building, Very crowded. Worth doing once.  Special tickets are required for arena access

The visit experience is a bit overwhelming given the sheer volume of tourists visiting and in summer the heat as there is limited shade. Definitely take water and there is opportunity on the first level to buy more

We had been to the colosseum many years ago and would probably not return again. 

It is worth doing once but be prepared!

title: A Truly Iconic Experience at the Colosseum
text:
Visiting the Colosseum was a highlight of our trip to Rome, especially with the Arena Full Experience tickets we booked. The entire experience felt well-organized from start to finish. We were able to explore not only the Colosseum itself but also the nearby Palatine Hill and Roman Forum with this same ticket—a fantastic added value!

For anyone planning to visit, it’s worth noting that there are specialized tickets if you’re interested in seeing the Atrium or the Underground, which weren’t part of our visit. Similarly, if a guided tour is on your list, remember to select tickets that include this, as our self-guided experience allowed us to roam freely but might not satisfy those looking for historical details. We noticed that if you're an avid history lover, using an audio guide, such as those from Rick Steves Audio Europe, can be a great way to gain insights at your own pace.

Though we booked our tickets a week ahead, purchasing at least a month in advance is generally recommended, especially through the Colosseum's official site, Tripadvisor or GetYourGuide to avoid scams. Since it wasn’t overly crowded, we didn’t need the skip-the-line option, but this might be helpful for peak times. For budget travelers, it’s good to remember that entry is free on the first Sunday of each month, though crowds are likely heavier on those days.

Security was smooth and efficient, like airport checks. Without a guide, we had all the time we wanted for photos and to absorb the atmosphere of this ancient marvel. A souvenir shop inside offers a range of memorabilia for anyone looking to take a piece of Rome back home.

Seeing one of the wonders of the world in person was beyond words—standing in the arena where history was made so long ago felt surreal and deeply moving. Highly recommend adding the Colosseum to your bucket list for anyone visiting Rome; it’s truly worth experiencing at least once in your life!

P.S. Wear comfortable shoes—the historic grounds and stairs can be a workout!

title: Ruined by rude staff
text:
I enjoyed my visit to the Roman Forum I felt underwhelmed with the Colosseum. I booked the first time slot. 
However, My digital ticket did not say I had to bring my passport so I did get turned away at first. 
I do believe the staff could handle it better rather than being extremely rude and abrupt. The staff member I went to first kept saying “passport “. 
I then explained I could log into my bank to prove my identity. He said “yes show me” , he stood and watched as I logged into my banking to prove my name matched the ticket, he then did a chuckle and went “no”. 
All this whilst people were watching me. 
The second member of staff still wasn’t the nicest but explained it better to me, he allowed me to stand to the side and get a photo of any photo id I had. I eventually showed a picture of my drivers license. 
The staff are really cold and unwelcoming unfortunately it put me in such an upset  mood and tainted my experience as I felt I had been made a spectacle of.

title: Mesmerized by the old colosseum
text:
Rome Colosseum

Visiting the Colosseum in Rome is truly an unforgettable experience! This ancient amphitheater, dating back to 70-80 AD, is awe-inspiring with its massive structure and fascinating history. Walking through the arches and seeing the remnants of where gladiators once battled gives a vivid sense of Rome’s rich past. 

The Colosseum is well-preserved considering its age, and I’d recommend getting a guided tour to understand more about its significance, including details on the architecture, events, and Roman society. The views from the upper levels are spectacular, offering a glimpse over Rome and the nearby Roman Forum.

**Tips**: 
1. Buy tickets in advance to skip the long lines.
2. Go early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the crowds.
3. Be sure to wear comfortable shoes, as you’ll be doing a lot of walking on uneven surfaces.

Overall, the Colosseum is a must-visit historical site that brings the grandeur of ancient Rome to life!

title: Magnificent
text:

title: Squashed in
text:
After touring Castel Sant Angelo the day prior, this place as a disappointment in terms of the overall experience.

Obviously it is tremendously busy and I couldn’t tell you how to make it less chaotic than it is, but be under no illusion, it is chaos. Be sure to book a ticket in advance and take your passports as these will be checked. 

People are just totally crammed in - I get it, the place is huge and can fit plenty of people but it was just so unpleasant. I was glad for decent security screening (airport style).

The views from the upper level across the arena are good, excellent really,  and there is full access up there, but again it is packed full. Tour group upon tour group shuffling about with headphones in. 

I’m glad I’ve been and I’m glad my kids have seen it, it is worth doing but the experience wasn’t fun and I can’t foresee a circumstance that I would go back.

Tip - if you are also doing Forum and Palatine Hill, head down the street a little for access there. There’s still a queue but not as big as the one closer to Colosseo.

title: Attic - a must.

title: Bad service ever!!!
text:
If possible, I wouldn't give a single star! Worst service ever!
We booked tickets for the Colosseum through Viator (€179 for 3 adults!) and showed up at the announced redemption point 15 minutes before! The queue was so long that it took us more than 45 minutes to get to the only employee on site, who unfortunately had to tell us that our tickets were not there! Instead of dealing with the matter, we were literally left behind and other customers served; only after decisive inquiries on our part did she try to find a solution. At 11.15 we finally got a mail with the tickets and 1 minute later another one with the note our tickets had expired! I have to say that I especially Considering the price, have never experienced a worse service! 
So the visit to the Colosseum never happened for us despite almost €180 and I will never book with Viator again! What a shame!
